sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।एवं निपातिते कर्णे समरे सव्यसाचिना ।अल्पावशिष्टाः कुरवः किमकुर्वत वै द्विज ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,evaṁ nipātite karṇe samare savyasācinā ,alpāvaśiṣṭāḥ kuravaḥ kimakurvata vai dvija.
english: Janamejaya said: "O brahmin (dvija), when Karṇa was thus struck down in battle by Arjuna (Savyasācin), what did the few remaining Kuru warriors then do?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "O brahmin (dvija), when Karṇa was thus struck down in battle by Arjuna (Savyasācin), what did the few remaining Kuru warriors then do?"

sanskrit: उदीर्यमाणं च बलं दृष्ट्वा राजा सुयोधनः ।पाण्डवैः प्राप्तकालं च किं प्रापद्यत कौरवः ॥२॥
transliteration: udīryamāṇaṁ ca balaṁ dṛṣṭvā rājā suyodhanaḥ ,pāṇḍavaiḥ prāptakālaṁ ca kiṁ prāpadyata kauravaḥ.
english: And seeing the army being roused by the Pāṇḍavas, which was timely, what course of action did King Duryodhana (Suyodhana), the Kuru prince, then adopt?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. And seeing the army being roused by the Pāṇḍavas, which was timely, what course of action did King Duryodhana (Suyodhana), the Kuru prince, then adopt?

sanskrit: एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं तदाचक्ष्व द्विजोत्तम ।न हि तृप्यामि पूर्वेषां शृण्वानश्चरितं महत् ॥३॥
transliteration: etadicchāmyahaṁ śrotuṁ tadācakṣva dvijottama ,na hi tṛpyāmi pūrveṣāṁ śṛṇvānaścaritaṁ mahat.
english: I wish to hear this; therefore, narrate it, O best of brahmins (dvijottama)! Indeed, I am never satisfied hearing the great deeds of the ancients.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. I wish to hear this; therefore, narrate it, O best of brahmins (dvijottama)! Indeed, I am never satisfied hearing the great deeds of the ancients.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः कर्णे हते राजन्धार्तराष्ट्रः सुयोधनः ।भृशं शोकार्णवे मग्नो निराशः सर्वतोऽभवत् ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ karṇe hate rājandhārtarāṣṭraḥ suyodhanaḥ ,bhṛśaṁ śokārṇave magno nirāśaḥ sarvato'bhavat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O king, then, when Karṇa was slain, Duryodhana (Suyodhana), the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Dhārtarāṣṭra), was deeply plunged into an ocean of grief, completely hopeless from all sides."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: "O king, then, when Karṇa was slain, Duryodhana (Suyodhana), the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Dhārtarāṣṭra), was deeply plunged into an ocean of grief, completely hopeless from all sides."

sanskrit: हा कर्ण हा कर्ण इति शोचमानः पुनः पुनः ।कृच्छ्रात्स्वशिबिरं प्रायाद्धतशेषैर्नृपैः सह ॥५॥
transliteration: hā karṇa hā karṇa iti śocamānaḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,kṛcchrātsvaśibiraṁ prāyāddhataśeṣairnṛpaiḥ saha.
english: Lamenting "Alas, Karṇa! Alas, Karṇa!" repeatedly, he returned with great difficulty to his own camp, accompanied by the kings who had survived the slaughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Lamenting "Alas, Karṇa! Alas, Karṇa!" repeatedly, he returned with great difficulty to his own camp, accompanied by the kings who had survived the slaughter.

sanskrit: स समाश्वास्यमानोऽपि हेतुभिः शास्त्रनिश्चितैः ।राजभिर्नालभच्छर्म सूतपुत्रवधं स्मरन् ॥६॥
transliteration: sa samāśvāsyamāno'pi hetubhiḥ śāstraniścitaiḥ ,rājabhirnālabhaccharma sūtaputravadhaṁ smaran.
english: Even though he was being consoled by kings with arguments established by the sacred texts (śāstra), he found no peace, remembering the killing of Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. Even though he was being consoled by kings with arguments established by the sacred texts (śāstra), he found no peace, remembering the killing of Karṇa.

sanskrit: स दैवं बलवन्मत्वा भवितव्यं च पार्थिवः ।संग्रामे निश्चयं कृत्वा पुनर्युद्धाय निर्ययौ ॥७॥
transliteration: sa daivaṁ balavanmatvā bhavitavyaṁ ca pārthivaḥ ,saṁgrāme niścayaṁ kṛtvā punaryuddhāya niryayau.
english: Considering destiny (daiva) and what must inevitably occur to be potent, that king (pārthiva), having made a firm resolution for battle, again set forth for war.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Considering destiny (daiva) and what must inevitably occur to be potent, that king (pārthiva), having made a firm resolution for battle, again set forth for war.

sanskrit: शल्यं सेनापतिं कृत्वा विधिवद्राजपुंगवः ।रणाय निर्ययौ राजा हतशेषैर्नृपैः सह ॥८॥
transliteration: śalyaṁ senāpatiṁ kṛtvā vidhivadrājapuṁgavaḥ ,raṇāya niryayau rājā hataśeṣairnṛpaiḥ saha.
english: Having properly appointed Śalya as commander-in-chief, that chief among kings (rājapuṅgava), the king, set out for battle accompanied by the surviving kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. Having properly appointed Śalya as commander-in-chief, that chief among kings (rājapuṅgava), the king, set out for battle accompanied by the surviving kings.

sanskrit: ततः सुतुमुलं युद्धं कुरुपाण्डवसेनयोः ।बभूव भरतश्रेष्ठ देवासुररणोपमम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ sutumulaṁ yuddhaṁ kurupāṇḍavasenayoḥ ,babhūva bharataśreṣṭha devāsuraraṇopamam.
english: Then, O best among the Bharatas, a very tumultuous battle ensued between the armies of the Kurus and Pandavas, resembling the war between the devas and asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Then, O best among the Bharatas, a very tumultuous battle ensued between the armies of the Kurus and Pandavas, resembling the war between the devas and asuras.

sanskrit: ततः शल्यो महाराज कृत्वा कदनमाहवे ।पाण्डुसैन्यस्य मध्याह्ने धर्मराजेन पातितः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ śalyo mahārāja kṛtvā kadanamāhave ,pāṇḍusainyasya madhyāhne dharmarājena pātitaḥ.
english: Then, O great king, Śalya, having caused great slaughter to the Pandava army in battle, was struck down by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) at midday.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. Then, O great king, Śalya, having caused great slaughter to the Pandava army in battle, was struck down by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira) at midday.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा हतबन्धू रणाजिरात् ।अपसृत्य ह्रदं घोरं विवेश रिपुजाद्भयात् ॥११॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā hatabandhū raṇājirāt ,apasṛtya hradaṁ ghoraṁ viveśa ripujādbhayāt.
english: Then, King Duryodhana, whose kinsmen had been slain, retreated from the battlefield and, out of fear of his enemies, entered a dreadful lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. Then, King Duryodhana, whose kinsmen had been slain, retreated from the battlefield and, out of fear of his enemies, entered a dreadful lake.

sanskrit: अथापराह्णे तस्याह्नः परिवार्य महारथैः ।ह्रदादाहूय योगेन भीमसेनेन पातितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: athāparāhṇe tasyāhnaḥ parivārya mahārathaiḥ ,hradādāhūya yogena bhīmasenena pātitaḥ.
english: Then, in the afternoon of that same day, having been surrounded by great charioteers and summoned from the lake, he was struck down by Bhīmasena through strategic skill (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. Then, in the afternoon of that same day, having been surrounded by great charioteers and summoned from the lake, he was struck down by Bhīmasena through strategic skill (yoga).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्हते महेष्वासे हतशिष्टास्त्रयो रथाः ।संरभान्निशि राजेन्द्र जघ्नुः पाञ्चालसैनिकान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasminhate maheṣvāse hataśiṣṭāstrayo rathāḥ ,saṁrabhānniśi rājendra jaghnuḥ pāñcālasainikān.
english: O king (rāja-indra), after that great archer was slain, the three surviving charioteers, filled with rage, killed the Pañcāla soldiers during the night.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. O king (rāja-indra), after that great archer was slain, the three surviving charioteers, filled with rage, killed the Pañcāla soldiers during the night.

sanskrit: ततः पूर्वाह्णसमये शिबिरादेत्य संजयः ।प्रविवेश पुरीं दीनो दुःखशोकसमन्वितः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ pūrvāhṇasamaye śibirādetya saṁjayaḥ ,praviveśa purīṁ dīno duḥkhaśokasamanvitaḥ.
english: Then, in the forenoon, Saṃjaya, having arrived from the camp, entered the city, dejected and overwhelmed with sorrow and grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. Then, in the forenoon, Saṃjaya, having arrived from the camp, entered the city, dejected and overwhelmed with sorrow and grief.

sanskrit: प्रविश्य च पुरं तूर्णं भुजावुच्छ्रित्य दुःखितः ।वेपमानस्ततो राज्ञः प्रविवेश निवेशनम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: praviśya ca puraṁ tūrṇaṁ bhujāvucchritya duḥkhitaḥ ,vepamānastato rājñaḥ praviveśa niveśanam.
english: And having quickly entered the city, he, distressed and raising his arms, then, trembling, entered the king's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. And having quickly entered the city, he, distressed and raising his arms, then, trembling, entered the king's abode.

sanskrit: रुरोद च नरव्याघ्र हा राजन्निति दुःखितः ।अहो बत विविग्नाः स्म निधनेन महात्मनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ruroda ca naravyāghra hā rājanniti duḥkhitaḥ ,aho bata vivignāḥ sma nidhanena mahātmanaḥ.
english: And distressed, he wept, exclaiming, 'Alas, O tiger among men (nara-vyāghra), O king! Oh, alas, we are greatly distressed by the demise of the great soul (mahā-ātman)!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. And distressed, he wept, exclaiming, 'Alas, O tiger among men (nara-vyāghra), O king! Oh, alas, we are greatly distressed by the demise of the great soul (mahā-ātman)!'

sanskrit: अहो सुबलवान्कालो गतिश्च परमा तथा ।शक्रतुल्यबलाः सर्वे यत्रावध्यन्त पार्थिवाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: aho subalavānkālo gatiśca paramā tathā ,śakratulyabalāḥ sarve yatrāvadhyanta pārthivāḥ.
english: Alas, how immensely powerful is time, and how supreme is its movement (gati)! For it was during this time that all kings, whose strength was equal to that of Indra (Śakra), were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. Alas, how immensely powerful is time, and how supreme is its movement (gati)! For it was during this time that all kings, whose strength was equal to that of Indra (Śakra), were slain.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वैव च पुरो राजञ्जनः सर्वः स संजयम् ।प्ररुरोद भृशोद्विग्नो हा राजन्निति सस्वरम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvaiva ca puro rājañjanaḥ sarvaḥ sa saṁjayam ,praruroda bhṛśodvigno hā rājanniti sasvaram.
english: O King, as soon as all those people saw Sanjaya before them, they, deeply distressed, wept loudly with cries of "Alas, O King!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. O King, as soon as all those people saw Sanjaya before them, they, deeply distressed, wept loudly with cries of "Alas, O King!"

sanskrit: आकुमारं नरव्याघ्र तत्पुरं वै समन्ततः ।आर्तनादं महच्चक्रे श्रुत्वा विनिहतं नृपम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: ākumāraṁ naravyāghra tatpuraṁ vai samantataḥ ,ārtanādaṁ mahaccakre śrutvā vinihataṁ nṛpam.
english: O tiger among men, that city, from all directions and even down to its children, raised a great cry of agony (ārtanāda), having heard that the king had been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. O tiger among men, that city, from all directions and even down to its children, raised a great cry of agony (ārtanāda), having heard that the king had been killed.

sanskrit: धावतश्चाप्यपश्यच्च तत्र त्रीन्पुरुषर्षभान् ।नष्टचित्तानिवोन्मत्ताञ्शोकेन भृशपीडितान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: dhāvataścāpyapaśyacca tatra trīnpuruṣarṣabhān ,naṣṭacittānivonmattāñśokena bhṛśapīḍitān.
english: And there, he also saw three foremost men (puruṣarṣabhān) who were running, appearing like madmen, their minds distraught and intensely afflicted by sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. And there, he also saw three foremost men (puruṣarṣabhān) who were running, appearing like madmen, their minds distraught and intensely afflicted by sorrow.

sanskrit: तथा स विह्वलः सूतः प्रविश्य नृपतिक्षयम् ।ददर्श नृपतिश्रेष्ठं प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमीश्वरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathā sa vihvalaḥ sūtaḥ praviśya nṛpatikṣayam ,dadarśa nṛpatiśreṣṭhaṁ prajñācakṣuṣamīśvaram.
english: Thus, the distraught charioteer, upon entering the king's chambers, saw the foremost king, the ruler who possessed the eye of wisdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. Thus, the distraught charioteer, upon entering the king's chambers, saw the foremost king, the ruler who possessed the eye of wisdom.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा चासीनमनघं समन्तात्परिवारितम् ।स्नुषाभिर्भरतश्रेष्ठ गान्धार्या विदुरेण च ॥२२॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā cāsīnamanaghaṁ samantātparivāritam ,snuṣābhirbharataśreṣṭha gāndhāryā vidureṇa ca.
english: And seeing him, the faultless one, seated and surrounded on all sides by his daughters-in-law, by Gāndhārī, and by Vidura, O foremost of the Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. And seeing him, the faultless one, seated and surrounded on all sides by his daughters-in-law, by Gāndhārī, and by Vidura, O foremost of the Bharatas.

sanskrit: तथान्यैश्च सुहृद्भिश्च ज्ञातिभिश्च हितैषिभिः ।तमेव चार्थं ध्यायन्तं कर्णस्य निधनं प्रति ॥२३॥
transliteration: tathānyaiśca suhṛdbhiśca jñātibhiśca hitaiṣibhiḥ ,tameva cārthaṁ dhyāyantaṁ karṇasya nidhanaṁ prati.
english: And furthermore, he was surrounded by other friends, kinsmen, and well-wishers; and the king himself was intently pondering that very matter concerning Karṇa's demise.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. And furthermore, he was surrounded by other friends, kinsmen, and well-wishers; and the king himself was intently pondering that very matter concerning Karṇa's demise.

sanskrit: रुदन्नेवाब्रवीद्वाक्यं राजानं जनमेजय ।नातिहृष्टमनाः सूतो बाष्पसंदिग्धया गिरा ॥२४॥
transliteration: rudannevābravīdvākyaṁ rājānaṁ janamejaya ,nātihṛṣṭamanāḥ sūto bāṣpasaṁdigdhayā girā.
english: O Janamejaya, the charioteer, weeping and with a heavy heart, spoke these words to the king in a voice choked with tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 24. O Janamejaya, the charioteer, weeping and with a heavy heart, spoke these words to the king in a voice choked with tears.

sanskrit: संजयोऽहं नरव्याघ्र नमस्ते भरतर्षभ ।मद्राधिपो हतः शल्यः शकुनिः सौबलस्तथा ।उलूकः पुरुषव्याघ्र कैतव्यो दृढविक्रमः ॥२५॥
transliteration: saṁjayo'haṁ naravyāghra namaste bharatarṣabha ,madrādhipo hataḥ śalyaḥ śakuniḥ saubalastathā ,ulūkaḥ puruṣavyāghra kaitavyo dṛḍhavikramaḥ.
english: O tiger among men (naravyāghra), O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), I, Sañjaya, offer my salutations to you. The king of the Madras, Śalya, has been killed. So have Śakuni, son of Subala, Ulūka, the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), and Kaitavya, who possessed firm valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 25. O tiger among men (naravyāghra), O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), I, Sañjaya, offer my salutations to you. The king of the Madras, Śalya, has been killed. So have Śakuni, son of Subala, Ulūka, the tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), and Kaitavya, who possessed firm valor.

sanskrit: संशप्तका हताः सर्वे काम्बोजाश्च शकैः सह ।म्लेच्छाश्च पार्वतीयाश्च यवनाश्च निपातिताः ॥२६॥
transliteration: saṁśaptakā hatāḥ sarve kāmbojāśca śakaiḥ saha ,mlecchāśca pārvatīyāśca yavanāśca nipātitāḥ.
english: All the Saṃśaptakas have been slain, and the Kāmbhojas along with the Śakas. The Mlecchas, the Pārvatīyas, and the Yavanas have also been struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 26. All the Saṃśaptakas have been slain, and the Kāmbhojas along with the Śakas. The Mlecchas, the Pārvatīyas, and the Yavanas have also been struck down.

sanskrit: प्राच्या हता महाराज दाक्षिणात्याश्च सर्वशः ।उदीच्या निहताः सर्वे प्रतीच्याश्च नराधिप ।राजानो राजपुत्राश्च सर्वतो निहता नृप ॥२७॥
transliteration: prācyā hatā mahārāja dākṣiṇātyāśca sarvaśaḥ ,udīcyā nihatāḥ sarve pratīcyāśca narādhipa ,rājāno rājaputrāśca sarvato nihatā nṛpa.
english: O great king (mahārāja), the Easterners have been slain, and the Southerners entirely. O lord of men (narādhipa), all the Northerners have been killed, and the Westerners too. O king (nṛpa), kings and princes everywhere have been slaughtered.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 27. O great king (mahārāja), the Easterners have been slain, and the Southerners entirely. O lord of men (narādhipa), all the Northerners have been killed, and the Westerners too. O king (nṛpa), kings and princes everywhere have been slaughtered.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो हतो राजन्यथोक्तं पाण्डवेन च ।भग्नसक्थो महाराज शेते पांसुषु रूषितः ॥२८॥
transliteration: duryodhano hato rājanyathoktaṁ pāṇḍavena ca ,bhagnasaktho mahārāja śete pāṁsuṣu rūṣitaḥ.
english: O king (rājan), Duryodhana has been slain, just as the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) had prophesied. O great king (mahārāja), with his thighs broken, he lies in the dust, completely smeared with it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 28. O king (rājan), Duryodhana has been slain, just as the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) had prophesied. O great king (mahārāja), with his thighs broken, he lies in the dust, completely smeared with it.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नो हतो राजञ्शिखण्डी चापराजितः ।उत्तमौजा युधामन्युस्तथा राजन्प्रभद्रकाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumno hato rājañśikhaṇḍī cāparājitaḥ ,uttamaujā yudhāmanyustathā rājanprabhadrakāḥ.
english: O King, Dhṛṣṭadyumna has been slain, and Śikhaṇḍī, the unconquered. Uttamaujas, Yudhamanyu, and also the Prabhadrakas have (likewise) been slain, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 29. O King, Dhṛṣṭadyumna has been slain, and Śikhaṇḍī, the unconquered. Uttamaujas, Yudhamanyu, and also the Prabhadrakas have (likewise) been slain, O king.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालाश्च नरव्याघ्राश्चेदयश्च निषूदिताः ।तव पुत्रा हताः सर्वे द्रौपदेयाश्च भारत ।कर्णपुत्रो हतः शूरो वृषसेनो महाबलः ॥३०॥
transliteration: pāñcālāśca naravyāghrāścedayaśca niṣūditāḥ ,tava putrā hatāḥ sarve draupadeyāśca bhārata ,karṇaputro hataḥ śūro vṛṣaseno mahābalaḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata, the Pañcālas and the tiger-like warriors, along with the Cedis, have been annihilated. All your sons, and also the sons of Draupadī, have been slain. Karna's brave and mighty son, Vṛṣasena, has also been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 30. O descendant of Bharata, the Pañcālas and the tiger-like warriors, along with the Cedis, have been annihilated. All your sons, and also the sons of Draupadī, have been slain. Karna's brave and mighty son, Vṛṣasena, has also been killed.

sanskrit: नरा विनिहताः सर्वे गजाश्च विनिपातिताः ।रथिनश्च नरव्याघ्र हयाश्च निहता युधि ॥३१॥
transliteration: narā vinihatāḥ sarve gajāśca vinipātitāḥ ,rathinaśca naravyāghra hayāśca nihatā yudhi.
english: O tiger among men, all men have been completely slain, and elephants have been felled. Charioteers and horses have also been killed in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 31. O tiger among men, all men have been completely slain, and elephants have been felled. Charioteers and horses have also been killed in the battle.

sanskrit: किंचिच्छेषं च शिबिरं तावकानां कृतं विभो ।पाण्डवानां च शूराणां समासाद्य परस्परम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: kiṁciccheṣaṁ ca śibiraṁ tāvakānāṁ kṛtaṁ vibho ,pāṇḍavānāṁ ca śūrāṇāṁ samāsādya parasparam.
english: O lord, your camp has been left with only a small remnant. And (the camp) of the brave Pāṇḍavas too (has suffered greatly), after they confronted each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 32. O lord, your camp has been left with only a small remnant. And (the camp) of the brave Pāṇḍavas too (has suffered greatly), after they confronted each other.

sanskrit: प्रायः स्त्रीशेषमभवज्जगत्कालेन मोहितम् ।सप्त पाण्डवतः शेषा धार्तराष्ट्रास्तथा त्रयः ॥३३॥
transliteration: prāyaḥ strīśeṣamabhavajjagatkālena mohitam ,sapta pāṇḍavataḥ śeṣā dhārtarāṣṭrāstathā trayaḥ.
english: Almost all the world (jagat) became one where only women remained, overwhelmed by time (kāla). Seven from the Pāṇḍavas survived, and likewise, three from the Dhṛtarāṣṭras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 33. Almost all the world (jagat) became one where only women remained, overwhelmed by time (kāla). Seven from the Pāṇḍavas survived, and likewise, three from the Dhṛtarāṣṭras.

sanskrit: ते चैव भ्रातरः पञ्च वासुदेवोऽथ सात्यकिः ।कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च द्रौणिश्च जयतां वरः ॥३४॥
transliteration: te caiva bhrātaraḥ pañca vāsudevo'tha sātyakiḥ ,kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca drauṇiśca jayatāṁ varaḥ.
english: And those five brothers, along with Vāsudeva and Sātyaki; as well as Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and Drauṇi, the foremost among the victors (jayatām).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 34. And those five brothers, along with Vāsudeva and Sātyaki; as well as Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and Drauṇi, the foremost among the victors (jayatām).

sanskrit: तवाप्येते महाराज रथिनो नृपसत्तम ।अक्षौहिणीनां सर्वासां समेतानां जनेश्वर ।एते शेषा महाराज सर्वेऽन्ये निधनं गताः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tavāpyete mahārāja rathino nṛpasattama ,akṣauhiṇīnāṁ sarvāsāṁ sametānāṁ janeśvara ,ete śeṣā mahārāja sarve'nye nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), O best among kings (nṛpasattama), these charioteers are also from your side. O lord of people (janeśvara), from all the assembled akṣauhiṇīs, O great king, these are the only ones remaining; all others have met their demise.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 35. O great king (mahārāja), O best among kings (nṛpasattama), these charioteers are also from your side. O lord of people (janeśvara), from all the assembled akṣauhiṇīs, O great king, these are the only ones remaining; all others have met their demise.

sanskrit: कालेन निहतं सर्वं जगद्वै भरतर्षभ ।दुर्योधनं वै पुरतः कृत्वा वैरस्य भारत ॥३६॥
transliteration: kālena nihataṁ sarvaṁ jagadvai bharatarṣabha ,duryodhanaṁ vai purataḥ kṛtvā vairasya bhārata.
english: Indeed, O best among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), all of creation (jagat) has been destroyed by time (kāla). O Bhārata, this happened because Duryodhana was placed at the forefront of the hostility.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 36. Indeed, O best among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), all of creation (jagat) has been destroyed by time (kāla). O Bhārata, this happened because Duryodhana was placed at the forefront of the hostility.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचः क्रूरं धृतराष्ट्रो जनेश्वरः ।निपपात महाराज गतसत्त्वो महीतले ॥३७॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā vacaḥ krūraṁ dhṛtarāṣṭro janeśvaraḥ ,nipapāta mahārāja gatasattvo mahītale.
english: O great king, having heard this cruel speech, Dhritarashtra, the lord of men, fell to the ground, lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 37. O great king, having heard this cruel speech, Dhritarashtra, the lord of men, fell to the ground, lifeless.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते भूमौ विदुरोऽपि महायशाः ।निपपात महाराज राजव्यसनकर्शितः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite bhūmau viduro'pi mahāyaśāḥ ,nipapāta mahārāja rājavyasanakarśitaḥ.
english: O great king, when he (Dhritarashtra) had fallen to the ground, even the highly renowned Vidura, afflicted by the king's great sorrow, also fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 38. O great king, when he (Dhritarashtra) had fallen to the ground, even the highly renowned Vidura, afflicted by the king's great sorrow, also fell.

sanskrit: गान्धारी च नृपश्रेष्ठ सर्वाश्च कुरुयोषितः ।पतिताः सहसा भूमौ श्रुत्वा क्रूरं वचश्च ताः ॥३९॥
transliteration: gāndhārī ca nṛpaśreṣṭha sarvāśca kuruyoṣitaḥ ,patitāḥ sahasā bhūmau śrutvā krūraṁ vacaśca tāḥ.
english: O best of kings, having heard that cruel speech, Gandhari and all the Kuru women also suddenly fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 39. O best of kings, having heard that cruel speech, Gandhari and all the Kuru women also suddenly fell to the ground.

sanskrit: निःसंज्ञं पतितं भूमौ तदासीद्राजमण्डलम् ।प्रलापयुक्ता महती कथा न्यस्ता पटे यथा ॥४०॥
transliteration: niḥsaṁjñaṁ patitaṁ bhūmau tadāsīdrājamaṇḍalam ,pralāpayuktā mahatī kathā nyastā paṭe yathā.
english: Then the entire royal assembly lay fallen on the ground, unconscious. It was a great, lamentation-filled scene, as if painted on a canvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 40. Then the entire royal assembly lay fallen on the ground, unconscious. It was a great, lamentation-filled scene, as if painted on a canvas.

sanskrit: कृच्छ्रेण तु ततो राजा धृतराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ।शनैरलभत प्राणान्पुत्रव्यसनकर्शितः ॥४१॥
transliteration: kṛcchreṇa tu tato rājā dhṛtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ ,śanairalabhata prāṇānputravyasanakarśitaḥ.
english: Then, King Dhritarashtra, the lord of the earth, who was emaciated by the grief over his sons, slowly and with great difficulty regained his vital breath.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 41. Then, King Dhritarashtra, the lord of the earth, who was emaciated by the grief over his sons, slowly and with great difficulty regained his vital breath.

sanskrit: लब्ध्वा तु स नृपः संज्ञां वेपमानः सुदुःखितः ।उदीक्ष्य च दिशः सर्वाः क्षत्तारं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥४२॥
transliteration: labdhvā tu sa nṛpaḥ saṁjñāṁ vepamānaḥ suduḥkhitaḥ ,udīkṣya ca diśaḥ sarvāḥ kṣattāraṁ vākyamabravīt.
english: But that king, having regained consciousness, trembling and deeply distressed, looked up at all directions and then spoke these words to the charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 42. But that king, having regained consciousness, trembling and deeply distressed, looked up at all directions and then spoke these words to the charioteer.

sanskrit: विद्वन्क्षत्तर्महाप्राज्ञ त्वं गतिर्भरतर्षभ ।ममानाथस्य सुभृशं पुत्रैर्हीनस्य सर्वशः ।एवमुक्त्वा ततो भूयो विसंज्ञो निपपात ह ॥४३॥
transliteration: vidvankṣattarmahāprājña tvaṁ gatirbharatarṣabha ,mamānāthasya subhṛśaṁ putrairhīnasya sarvaśaḥ ,evamuktvā tato bhūyo visaṁjño nipapāta ha.
english: O learned charioteer (kṣattṛ), O greatly wise one, O best among the Bharatas! You are the refuge for me, who is utterly helpless and entirely bereft of sons. Having spoken thus, he then indeed fell unconscious once more.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 43. O learned charioteer (kṣattṛ), O greatly wise one, O best among the Bharatas! You are the refuge for me, who is utterly helpless and entirely bereft of sons. Having spoken thus, he then indeed fell unconscious once more.

sanskrit: तं तथा पतितं दृष्ट्वा बान्धवा येऽस्य केचन ।शीतैस्तु सिषिचुस्तोयैर्विव्यजुर्व्यजनैरपि ॥४४॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā patitaṁ dṛṣṭvā bāndhavā ye'sya kecana ,śītaistu siṣicustoyairvivyajurvyajanairapi.
english: Seeing him fallen in that state, some of his relatives sprinkled him with cold water and also fanned him with fans.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 44. Seeing him fallen in that state, some of his relatives sprinkled him with cold water and also fanned him with fans.

sanskrit: स तु दीर्घेण कालेन प्रत्याश्वस्तो महीपतिः ।तूष्णीं दध्यौ महीपालः पुत्रव्यसनकर्शितः ।निःश्वसञ्जिह्मग इव कुम्भक्षिप्तो विशां पते ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa tu dīrgheṇa kālena pratyāśvasto mahīpatiḥ ,tūṣṇīṁ dadhyau mahīpālaḥ putravyasanakarśitaḥ ,niḥśvasañjihmaga iva kumbhakṣipto viśāṁ pate.
english: But that king (mahīpatiḥ), having recovered after a long time, silently pondered, emaciated by the grief for his sons. He sighed like a snake thrown into a pot, O lord of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 45. But that king (mahīpatiḥ), having recovered after a long time, silently pondered, emaciated by the grief for his sons. He sighed like a snake thrown into a pot, O lord of the people.

sanskrit: संजयोऽप्यरुदत्तत्र दृष्ट्वा राजानमातुरम् ।तथा सर्वाः स्त्रियश्चैव गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ॥४६॥
transliteration: saṁjayo'pyarudattatra dṛṣṭvā rājānamāturam ,tathā sarvāḥ striyaścaiva gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī.
english: Saṃjaya also wept there, seeing the distressed king. Similarly, all the women, and the glorious Gāndhārī, also wept.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 46. Saṃjaya also wept there, seeing the distressed king. Similarly, all the women, and the glorious Gāndhārī, also wept.

sanskrit: ततो दीर्घेण कालेन विदुरं वाक्यमब्रवीत् ।धृतराष्ट्रो नरव्याघ्रो मुह्यमानो मुहुर्मुहुः ॥४७॥
transliteration: tato dīrgheṇa kālena viduraṁ vākyamabravīt ,dhṛtarāṣṭro naravyāghro muhyamāno muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Then, after a long time, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, that tiger among men (naravyāghra), who was repeatedly becoming bewildered, spoke these words to Vidura.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 47. Then, after a long time, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, that tiger among men (naravyāghra), who was repeatedly becoming bewildered, spoke these words to Vidura.

sanskrit: गच्छन्तु योषितः सर्वा गान्धारी च यशस्विनी ।तथेमे सुहृदः सर्वे भ्रश्यते मे मनो भृशम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: gacchantu yoṣitaḥ sarvā gāndhārī ca yaśasvinī ,tatheme suhṛdaḥ sarve bhraśyate me mano bhṛśam.
english: Let all the women go, and also the glorious Gāndhārī. Similarly, let all these friends go. My mind is greatly wavering.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 48. Let all the women go, and also the glorious Gāndhārī. Similarly, let all these friends go. My mind is greatly wavering.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्ततः क्षत्ता ताः स्त्रियो भरतर्षभ ।विसर्जयामास शनैर्वेपमानः पुनः पुनः ॥४९॥
transliteration: evamuktastataḥ kṣattā tāḥ striyo bharatarṣabha ,visarjayāmāsa śanairvepamānaḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, thus spoken to, the charioteer, trembling again and again, slowly dismissed those women.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 49. O best among the Bharatas, thus spoken to, the charioteer, trembling again and again, slowly dismissed those women.

sanskrit: निश्चक्रमुस्ततः सर्वास्ताः स्त्रियो भरतर्षभ ।सुहृदश्च ततः सर्वे दृष्ट्वा राजानमातुरम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: niścakramustataḥ sarvāstāḥ striyo bharatarṣabha ,suhṛdaśca tataḥ sarve dṛṣṭvā rājānamāturam.
english: O best among the Bharatas, then all those women, and then all the friends, having seen the king distressed, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 50. O best among the Bharatas, then all those women, and then all the friends, having seen the king distressed, departed.

sanskrit: ततो नरपतिं तत्र लब्धसंज्ञं परंतप ।अवेक्ष्य संजयो दीनो रोदमानं भृशातुरम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: tato narapatiṁ tatra labdhasaṁjñaṁ paraṁtapa ,avekṣya saṁjayo dīno rodamānaṁ bhṛśāturam.
english: O tormentor of foes, then the miserable Sanjaya, having observed the lord of men there, who had regained consciousness, weeping and greatly distressed...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 51. O tormentor of foes, then the miserable Sanjaya, having observed the lord of men there, who had regained consciousness, weeping and greatly distressed...

sanskrit: प्राञ्जलिर्निःश्वसन्तं च तं नरेन्द्रं मुहुर्मुहुः ।समाश्वासयत क्षत्ता वचसा मधुरेण च ॥५२॥
transliteration: prāñjalirniḥśvasantaṁ ca taṁ narendraṁ muhurmuhuḥ ,samāśvāsayata kṣattā vacasā madhureṇa ca.
english: With folded hands, the charioteer consoled that king, who was sighing again and again, with sweet words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 1 Verse 52. With folded hands, the charioteer consoled that king, who was sighing again and again, with sweet words.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।विसृष्टास्वथ नारीषु धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।विललाप महाराज दुःखाद्दुःखतरं गतः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,visṛṣṭāsvatha nārīṣu dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,vilalāpa mahārāja duḥkhādduḥkhataraṁ gataḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, after the women had been dismissed, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Ambikā's son, wept greatly, having gone from sorrow to even greater sorrow, O great king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, after the women had been dismissed, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Ambikā's son, wept greatly, having gone from sorrow to even greater sorrow, O great king.

sanskrit: सधूममिव निःश्वस्य करौ धुन्वन्पुनः पुनः ।विचिन्त्य च महाराज ततो वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: sadhūmamiva niḥśvasya karau dhunvanpunaḥ punaḥ ,vicintya ca mahārāja tato vacanamabravīt.
english: Sighing as if with smoke, repeatedly shaking his hands, and having reflected, O great king, he then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. Sighing as if with smoke, repeatedly shaking his hands, and having reflected, O great king, he then spoke these words.

sanskrit: अहो बत महद्दुःखं यदहं पाण्डवान्रणे ।क्षेमिणश्चाव्ययांश्चैव त्वत्तः सूत शृणोमि वै ॥३॥
transliteration: aho bata mahadduḥkhaṁ yadahaṁ pāṇḍavānraṇe ,kṣemiṇaścāvyayāṁścaiva tvattaḥ sūta śṛṇomi vai.
english: Alas, what great sorrow it is that I, O Sūta, hear from you that the Pāṇḍavas are safe and unharmed in battle!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. Alas, what great sorrow it is that I, O Sūta, hear from you that the Pāṇḍavas are safe and unharmed in battle!

sanskrit: वज्रसारमयं नूनं हृदयं सुदृढं मम ।यच्छ्रुत्वा निहतान्पुत्रान्दीर्यते न सहस्रधा ॥४॥
transliteration: vajrasāramayaṁ nūnaṁ hṛdayaṁ sudṛḍhaṁ mama ,yacchrutvā nihatānputrāndīryate na sahasradhā.
english: My heart must surely be exceedingly firm, made of diamond-essence, since, having heard that my sons were slain, it does not burst into a thousand pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. My heart must surely be exceedingly firm, made of diamond-essence, since, having heard that my sons were slain, it does not burst into a thousand pieces.

sanskrit: चिन्तयित्वा वचस्तेषां बालक्रीडां च संजय ।अद्य श्रुत्वा हतान्पुत्रान्भृशं मे दीर्यते मनः ॥५॥
transliteration: cintayitvā vacasteṣāṁ bālakrīḍāṁ ca saṁjaya ,adya śrutvā hatānputrānbhṛśaṁ me dīryate manaḥ.
english: O Sanjaya, having reflected on their words and their childhood games, and having heard today that my sons have been killed, my heart is greatly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. O Sanjaya, having reflected on their words and their childhood games, and having heard today that my sons have been killed, my heart is greatly distressed.

sanskrit: अन्धत्वाद्यदि तेषां तु न मे रूपनिदर्शनम् ।पुत्रस्नेहकृता प्रीतिर्नित्यमेतेषु धारिता ॥६॥
transliteration: andhatvādyadi teṣāṁ tu na me rūpanidarśanam ,putrasnehakṛtā prītirnityameteṣu dhāritā.
english: Even if, due to my blindness, I never had the sight of their forms, the affection (prīti) born of a father's love for his sons was always sustained in these boys.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. Even if, due to my blindness, I never had the sight of their forms, the affection (prīti) born of a father's love for his sons was always sustained in these boys.

sanskrit: बालभावमतिक्रान्तान्यौवनस्थांश्च तानहम् ।मध्यप्राप्तांस्तथा श्रुत्वा हृष्ट आसं तथानघ ॥७॥
transliteration: bālabhāvamatikrāntānyauvanasthāṁśca tānaham ,madhyaprāptāṁstathā śrutvā hṛṣṭa āsaṁ tathānagha.
english: O sinless one (Sanjaya), having heard that they (my sons) had passed beyond childhood, entered youth, and then similarly reached middle age, I was consequently joyful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. O sinless one (Sanjaya), having heard that they (my sons) had passed beyond childhood, entered youth, and then similarly reached middle age, I was consequently joyful.

sanskrit: तानद्य निहताञ्श्रुत्वा हृतैश्वर्यान्हृतौजसः ।न लभे वै क्वचिच्छान्तिं पुत्राधिभिरभिप्लुतः ॥८॥
transliteration: tānadya nihatāñśrutvā hṛtaiśvaryānhṛtaujasaḥ ,na labhe vai kvacicchāntiṁ putrādhibhirabhiplutaḥ.
english: Having heard today that those sons have been slain, deprived of their dominion and their strength, I find no peace (śānti) anywhere, truly overwhelmed by grief for my sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. Having heard today that those sons have been slain, deprived of their dominion and their strength, I find no peace (śānti) anywhere, truly overwhelmed by grief for my sons.

sanskrit: एह्येहि पुत्र राजेन्द्र ममानाथस्य सांप्रतम् ।त्वया हीनो महाबाहो कां नु यास्याम्यहं गतिम् ॥९॥
transliteration: ehyehi putra rājendra mamānāthasya sāṁpratam ,tvayā hīno mahābāho kāṁ nu yāsyāmyahaṁ gatim.
english: Come, come, my son, O best of kings, O mighty-armed one, to me, who am now helpless! Deprived of you, what fate indeed shall I meet?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. Come, come, my son, O best of kings, O mighty-armed one, to me, who am now helpless! Deprived of you, what fate indeed shall I meet?

sanskrit: गतिर्भूत्वा महाराज ज्ञातीनां सुहृदां तथा ।अन्धं वृद्धं च मां वीर विहाय क्व नु गच्छसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: gatirbhūtvā mahārāja jñātīnāṁ suhṛdāṁ tathā ,andhaṁ vṛddhaṁ ca māṁ vīra vihāya kva nu gacchasi.
english: O great king, O hero, having been the refuge and support of your kinsmen and friends, where indeed are you going, abandoning me, who am blind and old?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. O great king, O hero, having been the refuge and support of your kinsmen and friends, where indeed are you going, abandoning me, who am blind and old?

sanskrit: सा कृपा सा च ते प्रीतिः सा च राजन्सुमानिता ।कथं विनिहतः पार्थैः संयुगेष्वपराजितः ॥११॥
transliteration: sā kṛpā sā ca te prītiḥ sā ca rājansumānitā ,kathaṁ vinihataḥ pārthaiḥ saṁyugeṣvaparājitaḥ.
english: O king, how can it be that you, who were unconquered (aparājita) in battles, and known for such compassion, such affection, and such high esteem, have been slain by the sons of Pṛthā (Pārtha)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. O king, how can it be that you, who were unconquered (aparājita) in battles, and known for such compassion, such affection, and such high esteem, have been slain by the sons of Pṛthā (Pārtha)?

sanskrit: कथं त्वं पृथिवीपालान्भुक्त्वा तात समागतान् ।शेषे विनिहतो भूमौ प्राकृतः कुनृपो यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: kathaṁ tvaṁ pṛthivīpālānbhuktvā tāta samāgatān ,śeṣe vinihato bhūmau prākṛtaḥ kunṛpo yathā.
english: My dear son, how is it that you, after having ruled over the assembled kings (protectors of the earth), now lie slain on the ground like a common, wicked king?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. My dear son, how is it that you, after having ruled over the assembled kings (protectors of the earth), now lie slain on the ground like a common, wicked king?

sanskrit: को नु मामुत्थितं काल्ये तात तातेति वक्ष्यति ।महाराजेति सततं लोकनाथेति चासकृत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: ko nu māmutthitaṁ kālye tāta tāteti vakṣyati ,mahārājeti satataṁ lokanātheti cāsakṛt.
english: Who indeed will address me as 'Father, oh Father' when I rise early in the morning? And who will constantly and repeatedly call me 'Great King' and 'Lord of the people'?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. Who indeed will address me as 'Father, oh Father' when I rise early in the morning? And who will constantly and repeatedly call me 'Great King' and 'Lord of the people'?

sanskrit: परिष्वज्य च मां कण्ठे स्नेहेनाक्लिन्नलोचनः ।अनुशाधीति कौरव्य तत्साधु वद मे वचः ॥१४॥
transliteration: pariṣvajya ca māṁ kaṇṭhe snehenāklinnalocanaḥ ,anuśādhīti kauravya tatsādhu vada me vacaḥ.
english: And who will embrace me around the neck with affection and dry eyes, saying, 'Instruct me!'? O descendant of Kuru, tell me that proper advice for my situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. And who will embrace me around the neck with affection and dry eyes, saying, 'Instruct me!'? O descendant of Kuru, tell me that proper advice for my situation.

sanskrit: ननु नामाहमश्रौषं वचनं तव पुत्रक ।भूयसी मम पृथ्वीयं यथा पार्थस्य नो तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: nanu nāmāhamaśrauṣaṁ vacanaṁ tava putraka ,bhūyasī mama pṛthvīyaṁ yathā pārthasya no tathā.
english: Indeed, O dear son, I certainly heard your words: 'This earth of mine is more extensive; it is not so for Pārtha (Arjuna).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. Indeed, O dear son, I certainly heard your words: 'This earth of mine is more extensive; it is not so for Pārtha (Arjuna).'

sanskrit: भगदत्तः कृपः शल्य आवन्त्योऽथ जयद्रथः ।भूरिश्रवाः सोमदत्तो महाराजोऽथ बाह्लिकः ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhagadattaḥ kṛpaḥ śalya āvantyo'tha jayadrathaḥ ,bhūriśravāḥ somadatto mahārājo'tha bāhlikaḥ.
english: Bhagadatta, Kṛpa, Śalya, the King of Avanti, and then Jayadratha; Bhūriśravas, Somadatta, the Great King, and then Bāhlika.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. Bhagadatta, Kṛpa, Śalya, the King of Avanti, and then Jayadratha; Bhūriśravas, Somadatta, the Great King, and then Bāhlika.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा च भोजश्च मागधश्च महाबलः ।बृहद्बलश्च काशीशः शकुनिश्चापि सौबलः ॥१७॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā ca bhojaśca māgadhaśca mahābalaḥ ,bṛhadbalaśca kāśīśaḥ śakuniścāpi saubalaḥ.
english: Aśvatthāmā, Bhoja, and the mighty Magadha; Bṛhadbala, the king of Kāśī; and also Śakuni, the son of Subala.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. Aśvatthāmā, Bhoja, and the mighty Magadha; Bṛhadbala, the king of Kāśī; and also Śakuni, the son of Subala.

sanskrit: म्लेच्छाश्च बहुसाहस्राः शकाश्च यवनैः सह ।सुदक्षिणश्च काम्बोजस्त्रिगर्ताधिपतिस्तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: mlecchāśca bahusāhasrāḥ śakāśca yavanaiḥ saha ,sudakṣiṇaśca kāmbojastrigartādhipatistathā.
english: And Mlecchas numbering in many thousands; and Śakas along with Yavanas; and also Sudakṣiṇa, the king of Kamboja and lord of Trigarta.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. And Mlecchas numbering in many thousands; and Śakas along with Yavanas; and also Sudakṣiṇa, the king of Kamboja and lord of Trigarta.

sanskrit: भीष्मः पितामहश्चैव भारद्वाजोऽथ गौतमः ।श्रुतायुश्चाच्युतायुश्च शतायुश्चापि वीर्यवान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhīṣmaḥ pitāmahaścaiva bhāradvājo'tha gautamaḥ ,śrutāyuścācyutāyuśca śatāyuścāpi vīryavān.
english: Bhīṣma, the great-grandfather; and indeed Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) and Gautama (Kṛpa); and Śrutāyu, Acyutāyu, and also the valorous Śatāyu.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. Bhīṣma, the great-grandfather; and indeed Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) and Gautama (Kṛpa); and Śrutāyu, Acyutāyu, and also the valorous Śatāyu.

sanskrit: जलसंधोऽथार्श्यशृङ्गी राक्षसश्चाप्यलायुधः ।अलंबुसो महाबाहुः सुबाहुश्च महारथः ॥२०॥
transliteration: jalasaṁdho'thārśyaśṛṅgī rākṣasaścāpyalāyudhaḥ ,alaṁbuso mahābāhuḥ subāhuśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Jalasandha, and Ṛśyaśr̥ṅgī; and also the demon Alāyudha; Alambusa, the mighty-armed; and Subāhu, the great chariot-warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. Jalasandha, and Ṛśyaśr̥ṅgī; and also the demon Alāyudha; Alambusa, the mighty-armed; and Subāhu, the great chariot-warrior.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो राजानो राजसत्तम ।मदर्थमुद्यताः सर्वे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा रणे प्रभो ॥२१॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo rājāno rājasattama ,madarthamudyatāḥ sarve prāṇāṁstyaktvā raṇe prabho.
english: O best of kings (rājasattama), and O lord (prabho), these and many other kings are all prepared to abandon their lives for my sake in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 21. O best of kings (rājasattama), and O lord (prabho), these and many other kings are all prepared to abandon their lives for my sake in battle.

sanskrit: येषां मध्ये स्थितो युद्धे भ्रातृभिः परिवारितः ।योधयिष्याम्यहं पार्थान्पाञ्चालांश्चैव सर्वशः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yeṣāṁ madhye sthito yuddhe bhrātṛbhiḥ parivāritaḥ ,yodhayiṣyāmyahaṁ pārthānpāñcālāṁścaiva sarvaśaḥ.
english: Standing amidst them (my allies) in battle, surrounded by my brothers, I will certainly fight all of the Pārthas and the Pāñcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 22. Standing amidst them (my allies) in battle, surrounded by my brothers, I will certainly fight all of the Pārthas and the Pāñcālas.

sanskrit: चेदींश्च नृपशार्दूल द्रौपदेयांश्च संयुगे ।सात्यकिं कुन्तिभोजं च राक्षसं च घटोत्कचम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: cedīṁśca nṛpaśārdūla draupadeyāṁśca saṁyuge ,sātyakiṁ kuntibhojaṁ ca rākṣasaṁ ca ghaṭotkacam.
english: O tiger among kings, in battle I will fight the Cedis and the sons of Draupadī; also Sātyaki, Kuntibhoja, and the demon Ghaṭotkaca.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 23. O tiger among kings, in battle I will fight the Cedis and the sons of Draupadī; also Sātyaki, Kuntibhoja, and the demon Ghaṭotkaca.

sanskrit: एकोऽप्येषां महाराज समर्थः संनिवारणे ।समरे पाण्डवेयानां संक्रुद्धो ह्यभिधावताम् ।किं पुनः सहिता वीराः कृतवैराश्च पाण्डवैः ॥२४॥
transliteration: eko'pyeṣāṁ mahārāja samarthaḥ saṁnivāraṇe ,samare pāṇḍaveyānāṁ saṁkruddho hyabhidhāvatām ,kiṁ punaḥ sahitā vīrāḥ kṛtavairāśca pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: O great king, even one of these (warriors) is capable of holding back the Pāṇḍavas as they rush forth in battle, especially when enraged. What then to say when these heroes are united and have made (such) enmity with the Pāṇḍavas?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 24. O great king, even one of these (warriors) is capable of holding back the Pāṇḍavas as they rush forth in battle, especially when enraged. What then to say when these heroes are united and have made (such) enmity with the Pāṇḍavas?

sanskrit: अथ वा सर्व एवैते पाण्डवस्यानुयायिभिः ।योत्स्यन्ति सह राजेन्द्र हनिष्यन्ति च तान्मृधे ॥२५॥
transliteration: atha vā sarva evaite pāṇḍavasyānuyāyibhiḥ ,yotsyanti saha rājendra haniṣyanti ca tānmṛdhe.
english: Or indeed, all of these [our warriors], O King (Rajendra), will fight alongside the followers of the Pandava and will kill them in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 25. Or indeed, all of these [our warriors], O King (Rajendra), will fight alongside the followers of the Pandava and will kill them in battle.

sanskrit: कर्णस्त्वेको मया सार्धं निहनिष्यति पाण्डवान् ।ततो नृपतयो वीराः स्थास्यन्ति मम शासने ॥२६॥
transliteration: karṇastveko mayā sārdhaṁ nihaniṣyati pāṇḍavān ,tato nṛpatayo vīrāḥ sthāsyanti mama śāsane.
english: However, Karna alone, along with me, will kill the Pandavas. Then, the heroic kings will stand under my command.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 26. However, Karna alone, along with me, will kill the Pandavas. Then, the heroic kings will stand under my command.

sanskrit: यश्च तेषां प्रणेता वै वासुदेवो महाबलः ।न स संनह्यते राजन्निति मामब्रवीद्वचः ॥२७॥
transliteration: yaśca teṣāṁ praṇetā vai vāsudevo mahābalaḥ ,na sa saṁnahyate rājanniti māmabravīdvacaḥ.
english: And he who is their leader, the greatly powerful Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), will not prepare for battle. This word, O King (Rajendra), was spoken to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 27. And he who is their leader, the greatly powerful Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), will not prepare for battle. This word, O King (Rajendra), was spoken to me.

sanskrit: तस्याहं वदतः सूत बहुशो मम संनिधौ ।युक्तितो ह्यनुपश्यामि निहतान्पाण्डवान्मृधे ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasyāhaṁ vadataḥ sūta bahuśo mama saṁnidhau ,yuktito hyanupaśyāmi nihatānpāṇḍavānmṛdhe.
english: O Charioteer (Sūta), while he (the one who spoke about Kṛṣṇa) was speaking many times in my presence, I logically foresee the Pandavas slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 28. O Charioteer (Sūta), while he (the one who spoke about Kṛṣṇa) was speaking many times in my presence, I logically foresee the Pandavas slain in battle.

sanskrit: तेषां मध्ये स्थिता यत्र हन्यन्ते मम पुत्रकाः ।व्यायच्छमानाः समरे किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ madhye sthitā yatra hanyante mama putrakāḥ ,vyāyacchamānāḥ samare kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: My sons, who stand among them and exert themselves in battle, are getting killed. What else can this be but destiny (bhāgadheyataḥ)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 29. My sons, who stand among them and exert themselves in battle, are getting killed. What else can this be but destiny (bhāgadheyataḥ)?

sanskrit: भीष्मश्च निहतो यत्र लोकनाथः प्रतापवान् ।शिखण्डिनं समासाद्य मृगेन्द्र इव जम्बुकम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: bhīṣmaśca nihato yatra lokanāthaḥ pratāpavān ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ samāsādya mṛgendra iva jambukam.
english: And where the mighty, glorious protector of the world (lokanāthaḥ), Bhishma, was killed, having encountered Shikhaṇḍin, (it was) like a lion (mṛgendra) [being overcome by] a jackal (jambuka).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 30. And where the mighty, glorious protector of the world (lokanāthaḥ), Bhishma, was killed, having encountered Shikhaṇḍin, (it was) like a lion (mṛgendra) [being overcome by] a jackal (jambuka).

sanskrit: द्रोणश्च ब्राह्मणो यत्र सर्वशस्त्रास्त्रपारगः ।निहतः पाण्डवैः संख्ये किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३१॥
transliteration: droṇaśca brāhmaṇo yatra sarvaśastrāstrapāragaḥ ,nihataḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ saṁkhye kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: And where Drona, the brahmin, who was skilled in all weapons and missiles, was killed by the Pāṇḍavas in battle. What else can this be but destiny (bhāgadheyataḥ)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 31. And where Drona, the brahmin, who was skilled in all weapons and missiles, was killed by the Pāṇḍavas in battle. What else can this be but destiny (bhāgadheyataḥ)?

sanskrit: भूरिश्रवा हतो यत्र सोमदत्तश्च संयुगे ।बाह्लीकश्च महाराज किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३२॥
transliteration: bhūriśravā hato yatra somadattaśca saṁyuge ,bāhlīkaśca mahārāja kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: O great king, where Bhūriśravas was killed in battle, and Somadatta, and Bāhlīka. What else can this be but destiny (bhāgadheyataḥ)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 32. O great king, where Bhūriśravas was killed in battle, and Somadatta, and Bāhlīka. What else can this be but destiny (bhāgadheyataḥ)?

sanskrit: सुदक्षिणो हतो यत्र जलसंधश्च कौरवः ।श्रुतायुश्चाच्युतायुश्च किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३३॥
transliteration: sudakṣiṇo hato yatra jalasaṁdhaśca kauravaḥ ,śrutāyuścācyutāyuśca kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: Where Sudakṣiṇa was slain, and Jayasaṃdha, the Kuru, along with Śrutāyu and Acyutāyu. What else could this be but destiny?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 33. Where Sudakṣiṇa was slain, and Jayasaṃdha, the Kuru, along with Śrutāyu and Acyutāyu. What else could this be but destiny?

sanskrit: बृहद्बलो हतो यत्र मागधश्च महाबलः ।आवन्त्यो निहतो यत्र त्रिगर्तश्च जनाधिपः ।संशप्तकाश्च बहवः किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३४॥
transliteration: bṛhadbalo hato yatra māgadhaśca mahābalaḥ ,āvantyo nihato yatra trigartaśca janādhipaḥ ,saṁśaptakāśca bahavaḥ kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: Where Bṛhadbala was slain, and the mighty king of Magadha. Where the king of Avanti was killed, and the ruler of Trigarta. And many Saṃśaptakas. What else could this be but destiny?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 34. Where Bṛhadbala was slain, and the mighty king of Magadha. Where the king of Avanti was killed, and the ruler of Trigarta. And many Saṃśaptakas. What else could this be but destiny?

sanskrit: अलंबुसस्तथा राजन्राक्षसश्चाप्यलायुधः ।आर्श्यशृङ्गश्च निहतः किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३५॥
transliteration: alaṁbusastathā rājanrākṣasaścāpyalāyudhaḥ ,ārśyaśṛṅgaśca nihataḥ kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: And similarly, O King, the Rākṣasa Alaṃbusa, and Alāyudha, and Ārṣyaśṛṅga were slain. What else could this be but destiny?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 35. And similarly, O King, the Rākṣasa Alaṃbusa, and Alāyudha, and Ārṣyaśṛṅga were slain. What else could this be but destiny?

sanskrit: नारायणा हता यत्र गोपाला युद्धदुर्मदाः ।म्लेच्छाश्च बहुसाहस्राः किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: nārāyaṇā hatā yatra gopālā yuddhadurmadāḥ ,mlecchāśca bahusāhasrāḥ kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: Where the Nārāyaṇas, those cowherds (gopālāḥ) maddened by battle, were slain, and many thousands of Mlecchas. What else could this be but destiny?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 36. Where the Nārāyaṇas, those cowherds (gopālāḥ) maddened by battle, were slain, and many thousands of Mlecchas. What else could this be but destiny?

sanskrit: शकुनिः सौबलो यत्र कैतव्यश्च महाबलः ।निहतः सबलो वीरः किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३७॥
transliteration: śakuniḥ saubalo yatra kaitavyaśca mahābalaḥ ,nihataḥ sabalo vīraḥ kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: Where Shakuni, the son of Subala, that cunning (kaitavya) and mighty hero, was slain along with his forces, what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 37. Where Shakuni, the son of Subala, that cunning (kaitavya) and mighty hero, was slain along with his forces, what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?

sanskrit: राजानो राजपुत्राश्च शूराः परिघबाहवः ।निहता बहवो यत्र किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३८॥
transliteration: rājāno rājaputrāśca śūrāḥ parighabāhavaḥ ,nihatā bahavo yatra kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: Where many kings, princes, and brave heroes with arms like maces (parighabāhavaḥ) were slain, what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 38. Where many kings, princes, and brave heroes with arms like maces (parighabāhavaḥ) were slain, what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?

sanskrit: नानादेशसमावृत्ताः क्षत्रिया यत्र संजय ।निहताः समरे सर्वे किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥३९॥
transliteration: nānādeśasamāvṛttāḥ kṣatriyā yatra saṁjaya ,nihatāḥ samare sarve kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: O Sanjaya, where all the kshatriyas, assembled from various countries, were slain in battle, what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 39. O Sanjaya, where all the kshatriyas, assembled from various countries, were slain in battle, what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?

sanskrit: पुत्राश्च मे विनिहताः पौत्राश्चैव महाबलाः ।वयस्या भ्रातरश्चैव किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥४०॥
transliteration: putrāśca me vinihatāḥ pautrāścaiva mahābalāḥ ,vayasyā bhrātaraścaiva kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: My sons were utterly slain, and my mighty grandsons as well; my friends and brothers too (were killed); what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 40. My sons were utterly slain, and my mighty grandsons as well; my friends and brothers too (were killed); what else could it be but due to destiny (bhāgadheya)?

sanskrit: भागधेयसमायुक्तो ध्रुवमुत्पद्यते नरः ।यश्च भाग्यसमायुक्तः स शुभं प्राप्नुयान्नरः ॥४१॥
transliteration: bhāgadheyasamāyukto dhruvamutpadyate naraḥ ,yaśca bhāgyasamāyuktaḥ sa śubhaṁ prāpnuyānnaraḥ.
english: A person who is born endowed with their destined share (bhāgadheya) will certainly exist. And such a person, if also endowed with good fortune (bhāgya), will surely attain prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 41. A person who is born endowed with their destined share (bhāgadheya) will certainly exist. And such a person, if also endowed with good fortune (bhāgya), will surely attain prosperity.

sanskrit: अहं वियुक्तः स्वैर्भाग्यैः पुत्रैश्चैवेह संजय ।कथमद्य भविष्यामि वृद्धः शत्रुवशं गतः ॥४२॥
transliteration: ahaṁ viyuktaḥ svairbhāgyaiḥ putraiścaiveha saṁjaya ,kathamadya bhaviṣyāmi vṛddhaḥ śatruvaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: Sañjaya, how will I, old and having fallen into the power of my enemies, survive today, having been deprived of my own fortunes (bhāgya) and sons, right here?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 42. Sañjaya, how will I, old and having fallen into the power of my enemies, survive today, having been deprived of my own fortunes (bhāgya) and sons, right here?

sanskrit: नान्यदत्र परं मन्ये वनवासादृते प्रभो ।सोऽहं वनं गमिष्यामि निर्बन्धुर्ज्ञातिसंक्षये ॥४३॥
transliteration: nānyadatra paraṁ manye vanavāsādṛte prabho ,so'haṁ vanaṁ gamiṣyāmi nirbandhurjñātisaṁkṣaye.
english: O Lord (prabhu), I consider nothing else here to be better than dwelling in the forest. Therefore, I, being without kinsmen due to the destruction of my relatives (jñāti-saṃkṣaya), shall go to the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 43. O Lord (prabhu), I consider nothing else here to be better than dwelling in the forest. Therefore, I, being without kinsmen due to the destruction of my relatives (jñāti-saṃkṣaya), shall go to the forest.

sanskrit: न हि मेऽन्यद्भवेच्छ्रेयो वनाभ्युपगमादृते ।इमामवस्थां प्राप्तस्य लूनपक्षस्य संजय ॥४४॥
transliteration: na hi me'nyadbhavecchreyo vanābhyupagamādṛte ,imāmavasthāṁ prāptasya lūnapakṣasya saṁjaya.
english: Sañjaya, for me, having reached this state, like one whose wings are clipped, there is surely no other better course than resorting to the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 44. Sañjaya, for me, having reached this state, like one whose wings are clipped, there is surely no other better course than resorting to the forest.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो हतो यत्र शल्यश्च निहतो युधि ।दुःशासनो विशस्तश्च विकर्णश्च महाबलः ॥४५॥
transliteration: duryodhano hato yatra śalyaśca nihato yudhi ,duḥśāsano viśastaśca vikarṇaśca mahābalaḥ.
english: Where Duryodhana was slain, and Śalya was killed in battle; where Duḥśāsana was disemboweled, and the mighty Vikarṇa (was also killed).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 45. Where Duryodhana was slain, and Śalya was killed in battle; where Duḥśāsana was disemboweled, and the mighty Vikarṇa (was also killed).

sanskrit: कथं हि भीमसेनस्य श्रोष्येऽहं शब्दमुत्तमम् ।एकेन समरे येन हतं पुत्रशतं मम ॥४६॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hi bhīmasenasya śroṣye'haṁ śabdamuttamam ,ekena samare yena hataṁ putraśataṁ mama.
english: How indeed shall I hear the triumphant roar of Bhīmasena, by whom alone in battle my hundred sons were slain?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 46. How indeed shall I hear the triumphant roar of Bhīmasena, by whom alone in battle my hundred sons were slain?

sanskrit: असकृद्वदतस्तस्य दुर्योधनवधेन च ।दुःखशोकाभिसंतप्तो न श्रोष्ये परुषा गिरः ॥४७॥
transliteration: asakṛdvadatastasya duryodhanavadhena ca ,duḥkhaśokābhisaṁtapto na śroṣye paruṣā giraḥ.
english: Intensely distressed by sorrow and grief due to the slaying of Duryodhana, I shall not hear his (Bhīmasena's) harsh words spoken repeatedly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 47. Intensely distressed by sorrow and grief due to the slaying of Duryodhana, I shall not hear his (Bhīmasena's) harsh words spoken repeatedly.

sanskrit: एवं स शोकसंतप्तः पार्थिवो हतबान्धवः ।मुहुर्मुहुर्मुह्यमानः पुत्राधिभिरभिप्लुतः ॥४८॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa śokasaṁtaptaḥ pārthivo hatabāndhavaḥ ,muhurmuhurmuhyamānaḥ putrādhibhirabhiplutaḥ.
english: Thus, that king (pārthiva), tormented by grief, whose kinsmen were slain, repeatedly fell into delusion, overwhelmed by mental anguish for his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 48. Thus, that king (pārthiva), tormented by grief, whose kinsmen were slain, repeatedly fell into delusion, overwhelmed by mental anguish for his sons.

sanskrit: विलप्य सुचिरं कालं धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।दीर्घमुष्णं च निःश्वस्य चिन्तयित्वा पराभवम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: vilapya suciraṁ kālaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,dīrghamuṣṇaṁ ca niḥśvasya cintayitvā parābhavam.
english: The son of Ambikā, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having lamented for a very long time, and having sighed deeply and hotly, contemplating the defeat,
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 49. The son of Ambikā, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having lamented for a very long time, and having sighed deeply and hotly, contemplating the defeat,

sanskrit: दुःखेन महता राजा संतप्तो भरतर्षभ ।पुनर्गावल्गणिं सूतं पर्यपृच्छद्यथातथम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: duḥkhena mahatā rājā saṁtapto bharatarṣabha ,punargāvalgaṇiṁ sūtaṁ paryapṛcchadyathātatham.
english: O bull among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), scorched by immense grief, again asked the charioteer, Gavalgaṇa's son (Sanjaya), for the precise details.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 50. O bull among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), scorched by immense grief, again asked the charioteer, Gavalgaṇa's son (Sanjaya), for the precise details.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणौ हतौ श्रुत्वा सूतपुत्रं च पातितम् ।सेनापतिं प्रणेतारं किमकुर्वत मामकाः ॥५१॥
transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇau hatau śrutvā sūtaputraṁ ca pātitam ,senāpatiṁ praṇetāraṁ kimakurvata māmakāḥ.
english: Having heard that Bhīṣma and Droṇa were slain, and the charioteer's son (Karṇa), their commander and leader, was felled, what did my people (māmakāḥ) then do?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 51. Having heard that Bhīṣma and Droṇa were slain, and the charioteer's son (Karṇa), their commander and leader, was felled, what did my people (māmakāḥ) then do?

sanskrit: यं यं सेनाप्रणेतारं युधि कुर्वन्ति मामकाः ।अचिरेणैव कालेन तं तं निघ्नन्ति पाण्डवाः ॥५२॥
transliteration: yaṁ yaṁ senāpraṇetāraṁ yudhi kurvanti māmakāḥ ,acireṇaiva kālena taṁ taṁ nighnanti pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Whomever among the army's leaders my people (māmakāḥ) appoint in battle, the Pāṇḍavas slay that very one within a very short time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 52. Whomever among the army's leaders my people (māmakāḥ) appoint in battle, the Pāṇḍavas slay that very one within a very short time.

sanskrit: रणमूर्ध्नि हतो भीष्मः पश्यतां वः किरीटिना ।एवमेव हतो द्रोणः सर्वेषामेव पश्यताम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: raṇamūrdhni hato bhīṣmaḥ paśyatāṁ vaḥ kirīṭinā ,evameva hato droṇaḥ sarveṣāmeva paśyatām.
english: Bhishma was killed by Arjuna on the battlefield while you were watching. Just so, Drona was also killed while all of you were watching.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 53. Bhishma was killed by Arjuna on the battlefield while you were watching. Just so, Drona was also killed while all of you were watching.

sanskrit: एवमेव हतः कर्णः सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।स राजकानां सर्वेषां पश्यतां वः किरीटिना ॥५४॥
transliteration: evameva hataḥ karṇaḥ sūtaputraḥ pratāpavān ,sa rājakānāṁ sarveṣāṁ paśyatāṁ vaḥ kirīṭinā.
english: Similarly, the mighty Karna, the son of Sūta, was killed by Arjuna while all you kings were watching.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 54. Similarly, the mighty Karna, the son of Sūta, was killed by Arjuna while all you kings were watching.

sanskrit: पूर्वमेवाहमुक्तो वै विदुरेण महात्मना ।दुर्योधनापराधेन प्रजेयं विनशिष्यति ॥५५॥
transliteration: pūrvamevāhamukto vai vidureṇa mahātmanā ,duryodhanāparādhena prajeyaṁ vinaśiṣyati.
english: Indeed, I was told previously by the great-souled Vidura: 'These people will perish because of Duryodhana's offense.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 55. Indeed, I was told previously by the great-souled Vidura: 'These people will perish because of Duryodhana's offense.'

sanskrit: केचिन्न सम्यक्पश्यन्ति मूढाः सम्यक्तथापरे ।तदिदं मम मूढस्य तथाभूतं वचः स्म ह ॥५६॥
transliteration: kecinna samyakpaśyanti mūḍhāḥ samyaktathāpare ,tadidaṁ mama mūḍhasya tathābhūtaṁ vacaḥ sma ha.
english: Some deluded people do not see clearly, while others do perceive correctly. Therefore, these words of mine, a deluded person, have indeed come to pass as foretold.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 56. Some deluded people do not see clearly, while others do perceive correctly. Therefore, these words of mine, a deluded person, have indeed come to pass as foretold.

sanskrit: यदब्रवीन्मे धर्मात्मा विदुरो दीर्घदर्शिवान् ।तत्तथा समनुप्राप्तं वचनं सत्यवादिनः ॥५७॥
transliteration: yadabravīnme dharmātmā viduro dīrghadarśivān ,tattathā samanuprāptaṁ vacanaṁ satyavādinaḥ.
english: What the righteous-minded (dharmātmā) Vidura, the far-sighted one, told me, that very word of the truth-teller has indeed come to pass exactly as he said.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 57. What the righteous-minded (dharmātmā) Vidura, the far-sighted one, told me, that very word of the truth-teller has indeed come to pass exactly as he said.

sanskrit: दैवोपहतचित्तेन यन्मयापकृतं पुरा ।अनयस्य फलं तस्य ब्रूहि गावल्गणे पुनः ॥५८॥
transliteration: daivopahatacittena yanmayāpakṛtaṁ purā ,anayasya phalaṁ tasya brūhi gāvalgaṇe punaḥ.
english: What was done wrongly by me in the past, with my mind afflicted by destiny, O Gāvalgaṇa, tell me again the consequence of that wrongdoing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 58. What was done wrongly by me in the past, with my mind afflicted by destiny, O Gāvalgaṇa, tell me again the consequence of that wrongdoing.

sanskrit: को वा मुखमनीकानामासीत्कर्णे निपातिते ।अर्जुनं वासुदेवं च को वा प्रत्युद्ययौ रथी ॥५९॥
transliteration: ko vā mukhamanīkānāmāsītkarṇe nipātite ,arjunaṁ vāsudevaṁ ca ko vā pratyudyayau rathī.
english: Who, indeed, became the leader of the armies when Karṇa was slain? And who was the charioteer (rathī) who went forth to confront Arjuna and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 59. Who, indeed, became the leader of the armies when Karṇa was slain? And who was the charioteer (rathī) who went forth to confront Arjuna and Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa)?

sanskrit: केऽरक्षन्दक्षिणं चक्रं मद्रराजस्य संयुगे ।वामं च योद्धुकामस्य के वा वीरस्य पृष्ठतः ॥६०॥
transliteration: ke'rakṣandakṣiṇaṁ cakraṁ madrarājasya saṁyuge ,vāmaṁ ca yoddhukāmasya ke vā vīrasya pṛṣṭhataḥ.
english: Who protected the right wheel of the king of Madras (Śalya) in battle? And who (guarded) the left (wheel) of that warrior (vīra) eager to fight, and who, indeed, stood behind him?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 60. Who protected the right wheel of the king of Madras (Śalya) in battle? And who (guarded) the left (wheel) of that warrior (vīra) eager to fight, and who, indeed, stood behind him?

sanskrit: कथं च वः समेतानां मद्रराजो महाबलः ।निहतः पाण्डवैः संख्ये पुत्रो वा मम संजय ॥६१॥
transliteration: kathaṁ ca vaḥ sametānāṁ madrarājo mahābalaḥ ,nihataḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ saṁkhye putro vā mama saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, tell me, how was your mighty King of Madra, who was assembled with you all, slain by the Pāṇḍavas in battle? And what about my son [Duryodhana]?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 61. O Sañjaya, tell me, how was your mighty King of Madra, who was assembled with you all, slain by the Pāṇḍavas in battle? And what about my son [Duryodhana]?

sanskrit: ब्रूहि सर्वं यथातत्त्वं भरतानां महाक्षयम् ।यथा च निहतः संख्ये पुत्रो दुर्योधनो मम ॥६२॥
transliteration: brūhi sarvaṁ yathātattvaṁ bharatānāṁ mahākṣayam ,yathā ca nihataḥ saṁkhye putro duryodhano mama.
english: Tell me everything truthfully about the great destruction of the Bhāratas, and how my son Duryodhana was slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 62. Tell me everything truthfully about the great destruction of the Bhāratas, and how my son Duryodhana was slain in battle.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालाश्च यथा सर्वे निहताः सपदानुगाः ।धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च द्रौपद्याः पञ्च चात्मजाः ॥६३॥
transliteration: pāñcālāśca yathā sarve nihatāḥ sapadānugāḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca draupadyāḥ pañca cātmajāḥ.
english: And how were all the Pañcālas, along with their followers, slain? How also were Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and Draupadī's five sons [slain]?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 63. And how were all the Pañcālas, along with their followers, slain? How also were Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and Draupadī's five sons [slain]?

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च यथा मुक्तास्तथोभौ सात्वतौ युधि ।कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च भारद्वाजस्य चात्मजः ॥६४॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca yathā muktāstathobhau sātvatau yudhi ,kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca bhāradvājasya cātmajaḥ.
english: And how were the Pāṇḍavas spared? And how were both Sātvatas [Kṛṣṇa and Sātyaki] spared in battle? And what about Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and the son of Bhāradvāja (Aśvatthāmā)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 64. And how were the Pāṇḍavas spared? And how were both Sātvatas [Kṛṣṇa and Sātyaki] spared in battle? And what about Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, and the son of Bhāradvāja (Aśvatthāmā)?

sanskrit: यद्यथा यादृशं चैव युद्धं वृत्तं च सांप्रतम् ।अखिलं श्रोतुमिच्छामि कुशलो ह्यसि संजय ॥६५॥
transliteration: yadyathā yādṛśaṁ caiva yuddhaṁ vṛttaṁ ca sāṁpratam ,akhilaṁ śrotumicchāmi kuśalo hyasi saṁjaya.
english: Sanjaya, I wish to hear everything about the battle - what it was like, what has transpired, and what is currently happening - for you are indeed skilled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 2 Verse 65. Sanjaya, I wish to hear everything about the battle - what it was like, what has transpired, and what is currently happening - for you are indeed skilled.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।शृणु राजन्नवहितो यथा वृत्तो महान्क्षयः ।कुरूणां पाण्डवानां च समासाद्य परस्परम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śṛṇu rājannavahito yathā vṛtto mahānkṣayaḥ ,kurūṇāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca samāsādya parasparam.
english: Sanjaya said: 'O King, listen attentively to how the great destruction of the Kurus and Pandavas transpired when they confronted each other.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: 'O King, listen attentively to how the great destruction of the Kurus and Pandavas transpired when they confronted each other.'

sanskrit: निहते सूतपुत्रे तु पाण्डवेन महात्मना ।विद्रुतेषु च सैन्येषु समानीतेषु चासकृत् ॥२॥
transliteration: nihate sūtaputre tu pāṇḍavena mahātmanā ,vidruteṣu ca sainyeṣu samānīteṣu cāsakṛt.
english: However, after the son of Suta (Karṇa) was slain by the great-souled Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), and even as the armies were repeatedly scattered and then rallied...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. However, after the son of Suta (Karṇa) was slain by the great-souled Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), and even as the armies were repeatedly scattered and then rallied...

sanskrit: विमुखे तव पुत्रे तु शोकोपहतचेतसि ।भृशोद्विग्नेषु सैन्येषु दृष्ट्वा पार्थस्य विक्रमम् ॥३॥
transliteration: vimukhe tava putre tu śokopahatacetasi ,bhṛśodvigneṣu sainyeṣu dṛṣṭvā pārthasya vikramam.
english: Moreover, with your son (Duryodhana) having turned away and his mind afflicted by sorrow, and the armies greatly disturbed after witnessing Partha's (Arjuna's) valor...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. Moreover, with your son (Duryodhana) having turned away and his mind afflicted by sorrow, and the armies greatly disturbed after witnessing Partha's (Arjuna's) valor...

sanskrit: ध्यायमानेषु सैन्येषु दुःखं प्राप्तेषु भारत ।बलानां मथ्यमानानां श्रुत्वा निनदमुत्तमम् ॥४॥
transliteration: dhyāyamāneṣu sainyeṣu duḥkhaṁ prāpteṣu bhārata ,balānāṁ mathyamānānāṁ śrutvā ninadamuttamam.
english: O Bhārata, while the armies, having fallen into distress, were being intently observed, and upon hearing the excellent roar of the forces being crushed...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. O Bhārata, while the armies, having fallen into distress, were being intently observed, and upon hearing the excellent roar of the forces being crushed...

sanskrit: अभिज्ञानं नरेन्द्राणां विकृतं प्रेक्ष्य संयुगे ।पतितान्रथनीडांश्च रथांश्चापि महात्मनाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: abhijñānaṁ narendrāṇāṁ vikṛtaṁ prekṣya saṁyuge ,patitānrathanīḍāṁśca rathāṁścāpi mahātmanām.
english: Having observed the disfigured insignia of the kings in battle, and the fallen chariot-seats and also the chariots of the mighty ones...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. Having observed the disfigured insignia of the kings in battle, and the fallen chariot-seats and also the chariots of the mighty ones...

sanskrit: रणे विनिहतान्नागान्दृष्ट्वा पत्तींश्च मारिष ।आयोधनं चातिघोरं रुद्रस्याक्रीडसंनिभम् ॥६॥
transliteration: raṇe vinihatānnāgāndṛṣṭvā pattīṁśca māriṣa ,āyodhanaṁ cātighoraṁ rudrasyākrīḍasaṁnibham.
english: And having seen the elephants and foot soldiers slain in battle, O respected one, and the exceedingly dreadful battlefield resembling Rudra's playground (ākrīḍa)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. And having seen the elephants and foot soldiers slain in battle, O respected one, and the exceedingly dreadful battlefield resembling Rudra's playground (ākrīḍa)...

sanskrit: अप्रख्यातिं गतानां तु राज्ञां शतसहस्रशः ।कृपाविष्टः कृपो राजन्वयःशीलसमन्वितः ॥७॥
transliteration: aprakhyātiṁ gatānāṁ tu rājñāṁ śatasahasraśaḥ ,kṛpāviṣṭaḥ kṛpo rājanvayaḥśīlasamanvitaḥ.
english: But when thousands upon thousands of kings had attained obscurity, Kṛpa, filled with compassion, O King, and endowed with age and good character (śīla)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. But when thousands upon thousands of kings had attained obscurity, Kṛpa, filled with compassion, O King, and endowed with age and good character (śīla)...

sanskrit: अब्रवीत्तत्र तेजस्वी सोऽभिसृत्य जनाधिपम् ।दुर्योधनं मन्युवशाद्वचनं वचनक्षमः ॥८॥
transliteration: abravīttatra tejasvī so'bhisṛtya janādhipam ,duryodhanaṁ manyuvaśādvacanaṁ vacanakṣamaḥ.
english: The brilliant one, eloquent in speech, approached King Duryodhana there and, overcome by anger, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. The brilliant one, eloquent in speech, approached King Duryodhana there and, overcome by anger, spoke these words.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन निबोधेदं यत्त्वा वक्ष्यामि कौरव ।श्रुत्वा कुरु महाराज यदि ते रोचतेऽनघ ॥९॥
transliteration: duryodhana nibodhedaṁ yattvā vakṣyāmi kaurava ,śrutvā kuru mahārāja yadi te rocate'nagha.
english: O Duryodhana, O Kaurava, listen to this which I will tell you. O great king, blameless one, having heard it, act accordingly if it pleases you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. O Duryodhana, O Kaurava, listen to this which I will tell you. O great king, blameless one, having heard it, act accordingly if it pleases you.

sanskrit: न युद्धधर्माच्छ्रेयान्वै पन्था राजेन्द्र विद्यते ।यं समाश्रित्य युध्यन्ते क्षत्रियाः क्षत्रियर्षभ ॥१०॥
transliteration: na yuddhadharmācchreyānvai panthā rājendra vidyate ,yaṁ samāśritya yudhyante kṣatriyāḥ kṣatriyarṣabha.
english: O chief of kings, O best of warriors, indeed, there is no path superior to the intrinsic nature (dharma) of battle, by resorting to which kṣatriyas fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. O chief of kings, O best of warriors, indeed, there is no path superior to the intrinsic nature (dharma) of battle, by resorting to which kṣatriyas fight.

sanskrit: पुत्रो भ्राता पिता चैव स्वस्रेयो मातुलस्तथा ।संबन्धिबान्धवाश्चैव योध्या वै क्षत्रजीविना ॥११॥
transliteration: putro bhrātā pitā caiva svasreyo mātulastathā ,saṁbandhibāndhavāścaiva yodhyā vai kṣatrajīvinā.
english: Son, brother, father, and also nephew, and maternal uncle, as well as other relatives and kinsmen - all these are indeed to be fought by a kṣatriya, one who lives by the warrior's code.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. Son, brother, father, and also nephew, and maternal uncle, as well as other relatives and kinsmen - all these are indeed to be fought by a kṣatriya, one who lives by the warrior's code.

sanskrit: वधे चैव परो धर्मस्तथाधर्मः पलायने ।ते स्म घोरां समापन्ना जीविकां जीवितार्थिनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vadhe caiva paro dharmastathādharmaḥ palāyane ,te sma ghorāṁ samāpannā jīvikāṁ jīvitārthinaḥ.
english: Indeed, killing is the supreme natural law (dharma), while fleeing is unrighteousness (adharma). They, desiring to live, have resorted to a dreadful livelihood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. Indeed, killing is the supreme natural law (dharma), while fleeing is unrighteousness (adharma). They, desiring to live, have resorted to a dreadful livelihood.

sanskrit: तत्र त्वां प्रतिवक्ष्यामि किंचिदेव हितं वचः ।हते भीष्मे च द्रोणे च कर्णे चैव महारथे ॥१३॥
transliteration: tatra tvāṁ prativakṣyāmi kiṁcideva hitaṁ vacaḥ ,hate bhīṣme ca droṇe ca karṇe caiva mahārathe.
english: Therefore, I shall speak to you some beneficial words. [This is] when Bhishma, Drona, and indeed the great warrior Karna have all been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. Therefore, I shall speak to you some beneficial words. [This is] when Bhishma, Drona, and indeed the great warrior Karna have all been killed.

sanskrit: जयद्रथे च निहते तव भ्रातृषु चानघ ।लक्ष्मणे तव पुत्रे च किं शेषं पर्युपास्महे ॥१४॥
transliteration: jayadrathe ca nihate tava bhrātṛṣu cānagha ,lakṣmaṇe tava putre ca kiṁ śeṣaṁ paryupāsmahe.
english: And, O sinless one, when Jayadratha is killed, and (when) your brothers (are killed), and your son Lakshmana (is killed), what remains for us to wait for?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. And, O sinless one, when Jayadratha is killed, and (when) your brothers (are killed), and your son Lakshmana (is killed), what remains for us to wait for?

sanskrit: येषु भारं समासज्य राज्ये मतिमकुर्महि ।ते संत्यज्य तनूर्याताः शूरा ब्रह्मविदां गतिम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yeṣu bhāraṁ samāsajya rājye matimakurmahi ,te saṁtyajya tanūryātāḥ śūrā brahmavidāṁ gatim.
english: Those heroes, on whom we had placed the responsibility for the kingdom and formed our plans for sovereignty, have abandoned their bodies and attained the state (gati) of those who know (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. Those heroes, on whom we had placed the responsibility for the kingdom and formed our plans for sovereignty, have abandoned their bodies and attained the state (gati) of those who know (brahman).

sanskrit: वयं त्विह विनाभूता गुणवद्भिर्महारथैः ।कृपणं वर्तयिष्याम पातयित्वा नृपान्बहून् ॥१६॥
transliteration: vayaṁ tviha vinābhūtā guṇavadbhirmahārathaiḥ ,kṛpaṇaṁ vartayiṣyāma pātayitvā nṛpānbahūn.
english: Indeed, we, separated here from virtuous great charioteers (mahāratha), will lead a miserable existence after striking down many kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Indeed, we, separated here from virtuous great charioteers (mahāratha), will lead a miserable existence after striking down many kings.

sanskrit: सर्वैरपि च जीवद्भिर्बीभत्सुरपराजितः ।कृष्णनेत्रो महाबाहुर्देवैरपि दुरासदः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sarvairapi ca jīvadbhirbībhatsuraparājitaḥ ,kṛṣṇanetro mahābāhurdevairapi durāsadaḥ.
english: And by all living beings, he is Bhībhatsu, the unconquered, whose guide is Krishna (kṛṣṇa-netra), the mighty-armed, unassailable even by the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. And by all living beings, he is Bhībhatsu, the unconquered, whose guide is Krishna (kṛṣṇa-netra), the mighty-armed, unassailable even by the gods.

sanskrit: इन्द्रकार्मुकवज्राभमिन्द्रकेतुमिवोच्छ्रितम् ।वानरं केतुमासाद्य संचचाल महाचमूः ॥१८॥
transliteration: indrakārmukavajrābhamindraketumivocchritam ,vānaraṁ ketumāsādya saṁcacāla mahācamūḥ.
english: The great army (mahācamū) marched forth, having obtained the monkey banner, which was raised high like Indra's standard and resembled Indra's bow and thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. The great army (mahācamū) marched forth, having obtained the monkey banner, which was raised high like Indra's standard and resembled Indra's bow and thunderbolt.

sanskrit: सिंहनादेन भीमस्य पाञ्चजन्यस्वनेन च ।गाण्डीवस्य च निर्घोषात्संहृष्यन्ति मनांसि नः ॥१९॥
transliteration: siṁhanādena bhīmasya pāñcajanyasvanena ca ,gāṇḍīvasya ca nirghoṣātsaṁhṛṣyanti manāṁsi naḥ.
english: Our hearts are thrilled by Bhīma's lion-like roar, by the sound of the Pāñcajanya (Krishna's conch), and by the twang of the Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna's bow).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. Our hearts are thrilled by Bhīma's lion-like roar, by the sound of the Pāñcajanya (Krishna's conch), and by the twang of the Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna's bow).

sanskrit: चरन्तीव महाविद्युन्मुष्णन्ती नयनप्रभाम् ।अलातमिव चाविद्धं गाण्डीवं समदृश्यत ॥२०॥
transliteration: carantīva mahāvidyunmuṣṇantī nayanaprabhām ,alātamiva cāviddhaṁ gāṇḍīvaṁ samadṛśyata.
english: The Gaṇḍīva bow was seen moving like a great flash of lightning, stealing the light from the eyes, and whirling like a firebrand.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 20. The Gaṇḍīva bow was seen moving like a great flash of lightning, stealing the light from the eyes, and whirling like a firebrand.

sanskrit: जाम्बूनदविचित्रं च धूयमानं महद्धनुः ।दृश्यते दिक्षु सर्वासु विद्युदभ्रघनेष्विव ॥२१॥
transliteration: jāmbūnadavicitraṁ ca dhūyamānaṁ mahaddhanuḥ ,dṛśyate dikṣu sarvāsu vidyudabhraghaneṣviva.
english: The great bow, adorned with gold and being waved, is seen in all directions, appearing like lightning amidst dense clouds.
