search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 21. The great bow, adorned with gold and being waved, is seen in all directions, appearing like lightning amidst dense clouds.

sanskrit: उह्यमानश्च कृष्णेन वायुनेव बलाहकः ।तावकं तद्बलं राजन्नर्जुनोऽस्त्रविदां वरः ।गहनं शिशिरे कक्षं ददाहाग्निरिवोत्थितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: uhyamānaśca kṛṣṇena vāyuneva balāhakaḥ ,tāvakaṁ tadbalaṁ rājannarjuno'stravidāṁ varaḥ ,gahanaṁ śiśire kakṣaṁ dadāhāgnirivotthitaḥ.
english: O King, Arjuna, who is the foremost among weapon-experts, carried by Kṛṣṇa just as a cloud is carried by the wind, consumed your army as a blazing fire consumes a dense, dry thicket.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 22. O King, Arjuna, who is the foremost among weapon-experts, carried by Kṛṣṇa just as a cloud is carried by the wind, consumed your army as a blazing fire consumes a dense, dry thicket.

sanskrit: गाहमानमनीकानि महेन्द्रसदृशप्रभम् ।धनंजयमपश्याम चतुर्दन्तमिव द्विपम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: gāhamānamanīkāni mahendrasadṛśaprabham ,dhanaṁjayamapaśyāma caturdantamiva dvipam.
english: We saw Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), penetrating the armies, whose splendor was like that of Mahendra, resembling a four-tusked elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 23. We saw Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), penetrating the armies, whose splendor was like that of Mahendra, resembling a four-tusked elephant.

sanskrit: विक्षोभयन्तं सेनां ते त्रासयन्तं च पार्थिवान् ।धनंजयमपश्याम नलिनीमिव कुञ्जरम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: vikṣobhayantaṁ senāṁ te trāsayantaṁ ca pārthivān ,dhanaṁjayamapaśyāma nalinīmiva kuñjaram.
english: We saw Dhananjaya (Arjuna) agitating your army and terrifying the kings, just as an elephant agitates a lotus pond.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 24. We saw Dhananjaya (Arjuna) agitating your army and terrifying the kings, just as an elephant agitates a lotus pond.

sanskrit: त्रासयन्तं तथा योधान्धनुर्घोषेण पाण्डवम् ।भूय एनमपश्याम सिंहं मृगगणा इव ॥२५॥
transliteration: trāsayantaṁ tathā yodhāndhanurghoṣeṇa pāṇḍavam ,bhūya enamapaśyāma siṁhaṁ mṛgagaṇā iva.
english: Similarly, we saw him (Arjuna), the Pandava, again, terrifying the warriors with the roar of his bow, just as a lion terrifies herds of deer.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 25. Similarly, we saw him (Arjuna), the Pandava, again, terrifying the warriors with the roar of his bow, just as a lion terrifies herds of deer.

sanskrit: सर्वलोकमहेष्वासौ वृषभौ सर्वधन्विनाम् ।आमुक्तकवचौ कृष्णौ लोकमध्ये विरेजतुः ॥२६॥
transliteration: sarvalokamaheṣvāsau vṛṣabhau sarvadhanvinām ,āmuktakavacau kṛṣṇau lokamadhye virejatuḥ.
english: The two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), who were the greatest archers in all the world and the foremost among all bowmen, and who were wearing armor, shone brilliantly in the midst of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 26. The two Krishnas (Arjuna and Vāsudeva), who were the greatest archers in all the world and the foremost among all bowmen, and who were wearing armor, shone brilliantly in the midst of the people.

sanskrit: अद्य सप्तदशाहानि वर्तमानस्य भारत ।संग्रामस्यातिघोरस्य वध्यतां चाभितो युधि ॥२७॥
transliteration: adya saptadaśāhāni vartamānasya bhārata ,saṁgrāmasyātighorasya vadhyatāṁ cābhito yudhi.
english: O Bhārata, today marks the seventeenth day of this exceedingly fierce battle, and the slaughter continues on all sides in the war.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 27. O Bhārata, today marks the seventeenth day of this exceedingly fierce battle, and the slaughter continues on all sides in the war.

sanskrit: वायुनेव विधूतानि तवानीकानि सर्वशः ।शरदम्भोदजालानि व्यशीर्यन्त समन्ततः ॥२८॥
transliteration: vāyuneva vidhūtāni tavānīkāni sarvaśaḥ ,śaradambhodajālāni vyaśīryanta samantataḥ.
english: Your armies, like masses of autumn clouds scattered by the wind, were completely dispersed everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 28. Your armies, like masses of autumn clouds scattered by the wind, were completely dispersed everywhere.

sanskrit: तां नावमिव पर्यस्तां भ्रान्तवातां महार्णवे ।तव सेनां महाराज सव्यसाची व्यकम्पयत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tāṁ nāvamiva paryastāṁ bhrāntavātāṁ mahārṇave ,tava senāṁ mahārāja savyasācī vyakampayat.
english: O great king, Savyasācin (Arjuna) shook your army, as if it were a boat capsized and buffeted by swirling winds in the vast ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 29. O great king, Savyasācin (Arjuna) shook your army, as if it were a boat capsized and buffeted by swirling winds in the vast ocean.

sanskrit: क्व नु ते सूतपुत्रोऽभूत्क्व नु द्रोणः सहानुगः ।अहं क्व च क्व चात्मा ते हार्दिक्यश्च तथा क्व नु ।दुःशासनश्च भ्राता ते भ्रातृभिः सहितः क्व नु ॥३०॥
transliteration: kva nu te sūtaputro'bhūtkva nu droṇaḥ sahānugaḥ ,ahaṁ kva ca kva cātmā te hārdikyaśca tathā kva nu ,duḥśāsanaśca bhrātā te bhrātṛbhiḥ sahitaḥ kva nu.
english: Where indeed was your son of Sūta (Karṇa)? Where was Droṇa with his followers? Where was I? And where was your own inner self (ātman)? And where, too, was Hārdikya (Kṛpa)? And where, indeed, was your brother Duḥśāsana, along with his other brothers?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 30. Where indeed was your son of Sūta (Karṇa)? Where was Droṇa with his followers? Where was I? And where was your own inner self (ātman)? And where, too, was Hārdikya (Kṛpa)? And where, indeed, was your brother Duḥśāsana, along with his other brothers?

sanskrit: बाणगोचरसंप्राप्तं प्रेक्ष्य चैव जयद्रथम् ।संबन्धिनस्ते भ्रातॄंश्च सहायान्मातुलांस्तथा ॥३१॥
transliteration: bāṇagocarasaṁprāptaṁ prekṣya caiva jayadratham ,saṁbandhinaste bhrātṝṁśca sahāyānmātulāṁstathā.
english: And indeed, seeing Jayadratha, your relatives, brothers, allies, and uncles, all of whom had come within the range of arrows...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 31. And indeed, seeing Jayadratha, your relatives, brothers, allies, and uncles, all of whom had come within the range of arrows...

sanskrit: सर्वान्विक्रम्य मिषतो लोकांश्चाक्रम्य मूर्धनि ।जयद्रथो हतो राजन्किं नु शेषमुपास्महे ॥३२॥
transliteration: sarvānvikramya miṣato lokāṁścākramya mūrdhani ,jayadratho hato rājankiṁ nu śeṣamupāsmahe.
english: O King, since Jayadratha, having overpowered all those who watched and having asserted his dominion over the worlds, has been killed, what more indeed remains for us to accomplish?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 32. O King, since Jayadratha, having overpowered all those who watched and having asserted his dominion over the worlds, has been killed, what more indeed remains for us to accomplish?

sanskrit: को वेह स पुमानस्ति यो विजेष्यति पाण्डवम् ।तस्य चास्त्राणि दिव्यानि विविधानि महात्मनः ।गाण्डीवस्य च निर्घोषो वीर्याणि हरते हि नः ॥३३॥
transliteration: ko veha sa pumānasti yo vijeṣyati pāṇḍavam ,tasya cāstrāṇi divyāni vividhāni mahātmanaḥ ,gāṇḍīvasya ca nirghoṣo vīryāṇi harate hi naḥ.
english: Who indeed is that man here who will conquer the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna)? And his divine, various weapons, along with the roar of his Gāṇḍīva bow, certainly diminish our valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 33. Who indeed is that man here who will conquer the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna)? And his divine, various weapons, along with the roar of his Gāṇḍīva bow, certainly diminish our valor.

sanskrit: नष्टचन्द्रा यथा रात्रिः सेनेयं हतनायका ।नागभग्नद्रुमा शुष्का नदीवाकुलतां गता ॥३४॥
transliteration: naṣṭacandrā yathā rātriḥ seneyaṁ hatanāyakā ,nāgabhagnadrumā śuṣkā nadīvākulatāṁ gatā.
english: This army, its leader slain, is like a night without a moon. It is distraught, like a dry river whose (bank's) trees have been shattered by elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 34. This army, its leader slain, is like a night without a moon. It is distraught, like a dry river whose (bank's) trees have been shattered by elephants.

sanskrit: ध्वजिन्यां हतनेत्रायां यथेष्टं श्वेतवाहनः ।चरिष्यति महाबाहुः कक्षेऽग्निरिव संज्वलन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: dhvajinyāṁ hatanetrāyāṁ yatheṣṭaṁ śvetavāhanaḥ ,cariṣyati mahābāhuḥ kakṣe'gniriva saṁjvalan.
english: In this army, whose leaders are slain, the mighty-armed Śvetavāhana (Arjuna) will move about as he pleases, blazing like a fire in a dry forest thicket.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 35. In this army, whose leaders are slain, the mighty-armed Śvetavāhana (Arjuna) will move about as he pleases, blazing like a fire in a dry forest thicket.

sanskrit: सात्यकेश्चैव यो वेगो भीमसेनस्य चोभयोः ।दारयेत गिरीन्सर्वाञ्शोषयेत च सागरान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: sātyakeścaiva yo vego bhīmasenasya cobhayoḥ ,dārayeta girīnsarvāñśoṣayeta ca sāgarān.
english: The impetuosity of both Satyaki and Bhimasena is such that it could tear apart all mountains and dry up the oceans.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 36. The impetuosity of both Satyaki and Bhimasena is such that it could tear apart all mountains and dry up the oceans.

sanskrit: उवाच वाक्यं यद्भीमः सभामध्ये विशां पते ।कृतं तत्सकलं तेन भूयश्चैव करिष्यति ॥३७॥
transliteration: uvāca vākyaṁ yadbhīmaḥ sabhāmadhye viśāṁ pate ,kṛtaṁ tatsakalaṁ tena bhūyaścaiva kariṣyati.
english: O lord of the people (viśām pate), whatever statement Bhima made in the assembly, he has accomplished all of it, and he will certainly do it again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 37. O lord of the people (viśām pate), whatever statement Bhima made in the assembly, he has accomplished all of it, and he will certainly do it again.

sanskrit: प्रमुखस्थे तदा कर्णे बलं पाण्डवरक्षितम् ।दुरासदं तथा गुप्तं गूढं गाण्डीवधन्वना ॥३८॥
transliteration: pramukhasthe tadā karṇe balaṁ pāṇḍavarakṣitam ,durāsadaṁ tathā guptaṁ gūḍhaṁ gāṇḍīvadhanvanā.
english: Even when Karna was at the forefront, the army protected by the Pandavas was difficult to approach, well-guarded, and concealed by Arjuna, the wielder of the Gandiva bow (gāṇḍīvadhanvan).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 38. Even when Karna was at the forefront, the army protected by the Pandavas was difficult to approach, well-guarded, and concealed by Arjuna, the wielder of the Gandiva bow (gāṇḍīvadhanvan).

sanskrit: युष्माभिस्तानि चीर्णानि यान्यसाधूनि साधुषु ।अकारणकृतान्येव तेषां वः फलमागतम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: yuṣmābhistāni cīrṇāni yānyasādhūni sādhuṣu ,akāraṇakṛtānyeva teṣāṁ vaḥ phalamāgatam.
english: Those wrongful deeds (asādhu) that you committed against virtuous people (sādhu), acts that were done without any cause – their consequence (phala) has now indeed come upon you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 39. Those wrongful deeds (asādhu) that you committed against virtuous people (sādhu), acts that were done without any cause – their consequence (phala) has now indeed come upon you.

sanskrit: आत्मनोऽर्थे त्वया लोको यत्नतः सर्व आहृतः ।स ते संशयितस्तात आत्मा च भरतर्षभ ॥४०॥
transliteration: ātmano'rthe tvayā loko yatnataḥ sarva āhṛtaḥ ,sa te saṁśayitastāta ātmā ca bharatarṣabha.
english: For your own sake, you have painstakingly gathered all the people. But now, dear one (tāta), that very world, and your own self (ātman), are in danger, O best of the Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 40. For your own sake, you have painstakingly gathered all the people. But now, dear one (tāta), that very world, and your own self (ātman), are in danger, O best of the Bharatas.

sanskrit: रक्ष दुर्योधनात्मानमात्मा सर्वस्य भाजनम् ।भिन्ने हि भाजने तात दिशो गच्छति तद्गतम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: rakṣa duryodhanātmānamātmā sarvasya bhājanam ,bhinne hi bhājane tāta diśo gacchati tadgatam.
english: O Duryodhana, protect your own self (ātman), for the self (ātman) is the receptacle of all. Indeed, dear one (tāta), when the container is shattered, what was contained within it disperses in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 41. O Duryodhana, protect your own self (ātman), for the self (ātman) is the receptacle of all. Indeed, dear one (tāta), when the container is shattered, what was contained within it disperses in all directions.

sanskrit: हीयमानेन वै संधिः पर्येष्टव्यः समेन च ।विग्रहो वर्धमानेन नीतिरेषा बृहस्पतेः ॥४२॥
transliteration: hīyamānena vai saṁdhiḥ paryeṣṭavyaḥ samena ca ,vigraho vardhamānena nītireṣā bṛhaspateḥ.
english: Indeed, a weaker party should seek an alliance, and so should one of equal strength. However, a stronger party should engage in conflict (vigraha). This is the policy (nīti) of Bṛhaspati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 42. Indeed, a weaker party should seek an alliance, and so should one of equal strength. However, a stronger party should engage in conflict (vigraha). This is the policy (nīti) of Bṛhaspati.

sanskrit: ते वयं पाण्डुपुत्रेभ्यो हीनाः स्वबलशक्तितः ।अत्र ते पाण्डवैः सार्धं संधिं मन्ये क्षमं प्रभो ॥४३॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ pāṇḍuputrebhyo hīnāḥ svabalaśaktitaḥ ,atra te pāṇḍavaiḥ sārdhaṁ saṁdhiṁ manye kṣamaṁ prabho.
english: We are inferior to the sons of Pāṇḍu in our own strength and capabilities. Therefore, in this situation, O lord (prabho), I consider an alliance with the Pāṇḍavas to be appropriate for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 43. We are inferior to the sons of Pāṇḍu in our own strength and capabilities. Therefore, in this situation, O lord (prabho), I consider an alliance with the Pāṇḍavas to be appropriate for you.

sanskrit: न जानीते हि यः श्रेयः श्रेयसश्चावमन्यते ।स क्षिप्रं भ्रश्यते राज्यान्न च श्रेयोऽनुविन्दति ॥४४॥
transliteration: na jānīte hi yaḥ śreyaḥ śreyasaścāvamanyate ,sa kṣipraṁ bhraśyate rājyānna ca śreyo'nuvindati.
english: Indeed, whoever does not know what is good fortune and disregards what is good fortune (śreyas), quickly falls from his kingdom and does not attain welfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 44. Indeed, whoever does not know what is good fortune and disregards what is good fortune (śreyas), quickly falls from his kingdom and does not attain welfare.

sanskrit: प्रणिपत्य हि राजानं राज्यं यदि लभेमहि ।श्रेयः स्यान्न तु मौढ्येन राजन्गन्तुं पराभवम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: praṇipatya hi rājānaṁ rājyaṁ yadi labhemahi ,śreyaḥ syānna tu mauḍhyena rājangantuṁ parābhavam.
english: Indeed, if we were to obtain the kingdom by bowing down to the king, that would be good fortune (śreyas). But, O King, it is not proper to invite defeat through foolishness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 45. Indeed, if we were to obtain the kingdom by bowing down to the king, that would be good fortune (śreyas). But, O King, it is not proper to invite defeat through foolishness.

sanskrit: वैचित्रवीर्यवचनात्कृपाशीलो युधिष्ठिरः ।विनियुञ्जीत राज्ये त्वां गोविन्दवचनेन च ॥४६॥
transliteration: vaicitravīryavacanātkṛpāśīlo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,viniyuñjīta rājye tvāṁ govindavacanena ca.
english: By the command of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (son of Vicitravīrya), and also by the counsel of Govinda, the compassionate Yudhiṣṭhira would appoint you to the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 46. By the command of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (son of Vicitravīrya), and also by the counsel of Govinda, the compassionate Yudhiṣṭhira would appoint you to the kingdom.

sanskrit: यद्ब्रूयाद्धि हृषीकेशो राजानमपराजितम् ।अर्जुनं भीमसेनं च सर्वं कुर्युरसंशयम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: yadbrūyāddhi hṛṣīkeśo rājānamaparājitam ,arjunaṁ bhīmasenaṁ ca sarvaṁ kuryurasaṁśayam.
english: Indeed, whatever Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna) might say to the unconquered King (Yudhiṣṭhira), Arjuna, and Bhīmasena, they would all undoubtedly accomplish.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 47. Indeed, whatever Hṛṣīkeśa (Krishna) might say to the unconquered King (Yudhiṣṭhira), Arjuna, and Bhīmasena, they would all undoubtedly accomplish.

sanskrit: नातिक्रमिष्यते कृष्णो वचनं कौरवस्य ह ।धृतराष्ट्रस्य मन्येऽहं नापि कृष्णस्य पाण्डवः ॥४८॥
transliteration: nātikramiṣyate kṛṣṇo vacanaṁ kauravasya ha ,dhṛtarāṣṭrasya manye'haṁ nāpi kṛṣṇasya pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa will certainly not transgress the word of the Kaurava. I believe that Dhṛtarāṣṭra will not transgress Kṛṣṇa's word, nor will the Pāṇḍava brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 48. Kṛṣṇa will certainly not transgress the word of the Kaurava. I believe that Dhṛtarāṣṭra will not transgress Kṛṣṇa's word, nor will the Pāṇḍava brothers.

sanskrit: एतत्क्षममहं मन्ये तव पार्थैरविग्रहम् ।न त्वा ब्रवीमि कार्पण्यान्न प्राणपरिरक्षणात् ।पथ्यं राजन्ब्रवीमि त्वां तत्परासुः स्मरिष्यसि ॥४९॥
transliteration: etatkṣamamahaṁ manye tava pārthairavigraham ,na tvā bravīmi kārpaṇyānna prāṇaparirakṣaṇāt ,pathyaṁ rājanbravīmi tvāṁ tatparāsuḥ smariṣyasi.
english: I consider this, a state of non-conflict with the Pāṇḍavas, to be suitable for you. I do not speak to you out of weakness or miserliness (kārpaṇya), nor for the sake of preserving my own life. O King, I speak what is beneficial to you; you will remember this when your life-breath (prāṇa) is about to depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 49. I consider this, a state of non-conflict with the Pāṇḍavas, to be suitable for you. I do not speak to you out of weakness or miserliness (kārpaṇya), nor for the sake of preserving my own life. O King, I speak what is beneficial to you; you will remember this when your life-breath (prāṇa) is about to depart.

sanskrit: इति वृद्धो विलप्यैतत्कृपः शारद्वतो वचः ।दीर्घमुष्णं च निःश्वस्य शुशोच च मुमोह च ॥५०॥
transliteration: iti vṛddho vilapyaitatkṛpaḥ śāradvato vacaḥ ,dīrghamuṣṇaṁ ca niḥśvasya śuśoca ca mumoha ca.
english: Having thus lamented this speech, the aged Kṛpa, son of Śāradvat, sighed long and hot, then grieved and fainted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 3 Verse 50. Having thus lamented this speech, the aged Kṛpa, son of Śāradvat, sighed long and hot, then grieved and fainted.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्ततो राजा गौतमेन यशस्विना ।निःश्वस्य दीर्घमुष्णं च तूष्णीमासीद्विशां पते ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evamuktastato rājā gautamena yaśasvinā ,niḥśvasya dīrghamuṣṇaṁ ca tūṣṇīmāsīdviśāṁ pate.
english: Sañjaya said: "Having thus been addressed by the renowned Gautama, the king then sighed deeply and warmly, and remained silent, O lord of men."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: "Having thus been addressed by the renowned Gautama, the king then sighed deeply and warmly, and remained silent, O lord of men."

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्तं स ध्यात्वा धार्तराष्ट्रो महामनाः ।कृपं शारद्वतं वाक्यमित्युवाच परंतपः ॥२॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtaṁ sa dhyātvā dhārtarāṣṭro mahāmanāḥ ,kṛpaṁ śāradvataṁ vākyamityuvāca paraṁtapaḥ.
english: Then, having reflected (dhyāna) for a moment, that great-minded Duryodhana, the tormentor of foes, spoke these words to Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. Then, having reflected (dhyāna) for a moment, that great-minded Duryodhana, the tormentor of foes, spoke these words to Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat.

sanskrit: यत्किंचित्सुहृदा वाच्यं तत्सर्वं श्रावितो ह्यहम् ।कृतं च भवता सर्वं प्राणान्संत्यज्य युध्यता ॥३॥
transliteration: yatkiṁcitsuhṛdā vācyaṁ tatsarvaṁ śrāvito hyaham ,kṛtaṁ ca bhavatā sarvaṁ prāṇānsaṁtyajya yudhyatā.
english: I have indeed been told all that a loyal friend (suhṛd) should say. And you have accomplished everything, fighting even to the point of giving up your very life (prāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. I have indeed been told all that a loyal friend (suhṛd) should say. And you have accomplished everything, fighting even to the point of giving up your very life (prāṇa).

sanskrit: गाहमानमनीकानि युध्यमानं महारथैः ।पाण्डवैरतितेजोभिर्लोकस्त्वामनुदृष्टवान् ॥४॥
transliteration: gāhamānamanīkāni yudhyamānaṁ mahārathaiḥ ,pāṇḍavairatitejobhirlokastvāmanudṛṣṭavān.
english: The world (loka) has seen you plunging into armies and fighting against great chariot-warriors (mahāratha) and the exceedingly powerful Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. The world (loka) has seen you plunging into armies and fighting against great chariot-warriors (mahāratha) and the exceedingly powerful Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: सुहृदा यदिदं वाच्यं भवता श्रावितो ह्यहम् ।न मां प्रीणाति तत्सर्वं मुमूर्षोरिव भेषजम् ॥५॥
transliteration: suhṛdā yadidaṁ vācyaṁ bhavatā śrāvito hyaham ,na māṁ prīṇāti tatsarvaṁ mumūrṣoriva bheṣajam.
english: I have indeed been informed by you, my loyal friend (suhṛd), of all that should be said. However, all of it does not please me, just as medicine (bheṣaja) does not please one who desires to die.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. I have indeed been informed by you, my loyal friend (suhṛd), of all that should be said. However, all of it does not please me, just as medicine (bheṣaja) does not please one who desires to die.

sanskrit: हेतुकारणसंयुक्तं हितं वचनमुत्तमम् ।उच्यमानं महाबाहो न मे विप्राग्र्य रोचते ॥६॥
transliteration: hetukāraṇasaṁyuktaṁ hitaṁ vacanamuttamam ,ucyamānaṁ mahābāho na me viprāgrya rocate.
english: O mighty-armed one, O best among Brahmins, even excellent and beneficial counsel, supported by reasons and logic, does not please me when it is spoken.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. O mighty-armed one, O best among Brahmins, even excellent and beneficial counsel, supported by reasons and logic, does not please me when it is spoken.

sanskrit: राज्याद्विनिकृतोऽस्माभिः कथं सोऽस्मासु विश्वसेत् ।अक्षद्यूते च नृपतिर्जितोऽस्माभिर्महाधनः ।स कथं मम वाक्यानि श्रद्दध्याद्भूय एव तु ॥७॥
transliteration: rājyādvinikṛto'smābhiḥ kathaṁ so'smāsu viśvaset ,akṣadyūte ca nṛpatirjito'smābhirmahādhanaḥ ,sa kathaṁ mama vākyāni śraddadhyādbhūya eva tu.
english: How would he trust us, after being deprived of his kingdom by us? Furthermore, how would that king, who was of great wealth and defeated by us in the game of dice, ever believe my words again?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. How would he trust us, after being deprived of his kingdom by us? Furthermore, how would that king, who was of great wealth and defeated by us in the game of dice, ever believe my words again?

sanskrit: तथा दौत्येन संप्राप्तः कृष्णः पार्थहिते रतः ।प्रलब्धश्च हृषीकेशस्तच्च कर्म विरोधितम् ।स च मे वचनं ब्रह्मन्कथमेवाभिमंस्यते ॥८॥
transliteration: tathā dautyena saṁprāptaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ pārthahite rataḥ ,pralabdhaśca hṛṣīkeśastacca karma virodhitam ,sa ca me vacanaṁ brahmankathamevābhimaṁsyate.
english: Indeed, Krishna (Hṛṣīkeśa) arrived as an envoy, intent on the welfare of Pārtha (Arjuna). He was misled, and that mission was obstructed by us. So how, O Brahmin, will he (Krishna) ever approve of my words?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. Indeed, Krishna (Hṛṣīkeśa) arrived as an envoy, intent on the welfare of Pārtha (Arjuna). He was misled, and that mission was obstructed by us. So how, O Brahmin, will he (Krishna) ever approve of my words?

sanskrit: विललाप हि यत्कृष्णा सभामध्ये समेयुषी ।न तन्मर्षयते कृष्णो न राज्यहरणं तथा ॥९॥
transliteration: vilalāpa hi yatkṛṣṇā sabhāmadhye sameyuṣī ,na tanmarṣayate kṛṣṇo na rājyaharaṇaṁ tathā.
english: Indeed, Krishna (Draupadī) lamented in the midst of the assembly; Krishna (Vāsudeva) does not tolerate that, nor does he tolerate the seizure of the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. Indeed, Krishna (Draupadī) lamented in the midst of the assembly; Krishna (Vāsudeva) does not tolerate that, nor does he tolerate the seizure of the kingdom.

sanskrit: एकप्राणावुभौ कृष्णावन्योन्यं प्रति संहतौ ।पुरा यच्छ्रुतमेवासीदद्य पश्यामि तत्प्रभो ॥१०॥
transliteration: ekaprāṇāvubhau kṛṣṇāvanyonyaṁ prati saṁhatau ,purā yacchrutamevāsīdadya paśyāmi tatprabho.
english: O Lord (prabho), I see today what was heard previously: that the two Kṛṣṇas, sharing a single vital breath (prāṇa), are united with each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. O Lord (prabho), I see today what was heard previously: that the two Kṛṣṇas, sharing a single vital breath (prāṇa), are united with each other.

sanskrit: स्वस्रीयं च हतं श्रुत्वा दुःखं स्वपिति केशवः ।कृतागसो वयं तस्य स मदर्थं कथं क्षमेत् ॥११॥
transliteration: svasrīyaṁ ca hataṁ śrutvā duḥkhaṁ svapiti keśavaḥ ,kṛtāgaso vayaṁ tasya sa madarthaṁ kathaṁ kṣamet.
english: Upon hearing that his nephew has been killed, Keśava sleeps in sorrow. We have committed offenses against him. How will he forgive (kṣamet) us for my sake?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. Upon hearing that his nephew has been killed, Keśava sleeps in sorrow. We have committed offenses against him. How will he forgive (kṣamet) us for my sake?

sanskrit: अभिमन्योर्विनाशेन न शर्म लभतेऽर्जुनः ।स कथं मद्धिते यत्नं प्रकरिष्यति याचितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: abhimanyorvināśena na śarma labhate'rjunaḥ ,sa kathaṁ maddhite yatnaṁ prakariṣyati yācitaḥ.
english: Arjuna finds no happiness after Abhimanyu's destruction. How will he, when requested, make an effort (yatna) for my welfare?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. Arjuna finds no happiness after Abhimanyu's destruction. How will he, when requested, make an effort (yatna) for my welfare?

sanskrit: मध्यमः पाण्डवस्तीक्ष्णो भीमसेनो महाबलः ।प्रतिज्ञातं च तेनोग्रं स भज्येत न संनमेत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: madhyamaḥ pāṇḍavastīkṣṇo bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,pratijñātaṁ ca tenograṁ sa bhajyeta na saṁnamet.
english: Bhīmasena, the fierce and very mighty middle Pāṇḍava, also made a terrible vow. He would break rather than bend.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. Bhīmasena, the fierce and very mighty middle Pāṇḍava, also made a terrible vow. He would break rather than bend.

sanskrit: उभौ तौ बद्धनिस्त्रिंशावुभौ चाबद्धकङ्कटौ ।कृतवैरावुभौ वीरौ यमावपि यमोपमौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: ubhau tau baddhanistriṁśāvubhau cābaddhakaṅkaṭau ,kṛtavairāvubhau vīrau yamāvapi yamopamau.
english: Both of them had their swords fastened, and both had their armor secured. These two heroes, having incurred enmity, were like two Yamas, indeed, comparable to Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. Both of them had their swords fastened, and both had their armor secured. These two heroes, having incurred enmity, were like two Yamas, indeed, comparable to Yama.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च कृतवैरौ मया सह ।तौ कथं मद्धिते यत्नं प्रकुर्यातां द्विजोत्तम ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca kṛtavairau mayā saha ,tau kathaṁ maddhite yatnaṁ prakuryātāṁ dvijottama.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin have incurred enmity with me. How, then, would those two make an effort for my welfare, O best among the twice-born (dvija)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍin have incurred enmity with me. How, then, would those two make an effort for my welfare, O best among the twice-born (dvija)?

sanskrit: दुःशासनेन यत्कृष्णा एकवस्त्रा रजस्वला ।परिक्लिष्टा सभामध्ये सर्वलोकस्य पश्यतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanena yatkṛṣṇā ekavastrā rajasvalā ,parikliṣṭā sabhāmadhye sarvalokasya paśyataḥ.
english: The fact that Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), dressed in a single garment and menstruating, was tormented by Duḥśāsana in the middle of the assembly, while all the people watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. The fact that Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), dressed in a single garment and menstruating, was tormented by Duḥśāsana in the middle of the assembly, while all the people watched.

sanskrit: तथा विवसनां दीनां स्मरन्त्यद्यापि पाण्डवाः ।न निवारयितुं शक्याः संग्रामात्ते परंतपाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tathā vivasanāṁ dīnāṁ smarantyadyāpi pāṇḍavāḥ ,na nivārayituṁ śakyāḥ saṁgrāmātte paraṁtapāḥ.
english: Therefore, the Pāṇḍavas, those tormentors of enemies, remembering her, unclothed and pitiable, even today, cannot be prevented from engaging in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. Therefore, the Pāṇḍavas, those tormentors of enemies, remembering her, unclothed and pitiable, even today, cannot be prevented from engaging in battle.

sanskrit: यदा च द्रौपदी कृष्णा मद्विनाशाय दुःखिता ।उग्रं तेपे तपः कृष्णा भर्तॄणामर्थसिद्धये ।स्थण्डिले नित्यदा शेते यावद्वैरस्य यातना ॥१८॥
transliteration: yadā ca draupadī kṛṣṇā madvināśāya duḥkhitā ,ugraṁ tepe tapaḥ kṛṣṇā bhartṝṇāmarthasiddhaye ,sthaṇḍile nityadā śete yāvadvairasya yātanā.
english: And when Draupadī Kṛṣṇā, anguished over my impending destruction, performed severe asceticism (tapas) for the accomplishment of her husbands' aims. She continuously sleeps on the bare ground as long as the torment of enmity (vaira) persists.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. And when Draupadī Kṛṣṇā, anguished over my impending destruction, performed severe asceticism (tapas) for the accomplishment of her husbands' aims. She continuously sleeps on the bare ground as long as the torment of enmity (vaira) persists.

sanskrit: निक्षिप्य मानं दर्पं च वासुदेवसहोदरा ।कृष्णायाः प्रेष्यवद्भूत्वा शुश्रूषां कुरुते सदा ॥१९॥
transliteration: nikṣipya mānaṁ darpaṁ ca vāsudevasahodarā ,kṛṣṇāyāḥ preṣyavadbhūtvā śuśrūṣāṁ kurute sadā.
english: Setting aside pride and arrogance, the sister of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), becoming like a servant to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), always performs service.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. Setting aside pride and arrogance, the sister of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), becoming like a servant to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), always performs service.

sanskrit: इति सर्वं समुन्नद्धं न निर्वाति कथंचन ।अभिमन्योर्विनाशेन स संधेयः कथं मया ॥२०॥
transliteration: iti sarvaṁ samunnaddhaṁ na nirvāti kathaṁcana ,abhimanyorvināśena sa saṁdheyaḥ kathaṁ mayā.
english: Thus, this entire entangled situation never subsides. With the destruction of Abhimanyu, how can reconciliation be brought about by me?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 20. Thus, this entire entangled situation never subsides. With the destruction of Abhimanyu, how can reconciliation be brought about by me?

sanskrit: कथं च नाम भुक्त्वेमां पृथिवीं सागराम्बराम् ।पाण्डवानां प्रसादेन भुञ्जीयां राज्यमल्पकम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: kathaṁ ca nāma bhuktvemāṁ pṛthivīṁ sāgarāmbarām ,pāṇḍavānāṁ prasādena bhuñjīyāṁ rājyamalpakam.
english: And how indeed could I, having ruled this earth with the oceans as its garment, then accept and enjoy a mere small kingdom by the favor of the Pāṇḍavas?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 21. And how indeed could I, having ruled this earth with the oceans as its garment, then accept and enjoy a mere small kingdom by the favor of the Pāṇḍavas?

sanskrit: उपर्युपरि राज्ञां वै ज्वलितो भास्करो यथा ।युधिष्ठिरं कथं पश्चादनुयास्यामि दासवत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: uparyupari rājñāṁ vai jvalito bhāskaro yathā ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ kathaṁ paścādanuyāsyāmi dāsavat.
english: Just as the blazing sun shines high above all kings, how can I then follow Yudhishthira like a servant?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 22. Just as the blazing sun shines high above all kings, how can I then follow Yudhishthira like a servant?

sanskrit: कथं भुक्त्वा स्वयं भोगान्दत्त्वा दायांश्च पुष्कलान् ।कृपणं वर्तयिष्यामि कृपणैः सह जीविकाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ bhuktvā svayaṁ bhogāndattvā dāyāṁśca puṣkalān ,kṛpaṇaṁ vartayiṣyāmi kṛpaṇaiḥ saha jīvikām.
english: After having personally enjoyed pleasures and given abundant gifts, how can I then lead a miserable existence alongside the wretched?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 23. After having personally enjoyed pleasures and given abundant gifts, how can I then lead a miserable existence alongside the wretched?

sanskrit: नाभ्यसूयामि ते वाक्यमुक्तं स्निग्धं हितं त्वया ।न तु संधिमहं मन्ये प्राप्तकालं कथंचन ॥२४॥
transliteration: nābhyasūyāmi te vākyamuktaṁ snigdhaṁ hitaṁ tvayā ,na tu saṁdhimahaṁ manye prāptakālaṁ kathaṁcana.
english: I do not resent your words, which were spoken affectionately and beneficially by you. However, I do not consider a peace treaty (sandhi) to be opportune at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 24. I do not resent your words, which were spoken affectionately and beneficially by you. However, I do not consider a peace treaty (sandhi) to be opportune at all.

sanskrit: सुनीतमनुपश्यामि सुयुद्धेन परंतप ।नायं क्लीबयितुं कालः संयोद्धुं काल एव नः ॥२५॥
transliteration: sunītamanupaśyāmi suyuddhena paraṁtapa ,nāyaṁ klībayituṁ kālaḥ saṁyoddhuṁ kāla eva naḥ.
english: O scorcher of foes, I perceive a righteous battle as the proper policy. This is not the time to be weak; rather, it is indeed our time to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 25. O scorcher of foes, I perceive a righteous battle as the proper policy. This is not the time to be weak; rather, it is indeed our time to fight.

sanskrit: इष्टं मे बहुभिर्यज्ञैर्दत्ता विप्रेषु दक्षिणाः ।प्राप्ताः क्रमश्रुता वेदाः शत्रूणां मूर्ध्नि च स्थितम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: iṣṭaṁ me bahubhiryajñairdattā vipreṣu dakṣiṇāḥ ,prāptāḥ kramaśrutā vedāḥ śatrūṇāṁ mūrdhni ca sthitam.
english: Many rituals (yajña) have been performed by me, and sacrificial fees have been given to the Brahmins. I have acquired the Vedas, learned in their proper sequence, and I have stood victorious over my enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 26. Many rituals (yajña) have been performed by me, and sacrificial fees have been given to the Brahmins. I have acquired the Vedas, learned in their proper sequence, and I have stood victorious over my enemies.

sanskrit: भृत्या मे सुभृतास्तात दीनश्चाभ्युद्धृतो जनः ।यातानि परराष्ट्राणि स्वराष्ट्रमनुपालितम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: bhṛtyā me subhṛtāstāta dīnaścābhyuddhṛto janaḥ ,yātāni pararāṣṭrāṇi svarāṣṭramanupālitam.
english: O dear one, my dependents have been well-maintained, and the distressed people have been uplifted. Foreign kingdoms have been conquered, and my own kingdom has been protected.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 27. O dear one, my dependents have been well-maintained, and the distressed people have been uplifted. Foreign kingdoms have been conquered, and my own kingdom has been protected.

sanskrit: भुक्ताश्च विविधा भोगास्त्रिवर्गः सेवितो मया ।पितॄणां गतमानृण्यं क्षत्रधर्मस्य चोभयोः ॥२८॥
transliteration: bhuktāśca vividhā bhogāstrivargaḥ sevito mayā ,pitṝṇāṁ gatamānṛṇyaṁ kṣatradharmasya cobhayoḥ.
english: I have enjoyed various pleasures and pursued the three aims of human life. The debt to my ancestors has been discharged, and the obligations inherent in the constitution (dharma) of a kṣatriya have also been fulfilled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 28. I have enjoyed various pleasures and pursued the three aims of human life. The debt to my ancestors has been discharged, and the obligations inherent in the constitution (dharma) of a kṣatriya have also been fulfilled.

sanskrit: न ध्रुवं सुखमस्तीह कुतो राज्यं कुतो यशः ।इह कीर्तिर्विधातव्या सा च युद्धेन नान्यथा ॥२९॥
transliteration: na dhruvaṁ sukhamastīha kuto rājyaṁ kuto yaśaḥ ,iha kīrtirvidhātavyā sā ca yuddhena nānyathā.
english: There is no lasting happiness in this world, so how can there be a kingdom, and how much less fame? Here, renown must be achieved, and that must be done through battle, not otherwise.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 29. There is no lasting happiness in this world, so how can there be a kingdom, and how much less fame? Here, renown must be achieved, and that must be done through battle, not otherwise.

sanskrit: गृहे यत्क्षत्रियस्यापि निधनं तद्विगर्हितम् ।अधर्मः सुमहानेष यच्छय्यामरणं गृहे ॥३०॥
transliteration: gṛhe yatkṣatriyasyāpi nidhanaṁ tadvigarhitam ,adharmaḥ sumahāneṣa yacchayyāmaraṇaṁ gṛhe.
english: Indeed, a kshatriya's death in the home is reprehensible. This dying on a bed in the house is a very great violation of natural law (adharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 30. Indeed, a kshatriya's death in the home is reprehensible. This dying on a bed in the house is a very great violation of natural law (adharma).

sanskrit: अरण्ये यो विमुञ्चेत संग्रामे वा तनुं नरः ।क्रतूनाहृत्य महतो महिमानं स गच्छति ॥३१॥
transliteration: araṇye yo vimuñceta saṁgrāme vā tanuṁ naraḥ ,kratūnāhṛtya mahato mahimānaṁ sa gacchati.
english: A man who gives up his body in the wilderness or in battle attains great glory after having performed great sacrifices (kratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 31. A man who gives up his body in the wilderness or in battle attains great glory after having performed great sacrifices (kratu).

sanskrit: कृपणं विलपन्नार्तो जरयाभिपरिप्लुतः ।म्रियते रुदतां मध्ये ज्ञातीनां न स पूरुषः ॥३२॥
transliteration: kṛpaṇaṁ vilapannārto jarayābhipariplutaḥ ,mriyate rudatāṁ madhye jñātīnāṁ na sa pūruṣaḥ.
english: One who, distressed and overcome by old age, laments piteously and dies amidst weeping relatives, that is not a true person (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 32. One who, distressed and overcome by old age, laments piteously and dies amidst weeping relatives, that is not a true person (puruṣa).

sanskrit: त्यक्त्वा तु विविधान्भोगान्प्राप्तानां परमां गतिम् ।अपीदानीं सुयुद्धेन गच्छेयं सत्सलोकताम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tyaktvā tu vividhānbhogānprāptānāṁ paramāṁ gatim ,apīdānīṁ suyuddhena gaccheyaṁ satsalokatām.
english: Having renounced various worldly enjoyments, and aspiring to be among those who have attained the supreme state, may I now achieve the state of dwelling with the virtuous through a noble battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 33. Having renounced various worldly enjoyments, and aspiring to be among those who have attained the supreme state, may I now achieve the state of dwelling with the virtuous through a noble battle.

sanskrit: शूराणामार्यवृत्तानां संग्रामेष्वनिवर्तिनाम् ।धीमतां सत्यसंधानां सर्वेषां क्रतुयाजिनाम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: śūrāṇāmāryavṛttānāṁ saṁgrāmeṣvanivartinām ,dhīmatāṁ satyasaṁdhānāṁ sarveṣāṁ kratuyājinām.
english: (Referring to) the brave, those of noble conduct, those who do not retreat in battles, the intelligent, those faithful to their vows, and all who perform Vedic sacrifices (kratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 34. (Referring to) the brave, those of noble conduct, those who do not retreat in battles, the intelligent, those faithful to their vows, and all who perform Vedic sacrifices (kratu).

sanskrit: शस्त्रावभृथमाप्तानां ध्रुवं वासस्त्रिविष्टपे ।मुदा नूनं प्रपश्यन्ति शुभ्रा ह्यप्सरसां गणाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: śastrāvabhṛthamāptānāṁ dhruvaṁ vāsastriviṣṭape ,mudā nūnaṁ prapaśyanti śubhrā hyapsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ.
english: For, bright hosts of celestial nymphs (apsaras) surely behold with joy the certain dwelling in heaven (triviṣṭapa) of those who have attained the purification bath of weapons (śastrāvabhṛtha) [i.e., died gloriously in battle].
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 35. For, bright hosts of celestial nymphs (apsaras) surely behold with joy the certain dwelling in heaven (triviṣṭapa) of those who have attained the purification bath of weapons (śastrāvabhṛtha) [i.e., died gloriously in battle].

sanskrit: पश्यन्ति नूनं पितरः पूजिताञ्शक्रसंसदि ।अप्सरोभिः परिवृतान्मोदमानांस्त्रिविष्टपे ॥३६॥
transliteration: paśyanti nūnaṁ pitaraḥ pūjitāñśakrasaṁsadi ,apsarobhiḥ parivṛtānmodamānāṁstriviṣṭape.
english: Surely the ancestors (pitaraḥ) behold [those warriors], honored in Indra's assembly (śakrasaṃsad), surrounded by celestial nymphs (apsaras) and rejoicing in heaven (triviṣṭapa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 36. Surely the ancestors (pitaraḥ) behold [those warriors], honored in Indra's assembly (śakrasaṃsad), surrounded by celestial nymphs (apsaras) and rejoicing in heaven (triviṣṭapa).

sanskrit: पन्थानममरैर्यातं शूरैश्चैवानिवर्तिभिः ।अपि तैः संगतं मार्गं वयमप्यारुहेमहि ॥३७॥
transliteration: panthānamamarairyātaṁ śūraiścaivānivartibhiḥ ,api taiḥ saṁgataṁ mārgaṁ vayamapyāruhemahi.
english: May we too ascend that path (mārga) which has been traversed by the immortals and indeed by the unretreating brave warriors, the path joined by them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 37. May we too ascend that path (mārga) which has been traversed by the immortals and indeed by the unretreating brave warriors, the path joined by them.

sanskrit: पितामहेन वृद्धेन तथाचार्येण धीमता ।जयद्रथेन कर्णेन तथा दुःशासनेन च ॥३८॥
transliteration: pitāmahena vṛddhena tathācāryeṇa dhīmatā ,jayadrathena karṇena tathā duḥśāsanena ca.
english: By the aged grandfather, and by the wise teacher; by Jayadratha, by Karṇa, and by Duśśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 38. By the aged grandfather, and by the wise teacher; by Jayadratha, by Karṇa, and by Duśśāsana.

sanskrit: घटमाना मदर्थेऽस्मिन्हताः शूरा जनाधिपाः ।शेरते लोहिताक्ताङ्गाः पृथिव्यां शरविक्षताः ॥३९॥
transliteration: ghaṭamānā madarthe'sminhatāḥ śūrā janādhipāḥ ,śerate lohitāktāṅgāḥ pṛthivyāṁ śaravikṣatāḥ.
english: These brave kings, who were striving here for my sake, have been slain. Their bodies smeared with blood and pierced by arrows, they lie on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 39. These brave kings, who were striving here for my sake, have been slain. Their bodies smeared with blood and pierced by arrows, they lie on the ground.

sanskrit: उत्तमास्त्रविदः शूरा यथोक्तक्रतुयाजिनः ।त्यक्त्वा प्राणान्यथान्यायमिन्द्रसद्मसु धिष्ठिताः ॥४०॥
transliteration: uttamāstravidaḥ śūrā yathoktakratuyājinaḥ ,tyaktvā prāṇānyathānyāyamindrasadmasu dhiṣṭhitāḥ.
english: The heroic ones, expert in the highest weapons and performing Vedic rituals (kratu) as prescribed, having righteously given up their lives, are established in the abodes of Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 40. The heroic ones, expert in the highest weapons and performing Vedic rituals (kratu) as prescribed, having righteously given up their lives, are established in the abodes of Indra.

sanskrit: तैस्त्वयं रचितः पन्था दुर्गमो हि पुनर्भवेत् ।संपतद्भिर्महावेगैरितो याद्भिश्च सद्गतिम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: taistvayaṁ racitaḥ panthā durgamo hi punarbhavet ,saṁpatadbhirmahāvegairito yādbhiśca sadgatim.
english: Indeed, this path (to heaven), created by them, would be difficult for those rushing from here with great speed and for those who seek a good destination (sadgati).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 41. Indeed, this path (to heaven), created by them, would be difficult for those rushing from here with great speed and for those who seek a good destination (sadgati).

sanskrit: ये मदर्थे हताः शूरास्तेषां कृतमनुस्मरन् ।ऋणं तत्प्रतिमुञ्चानो न राज्ये मन आदधे ॥४२॥
transliteration: ye madarthe hatāḥ śūrāsteṣāṁ kṛtamanusmaran ,ṛṇaṁ tatpratimuñcāno na rājye mana ādadhe.
english: Remembering the actions of those brave warriors who died for my cause, and considering that a debt to be repaid, I do not set my mind on ruling the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 42. Remembering the actions of those brave warriors who died for my cause, and considering that a debt to be repaid, I do not set my mind on ruling the kingdom.

sanskrit: पातयित्वा वयस्यांश्च भ्रातॄनथ पितामहान् ।जीवितं यदि रक्षेयं लोको मां गर्हयेद्ध्रुवम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: pātayitvā vayasyāṁśca bhrātṝnatha pitāmahān ,jīvitaṁ yadi rakṣeyaṁ loko māṁ garhayeddhruvam.
english: The world would surely condemn me if I were to preserve my life after having slain my friends, brothers, and also my grandfathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 43. The world would surely condemn me if I were to preserve my life after having slain my friends, brothers, and also my grandfathers.

sanskrit: कीदृशं च भवेद्राज्यं मम हीनस्य बन्धुभिः ।सखिभिश्च सुहृद्भिश्च प्रणिपत्य च पाण्डवम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: kīdṛśaṁ ca bhavedrājyaṁ mama hīnasya bandhubhiḥ ,sakhibhiśca suhṛdbhiśca praṇipatya ca pāṇḍavam.
english: And what kind of kingdom would it be for me, who am deprived of my kinsmen, friends, and well-wishers, especially after bowing down to the Pāṇḍava?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 44. And what kind of kingdom would it be for me, who am deprived of my kinsmen, friends, and well-wishers, especially after bowing down to the Pāṇḍava?

sanskrit: सोऽहमेतादृशं कृत्वा जगतोऽस्य पराभवम् ।सुयुद्धेन ततः स्वर्गं प्राप्स्यामि न तदन्यथा ॥४५॥
transliteration: so'hametādṛśaṁ kṛtvā jagato'sya parābhavam ,suyuddhena tataḥ svargaṁ prāpsyāmi na tadanyathā.
english: Therefore, after bringing about such a devastation (parābhava) for this world (jagat), I will then attain heaven through a righteous battle, and not in any other way.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 45. Therefore, after bringing about such a devastation (parābhava) for this world (jagat), I will then attain heaven through a righteous battle, and not in any other way.

sanskrit: एवं दुर्योधनेनोक्तं सर्वे संपूज्य तद्वचः ।साधु साध्विति राजानं क्षत्रियाः संबभाषिरे ॥४६॥
transliteration: evaṁ duryodhanenoktaṁ sarve saṁpūjya tadvacaḥ ,sādhu sādhviti rājānaṁ kṣatriyāḥ saṁbabhāṣire.
english: After Duryodhana had spoken in this manner, all the warriors (kṣatriyas), honoring his words, addressed the king, saying, 'Excellent! Excellent!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 46. After Duryodhana had spoken in this manner, all the warriors (kṣatriyas), honoring his words, addressed the king, saying, 'Excellent! Excellent!'

sanskrit: पराजयमशोचन्तः कृतचित्ताश्च विक्रमे ।सर्वे सुनिश्चिता योद्धुमुदग्रमनसोऽभवन् ॥४७॥
transliteration: parājayamaśocantaḥ kṛtacittāśca vikrame ,sarve suniścitā yoddhumudagramanaso'bhavan.
english: Not lamenting defeat, and with their minds resolute in valor, all of them became completely determined to fight, their spirits eager.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 47. Not lamenting defeat, and with their minds resolute in valor, all of them became completely determined to fight, their spirits eager.

sanskrit: ततो वाहान्समाश्वास्य सर्वे युद्धाभिनन्दिनः ।ऊने द्वियोजने गत्वा प्रत्यतिष्ठन्त कौरवाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: tato vāhānsamāśvāsya sarve yuddhābhinandinaḥ ,ūne dviyojane gatvā pratyatiṣṭhanta kauravāḥ.
english: Then, all the Kauravas, who delighted in battle, having comforted their steeds, traveled less than two yojanas and halted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 48. Then, all the Kauravas, who delighted in battle, having comforted their steeds, traveled less than two yojanas and halted.

sanskrit: आकाशे विद्रुमे पुण्ये प्रस्थे हिमवतः शुभे ।अरुणां सरस्वतीं प्राप्य पपुः सस्नुश्च तज्जलम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: ākāśe vidrume puṇye prasthe himavataḥ śubhe ,aruṇāṁ sarasvatīṁ prāpya papuḥ sasnuśca tajjalam.
english: They reached the reddish (Aruṇā) Sarasvati river on a holy and auspicious plateau of the Himalaya, an open, treeless expanse. There, they drank its water and bathed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 49. They reached the reddish (Aruṇā) Sarasvati river on a holy and auspicious plateau of the Himalaya, an open, treeless expanse. There, they drank its water and bathed.

sanskrit: तव पुत्राः कृतोत्साहाः पर्यवर्तन्त ते ततः ।पर्यवस्थाप्य चात्मानमन्योन्येन पुनस्तदा ।सर्वे राजन्न्यवर्तन्त क्षत्रियाः कालचोदिताः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tava putrāḥ kṛtotsāhāḥ paryavartanta te tataḥ ,paryavasthāpya cātmānamanyonyena punastadā ,sarve rājannyavartanta kṣatriyāḥ kālacoditāḥ.
english: O King, your sons, inspired with zeal, then turned back. And having mutually recovered their composure, all those warriors (kṣatriya), impelled by time, then returned again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 4 Verse 50. O King, your sons, inspired with zeal, then turned back. And having mutually recovered their composure, all those warriors (kṣatriya), impelled by time, then returned again.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अथ हैमवते प्रस्थे स्थित्वा युद्धाभिनन्दिनः ।सर्व एव महाराज योधास्तत्र समागताः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,atha haimavate prasthe sthitvā yuddhābhinandinaḥ ,sarva eva mahārāja yodhāstatra samāgatāḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: Then, O great King, all those warriors, delighting in battle, having stayed on the Himālayan plateau, assembled there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: Then, O great King, all those warriors, delighting in battle, having stayed on the Himālayan plateau, assembled there.

sanskrit: शल्यश्च चित्रसेनश्च शकुनिश्च महारथः ।अश्वत्थामा कृपश्चैव कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ॥२॥
transliteration: śalyaśca citrasenaśca śakuniśca mahārathaḥ ,aśvatthāmā kṛpaścaiva kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ.
english: Śalya, Citrasena, and Śakuni, the great warrior (mahāratha), along with Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, and Kṛtavarmā of the Sātvata clan.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. Śalya, Citrasena, and Śakuni, the great warrior (mahāratha), along with Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, and Kṛtavarmā of the Sātvata clan.

sanskrit: सुषेणोऽरिष्टसेनश्च धृतसेनश्च वीर्यवान् ।जयत्सेनश्च राजानस्ते रात्रिमुषितास्ततः ॥३॥
transliteration: suṣeṇo'riṣṭasenaśca dhṛtasenaśca vīryavān ,jayatsenaśca rājānaste rātrimuṣitāstataḥ.
english: Suṣeṇa, Ariṣṭasena, and the valiant Dhṛtasena, as well as Jayatsena and (other) kings, they all spent the night there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. Suṣeṇa, Ariṣṭasena, and the valiant Dhṛtasena, as well as Jayatsena and (other) kings, they all spent the night there.

sanskrit: रणे कर्णे हते वीरे त्रासिता जितकाशिभिः ।नालभञ्शर्म ते पुत्रा हिमवन्तमृते गिरिम् ॥४॥
transliteration: raṇe karṇe hate vīre trāsitā jitakāśibhiḥ ,nālabhañśarma te putrā himavantamṛte girim.
english: After the heroic Karṇa was killed in battle, your sons, terrified by the victorious ones, could not find peace (śarma) anywhere, as if only the Himālaya mountain could offer them refuge.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. After the heroic Karṇa was killed in battle, your sons, terrified by the victorious ones, could not find peace (śarma) anywhere, as if only the Himālaya mountain could offer them refuge.

sanskrit: तेऽब्रुवन्सहितास्तत्र राजानं सैन्यसंनिधौ ।कृतयत्ना रणे राजन्संपूज्य विधिवत्तदा ॥५॥
transliteration: te'bruvansahitāstatra rājānaṁ sainyasaṁnidhau ,kṛtayatnā raṇe rājansaṁpūjya vidhivattadā.
english: Then, after having exerted themselves greatly in battle and having duly honored (sampūjya) the king, they (your sons), assembled there in the presence of the army, addressed the king: 'O King!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. Then, after having exerted themselves greatly in battle and having duly honored (sampūjya) the king, they (your sons), assembled there in the presence of the army, addressed the king: 'O King!'

sanskrit: कृत्वा सेनाप्रणेतारं परांस्त्वं योद्धुमर्हसि ।येनाभिगुप्ताः संग्रामे जयेमासुहृदो वयम् ॥६॥
transliteration: kṛtvā senāpraṇetāraṁ parāṁstvaṁ yoddhumarhasi ,yenābhiguptāḥ saṁgrāme jayemāsuhṛdo vayam.
english: Having appointed a leader for the army, you (O King) are fit to fight the enemies. Protected by him, we may conquer our foes (asuhṛdaḥ) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. Having appointed a leader for the army, you (O King) are fit to fight the enemies. Protected by him, we may conquer our foes (asuhṛdaḥ) in battle.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनः स्थित्वा रथे रथवरोत्तमम् ।सर्वयुद्धविभागज्ञमन्तकप्रतिमं युधि ॥७॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaḥ sthitvā rathe rathavarottamam ,sarvayuddhavibhāgajñamantakapratimaṁ yudhi.
english: Then Duryodhana, having taken his stand in his chariot, [beheld/considered] the supreme among chariot warriors (rathavarottamam), who was knowledgeable in all branches of warfare and was like Antaka (god of death) in battle (yudhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Then Duryodhana, having taken his stand in his chariot, [beheld/considered] the supreme among chariot warriors (rathavarottamam), who was knowledgeable in all branches of warfare and was like Antaka (god of death) in battle (yudhi).

sanskrit: स्वङ्गं प्रच्छन्नशिरसं कम्बुग्रीवं प्रियंवदम् ।व्याकोशपद्माभिमुखं व्याघ्रास्यं मेरुगौरवम् ॥८॥
transliteration: svaṅgaṁ pracchannaśirasaṁ kambugrīvaṁ priyaṁvadam ,vyākośapadmābhimukhaṁ vyāghrāsyaṁ merugauravam.
english: (He was) well-formed, with a covered head, a conch-like neck, pleasant-speaking, with a face like a blooming lotus, a tiger-like countenance, and the dignity of Mount Meru.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. (He was) well-formed, with a covered head, a conch-like neck, pleasant-speaking, with a face like a blooming lotus, a tiger-like countenance, and the dignity of Mount Meru.

sanskrit: स्थाणोर्वृषस्य सदृशं स्कन्धनेत्रगतिस्वरैः ।पुष्टश्लिष्टायतभुजं सुविस्तीर्णघनोरसम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sthāṇorvṛṣasya sadṛśaṁ skandhanetragatisvaraiḥ ,puṣṭaśliṣṭāyatabhujaṁ suvistīrṇaghanorasam.
english: (He was) similar to Śiva's (Sthāṇu's) bull (Vṛṣa) in his shoulders, eyes, gait, and voice; having strong, firmly-joined, and long arms, and a very broad and solid chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. (He was) similar to Śiva's (Sthāṇu's) bull (Vṛṣa) in his shoulders, eyes, gait, and voice; having strong, firmly-joined, and long arms, and a very broad and solid chest.

sanskrit: जवे बले च सदृशमरुणानुजवातयोः ।आदित्यस्य त्विषा तुल्यं बुद्ध्या चोशनसा समम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: jave bale ca sadṛśamaruṇānujavātayoḥ ,ādityasya tviṣā tulyaṁ buddhyā cośanasā samam.
english: (He was) similar in speed and strength to Aruṇa's younger brother (Garuḍa) and Vāta; equal to the sun (āditya) in splendor, and equal to Uśanas (Śukra) in intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. (He was) similar in speed and strength to Aruṇa's younger brother (Garuḍa) and Vāta; equal to the sun (āditya) in splendor, and equal to Uśanas (Śukra) in intellect.

sanskrit: कान्तिरूपमुखैश्वर्यैस्त्रिभिश्चन्द्रमसोपमम् ।काञ्चनोपलसंघातैः सदृशं श्लिष्टसंधिकम् ॥११॥
transliteration: kāntirūpamukhaiśvaryaistribhiścandramasopamam ,kāñcanopalasaṁghātaiḥ sadṛśaṁ śliṣṭasaṁdhikam.
english: (He was) similar to the moon in three aspects: radiance, beauty, and facial majesty; similar to masses of gold and precious stones, with well-formed joints.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. (He was) similar to the moon in three aspects: radiance, beauty, and facial majesty; similar to masses of gold and precious stones, with well-formed joints.

sanskrit: सुवृत्तोरुकटीजङ्घं सुपादं स्वङ्गुलीनखम् ।स्मृत्वा स्मृत्वैव च गुणान्धात्रा यत्नाद्विनिर्मितम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: suvṛttorukaṭījaṅghaṁ supādaṁ svaṅgulīnakham ,smṛtvā smṛtvaiva ca guṇāndhātrā yatnādvinirmitam.
english: Remembering its qualities again and again, it was diligently fashioned by the Creator (Dhātṛ), endowed with beautifully rounded broad hips and thighs, well-formed feet, and attractive fingers, toes, and nails.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. Remembering its qualities again and again, it was diligently fashioned by the Creator (Dhātṛ), endowed with beautifully rounded broad hips and thighs, well-formed feet, and attractive fingers, toes, and nails.

sanskrit: सर्वलक्षणसंपन्नं निपुणं श्रुतिसागरम् ।जेतारं तरसारीणामजेयं शत्रुभिर्बलात् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sarvalakṣaṇasaṁpannaṁ nipuṇaṁ śrutisāgaram ,jetāraṁ tarasārīṇāmajeyaṁ śatrubhirbalāt.
english: He (was) endowed with all auspicious marks, skillful, an ocean of scriptures, a conqueror of swift-moving rivals, and unconquerable by enemies through force.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. He (was) endowed with all auspicious marks, skillful, an ocean of scriptures, a conqueror of swift-moving rivals, and unconquerable by enemies through force.

sanskrit: दशाङ्गं यश्चतुष्पादमिष्वस्त्रं वेद तत्त्वतः ।साङ्गांश्च चतुरो वेदान्सम्यगाख्यानपञ्चमान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: daśāṅgaṁ yaścatuṣpādamiṣvastraṁ veda tattvataḥ ,sāṅgāṁśca caturo vedānsamyagākhyānapañcamān.
english: He who truly knows the science of archery (iṣvastra) with its ten components (daśāṅga) and four divisions (catuṣpāda), and also thoroughly comprehends the four Vedas along with their auxiliary branches (sāṅga), deeming the narrative traditions (ākhyāna) as the fifth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. He who truly knows the science of archery (iṣvastra) with its ten components (daśāṅga) and four divisions (catuṣpāda), and also thoroughly comprehends the four Vedas along with their auxiliary branches (sāṅga), deeming the narrative traditions (ākhyāna) as the fifth.

sanskrit: आराध्य त्र्यम्बकं यत्नाद्व्रतैरुग्रैर्महातपाः ।अयोनिजायामुत्पन्नो द्रोणेनायोनिजेन यः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ārādhya tryambakaṁ yatnādvratairugrairmahātapāḥ ,ayonijāyāmutpanno droṇenāyonijena yaḥ.
english: He who, a great ascetic (mahātapāḥ), diligently worshipped Tryambaka with severe vows, and who was born from a non-uterine source (ayonijā) by Droṇa, who himself was not born from a womb (ayonija).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. He who, a great ascetic (mahātapāḥ), diligently worshipped Tryambaka with severe vows, and who was born from a non-uterine source (ayonijā) by Droṇa, who himself was not born from a womb (ayonija).

sanskrit: तमप्रतिमकर्माणं रूपेणासदृशं भुवि ।पारगं सर्वविद्यानां गुणार्णवमनिन्दितम् ।तमभ्येत्यात्मजस्तुभ्यमश्वत्थामानमब्रवीत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tamapratimakarmāṇaṁ rūpeṇāsadṛśaṁ bhuvi ,pāragaṁ sarvavidyānāṁ guṇārṇavamaninditam ,tamabhyetyātmajastubhyamaśvatthāmānamabravīt.
english: Your son (Duryodhana), having approached that Aśvatthāman - who was unequaled in his deeds, unrivaled in beauty on earth, a master of all lores, an ocean of noble qualities, and blameless - spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. Your son (Duryodhana), having approached that Aśvatthāman - who was unequaled in his deeds, unrivaled in beauty on earth, a master of all lores, an ocean of noble qualities, and blameless - spoke to him.

sanskrit: यं पुरस्कृत्य सहिता युधि जेष्याम पाण्डवान् ।गुरुपुत्रोऽद्य सर्वेषामस्माकं परमा गतिः ।भवांस्तस्मान्नियोगात्ते कोऽस्तु सेनापतिर्मम ॥१७॥
transliteration: yaṁ puraskṛtya sahitā yudhi jeṣyāma pāṇḍavān ,guruputro'dya sarveṣāmasmākaṁ paramā gatiḥ ,bhavāṁstasmānniyogātte ko'stu senāpatirmama.
english: (Duryodhana said:) "Having placed whom at our forefront, we, united, shall conquer the Pāṇḍavas in battle? Now, you, the son of our guru (Aśvatthāman), are the supreme refuge (gati) for all of us. Therefore, by your appointment, who should be my commander-in-chief?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. (Duryodhana said:) "Having placed whom at our forefront, we, united, shall conquer the Pāṇḍavas in battle? Now, you, the son of our guru (Aśvatthāman), are the supreme refuge (gati) for all of us. Therefore, by your appointment, who should be my commander-in-chief?"

sanskrit: द्रौणिरुवाच ।अयं कुलेन वीर्येण तेजसा यशसा श्रिया ।सर्वैर्गुणैः समुदितः शल्यो नोऽस्तु चमूपतिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: drauṇiruvāca ,ayaṁ kulena vīryeṇa tejasā yaśasā śriyā ,sarvairguṇaiḥ samuditaḥ śalyo no'stu camūpatiḥ.
english: Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) said: "May this Śalya be our commander, who is endowed with noble lineage, valor, splendor, fame, glory, and all other qualities."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) said: "May this Śalya be our commander, who is endowed with noble lineage, valor, splendor, fame, glory, and all other qualities."

sanskrit: भागिनेयान्निजांस्त्यक्त्वा कृतज्ञोऽस्मानुपागतः ।महासेनो महाबाहुर्महासेन इवापरः ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhāgineyānnijāṁstyaktvā kṛtajño'smānupāgataḥ ,mahāseno mahābāhurmahāsena ivāparaḥ.
english: (He, Śalya,) having abandoned his own nephews (Pāṇḍavas), and being loyal to his commitments (kṛtajña), has joined us. He is a great general, mighty-armed, like another Mahāsena (Kārtikeya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. (He, Śalya,) having abandoned his own nephews (Pāṇḍavas), and being loyal to his commitments (kṛtajña), has joined us. He is a great general, mighty-armed, like another Mahāsena (Kārtikeya).

sanskrit: एनं सेनापतिं कृत्वा नृपतिं नृपसत्तम ।शक्यः प्राप्तुं जयोऽस्माभिर्देवैः स्कन्दमिवाजितम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: enaṁ senāpatiṁ kṛtvā nṛpatiṁ nṛpasattama ,śakyaḥ prāptuṁ jayo'smābhirdevaiḥ skandamivājitam.
english: O best of kings, by appointing this ruler as commander-in-chief, victory can be achieved by us, just as the unconquered Skanda (Kārtikeya) was gained by the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. O best of kings, by appointing this ruler as commander-in-chief, victory can be achieved by us, just as the unconquered Skanda (Kārtikeya) was gained by the gods.

sanskrit: तथोक्ते द्रोणपुत्रेण सर्व एव नराधिपाः ।परिवार्य स्थिताः शल्यं जयशब्दांश्च चक्रिरे ।युद्धाय च मतिं चक्रूरावेशं च परं ययुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathokte droṇaputreṇa sarva eva narādhipāḥ ,parivārya sthitāḥ śalyaṁ jayaśabdāṁśca cakrire ,yuddhāya ca matiṁ cakrūrāveśaṁ ca paraṁ yayuḥ.
english: After the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) had thus spoken, all the kings surrounded Śalya and stood, uttering shouts of victory. They also resolved for battle and attained supreme enthusiasm.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. After the son of Droṇa (Aśvatthāman) had thus spoken, all the kings surrounded Śalya and stood, uttering shouts of victory. They also resolved for battle and attained supreme enthusiasm.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनः शल्यं भूमौ स्थित्वा रथे स्थितम् ।उवाच प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा रामभीष्मसमं रणे ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaḥ śalyaṁ bhūmau sthitvā rathe sthitam ,uvāca prāñjalirbhūtvā rāmabhīṣmasamaṁ raṇe.
english: Then Duryodhana, standing on the ground, approached Śalya, who was seated in his chariot, and, with folded hands, addressed him, [praising him as] equal to Rāma and Bhīṣma in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. Then Duryodhana, standing on the ground, approached Śalya, who was seated in his chariot, and, with folded hands, addressed him, [praising him as] equal to Rāma and Bhīṣma in battle.

sanskrit: अयं स कालः संप्राप्तो मित्राणां मित्रवत्सल ।यत्र मित्रममित्रं वा परीक्षन्ते बुधा जनाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: ayaṁ sa kālaḥ saṁprāpto mitrāṇāṁ mitravatsala ,yatra mitramamitraṁ vā parīkṣante budhā janāḥ.
english: O friend-loving one, this is that opportune moment that has arrived for friends, when wise people discern whether someone is truly a friend or an enemy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. O friend-loving one, this is that opportune moment that has arrived for friends, when wise people discern whether someone is truly a friend or an enemy.

sanskrit: स भवानस्तु नः शूरः प्रणेता वाहिनीमुखे ।रणं च याते भवति पाण्डवा मन्दचेतसः ।भविष्यन्ति सहामात्याः पाञ्चालाश्च निरुद्यमाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa bhavānastu naḥ śūraḥ praṇetā vāhinīmukhe ,raṇaṁ ca yāte bhavati pāṇḍavā mandacetasaḥ ,bhaviṣyanti sahāmātyāḥ pāñcālāśca nirudyamāḥ.
english: May your honor be our brave leader at the forefront of the army. When you enter the battle, the Pandavas will become faint-hearted, and the Panchalas, along with their ministers, will also be devoid of effort.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. May your honor be our brave leader at the forefront of the army. When you enter the battle, the Pandavas will become faint-hearted, and the Panchalas, along with their ministers, will also be devoid of effort.

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।यत्तु मां मन्यसे राजन्कुरुराज करोमि तत् ।त्वत्प्रियार्थं हि मे सर्वं प्राणा राज्यं धनानि च ॥२५॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,yattu māṁ manyase rājankururāja karomi tat ,tvatpriyārthaṁ hi me sarvaṁ prāṇā rājyaṁ dhanāni ca.
english: Śalya said: 'O King, O Kuru King, whatever you consider appropriate for me, that I shall certainly do. Indeed, for your pleasure, all my life, kingdom, and wealth belong to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. Śalya said: 'O King, O Kuru King, whatever you consider appropriate for me, that I shall certainly do. Indeed, for your pleasure, all my life, kingdom, and wealth belong to you.'

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।सेनापत्येन वरये त्वामहं मातुलातुलम् ।सोऽस्मान्पाहि युधां श्रेष्ठ स्कन्दो देवानिवाहवे ॥२६॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,senāpatyena varaye tvāmahaṁ mātulātulam ,so'smānpāhi yudhāṁ śreṣṭha skando devānivāhave.
english: Duryodhana said: 'O unequalled maternal uncle, I choose you for the supreme command (senāpatyam). O best of warriors, protect us just as Skanda protects the gods in battle.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 26. Duryodhana said: 'O unequalled maternal uncle, I choose you for the supreme command (senāpatyam). O best of warriors, protect us just as Skanda protects the gods in battle.'

sanskrit: अभिषिच्यस्व राजेन्द्र देवानामिव पावकिः ।जहि शत्रून्रणे वीर महेन्द्रो दानवानिव ॥२७॥
transliteration: abhiṣicyasva rājendra devānāmiva pāvakiḥ ,jahi śatrūnraṇe vīra mahendro dānavāniva.
english: O King of kings, consecrate yourself (as commander) just as Pāvaki (Skanda) was consecrated among the gods. O hero, vanquish the enemies in battle, just as Mahendra (Indra) vanquishes the Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 5 Verse 27. O King of kings, consecrate yourself (as commander) just as Pāvaki (Skanda) was consecrated among the gods. O hero, vanquish the enemies in battle, just as Mahendra (Indra) vanquishes the Dānavas.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचो राज्ञो मद्रराजः प्रतापवान् ।दुर्योधनं तदा राजन्वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,etacchrutvā vaco rājño madrarājaḥ pratāpavān ,duryodhanaṁ tadā rājanvākyametaduvāca ha.
english: Sanjaya said: O King, having heard these words of the king, the powerful king of Madra then spoke this statement to Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: O King, having heard these words of the king, the powerful king of Madra then spoke this statement to Duryodhana.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन महाबाहो शृणु वाक्यविदां वर ।यावेतौ मन्यसे कृष्णौ रथस्थौ रथिनां वरौ ।न मे तुल्यावुभावेतौ बाहुवीर्ये कथंचन ॥२॥
transliteration: duryodhana mahābāho śṛṇu vākyavidāṁ vara ,yāvetau manyase kṛṣṇau rathasthau rathināṁ varau ,na me tulyāvubhāvetau bāhuvīrye kathaṁcana.
english: O Duryodhana, mighty-armed one, best among those who know words, listen! These two Kṛṣṇas, whom you consider to be the best of charioteers standing on chariots, these two are in no way equal to me in the strength of their arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. O Duryodhana, mighty-armed one, best among those who know words, listen! These two Kṛṣṇas, whom you consider to be the best of charioteers standing on chariots, these two are in no way equal to me in the strength of their arms.

sanskrit: उद्यतां पृथिवीं सर्वां ससुरासुरमानवाम् ।योधयेयं रणमुखे संक्रुद्धः किमु पाण्डवान् ।विजेष्ये च रणे पार्थान्सोमकांश्च समागतान् ॥३॥
transliteration: udyatāṁ pṛthivīṁ sarvāṁ sasurāsuramānavām ,yodhayeyaṁ raṇamukhe saṁkruddhaḥ kimu pāṇḍavān ,vijeṣye ca raṇe pārthānsomakāṁśca samāgatān.
english: Even if greatly enraged, I could fight the entire earth, including gods, asuras, and humans, at the forefront of battle; how much more so the Pāṇḍavas? And in battle, I will conquer the Pārthas (Arjuna and his brothers) and the Somakas who have assembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. Even if greatly enraged, I could fight the entire earth, including gods, asuras, and humans, at the forefront of battle; how much more so the Pāṇḍavas? And in battle, I will conquer the Pārthas (Arjuna and his brothers) and the Somakas who have assembled.

sanskrit: अहं सेनाप्रणेता ते भविष्यामि न संशयः ।तं च व्यूहं विधास्यामि न तरिष्यन्ति यं परे ।इति सत्यं ब्रवीम्येष दुर्योधन न संशयः ॥४॥
transliteration: ahaṁ senāpraṇetā te bhaviṣyāmi na saṁśayaḥ ,taṁ ca vyūhaṁ vidhāsyāmi na tariṣyanti yaṁ pare ,iti satyaṁ bravīmyeṣa duryodhana na saṁśayaḥ.
english: I will be your army commander, without a doubt. And I will arrange such a battle formation (vyūha) that others will not be able to overcome. This truth I speak, O Duryodhana, there is no doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. I will be your army commander, without a doubt. And I will arrange such a battle formation (vyūha) that others will not be able to overcome. This truth I speak, O Duryodhana, there is no doubt.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्ततो राजा मद्राधिपतिमञ्जसा ।अभ्यषिञ्चत सेनाया मध्ये भरतसत्तम ।विधिना शास्त्रदृष्टेन हृष्टरूपो विशां पते ॥५॥
transliteration: evamuktastato rājā madrādhipatimañjasā ,abhyaṣiñcata senāyā madhye bharatasattama ,vidhinā śāstradṛṣṭena hṛṣṭarūpo viśāṁ pate.
english: O best among the Bharatas, O lord of men, thus addressed, the king (Duryodhana) then swiftly consecrated the lord of Madras (Shalya) in the midst of the army, with a joyful demeanor, according to the method prescribed by the sacred texts (śāstra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. O best among the Bharatas, O lord of men, thus addressed, the king (Duryodhana) then swiftly consecrated the lord of Madras (Shalya) in the midst of the army, with a joyful demeanor, according to the method prescribed by the sacred texts (śāstra).

sanskrit: अभिषिक्ते ततस्तस्मिन्सिंहनादो महानभूत् ।तव सैन्येष्ववाद्यन्त वादित्राणि च भारत ॥६॥
transliteration: abhiṣikte tatastasminsiṁhanādo mahānabhūt ,tava sainyeṣvavādyanta vāditrāṇi ca bhārata.
english: O Bharata, when he was consecrated, a great lion's roar (siṃhanāda) arose, and musical instruments were sounded in your armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. O Bharata, when he was consecrated, a great lion's roar (siṃhanāda) arose, and musical instruments were sounded in your armies.

sanskrit: हृष्टाश्चासंस्तदा योधा मद्रकाश्च महारथाः ।तुष्टुवुश्चैव राजानं शल्यमाहवशोभिनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: hṛṣṭāścāsaṁstadā yodhā madrakāśca mahārathāḥ ,tuṣṭuvuścaiva rājānaṁ śalyamāhavaśobhinam.
english: And then, the warriors and the great charioteers (mahārathas) from Madra were delighted. Indeed, they praised King Shalya, who shone splendidly in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. And then, the warriors and the great charioteers (mahārathas) from Madra were delighted. Indeed, they praised King Shalya, who shone splendidly in battle.

sanskrit: जय राजंश्चिरं जीव जहि शत्रून्समागतान् ।तव बाहुबलं प्राप्य धार्तराष्ट्रा महाबलाः ।निखिलां पृथिवीं सर्वां प्रशासन्तु हतद्विषः ॥८॥
transliteration: jaya rājaṁściraṁ jīva jahi śatrūnsamāgatān ,tava bāhubalaṁ prāpya dhārtarāṣṭrā mahābalāḥ ,nikhilāṁ pṛthivīṁ sarvāṁ praśāsantu hatadviṣaḥ.
english: Be victorious, O king! Live long! Conquer all the assembled enemies! Having attained your mighty arm-strength, let the powerful sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭras) rule the entire earth, having slain their foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. Be victorious, O king! Live long! Conquer all the assembled enemies! Having attained your mighty arm-strength, let the powerful sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭras) rule the entire earth, having slain their foes.

sanskrit: त्वं हि शक्तो रणे जेतुं ससुरासुरमानवान् ।मर्त्यधर्माण इह तु किमु सोमकसृञ्जयान् ॥९॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi śakto raṇe jetuṁ sasurāsuramānavān ,martyadharmāṇa iha tu kimu somakasṛñjayān.
english: Indeed, you are capable of conquering in battle even gods, asuras, and humans. So what then of these Somakas and Sṛñjayas here, who possess a mortal nature?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. Indeed, you are capable of conquering in battle even gods, asuras, and humans. So what then of these Somakas and Sṛñjayas here, who possess a mortal nature?

sanskrit: एवं संस्तूयमानस्तु मद्राणामधिपो बली ।हर्षं प्राप तदा वीरो दुरापमकृतात्मभिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁstūyamānastu madrāṇāmadhipo balī ,harṣaṁ prāpa tadā vīro durāpamakṛtātmabhiḥ.
english: Being thus praised, that powerful hero, the lord of the Madras, then attained a joy difficult for those with uncultivated selves (ātman) to achieve.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. Being thus praised, that powerful hero, the lord of the Madras, then attained a joy difficult for those with uncultivated selves (ātman) to achieve.

sanskrit: शल्य उवाच ।अद्यैवाहं रणे सर्वान्पाञ्चालान्सह पाण्डवैः ।निहनिष्यामि राजेन्द्र स्वर्गं यास्यामि वा हतः ॥११॥
transliteration: śalya uvāca ,adyaivāhaṁ raṇe sarvānpāñcālānsaha pāṇḍavaiḥ ,nihaniṣyāmi rājendra svargaṁ yāsyāmi vā hataḥ.
english: Śalya said: 'O King, today itself I will either slay all the Pāñcālas along with the Pāṇḍavas in battle, or, if killed, I will go to heaven.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. Śalya said: 'O King, today itself I will either slay all the Pāñcālas along with the Pāṇḍavas in battle, or, if killed, I will go to heaven.'

sanskrit: अद्य पश्यन्तु मां लोका विचरन्तमभीतवत् ।अद्य पाण्डुसुताः सर्वे वासुदेवः ससात्यकिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: adya paśyantu māṁ lokā vicarantamabhītavat ,adya pāṇḍusutāḥ sarve vāsudevaḥ sasātyakiḥ.
english: Today, let the people behold me moving fearlessly; today, let them also behold all the sons of Pāṇḍu, Vāsudeva, along with Sātyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. Today, let the people behold me moving fearlessly; today, let them also behold all the sons of Pāṇḍu, Vāsudeva, along with Sātyaki.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालाश्चेदयश्चैव द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः ।धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च सर्वे चापि प्रभद्रकाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: pāñcālāścedayaścaiva draupadeyāśca sarvaśaḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca sarve cāpi prabhadrakāḥ.
english: And also the Panchalas, the Chedis, and completely all the sons of Draupadi (Draupadeyas); and Dhrishtadyumna, Shikhandi, and indeed all the Prabhadrakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. And also the Panchalas, the Chedis, and completely all the sons of Draupadi (Draupadeyas); and Dhrishtadyumna, Shikhandi, and indeed all the Prabhadrakas.

sanskrit: विक्रमं मम पश्यन्तु धनुषश्च महद्बलम् ।लाघवं चास्त्रवीर्यं च भुजयोश्च बलं युधि ॥१४॥
transliteration: vikramaṁ mama paśyantu dhanuṣaśca mahadbalam ,lāghavaṁ cāstravīryaṁ ca bhujayośca balaṁ yudhi.
english: Let them witness my valor, the great power of my bow, my agility, the might of my weapons, and the strength of my arms in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. Let them witness my valor, the great power of my bow, my agility, the might of my weapons, and the strength of my arms in battle.

sanskrit: अद्य पश्यन्तु मे पार्थाः सिद्धाश्च सह चारणैः ।यादृशं मे बलं बाह्वोः संपदस्त्रेषु या च मे ॥१५॥
transliteration: adya paśyantu me pārthāḥ siddhāśca saha cāraṇaiḥ ,yādṛśaṁ me balaṁ bāhvoḥ saṁpadastreṣu yā ca me.
english: Today, let the sons of Pritha (Parthas), and the Siddhas along with the Charanas, witness the immense strength of my arms, and the proficiency I possess in weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. Today, let the sons of Pritha (Parthas), and the Siddhas along with the Charanas, witness the immense strength of my arms, and the proficiency I possess in weapons.

sanskrit: अद्य मे विक्रमं दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवानां महारथाः ।प्रतीकारपरा भूत्वा चेष्टन्तां विविधाः क्रियाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: adya me vikramaṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārathāḥ ,pratīkāraparā bhūtvā ceṣṭantāṁ vividhāḥ kriyāḥ.
english: Today, after witnessing my valor, let the great charioteers among the Pandavas, becoming intent on countermeasures, undertake various actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 16. Today, after witnessing my valor, let the great charioteers among the Pandavas, becoming intent on countermeasures, undertake various actions.

sanskrit: अद्य सैन्यानि पाण्डूनां द्रावयिष्ये समन्ततः ।द्रोणभीष्मावति विभो सूतपुत्रं च संयुगे ।विचरिष्ये रणे युध्यन्प्रियार्थं तव कौरव ॥१७॥
transliteration: adya sainyāni pāṇḍūnāṁ drāvayiṣye samantataḥ ,droṇabhīṣmāvati vibho sūtaputraṁ ca saṁyuge ,vicariṣye raṇe yudhyanpriyārthaṁ tava kaurava.
english: Today, O lord (vibho) and O Kaurava, for your pleasure, I will completely rout the armies of the Pāṇḍavas. In battle, fighting, I will move about, surpassing Drona and Bhishma, and Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 17. Today, O lord (vibho) and O Kaurava, for your pleasure, I will completely rout the armies of the Pāṇḍavas. In battle, fighting, I will move about, surpassing Drona and Bhishma, and Karna.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अभिषिक्ते तदा शल्ये तव सैन्येषु मानद ।न कर्णव्यसनं किंचिन्मेनिरे तत्र भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,abhiṣikte tadā śalye tava sainyeṣu mānada ,na karṇavyasanaṁ kiṁcinmenire tatra bhārata.
english: Sanjaya said: O bestower of honor (mānada), O scion of Bharata, when Salya was then appointed in command of your armies, the soldiers there felt no loss for Karna (karṇavyasanam) at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 18. Sanjaya said: O bestower of honor (mānada), O scion of Bharata, when Salya was then appointed in command of your armies, the soldiers there felt no loss for Karna (karṇavyasanam) at all.

sanskrit: हृष्टाः सुमनसश्चैव बभूवुस्तत्र सैनिकाः ।मेनिरे निहतान्पार्थान्मद्रराजवशं गतान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: hṛṣṭāḥ sumanasaścaiva babhūvustatra sainikāḥ ,menire nihatānpārthānmadrarājavaśaṁ gatān.
english: The soldiers there were indeed joyful and cheerful. They considered the Pāṇḍavas as good as slain, having come under the sway of the King of Madras (Madrarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 19. The soldiers there were indeed joyful and cheerful. They considered the Pāṇḍavas as good as slain, having come under the sway of the King of Madras (Madrarāja).

sanskrit: प्रहर्षं प्राप्य सेना तु तावकी भरतर्षभ ।तां रात्रिं सुखिनी सुप्ता स्वस्थचित्तेव साभवत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: praharṣaṁ prāpya senā tu tāvakī bharatarṣabha ,tāṁ rātriṁ sukhinī suptā svasthacitteva sābhavat.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), your army, having attained great joy, slept contentedly that night, as if it possessed a tranquil mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 20. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), your army, having attained great joy, slept contentedly that night, as if it possessed a tranquil mind.

sanskrit: सैन्यस्य तव तं शब्दं श्रुत्वा राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।वार्ष्णेयमब्रवीद्वाक्यं सर्वक्षत्रस्य शृण्वतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: sainyasya tava taṁ śabdaṁ śrutvā rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,vārṣṇeyamabravīdvākyaṁ sarvakṣatrasya śṛṇvataḥ.
english: Having heard that sound of your army, King Yudhishthira spoke to Vārṣṇeya (Krishna) while all the kṣatriyas (warriors) were listening.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 21. Having heard that sound of your army, King Yudhishthira spoke to Vārṣṇeya (Krishna) while all the kṣatriyas (warriors) were listening.

sanskrit: मद्रराजः कृतः शल्यो धार्तराष्ट्रेण माधव ।सेनापतिर्महेष्वासः सर्वसैन्येषु पूजितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: madrarājaḥ kṛtaḥ śalyo dhārtarāṣṭreṇa mādhava ,senāpatirmaheṣvāsaḥ sarvasainyeṣu pūjitaḥ.
english: O Mādhava (Krishna), King Shalya of Madra has been made the general by Duryodhana; he is a great archer and is honored by all armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 22. O Mādhava (Krishna), King Shalya of Madra has been made the general by Duryodhana; he is a great archer and is honored by all armies.

sanskrit: एतज्ज्ञात्वा यथाभूतं कुरु माधव यत्क्षमम् ।भवान्नेता च गोप्ता च विधत्स्व यदनन्तरम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: etajjñātvā yathābhūtaṁ kuru mādhava yatkṣamam ,bhavānnetā ca goptā ca vidhatsva yadanantaram.
english: O Mādhava (Krishna), knowing this as it truly is, please do what is appropriate. You are both our leader and protector, so arrange what is to follow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 23. O Mādhava (Krishna), knowing this as it truly is, please do what is appropriate. You are both our leader and protector, so arrange what is to follow.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीन्महाराज वासुदेवो जनाधिपम् ।आर्तायनिमहं जाने यथातत्त्वेन भारत ॥२४॥
