transliteration: tamabravīnmahārāja vāsudevo janādhipam ,ārtāyanimahaṁ jāne yathātattvena bhārata.
english: O great king, Vāsudeva (Krishna) then spoke to the king of men (Yudhishthira): "O Bhārata (Yudhishthira), I know Ārtāyani (Shalya) as he truly is."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 24. O great king, Vāsudeva (Krishna) then spoke to the king of men (Yudhishthira): "O Bhārata (Yudhishthira), I know Ārtāyani (Shalya) as he truly is."

sanskrit: वीर्यवांश्च महातेजा महात्मा च विशेषतः ।कृती च चित्रयोधी च संयुक्तो लाघवेन च ॥२५॥
transliteration: vīryavāṁśca mahātejā mahātmā ca viśeṣataḥ ,kṛtī ca citrayodhī ca saṁyukto lāghavena ca.
english: He is valorous and greatly energetic, and especially a noble (mahātman) person. He is also accomplished and a wonderful fighter, endowed with great swiftness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 25. He is valorous and greatly energetic, and especially a noble (mahātman) person. He is also accomplished and a wonderful fighter, endowed with great swiftness.

sanskrit: यादृग्भीष्मस्तथा द्रोणो यादृक्कर्णश्च संयुगे ।तादृशस्तद्विशिष्टो वा मद्रराजो मतो मम ॥२६॥
transliteration: yādṛgbhīṣmastathā droṇo yādṛkkarṇaśca saṁyuge ,tādṛśastadviśiṣṭo vā madrarājo mato mama.
english: The king of Madra (Madrarāja), in my opinion, is like Bhishma, or Drona, or Karna in battle, or even superior to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 26. The king of Madra (Madrarāja), in my opinion, is like Bhishma, or Drona, or Karna in battle, or even superior to them.

sanskrit: युध्यमानस्य तस्याजौ चिन्तयन्नेव भारत ।योद्धारं नाधिगच्छामि तुल्यरूपं जनाधिप ॥२७॥
transliteration: yudhyamānasya tasyājau cintayanneva bhārata ,yoddhāraṁ nādhigacchāmi tulyarūpaṁ janādhipa.
english: O scion of Bharata (Bhārata), O ruler of men (Janādhipa), when I reflect on him fighting in battle, I cannot find any warrior who is his equal.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 27. O scion of Bharata (Bhārata), O ruler of men (Janādhipa), when I reflect on him fighting in battle, I cannot find any warrior who is his equal.

sanskrit: शिखण्ड्यर्जुनभीमानां सात्वतस्य च भारत ।धृष्टद्युम्नस्य च तथा बलेनाभ्यधिको रणे ॥२८॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍyarjunabhīmānāṁ sātvatasya ca bhārata ,dhṛṣṭadyumnasya ca tathā balenābhyadhiko raṇe.
english: O scion of Bharata (Bhārata), he is superior in strength in battle even to Shikhaṇḍī, Arjuna, Bhīma, Sātvata, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 28. O scion of Bharata (Bhārata), he is superior in strength in battle even to Shikhaṇḍī, Arjuna, Bhīma, Sātvata, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna.

sanskrit: मद्रराजो महाराज सिंहद्विरदविक्रमः ।विचरिष्यत्यभीः काले कालः क्रुद्धः प्रजास्विव ॥२९॥
transliteration: madrarājo mahārāja siṁhadviradavikramaḥ ,vicariṣyatyabhīḥ kāle kālaḥ kruddhaḥ prajāsviva.
english: The great king, the king of Madras, possessing the valor of a lion and an elephant, will roam about fearlessly in due time, just like enraged Death (kāla) among living beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 29. The great king, the king of Madras, possessing the valor of a lion and an elephant, will roam about fearlessly in due time, just like enraged Death (kāla) among living beings.

sanskrit: तस्याद्य न प्रपश्यामि प्रतियोद्धारमाहवे ।त्वामृते पुरुषव्याघ्र शार्दूलसमविक्रमम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tasyādya na prapaśyāmi pratiyoddhāramāhave ,tvāmṛte puruṣavyāghra śārdūlasamavikramam.
english: At present, I do not see any opponent for him in battle, except for you, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), whose valor is equal to a tiger's.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 30. At present, I do not see any opponent for him in battle, except for you, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), whose valor is equal to a tiger's.

sanskrit: सदेवलोके कृत्स्नेऽस्मिन्नान्यस्त्वत्तः पुमान्भवेत् ।मद्रराजं रणे क्रुद्धं यो हन्यात्कुरुनन्दन ।अहन्यहनि युध्यन्तं क्षोभयन्तं बलं तव ॥३१॥
transliteration: sadevaloke kṛtsne'sminnānyastvattaḥ pumānbhavet ,madrarājaṁ raṇe kruddhaṁ yo hanyātkurunandana ,ahanyahani yudhyantaṁ kṣobhayantaṁ balaṁ tava.
english: In this entire world, including the realm of the gods, there would be no other man but you, O son of Kuru (kurunandana), who could kill the enraged king of Madras in battle, the one who is fighting day after day and agitating your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 31. In this entire world, including the realm of the gods, there would be no other man but you, O son of Kuru (kurunandana), who could kill the enraged king of Madras in battle, the one who is fighting day after day and agitating your army.

sanskrit: तस्माज्जहि रणे शल्यं मघवानिव शम्बरम् ।अतिपश्चादसौ वीरो धार्तराष्ट्रेण सत्कृतः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasmājjahi raṇe śalyaṁ maghavāniva śambaram ,atipaścādasau vīro dhārtarāṣṭreṇa satkṛtaḥ.
english: Therefore, slay Śalya in battle, just as Maghavan (Indra) slew Śambara. This hero was honored too late by the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 32. Therefore, slay Śalya in battle, just as Maghavan (Indra) slew Śambara. This hero was honored too late by the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: तवैव हि जयो नूनं हते मद्रेश्वरे युधि ।तस्मिन्हते हतं सर्वं धार्तराष्ट्रबलं महत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tavaiva hi jayo nūnaṁ hate madreśvare yudhi ,tasminhate hataṁ sarvaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrabalaṁ mahat.
english: Indeed, your victory is certain once the king of Madra is slain in battle. With his demise, the entire formidable army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra will be annihilated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 33. Indeed, your victory is certain once the king of Madra is slain in battle. With his demise, the entire formidable army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra will be annihilated.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा महाराज वचनं मम सांप्रतम् ।प्रत्युद्याहि रणे पार्थ मद्रराजं महाबलम् ।जहि चैनं महाबाहो वासवो नमुचिं यथा ॥३४॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā mahārāja vacanaṁ mama sāṁpratam ,pratyudyāhi raṇe pārtha madrarājaṁ mahābalam ,jahi cainaṁ mahābāho vāsavo namuciṁ yathā.
english: O great king, having heard these current words of mine, O Pārtha, advance into battle against the immensely powerful king of Madra. And slay him, O mighty-armed one, just as Vāsava (Indra) slew Namuci.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 34. O great king, having heard these current words of mine, O Pārtha, advance into battle against the immensely powerful king of Madra. And slay him, O mighty-armed one, just as Vāsava (Indra) slew Namuci.

sanskrit: न चैवात्र दया कार्या मातुलोऽयं ममेति वै ।क्षत्रधर्मं पुरस्कृत्य जहि मद्रजनेश्वरम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: na caivātra dayā kāryā mātulo'yaṁ mameti vai ,kṣatradharmaṁ puraskṛtya jahi madrajaneśvaram.
english: And indeed, no pity should be shown here by thinking, "he is my maternal uncle." Upholding the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatriya), slay the lord of the Madra people.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 35. And indeed, no pity should be shown here by thinking, "he is my maternal uncle." Upholding the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatriya), slay the lord of the Madra people.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणार्णवं तीर्त्वा कर्णपातालसंभवम् ।मा निमज्जस्व सगणः शल्यमासाद्य गोष्पदम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇārṇavaṁ tīrtvā karṇapātālasaṁbhavam ,mā nimajjasva sagaṇaḥ śalyamāsādya goṣpadam.
english: Having successfully navigated the ocean (of obstruction) represented by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, and which had Karṇa as its perilous netherworld, do not now, along with your retinue, drown in the shallow puddle (goṣpada) that you have reached, which is Śalya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 36. Having successfully navigated the ocean (of obstruction) represented by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, and which had Karṇa as its perilous netherworld, do not now, along with your retinue, drown in the shallow puddle (goṣpada) that you have reached, which is Śalya.

sanskrit: यच्च ते तपसो वीर्यं यच्च क्षात्रं बलं तव ।तद्दर्शय रणे सर्वं जहि चैनं महारथम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: yacca te tapaso vīryaṁ yacca kṣātraṁ balaṁ tava ,taddarśaya raṇe sarvaṁ jahi cainaṁ mahāratham.
english: Show all the power of your austerity (tapas) and your martial strength in battle, and strike down this great warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 37. Show all the power of your austerity (tapas) and your martial strength in battle, and strike down this great warrior.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं केशवः परवीरहा ।जगाम शिबिरं सायं पूज्यमानोऽथ पाण्डवैः ॥३८॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā vacanaṁ keśavaḥ paravīrahā ,jagāma śibiraṁ sāyaṁ pūjyamāno'tha pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: Keśava, the slayer of enemy heroes, having spoken these words, went to the camp in the evening, being honored by the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 38. Keśava, the slayer of enemy heroes, having spoken these words, went to the camp in the evening, being honored by the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: केशवे तु तदा याते धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।विसृज्य सर्वान्भ्रातॄंश्च पाञ्चालानथ सोमकान् ।सुष्वाप रजनीं तां तु विशल्य इव कुञ्जरः ॥३९॥
transliteration: keśave tu tadā yāte dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,visṛjya sarvānbhrātṝṁśca pāñcālānatha somakān ,suṣvāpa rajanīṁ tāṁ tu viśalya iva kuñjaraḥ.
english: When Keśava had departed, King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), after dismissing all his brothers, the Pāñcālas, and the Somakas, indeed slept that night like an elephant relieved of its spear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 39. When Keśava had departed, King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), after dismissing all his brothers, the Pāñcālas, and the Somakas, indeed slept that night like an elephant relieved of its spear.

sanskrit: ते च सर्वे महेष्वासाः पाञ्चालाः पाण्डवास्तथा ।कर्णस्य निधने हृष्टाः सुषुपुस्तां निशां तदा ॥४०॥
transliteration: te ca sarve maheṣvāsāḥ pāñcālāḥ pāṇḍavāstathā ,karṇasya nidhane hṛṣṭāḥ suṣupustāṁ niśāṁ tadā.
english: All those great archers, the Pāñcālas and the Pāṇḍavas, were delighted at Karṇa's death, and they slept that night.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 40. All those great archers, the Pāñcālas and the Pāṇḍavas, were delighted at Karṇa's death, and they slept that night.

sanskrit: गतज्वरं महेष्वासं तीर्णपारं महारथम् ।बभूव पाण्डवेयानां सैन्यं प्रमुदितं निशि ।सूतपुत्रस्य निधने जयं लब्ध्वा च मारिष ॥४१॥
transliteration: gatajvaraṁ maheṣvāsaṁ tīrṇapāraṁ mahāratham ,babhūva pāṇḍaveyānāṁ sainyaṁ pramuditaṁ niśi ,sūtaputrasya nidhane jayaṁ labdhvā ca māriṣa.
english: O respected one, after achieving victory with the death of Karṇa, the son of Sūta (sūtaputra), the army of the Pāṇḍavas, now free from anxiety, equipped with mighty archers and great charioteers, and having overcome its adversities, became greatly delighted that night.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 6 Verse 41. O respected one, after achieving victory with the death of Karṇa, the son of Sūta (sūtaputra), the army of the Pāṇḍavas, now free from anxiety, equipped with mighty archers and great charioteers, and having overcome its adversities, became greatly delighted that night.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।व्यतीतायां रजन्यां तु राजा दुर्योधनस्तदा ।अब्रवीत्तावकान्सर्वान्संनह्यन्तां महारथाः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,vyatītāyāṁ rajanyāṁ tu rājā duryodhanastadā ,abravīttāvakānsarvānsaṁnahyantāṁ mahārathāḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: 'Then, when that night had passed, King Duryodhana spoke to all your men (the Kauravas), saying: "Let the great charioteers prepare for battle!"'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: 'Then, when that night had passed, King Duryodhana spoke to all your men (the Kauravas), saying: "Let the great charioteers prepare for battle!"'

sanskrit: राज्ञस्तु मतमाज्ञाय समनह्यत सा चमूः ।अयोजयन्रथांस्तूर्णं पर्यधावंस्तथापरे ॥२॥
transliteration: rājñastu matamājñāya samanahyata sā camūḥ ,ayojayanrathāṁstūrṇaṁ paryadhāvaṁstathāpare.
english: And that army, having understood the king's command, prepared itself. Some quickly harnessed the chariots, while others hurried about.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. And that army, having understood the king's command, prepared itself. Some quickly harnessed the chariots, while others hurried about.

sanskrit: अकल्प्यन्त च मातङ्गाः समनह्यन्त पत्तयः ।हयानास्तरणोपेतांश्चक्रुरन्ये सहस्रशः ॥३॥
transliteration: akalpyanta ca mātaṅgāḥ samanahyanta pattayaḥ ,hayānāstaraṇopetāṁścakruranye sahasraśaḥ.
english: And the elephants were arranged, and the foot soldiers equipped themselves. In thousands, others prepared the horses, furnishing them with trappings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. And the elephants were arranged, and the foot soldiers equipped themselves. In thousands, others prepared the horses, furnishing them with trappings.

sanskrit: वादित्राणां च निनदः प्रादुरासीद्विशां पते ।बोधनार्थं हि योधानां सैन्यानां चाप्युदीर्यताम् ॥४॥
transliteration: vāditrāṇāṁ ca ninadaḥ prādurāsīdviśāṁ pate ,bodhanārthaṁ hi yodhānāṁ sainyānāṁ cāpyudīryatām.
english: O lord of people, the clamor of musical instruments arose. Indeed, it was caused to be sounded for the purpose of awakening the warriors and the armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. O lord of people, the clamor of musical instruments arose. Indeed, it was caused to be sounded for the purpose of awakening the warriors and the armies.

sanskrit: ततो बलानि सर्वाणि सेनाशिष्टानि भारत ।संनद्धान्येव ददृशुर्मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tato balāni sarvāṇi senāśiṣṭāni bhārata ,saṁnaddhānyeva dadṛśurmṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: O descendant of Bharata, then all the remaining divisions of the army, fully prepared, looked upon death as their ultimate retreat (meaning they resolved to fight to the death).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. O descendant of Bharata, then all the remaining divisions of the army, fully prepared, looked upon death as their ultimate retreat (meaning they resolved to fight to the death).

sanskrit: शल्यं सेनापतिं कृत्वा मद्रराजं महारथाः ।प्रविभज्य बलं सर्वमनीकेषु व्यवस्थिताः ॥६॥
transliteration: śalyaṁ senāpatiṁ kṛtvā madrarājaṁ mahārathāḥ ,pravibhajya balaṁ sarvamanīkeṣu vyavasthitāḥ.
english: The great charioteers, having appointed King Śalya (madrarāja) as the army commander (senāpati), divided their entire force into various divisions and took their positions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. The great charioteers, having appointed King Śalya (madrarāja) as the army commander (senāpati), divided their entire force into various divisions and took their positions.

sanskrit: ततः सर्वे समागम्य पुत्रेण तव सैनिकाः ।कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च द्रौणिः शल्योऽथ सौबलः ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarve samāgamya putreṇa tava sainikāḥ ,kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca drauṇiḥ śalyo'tha saubalaḥ.
english: Then all the soldiers, along with your son, assembled. Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā), Śalya, and then Saubala (Śakuni) were among them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. Then all the soldiers, along with your son, assembled. Kṛpa, Kṛtavarmā, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā), Śalya, and then Saubala (Śakuni) were among them.

sanskrit: अन्ये च पार्थिवाः शेषाः समयं चक्रिरे तदा ।न न एकेन योद्धव्यं कथंचिदपि पाण्डवैः ॥८॥
transliteration: anye ca pārthivāḥ śeṣāḥ samayaṁ cakrire tadā ,na na ekena yoddhavyaṁ kathaṁcidapi pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: The other remaining kings then made an agreement: "The Pāṇḍavas must not be fought by any single person under any circumstances."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. The other remaining kings then made an agreement: "The Pāṇḍavas must not be fought by any single person under any circumstances."

sanskrit: यो ह्येकः पाण्डवैर्युध्येद्यो वा युध्यन्तमुत्सृजेत् ।स पञ्चभिर्भवेद्युक्तः पातकैः सोपपातकैः ।अन्योन्यं परिरक्षद्भिर्योद्धव्यं सहितैश्च नः ॥९॥
transliteration: yo hyekaḥ pāṇḍavairyudhyedyo vā yudhyantamutsṛjet ,sa pañcabhirbhavedyuktaḥ pātakaiḥ sopapātakaiḥ ,anyonyaṁ parirakṣadbhiryoddhavyaṁ sahitaiśca naḥ.
english: Indeed, whoever fights the Pāṇḍavas alone, or whoever abandons someone who is fighting, that person will incur five grave sins (pātaka) and minor sins (upapātaka). We must fight together, protecting one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. Indeed, whoever fights the Pāṇḍavas alone, or whoever abandons someone who is fighting, that person will incur five grave sins (pātaka) and minor sins (upapātaka). We must fight together, protecting one another.

sanskrit: एवं ते समयं कृत्वा सर्वे तत्र महारथाः ।मद्रराजं पुरस्कृत्य तूर्णमभ्यद्रवन्परान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁ te samayaṁ kṛtvā sarve tatra mahārathāḥ ,madrarājaṁ puraskṛtya tūrṇamabhyadravanparān.
english: Having thus made that agreement, all those great charioteers (maharatha) there, placing the king of Madra at the forefront, swiftly advanced towards their enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. Having thus made that agreement, all those great charioteers (maharatha) there, placing the king of Madra at the forefront, swiftly advanced towards their enemies.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवा राजन्व्यूह्य सैन्यं महारणे ।अभ्ययुः कौरवान्सर्वान्योत्स्यमानाः समन्ततः ॥११॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavā rājanvyūhya sainyaṁ mahāraṇe ,abhyayuḥ kauravānsarvānyotsyamānāḥ samantataḥ.
english: Likewise, O King, the Pāṇḍavas, having arrayed their army (sainya) for the great battle, advanced from all sides towards all the Kauravas, intending to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. Likewise, O King, the Pāṇḍavas, having arrayed their army (sainya) for the great battle, advanced from all sides towards all the Kauravas, intending to fight.

sanskrit: तद्बलं भरतश्रेष्ठ क्षुब्धार्णवसमस्वनम् ।समुद्धूतार्णवाकारमुद्धूतरथकुञ्जरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tadbalaṁ bharataśreṣṭha kṣubdhārṇavasamasvanam ,samuddhūtārṇavākāramuddhūtarathakuñjaram.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, that army had a roar like a turbulent ocean, its appearance was like a churning ocean, with chariots and elephants being tossed about within it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. O best among the Bhāratas, that army had a roar like a turbulent ocean, its appearance was like a churning ocean, with chariots and elephants being tossed about within it.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।द्रोणस्य भीष्मस्य च वै राधेयस्य च मे श्रुतम् ।पातनं शंस मे भूयः शल्यस्याथ सुतस्य मे ॥१३॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,droṇasya bhīṣmasya ca vai rādheyasya ca me śrutam ,pātanaṁ śaṁsa me bhūyaḥ śalyasyātha sutasya me.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke: I have already heard about the demise of Droṇa, Bhīṣma, and Radheya (Karṇa). Now, tell me further about the fall of Śalya and my son (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. Dhṛtarāṣṭra spoke: I have already heard about the demise of Droṇa, Bhīṣma, and Radheya (Karṇa). Now, tell me further about the fall of Śalya and my son (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: कथं रणे हतः शल्यो धर्मराजेन संजय ।भीमेन च महाबाहुः पुत्रो दुर्योधनो मम ॥१४॥
transliteration: kathaṁ raṇe hataḥ śalyo dharmarājena saṁjaya ,bhīmena ca mahābāhuḥ putro duryodhano mama.
english: O Saṃjaya, how was Śalya killed in battle by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira)? And how was my mighty-armed son Duryodhana slain by Bhīma?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. O Saṃjaya, how was Śalya killed in battle by Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira)? And how was my mighty-armed son Duryodhana slain by Bhīma?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।क्षयं मनुष्यदेहानां रथनागाश्वसंक्षयम् ।शृणु राजन्स्थिरो भूत्वा संग्रामं शंसतो मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,kṣayaṁ manuṣyadehānāṁ rathanāgāśvasaṁkṣayam ,śṛṇu rājansthiro bhūtvā saṁgrāmaṁ śaṁsato mama.
english: Saṃjaya spoke: O King, remaining steady, listen to me as I recount the war's devastation – the destruction of human bodies, and the ruin of chariots, elephants, and horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. Saṃjaya spoke: O King, remaining steady, listen to me as I recount the war's devastation – the destruction of human bodies, and the ruin of chariots, elephants, and horses.

sanskrit: आशा बलवती राजन्पुत्राणां तेऽभवत्तदा ।हते भीष्मे च द्रोणे च सूतपुत्रे च पातिते ।शल्यः पार्थान्रणे सर्वान्निहनिष्यति मारिष ॥१६॥
transliteration: āśā balavatī rājanputrāṇāṁ te'bhavattadā ,hate bhīṣme ca droṇe ca sūtaputre ca pātite ,śalyaḥ pārthānraṇe sarvānnihaniṣyati māriṣa.
english: O King, your sons then held a strong hope: that even after Bhishma was slain, and Drona and the son of a charioteer (Karṇa) were struck down, Shalya, O respectable one, would certainly kill all the Pāṇḍavas in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. O King, your sons then held a strong hope: that even after Bhishma was slain, and Drona and the son of a charioteer (Karṇa) were struck down, Shalya, O respectable one, would certainly kill all the Pāṇḍavas in battle.

sanskrit: तामाशां हृदये कृत्वा समाश्वास्य च भारत ।मद्रराजं च समरे समाश्रित्य महारथम् ।नाथवन्तमथात्मानममन्यत सुतस्तव ॥१७॥
transliteration: tāmāśāṁ hṛdaye kṛtvā samāśvāsya ca bhārata ,madrarājaṁ ca samare samāśritya mahāratham ,nāthavantamathātmānamamanyata sutastava.
english: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), your son, having held that hope in his heart and having reassured himself, and having taken refuge in the great charioteer, the king of Madra, in battle, then considered himself to have a protector.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), your son, having held that hope in his heart and having reassured himself, and having taken refuge in the great charioteer, the king of Madra, in battle, then considered himself to have a protector.

sanskrit: यदा कर्णे हते पार्थाः सिंहनादं प्रचक्रिरे ।तदा राजन्धार्तराष्ट्रानाविवेश महद्भयम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yadā karṇe hate pārthāḥ siṁhanādaṁ pracakrire ,tadā rājandhārtarāṣṭrānāviveśa mahadbhayam.
english: O King, when Karṇa was slain, the Pāṇḍavas let out a lion's roar. At that moment, a great fear assailed the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (dhārtarāṣṭra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. O King, when Karṇa was slain, the Pāṇḍavas let out a lion's roar. At that moment, a great fear assailed the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (dhārtarāṣṭra).

sanskrit: तान्समाश्वास्य तु तदा मद्रराजः प्रतापवान् ।व्यूह्य व्यूहं महाराज सर्वतोभद्रमृद्धिमत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tānsamāśvāsya tu tadā madrarājaḥ pratāpavān ,vyūhya vyūhaṁ mahārāja sarvatobhadramṛddhimat.
english: But then, O great King, the powerful king of Madra, having reassured them and having arranged an excellent formation (vyūha), auspicious on all sides...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. But then, O great King, the powerful king of Madra, having reassured them and having arranged an excellent formation (vyūha), auspicious on all sides...

sanskrit: प्रत्युद्यातो रणे पार्थान्मद्रराजः प्रतापवान् ।विधुन्वन्कार्मुकं चित्रं भारघ्नं वेगवत्तरम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: pratyudyāto raṇe pārthānmadrarājaḥ pratāpavān ,vidhunvankārmukaṁ citraṁ bhāraghnaṁ vegavattaram.
english: The mighty king of Madra, having been confronted in battle by Arjuna (Pārtha), was shaking his marvelous, foe-destroying, exceptionally swift bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 20. The mighty king of Madra, having been confronted in battle by Arjuna (Pārtha), was shaking his marvelous, foe-destroying, exceptionally swift bow.

sanskrit: रथप्रवरमास्थाय सैन्धवाश्वं महारथः ।तस्य सीता महाराज रथस्थाशोभयद्रथम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: rathapravaramāsthāya saindhavāśvaṁ mahārathaḥ ,tasya sītā mahārāja rathasthāśobhayadratham.
english: O great king, that great charioteer, having mounted his excellent chariot equipped with Sindhu horses, his chariot seat (sītā) positioned on the chariot, made it splendid.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 21. O great king, that great charioteer, having mounted his excellent chariot equipped with Sindhu horses, his chariot seat (sītā) positioned on the chariot, made it splendid.

sanskrit: स तेन संवृतो वीरो रथेनामित्रकर्शनः ।तस्थौ शूरो महाराज पुत्राणां ते भयप्रणुत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa tena saṁvṛto vīro rathenāmitrakarśanaḥ ,tasthau śūro mahārāja putrāṇāṁ te bhayapraṇut.
english: O great king, that heroic vanquisher of enemies, surrounded by that chariot, stood bravely, dispelling the fear of your sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 22. O great king, that heroic vanquisher of enemies, surrounded by that chariot, stood bravely, dispelling the fear of your sons.

sanskrit: प्रयाणे मद्रराजोऽभून्मुखं व्यूहस्य दंशितः ।मद्रकैः सहितो वीरैः कर्णपुत्रैश्च दुर्जयैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: prayāṇe madrarājo'bhūnmukhaṁ vyūhasya daṁśitaḥ ,madrakaiḥ sahito vīraiḥ karṇaputraiśca durjayaiḥ.
english: In the advance, the armored king of Madra became the forefront of the battle array (vyūha), accompanied by brave Madras and by the unconquerable sons of Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 23. In the advance, the armored king of Madra became the forefront of the battle array (vyūha), accompanied by brave Madras and by the unconquerable sons of Karṇa.

sanskrit: सव्येऽभूत्कृतवर्मा च त्रिगर्तैः परिवारितः ।गौतमो दक्षिणे पार्श्वे शकैश्च यवनैः सह ॥२४॥
transliteration: savye'bhūtkṛtavarmā ca trigartaiḥ parivāritaḥ ,gautamo dakṣiṇe pārśve śakaiśca yavanaiḥ saha.
english: Kṛtavarmā was on the left flank, surrounded by the Trigartas. Gautama (Kṛpa) was on the right flank, accompanied by the Śakas and Yavanas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 24. Kṛtavarmā was on the left flank, surrounded by the Trigartas. Gautama (Kṛpa) was on the right flank, accompanied by the Śakas and Yavanas.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा पृष्ठतोऽभूत्काम्बोजैः परिवारितः ।दुर्योधनोऽभवन्मध्ये रक्षितः कुरुपुंगवैः ॥२५॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā pṛṣṭhato'bhūtkāmbojaiḥ parivāritaḥ ,duryodhano'bhavanmadhye rakṣitaḥ kurupuṁgavaiḥ.
english: Aśvatthāmā was at the rear, surrounded by the Kambojas. Duryodhana himself was in the center, protected by the finest warriors among the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 25. Aśvatthāmā was at the rear, surrounded by the Kambojas. Duryodhana himself was in the center, protected by the finest warriors among the Kurus.

sanskrit: हयानीकेन महता सौबलश्चापि संवृतः ।प्रययौ सर्वसैन्येन कैतव्यश्च महारथः ॥२६॥
transliteration: hayānīkena mahatā saubalaścāpi saṁvṛtaḥ ,prayayau sarvasainyena kaitavyaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Saubala (Śakuni), a great chariot warrior and descendant of Ketu, was also surrounded by a large cavalry force. He advanced with his entire army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 26. Saubala (Śakuni), a great chariot warrior and descendant of Ketu, was also surrounded by a large cavalry force. He advanced with his entire army.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च महेष्वासा व्यूह्य सैन्यमरिंदमाः ।त्रिधा भूत्वा महाराज तव सैन्यमुपाद्रवन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca maheṣvāsā vyūhya sainyamariṁdamāḥ ,tridhā bhūtvā mahārāja tava sainyamupādravan.
english: O great king, the Pāṇḍavas, those great archers who subdue their enemies, having arrayed their own army, divided themselves into three parts and attacked your forces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 27. O great king, the Pāṇḍavas, those great archers who subdue their enemies, having arrayed their own army, divided themselves into three parts and attacked your forces.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च सात्यकिश्च महारथः ।शल्यस्य वाहिनीं तूर्णमभिदुद्रुवुराहवे ॥२८॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca sātyakiśca mahārathaḥ ,śalyasya vāhinīṁ tūrṇamabhidudruvurāhave.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and Sātyaki, a great warrior, swiftly charged towards Śalya's army in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 28. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and Sātyaki, a great warrior, swiftly charged towards Śalya's army in the battle.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा स्वेनानीकेन संवृतः ।शल्यमेवाभिदुद्राव जिघांसुर्भरतर्षभ ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā svenānīkena saṁvṛtaḥ ,śalyamevābhidudrāva jighāṁsurbharatarṣabha.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, surrounded by his own army, O best of the Bhāratas, rushed towards Śalya himself, eager to kill him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 29. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, surrounded by his own army, O best of the Bhāratas, rushed towards Śalya himself, eager to kill him.

sanskrit: हार्दिक्यं तु महेष्वासमर्जुनः शत्रुपूगहा ।संशप्तकगणांश्चैव वेगतोऽभिविदुद्रुवे ॥३०॥
transliteration: hārdikyaṁ tu maheṣvāsamarjunaḥ śatrupūgahā ,saṁśaptakagaṇāṁścaiva vegato'bhividudruve.
english: But Arjuna, who was the slayer of multitudes of foes, swiftly rushed towards Hārdikya, the great archer, as well as the battalions of the Saṃśaptakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 30. But Arjuna, who was the slayer of multitudes of foes, swiftly rushed towards Hārdikya, the great archer, as well as the battalions of the Saṃśaptakas.

sanskrit: गौतमं भीमसेनो वै सोमकाश्च महारथाः ।अभ्यद्रवन्त राजेन्द्र जिघांसन्तः परान्युधि ॥३१॥
transliteration: gautamaṁ bhīmaseno vai somakāśca mahārathāḥ ,abhyadravanta rājendra jighāṁsantaḥ parānyudhi.
english: Bhīmasena and the great Somaka warriors, O best of kings, rushed towards Gautama in battle, intent on killing their enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 31. Bhīmasena and the great Somaka warriors, O best of kings, rushed towards Gautama in battle, intent on killing their enemies.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रौ तु शकुनिमुलूकं च महारथौ ।ससैन्यौ सहसेनौ तावुपतस्थतुराहवे ॥३२॥
transliteration: mādrīputrau tu śakunimulūkaṁ ca mahārathau ,sasainyau sahasenau tāvupatasthaturāhave.
english: But the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva) confronted those two great chariot-warriors, Shakuni and Uluka, who were accompanied by their armies and forces, in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 32. But the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva) confronted those two great chariot-warriors, Shakuni and Uluka, who were accompanied by their armies and forces, in battle.

sanskrit: तथैवायुतशो योधास्तावकाः पाण्डवान्रणे ।अभ्यद्रवन्त संक्रुद्धा विविधायुधपाणयः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tathaivāyutaśo yodhāstāvakāḥ pāṇḍavānraṇe ,abhyadravanta saṁkruddhā vividhāyudhapāṇayaḥ.
english: Similarly, tens of thousands of your warriors, greatly enraged and holding various weapons in their hands, rushed towards the Pandavas in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 33. Similarly, tens of thousands of your warriors, greatly enraged and holding various weapons in their hands, rushed towards the Pandavas in battle.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।हते भीष्मे महेष्वासे द्रोणे कर्णे महारथे ।कुरुष्वल्पावशिष्टेषु पाण्डवेषु च संयुगे ॥३४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,hate bhīṣme maheṣvāse droṇe karṇe mahārathe ,kuruṣvalpāvaśiṣṭeṣu pāṇḍaveṣu ca saṁyuge.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "When Bhishma, the great archer, and Drona, and Karna, the great chariot-warrior, had been slain, and with only a few of the Kuru warriors remaining, and while the Pandavas were in battle..."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 34. Dhritarashtra said: "When Bhishma, the great archer, and Drona, and Karna, the great chariot-warrior, had been slain, and with only a few of the Kuru warriors remaining, and while the Pandavas were in battle..."

sanskrit: सुसंरब्धेषु पार्थेषु पराक्रान्तेषु संजय ।मामकानां परेषां च किं शिष्टमभवद्बलम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: susaṁrabdheṣu pārtheṣu parākrānteṣu saṁjaya ,māmakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca kiṁ śiṣṭamabhavadbalam.
english: ...with the sons of Pritha (Pandavas) greatly enraged and valorous, O Sanjaya, what strength (balam) was left to my people and to the enemies?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 35. ...with the sons of Pritha (Pandavas) greatly enraged and valorous, O Sanjaya, what strength (balam) was left to my people and to the enemies?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।यथा वयं परे राजन्युद्धाय समवस्थिताः ।यावच्चासीद्बलं शिष्टं संग्रामे तन्निबोध मे ॥३६॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,yathā vayaṁ pare rājanyuddhāya samavasthitāḥ ,yāvaccāsīdbalaṁ śiṣṭaṁ saṁgrāme tannibodha me.
english: Sañjaya said: "O King, just as we stood arrayed for battle against the other side, learn from me the extent of the forces that remained in the war."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 36. Sañjaya said: "O King, just as we stood arrayed for battle against the other side, learn from me the extent of the forces that remained in the war."

sanskrit: एकादश सहस्राणि रथानां भरतर्षभ ।दश दन्तिसहस्राणि सप्त चैव शतानि च ॥३७॥
transliteration: ekādaśa sahasrāṇi rathānāṁ bharatarṣabha ,daśa dantisahasrāṇi sapta caiva śatāni ca.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), there were eleven thousand chariots, and ten thousand seven hundred elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 37. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), there were eleven thousand chariots, and ten thousand seven hundred elephants.

sanskrit: पूर्णे शतसहस्रे द्वे हयानां भरतर्षभ ।नरकोट्यस्तथा तिस्रो बलमेतत्तवाभवत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: pūrṇe śatasahasre dve hayānāṁ bharatarṣabha ,narakoṭyastathā tisro balametattavābhavat.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), there were two full hundred thousand horses, and three crores (thirty million) of infantry. This was your army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 38. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), there were two full hundred thousand horses, and three crores (thirty million) of infantry. This was your army.

sanskrit: रथानां षट्सहस्राणि षट्सहस्राश्च कुञ्जराः ।दश चाश्वसहस्राणि पत्तिकोटी च भारत ॥३९॥
transliteration: rathānāṁ ṣaṭsahasrāṇi ṣaṭsahasrāśca kuñjarāḥ ,daśa cāśvasahasrāṇi pattikoṭī ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, there were six thousand chariots, and six thousand elephants, and ten thousand horses, and one crore (ten million) of infantry.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 39. O Bhārata, there were six thousand chariots, and six thousand elephants, and ten thousand horses, and one crore (ten million) of infantry.

sanskrit: एतद्बलं पाण्डवानामभवच्छेषमाहवे ।एत एव समाजग्मुर्युद्धाय भरतर्षभ ॥४०॥
transliteration: etadbalaṁ pāṇḍavānāmabhavaccheṣamāhave ,eta eva samājagmuryuddhāya bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, this was the remaining force of the Pāṇḍavas in battle. Indeed, these very warriors assembled for the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 40. O best of the Bhāratas, this was the remaining force of the Pāṇḍavas in battle. Indeed, these very warriors assembled for the fight.

sanskrit: एवं विभज्य राजेन्द्र मद्रराजमते स्थिताः ।पाण्डवान्प्रत्युदीयाम जयगृद्धाः प्रमन्यवः ॥४१॥
transliteration: evaṁ vibhajya rājendra madrarājamate sthitāḥ ,pāṇḍavānpratyudīyāma jayagṛddhāḥ pramanyavaḥ.
english: Therefore, O great king, having divided our forces and abiding by the counsel of the king of Madra, we should advance against the Pāṇḍavas, eager for victory and filled with intense anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 41. Therefore, O great king, having divided our forces and abiding by the counsel of the king of Madra, we should advance against the Pāṇḍavas, eager for victory and filled with intense anger.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवाः शूराः समरे जितकाशिनः ।उपयाता नरव्याघ्राः पाञ्चालाश्च यशस्विनः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavāḥ śūrāḥ samare jitakāśinaḥ ,upayātā naravyāghrāḥ pāñcālāśca yaśasvinaḥ.
english: In the same way, the valiant Pāṇḍavas, victorious in battle, and the glorious Pāñcālas, those tigers among men, also advanced.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 42. In the same way, the valiant Pāṇḍavas, victorious in battle, and the glorious Pāñcālas, those tigers among men, also advanced.

sanskrit: एवमेते बलौघेन परस्परवधैषिणः ।उपयाता नरव्याघ्राः पूर्वां संध्यां प्रति प्रभो ॥४३॥
transliteration: evamete balaughena parasparavadhaiṣiṇaḥ ,upayātā naravyāghrāḥ pūrvāṁ saṁdhyāṁ prati prabho.
english: In this manner, O lord, these tigers among men, eager to kill each other, advanced with their masses of troops towards the morning twilight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 43. In this manner, O lord, these tigers among men, eager to kill each other, advanced with their masses of troops towards the morning twilight.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं घोररूपं भयानकम् ।तावकानां परेषां च निघ्नतामितरेतरम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ ghorarūpaṁ bhayānakam ,tāvakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca nighnatāmitaretaram.
english: Then a battle of a terrible and frightening nature commenced, with your people and the enemies striking each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 7 Verse 44. Then a battle of a terrible and frightening nature commenced, with your people and the enemies striking each other.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः प्रववृते युद्धं कुरूणां भयवर्धनम् ।सृञ्जयैः सह राजेन्द्र घोरं देवासुरोपमम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ kurūṇāṁ bhayavardhanam ,sṛñjayaiḥ saha rājendra ghoraṁ devāsuropamam.
english: Saṃjaya said: O King, then a dreadful battle commenced, terrifying for the Kurus, along with the Sṛñjayas, a conflict resembling that between gods and asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: O King, then a dreadful battle commenced, terrifying for the Kurus, along with the Sṛñjayas, a conflict resembling that between gods and asuras.

sanskrit: नरा रथा गजौघाश्च सादिनश्च सहस्रशः ।वाजिनश्च पराक्रान्ताः समाजग्मुः परस्परम् ॥२॥
transliteration: narā rathā gajaughāśca sādinaśca sahasraśaḥ ,vājinaśca parākrāntāḥ samājagmuḥ parasparam.
english: Thousands of men, chariots, throngs of elephants, horsemen, and valorous horses encountered each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. Thousands of men, chariots, throngs of elephants, horsemen, and valorous horses encountered each other.

sanskrit: नागानां भीमरूपाणां द्रवतां निस्वनो महान् ।अश्रूयत यथा काले जलदानां नभस्तले ॥३॥
transliteration: nāgānāṁ bhīmarūpāṇāṁ dravatāṁ nisvano mahān ,aśrūyata yathā kāle jaladānāṁ nabhastale.
english: A great roar from the dreadful-looking, stampeding elephants was heard, just as the sound of clouds is heard in the sky during the (rainy) season.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. A great roar from the dreadful-looking, stampeding elephants was heard, just as the sound of clouds is heard in the sky during the (rainy) season.

sanskrit: नागैरभ्याहताः केचित्सरथा रथिनोऽपतन् ।व्यद्रवन्त रणे वीरा द्राव्यमाणा मदोत्कटैः ॥४॥
transliteration: nāgairabhyāhatāḥ kecitsarathā rathino'patan ,vyadravanta raṇe vīrā drāvyamāṇā madotkaṭaiḥ.
english: Some charioteers, along with their chariots, were struck down by the elephants and fell. Other heroes, being driven away by the infuriated (madotkaṭa) elephants, fled from the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. Some charioteers, along with their chariots, were struck down by the elephants and fell. Other heroes, being driven away by the infuriated (madotkaṭa) elephants, fled from the battle.

sanskrit: हयौघान्पादरक्षांश्च रथिनस्तत्र शिक्षिताः ।शरैः संप्रेषयामासुः परलोकाय भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: hayaughānpādarakṣāṁśca rathinastatra śikṣitāḥ ,śaraiḥ saṁpreṣayāmāsuḥ paralokāya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the skilled charioteers there dispatched many horses and foot soldiers to the other world (paraloka) with their arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. O Bhārata, the skilled charioteers there dispatched many horses and foot soldiers to the other world (paraloka) with their arrows.

sanskrit: सादिनः शिक्षिता राजन्परिवार्य महारथान् ।विचरन्तो रणेऽभ्यघ्नन्प्रासशक्त्यृष्टिभिस्तथा ॥६॥
transliteration: sādinaḥ śikṣitā rājanparivārya mahārathān ,vicaranto raṇe'bhyaghnanprāsaśaktyṛṣṭibhistathā.
english: O King, the trained horsemen, surrounding the great charioteers (mahāratha) and moving about in the battle, thus struck them with spears (prāsa), lances (śakti), and javelins (ṛṣṭi).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. O King, the trained horsemen, surrounding the great charioteers (mahāratha) and moving about in the battle, thus struck them with spears (prāsa), lances (śakti), and javelins (ṛṣṭi).

sanskrit: धन्विनः पुरुषाः केचित्संनिवार्य महारथान् ।एकं बहव आसाद्य प्रेषयेयुर्यमक्षयम् ॥७॥
transliteration: dhanvinaḥ puruṣāḥ kecitsaṁnivārya mahārathān ,ekaṁ bahava āsādya preṣayeyuryamakṣayam.
english: Some archers (dhanvin), having checked the great charioteers (mahāratha), would then, many of them together, approach one (of them) and dispatch him to the abode of Yama (yamakṣaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. Some archers (dhanvin), having checked the great charioteers (mahāratha), would then, many of them together, approach one (of them) and dispatch him to the abode of Yama (yamakṣaya).

sanskrit: नागं रथवरांश्चान्ये परिवार्य महारथाः ।सोत्तरायुधिनं जघ्नुर्द्रवमाणा महारवम् ॥८॥
transliteration: nāgaṁ rathavarāṁścānye parivārya mahārathāḥ ,sottarāyudhinaṁ jaghnurdravamāṇā mahāravam.
english: Other great charioteers, rushing forth, surrounded an elephant and excellent charioteers, and then struck down a warrior possessing superior weapons, creating a great roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. Other great charioteers, rushing forth, surrounded an elephant and excellent charioteers, and then struck down a warrior possessing superior weapons, creating a great roar.

sanskrit: तथा च रथिनं क्रुद्धं विकिरन्तं शरान्बहून् ।नागा जघ्नुर्महाराज परिवार्य समन्ततः ॥९॥
transliteration: tathā ca rathinaṁ kruddhaṁ vikirantaṁ śarānbahūn ,nāgā jaghnurmahārāja parivārya samantataḥ.
english: And similarly, O great king, the elephants, having surrounded him from all sides, struck down the enraged charioteer who was scattering many arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. And similarly, O great king, the elephants, having surrounded him from all sides, struck down the enraged charioteer who was scattering many arrows.

sanskrit: नागो नागमभिद्रुत्य रथी च रथिनं रणे ।शक्तितोमरनाराचैर्निजघ्नुस्तत्र तत्र ह ॥१०॥
transliteration: nāgo nāgamabhidrutya rathī ca rathinaṁ raṇe ,śaktitomaranārācairnijaghnustatra tatra ha.
english: In that battle, an elephant, having rushed towards another elephant, and a charioteer, towards another charioteer, indeed struck them down in various places with spears (śakti), javelins, and iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. In that battle, an elephant, having rushed towards another elephant, and a charioteer, towards another charioteer, indeed struck them down in various places with spears (śakti), javelins, and iron arrows.

sanskrit: पादातानवमृद्नन्तो रथवारणवाजिनः ।रणमध्ये व्यदृश्यन्त कुर्वन्तो महदाकुलम् ॥११॥
transliteration: pādātānavamṛdnanto rathavāraṇavājinaḥ ,raṇamadhye vyadṛśyanta kurvanto mahadākulam.
english: Chariots, elephants, and horses, trampling the foot soldiers, were seen in the midst of battle, causing great confusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. Chariots, elephants, and horses, trampling the foot soldiers, were seen in the midst of battle, causing great confusion.

sanskrit: हयाश्च पर्यधावन्त चामरैरुपशोभिताः ।हंसा हिमवतः प्रस्थे पिबन्त इव मेदिनीम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: hayāśca paryadhāvanta cāmarairupaśobhitāḥ ,haṁsā himavataḥ prasthe pibanta iva medinīm.
english: The horses, adorned with yak-tail whisks, galloped, as if swans were drinking the water from the earth on the slope of the Himalayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. The horses, adorned with yak-tail whisks, galloped, as if swans were drinking the water from the earth on the slope of the Himalayas.

sanskrit: तेषां तु वाजिनां भूमिः खुरैश्चित्रा विशां पते ।अशोभत यथा नारी करजक्षतविक्षता ॥१३॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu vājināṁ bhūmiḥ khuraiścitrā viśāṁ pate ,aśobhata yathā nārī karajakṣatavikṣatā.
english: But, O lord of the people, the earth, made variegated by the hooves of those horses, looked beautiful like a woman whose body is marked with fingernail scratches.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. But, O lord of the people, the earth, made variegated by the hooves of those horses, looked beautiful like a woman whose body is marked with fingernail scratches.

sanskrit: वाजिनां खुरशब्देन रथनेमिस्वनेन च ।पत्तीनां चापि शब्देन नागानां बृंहितेन च ॥१४॥
transliteration: vājināṁ khuraśabdena rathanemisvanena ca ,pattīnāṁ cāpi śabdena nāgānāṁ bṛṁhitena ca.
english: By the sound of the horses' hooves, by the rattling of chariot wheels, and also by the noise of the infantry, and by the trumpeting of the elephants,
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. By the sound of the horses' hooves, by the rattling of chariot wheels, and also by the noise of the infantry, and by the trumpeting of the elephants,

sanskrit: वादित्राणां च घोषेण शङ्खानां निस्वनेन च ।अभवन्नादिता भूमिर्निर्घातैरिव भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: vāditrāṇāṁ ca ghoṣeṇa śaṅkhānāṁ nisvanena ca ,abhavannāditā bhūmirnirghātairiva bhārata.
english: And by the sound of the musical instruments, and by the blast of conch shells, the earth became filled with noise, O Bhārata, as if by thunderclaps.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. And by the sound of the musical instruments, and by the blast of conch shells, the earth became filled with noise, O Bhārata, as if by thunderclaps.

sanskrit: धनुषां कूजमानानां निस्त्रिंशानां च दीप्यताम् ।कवचानां प्रभाभिश्च न प्राज्ञायत किंचन ॥१६॥
transliteration: dhanuṣāṁ kūjamānānāṁ nistriṁśānāṁ ca dīpyatām ,kavacānāṁ prabhābhiśca na prājñāyata kiṁcana.
english: Due to the twanging of the bows, the shining of the swords, and the gleams from the armors, nothing could be clearly discerned.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. Due to the twanging of the bows, the shining of the swords, and the gleams from the armors, nothing could be clearly discerned.

sanskrit: बहवो बाहवश्छिन्ना नागराजकरोपमाः ।उद्वेष्टन्ते विवेष्टन्ते वेगं कुर्वन्ति दारुणम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: bahavo bāhavaśchinnā nāgarājakaropamāḥ ,udveṣṭante viveṣṭante vegaṁ kurvanti dāruṇam.
english: Many severed arms, resembling the trunks of great elephants, twist and writhe, creating a terrible momentum.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. Many severed arms, resembling the trunks of great elephants, twist and writhe, creating a terrible momentum.

sanskrit: शिरसां च महाराज पततां वसुधातले ।च्युतानामिव तालेभ्यः फलानां श्रूयते स्वनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: śirasāṁ ca mahārāja patatāṁ vasudhātale ,cyutānāmiva tālebhyaḥ phalānāṁ śrūyate svanaḥ.
english: And, O great king, the sound of heads falling upon the earth is heard, just like the thud of fruits that have dropped from palm trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. And, O great king, the sound of heads falling upon the earth is heard, just like the thud of fruits that have dropped from palm trees.

sanskrit: शिरोभिः पतितैर्भाति रुधिरार्द्रैर्वसुंधरा ।तपनीयनिभैः काले नलिनैरिव भारत ॥१९॥
transliteration: śirobhiḥ patitairbhāti rudhirārdrairvasuṁdharā ,tapanīyanibhaiḥ kāle nalinairiva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the earth appears radiant with fallen heads, wet with blood, resembling golden lotuses at the proper season.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. O Bhārata, the earth appears radiant with fallen heads, wet with blood, resembling golden lotuses at the proper season.

sanskrit: उद्वृत्तनयनैस्तैस्तु गतसत्त्वैः सुविक्षतैः ।व्यभ्राजत महाराज पुण्डरीकैरिवावृता ॥२०॥
transliteration: udvṛttanayanaistaistu gatasattvaiḥ suvikṣataiḥ ,vyabhrājata mahārāja puṇḍarīkairivāvṛtā.
english: O great king, the battlefield appeared as if covered by white lotuses, though it was actually strewn with those (corpses) whose eyes were rolled up, who had lost their vitality, and were severely wounded.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. O great king, the battlefield appeared as if covered by white lotuses, though it was actually strewn with those (corpses) whose eyes were rolled up, who had lost their vitality, and were severely wounded.

sanskrit: बाहुभिश्चन्दनादिग्धैः सकेयूरैर्महाधनैः ।पतितैर्भाति राजेन्द्र मही शक्रध्वजैरिव ॥२१॥
transliteration: bāhubhiścandanādigdhaiḥ sakeyūrairmahādhanaiḥ ,patitairbhāti rājendra mahī śakradhvajairiva.
english: O king of kings, the earth shines as if covered by the fallen banners of Indra, (but it is actually covered) by fallen arms, smeared with sandalwood paste, adorned with expensive armlets.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. O king of kings, the earth shines as if covered by the fallen banners of Indra, (but it is actually covered) by fallen arms, smeared with sandalwood paste, adorned with expensive armlets.

sanskrit: ऊरुभिश्च नरेन्द्राणां विनिकृत्तैर्महाहवे ।हस्तिहस्तोपमैरन्यैः संवृतं तद्रणाङ्गणम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: ūrubhiśca narendrāṇāṁ vinikṛttairmahāhave ,hastihastopamairanyaiḥ saṁvṛtaṁ tadraṇāṅgaṇam.
english: And in that great battle, that battlefield was covered by the severed thighs of kings, and by other (limbs) resembling elephant trunks.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. And in that great battle, that battlefield was covered by the severed thighs of kings, and by other (limbs) resembling elephant trunks.

sanskrit: कबन्धशतसंकीर्णं छत्रचामरशोभितम् ।सेनावनं तच्छुशुभे वनं पुष्पाचितं यथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: kabandhaśatasaṁkīrṇaṁ chatracāmaraśobhitam ,senāvanaṁ tacchuśubhe vanaṁ puṣpācitaṁ yathā.
english: That 'army-forest' (the battlefield), filled with hundreds of headless trunks and adorned with (fallen) parasols and whisks, shone just like a forest covered with flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. That 'army-forest' (the battlefield), filled with hundreds of headless trunks and adorned with (fallen) parasols and whisks, shone just like a forest covered with flowers.

sanskrit: तत्र योधा महाराज विचरन्तो ह्यभीतवत् ।दृश्यन्ते रुधिराक्ताङ्गाः पुष्पिता इव किंशुकाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tatra yodhā mahārāja vicaranto hyabhītavat ,dṛśyante rudhirāktāṅgāḥ puṣpitā iva kiṁśukāḥ.
english: O great king, the warriors, moving fearlessly, are seen with their limbs smeared with blood, resembling blooming palāśa (kiṃśuka) trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. O great king, the warriors, moving fearlessly, are seen with their limbs smeared with blood, resembling blooming palāśa (kiṃśuka) trees.

sanskrit: मातङ्गाश्चाप्यदृश्यन्त शरतोमरपीडिताः ।पतन्तस्तत्र तत्रैव छिन्नाभ्रसदृशा रणे ॥२५॥
transliteration: mātaṅgāścāpyadṛśyanta śaratomarapīḍitāḥ ,patantastatra tatraiva chinnābhrasadṛśā raṇe.
english: And indeed, elephants, afflicted by arrows and spears, were seen falling here and there in the battle, resembling scattered clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. And indeed, elephants, afflicted by arrows and spears, were seen falling here and there in the battle, resembling scattered clouds.

sanskrit: गजानीकं महाराज वध्यमानं महात्मभिः ।व्यदीर्यत दिशः सर्वा वातनुन्ना घना इव ॥२६॥
transliteration: gajānīkaṁ mahārāja vadhyamānaṁ mahātmabhiḥ ,vyadīryata diśaḥ sarvā vātanunnā ghanā iva.
english: O great king, the elephant division, being slaughtered by the great souls (mahā-ātman), was scattered in all directions, just like clouds are driven by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. O great king, the elephant division, being slaughtered by the great souls (mahā-ātman), was scattered in all directions, just like clouds are driven by the wind.

sanskrit: ते गजा घनसंकाशाः पेतुरुर्व्यां समन्ततः ।वज्ररुग्णा इव बभुः पर्वता युगसंक्षये ॥२७॥
transliteration: te gajā ghanasaṁkāśāḥ petururvyāṁ samantataḥ ,vajrarugṇā iva babhuḥ parvatā yugasaṁkṣaye.
english: Those elephants, which resembled clouds, fell everywhere on the earth. They looked like mountains shattered by a thunderbolt at the time of the cosmic dissolution (yuga-saṃkṣaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. Those elephants, which resembled clouds, fell everywhere on the earth. They looked like mountains shattered by a thunderbolt at the time of the cosmic dissolution (yuga-saṃkṣaya).

sanskrit: हयानां सादिभिः सार्धं पतितानां महीतले ।राशयः संप्रदृश्यन्ते गिरिमात्रास्ततस्ततः ॥२८॥
transliteration: hayānāṁ sādibhiḥ sārdhaṁ patitānāṁ mahītale ,rāśayaḥ saṁpradṛśyante girimātrāstatastataḥ.
english: Piles of horses, along with their fallen riders, were visible on the battlefield, appearing like mountains scattered here and there on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. Piles of horses, along with their fallen riders, were visible on the battlefield, appearing like mountains scattered here and there on the ground.

sanskrit: संजज्ञे रणभूमौ तु परलोकवहा नदी ।शोणितोदा रथावर्ता ध्वजवृक्षास्थिशर्करा ॥२९॥
transliteration: saṁjajñe raṇabhūmau tu paralokavahā nadī ,śoṇitodā rathāvartā dhvajavṛkṣāsthiśarkarā.
english: A river, indeed, formed on the battlefield, carrying souls to the other world, with blood as its water, chariots as its whirlpools, and standards as its trees and bones as its pebbles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. A river, indeed, formed on the battlefield, carrying souls to the other world, with blood as its water, chariots as its whirlpools, and standards as its trees and bones as its pebbles.

sanskrit: भुजनक्रा धनुःस्रोता हस्तिशैला हयोपला ।मेदोमज्जाकर्दमिनी छत्रहंसा गदोडुपा ॥३०॥
transliteration: bhujanakrā dhanuḥsrotā hastiśailā hayopalā ,medomajjākardaminī chatrahaṁsā gadoḍupā.
english: (The river) had arms for alligators, bows for its currents, elephants for its mountains, horses for its rocks, fat and marrow for its mud, parasols for its swans, and maces for its rafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. (The river) had arms for alligators, bows for its currents, elephants for its mountains, horses for its rocks, fat and marrow for its mud, parasols for its swans, and maces for its rafts.

sanskrit: कवचोष्णीषसंछन्ना पताकारुचिरद्रुमा ।चक्रचक्रावलीजुष्टा त्रिवेणूदण्डकावृता ॥३१॥
transliteration: kavacoṣṇīṣasaṁchannā patākāruciradrumā ,cakracakrāvalījuṣṭā triveṇūdaṇḍakāvṛtā.
english: (The river was) covered with armor and turbans, adorned with beautiful banner-trees, frequented by rows of (chariot) wheels, and surrounded by triple-braided hair and staffs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. (The river was) covered with armor and turbans, adorned with beautiful banner-trees, frequented by rows of (chariot) wheels, and surrounded by triple-braided hair and staffs.

sanskrit: शूराणां हर्षजननी भीरूणां भयवर्धिनी ।प्रावर्तत नदी रौद्रा कुरुसृञ्जयसंकुला ॥३२॥
transliteration: śūrāṇāṁ harṣajananī bhīrūṇāṁ bhayavardhinī ,prāvartata nadī raudrā kurusṛñjayasaṁkulā.
english: A terrifying river (of battle) unfolded, bringing joy to heroes and increasing fear in cowards, crowded with Kurus and Sṛñjayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. A terrifying river (of battle) unfolded, bringing joy to heroes and increasing fear in cowards, crowded with Kurus and Sṛñjayas.

sanskrit: तां नदीं पितृलोकाय वहन्तीमतिभैरवाम् ।तेरुर्वाहननौभिस्ते शूराः परिघबाहवः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tāṁ nadīṁ pitṛlokāya vahantīmatibhairavām ,terurvāhananaubhiste śūrāḥ parighabāhavaḥ.
english: Those heroes, whose arms were like iron bars, crossed that exceedingly terrifying river [of battle] – which swept many towards the ancestral realm – by means of their mounts and conveyances.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. Those heroes, whose arms were like iron bars, crossed that exceedingly terrifying river [of battle] – which swept many towards the ancestral realm – by means of their mounts and conveyances.

sanskrit: वर्तमाने तथा युद्धे निर्मर्यादे विशां पते ।चतुरङ्गक्षये घोरे पूर्वं देवासुरोपमे ॥३४॥
transliteration: vartamāne tathā yuddhe nirmaryāde viśāṁ pate ,caturaṅgakṣaye ghore pūrvaṁ devāsuropame.
english: O Lord of the people, while that dreadful battle was ongoing - a boundless conflict leading to the destruction of the four-limbed army, and resembling the ancient war between the gods and asuras...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 34. O Lord of the people, while that dreadful battle was ongoing - a boundless conflict leading to the destruction of the four-limbed army, and resembling the ancient war between the gods and asuras...

sanskrit: अक्रोशन्बान्धवानन्ये तत्र तत्र परंतप ।क्रोशद्भिर्बान्धवैश्चान्ये भयार्ता न निवर्तिरे ॥३५॥
transliteration: akrośanbāndhavānanye tatra tatra paraṁtapa ,krośadbhirbāndhavaiścānye bhayārtā na nivartire.
english: O tormentor of foes, some cried out for their kinsmen here and there; and others, terrified by the wailing of their relatives, did not retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 35. O tormentor of foes, some cried out for their kinsmen here and there; and others, terrified by the wailing of their relatives, did not retreat.

sanskrit: निर्मर्यादे तथा युद्धे वर्तमाने भयानके ।अर्जुनो भीमसेनश्च मोहयां चक्रतुः परान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: nirmaryāde tathā yuddhe vartamāne bhayānake ,arjuno bhīmasenaśca mohayāṁ cakratuḥ parān.
english: And in that terrible battle, which was raging without any moral limits, Arjuna and Bhimasena bewildered their enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 36. And in that terrible battle, which was raging without any moral limits, Arjuna and Bhimasena bewildered their enemies.

sanskrit: सा वध्यमाना महती सेना तव जनाधिप ।अमुह्यत्तत्र तत्रैव योषिन्मदवशादिव ॥३७॥
transliteration: sā vadhyamānā mahatī senā tava janādhipa ,amuhyattatra tatraiva yoṣinmadavaśādiva.
english: That great army of yours, O lord of men, while being struck down, became utterly bewildered right there, as if a woman overcome by intoxication.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 37. That great army of yours, O lord of men, while being struck down, became utterly bewildered right there, as if a woman overcome by intoxication.

sanskrit: मोहयित्वा च तां सेनां भीमसेनधनंजयौ ।दध्मतुर्वारिजौ तत्र सिंहनादं च नेदतुः ॥३८॥
transliteration: mohayitvā ca tāṁ senāṁ bhīmasenadhanaṁjayau ,dadhmaturvārijau tatra siṁhanādaṁ ca nedatuḥ.
english: Having bewildered that army, Bhimasena and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) then blew their conch shells and roared a lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 38. Having bewildered that army, Bhimasena and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) then blew their conch shells and roared a lion's roar.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वैव तु महाशब्दं धृष्टद्युम्नशिखण्डिनौ ।धर्मराजं पुरस्कृत्य मद्रराजमभिद्रुतौ ॥३९॥
transliteration: śrutvaiva tu mahāśabdaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaśikhaṇḍinau ,dharmarājaṁ puraskṛtya madrarājamabhidrutau.
english: Upon hearing that great sound, Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍī, placing the King of dharma (dharmarāja) at the forefront, rushed towards the King of Madra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 39. Upon hearing that great sound, Dhṛṣṭadyumna and Śikhaṇḍī, placing the King of dharma (dharmarāja) at the forefront, rushed towards the King of Madra.

sanskrit: तत्राश्चर्यमपश्याम घोररूपं विशां पते ।शल्येन संगताः शूरा यदयुध्यन्त भागशः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tatrāścaryamapaśyāma ghorarūpaṁ viśāṁ pate ,śalyena saṁgatāḥ śūrā yadayudhyanta bhāgaśaḥ.
english: O ruler of the people, there we witnessed a terrible spectacle: how the brave warriors, joined by Śalya, fought in separate divisions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 40. O ruler of the people, there we witnessed a terrible spectacle: how the brave warriors, joined by Śalya, fought in separate divisions.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रौ सरभसौ कृतास्त्रौ युद्धदुर्मदौ ।अभ्ययातां त्वरायुक्तौ जिगीषन्तौ बलं तव ॥४१॥
transliteration: mādrīputrau sarabhasau kṛtāstrau yuddhadurmadau ,abhyayātāṁ tvarāyuktau jigīṣantau balaṁ tava.
english: The two sons of Mādrī, swift and impetuous, skilled in warfare, and mad with battle-fury, advanced quickly, desiring to conquer your forces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 41. The two sons of Mādrī, swift and impetuous, skilled in warfare, and mad with battle-fury, advanced quickly, desiring to conquer your forces.

sanskrit: ततो न्यवर्तत बलं तावकं भरतर्षभ ।शरैः प्रणुन्नं बहुधा पाण्डवैर्जितकाशिभिः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tato nyavartata balaṁ tāvakaṁ bharatarṣabha ,śaraiḥ praṇunnaṁ bahudhā pāṇḍavairjitakāśibhiḥ.
english: Then, O best of the Bhāratas, your army retreated, having been greatly repelled by the arrows of the Pāṇḍavas, who shone with victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 42. Then, O best of the Bhāratas, your army retreated, having been greatly repelled by the arrows of the Pāṇḍavas, who shone with victory.

sanskrit: वध्यमाना चमूः सा तु पुत्राणां प्रेक्षतां तव ।भेजे दिशो महाराज प्रणुन्ना दृढधन्विभिः ।हाहाकारो महाञ्जज्ञे योधानां तव भारत ॥४३॥
transliteration: vadhyamānā camūḥ sā tu putrāṇāṁ prekṣatāṁ tava ,bheje diśo mahārāja praṇunnā dṛḍhadhanvibhiḥ ,hāhākāro mahāñjajñe yodhānāṁ tava bhārata.
english: But that army, while your sons watched it being slaughtered, O great king, scattered in all directions, driven back by the strong archers. A great lamentation arose from your warriors, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 43. But that army, while your sons watched it being slaughtered, O great king, scattered in all directions, driven back by the strong archers. A great lamentation arose from your warriors, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति वागासीद्द्रावितानां महात्मनाम् ।क्षत्रियाणां तदान्योन्यं संयुगे जयमिच्छताम् ।आद्रवन्नेव भग्नास्ते पाण्डवैस्तव सैनिकाः ॥४४॥
transliteration: tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti vāgāsīddrāvitānāṁ mahātmanām ,kṣatriyāṇāṁ tadānyonyaṁ saṁyuge jayamicchatām ,ādravanneva bhagnāste pāṇḍavaistava sainikāḥ.
english: "Stop! Stop!" such was the cry from the great warriors (mahātmanām) of the Kṣatriyas who were fleeing and yet, at that very moment, were mutually wishing for victory in battle. Even as your soldiers were running away, they were shattered by the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 44. "Stop! Stop!" such was the cry from the great warriors (mahātmanām) of the Kṣatriyas who were fleeing and yet, at that very moment, were mutually wishing for victory in battle. Even as your soldiers were running away, they were shattered by the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: त्यक्त्वा युद्धे प्रियान्पुत्रान्भ्रातॄनथ पितामहान् ।मातुलान्भागिनेयांश्च तथा संबन्धिबान्धवान् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tyaktvā yuddhe priyānputrānbhrātṝnatha pitāmahān ,mātulānbhāgineyāṁśca tathā saṁbandhibāndhavān.
english: Having abandoned in battle their dear sons, brothers, grandfathers, maternal uncles, nephews, and also their relatives and kinsmen.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 45. Having abandoned in battle their dear sons, brothers, grandfathers, maternal uncles, nephews, and also their relatives and kinsmen.

sanskrit: हयान्द्विपांस्त्वरयन्तो योधा जग्मुः समन्ततः ।आत्मत्राणकृतोत्साहास्तावका भरतर्षभ ॥४६॥
transliteration: hayāndvipāṁstvarayanto yodhā jagmuḥ samantataḥ ,ātmatrāṇakṛtotsāhāstāvakā bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), your soldiers (tāvakāḥ), hurrying their horses and elephants, went in all directions, filled with zeal for self-preservation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 8 Verse 46. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), your soldiers (tāvakāḥ), hurrying their horses and elephants, went in all directions, filled with zeal for self-preservation.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तत्प्रभग्नं बलं दृष्ट्वा मद्रराजः प्रतापवान् ।उवाच सारथिं तूर्णं चोदयाश्वान्महाजवान् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tatprabhagnaṁ balaṁ dṛṣṭvā madrarājaḥ pratāpavān ,uvāca sārathiṁ tūrṇaṁ codayāśvānmahājavān.
english: Saṃjaya said: Seeing that shattered army, the powerful king of Madra (Madrarāja) quickly said to his charioteer, "Drive the swift horses!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: Seeing that shattered army, the powerful king of Madra (Madrarāja) quickly said to his charioteer, "Drive the swift horses!"

sanskrit: एष तिष्ठति वै राजा पाण्डुपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।छत्रेण ध्रियमाणेन पाण्डुरेण विराजता ॥२॥
transliteration: eṣa tiṣṭhati vai rājā pāṇḍuputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,chatreṇa dhriyamāṇena pāṇḍureṇa virājatā.
english: Indeed, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu, stands here, resplendent with a white umbrella held over him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. Indeed, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu, stands here, resplendent with a white umbrella held over him.

sanskrit: अत्र मां प्रापय क्षिप्रं पश्य मे सारथे बलम् ।न समर्था हि मे पार्थाः स्थातुमद्य पुरो युधि ॥३॥
transliteration: atra māṁ prāpaya kṣipraṁ paśya me sārathe balam ,na samarthā hi me pārthāḥ sthātumadya puro yudhi.
english: O charioteer, bring me here swiftly! Behold my might. Indeed, my Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā) are not capable of standing steadfast in battle today.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. O charioteer, bring me here swiftly! Behold my might. Indeed, my Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā) are not capable of standing steadfast in battle today.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्ततः प्रायान्मद्रराजस्य सारथिः ।यत्र राजा सत्यसंधो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥४॥
transliteration: evamuktastataḥ prāyānmadrarājasya sārathiḥ ,yatra rājā satyasaṁdho dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Thus addressed, the charioteer of the Madra king then proceeded to where King Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of (dharma) who is true to his vow, stood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. Thus addressed, the charioteer of the Madra king then proceeded to where King Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of (dharma) who is true to his vow, stood.

sanskrit: आपतन्तं च सहसा पाण्डवानां महद्बलम् ।दधारैको रणे शल्यो वेलेवोद्धृतमर्णवम् ॥५॥
transliteration: āpatantaṁ ca sahasā pāṇḍavānāṁ mahadbalam ,dadhāraiko raṇe śalyo velevoddhṛtamarṇavam.
english: And suddenly, Śalya alone in battle held back the great, approaching force of the Pāṇḍavas, just as a shore holds back an agitated ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. And suddenly, Śalya alone in battle held back the great, approaching force of the Pāṇḍavas, just as a shore holds back an agitated ocean.

sanskrit: पाण्डवानां बलौघस्तु शल्यमासाद्य मारिष ।व्यतिष्ठत तदा युद्धे सिन्धोर्वेग इवाचलम् ॥६॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavānāṁ balaughastu śalyamāsādya māriṣa ,vyatiṣṭhata tadā yuddhe sindhorvega ivācalam.
english: O respectable one, the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas, having confronted Śalya, then stood firm in battle, like a river's powerful current meeting an immovable mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. O respectable one, the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas, having confronted Śalya, then stood firm in battle, like a river's powerful current meeting an immovable mountain.

sanskrit: मद्रराजं तु समरे दृष्ट्वा युद्धाय विष्ठितम् ।कुरवः संन्यवर्तन्त मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: madrarājaṁ tu samare dṛṣṭvā yuddhāya viṣṭhitam ,kuravaḥ saṁnyavartanta mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: But having seen the King of Madra (madrarāja) arrayed for battle, the Kurus retreated from the fight, considering their retreat itself to be akin to death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. But having seen the King of Madra (madrarāja) arrayed for battle, the Kurus retreated from the fight, considering their retreat itself to be akin to death.

sanskrit: तेषु राजन्निवृत्तेषु व्यूढानीकेषु भागशः ।प्रावर्तत महारौद्रः संग्रामः शोणितोदकः ।समार्छच्चित्रसेनेन नकुलो युद्धदुर्मदः ॥८॥
transliteration: teṣu rājannivṛtteṣu vyūḍhānīkeṣu bhāgaśaḥ ,prāvartata mahāraudraḥ saṁgrāmaḥ śoṇitodakaḥ ,samārchaccitrasenena nakulo yuddhadurmadaḥ.
english: O King, as those (Kurus) retreated, with their armies arrayed in divisions, a greatly terrifying battle commenced, its waters flowing with blood. Nakula, fiercely proud in combat, then encountered Citrasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. O King, as those (Kurus) retreated, with their armies arrayed in divisions, a greatly terrifying battle commenced, its waters flowing with blood. Nakula, fiercely proud in combat, then encountered Citrasena.

sanskrit: तौ परस्परमासाद्य चित्रकार्मुकधारिणौ ।मेघाविव यथोद्वृत्तौ दक्षिणोत्तरवर्षिणौ ॥९॥
transliteration: tau parasparamāsādya citrakārmukadhāriṇau ,meghāviva yathodvṛttau dakṣiṇottaravarṣiṇau.
english: Having engaged each other, those two, wielders of splendid bows, were like two fiercely risen clouds raining down from the south and the north.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. Having engaged each other, those two, wielders of splendid bows, were like two fiercely risen clouds raining down from the south and the north.

sanskrit: शरतोयैः सिषिचतुस्तौ परस्परमाहवे ।नान्तरं तत्र पश्यामि पाण्डवस्येतरस्य वा ॥१०॥
transliteration: śaratoyaiḥ siṣicatustau parasparamāhave ,nāntaraṁ tatra paśyāmi pāṇḍavasyetarasya vā.
english: In that battle, the two warriors drenched each other with showers of arrows. I see no distinction there between the Pāṇḍava and the other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. In that battle, the two warriors drenched each other with showers of arrows. I see no distinction there between the Pāṇḍava and the other.

sanskrit: उभौ कृतास्त्रौ बलिनौ रथचर्याविशारदौ ।परस्परवधे यत्तौ छिद्रान्वेषणतत्परौ ॥११॥
transliteration: ubhau kṛtāstrau balinau rathacaryāviśāradau ,parasparavadhe yattau chidrānveṣaṇatatparau.
english: Both warriors were expert in the use of weapons, powerful, and highly skilled in maneuvering chariots. They were both intent on slaying each other, constantly eager to discover a weakness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. Both warriors were expert in the use of weapons, powerful, and highly skilled in maneuvering chariots. They were both intent on slaying each other, constantly eager to discover a weakness.

sanskrit: चित्रसेनस्तु भल्लेन पीतेन निशितेन च ।नकुलस्य महाराज मुष्टिदेशेऽच्छिनद्धनुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: citrasenastu bhallena pītena niśitena ca ,nakulasya mahārāja muṣṭideśe'cchinaddhanuḥ.
english: But Citrasena, O great king, with a sharp, whetted missile (bhallena), cut Nakula's bow at the hand-grip.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. But Citrasena, O great king, with a sharp, whetted missile (bhallena), cut Nakula's bow at the hand-grip.

sanskrit: अथैनं छिन्नधन्वानं रुक्मपुङ्खैः शिलाशितैः ।त्रिभिः शरैरसंभ्रान्तो ललाटे वै समर्पयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: athainaṁ chinnadhanvānaṁ rukmapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaiḥ ,tribhiḥ śarairasaṁbhrānto lalāṭe vai samarpayat.
english: Then, unperturbed, he struck him, whose bow was broken, right in the forehead with three arrows that had golden shafts and were sharpened on stone.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. Then, unperturbed, he struck him, whose bow was broken, right in the forehead with three arrows that had golden shafts and were sharpened on stone.

sanskrit: हयांश्चास्य शरैस्तीक्ष्णैः प्रेषयामास मृत्यवे ।तथा ध्वजं सारथिं च त्रिभिस्त्रिभिरपातयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: hayāṁścāsya śaraistīkṣṇaiḥ preṣayāmāsa mṛtyave ,tathā dhvajaṁ sārathiṁ ca tribhistribhirapātayat.
english: With his sharp arrows, he sent his horses to their death. Similarly, he also struck down the banner and the charioteer, each with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. With his sharp arrows, he sent his horses to their death. Similarly, he also struck down the banner and the charioteer, each with three arrows.

sanskrit: स शत्रुभुजनिर्मुक्तैर्ललाटस्थैस्त्रिभिः शरैः ।नकुलः शुशुभे राजंस्त्रिशृङ्ग इव पर्वतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa śatrubhujanirmuktairlalāṭasthaistribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,nakulaḥ śuśubhe rājaṁstriśṛṅga iva parvataḥ.
english: O King, Nakula, with three arrows from the enemy's arm fixed on his forehead, shone like a three-peaked mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. O King, Nakula, with three arrows from the enemy's arm fixed on his forehead, shone like a three-peaked mountain.

sanskrit: स छिन्नधन्वा विरथः खड्गमादाय चर्म च ।रथादवातरद्वीरः शैलाग्रादिव केसरी ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa chinnadhanvā virathaḥ khaḍgamādāya carma ca ,rathādavātaradvīraḥ śailāgrādiva kesarī.
english: With his bow cut and deprived of his chariot, the hero, taking his sword and shield, descended from the chariot like a lion from a mountain peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. With his bow cut and deprived of his chariot, the hero, taking his sword and shield, descended from the chariot like a lion from a mountain peak.

sanskrit: पद्भ्यामापततस्तस्य शरवृष्टिमवासृजत् ।नकुलोऽप्यग्रसत्तां वै चर्मणा लघुविक्रमः ॥१७॥
transliteration: padbhyāmāpatatastasya śaravṛṣṭimavāsṛjat ,nakulo'pyagrasattāṁ vai carmaṇā laghuvikramaḥ.
english: The opponent released a shower of arrows at Nakula, who was advancing on foot. But Nakula, swift in action, indeed maintained his pre-eminence even with his shield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. The opponent released a shower of arrows at Nakula, who was advancing on foot. But Nakula, swift in action, indeed maintained his pre-eminence even with his shield.

sanskrit: चित्रसेनरथं प्राप्य चित्रयोधी जितश्रमः ।आरुरोह महाबाहुः सर्वसैन्यस्य पश्यतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: citrasenarathaṁ prāpya citrayodhī jitaśramaḥ ,āruroha mahābāhuḥ sarvasainyasya paśyataḥ.
english: Having reached Citrasena's chariot, the skillful warrior, the mighty-armed one, free from weariness, mounted it while the entire army watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. Having reached Citrasena's chariot, the skillful warrior, the mighty-armed one, free from weariness, mounted it while the entire army watched.

sanskrit: सकुण्डलं समुकुटं सुनसं स्वायतेक्षणम् ।चित्रसेनशिरः कायादपाहरत पाण्डवः ।स पपात रथोपस्थाद्दिवाकरसमप्रभः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sakuṇḍalaṁ samukuṭaṁ sunasaṁ svāyatekṣaṇam ,citrasenaśiraḥ kāyādapāharata pāṇḍavaḥ ,sa papāta rathopasthāddivākarasamaprabhaḥ.
english: The son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) severed Citrasena's head from his body; it was adorned with earrings, a crown, a beautiful nose, and wide, beautiful eyes. That head, shining like the sun, fell from the chariot platform.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. The son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) severed Citrasena's head from his body; it was adorned with earrings, a crown, a beautiful nose, and wide, beautiful eyes. That head, shining like the sun, fell from the chariot platform.

sanskrit: चित्रसेनं विशस्तं तु दृष्ट्वा तत्र महारथाः ।साधुवादस्वनांश्चक्रुः सिंहनादांश्च पुष्कलान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: citrasenaṁ viśastaṁ tu dṛṣṭvā tatra mahārathāḥ ,sādhuvādasvanāṁścakruḥ siṁhanādāṁśca puṣkalān.
english: But then, having seen Citrasena slain there, the great warriors uttered shouts of approval and mighty lion-roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. But then, having seen Citrasena slain there, the great warriors uttered shouts of approval and mighty lion-roars.

sanskrit: विशस्तं भ्रातरं दृष्ट्वा कर्णपुत्रौ महारथौ ।सुषेणः सत्यसेनश्च मुञ्चन्तौ निशिताञ्शरान् ॥२१॥
transliteration: viśastaṁ bhrātaraṁ dṛṣṭvā karṇaputrau mahārathau ,suṣeṇaḥ satyasenaśca muñcantau niśitāñśarān.
english: Having seen their slain brother, the two great charioteers, Suṣeṇa and Satyasena, sons of Karṇa, began releasing sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. Having seen their slain brother, the two great charioteers, Suṣeṇa and Satyasena, sons of Karṇa, began releasing sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽभ्यधावतां तूर्णं पाण्डवं रथिनां वरम् ।जिघांसन्तौ यथा नागं व्याघ्रौ राजन्महावने ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato'bhyadhāvatāṁ tūrṇaṁ pāṇḍavaṁ rathināṁ varam ,jighāṁsantau yathā nāgaṁ vyāghrau rājanmahāvane.
english: O King, then those two, desiring to kill the Pandava, the best among charioteers, swiftly rushed towards him, just as two tigers would rush towards an elephant in a great forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 22. O King, then those two, desiring to kill the Pandava, the best among charioteers, swiftly rushed towards him, just as two tigers would rush towards an elephant in a great forest.

sanskrit: तावभ्यधावतां तीक्ष्णौ द्वावप्येनं महारथम् ।शरौघान्सम्यगस्यन्तौ जीमूतौ सलिलं यथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: tāvabhyadhāvatāṁ tīkṣṇau dvāvapyenaṁ mahāratham ,śaraughānsamyagasyantau jīmūtau salilaṁ yathā.
english: Both of those two fierce warriors rushed towards him, the great charioteer, accurately unleashing showers of arrows, just as two clouds pour down rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 23. Both of those two fierce warriors rushed towards him, the great charioteer, accurately unleashing showers of arrows, just as two clouds pour down rain.

sanskrit: स शरैः सर्वतो विद्धः प्रहृष्ट इव पाण्डवः ।अन्यत्कार्मुकमादाय रथमारुह्य वीर्यवान् ।अतिष्ठत रणे वीरः क्रुद्धरूप इवान्तकः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa śaraiḥ sarvato viddhaḥ prahṛṣṭa iva pāṇḍavaḥ ,anyatkārmukamādāya rathamāruhya vīryavān ,atiṣṭhata raṇe vīraḥ kruddharūpa ivāntakaḥ.
english: Though pierced by arrows from all sides, the Pandava was as if delighted. That mighty hero, taking another bow and ascending his chariot, stood in battle like Yama (antaka), the enraged embodiment of death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 24. Though pierced by arrows from all sides, the Pandava was as if delighted. That mighty hero, taking another bow and ascending his chariot, stood in battle like Yama (antaka), the enraged embodiment of death.

sanskrit: तस्य तौ भ्रातरौ राजञ्शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।रथं विशकलीकर्तुं समारब्धौ विशां पते ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasya tau bhrātarau rājañśaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,rathaṁ viśakalīkartuṁ samārabdhau viśāṁ pate.
english: O King, O lord of men, those two brothers of his began to shatter his chariot into pieces with their well-formed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 25. O King, O lord of men, those two brothers of his began to shatter his chariot into pieces with their well-formed arrows.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्य नकुलश्चतुर्भिश्चतुरो रणे ।जघान निशितैस्तीक्ष्णैः सत्यसेनस्य वाजिनः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasya nakulaścaturbhiścaturo raṇe ,jaghāna niśitaistīkṣṇaiḥ satyasenasya vājinaḥ.
english: Then, smiling, Nakula struck down four of Satyaseṇa's horses in battle with four sharp, piercing [arrows].
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 26. Then, smiling, Nakula struck down four of Satyaseṇa's horses in battle with four sharp, piercing [arrows].

sanskrit: ततः संधाय नाराचं रुक्मपुङ्खं शिलाशितम् ।धनुश्चिच्छेद राजेन्द्र सत्यसेनस्य पाण्डवः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁdhāya nārācaṁ rukmapuṅkhaṁ śilāśitam ,dhanuściccheda rājendra satyasenasya pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Then, O king, the Pāṇḍava (Nakula), having aimed a metal arrow with golden fletching, sharpened on a stone, cut off Satyaseṇa's bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 27. Then, O king, the Pāṇḍava (Nakula), having aimed a metal arrow with golden fletching, sharpened on a stone, cut off Satyaseṇa's bow.

sanskrit: अथान्यं रथमास्थाय धनुरादाय चापरम् ।सत्यसेनः सुषेणश्च पाण्डवं पर्यधावताम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: athānyaṁ rathamāsthāya dhanurādāya cāparam ,satyasenaḥ suṣeṇaśca pāṇḍavaṁ paryadhāvatām.
english: Then, having mounted another chariot and taken another bow, Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa rushed towards the Pāṇḍava (Nakula).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 28. Then, having mounted another chariot and taken another bow, Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa rushed towards the Pāṇḍava (Nakula).

sanskrit: अविध्यत्तावसंभ्रान्तौ माद्रीपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।द्वाभ्यां द्वाभ्यां महाराज शराभ्यां रणमूर्धनि ॥२९॥
transliteration: avidhyattāvasaṁbhrāntau mādrīputraḥ pratāpavān ,dvābhyāṁ dvābhyāṁ mahārāja śarābhyāṁ raṇamūrdhani.
