english: O great king, the glorious son of Mādrī (Nakula) struck those two (Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa), who were still undismayed, with two arrows each in the forefront of battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 29. O great king, the glorious son of Mādrī (Nakula) struck those two (Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa), who were still undismayed, with two arrows each in the forefront of battle.

sanskrit: सुषेणस्तु ततः क्रुद्धः पाण्डवस्य महद्धनुः ।चिच्छेद प्रहसन्युद्धे क्षुरप्रेण महारथः ॥३०॥
transliteration: suṣeṇastu tataḥ kruddhaḥ pāṇḍavasya mahaddhanuḥ ,ciccheda prahasanyuddhe kṣurapreṇa mahārathaḥ.
english: Then, the great warrior Suṣeṇa, enraged and laughing in battle, cut off the large bow of the son of Pāṇḍu with a razor-sharp arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 30. Then, the great warrior Suṣeṇa, enraged and laughing in battle, cut off the large bow of the son of Pāṇḍu with a razor-sharp arrow.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय नकुलः क्रोधमूर्छितः ।सुषेणं पञ्चभिर्विद्ध्वा ध्वजमेकेन चिच्छिदे ॥३१॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya nakulaḥ krodhamūrchitaḥ ,suṣeṇaṁ pañcabhirviddhvā dhvajamekena cicchide.
english: Then, Nakula, overcome with anger (krodhamūrcchita), took up another bow. Having pierced Suṣeṇa with five arrows, he cut off his banner with one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 31. Then, Nakula, overcome with anger (krodhamūrcchita), took up another bow. Having pierced Suṣeṇa with five arrows, he cut off his banner with one.

sanskrit: सत्यसेनस्य च धनुर्हस्तावापं च मारिष ।चिच्छेद तरसा युद्धे तत उच्चुक्रुशुर्जनाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: satyasenasya ca dhanurhastāvāpaṁ ca māriṣa ,ciccheda tarasā yuddhe tata uccukruśurjanāḥ.
english: And, O respected one (māriṣa), in battle, he swiftly (tarasā) cut off Satyaseṇa's bow and arm-guard. Then, the people (janāḥ) cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 32. And, O respected one (māriṣa), in battle, he swiftly (tarasā) cut off Satyaseṇa's bow and arm-guard. Then, the people (janāḥ) cried out.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय वेगघ्नं भारसाधनम् ।शरैः संछादयामास समन्तात्पाण्डुनन्दनम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya vegaghnaṁ bhārasādhanam ,śaraiḥ saṁchādayāmāsa samantātpāṇḍunandanam.
english: Then, taking up another bow - one that was potent for great tasks (bhārasādhanam) and destructive of speed (vegaghnam) - he completely covered the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍunandana) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 33. Then, taking up another bow - one that was potent for great tasks (bhārasādhanam) and destructive of speed (vegaghnam) - he completely covered the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍunandana) with arrows.

sanskrit: संनिवार्य तु तान्बाणान्नकुलः परवीरहा ।सत्यसेनं सुषेणं च द्वाभ्यां द्वाभ्यामविध्यत ॥३४॥
transliteration: saṁnivārya tu tānbāṇānnakulaḥ paravīrahā ,satyasenaṁ suṣeṇaṁ ca dvābhyāṁ dvābhyāmavidhyata.
english: But Nakula, the slayer of enemy heroes, having completely warded off those arrows, pierced Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa with two arrows each.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 34. But Nakula, the slayer of enemy heroes, having completely warded off those arrows, pierced Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa with two arrows each.

sanskrit: तावेनं प्रत्यविध्येतां पृथक्पृथगजिह्मगैः ।सारथिं चास्य राजेन्द्र शरैर्विव्यधतुः शितैः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tāvenaṁ pratyavidhyetāṁ pṛthakpṛthagajihmagaiḥ ,sārathiṁ cāsya rājendra śarairvivyadhatuḥ śitaiḥ.
english: O best of kings, those two (Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa) in turn pierced him (Nakula) separately with straight-flying arrows, and they also pierced his charioteer with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 35. O best of kings, those two (Satyaseṇa and Suṣeṇa) in turn pierced him (Nakula) separately with straight-flying arrows, and they also pierced his charioteer with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: सत्यसेनो रथेषां तु नकुलस्य धनुस्तथा ।पृथक्शराभ्यां चिच्छेद कृतहस्तः प्रतापवान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: satyaseno ratheṣāṁ tu nakulasya dhanustathā ,pṛthakśarābhyāṁ ciccheda kṛtahastaḥ pratāpavān.
english: But the mighty and skilled Satyaseṇa, with two separate arrows, cut Nakula's chariot-pole and similarly (cut his) bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 36. But the mighty and skilled Satyaseṇa, with two separate arrows, cut Nakula's chariot-pole and similarly (cut his) bow.

sanskrit: स रथेऽतिरथस्तिष्ठन्रथशक्तिं परामृशत् ।स्वर्णदण्डामकुण्ठाग्रां तैलधौतां सुनिर्मलाम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sa rathe'tirathastiṣṭhanrathaśaktiṁ parāmṛśat ,svarṇadaṇḍāmakuṇṭhāgrāṁ tailadhautāṁ sunirmalām.
english: Standing in his chariot, that great warrior (Nakula) grasped a chariot-spear with a golden shaft, an unblunted tip, cleansed with oil, and very pure.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 37. Standing in his chariot, that great warrior (Nakula) grasped a chariot-spear with a golden shaft, an unblunted tip, cleansed with oil, and very pure.

sanskrit: लेलिहानामिव विभो नागकन्यां महाविषाम् ।समुद्यम्य च चिक्षेप सत्यसेनस्य संयुगे ॥३८॥
transliteration: lelihānāmiva vibho nāgakanyāṁ mahāviṣām ,samudyamya ca cikṣepa satyasenasya saṁyuge.
english: O lord, [he] lifted [his weapon] and hurled it at Satyāsena in battle, as if it were a highly venomous serpent maiden, flickering [its tongue] and full of great poison.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 38. O lord, [he] lifted [his weapon] and hurled it at Satyāsena in battle, as if it were a highly venomous serpent maiden, flickering [its tongue] and full of great poison.

sanskrit: सा तस्य हृदयं संख्ये बिभेद शतधा नृप ।स पपात रथाद्भूमौ गतसत्त्वोऽल्पचेतनः ॥३९॥
transliteration: sā tasya hṛdayaṁ saṁkhye bibheda śatadhā nṛpa ,sa papāta rathādbhūmau gatasattvo'lpacetanaḥ.
english: O king, that [weapon] pierced his heart a hundredfold in battle. He fell from his chariot to the ground, his life (sattva) having departed, and with little consciousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 39. O king, that [weapon] pierced his heart a hundredfold in battle. He fell from his chariot to the ground, his life (sattva) having departed, and with little consciousness.

sanskrit: भ्रातरं निहतं दृष्ट्वा सुषेणः क्रोधमूर्छितः ।अभ्यवर्षच्छरैस्तूर्णं पदातिं पाण्डुनन्दनम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: bhrātaraṁ nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā suṣeṇaḥ krodhamūrchitaḥ ,abhyavarṣaccharaistūrṇaṁ padātiṁ pāṇḍunandanam.
english: Having seen his brother killed, Suṣeṇa, overcome with anger, quickly showered arrows upon the Pāṇḍava prince who was on foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 40. Having seen his brother killed, Suṣeṇa, overcome with anger, quickly showered arrows upon the Pāṇḍava prince who was on foot.

sanskrit: नकुलं विरथं दृष्ट्वा द्रौपदेयो महाबलः ।सुतसोमोऽभिदुद्राव परीप्सन्पितरं रणे ॥४१॥
transliteration: nakulaṁ virathaṁ dṛṣṭvā draupadeyo mahābalaḥ ,sutasomo'bhidudrāva parīpsanpitaraṁ raṇe.
english: Having seen Nakula dismounted from his chariot, Sutasoma, the mighty son of Draupadī, rushed forward, wishing to protect his father in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 41. Having seen Nakula dismounted from his chariot, Sutasoma, the mighty son of Draupadī, rushed forward, wishing to protect his father in battle.

sanskrit: ततोऽधिरुह्य नकुलः सुतसोमस्य तं रथम् ।शुशुभे भरतश्रेष्ठो गिरिस्थ इव केसरी ।सोऽन्यत्कार्मुकमादाय सुषेणं समयोधयत् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tato'dhiruhya nakulaḥ sutasomasya taṁ ratham ,śuśubhe bharataśreṣṭho giristha iva kesarī ,so'nyatkārmukamādāya suṣeṇaṁ samayodhayat.
english: Then Nakula, the best among the Bharatas, having ascended that chariot of Sutasoma, shone like a lion standing on a mountain. He then took another bow and fought Sushena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 42. Then Nakula, the best among the Bharatas, having ascended that chariot of Sutasoma, shone like a lion standing on a mountain. He then took another bow and fought Sushena.

sanskrit: तावुभौ शरवर्षाभ्यां समासाद्य परस्परम् ।परस्परवधे यत्नं चक्रतुः सुमहारथौ ॥४३॥
transliteration: tāvubhau śaravarṣābhyāṁ samāsādya parasparam ,parasparavadhe yatnaṁ cakratuḥ sumahārathau.
english: Having confronted each other with showers of arrows, those two great charioteers made an effort for each other's destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 43. Having confronted each other with showers of arrows, those two great charioteers made an effort for each other's destruction.

sanskrit: सुषेणस्तु ततः क्रुद्धः पाण्डवं विशिखैस्त्रिभिः ।सुतसोमं च विंशत्या बाह्वोरुरसि चार्पयत् ॥४४॥
transliteration: suṣeṇastu tataḥ kruddhaḥ pāṇḍavaṁ viśikhaistribhiḥ ,sutasomaṁ ca viṁśatyā bāhvorurasi cārpayat.
english: But Sushena, enraged, then struck the Pandava (Nakula) with three arrows, and pierced Sutasoma with twenty arrows on his two arms and chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 44. But Sushena, enraged, then struck the Pandava (Nakula) with three arrows, and pierced Sutasoma with twenty arrows on his two arms and chest.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो महाराज नकुलः परवीरहा ।शरैस्तस्य दिशः सर्वाश्छादयामास वीर्यवान् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho mahārāja nakulaḥ paravīrahā ,śaraistasya diśaḥ sarvāśchādayāmāsa vīryavān.
english: Then, O great king, the enraged, valiant Nakula, slayer of enemy heroes, covered all directions with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 45. Then, O great king, the enraged, valiant Nakula, slayer of enemy heroes, covered all directions with his arrows.

sanskrit: ततो गृहीत्वा तीक्ष्णाग्रमर्धचन्द्रं सुतेजनम् ।स वेगयुक्तं चिक्षेप कर्णपुत्रस्य संयुगे ॥४६॥
transliteration: tato gṛhītvā tīkṣṇāgramardhacandraṁ sutejanam ,sa vegayuktaṁ cikṣepa karṇaputrasya saṁyuge.
english: Then he took the very sharp, sharp-pointed half-moon-shaped weapon and swiftly threw it at Karna's son in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 46. Then he took the very sharp, sharp-pointed half-moon-shaped weapon and swiftly threw it at Karna's son in battle.

sanskrit: तस्य तेन शिरः कायाज्जहार नृपसत्तम ।पश्यतां सर्वसैन्यानां तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tasya tena śiraḥ kāyājjahāra nṛpasattama ,paśyatāṁ sarvasainyānāṁ tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: O best of kings, with that weapon he severed his (Karna's son's) head from his body. This event appeared as a marvel to all the armies watching.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 47. O best of kings, with that weapon he severed his (Karna's son's) head from his body. This event appeared as a marvel to all the armies watching.

sanskrit: स हतः प्रापतद्राजन्नकुलेन महात्मना ।नदीवेगादिवारुग्णस्तीरजः पादपो महान् ॥४८॥
transliteration: sa hataḥ prāpatadrājannakulena mahātmanā ,nadīvegādivārugṇastīrajaḥ pādapo mahān.
english: O king, he (Karna's son), having been killed by the noble (mahātman) Nakula, fell down, just as a great tree grown on a riverbank is broken by the force of the river current.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 48. O king, he (Karna's son), having been killed by the noble (mahātman) Nakula, fell down, just as a great tree grown on a riverbank is broken by the force of the river current.

sanskrit: कर्णपुत्रवधं दृष्ट्वा नकुलस्य च विक्रमम् ।प्रदुद्राव भयात्सेना तावकी भरतर्षभ ॥४९॥
transliteration: karṇaputravadhaṁ dṛṣṭvā nakulasya ca vikramam ,pradudrāva bhayātsenā tāvakī bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, upon seeing the slaying of Karna's son and Nakula's valor, your army fled in fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 49. O best of Bharatas, upon seeing the slaying of Karna's son and Nakula's valor, your army fled in fear.

sanskrit: तां तु सेनां महाराज मद्रराजः प्रतापवान् ।अपालयद्रणे शूरः सेनापतिररिंदमः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tāṁ tu senāṁ mahārāja madrarājaḥ pratāpavān ,apālayadraṇe śūraḥ senāpatirariṁdamaḥ.
english: O great king, the mighty, brave commander, the subduer of enemies, the king of Madra, indeed protected that army in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 50. O great king, the mighty, brave commander, the subduer of enemies, the king of Madra, indeed protected that army in battle.

sanskrit: विभीस्तस्थौ महाराज व्यवस्थाप्य च वाहिनीम् ।सिंहनादं भृशं कृत्वा धनुःशब्दं च दारुणम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: vibhīstasthau mahārāja vyavasthāpya ca vāhinīm ,siṁhanādaṁ bhṛśaṁ kṛtvā dhanuḥśabdaṁ ca dāruṇam.
english: O great king, Bhishma stood, having deployed his army, and having let out a mighty lion's roar and a terrifying sound of bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 51. O great king, Bhishma stood, having deployed his army, and having let out a mighty lion's roar and a terrifying sound of bows.

sanskrit: तावकाः समरे राजन्रक्षिता दृढधन्वना ।प्रत्युद्ययुररातींस्ते समन्ताद्विगतव्यथाः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tāvakāḥ samare rājanrakṣitā dṛḍhadhanvanā ,pratyudyayurarātīṁste samantādvigatavyathāḥ.
english: O King, your men, protected by the steadfast archer (Bhishma), were free from distress and advanced against the enemies from all sides in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 52. O King, your men, protected by the steadfast archer (Bhishma), were free from distress and advanced against the enemies from all sides in battle.

sanskrit: मद्रराजं महेष्वासं परिवार्य समन्ततः ।स्थिता राजन्महासेना योद्धुकामाः समन्ततः ॥५३॥
transliteration: madrarājaṁ maheṣvāsaṁ parivārya samantataḥ ,sthitā rājanmahāsenā yoddhukāmāḥ samantataḥ.
english: O King, the great armies, desiring to fight, surrounded the great archer, the king of Madra, from all sides and stood deployed everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 53. O King, the great armies, desiring to fight, surrounded the great archer, the king of Madra, from all sides and stood deployed everywhere.

sanskrit: सात्यकिर्भीमसेनश्च माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।युधिष्ठिरं पुरस्कृत्य ह्रीनिषेधमरिंदमम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: sātyakirbhīmasenaśca mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ puraskṛtya hrīniṣedhamariṁdamam.
english: Sātyaki, Bhīmasena, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, placing Yudhiṣṭhira – the subduer of enemies and one who prevents disgrace – at their forefront,
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 54. Sātyaki, Bhīmasena, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, placing Yudhiṣṭhira – the subduer of enemies and one who prevents disgrace – at their forefront,

sanskrit: परिवार्य रणे वीराः सिंहनादं प्रचक्रिरे ।बाणशब्दरवांश्चोग्रान्क्ष्वेडांश्च विविधान्दधुः ॥५५॥
transliteration: parivārya raṇe vīrāḥ siṁhanādaṁ pracakrire ,bāṇaśabdaravāṁścogrānkṣveḍāṁśca vividhāndadhuḥ.
english: Surrounding him in battle, the heroes let out lion's roars and produced terrible sounds of arrows and various war-cries.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 55. Surrounding him in battle, the heroes let out lion's roars and produced terrible sounds of arrows and various war-cries.

sanskrit: तथैव तावकाः सर्वे मद्राधिपतिमञ्जसा ।परिवार्य सुसंरब्धाः पुनर्युद्धमरोचयन् ॥५६॥
transliteration: tathaiva tāvakāḥ sarve madrādhipatimañjasā ,parivārya susaṁrabdhāḥ punaryuddhamarocayan.
english: Similarly, all your warriors, greatly enraged, swiftly surrounded the lord of Madra (Śalya) and again prepared for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 56. Similarly, all your warriors, greatly enraged, swiftly surrounded the lord of Madra (Śalya) and again prepared for battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं भीरूणां भयवर्धनम् ।तावकानां परेषां च मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ bhīrūṇāṁ bhayavardhanam ,tāvakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: Then the battle began, which intensified the fear of the timid, for both your warriors and the enemies, as death became the only way to cease engagement (lit. return).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 57. Then the battle began, which intensified the fear of the timid, for both your warriors and the enemies, as death became the only way to cease engagement (lit. return).

sanskrit: यथा देवासुरं युद्धं पूर्वमासीद्विशां पते ।अभीतानां तथा राजन्यमराष्ट्रविवर्धनम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: yathā devāsuraṁ yuddhaṁ pūrvamāsīdviśāṁ pate ,abhītānāṁ tathā rājanyamarāṣṭravivardhanam.
english: Just as there was formerly a great battle between the gods and asuras (devāsuram yuddham), O lord of men (viśām pate), so too, O king (rājan), for those who were fearless, this battle was one that increased the dominion of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 58. Just as there was formerly a great battle between the gods and asuras (devāsuram yuddham), O lord of men (viśām pate), so too, O king (rājan), for those who were fearless, this battle was one that increased the dominion of Yama.

sanskrit: ततः कपिध्वजो राजन्हत्वा संशप्तकान्रणे ।अभ्यद्रवत तां सेनां कौरवीं पाण्डुनन्दनः ॥५९॥
transliteration: tataḥ kapidhvajo rājanhatvā saṁśaptakānraṇe ,abhyadravata tāṁ senāṁ kauravīṁ pāṇḍunandanaḥ.
english: Then, O king (rājan), Arjuna, he of the monkey banner (kapidhvajaḥ) and son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍunandanaḥ), having killed the Saṃśaptakas in battle (raṇe), rushed towards that Kaurava army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 59. Then, O king (rājan), Arjuna, he of the monkey banner (kapidhvajaḥ) and son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍunandanaḥ), having killed the Saṃśaptakas in battle (raṇe), rushed towards that Kaurava army.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवाः शेषा धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमाः ।अभ्यधावन्त तां सेनां विसृजन्तः शिताञ्शरान् ॥६०॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavāḥ śeṣā dhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamāḥ ,abhyadhāvanta tāṁ senāṁ visṛjantaḥ śitāñśarān.
english: Similarly (tathaiva), the remaining Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavāḥ śeṣāḥ), led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna (dhṛṣṭadyumnapurōgamāḥ), rushed towards that army, showering sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 60. Similarly (tathaiva), the remaining Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavāḥ śeṣāḥ), led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna (dhṛṣṭadyumnapurōgamāḥ), rushed towards that army, showering sharp arrows.

sanskrit: पाण्डवैरवकीर्णानां संमोहः समजायत ।न च जज्ञुरनीकानि दिशो वा प्रदिशस्तथा ॥६१॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavairavakīrṇānāṁ saṁmohaḥ samajāyata ,na ca jajñuranīkāni diśo vā pradiśastathā.
english: Great confusion (saṃmōhaḥ) arose among those who were overwhelmed by the Pāṇḍavas. They could not discern their own battle formations (anīkāni), nor the cardinal directions (diśo), nor even the intermediate directions (pradiśas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 61. Great confusion (saṃmōhaḥ) arose among those who were overwhelmed by the Pāṇḍavas. They could not discern their own battle formations (anīkāni), nor the cardinal directions (diśo), nor even the intermediate directions (pradiśas).

sanskrit: आपूर्यमाणा निशितैः शरैः पाण्डवचोदितैः ।हतप्रवीरा विध्वस्ता कीर्यमाणा समन्ततः ।कौरव्यवध्यत चमूः पाण्डुपुत्रैर्महारथैः ॥६२॥
transliteration: āpūryamāṇā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ pāṇḍavacoditaiḥ ,hatapravīrā vidhvastā kīryamāṇā samantataḥ ,kauravyavadhyata camūḥ pāṇḍuputrairmahārathaiḥ.
english: The Kaurava army (camūḥ), overwhelmed by sharp arrows impelled by the Pāṇḍavas, with its chief heroes slain, shattered and scattered from all sides, was being destroyed by the great charioteers, the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 62. The Kaurava army (camūḥ), overwhelmed by sharp arrows impelled by the Pāṇḍavas, with its chief heroes slain, shattered and scattered from all sides, was being destroyed by the great charioteers, the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवी सेना शरै राजन्समन्ततः ।रणेऽहन्यत पुत्रैस्ते शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥६३॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavī senā śarai rājansamantataḥ ,raṇe'hanyata putraiste śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Just so, O King, the Pāṇḍava army (senā) was being killed in battle by your sons, by hundreds and by thousands, by arrows from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 63. Just so, O King, the Pāṇḍava army (senā) was being killed in battle by your sons, by hundreds and by thousands, by arrows from all sides.

sanskrit: ते सेने भृशसंतप्ते वध्यमाने परस्परम् ।व्याकुले समपद्येतां वर्षासु सरिताविव ॥६४॥
transliteration: te sene bhṛśasaṁtapte vadhyamāne parasparam ,vyākule samapadyetāṁ varṣāsu saritāviva.
english: Those two armies (senā), greatly afflicted and engaged in mutual slaughter, became agitated and bewildered, just like two rivers (saritā) during the rainy season.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 64. Those two armies (senā), greatly afflicted and engaged in mutual slaughter, became agitated and bewildered, just like two rivers (saritā) during the rainy season.

sanskrit: आविवेश ततस्तीव्रं तावकानां महद्भयम् ।पाण्डवानां च राजेन्द्र तथाभूते महाहवे ॥६५॥
transliteration: āviveśa tatastīvraṁ tāvakānāṁ mahadbhayam ,pāṇḍavānāṁ ca rājendra tathābhūte mahāhave.
english: Thereupon, O King of kings (rājendra), intense and great fear pervaded both your (sons') party and the Pāṇḍavas', as the great battle (mahāhava) had reached such a critical state.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 9 Verse 65. Thereupon, O King of kings (rājendra), intense and great fear pervaded both your (sons') party and the Pāṇḍavas', as the great battle (mahāhava) had reached such a critical state.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तस्मिन्विलुलिते सैन्ये वध्यमाने परस्परम् ।द्रवमाणेषु योधेषु निनदत्सु च दन्तिषु ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tasminvilulite sainye vadhyamāne parasparam ,dravamāṇeṣu yodheṣu ninadatsu ca dantiṣu.
english: Sanjaya said: "When that army was thrown into disarray, with soldiers killing each other; when the warriors were fleeing and the elephants were trumpeting...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: "When that army was thrown into disarray, with soldiers killing each other; when the warriors were fleeing and the elephants were trumpeting...

sanskrit: कूजतां स्तनतां चैव पदातीनां महाहवे ।विद्रुतेषु महाराज हयेषु बहुधा तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: kūjatāṁ stanatāṁ caiva padātīnāṁ mahāhave ,vidruteṣu mahārāja hayeṣu bahudhā tadā.
english: ...when, in that great battle, the foot soldiers were crying out and roaring, and the horses had scattered in many directions, O great king, at that very moment...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. ...when, in that great battle, the foot soldiers were crying out and roaring, and the horses had scattered in many directions, O great king, at that very moment...

sanskrit: प्रक्षये दारुणे जाते संहारे सर्वदेहिनाम् ।नानाशस्त्रसमावापे व्यतिषक्तरथद्विपे ॥३॥
transliteration: prakṣaye dāruṇe jāte saṁhāre sarvadehinām ,nānāśastrasamāvāpe vyatiṣaktarathadvipe.
english: ...when a dreadful destruction, a slaughter of all embodied beings, had taken place, with countless weapons being discharged and chariots and elephants entangled...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. ...when a dreadful destruction, a slaughter of all embodied beings, had taken place, with countless weapons being discharged and chariots and elephants entangled...

sanskrit: हर्षणे युद्धशौण्डानां भीरूणां भयवर्धने ।गाहमानेषु योधेषु परस्परवधैषिषु ॥४॥
transliteration: harṣaṇe yuddhaśauṇḍānāṁ bhīrūṇāṁ bhayavardhane ,gāhamāneṣu yodheṣu parasparavadhaiṣiṣu.
english: ...and when warriors, fiercely seeking to kill one another, plunged deeper into the fray – a spectacle that brought joy to those valorous in battle but intensified the terror for the fearful...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. ...and when warriors, fiercely seeking to kill one another, plunged deeper into the fray – a spectacle that brought joy to those valorous in battle but intensified the terror for the fearful...

sanskrit: प्राणादाने महाघोरे वर्तमाने दुरोदरे ।संग्रामे घोररूपे तु यमराष्ट्रविवर्धने ॥५॥
transliteration: prāṇādāne mahāghore vartamāne durodare ,saṁgrāme ghorarūpe tu yamarāṣṭravivardhane.
english: While a terrible gamble involving the loss of lives was taking place, indeed, in the battle of dreadful form, which greatly increased the dominion of Yama (death).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. While a terrible gamble involving the loss of lives was taking place, indeed, in the battle of dreadful form, which greatly increased the dominion of Yama (death).

sanskrit: पाण्डवास्तावकं सैन्यं व्यधमन्निशितैः शरैः ।तथैव तावका योधा जघ्नुः पाण्डवसैनिकान् ॥६॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāstāvakaṁ sainyaṁ vyadhamanniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,tathaiva tāvakā yodhā jaghnuḥ pāṇḍavasainikān.
english: The Pāṇḍavas dispersed your army with sharp arrows. Similarly, your warriors killed the Pāṇḍava soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. The Pāṇḍavas dispersed your army with sharp arrows. Similarly, your warriors killed the Pāṇḍava soldiers.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तथा वर्तमाने युद्धे भीरुभयावहे ।पूर्वाह्णे चैव संप्राप्ते भास्करोदयनं प्रति ॥७॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstathā vartamāne yuddhe bhīrubhayāvahe ,pūrvāhṇe caiva saṁprāpte bhāskarodayanaṁ prati.
english: As that terrifying battle, which struck fear into the timid, was thus ongoing, and the forenoon had indeed arrived, specifically at the time of the sun's rising.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. As that terrifying battle, which struck fear into the timid, was thus ongoing, and the forenoon had indeed arrived, specifically at the time of the sun's rising.

sanskrit: लब्धलक्षाः परे राजन्रक्षिताश्च महात्मना ।अयोधयंस्तव बलं मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: labdhalakṣāḥ pare rājanrakṣitāśca mahātmanā ,ayodhayaṁstava balaṁ mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: O King, the enemies, who had achieved their aim and were protected by a great soul (mahātman), fought your army, treating death as their final retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. O King, the enemies, who had achieved their aim and were protected by a great soul (mahātman), fought your army, treating death as their final retreat.

sanskrit: बलिभिः पाण्डवैर्दृप्तैर्लब्धलक्षैः प्रहारिभिः ।कौरव्यसीदत्पृतना मृगीवाग्निसमाकुला ॥९॥
transliteration: balibhiḥ pāṇḍavairdṛptairlabdhalakṣaiḥ prahāribhiḥ ,kauravyasīdatpṛtanā mṛgīvāgnisamākulā.
english: O scion of Kuru, your army was perishing, agitated like a female deer surrounded by fire, due to the powerful, fierce Pandavas who had found their targets and were striking effectively.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. O scion of Kuru, your army was perishing, agitated like a female deer surrounded by fire, due to the powerful, fierce Pandavas who had found their targets and were striking effectively.

sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा सीदतीं सेनां पङ्के गामिव दुर्बलाम् ।उज्जिहीर्षुस्तदा शल्यः प्रायात्पाण्डुचमूं प्रति ॥१०॥
transliteration: tāṁ dṛṣṭvā sīdatīṁ senāṁ paṅke gāmiva durbalām ,ujjihīrṣustadā śalyaḥ prāyātpāṇḍucamūṁ prati.
english: Having seen that weak, perishing army, like a cow sinking in the mud, Śalya then, desiring to rescue it, advanced towards the Pandava army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. Having seen that weak, perishing army, like a cow sinking in the mud, Śalya then, desiring to rescue it, advanced towards the Pandava army.

sanskrit: मद्रराजस्तु संक्रुद्धो गृहीत्वा धनुरुत्तमम् ।अभ्यद्रवत संग्रामे पाण्डवानाततायिनः ॥११॥
transliteration: madrarājastu saṁkruddho gṛhītvā dhanuruttamam ,abhyadravata saṁgrāme pāṇḍavānātatāyinaḥ.
english: But the king of Madras, greatly enraged, took his excellent bow and rushed into battle against the Pandavas, who were the aggressors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. But the king of Madras, greatly enraged, took his excellent bow and rushed into battle against the Pandavas, who were the aggressors.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च महाराज समरे जितकाशिनः ।मद्रराजं समासाद्य विव्यधुर्निशितैः शरैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca mahārāja samare jitakāśinaḥ ,madrarājaṁ samāsādya vivyadhurniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: And O great king, the Pandavas, who were victorious in battle, approached the king of Madras and pierced him with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. And O great king, the Pandavas, who were victorious in battle, approached the king of Madras and pierced him with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शरशतैस्तीक्ष्णैर्मद्रराजो महाबलः ।अर्दयामास तां सेनां धर्मराजस्य पश्यतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraśataistīkṣṇairmadrarājo mahābalaḥ ,ardayāmāsa tāṁ senāṁ dharmarājasya paśyataḥ.
english: Then, the very powerful King of Madra, with hundreds of sharp arrows, harassed that army, right before the eyes of the King of Righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. Then, the very powerful King of Madra, with hundreds of sharp arrows, harassed that army, right before the eyes of the King of Righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: प्रादुरासंस्ततो राजन्नानारूपाण्यनेकशः ।चचाल शब्दं कुर्वाणा मही चापि सपर्वता ॥१४॥
transliteration: prādurāsaṁstato rājannānārūpāṇyanekaśaḥ ,cacāla śabdaṁ kurvāṇā mahī cāpi saparvatā.
english: Then, O King, various forms appeared in many ways. And the earth, along with its mountains, shook, making a rumbling sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 14. Then, O King, various forms appeared in many ways. And the earth, along with its mountains, shook, making a rumbling sound.

sanskrit: सदण्डशूला दीप्ताग्राः शीर्यमाणाः समन्ततः ।उल्का भूमिं दिवः पेतुराहत्य रविमण्डलम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: sadaṇḍaśūlā dīptāgrāḥ śīryamāṇāḥ samantataḥ ,ulkā bhūmiṁ divaḥ peturāhatya ravimaṇḍalam.
english: Meteors, appearing like staffs and spears with blazing tips, fell from the sky to the earth, crumbling on all sides after striking the sun's orb.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 15. Meteors, appearing like staffs and spears with blazing tips, fell from the sky to the earth, crumbling on all sides after striking the sun's orb.

sanskrit: मृगाश्च माहिषाश्चापि पक्षिणश्च विशां पते ।अपसव्यं तदा चक्रुः सेनां ते बहुशो नृप ॥१६॥
transliteration: mṛgāśca māhiṣāścāpi pakṣiṇaśca viśāṁ pate ,apasavyaṁ tadā cakruḥ senāṁ te bahuśo nṛpa.
english: O Lord of people (viśāmpate), O King (nṛpa), then deer, buffaloes, and also birds, frequently circled the army to the left, which was an inauspicious sign.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 16. O Lord of people (viśāmpate), O King (nṛpa), then deer, buffaloes, and also birds, frequently circled the army to the left, which was an inauspicious sign.

sanskrit: ततस्तद्युद्धमत्युग्रमभवत्संघचारिणाम् ।तथा सर्वाण्यनीकानि संनिपत्य जनाधिप ।अभ्ययुः कौरवा राजन्पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatastadyuddhamatyugramabhavatsaṁghacāriṇām ,tathā sarvāṇyanīkāni saṁnipatya janādhipa ,abhyayuḥ kauravā rājanpāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm.
english: O King, then that exceedingly fierce battle of the allied forces began. And thus, O ruler of men, all the Kaurava divisions, having assembled, advanced upon the army of the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 17. O King, then that exceedingly fierce battle of the allied forces began. And thus, O ruler of men, all the Kaurava divisions, having assembled, advanced upon the army of the Pandavas.

sanskrit: शल्यस्तु शरवर्षेण वर्षन्निव सहस्रदृक् ।अभ्यवर्षददीनात्मा कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: śalyastu śaravarṣeṇa varṣanniva sahasradṛk ,abhyavarṣadadīnātmā kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: But Śalya, with his undaunted spirit (ātman), rained down a shower of arrows upon Yudhiṣṭhira, Kuntī's son, as if he were the thousand-eyed (Indra) raining.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 18. But Śalya, with his undaunted spirit (ātman), rained down a shower of arrows upon Yudhiṣṭhira, Kuntī's son, as if he were the thousand-eyed (Indra) raining.

sanskrit: भीमसेनं शरैश्चापि रुक्मपुङ्खैः शिलाशितः ।द्रौपदेयांस्तथा सर्वान्माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaṁ śaraiścāpi rukmapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaḥ ,draupadeyāṁstathā sarvānmādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau.
english: And Śalya, resolute as if sharpened on stone, also showered Bhimasena with golden-feathered arrows. Likewise, he showered all the sons of Draupadī, and the two Pandavas, the sons of Mādrī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 19. And Śalya, resolute as if sharpened on stone, also showered Bhimasena with golden-feathered arrows. Likewise, he showered all the sons of Draupadī, and the two Pandavas, the sons of Mādrī.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं च शैनेयं शिखण्डिनमथापि च ।एकैकं दशभिर्बाणैर्विव्याध च महाबलः ।ततोऽसृजद्बाणवर्षं घर्मान्ते मघवानिव ॥२०॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca śaineyaṁ śikhaṇḍinamathāpi ca ,ekaikaṁ daśabhirbāṇairvivyādha ca mahābalaḥ ,tato'sṛjadbāṇavarṣaṁ gharmānte maghavāniva.
english: And the mighty Śalya struck each of them – Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śaineya, and also Śikhaṇḍin – with ten arrows. Then, like Indra at the end of the summer season, he released a torrent of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 20. And the mighty Śalya struck each of them – Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śaineya, and also Śikhaṇḍin – with ten arrows. Then, like Indra at the end of the summer season, he released a torrent of arrows.

sanskrit: ततः प्रभद्रका राजन्सोमकाश्च सहस्रशः ।पतिताः पात्यमानाश्च दृश्यन्ते शल्यसायकैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ prabhadrakā rājansomakāśca sahasraśaḥ ,patitāḥ pātyamānāśca dṛśyante śalyasāyakaiḥ.
english: Then, O king, thousands of Prabhadrakas and Somakas are seen, both fallen and being struck down by Śalya's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 21. Then, O king, thousands of Prabhadrakas and Somakas are seen, both fallen and being struck down by Śalya's arrows.

sanskrit: भ्रमराणामिव व्राताः शलभानामिव व्रजाः ।ह्रादिन्य इव मेघेभ्यः शल्यस्य न्यपतञ्शराः ॥२२॥
transliteration: bhramarāṇāmiva vrātāḥ śalabhānāmiva vrajāḥ ,hrādinya iva meghebhyaḥ śalyasya nyapatañśarāḥ.
english: Śalya's arrows fell like swarms of bees, like multitudes of locusts, and like thunderclaps from the clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 22. Śalya's arrows fell like swarms of bees, like multitudes of locusts, and like thunderclaps from the clouds.

sanskrit: द्विरदास्तुरगाश्चार्ताः पत्तयो रथिनस्तथा ।शल्यस्य बाणैर्न्यपतन्बभ्रमुर्व्यनदंस्तथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: dviradāsturagāścārtāḥ pattayo rathinastathā ,śalyasya bāṇairnyapatanbabhramurvyanadaṁstathā.
english: Elephants and horses, foot soldiers and charioteers, all distressed, fell down due to Śalya's arrows; they staggered and roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 23. Elephants and horses, foot soldiers and charioteers, all distressed, fell down due to Śalya's arrows; they staggered and roared.

sanskrit: आविष्ट इव मद्रेशो मन्युना पौरुषेण च ।प्राच्छादयदरीन्संख्ये कालसृष्ट इवान्तकः ।विनर्दमानो मद्रेशो मेघह्रादो महाबलः ॥२४॥
transliteration: āviṣṭa iva madreśo manyunā pauruṣeṇa ca ,prācchādayadarīnsaṁkhye kālasṛṣṭa ivāntakaḥ ,vinardamāno madreśo meghahrādo mahābalaḥ.
english: Overcome by wrath and valor, the lord of Madras (Śalya) overwhelmed his enemies in battle, just like Yama (antaka), the destroyer created by time. Roaring like a thunderous cloud, the mighty lord of Madras [fought].
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 24. Overcome by wrath and valor, the lord of Madras (Śalya) overwhelmed his enemies in battle, just like Yama (antaka), the destroyer created by time. Roaring like a thunderous cloud, the mighty lord of Madras [fought].

sanskrit: सा वध्यमाना शल्येन पाण्डवानामनीकिनी ।अजातशत्रुं कौन्तेयमभ्यधावद्युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: sā vadhyamānā śalyena pāṇḍavānāmanīkinī ,ajātaśatruṁ kaunteyamabhyadhāvadyudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Being struck down by Śalya, that army of the Pāṇḍavas fled towards Yudhiṣṭhira, also known as Ajātaśatru, the son of Kuntī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 25. Being struck down by Śalya, that army of the Pāṇḍavas fled towards Yudhiṣṭhira, also known as Ajātaśatru, the son of Kuntī.

sanskrit: तां समर्प्य ततः संख्ये लघुहस्तः शितैः शरैः ।शरवर्षेण महता युधिष्ठिरमपीडयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tāṁ samarpya tataḥ saṁkhye laghuhastaḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,śaravarṣeṇa mahatā yudhiṣṭhiramapīḍayat.
english: Then, in that battle, the swift-handed [Śalya], having assailed that [Pāṇḍava] army with sharp arrows, afflicted Yudhiṣṭhira with a great volley of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 26. Then, in that battle, the swift-handed [Śalya], having assailed that [Pāṇḍava] army with sharp arrows, afflicted Yudhiṣṭhira with a great volley of arrows.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं पत्त्यश्वैः क्रुद्धो राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।अवारयच्छरैस्तीक्ष्णैर्मत्तं द्विपमिवाङ्कुशैः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ pattyaśvaiḥ kruddho rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,avārayaccharaistīkṣṇairmattaṁ dvipamivāṅkuśaiḥ.
english: Enraged, King Yudhiṣṭhira checked [Śalya], who was advancing with infantry and cavalry, with sharp arrows, just as one would control a maddened elephant with goads.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 27. Enraged, King Yudhiṣṭhira checked [Śalya], who was advancing with infantry and cavalry, with sharp arrows, just as one would control a maddened elephant with goads.

sanskrit: तस्य शल्यः शरं घोरं मुमोचाशीविषोपमम् ।सोऽभ्यविध्यन्महात्मानं वेगेनाभ्यपतच्च गाम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasya śalyaḥ śaraṁ ghoraṁ mumocāśīviṣopamam ,so'bhyavidhyanmahātmānaṁ vegenābhyapatacca gām.
english: Śalya then shot a terrible arrow, which was like a venomous snake, at him (Yudhiṣṭhira). That [arrow] pierced the magnanimous (mahātman) one and swiftly fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 28. Śalya then shot a terrible arrow, which was like a venomous snake, at him (Yudhiṣṭhira). That [arrow] pierced the magnanimous (mahātman) one and swiftly fell to the ground.

sanskrit: ततो वृकोदरः क्रुद्धः शल्यं विव्याध सप्तभिः ।पञ्चभिः सहदेवस्तु नकुलो दशभिः शरैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato vṛkodaraḥ kruddhaḥ śalyaṁ vivyādha saptabhiḥ ,pañcabhiḥ sahadevastu nakulo daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, an enraged Vṛkodara (Bhīma) pierced Śalya with seven arrows. Sahadeva, in turn, struck him with five, and Nakula with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 29. Then, an enraged Vṛkodara (Bhīma) pierced Śalya with seven arrows. Sahadeva, in turn, struck him with five, and Nakula with ten arrows.

sanskrit: द्रौपदेयाश्च शत्रुघ्नं शूरमार्तायनिं शरैः ।अभ्यवर्षन्महाभागं मेघा इव महीधरम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: draupadeyāśca śatrughnaṁ śūramārtāyaniṁ śaraiḥ ,abhyavarṣanmahābhāgaṁ meghā iva mahīdharam.
english: And the sons of Draupadī, with their arrows, rained down upon the heroic, illustrious foe-slayer, the son of Artāyana (Śalya), just as clouds pour rain upon a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 30. And the sons of Draupadī, with their arrows, rained down upon the heroic, illustrious foe-slayer, the son of Artāyana (Śalya), just as clouds pour rain upon a mountain.

sanskrit: ततो दृष्ट्वा तुद्यमानं शल्यं पार्थैः समन्ततः ।कृतवर्मा कृपश्चैव संक्रुद्धावभ्यधावताम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tato dṛṣṭvā tudyamānaṁ śalyaṁ pārthaiḥ samantataḥ ,kṛtavarmā kṛpaścaiva saṁkruddhāvabhyadhāvatām.
english: Then, having seen Śalya being tormented from all sides by the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā), Kṛtavarmā and Kṛpa, both greatly enraged, rushed forward.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 31. Then, having seen Śalya being tormented from all sides by the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā), Kṛtavarmā and Kṛpa, both greatly enraged, rushed forward.

sanskrit: उलूकश्च पतत्री च शकुनिश्चापि सौबलः ।स्मयमानश्च शनकैरश्वत्थामा महारथः ।तव पुत्राश्च कार्त्स्न्येन जुगुपुः शल्यमाहवे ॥३२॥
transliteration: ulūkaśca patatrī ca śakuniścāpi saubalaḥ ,smayamānaśca śanakairaśvatthāmā mahārathaḥ ,tava putrāśca kārtsnyena jugupuḥ śalyamāhave.
english: And Ulūka, Patatrī, Śakuni (the son of Subala), and Aśvatthāmā, the great charioteer - who was smiling gently - along with your sons, completely protected Śalya in that battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 32. And Ulūka, Patatrī, Śakuni (the son of Subala), and Aśvatthāmā, the great charioteer - who was smiling gently - along with your sons, completely protected Śalya in that battle.

sanskrit: भीमसेनं त्रिभिर्विद्ध्वा कृतवर्मा शिलीमुखैः ।बाणवर्षेण महता क्रुद्धरूपमवारयत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaṁ tribhirviddhvā kṛtavarmā śilīmukhaiḥ ,bāṇavarṣeṇa mahatā kruddharūpamavārayat.
english: Kṛtavarmā, having struck Bhimasena with three arrows, then obstructed his wrathful advance with a great volley of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 33. Kṛtavarmā, having struck Bhimasena with three arrows, then obstructed his wrathful advance with a great volley of arrows.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नं कृपः क्रुद्धो बाणवर्षैरपीडयत् ।द्रौपदेयांश्च शकुनिर्यमौ च द्रौणिरभ्ययात् ॥३४॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ kṛpaḥ kruddho bāṇavarṣairapīḍayat ,draupadeyāṁśca śakuniryamau ca drauṇirabhyayāt.
english: Enraged Kṛpa harassed Dhṛṣṭadyumna with volleys of arrows. Śakuni attacked the sons of Draupadi, and Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) confronted the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 34. Enraged Kṛpa harassed Dhṛṣṭadyumna with volleys of arrows. Śakuni attacked the sons of Draupadi, and Droṇa's son (Drauṇi) confronted the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva).

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो युधां श्रेष्ठावाहवे केशवार्जुनौ ।समभ्ययादुग्रतेजाः शरैश्चाभ्यहनद्बली ॥३५॥
transliteration: duryodhano yudhāṁ śreṣṭhāvāhave keśavārjunau ,samabhyayādugratejāḥ śaraiścābhyahanadbalī.
english: Duryodhana, fierce in his valor and mighty, confronted Keśava (Krishna) and Arjuna, the foremost among warriors, in battle and struck them with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 35. Duryodhana, fierce in his valor and mighty, confronted Keśava (Krishna) and Arjuna, the foremost among warriors, in battle and struck them with arrows.

sanskrit: एवं द्वंद्वशतान्यासंस्त्वदीयानां परैः सह ।घोररूपाणि चित्राणि तत्र तत्र विशां पते ॥३६॥
transliteration: evaṁ dvaṁdvaśatānyāsaṁstvadīyānāṁ paraiḥ saha ,ghorarūpāṇi citrāṇi tatra tatra viśāṁ pate.
english: Thus, O lord of men, hundreds of dreadful and diverse duels occurred here and there between your warriors and the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 36. Thus, O lord of men, hundreds of dreadful and diverse duels occurred here and there between your warriors and the enemies.

sanskrit: ऋश्यवर्णाञ्जघानाश्वान्भोजो भीमस्य संयुगे ।सोऽवतीर्य रथोपस्थाद्धताश्वः पाण्डुनन्दनः ।कालो दण्डमिवोद्यम्य गदापाणिरयुध्यत ॥३७॥
transliteration: ṛśyavarṇāñjaghānāśvānbhojo bhīmasya saṁyuge ,so'vatīrya rathopasthāddhatāśvaḥ pāṇḍunandanaḥ ,kālo daṇḍamivodyamya gadāpāṇirayudhyata.
english: In battle, Bhoja (Kṛtavarman) killed Bhīma's deer-colored horses. With his horses slain, that son of Pāṇḍu (Bhīma) dismounted from his chariot's platform and fought, mace in hand, as if Death (kāla) itself had raised its club.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 37. In battle, Bhoja (Kṛtavarman) killed Bhīma's deer-colored horses. With his horses slain, that son of Pāṇḍu (Bhīma) dismounted from his chariot's platform and fought, mace in hand, as if Death (kāla) itself had raised its club.

sanskrit: प्रमुखे सहदेवस्य जघानाश्वांश्च मद्रराट् ।ततः शल्यस्य तनयं सहदेवोऽसिनावधीत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: pramukhe sahadevasya jaghānāśvāṁśca madrarāṭ ,tataḥ śalyasya tanayaṁ sahadevo'sināvadhīt.
english: The king of Madras (Śalya) killed Sahadeva's horses right in front of him. Then, Sahadeva, with his sword, slew Śalya's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 38. The king of Madras (Śalya) killed Sahadeva's horses right in front of him. Then, Sahadeva, with his sword, slew Śalya's son.

sanskrit: गौतमः पुनराचार्यो धृष्टद्युम्नमयोधयत् ।असंभ्रान्तमसंभ्रान्तो यत्नवान्यत्नवत्तरम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: gautamaḥ punarācāryo dhṛṣṭadyumnamayodhayat ,asaṁbhrāntamasaṁbhrānto yatnavānyatnavattaram.
english: The preceptor (ācārya) Gautama (Droṇa), in turn, fought Dhṛṣṭadyumna. Remaining composed, he fought the equally composed opponent; though diligent, he faced one who fought with even greater diligence.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 39. The preceptor (ācārya) Gautama (Droṇa), in turn, fought Dhṛṣṭadyumna. Remaining composed, he fought the equally composed opponent; though diligent, he faced one who fought with even greater diligence.

sanskrit: द्रौपदेयांस्तथा वीरानेकैकं दशभिः शरैः ।अविध्यदाचार्यसुतो नातिक्रुद्धः स्मयन्निव ॥४०॥
transliteration: draupadeyāṁstathā vīrānekaikaṁ daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,avidhyadācāryasuto nātikruddhaḥ smayanniva.
english: And the preceptor's son (Aśvatthāman), not exceedingly enraged, as if smiling, pierced each of those valiant sons of Draupadī with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 40. And the preceptor's son (Aśvatthāman), not exceedingly enraged, as if smiling, pierced each of those valiant sons of Draupadī with ten arrows.

sanskrit: शल्योऽपि राजन्संक्रुद्धो निघ्नन्सोमकपाण्डवान् ।पुनरेव शितैर्बाणैर्युधिष्ठिरमपीडयत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: śalyo'pi rājansaṁkruddho nighnansomakapāṇḍavān ,punareva śitairbāṇairyudhiṣṭhiramapīḍayat.
english: O king, Śalya, greatly enraged, while striking down the Somakas and Pāṇḍavas, again tormented Yudhiṣṭhira with his sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 41. O king, Śalya, greatly enraged, while striking down the Somakas and Pāṇḍavas, again tormented Yudhiṣṭhira with his sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य भीमो रणे क्रुद्धः संदष्टदशनच्छदः ।विनाशायाभिसंधाय गदामादत्त वीर्यवान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tasya bhīmo raṇe kruddhaḥ saṁdaṣṭadaśanacchadaḥ ,vināśāyābhisaṁdhāya gadāmādatta vīryavān.
english: Mighty Bhīma, enraged in battle and biting his lips, then took up his mace, intent on [Śalya's] destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 42. Mighty Bhīma, enraged in battle and biting his lips, then took up his mace, intent on [Śalya's] destruction.

sanskrit: यमदण्डप्रतीकाशां कालरात्रिमिवोद्यताम् ।गजवाजिमनुष्याणां प्राणान्तकरणीमपि ॥४३॥
transliteration: yamadaṇḍapratīkāśāṁ kālarātrimivodyatām ,gajavājimanuṣyāṇāṁ prāṇāntakaraṇīmapi.
english: It was like Yama's staff, or the arisen Night of Destruction (kālarātri), capable of ending the lives of elephants, horses, and men.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 43. It was like Yama's staff, or the arisen Night of Destruction (kālarātri), capable of ending the lives of elephants, horses, and men.

sanskrit: हेमपट्टपरिक्षिप्तामुल्कां प्रज्वलितामिव ।शैक्यां व्यालीमिवात्युग्रां वज्रकल्पामयस्मयीम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: hemapaṭṭaparikṣiptāmulkāṁ prajvalitāmiva ,śaikyāṁ vyālīmivātyugrāṁ vajrakalpāmayasmayīm.
english: Adorned with golden plates, it was like a blazing meteor (ulkā), or an exceedingly fierce Śaivya female serpent (vyālī); it resembled a thunderbolt (vajra) and was made of iron.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 44. Adorned with golden plates, it was like a blazing meteor (ulkā), or an exceedingly fierce Śaivya female serpent (vyālī); it resembled a thunderbolt (vajra) and was made of iron.

sanskrit: चन्दनागुरुपङ्काक्तां प्रमदामीप्सितामिव ।वसामेदोसृगादिग्धां जिह्वां वैवस्वतीमिव ॥४५॥
transliteration: candanāgurupaṅkāktāṁ pramadāmīpsitāmiva ,vasāmedosṛgādigdhāṁ jihvāṁ vaivasvatīmiva.
english: It was like a desired woman, anointed with a paste of sandalwood and aloewood, and like the tongue of Yama (Vaivasvata), smeared with fat, marrow, and blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 45. It was like a desired woman, anointed with a paste of sandalwood and aloewood, and like the tongue of Yama (Vaivasvata), smeared with fat, marrow, and blood.

sanskrit: पटुघण्टारवशतां वासवीमशनीमिव ।निर्मुक्ताशीविषाकारां पृक्तां गजमदैरपि ॥४६॥
transliteration: paṭughaṇṭāravaśatāṁ vāsavīmaśanīmiva ,nirmuktāśīviṣākārāṁ pṛktāṁ gajamadairapi.
english: It was like Indra's (Vāsava) thunderbolt, with the loud sound of hundreds of bells; possessing the appearance of a freed venomous snake and even smeared with elephant ichor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 46. It was like Indra's (Vāsava) thunderbolt, with the loud sound of hundreds of bells; possessing the appearance of a freed venomous snake and even smeared with elephant ichor.

sanskrit: त्रासनीं रिपुसैन्यानां स्वसैन्यपरिहर्षिणीम् ।मनुष्यलोके विख्यातां गिरिशृङ्गविदारिणीम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: trāsanīṁ ripusainyānāṁ svasainyapariharṣiṇīm ,manuṣyaloke vikhyātāṁ giriśṛṅgavidāriṇīm.
english: It was terrifying to enemy armies, yet delighting to one's own army; renowned in the human world and capable of splitting mountain peaks.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 47. It was terrifying to enemy armies, yet delighting to one's own army; renowned in the human world and capable of splitting mountain peaks.

sanskrit: यया कैलासभवने महेश्वरसखं बली ।आह्वयामास कौन्तेयः संक्रुद्धमलकाधिपम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: yayā kailāsabhavane maheśvarasakhaṁ balī ,āhvayāmāsa kaunteyaḥ saṁkruddhamalakādhipam.
english: By which (weapon), the powerful son of Kuntī (Arjuna) challenged the enraged lord of Alakā (Kubera), the friend of Maheśvara (Śiva), in the abode of Kailāsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 48. By which (weapon), the powerful son of Kuntī (Arjuna) challenged the enraged lord of Alakā (Kubera), the friend of Maheśvara (Śiva), in the abode of Kailāsa.

sanskrit: यया मायाविनो दृप्तान्सुबहून्धनदालये ।जघान गुह्यकान्क्रुद्धो मन्दारार्थे महाबलः ।निवार्यमाणो बहुभिर्द्रौपद्याः प्रियमास्थितः ॥४९॥
transliteration: yayā māyāvino dṛptānsubahūndhanadālaye ,jaghāna guhyakānkruddho mandārārthe mahābalaḥ ,nivāryamāṇo bahubhirdraupadyāḥ priyamāsthitaḥ.
english: With which (mace), the mighty one (mahābalaḥ), enraged (kruddhaḥ) and dedicated to Draupadi's (Draupadyāḥ) cherished desire (priyam āsthitaḥ), though restrained by many, killed very many arrogant and deceitful Guhyakas (guhyakān) in Kubera's abode (dhanadālaye) for the sake of the Mandāra tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 49. With which (mace), the mighty one (mahābalaḥ), enraged (kruddhaḥ) and dedicated to Draupadi's (Draupadyāḥ) cherished desire (priyam āsthitaḥ), though restrained by many, killed very many arrogant and deceitful Guhyakas (guhyakān) in Kubera's abode (dhanadālaye) for the sake of the Mandāra tree.

sanskrit: तां वज्रमणिरत्नौघामष्टाश्रिं वज्रगौरवाम् ।समुद्यम्य महाबाहुः शल्यमभ्यद्रवद्रणे ॥५०॥
transliteration: tāṁ vajramaṇiratnaughāmaṣṭāśriṁ vajragauravām ,samudyamya mahābāhuḥ śalyamabhyadravadraṇe.
english: The mighty-armed one (mahābāhuḥ), having lifted that mace – which was eight-edged (aṣṭāśrim), heavy like a diamond (vajragauravām), and had a multitude of diamonds, gems, and jewels (vajramaṇiratnaughām) – rushed towards Shalya (śalyam) in battle (raṇe).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 50. The mighty-armed one (mahābāhuḥ), having lifted that mace – which was eight-edged (aṣṭāśrim), heavy like a diamond (vajragauravām), and had a multitude of diamonds, gems, and jewels (vajramaṇiratnaughām) – rushed towards Shalya (śalyam) in battle (raṇe).

sanskrit: गदया युद्धकुशलस्तया दारुणनादया ।पोथयामास शल्यस्य चतुरोऽश्वान्महाजवान् ॥५१॥
transliteration: gadayā yuddhakuśalastayā dāruṇanādayā ,pothayāmāsa śalyasya caturo'śvānmahājavān.
english: Skilled in battle (yuddhakusalaḥ), he crushed Shalya's (śalyasya) four very swift (mahājavān) horses (aśvān) with that mace (tayā gadayā) of terrible sound (dāruṇanādayā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 51. Skilled in battle (yuddhakusalaḥ), he crushed Shalya's (śalyasya) four very swift (mahājavān) horses (aśvān) with that mace (tayā gadayā) of terrible sound (dāruṇanādayā).

sanskrit: ततः शल्यो रणे क्रुद्धः पीने वक्षसि तोमरम् ।निचखान नदन्वीरो वर्म भित्त्वा च सोऽभ्यगात् ॥५२॥
transliteration: tataḥ śalyo raṇe kruddhaḥ pīne vakṣasi tomaram ,nicakhāna nadanvīro varma bhittvā ca so'bhyagāt.
english: Then (tataḥ), the hero (vīraḥ) Shalya (śalyaḥ), enraged in battle (raṇe kruddhaḥ) and roaring (nadan), plunged a javelin (tomaram) into (Bhima's) broad chest (pīne vakṣasi). And it (the javelin), having pierced (bhittvā) the armor (varma), passed through (abhyagāt).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 52. Then (tataḥ), the hero (vīraḥ) Shalya (śalyaḥ), enraged in battle (raṇe kruddhaḥ) and roaring (nadan), plunged a javelin (tomaram) into (Bhima's) broad chest (pīne vakṣasi). And it (the javelin), having pierced (bhittvā) the armor (varma), passed through (abhyagāt).

sanskrit: वृकोदरस्त्वसंभ्रातस्तमेवोद्धृत्य तोमरम् ।यन्तारं मद्रराजस्य निर्बिभेद ततो हृदि ॥५३॥
transliteration: vṛkodarastvasaṁbhrātastamevoddhṛtya tomaram ,yantāraṁ madrarājasya nirbibheda tato hṛdi.
english: Bhima, completely undeterred, then picked up that very javelin and pierced the charioteer of the Madra king in the heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 53. Bhima, completely undeterred, then picked up that very javelin and pierced the charioteer of the Madra king in the heart.

sanskrit: स भिन्नवर्मा रुधिरं वमन्वित्रस्तमानसः ।पपाताभिमुखो दीनो मद्रराजस्त्वपाक्रमत् ॥५४॥
transliteration: sa bhinnavarmā rudhiraṁ vamanvitrastamānasaḥ ,papātābhimukho dīno madrarājastvapākramat.
english: With his armor shattered, spewing blood, and his mind terrified, he (the charioteer) fell miserably face down, while the Madra king retreated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 54. With his armor shattered, spewing blood, and his mind terrified, he (the charioteer) fell miserably face down, while the Madra king retreated.

sanskrit: कृतप्रतिकृतं दृष्ट्वा शल्यो विस्मितमानसः ।गदामाश्रित्य धीरात्मा प्रत्यमित्रमवैक्षत ॥५५॥
transliteration: kṛtapratikṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā śalyo vismitamānasaḥ ,gadāmāśritya dhīrātmā pratyamitramavaikṣata.
english: Having witnessed the deed and the counter-deed, Shalya, astonished in mind but firm in spirit (ātman), grasped his mace and observed his adversary.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 55. Having witnessed the deed and the counter-deed, Shalya, astonished in mind but firm in spirit (ātman), grasped his mace and observed his adversary.

sanskrit: ततः सुमनसः पार्था भीमसेनमपूजयन् ।तद्दृष्ट्वा कर्म संग्रामे घोरमक्लिष्टकर्मणः ॥५६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sumanasaḥ pārthā bhīmasenamapūjayan ,taddṛṣṭvā karma saṁgrāme ghoramakliṣṭakarmaṇaḥ.
english: Then, having seen that terrible deed of the untiring Bhimasena in battle, the joyful Pārthas honored him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 10 Verse 56. Then, having seen that terrible deed of the untiring Bhimasena in battle, the joyful Pārthas honored him.

sanskrit: प्रेक्षन्तः सर्वतस्तौ हि योधा योधमहाद्विपौ ।तावकाश्च परे चैव साधु साध्वित्यथाब्रुवन् ॥५॥
transliteration: prekṣantaḥ sarvatastau hi yodhā yodhamahādvipau ,tāvakāśca pare caiva sādhu sādhvityathābruvan.
english: From all sides, the warriors, seeing those two who were like great elephants among fighters, indeed, both your people and the others then exclaimed, 'Excellent! Excellent!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. From all sides, the warriors, seeing those two who were like great elephants among fighters, indeed, both your people and the others then exclaimed, 'Excellent! Excellent!'

sanskrit: न हि मद्राधिपादन्यो रामाद्वा यदुनन्दनात् ।सोढुमुत्सहते वेगं भीमसेनस्य संयुगे ॥६॥
transliteration: na hi madrādhipādanyo rāmādvā yadunandanāt ,soḍhumutsahate vegaṁ bhīmasenasya saṁyuge.
english: Indeed, no one other than the king of Madras (Madrarāja), or Rāma, or the delight of the Yadus (Balarāma), is able to endure Bhimasena's might in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. Indeed, no one other than the king of Madras (Madrarāja), or Rāma, or the delight of the Yadus (Balarāma), is able to endure Bhimasena's might in battle.

sanskrit: तथा मद्राधिपस्यापि गदावेगं महात्मनः ।सोढुमुत्सहते नान्यो योधो युधि वृकोदरात् ॥७॥
transliteration: tathā madrādhipasyāpi gadāvegaṁ mahātmanaḥ ,soḍhumutsahate nānyo yodho yudhi vṛkodarāt.
english: Similarly, in battle, no other warrior (yodha) except Vṛkodara (Bhīma) is able to endure the mace's might of the great-souled (mahātman) king of Madras (Madrarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. Similarly, in battle, no other warrior (yodha) except Vṛkodara (Bhīma) is able to endure the mace's might of the great-souled (mahātman) king of Madras (Madrarāja).

sanskrit: तौ वृषाविव नर्दन्तौ मण्डलानि विचेरतुः ।आवल्गितौ गदाहस्तौ मद्रराजवृकोदरौ ॥८॥
transliteration: tau vṛṣāviva nardantau maṇḍalāni viceratuḥ ,āvalgitau gadāhastau madrarājavṛkodarau.
english: Those two, Madrarāja and Vṛkodara (Bhīma), holding maces in their hands, roaring like two bulls, and leaping, moved about in circles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. Those two, Madrarāja and Vṛkodara (Bhīma), holding maces in their hands, roaring like two bulls, and leaping, moved about in circles.

sanskrit: मण्डलावर्तमार्गेषु गदाविहरणेषु च ।निर्विशेषमभूद्युद्धं तयोः पुरुषसिंहयोः ॥९॥
transliteration: maṇḍalāvartamārgeṣu gadāviharaṇeṣu ca ,nirviśeṣamabhūdyuddhaṁ tayoḥ puruṣasiṁhayoḥ.
english: In the paths of circular maneuvers and in the wielding of their maces, the battle between those two mighty warriors was indistinguishable.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. In the paths of circular maneuvers and in the wielding of their maces, the battle between those two mighty warriors was indistinguishable.

sanskrit: तप्तहेममयैः शुभ्रैर्बभूव भयवर्धनी ।अग्निज्वालैरिवाविद्धा पट्टैः शल्यस्य सा गदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: taptahemamayaiḥ śubhrairbabhūva bhayavardhanī ,agnijvālairivāviddhā paṭṭaiḥ śalyasya sā gadā.
english: That mace (gadā) of Shalya, adorned with brilliant, molten gold plates and appearing as if pierced by flames of fire, became a terrifying sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. That mace (gadā) of Shalya, adorned with brilliant, molten gold plates and appearing as if pierced by flames of fire, became a terrifying sight.

sanskrit: तथैव चरतो मार्गान्मण्डलेषु महात्मनः ।विद्युदभ्रप्रतीकाशा भीमस्य शुशुभे गदा ॥११॥
transliteration: tathaiva carato mārgānmaṇḍaleṣu mahātmanaḥ ,vidyudabhrapratīkāśā bhīmasya śuśubhe gadā.
english: Similarly, as the great warrior (mahātman) moved in circular paths, Bhima's mace (gadā) shone splendidly, resembling lightning and a cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. Similarly, as the great warrior (mahātman) moved in circular paths, Bhima's mace (gadā) shone splendidly, resembling lightning and a cloud.

sanskrit: ताडिता मद्रराजेन भीमस्य गदया गदा ।दीप्यमानेव वै राजन्ससृजे पावकार्चिषः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tāḍitā madrarājena bhīmasya gadayā gadā ,dīpyamāneva vai rājansasṛje pāvakārciṣaḥ.
english: O King, when Bhima's mace (gadā) was struck by the mace (gadayā) of the King of Madras, it truly emitted flames of fire (pāvakārciṣaḥ), as if shining brightly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. O King, when Bhima's mace (gadā) was struck by the mace (gadayā) of the King of Madras, it truly emitted flames of fire (pāvakārciṣaḥ), as if shining brightly.

sanskrit: विजये धृतसंकल्पाः समभित्यक्तजीविताः ।प्राविशंस्तावका राजन्हंसा इव महत्सरः ॥३७॥
transliteration: vijaye dhṛtasaṁkalpāḥ samabhityaktajīvitāḥ ,prāviśaṁstāvakā rājanhaṁsā iva mahatsaraḥ.
english: O King, your warriors, resolute for victory and having renounced their lives, entered a great lake like swans.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 37. O King, your warriors, resolute for victory and having renounced their lives, entered a great lake like swans.

sanskrit: ततो युद्धमभूद्घोरं परस्परवधैषिणाम् ।अन्योन्यवधसंयुक्तमन्योन्यप्रीतिवर्धनम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tato yuddhamabhūdghoraṁ parasparavadhaiṣiṇām ,anyonyavadhasaṁyuktamanyonyaprītivardhanam.
english: Then a dreadful battle ensued among those who desired to slay each other, a battle characterized by mutual destruction and increasing mutual delight in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 38. Then a dreadful battle ensued among those who desired to slay each other, a battle characterized by mutual destruction and increasing mutual delight in combat.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्प्रवृत्ते संग्रामे राजन्वीरवरक्षये ।अनिलेनेरितं घोरमुत्तस्थौ पार्थिवं रजः ॥३९॥
transliteration: tasminpravṛtte saṁgrāme rājanvīravarakṣaye ,anileneritaṁ ghoramuttasthau pārthivaṁ rajaḥ.
english: O King, as that battle, which involved the destruction of the foremost heroes, commenced, a dreadful cloud of earthly dust, stirred by the wind, rose up.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 39. O King, as that battle, which involved the destruction of the foremost heroes, commenced, a dreadful cloud of earthly dust, stirred by the wind, rose up.

sanskrit: श्रवणान्नामधेयानां पाण्डवानां च कीर्तनात् ।परस्परं विजानीमो ये चायुध्यन्नभीतवत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: śravaṇānnāmadheyānāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca kīrtanāt ,parasparaṁ vijānīmo ye cāyudhyannabhītavat.
english: By hearing the names, by the renown of the Pāṇḍavas, and by (observing) those who fought fearlessly, we distinguish among them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 40. By hearing the names, by the renown of the Pāṇḍavas, and by (observing) those who fought fearlessly, we distinguish among them.

sanskrit: तद्रजः पुरुषव्याघ्र शोणितेन प्रशामितम् ।दिशश्च विमला जज्ञुस्तस्मिन्रजसि शामिते ॥४१॥
transliteration: tadrajaḥ puruṣavyāghra śoṇitena praśāmitam ,diśaśca vimalā jajñustasminrajasi śāmite.
english: O tiger among men, that dust was completely settled by blood. When that dust had settled, the directions became clear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 41. O tiger among men, that dust was completely settled by blood. When that dust had settled, the directions became clear.

sanskrit: तथा प्रवृत्ते संग्रामे घोररूपे भयानके ।तावकानां परेषां च नासीत्कश्चित्पराङ्मुखः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tathā pravṛtte saṁgrāme ghorarūpe bhayānake ,tāvakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca nāsītkaścitparāṅmukhaḥ.
english: In that battle, which was thus dreadful and terrifying, no one, neither from your side nor from the enemy's side, turned away in retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 42. In that battle, which was thus dreadful and terrifying, no one, neither from your side nor from the enemy's side, turned away in retreat.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मलोकपरा भूत्वा प्रार्थयन्तो जयं युधि ।सुयुद्धेन पराक्रान्ता नराः स्वर्गमभीप्सवः ॥४३॥
transliteration: brahmalokaparā bhūtvā prārthayanto jayaṁ yudhi ,suyuddhena parākrāntā narāḥ svargamabhīpsavaḥ.
english: Those men, with their ultimate aim being the realm of Brahman (brahmaloka) and desiring victory in battle, fought valiantly with great skill, yearning for heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 43. Those men, with their ultimate aim being the realm of Brahman (brahmaloka) and desiring victory in battle, fought valiantly with great skill, yearning for heaven.

sanskrit: भर्तृपिण्डविमोक्षार्थं भर्तृकार्यविनिश्चिताः ।स्वर्गसंसक्तमनसो योधा युयुधिरे तदा ॥४४॥
transliteration: bhartṛpiṇḍavimokṣārthaṁ bhartṛkāryaviniścitāḥ ,svargasaṁsaktamanaso yodhā yuyudhire tadā.
english: At that time, the warriors, determined to fulfill their master's duty and be freed from the obligation of his sustenance, fought with their minds set on heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 44. At that time, the warriors, determined to fulfill their master's duty and be freed from the obligation of his sustenance, fought with their minds set on heaven.

sanskrit: नानारूपाणि शस्त्राणि विसृजन्तो महारथाः ।अन्योन्यमभिगर्जन्तः प्रहरन्तः परस्परम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: nānārūpāṇi śastrāṇi visṛjanto mahārathāḥ ,anyonyamabhigarjantaḥ praharantaḥ parasparam.
english: The great warriors (mahārathas), showering various kinds of weapons, roared at each other and struck one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 45. The great warriors (mahārathas), showering various kinds of weapons, roared at each other and struck one another.

sanskrit: हत विध्यत गृह्णीत प्रहरध्वं निकृन्तत ।इति स्म वाचः श्रूयन्ते तव तेषां च वै बले ॥४६॥
transliteration: hata vidhyata gṛhṇīta praharadhvaṁ nikṛntata ,iti sma vācaḥ śrūyante tava teṣāṁ ca vai bale.
english: 'Kill! Pierce! Seize! Strike! Cut down!' Such cries were indeed heard in your army and theirs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 46. 'Kill! Pierce! Seize! Strike! Cut down!' Such cries were indeed heard in your army and theirs.

sanskrit: ततः शल्यो महाराज धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।विव्याध निशितैर्बाणैर्हन्तुकामो महारथम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śalyo mahārāja dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,vivyādha niśitairbāṇairhantukāmo mahāratham.
english: Then, O great king, Shalya, desirous of killing the great warrior (mahāratha), Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), pierced him with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 47. Then, O great king, Shalya, desirous of killing the great warrior (mahāratha), Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), pierced him with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य पार्थो महाराज नाराचान्वै चतुर्दश ।मर्माण्युद्दिश्य मर्मज्ञो निचखान हसन्निव ॥४८॥
transliteration: tasya pārtho mahārāja nārācānvai caturdaśa ,marmāṇyuddiśya marmajño nicakhāna hasanniva.
english: O great king, Partha (Arjuna), an expert in vital spots, aimed at Shalya's vital spots and indeed embedded fourteen iron arrows into him, as if laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 48. O great king, Partha (Arjuna), an expert in vital spots, aimed at Shalya's vital spots and indeed embedded fourteen iron arrows into him, as if laughing.

sanskrit: तं वार्य पाण्डवं बाणैर्हन्तुकामो महायशाः ।विव्याध समरे क्रुद्धो बहुभिः कङ्कपत्रिभिः ॥४९॥
transliteration: taṁ vārya pāṇḍavaṁ bāṇairhantukāmo mahāyaśāḥ ,vivyādha samare kruddho bahubhiḥ kaṅkapatribhiḥ.
english: The greatly renowned [Śalya], enraged and intent on killing the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava) [Yudhiṣṭhira], first checked him with arrows, and then pierced him in battle with many arrows feathered with vulture (kaṅka) feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 49. The greatly renowned [Śalya], enraged and intent on killing the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍava) [Yudhiṣṭhira], first checked him with arrows, and then pierced him in battle with many arrows feathered with vulture (kaṅka) feathers.

sanskrit: अथ भूयो महाराज शरेण नतपर्वणा ।युधिष्ठिरं समाजघ्ने सर्वसैन्यस्य पश्यतः ॥५०॥
transliteration: atha bhūyo mahārāja śareṇa nataparvaṇā ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ samājaghne sarvasainyasya paśyataḥ.
english: Then again, O great king, [Śalya] struck Yudhiṣṭhira with a straight-flying (nataparvaṇā) arrow, as the entire army (sarvasainya) watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 50. Then again, O great king, [Śalya] struck Yudhiṣṭhira with a straight-flying (nataparvaṇā) arrow, as the entire army (sarvasainya) watched.

sanskrit: धर्मराजोऽपि संक्रुद्धो मद्रराजं महायशाः ।विव्याध निशितैर्बाणैः कङ्कबर्हिणवाजितैः ॥५१॥
transliteration: dharmarājo'pi saṁkruddho madrarājaṁ mahāyaśāḥ ,vivyādha niśitairbāṇaiḥ kaṅkabarhiṇavājitaiḥ.
english: The greatly renowned (mahāyaśāḥ) King of (dharma) (dharmarāja) [Yudhiṣṭhira], also very enraged, pierced the King of Madra (madrarāja) [Śalya] with sharp arrows that were feathered with both vulture (kaṅka) and peacock (barhiṇa) feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 51. The greatly renowned (mahāyaśāḥ) King of (dharma) (dharmarāja) [Yudhiṣṭhira], also very enraged, pierced the King of Madra (madrarāja) [Śalya] with sharp arrows that were feathered with both vulture (kaṅka) and peacock (barhiṇa) feathers.

sanskrit: चन्द्रसेनं च सप्तत्या सूतं च नवभिः शरैः ।द्रुमसेनं चतुःषष्ट्या निजघान महारथः ॥५२॥
transliteration: candrasenaṁ ca saptatyā sūtaṁ ca navabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,drumasenaṁ catuḥṣaṣṭyā nijaghāna mahārathaḥ.
english: The great charioteer (mahāratha) [Yudhiṣṭhira] struck Candrasena with seventy [arrows], his charioteer (sūta) with nine arrows, and Drumasena with sixty-four [arrows].
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 52. The great charioteer (mahāratha) [Yudhiṣṭhira] struck Candrasena with seventy [arrows], his charioteer (sūta) with nine arrows, and Drumasena with sixty-four [arrows].

sanskrit: चक्ररक्षे हते शल्यः पाण्डवेन महात्मना ।निजघान ततो राजंश्चेदीन्वै पञ्चविंशतिम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: cakrarakṣe hate śalyaḥ pāṇḍavena mahātmanā ,nijaghāna tato rājaṁścedīnvai pañcaviṁśatim.
english: O king, when the wheel-protectors were killed by the great-souled Pandava, Shalya then slew twenty-five Chedis.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 53. O king, when the wheel-protectors were killed by the great-souled Pandava, Shalya then slew twenty-five Chedis.

sanskrit: सात्यकिं पञ्चविंशत्या भीमसेनं च पञ्चभिः ।माद्रीपुत्रौ शतेनाजौ विव्याध निशितैः शरैः ॥५४॥
transliteration: sātyakiṁ pañcaviṁśatyā bhīmasenaṁ ca pañcabhiḥ ,mādrīputrau śatenājau vivyādha niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: In battle, he (Shalya) pierced Satyaki with twenty-five, Bhimasena with five, and the two sons of Madri with a hundred sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 54. In battle, he (Shalya) pierced Satyaki with twenty-five, Bhimasena with five, and the two sons of Madri with a hundred sharp arrows.

sanskrit: एवं विचरतस्तस्य संग्रामे राजसत्तम ।संप्रेषयच्छितान्पार्थः शरानाशीविषोपमान् ॥५५॥
transliteration: evaṁ vicaratastasya saṁgrāme rājasattama ,saṁpreṣayacchitānpārthaḥ śarānāśīviṣopamān.
english: O best of kings, as he (Shalya) was thus moving about in battle, Partha (Arjuna) dispatched sharp arrows resembling venomous serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 55. O best of kings, as he (Shalya) was thus moving about in battle, Partha (Arjuna) dispatched sharp arrows resembling venomous serpents.

sanskrit: ध्वजाग्रं चास्य समरे कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।प्रमुखे वर्तमानस्य भल्लेनापहरद्रथात् ॥५६॥
transliteration: dhvajāgraṁ cāsya samare kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,pramukhe vartamānasya bhallenāpaharadrathāt.
english: And Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, in battle, with a crescent-shaped arrow (bhalla), cut off the top of his (Shalya's) banner from the chariot, while he (Shalya) was standing in front.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 56. And Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, in battle, with a crescent-shaped arrow (bhalla), cut off the top of his (Shalya's) banner from the chariot, while he (Shalya) was standing in front.

sanskrit: पाण्डुपुत्रेण वै तस्य केतुं छिन्नं महात्मना ।निपतन्तमपश्याम गिरिशृङ्गमिवाहतम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: pāṇḍuputreṇa vai tasya ketuṁ chinnaṁ mahātmanā ,nipatantamapaśyāma giriśṛṅgamivāhatam.
english: Indeed, we saw his banner, cut down by the great-souled son of Pāṇḍu, falling like a shattered mountain peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 57. Indeed, we saw his banner, cut down by the great-souled son of Pāṇḍu, falling like a shattered mountain peak.

sanskrit: ध्वजं निपतितं दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवं च व्यवस्थितम् ।संक्रुद्धो मद्रराजोऽभूच्छरवर्षं मुमोच ह ॥५८॥
transliteration: dhvajaṁ nipatitaṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavaṁ ca vyavasthitam ,saṁkruddho madrarājo'bhūccharavarṣaṁ mumoca ha.
english: Seeing his banner fallen and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) standing firm, the king of Madras (Śalya) became greatly enraged and indeed released a shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 58. Seeing his banner fallen and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) standing firm, the king of Madras (Śalya) became greatly enraged and indeed released a shower of arrows.

sanskrit: शल्यः सायकवर्षेण पर्जन्य इव वृष्टिमान् ।अभ्यवर्षदमेयात्मा क्षत्रियं क्षत्रियर्षभः ॥५९॥
transliteration: śalyaḥ sāyakavarṣeṇa parjanya iva vṛṣṭimān ,abhyavarṣadameyātmā kṣatriyaṁ kṣatriyarṣabhaḥ.
english: Śalya, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable and who was a bull among warriors (kṣatriyas), showered upon the warrior (Arjuna) with a volley of arrows, just as a rain-cloud (parjanya) showers down rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 59. Śalya, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable and who was a bull among warriors (kṣatriyas), showered upon the warrior (Arjuna) with a volley of arrows, just as a rain-cloud (parjanya) showers down rain.

sanskrit: सात्यकिं भीमसेनं च माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।एकैकं पञ्चभिर्विद्ध्वा युधिष्ठिरमपीडयत् ॥६०॥
transliteration: sātyakiṁ bhīmasenaṁ ca mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,ekaikaṁ pañcabhirviddhvā yudhiṣṭhiramapīḍayat.
english: Having pierced Satyaki, Bhīmasena, and the two Pāṇḍavas who were sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), each with five arrows, he then afflicted Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 60. Having pierced Satyaki, Bhīmasena, and the two Pāṇḍavas who were sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), each with five arrows, he then afflicted Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: ततो बाणमयं जालं विततं पाण्डवोरसि ।अपश्याम महाराज मेघजालमिवोद्गतम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: tato bāṇamayaṁ jālaṁ vitataṁ pāṇḍavorasi ,apaśyāma mahārāja meghajālamivodgatam.
english: Then, O great king, we saw a net of arrows, spread out upon the Pāṇḍava's chest, just like a rising multitude of clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 61. Then, O great king, we saw a net of arrows, spread out upon the Pāṇḍava's chest, just like a rising multitude of clouds.

sanskrit: तस्य शल्यो रणे क्रुद्धो बाणैः संनतपर्वभिः ।दिशः प्रच्छादयामास प्रदिशश्च महारथः ॥६२॥
transliteration: tasya śalyo raṇe kruddho bāṇaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,diśaḥ pracchādayāmāsa pradiśaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Enraged in battle, that great warrior Śalya covered all the directions and intermediate directions with his well-jointed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 62. Enraged in battle, that great warrior Śalya covered all the directions and intermediate directions with his well-jointed arrows.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा बाणजालेन पीडितः ।बभूव हृतविक्रान्तो जम्भो वृत्रहणा यथा ॥६३॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā bāṇajālena pīḍitaḥ ,babhūva hṛtavikrānto jambho vṛtrahaṇā yathā.
english: Then, King Yudhiṣṭhira, afflicted by the net of arrows, became deprived of his valor, just as Jambha was by Vṛtrahan (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 11 Verse 63. Then, King Yudhiṣṭhira, afflicted by the net of arrows, became deprived of his valor, just as Jambha was by Vṛtrahan (Indra).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।पीडिते धर्मराजे तु मद्रराजेन मारिष ।सात्यकिर्भीमसेनश्च माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।परिवार्य रथैः शल्यं पीडयामासुराहवे ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,pīḍite dharmarāje tu madrarājena māriṣa ,sātyakirbhīmasenaśca mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,parivārya rathaiḥ śalyaṁ pīḍayāmāsurāhave.
english: Saṃjaya said: "But, O venerable one, when the king of justice (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, was tormented by the king of Madras (Śalya), Sātyaki, Bhīmasena, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), surrounded Śalya with their chariots and tormented him in battle."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: "But, O venerable one, when the king of justice (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira, was tormented by the king of Madras (Śalya), Sātyaki, Bhīmasena, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), surrounded Śalya with their chariots and tormented him in battle."

sanskrit: तमेकं बहुभिर्दृष्ट्वा पीड्यमानं महारथैः ।साधुवादो महाञ्जज्ञे सिद्धाश्चासन्प्रहर्षिताः ।आश्चर्यमित्यभाषन्त मुनयश्चापि संगताः ॥२॥
transliteration: tamekaṁ bahubhirdṛṣṭvā pīḍyamānaṁ mahārathaiḥ ,sādhuvādo mahāñjajñe siddhāścāsanpraharṣitāḥ ,āścaryamityabhāṣanta munayaścāpi saṁgatāḥ.
english: When they saw that one (warrior) being pressed hard by many great charioteers, a mighty cheer (sādhuvāda) arose, and the perfected beings (siddhas) became greatly delighted. And the assembled sages also exclaimed, "This is astonishing!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. When they saw that one (warrior) being pressed hard by many great charioteers, a mighty cheer (sādhuvāda) arose, and the perfected beings (siddhas) became greatly delighted. And the assembled sages also exclaimed, "This is astonishing!"

sanskrit: भीमसेनो रणे शल्यं शल्यभूतं पराक्रमे ।एकेन विद्ध्वा बाणेन पुनर्विव्याध सप्तभिः ॥३॥
transliteration: bhīmaseno raṇe śalyaṁ śalyabhūtaṁ parākrame ,ekena viddhvā bāṇena punarvivyādha saptabhiḥ.
english: Bhīmasena, in battle, first pierced Shalya - who was a veritable thorn (śalya) to their prowess - with one arrow, and then again pierced him with seven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. Bhīmasena, in battle, first pierced Shalya - who was a veritable thorn (śalya) to their prowess - with one arrow, and then again pierced him with seven.

sanskrit: सात्यकिश्च शतेनैनं धर्मपुत्रपरीप्सया ।मद्रेश्वरमवाकीर्य सिंहनादमथानदत् ॥४॥
transliteration: sātyakiśca śatenainaṁ dharmaputraparīpsayā ,madreśvaramavākīrya siṁhanādamathānadat.
english: And Sātyaki, desiring to protect the son of (dharma) (Dharmaputra Yudhiṣṭhira), showered that king of Madra with a hundred (arrows), and then let out a lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. And Sātyaki, desiring to protect the son of (dharma) (Dharmaputra Yudhiṣṭhira), showered that king of Madra with a hundred (arrows), and then let out a lion's roar.

sanskrit: नकुलः पञ्चभिश्चैनं सहदेवश्च सप्तभिः ।विद्ध्वा तं तु ततस्तूर्णं पुनर्विव्याध सप्तभिः ॥५॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ pañcabhiścainaṁ sahadevaśca saptabhiḥ ,viddhvā taṁ tu tatastūrṇaṁ punarvivyādha saptabhiḥ.
english: Nakula pierced him with five (arrows), and Sahadeva pierced him with seven. Having struck him, they then swiftly pierced him again with seven (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. Nakula pierced him with five (arrows), and Sahadeva pierced him with seven. Having struck him, they then swiftly pierced him again with seven (arrows).

sanskrit: स तु शूरो रणे यत्तः पीडितस्तैर्महारथैः ।विकृष्य कार्मुकं घोरं वेगघ्नं भारसाधनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sa tu śūro raṇe yattaḥ pīḍitastairmahārathaiḥ ,vikṛṣya kārmukaṁ ghoraṁ vegaghnaṁ bhārasādhanam.
english: But he, the valiant one, though engaged in battle and afflicted by those great charioteers (mahārathas), drew his terrible bow, which checks the speed (of attacks) and accomplishes mighty deeds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. But he, the valiant one, though engaged in battle and afflicted by those great charioteers (mahārathas), drew his terrible bow, which checks the speed (of attacks) and accomplishes mighty deeds.

sanskrit: सात्यकिं पञ्चविंशत्या शल्यो विव्याध मारिष ।भीमसेनं त्रिसप्तत्या नकुलं सप्तभिस्तथा ॥७॥
transliteration: sātyakiṁ pañcaviṁśatyā śalyo vivyādha māriṣa ,bhīmasenaṁ trisaptatyā nakulaṁ saptabhistathā.
english: O respected one, Shalya pierced Satyaki with twenty-five (arrows), Bhimasena with seventy-three (arrows), and similarly, Nakula with seven (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. O respected one, Shalya pierced Satyaki with twenty-five (arrows), Bhimasena with seventy-three (arrows), and similarly, Nakula with seven (arrows).

sanskrit: ततः सविशिखं चापं सहदेवस्य धन्विनः ।छित्त्वा भल्लेन समरे विव्याधैनं त्रिसप्तभिः ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ saviśikhaṁ cāpaṁ sahadevasya dhanvinaḥ ,chittvā bhallena samare vivyādhainaṁ trisaptabhiḥ.
english: Then, in battle, having cut the arrow-equipped bow of the archer Sahadeva with a spear, he (Shalya) pierced him (Sahadeva) with seventy-three (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. Then, in battle, having cut the arrow-equipped bow of the archer Sahadeva with a spear, he (Shalya) pierced him (Sahadeva) with seventy-three (arrows).

sanskrit: सहदेवस्तु समरे मातुलं भूरिवर्चसम् ।सज्यमन्यद्धनुः कृत्वा पञ्चभिः समताडयत् ।शरैराशीविषाकारैर्ज्वलज्ज्वलनसंनिभैः ॥९॥
transliteration: sahadevastu samare mātulaṁ bhūrivarcasam ,sajyamanyaddhanuḥ kṛtvā pañcabhiḥ samatāḍayat ,śarairāśīviṣākārairjvalajjvalanasaṁnibhaiḥ.
