english: But Sahadeva, in battle, having taken up another strung bow, struck his highly effulgent maternal uncle (mātula) with five arrows that resembled venomous snakes and blazing fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. But Sahadeva, in battle, having taken up another strung bow, struck his highly effulgent maternal uncle (mātula) with five arrows that resembled venomous snakes and blazing fire.

sanskrit: सारथिं चास्य समरे शरेणानतपर्वणा ।विव्याध भृशसंक्रुद्धस्तं च भूयस्त्रिभिः शरैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sārathiṁ cāsya samare śareṇānataparvaṇā ,vivyādha bhṛśasaṁkruddhastaṁ ca bhūyastribhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: And, greatly enraged, he pierced his charioteer in battle with an arrow having a bent shaft. He then struck him again with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. And, greatly enraged, he pierced his charioteer in battle with an arrow having a bent shaft. He then struck him again with three arrows.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्त्रिसप्तत्या सात्यकिर्नवभिः शरैः ।धर्मराजस्तथा षष्ट्या गात्रे शल्यं समर्पयत् ॥११॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastrisaptatyā sātyakirnavabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,dharmarājastathā ṣaṣṭyā gātre śalyaṁ samarpayat.
english: Bhīmasena, with seventy-three arrows, Sātyaki with nine arrows, and Dharmarāja (dharma king) similarly with sixty arrows, struck arrows into his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. Bhīmasena, with seventy-three arrows, Sātyaki with nine arrows, and Dharmarāja (dharma king) similarly with sixty arrows, struck arrows into his body.

sanskrit: ततः शल्यो महाराज निर्विद्धस्तैर्महारथैः ।सुस्राव रुधिरं गात्रैर्गैरिकं पर्वतो यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ śalyo mahārāja nirviddhastairmahārathaiḥ ,susrāva rudhiraṁ gātrairgairikaṁ parvato yathā.
english: Then, O great king, Shalya, having been pierced by those great warriors (mahārathas), poured forth blood from his limbs, just as a mountain pours forth red ochre.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. Then, O great king, Shalya, having been pierced by those great warriors (mahārathas), poured forth blood from his limbs, just as a mountain pours forth red ochre.

sanskrit: तांश्च सर्वान्महेष्वासान्पञ्चभिः पञ्चभिः शरैः ।विव्याध तरसा राजंस्तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tāṁśca sarvānmaheṣvāsānpañcabhiḥ pañcabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,vivyādha tarasā rājaṁstadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: And, O King, he powerfully struck all those great archers (maheṣvāsas) with five arrows each. That seemed marvelous.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. And, O King, he powerfully struck all those great archers (maheṣvāsas) with five arrows each. That seemed marvelous.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरेण भल्लेन धर्मपुत्रस्य मारिष ।धनुश्चिच्छेद समरे सज्यं स सुमहारथः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato'pareṇa bhallena dharmaputrasya māriṣa ,dhanuściccheda samare sajyaṁ sa sumahārathaḥ.
english: Then, O venerable one, that mighty charioteer (sumahārathaḥ) shattered the strung bow (dhanuḥ) of the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra) with another arrow in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. Then, O venerable one, that mighty charioteer (sumahārathaḥ) shattered the strung bow (dhanuḥ) of the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra) with another arrow in battle.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय धर्मपुत्रो महारथः ।साश्वसूतध्वजरथं शल्यं प्राच्छादयच्छरैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya dharmaputro mahārathaḥ ,sāśvasūtadhvajarathaṁ śalyaṁ prācchādayaccharaiḥ.
english: Then the great charioteer, the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra), taking another bow, covered Śalya - who was with his horses, charioteer, and banner-adorned chariot - with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 15. Then the great charioteer, the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra), taking another bow, covered Śalya - who was with his horses, charioteer, and banner-adorned chariot - with arrows.

sanskrit: स च्छाद्यमानः समरे धर्मपुत्रस्य सायकैः ।युधिष्ठिरमथाविध्यद्दशभिर्निशितैः शरैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa cchādyamānaḥ samare dharmaputrasya sāyakaiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiramathāvidhyaddaśabhirniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: While being covered in battle by the arrows of the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra), Śalya then struck Yudhiṣṭhira with ten sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 16. While being covered in battle by the arrows of the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra), Śalya then struck Yudhiṣṭhira with ten sharp arrows.

sanskrit: सात्यकिस्तु ततः क्रुद्धो धर्मपुत्रे शरार्दिते ।मद्राणामधिपं शूरं शरौघैः समवारयत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sātyakistu tataḥ kruddho dharmaputre śarārdite ,madrāṇāmadhipaṁ śūraṁ śaraughaiḥ samavārayat.
english: But then, Sātyaki, enraged when the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra) was struck by arrows, checked the valiant lord of the Madras (Śalya) with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 17. But then, Sātyaki, enraged when the son of Dharma (Dharmaputra) was struck by arrows, checked the valiant lord of the Madras (Śalya) with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: स सात्यकेः प्रचिच्छेद क्षुरप्रेण महद्धनुः ।भीमसेनमुखांस्तांश्च त्रिभिस्त्रिभिरताडयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa sātyakeḥ praciccheda kṣurapreṇa mahaddhanuḥ ,bhīmasenamukhāṁstāṁśca tribhistribhiratāḍayat.
english: He completely cut off Satyaki's great bow with a razor-sharp arrow, and he struck those led by Bhimasena, hitting each of them with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 18. He completely cut off Satyaki's great bow with a razor-sharp arrow, and he struck those led by Bhimasena, hitting each of them with three arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य क्रुद्धो महाराज सात्यकिः सत्यविक्रमः ।तोमरं प्रेषयामास स्वर्णदण्डं महाधनम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasya kruddho mahārāja sātyakiḥ satyavikramaḥ ,tomaraṁ preṣayāmāsa svarṇadaṇḍaṁ mahādhanam.
english: O great king, Satyaki, whose prowess was true and who was enraged at him, hurled a very valuable javelin (tomara) with a golden shaft.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 19. O great king, Satyaki, whose prowess was true and who was enraged at him, hurled a very valuable javelin (tomara) with a golden shaft.

sanskrit: भीमसेनोऽथ नाराचं ज्वलन्तमिव पन्नगम् ।नकुलः समरे शक्तिं सहदेवो गदां शुभाम् ।धर्मराजः शतघ्नीं तु जिघांसुः शल्यमाहवे ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhīmaseno'tha nārācaṁ jvalantamiva pannagam ,nakulaḥ samare śaktiṁ sahadevo gadāṁ śubhām ,dharmarājaḥ śataghnīṁ tu jighāṁsuḥ śalyamāhave.
english: Then Bhimasena hurled an iron arrow (nārāca) that was like a burning snake. Nakula, in battle, hurled a spear (śakti). Sahadeva hurled an auspicious mace (gadā). And Dharmaraja (king of (dharma)) Yudhishthira, desiring to slay Salya in battle, hurled a shataghni weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 20. Then Bhimasena hurled an iron arrow (nārāca) that was like a burning snake. Nakula, in battle, hurled a spear (śakti). Sahadeva hurled an auspicious mace (gadā). And Dharmaraja (king of (dharma)) Yudhishthira, desiring to slay Salya in battle, hurled a shataghni weapon.

sanskrit: तानापतत एवाशु पञ्चानां वै भुजच्युतान् ।सात्यकिप्रहितं शल्यो भल्लैश्चिच्छेद तोमरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tānāpatata evāśu pañcānāṁ vai bhujacyutān ,sātyakiprahitaṁ śalyo bhallaiściccheda tomaram.
english: Indeed, Salya swiftly cut down with his arrows those weapons, which were released from the arms of the five (Pandavas) and were coming at him. He also cut down the javelin (tomara) hurled by Satyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 21. Indeed, Salya swiftly cut down with his arrows those weapons, which were released from the arms of the five (Pandavas) and were coming at him. He also cut down the javelin (tomara) hurled by Satyaki.

sanskrit: भीमेन प्रहितं चापि शरं कनकभूषणम् ।द्विधा चिच्छेद समरे कृतहस्तः प्रतापवान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: bhīmena prahitaṁ cāpi śaraṁ kanakabhūṣaṇam ,dvidhā ciccheda samare kṛtahastaḥ pratāpavān.
english: The mighty and skilled warrior cut into two pieces even the gold-adorned arrow sent by Bhīma in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 22. The mighty and skilled warrior cut into two pieces even the gold-adorned arrow sent by Bhīma in battle.

sanskrit: नकुलप्रेषितां शक्तिं हेमदण्डां भयावहाम् ।गदां च सहदेवेन शरौघैः समवारयत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: nakulapreṣitāṁ śaktiṁ hemadaṇḍāṁ bhayāvahām ,gadāṁ ca sahadevena śaraughaiḥ samavārayat.
english: He warded off with volleys of arrows both the terrifying, golden-staffed spear sent by Nakula and the mace (sent) by Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 23. He warded off with volleys of arrows both the terrifying, golden-staffed spear sent by Nakula and the mace (sent) by Sahadeva.

sanskrit: शराभ्यां च शतघ्नीं तां राज्ञश्चिच्छेद भारत ।पश्यतां पाण्डुपुत्राणां सिंहनादं ननाद च ।नामृष्यत्तं तु शैनेयः शत्रोर्विजयमाहवे ॥२४॥
transliteration: śarābhyāṁ ca śataghnīṁ tāṁ rājñaściccheda bhārata ,paśyatāṁ pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ siṁhanādaṁ nanāda ca ,nāmṛṣyattaṁ tu śaineyaḥ śatrorvijayamāhave.
english: O Bhārata, he cut that śataghnī of the king with two arrows and roared a lion's roar as the Pāṇḍava brothers watched. However, Śaineya (Sātyaki) could not tolerate the enemy's victory in that battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 24. O Bhārata, he cut that śataghnī of the king with two arrows and roared a lion's roar as the Pāṇḍava brothers watched. However, Śaineya (Sātyaki) could not tolerate the enemy's victory in that battle.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय सात्यकिः क्रोधमूर्छितः ।द्वाभ्यां मद्रेश्वरं विद्ध्वा सारथिं च त्रिभिः शरैः ॥२५॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya sātyakiḥ krodhamūrchitaḥ ,dvābhyāṁ madreśvaraṁ viddhvā sārathiṁ ca tribhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then Sātyaki, enraged, took up another bow, piercing the lord of Madras with two arrows and his charioteer with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 25. Then Sātyaki, enraged, took up another bow, piercing the lord of Madras with two arrows and his charioteer with three arrows.

sanskrit: ततः शल्यो महाराज सर्वांस्तान्दशभिः शरैः ।विव्याध सुभृशं क्रुद्धस्तोत्त्रैरिव महाद्विपान् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ śalyo mahārāja sarvāṁstāndaśabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,vivyādha subhṛśaṁ kruddhastottrairiva mahādvipān.
english: Then, O great king, Śalya, greatly enraged, pierced all those warriors exceedingly with ten arrows, just as one would pierce mighty elephants with goads.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 26. Then, O great king, Śalya, greatly enraged, pierced all those warriors exceedingly with ten arrows, just as one would pierce mighty elephants with goads.

sanskrit: ते वार्यमाणाः समरे मद्रराज्ञा महारथाः ।न शेकुः प्रमुखे स्थातुं तस्य शत्रुनिषूदनाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: te vāryamāṇāḥ samare madrarājñā mahārathāḥ ,na śekuḥ pramukhe sthātuṁ tasya śatruniṣūdanāḥ.
english: Though those great chariot-warriors, who were destroyers of enemies, were being repelled in battle by the king of Madra (Śalya), they were unable to stand before him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 27. Though those great chariot-warriors, who were destroyers of enemies, were being repelled in battle by the king of Madra (Śalya), they were unable to stand before him.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा दृष्ट्वा शल्यस्य विक्रमम् ।निहतान्पाण्डवान्मेने पाञ्चालानथ सृञ्जयान् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā dṛṣṭvā śalyasya vikramam ,nihatānpāṇḍavānmene pāñcālānatha sṛñjayān.
english: Then King Duryodhana, upon witnessing Śalya's valor, believed the Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas and Sṛñjayas, to be as good as dead.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 28. Then King Duryodhana, upon witnessing Śalya's valor, believed the Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas and Sṛñjayas, to be as good as dead.

sanskrit: ततो राजन्महाबाहुर्भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ।संत्यज्य मनसा प्राणान्मद्राधिपमयोधयत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato rājanmahābāhurbhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān ,saṁtyajya manasā prāṇānmadrādhipamayodhayat.
english: Then, O King, the mighty-armed and glorious Bhīmasena, mentally renouncing his life, fought the lord of Madra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 29. Then, O King, the mighty-armed and glorious Bhīmasena, mentally renouncing his life, fought the lord of Madra.

sanskrit: नकुलः सहदेवश्च सात्यकिश्च महारथः ।परिवार्य तदा शल्यं समन्ताद्व्यकिरञ्शरैः ॥३०॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ sahadevaśca sātyakiśca mahārathaḥ ,parivārya tadā śalyaṁ samantādvyakirañśaraiḥ.
english: Nakula, Sahadeva, and Satyaki, the great chariot-fighters, then surrounded Shalya and showered him with arrows from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 30. Nakula, Sahadeva, and Satyaki, the great chariot-fighters, then surrounded Shalya and showered him with arrows from all directions.

sanskrit: स चतुर्भिर्महेष्वासैः पाण्डवानां महारथैः ।वृतस्तान्योधयामास मद्रराजः प्रतापवान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa caturbhirmaheṣvāsaiḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārathaiḥ ,vṛtastānyodhayāmāsa madrarājaḥ pratāpavān.
english: Surrounded by the four great archers and mighty chariot-fighters of the Pandavas, that powerful king of Madras (Shalya) fought against them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 31. Surrounded by the four great archers and mighty chariot-fighters of the Pandavas, that powerful king of Madras (Shalya) fought against them.

sanskrit: तस्य धर्मसुतो राजन्क्षुरप्रेण महाहवे ।चक्ररक्षं जघानाशु मद्रराजस्य पार्थिव ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasya dharmasuto rājankṣurapreṇa mahāhave ,cakrarakṣaṁ jaghānāśu madrarājasya pārthiva.
english: O King, in that great battle, the son of Dharma (Yudhishthira) swiftly struck down the chariot-wheel protector of the king of Madras (Shalya) with a razor-sharp arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 32. O King, in that great battle, the son of Dharma (Yudhishthira) swiftly struck down the chariot-wheel protector of the king of Madras (Shalya) with a razor-sharp arrow.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु निहते शूरे चक्ररक्षे महारथे ।मद्रराजोऽतिबलवान्सैनिकानास्तृणोच्छरैः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu nihate śūre cakrarakṣe mahārathe ,madrarājo'tibalavānsainikānāstṛṇoccharaiḥ.
english: But when that brave, great chariot-warrior who was the chariot-wheel protector was killed, the exceedingly powerful king of Madras (Shalya) overwhelmed the enemy soldiers with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 33. But when that brave, great chariot-warrior who was the chariot-wheel protector was killed, the exceedingly powerful king of Madras (Shalya) overwhelmed the enemy soldiers with arrows.

sanskrit: समाच्छन्नांस्ततस्तांस्तु राजन्वीक्ष्य स सैनिकान् ।चिन्तयामास समरे धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥३४॥
transliteration: samācchannāṁstatastāṁstu rājanvīkṣya sa sainikān ,cintayāmāsa samare dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Then, O king, seeing those soldiers completely covered, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), reflected in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 34. Then, O king, seeing those soldiers completely covered, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), reflected in battle.

sanskrit: कथं नु न भवेत्सत्यं तन्माधववचो महत् ।न हि क्रुद्धो रणे राजा क्षपयेत बलं मम ॥३५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu na bhavetsatyaṁ tanmādhavavaco mahat ,na hi kruddho raṇe rājā kṣapayeta balaṁ mama.
english: How could that great statement of Kṛṣṇa (Mādhava) not be true? Surely, an enraged king would not destroy my army in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 35. How could that great statement of Kṛṣṇa (Mādhava) not be true? Surely, an enraged king would not destroy my army in battle.

sanskrit: ततः सरथनागाश्वाः पाण्डवाः पाण्डुपूर्वज ।मद्रेश्वरं समासेदुः पीडयन्तः समन्ततः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarathanāgāśvāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ pāṇḍupūrvaja ,madreśvaraṁ samāseduḥ pīḍayantaḥ samantataḥ.
english: Then, O elder of Pāṇḍu, the Pāṇḍavas, with their chariots, elephants, and horses, approached the king of Madra (Madreśvara), pressing him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 36. Then, O elder of Pāṇḍu, the Pāṇḍavas, with their chariots, elephants, and horses, approached the king of Madra (Madreśvara), pressing him from all sides.

sanskrit: नानाशस्त्रौघबहुलां शस्त्रवृष्टिं समुत्थिताम् ।व्यधमत्समरे राजन्महाभ्राणीव मारुतः ॥३७॥
transliteration: nānāśastraughabahulāṁ śastravṛṣṭiṁ samutthitām ,vyadhamatsamare rājanmahābhrāṇīva mārutaḥ.
english: O king, in battle, he (the king of Madra) scattered the rising shower of weapons, which was abundant with various types of missiles, just as the wind scatters great clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 37. O king, in battle, he (the king of Madra) scattered the rising shower of weapons, which was abundant with various types of missiles, just as the wind scatters great clouds.

sanskrit: ततः कनकपुङ्खां तां शल्यक्षिप्तां वियद्गताम् ।शरवृष्टिमपश्याम शलभानामिवाततिम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kanakapuṅkhāṁ tāṁ śalyakṣiptāṁ viyadgatām ,śaravṛṣṭimapaśyāma śalabhānāmivātatim.
english: Then we saw that rain of gold-feathered arrows, launched by Śalya and rising into the sky, resembling a multitude of locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 38. Then we saw that rain of gold-feathered arrows, launched by Śalya and rising into the sky, resembling a multitude of locusts.

sanskrit: ते शरा मद्रराजेन प्रेषिता रणमूर्धनि ।संपतन्तः स्म दृश्यन्ते शलभानां व्रजा इव ॥३९॥
transliteration: te śarā madrarājena preṣitā raṇamūrdhani ,saṁpatantaḥ sma dṛśyante śalabhānāṁ vrajā iva.
english: Those arrows, dispatched by the King of Madra (madrarāja) on the battlefield, were seen falling like swarms of locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 39. Those arrows, dispatched by the King of Madra (madrarāja) on the battlefield, were seen falling like swarms of locusts.

sanskrit: मद्रराजधनुर्मुक्तैः शरैः कनकभूषणैः ।निरन्तरमिवाकाशं संबभूव जनाधिप ॥४०॥
transliteration: madrarājadhanurmuktaiḥ śaraiḥ kanakabhūṣaṇaiḥ ,nirantaramivākāśaṁ saṁbabhūva janādhipa.
english: O lord of men (janādhipa), the sky became completely filled, as if without any gaps, by the gold-ornamented arrows released from the King of Madra's (madrarāja) bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 40. O lord of men (janādhipa), the sky became completely filled, as if without any gaps, by the gold-ornamented arrows released from the King of Madra's (madrarāja) bow.

sanskrit: न पाण्डवानां नास्माकं तत्र कश्चिद्व्यदृश्यत ।बाणान्धकारे महति कृते तत्र महाभये ॥४१॥
transliteration: na pāṇḍavānāṁ nāsmākaṁ tatra kaścidvyadṛśyata ,bāṇāndhakāre mahati kṛte tatra mahābhaye.
english: In that great and terrifying darkness created by the arrows, neither any of the Pāṇḍavas nor any of us could be seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 41. In that great and terrifying darkness created by the arrows, neither any of the Pāṇḍavas nor any of us could be seen.

sanskrit: मद्रराजेन बलिना लाघवाच्छरवृष्टिभिः ।लोड्यमानं तथा दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवानां बलार्णवम् ।विस्मयं परमं जग्मुर्देवगन्धर्वदानवाः ॥४२॥
transliteration: madrarājena balinā lāghavāccharavṛṣṭibhiḥ ,loḍyamānaṁ tathā dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavānāṁ balārṇavam ,vismayaṁ paramaṁ jagmurdevagandharvadānavāḥ.
english: Seeing the Pāṇḍavas' vast army (balārṇavam) being tormented by the powerful king of Madras (madrarājena) with agile showers of arrows, the gods, Gandharvas, and Dānavas were struck with great astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 42. Seeing the Pāṇḍavas' vast army (balārṇavam) being tormented by the powerful king of Madras (madrarājena) with agile showers of arrows, the gods, Gandharvas, and Dānavas were struck with great astonishment.

sanskrit: स तु तान्सर्वतो यत्ताञ्शरैः संपीड्य मारिष ।धर्मराजमवच्छाद्य सिंहवद्व्यनदन्मुहुः ॥४३॥
transliteration: sa tu tānsarvato yattāñśaraiḥ saṁpīḍya māriṣa ,dharmarājamavacchādya siṁhavadvyanadanmuhuḥ.
english: O honorable one (māriṣa), he (Shalya) indeed, having tormented those striving warriors from all sides with arrows, and having enveloped King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarājam), repeatedly roared like a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 43. O honorable one (māriṣa), he (Shalya) indeed, having tormented those striving warriors from all sides with arrows, and having enveloped King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarājam), repeatedly roared like a lion.

sanskrit: ते छन्नाः समरे तेन पाण्डवानां महारथाः ।न शेकुस्तं तदा युद्धे प्रत्युद्यातुं महारथम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: te channāḥ samare tena pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārathāḥ ,na śekustaṁ tadā yuddhe pratyudyātuṁ mahāratham.
english: Covered by him (Shalya) in battle, those great chariot warriors (mahārathāḥ) of the Pāṇḍavas were then unable to advance against that great chariot warrior (mahāratham) in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 44. Covered by him (Shalya) in battle, those great chariot warriors (mahārathāḥ) of the Pāṇḍavas were then unable to advance against that great chariot warrior (mahāratham) in the fight.

sanskrit: धर्मराजपुरोगास्तु भीमसेनमुखा रथाः ।न जहुः समरे शूरं शल्यमाहवशोभिनम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: dharmarājapurogāstu bhīmasenamukhā rathāḥ ,na jahuḥ samare śūraṁ śalyamāhavaśobhinam.
english: But indeed, the chariot warriors (rathāḥ) led by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarājapurogāḥ) and with Bhīmasena prominent among them, did not abandon the brave Shalya, who shone brightly in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 12 Verse 45. But indeed, the chariot warriors (rathāḥ) led by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarājapurogāḥ) and with Bhīmasena prominent among them, did not abandon the brave Shalya, who shone brightly in battle.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अर्जुनो द्रौणिना विद्धो युद्धे बहुभिरायसैः ।तस्य चानुचरैः शूरैस्त्रिगर्तानां महारथैः ।द्रौणिं विव्याध समरे त्रिभिरेव शिलीमुखैः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,arjuno drauṇinā viddho yuddhe bahubhirāyasaiḥ ,tasya cānucaraiḥ śūraistrigartānāṁ mahārathaiḥ ,drauṇiṁ vivyādha samare tribhireva śilīmukhaiḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: Arjuna, though struck in battle by Droṇi with many iron arrows, and also by his brave followers, the great charioteers of the Trigartas, wounded Droṇi in that battle with just three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Arjuna, though struck in battle by Droṇi with many iron arrows, and also by his brave followers, the great charioteers of the Trigartas, wounded Droṇi in that battle with just three arrows.

sanskrit: तथेतरान्महेष्वासान्द्वाभ्यां द्वाभ्यां धनंजयः ।भूयश्चैव महाबाहुः शरवर्षैरवाकिरत् ॥२॥
transliteration: tathetarānmaheṣvāsāndvābhyāṁ dvābhyāṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,bhūyaścaiva mahābāhuḥ śaravarṣairavākirat.
english: And similarly, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the mighty-armed, showered other great bowmen with two arrows each, and then again rained down torrents of arrows upon them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. And similarly, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the mighty-armed, showered other great bowmen with two arrows each, and then again rained down torrents of arrows upon them.

sanskrit: शरकण्टकितास्ते तु तावका भरतर्षभ ।न जहुः समरे पार्थं वध्यमानाः शितैः शरैः ॥३॥
transliteration: śarakaṇṭakitāste tu tāvakā bharatarṣabha ,na jahuḥ samare pārthaṁ vadhyamānāḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: But those warriors of your side (tāvaka), O best among the Bhāratas, though bristling with arrows like thorns, did not abandon Pārtha (Arjuna) in battle, even as they were being struck by sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. But those warriors of your side (tāvaka), O best among the Bhāratas, though bristling with arrows like thorns, did not abandon Pārtha (Arjuna) in battle, even as they were being struck by sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तेऽर्जुनं रथवंशेन द्रोणपुत्रपुरोगमाः ।अयोधयन्त समरे परिवार्य महारथाः ॥४॥
transliteration: te'rjunaṁ rathavaṁśena droṇaputrapurogamāḥ ,ayodhayanta samare parivārya mahārathāḥ.
english: Those great charioteers, led by Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), surrounded Arjuna in battle with their chariot divisions and engaged him in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. Those great charioteers, led by Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), surrounded Arjuna in battle with their chariot divisions and engaged him in combat.

sanskrit: तैस्तु क्षिप्ताः शरा राजन्कार्तस्वरविभूषिताः ।अर्जुनस्य रथोपस्थं पूरयामासुरञ्जसा ॥५॥
transliteration: taistu kṣiptāḥ śarā rājankārtasvaravibhūṣitāḥ ,arjunasya rathopasthaṁ pūrayāmāsurañjasā.
english: O King, the gold-adorned arrows that were indeed hurled by them quickly filled Arjuna's chariot-seat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. O King, the gold-adorned arrows that were indeed hurled by them quickly filled Arjuna's chariot-seat.

sanskrit: तथा कृष्णौ महेष्वासौ वृषभौ सर्वधन्विनाम् ।शरैर्वीक्ष्य वितुन्नाङ्गौ प्रहृष्टौ युद्धदुर्मदौ ॥६॥
transliteration: tathā kṛṣṇau maheṣvāsau vṛṣabhau sarvadhanvinām ,śarairvīkṣya vitunnāṅgau prahṛṣṭau yuddhadurmadau.
english: Similarly, the two great bowmen, Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna - who were like bulls among all archers - upon seeing their limbs pierced by the arrows, became greatly delighted, being maddened by battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. Similarly, the two great bowmen, Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna - who were like bulls among all archers - upon seeing their limbs pierced by the arrows, became greatly delighted, being maddened by battle.

sanskrit: कूबरं रथचक्राणि ईषा योक्त्राणि चाभिभो ।युगं चैवानुकर्षं च शरभूतमभूत्तदा ॥७॥
transliteration: kūbaraṁ rathacakrāṇi īṣā yoktrāṇi cābhibho ,yugaṁ caivānukarṣaṁ ca śarabhūtamabhūttadā.
english: And at that time, O Chief, the chariot's pole, wheels, shafts, yokes, as well as the yoke-pin and the drag-pole, all became a mass of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. And at that time, O Chief, the chariot's pole, wheels, shafts, yokes, as well as the yoke-pin and the drag-pole, all became a mass of arrows.

sanskrit: नैतादृशं दृष्टपूर्वं राजन्नैव च नः श्रुतम् ।यादृशं तत्र पार्थस्य तावकाः संप्रचक्रिरे ॥८॥
transliteration: naitādṛśaṁ dṛṣṭapūrvaṁ rājannaiva ca naḥ śrutam ,yādṛśaṁ tatra pārthasya tāvakāḥ saṁpracakrire.
english: O King, such a sight was never seen before by us, nor was it ever heard of, as what your warriors (tāvakāḥ) accomplished there concerning Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. O King, such a sight was never seen before by us, nor was it ever heard of, as what your warriors (tāvakāḥ) accomplished there concerning Arjuna.

sanskrit: स रथः सर्वतो भाति चित्रपुङ्खैः शितैः शरैः ।उल्काशतैः संप्रदीप्तं विमानमिव भूतले ॥९॥
transliteration: sa rathaḥ sarvato bhāti citrapuṅkhaiḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,ulkāśataiḥ saṁpradīptaṁ vimānamiva bhūtale.
english: That chariot shines everywhere with its sharp arrows, adorned with colorful fletchings, like an aerial car intensely illumined by hundreds of meteors on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. That chariot shines everywhere with its sharp arrows, adorned with colorful fletchings, like an aerial car intensely illumined by hundreds of meteors on the ground.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो महाराज शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।अवाकिरत्तां पृतनां मेघो वृष्ट्या यथाचलम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno mahārāja śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,avākirattāṁ pṛtanāṁ megho vṛṣṭyā yathācalam.
english: Then, O great king, Arjuna showered that army with arrows having well-jointed shafts, just as a cloud showers a mountain with rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. Then, O great king, Arjuna showered that army with arrows having well-jointed shafts, just as a cloud showers a mountain with rain.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः समरे पार्थनामाङ्कितैः शरैः ।पार्थभूतममन्यन्त प्रेक्षमाणास्तथाविधम् ॥११॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ samare pārthanāmāṅkitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,pārthabhūtamamanyanta prekṣamāṇāstathāvidham.
english: As they were being struck down in battle by arrows marked with Pārtha's name, and witnessing him in such a form, they believed him to be Pārtha (Arjuna) himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. As they were being struck down in battle by arrows marked with Pārtha's name, and witnessing him in such a form, they believed him to be Pārtha (Arjuna) himself.

sanskrit: ततोऽद्भुतशरज्वालो धनुःशब्दानिलो महान् ।सेनेन्धनं ददाहाशु तावकं पार्थपावकः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato'dbhutaśarajvālo dhanuḥśabdānilo mahān ,senendhanaṁ dadāhāśu tāvakaṁ pārthapāvakaḥ.
english: Then, that great fire which was Pārtha (Arjuna), with wonderful arrows as its flames and the sounds of his bow as its wind, quickly consumed your army, treating it as fuel.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. Then, that great fire which was Pārtha (Arjuna), with wonderful arrows as its flames and the sounds of his bow as its wind, quickly consumed your army, treating it as fuel.

sanskrit: चक्राणां पततां चैव युगानां च धरातले ।तूणीराणां पताकानां ध्वजानां च रथैः सह ॥१३॥
transliteration: cakrāṇāṁ patatāṁ caiva yugānāṁ ca dharātale ,tūṇīrāṇāṁ patākānāṁ dhvajānāṁ ca rathaiḥ saha.
english: One could see falling wheels and yokes scattered on the ground, as well as quivers, banners, standards, and chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. One could see falling wheels and yokes scattered on the ground, as well as quivers, banners, standards, and chariots.

sanskrit: ईषाणामनुकर्षाणां त्रिवेणूनां च भारत ।अक्षाणामथ योक्त्राणां प्रतोदानां च सर्वशः ॥१४॥
transliteration: īṣāṇāmanukarṣāṇāṁ triveṇūnāṁ ca bhārata ,akṣāṇāmatha yoktrāṇāṁ pratodānāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, one could also see chariot poles, secondary poles, triple shafts, axles, harnesses, and goads everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. O Bhārata, one could also see chariot poles, secondary poles, triple shafts, axles, harnesses, and goads everywhere.

sanskrit: शिरसां पततां चैव कुण्डलोष्णीषधारिणाम् ।भुजानां च महाराज स्कन्धानां च समन्ततः ॥१५॥
transliteration: śirasāṁ patatāṁ caiva kuṇḍaloṣṇīṣadhāriṇām ,bhujānāṁ ca mahārāja skandhānāṁ ca samantataḥ.
english: O great king, one could also see falling heads of warriors wearing earrings and turbans, and arms and shoulders all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. O great king, one could also see falling heads of warriors wearing earrings and turbans, and arms and shoulders all around.

sanskrit: छत्राणां व्यजनैः सार्धं मुकुटानां च राशयः ।समदृश्यन्त पार्थस्य रथमार्गेषु भारत ॥१६॥
transliteration: chatrāṇāṁ vyajanaiḥ sārdhaṁ mukuṭānāṁ ca rāśayaḥ ,samadṛśyanta pārthasya rathamārgeṣu bhārata.
english: Heaps of umbrellas, along with fans and crowns, O Bhārata, were seen on Arjuna's chariot paths.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. Heaps of umbrellas, along with fans and crowns, O Bhārata, were seen on Arjuna's chariot paths.

sanskrit: अगम्यरूपा पृथिवी मांसशोणितकर्दमा ।बभूव भरतश्रेष्ठ रुद्रस्याक्रीडनं यथा ।भीरूणां त्रासजननी शूराणां हर्षवर्धनी ॥१७॥
transliteration: agamyarūpā pṛthivī māṁsaśoṇitakardamā ,babhūva bharataśreṣṭha rudrasyākrīḍanaṁ yathā ,bhīrūṇāṁ trāsajananī śūrāṇāṁ harṣavardhanī.
english: O best among the Bharatas, the earth became a formidable sight, a mire of flesh and blood, as if it were the playground of Rudra. It caused terror for the fearful, and increased the joy of the brave.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. O best among the Bharatas, the earth became a formidable sight, a mire of flesh and blood, as if it were the playground of Rudra. It caused terror for the fearful, and increased the joy of the brave.

sanskrit: हत्वा तु समरे पार्थः सहस्रे द्वे परंतप ।रथानां सवरूथानां विधूमोऽग्निरिव ज्वलन् ॥१८॥
transliteration: hatvā tu samare pārthaḥ sahasre dve paraṁtapa ,rathānāṁ savarūthānāṁ vidhūmo'gniriva jvalan.
english: Indeed, O scorcher of foes, in that battle, Partha, having destroyed two thousand chariots along with their retinues, shone blazing like a smokeless fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. Indeed, O scorcher of foes, in that battle, Partha, having destroyed two thousand chariots along with their retinues, shone blazing like a smokeless fire.

sanskrit: यथा हि भगवानग्निर्जगद्दग्ध्वा चराचरम् ।विधूमो दृश्यते राजंस्तथा पार्थो महारथः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yathā hi bhagavānagnirjagaddagdhvā carācaram ,vidhūmo dṛśyate rājaṁstathā pārtho mahārathaḥ.
english: Indeed, O king, just as the glorious fire, having consumed the entire world, both moving and unmoving, is seen without smoke, so too was Partha, the great charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. Indeed, O king, just as the glorious fire, having consumed the entire world, both moving and unmoving, is seen without smoke, so too was Partha, the great charioteer.

sanskrit: द्रौणिस्तु समरे दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवस्य पराक्रमम् ।रथेनातिपताकेन पाण्डवं प्रत्यवारयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: drauṇistu samare dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavasya parākramam ,rathenātipatākena pāṇḍavaṁ pratyavārayat.
english: But Droṇi's son (Aśvatthāmā), having witnessed the Pandava's (Arjuna's) valor in battle, then intercepted the Pandava with his conspicuously bannered chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 20. But Droṇi's son (Aśvatthāmā), having witnessed the Pandava's (Arjuna's) valor in battle, then intercepted the Pandava with his conspicuously bannered chariot.

sanskrit: तावुभौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ श्वेताश्वौ धन्विनां वरौ ।समीयतुस्तदा तूर्णं परस्परवधैषिणौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: tāvubhau puruṣavyāghrau śvetāśvau dhanvināṁ varau ,samīyatustadā tūrṇaṁ parasparavadhaiṣiṇau.
english: Those two, tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau), riding white horses, and foremost among archers, then quickly advanced towards each other, intent on killing one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 21. Those two, tigers among men (puruṣavyāghrau), riding white horses, and foremost among archers, then quickly advanced towards each other, intent on killing one another.

sanskrit: तयोरासीन्महाराज बाणवर्षं सुदारुणम् ।जीमूतानां यथा वृष्टिस्तपान्ते भरतर्षभ ॥२२॥
transliteration: tayorāsīnmahārāja bāṇavarṣaṁ sudāruṇam ,jīmūtānāṁ yathā vṛṣṭistapānte bharatarṣabha.
english: O great king (mahārāja), O bull among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), there was a very dreadful rain of arrows from them both, just like the rainfall from clouds at the end of summer.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 22. O great king (mahārāja), O bull among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), there was a very dreadful rain of arrows from them both, just like the rainfall from clouds at the end of summer.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यस्पर्धिनौ तौ तु शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।ततक्षतुर्मृधेऽन्योन्यं शृङ्गाभ्यां वृषभाविव ॥२३॥
transliteration: anyonyaspardhinau tau tu śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,tatakṣaturmṛdhe'nyonyaṁ śṛṅgābhyāṁ vṛṣabhāviva.
english: Those two rivals, using arrows with well-made (bent) shafts, wounded each other in battle, just as two bulls gore each other with their horns.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 23. Those two rivals, using arrows with well-made (bent) shafts, wounded each other in battle, just as two bulls gore each other with their horns.

sanskrit: तयोर्युद्धं महाराज चिरं सममिवाभवत् ।अस्त्राणां संगमश्चैव घोरस्तत्राभवन्महान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tayoryuddhaṁ mahārāja ciraṁ samamivābhavat ,astrāṇāṁ saṁgamaścaiva ghorastatrābhavanmahān.
english: O great king (mahārāja), their battle remained evenly matched for a long time, and a great and terrible encounter of mystic weapons (astras) took place there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 24. O great king (mahārāja), their battle remained evenly matched for a long time, and a great and terrible encounter of mystic weapons (astras) took place there.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनं द्वादशभी रुक्मपुङ्खैः सुतेजनैः ।वासुदेवं च दशभिर्द्रौणिर्विव्याध भारत ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaṁ dvādaśabhī rukmapuṅkhaiḥ sutejanaiḥ ,vāsudevaṁ ca daśabhirdrauṇirvivyādha bhārata.
english: Then, O descendant of Bhārata, Droṇi (Ashvatthaman) pierced Arjuna with twelve well-sharpened, gold-tipped arrows, and Kṛṣṇa (Vāsudeva) with ten.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 25. Then, O descendant of Bhārata, Droṇi (Ashvatthaman) pierced Arjuna with twelve well-sharpened, gold-tipped arrows, and Kṛṣṇa (Vāsudeva) with ten.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्य बीभत्सुर्व्याक्षिपद्गाण्डिवं धनुः ।मानयित्वा मुहूर्तं च गुरुपुत्रं महाहवे ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasya bībhatsurvyākṣipadgāṇḍivaṁ dhanuḥ ,mānayitvā muhūrtaṁ ca guruputraṁ mahāhave.
english: Then, smiling, and having respectfully acknowledged the guru's son (Ashvatthaman) for a moment in that great battle, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) drew back his Gaṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 26. Then, smiling, and having respectfully acknowledged the guru's son (Ashvatthaman) for a moment in that great battle, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) drew back his Gaṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: व्यश्वसूतरथं चक्रे सव्यसाची महारथः ।मृदुपूर्वं ततश्चैनं त्रिभिर्विव्याध सायकैः ॥२७॥
transliteration: vyaśvasūtarathaṁ cakre savyasācī mahārathaḥ ,mṛdupūrvaṁ tataścainaṁ tribhirvivyādha sāyakaiḥ.
english: The great charioteer Savyasācin (Arjuna) made (Ashvatthaman's) chariot devoid of horses and charioteer. Then, at first gently, he pierced him with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 27. The great charioteer Savyasācin (Arjuna) made (Ashvatthaman's) chariot devoid of horses and charioteer. Then, at first gently, he pierced him with three arrows.

sanskrit: हताश्वे तु रथे तिष्ठन्द्रोणपुत्रस्त्वयस्मयम् ।मुसलं पाण्डुपुत्राय चिक्षेप परिघोपमम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: hatāśve tu rathe tiṣṭhandroṇaputrastvayasmayam ,musalaṁ pāṇḍuputrāya cikṣepa parighopamam.
english: However, Droṇa's son (Ashvatthaman), standing in his chariot whose horses had been killed, hurled an iron mace, comparable to a heavy iron club, at the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 28. However, Droṇa's son (Ashvatthaman), standing in his chariot whose horses had been killed, hurled an iron mace, comparable to a heavy iron club, at the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं सहसा हेमपट्टविभूषितम् ।चिच्छेद सप्तधा वीरः पार्थः शत्रुनिबर्हणः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ sahasā hemapaṭṭavibhūṣitam ,ciccheda saptadhā vīraḥ pārthaḥ śatrunibarhaṇaḥ.
english: The brave Arjuna, the destroyer of enemies, swiftly cut that mace, which was adorned with golden plates and descending rapidly, into seven pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 29. The brave Arjuna, the destroyer of enemies, swiftly cut that mace, which was adorned with golden plates and descending rapidly, into seven pieces.

sanskrit: स च्छिन्नं मुसलं दृष्ट्वा द्रौणिः परमकोपनः ।आददे परिघं घोरं नगेन्द्रशिखरोपमम् ।चिक्षेप चैव पार्थाय द्रौणिर्युद्धविशारदः ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa cchinnaṁ musalaṁ dṛṣṭvā drauṇiḥ paramakopanaḥ ,ādade parighaṁ ghoraṁ nagendraśikharopamam ,cikṣepa caiva pārthāya drauṇiryuddhaviśāradaḥ.
english: Seeing his mace severed, Drauṇi, who was exceedingly wrathful, picked up a dreadful iron club that resembled a mountain peak. Indeed, that Drauṇi, skilled in battle, then hurled it at Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 30. Seeing his mace severed, Drauṇi, who was exceedingly wrathful, picked up a dreadful iron club that resembled a mountain peak. Indeed, that Drauṇi, skilled in battle, then hurled it at Arjuna.

sanskrit: तमन्तकमिव क्रुद्धं परिघं प्रेक्ष्य पाण्डवः ।अर्जुनस्त्वरितो जघ्ने पञ्चभिः सायकोत्तमैः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tamantakamiva kruddhaṁ parighaṁ prekṣya pāṇḍavaḥ ,arjunastvarito jaghne pañcabhiḥ sāyakottamaiḥ.
english: Having seen that iron club, enraged and resembling Death (Antaka) itself, Arjuna, the son of Pāṇḍu, swiftly struck it with five excellent arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 31. Having seen that iron club, enraged and resembling Death (Antaka) itself, Arjuna, the son of Pāṇḍu, swiftly struck it with five excellent arrows.

sanskrit: स च्छिन्नः पतितो भूमौ पार्थबाणैर्महाहवे ।दारयन्पृथिवीन्द्राणां मनः शब्देन भारत ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa cchinnaḥ patito bhūmau pārthabāṇairmahāhave ,dārayanpṛthivīndrāṇāṁ manaḥ śabdena bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, that (club), severed by Arjuna's arrows in the great battle, fell to the ground, tearing apart the hearts of kings with its tremendous sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 32. O Bhārata, that (club), severed by Arjuna's arrows in the great battle, fell to the ground, tearing apart the hearts of kings with its tremendous sound.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरैस्त्रिभिर्बाणैर्द्रौणिं विव्याध पाण्डवः ।सोऽतिविद्धो बलवता पार्थेन सुमहाबलः ।न संभ्रान्तस्तदा द्रौणिः पौरुषे स्वे व्यवस्थितः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tato'paraistribhirbāṇairdrauṇiṁ vivyādha pāṇḍavaḥ ,so'tividdho balavatā pārthena sumahābalaḥ ,na saṁbhrāntastadā drauṇiḥ pauruṣe sve vyavasthitaḥ.
english: Then, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) pierced Drauṇi with three more arrows. Although greatly wounded by the mighty Pārtha (Arjuna), that extremely powerful Drauṇi was not bewildered then; he remained firm in his own manly valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 33. Then, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) pierced Drauṇi with three more arrows. Although greatly wounded by the mighty Pārtha (Arjuna), that extremely powerful Drauṇi was not bewildered then; he remained firm in his own manly valor.

sanskrit: सुधर्मा तु ततो राजन्भारद्वाजं महारथम् ।अवाकिरच्छरव्रातैः सर्वक्षत्रस्य पश्यतः ॥३४॥
transliteration: sudharmā tu tato rājanbhāradvājaṁ mahāratham ,avākiraccharavrātaiḥ sarvakṣatrasya paśyataḥ.
english: But then, O King, Sudharmā showered the great warrior Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) with a multitude of arrows, while all the Kṣatriyas watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 34. But then, O King, Sudharmā showered the great warrior Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) with a multitude of arrows, while all the Kṣatriyas watched.

sanskrit: ततस्तु सुरथोऽप्याजौ पाञ्चालानां महारथः ।रथेन मेघघोषेण द्रौणिमेवाभ्यधावत ॥३५॥
transliteration: tatastu suratho'pyājau pāñcālānāṁ mahārathaḥ ,rathena meghaghoṣeṇa drauṇimevābhyadhāvata.
english: But then, Suratha, a great warrior of the Pāñcālas, also rushed towards Drauṇi in battle with his chariot, which had a roar like thunder.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 35. But then, Suratha, a great warrior of the Pāñcālas, also rushed towards Drauṇi in battle with his chariot, which had a roar like thunder.

sanskrit: विकर्षन्वै धनुः श्रेष्ठं सर्वभारसहं दृढम् ।ज्वलनाशीविषनिभैः शरैश्चैनमवाकिरत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: vikarṣanvai dhanuḥ śreṣṭhaṁ sarvabhārasahaṁ dṛḍham ,jvalanāśīviṣanibhaiḥ śaraiścainamavākirat.
english: Indeed, drawing his excellent bow, which was firm and capable of bearing all strain, he showered him with arrows resembling fiery, venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 36. Indeed, drawing his excellent bow, which was firm and capable of bearing all strain, he showered him with arrows resembling fiery, venomous snakes.

sanskrit: सुरथं तु ततः क्रुद्धमापतन्तं महारथम् ।चुकोप समरे द्रौणिर्दण्डाहत इवोरगः ॥३७॥
transliteration: surathaṁ tu tataḥ kruddhamāpatantaṁ mahāratham ,cukopa samare drauṇirdaṇḍāhata ivoragaḥ.
english: Then, Droni's son (Drauni) became enraged in battle, like a snake struck by a stick, when the great charioteer Suratha, enraged, came rushing towards him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 37. Then, Droni's son (Drauni) became enraged in battle, like a snake struck by a stick, when the great charioteer Suratha, enraged, came rushing towards him.

sanskrit: त्रिशिखां भ्रुकुटीं कृत्वा सृक्किणी परिलेलिहन् ।उद्वीक्ष्य सुरथं रोषाद्धनुर्ज्यामवमृज्य च ।मुमोच तीक्ष्णं नाराचं यमदण्डसमद्युतिम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: triśikhāṁ bhrukuṭīṁ kṛtvā sṛkkiṇī parilelihan ,udvīkṣya surathaṁ roṣāddhanurjyāmavamṛjya ca ,mumoca tīkṣṇaṁ nārācaṁ yamadaṇḍasamadyutim.
english: Forming a three-pointed frown, licking the corners of his mouth, and angrily looking up at Suratha, Droni's son tightened his bowstring. He then released a sharp arrow, its radiance resembling the staff of Yama (Yama).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 38. Forming a three-pointed frown, licking the corners of his mouth, and angrily looking up at Suratha, Droni's son tightened his bowstring. He then released a sharp arrow, its radiance resembling the staff of Yama (Yama).

sanskrit: स तस्य हृदयं भित्त्वा प्रविवेशातिवेगतः ।शक्राशनिरिवोत्सृष्टा विदार्य धरणीतलम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: sa tasya hṛdayaṁ bhittvā praviveśātivegataḥ ,śakrāśanirivotsṛṣṭā vidārya dharaṇītalam.
english: Having pierced his heart, the arrow entered with extreme speed, just as Indra's (Śakra) thunderbolt (aśani), once released, tears apart the surface of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 39. Having pierced his heart, the arrow entered with extreme speed, just as Indra's (Śakra) thunderbolt (aśani), once released, tears apart the surface of the earth.

sanskrit: ततस्तं पतितं भूमौ नाराचेन समाहतम् ।वज्रेणेव यथा शृङ्गं पर्वतस्य महाधनम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: tatastaṁ patitaṁ bhūmau nārācena samāhatam ,vajreṇeva yathā śṛṅgaṁ parvatasya mahādhanam.
english: Then he (Suratha) lay fallen on the ground, struck by the arrow, just as a magnificent peak of a mountain (parvata), rich in treasures, is struck down by a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 40. Then he (Suratha) lay fallen on the ground, struck by the arrow, just as a magnificent peak of a mountain (parvata), rich in treasures, is struck down by a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु निहते वीरे द्रोणपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।आरुरोह रथं तूर्णं तमेव रथिनां वरः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu nihate vīre droṇaputraḥ pratāpavān ,āruroha rathaṁ tūrṇaṁ tameva rathināṁ varaḥ.
english: But when that hero was slain, Drona's powerful son, the best among charioteers, quickly mounted that very chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 41. But when that hero was slain, Drona's powerful son, the best among charioteers, quickly mounted that very chariot.

sanskrit: ततः सज्जो महाराज द्रौणिराहवदुर्मदः ।अर्जुनं योधयामास संशप्तकवृतो रणे ॥४२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sajjo mahārāja drauṇirāhavadurmadaḥ ,arjunaṁ yodhayāmāsa saṁśaptakavṛto raṇe.
english: Then, O great king, Droni, ready and intoxicated with battle, surrounded by the Saṃśaptakas, engaged Arjuna in combat on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 42. Then, O great king, Droni, ready and intoxicated with battle, surrounded by the Saṃśaptakas, engaged Arjuna in combat on the battlefield.

sanskrit: तत्र युद्धं महच्चासीदर्जुनस्य परैः सह ।मध्यंदिनगते सूर्ये यमराष्ट्रविवर्धनम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tatra yuddhaṁ mahaccāsīdarjunasya paraiḥ saha ,madhyaṁdinagate sūrye yamarāṣṭravivardhanam.
english: There, a great battle took place between Arjuna and his enemies at midday, which greatly expanded the realm of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 43. There, a great battle took place between Arjuna and his enemies at midday, which greatly expanded the realm of Yama.

sanskrit: तत्राश्चर्यमपश्याम दृष्ट्वा तेषां पराक्रमम् ।यदेको युगपद्वीरान्समयोधयदर्जुनः ॥४४॥
transliteration: tatrāścaryamapaśyāma dṛṣṭvā teṣāṁ parākramam ,yadeko yugapadvīrānsamayodhayadarjunaḥ.
english: There we saw an astonishing sight, observing the valor of those many heroes, as Arjuna, alone, fought them simultaneously.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 44. There we saw an astonishing sight, observing the valor of those many heroes, as Arjuna, alone, fought them simultaneously.

sanskrit: विमर्दस्तु महानासीदर्जुनस्य परैः सह ।शतक्रतोर्यथा पूर्वं महत्या दैत्यसेनया ॥४५॥
transliteration: vimardastu mahānāsīdarjunasya paraiḥ saha ,śatakratoryathā pūrvaṁ mahatyā daityasenayā.
english: Indeed, a great conflict occurred for Arjuna with his enemies, just as it formerly did for Indra (śatakratu) with the mighty army of demons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 13 Verse 45. Indeed, a great conflict occurred for Arjuna with his enemies, just as it formerly did for Indra (śatakratu) with the mighty army of demons.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।दुर्योधनो महाराज धृष्टद्युम्नश्च पार्षतः ।चक्रतुः सुमहद्युद्धं शरशक्तिसमाकुलम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,duryodhano mahārāja dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca pārṣataḥ ,cakratuḥ sumahadyuddhaṁ śaraśaktisamākulam.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, Duryodhana and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, engaged in a very great battle (yuddha), filled with arrows and spears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O great king, Duryodhana and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, engaged in a very great battle (yuddha), filled with arrows and spears.

sanskrit: तयोरासन्महाराज शरधाराः सहस्रशः ।अम्बुदानां यथा काले जलधाराः समन्ततः ॥२॥
transliteration: tayorāsanmahārāja śaradhārāḥ sahasraśaḥ ,ambudānāṁ yathā kāle jaladhārāḥ samantataḥ.
english: O great king, from them came thousands of streams of arrows, just as, at the proper time, streams of water (jaladhārāḥ) pour from the clouds all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. O great king, from them came thousands of streams of arrows, just as, at the proper time, streams of water (jaladhārāḥ) pour from the clouds all around.

sanskrit: राजा तु पार्षतं विद्ध्वा शरैः पञ्चभिरायसैः ।द्रोणहन्तारमुग्रेषुः पुनर्विव्याध सप्तभिः ॥३॥
transliteration: rājā tu pārṣataṁ viddhvā śaraiḥ pañcabhirāyasaiḥ ,droṇahantāramugreṣuḥ punarvivyādha saptabhiḥ.
english: But the king, a fierce archer, having first pierced the son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with five iron arrows, again struck the slayer of Droṇa (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with seven (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. But the king, a fierce archer, having first pierced the son of Pṛṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with five iron arrows, again struck the slayer of Droṇa (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with seven (arrows).

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु समरे बलवान्दृढविक्रमः ।सप्तत्या विशिखानां वै दुर्योधनमपीडयत् ॥४॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastu samare balavāndṛḍhavikramaḥ ,saptatyā viśikhānāṁ vai duryodhanamapīḍayat.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who was mighty and of firm valor, truly tormented Duryodhana in battle with seventy arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who was mighty and of firm valor, truly tormented Duryodhana in battle with seventy arrows.

sanskrit: पीडितं प्रेक्ष्य राजानं सोदर्या भरतर्षभ ।महत्या सेनया सार्धं परिवव्रुः स्म पार्षतम् ॥५॥
transliteration: pīḍitaṁ prekṣya rājānaṁ sodaryā bharatarṣabha ,mahatyā senayā sārdhaṁ parivavruḥ sma pārṣatam.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), seeing the king (Duryodhana) thus afflicted, his brothers, accompanied by a vast army, surrounded Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), seeing the king (Duryodhana) thus afflicted, his brothers, accompanied by a vast army, surrounded Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna).

sanskrit: स तैः परिवृतः शूरैः सर्वतोऽतिरथैर्भृशम् ।व्यचरत्समरे राजन्दर्शयन्हस्तलाघवम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ parivṛtaḥ śūraiḥ sarvato'tirathairbhṛśam ,vyacaratsamare rājandarśayanhastalāghavam.
english: O king (rājan), though greatly surrounded by those heroes and mighty charioteers from all sides, he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) moved about in battle, displaying his extraordinary dexterity (hastalāghavam).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. O king (rājan), though greatly surrounded by those heroes and mighty charioteers from all sides, he (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) moved about in battle, displaying his extraordinary dexterity (hastalāghavam).

sanskrit: शिखण्डी कृतवर्माणं गौतमं च महारथम् ।प्रभद्रकैः समायुक्तो योधयामास धन्विनौ ॥७॥
transliteration: śikhaṇḍī kṛtavarmāṇaṁ gautamaṁ ca mahāratham ,prabhadrakaiḥ samāyukto yodhayāmāsa dhanvinau.
english: Śikhaṇḍin, accompanied by the Prabhadrakas, engaged in battle with Kṛtavarman and Gautama, the great charioteer; both were skilled bowmen.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. Śikhaṇḍin, accompanied by the Prabhadrakas, engaged in battle with Kṛtavarman and Gautama, the great charioteer; both were skilled bowmen.

sanskrit: तत्रापि सुमहद्युद्धं घोररूपं विशां पते ।प्राणान्संत्यजतां युद्धे प्राणद्यूताभिदेवने ॥८॥
transliteration: tatrāpi sumahadyuddhaṁ ghorarūpaṁ viśāṁ pate ,prāṇānsaṁtyajatāṁ yuddhe prāṇadyūtābhidevane.
english: O lord of the people, even there, a very great battle took place, terrifying in its nature, where lives were abandoned in a game of chance with life itself as the stake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. O lord of the people, even there, a very great battle took place, terrifying in its nature, where lives were abandoned in a game of chance with life itself as the stake.

sanskrit: शल्यस्तु शरवर्षाणि विमुञ्चन्सर्वतोदिशम् ।पाण्डवान्पीडयामास ससात्यकिवृकोदरान् ॥९॥
transliteration: śalyastu śaravarṣāṇi vimuñcansarvatodiśam ,pāṇḍavānpīḍayāmāsa sasātyakivṛkodarān.
english: But Śalya, unleashing showers of arrows in all directions, tormented the Pāṇḍavas, including Sātyaki and Vṛkodara (Bhīma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. But Śalya, unleashing showers of arrows in all directions, tormented the Pāṇḍavas, including Sātyaki and Vṛkodara (Bhīma).

sanskrit: तथोभौ च यमौ युद्धे यमतुल्यपराक्रमौ ।योधयामास राजेन्द्र वीर्येण च बलेन च ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathobhau ca yamau yuddhe yamatulyaparākramau ,yodhayāmāsa rājendra vīryeṇa ca balena ca.
english: And similarly, O king of kings, he (Śalya) fought against both the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), whose prowess was equal to Yama (the god of death), with his own valor and might.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10. And similarly, O king of kings, he (Śalya) fought against both the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), whose prowess was equal to Yama (the god of death), with his own valor and might.

sanskrit: शल्यसायकनुन्नानां पाण्डवानां महामृधे ।त्रातारं नाध्यगच्छन्त केचित्तत्र महारथाः ॥११॥
transliteration: śalyasāyakanunnānāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahāmṛdhe ,trātāraṁ nādhyagacchanta kecittatra mahārathāḥ.
english: In that great battle, some great charioteers among the Pāṇḍavas, who were driven back by Śalya's arrows, could not find a savior.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. In that great battle, some great charioteers among the Pāṇḍavas, who were driven back by Śalya's arrows, could not find a savior.

sanskrit: ततस्तु नकुलः शूरो धर्मराजे प्रपीडिते ।अभिदुद्राव वेगेन मातुलं माद्रिनन्दनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatastu nakulaḥ śūro dharmarāje prapīḍite ,abhidudrāva vegena mātulaṁ mādrinandanaḥ.
english: Then, when King Yudhishthira (dharmarāja) was greatly afflicted, the brave Nakula, son of Madri, swiftly rushed towards his maternal uncle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. Then, when King Yudhishthira (dharmarāja) was greatly afflicted, the brave Nakula, son of Madri, swiftly rushed towards his maternal uncle.

sanskrit: संछाद्य समरे शल्यं नकुलः परवीरहा ।विव्याध चैनं दशभिः स्मयमानः स्तनान्तरे ॥१३॥
transliteration: saṁchādya samare śalyaṁ nakulaḥ paravīrahā ,vivyādha cainaṁ daśabhiḥ smayamānaḥ stanāntare.
english: Having enveloped Shalya in battle, Nakula, the slayer of enemy warriors, smiling, pierced him in the chest with ten (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. Having enveloped Shalya in battle, Nakula, the slayer of enemy warriors, smiling, pierced him in the chest with ten (arrows).

sanskrit: सर्वपारशवैर्बाणैः कर्मारपरिमार्जितैः ।स्वर्णपुङ्खैः शिलाधौतैर्धनुर्यन्त्रप्रचोदितैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sarvapāraśavairbāṇaiḥ karmāraparimārjitaiḥ ,svarṇapuṅkhaiḥ śilādhautairdhanuryantrapracoditaiḥ.
english: With arrows made entirely of iron, polished by blacksmiths, having golden shafts, honed on stone, and propelled by a mechanical bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. With arrows made entirely of iron, polished by blacksmiths, having golden shafts, honed on stone, and propelled by a mechanical bow.

sanskrit: शल्यस्तु पीडितस्तेन स्वस्रीयेण महात्मना ।नकुलं पीडयामास पत्रिभिर्नतपर्वभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: śalyastu pīḍitastena svasrīyeṇa mahātmanā ,nakulaṁ pīḍayāmāsa patribhirnataparvabhiḥ.
english: But Shalya, afflicted by that great-souled (mahātman) nephew, in turn afflicted Nakula with winged arrows having well-formed shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. But Shalya, afflicted by that great-souled (mahātman) nephew, in turn afflicted Nakula with winged arrows having well-formed shafts.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा भीमसेनोऽथ सात्यकिः ।सहदेवश्च माद्रेयो मद्रराजमुपाद्रवन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā bhīmaseno'tha sātyakiḥ ,sahadevaśca mādreyo madrarājamupādravan.
english: Then King Yudhishthira, Bhimasena, Satyaki, and Sahadeva, the son of Madri, rushed towards the king of Madra (Shalya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. Then King Yudhishthira, Bhimasena, Satyaki, and Sahadeva, the son of Madri, rushed towards the king of Madra (Shalya).

sanskrit: तानापतत एवाशु पूरयानान्रथस्वनैः ।दिशश्च प्रदिशश्चैव कम्पयानांश्च मेदिनीम् ।प्रतिजग्राह समरे सेनापतिरमित्रजित् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tānāpatata evāśu pūrayānānrathasvanaiḥ ,diśaśca pradiśaścaiva kampayānāṁśca medinīm ,pratijagrāha samare senāpatiramitrajit.
english: The commander of the army (senāpati), Shalya, who was a conqueror of foes (amitrajit), intercepted them in battle as they quickly approached, filling all cardinal and intermediate directions with the sounds of their chariots and making the earth tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 17. The commander of the army (senāpati), Shalya, who was a conqueror of foes (amitrajit), intercepted them in battle as they quickly approached, filling all cardinal and intermediate directions with the sounds of their chariots and making the earth tremble.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं त्रिभिर्विद्ध्वा भीमसेनं च सप्तभिः ।सात्यकिं च शतेनाजौ सहदेवं त्रिभिः शरैः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ tribhirviddhvā bhīmasenaṁ ca saptabhiḥ ,sātyakiṁ ca śatenājau sahadevaṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Having pierced Yudhishthira with three arrows, Bhimasena with seven, Satyaki with a hundred in battle, and Sahadeva with three arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 18. Having pierced Yudhishthira with three arrows, Bhimasena with seven, Satyaki with a hundred in battle, and Sahadeva with three arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्तु सशरं चापं नकुलस्य महात्मनः ।मद्रेश्वरः क्षुरप्रेण तदा चिच्छेद मारिष ।तदशीर्यत विच्छिन्नं धनुः शल्यस्य सायकैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tatastu saśaraṁ cāpaṁ nakulasya mahātmanaḥ ,madreśvaraḥ kṣurapreṇa tadā ciccheda māriṣa ,tadaśīryata vicchinnaṁ dhanuḥ śalyasya sāyakaiḥ.
english: Then, O venerable one (māriṣa), the lord of Madra (madreśvara), Shalya, immediately cut off the great-souled Nakula's bow, which was fitted with an arrow, with a razor-sharp arrow. That severed bow was then shattered by Shalya's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 19. Then, O venerable one (māriṣa), the lord of Madra (madreśvara), Shalya, immediately cut off the great-souled Nakula's bow, which was fitted with an arrow, with a razor-sharp arrow. That severed bow was then shattered by Shalya's arrows.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय माद्रीपुत्रो महारथः ।मद्रराजरथं तूर्णं पूरयामास पत्रिभिः ॥२०॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya mādrīputro mahārathaḥ ,madrarājarathaṁ tūrṇaṁ pūrayāmāsa patribhiḥ.
english: Then, Sahadeva, the son of Madri and a great chariot warrior, took another bow and swiftly showered the chariot of the Madra king with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 20. Then, Sahadeva, the son of Madri and a great chariot warrior, took another bow and swiftly showered the chariot of the Madra king with arrows.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु मद्रेशं सहदेवश्च मारिष ।दशभिर्दशभिर्बाणैरुरस्येनमविध्यताम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu madreśaṁ sahadevaśca māriṣa ,daśabhirdaśabhirbāṇairurasyenamavidhyatām.
english: O venerable one (māriṣa), Yudhishthira and Sahadeva then each pierced the king of Madras in the chest with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 21. O venerable one (māriṣa), Yudhishthira and Sahadeva then each pierced the king of Madras in the chest with ten arrows.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्ततः षष्ट्या सात्यकिर्नवभिः शरैः ।मद्रराजमभिद्रुत्य जघ्नतुः कङ्कपत्रिभिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastataḥ ṣaṣṭyā sātyakirnavabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,madrarājamabhidrutya jaghnatuḥ kaṅkapatribhiḥ.
english: Then, Bhimasena, with sixty arrows, and Satyaki, with nine arrows, having swiftly attacked the king of Madras, struck him with arrows fletched with vulture feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 22. Then, Bhimasena, with sixty arrows, and Satyaki, with nine arrows, having swiftly attacked the king of Madras, struck him with arrows fletched with vulture feathers.

sanskrit: मद्रराजस्ततः क्रुद्धः सात्यकिं नवभिः शरैः ।विव्याध भूयः सप्तत्या शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: madrarājastataḥ kruddhaḥ sātyakiṁ navabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,vivyādha bhūyaḥ saptatyā śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām.
english: Then, the enraged king of Madras pierced Satyaki with nine arrows, and again (further) with seventy well-crafted arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 23. Then, the enraged king of Madras pierced Satyaki with nine arrows, and again (further) with seventy well-crafted arrows.

sanskrit: अथास्य सशरं चापं मुष्टौ चिच्छेद मारिष ।हयांश्च चतुरः संख्ये प्रेषयामास मृत्यवे ॥२४॥
transliteration: athāsya saśaraṁ cāpaṁ muṣṭau ciccheda māriṣa ,hayāṁśca caturaḥ saṁkhye preṣayāmāsa mṛtyave.
english: Then, O venerable one, he severed his opponent's bow, which was fitted with an arrow, from his hand. And in that battle, he sent his opponent's four horses to their death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 24. Then, O venerable one, he severed his opponent's bow, which was fitted with an arrow, from his hand. And in that battle, he sent his opponent's four horses to their death.

sanskrit: विरथं सात्यकिं कृत्वा मद्रराजो महाबलः ।विशिखानां शतेनैनमाजघान समन्ततः ॥२५॥
transliteration: virathaṁ sātyakiṁ kṛtvā madrarājo mahābalaḥ ,viśikhānāṁ śatenainamājaghāna samantataḥ.
english: The mighty King of Madras, having dismounted Satyaki from his chariot, then struck him with a hundred arrows from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 25. The mighty King of Madras, having dismounted Satyaki from his chariot, then struck him with a hundred arrows from all sides.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रौ तु संरब्धौ भीमसेनं च पाण्डवम् ।युधिष्ठिरं च कौरव्य विव्याध दशभिः शरैः ॥२६॥
transliteration: mādrīputrau tu saṁrabdhau bhīmasenaṁ ca pāṇḍavam ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ ca kauravya vivyādha daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: But, O descendant of Kuru, he pierced the two enraged sons of Madri, and Bhimasena the Pandava, and Yudhishthira, with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 26. But, O descendant of Kuru, he pierced the two enraged sons of Madri, and Bhimasena the Pandava, and Yudhishthira, with ten arrows.

sanskrit: तत्राद्भुतमपश्याम मद्रराजस्य पौरुषम् ।यदेनं सहिताः पार्था नाभ्यवर्तन्त संयुगे ॥२७॥
transliteration: tatrādbhutamapaśyāma madrarājasya pauruṣam ,yadenaṁ sahitāḥ pārthā nābhyavartanta saṁyuge.
english: In that situation, we witnessed the astonishing valor of the King of Madras, in that even the combined sons of Pritha (Pandavas) could not withstand him in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 27. In that situation, we witnessed the astonishing valor of the King of Madras, in that even the combined sons of Pritha (Pandavas) could not withstand him in battle.

sanskrit: अथान्यं रथमास्थाय सात्यकिः सत्यविक्रमः ।पीडितान्पाण्डवान्दृष्ट्वा मद्रराजवशं गतान् ।अभिदुद्राव वेगेन मद्राणामधिपं बली ॥२८॥
transliteration: athānyaṁ rathamāsthāya sātyakiḥ satyavikramaḥ ,pīḍitānpāṇḍavāndṛṣṭvā madrarājavaśaṁ gatān ,abhidudrāva vegena madrāṇāmadhipaṁ balī.
english: Then, Satyaki, a man of true valor and great strength, having mounted another chariot, saw the Pāṇḍavas distressed and fallen under the sway of the Madra king. He then swiftly rushed towards the lord of the Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 28. Then, Satyaki, a man of true valor and great strength, having mounted another chariot, saw the Pāṇḍavas distressed and fallen under the sway of the Madra king. He then swiftly rushed towards the lord of the Madras.

sanskrit: आपतन्तं रथं तस्य शल्यः समितिशोभनः ।प्रत्युद्ययौ रथेनैव मत्तो मत्तमिव द्विपम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: āpatantaṁ rathaṁ tasya śalyaḥ samitiśobhanaḥ ,pratyudyayau rathenaiva matto mattamiva dvipam.
english: Śalya, who was glorious in battle, advanced with his own chariot to meet Satyaki's approaching chariot, just as a maddened elephant meets another maddened elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 29. Śalya, who was glorious in battle, advanced with his own chariot to meet Satyaki's approaching chariot, just as a maddened elephant meets another maddened elephant.

sanskrit: स संनिपातस्तुमुलो बभूवाद्भुतदर्शनः ।सात्यकेश्चैव शूरस्य मद्राणामधिपस्य च ।यादृशो वै पुरा वृत्तः शम्बरामरराजयोः ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa saṁnipātastumulo babhūvādbhutadarśanaḥ ,sātyakeścaiva śūrasya madrāṇāmadhipasya ca ,yādṛśo vai purā vṛttaḥ śambarāmararājayoḥ.
english: That tumultuous encounter, wonderful to behold, took place between the heroic Satyaki and the lord of the Madras, just as one had previously occurred between Śambara and the king of the gods (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 30. That tumultuous encounter, wonderful to behold, took place between the heroic Satyaki and the lord of the Madras, just as one had previously occurred between Śambara and the king of the gods (Indra).

sanskrit: सात्यकिः प्रेक्ष्य समरे मद्रराजं व्यवस्थितम् ।विव्याध दशभिर्बाणैस्तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: sātyakiḥ prekṣya samare madrarājaṁ vyavasthitam ,vivyādha daśabhirbāṇaistiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt.
english: Satyaki, having seen the Madra king positioned in battle, struck him with ten arrows and exclaimed, 'Halt! Halt!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 31. Satyaki, having seen the Madra king positioned in battle, struck him with ten arrows and exclaimed, 'Halt! Halt!'

sanskrit: मद्रराजस्तु सुभृशं विद्धस्तेन महात्मना ।सात्यकिं प्रतिविव्याध चित्रपुङ्खैः शितैः शरैः ॥३२॥
transliteration: madrarājastu subhṛśaṁ viddhastena mahātmanā ,sātyakiṁ prativivyādha citrapuṅkhaiḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Although greatly wounded by that great-souled Sātyaki, the king of Madra shot back at Sātyaki with sharp arrows that had variegated feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 32. Although greatly wounded by that great-souled Sātyaki, the king of Madra shot back at Sātyaki with sharp arrows that had variegated feathers.

sanskrit: ततः पार्था महेष्वासाः सात्वताभिसृतं नृपम् ।अभ्यद्रवन्रथैस्तूर्णं मातुलं वधकाम्यया ॥३३॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārthā maheṣvāsāḥ sātvatābhisṛtaṁ nṛpam ,abhyadravanrathaistūrṇaṁ mātulaṁ vadhakāmyayā.
english: Then, the Pārthas, those great archers, swiftly rushed in their chariots towards the king (their maternal uncle Madrarāja), who was being pursued by Sātyaki, desiring his death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 33. Then, the Pārthas, those great archers, swiftly rushed in their chariots towards the king (their maternal uncle Madrarāja), who was being pursued by Sātyaki, desiring his death.

sanskrit: तत आसीत्परामर्दस्तुमुलः शोणितोदकः ।शूराणां युध्यमानानां सिंहानामिव नर्दताम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tata āsītparāmardastumulaḥ śoṇitodakaḥ ,śūrāṇāṁ yudhyamānānāṁ siṁhānāmiva nardatām.
english: Then there was a tumultuous and fierce battle, with blood flowing like water, among the fighting heroes, who roared like lions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 34. Then there was a tumultuous and fierce battle, with blood flowing like water, among the fighting heroes, who roared like lions.

sanskrit: तेषामासीन्महाराज व्यतिक्षेपः परस्परम् ।सिंहानामामिषेप्सूनां कूजतामिव संयुगे ॥३५॥
transliteration: teṣāmāsīnmahārāja vyatikṣepaḥ parasparam ,siṁhānāmāmiṣepsūnāṁ kūjatāmiva saṁyuge.
english: O great king, among them there was a mutual clash, like in a battle among lions roaring and desirous of flesh.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 35. O great king, among them there was a mutual clash, like in a battle among lions roaring and desirous of flesh.

sanskrit: तेषां बाणसहस्रौघैराकीर्णा वसुधाभवत् ।अन्तरिक्षं च सहसा बाणभूतमभूत्तदा ॥३६॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ bāṇasahasraughairākīrṇā vasudhābhavat ,antarikṣaṁ ca sahasā bāṇabhūtamabhūttadā.
english: The earth became covered with a torrent of thousands of their arrows, and then suddenly the sky also became filled with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 36. The earth became covered with a torrent of thousands of their arrows, and then suddenly the sky also became filled with arrows.

sanskrit: शरान्धकारं बहुधा कृतं तत्र समन्ततः ।अभ्रच्छायेव संजज्ञे शरैर्मुक्तैर्महात्मभिः ॥३७॥
transliteration: śarāndhakāraṁ bahudhā kṛtaṁ tatra samantataḥ ,abhracchāyeva saṁjajñe śarairmuktairmahātmabhiḥ.
english: There, a manifold darkness of arrows was created everywhere. It arose like a cloud's shadow, from the arrows released by the great warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 37. There, a manifold darkness of arrows was created everywhere. It arose like a cloud's shadow, from the arrows released by the great warriors.

sanskrit: तत्र राजञ्शरैर्मुक्तैर्निर्मुक्तैरिव पन्नगैः ।स्वर्णपुङ्खैः प्रकाशद्भिर्व्यरोचन्त दिशस्तथा ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatra rājañśarairmuktairnirmuktairiva pannagaiḥ ,svarṇapuṅkhaiḥ prakāśadbhirvyarocanta diśastathā.
english: There, O king, the directions shone, made bright by the released arrows which, gleaming with golden shafts, appeared like snakes that had shed their skin.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 38. There, O king, the directions shone, made bright by the released arrows which, gleaming with golden shafts, appeared like snakes that had shed their skin.

sanskrit: तत्राद्भुतं परं चक्रे शल्यः शत्रुनिबर्हणः ।यदेकः समरे शूरो योधयामास वै बहून् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tatrādbhutaṁ paraṁ cakre śalyaḥ śatrunibarhaṇaḥ ,yadekaḥ samare śūro yodhayāmāsa vai bahūn.
english: There, Shalya, the destroyer of enemies, performed a great wonder, namely that he alone, a hero, indeed fought against many in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 39. There, Shalya, the destroyer of enemies, performed a great wonder, namely that he alone, a hero, indeed fought against many in battle.

sanskrit: मद्रराजभुजोत्सृष्टैः कङ्कबर्हिणवाजितैः ।संपतद्भिः शरैर्घोरैरवाकीर्यत मेदिनी ॥४०॥
transliteration: madrarājabhujotsṛṣṭaiḥ kaṅkabarhiṇavājitaiḥ ,saṁpatadbhiḥ śarairghorairavākīryata medinī.
english: The earth was covered with dreadful arrows, released from the arms of the king of Madras, which were adorned with the feathers of kites and peacocks, as they fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 40. The earth was covered with dreadful arrows, released from the arms of the king of Madras, which were adorned with the feathers of kites and peacocks, as they fell.

sanskrit: तत्र शल्यरथं राजन्विचरन्तं महाहवे ।अपश्याम यथा पूर्वं शक्रस्यासुरसंक्षये ॥४१॥
transliteration: tatra śalyarathaṁ rājanvicarantaṁ mahāhave ,apaśyāma yathā pūrvaṁ śakrasyāsurasaṁkṣaye.
english: O King, there, in that great battle, we saw Śalya's chariot moving about, just as we had formerly seen Indra's in the destruction of the Asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 14 Verse 41. O King, there, in that great battle, we saw Śalya's chariot moving about, just as we had formerly seen Indra's in the destruction of the Asuras.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः सैन्यास्तव विभो मद्रराजपुरस्कृताः ।पुनरभ्यद्रवन्पार्थान्वेगेन महता रणे ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ sainyāstava vibho madrarājapuraskṛtāḥ ,punarabhyadravanpārthānvegena mahatā raṇe.
english: Sañjaya said: "Then, O lord, your armies, led by the king of Madras, rushed again towards the Pārthas in the battle with great speed."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: "Then, O lord, your armies, led by the king of Madras, rushed again towards the Pārthas in the battle with great speed."

sanskrit: पीडितास्तावकाः सर्वे प्रधावन्तो रणोत्कटाः ।क्षणेनैव च पार्थांस्ते बहुत्वात्समलोडयन् ॥२॥
transliteration: pīḍitāstāvakāḥ sarve pradhāvanto raṇotkaṭāḥ ,kṣaṇenaiva ca pārthāṁste bahutvātsamaloḍayan.
english: And all your soldiers, though harassed, rushing forward and fierce in battle, in a moment overwhelmed the Pārthas due to their great numbers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. And all your soldiers, though harassed, rushing forward and fierce in battle, in a moment overwhelmed the Pārthas due to their great numbers.

sanskrit: ते वध्यमानाः कुरुभिः पाण्डवा नावतस्थिरे ।निवार्यमाणा भीमेन पश्यतोः कृष्णपार्थयोः ॥३॥
transliteration: te vadhyamānāḥ kurubhiḥ pāṇḍavā nāvatasthire ,nivāryamāṇā bhīmena paśyatoḥ kṛṣṇapārthayoḥ.
english: Being struck down by the Kurus, the Pāṇḍavas could not maintain their position. They were being restrained by Bhīma, while Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna (Pārtha) watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. Being struck down by the Kurus, the Pāṇḍavas could not maintain their position. They were being restrained by Bhīma, while Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna (Pārtha) watched.

sanskrit: ततो धनंजयः क्रुद्धः कृपं सह पदानुगैः ।अवाकिरच्छरौघेण कृतवर्माणमेव च ॥४॥
transliteration: tato dhanaṁjayaḥ kruddhaḥ kṛpaṁ saha padānugaiḥ ,avākiraccharaugheṇa kṛtavarmāṇameva ca.
english: Then, Arjuna (Dhānañjaya), enraged, showered Kṛpa along with his followers, and also Kṛtavarmā, with a volley of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. Then, Arjuna (Dhānañjaya), enraged, showered Kṛpa along with his followers, and also Kṛtavarmā, with a volley of arrows.

sanskrit: शकुनिं सहदेवस्तु सहसैन्यमवारयत् ।नकुलः पार्श्वतः स्थित्वा मद्रराजमवैक्षत ॥५॥
transliteration: śakuniṁ sahadevastu sahasainyamavārayat ,nakulaḥ pārśvataḥ sthitvā madrarājamavaikṣata.
english: Sahadeva, however, checked Śakuni along with his army. Nakula, standing to his side, observed the King of Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. Sahadeva, however, checked Śakuni along with his army. Nakula, standing to his side, observed the King of Madras.

sanskrit: द्रौपदेया नरेन्द्रांश्च भूयिष्ठं समवारयन् ।द्रोणपुत्रं च पाञ्चाल्यः शिखण्डी समवारयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: draupadeyā narendrāṁśca bhūyiṣṭhaṁ samavārayan ,droṇaputraṁ ca pāñcālyaḥ śikhaṇḍī samavārayat.
english: The sons of Draupadī mostly checked the kings. And Śikhaṇḍī, the Pāñcāla prince, checked Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. The sons of Draupadī mostly checked the kings. And Śikhaṇḍī, the Pāñcāla prince, checked Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā).

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु राजानं गदापाणिरवारयत् ।शल्यं तु सह सैन्येन कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu rājānaṁ gadāpāṇiravārayat ,śalyaṁ tu saha sainyena kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Bhīmasena, mace in hand, restrained the king. And Yudhishṭhira, Kuntī's son, confronted Śalya along with his army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. Bhīmasena, mace in hand, restrained the king. And Yudhishṭhira, Kuntī's son, confronted Śalya along with his army.

sanskrit: ततः समभवद्युद्धं संसक्तं तत्र तत्र ह ।तावकानां परेषां च संग्रामेष्वनिवर्तिनाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ samabhavadyuddhaṁ saṁsaktaṁ tatra tatra ha ,tāvakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca saṁgrāmeṣvanivartinām.
english: Then, a fierce battle ensued here and there, indeed, among your (Kaurava) warriors and those of the other side (Pāṇḍavas), who were unyielding in their engagements.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. Then, a fierce battle ensued here and there, indeed, among your (Kaurava) warriors and those of the other side (Pāṇḍavas), who were unyielding in their engagements.

sanskrit: तत्र पश्यामहे कर्म शल्यस्यातिमहद्रणे ।यदेकः सर्वसैन्यानि पाण्डवानामयुध्यत ॥९॥
transliteration: tatra paśyāmahe karma śalyasyātimahadraṇe ,yadekaḥ sarvasainyāni pāṇḍavānāmayudhyata.
english: There we witnessed Śalya's exceedingly great feat (karma) in battle, for he alone fought against all the armies of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. There we witnessed Śalya's exceedingly great feat (karma) in battle, for he alone fought against all the armies of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: व्यदृश्यत तदा शल्यो युधिष्ठिरसमीपतः ।रणे चन्द्रमसोऽभ्याशे शनैश्चर इव ग्रहः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vyadṛśyata tadā śalyo yudhiṣṭhirasamīpataḥ ,raṇe candramaso'bhyāśe śanaiścara iva grahaḥ.
english: Then, Śalya was seen near Yudhishthira, appearing like the planet Saturn near the Moon in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. Then, Śalya was seen near Yudhishthira, appearing like the planet Saturn near the Moon in battle.

sanskrit: पीडयित्वा तु राजानं शरैराशीविषोपमैः ।अभ्यधावत्पुनर्भीमं शरवर्षैरवाकिरत् ॥११॥
transliteration: pīḍayitvā tu rājānaṁ śarairāśīviṣopamaiḥ ,abhyadhāvatpunarbhīmaṁ śaravarṣairavākirat.
english: Having tormented the king with arrows resembling venomous snakes, he (Karṇa) again rushed towards Bhīma and covered him with a volley of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. Having tormented the king with arrows resembling venomous snakes, he (Karṇa) again rushed towards Bhīma and covered him with a volley of arrows.

sanskrit: तस्य तल्लाघवं दृष्ट्वा तथैव च कृतास्त्रताम् ।अपूजयन्ननीकानि परेषां तावकानि च ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasya tallāghavaṁ dṛṣṭvā tathaiva ca kṛtāstratām ,apūjayannanīkāni pareṣāṁ tāvakāni ca.
english: Seeing his (Karṇa's) swiftness and equally his mastery of weapons, both the armies of the enemies and your own forces praised him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. Seeing his (Karṇa's) swiftness and equally his mastery of weapons, both the armies of the enemies and your own forces praised him.

sanskrit: पीड्यमानास्तु शल्येन पाण्डवा भृशविक्षताः ।प्राद्रवन्त रणं हित्वा क्रोशमाने युधिष्ठिरे ॥१३॥
transliteration: pīḍyamānāstu śalyena pāṇḍavā bhṛśavikṣatāḥ ,prādravanta raṇaṁ hitvā krośamāne yudhiṣṭhire.
english: But the Pāṇḍavas, greatly wounded and being tormented by Śalya, fled the battle, abandoning the field while Yudhiṣṭhira cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. But the Pāṇḍavas, greatly wounded and being tormented by Śalya, fled the battle, abandoning the field while Yudhiṣṭhira cried out.

sanskrit: वध्यमानेष्वनीकेषु मद्रराजेन पाण्डवः ।अमर्षवशमापन्नो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।ततः पौरुषमास्थाय मद्रराजमपीडयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vadhyamāneṣvanīkeṣu madrarājena pāṇḍavaḥ ,amarṣavaśamāpanno dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tataḥ pauruṣamāsthāya madrarājamapīḍayat.
english: While the armies were being slaughtered by the king of Madras, the Pāṇḍava king Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) became filled with indignation. Then, taking recourse to his valor, he tormented the king of Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. While the armies were being slaughtered by the king of Madras, the Pāṇḍava king Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) became filled with indignation. Then, taking recourse to his valor, he tormented the king of Madras.

sanskrit: जयो वास्तु वधो वेति कृतबुद्धिर्महारथः ।समाहूयाब्रवीत्सर्वान्भ्रातॄन्कृष्णं च माधवम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: jayo vāstu vadho veti kṛtabuddhirmahārathaḥ ,samāhūyābravītsarvānbhrātṝnkṛṣṇaṁ ca mādhavam.
english: The great chariot-warrior, having firmly resolved that there would either be victory or death, summoned all his brothers and Kṛṣṇa (Madhava) and addressed them.
