search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. The great chariot-warrior, having firmly resolved that there would either be victory or death, summoned all his brothers and Kṛṣṇa (Madhava) and addressed them.

sanskrit: भीष्मो द्रोणश्च कर्णश्च ये चान्ये पृथिवीक्षितः ।कौरवार्थे पराक्रान्ताः संग्रामे निधनं गताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhīṣmo droṇaśca karṇaśca ye cānye pṛthivīkṣitaḥ ,kauravārthe parākrāntāḥ saṁgrāme nidhanaṁ gatāḥ.
english: Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and Karṇa, as well as those other kings who bravely fought for the sake of the Kauravas, have all met their death in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and Karṇa, as well as those other kings who bravely fought for the sake of the Kauravas, have all met their death in battle.

sanskrit: यथाभागं यथोत्साहं भवन्तः कृतपौरुषाः ।भागोऽवशिष्ट एकोऽयं मम शल्यो महारथः ॥१७॥
transliteration: yathābhāgaṁ yathotsāhaṁ bhavantaḥ kṛtapauruṣāḥ ,bhāgo'vaśiṣṭa eko'yaṁ mama śalyo mahārathaḥ.
english: You (honorable sirs) have all exerted your valor according to your assigned portion and enthusiasm. This one remaining portion, for me, is Śalya, the great chariot-warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 17. You (honorable sirs) have all exerted your valor according to your assigned portion and enthusiasm. This one remaining portion, for me, is Śalya, the great chariot-warrior.

sanskrit: सोऽहमद्य युधा जेतुमाशंसे मद्रकेश्वरम् ।तत्र यन्मानसं मह्यं तत्सर्वं निगदामि वः ॥१८॥
transliteration: so'hamadya yudhā jetumāśaṁse madrakeśvaram ,tatra yanmānasaṁ mahyaṁ tatsarvaṁ nigadāmi vaḥ.
english: So I, today, intend to conquer the king of the Madras (Śalya) in battle. I shall tell you all my intentions regarding that.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 18. So I, today, intend to conquer the king of the Madras (Śalya) in battle. I shall tell you all my intentions regarding that.

sanskrit: चक्ररक्षाविमौ शूरौ मम माद्रवतीसुतौ ।अजेयौ वासवेनापि समरे वीरसंमतौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: cakrarakṣāvimau śūrau mama mādravatīsutau ,ajeyau vāsavenāpi samare vīrasaṁmatau.
english: My two brave sons of Madravati (Nakula and Sahadeva), who protect the chariot wheels, are unconquerable even by Indra in battle and are highly regarded by heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 19. My two brave sons of Madravati (Nakula and Sahadeva), who protect the chariot wheels, are unconquerable even by Indra in battle and are highly regarded by heroes.

sanskrit: साध्विमौ मातुलं युद्धे क्षत्रधर्मपुरस्कृतौ ।मदर्थं प्रतियुध्येतां मानार्हौ सत्यसंगरौ ॥२०॥
transliteration: sādhvimau mātulaṁ yuddhe kṣatradharmapuraskṛtau ,madarthaṁ pratiyudhyetāṁ mānārhau satyasaṁgarau.
english: Indeed, these two, upholding the warrior's (kṣatra) intrinsic nature (dharma), worthy of honor, and true to their pledges, should fight against their maternal uncle (Śalya) in battle for my sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 20. Indeed, these two, upholding the warrior's (kṣatra) intrinsic nature (dharma), worthy of honor, and true to their pledges, should fight against their maternal uncle (Śalya) in battle for my sake.

sanskrit: मां वा शल्यो रणे हन्ता तं वाहं भद्रमस्तु वः ।इति सत्यामिमां वाणीं लोकवीरा निबोधत ॥२१॥
transliteration: māṁ vā śalyo raṇe hantā taṁ vāhaṁ bhadramastu vaḥ ,iti satyāmimāṁ vāṇīṁ lokavīrā nibodhata.
english: Either Śalya will kill me in battle, or I will kill him. May auspiciousness (bhadra) be upon you all! O heroes of the world, comprehend this true declaration.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 21. Either Śalya will kill me in battle, or I will kill him. May auspiciousness (bhadra) be upon you all! O heroes of the world, comprehend this true declaration.

sanskrit: योत्स्येऽहं मातुलेनाद्य क्षत्रधर्मेण पार्थिवाः ।स्वयं समभिसंधाय विजयायेतराय वा ॥२२॥
transliteration: yotsye'haṁ mātulenādya kṣatradharmeṇa pārthivāḥ ,svayaṁ samabhisaṁdhāya vijayāyetarāya vā.
english: O kings, I shall fight today with my maternal uncle, upholding the warrior's (kṣatra) intrinsic nature (dharma), having personally resolved myself for either victory or the alternative (death/defeat).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 22. O kings, I shall fight today with my maternal uncle, upholding the warrior's (kṣatra) intrinsic nature (dharma), having personally resolved myself for either victory or the alternative (death/defeat).

sanskrit: तस्य मेऽभ्यधिकं शस्त्रं सर्वोपकरणानि च ।संयुञ्जन्तु रणे क्षिप्रं शास्त्रवद्रथयोजकाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tasya me'bhyadhikaṁ śastraṁ sarvopakaraṇāni ca ,saṁyuñjantu raṇe kṣipraṁ śāstravadrathayojakāḥ.
english: For that purpose, let the charioteers quickly equip my chariot with superior weapons and all equipment, in battle, according to the treatises (śāstra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 23. For that purpose, let the charioteers quickly equip my chariot with superior weapons and all equipment, in battle, according to the treatises (śāstra).

sanskrit: शैनेयो दक्षिणं चक्रं धृष्टद्युम्नस्तथोत्तरम् ।पृष्ठगोपो भवत्वद्य मम पार्थो धनंजयः ॥२४॥
transliteration: śaineyo dakṣiṇaṁ cakraṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnastathottaram ,pṛṣṭhagopo bhavatvadya mama pārtho dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Let Śaineya guard the right wheel, and similarly, Dhṛṣṭadyumna the left. Let Arjuna (Pārtha Dhanaṃjaya) be my rear guard today.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 24. Let Śaineya guard the right wheel, and similarly, Dhṛṣṭadyumna the left. Let Arjuna (Pārtha Dhanaṃjaya) be my rear guard today.

sanskrit: पुरःसरो ममाद्यास्तु भीमः शस्त्रभृतां वरः ।एवमभ्यधिकः शल्याद्भविष्यामि महामृधे ॥२५॥
transliteration: puraḥsaro mamādyāstu bhīmaḥ śastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,evamabhyadhikaḥ śalyādbhaviṣyāmi mahāmṛdhe.
english: Let Bhīma, the best among weapon-bearers, be my vanguard today. Thus, I shall be superior to Śalya in this great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 25. Let Bhīma, the best among weapon-bearers, be my vanguard today. Thus, I shall be superior to Śalya in this great battle.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तास्तथा चक्रुः सर्वे राज्ञः प्रियैषिणः ।ततः प्रहर्षः सैन्यानां पुनरासीत्तदा नृप ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktāstathā cakruḥ sarve rājñaḥ priyaiṣiṇaḥ ,tataḥ praharṣaḥ sainyānāṁ punarāsīttadā nṛpa.
english: O King, thus addressed, all the king's well-wishers acted accordingly. Then, great joy again arose among the armies at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 26. O King, thus addressed, all the king's well-wishers acted accordingly. Then, great joy again arose among the armies at that time.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालानां सोमकानां मत्स्यानां च विशेषतः ।प्रतिज्ञां तां च संग्रामे धर्मराजस्य पूरयन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: pāñcālānāṁ somakānāṁ matsyānāṁ ca viśeṣataḥ ,pratijñāṁ tāṁ ca saṁgrāme dharmarājasya pūrayan.
english: And fulfilling that vow of the king of righteousness (dharmarāja) in battle, especially on behalf of the Pañcālas, Somakas, and Matsyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 27. And fulfilling that vow of the king of righteousness (dharmarāja) in battle, especially on behalf of the Pañcālas, Somakas, and Matsyas.

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खांश्च भेरीश्च शतशश्चैव पुष्करान् ।अवादयन्त पाञ्चालाः सिंहनादांश्च नेदिरे ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhāṁśca bherīśca śataśaścaiva puṣkarān ,avādayanta pāñcālāḥ siṁhanādāṁśca nedire.
english: Then, the Pañcālas blew conches, and also drums (bherī) and hundreds of other musical instruments (puṣkarān), and they also let out lion-roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 28. Then, the Pañcālas blew conches, and also drums (bherī) and hundreds of other musical instruments (puṣkarān), and they also let out lion-roars.

sanskrit: तेऽभ्यधावन्त संरब्धा मद्रराजं तरस्विनः ।महता हर्षजेनाथ नादेन कुरुपुंगवाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: te'bhyadhāvanta saṁrabdhā madrarājaṁ tarasvinaḥ ,mahatā harṣajenātha nādena kurupuṁgavāḥ.
english: Those mighty and enraged best of the Kurus (kurupuṅgavāḥ) then rushed towards the king of Madras (madrarāja) with a great, joyful roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 29. Those mighty and enraged best of the Kurus (kurupuṅgavāḥ) then rushed towards the king of Madras (madrarāja) with a great, joyful roar.

sanskrit: ह्रादेन गजघण्टानां शङ्खानां निनदेन च ।तूर्यशब्देन महता नादयन्तश्च मेदिनीम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: hrādena gajaghaṇṭānāṁ śaṅkhānāṁ ninadena ca ,tūryaśabdena mahatā nādayantaśca medinīm.
english: And with the roar of elephant bells, the blare of conches, and the great sound of trumpets, they were making the earth resound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 30. And with the roar of elephant bells, the blare of conches, and the great sound of trumpets, they were making the earth resound.

sanskrit: तान्प्रत्यगृह्णात्पुत्रस्ते मद्रराजश्च वीर्यवान् ।महामेघानिव बहूञ्शैलावस्तोदयावुभौ ॥३१॥
transliteration: tānpratyagṛhṇātputraste madrarājaśca vīryavān ,mahāmeghāniva bahūñśailāvastodayāvubhau.
english: Your valiant son and the powerful king of Madra stood firm against them, just as the two mountains, Asta (setting) and Udaya (rising), withstand many great clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 31. Your valiant son and the powerful king of Madra stood firm against them, just as the two mountains, Asta (setting) and Udaya (rising), withstand many great clouds.

sanskrit: शल्यस्तु समरश्लाघी धर्मराजमरिंदमम् ।ववर्ष शरवर्षेण वर्षेण मघवानिव ॥३२॥
transliteration: śalyastu samaraślāghī dharmarājamariṁdamam ,vavarṣa śaravarṣeṇa varṣeṇa maghavāniva.
english: But Śalya, proud of his skill in battle, showered the enemy-subduing king Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja) with an arrow-rain, just as Indra (Maghavān) showers with rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 32. But Śalya, proud of his skill in battle, showered the enemy-subduing king Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmarāja) with an arrow-rain, just as Indra (Maghavān) showers with rain.

sanskrit: तथैव कुरुराजोऽपि प्रगृह्य रुचिरं धनुः ।द्रोणोपदेशान्विविधान्दर्शयानो महामनाः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tathaiva kururājo'pi pragṛhya ruciraṁ dhanuḥ ,droṇopadeśānvividhāndarśayāno mahāmanāḥ.
english: Similarly, the great-minded Kuru king (Duryodhana) also, having grasped his splendid bow, displayed various teachings he had received from Droṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 33. Similarly, the great-minded Kuru king (Duryodhana) also, having grasped his splendid bow, displayed various teachings he had received from Droṇa.

sanskrit: ववर्ष शरवर्षाणि चित्रं लघु च सुष्ठु च ।न चास्य विवरं कश्चिद्ददर्श चरतो रणे ॥३४॥
transliteration: vavarṣa śaravarṣāṇi citraṁ laghu ca suṣṭhu ca ,na cāsya vivaraṁ kaściddadarśa carato raṇe.
english: He showered arrows in an amazing, swift, and excellent manner. And no one could perceive any opening in him as he moved (and fought) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 34. He showered arrows in an amazing, swift, and excellent manner. And no one could perceive any opening in him as he moved (and fought) in battle.

sanskrit: तावुभौ विविधैर्बाणैस्ततक्षाते परस्परम् ।शार्दूलावामिषप्रेप्सू पराक्रान्ताविवाहवे ॥३५॥
transliteration: tāvubhau vividhairbāṇaistatakṣāte parasparam ,śārdūlāvāmiṣaprepsū parākrāntāvivāhave.
english: Both of them, like two mighty tigers eager for prey, pierced each other with various arrows in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 35. Both of them, like two mighty tigers eager for prey, pierced each other with various arrows in battle.

sanskrit: भीमस्तु तव पुत्रेण रणशौण्डेन संगतः ।पाञ्चाल्यः सात्यकिश्चैव माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।शकुनिप्रमुखान्वीरान्प्रत्यगृह्णन्समन्ततः ॥३६॥
transliteration: bhīmastu tava putreṇa raṇaśauṇḍena saṁgataḥ ,pāñcālyaḥ sātyakiścaiva mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,śakunipramukhānvīrānpratyagṛhṇansamantataḥ.
english: Bhīma, however, confronted your son, who was skilled in battle. Pāñcālya (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), Sātyaki, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, Nakula and Sahadeva, similarly engaged the warriors led by Śakuni from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 36. Bhīma, however, confronted your son, who was skilled in battle. Pāñcālya (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), Sātyaki, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, Nakula and Sahadeva, similarly engaged the warriors led by Śakuni from all directions.

sanskrit: तदासीत्तुमुलं युद्धं पुनरेव जयैषिणाम् ।तावकानां परेषां च राजन्दुर्मन्त्रिते तव ॥३७॥
transliteration: tadāsīttumulaṁ yuddhaṁ punareva jayaiṣiṇām ,tāvakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca rājandurmantrite tava.
english: O King, there was again a tumultuous battle between your side and the adversaries, both desirous of victory, due to your misguided policy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 37. O King, there was again a tumultuous battle between your side and the adversaries, both desirous of victory, due to your misguided policy.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु भीमस्य शरेणानतपर्वणा ।चिच्छेदादिश्य संग्रामे ध्वजं हेमविभूषितम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu bhīmasya śareṇānataparvaṇā ,cicchedādiśya saṁgrāme dhvajaṁ hemavibhūṣitam.
english: Duryodhana, however, in battle, aimed and severed Bhīma's gold-adorned banner with an arrow having bent shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 38. Duryodhana, however, in battle, aimed and severed Bhīma's gold-adorned banner with an arrow having bent shafts.

sanskrit: सकिङ्किणीकजालेन महता चारुदर्शनः ।पपात रुचिरः सिंहो भीमसेनस्य नानदन् ॥३९॥
transliteration: sakiṅkiṇīkajālena mahatā cārudarśanaḥ ,papāta ruciraḥ siṁho bhīmasenasya nānadan.
english: The beautiful lion, charming to behold and adorned with a great network of small bells, fell down roaring for Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 39. The beautiful lion, charming to behold and adorned with a great network of small bells, fell down roaring for Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: पुनश्चास्य धनुश्चित्रं गजराजकरोपमम् ।क्षुरेण शितधारेण प्रचकर्त नराधिपः ॥४०॥
transliteration: punaścāsya dhanuścitraṁ gajarājakaropamam ,kṣureṇa śitadhāreṇa pracakarta narādhipaḥ.
english: And again, the king cut his wondrous bow, which resembled the trunk of a lordly elephant, with a sharp-edged razor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 40. And again, the king cut his wondrous bow, which resembled the trunk of a lordly elephant, with a sharp-edged razor.

sanskrit: स च्छिन्नधन्वा तेजस्वी रथशक्त्या सुतं तव ।बिभेदोरसि विक्रम्य स रथोपस्थ आविशत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: sa cchinnadhanvā tejasvī rathaśaktyā sutaṁ tava ,bibhedorasi vikramya sa rathopastha āviśat.
english: That glorious one, whose bow was broken, having acted heroically, struck your son in the chest with a chariot spear; and that (son) then slumped into the chariot's seat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 41. That glorious one, whose bow was broken, having acted heroically, struck your son in the chest with a chariot spear; and that (son) then slumped into the chariot's seat.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्मोहमनुप्राप्ते पुनरेव वृकोदरः ।यन्तुरेव शिरः कायात्क्षुरप्रेणाहरत्तदा ॥४२॥
transliteration: tasminmohamanuprāpte punareva vṛkodaraḥ ,yantureva śiraḥ kāyātkṣurapreṇāharattadā.
english: When he had thus fallen into a swoon, then again Vṛkodara (Bhīmasena), with a razor-arrow, severed the charioteer's head from his body at that very moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 42. When he had thus fallen into a swoon, then again Vṛkodara (Bhīmasena), with a razor-arrow, severed the charioteer's head from his body at that very moment.

sanskrit: हतसूता हयास्तस्य रथमादाय भारत ।व्यद्रवन्त दिशो राजन्हाहाकारस्तदाभवत् ॥४३॥
transliteration: hatasūtā hayāstasya rathamādāya bhārata ,vyadravanta diśo rājanhāhākārastadābhavat.
english: O Bhārata, O King, his horses, their charioteer having been slain, fled in all directions, carrying his chariot. Then, a great cry of lamentation arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 43. O Bhārata, O King, his horses, their charioteer having been slain, fled in all directions, carrying his chariot. Then, a great cry of lamentation arose.

sanskrit: तमभ्यधावत्त्राणार्थं द्रोणपुत्रो महारथः ।कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च पुत्रं तेऽभिपरीप्सवः ॥४४॥
transliteration: tamabhyadhāvattrāṇārthaṁ droṇaputro mahārathaḥ ,kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca putraṁ te'bhiparīpsavaḥ.
english: The great warrior, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), along with Kṛpa and Kṛtavarmā, ran towards him, wishing to protect your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 44. The great warrior, Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāman), along with Kṛpa and Kṛtavarmā, ran towards him, wishing to protect your son.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्विलुलिते सैन्ये त्रस्तास्तस्य पदानुगाः ।गाण्डीवधन्वा विस्फार्य धनुस्तानहनच्छरैः ॥४५॥
transliteration: tasminvilulite sainye trastāstasya padānugāḥ ,gāṇḍīvadhanvā visphārya dhanustānahanaccharaiḥ.
english: As that army was scattered and routed, his (Duryodhana's) infantry became terrified. Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, having drawn his bow, struck them with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 45. As that army was scattered and routed, his (Duryodhana's) infantry became terrified. Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, having drawn his bow, struck them with arrows.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु मद्रेशमभ्यधावदमर्षितः ।स्वयं संचोदयन्नश्वान्दन्तवर्णान्मनोजवान् ॥४६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu madreśamabhyadhāvadamarṣitaḥ ,svayaṁ saṁcodayannaśvāndantavarṇānmanojavān.
english: But Yudhiṣṭhira, enraged, himself urging on his ivory-colored, mind-swift horses, rushed towards the king of Madras (Śalya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 46. But Yudhiṣṭhira, enraged, himself urging on his ivory-colored, mind-swift horses, rushed towards the king of Madras (Śalya).

sanskrit: तत्राद्भुतमपश्याम कुन्तीपुत्रे युधिष्ठिरे ।पुरा भूत्वा मृदुर्दान्तो यत्तदा दारुणोऽभवत् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tatrādbhutamapaśyāma kuntīputre yudhiṣṭhire ,purā bhūtvā mṛdurdānto yattadā dāruṇo'bhavat.
english: There, we saw a marvel in Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti: he, who was formerly gentle and self-controlled, had then become fierce.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 47. There, we saw a marvel in Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti: he, who was formerly gentle and self-controlled, had then become fierce.

sanskrit: विवृताक्षश्च कौन्तेयो वेपमानश्च मन्युना ।चिच्छेद योधान्निशितैः शरैः शतसहस्रशः ॥४८॥
transliteration: vivṛtākṣaśca kaunteyo vepamānaśca manyunā ,ciccheda yodhānniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ śatasahasraśaḥ.
english: And the son of Kunti (Kaunteya), with wide-open eyes and trembling with fury, cut down warriors by the hundreds of thousands with his sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 48. And the son of Kunti (Kaunteya), with wide-open eyes and trembling with fury, cut down warriors by the hundreds of thousands with his sharp arrows.

sanskrit: यां यां प्रत्युद्ययौ सेनां तां तां ज्येष्ठः स पाण्डवः ।शरैरपातयद्राजन्गिरीन्वज्रैरिवोत्तमैः ॥४९॥
transliteration: yāṁ yāṁ pratyudyayau senāṁ tāṁ tāṁ jyeṣṭhaḥ sa pāṇḍavaḥ ,śarairapātayadrājangirīnvajrairivottamaiḥ.
english: O King, whichever army that eldest Pandava attacked, he struck it down with arrows, just as (Indra strikes down) mountains with excellent thunderbolts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 49. O King, whichever army that eldest Pandava attacked, he struck it down with arrows, just as (Indra strikes down) mountains with excellent thunderbolts.

sanskrit: साश्वसूतध्वजरथान्रथिनः पातयन्बहून् ।आक्रीडदेको बलवान्पवनस्तोयदानिव ॥५०॥
transliteration: sāśvasūtadhvajarathānrathinaḥ pātayanbahūn ,ākrīḍadeko balavānpavanastoyadāniva.
english: The powerful warrior, acting alone, striking down many chariot-warriors (rathin) complete with their horses, charioteers, banners, and chariots, toyed (with them) like the wind (god) with clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 50. The powerful warrior, acting alone, striking down many chariot-warriors (rathin) complete with their horses, charioteers, banners, and chariots, toyed (with them) like the wind (god) with clouds.

sanskrit: साश्वारोहांश्च तुरगान्पत्तींश्चैव सहस्रशः ।व्यपोथयत संग्रामे क्रुद्धो रुद्रः पशूनिव ॥५१॥
transliteration: sāśvārohāṁśca turagānpattīṁścaiva sahasraśaḥ ,vyapothayata saṁgrāme kruddho rudraḥ paśūniva.
english: Like an enraged Rudra [Rudra] crushes animals, he destroyed thousands of horses with their riders and infantrymen in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 51. Like an enraged Rudra [Rudra] crushes animals, he destroyed thousands of horses with their riders and infantrymen in the battle.

sanskrit: शून्यमायोधनं कृत्वा शरवर्षैः समन्ततः ।अभ्यद्रवत मद्रेशं तिष्ठ शल्येति चाब्रवीत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: śūnyamāyodhanaṁ kṛtvā śaravarṣaiḥ samantataḥ ,abhyadravata madreśaṁ tiṣṭha śalyeti cābravīt.
english: After making the battlefield empty everywhere with showers of arrows, he rushed towards the king of Madras and exclaimed, "Stop, Śalya!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 52. After making the battlefield empty everywhere with showers of arrows, he rushed towards the king of Madras and exclaimed, "Stop, Śalya!"

sanskrit: तस्य तच्चरितं दृष्ट्वा संग्रामे भीमकर्मणः ।वित्रेसुस्तावकाः सर्वे शल्यस्त्वेनं समभ्ययात् ॥५३॥
transliteration: tasya taccaritaṁ dṛṣṭvā saṁgrāme bhīmakarmaṇaḥ ,vitresustāvakāḥ sarve śalyastvenaṁ samabhyayāt.
english: Seeing that terrible deed of his in battle – (the deed) of the one who performed fearful acts – all your men became terrified, but Śalya himself advanced towards him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 53. Seeing that terrible deed of his in battle – (the deed) of the one who performed fearful acts – all your men became terrified, but Śalya himself advanced towards him.

sanskrit: ततस्तौ तु सुसंरब्धौ प्रध्माप्य सलिलोद्भवौ ।समाहूय तदान्योन्यं भर्त्सयन्तौ समीयतुः ॥५४॥
transliteration: tatastau tu susaṁrabdhau pradhmāpya salilodbhavau ,samāhūya tadānyonyaṁ bhartsayantau samīyatuḥ.
english: Then, those two (warriors), greatly enraged, having blown their conches (literally, "born from water"), and having challenged each other, advanced while reviling one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 54. Then, those two (warriors), greatly enraged, having blown their conches (literally, "born from water"), and having challenged each other, advanced while reviling one another.

sanskrit: शल्यस्तु शरवर्षेण युधिष्ठिरमवाकिरत् ।मद्रराजं च कौन्तेयः शरवर्षैरवाकिरत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: śalyastu śaravarṣeṇa yudhiṣṭhiramavākirat ,madrarājaṁ ca kaunteyaḥ śaravarṣairavākirat.
english: Śalya, on his part, showered Yudhiṣṭhira with arrows. In turn, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, showered the king of Madras (Śalya) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 55. Śalya, on his part, showered Yudhiṣṭhira with arrows. In turn, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, showered the king of Madras (Śalya) with arrows.

sanskrit: व्यदृश्येतां तदा राजन्कङ्कपत्रिभिराहवे ।उद्भिन्नरुधिरौ शूरौ मद्रराजयुधिष्ठिरौ ॥५६॥
transliteration: vyadṛśyetāṁ tadā rājankaṅkapatribhirāhave ,udbhinnarudhirau śūrau madrarājayudhiṣṭhirau.
english: O King, in that battle, the two heroes, Śalya, the king of Madras, and Yudhiṣṭhira, appeared covered in gushing blood from arrows feathered with Kanka feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 56. O King, in that battle, the two heroes, Śalya, the king of Madras, and Yudhiṣṭhira, appeared covered in gushing blood from arrows feathered with Kanka feathers.

sanskrit: पुष्पिताविव रेजाते वने शल्मलिकिंशुकौ ।दीप्यमानौ महात्मानौ प्राणयोर्युद्धदुर्मदौ ॥५७॥
transliteration: puṣpitāviva rejāte vane śalmalikiṁśukau ,dīpyamānau mahātmānau prāṇayoryuddhadurmadau.
english: The two great souls (mahātman), fiercely engaged in battle, shone like two blossoming Śālmali and Kiṃśuka trees in a forest, both appearing ablaze and intoxicated by the fight for their very lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 57. The two great souls (mahātman), fiercely engaged in battle, shone like two blossoming Śālmali and Kiṃśuka trees in a forest, both appearing ablaze and intoxicated by the fight for their very lives.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा सर्वाणि सैन्यानि नाध्यवस्यंस्तयोर्जयम् ।हत्वा मद्राधिपं पार्थो भोक्ष्यतेऽद्य वसुंधराम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā sarvāṇi sainyāni nādhyavasyaṁstayorjayam ,hatvā madrādhipaṁ pārtho bhokṣyate'dya vasuṁdharām.
english: All the armies, witnessing this, could not ascertain victory for either of the two. (They speculated,) "After killing the lord of Madras (Śalya), Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira) will surely rule the earth today."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 58. All the armies, witnessing this, could not ascertain victory for either of the two. (They speculated,) "After killing the lord of Madras (Śalya), Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira) will surely rule the earth today."

sanskrit: शल्यो वा पाण्डवं हत्वा दद्याद्दुर्योधनाय गाम् ।इतीव निश्चयो नाभूद्योधानां तत्र भारत ॥५९॥
transliteration: śalyo vā pāṇḍavaṁ hatvā dadyādduryodhanāya gām ,itīva niścayo nābhūdyodhānāṁ tatra bhārata.
english: Among the warriors there, O Bhārata, there was no such certainty that Śalya, having killed the Pāṇḍava, would grant victory to Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 59. Among the warriors there, O Bhārata, there was no such certainty that Śalya, having killed the Pāṇḍava, would grant victory to Duryodhana.

sanskrit: प्रदक्षिणमभूत्सर्वंधर्मराजस्य युध्यतः ॥६०॥
transliteration: pradakṣiṇamabhūtsarvaṁdharmarājasya yudhyataḥ.
english: All became auspicious for Dharmarāja, the king of natural law (dharma), as he fought.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 60. All became auspicious for Dharmarāja, the king of natural law (dharma), as he fought.

sanskrit: ततः शरशतं शल्यो मुमोचाशु युधिष्ठिरे ।धनुश्चास्य शिताग्रेण बाणेन निरकृन्तत ॥६१॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaraśataṁ śalyo mumocāśu yudhiṣṭhire ,dhanuścāsya śitāgreṇa bāṇena nirakṛntata.
english: Then, Śalya swiftly loosed a hundred arrows at Yudhishthira. And with a sharp-pointed arrow, he severed Yudhishthira's bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 61. Then, Śalya swiftly loosed a hundred arrows at Yudhishthira. And with a sharp-pointed arrow, he severed Yudhishthira's bow.

sanskrit: सोऽन्यत्कार्मुकमादाय शल्यं शरशतैस्त्रिभिः ।अविध्यत्कार्मुकं चास्य क्षुरेण निरकृन्तत ॥६२॥
transliteration: so'nyatkārmukamādāya śalyaṁ śaraśataistribhiḥ ,avidhyatkārmukaṁ cāsya kṣureṇa nirakṛntata.
english: Taking up another bow, he (Yudhishthira) pierced Śalya with three hundred arrows. And with a razor-sharp arrow, he cut off Śalya's bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 62. Taking up another bow, he (Yudhishthira) pierced Śalya with three hundred arrows. And with a razor-sharp arrow, he cut off Śalya's bow.

sanskrit: अथास्य निजघानाश्वांश्चतुरो नतपर्वभिः ।द्वाभ्यामथ शिताग्राभ्यामुभौ च पार्ष्णिसारथी ॥६३॥
transliteration: athāsya nijaghānāśvāṁścaturo nataparvabhiḥ ,dvābhyāmatha śitāgrābhyāmubhau ca pārṣṇisārathī.
english: He then struck down his four horses with arrows that had sharp nodes. And with two sharp-tipped arrows, he also struck down both the charioteers (pārṣṇisārathī) who were driving from the rear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 63. He then struck down his four horses with arrows that had sharp nodes. And with two sharp-tipped arrows, he also struck down both the charioteers (pārṣṇisārathī) who were driving from the rear.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्य दीप्यमानेन पीतेन निशितेन च ।प्रमुखे वर्तमानस्य भल्लेनापाहरद्ध्वजम् ।ततः प्रभग्नं तत्सैन्यं दौर्योधनमरिंदम ॥६४॥
transliteration: tato'sya dīpyamānena pītena niśitena ca ,pramukhe vartamānasya bhallenāpāharaddhvajam ,tataḥ prabhagnaṁ tatsainyaṁ dauryodhanamariṁdama.
english: Then, with a shining, yellow, and sharp broad-headed arrow (bhalla), he struck down the banner of the one who was standing at the forefront. Thereupon, O subduer of enemies (arindama), that army belonging to Duryodhana was completely shattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 64. Then, with a shining, yellow, and sharp broad-headed arrow (bhalla), he struck down the banner of the one who was standing at the forefront. Thereupon, O subduer of enemies (arindama), that army belonging to Duryodhana was completely shattered.

sanskrit: ततो मद्राधिपं द्रौणिरभ्यधावत्तथाकृतम् ।आरोप्य चैनं स्वरथं त्वरमाणः प्रदुद्रुवे ॥६५॥
transliteration: tato madrādhipaṁ drauṇirabhyadhāvattathākṛtam ,āropya cainaṁ svarathaṁ tvaramāṇaḥ pradudruve.
english: Then Drauṇi, Dronacharya's son, rushed towards the king of Madra, who had been thus afflicted. Placing him upon his own chariot, he swiftly drove away.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 65. Then Drauṇi, Dronacharya's son, rushed towards the king of Madra, who had been thus afflicted. Placing him upon his own chariot, he swiftly drove away.

sanskrit: मुहूर्तमिव तौ गत्वा नर्दमाने युधिष्ठिरे ।स्थित्वा ततो मद्रपतिरन्यं स्यन्दनमास्थितः ॥६६॥
transliteration: muhūrtamiva tau gatvā nardamāne yudhiṣṭhire ,sthitvā tato madrapatiranyaṁ syandanamāsthitaḥ.
english: After those two had gone for about a moment, and while Yudhishthira was roaring, the king of Madra stopped. Then, he mounted another chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 66. After those two had gone for about a moment, and while Yudhishthira was roaring, the king of Madra stopped. Then, he mounted another chariot.

sanskrit: विधिवत्कल्पितं शुभ्रं महाम्बुदनिनादिनम् ।सज्जयन्त्रोपकरणं द्विषतां रोमहर्षणम् ॥६७॥
transliteration: vidhivatkalpitaṁ śubhraṁ mahāmbudaninādinam ,sajjayantropakaraṇaṁ dviṣatāṁ romaharṣaṇam.
english: It was properly constructed, shining brightly, roaring like a great cloud, equipped with suitable instruments, and caused the hair of enemies to stand on end.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 15 Verse 67. It was properly constructed, shining brightly, roaring like a great cloud, equipped with suitable instruments, and caused the hair of enemies to stand on end.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अथान्यद्धनुरादाय बलवद्वेगवत्तरम् ।युधिष्ठिरं मद्रपतिर्विद्ध्वा सिंह इवानदत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,athānyaddhanurādāya balavadvegavattaram ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ madrapatirviddhvā siṁha ivānadat.
english: Sañjaya said: Then, the king of Madra, taking another bow that was even more powerful and faster, roared like a lion after piercing Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then, the king of Madra, taking another bow that was even more powerful and faster, roared like a lion after piercing Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: ततः स शरवर्षेण पर्जन्य इव वृष्टिमान् ।अभ्यवर्षदमेयात्मा क्षत्रियान्क्षत्रियर्षभः ॥२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa śaravarṣeṇa parjanya iva vṛṣṭimān ,abhyavarṣadameyātmā kṣatriyānkṣatriyarṣabhaḥ.
english: Then he, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable and who was the best among Kṣatriyas, rained arrows upon the Kṣatriyas like a rain-bearing cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. Then he, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable and who was the best among Kṣatriyas, rained arrows upon the Kṣatriyas like a rain-bearing cloud.

sanskrit: सात्यकिं दशभिर्विद्ध्वा भीमसेनं त्रिभिः शरैः ।सहदेवं त्रिभिर्विद्ध्वा युधिष्ठिरमपीडयत् ॥३॥
transliteration: sātyakiṁ daśabhirviddhvā bhīmasenaṁ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,sahadevaṁ tribhirviddhvā yudhiṣṭhiramapīḍayat.
english: Having pierced Sātyaki with ten (arrows), and Bhīmasena with three arrows, and having pierced Sahadeva with three (arrows), he then distressed Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. Having pierced Sātyaki with ten (arrows), and Bhīmasena with three arrows, and having pierced Sahadeva with three (arrows), he then distressed Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: तांस्तानन्यान्महेष्वासान्साश्वान्सरथकुञ्जरान् ।कुञ्जरान्कुञ्जरारोहानश्वानश्वप्रयायिनः ।रथांश्च रथिभिः सार्धं जघान रथिनां वरः ॥४॥
transliteration: tāṁstānanyānmaheṣvāsānsāśvānsarathakuñjarān ,kuñjarānkuñjarārohānaśvānaśvaprayāyinaḥ ,rathāṁśca rathibhiḥ sārdhaṁ jaghāna rathināṁ varaḥ.
english: That foremost among charioteers (rathinām varaḥ) slew those various other great archers, along with their horses, chariots, and elephants. He also killed elephants with their riders, horses with their drivers, and chariots together with their charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. That foremost among charioteers (rathinām varaḥ) slew those various other great archers, along with their horses, chariots, and elephants. He also killed elephants with their riders, horses with their drivers, and chariots together with their charioteers.

sanskrit: बाहूंश्चिच्छेद च तथा सायुधान्केतनानि च ।चकार च महीं योधैस्तीर्णां वेदीं कुशैरिव ॥५॥
transliteration: bāhūṁściccheda ca tathā sāyudhānketanāni ca ,cakāra ca mahīṁ yodhaistīrṇāṁ vedīṁ kuśairiva.
english: And similarly, he cut off arms along with their weapons, and also banners. He also made the earth strewn with warriors, just as an altar is covered with kuśa grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. And similarly, he cut off arms along with their weapons, and also banners. He also made the earth strewn with warriors, just as an altar is covered with kuśa grass.

sanskrit: तथा तमरिसैन्यानि घ्नन्तं मृत्युमिवान्तकम् ।परिवव्रुर्भृशं क्रुद्धाः पाण्डुपाञ्चालसोमकाः ॥६॥
transliteration: tathā tamarisainyāni ghnantaṁ mṛtyumivāntakam ,parivavrurbhṛśaṁ kruddhāḥ pāṇḍupāñcālasomakāḥ.
english: Thus, the Pāṇḍavas, Pāñcālas, and Somakas, greatly enraged, surrounded him as he was slaying the enemy armies, like death (mṛtyu) or the destroyer (antaka).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. Thus, the Pāṇḍavas, Pāñcālas, and Somakas, greatly enraged, surrounded him as he was slaying the enemy armies, like death (mṛtyu) or the destroyer (antaka).

sanskrit: तं भीमसेनश्च शिनेश्च नप्ता माद्र्याश्च पुत्रौ पुरुषप्रवीरौ ।समागतं भीमबलेन राज्ञा पर्यापुरन्योन्यमथाह्वयन्तः ॥७॥
transliteration: taṁ bhīmasenaśca śineśca naptā; mādryāśca putrau puruṣapravīrau ,samāgataṁ bhīmabalena rājñā; paryāpuranyonyamathāhvayantaḥ.
english: Then, Bhīmasena, the grandson of Śini (Sātyaki), and the two foremost among men, the sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), surrounded him (the enemy warrior) – who had joined forces with King Bhīmabala – all the while challenging each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. Then, Bhīmasena, the grandson of Śini (Sātyaki), and the two foremost among men, the sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), surrounded him (the enemy warrior) – who had joined forces with King Bhīmabala – all the while challenging each other.

sanskrit: ततस्तु शूराः समरे नरेन्द्रं मद्रेश्वरं प्राप्य युधां वरिष्ठम् ।आवार्य चैनं समरे नृवीरा जघ्नुः शरैः पत्रिभिरुग्रवेगैः ॥८॥
transliteration: tatastu śūrāḥ samare narendraṁ; madreśvaraṁ prāpya yudhāṁ variṣṭham ,āvārya cainaṁ samare nṛvīrā; jaghnuḥ śaraiḥ patribhirugravegaiḥ.
english: Then, the brave warriors, these heroes among men, having confronted King Śalya, the lord of the Madras and the foremost among fighters, in battle, surrounded him there and struck him with fiercely-flying, feathered arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. Then, the brave warriors, these heroes among men, having confronted King Śalya, the lord of the Madras and the foremost among fighters, in battle, surrounded him there and struck him with fiercely-flying, feathered arrows.

sanskrit: संरक्षितो भीमसेनेन राजा माद्रीसुताभ्यामथ माधवेन ।मद्राधिपं पत्रिभिरुग्रवेगैः स्तनान्तरे धर्मसुतो निजघ्ने ॥९॥
transliteration: saṁrakṣito bhīmasenena rājā; mādrīsutābhyāmatha mādhavena ,madrādhipaṁ patribhirugravegaiḥ; stanāntare dharmasuto nijaghne.
english: The king (Yudhiṣṭhira), protected by Bhīmasena, by the two sons of Mādrī, and by Kṛṣṇa (Mādhava), then the son of Dharma (dharma-suta) struck the king of the Madras in the chest with fiercely-flying feathered arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. The king (Yudhiṣṭhira), protected by Bhīmasena, by the two sons of Mādrī, and by Kṛṣṇa (Mādhava), then the son of Dharma (dharma-suta) struck the king of the Madras in the chest with fiercely-flying feathered arrows.

sanskrit: ततो रणे तावकानां रथौघाः समीक्ष्य मद्राधिपतिं शरार्तम् ।पर्यावव्रुः प्रवराः सर्वशश्च दुर्योधनस्यानुमते समन्तात् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato raṇe tāvakānāṁ rathaughāḥ; samīkṣya madrādhipatiṁ śarārtam ,paryāvavruḥ pravarāḥ sarvaśaśca; duryodhanasyānumate samantāt.
english: Then, in the battle, the excellent chariots of your (Kaurava) side, seeing the lord of the Madras tormented by arrows, surrounded him completely from all directions with Duryodhana's approval.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. Then, in the battle, the excellent chariots of your (Kaurava) side, seeing the lord of the Madras tormented by arrows, surrounded him completely from all directions with Duryodhana's approval.

sanskrit: ततो द्रुतं मद्रजनाधिपो रणे युधिष्ठिरं सप्तभिरभ्यविध्यत् ।तं चापि पार्थो नवभिः पृषत्कैर्विव्याध राजंस्तुमुले महात्मा ॥११॥
transliteration: tato drutaṁ madrajanādhipo raṇe; yudhiṣṭhiraṁ saptabhirabhyavidhyat ,taṁ cāpi pārtho navabhiḥ pṛṣatkai;rvivyādha rājaṁstumule mahātmā.
english: Then, swiftly, the lord of the Madra people (Śalya) struck Yudhiṣṭhira with seven (arrows) in the battle. O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the great-souled (mahātmā) Pārtha (Arjuna) also struck him (Śalya) with nine feathered arrows in that tumultuous encounter.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. Then, swiftly, the lord of the Madra people (Śalya) struck Yudhiṣṭhira with seven (arrows) in the battle. O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the great-souled (mahātmā) Pārtha (Arjuna) also struck him (Śalya) with nine feathered arrows in that tumultuous encounter.

sanskrit: आकर्णपूर्णायतसंप्रयुक्तैः शरैस्तदा संयति तैलधौतैः ।अन्योन्यमाच्छादयतां महारथौ मद्राधिपश्चापि युधिष्ठिरश्च ॥१२॥
transliteration: ākarṇapūrṇāyatasaṁprayuktaiḥ; śaraistadā saṁyati tailadhautaiḥ ,anyonyamācchādayatāṁ mahārathau; madrādhipaścāpi yudhiṣṭhiraśca.
english: Then, in that battle, the two great charioteers, the king of Madras and Yudhiṣṭhira, covered each other with long, well-aimed arrows, drawn fully to the ear and polished with oil.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. Then, in that battle, the two great charioteers, the king of Madras and Yudhiṣṭhira, covered each other with long, well-aimed arrows, drawn fully to the ear and polished with oil.

sanskrit: ततस्तु तूर्णं समरे महारथौ परस्परस्यान्तरमीक्षमाणौ ।शरैर्भृशं विव्यधतुर्नृपोत्तमौ महाबलौ शत्रुभिरप्रधृष्यौ ॥१३॥
transliteration: tatastu tūrṇaṁ samare mahārathau; parasparasyāntaramīkṣamāṇau ,śarairbhṛśaṁ vivyadhaturnṛpottamau; mahābalau śatrubhirapradhṛṣyau.
english: Then, in that battle, those two great charioteers, who were supreme among kings, immensely powerful, and unassailable by enemies, swiftly observed each other's vulnerable spots and pierced them greatly with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. Then, in that battle, those two great charioteers, who were supreme among kings, immensely powerful, and unassailable by enemies, swiftly observed each other's vulnerable spots and pierced them greatly with arrows.

sanskrit: तयोर्धनुर्ज्यातलनिस्वनो महान्महेन्द्रवज्राशनितुल्यनिस्वनः ।परस्परं बाणगणैर्महात्मनोः प्रवर्षतोर्मद्रपपाण्डुवीरयोः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tayordhanurjyātalanisvano mahā;nmahendravajrāśanitulyanisvanaḥ ,parasparaṁ bāṇagaṇairmahātmanoḥ; pravarṣatormadrapapāṇḍuvīrayoḥ.
english: As those two great-souled heroes, the king of Madras and the Pāṇḍu warrior, rained down floods of arrows upon each other, a mighty sound arose from the striking of their bowstrings by their palms, a sound comparable to the thunderbolt and lightning of Mahendra (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. As those two great-souled heroes, the king of Madras and the Pāṇḍu warrior, rained down floods of arrows upon each other, a mighty sound arose from the striking of their bowstrings by their palms, a sound comparable to the thunderbolt and lightning of Mahendra (Indra).

sanskrit: तौ चेरतुर्व्याघ्रशिशुप्रकाशौ महावनेष्वामिषगृद्धिनाविव ।विषाणिनौ नागवराविवोभौ ततक्षतुः संयुगजातदर्पौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: tau ceraturvyāghraśiśuprakāśau; mahāvaneṣvāmiṣagṛddhināviva ,viṣāṇinau nāgavarāvivobhau; tatakṣatuḥ saṁyugajātadarpau.
english: Those two warriors, both filled with battle-born pride, moved about like two tiger cubs eager for prey in vast forests, and like two tusked, excellent elephants, assailing each other fiercely.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. Those two warriors, both filled with battle-born pride, moved about like two tiger cubs eager for prey in vast forests, and like two tusked, excellent elephants, assailing each other fiercely.

sanskrit: ततस्तु मद्राधिपतिर्महात्मा युधिष्ठिरं भीमबलं प्रसह्य ।विव्याध वीरं हृदयेऽतिवेगं शरेण सूर्याग्निसमप्रभेण ॥१६॥
transliteration: tatastu madrādhipatirmahātmā; yudhiṣṭhiraṁ bhīmabalaṁ prasahya ,vivyādha vīraṁ hṛdaye'tivegaṁ; śareṇa sūryāgnisamaprabheṇa.
english: Then, the great-souled king of Madras, Shalya, forcefully attacked the mighty Yudhishthira and swiftly pierced the hero in the heart with an arrow that shone like the sun and fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. Then, the great-souled king of Madras, Shalya, forcefully attacked the mighty Yudhishthira and swiftly pierced the hero in the heart with an arrow that shone like the sun and fire.

sanskrit: ततोऽतिविद्धोऽथ युधिष्ठिरोऽपि सुसंप्रयुक्तेन शरेण राजन् ।जघान मद्राधिपतिं महात्मा मुदं च लेभे ऋषभः कुरूणाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tato'tividdho'tha yudhiṣṭhiro'pi; susaṁprayuktena śareṇa rājan ,jaghāna madrādhipatiṁ mahātmā; mudaṁ ca lebhe ṛṣabhaḥ kurūṇām.
english: Then, O king, Yudhishthira, though deeply wounded, the great-souled foremost (ṛṣabha) among the Kurus, in turn, struck the king of Madras with a well-aimed arrow and felt joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 17. Then, O king, Yudhishthira, though deeply wounded, the great-souled foremost (ṛṣabha) among the Kurus, in turn, struck the king of Madras with a well-aimed arrow and felt joy.

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्तादिव पार्थिवेन्द्रो लब्ध्वा संज्ञां क्रोधसंरक्तनेत्रः ।शतेन पार्थं त्वरितो जघान सहस्रनेत्रप्रतिमप्रभावः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtādiva pārthivendro; labdhvā saṁjñāṁ krodhasaṁraktanetraḥ ,śatena pārthaṁ tvarito jaghāna; sahasranetrapratimaprabhāvaḥ.
english: Then, as if in a moment, the great king Shalya, having regained consciousness, his eyes red with rage, swiftly struck Partha (Yudhishthira) with a hundred arrows, displaying power comparable to that of Indra, the thousand-eyed one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 18. Then, as if in a moment, the great king Shalya, having regained consciousness, his eyes red with rage, swiftly struck Partha (Yudhishthira) with a hundred arrows, displaying power comparable to that of Indra, the thousand-eyed one.

sanskrit: त्वरंस्ततो धर्मसुतो महात्मा शल्यस्य क्रुद्धो नवभिः पृषत्कैः ।भित्त्वा ह्युरस्तपनीयं च वर्म जघान षड्भिस्त्वपरैः पृषत्कैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tvaraṁstato dharmasuto mahātmā; śalyasya kruddho navabhiḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ ,bhittvā hyurastapanīyaṁ ca varma; jaghāna ṣaḍbhistvaparaiḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ.
english: Then, hastening and enraged, the great-souled son of dharma (natural law) (Yudhishthira), first pierced Shalya's chest and his golden armor with nine arrows, and then, he struck him again with six additional arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 19. Then, hastening and enraged, the great-souled son of dharma (natural law) (Yudhishthira), first pierced Shalya's chest and his golden armor with nine arrows, and then, he struck him again with six additional arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्तु मद्राधिपतिः प्रहृष्टो धनुर्विकृष्य व्यसृजत्पृषत्कान् ।द्वाभ्यां क्षुराभ्यां च तथैव राज्ञश्चिच्छेद चापं कुरुपुंगवस्य ॥२०॥
transliteration: tatastu madrādhipatiḥ prahṛṣṭo; dhanurvikṛṣya vyasṛjatpṛṣatkān ,dvābhyāṁ kṣurābhyāṁ ca tathaiva rājña;ściccheda cāpaṁ kurupuṁgavasya.
english: Then, the greatly delighted lord of Madra, drawing his bow, released arrows. And with two razor-sharp arrows, he similarly cut the bow of King Yudhishthira, the foremost among the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 20. Then, the greatly delighted lord of Madra, drawing his bow, released arrows. And with two razor-sharp arrows, he similarly cut the bow of King Yudhishthira, the foremost among the Kurus.

sanskrit: नवं ततोऽन्यत्समरे प्रगृह्य राजा धनुर्घोरतरं महात्मा ।शल्यं तु विद्ध्वा निशितैः समन्ताद्यथा महेन्द्रो नमुचिं शिताग्रैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: navaṁ tato'nyatsamare pragṛhya; rājā dhanurghorataraṁ mahātmā ,śalyaṁ tu viddhvā niśitaiḥ samantā;dyathā mahendro namuciṁ śitāgraiḥ.
english: Then, the great-souled king, having taken up another new and more dreadful bow in battle, having pierced Salya on all sides with sharpened (arrows), just as the great Indra (Mahendra) (pierced) Namuci with sharp-pointed (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 21. Then, the great-souled king, having taken up another new and more dreadful bow in battle, having pierced Salya on all sides with sharpened (arrows), just as the great Indra (Mahendra) (pierced) Namuci with sharp-pointed (arrows).

sanskrit: ततस्तु शल्यो नवभिः पृषत्कैर्भीमस्य राज्ञश्च युधिष्ठिरस्य ।निकृत्य रौक्मे पटुवर्मणी तयोर्विदारयामास भुजौ महात्मा ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatastu śalyo navabhiḥ pṛṣatkai;rbhīmasya rājñaśca yudhiṣṭhirasya ,nikṛtya raukme paṭuvarmaṇī tayo;rvidārayāmāsa bhujau mahātmā.
english: Then, the great-souled Salya, with nine arrows, having severed the strong golden armors of Bhima and King Yudhishthira, tore into their arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 22. Then, the great-souled Salya, with nine arrows, having severed the strong golden armors of Bhima and King Yudhishthira, tore into their arms.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरेण ज्वलितार्कतेजसा क्षुरेण राज्ञो धनुरुन्ममाथ ।कृपश्च तस्यैव जघान सूतं षड्भिः शरैः सोऽभिमुखं पपात ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato'pareṇa jvalitārkatejasā; kṣureṇa rājño dhanurunmamātha ,kṛpaśca tasyaiva jaghāna sūtaṁ; ṣaḍbhiḥ śaraiḥ so'bhimukhaṁ papāta.
english: Then, with another razor-sharp arrow, blazing with the brilliance of the sun, (Salya) broke the king's (Yudhishthira's) bow. And Kṛpa, with six arrows, killed his (Yudhishthira's) charioteer, who then fell forward.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 23. Then, with another razor-sharp arrow, blazing with the brilliance of the sun, (Salya) broke the king's (Yudhishthira's) bow. And Kṛpa, with six arrows, killed his (Yudhishthira's) charioteer, who then fell forward.

sanskrit: मद्राधिपश्चापि युधिष्ठिरस्य शरैश्चतुर्भिर्निजघान वाहान् ।वाहांश्च हत्वा व्यकरोन्महात्मा योधक्षयं धर्मसुतस्य राज्ञः ॥२४॥
transliteration: madrādhipaścāpi yudhiṣṭhirasya; śaraiścaturbhirnijaghāna vāhān ,vāhāṁśca hatvā vyakaronmahātmā; yodhakṣayaṁ dharmasutasya rājñaḥ.
english: And the king of Madra (Śalya) also struck down Yudhiṣṭhira's chariot horses with four arrows. Having killed the horses, that great-souled warrior then caused the destruction of the warriors of King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 24. And the king of Madra (Śalya) also struck down Yudhiṣṭhira's chariot horses with four arrows. Having killed the horses, that great-souled warrior then caused the destruction of the warriors of King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma).

sanskrit: तथा कृते राजनि भीमसेनो मद्राधिपस्याशु ततो महात्मा ।छित्त्वा धनुर्वेगवता शरेण द्वाभ्यामविध्यत्सुभृशं नरेन्द्रम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tathā kṛte rājani bhīmaseno; madrādhipasyāśu tato mahātmā ,chittvā dhanurvegavatā śareṇa; dvābhyāmavidhyatsubhṛśaṁ narendram.
english: When that had been done (against Yudhiṣṭhira), the great-souled Bhīmasena then quickly, having cut the king of Madra's (Śalya's) bow with a swift arrow, pierced that king severely with two (other) arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 25. When that had been done (against Yudhiṣṭhira), the great-souled Bhīmasena then quickly, having cut the king of Madra's (Śalya's) bow with a swift arrow, pierced that king severely with two (other) arrows.

sanskrit: अथापरेणास्य जहार यन्तुः कायाच्छिरः संनहनीयमध्यात् ।जघान चाश्वांश्चतुरः स शीघ्रं तथा भृशं कुपितो भीमसेनः ॥२६॥
transliteration: athāpareṇāsya jahāra yantuḥ; kāyācchiraḥ saṁnahanīyamadhyāt ,jaghāna cāśvāṁścaturaḥ sa śīghraṁ; tathā bhṛśaṁ kupito bhīmasenaḥ.
english: Then, with another arrow, he (Bhīmasena) severed the charioteer's head from his body, right from the midst of his armor. And that Bhīmasena, greatly enraged, quickly killed the four horses as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 26. Then, with another arrow, he (Bhīmasena) severed the charioteer's head from his body, right from the midst of his armor. And that Bhīmasena, greatly enraged, quickly killed the four horses as well.

sanskrit: तमग्रणीः सर्वधनुर्धराणामेकं चरन्तं समरेऽतिवेगम् ।भीमः शतेन व्यकिरच्छराणां माद्रीपुत्रः सहदेवस्तथैव ॥२७॥
transliteration: tamagraṇīḥ sarvadhanurdharāṇā;mekaṁ carantaṁ samare'tivegam ,bhīmaḥ śatena vyakiraccharāṇāṁ; mādrīputraḥ sahadevastathaiva.
english: Bhīma, the foremost of all bow-wielders, showered him (Śalya), who was fighting alone and with great speed in battle, with a hundred arrows. And Mādrī's son, Sahadeva, did likewise.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 27. Bhīma, the foremost of all bow-wielders, showered him (Śalya), who was fighting alone and with great speed in battle, with a hundred arrows. And Mādrī's son, Sahadeva, did likewise.

sanskrit: तैः सायकैर्मोहितं वीक्ष्य शल्यं भीमः शरैरस्य चकर्त वर्म ।स भीमसेनेन निकृत्तवर्मा मद्राधिपश्चर्म सहस्रतारम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: taiḥ sāyakairmohitaṁ vīkṣya śalyaṁ; bhīmaḥ śarairasya cakarta varma ,sa bhīmasenena nikṛttavarmā; madrādhipaścarma sahasratāram.
english: When Bhīma saw Śalya bewildered by those arrows, he cut off Śalya's armor with his own arrows. The King of Madra, Śalya, whose armor had been severed by Bhīmasena, then grasped his shield, which was adorned with a thousand stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 28. When Bhīma saw Śalya bewildered by those arrows, he cut off Śalya's armor with his own arrows. The King of Madra, Śalya, whose armor had been severed by Bhīmasena, then grasped his shield, which was adorned with a thousand stars.

sanskrit: प्रगृह्य खड्गं च रथान्महात्मा प्रस्कन्द्य कुन्तीसुतमभ्यधावत् ।छित्त्वा रथेषां नकुलस्य सोऽथ युधिष्ठिरं भीमबलोऽभ्यधावत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: pragṛhya khaḍgaṁ ca rathānmahātmā; praskandya kuntīsutamabhyadhāvat ,chittvā ratheṣāṁ nakulasya so'tha; yudhiṣṭhiraṁ bhīmabalo'bhyadhāvat.
english: The great-souled (Śalya), grasping his sword, leapt down from his chariot and charged towards Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī. Then, after cutting the pole of Nakula's chariot, he, of formidable strength, again rushed towards Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 29. The great-souled (Śalya), grasping his sword, leapt down from his chariot and charged towards Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī. Then, after cutting the pole of Nakula's chariot, he, of formidable strength, again rushed towards Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: तं चापि राजानमथोत्पतन्तं क्रुद्धं यथैवान्तकमापतन्तम् ।धृष्टद्युम्नो द्रौपदेयाः शिखण्डी शिनेश्च नप्ता सहसा परीयुः ॥३०॥
transliteration: taṁ cāpi rājānamathotpatantaṁ; kruddhaṁ yathaivāntakamāpatantam ,dhṛṣṭadyumno draupadeyāḥ śikhaṇḍī; śineśca naptā sahasā parīyuḥ.
english: Then, as that enraged king (Śalya) rose up, rushing like Death (Antaka) itself, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the sons of Draupadī, Śikhaṇḍī, and Śini's grandson (Sātyaki) suddenly surrounded him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 30. Then, as that enraged king (Śalya) rose up, rushing like Death (Antaka) itself, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the sons of Draupadī, Śikhaṇḍī, and Śini's grandson (Sātyaki) suddenly surrounded him.

sanskrit: अथास्य चर्माप्रतिमं न्यकृन्तद्भीमो महात्मा दशभिः पृषत्कः ।खड्गं च भल्लैर्निचकर्त मुष्टौ नदन्प्रहृष्टस्तव सैन्यमध्ये ॥३१॥
transliteration: athāsya carmāpratimaṁ nyakṛnta;dbhīmo mahātmā daśabhiḥ pṛṣatkaḥ ,khaḍgaṁ ca bhallairnicakarta muṣṭau; nadanprahṛṣṭastava sainyamadhye.
english: Then, the great-souled Bhīma, roaring and greatly delighted, cut down Śalya's matchless shield with ten arrows. In the midst of your (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) army, he also cut off Śalya's sword at the hilt with lances.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 31. Then, the great-souled Bhīma, roaring and greatly delighted, cut down Śalya's matchless shield with ten arrows. In the midst of your (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) army, he also cut off Śalya's sword at the hilt with lances.

sanskrit: तत्कर्म भीमस्य समीक्ष्य हृष्टास्ते पाण्डवानां प्रवरा रथौघाः ।नादं च चक्रुर्भृशमुत्स्मयन्तः शङ्खांश्च दध्मुः शशिसंनिकाशान् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tatkarma bhīmasya samīkṣya hṛṣṭā;ste pāṇḍavānāṁ pravarā rathaughāḥ ,nādaṁ ca cakrurbhṛśamutsmayantaḥ; śaṅkhāṁśca dadhmuḥ śaśisaṁnikāśān.
english: Having witnessed that feat of Bhīma, those excellent charioteers among the Pāṇḍavas became greatly delighted. They made a loud sound, smiling profusely, and blew their conches which gleamed like the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 32. Having witnessed that feat of Bhīma, those excellent charioteers among the Pāṇḍavas became greatly delighted. They made a loud sound, smiling profusely, and blew their conches which gleamed like the moon.

sanskrit: तेनाथ शब्देन विभीषणेन तवाभितप्तं बलमप्रहृष्टम् ।स्वेदाभिभूतं रुधिरोक्षिताङ्गं विसंज्ञकल्पं च तथा विषण्णम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tenātha śabdena vibhīṣaṇena; tavābhitaptaṁ balamaprahṛṣṭam ,svedābhibhūtaṁ rudhirokṣitāṅgaṁ; visaṁjñakalpaṁ ca tathā viṣaṇṇam.
english: Then, by that terrifying sound, your army (balam) became tormented and dejected, overwhelmed by sweat, with limbs drenched in blood, nearly senseless, and thus utterly despondent.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 33. Then, by that terrifying sound, your army (balam) became tormented and dejected, overwhelmed by sweat, with limbs drenched in blood, nearly senseless, and thus utterly despondent.

sanskrit: स मद्रराजः सहसावकीर्णो भीमाग्रगैः पाण्डवयोधमुख्यैः ।युधिष्ठिरस्याभिमुखं जवेन सिंहो यथा मृगहेतोः प्रयातः ॥३४॥
transliteration: sa madrarājaḥ sahasāvakīrṇo; bhīmāgragaiḥ pāṇḍavayodhamukhyaiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasyābhimukhaṁ javena; siṁho yathā mṛgahetoḥ prayātaḥ.
english: That King of Madra (Śalya), though suddenly surrounded by Bhīma and the foremost Pāṇḍava warriors, advanced swiftly towards Yudhiṣṭhira, just as a lion proceeds for the sake of its prey.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 34. That King of Madra (Śalya), though suddenly surrounded by Bhīma and the foremost Pāṇḍava warriors, advanced swiftly towards Yudhiṣṭhira, just as a lion proceeds for the sake of its prey.

sanskrit: स धर्मराजो निहताश्वसूतं क्रोधेन दीप्तज्वलनप्रकाशम् ।दृष्ट्वा तु मद्राधिपतिं स तूर्णं समभ्यधावत्तमरिं बलेन ॥३५॥
transliteration: sa dharmarājo nihatāśvasūtaṁ; krodhena dīptajvalanaprakāśam ,dṛṣṭvā tu madrādhipatiṁ sa tūrṇaṁ; samabhyadhāvattamariṁ balena.
english: That King of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), having seen the lord of Madra (Śalya) - whose horses and charioteer were slain, and who, due to his anger, shone like a blazing fire - he swiftly charged towards that enemy (arim) with great force.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 35. That King of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), having seen the lord of Madra (Śalya) - whose horses and charioteer were slain, and who, due to his anger, shone like a blazing fire - he swiftly charged towards that enemy (arim) with great force.

sanskrit: गोविन्दवाक्यं त्वरितं विचिन्त्य दध्रे मतिं शल्यविनाशनाय ।स धर्मराजो निहताश्वसूते रथे तिष्ठञ्शक्तिमेवाभिकाङ्क्षन् ॥३६॥
transliteration: govindavākyaṁ tvaritaṁ vicintya; dadhre matiṁ śalyavināśanāya ,sa dharmarājo nihatāśvasūte; rathe tiṣṭhañśaktimevābhikāṅkṣan.
english: Quickly pondering Kṛṣṇa's words, that king of righteousness (dharma), standing in his chariot whose horses and charioteer had been slain, fixed his mind on Śalya's destruction, desiring only the divine power (śakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 36. Quickly pondering Kṛṣṇa's words, that king of righteousness (dharma), standing in his chariot whose horses and charioteer had been slain, fixed his mind on Śalya's destruction, desiring only the divine power (śakti).

sanskrit: तच्चापि शल्यस्य निशम्य कर्म महात्मनो भागमथावशिष्टम् ।स्मृत्वा मनः शल्यवधे यतात्मा यथोक्तमिन्द्रावरजस्य चक्रे ॥३७॥
transliteration: taccāpi śalyasya niśamya karma; mahātmano bhāgamathāvaśiṣṭam ,smṛtvā manaḥ śalyavadhe yatātmā; yathoktamindrāvarajasya cakre.
english: And also, having heard of Śalya's deed (karma) and then recalling the remaining part of the great-souled one's destiny, he, with his self (ātman) controlled for the slaying of Śalya, did as spoken by the younger brother of Indra (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 37. And also, having heard of Śalya's deed (karma) and then recalling the remaining part of the great-souled one's destiny, he, with his self (ātman) controlled for the slaying of Śalya, did as spoken by the younger brother of Indra (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: स धर्मराजो मणिहेमदण्डां जग्राह शक्तिं कनकप्रकाशाम् ।नेत्रे च दीप्ते सहसा विवृत्य मद्राधिपं क्रुद्धमना निरैक्षत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: sa dharmarājo maṇihemadaṇḍāṁ; jagrāha śaktiṁ kanakaprakāśām ,netre ca dīpte sahasā vivṛtya; madrādhipaṁ kruddhamanā niraikṣat.
english: That king of righteousness (dharma) grasped the divine power (śakti) with a jeweled and golden staff, shining like gold. And suddenly opening his radiant eyes, he, with an enraged mind, gazed at the king of the Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 38. That king of righteousness (dharma) grasped the divine power (śakti) with a jeweled and golden staff, shining like gold. And suddenly opening his radiant eyes, he, with an enraged mind, gazed at the king of the Madras.

sanskrit: निरीक्षितो वै नरदेव राज्ञा पूतात्मना निर्हृतकल्मषेण ।अभून्न यद्भस्मसान्मद्रराजस्तदद्भुतं मे प्रतिभाति राजन् ॥३९॥
transliteration: nirīkṣito vai naradeva rājñā; pūtātmanā nirhṛtakalmaṣeṇa ,abhūnna yadbhasmasānmadrarāja;stadadbhutaṁ me pratibhāti rājan.
english: O king, it appears wonderful to me that the king of the Madras, having been gazed upon by the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), whose self (ātman) was purified and whose impurities were removed, did not instantly turn into ashes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 39. O king, it appears wonderful to me that the king of the Madras, having been gazed upon by the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), whose self (ātman) was purified and whose impurities were removed, did not instantly turn into ashes.

sanskrit: ततस्तु शक्तिं रुचिरोग्रदण्डां मणिप्रवालोज्ज्वलितां प्रदीप्ताम् ।चिक्षेप वेगात्सुभृशं महात्मा मद्राधिपाय प्रवरः कुरूणाम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: tatastu śaktiṁ rucirogradaṇḍāṁ; maṇipravālojjvalitāṁ pradīptām ,cikṣepa vegātsubhṛśaṁ mahātmā; madrādhipāya pravaraḥ kurūṇām.
english: Then, the great-souled one, the foremost among the Kurus, forcefully and with great speed, hurled the spear (śakti) – which had a beautiful and terrible shaft, blazed with gems and coral, and was intensely radiant – at the king of Madra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 40. Then, the great-souled one, the foremost among the Kurus, forcefully and with great speed, hurled the spear (śakti) – which had a beautiful and terrible shaft, blazed with gems and coral, and was intensely radiant – at the king of Madra.

sanskrit: दीप्तामथैनां महता बलेन सविस्फुलिङ्गां सहसा पतन्तीम् ।प्रैक्षन्त सर्वे कुरवः समेता यथा युगान्ते महतीमिवोल्काम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: dīptāmathaināṁ mahatā balena; savisphuliṅgāṁ sahasā patantīm ,praikṣanta sarve kuravaḥ sametā; yathā yugānte mahatīmivolkām.
english: Then, all the assembled Kurus beheld it – blazing, falling swiftly with great force, and emitting sparks – just like a mighty meteor at the end of an eon (yuga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 41. Then, all the assembled Kurus beheld it – blazing, falling swiftly with great force, and emitting sparks – just like a mighty meteor at the end of an eon (yuga).

sanskrit: तां कालरात्रीमिव पाशहस्तां यमस्य धात्रीमिव चोग्ररूपाम् ।सब्रह्मदण्डप्रतिमाममोघां ससर्ज यत्तो युधि धर्मराजः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tāṁ kālarātrīmiva pāśahastāṁ; yamasya dhātrīmiva cograrūpām ,sabrahmadaṇḍapratimāmamoghāṁ; sasarja yatto yudhi dharmarājaḥ.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), exerting himself intently in battle, released that [spear], which was like Kalaratri holding a noose, and like Yama's nurse with a terrible form, resembling the staff (daṇḍa) of Brahma, and was infallible.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 42. King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), exerting himself intently in battle, released that [spear], which was like Kalaratri holding a noose, and like Yama's nurse with a terrible form, resembling the staff (daṇḍa) of Brahma, and was infallible.

sanskrit: गन्धस्रगग्र्यासनपानभोजनैरभ्यर्चितां पाण्डुसुतैः प्रयत्नात् ।संवर्तकाग्निप्रतिमां ज्वलन्तीं कृत्यामथर्वाङ्गिरसीमिवोग्राम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: gandhasragagryāsanapānabhojanai;rabhyarcitāṁ pāṇḍusutaiḥ prayatnāt ,saṁvartakāgnipratimāṁ jvalantīṁ; kṛtyāmatharvāṅgirasīmivogrām.
english: (Yudhiṣṭhira released that spear,) which was worshipped diligently by the sons of Pāṇḍu with perfumes, garlands, excellent seats, drinks, and food; resembling the fire (agni) of cosmic dissolution and blazing; and terrible like a magical incantation (kṛtyā) from the Atharvaveda (ātharvāṅgirasī).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 43. (Yudhiṣṭhira released that spear,) which was worshipped diligently by the sons of Pāṇḍu with perfumes, garlands, excellent seats, drinks, and food; resembling the fire (agni) of cosmic dissolution and blazing; and terrible like a magical incantation (kṛtyā) from the Atharvaveda (ātharvāṅgirasī).

sanskrit: ईशानहेतोः प्रतिनिर्मितां तां त्वष्ट्रा रिपूणामसुदेहभक्षाम् ।भूम्यन्तरिक्षादिजलाशयानि प्रसह्य भूतानि निहन्तुमीशाम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: īśānahetoḥ pratinirmitāṁ tāṁ; tvaṣṭrā ripūṇāmasudehabhakṣām ,bhūmyantarikṣādijalāśayāni; prasahya bhūtāni nihantumīśām.
english: For the sake of Īśāna (Śiva), Tvaṣṭā had fashioned her, who would devour the bodies of enemies. She was capable of forcibly destroying beings on the earth, in the sky, and in water reservoirs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 44. For the sake of Īśāna (Śiva), Tvaṣṭā had fashioned her, who would devour the bodies of enemies. She was capable of forcibly destroying beings on the earth, in the sky, and in water reservoirs.

sanskrit: घण्टापताकामणिवज्रभाजं वैडूर्यचित्रां तपनीयदण्डाम् ।त्वष्ट्रा प्रयत्नान्नियमेन कॢप्तां ब्रह्मद्विषामन्तकरीममोघाम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: ghaṇṭāpatākāmaṇivajrabhājaṁ; vaiḍūryacitrāṁ tapanīyadaṇḍām ,tvaṣṭrā prayatnānniyamena kḷptāṁ; brahmadviṣāmantakarīmamoghām.
english: Tvaṣṭā had diligently fashioned her, bearing bells, banners, jewels, and thunderbolts, adorned with variegated lapis lazuli and possessing a golden staff. She was unfailing and destined to bring an end to the enemies of Brahmins (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 45. Tvaṣṭā had diligently fashioned her, bearing bells, banners, jewels, and thunderbolts, adorned with variegated lapis lazuli and possessing a golden staff. She was unfailing and destined to bring an end to the enemies of Brahmins (brahman).

sanskrit: बलप्रयत्नादधिरूढवेगां मन्त्रैश्च घोरैरभिमन्त्रयित्वा ।ससर्ज मार्गेण च तां परेण वधाय मद्राधिपतेस्तदानीम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: balaprayatnādadhirūḍhavegāṁ; mantraiśca ghorairabhimantrayitvā ,sasarja mārgeṇa ca tāṁ pareṇa; vadhāya madrādhipatestadānīm.
english: Then, after consecrating her with formidable (mantras) and endowing her with immense speed through mighty effort, he launched her along a superior path for the slaying of the king of Madra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 46. Then, after consecrating her with formidable (mantras) and endowing her with immense speed through mighty effort, he launched her along a superior path for the slaying of the king of Madra.

sanskrit: हतोऽस्यसावित्यभिगर्जमानो रुद्रोऽन्तकायान्तकरं यथेषुम् ।प्रसार्य बाहुं सुदृढं सुपाणिं क्रोधेन नृत्यन्निव धर्मराजः ॥४७॥
transliteration: hato'syasāvityabhigarjamāno; rudro'ntakāyāntakaraṁ yatheṣum ,prasārya bāhuṁ sudṛḍhaṁ supāṇiṁ; krodhena nṛtyanniva dharmarājaḥ.
english: Roaring, "He is slain!", he (the sender), like Rudra (Śiva), a destroyer even to Antaka (Death), stretched out his very strong, capable arm, as if the King of (natural law) (dharma) (Yama) were dancing with anger, [and the missile was] like an arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 47. Roaring, "He is slain!", he (the sender), like Rudra (Śiva), a destroyer even to Antaka (Death), stretched out his very strong, capable arm, as if the King of (natural law) (dharma) (Yama) were dancing with anger, [and the missile was] like an arrow.

sanskrit: तां सर्वशक्त्या प्रहितां स शक्तिं युधिष्ठिरेणाप्रतिवार्यवीर्याम् ।प्रतिग्रहायाभिननर्द शल्यः सम्यग्घुतामग्निरिवाज्यधाराम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: tāṁ sarvaśaktyā prahitāṁ sa śaktiṁ; yudhiṣṭhireṇāprativāryavīryām ,pratigrahāyābhinanarda śalyaḥ; samyagghutāmagnirivājyadhārām.
english: King Śalya accepted that divine weapon (śakti), which was discharged by Yudhiṣṭhira with all his might (sarvaśakti) and possessed irresistible power, just as fire accepts an offering of ghee poured perfectly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 48. King Śalya accepted that divine weapon (śakti), which was discharged by Yudhiṣṭhira with all his might (sarvaśakti) and possessed irresistible power, just as fire accepts an offering of ghee poured perfectly.

sanskrit: सा तस्य मर्माणि विदार्य शुभ्रमुरो विशालं च तथैव वर्म ।विवेश गां तोयमिवाप्रसक्ता यशो विशालं नृपतेर्दहन्ती ॥४९॥
transliteration: sā tasya marmāṇi vidārya śubhra;muro viśālaṁ ca tathaiva varma ,viveśa gāṁ toyamivāprasaktā; yaśo viśālaṁ nṛpaterdahantī.
english: That divine weapon (śakti), having pierced his vital spots, his bright, broad chest, and likewise his armor, entered the earth effortlessly like water, burning away the king's great fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 49. That divine weapon (śakti), having pierced his vital spots, his bright, broad chest, and likewise his armor, entered the earth effortlessly like water, burning away the king's great fame.

sanskrit: नासाक्षिकर्णास्यविनिःसृतेन प्रस्यन्दता च व्रणसंभवेन ।संसिक्तगात्रो रुधिरेण सोऽभूत्क्रौञ्चो यथा स्कन्दहतो महाद्रिः ॥५०॥
transliteration: nāsākṣikarṇāsyaviniḥsṛtena; prasyandatā ca vraṇasaṁbhavena ,saṁsiktagātro rudhireṇa so'bhū;tkrauñco yathā skandahato mahādriḥ.
english: He became drenched in blood that flowed from his nose, eyes, ears, and mouth, and also from the wounds, just like Mount Krauñca, struck by Skanda.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 50. He became drenched in blood that flowed from his nose, eyes, ears, and mouth, and also from the wounds, just like Mount Krauñca, struck by Skanda.

sanskrit: प्रसार्य बाहू स रथाद्गतो गां संछिन्नवर्मा कुरुनन्दनेन ।महेन्द्रवाहप्रतिमो महात्मा वज्राहतं शृङ्गमिवाचलस्य ॥५१॥
transliteration: prasārya bāhū sa rathādgato gāṁ; saṁchinnavarmā kurunandanena ,mahendravāhapratimo mahātmā; vajrāhataṁ śṛṅgamivācalasya.
english: His armor having been torn by the Kuru scion (Yudhiṣṭhira), that great-souled hero, who resembled the vehicle of Indra (Airāvata), fell to the earth from his chariot, spreading his arms, like a mountain peak struck by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 51. His armor having been torn by the Kuru scion (Yudhiṣṭhira), that great-souled hero, who resembled the vehicle of Indra (Airāvata), fell to the earth from his chariot, spreading his arms, like a mountain peak struck by a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: बाहू प्रसार्याभिमुखो धर्मराजस्य मद्रराट् ।ततो निपतितो भूमाविन्द्रध्वज इवोच्छ्रितः ॥५२॥
transliteration: bāhū prasāryābhimukho dharmarājasya madrarāṭ ,tato nipatito bhūmāvindradhvaja ivocchritaḥ.
english: Then the king of Madra, Śalya, spreading his arms and facing Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), fell to the earth like an erected Indra-banner (Indra-dhvaja) that has been brought down.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 52. Then the king of Madra, Śalya, spreading his arms and facing Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja), fell to the earth like an erected Indra-banner (Indra-dhvaja) that has been brought down.

sanskrit: स तथा भिन्नसर्वाङ्गो रुधिरेण समुक्षितः ।प्रत्युद्गत इव प्रेम्णा भूम्या स नरपुंगवः ॥५३॥
transliteration: sa tathā bhinnasarvāṅgo rudhireṇa samukṣitaḥ ,pratyudgata iva premṇā bhūmyā sa narapuṁgavaḥ.
english: That best of men (narapuṅgava), with all his limbs shattered and drenched in blood, appeared as if lovingly welcomed by the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 53. That best of men (narapuṅgava), with all his limbs shattered and drenched in blood, appeared as if lovingly welcomed by the earth.

sanskrit: प्रियया कान्तया कान्तः पतमान इवोरसि ।चिरं भुक्त्वा वसुमतीं प्रियां कान्तामिव प्रभुः ।सर्वैरङ्गैः समाश्लिष्य प्रसुप्त इव सोऽभवत् ॥५४॥
transliteration: priyayā kāntayā kāntaḥ patamāna ivorasi ,ciraṁ bhuktvā vasumatīṁ priyāṁ kāntāmiva prabhuḥ ,sarvairaṅgaiḥ samāśliṣya prasupta iva so'bhavat.
english: The lord (prabhu), having long enjoyed the earth (vasumatī), embraced it with all his limbs as if it were a dear, beloved wife (kāntā). He lay as if a beloved man (kānta) falling upon the chest of his dear, lovely one (kāntā), and thus he became as if asleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 54. The lord (prabhu), having long enjoyed the earth (vasumatī), embraced it with all his limbs as if it were a dear, beloved wife (kāntā). He lay as if a beloved man (kānta) falling upon the chest of his dear, lovely one (kāntā), and thus he became as if asleep.

sanskrit: धर्म्ये धर्मात्मना युद्धे निहतो धर्मसूनुना ।सम्यग्घुत इव स्विष्टः प्रशान्तोऽग्निरिवाध्वरे ॥५५॥
transliteration: dharmye dharmātmanā yuddhe nihato dharmasūnunā ,samyagghuta iva sviṣṭaḥ praśānto'gnirivādhvare.
english: Killed in a righteous (dharmya) battle by the righteous (dharmātman) Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharmasūnu), he became like a properly offered oblation (ghuta) or a well-offered and appeased fire in a sacrificial ceremony (adhvara).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 55. Killed in a righteous (dharmya) battle by the righteous (dharmātman) Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharmasūnu), he became like a properly offered oblation (ghuta) or a well-offered and appeased fire in a sacrificial ceremony (adhvara).

sanskrit: शक्त्या विभिन्नहृदयं विप्रविद्धायुधध्वजम् ।संशान्तमपि मद्रेशं लक्ष्मीर्नैव व्यमुञ्चत ॥५६॥
transliteration: śaktyā vibhinnahṛdayaṁ vipraviddhāyudhadhvajam ,saṁśāntamapi madreśaṁ lakṣmīrnaiva vyamuñcata.
english: Even though the King of Madra (Madreśa), whose heart had been pierced by a spear (śakti) and whose weapons and banner lay scattered, had fallen still (died), Fortune (Lakṣmī) never abandoned him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 56. Even though the King of Madra (Madreśa), whose heart had been pierced by a spear (śakti) and whose weapons and banner lay scattered, had fallen still (died), Fortune (Lakṣmī) never abandoned him.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरश्चापमादायेन्द्रधनुष्प्रभम् ।व्यधमद्द्विषतः संख्ये खगराडिव पन्नगान् ।देहासून्निशितैर्भल्लै रिपूणां नाशयन्क्षणात् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiraścāpamādāyendradhanuṣprabham ,vyadhamaddviṣataḥ saṁkhye khagarāḍiva pannagān ,dehāsūnniśitairbhallai ripūṇāṁ nāśayankṣaṇāt.
english: Then Yudhiṣṭhira, taking up a bow shining like Indra's rainbow, instantly scattered the enemies in battle with sharp arrows, destroying the bodies and vital breaths of his foes, just as Garuḍa, the king of birds, destroys serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 57. Then Yudhiṣṭhira, taking up a bow shining like Indra's rainbow, instantly scattered the enemies in battle with sharp arrows, destroying the bodies and vital breaths of his foes, just as Garuḍa, the king of birds, destroys serpents.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थस्य बाणौघैरावृताः सैनिकास्तव ।निमीलिताक्षाः क्षिण्वन्तो भृशमन्योन्यमर्दिताः ।संन्यस्तकवचा देहैर्विपत्रायुधजीविताः ॥५८॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārthasya bāṇaughairāvṛtāḥ sainikāstava ,nimīlitākṣāḥ kṣiṇvanto bhṛśamanyonyamarditāḥ ,saṁnyastakavacā dehairvipatrāyudhajīvitāḥ.
english: Then, your soldiers, enveloped by Arjuna's (Pārtha's) multitude of arrows, their eyes closed, grew exceedingly weary, crushed by one another. Having cast off their armors, their very bodies were deprived of arrows, weapons, and life.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 58. Then, your soldiers, enveloped by Arjuna's (Pārtha's) multitude of arrows, their eyes closed, grew exceedingly weary, crushed by one another. Having cast off their armors, their very bodies were deprived of arrows, weapons, and life.

sanskrit: ततः शल्ये निपतिते मद्रराजानुजो युवा ।भ्रातुः सर्वैर्गुणैस्तुल्यो रथी पाण्डवमभ्ययात् ॥५९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śalye nipatite madrarājānujo yuvā ,bhrātuḥ sarvairguṇaistulyo rathī pāṇḍavamabhyayāt.
english: Then, after Shalya had fallen, the young younger brother of the Madra king, a charioteer equal to his brother in all qualities, approached (and attacked) the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 59. Then, after Shalya had fallen, the young younger brother of the Madra king, a charioteer equal to his brother in all qualities, approached (and attacked) the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: विव्याध च नरश्रेष्ठो नाराचैर्बहुभिस्त्वरन् ।हतस्यापचितिं भ्रातुश्चिकीर्षुर्युद्धदुर्मदः ॥६०॥
transliteration: vivyādha ca naraśreṣṭho nārācairbahubhistvaran ,hatasyāpacitiṁ bhrātuścikīrṣuryuddhadurmadaḥ.
english: And that best among men, intoxicated with battle, swiftly pierced (him) with many arrows, desiring to avenge his slain brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 60. And that best among men, intoxicated with battle, swiftly pierced (him) with many arrows, desiring to avenge his slain brother.

sanskrit: तं विव्याधाशुगैः षड्भिर्धर्मराजस्त्वरन्निव ।कार्मुकं चास्य चिच्छेद क्षुराभ्यां ध्वजमेव च ॥६१॥
transliteration: taṁ vivyādhāśugaiḥ ṣaḍbhirdharmarājastvaranniva ,kārmukaṁ cāsya ciccheda kṣurābhyāṁ dhvajameva ca.
english: Dharmarāja, as if hastening, pierced him with six swift arrows. And with two razor-edged arrows, he cut down his bow and also his banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 61. Dharmarāja, as if hastening, pierced him with six swift arrows. And with two razor-edged arrows, he cut down his bow and also his banner.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्य दीप्यमानेन सुदृढेन शितेन च ।प्रमुखे वर्तमानस्य भल्लेनापाहरच्छिरः ॥६२॥
transliteration: tato'sya dīpyamānena sudṛḍhena śitena ca ,pramukhe vartamānasya bhallenāpāharacchiraḥ.
english: Then, with a shining, very strong, and sharp spear, he severed the head of (the one) standing at the forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 62. Then, with a shining, very strong, and sharp spear, he severed the head of (the one) standing at the forefront.

sanskrit: सकुण्डलं तद्ददृशे पतमानं शिरो रथात् ।पुण्यक्षयमिव प्राप्य पतन्तं स्वर्गवासिनम् ॥६३॥
transliteration: sakuṇḍalaṁ taddadṛśe patamānaṁ śiro rathāt ,puṇyakṣayamiva prāpya patantaṁ svargavāsinam.
english: That head, complete with earrings, was seen falling from the chariot, like a dweller of heaven falling after having exhausted their merit.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 63. That head, complete with earrings, was seen falling from the chariot, like a dweller of heaven falling after having exhausted their merit.

sanskrit: तस्यापकृष्टशीर्षं तच्छरीरं पतितं रथात् ।रुधिरेणावसिक्ताङ्गं दृष्ट्वा सैन्यमभज्यत ॥६४॥
transliteration: tasyāpakṛṣṭaśīrṣaṁ taccharīraṁ patitaṁ rathāt ,rudhireṇāvasiktāṅgaṁ dṛṣṭvā sainyamabhajyata.
english: Upon seeing his body, with its head severed and limbs drenched in blood, fallen from the chariot, the army broke and fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 64. Upon seeing his body, with its head severed and limbs drenched in blood, fallen from the chariot, the army broke and fled.

sanskrit: विचित्रकवचे तस्मिन्हते मद्रनृपानुजे ।हाहाकारं विकुर्वाणाः कुरवो विप्रदुद्रुवुः ॥६५॥
transliteration: vicitrakavace tasminhate madranṛpānuje ,hāhākāraṁ vikurvāṇāḥ kuravo vipradudruvuḥ.
english: When that younger brother of the Madra king, adorned with wondrous armor, was slain, the Kauravas, uttering cries of distress, scattered and fled in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 65. When that younger brother of the Madra king, adorned with wondrous armor, was slain, the Kauravas, uttering cries of distress, scattered and fled in all directions.

sanskrit: शल्यानुजं हतं दृष्ट्वा तावकास्त्यक्तजीविताः ।वित्रेसुः पाण्डवभयाद्रजोध्वस्तास्तथा भृशम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: śalyānujaṁ hataṁ dṛṣṭvā tāvakāstyaktajīvitāḥ ,vitresuḥ pāṇḍavabhayādrajodhvastāstathā bhṛśam.
english: Upon seeing Śalya's younger brother slain, your warriors, having given up all hope for their lives and covered in dust, trembled greatly from fear of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 66. Upon seeing Śalya's younger brother slain, your warriors, having given up all hope for their lives and covered in dust, trembled greatly from fear of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तांस्तथा भज्यतस्त्रस्तान्कौरवान्भरतर्षभ ।शिनेर्नप्ता किरन्बाणैरभ्यवर्तत सात्यकिः ॥६७॥
transliteration: tāṁstathā bhajyatastrastānkauravānbharatarṣabha ,śinernaptā kiranbāṇairabhyavartata sātyakiḥ.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, Sātyaki, the grandson of Śini, thus attacked those routed and terrified Kauravas, showering them with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 67. O best of the Bhāratas, Sātyaki, the grandson of Śini, thus attacked those routed and terrified Kauravas, showering them with arrows.

sanskrit: तमायान्तं महेष्वासमप्रसह्यं दुरासदम् ।हार्दिक्यस्त्वरितो राजन्प्रत्यगृह्णादभीतवत् ॥६८॥
transliteration: tamāyāntaṁ maheṣvāsamaprasahyaṁ durāsadam ,hārdikyastvarito rājanpratyagṛhṇādabhītavat.
english: O king, Hārdikya swiftly and fearlessly met him, who was approaching, wielding a mighty bow, and was irresistible and difficult to confront.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 68. O king, Hārdikya swiftly and fearlessly met him, who was approaching, wielding a mighty bow, and was irresistible and difficult to confront.

sanskrit: तौ समेतौ महात्मानौ वार्ष्णेयावपराजितौ ।हार्दिक्यः सात्यकिश्चैव सिंहाविव मदोत्कटौ ॥६९॥
transliteration: tau sametau mahātmānau vārṣṇeyāvaparājitau ,hārdikyaḥ sātyakiścaiva siṁhāviva madotkaṭau.
english: Those two great-souled Vṛṣṇis (descendants of Vṛṣṇi), unconquered, Hārdikya and Sātyaki, met each other, indeed like two lions fierce with ardor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 69. Those two great-souled Vṛṣṇis (descendants of Vṛṣṇi), unconquered, Hārdikya and Sātyaki, met each other, indeed like two lions fierce with ardor.

sanskrit: इषुभिर्विमलाभासैश्छादयन्तौ परस्परम् ।अर्चिर्भिरिव सूर्यस्य दिवाकरसमप्रभौ ॥७०॥
transliteration: iṣubhirvimalābhāsaiśchādayantau parasparam ,arcirbhiriva sūryasya divākarasamaprabhau.
english: Whose radiance was equal to the sun, those two covered each other with arrows of pure brilliance, just like the rays of the sun.
