search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 70. Whose radiance was equal to the sun, those two covered each other with arrows of pure brilliance, just like the rays of the sun.

sanskrit: चापमार्गबलोद्धूतान्मार्गणान्वृष्णिसिंहयोः ।आकाशे समपश्याम पतंगानिव शीघ्रगान् ॥७१॥
transliteration: cāpamārgabaloddhūtānmārgaṇānvṛṣṇisiṁhayoḥ ,ākāśe samapaśyāma pataṁgāniva śīghragān.
english: In the sky, we saw the swift-moving arrows of the two Vṛṣṇi-lions, propelled by the force of their bows' paths, like birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 71. In the sky, we saw the swift-moving arrows of the two Vṛṣṇi-lions, propelled by the force of their bows' paths, like birds.

sanskrit: सात्यकिं दशभिर्विद्ध्वा हयांश्चास्य त्रिभिः शरैः ।चापमेकेन चिच्छेद हार्दिक्यो नतपर्वणा ॥७२॥
transliteration: sātyakiṁ daśabhirviddhvā hayāṁścāsya tribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,cāpamekena ciccheda hārdikyo nataparvaṇā.
english: Hārdikya, having pierced Satyaki with ten arrows and his horses with three arrows, then cut his bow with a single straight-jointed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 72. Hārdikya, having pierced Satyaki with ten arrows and his horses with three arrows, then cut his bow with a single straight-jointed arrow.

sanskrit: तन्निकृत्तं धनुः श्रेष्ठमपास्य शिनिपुंगवः ।अन्यदादत्त वेगेन वेगवत्तरमायुधम् ॥७३॥
transliteration: tannikṛttaṁ dhanuḥ śreṣṭhamapāsya śinipuṁgavaḥ ,anyadādatta vegena vegavattaramāyudham.
english: Having cast aside that excellent, severed bow, Satyaki (śinipuṅgava) swiftly took up another, even swifter weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 73. Having cast aside that excellent, severed bow, Satyaki (śinipuṅgava) swiftly took up another, even swifter weapon.

sanskrit: तदादाय धनुः श्रेष्ठं वरिष्ठः सर्वधन्विनाम् ।हार्दिक्यं दशभिर्बाणैः प्रत्यविध्यत्स्तनान्तरे ॥७४॥
transliteration: tadādāya dhanuḥ śreṣṭhaṁ variṣṭhaḥ sarvadhanvinām ,hārdikyaṁ daśabhirbāṇaiḥ pratyavidhyatstanāntare.
english: Having taken up that excellent bow, the foremost of all archers (Satyaki) pierced Hārdikya in the middle of his chest with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 74. Having taken up that excellent bow, the foremost of all archers (Satyaki) pierced Hārdikya in the middle of his chest with ten arrows.

sanskrit: ततो रथं युगेषां च छित्त्वा भल्लैः सुसंयतैः ।अश्वांस्तस्यावधीत्तूर्णमुभौ च पार्ष्णिसारथी ॥७५॥
transliteration: tato rathaṁ yugeṣāṁ ca chittvā bhallaiḥ susaṁyataiḥ ,aśvāṁstasyāvadhīttūrṇamubhau ca pārṣṇisārathī.
english: Then, with well-aimed broad-headed arrows, he cut his chariot, its yoke, and pole, and swiftly killed his horses, as well as both the charioteer and his attendant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 75. Then, with well-aimed broad-headed arrows, he cut his chariot, its yoke, and pole, and swiftly killed his horses, as well as both the charioteer and his attendant.

sanskrit: मद्रराजे हते राजन्विरथे कृतवर्मणि ।दुर्योधनबलं सर्वं पुनरासीत्पराङ्मुखम् ॥७६॥
transliteration: madrarāje hate rājanvirathe kṛtavarmaṇi ,duryodhanabalaṁ sarvaṁ punarāsītparāṅmukham.
english: O King, when the king of the Madras was slain and Kritavarman was dismounted from his chariot, Duryodhana's entire army again turned away in retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 76. O King, when the king of the Madras was slain and Kritavarman was dismounted from his chariot, Duryodhana's entire army again turned away in retreat.

sanskrit: तत्परे नावबुध्यन्त सैन्येन रजसा वृते ।बलं तु हतभूयिष्ठं तत्तदासीत्पराङ्मुखम् ॥७७॥
transliteration: tatpare nāvabudhyanta sainyena rajasā vṛte ,balaṁ tu hatabhūyiṣṭhaṁ tattadāsītparāṅmukham.
english: Others among them did not realize, as the army was enveloped in dust. But that army, with most of its men slain, was indeed in full retreat at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 77. Others among them did not realize, as the army was enveloped in dust. But that army, with most of its men slain, was indeed in full retreat at that time.

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्तात्तेऽपश्यन्रजो भौमं समुत्थितम् ।विविधैः शोणितस्रावैः प्रशान्तं पुरुषर्षभ ॥७८॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtātte'paśyanrajo bhaumaṁ samutthitam ,vividhaiḥ śoṇitasrāvaiḥ praśāntaṁ puruṣarṣabha.
english: O best of men, then after a moment, they saw the earthy dust that had risen, now subsided by various streams of blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 78. O best of men, then after a moment, they saw the earthy dust that had risen, now subsided by various streams of blood.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो दृष्ट्वा भग्नं स्वबलमन्तिकात् ।जवेनापततः पार्थानेकः सर्वानवारयत् ॥७९॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano dṛṣṭvā bhagnaṁ svabalamantikāt ,javenāpatataḥ pārthānekaḥ sarvānavārayat.
english: Then Duryodhana, having seen his own army shattered from close by, alone resisted all the Pārthas who were swiftly attacking.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 79. Then Duryodhana, having seen his own army shattered from close by, alone resisted all the Pārthas who were swiftly attacking.

sanskrit: पाण्डवान्सरथान्दृष्ट्वा धृष्टद्युम्नं च पार्षतम् ।आनर्तं च दुराधर्षं शितैर्बाणैरवाकिरत् ॥८०॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavānsarathāndṛṣṭvā dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pārṣatam ,ānartaṁ ca durādharṣaṁ śitairbāṇairavākirat.
english: Having seen the Pāṇḍavas with their chariots, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, as well as the unassailable Anarta, he showered them with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 80. Having seen the Pāṇḍavas with their chariots, and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, as well as the unassailable Anarta, he showered them with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तं परे नाभ्यवर्तन्त मर्त्या मृत्युमिवागतम् ।अथान्यं रथमास्थाय हार्दिक्योऽपि न्यवर्तत ॥८१॥
transliteration: taṁ pare nābhyavartanta martyā mṛtyumivāgatam ,athānyaṁ rathamāsthāya hārdikyo'pi nyavartata.
english: The others (his enemies) did not approach him, just as mortals would not approach imminent death. Then, Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman) also retreated, having mounted another chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 81. The others (his enemies) did not approach him, just as mortals would not approach imminent death. Then, Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman) also retreated, having mounted another chariot.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा त्वरमाणो महारथः ।चतुर्भिर्निजघानाश्वान्पत्रिभिः कृतवर्मणः ।विव्याध गौतमं चापि षड्भिर्भल्लैः सुतेजनैः ॥८२॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā tvaramāṇo mahārathaḥ ,caturbhirnijaghānāśvānpatribhiḥ kṛtavarmaṇaḥ ,vivyādha gautamaṁ cāpi ṣaḍbhirbhallaiḥ sutejanaiḥ.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, the great warrior, swiftly struck down Kṛtavarman's horses with four feathered arrows. He also pierced Gautama (Kṛpācārya) with six very sharp, broad-headed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 82. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, the great warrior, swiftly struck down Kṛtavarman's horses with four feathered arrows. He also pierced Gautama (Kṛpācārya) with six very sharp, broad-headed arrows.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा ततो राज्ञा हताश्वं विरथीकृतम् ।समपोवाह हार्दिक्यं स्वरथेन युधिष्ठिरात् ॥८३॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā tato rājñā hatāśvaṁ virathīkṛtam ,samapovāha hārdikyaṁ svarathena yudhiṣṭhirāt.
english: Then Aśvatthāman, using his own chariot, rescued Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman) from Yudhiṣṭhira, as Hārdikya's horses had been killed and he had been rendered chariot-less by the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 83. Then Aśvatthāman, using his own chariot, rescued Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman) from Yudhiṣṭhira, as Hārdikya's horses had been killed and he had been rendered chariot-less by the king.

sanskrit: ततः शारद्वतोऽष्टाभिः प्रत्यविध्यद्युधिष्ठिरम् ।विव्याध चाश्वान्निशितैस्तस्याष्टाभिः शिलीमुखैः ॥८४॥
transliteration: tataḥ śāradvato'ṣṭābhiḥ pratyavidhyadyudhiṣṭhiram ,vivyādha cāśvānniśitaistasyāṣṭābhiḥ śilīmukhaiḥ.
english: Then Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvata, pierced Yudhiṣṭhira with eight arrows. He also pierced Yudhiṣṭhira's horses with eight sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 84. Then Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvata, pierced Yudhiṣṭhira with eight arrows. He also pierced Yudhiṣṭhira's horses with eight sharp arrows.

sanskrit: एवमेतन्महाराज युद्धशेषमवर्तत ।तव दुर्मन्त्रिते राजन्सहपुत्रस्य भारत ॥८५॥
transliteration: evametanmahārāja yuddhaśeṣamavartata ,tava durmantrite rājansahaputrasya bhārata.
english: O great king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), this remainder of the battle thus transpired due to your ill-advised plan, along with that of your sons, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 85. O great king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), this remainder of the battle thus transpired due to your ill-advised plan, along with that of your sons, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्महेष्वासवरे विशस्ते संग्राममध्ये कुरुपुंगवेन ।पार्थाः समेताः परमप्रहृष्टाः शङ्खान्प्रदध्मुर्हतमीक्ष्य शल्यम् ॥८६॥
transliteration: tasminmaheṣvāsavare viśaste; saṁgrāmamadhye kurupuṁgavena ,pārthāḥ sametāḥ paramaprahṛṣṭāḥ; śaṅkhānpradadhmurhatamīkṣya śalyam.
english: When Śalya, that best of great archers, was slain in the midst of battle by Yudhiṣṭhira, the bull among the Kurus, the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā), having seen him killed, assembled and, exceedingly joyful, blew their conch shells.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 86. When Śalya, that best of great archers, was slain in the midst of battle by Yudhiṣṭhira, the bull among the Kurus, the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā), having seen him killed, assembled and, exceedingly joyful, blew their conch shells.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं च प्रशशंसुराजौ पुरा सुरा वृत्रवधे यथेन्द्रम् ।चक्रुश्च नानाविधवाद्यशब्दान्निनादयन्तो वसुधां समन्तात् ॥८७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ ca praśaśaṁsurājau; purā surā vṛtravadhe yathendram ,cakruśca nānāvidhavādyaśabdā;nninādayanto vasudhāṁ samantāt.
english: And they praised Yudhiṣṭhira in battle, just as the gods formerly praised Indra for the slaying of Vṛtra. And they made various kinds of musical sounds, filling the entire earth with their resonance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 16 Verse 87. And they praised Yudhiṣṭhira in battle, just as the gods formerly praised Indra for the slaying of Vṛtra. And they made various kinds of musical sounds, filling the entire earth with their resonance.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।शल्ये तु निहते राजन्मद्रराजपदानुगाः ।रथाः सप्तशता वीरा निर्ययुर्महतो बलात् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śalye tu nihate rājanmadrarājapadānugāḥ ,rathāḥ saptaśatā vīrā niryayurmahato balāt.
english: Sañjaya said: O King, when Śalya had been killed, seven hundred brave charioteers, followers of the king of Madras, emerged from the vast army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O King, when Śalya had been killed, seven hundred brave charioteers, followers of the king of Madras, emerged from the vast army.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु द्विरदमारुह्याचलसंनिभम् ।छत्रेण ध्रियमाणेन वीज्यमानश्च चामरैः ।न गन्तव्यं न गन्तव्यमिति मद्रानवारयत् ॥२॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu dviradamāruhyācalasaṁnibham ,chatreṇa dhriyamāṇena vījyamānaśca cāmaraiḥ ,na gantavyaṁ na gantavyamiti madrānavārayat.
english: But Duryodhana, having mounted a mountain-like elephant, with an umbrella held (over him) and being fanned by whisks, restrained the Madras, saying, "Do not go! Do not go!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. But Duryodhana, having mounted a mountain-like elephant, with an umbrella held (over him) and being fanned by whisks, restrained the Madras, saying, "Do not go! Do not go!"

sanskrit: दुर्योधनेन ते वीरा वार्यमाणाः पुनः पुनः ।युधिष्ठिरं जिघांसन्तः पाण्डूनां प्राविशन्बलम् ॥३॥
transliteration: duryodhanena te vīrā vāryamāṇāḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ jighāṁsantaḥ pāṇḍūnāṁ prāviśanbalam.
english: Though repeatedly restrained by Duryodhana, those brave warriors, desiring to kill Yudhiṣṭhira, entered the Pāṇḍava army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. Though repeatedly restrained by Duryodhana, those brave warriors, desiring to kill Yudhiṣṭhira, entered the Pāṇḍava army.

sanskrit: ते तु शूरा महाराज कृतचित्ताः स्म योधने ।धनुःशब्दं महत्कृत्वा सहायुध्यन्त पाण्डवैः ॥४॥
transliteration: te tu śūrā mahārāja kṛtacittāḥ sma yodhane ,dhanuḥśabdaṁ mahatkṛtvā sahāyudhyanta pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: O great King, those brave warriors, resolute in battle, made a loud sound with their bows and fought with the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. O great King, those brave warriors, resolute in battle, made a loud sound with their bows and fought with the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु निहतं शल्यं धर्मपुत्रं च पीडितम् ।मद्रराजप्रिये युक्तैर्मद्रकाणां महारथैः ॥५॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu nihataṁ śalyaṁ dharmaputraṁ ca pīḍitam ,madrarājapriye yuktairmadrakāṇāṁ mahārathaiḥ.
english: Having heard that Shalya was slain and the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) was distressed, along with the well-equipped great charioteers of the Madras, O beloved of the Madra king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. Having heard that Shalya was slain and the son of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) was distressed, along with the well-equipped great charioteers of the Madras, O beloved of the Madra king.

sanskrit: आजगाम ततः पार्थो गाण्डीवं विक्षिपन्धनुः ।पूरयन्रथघोषेण दिशः सर्वा महारथः ॥६॥
transliteration: ājagāma tataḥ pārtho gāṇḍīvaṁ vikṣipandhanuḥ ,pūrayanrathaghoṣeṇa diśaḥ sarvā mahārathaḥ.
english: Thereupon, Partha (Arjuna), the great charioteer, arrived, waving his Gāṇḍīva bow and filling all directions with the sound of his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. Thereupon, Partha (Arjuna), the great charioteer, arrived, waving his Gāṇḍīva bow and filling all directions with the sound of his chariot.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनश्च भीमश्च माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।सात्यकिश्च नरव्याघ्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato'rjunaśca bhīmaśca mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,sātyakiśca naravyāghro draupadeyāśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then Arjuna and Bhīma, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Sātyaki, the tiger among men, and all the sons of Draupadī,
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. Then Arjuna and Bhīma, and the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Sātyaki, the tiger among men, and all the sons of Draupadī,

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च पाञ्चालाः सह सोमकैः ।युधिष्ठिरं परीप्सन्तः समन्तात्पर्यवारयन् ॥८॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca pāñcālāḥ saha somakaiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ parīpsantaḥ samantātparyavārayan.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and the Pāñcālas with the Somakas, wishing to protect Yudhiṣṭhira, surrounded him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and the Pāñcālas with the Somakas, wishing to protect Yudhiṣṭhira, surrounded him from all sides.

sanskrit: ते समन्तात्परिवृताः पाण्डवैः पुरुषर्षभाः ।क्षोभयन्ति स्म तां सेनां मकराः सागरं यथा ॥९॥
transliteration: te samantātparivṛtāḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ puruṣarṣabhāḥ ,kṣobhayanti sma tāṁ senāṁ makarāḥ sāgaraṁ yathā.
english: Surrounded on all sides by the Pāṇḍavas, those foremost among men (the Kuru warriors) agitated that army, just as sea monsters agitate the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. Surrounded on all sides by the Pāṇḍavas, those foremost among men (the Kuru warriors) agitated that army, just as sea monsters agitate the ocean.

sanskrit: पुरोवातेन गङ्गेव क्षोभ्यमाना महानदी ।अक्षोभ्यत तदा राजन्पाण्डूनां ध्वजिनी पुनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: purovātena gaṅgeva kṣobhyamānā mahānadī ,akṣobhyata tadā rājanpāṇḍūnāṁ dhvajinī punaḥ.
english: O king, the army (dhvajinī) of the Pāṇḍavas was then again agitated, just like the great river Gaṅgā being disturbed by a strong headwind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. O king, the army (dhvajinī) of the Pāṇḍavas was then again agitated, just like the great river Gaṅgā being disturbed by a strong headwind.

sanskrit: प्रस्कन्द्य सेनां महतीं त्यक्तात्मानो महारथाः ।वृक्षानिव महावाताः कम्पयन्ति स्म तावकाः ॥११॥
transliteration: praskandya senāṁ mahatīṁ tyaktātmāno mahārathāḥ ,vṛkṣāniva mahāvātāḥ kampayanti sma tāvakāḥ.
english: Your (Kaurava) great chariot-warriors (mahārathāḥ), fearless (tyaktātmanāḥ) and having assailed the great army, were shaking it, just as great winds shake trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. Your (Kaurava) great chariot-warriors (mahārathāḥ), fearless (tyaktātmanāḥ) and having assailed the great army, were shaking it, just as great winds shake trees.

sanskrit: बहवश्चुक्रुशुस्तत्र क्व स राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।भ्रातरो वास्य ते शूरा दृश्यन्ते नेह केचन ॥१२॥
transliteration: bahavaścukruśustatra kva sa rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhrātaro vāsya te śūrā dṛśyante neha kecana.
english: Many there cried out, "Where is that King Yudhiṣṭhira? Neither he nor any of his brave brothers are seen here!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. Many there cried out, "Where is that King Yudhiṣṭhira? Neither he nor any of his brave brothers are seen here!"

sanskrit: पाञ्चालानां महावीर्याः शिखण्डी च महारथः ।धृष्टद्युम्नोऽथ शैनेयो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः ॥१३॥
transliteration: pāñcālānāṁ mahāvīryāḥ śikhaṇḍī ca mahārathaḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumno'tha śaineyo draupadeyāśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Among the greatly valorous warriors of the Pañcālas are Shikhaṇḍī, a great charioteer; Dhṛṣṭadyumna; Śaineya; and all the sons of Draupadī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. Among the greatly valorous warriors of the Pañcālas are Shikhaṇḍī, a great charioteer; Dhṛṣṭadyumna; Śaineya; and all the sons of Draupadī.

sanskrit: एवं तान्वादिनः शूरान्द्रौपदेया महारथाः ।अभ्यघ्नन्युयुधानश्च मद्रराजपदानुगान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: evaṁ tānvādinaḥ śūrāndraupadeyā mahārathāḥ ,abhyaghnanyuyudhānaśca madrarājapadānugān.
english: Thus, the great charioteers, the sons of Draupadī, and Yuyudhāna, struck down those brave opponents who followed the Madra king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. Thus, the great charioteers, the sons of Draupadī, and Yuyudhāna, struck down those brave opponents who followed the Madra king.

sanskrit: चक्रैर्विमथितैः केचित्केचिच्छिन्नैर्महाध्वजैः ।प्रत्यदृश्यन्त समरे तावका निहताः परैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: cakrairvimathitaiḥ kecitkecicchinnairmahādhvajaiḥ ,pratyadṛśyanta samare tāvakā nihatāḥ paraiḥ.
english: In the battle, your men were seen killed by the enemies; some were crushed by chariot wheels, while others had their great banners cut down.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. In the battle, your men were seen killed by the enemies; some were crushed by chariot wheels, while others had their great banners cut down.

sanskrit: आलोक्य पाण्डवान्युद्धे योधा राजन्समन्ततः ।वार्यमाणा ययुर्वेगात्तव पुत्रेण भारत ॥१६॥
transliteration: ālokya pāṇḍavānyuddhe yodhā rājansamantataḥ ,vāryamāṇā yayurvegāttava putreṇa bhārata.
english: O King (rājan), O Bhārata, your warriors, perceiving the Pāṇḍavas everywhere in the battle, despite being restrained by your son, fled swiftly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. O King (rājan), O Bhārata, your warriors, perceiving the Pāṇḍavas everywhere in the battle, despite being restrained by your son, fled swiftly.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु तान्वीरान्वारयामास सान्त्वयन् ।न चास्य शासनं कश्चित्तत्र चक्रे महारथः ॥१७॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu tānvīrānvārayāmāsa sāntvayan ,na cāsya śāsanaṁ kaścittatra cakre mahārathaḥ.
english: Duryodhana, however, was pacifying and restraining those warriors. But no great charioteer there obeyed his command.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. Duryodhana, however, was pacifying and restraining those warriors. But no great charioteer there obeyed his command.

sanskrit: ततो गान्धारराजस्य पुत्रः शकुनिरब्रवीत् ।दुर्योधनं महाराज वचनं वचनक्षमः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato gāndhārarājasya putraḥ śakunirabravīt ,duryodhanaṁ mahārāja vacanaṁ vacanakṣamaḥ.
english: Then, the son of the Gandhara king, Shakuni, who was skilled in speech, spoke these words to King Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. Then, the son of the Gandhara king, Shakuni, who was skilled in speech, spoke these words to King Duryodhana.

sanskrit: किं नः संप्रेक्षमाणानां मद्राणां हन्यते बलम् ।न युक्तमेतत्समरे त्वयि तिष्ठति भारत ॥१९॥
transliteration: kiṁ naḥ saṁprekṣamāṇānāṁ madrāṇāṁ hanyate balam ,na yuktametatsamare tvayi tiṣṭhati bhārata.
english: Why is the strength of the Madras being destroyed while we are looking on? This is not proper, O Bharata, while you are standing on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. Why is the strength of the Madras being destroyed while we are looking on? This is not proper, O Bharata, while you are standing on the battlefield.

sanskrit: सहितैर्नाम योद्धव्यमित्येष समयः कृतः ।अथ कस्मात्परानेव घ्नतो मर्षयसे नृप ॥२०॥
transliteration: sahitairnāma yoddhavyamityeṣa samayaḥ kṛtaḥ ,atha kasmātparāneva ghnato marṣayase nṛpa.
english: Indeed, it was agreed that we should fight together – this arrangement was made. So, O King, why do you tolerate the enemies, even as they are killing (our men)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. Indeed, it was agreed that we should fight together – this arrangement was made. So, O King, why do you tolerate the enemies, even as they are killing (our men)?

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।वार्यमाणा मया पूर्वं नैते चक्रुर्वचो मम ।एते हि निहताः सर्वे प्रस्कन्नाः पाण्डुवाहिनीम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,vāryamāṇā mayā pūrvaṁ naite cakrurvaco mama ,ete hi nihatāḥ sarve praskannāḥ pāṇḍuvāhinīm.
english: Duryodhana said: Though previously restrained by me, these (warriors) did not heed my words. Indeed, all of them have been struck down (defeated) and have scattered towards the Pāṇḍava army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. Duryodhana said: Though previously restrained by me, these (warriors) did not heed my words. Indeed, all of them have been struck down (defeated) and have scattered towards the Pāṇḍava army.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।न भर्तुः शासनं वीरा रणे कुर्वन्त्यमर्षिताः ।अलं क्रोद्धुं तथैतेषां नायं काल उपेक्षितुम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,na bhartuḥ śāsanaṁ vīrā raṇe kurvantyamarṣitāḥ ,alaṁ kroddhuṁ tathaiteṣāṁ nāyaṁ kāla upekṣitum.
english: Śakuni said: Indeed, exasperated heroes do not follow their master's command in battle. There is no need to be angry at them; this is not the time to neglect (the situation).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. Śakuni said: Indeed, exasperated heroes do not follow their master's command in battle. There is no need to be angry at them; this is not the time to neglect (the situation).

sanskrit: यामः सर्वेऽत्र संभूय सवाजिरथकुञ्जराः ।परित्रातुं महेष्वासान्मद्रराजपदानुगान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: yāmaḥ sarve'tra saṁbhūya savājirathakuñjarāḥ ,paritrātuṁ maheṣvāsānmadrarājapadānugān.
english: Let all of us go there together, with horses, chariots, and elephants, to protect the great archers, the followers of the King of Madras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. Let all of us go there together, with horses, chariots, and elephants, to protect the great archers, the followers of the King of Madras.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं परिरक्षामो यत्नेन महता नृप ।एवं सर्वेऽनुसंचिन्त्य प्रययुर्यत्र सैनिकाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ parirakṣāmo yatnena mahatā nṛpa ,evaṁ sarve'nusaṁcintya prayayuryatra sainikāḥ.
english: O King, let us protect each other with great effort. Having thus deliberated, all (of them) went to where the warriors were.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 24. O King, let us protect each other with great effort. Having thus deliberated, all (of them) went to where the warriors were.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्ततो राजा बलेन महता वृतः ।प्रययौ सिंहनादेन कम्पयन्वै वसुंधराम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evamuktastato rājā balena mahatā vṛtaḥ ,prayayau siṁhanādena kampayanvai vasuṁdharām.
english: Sañjaya said: Having been addressed thus, the king, surrounded by a mighty army, set forth with a lion's roar, indeed causing the earth to tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 25. Sañjaya said: Having been addressed thus, the king, surrounded by a mighty army, set forth with a lion's roar, indeed causing the earth to tremble.

sanskrit: हत विध्यत गृह्णीत प्रहरध्वं निकृन्तत ।इत्यासीत्तुमुलः शब्दस्तव सैन्यस्य भारत ॥२६॥
transliteration: hata vidhyata gṛhṇīta praharadhvaṁ nikṛntata ,ityāsīttumulaḥ śabdastava sainyasya bhārata.
english: “Kill! Pierce! Seize! Strike! Cut down!” – such was the tumultuous cry of your army, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 26. “Kill! Pierce! Seize! Strike! Cut down!” – such was the tumultuous cry of your army, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: पाण्डवास्तु रणे दृष्ट्वा मद्रराजपदानुगान् ।सहितानभ्यवर्तन्त गुल्ममास्थाय मध्यमम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāstu raṇe dṛṣṭvā madrarājapadānugān ,sahitānabhyavartanta gulmamāsthāya madhyamam.
english: But the Pāṇḍavas, having observed the united followers of the Madra king in battle, advanced against them, forming a central tactical formation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 27. But the Pāṇḍavas, having observed the united followers of the Madra king in battle, advanced against them, forming a central tactical formation.

sanskrit: ते मुहूर्ताद्रणे वीरा हस्ताहस्तं विशां पते ।निहताः प्रत्यदृश्यन्त मद्रराजपदानुगाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: te muhūrtādraṇe vīrā hastāhastaṁ viśāṁ pate ,nihatāḥ pratyadṛśyanta madrarājapadānugāḥ.
english: O lord of the people, within a moment, those valiant followers of the Madra king were seen slain in hand-to-hand combat on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 28. O lord of the people, within a moment, those valiant followers of the Madra king were seen slain in hand-to-hand combat on the battlefield.

sanskrit: ततो नः संप्रयातानां हतामित्रास्तरस्विनः ।हृष्टाः किलकिलाशब्दमकुर्वन्सहिताः परे ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato naḥ saṁprayātānāṁ hatāmitrāstarasvinaḥ ,hṛṣṭāḥ kilakilāśabdamakurvansahitāḥ pare.
english: Then, as our mighty ones, having slain their enemies, were advancing, others (of our forces), united and joyful, made loud cheering sounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 29. Then, as our mighty ones, having slain their enemies, were advancing, others (of our forces), united and joyful, made loud cheering sounds.

sanskrit: अथोत्थितानि रुण्डानि समदृश्यन्त सर्वशः ।पपात महती चोल्का मध्येनादित्यमण्डलम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: athotthitāni ruṇḍāni samadṛśyanta sarvaśaḥ ,papāta mahatī colkā madhyenādityamaṇḍalam.
english: Then, headless trunks rose up and were seen everywhere. And a great meteor fell through the middle of the sun's disk.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 30. Then, headless trunks rose up and were seen everywhere. And a great meteor fell through the middle of the sun's disk.

sanskrit: रथैर्भग्नैर्युगाक्षैश्च निहतैश्च महारथैः ।अश्वैर्निपतितैश्चैव संछन्नाभूद्वसुंधरा ॥३१॥
transliteration: rathairbhagnairyugākṣaiśca nihataiśca mahārathaiḥ ,aśvairnipatitaiścaiva saṁchannābhūdvasuṁdharā.
english: The earth became covered with broken chariots, with poles and axles, with slain great warriors, and with fallen horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 31. The earth became covered with broken chariots, with poles and axles, with slain great warriors, and with fallen horses.

sanskrit: वातायमानैस्तुरगैर्युगासक्तैस्तुरंगमैः ।अदृश्यन्त महाराज योधास्तत्र रणाजिरे ॥३२॥
transliteration: vātāyamānaisturagairyugāsaktaisturaṁgamaiḥ ,adṛśyanta mahārāja yodhāstatra raṇājire.
english: O great king, the warriors were seen there on the battlefield, accompanied by horses that were swift as the wind and attached to their yokes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 32. O great king, the warriors were seen there on the battlefield, accompanied by horses that were swift as the wind and attached to their yokes.

sanskrit: भग्नचक्रान्रथान्केचिदवहंस्तुरगा रणे ।रथार्धं केचिदादाय दिशो दश विबभ्रमुः ।तत्र तत्र च दृश्यन्ते योक्त्रैः श्लिष्टाः स्म वाजिनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: bhagnacakrānrathānkecidavahaṁsturagā raṇe ,rathārdhaṁ kecidādāya diśo daśa vibabhramuḥ ,tatra tatra ca dṛśyante yoktraiḥ śliṣṭāḥ sma vājinaḥ.
english: On the battlefield, some horses dragged chariots with broken wheels. Others, taking half a chariot, roamed in the ten directions. And here and there, horses were seen entangled by their harnesses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 33. On the battlefield, some horses dragged chariots with broken wheels. Others, taking half a chariot, roamed in the ten directions. And here and there, horses were seen entangled by their harnesses.

sanskrit: रथिनः पतमानाश्च व्यदृश्यन्त नरोत्तम ।गगनात्प्रच्युताः सिद्धाः पुण्यानामिव संक्षये ॥३४॥
transliteration: rathinaḥ patamānāśca vyadṛśyanta narottama ,gaganātpracyutāḥ siddhāḥ puṇyānāmiva saṁkṣaye.
english: And fallen charioteers were seen, O best of men, like Siddhas fallen from the heavens at the exhaustion of their merits.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 34. And fallen charioteers were seen, O best of men, like Siddhas fallen from the heavens at the exhaustion of their merits.

sanskrit: निहतेषु च शूरेषु मद्रराजानुगेषु च ।अस्मानापततश्चापि दृष्ट्वा पार्था महारथाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: nihateṣu ca śūreṣu madrarājānugeṣu ca ,asmānāpatataścāpi dṛṣṭvā pārthā mahārathāḥ.
english: And when the brave warriors and the followers of the Madra king were slain, and seeing us charging upon them, the great charioteers (mahārathas), the sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 35. And when the brave warriors and the followers of the Madra king were slain, and seeing us charging upon them, the great charioteers (mahārathas), the sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas)...

sanskrit: अभ्यवर्तन्त वेगेन जयगृध्राः प्रहारिणः ।बाणशब्दरवान्कृत्वा विमिश्राञ्शङ्खनिस्वनैः ॥३६॥
transliteration: abhyavartanta vegena jayagṛdhrāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,bāṇaśabdaravānkṛtvā vimiśrāñśaṅkhanisvanaiḥ.
english: ...they (the Pārthas and mahārathas from the previous verse) turned back swiftly, striking and eager for victory, producing loud roars from their arrows mingled with the sounds of conch shells.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 36. ...they (the Pārthas and mahārathas from the previous verse) turned back swiftly, striking and eager for victory, producing loud roars from their arrows mingled with the sounds of conch shells.

sanskrit: अस्मांस्तु पुनरासाद्य लब्धलक्षाः प्रहारिणः ।शरासनानि धुन्वानाः सिंहनादान्प्रचुक्रुशुः ॥३७॥
transliteration: asmāṁstu punarāsādya labdhalakṣāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,śarāsanāni dhunvānāḥ siṁhanādānpracukruśuḥ.
english: Having again approached us, the attackers, who had found their target, twanged their bows and roared lion-like roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 37. Having again approached us, the attackers, who had found their target, twanged their bows and roared lion-like roars.

sanskrit: ततो हतमभिप्रेक्ष्य मद्रराजबलं महत् ।मद्रराजं च समरे दृष्ट्वा शूरं निपातितम् ।दुर्योधनबलं सर्वं पुनरासीत्पराङ्मुखम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tato hatamabhiprekṣya madrarājabalaṁ mahat ,madrarājaṁ ca samare dṛṣṭvā śūraṁ nipātitam ,duryodhanabalaṁ sarvaṁ punarāsītparāṅmukham.
english: Then, seeing the great army of the Madra king slain, and seeing the heroic Madra king himself fallen in battle, Duryodhana's entire army again turned away.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 38. Then, seeing the great army of the Madra king slain, and seeing the heroic Madra king himself fallen in battle, Duryodhana's entire army again turned away.

sanskrit: वध्यमानं महाराज पाण्डवैर्जितकाशिभिः ।दिशो भेजेऽथ संभ्रान्तं त्रासितं दृढधन्विभिः ॥३९॥
transliteration: vadhyamānaṁ mahārāja pāṇḍavairjitakāśibhiḥ ,diśo bheje'tha saṁbhrāntaṁ trāsitaṁ dṛḍhadhanvibhiḥ.
english: O great king, being slain by the Pandavas, who shone with victory, and terrified and bewildered by those with strong bows, it (Duryodhana's army) then fled in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 17 Verse 39. O great king, being slain by the Pandavas, who shone with victory, and terrified and bewildered by those with strong bows, it (Duryodhana's army) then fled in all directions.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।पातिते युधि दुर्धर्षे मद्रराजे महारथे ।तावकास्तव पुत्राश्च प्रायशो विमुखाभवन् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,pātite yudhi durdharṣe madrarāje mahārathe ,tāvakāstava putrāśca prāyaśo vimukhābhavan.
english: Sañjaya said: When the unconquerable Madra king, a great charioteer, had fallen in battle, your people and your sons, for the most part, turned away and fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: When the unconquerable Madra king, a great charioteer, had fallen in battle, your people and your sons, for the most part, turned away and fled.

sanskrit: वणिजो नावि भिन्नायां यथागाधेऽप्लवेऽर्णवे ।अपारे पारमिच्छन्तो हते शूरे महात्मनि ॥२॥
transliteration: vaṇijo nāvi bhinnāyāṁ yathāgādhe'plave'rṇave ,apāre pāramicchanto hate śūre mahātmani.
english: Just as merchants, with their boat shattered in a fathomless, uncrossable ocean, long for the shore in that boundless sea, such was our state when the great-souled hero was slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. Just as merchants, with their boat shattered in a fathomless, uncrossable ocean, long for the shore in that boundless sea, such was our state when the great-souled hero was slain.

sanskrit: मद्रराजे महाराज वित्रस्ताः शरविक्षताः ।अनाथा नाथमिच्छन्तो मृगाः सिंहार्दिता इव ॥३॥
transliteration: madrarāje mahārāja vitrastāḥ śaravikṣatāḥ ,anāthā nāthamicchanto mṛgāḥ siṁhārditā iva.
english: O great King, lord of Madra, we are terrified and pierced by arrows, helpless, longing for a protector, just like deer afflicted by a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. O great King, lord of Madra, we are terrified and pierced by arrows, helpless, longing for a protector, just like deer afflicted by a lion.

sanskrit: वृषा यथा भग्नशृङ्गाः शीर्णदन्ता गजा इव ।मध्याह्ने प्रत्यपायाम निर्जिता धर्मसूनुना ॥४॥
transliteration: vṛṣā yathā bhagnaśṛṅgāḥ śīrṇadantā gajā iva ,madhyāhne pratyapāyāma nirjitā dharmasūnunā.
english: Just like bulls with broken horns or elephants with shattered tusks, we retreated at midday, utterly defeated by the son of righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. Just like bulls with broken horns or elephants with shattered tusks, we retreated at midday, utterly defeated by the son of righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: न संधातुमनीकानि न च राजन्पराक्रमे ।आसीद्बुद्धिर्हते शल्ये तव योधस्य कस्यचित् ॥५॥
transliteration: na saṁdhātumanīkāni na ca rājanparākrame ,āsīdbuddhirhate śalye tava yodhasya kasyacit.
english: O King, once Śalya was slain, no warrior of yours possessed the resolve to rally the armies or to engage in valorous acts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. O King, once Śalya was slain, no warrior of yours possessed the resolve to rally the armies or to engage in valorous acts.

sanskrit: भीष्मे द्रोणे च निहते सूतपुत्रे च भारत ।यद्दुःखं तव योधानां भयं चासीद्विशां पते ।तद्भयं स च नः शोको भूय एवाभ्यवर्तत ॥६॥
transliteration: bhīṣme droṇe ca nihate sūtaputre ca bhārata ,yadduḥkhaṁ tava yodhānāṁ bhayaṁ cāsīdviśāṁ pate ,tadbhayaṁ sa ca naḥ śoko bhūya evābhyavartata.
english: O Bhārata, O lord of the people, the fear and grief that your warriors felt when Bhishma, Drona, and also Karna, the son of Sūta, were killed - that very fear and grief has now returned to us again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. O Bhārata, O lord of the people, the fear and grief that your warriors felt when Bhishma, Drona, and also Karna, the son of Sūta, were killed - that very fear and grief has now returned to us again.

sanskrit: निराशाश्च जये तस्मिन्हते शल्ये महारथे ।हतप्रवीरा विध्वस्ता विकृत्ताश्च शितैः शरैः ।मद्रराजे हते राजन्योधास्ते प्राद्रवन्भयात् ॥७॥
transliteration: nirāśāśca jaye tasminhate śalye mahārathe ,hatapravīrā vidhvastā vikṛttāśca śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,madrarāje hate rājanyodhāste prādravanbhayāt.
english: O King, when the great charioteer Shalya, who was also the King of the Madras, was killed, those warriors - deprived of hope for victory, their chief heroes slain, shattered, and cut to pieces by sharp arrows - fled in fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. O King, when the great charioteer Shalya, who was also the King of the Madras, was killed, those warriors - deprived of hope for victory, their chief heroes slain, shattered, and cut to pieces by sharp arrows - fled in fear.

sanskrit: अश्वानन्ये गजानन्ये रथानन्ये महारथाः ।आरुह्य जवसंपन्नाः पादाताः प्राद्रवन्भयात् ॥८॥
transliteration: aśvānanye gajānanye rathānanye mahārathāḥ ,āruhya javasaṁpannāḥ pādātāḥ prādravanbhayāt.
english: Some (warriors), having mounted horses, others elephants, and others chariots (while the great charioteers mounted their own), fled in fear; even the swift foot soldiers fled in fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. Some (warriors), having mounted horses, others elephants, and others chariots (while the great charioteers mounted their own), fled in fear; even the swift foot soldiers fled in fear.

sanskrit: द्विसाहस्राश्च मातङ्गा गिरिरूपाः प्रहारिणः ।संप्राद्रवन्हते शल्ये अङ्कुशाङ्गुष्ठचोदिताः ॥९॥
transliteration: dvisāhasrāśca mātaṅgā girirūpāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,saṁprādravanhate śalye aṅkuśāṅguṣṭhacoditāḥ.
english: And two thousand elephants, mountain-like in form and capable of striking, even though urged by goads and the big toes of their riders, also fled away when Shalya was killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. And two thousand elephants, mountain-like in form and capable of striking, even though urged by goads and the big toes of their riders, also fled away when Shalya was killed.

sanskrit: ते रणाद्भरतश्रेष्ठ तावकाः प्राद्रवन्दिशः ।धावन्तश्चाप्यदृश्यन्त श्वसमानाः शरातुराः ॥१०॥
transliteration: te raṇādbharataśreṣṭha tāvakāḥ prādravandiśaḥ ,dhāvantaścāpyadṛśyanta śvasamānāḥ śarāturāḥ.
english: O best of the Bharatas (bharataśreṣṭha), your (warriors) fled from the battle in all directions. And they were seen running, panting heavily, and afflicted by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. O best of the Bharatas (bharataśreṣṭha), your (warriors) fled from the battle in all directions. And they were seen running, panting heavily, and afflicted by arrows.

sanskrit: तान्प्रभग्नान्द्रुतान्दृष्ट्वा हतोत्साहान्पराजितान् ।अभ्यद्रवन्त पाञ्चालाः पाण्डवाश्च जयैषिणः ॥११॥
transliteration: tānprabhagnāndrutāndṛṣṭvā hatotsāhānparājitān ,abhyadravanta pāñcālāḥ pāṇḍavāśca jayaiṣiṇaḥ.
english: Having seen those (warriors) broken, fled, disheartened, and defeated, the Pañcālas and the Pāṇḍavas, desirous of victory (jayaiṣiṇaḥ), charged towards them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. Having seen those (warriors) broken, fled, disheartened, and defeated, the Pañcālas and the Pāṇḍavas, desirous of victory (jayaiṣiṇaḥ), charged towards them.

sanskrit: बाणशब्दरवश्चापि सिंहनादश्च पुष्कलः ।शङ्खशब्दश्च शूराणां दारुणः समपद्यत ॥१२॥
transliteration: bāṇaśabdaravaścāpi siṁhanādaśca puṣkalaḥ ,śaṅkhaśabdaśca śūrāṇāṁ dāruṇaḥ samapadyata.
english: And an abundant clamor of arrow sounds and lion-roars, along with the dreadful sound of conch shells from the heroes, arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. And an abundant clamor of arrow sounds and lion-roars, along with the dreadful sound of conch shells from the heroes, arose.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु कौरवं सैन्यं भयत्रस्तं प्रविद्रुतम् ।अन्योन्यं समभाषन्त पाञ्चालाः पाण्डवैः सह ॥१३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu kauravaṁ sainyaṁ bhayatrastaṁ pravidrutam ,anyonyaṁ samabhāṣanta pāñcālāḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha.
english: But having seen the Kaurava army, terrified by fear (bhayatrasta) and thoroughly routed, the Pañcālas conversed with each other along with the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 13. But having seen the Kaurava army, terrified by fear (bhayatrasta) and thoroughly routed, the Pañcālas conversed with each other along with the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: अद्य राजा सत्यधृतिर्जितामित्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।अद्य दुर्योधनो हीनो दीप्तया नृपतिश्रिया ॥१४॥
transliteration: adya rājā satyadhṛtirjitāmitro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,adya duryodhano hīno dīptayā nṛpatiśriyā.
english: Today, King Yudhishthira, firm in truth and conqueror of his enemies, reigns. Today, Duryodhana is bereft of his shining royal glory.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 14. Today, King Yudhishthira, firm in truth and conqueror of his enemies, reigns. Today, Duryodhana is bereft of his shining royal glory.

sanskrit: अद्य श्रुत्वा हतं पुत्रं धृतराष्ट्रो जनेश्वरः ।निःसंज्ञः पतितो भूमौ किल्बिषं प्रतिपद्यताम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: adya śrutvā hataṁ putraṁ dhṛtarāṣṭro janeśvaraḥ ,niḥsaṁjñaḥ patito bhūmau kilbiṣaṁ pratipadyatām.
english: Today, upon hearing that his son has been slain, King Dhritarashtra, senseless and fallen to the ground, may he experience his transgression (kilbiṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 15. Today, upon hearing that his son has been slain, King Dhritarashtra, senseless and fallen to the ground, may he experience his transgression (kilbiṣa).

sanskrit: अद्य जानातु कौन्तेयं समर्थं सर्वधन्विनाम् ।अद्यात्मानं च दुर्मेधा गर्हयिष्यति पापकृत् ।अद्य क्षत्तुर्वचः सत्यं स्मरतां ब्रुवतो हितम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: adya jānātu kaunteyaṁ samarthaṁ sarvadhanvinām ,adyātmānaṁ ca durmedhā garhayiṣyati pāpakṛt ,adya kṣatturvacaḥ satyaṁ smaratāṁ bruvato hitam.
english: Today, let him know Kunti's son (Arjuna) to be capable among all archers. And today, that evil-minded sinner will condemn himself. Today, let him remember the true and beneficial words spoken by Vidura.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 16. Today, let him know Kunti's son (Arjuna) to be capable among all archers. And today, that evil-minded sinner will condemn himself. Today, let him remember the true and beneficial words spoken by Vidura.

sanskrit: अद्यप्रभृति पार्थांश्च प्रेष्यभूत उपाचरन् ।विजानातु नृपो दुःखं यत्प्राप्तं पाण्डुनन्दनैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: adyaprabhṛti pārthāṁśca preṣyabhūta upācaran ,vijānātu nṛpo duḥkhaṁ yatprāptaṁ pāṇḍunandanaiḥ.
english: From today onwards, let the king, having become a servant and attending to the sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas), truly comprehend the suffering that was experienced by the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 17. From today onwards, let the king, having become a servant and attending to the sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas), truly comprehend the suffering that was experienced by the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: अद्य कृष्णस्य माहात्म्यं जानातु स महीपतिः ।अद्यार्जुनधनुर्घोषं घोरं जानातु संयुगे ॥१८॥
transliteration: adya kṛṣṇasya māhātmyaṁ jānātu sa mahīpatiḥ ,adyārjunadhanurghoṣaṁ ghoraṁ jānātu saṁyuge.
english: Today, let that king understand the greatness of Krishna. Today, in battle, let him recognize the dreadful sound of Arjuna's bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 18. Today, let that king understand the greatness of Krishna. Today, in battle, let him recognize the dreadful sound of Arjuna's bow.

sanskrit: अस्त्राणां च बलं सर्वं बाह्वोश्च बलमाहवे ।अद्य ज्ञास्यति भीमस्य बलं घोरं महात्मनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: astrāṇāṁ ca balaṁ sarvaṁ bāhvośca balamāhave ,adya jñāsyati bhīmasya balaṁ ghoraṁ mahātmanaḥ.
english: And today, in battle, he will understand the full power of (Bhima's) weapons and the might of (Bhima's) arms. Today, he will come to know the terrible strength of the great-souled (mahātman) Bhima.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 19. And today, in battle, he will understand the full power of (Bhima's) weapons and the might of (Bhima's) arms. Today, he will come to know the terrible strength of the great-souled (mahātman) Bhima.

sanskrit: हते दुर्योधने युद्धे शक्रेणेवासुरे मये ।यत्कृतं भीमसेनेन दुःशासनवधे तदा ।नान्यः कर्तास्ति लोके तदृते भीमं महाबलम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: hate duryodhane yuddhe śakreṇevāsure maye ,yatkṛtaṁ bhīmasenena duḥśāsanavadhe tadā ,nānyaḥ kartāsti loke tadṛte bhīmaṁ mahābalam.
english: When Duryodhana is slain in battle, just as the demon Maya was killed by Indra, (it will be like) what Bhimasena accomplished then in the killing of Duḥśāsana. No other doer of such a deed exists in the world except the exceedingly powerful Bhima.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 20. When Duryodhana is slain in battle, just as the demon Maya was killed by Indra, (it will be like) what Bhimasena accomplished then in the killing of Duḥśāsana. No other doer of such a deed exists in the world except the exceedingly powerful Bhima.

sanskrit: जानीतामद्य ज्येष्ठस्य पाण्डवस्य पराक्रमम् ।मद्रराजं हतं श्रुत्वा देवैरपि सुदुःसहम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: jānītāmadya jyeṣṭhasya pāṇḍavasya parākramam ,madrarājaṁ hataṁ śrutvā devairapi suduḥsaham.
english: Today, let them learn of the prowess of the eldest Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), upon hearing that the King of Madras, who was difficult for even the gods to withstand, has been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 21. Today, let them learn of the prowess of the eldest Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), upon hearing that the King of Madras, who was difficult for even the gods to withstand, has been slain.

sanskrit: अद्य ज्ञास्यति संग्रामे माद्रीपुत्रौ महाबलौ ।निहते सौबले शूरे गान्धारेषु च सर्वशः ॥२२॥
transliteration: adya jñāsyati saṁgrāme mādrīputrau mahābalau ,nihate saubale śūre gāndhāreṣu ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Today, in battle, the two mighty sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva) will realize [the truth] once the brave son of Subala (Shakuni) and all the Gandharas are utterly destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 22. Today, in battle, the two mighty sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva) will realize [the truth] once the brave son of Subala (Shakuni) and all the Gandharas are utterly destroyed.

sanskrit: कथं तेषां जयो न स्याद्येषां योद्धा धनंजयः ।सात्यकिर्भीमसेनश्च धृष्टद्युम्नश्च पार्षतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ teṣāṁ jayo na syādyeṣāṁ yoddhā dhanaṁjayaḥ ,sātyakirbhīmasenaśca dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca pārṣataḥ.
english: How can there not be victory for them, whose warriors are Dhananjaya (Arjuna), Satyaki, Bhimasena (Bhima), and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 23. How can there not be victory for them, whose warriors are Dhananjaya (Arjuna), Satyaki, Bhimasena (Bhima), and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata?

sanskrit: द्रौपद्यास्तनयाः पञ्च माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।शिखण्डी च महेष्वासो राजा चैव युधिष्ठिरः ॥२४॥
transliteration: draupadyāstanayāḥ pañca mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,śikhaṇḍī ca maheṣvāso rājā caiva yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Draupadi's five sons, and the two Pandavas who are sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva); also Shikhandi, the great archer; and indeed King Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 24. Draupadi's five sons, and the two Pandavas who are sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva); also Shikhandi, the great archer; and indeed King Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: येषां च जगतां नाथो नाथः कृष्णो जनार्दनः ।कथं तेषां जयो न स्याद्येषां धर्मो व्यपाश्रयः ॥२५॥
transliteration: yeṣāṁ ca jagatāṁ nātho nāthaḥ kṛṣṇo janārdanaḥ ,kathaṁ teṣāṁ jayo na syādyeṣāṁ dharmo vyapāśrayaḥ.
english: And how could there be no victory for them, whose lord and protector of the worlds is Krishna (Janardana), and whose support is natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 25. And how could there be no victory for them, whose lord and protector of the worlds is Krishna (Janardana), and whose support is natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: भीष्मं द्रोणं च कर्णं च मद्रराजानमेव च ।तथान्यान्नृपतीन्वीराञ्शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhīṣmaṁ droṇaṁ ca karṇaṁ ca madrarājānameva ca ,tathānyānnṛpatīnvīrāñśataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Bhishma, Drona, Karna, and also the king of Madra; and similarly, hundreds and thousands of other heroic kings (were there).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 26. Bhishma, Drona, Karna, and also the king of Madra; and similarly, hundreds and thousands of other heroic kings (were there).

sanskrit: कोऽन्यः शक्तो रणे जेतुमृते पार्थं युधिष्ठिरम् ।यस्य नाथो हृषीकेशः सदा धर्मयशोनिधिः ॥२७॥
transliteration: ko'nyaḥ śakto raṇe jetumṛte pārthaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,yasya nātho hṛṣīkeśaḥ sadā dharmayaśonidhiḥ.
english: Who else is capable of conquering in battle, except Partha (Arjuna) and Yudhishthira, whose lord is Hrishikesha (Krishna), who is always a treasure of righteousness (dharma) and fame?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 27. Who else is capable of conquering in battle, except Partha (Arjuna) and Yudhishthira, whose lord is Hrishikesha (Krishna), who is always a treasure of righteousness (dharma) and fame?

sanskrit: इत्येवं वदमानास्ते हर्षेण महता युताः ।प्रभग्नांस्तावकान्राजन्सृञ्जयाः पृष्ठतोऽन्वयुः ॥२८॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ vadamānāste harṣeṇa mahatā yutāḥ ,prabhagnāṁstāvakānrājansṛñjayāḥ pṛṣṭhato'nvayuḥ.
english: O King, thus speaking and filled with great joy, the Sanjayas pursued your shattered troops from behind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 28. O King, thus speaking and filled with great joy, the Sanjayas pursued your shattered troops from behind.

sanskrit: धनंजयो रथानीकमभ्यवर्तत वीर्यवान् ।माद्रीपुत्रौ च शकुनिं सात्यकिश्च महारथः ॥२९॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayo rathānīkamabhyavartata vīryavān ,mādrīputrau ca śakuniṁ sātyakiśca mahārathaḥ.
english: The valorous Dhananjaya (Arjuna) attacked the host of chariots; and the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva attacked) Shakuni; and Satyaki, the great chariot-warrior (also attacked).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 29. The valorous Dhananjaya (Arjuna) attacked the host of chariots; and the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva attacked) Shakuni; and Satyaki, the great chariot-warrior (also attacked).

sanskrit: तान्प्रेक्ष्य द्रवतः सर्वान्भीमसेनभयार्दितान् ।दुर्योधनस्तदा सूतमब्रवीदुत्स्मयन्निव ॥३०॥
transliteration: tānprekṣya dravataḥ sarvānbhīmasenabhayārditān ,duryodhanastadā sūtamabravīdutsmayanniva.
english: Seeing all of them fleeing, terrified by Bhimasena, Duryodhana then spoke to his charioteer, as if with a smile.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 30. Seeing all of them fleeing, terrified by Bhimasena, Duryodhana then spoke to his charioteer, as if with a smile.

sanskrit: न मातिक्रमते पार्थो धनुष्पाणिमवस्थितम् ।जघने सर्वसैन्यानां ममाश्वान्प्रतिपादय ॥३१॥
transliteration: na mātikramate pārtho dhanuṣpāṇimavasthitam ,jaghane sarvasainyānāṁ mamāśvānpratipādaya.
english: Arjuna (Pārtha) cannot bypass me while I stand here, bow in hand. Drive my horses to the rear of all the armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 31. Arjuna (Pārtha) cannot bypass me while I stand here, bow in hand. Drive my horses to the rear of all the armies.

sanskrit: जघने युध्यमानं हि कौन्तेयो मां धनंजयः ।नोत्सहेताभ्यतिक्रान्तुं वेलामिव महोदधिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: jaghane yudhyamānaṁ hi kaunteyo māṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,notsahetābhyatikrāntuṁ velāmiva mahodadhiḥ.
english: Indeed, Arjuna (Kaunteya Dhanañjaya) would not dare to bypass me while I am fighting in the rear, just as a great ocean cannot transgress its boundary (velā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 32. Indeed, Arjuna (Kaunteya Dhanañjaya) would not dare to bypass me while I am fighting in the rear, just as a great ocean cannot transgress its boundary (velā).

sanskrit: पश्य सैन्यं महत्सूत पाण्डवैः समभिद्रुतम् ।सैन्यरेणुं समुद्धूतं पश्यस्वैनं समन्ततः ॥३३॥
transliteration: paśya sainyaṁ mahatsūta pāṇḍavaiḥ samabhidrutam ,sainyareṇuṁ samuddhūtaṁ paśyasvainaṁ samantataḥ.
english: O charioteer, behold the great army being assailed by the Pandavas. Observe this army dust, stirred up everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 33. O charioteer, behold the great army being assailed by the Pandavas. Observe this army dust, stirred up everywhere.

sanskrit: सिंहनादांश्च बहुशः शृणु घोरान्भयानकान् ।तस्माद्याहि शनैः सूत जघनं परिपालय ॥३४॥
transliteration: siṁhanādāṁśca bahuśaḥ śṛṇu ghorānbhayānakān ,tasmādyāhi śanaiḥ sūta jaghanaṁ paripālaya.
english: O charioteer, listen repeatedly to the many terrible and frightening roars, like those of lions! Therefore, proceed slowly and protect the rear of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 34. O charioteer, listen repeatedly to the many terrible and frightening roars, like those of lions! Therefore, proceed slowly and protect the rear of the army.

sanskrit: मयि स्थिते च समरे निरुद्धेषु च पाण्डुषु ।पुनरावर्तते तूर्णं मामकं बलमोजसा ॥३५॥
transliteration: mayi sthite ca samare niruddheṣu ca pāṇḍuṣu ,punarāvartate tūrṇaṁ māmakaṁ balamojasā.
english: As long as I remain in battle and the Pāṇḍavas are held back, my army will swiftly return with full strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 35. As long as I remain in battle and the Pāṇḍavas are held back, my army will swiftly return with full strength.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा तव पुत्रस्य शूराग्र्यसदृशं वचः ।सारथिर्हेमसंछन्नाञ्शनैरश्वानचोदयत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā tava putrasya śūrāgryasadṛśaṁ vacaḥ ,sārathirhemasaṁchannāñśanairaśvānacodayat.
english: Having heard those words of your son, which were befitting the foremost of warriors, the charioteer slowly urged on the horses adorned with gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 36. Having heard those words of your son, which were befitting the foremost of warriors, the charioteer slowly urged on the horses adorned with gold.

sanskrit: गजाश्वरथिभिर्हीनास्त्यक्तात्मानः पदातयः ।एकविंशतिसाहस्राः संयुगायावतस्थिरे ॥३७॥
transliteration: gajāśvarathibhirhīnāstyaktātmānaḥ padātayaḥ ,ekaviṁśatisāhasrāḥ saṁyugāyāvatasthire.
english: Twenty-one thousand foot-soldiers, devoid of elephants, horses, and chariots, and having abandoned all hope for their own lives, stood firm for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 37. Twenty-one thousand foot-soldiers, devoid of elephants, horses, and chariots, and having abandoned all hope for their own lives, stood firm for battle.

sanskrit: नानादेशसमुद्भूता नानारञ्जितवाससः ।अवस्थितास्तदा योधाः प्रार्थयन्तो महद्यशः ॥३८॥
transliteration: nānādeśasamudbhūtā nānārañjitavāsasaḥ ,avasthitāstadā yodhāḥ prārthayanto mahadyaśaḥ.
english: Then, warriors who had come from many different lands and were wearing diverse colored garments stood there, eager to gain great fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 38. Then, warriors who had come from many different lands and were wearing diverse colored garments stood there, eager to gain great fame.

sanskrit: तेषामापततां तत्र संहृष्टानां परस्परम् ।संमर्दः सुमहाञ्जज्ञे घोररूपो भयानकः ॥३९॥
transliteration: teṣāmāpatatāṁ tatra saṁhṛṣṭānāṁ parasparam ,saṁmardaḥ sumahāñjajñe ghorarūpo bhayānakaḥ.
english: As they rushed forth, joyful and clashing against each other, a truly immense, dreadful, and terrifying conflict erupted there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 39. As they rushed forth, joyful and clashing against each other, a truly immense, dreadful, and terrifying conflict erupted there.

sanskrit: भीमसेनं तदा राजन्धृष्टद्युम्नं च पार्षतम् ।बलेन चतुरङ्गेण नानादेश्या न्यवारयन् ॥४०॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaṁ tadā rājandhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pārṣatam ,balena caturaṅgeṇa nānādeśyā nyavārayan.
english: O king, at that moment, soldiers from diverse regions tried to stop Bhīmasena and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, with their four-fold army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 40. O king, at that moment, soldiers from diverse regions tried to stop Bhīmasena and Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the son of Pṛṣata, with their four-fold army.

sanskrit: भीममेवाभ्यवर्तन्त रणेऽन्ये तु पदातयः ।प्रक्ष्वेड्यास्फोट्य संहृष्टा वीरलोकं यियासवः ॥४१॥
transliteration: bhīmamevābhyavartanta raṇe'nye tu padātayaḥ ,prakṣveḍyāsphoṭya saṁhṛṣṭā vīralokaṁ yiyāsavaḥ.
english: But other infantrymen, roaring loudly, slapping their arms in delight, and eager to attain the realm of heroes, indeed focused their attack on Bhīma in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 41. But other infantrymen, roaring loudly, slapping their arms in delight, and eager to attain the realm of heroes, indeed focused their attack on Bhīma in the battle.

sanskrit: आसाद्य भीमसेनं तु संरब्धा युद्धदुर्मदाः ।धार्तराष्ट्रा विनेदुर्हि नान्यां चाकथयन्कथाम् ।परिवार्य रणे भीमं निजघ्नुस्ते समन्ततः ॥४२॥
transliteration: āsādya bhīmasenaṁ tu saṁrabdhā yuddhadurmadāḥ ,dhārtarāṣṭrā vinedurhi nānyāṁ cākathayankathām ,parivārya raṇe bhīmaṁ nijaghnuste samantataḥ.
english: Upon attacking Bhimasena, the furious and battle-intoxicated sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭras) indeed roared, speaking of nothing else. Surrounding Bhima in battle, they struck him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 42. Upon attacking Bhimasena, the furious and battle-intoxicated sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭras) indeed roared, speaking of nothing else. Surrounding Bhima in battle, they struck him from all sides.

sanskrit: स वध्यमानः समरे पदातिगणसंवृतः ।न चचाल रथोपस्थे मैनाक इव पर्वतः ॥४३॥
transliteration: sa vadhyamānaḥ samare padātigaṇasaṁvṛtaḥ ,na cacāla rathopasthe maināka iva parvataḥ.
english: Though being struck in battle and surrounded by hordes of foot-soldiers, he (Bhima) did not stir from his chariot platform (rathopastha), just like Mount Mainaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 43. Though being struck in battle and surrounded by hordes of foot-soldiers, he (Bhima) did not stir from his chariot platform (rathopastha), just like Mount Mainaka.

sanskrit: ते तु क्रुद्धा महाराज पाण्डवस्य महारथम् ।निग्रहीतुं प्रचक्रुर्हि योधांश्चान्यानवारयन् ॥४४॥
transliteration: te tu kruddhā mahārāja pāṇḍavasya mahāratham ,nigrahītuṁ pracakrurhi yodhāṁścānyānavārayan.
english: But those warriors, enraged, O great king, indeed began to seize the Pāṇḍava's (Pāṇḍavasya) great chariot-warrior (Bhima), and they prevented other warriors from interfering.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 44. But those warriors, enraged, O great king, indeed began to seize the Pāṇḍava's (Pāṇḍavasya) great chariot-warrior (Bhima), and they prevented other warriors from interfering.

sanskrit: अक्रुध्यत रणे भीमस्तैस्तदा पर्यवस्थितैः ।सोऽवतीर्य रथात्तूर्णं पदातिः समवस्थितः ॥४५॥
transliteration: akrudhyata raṇe bhīmastaistadā paryavasthitaiḥ ,so'vatīrya rathāttūrṇaṁ padātiḥ samavasthitaḥ.
english: Bhima did not become angry in battle when surrounded by them at that time. He quickly descended from his chariot and stood firm as a foot-soldier.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 45. Bhima did not become angry in battle when surrounded by them at that time. He quickly descended from his chariot and stood firm as a foot-soldier.

sanskrit: जातरूपपरिच्छन्नां प्रगृह्य महतीं गदाम् ।अवधीत्तावकान्योधान्दण्डपाणिरिवान्तकः ॥४६॥
transliteration: jātarūpaparicchannāṁ pragṛhya mahatīṁ gadām ,avadhīttāvakānyodhāndaṇḍapāṇirivāntakaḥ.
english: Grasping his mighty mace, adorned with gold, he struck down your warriors, just like Yama, the wielder of the staff, the very embodiment of destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 46. Grasping his mighty mace, adorned with gold, he struck down your warriors, just like Yama, the wielder of the staff, the very embodiment of destruction.

sanskrit: रथाश्वद्विपहीनांस्तु तान्भीमो गदया बली ।एकविंशतिसाहस्रान्पदातीनवपोथयत् ॥४७॥
transliteration: rathāśvadvipahīnāṁstu tānbhīmo gadayā balī ,ekaviṁśatisāhasrānpadātīnavapothayat.
english: But the mighty Bhīma, with his mace, crushed those twenty-one thousand foot soldiers who were deprived of their chariots, horses, and elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 47. But the mighty Bhīma, with his mace, crushed those twenty-one thousand foot soldiers who were deprived of their chariots, horses, and elephants.

sanskrit: हत्वा तत्पुरुषानीकं भीमः सत्यपराक्रमः ।धृष्टद्युम्नं पुरस्कृत्य नचिरात्प्रत्यदृश्यत ॥४८॥
transliteration: hatvā tatpuruṣānīkaṁ bhīmaḥ satyaparākramaḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ puraskṛtya nacirātpratyadṛśyata.
english: After destroying that army of men, the truly valorous Bhīma, having Dhṛṣṭadyumna at the forefront, soon reappeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 48. After destroying that army of men, the truly valorous Bhīma, having Dhṛṣṭadyumna at the forefront, soon reappeared.

sanskrit: पादाता निहता भूमौ शिश्यिरे रुधिरोक्षिताः ।संभग्ना इव वातेन कर्णिकाराः सुपुष्पिताः ॥४९॥
transliteration: pādātā nihatā bhūmau śiśyire rudhirokṣitāḥ ,saṁbhagnā iva vātena karṇikārāḥ supuṣpitāḥ.
english: The slain foot soldiers, smeared with blood, lay on the ground like fully blossomed karṇikāra trees broken by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 49. The slain foot soldiers, smeared with blood, lay on the ground like fully blossomed karṇikāra trees broken by the wind.

sanskrit: नानापुष्पस्रजोपेता नानाकुण्डलधारिणः ।नानाजात्या हतास्तत्र नानादेशसमागताः ॥५०॥
transliteration: nānāpuṣpasrajopetā nānākuṇḍaladhāriṇaḥ ,nānājātyā hatāstatra nānādeśasamāgatāḥ.
english: There, those who had gathered from various countries, adorned with garlands of various flowers, wearing various earrings, and belonging to diverse races, were killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 50. There, those who had gathered from various countries, adorned with garlands of various flowers, wearing various earrings, and belonging to diverse races, were killed.

sanskrit: पताकाध्वजसंछन्नं पदातीनां महद्बलम् ।निकृत्तं विबभौ तत्र घोररूपं भयानकम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: patākādhvajasaṁchannaṁ padātīnāṁ mahadbalam ,nikṛttaṁ vibabhau tatra ghorarūpaṁ bhayānakam.
english: There, a great army of foot-soldiers, covered with banners and flags, appeared cut down, terrible-looking, and dreadful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 51. There, a great army of foot-soldiers, covered with banners and flags, appeared cut down, terrible-looking, and dreadful.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरपुरोगास्तु सर्वसैन्यमहारथाः ।अभ्यधावन्महात्मानं पुत्रं दुर्योधनं तव ॥५२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirapurogāstu sarvasainyamahārathāḥ ,abhyadhāvanmahātmānaṁ putraṁ duryodhanaṁ tava.
english: And indeed, all the great charioteers of the army, with Yudhishthira at their forefront, rushed towards your great-souled son, Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 52. And indeed, all the great charioteers of the army, with Yudhishthira at their forefront, rushed towards your great-souled son, Duryodhana.

sanskrit: ते सर्वे तावकान्दृष्ट्वा महेष्वासान्पराङ्मुखान् ।नाभ्यवर्तन्त ते पुत्रं वेलेव मकरालयम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: te sarve tāvakāndṛṣṭvā maheṣvāsānparāṅmukhān ,nābhyavartanta te putraṁ veleva makarālayam.
english: All of them, having seen your great archers turning away, did not approach your son, just as a shore does not approach the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 53. All of them, having seen your great archers turning away, did not approach your son, just as a shore does not approach the ocean.

sanskrit: तदद्भुतमपश्याम तव पुत्रस्य पौरुषम् ।यदेकं सहिताः पार्था न शेकुरतिवर्तितुम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: tadadbhutamapaśyāma tava putrasya pauruṣam ,yadekaṁ sahitāḥ pārthā na śekurativartitum.
english: We witnessed that extraordinary valor of your son, a single prowess which even the united Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā) were unable to overcome.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 54. We witnessed that extraordinary valor of your son, a single prowess which even the united Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā) were unable to overcome.

sanskrit: नातिदूरापयातं तु कृतबुद्धिं पलायने ।दुर्योधनः स्वकं सैन्यमब्रवीद्भृशविक्षतम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: nātidūrāpayātaṁ tu kṛtabuddhiṁ palāyane ,duryodhanaḥ svakaṁ sainyamabravīdbhṛśavikṣatam.
english: Duryodhana addressed his own severely wounded army, which, though not fled too far, was resolute in its decision to retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 55. Duryodhana addressed his own severely wounded army, which, though not fled too far, was resolute in its decision to retreat.

sanskrit: न तं देशं प्रपश्यामि पृथिव्यां पर्वतेषु वा ।यत्र यातान्न वो हन्युः पाण्डवाः किं सृतेन वः ॥५६॥
transliteration: na taṁ deśaṁ prapaśyāmi pṛthivyāṁ parvateṣu vā ,yatra yātānna vo hanyuḥ pāṇḍavāḥ kiṁ sṛtena vaḥ.
english: I do not see any place on earth or in the mountains where, even if you were to go, the Pāṇḍavas (sons of Pāṇḍu) would not kill you. So, what is the use of your retreat?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 56. I do not see any place on earth or in the mountains where, even if you were to go, the Pāṇḍavas (sons of Pāṇḍu) would not kill you. So, what is the use of your retreat?

sanskrit: अल्पं च बलमेतेषां कृष्णौ च भृशविक्षतौ ।यदि सर्वेऽत्र तिष्ठामो ध्रुवो नो विजयो भवेत् ॥५७॥
transliteration: alpaṁ ca balameteṣāṁ kṛṣṇau ca bhṛśavikṣatau ,yadi sarve'tra tiṣṭhāmo dhruvo no vijayo bhavet.
english: Their army is small, and both Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna (the two Kṛṣṇas) are severely wounded. If all of us remain here, our victory will surely be achieved.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 57. Their army is small, and both Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna (the two Kṛṣṇas) are severely wounded. If all of us remain here, our victory will surely be achieved.

sanskrit: विप्रयातांस्तु वो भिन्नान्पाण्डवाः कृतकिल्बिषान् ।अनुसृत्य हनिष्यन्ति श्रेयो नः समरे स्थितम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: viprayātāṁstu vo bhinnānpāṇḍavāḥ kṛtakilbiṣān ,anusṛtya haniṣyanti śreyo naḥ samare sthitam.
english: The Pāṇḍavas will pursue and kill you, who are scattered and have committed offenses. Our ultimate good (śreyaḥ) is to stand firm in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 58. The Pāṇḍavas will pursue and kill you, who are scattered and have committed offenses. Our ultimate good (śreyaḥ) is to stand firm in battle.

sanskrit: शृणुध्वं क्षत्रियाः सर्वे यावन्तः स्थ समागताः ।यदा शूरं च भीरुं च मारयत्यन्तकः सदा ।को नु मूढो न युध्येत पुरुषः क्षत्रियब्रुवः ॥५९॥
transliteration: śṛṇudhvaṁ kṣatriyāḥ sarve yāvantaḥ stha samāgatāḥ ,yadā śūraṁ ca bhīruṁ ca mārayatyantakaḥ sadā ,ko nu mūḍho na yudhyeta puruṣaḥ kṣatriyabruvaḥ.
english: O Kṣatriyas, all of you who are assembled, listen! Since Death (antakaḥ) always slays both the brave and the timid, what foolish person (puruṣa), who merely calls himself a Kṣatriya, would not fight?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 59. O Kṣatriyas, all of you who are assembled, listen! Since Death (antakaḥ) always slays both the brave and the timid, what foolish person (puruṣa), who merely calls himself a Kṣatriya, would not fight?

sanskrit: श्रेयो नो भीमसेनस्य क्रुद्धस्य प्रमुखे स्थितम् ।सुखः सांग्रामिको मृत्युः क्षत्रधर्मेण युध्यताम् ।जित्वेह सुखमाप्नोति हतः प्रेत्य महत्फलम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: śreyo no bhīmasenasya kruddhasya pramukhe sthitam ,sukhaḥ sāṁgrāmiko mṛtyuḥ kṣatradharmeṇa yudhyatām ,jitveha sukhamāpnoti hataḥ pretya mahatphalam.
english: Our ultimate good (śreyaḥ) lies in the presence of the enraged Bhīmasena. A warrior's death (sāṃgrāmikaḥ mṛtyuḥ) is pleasant for those who fight according to the natural law (dharma) of a Kṣatriya. Having conquered, one attains happiness here in this world; if slain, one obtains a great reward after death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 60. Our ultimate good (śreyaḥ) lies in the presence of the enraged Bhīmasena. A warrior's death (sāṃgrāmikaḥ mṛtyuḥ) is pleasant for those who fight according to the natural law (dharma) of a Kṣatriya. Having conquered, one attains happiness here in this world; if slain, one obtains a great reward after death.

sanskrit: न युद्धधर्माच्छ्रेयान्वै पन्थाः स्वर्गस्य कौरवाः ।अचिरेण जिताँल्लोकान्हतो युद्धे समश्नुते ॥६१॥
transliteration: na yuddhadharmācchreyānvai panthāḥ svargasya kauravāḥ ,acireṇa jitāँllokānhato yuddhe samaśnute.
english: O Kauravas! Indeed, there is no path to heaven (svarga) superior to the natural law (dharma) of war. One who is slain in battle swiftly attains the worlds (lokas) that are conquered (through such action).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 61. O Kauravas! Indeed, there is no path to heaven (svarga) superior to the natural law (dharma) of war. One who is slain in battle swiftly attains the worlds (lokas) that are conquered (through such action).

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु वचनं तस्य पूजयित्वा च पार्थिवाः ।पुनरेवान्ववर्तन्त पाण्डवानाततायिनः ॥६२॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu vacanaṁ tasya pūjayitvā ca pārthivāḥ ,punarevānvavartanta pāṇḍavānātatāyinaḥ.
english: Having heard his words and honored him, the kings, acting as aggressors, again turned their attention towards the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 62. Having heard his words and honored him, the kings, acting as aggressors, again turned their attention towards the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तानापतत एवाशु व्यूढानीकाः प्रहारिणः ।प्रत्युद्ययुस्तदा पार्था जयगृध्राः प्रहारिणः ॥६३॥
transliteration: tānāpatata evāśu vyūḍhānīkāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,pratyudyayustadā pārthā jayagṛdhrāḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: Then, the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas)  -  who had their battalions arrayed, were eager for victory, and were powerful assailants  -  quickly counter-attacked those very attackers who were advancing upon them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 63. Then, the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas)  -  who had their battalions arrayed, were eager for victory, and were powerful assailants  -  quickly counter-attacked those very attackers who were advancing upon them.

sanskrit: धनंजयो रथेनाजावभ्यवर्तत वीर्यवान् ।विश्रुतं त्रिषु लोकेषु गाण्डीवं विक्षिपन्धनुः ॥६४॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayo rathenājāvabhyavartata vīryavān ,viśrutaṁ triṣu lokeṣu gāṇḍīvaṁ vikṣipandhanuḥ.
english: The valiant Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) advanced into battle in his chariot, brandishing his bow, the Gāṇḍīva, which was renowned throughout the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 64. The valiant Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) advanced into battle in his chariot, brandishing his bow, the Gāṇḍīva, which was renowned throughout the three worlds.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रौ च शकुनिं सात्यकिश्च महाबलः ।जवेनाभ्यपतन्हृष्टा यतो वै तावकं बलम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: mādrīputrau ca śakuniṁ sātyakiśca mahābalaḥ ,javenābhyapatanhṛṣṭā yato vai tāvakaṁ balam.
english: And the two sons of Mādrī and the mighty Sātyaki, delighted, swiftly attacked Śakuni, rushing to where your army (bala) was located.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 18 Verse 65. And the two sons of Mādrī and the mighty Sātyaki, delighted, swiftly attacked Śakuni, rushing to where your army (bala) was located.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।संनिवृत्ते बलौघे तु शाल्वो म्लेच्छगणाधिपः ।अभ्यवर्तत संक्रुद्धः पाण्डूनां सुमहद्बलम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,saṁnivṛtte balaughe tu śālvo mlecchagaṇādhipaḥ ,abhyavartata saṁkruddhaḥ pāṇḍūnāṁ sumahadbalam.
english: Sañjaya said: But when the massive army had retreated, Śālva, the enraged chief of the barbarian hordes, attacked the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: But when the massive army had retreated, Śālva, the enraged chief of the barbarian hordes, attacked the vast army of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: आस्थाय सुमहानागं प्रभिन्नं पर्वतोपमम् ।दृप्तमैरावतप्रख्यममित्रगणमर्दनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: āsthāya sumahānāgaṁ prabhinnaṁ parvatopamam ,dṛptamairāvataprakhyamamitragaṇamardanam.
english: He did so having mounted a very mighty elephant, which was in rut, resembling a mountain, like the proud Airāvata, and capable of crushing enemy hordes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 2. He did so having mounted a very mighty elephant, which was in rut, resembling a mountain, like the proud Airāvata, and capable of crushing enemy hordes.

sanskrit: योऽसौ महाभद्रकुलप्रसूतः सुपूजितो धार्तराष्ट्रेण नित्यम् ।सुकल्पितः शास्त्रविनिश्चयज्ञैः सदोपवाह्यः समरेषु राजन् ॥३॥
transliteration: yo'sau mahābhadrakulaprasūtaḥ; supūjito dhārtarāṣṭreṇa nityam ,sukalpitaḥ śāstraviniścayajñaiḥ; sadopavāhyaḥ samareṣu rājan.
english: O King, this elephant, born of the noble Bhadra lineage, always highly esteemed by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, and expertly trained by those skilled in the principles of military science, was always fit to be ridden in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 3. O King, this elephant, born of the noble Bhadra lineage, always highly esteemed by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, and expertly trained by those skilled in the principles of military science, was always fit to be ridden in battles.

sanskrit: तमास्थितो राजवरो बभूव यथोदयस्थः सविता क्षपान्ते ।स तेन नागप्रवरेण राजन्नभ्युद्ययौ पाण्डुसुतान्समन्तात् ।शितैः पृषत्कैर्विददार चापि महेन्द्रवज्रप्रतिमैः सुघोरैः ॥४॥
transliteration: tamāsthito rājavaro babhūva; yathodayasthaḥ savitā kṣapānte ,sa tena nāgapravareṇa rāja;nnabhyudyayau pāṇḍusutānsamantāt ,śitaiḥ pṛṣatkairvidadāra cāpi; mahendravajrapratimaiḥ sughoraiḥ.
english: O King, having mounted that elephant, the excellent king (Śālva) appeared like the rising sun at dawn. With that foremost of elephants, he advanced against the Pāṇḍavas from all sides. And he tore them apart with sharp, very terrible arrows that resembled the thunderbolt of the great Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 4. O King, having mounted that elephant, the excellent king (Śālva) appeared like the rising sun at dawn. With that foremost of elephants, he advanced against the Pāṇḍavas from all sides. And he tore them apart with sharp, very terrible arrows that resembled the thunderbolt of the great Indra.

sanskrit: ततः शरान्वै सृजतो महारणे योधांश्च राजन्नयतो यमाय ।नास्यान्तरं ददृशुः स्वे परे वा यथा पुरा वज्रधरस्य दैत्याः ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarānvai sṛjato mahāraṇe; yodhāṁśca rājannayato yamāya ,nāsyāntaraṁ dadṛśuḥ sve pare vā; yathā purā vajradharasya daityāḥ.
english: O King, as that warrior discharged arrows in the great battle and sent warriors to the realm of Yama, neither his own men nor his enemies could find a weak spot in him. This was just like how the Daityas of old could not find a weak spot in Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 5. O King, as that warrior discharged arrows in the great battle and sent warriors to the realm of Yama, neither his own men nor his enemies could find a weak spot in him. This was just like how the Daityas of old could not find a weak spot in Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.

sanskrit: ते पाण्डवाः सोमकाः सृञ्जयाश्च तमेव नागं ददृशुः समन्तात् ।सहस्रशो वै विचरन्तमेकं यथा महेन्द्रस्य गजं समीपे ॥६॥
transliteration: te pāṇḍavāḥ somakāḥ sṛñjayāśca; tameva nāgaṁ dadṛśuḥ samantāt ,sahasraśo vai vicarantamekaṁ; yathā mahendrasya gajaṁ samīpe.
english: Those Pandavas, Somakas, and Srinjayas saw that very elephant from all sides, appearing as if it were a thousand elephants moving about. It was like seeing Indra's (Mahendra's) elephant close by.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 6. Those Pandavas, Somakas, and Srinjayas saw that very elephant from all sides, appearing as if it were a thousand elephants moving about. It was like seeing Indra's (Mahendra's) elephant close by.

sanskrit: संद्राव्यमाणं तु बलं परेषां परीतकल्पं विबभौ समन्तात् ।नैवावतस्थे समरे भृशं भयाद्विमर्दमानं तु परस्परं तदा ॥७॥
transliteration: saṁdrāvyamāṇaṁ tu balaṁ pareṣāṁ; parītakalpaṁ vibabhau samantāt ,naivāvatasthe samare bhṛśaṁ bhayā;dvimardamānaṁ tu parasparaṁ tadā.
english: But the enemy's force, being routed, appeared as though completely surrounded on all sides. Then, out of intense fear, trampling each other, it could not stand firm in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 7. But the enemy's force, being routed, appeared as though completely surrounded on all sides. Then, out of intense fear, trampling each other, it could not stand firm in battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रभग्ना सहसा महाचमूः सा पाण्डवी तेन नराधिपेन ।दिशश्चतस्रः सहसा प्रधाविता गजेन्द्रवेगं तमपारयन्ती ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ prabhagnā sahasā mahācamūḥ; sā pāṇḍavī tena narādhipena ,diśaścatasraḥ sahasā pradhāvitā; gajendravegaṁ tamapārayantī.
english: Then, that great Pandava army was suddenly routed by that king. Unable to resist the charge of that lordly elephant, it suddenly fled in all four directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 8. Then, that great Pandava army was suddenly routed by that king. Unable to resist the charge of that lordly elephant, it suddenly fled in all four directions.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा च तां वेगवता प्रभग्नां सर्वे त्वदीया युधि योधमुख्याः ।अपूजयंस्तत्र नराधिपं तं दध्मुश्च शङ्खाञ्शशिसंनिकाशान् ॥९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā ca tāṁ vegavatā prabhagnāṁ; sarve tvadīyā yudhi yodhamukhyāḥ ,apūjayaṁstatra narādhipaṁ taṁ; dadhmuśca śaṅkhāñśaśisaṁnikāśān.
english: And having seen that [enemy] formation swiftly broken, all your chief warriors in battle then honored that king and blew conches as brilliant as the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 9. And having seen that [enemy] formation swiftly broken, all your chief warriors in battle then honored that king and blew conches as brilliant as the moon.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा निनादं त्वथ कौरवाणां हर्षाद्विमुक्तं सह शङ्खशब्दैः ।सेनापतिः पाण्डवसृञ्जयानां पाञ्चालपुत्रो न ममर्ष रोषात् ॥१०॥
transliteration: śrutvā ninādaṁ tvatha kauravāṇāṁ; harṣādvimuktaṁ saha śaṅkhaśabdaiḥ ,senāpatiḥ pāṇḍavasṛñjayānāṁ; pāñcālaputro na mamarṣa roṣāt.
english: But then, hearing the joyous sound of the Kauravas, released along with the sounds of their conches, the commander of the Pāṇḍavas and Sṛñjayas, the son of Pāñcāla (Pāñcālaputra), could not endure it due to rage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 10. But then, hearing the joyous sound of the Kauravas, released along with the sounds of their conches, the commander of the Pāṇḍavas and Sṛñjayas, the son of Pāñcāla (Pāñcālaputra), could not endure it due to rage.

sanskrit: ततस्तु तं वै द्विरदं महात्मा प्रत्युद्ययौ त्वरमाणो जयाय ।जम्भो यथा शक्रसमागमे वै नागेन्द्रमैरावणमिन्द्रवाह्यम् ॥११॥
transliteration: tatastu taṁ vai dviradaṁ mahātmā; pratyudyayau tvaramāṇo jayāya ,jambho yathā śakrasamāgame vai; nāgendramairāvaṇamindravāhyam.
english: Thereupon, the great-souled one (mahātmā), hurrying for victory, indeed advanced against that elephant, just as Jambha did against Airāvaṇa, the king of elephants and Indra's mount, in an encounter with Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 11. Thereupon, the great-souled one (mahātmā), hurrying for victory, indeed advanced against that elephant, just as Jambha did against Airāvaṇa, the king of elephants and Indra's mount, in an encounter with Indra.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं सहसा तु दृष्ट्वा पाञ्चालराजं युधि राजसिंहः ।तं वै द्विपं प्रेषयामास तूर्णं वधाय राजन्द्रुपदात्मजस्य ॥१२॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ sahasā tu dṛṣṭvā; pāñcālarājaṁ yudhi rājasiṁhaḥ ,taṁ vai dvipaṁ preṣayāmāsa tūrṇaṁ; vadhāya rājandrupadātmajasya.
english: But, O king (rājan), seeing that Pāñcāla king (Pāñcālarāja) swiftly approaching in battle, the lion among kings (rājasiṃhaḥ) immediately dispatched that elephant for the slaying of Drupada's son (Drupādātmaja).
