search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 12. But, O king (rājan), seeing that Pāñcāla king (Pāñcālarāja) swiftly approaching in battle, the lion among kings (rājasiṃhaḥ) immediately dispatched that elephant for the slaying of Drupada's son (Drupādātmaja).

sanskrit: स तं द्विपं सहसाभ्यापतन्तमविध्यदर्कप्रतिमैः पृषत्कैः ।कर्मारधौतैर्निशितैर्ज्वलद्भिर्नाराचमुख्यैस्त्रिभिरुग्रवेगैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa taṁ dvipaṁ sahasābhyāpatanta;mavidhyadarkapratimaiḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ ,karmāradhautairniśitairjvaladbhi;rnārācamukhyaistribhirugravegaiḥ.
english: He pierced that elephant, which was suddenly charging towards him, with three intensely swift, chief iron arrows (nārāca), which were blazing, sharpened by blacksmiths, and shone like the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 13. He pierced that elephant, which was suddenly charging towards him, with three intensely swift, chief iron arrows (nārāca), which were blazing, sharpened by blacksmiths, and shone like the sun.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरान्पञ्च शितान्महात्मा नाराचमुख्यान्विससर्ज कुम्भे ।स तैस्तु विद्धः परमद्विपो रणे तदा परावृत्य भृशं प्रदुद्रुवे ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato'parānpañca śitānmahātmā; nārācamukhyānvisasarja kumbhe ,sa taistu viddhaḥ paramadvipo raṇe; tadā parāvṛtya bhṛśaṁ pradudruve.
english: Then, the great-souled (mahātman) one discharged five other keen, principal iron arrows (nārāca) at the elephant's temple. That supreme elephant (paramadvipa), wounded by them in battle, then turned back and fled swiftly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 14. Then, the great-souled (mahātman) one discharged five other keen, principal iron arrows (nārāca) at the elephant's temple. That supreme elephant (paramadvipa), wounded by them in battle, then turned back and fled swiftly.

sanskrit: तं नागराजं सहसा प्रणुन्नं विद्राव्यमाणं च निगृह्य शाल्वः ।तोत्त्राङ्कुशैः प्रेषयामास तूर्णं पाञ्चालराजस्य रथं प्रदिश्य ॥१५॥
transliteration: taṁ nāgarājaṁ sahasā praṇunnaṁ; vidrāvyamāṇaṁ ca nigṛhya śālvaḥ ,tottrāṅkuśaiḥ preṣayāmāsa tūrṇaṁ; pāñcālarājasya rathaṁ pradiśya.
english: Salva, suddenly restraining that king of elephants (nāgarāja) which was being driven away and put to flight, quickly dispatched it with goads and hooks, directing it towards the chariot of the Pāñcāla king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 15. Salva, suddenly restraining that king of elephants (nāgarāja) which was being driven away and put to flight, quickly dispatched it with goads and hooks, directing it towards the chariot of the Pāñcāla king.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वापतन्तं सहसा तु नागं धृष्टद्युम्नः स्वरथाच्छीघ्रमेव ।गदां प्रगृह्याशु जवेन वीरो भूमिं प्रपन्नो भयविह्वलाङ्गः ॥१६॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvāpatantaṁ sahasā tu nāgaṁ; dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ svarathācchīghrameva ,gadāṁ pragṛhyāśu javena vīro; bhūmiṁ prapanno bhayavihvalāṅgaḥ.
english: But the hero Dhṛṣṭadyumna, seeing the elephant (nāga) suddenly charging, immediately (with his limbs bewildered by fear) quickly seized his mace and descended from his own chariot to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 16. But the hero Dhṛṣṭadyumna, seeing the elephant (nāga) suddenly charging, immediately (with his limbs bewildered by fear) quickly seized his mace and descended from his own chariot to the ground.

sanskrit: स तं रथं हेमविभूषिताङ्गं साश्वं ससूतं सहसा विमृद्य ।उत्क्षिप्य हस्तेन तदा महाद्विपो विपोथयामास वसुंधरातले ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa taṁ rathaṁ hemavibhūṣitāṅgaṁ; sāśvaṁ sasūtaṁ sahasā vimṛdya ,utkṣipya hastena tadā mahādvipo; vipothayāmāsa vasuṁdharātale.
english: Then, the great elephant suddenly crushed that chariot, whose parts were adorned with gold, along with its horses and charioteer. Lifting it up with its trunk, it smashed it onto the surface of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 17. Then, the great elephant suddenly crushed that chariot, whose parts were adorned with gold, along with its horses and charioteer. Lifting it up with its trunk, it smashed it onto the surface of the earth.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालराजस्य सुतं स दृष्ट्वा तदार्दितं नागवरेण तेन ।तमभ्यधावत्सहसा जवेन भीमः शिखण्डी च शिनेश्च नप्ता ॥१८॥
transliteration: pāñcālarājasya sutaṁ sa dṛṣṭvā; tadārditaṁ nāgavareṇa tena ,tamabhyadhāvatsahasā javena; bhīmaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca śineśca naptā.
english: Having seen the son of the Pāñcāla king (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) distressed by that excellent elephant, Bhīma, Śikhaṇḍī, and the grandson of Śini (Sātyaki) rushed towards him with great speed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 18. Having seen the son of the Pāñcāla king (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) distressed by that excellent elephant, Bhīma, Śikhaṇḍī, and the grandson of Śini (Sātyaki) rushed towards him with great speed.

sanskrit: शरैश्च वेगं सहसा निगृह्य तस्याभितोऽभ्यापततो गजस्य ।स संगृहीतो रथिभिर्गजो वै चचाल तैर्वार्यमाणश्च संख्ये ॥१९॥
transliteration: śaraiśca vegaṁ sahasā nigṛhya; tasyābhito'bhyāpatato gajasya ,sa saṁgṛhīto rathibhirgajo vai; cacāla tairvāryamāṇaśca saṁkhye.
english: With arrows, they suddenly checked the speed of that elephant, which was rushing from all sides. That elephant, indeed, was held by the charioteers and, being restrained by them in battle, it wavered.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 19. With arrows, they suddenly checked the speed of that elephant, which was rushing from all sides. That elephant, indeed, was held by the charioteers and, being restrained by them in battle, it wavered.

sanskrit: ततः पृषत्कान्प्रववर्ष राजा सूर्यो यथा रश्मिजालं समन्तात् ।तेनाशुगैर्वध्यमाना रथौघाः प्रदुद्रुवुस्तत्र ततस्तु सर्वे ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ pṛṣatkānpravavarṣa rājā; sūryo yathā raśmijālaṁ samantāt ,tenāśugairvadhyamānā rathaughāḥ; pradudruvustatra tatastu sarve.
english: Then, the king rained down arrows just as the sun spreads its network of rays everywhere. All the throngs of chariots, being struck by those swift arrows, then fled from that spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 20. Then, the king rained down arrows just as the sun spreads its network of rays everywhere. All the throngs of chariots, being struck by those swift arrows, then fled from that spot.

sanskrit: तत्कर्म शाल्वस्य समीक्ष्य सर्वे पाञ्चालमत्स्या नृप सृञ्जयाश्च ।हाहाकारैर्नादयन्तः स्म युद्धे द्विपं समन्ताद्रुरुधुर्नराग्र्याः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tatkarma śālvasya samīkṣya sarve; pāñcālamatsyā nṛpa sṛñjayāśca ,hāhākārairnādayantaḥ sma yuddhe; dvipaṁ samantādrurudhurnarāgryāḥ.
english: O King, observing that deed of Salva, all the Pañcālas, Matsyas, and Sañjayas - those foremost men - roared "Hāhā!" throughout the battle and surrounded the elephant from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 21. O King, observing that deed of Salva, all the Pañcālas, Matsyas, and Sañjayas - those foremost men - roared "Hāhā!" throughout the battle and surrounded the elephant from all sides.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालराजस्त्वरितस्तु शूरो गदां प्रगृह्याचलशृङ्गकल्पाम् ।असंभ्रमं भारत शत्रुघाती जवेन वीरोऽनुससार नागम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: pāñcālarājastvaritastu śūro; gadāṁ pragṛhyācalaśṛṅgakalpām ,asaṁbhramaṁ bhārata śatrughātī; javena vīro'nusasāra nāgam.
english: O Bhārata, the valiant king of Pañcāla, swift and a foe-slayer, grasping a mace resembling a mountain peak, unflustered, swiftly followed the elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 22. O Bhārata, the valiant king of Pañcāla, swift and a foe-slayer, grasping a mace resembling a mountain peak, unflustered, swiftly followed the elephant.

sanskrit: ततोऽथ नागं धरणीधराभं मदं स्रवन्तं जलदप्रकाशम् ।गदां समाविध्य भृशं जघान पाञ्चालराजस्य सुतस्तरस्वी ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato'tha nāgaṁ dharaṇīdharābhaṁ; madaṁ sravantaṁ jaladaprakāśam ,gadāṁ samāvidhya bhṛśaṁ jaghāna; pāñcālarājasya sutastarasvī.
english: Then, the mighty son of the Pañcāla king, having whirled his mace, struck hard that elephant, which resembled a mountain, exuding ichor, and shining like a rain cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 23. Then, the mighty son of the Pañcāla king, having whirled his mace, struck hard that elephant, which resembled a mountain, exuding ichor, and shining like a rain cloud.

sanskrit: स भिन्नकुम्भः सहसा विनद्य मुखात्प्रभूतं क्षतजं विमुञ्चन् ।पपात नागो धरणीधराभः क्षितिप्रकम्पाच्चलितो यथाद्रिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa bhinnakumbhaḥ sahasā vinadya; mukhātprabhūtaṁ kṣatajaṁ vimuñcan ,papāta nāgo dharaṇīdharābhaḥ; kṣitiprakampāccalito yathādriḥ.
english: With its temples shattered, that elephant, which resembled a mountain, suddenly roared and, gushing forth copious blood from its mouth, fell to the ground, just like a mountain dislodged by an earthquake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 24. With its temples shattered, that elephant, which resembled a mountain, suddenly roared and, gushing forth copious blood from its mouth, fell to the ground, just like a mountain dislodged by an earthquake.

sanskrit: निपात्यमाने तु तदा गजेन्द्रे हाहाकृते तव पुत्रस्य सैन्ये ।स शाल्वराजस्य शिनिप्रवीरो जहार भल्लेन शिरः शितेन ॥२५॥
transliteration: nipātyamāne tu tadā gajendre; hāhākṛte tava putrasya sainye ,sa śālvarājasya śinipravīro; jahāra bhallena śiraḥ śitena.
english: When that great elephant (gajendra) was being felled, and a cry of distress arose in your son's army, that foremost warrior of the Śinis (Sātyaki) severed the head of King Śālva with a sharp spear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 25. When that great elephant (gajendra) was being felled, and a cry of distress arose in your son's army, that foremost warrior of the Śinis (Sātyaki) severed the head of King Śālva with a sharp spear.

sanskrit: हृतोत्तमाङ्गो युधि सात्वतेन पपात भूमौ सह नागराज्ञा ।यथाद्रिशृङ्गं सुमहत्प्रणुन्नं वज्रेण देवाधिपचोदितेन ॥२६॥
transliteration: hṛtottamāṅgo yudhi sātvatena; papāta bhūmau saha nāgarājñā ,yathādriśṛṅgaṁ sumahatpraṇunnaṁ; vajreṇa devādhipacoditena.
english: With his head severed by Sātyaki in battle, he fell to the ground along with the king of elephants, just as a huge mountain peak, struck down by a thunderbolt (vajra) hurled by the chief of the gods (Indra), would fall.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 19 Verse 26. With his head severed by Sātyaki in battle, he fell to the ground along with the king of elephants, just as a huge mountain peak, struck down by a thunderbolt (vajra) hurled by the chief of the gods (Indra), would fall.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तस्मिंस्तु निहते शूरे शाल्वे समितिशोभने ।तवाभज्यद्बलं वेगाद्वातेनेव महाद्रुमः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tasmiṁstu nihate śūre śālve samitiśobhane ,tavābhajyadbalaṁ vegādvāteneva mahādrumaḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: When that heroic Śālva, who was splendid in assembly and battle, was struck down, your army swiftly broke apart, just like a great tree is shattered by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: When that heroic Śālva, who was splendid in assembly and battle, was struck down, your army swiftly broke apart, just like a great tree is shattered by the wind.

sanskrit: तत्प्रभग्नं बलं दृष्ट्वा कृतवर्मा महारथः ।दधार समरे शूरः शत्रुसैन्यं महाबलः ॥२॥
transliteration: tatprabhagnaṁ balaṁ dṛṣṭvā kṛtavarmā mahārathaḥ ,dadhāra samare śūraḥ śatrusainyaṁ mahābalaḥ.
english: Seeing that shattered army, Kṛtavarmā, the great charioteer, who was heroic and immensely powerful, held back the enemy forces in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 2. Seeing that shattered army, Kṛtavarmā, the great charioteer, who was heroic and immensely powerful, held back the enemy forces in battle.

sanskrit: संनिवृत्तास्तु ते शूरा दृष्ट्वा सात्वतमाहवे ।शैलोपमं स्थितं राजन्कीर्यमाणं शरैर्युधि ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁnivṛttāstu te śūrā dṛṣṭvā sātvatamāhave ,śailopamaṁ sthitaṁ rājankīryamāṇaṁ śarairyudhi.
english: O King, those brave warriors turned back upon seeing Sātvata, who stood firm like a mountain in the fight, being showered by arrows in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 3. O King, those brave warriors turned back upon seeing Sātvata, who stood firm like a mountain in the fight, being showered by arrows in battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं कुरूणां पाण्डवैः सह ।निवृत्तानां महाराज मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ kurūṇāṁ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha ,nivṛttānāṁ mahārāja mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: Then, O great king, the battle between the Kurus and the Pāṇḍavas resumed; for those who had previously turned back, retreat now meant facing death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 4. Then, O great king, the battle between the Kurus and the Pāṇḍavas resumed; for those who had previously turned back, retreat now meant facing death.

sanskrit: तत्राश्चर्यमभूद्युद्धं सात्वतस्य परैः सह ।यदेको वारयामास पाण्डुसेनां दुरासदाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tatrāścaryamabhūdyuddhaṁ sātvatasya paraiḥ saha ,yadeko vārayāmāsa pāṇḍusenāṁ durāsadām.
english: There, an astonishing battle occurred involving Sātvata against his enemies, as he alone held back the formidable Pāṇḍava army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 5. There, an astonishing battle occurred involving Sātvata against his enemies, as he alone held back the formidable Pāṇḍava army.

sanskrit: तेषामन्योन्यसुहृदां कृते कर्मणि दुष्करे ।सिंहनादः प्रहृष्टानां दिवःस्पृक्सुमहानभूत् ॥६॥
transliteration: teṣāmanyonyasuhṛdāṁ kṛte karmaṇi duṣkare ,siṁhanādaḥ prahṛṣṭānāṁ divaḥspṛksumahānabhūt.
english: When that difficult deed (karma) was accomplished by those mutually friendly warriors, a tremendously loud (sumahān), sky-piercing lion's roar (siṃhanādaḥ) arose from those who were exultant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 6. When that difficult deed (karma) was accomplished by those mutually friendly warriors, a tremendously loud (sumahān), sky-piercing lion's roar (siṃhanādaḥ) arose from those who were exultant.

sanskrit: तेन शब्देन वित्रस्तान्पाञ्चालान्भरतर्षभ ।शिनेर्नप्ता महाबाहुरन्वपद्यत सात्यकिः ॥७॥
transliteration: tena śabdena vitrastānpāñcālānbharatarṣabha ,śinernaptā mahābāhuranvapadyata sātyakiḥ.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, the mighty-armed Sātyaki, grandson of Śini, pursued the Pañcālas who were terrified by that sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 7. O best among the Bhāratas, the mighty-armed Sātyaki, grandson of Śini, pursued the Pañcālas who were terrified by that sound.

sanskrit: स समासाद्य राजानं क्षेमधूर्तिं महाबलम् ।सप्तभिर्निशितैर्बाणैरनयद्यमसादनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sa samāsādya rājānaṁ kṣemadhūrtiṁ mahābalam ,saptabhirniśitairbāṇairanayadyamasādanam.
english: Having approached King Kṣemadhūrti, who was very powerful, he sent him to the abode of Yama with seven sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 8. Having approached King Kṣemadhūrti, who was very powerful, he sent him to the abode of Yama with seven sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तमायान्तं महाबाहुं प्रवपन्तं शिताञ्शरान् ।जवेनाभ्यपतद्धीमान्हार्दिक्यः शिनिपुंगवम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tamāyāntaṁ mahābāhuṁ pravapantaṁ śitāñśarān ,javenābhyapataddhīmānhārdikyaḥ śinipuṁgavam.
english: The wise Hārdikya, with great speed, rushed towards the mighty-armed chief of the Śinis, who was showering sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 9. The wise Hārdikya, with great speed, rushed towards the mighty-armed chief of the Śinis, who was showering sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तौ सिंहाविव नर्दन्तौ धन्विनौ रथिनां वरौ ।अन्योन्यमभ्यधावेतां शस्त्रप्रवरधारिणौ ॥१०॥
transliteration: tau siṁhāviva nardantau dhanvinau rathināṁ varau ,anyonyamabhyadhāvetāṁ śastrapravaradhāriṇau.
english: Roaring like lions, those two archers, who were the best among charioteers and wielded excellent weapons, rushed towards each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 10. Roaring like lions, those two archers, who were the best among charioteers and wielded excellent weapons, rushed towards each other.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाः सह पाञ्चालैर्योधाश्चान्ये नृपोत्तमाः ।प्रेक्षकाः समपद्यन्त तयोः पुरुषसिंहयोः ॥११॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāḥ saha pāñcālairyodhāścānye nṛpottamāḥ ,prekṣakāḥ samapadyanta tayoḥ puruṣasiṁhayoḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas and other excellent kings and warriors, became spectators of those two lion-like heroes (puruṣasiṃhayoḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 11. The Pāṇḍavas, along with the Pāñcālas and other excellent kings and warriors, became spectators of those two lion-like heroes (puruṣasiṃhayoḥ).

sanskrit: नाराचैर्वत्सदन्तैश्च वृष्ण्यन्धकमहारथौ ।अभिजघ्नतुरन्योन्यं प्रहृष्टाविव कुञ्जरौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: nārācairvatsadantaiśca vṛṣṇyandhakamahārathau ,abhijaghnaturanyonyaṁ prahṛṣṭāviva kuñjarau.
english: The two great charioteers of the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans struck each other with iron arrows (nārācaiḥ) and calf-tooth arrows, just like two delighted elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 12. The two great charioteers of the Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans struck each other with iron arrows (nārācaiḥ) and calf-tooth arrows, just like two delighted elephants.

sanskrit: चरन्तौ विविधान्मार्गान्हार्दिक्यशिनिपुंगवौ ।मुहुरन्तर्दधाते तौ बाणवृष्ट्या परस्परम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: carantau vividhānmārgānhārdikyaśinipuṁgavau ,muhurantardadhāte tau bāṇavṛṣṭyā parasparam.
english: The two bulls (puṅgavau) of the Hārdikya and Śini clans, moving through various paths, repeatedly obscured each other with a shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 13. The two bulls (puṅgavau) of the Hārdikya and Śini clans, moving through various paths, repeatedly obscured each other with a shower of arrows.

sanskrit: चापवेगबलोद्धूतान्मार्गणान्वृष्णिसिंहयोः ।आकाशे समपश्याम पतंगानिव शीघ्रगान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: cāpavegabaloddhūtānmārgaṇānvṛṣṇisiṁhayoḥ ,ākāśe samapaśyāma pataṁgāniva śīghragān.
english: We saw in the sky the arrows, impelled by the swift force of the bow, from the two lion-like Vṛṣṇis, resembling fast-moving birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 14. We saw in the sky the arrows, impelled by the swift force of the bow, from the two lion-like Vṛṣṇis, resembling fast-moving birds.

sanskrit: तमेकं सत्यकर्माणमासाद्य हृदिकात्मजः ।अविध्यन्निशितैर्बाणैश्चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tamekaṁ satyakarmāṇamāsādya hṛdikātmajaḥ ,avidhyanniśitairbāṇaiścaturbhiścaturo hayān.
english: Approaching that one whose deeds (karma) were truthful, Hṛdika's son pierced his four horses with four sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 15. Approaching that one whose deeds (karma) were truthful, Hṛdika's son pierced his four horses with four sharp arrows.

sanskrit: स दीर्घबाहुः संक्रुद्धस्तोत्त्रार्दित इव द्विपः ।अष्टाभिः कृतवर्माणमविध्यत्परमेषुभिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa dīrghabāhuḥ saṁkruddhastottrārdita iva dvipaḥ ,aṣṭābhiḥ kṛtavarmāṇamavidhyatparameṣubhiḥ.
english: Greatly enraged, that long-armed warrior, like an elephant tormented by a goad, pierced Kṛtavarman with eight excellent arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 16. Greatly enraged, that long-armed warrior, like an elephant tormented by a goad, pierced Kṛtavarman with eight excellent arrows.

sanskrit: ततः पूर्णायतोत्सृष्टैः कृतवर्मा शिलाशितैः ।सात्यकिं त्रिभिराहत्य धनुरेकेन चिच्छिदे ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ pūrṇāyatotsṛṣṭaiḥ kṛtavarmā śilāśitaiḥ ,sātyakiṁ tribhirāhatya dhanurekena cicchide.
english: Then Kṛtavarman, having struck Satyaki with three arrows that were sharpened on stone and shot with a full draw, cut his bow with one (arrow).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 17. Then Kṛtavarman, having struck Satyaki with three arrows that were sharpened on stone and shot with a full draw, cut his bow with one (arrow).

sanskrit: निकृत्तं तद्धनुःश्रेष्ठमपास्य शिनिपुंगवः ।अन्यदादत्त वेगेन शैनेयः सशरं धनुः ॥१८॥
transliteration: nikṛttaṁ taddhanuḥśreṣṭhamapāsya śinipuṁgavaḥ ,anyadādatta vegena śaineyaḥ saśaraṁ dhanuḥ.
english: Having cast aside that excellent broken bow, the foremost of the Shinis (Satyaki) quickly took up another bow, one equipped with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 18. Having cast aside that excellent broken bow, the foremost of the Shinis (Satyaki) quickly took up another bow, one equipped with arrows.

sanskrit: तदादाय धनुःश्रेष्ठं वरिष्ठः सर्वधन्विनाम् ।आरोप्य च महावीर्यो महाबुद्धिर्महाबलः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tadādāya dhanuḥśreṣṭhaṁ variṣṭhaḥ sarvadhanvinām ,āropya ca mahāvīryo mahābuddhirmahābalaḥ.
english: Then, having taken that excellent bow, the foremost among all archers, and having strung it, he who possessed great valor, great intellect, and great strength...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 19. Then, having taken that excellent bow, the foremost among all archers, and having strung it, he who possessed great valor, great intellect, and great strength...

sanskrit: अमृष्यमाणो धनुषश्छेदनं कृतवर्मणा ।कुपितोऽतिरथः शीघ्रं कृतवर्माणमभ्ययात् ॥२०॥
transliteration: amṛṣyamāṇo dhanuṣaśchedanaṁ kṛtavarmaṇā ,kupito'tirathaḥ śīghraṁ kṛtavarmāṇamabhyayāt.
english: Unable to tolerate Kṛtavarman's cutting of his bow, that highly enraged extraordinary charioteer swiftly advanced towards Kṛtavarman.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 20. Unable to tolerate Kṛtavarman's cutting of his bow, that highly enraged extraordinary charioteer swiftly advanced towards Kṛtavarman.

sanskrit: ततः सुनिशितैर्बाणैर्दशभिः शिनिपुंगवः ।जघान सूतमश्वांश्च ध्वजं च कृतवर्मणः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ suniśitairbāṇairdaśabhiḥ śinipuṁgavaḥ ,jaghāna sūtamaśvāṁśca dhvajaṁ ca kṛtavarmaṇaḥ.
english: Then, that foremost warrior of the Śini clan (Śinipuṅgava), with ten very sharp arrows, struck Kṛtavarman's charioteer, his horses, and his banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 21. Then, that foremost warrior of the Śini clan (Śinipuṅgava), with ten very sharp arrows, struck Kṛtavarman's charioteer, his horses, and his banner.

sanskrit: ततो राजन्महेष्वासः कृतवर्मा महारथः ।हताश्वसूतं संप्रेक्ष्य रथं हेमपरिष्कृतम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato rājanmaheṣvāsaḥ kṛtavarmā mahārathaḥ ,hatāśvasūtaṁ saṁprekṣya rathaṁ hemapariṣkṛtam.
english: Then, O King, Kṛtavarman, the great archer and a great charioteer (mahāratha), having seen his gold-adorned chariot with its horses and charioteer slain...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 22. Then, O King, Kṛtavarman, the great archer and a great charioteer (mahāratha), having seen his gold-adorned chariot with its horses and charioteer slain...

sanskrit: रोषेण महताविष्टः शूलमुद्यम्य मारिष ।चिक्षेप भुजवेगेन जिघांसुः शिनिपुंगवम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: roṣeṇa mahatāviṣṭaḥ śūlamudyamya māriṣa ,cikṣepa bhujavegena jighāṁsuḥ śinipuṁgavam.
english: Filled with great rage, the venerable warrior, raising his spear, hurled it with the force of his arm, desiring to slay the best of the Shinis (Śinipuṅgava).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 23. Filled with great rage, the venerable warrior, raising his spear, hurled it with the force of his arm, desiring to slay the best of the Shinis (Śinipuṅgava).

sanskrit: तच्छूलं सात्वतो ह्याजौ निर्भिद्य निशितैः शरैः ।चूर्णितं पातयामास मोहयन्निव माधवम् ।ततोऽपरेण भल्लेन हृद्येनं समताडयत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tacchūlaṁ sātvato hyājau nirbhidya niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,cūrṇitaṁ pātayāmāsa mohayanniva mādhavam ,tato'pareṇa bhallena hṛdyenaṁ samatāḍayat.
english: Sātvata (Sātyaki) indeed, in battle, having pierced and shattered that spear with sharp arrows, made it fall, as if bewildering Kṛtavarmā (Mādhava). Then, with another arrow, he struck him in the heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 24. Sātvata (Sātyaki) indeed, in battle, having pierced and shattered that spear with sharp arrows, made it fall, as if bewildering Kṛtavarmā (Mādhava). Then, with another arrow, he struck him in the heart.

sanskrit: स युद्धे युयुधानेन हताश्वो हतसारथिः ।कृतवर्मा कृतास्त्रेण धरणीमन्वपद्यत ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa yuddhe yuyudhānena hatāśvo hatasārathiḥ ,kṛtavarmā kṛtāstreṇa dharaṇīmanvapadyata.
english: In that battle, Kṛtavarmā, whose horses and charioteer had been killed by Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), the master of weapons (kṛtāstra), fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 25. In that battle, Kṛtavarmā, whose horses and charioteer had been killed by Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), the master of weapons (kṛtāstra), fell to the ground.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्सात्यकिना वीरे द्वैरथे विरथीकृते ।समपद्यत सर्वेषां सैन्यानां सुमहद्भयम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tasminsātyakinā vīre dvairathe virathīkṛte ,samapadyata sarveṣāṁ sainyānāṁ sumahadbhayam.
english: When that hero (Kṛtavarmā) was uncharioted by Sātyaki in a single combat, a very great fear arose among all the armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 26. When that hero (Kṛtavarmā) was uncharioted by Sātyaki in a single combat, a very great fear arose among all the armies.

sanskrit: पुत्रस्य तव चात्यर्थं विषादः समपद्यत ।हतसूते हताश्वे च विरथे कृतवर्मणि ॥२७॥
transliteration: putrasya tava cātyarthaṁ viṣādaḥ samapadyata ,hatasūte hatāśve ca virathe kṛtavarmaṇi.
english: And your son became extremely dejected when Kṛtavarman's charioteer was killed, his horses were killed, and he was left without a chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 27. And your son became extremely dejected when Kṛtavarman's charioteer was killed, his horses were killed, and he was left without a chariot.

sanskrit: हताश्वं च समालक्ष्य हतसूतमरिंदमम् ।अभ्यधावत्कृपो राजञ्जिघांसुः शिनिपुंगवम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: hatāśvaṁ ca samālakṣya hatasūtamariṁdamam ,abhyadhāvatkṛpo rājañjighāṁsuḥ śinipuṁgavam.
english: And O King, Kṛpa, having seen Kṛtavarman, whose horses and charioteer were killed, rushed forward, wishing to kill Sātyaki (śinipuṅgava), the subduer of enemies (arim-dama).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 28. And O King, Kṛpa, having seen Kṛtavarman, whose horses and charioteer were killed, rushed forward, wishing to kill Sātyaki (śinipuṅgava), the subduer of enemies (arim-dama).

sanskrit: तमारोप्य रथोपस्थे मिषतां सर्वधन्विनाम् ।अपोवाह महाबाहुस्तूर्णमायोधनादपि ॥२९॥
transliteration: tamāropya rathopasthe miṣatāṁ sarvadhanvinām ,apovāha mahābāhustūrṇamāyodhanādapi.
english: The mighty-armed Kṛpa, having placed Kṛtavarman on the platform of his chariot, quickly carried him away from the battlefield, even while all the archers were watching.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 29. The mighty-armed Kṛpa, having placed Kṛtavarman on the platform of his chariot, quickly carried him away from the battlefield, even while all the archers were watching.

sanskrit: शैनेयेऽधिष्ठिते राजन्विरथे कृतवर्मणि ।दुर्योधनबलं सर्वं पुनरासीत्पराङ्मुखम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: śaineye'dhiṣṭhite rājanvirathe kṛtavarmaṇi ,duryodhanabalaṁ sarvaṁ punarāsītparāṅmukham.
english: O King, when Sātyaki (śaineya) was situated (on the chariot) and Kṛtavarman was charioless, all of Duryodhana's army again turned away in retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 30. O King, when Sātyaki (śaineya) was situated (on the chariot) and Kṛtavarman was charioless, all of Duryodhana's army again turned away in retreat.

sanskrit: तत्परे नावबुध्यन्त सैन्येन रजसावृते ।तावकाः प्रद्रुता राजन्दुर्योधनमृते नृपम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tatpare nāvabudhyanta sainyena rajasāvṛte ,tāvakāḥ pradrutā rājanduryodhanamṛte nṛpam.
english: O King, at that moment, when the area was covered by the army's dust, your people did not perceive what was happening and fled, all except King Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 31. O King, at that moment, when the area was covered by the army's dust, your people did not perceive what was happening and fled, all except King Duryodhana.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु संप्रेक्ष्य भग्नं स्वबलमन्तिकात् ।जवेनाभ्यपतत्तूर्णं सर्वांश्चैको न्यवारयत् ॥३२॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu saṁprekṣya bhagnaṁ svabalamantikāt ,javenābhyapatattūrṇaṁ sarvāṁścaiko nyavārayat.
english: But Duryodhana, having observed his own army routed from nearby, swiftly rushed forward and, all alone, held back all of them (the advancing enemies).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 32. But Duryodhana, having observed his own army routed from nearby, swiftly rushed forward and, all alone, held back all of them (the advancing enemies).

sanskrit: पाण्डूंश्च सर्वान्संक्रुद्धो धृष्टद्युम्नं च पार्षतम् ।शिखण्डिनं द्रौपदेयान्पाञ्चालानां च ये गणाः ॥३३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍūṁśca sarvānsaṁkruddho dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pārṣatam ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ draupadeyānpāñcālānāṁ ca ye gaṇāḥ.
english: Greatly enraged, (he also checked) all the Pandavas, Dhrishtadyumna (pārṣata), Shikhandin, the Draupadeyas, and the troops who belonged to the Panchalas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 33. Greatly enraged, (he also checked) all the Pandavas, Dhrishtadyumna (pārṣata), Shikhandin, the Draupadeyas, and the troops who belonged to the Panchalas.

sanskrit: केकयान्सोमकांश्चैव पाञ्चालांश्चैव मारिष ।असंभ्रमं दुराधर्षः शितैरस्त्रैरवारयत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: kekayānsomakāṁścaiva pāñcālāṁścaiva māriṣa ,asaṁbhramaṁ durādharṣaḥ śitairastrairavārayat.
english: O respected one (māriṣa), the unassailable Duryodhana, undismayed, held back the Kekayas, as well as the Somakas, and also the Panchalas with his sharp weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 34. O respected one (māriṣa), the unassailable Duryodhana, undismayed, held back the Kekayas, as well as the Somakas, and also the Panchalas with his sharp weapons.

sanskrit: अतिष्ठदाहवे यत्तः पुत्रस्तव महाबलः ।यथा यज्ञे महानग्निर्मन्त्रपूतः प्रकाशयन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: atiṣṭhadāhave yattaḥ putrastava mahābalaḥ ,yathā yajñe mahānagnirmantrapūtaḥ prakāśayan.
english: Your immensely powerful son stood prepared in battle, just as a great fire, purified by sacred verses (mantra), shines brightly in a ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 35. Your immensely powerful son stood prepared in battle, just as a great fire, purified by sacred verses (mantra), shines brightly in a ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: तं परे नाभ्यवर्तन्त मर्त्या मृत्युमिवाहवे ।अथान्यं रथमास्थाय हार्दिक्यः समपद्यत ॥३६॥
transliteration: taṁ pare nābhyavartanta martyā mṛtyumivāhave ,athānyaṁ rathamāsthāya hārdikyaḥ samapadyata.
english: The enemies could not confront him in battle, just as mortals cannot confront death. Then Hārdikya, having mounted another chariot, joined the fray.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 20 Verse 36. The enemies could not confront him in battle, just as mortals cannot confront death. Then Hārdikya, having mounted another chariot, joined the fray.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।पुत्रस्तु ते महाराज रथस्थो रथिनां वरः ।दुरुत्सहो बभौ युद्धे यथा रुद्रः प्रतापवान् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,putrastu te mahārāja rathastho rathināṁ varaḥ ,durutsaho babhau yuddhe yathā rudraḥ pratāpavān.
english: Saṃjaya said: 'O great king, your son, the best of charioteers, standing on his chariot, shone irresistibly in battle, just like the mighty Rudra.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: 'O great king, your son, the best of charioteers, standing on his chariot, shone irresistibly in battle, just like the mighty Rudra.'

sanskrit: तस्य बाणसहस्रैस्तु प्रच्छन्ना ह्यभवन्मही ।परांश्च सिषिचे बाणैर्धाराभिरिव पर्वतान् ॥२॥
transliteration: tasya bāṇasahasraistu pracchannā hyabhavanmahī ,parāṁśca siṣice bāṇairdhārābhiriva parvatān.
english: Indeed, the earth became covered by his thousands of arrows. And he showered the enemies with arrows, just as clouds shower mountains with torrents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 2. Indeed, the earth became covered by his thousands of arrows. And he showered the enemies with arrows, just as clouds shower mountains with torrents.

sanskrit: न च सोऽस्ति पुमान्कश्चित्पाण्डवानां महाहवे ।हयो गजो रथो वापि योऽस्य बाणैरविक्षतः ॥३॥
transliteration: na ca so'sti pumānkaścitpāṇḍavānāṁ mahāhave ,hayo gajo ratho vāpi yo'sya bāṇairavikṣataḥ.
english: In that great battle, there was no man among the Pandavas, nor any horse, elephant, or chariot, that remained uninjured by Duryodhana's arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 3. In that great battle, there was no man among the Pandavas, nor any horse, elephant, or chariot, that remained uninjured by Duryodhana's arrows.

sanskrit: यं यं हि समरे योधं प्रपश्यामि विशां पते ।स स बाणैश्चितोऽभूद्वै पुत्रेण तव भारत ॥४॥
transliteration: yaṁ yaṁ hi samare yodhaṁ prapaśyāmi viśāṁ pate ,sa sa bāṇaiścito'bhūdvai putreṇa tava bhārata.
english: O lord of men, O Bharata, whichever warrior I indeed saw in that battle, that very warrior became covered with arrows by your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 4. O lord of men, O Bharata, whichever warrior I indeed saw in that battle, that very warrior became covered with arrows by your son.

sanskrit: यथा सैन्येन रजसा समुद्धूतेन वाहिनी ।प्रत्यदृश्यत संछन्ना तथा बाणैर्महात्मनः ॥५॥
transliteration: yathā sainyena rajasā samuddhūtena vāhinī ,pratyadṛśyata saṁchannā tathā bāṇairmahātmanaḥ.
english: Just as a host (of soldiers) became completely obscured by the dust raised by the army, so too was it thoroughly covered by the arrows of the great-souled one (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 5. Just as a host (of soldiers) became completely obscured by the dust raised by the army, so too was it thoroughly covered by the arrows of the great-souled one (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: बाणभूतामपश्याम पृथिवीं पृथिवीपते ।दुर्योधनेन प्रकृतां क्षिप्रहस्तेन धन्विना ॥६॥
transliteration: bāṇabhūtāmapaśyāma pṛthivīṁ pṛthivīpate ,duryodhanena prakṛtāṁ kṣiprahastena dhanvinā.
english: O lord of the earth, we saw the earth become transformed into (a field of) arrows, an effect created by Duryodhana, the swift-handed archer.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 6. O lord of the earth, we saw the earth become transformed into (a field of) arrows, an effect created by Duryodhana, the swift-handed archer.

sanskrit: तेषु योधसहस्रेषु तावकेषु परेषु च ।एको दुर्योधनो ह्यासीत्पुमानिति मतिर्मम ॥७॥
transliteration: teṣu yodhasahasreṣu tāvakeṣu pareṣu ca ,eko duryodhano hyāsītpumāniti matirmama.
english: Among those thousands of warriors, both yours and the enemy's, only Duryodhana was truly a man – this is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 7. Among those thousands of warriors, both yours and the enemy's, only Duryodhana was truly a man – this is my opinion.

sanskrit: तत्राद्भुतमपश्याम तव पुत्रस्य विक्रमम् ।यदेकं सहिताः पार्था नात्यवर्तन्त भारत ॥८॥
transliteration: tatrādbhutamapaśyāma tava putrasya vikramam ,yadekaṁ sahitāḥ pārthā nātyavartanta bhārata.
english: O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), we witnessed your son's astonishing valor there, because the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), even united, could not overcome him alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 8. O Bhārata (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), we witnessed your son's astonishing valor there, because the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), even united, could not overcome him alone.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं शतेनाजौ विव्याध भरतर्षभ ।भीमसेनं च सप्तत्या सहदेवं च सप्तभिः ॥९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ śatenājau vivyādha bharatarṣabha ,bhīmasenaṁ ca saptatyā sahadevaṁ ca saptabhiḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), in that battle, he (Duryodhana) wounded Yudhiṣṭhira with a hundred (arrows), Bhīmasena with seventy, and Sahadeva with seven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 9. O best of Bharatas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), in that battle, he (Duryodhana) wounded Yudhiṣṭhira with a hundred (arrows), Bhīmasena with seventy, and Sahadeva with seven.

sanskrit: नकुलं च चतुःषष्ट्या धृष्टद्युम्नं च पञ्चभिः ।सप्तभिर्द्रौपदेयांश्च त्रिभिर्विव्याध सात्यकिम् ।धनुश्चिच्छेद भल्लेन सहदेवस्य मारिष ॥१०॥
transliteration: nakulaṁ ca catuḥṣaṣṭyā dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ ca pañcabhiḥ ,saptabhirdraupadeyāṁśca tribhirvivyādha sātyakim ,dhanuściccheda bhallena sahadevasya māriṣa.
english: And, O honorable one (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he (Duryodhana) wounded Nakula with sixty-four (arrows), Dhṛṣṭadyumna with five, the sons of Draupadī with seven, and Sātyaki with three. He also cut Sahadeva's bow with a broad-headed arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 10. And, O honorable one (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he (Duryodhana) wounded Nakula with sixty-four (arrows), Dhṛṣṭadyumna with five, the sons of Draupadī with seven, and Sātyaki with three. He also cut Sahadeva's bow with a broad-headed arrow.

sanskrit: तदपास्य धनुश्छिन्नं माद्रीपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।अभ्यधावत राजानं प्रगृह्यान्यन्महद्धनुः ।ततो दुर्योधनं संख्ये विव्याध दशभिः शरैः ॥११॥
transliteration: tadapāsya dhanuśchinnaṁ mādrīputraḥ pratāpavān ,abhyadhāvata rājānaṁ pragṛhyānyanmahaddhanuḥ ,tato duryodhanaṁ saṁkhye vivyādha daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Having cast aside his broken bow, the mighty son of Mādrī (Sahadeva) ran towards the king (Duryodhana), taking up another great bow. Then, in battle, he pierced Duryodhana with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 11. Having cast aside his broken bow, the mighty son of Mādrī (Sahadeva) ran towards the king (Duryodhana), taking up another great bow. Then, in battle, he pierced Duryodhana with ten arrows.

sanskrit: नकुलश्च ततो वीरो राजानं नवभिः शरैः ।घोररूपैर्महेष्वासो विव्याध च ननाद च ॥१२॥
transliteration: nakulaśca tato vīro rājānaṁ navabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,ghorarūpairmaheṣvāso vivyādha ca nanāda ca.
english: And then, the heroic Nakula, a great archer, pierced the king (Duryodhana) with nine dreadful-looking arrows, and he roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 12. And then, the heroic Nakula, a great archer, pierced the king (Duryodhana) with nine dreadful-looking arrows, and he roared.

sanskrit: सात्यकिश्चापि राजानं शरेणानतपर्वणा ।द्रौपदेयास्त्रिसप्तत्या धर्मराजश्च सप्तभिः ।अशीत्या भीमसेनश्च शरै राजानमार्दयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sātyakiścāpi rājānaṁ śareṇānataparvaṇā ,draupadeyāstrisaptatyā dharmarājaśca saptabhiḥ ,aśītyā bhīmasenaśca śarai rājānamārdayat.
english: And Sātyaki also tormented the king (Duryodhana) with an unbent-jointed arrow. The sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyas) afflicted him with seventy-three arrows, and Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) with seven, and Bhīmasena with eighty arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 13. And Sātyaki also tormented the king (Duryodhana) with an unbent-jointed arrow. The sons of Draupadī (Draupadeyas) afflicted him with seventy-three arrows, and Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) with seven, and Bhīmasena with eighty arrows.

sanskrit: समन्तात्कीर्यमाणस्तु बाणसंघैर्महात्मभिः ।न चचाल महाराज सर्वसैन्यस्य पश्यतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: samantātkīryamāṇastu bāṇasaṁghairmahātmabhiḥ ,na cacāla mahārāja sarvasainyasya paśyataḥ.
english: But being covered from all sides by multitudes of arrows from the great-souled ones, he (Duryodhana) did not stir, O great king, while the entire army watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 14. But being covered from all sides by multitudes of arrows from the great-souled ones, he (Duryodhana) did not stir, O great king, while the entire army watched.

sanskrit: लाघवं सौष्ठवं चापि वीर्यं चैव महात्मनः ।अति सर्वाणि भूतानि ददृशुः सर्वमानवाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: lāghavaṁ sauṣṭhavaṁ cāpi vīryaṁ caiva mahātmanaḥ ,ati sarvāṇi bhūtāni dadṛśuḥ sarvamānavāḥ.
english: All humans indeed witnessed the agility, dexterity, and valor of that great soul, which surpassed all other beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 15. All humans indeed witnessed the agility, dexterity, and valor of that great soul, which surpassed all other beings.

sanskrit: धार्तराष्ट्रास्तु राजेन्द्र यात्वा तु स्वल्पमन्तरम् ।अपश्यमाना राजानं पर्यवर्तन्त दंशिताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: dhārtarāṣṭrāstu rājendra yātvā tu svalpamantaram ,apaśyamānā rājānaṁ paryavartanta daṁśitāḥ.
english: But, O best of kings, the sons of Dhritarashtra, having advanced only a short distance, turned back fully armored when they could not see the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 16. But, O best of kings, the sons of Dhritarashtra, having advanced only a short distance, turned back fully armored when they could not see the king.

sanskrit: तेषामापततां घोरस्तुमुलः समजायत ।क्षुब्धस्य हि समुद्रस्य प्रावृट्काले यथा निशि ॥१७॥
transliteration: teṣāmāpatatāṁ ghorastumulaḥ samajāyata ,kṣubdhasya hi samudrasya prāvṛṭkāle yathā niśi.
english: A dreadful and tumultuous uproar arose among those advancing, just as it does from an agitated ocean at night during the monsoon season.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 17. A dreadful and tumultuous uproar arose among those advancing, just as it does from an agitated ocean at night during the monsoon season.

sanskrit: समासाद्य रणे ते तु राजानमपराजितम् ।प्रत्युद्ययुर्महेष्वासाः पाण्डवानाततायिनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: samāsādya raṇe te tu rājānamaparājitam ,pratyudyayurmaheṣvāsāḥ pāṇḍavānātatāyinaḥ.
english: But the Pandavas, great archers (maheṣvāsāḥ), having encountered their unconquered king in battle, then advanced as aggressors (ātatāyinaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 18. But the Pandavas, great archers (maheṣvāsāḥ), having encountered their unconquered king in battle, then advanced as aggressors (ātatāyinaḥ).

sanskrit: भीमसेनं रणे क्रुद्धं द्रोणपुत्रो न्यवारयत् ।ततो बाणैर्महाराज प्रमुक्तैः सर्वतोदिशम् ।नाज्ञायन्त रणे वीरा न दिशः प्रदिशस्तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhīmasenaṁ raṇe kruddhaṁ droṇaputro nyavārayat ,tato bāṇairmahārāja pramuktaiḥ sarvatodiśam ,nājñāyanta raṇe vīrā na diśaḥ pradiśastathā.
english: Droṇa's son restrained the enraged Bhimasena in battle. Then, O great king, due to the arrows released in all directions, neither the heroes in battle nor the main and intermediate directions could be distinguished.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 19. Droṇa's son restrained the enraged Bhimasena in battle. Then, O great king, due to the arrows released in all directions, neither the heroes in battle nor the main and intermediate directions could be distinguished.

sanskrit: तावुभौ क्रूरकर्माणावुभौ भारत दुःसहौ ।घोररूपमयुध्येतां कृतप्रतिकृतैषिणौ ।त्रासयन्तौ जगत्सर्वं ज्याक्षेपविहतत्वचौ ॥२०॥
transliteration: tāvubhau krūrakarmāṇāvubhau bhārata duḥsahau ,ghorarūpamayudhyetāṁ kṛtapratikṛtaiṣiṇau ,trāsayantau jagatsarvaṁ jyākṣepavihatatvacau.
english: O descendant of Bharata, those two, both performing fierce deeds and both irresistible, fought terribly, seeking retaliation, and terrifying the entire world with their skin scarred by the friction of their bowstrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 20. O descendant of Bharata, those two, both performing fierce deeds and both irresistible, fought terribly, seeking retaliation, and terrifying the entire world with their skin scarred by the friction of their bowstrings.

sanskrit: शकुनिस्तु रणे वीरो युधिष्ठिरमपीडयत् ।तस्याश्वांश्चतुरो हत्वा सुबलस्य सुतो विभुः ।नादं चकार बलवान्सर्वसैन्यानि कम्पयन् ॥२१॥
transliteration: śakunistu raṇe vīro yudhiṣṭhiramapīḍayat ,tasyāśvāṁścaturo hatvā subalasya suto vibhuḥ ,nādaṁ cakāra balavānsarvasainyāni kampayan.
english: But the hero Shakuni harassed Yudhishthira in battle. The mighty son of Subala (Shakuni), after killing Yudhishthira's four horses, roared, making all the armies tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 21. But the hero Shakuni harassed Yudhishthira in battle. The mighty son of Subala (Shakuni), after killing Yudhishthira's four horses, roared, making all the armies tremble.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे वीरं राजानमपराजितम् ।अपोवाह रथेनाजौ सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: etasminnantare vīraṁ rājānamaparājitam ,apovāha rathenājau sahadevaḥ pratāpavān.
english: In the meantime, the glorious Sahadeva carried away the heroic, unconquered king (Yudhishthira) in his chariot from the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 22. In the meantime, the glorious Sahadeva carried away the heroic, unconquered king (Yudhishthira) in his chariot from the battle.

sanskrit: अथान्यं रथमास्थाय धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।शकुनिं नवभिर्विद्ध्वा पुनर्विव्याध पञ्चभिः ।ननाद च महानादं प्रवरः सर्वधन्विनाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: athānyaṁ rathamāsthāya dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śakuniṁ navabhirviddhvā punarvivyādha pañcabhiḥ ,nanāda ca mahānādaṁ pravaraḥ sarvadhanvinām.
english: Then, mounting another chariot, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma) and foremost among all archers, first pierced Shakuni with nine arrows, and then again struck him with five, letting out a mighty roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 23. Then, mounting another chariot, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma) and foremost among all archers, first pierced Shakuni with nine arrows, and then again struck him with five, letting out a mighty roar.

sanskrit: तद्युद्धमभवच्चित्रं घोररूपं च मारिष ।ईक्षितृप्रीतिजननं सिद्धचारणसेवितम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tadyuddhamabhavaccitraṁ ghorarūpaṁ ca māriṣa ,īkṣitṛprītijananaṁ siddhacāraṇasevitam.
english: And, O venerable one, that battle was remarkable and dreadful in form, generating joy for onlookers and attended by Siddhas and Charanas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 24. And, O venerable one, that battle was remarkable and dreadful in form, generating joy for onlookers and attended by Siddhas and Charanas.

sanskrit: उलूकस्तु महेष्वासं नकुलं युद्धदुर्मदम् ।अभ्यद्रवदमेयात्मा शरवर्षैः समन्ततः ॥२५॥
transliteration: ulūkastu maheṣvāsaṁ nakulaṁ yuddhadurmadam ,abhyadravadameyātmā śaravarṣaiḥ samantataḥ.
english: But Uluka, whose nature (ātman) was immeasurable, rushed towards Nakula, the great archer who was fierce in battle, showering him with arrows from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 25. But Uluka, whose nature (ātman) was immeasurable, rushed towards Nakula, the great archer who was fierce in battle, showering him with arrows from all sides.

sanskrit: तथैव नकुलः शूरः सौबलस्य सुतं रणे ।शरवर्षेण महता समन्तात्पर्यवारयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tathaiva nakulaḥ śūraḥ saubalasya sutaṁ raṇe ,śaravarṣeṇa mahatā samantātparyavārayat.
english: In the same way, the valiant Nakula, in battle, surrounded Saubala's son (Uluka) from all sides with a great shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 26. In the same way, the valiant Nakula, in battle, surrounded Saubala's son (Uluka) from all sides with a great shower of arrows.

sanskrit: तौ तत्र समरे वीरौ कुलपुत्रौ महारथौ ।योधयन्तावपश्येतां परस्परकृतागसौ ॥२७॥
transliteration: tau tatra samare vīrau kulaputrau mahārathau ,yodhayantāvapaśyetāṁ parasparakṛtāgasau.
english: There in the battle, those two heroic sons of noble families, great warriors who had wronged each other, saw each other fighting.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 27. There in the battle, those two heroic sons of noble families, great warriors who had wronged each other, saw each other fighting.

sanskrit: तथैव कृतवर्मा तु शैनेयं शत्रुतापनम् ।योधयञ्शुशुभे राजन्बलं शक्र इवाहवे ॥२८॥
transliteration: tathaiva kṛtavarmā tu śaineyaṁ śatrutāpanam ,yodhayañśuśubhe rājanbalaṁ śakra ivāhave.
english: O King, Kṛtavarmā, fighting with Śaineya, the tormentor of enemies, shone splendidly in battle, just like Indra (shines with his) army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 28. O King, Kṛtavarmā, fighting with Śaineya, the tormentor of enemies, shone splendidly in battle, just like Indra (shines with his) army.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो धनुश्छित्त्वा धृष्टद्युम्नस्य संयुगे ।अथैनं छिन्नधन्वानं विव्याध निशितैः शरैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: duryodhano dhanuśchittvā dhṛṣṭadyumnasya saṁyuge ,athainaṁ chinnadhanvānaṁ vivyādha niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Duryodhana, having cut Dṛṣṭadyumna's bow in battle, then pierced him, who had a broken bow, with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 29. Duryodhana, having cut Dṛṣṭadyumna's bow in battle, then pierced him, who had a broken bow, with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नोऽपि समरे प्रगृह्य परमायुधम् ।राजानं योधयामास पश्यतां सर्वधन्विनाम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumno'pi samare pragṛhya paramāyudham ,rājānaṁ yodhayāmāsa paśyatāṁ sarvadhanvinām.
english: Dṛṣṭadyumna also, having taken up a supreme weapon in battle, fought the king (Duryodhana), while all the archers watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 30. Dṛṣṭadyumna also, having taken up a supreme weapon in battle, fought the king (Duryodhana), while all the archers watched.

sanskrit: तयोर्युद्धं महच्चासीत्संग्रामे भरतर्षभ ।प्रभिन्नयोर्यथा सक्तं मत्तयोर्वरहस्तिनोः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tayoryuddhaṁ mahaccāsītsaṁgrāme bharatarṣabha ,prabhinnayoryathā saktaṁ mattayorvarahastinoḥ.
english: O bull among the Bharatas, a great battle ensued between the two of them in that conflict, just as two rutting, intoxicated, best elephants might fiercely engage each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 31. O bull among the Bharatas, a great battle ensued between the two of them in that conflict, just as two rutting, intoxicated, best elephants might fiercely engage each other.

sanskrit: गौतमस्तु रणे क्रुद्धो द्रौपदेयान्महाबलान् ।विव्याध बहुभिः शूरः शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: gautamastu raṇe kruddho draupadeyānmahābalān ,vivyādha bahubhiḥ śūraḥ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: However, the heroic Gautama, enraged in battle, struck the immensely powerful sons of Draupadi with many sharp arrows, whose shafts were well-fitted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 32. However, the heroic Gautama, enraged in battle, struck the immensely powerful sons of Draupadi with many sharp arrows, whose shafts were well-fitted.

sanskrit: तस्य तैरभवद्युद्धमिन्द्रियैरिव देहिनः ।घोररूपमसंवार्यं निर्मर्यादमतीव च ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasya tairabhavadyuddhamindriyairiva dehinaḥ ,ghorarūpamasaṁvāryaṁ nirmaryādamatīva ca.
english: His battle with them became terrible in form, irresistible, and exceedingly boundless, like the struggle of an embodied being (dehinaḥ) with its own senses (indriyāṇi).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 33. His battle with them became terrible in form, irresistible, and exceedingly boundless, like the struggle of an embodied being (dehinaḥ) with its own senses (indriyāṇi).

sanskrit: ते च तं पीडयामासुरिन्द्रियाणीव बालिशम् ।स च तान्प्रतिसंरब्धः प्रत्ययोधयदाहवे ॥३४॥
transliteration: te ca taṁ pīḍayāmāsurindriyāṇīva bāliśam ,sa ca tānpratisaṁrabdhaḥ pratyayodhayadāhave.
english: And they tormented him, just as the senses (indriyāṇi) afflict a foolish person. And he, enraged in return, fought back against them in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 34. And they tormented him, just as the senses (indriyāṇi) afflict a foolish person. And he, enraged in return, fought back against them in battle.

sanskrit: एवं चित्रमभूद्युद्धं तस्य तैः सह भारत ।उत्थायोत्थाय हि यथा देहिनामिन्द्रियैर्विभो ॥३५॥
transliteration: evaṁ citramabhūdyuddhaṁ tasya taiḥ saha bhārata ,utthāyotthāya hi yathā dehināmindriyairvibho.
english: Thus, O Bhārata, the battle between him and them was astonishing, O mighty one, just as the senses (indriya) of embodied beings (dehin) repeatedly assert themselves.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 35. Thus, O Bhārata, the battle between him and them was astonishing, O mighty one, just as the senses (indriya) of embodied beings (dehin) repeatedly assert themselves.

sanskrit: नराश्चैव नरैः सार्धं दन्तिनो दन्तिभिस्तथा ।हया हयैः समासक्ता रथिनो रथिभिस्तथा ।संकुलं चाभवद्भूयो घोररूपं विशां पते ॥३६॥
transliteration: narāścaiva naraiḥ sārdhaṁ dantino dantibhistathā ,hayā hayaiḥ samāsaktā rathino rathibhistathā ,saṁkulaṁ cābhavadbhūyo ghorarūpaṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: And men fought with men, elephants with elephants, likewise horses were intensely engaged with horses, and charioteers with charioteers. And, O lord of the people, the battlefield became exceedingly crowded and took on a dreadful form.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 36. And men fought with men, elephants with elephants, likewise horses were intensely engaged with horses, and charioteers with charioteers. And, O lord of the people, the battlefield became exceedingly crowded and took on a dreadful form.

sanskrit: इदं चित्रमिदं घोरमिदं रौद्रमिति प्रभो ।युद्धान्यासन्महाराज घोराणि च बहूनि च ॥३७॥
transliteration: idaṁ citramidaṁ ghoramidaṁ raudramiti prabho ,yuddhānyāsanmahārāja ghorāṇi ca bahūni ca.
english: O lord, one would exclaim 'This is wondrous! This is dreadful! This is terrible!', for indeed, O great king, there were many fierce battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 37. O lord, one would exclaim 'This is wondrous! This is dreadful! This is terrible!', for indeed, O great king, there were many fierce battles.

sanskrit: ते समासाद्य समरे परस्परमरिंदमाः ।विव्यधुश्चैव जघ्नुश्च समासाद्य महाहवे ॥३८॥
transliteration: te samāsādya samare parasparamariṁdamāḥ ,vivyadhuścaiva jaghnuśca samāsādya mahāhave.
english: Those subduers of enemies, having come together mutually in battle, indeed pierced and struck each other, having confronted each other in the great war.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 38. Those subduers of enemies, having come together mutually in battle, indeed pierced and struck each other, having confronted each other in the great war.

sanskrit: तेषां शस्त्रसमुद्भूतं रजस्तीव्रमदृश्यत ।प्रवातेनोद्धतं राजन्धावद्भिश्चाश्वसादिभिः ॥३९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ śastrasamudbhūtaṁ rajastīvramadṛśyata ,pravātenoddhataṁ rājandhāvadbhiścāśvasādibhiḥ.
english: O King, an intense dust, produced by their weapons and stirred up by the wind and by the galloping horsemen, became visible.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 39. O King, an intense dust, produced by their weapons and stirred up by the wind and by the galloping horsemen, became visible.

sanskrit: रथनेमिसमुद्भूतं निःश्वासैश्चापि दन्तिनाम् ।रजः संध्याभ्रकपिलं दिवाकरपथं ययौ ॥४०॥
transliteration: rathanemisamudbhūtaṁ niḥśvāsaiścāpi dantinām ,rajaḥ saṁdhyābhrakapilaṁ divākarapathaṁ yayau.
english: Also, the dust, churned up by the chariot wheels and the breaths of the elephants, reddish-brown like twilight clouds, ascended to the path of the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 40. Also, the dust, churned up by the chariot wheels and the breaths of the elephants, reddish-brown like twilight clouds, ascended to the path of the sun.

sanskrit: रजसा तेन संपृक्ते भास्करे निष्प्रभीकृते ।संछादिताभवद्भूमिस्ते च शूरा महारथाः ॥४१॥
transliteration: rajasā tena saṁpṛkte bhāskare niṣprabhīkṛte ,saṁchāditābhavadbhūmiste ca śūrā mahārathāḥ.
english: With the sun, being covered by that dust, deprived of its brilliance, the earth became completely hidden, and those heroic great warriors (also became hidden).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 41. With the sun, being covered by that dust, deprived of its brilliance, the earth became completely hidden, and those heroic great warriors (also became hidden).

sanskrit: मुहूर्तादिव संवृत्तं नीरजस्कं समन्ततः ।वीरशोणितसिक्तायां भूमौ भरतसत्तम ।उपाशाम्यत्ततस्तीव्रं तद्रजो घोरदर्शनम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: muhūrtādiva saṁvṛttaṁ nīrajaskaṁ samantataḥ ,vīraśoṇitasiktāyāṁ bhūmau bharatasattama ,upāśāmyattatastīvraṁ tadrajo ghoradarśanam.
english: O best of the Bharatas, as if in an instant, everything became free of dust. Then that intense, dreadful-looking dust settled on the ground, which was drenched with the blood of heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 42. O best of the Bharatas, as if in an instant, everything became free of dust. Then that intense, dreadful-looking dust settled on the ground, which was drenched with the blood of heroes.

sanskrit: ततोऽपश्यं महाराज द्वंद्वयुद्धानि भारत ।यथाप्राग्र्यं यथाज्येष्ठं मध्याह्ने वै सुदारुणे ।वर्मणां तत्र राजेन्द्र व्यदृश्यन्तोज्ज्वलाः प्रभाः ॥४३॥
transliteration: tato'paśyaṁ mahārāja dvaṁdvayuddhāni bhārata ,yathāprāgryaṁ yathājyeṣṭhaṁ madhyāhne vai sudāruṇe ,varmaṇāṁ tatra rājendra vyadṛśyantojjvalāḥ prabhāḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), O Bhārata, then I witnessed many duels (dvandvayuddhāni) fought according to precedence and seniority, particularly during that exceptionally fierce midday. O best of kings (rājendra), the brilliant gleams of the armors were clearly visible there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 43. O great king (mahārāja), O Bhārata, then I witnessed many duels (dvandvayuddhāni) fought according to precedence and seniority, particularly during that exceptionally fierce midday. O best of kings (rājendra), the brilliant gleams of the armors were clearly visible there.

sanskrit: शब्दः सुतुमुलः संख्ये शराणां पततामभूत् ।महावेणुवनस्येव दह्यमानस्य सर्वतः ॥४४॥
transliteration: śabdaḥ sutumulaḥ saṁkhye śarāṇāṁ patatāmabhūt ,mahāveṇuvanasyeva dahyamānasya sarvataḥ.
english: A tremendously tumultuous sound of falling arrows arose in the battle, like the sound of a vast bamboo forest burning on all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 21 Verse 44. A tremendously tumultuous sound of falling arrows arose in the battle, like the sound of a vast bamboo forest burning on all sides.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।वर्तमाने तथा युद्धे घोररूपे भयानके ।अभज्यत बलं तत्र तव पुत्रस्य पाण्डवैः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,vartamāne tathā yuddhe ghorarūpe bhayānake ,abhajyata balaṁ tatra tava putrasya pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: 'While that terrifying and dreadful battle was thus raging, your son's army (bala) was shattered there by the Pāṇḍavas.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: 'While that terrifying and dreadful battle was thus raging, your son's army (bala) was shattered there by the Pāṇḍavas.'

sanskrit: तांस्तु यत्नेन महता संनिवार्य महारथान् ।पुत्रस्ते योधयामास पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tāṁstu yatnena mahatā saṁnivārya mahārathān ,putraste yodhayāmāsa pāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm.
english: But your son, having with great effort checked those great charioteers (mahāratha), then engaged in battle with the army (anīkinī) of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 2. But your son, having with great effort checked those great charioteers (mahāratha), then engaged in battle with the army (anīkinī) of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: निवृत्ताः सहसा योधास्तव पुत्रप्रियैषिणः ।संनिवृत्तेषु तेष्वेवं युद्धमासीत्सुदारुणम् ॥३॥
transliteration: nivṛttāḥ sahasā yodhāstava putrapriyaiṣiṇaḥ ,saṁnivṛtteṣu teṣvevaṁ yuddhamāsītsudāruṇam.
english: Your warriors, who desired the welfare of your sons, suddenly retreated. When they had thus fully withdrawn, the battle among them became exceedingly fierce.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 3. Your warriors, who desired the welfare of your sons, suddenly retreated. When they had thus fully withdrawn, the battle among them became exceedingly fierce.

sanskrit: तावकानां परेषां च देवासुररणोपमम् ।परेषां तव सैन्ये च नासीत्कश्चित्पराङ्मुखः ॥४॥
transliteration: tāvakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca devāsuraraṇopamam ,pareṣāṁ tava sainye ca nāsītkaścitparāṅmukhaḥ.
english: The battle between your forces and those of the enemy was like a war between gods and demons. And in both the enemy's army and your own, no one turned their back in retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 4. The battle between your forces and those of the enemy was like a war between gods and demons. And in both the enemy's army and your own, no one turned their back in retreat.

sanskrit: अनुमानेन युध्यन्ते संज्ञाभिश्च परस्परम् ।तेषां क्षयो महानासीद्युध्यतामितरेतरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: anumānena yudhyante saṁjñābhiśca parasparam ,teṣāṁ kṣayo mahānāsīdyudhyatāmitaretaram.
english: They fought each other using inferences and signs. A great destruction occurred among them as they fought one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 5. They fought each other using inferences and signs. A great destruction occurred among them as they fought one another.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा क्रोधेन महता युतः ।जिगीषमाणः संग्रामे धार्तराष्ट्रान्सराजकान् ॥६॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā krodhena mahatā yutaḥ ,jigīṣamāṇaḥ saṁgrāme dhārtarāṣṭrānsarājakān.
english: Then King Yudhishthira, endowed with great wrath, wishing to conquer the sons of Dhritarashtra along with their allied rulers in battle...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 6. Then King Yudhishthira, endowed with great wrath, wishing to conquer the sons of Dhritarashtra along with their allied rulers in battle...

sanskrit: त्रिभिः शारद्वतं विद्ध्वा रुक्मपुङ्खैः शिलाशितैः ।चतुर्भिर्निजघानाश्वान्कल्याणान्कृतवर्मणः ॥७॥
transliteration: tribhiḥ śāradvataṁ viddhvā rukmapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaiḥ ,caturbhirnijaghānāśvānkalyāṇānkṛtavarmaṇaḥ.
english: Having pierced Śāradvata (Kṛpa) with three arrows, which were golden-feathered and sharpened on stone, he then struck down Kṛtavarman's four excellent horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 7. Having pierced Śāradvata (Kṛpa) with three arrows, which were golden-feathered and sharpened on stone, he then struck down Kṛtavarman's four excellent horses.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा तु हार्दिक्यमपोवाह यशस्विनम् ।अथ शारद्वतोऽष्टाभिः प्रत्यविध्यद्युधिष्ठिरम् ॥८॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā tu hārdikyamapovāha yaśasvinam ,atha śāradvato'ṣṭābhiḥ pratyavidhyadyudhiṣṭhiram.
english: However, Aśvatthāmā carried away the glorious Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman). Then, Śāradvata (Kṛpa) struck Yudhiṣṭhira back with eight arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 8. However, Aśvatthāmā carried away the glorious Hārdikya (Kṛtavarman). Then, Śāradvata (Kṛpa) struck Yudhiṣṭhira back with eight arrows.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा रथान्सप्तशतान्रणे ।प्रेषयद्यत्र राजासौ धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ॥९॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā rathānsaptaśatānraṇe ,preṣayadyatra rājāsau dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Then, King Duryodhana dispatched seven hundred chariots into the battle where that king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), was present.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 9. Then, King Duryodhana dispatched seven hundred chariots into the battle where that king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), was present.

sanskrit: ते रथा रथिभिर्युक्ता मनोमारुतरंहसः ।अभ्यद्रवन्त संग्रामे कौन्तेयस्य रथं प्रति ॥१०॥
transliteration: te rathā rathibhiryuktā manomārutaraṁhasaḥ ,abhyadravanta saṁgrāme kaunteyasya rathaṁ prati.
english: Those chariots, manned by charioteers and possessing the speed of mind and wind, rushed into the battle towards Kaunteya's (Arjuna's) chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 10. Those chariots, manned by charioteers and possessing the speed of mind and wind, rushed into the battle towards Kaunteya's (Arjuna's) chariot.

sanskrit: ते समन्तान्महाराज परिवार्य युधिष्ठिरम् ।अदृश्यं सायकैश्चक्रुर्मेघा इव दिवाकरम् ॥११॥
transliteration: te samantānmahārāja parivārya yudhiṣṭhiram ,adṛśyaṁ sāyakaiścakrurmeghā iva divākaram.
english: O great king, they surrounded Yudhishthira from all sides and made him invisible with their arrows, just as clouds obscure the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 11. O great king, they surrounded Yudhishthira from all sides and made him invisible with their arrows, just as clouds obscure the sun.

sanskrit: नामृष्यन्त सुसंरब्धाः शिखण्डिप्रमुखा रथाः ।रथैरग्र्यजवैर्युक्तैः किङ्किणीजालसंवृतैः ।आजग्मुरभिरक्षन्तः कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: nāmṛṣyanta susaṁrabdhāḥ śikhaṇḍipramukhā rathāḥ ,rathairagryajavairyuktaiḥ kiṅkiṇījālasaṁvṛtaiḥ ,ājagmurabhirakṣantaḥ kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: The highly enraged charioteers, led by Shikhandi, did not tolerate this. They came forward, protecting Yudhishthira, Kunti's son, with chariots that were swiftly moving, equipped, and adorned with networks of small bells.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 12. The highly enraged charioteers, led by Shikhandi, did not tolerate this. They came forward, protecting Yudhishthira, Kunti's son, with chariots that were swiftly moving, equipped, and adorned with networks of small bells.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते रौद्रः संग्रामः शोणितोदकः ।पाण्डवानां कुरूणां च यमराष्ट्रविवर्धनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte raudraḥ saṁgrāmaḥ śoṇitodakaḥ ,pāṇḍavānāṁ kurūṇāṁ ca yamarāṣṭravivardhanaḥ.
english: Then a fierce battle began, characterized by blood as its water, for the Pandavas and the Kurus, a battle that expanded the realm of Yama (the god of death).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 13. Then a fierce battle began, characterized by blood as its water, for the Pandavas and the Kurus, a battle that expanded the realm of Yama (the god of death).

sanskrit: रथान्सप्तशतान्हत्वा कुरूणामाततायिनाम् ।पाण्डवाः सह पाञ्चालैः पुनरेवाभ्यवारयन् ॥१४॥
transliteration: rathānsaptaśatānhatvā kurūṇāmātatāyinām ,pāṇḍavāḥ saha pāñcālaiḥ punarevābhyavārayan.
english: Having destroyed seven hundred chariots of the aggressive Kurus, the Pandavas, along with the Panchalas, indeed attacked again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 14. Having destroyed seven hundred chariots of the aggressive Kurus, the Pandavas, along with the Panchalas, indeed attacked again.

sanskrit: तत्र युद्धं महच्चासीत्तव पुत्रस्य पाण्डवैः ।न च नस्तादृशं दृष्टं नैव चापि परिश्रुतम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tatra yuddhaṁ mahaccāsīttava putrasya pāṇḍavaiḥ ,na ca nastādṛśaṁ dṛṣṭaṁ naiva cāpi pariśrutam.
english: There, a great battle took place between your son and the Pāṇḍavas. We have never seen nor even heard of such a battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 15. There, a great battle took place between your son and the Pāṇḍavas. We have never seen nor even heard of such a battle.

sanskrit: वर्तमाने तथा युद्धे निर्मर्यादे समन्ततः ।वध्यमानेषु योधेषु तावकेष्वितरेषु च ॥१६॥
transliteration: vartamāne tathā yuddhe nirmaryāde samantataḥ ,vadhyamāneṣu yodheṣu tāvakeṣvitareṣu ca.
english: Thus, as that unrestricted battle raged on all sides, with warriors being slaughtered, both among your ranks and among the others...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 16. Thus, as that unrestricted battle raged on all sides, with warriors being slaughtered, both among your ranks and among the others...

sanskrit: निनदत्सु च योधेषु शङ्खवर्यैश्च पूरितैः ।उत्कृष्टैः सिंहनादैश्च गर्जितेन च धन्विनाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ninadatsu ca yodheṣu śaṅkhavaryaiśca pūritaiḥ ,utkṛṣṭaiḥ siṁhanādaiśca garjitena ca dhanvinām.
english: And as the warriors roared, with the sounds of excellent conch shells filling the air, along with mighty lion-roars, and the shouts of the archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 17. And as the warriors roared, with the sounds of excellent conch shells filling the air, along with mighty lion-roars, and the shouts of the archers.

sanskrit: अतिप्रवृद्धे युद्धे च छिद्यमानेषु मर्मसु ।धावमानेषु योधेषु जयगृद्धिषु मारिष ॥१८॥
transliteration: atipravṛddhe yuddhe ca chidyamāneṣu marmasu ,dhāvamāneṣu yodheṣu jayagṛddhiṣu māriṣa.
english: And as the battle intensified greatly, with vital spots being pierced, and warriors, greedy for victory, rushing about, O respected one (māriṣa)!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 18. And as the battle intensified greatly, with vital spots being pierced, and warriors, greedy for victory, rushing about, O respected one (māriṣa)!

sanskrit: संहारे सर्वतो जाते पृथिव्यां शोकसंभवे ।बह्वीनामुत्तमस्त्रीणां सीमन्तोद्धरणे तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁhāre sarvato jāte pṛthivyāṁ śokasaṁbhave ,bahvīnāmuttamastrīṇāṁ sīmantoddharaṇe tathā.
english: When widespread destruction, causing great sorrow, occurred everywhere on earth, and similarly, when the hair-partings of many excellent women were dishevelled (a sign of widowhood).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 19. When widespread destruction, causing great sorrow, occurred everywhere on earth, and similarly, when the hair-partings of many excellent women were dishevelled (a sign of widowhood).

sanskrit: निर्मर्यादे तथा युद्धे वर्तमाने सुदारुणे ।प्रादुरासन्विनाशाय तदोत्पाताः सुदारुणाः ।चचाल शब्दं कुर्वाणा सपर्वतवना मही ॥२०॥
transliteration: nirmaryāde tathā yuddhe vartamāne sudāruṇe ,prādurāsanvināśāya tadotpātāḥ sudāruṇāḥ ,cacāla śabdaṁ kurvāṇā saparvatavanā mahī.
english: Similarly, when an exceedingly terrible and unrestrained war was raging, then very dreadful portents (utpāta) appeared, foretelling destruction. The earth (mahī), along with its mountains and forests, shook, making a rumbling sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 20. Similarly, when an exceedingly terrible and unrestrained war was raging, then very dreadful portents (utpāta) appeared, foretelling destruction. The earth (mahī), along with its mountains and forests, shook, making a rumbling sound.

sanskrit: सदण्डाः सोल्मुका राजञ्शीर्यमाणाः समन्ततः ।उल्काः पेतुर्दिवो भूमावाहत्य रविमण्डलम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sadaṇḍāḥ solmukā rājañśīryamāṇāḥ samantataḥ ,ulkāḥ peturdivo bhūmāvāhatya ravimaṇḍalam.
english: O King, blazing meteors (ulkāḥ) with fiery tails, falling from all directions, struck the sun's orb (ravimaṇḍala) and then fell upon the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 21. O King, blazing meteors (ulkāḥ) with fiery tails, falling from all directions, struck the sun's orb (ravimaṇḍala) and then fell upon the earth.

sanskrit: विष्वग्वाताः प्रादुरासन्नीचैः शर्करवर्षिणः ।अश्रूणि मुमुचुर्नागा वेपथुश्चास्पृशद्भृशम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: viṣvagvātāḥ prādurāsannīcaiḥ śarkaravarṣiṇaḥ ,aśrūṇi mumucurnāgā vepathuścāspṛśadbhṛśam.
english: Winds (vāta) blew in every direction, rising from below and showering gravel. The celestial serpents (nāga) shed tears, and an intense trembling (vepathu) gripped them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 22. Winds (vāta) blew in every direction, rising from below and showering gravel. The celestial serpents (nāga) shed tears, and an intense trembling (vepathu) gripped them.

sanskrit: एतान्घोराननादृत्य समुत्पातान्सुदारुणान् ।पुनर्युद्धाय संमन्त्र्य क्षत्रियास्तस्थुरव्यथाः ।रमणीये कुरुक्षेत्रे पुण्ये स्वर्गं यियासवः ॥२३॥
transliteration: etānghorānanādṛtya samutpātānsudāruṇān ,punaryuddhāya saṁmantrya kṣatriyāstasthuravyathāḥ ,ramaṇīye kurukṣetre puṇye svargaṁ yiyāsavaḥ.
english: The kṣatriyas, disregarding these terrible and extremely dreadful omens, consulted again for battle and stood fearless in the beautiful and sacred Kurukṣetra, desiring to go to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 23. The kṣatriyas, disregarding these terrible and extremely dreadful omens, consulted again for battle and stood fearless in the beautiful and sacred Kurukṣetra, desiring to go to heaven.

sanskrit: ततो गान्धारराजस्य पुत्रः शकुनिरब्रवीत् ।युध्यध्वमग्रतो यावत्पृष्ठतो हन्मि पाण्डवान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato gāndhārarājasya putraḥ śakunirabravīt ,yudhyadhvamagrato yāvatpṛṣṭhato hanmi pāṇḍavān.
english: Then Śakuni, the son of the King of Gandhāra, said, "You all fight in front while I strike down the Pāṇḍavas from behind."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 24. Then Śakuni, the son of the King of Gandhāra, said, "You all fight in front while I strike down the Pāṇḍavas from behind."

sanskrit: ततो नः संप्रयातानां मद्रयोधास्तरस्विनः ।हृष्टाः किलकिलाशब्दमकुर्वन्तापरे तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato naḥ saṁprayātānāṁ madrayodhāstarasvinaḥ ,hṛṣṭāḥ kilakilāśabdamakurvantāpare tathā.
english: Then, as we advanced, the powerful Madra warriors joyfully made a loud clamor, and others similarly shouted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 25. Then, as we advanced, the powerful Madra warriors joyfully made a loud clamor, and others similarly shouted.

sanskrit: अस्मांस्तु पुनरासाद्य लब्धलक्षा दुरासदाः ।शरासनानि धुन्वन्तः शरवर्षैरवाकिरन् ॥२६॥
transliteration: asmāṁstu punarāsādya labdhalakṣā durāsadāḥ ,śarāsanāni dhunvantaḥ śaravarṣairavākiran.
english: But, having again confronted us, those formidable warriors, having achieved their aim, shook their bows and showered us with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 26. But, having again confronted us, those formidable warriors, having achieved their aim, shook their bows and showered us with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: ततो हतं परैस्तत्र मद्रराजबलं तदा ।दुर्योधनबलं दृष्ट्वा पुनरासीत्पराङ्मुखम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tato hataṁ paraistatra madrarājabalaṁ tadā ,duryodhanabalaṁ dṛṣṭvā punarāsītparāṅmukham.
english: At that time, seeing the army of the king of Madras (Śalya) being struck down there by the enemies, Duryodhana's army again turned away.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 27. At that time, seeing the army of the king of Madras (Śalya) being struck down there by the enemies, Duryodhana's army again turned away.

sanskrit: गान्धारराजस्तु पुनर्वाक्यमाह ततो बली ।निवर्तध्वमधर्मज्ञा युध्यध्वं किं सृतेन वः ॥२८॥
transliteration: gāndhārarājastu punarvākyamāha tato balī ,nivartadhvamadharmajñā yudhyadhvaṁ kiṁ sṛtena vaḥ.
english: But then, the mighty king of Gandhāra (Śakuni) spoke again, saying: "O you who are ignorant of (natural) law (dharma)! Turn back and fight! What good is fleeing for you?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 28. But then, the mighty king of Gandhāra (Śakuni) spoke again, saying: "O you who are ignorant of (natural) law (dharma)! Turn back and fight! What good is fleeing for you?"

sanskrit: अनीकं दशसाहस्रमश्वानां भरतर्षभ ।आसीद्गान्धारराजस्य विमलप्रासयोधिनाम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: anīkaṁ daśasāhasramaśvānāṁ bharatarṣabha ,āsīdgāndhārarājasya vimalaprāsayodhinām.
english: O best of Bharatas, there was an army of ten thousand horses belonging to the king of Gandhāra (Śakuni), whose warriors fought with shining spears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 29. O best of Bharatas, there was an army of ten thousand horses belonging to the king of Gandhāra (Śakuni), whose warriors fought with shining spears.

sanskrit: बलेन तेन विक्रम्य वर्तमाने जनक्षये ।पृष्ठतः पाण्डवानीकमभ्यघ्नन्निशितैः शरैः ॥३०॥
transliteration: balena tena vikramya vartamāne janakṣaye ,pṛṣṭhataḥ pāṇḍavānīkamabhyaghnanniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Having advanced with that army, while the great slaughter of people was occurring, they struck the Pandava army from behind with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 30. Having advanced with that army, while the great slaughter of people was occurring, they struck the Pandava army from behind with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: तदभ्रमिव वातेन क्षिप्यमाणं समन्ततः ।अभज्यत महाराज पाण्डूनां सुमहद्बलम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tadabhramiva vātena kṣipyamāṇaṁ samantataḥ ,abhajyata mahārāja pāṇḍūnāṁ sumahadbalam.
english: O great king, the immense army of the Pandavas was shattered, like a cloud being scattered from all sides by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 31. O great king, the immense army of the Pandavas was shattered, like a cloud being scattered from all sides by the wind.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरः प्रेक्ष्य भग्नं स्वबलमन्तिकात् ।अभ्यचोदयदव्यग्रः सहदेवं महाबलम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiraḥ prekṣya bhagnaṁ svabalamantikāt ,abhyacodayadavyagraḥ sahadevaṁ mahābalam.
english: Then Yudhishthira, observing his own shattered army from close by, calmly encouraged the mighty Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 32. Then Yudhishthira, observing his own shattered army from close by, calmly encouraged the mighty Sahadeva.

sanskrit: असौ सुबलपुत्रो नो जघनं पीड्य दंशितः ।सेनां निसूदयन्त्येष पश्य पाण्डव दुर्मतिम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: asau subalaputro no jaghanaṁ pīḍya daṁśitaḥ ,senāṁ nisūdayantyeṣa paśya pāṇḍava durmatim.
english: O Pandava, observe this son of Subala, who is armored and pressing our rear flank; behold this evil-minded one destroying the army!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 33. O Pandava, observe this son of Subala, who is armored and pressing our rear flank; behold this evil-minded one destroying the army!

sanskrit: गच्छ त्वं द्रौपदेयाश्च शकुनिं सौबलं जहि ।रथानीकमहं रक्ष्ये पाञ्चालसहितोऽनघ ॥३४॥
transliteration: gaccha tvaṁ draupadeyāśca śakuniṁ saubalaṁ jahi ,rathānīkamahaṁ rakṣye pāñcālasahito'nagha.
english: O sinless one (Sahadeva), you and the sons of Draupadi go and slay Shakuni, son of Subala. I, accompanied by the Panchalas, will protect the chariot divisions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 34. O sinless one (Sahadeva), you and the sons of Draupadi go and slay Shakuni, son of Subala. I, accompanied by the Panchalas, will protect the chariot divisions.

sanskrit: गच्छन्तु कुञ्जराः सर्वे वाजिनश्च सह त्वया ।पादाताश्च त्रिसाहस्राः शकुनिं सौबलं जहि ॥३५॥
transliteration: gacchantu kuñjarāḥ sarve vājinaśca saha tvayā ,pādātāśca trisāhasrāḥ śakuniṁ saubalaṁ jahi.
english: Let all the elephants and horses go with you, along with three thousand foot soldiers. Kill Shakuni, the son of Subala.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 35. Let all the elephants and horses go with you, along with three thousand foot soldiers. Kill Shakuni, the son of Subala.

sanskrit: ततो गजाः सप्तशताश्चापपाणिभिरास्थिताः ।पञ्च चाश्वसहस्राणि सहदेवश्च वीर्यवान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tato gajāḥ saptaśatāścāpapāṇibhirāsthitāḥ ,pañca cāśvasahasrāṇi sahadevaśca vīryavān.
english: Then, seven hundred elephants, ridden by those holding bows in their hands, and five thousand horses, and the mighty Sahadeva (were present).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 36. Then, seven hundred elephants, ridden by those holding bows in their hands, and five thousand horses, and the mighty Sahadeva (were present).

sanskrit: पादाताश्च त्रिसाहस्रा द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः ।रणे ह्यभ्यद्रवंस्ते तु शकुनिं युद्धदुर्मदम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: pādātāśca trisāhasrā draupadeyāśca sarvaśaḥ ,raṇe hyabhyadravaṁste tu śakuniṁ yuddhadurmadam.
english: And three thousand foot soldiers, along with all the sons of Draupadi, indeed rushed into battle against Shakuni, who was arrogant and difficult to subdue in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 37. And three thousand foot soldiers, along with all the sons of Draupadi, indeed rushed into battle against Shakuni, who was arrogant and difficult to subdue in combat.

sanskrit: ततस्तु सौबलो राजन्नभ्यतिक्रम्य पाण्डवान् ।जघान पृष्ठतः सेनां जयगृध्रः प्रतापवान् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatastu saubalo rājannabhyatikramya pāṇḍavān ,jaghāna pṛṣṭhataḥ senāṁ jayagṛdhraḥ pratāpavān.
english: Then, O King, the mighty son of Subala (Shakuni), greedy for victory, having bypassed the Pāṇḍavas, attacked the army from the rear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 38. Then, O King, the mighty son of Subala (Shakuni), greedy for victory, having bypassed the Pāṇḍavas, attacked the army from the rear.

sanskrit: अश्वारोहास्तु संरब्धाः पाण्डवानां तरस्विनाम् ।प्राविशन्सौबलानीकमभ्यतिक्रम्य तान्रथान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: aśvārohāstu saṁrabdhāḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ tarasvinām ,prāviśansaubalānīkamabhyatikramya tānrathān.
english: The enraged cavalrymen of the mighty Pāṇḍavas, having bypassed those chariots, entered Saubala's army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 39. The enraged cavalrymen of the mighty Pāṇḍavas, having bypassed those chariots, entered Saubala's army.

sanskrit: ते तत्र सादिनः शूराः सौबलस्य महद्बलम् ।गजमध्येऽवतिष्ठन्तः शरवर्षैरवाकिरन् ॥४०॥
transliteration: te tatra sādinaḥ śūrāḥ saubalasya mahadbalam ,gajamadhye'vatiṣṭhantaḥ śaravarṣairavākiran.
