english: Standing there amidst the elephants of Saubala's great army, those brave horsemen showered (the enemy) with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 40. Standing there amidst the elephants of Saubala's great army, those brave horsemen showered (the enemy) with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: तदुद्यतगदाप्रासमकापुरुषसेवितम् ।प्रावर्तत महद्युद्धं राजन्दुर्मन्त्रिते तव ॥४१॥
transliteration: tadudyatagadāprāsamakāpuruṣasevitam ,prāvartata mahadyuddhaṁ rājandurmantrite tava.
english: O King, that great battle, where maces and javelins were raised and which was not a place for cowards, proceeded due to your ill counsel.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 41. O King, that great battle, where maces and javelins were raised and which was not a place for cowards, proceeded due to your ill counsel.

sanskrit: उपारमन्त ज्याशब्दाः प्रेक्षका रथिनोऽभवन् ।न हि स्वेषां परेषां वा विशेषः प्रत्यदृश्यत ॥४२॥
transliteration: upāramanta jyāśabdāḥ prekṣakā rathino'bhavan ,na hi sveṣāṁ pareṣāṁ vā viśeṣaḥ pratyadṛśyata.
english: The sounds of bowstrings ceased, and the charioteers became mere spectators. Indeed, no distinction could be seen between their own (men) or the enemy's.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 42. The sounds of bowstrings ceased, and the charioteers became mere spectators. Indeed, no distinction could be seen between their own (men) or the enemy's.

sanskrit: शूरबाहुविसृष्टानां शक्तीनां भरतर्षभ ।ज्योतिषामिव संपातमपश्यन्कुरुपाण्डवाः ॥४३॥
transliteration: śūrabāhuvisṛṣṭānāṁ śaktīnāṁ bharatarṣabha ,jyotiṣāmiva saṁpātamapaśyankurupāṇḍavāḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, the Kauravas and Pandavas saw the spears (śakti), hurled by the mighty arms of warriors, falling like a shower of stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 43. O best among the Bharatas, the Kauravas and Pandavas saw the spears (śakti), hurled by the mighty arms of warriors, falling like a shower of stars.

sanskrit: ऋष्टिभिर्विमलाभिश्च तत्र तत्र विशां पते ।संपतन्तीभिराकाशमावृतं बह्वशोभत ॥४४॥
transliteration: ṛṣṭibhirvimalābhiśca tatra tatra viśāṁ pate ,saṁpatantībhirākāśamāvṛtaṁ bahvaśobhata.
english: And, O lord of the people, the sky, covered by the bright lances falling everywhere, appeared exceedingly splendid.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 44. And, O lord of the people, the sky, covered by the bright lances falling everywhere, appeared exceedingly splendid.

sanskrit: प्रासानां पततां राजन्रूपमासीत्समन्ततः ।शलभानामिवाकाशे तदा भरतसत्तम ॥४५॥
transliteration: prāsānāṁ patatāṁ rājanrūpamāsītsamantataḥ ,śalabhānāmivākāśe tadā bharatasattama.
english: O King, O best among the Bharatas, at that time, the sight of falling darts was everywhere, resembling a swarm of locusts in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 45. O King, O best among the Bharatas, at that time, the sight of falling darts was everywhere, resembling a swarm of locusts in the sky.

sanskrit: रुधिरोक्षितसर्वाङ्गा विप्रविद्धैर्नियन्तृभिः ।हयाः परिपतन्ति स्म शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥४६॥
transliteration: rudhirokṣitasarvāṅgā vipraviddhairniyantṛbhiḥ ,hayāḥ paripatanti sma śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Their entire bodies drenched in blood, with their drivers struck down, hundreds and thousands of horses fell or ran wildly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 46. Their entire bodies drenched in blood, with their drivers struck down, hundreds and thousands of horses fell or ran wildly.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यपरिपिष्टाश्च समासाद्य परस्परम् ।अविक्षताः स्म दृश्यन्ते वमन्तो रुधिरं मुखैः ॥४७॥
transliteration: anyonyaparipiṣṭāśca samāsādya parasparam ,avikṣatāḥ sma dṛśyante vamanto rudhiraṁ mukhaiḥ.
english: Even after clashing violently and being crushed against one another, they appeared externally uninjured, though they were vomiting blood from their mouths.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 47. Even after clashing violently and being crushed against one another, they appeared externally uninjured, though they were vomiting blood from their mouths.

sanskrit: ततोऽभवत्तमो घोरं सैन्येन रजसा वृते ।तानपाक्रमतोऽद्राक्षं तस्माद्देशादरिंदमान् ।अश्वान्राजन्मनुष्यांश्च रजसा संवृते सति ॥४८॥
transliteration: tato'bhavattamo ghoraṁ sainyena rajasā vṛte ,tānapākramato'drākṣaṁ tasmāddeśādariṁdamān ,aśvānrājanmanuṣyāṁśca rajasā saṁvṛte sati.
english: Then, a terrible darkness enveloped everything as the army was covered in dust. O King (rājan), subduer of enemies (ariṃdaman), I saw horses and men retreating from that place, with the entire area obscured by the dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 48. Then, a terrible darkness enveloped everything as the army was covered in dust. O King (rājan), subduer of enemies (ariṃdaman), I saw horses and men retreating from that place, with the entire area obscured by the dust.

sanskrit: भूमौ निपतिताश्चान्ये वमन्तो रुधिरं बहु ।केशाकेशिसमालग्ना न शेकुश्चेष्टितुं जनाः ॥४९॥
transliteration: bhūmau nipatitāścānye vamanto rudhiraṁ bahu ,keśākeśisamālagnā na śekuśceṣṭituṁ janāḥ.
english: And others had fallen to the ground, vomiting copious amounts of blood. People, closely grappling with each other, hair-to-hair, were unable to move.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 49. And others had fallen to the ground, vomiting copious amounts of blood. People, closely grappling with each other, hair-to-hair, were unable to move.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यमश्वपृष्ठेभ्यो विकर्षन्तो महाबलाः ।मल्ला इव समासाद्य निजघ्नुरितरेतरम् ।अश्वैश्च व्यपकृष्यन्त बहवोऽत्र गतासवः ॥५०॥
transliteration: anyonyamaśvapṛṣṭhebhyo vikarṣanto mahābalāḥ ,mallā iva samāsādya nijaghnuritaretaram ,aśvaiśca vyapakṛṣyanta bahavo'tra gatāsavaḥ.
english: The mighty warriors, pulling each other from horseback, engaged in combat like wrestlers and struck one another down. Many, whose lives had departed (gatāsavaḥ), were then dragged away by horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 50. The mighty warriors, pulling each other from horseback, engaged in combat like wrestlers and struck one another down. Many, whose lives had departed (gatāsavaḥ), were then dragged away by horses.

sanskrit: भूमौ निपतिताश्चान्ये बहवो विजयैषिणः ।तत्र तत्र व्यदृश्यन्त पुरुषाः शूरमानिनः ॥५१॥
transliteration: bhūmau nipatitāścānye bahavo vijayaiṣiṇaḥ ,tatra tatra vyadṛśyanta puruṣāḥ śūramāninaḥ.
english: Many other men, who had desired victory and considered themselves heroes, were seen fallen here and there on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 51. Many other men, who had desired victory and considered themselves heroes, were seen fallen here and there on the ground.

sanskrit: रक्तोक्षितैश्छिन्नभुजैरपकृष्टशिरोरुहैः ।व्यदृश्यत मही कीर्णा शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥५२॥
transliteration: raktokṣitaiśchinnabhujairapakṛṣṭaśiroruhaiḥ ,vyadṛśyata mahī kīrṇā śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: The earth was seen strewn with hundreds and thousands of bodies that were soaked in blood, had severed arms, and had their hair torn out.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 52. The earth was seen strewn with hundreds and thousands of bodies that were soaked in blood, had severed arms, and had their hair torn out.

sanskrit: दूरं न शक्यं तत्रासीद्गन्तुमश्वेन केनचित् ।साश्वारोहैर्हतैरश्वैरावृते वसुधातले ॥५३॥
transliteration: dūraṁ na śakyaṁ tatrāsīdgantumaśvena kenacit ,sāśvārohairhatairaśvairāvṛte vasudhātale.
english: It was not possible for any horse to go far there, because the surface of the earth was covered with dead horses, along with their riders.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 53. It was not possible for any horse to go far there, because the surface of the earth was covered with dead horses, along with their riders.

sanskrit: रुधिरोक्षितसंनाहैरात्तशस्त्रैरुदायुधैः ।नानाप्रहरणैर्घोरैः परस्परवधैषिभिः ।सुसंनिकृष्टैः संग्रामे हतभूयिष्ठसैनिकैः ॥५४॥
transliteration: rudhirokṣitasaṁnāhairāttaśastrairudāyudhaiḥ ,nānāpraharaṇairghoraiḥ parasparavadhaiṣibhiḥ ,susaṁnikṛṣṭaiḥ saṁgrāme hatabhūyiṣṭhasainikaiḥ.
english: The battlefield was covered with warriors whose armor was soaked in blood, who had grasped their weapons and held them aloft, wielding various dreadful arms; they were intent on mutual slaughter, had fought in very close proximity, and their ranks were predominantly slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 54. The battlefield was covered with warriors whose armor was soaked in blood, who had grasped their weapons and held them aloft, wielding various dreadful arms; they were intent on mutual slaughter, had fought in very close proximity, and their ranks were predominantly slain.

sanskrit: स मुहूर्तं ततो युद्ध्वा सौबलोऽथ विशां पते ।षट्सहस्रैर्हयैः शिष्टैरपायाच्छकुनिस्ततः ॥५५॥
transliteration: sa muhūrtaṁ tato yuddhvā saubalo'tha viśāṁ pate ,ṣaṭsahasrairhayaiḥ śiṣṭairapāyācchakunistataḥ.
english: O lord of the people, Saubala (Shakuni), after fighting for a short while, then Shakuni departed from that place with his remaining six thousand horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 55. O lord of the people, Saubala (Shakuni), after fighting for a short while, then Shakuni departed from that place with his remaining six thousand horses.

sanskrit: तथैव पाण्डवानीकं रुधिरेण समुक्षितम् ।षट्सहस्रैर्हयैः शिष्टैरपायाच्छ्रान्तवाहनम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: tathaiva pāṇḍavānīkaṁ rudhireṇa samukṣitam ,ṣaṭsahasrairhayaiḥ śiṣṭairapāyācchrāntavāhanam.
english: Similarly, the Pandava army, drenched in blood and with exhausted mounts, withdrew with its remaining six thousand horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 56. Similarly, the Pandava army, drenched in blood and with exhausted mounts, withdrew with its remaining six thousand horses.

sanskrit: अश्वारोहास्तु पाण्डूनामब्रुवन्रुधिरोक्षिताः ।सुसंनिकृष्टाः संग्रामे भूयिष्ठं त्यक्तजीविताः ॥५७॥
transliteration: aśvārohāstu pāṇḍūnāmabruvanrudhirokṣitāḥ ,susaṁnikṛṣṭāḥ saṁgrāme bhūyiṣṭhaṁ tyaktajīvitāḥ.
english: But the horsemen of the Pandavas, drenched in blood, who were fighting in close proximity, and had for the most part given up their lives, spoke as follows:
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 57. But the horsemen of the Pandavas, drenched in blood, who were fighting in close proximity, and had for the most part given up their lives, spoke as follows:

sanskrit: नेह शक्यं रथैर्योद्धुं कुत एव महागजैः ।रथानेव रथा यान्तु कुञ्जराः कुञ्जरानपि ॥५८॥
transliteration: neha śakyaṁ rathairyoddhuṁ kuta eva mahāgajaiḥ ,rathāneva rathā yāntu kuñjarāḥ kuñjarānapi.
english: Here, it is not possible to fight effectively with chariots, let alone with great elephants. Therefore, let chariots engage only with chariots, and elephants with elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 58. Here, it is not possible to fight effectively with chariots, let alone with great elephants. Therefore, let chariots engage only with chariots, and elephants with elephants.

sanskrit: प्रतियातो हि शकुनिः स्वमनीकमवस्थितः ।न पुनः सौबलो राजा युद्धमभ्यागमिष्यति ॥५९॥
transliteration: pratiyāto hi śakuniḥ svamanīkamavasthitaḥ ,na punaḥ saubalo rājā yuddhamabhyāgamiṣyati.
english: Shakuni, indeed, returned and positioned himself with his own army. King Saubala will surely not come to battle again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 59. Shakuni, indeed, returned and positioned himself with his own army. King Saubala will surely not come to battle again.

sanskrit: ततस्तु द्रौपदेयाश्च ते च मत्ता महाद्विपाः ।प्रययुर्यत्र पाञ्चाल्यो धृष्टद्युम्नो महारथः ॥६०॥
transliteration: tatastu draupadeyāśca te ca mattā mahādvipāḥ ,prayayuryatra pāñcālyo dhṛṣṭadyumno mahārathaḥ.
english: Then, the sons of Draupadi, along with those furious great elephants, proceeded to where Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the great charioteer and prince of Pañcāla, stood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 60. Then, the sons of Draupadi, along with those furious great elephants, proceeded to where Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the great charioteer and prince of Pañcāla, stood.

sanskrit: सहदेवोऽपि कौरव्य रजोमेघे समुत्थिते ।एकाकी प्रययौ तत्र यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥६१॥
transliteration: sahadevo'pi kauravya rajomeghe samutthite ,ekākī prayayau tatra yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: O Kauravya, when the dust cloud arose, Sahadeva also went forth alone to where King Yudhiṣṭhira was.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 61. O Kauravya, when the dust cloud arose, Sahadeva also went forth alone to where King Yudhiṣṭhira was.

sanskrit: ततस्तेषु प्रयातेषु शकुनिः सौबलः पुनः ।पार्श्वतोऽभ्यहनत्क्रुद्धो धृष्टद्युम्नस्य वाहिनीम् ॥६२॥
transliteration: tatasteṣu prayāteṣu śakuniḥ saubalaḥ punaḥ ,pārśvato'bhyahanatkruddho dhṛṣṭadyumnasya vāhinīm.
english: Then, after those (soldiers) had departed, Shakuni, the son of Subala, enraged, once again attacked Dhṛṣṭadyumna's army from the flank.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 62. Then, after those (soldiers) had departed, Shakuni, the son of Subala, enraged, once again attacked Dhṛṣṭadyumna's army from the flank.

sanskrit: तत्पुनस्तुमुलं युद्धं प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वाभ्यवर्तत ।तावकानां परेषां च परस्परवधैषिणाम् ॥६३॥
transliteration: tatpunastumulaṁ yuddhaṁ prāṇāṁstyaktvābhyavartata ,tāvakānāṁ pareṣāṁ ca parasparavadhaiṣiṇām.
english: Again, that tumultuous battle recommenced, with both your side and the enemy's side, intent on mutual slaughter, giving up their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 63. Again, that tumultuous battle recommenced, with both your side and the enemy's side, intent on mutual slaughter, giving up their lives.

sanskrit: ते ह्यन्योन्यमवेक्षन्त तस्मिन्वीरसमागमे ।योधाः पर्यपतन्राजञ्शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥६४॥
transliteration: te hyanyonyamavekṣanta tasminvīrasamāgame ,yodhāḥ paryapatanrājañśataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Indeed, in that gathering of heroes, they faced each other, and warriors, O King, fell by hundreds and then by thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 64. Indeed, in that gathering of heroes, they faced each other, and warriors, O King, fell by hundreds and then by thousands.

sanskrit: असिभिश्छिद्यमानानां शिरसां लोकसंक्षये ।प्रादुरासीन्महाशब्दस्तालानां पततामिव ॥६५॥
transliteration: asibhiśchidyamānānāṁ śirasāṁ lokasaṁkṣaye ,prādurāsīnmahāśabdastālānāṁ patatāmiva.
english: During that cataclysmic destruction (lokasaṃkṣaya), as heads were being severed by swords, a tremendous sound emerged, resembling that of falling palm trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 65. During that cataclysmic destruction (lokasaṃkṣaya), as heads were being severed by swords, a tremendous sound emerged, resembling that of falling palm trees.

sanskrit: विमुक्तानां शरीराणां भिन्नानां पततां भुवि ।सायुधानां च बाहूनामुरूणां च विशां पते ।आसीत्कटकटाशब्दः सुमहान्रोमहर्षणः ॥६६॥
transliteration: vimuktānāṁ śarīrāṇāṁ bhinnānāṁ patatāṁ bhuvi ,sāyudhānāṁ ca bāhūnāmurūṇāṁ ca viśāṁ pate ,āsītkaṭakaṭāśabdaḥ sumahānromaharṣaṇaḥ.
english: O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), a tremendous and hair-raising "kaṭakaṭa" sound arose from the separated bodies, the shattered limbs falling to the ground, and from arms still grasping weapons, as well as from thighs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 66. O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), a tremendous and hair-raising "kaṭakaṭa" sound arose from the separated bodies, the shattered limbs falling to the ground, and from arms still grasping weapons, as well as from thighs.

sanskrit: निघ्नन्तो निशितैः शस्त्रैर्भ्रातॄन्पुत्रान्सखीनपि ।योधाः परिपतन्ति स्म यथामिषकृते खगाः ॥६७॥
transliteration: nighnanto niśitaiḥ śastrairbhrātṝnputrānsakhīnapi ,yodhāḥ paripatanti sma yathāmiṣakṛte khagāḥ.
english: The warriors rushed about, striking down brothers, sons, and even friends with their sharp weapons, just as birds swoop down for a piece of meat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 67. The warriors rushed about, striking down brothers, sons, and even friends with their sharp weapons, just as birds swoop down for a piece of meat.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं प्रतिसंरब्धाः समासाद्य परस्परम् ।अहं पूर्वमहं पूर्वमिति न्यघ्नन्सहस्रशः ॥६८॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ pratisaṁrabdhāḥ samāsādya parasparam ,ahaṁ pūrvamahaṁ pūrvamiti nyaghnansahasraśaḥ.
english: Enraged at each other, they met face to face and struck down thousands, each one shouting, "I go first! I go first!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 68. Enraged at each other, they met face to face and struck down thousands, each one shouting, "I go first! I go first!"

sanskrit: संघातैरासनभ्रष्टैरश्वारोहैर्गतासुभिः ।हयाः परिपतन्ति स्म शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥६९॥
transliteration: saṁghātairāsanabhraṣṭairaśvārohairgatāsubhiḥ ,hayāḥ paripatanti sma śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: The horses rushed about in hundreds and thousands, amidst the masses of dead horsemen who had fallen from their saddles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 69. The horses rushed about in hundreds and thousands, amidst the masses of dead horsemen who had fallen from their saddles.

sanskrit: स्फुरतां प्रतिपिष्टानामश्वानां शीघ्रसारिणाम् ।स्तनतां च मनुष्याणां संनद्धानां विशां पते ॥७०॥
transliteration: sphuratāṁ pratipiṣṭānāmaśvānāṁ śīghrasāriṇām ,stanatāṁ ca manuṣyāṇāṁ saṁnaddhānāṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), (there was the sound/sight) of horses struggling and being crushed, of swiftly moving horses, and of groaning men who were armed for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 70. O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), (there was the sound/sight) of horses struggling and being crushed, of swiftly moving horses, and of groaning men who were armed for battle.

sanskrit: शक्त्यृष्टिप्रासशब्दश्च तुमुलः समजायत ।भिन्दतां परमर्माणि राजन्दुर्मन्त्रिते तव ॥७१॥
transliteration: śaktyṛṣṭiprāsaśabdaśca tumulaḥ samajāyata ,bhindatāṁ paramarmāṇi rājandurmantrite tava.
english: O King, because of your poor counsel, a tumultuous sound arose from spears, lances, and javelins piercing the vital spots of the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 71. O King, because of your poor counsel, a tumultuous sound arose from spears, lances, and javelins piercing the vital spots of the enemies.

sanskrit: श्रमाभिभूताः संरब्धाः श्रान्तवाहाः पिपासिताः ।विक्षताश्च शितैः शस्त्रैरभ्यवर्तन्त तावकाः ॥७२॥
transliteration: śramābhibhūtāḥ saṁrabdhāḥ śrāntavāhāḥ pipāsitāḥ ,vikṣatāśca śitaiḥ śastrairabhyavartanta tāvakāḥ.
english: Your men, overcome by exhaustion, enraged, with weary mounts, thirsty, and wounded by sharp weapons, were pressed upon (or engaged in fierce battle).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 72. Your men, overcome by exhaustion, enraged, with weary mounts, thirsty, and wounded by sharp weapons, were pressed upon (or engaged in fierce battle).

sanskrit: मत्ता रुधिरगन्धेन बहवोऽत्र विचेतसः ।जघ्नुः परान्स्वकांश्चैव प्राप्तान्प्राप्ताननन्तरान् ॥७३॥
transliteration: mattā rudhiragandhena bahavo'tra vicetasaḥ ,jaghnuḥ parānsvakāṁścaiva prāptānprāptānanantarān.
english: Here, many, maddened by the smell of blood and out of their minds, killed enemies and even their own nearby men, whoever came close.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 73. Here, many, maddened by the smell of blood and out of their minds, killed enemies and even their own nearby men, whoever came close.

sanskrit: बहवश्च गतप्राणाः क्षत्रिया जयगृद्धिनः ।भूमावभ्यपतन्राजञ्शरवृष्टिभिरावृताः ॥७४॥
transliteration: bahavaśca gataprāṇāḥ kṣatriyā jayagṛddhinaḥ ,bhūmāvabhyapatanrājañśaravṛṣṭibhirāvṛtāḥ.
english: O King, many kṣatriya warriors, eager for victory, overwhelmed by showers of arrows, fell lifeless onto the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 74. O King, many kṣatriya warriors, eager for victory, overwhelmed by showers of arrows, fell lifeless onto the ground.

sanskrit: वृकगृध्रशृगालानां तुमुले मोदनेऽहनि ।आसीद्बलक्षयो घोरस्तव पुत्रस्य पश्यतः ॥७५॥
transliteration: vṛkagṛdhraśṛgālānāṁ tumule modane'hani ,āsīdbalakṣayo ghorastava putrasya paśyataḥ.
english: On that dreadful day, which brought joy to wolves, vultures, and jackals, a terrible destruction of your son's forces occurred while he watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 75. On that dreadful day, which brought joy to wolves, vultures, and jackals, a terrible destruction of your son's forces occurred while he watched.

sanskrit: नराश्वकायसंछन्ना भूमिरासीद्विशां पते ।रुधिरोदकचित्रा च भीरूणां भयवर्धिनी ॥७६॥
transliteration: narāśvakāyasaṁchannā bhūmirāsīdviśāṁ pate ,rudhirodakacitrā ca bhīrūṇāṁ bhayavardhinī.
english: O lord of the people, the ground became covered with the bodies of men and horses. It was stained with blood and water, intensifying the fear of the timid.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 76. O lord of the people, the ground became covered with the bodies of men and horses. It was stained with blood and water, intensifying the fear of the timid.

sanskrit: असिभिः पट्टिशैः शूलैस्तक्षमाणाः पुनः पुनः ।तावकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव नाभ्यवर्तन्त भारत ॥७७॥
transliteration: asibhiḥ paṭṭiśaiḥ śūlaistakṣamāṇāḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,tāvakāḥ pāṇḍavāścaiva nābhyavartanta bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, even while being repeatedly hewn down by swords, pattiśas (spears), and lances, your warriors and the Pāṇḍavas did not retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 77. O Bhārata, even while being repeatedly hewn down by swords, pattiśas (spears), and lances, your warriors and the Pāṇḍavas did not retreat.

sanskrit: प्रहरन्तो यथाशक्ति यावत्प्राणस्य धारणम् ।योधाः परिपतन्ति स्म वमन्तो रुधिरं व्रणैः ॥७८॥
transliteration: praharanto yathāśakti yāvatprāṇasya dhāraṇam ,yodhāḥ paripatanti sma vamanto rudhiraṁ vraṇaiḥ.
english: The warriors, striking with all their might for as long as their life (prāṇa) could be sustained, fell down, gushing blood from their wounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 78. The warriors, striking with all their might for as long as their life (prāṇa) could be sustained, fell down, gushing blood from their wounds.

sanskrit: शिरो गृहीत्वा केशेषु कबन्धः समदृश्यत ।उद्यम्य निशितं खड्गं रुधिरेण समुक्षितम् ॥७९॥
transliteration: śiro gṛhītvā keśeṣu kabandhaḥ samadṛśyata ,udyamya niśitaṁ khaḍgaṁ rudhireṇa samukṣitam.
english: A headless torso (kabandha) was seen, having seized a head by its hair and brandishing a sharp sword drenched with blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 79. A headless torso (kabandha) was seen, having seized a head by its hair and brandishing a sharp sword drenched with blood.

sanskrit: अथोत्थितेषु बहुषु कबन्धेषु जनाधिप ।तथा रुधिरगन्धेन योधाः कश्मलमाविशन् ॥८०॥
transliteration: athotthiteṣu bahuṣu kabandheṣu janādhipa ,tathā rudhiragandhena yodhāḥ kaśmalamāviśan.
english: O ruler of men, when many headless torsos (kabandha) rose up, the warriors were thus seized by confusion (kaśmala) due to the smell of blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 80. O ruler of men, when many headless torsos (kabandha) rose up, the warriors were thus seized by confusion (kaśmala) due to the smell of blood.

sanskrit: मन्दीभूते ततः शब्दे पाण्डवानां महद्बलम् ।अल्पावशिष्टैस्तुरगैरभ्यवर्तत सौबलः ॥८१॥
transliteration: mandībhūte tataḥ śabde pāṇḍavānāṁ mahadbalam ,alpāvaśiṣṭaisturagairabhyavartata saubalaḥ.
english: Then, when the sound had subsided, Saubala (Śakuni) attacked the mighty army of the Pāṇḍavas with his few remaining horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 81. Then, when the sound had subsided, Saubala (Śakuni) attacked the mighty army of the Pāṇḍavas with his few remaining horses.

sanskrit: ततोऽभ्यधावंस्त्वरिताः पाण्डवा जयगृद्धिनः ।पदातयश्च नागाश्च सादिनश्चोद्यतायुधाः ॥८२॥
transliteration: tato'bhyadhāvaṁstvaritāḥ pāṇḍavā jayagṛddhinaḥ ,padātayaśca nāgāśca sādinaścodyatāyudhāḥ.
english: Then the Pāṇḍavas, swift and eager for victory, rushed forward. And (with them) were foot soldiers, elephants, and cavalry with uplifted weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 82. Then the Pāṇḍavas, swift and eager for victory, rushed forward. And (with them) were foot soldiers, elephants, and cavalry with uplifted weapons.

sanskrit: कोष्टकीकृत्य चाप्येनं परिक्षिप्य च सर्वशः ।शस्त्रैर्नानाविधैर्जघ्नुर्युद्धपारं तितीर्षवः ॥८३॥
transliteration: koṣṭakīkṛtya cāpyenaṁ parikṣipya ca sarvaśaḥ ,śastrairnānāvidhairjaghnuryuddhapāraṁ titīrṣavaḥ.
english: Having surrounded him and completely encircled him from all sides, they struck him with various kinds of weapons, eager to bring the battle to an end.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 83. Having surrounded him and completely encircled him from all sides, they struck him with various kinds of weapons, eager to bring the battle to an end.

sanskrit: त्वदीयास्तांस्तु संप्रेक्ष्य सर्वतः समभिद्रुतान् ।साश्वपत्तिद्विपरथाः पाण्डवानभिदुद्रुवुः ॥८४॥
transliteration: tvadīyāstāṁstu saṁprekṣya sarvataḥ samabhidrutān ,sāśvapattidviparathāḥ pāṇḍavānabhidudruvuḥ.
english: Then your men, having thoroughly observed those Pāṇḍavas being assailed from all directions, rushed upon the Pāṇḍavas with their forces of horses, infantry, elephants, and chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 84. Then your men, having thoroughly observed those Pāṇḍavas being assailed from all directions, rushed upon the Pāṇḍavas with their forces of horses, infantry, elephants, and chariots.

sanskrit: केचित्पदातयः पद्भिर्मुष्टिभिश्च परस्परम् ।निजघ्नुः समरे शूराः क्षीणशस्त्रास्ततोऽपतन् ॥८५॥
transliteration: kecitpadātayaḥ padbhirmuṣṭibhiśca parasparam ,nijaghnuḥ samare śūrāḥ kṣīṇaśastrāstato'patan.
english: Some brave foot-soldiers struck each other in battle with their feet and with their fists; then, their weapons being exhausted, they fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 85. Some brave foot-soldiers struck each other in battle with their feet and with their fists; then, their weapons being exhausted, they fell.

sanskrit: रथेभ्यो रथिनः पेतुर्द्विपेभ्यो हस्तिसादिनः ।विमानेभ्य इव भ्रष्टाः सिद्धाः पुण्यक्षयाद्यथा ॥८६॥
transliteration: rathebhyo rathinaḥ peturdvipebhyo hastisādinaḥ ,vimānebhya iva bhraṣṭāḥ siddhāḥ puṇyakṣayādyathā.
english: Charioteers fell from their chariots, and elephant-riders from their elephants, just as perfected beings (siddhas), having fallen from their celestial vehicles (vimānas) due to the exhaustion of their accumulated good (karma) (puṇya), descend.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 86. Charioteers fell from their chariots, and elephant-riders from their elephants, just as perfected beings (siddhas), having fallen from their celestial vehicles (vimānas) due to the exhaustion of their accumulated good (karma) (puṇya), descend.

sanskrit: एवमन्योन्यमायस्ता योधा जघ्नुर्महामृधे ।पितॄन्भ्रातॄन्वयस्यांश्च पुत्रानपि तथापरे ॥८७॥
transliteration: evamanyonyamāyastā yodhā jaghnurmahāmṛdhe ,pitṝnbhrātṝnvayasyāṁśca putrānapi tathāpare.
english: Thus, in that great battle, warriors, mutually enraged, killed fathers, brothers, companions, and even sons; and others likewise.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 87. Thus, in that great battle, warriors, mutually enraged, killed fathers, brothers, companions, and even sons; and others likewise.

sanskrit: एवमासीदमर्यादं युद्धं भरतसत्तम ।प्रासासिबाणकलिले वर्तमाने सुदारुणे ॥८८॥
transliteration: evamāsīdamaryādaṁ yuddhaṁ bharatasattama ,prāsāsibāṇakalile vartamāne sudāruṇe.
english: Thus, O best of Bharatas, this battle was unrestrained, exceedingly dreadful, and raged amidst a chaotic mix of spears, swords, and arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 22 Verse 88. Thus, O best of Bharatas, this battle was unrestrained, exceedingly dreadful, and raged amidst a chaotic mix of spears, swords, and arrows.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तस्मिञ्शब्दे मृदौ जाते पाण्डवैर्निहते बले ।अश्वैः सप्तशतैः शिष्टैरुपावर्तत सौबलः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tasmiñśabde mṛdau jāte pāṇḍavairnihate bale ,aśvaiḥ saptaśataiḥ śiṣṭairupāvartata saubalaḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: As the tumultuous sound of battle subsided and the army was being destroyed by the Pāṇḍavas, the son of Subala (Śakuni) retreated with seven hundred remaining horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: As the tumultuous sound of battle subsided and the army was being destroyed by the Pāṇḍavas, the son of Subala (Śakuni) retreated with seven hundred remaining horses.

sanskrit: स यात्वा वाहिनीं तूर्णमब्रवीत्त्वरयन्युधि ।युध्यध्वमिति संहृष्टाः पुनः पुनररिंदमः ।अपृच्छत्क्षत्रियांस्तत्र क्व नु राजा महारथः ॥२॥
transliteration: sa yātvā vāhinīṁ tūrṇamabravīttvarayanyudhi ,yudhyadhvamiti saṁhṛṣṭāḥ punaḥ punarariṁdamaḥ ,apṛcchatkṣatriyāṁstatra kva nu rājā mahārathaḥ.
english: He, the subduer of enemies (Śakuni), having swiftly gone to the army, repeatedly urged (the warriors) in battle, saying, 'Fight!' (The warriors were) invigorated. There he asked the Kṣatriyas, 'Where now is the great charioteer king?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 2. He, the subduer of enemies (Śakuni), having swiftly gone to the army, repeatedly urged (the warriors) in battle, saying, 'Fight!' (The warriors were) invigorated. There he asked the Kṣatriyas, 'Where now is the great charioteer king?'

sanskrit: शकुनेस्तु वचः श्रुत्वा त ऊचुर्भरतर्षभ ।असौ तिष्ठति कौरव्यो रणमध्ये महारथः ॥३॥
transliteration: śakunestu vacaḥ śrutvā ta ūcurbharatarṣabha ,asau tiṣṭhati kauravyo raṇamadhye mahārathaḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, after hearing Śakuni's words, they said: "That Kuru warrior, a great charioteer, stands in the midst of the battle."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 3. O best among the Bharatas, after hearing Śakuni's words, they said: "That Kuru warrior, a great charioteer, stands in the midst of the battle."

sanskrit: यत्रैतत्सुमहच्छत्रं पूर्णचन्द्रसमप्रभम् ।यत्रैते सतलत्राणा रथास्तिष्ठन्ति दंशिताः ॥४॥
transliteration: yatraitatsumahacchatraṁ pūrṇacandrasamaprabham ,yatraite satalatrāṇā rathāstiṣṭhanti daṁśitāḥ.
english: Where this magnificent umbrella shines with the brilliance of the full moon, and where these chariots, equipped with bowstrings and fully armored, stand.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 4. Where this magnificent umbrella shines with the brilliance of the full moon, and where these chariots, equipped with bowstrings and fully armored, stand.

sanskrit: यत्रैष शब्दस्तुमुलः पर्जन्यनिनदोपमः ।तत्र गच्छ द्रुतं राजंस्ततो द्रक्ष्यसि कौरवम् ॥५॥
transliteration: yatraiṣa śabdastumulaḥ parjanyaninadopamaḥ ,tatra gaccha drutaṁ rājaṁstato drakṣyasi kauravam.
english: O king, go quickly to where this tumultuous sound, resembling the roar of thunder, emanates; there you will then see the Kuru warrior (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 5. O king, go quickly to where this tumultuous sound, resembling the roar of thunder, emanates; there you will then see the Kuru warrior (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु तैः शूरैः शकुनिः सौबलस्तदा ।प्रययौ तत्र यत्रासौ पुत्रस्तव नराधिप ।सर्वतः संवृतो वीरैः समरेष्वनिवर्तिभिः ॥६॥
transliteration: evamuktastu taiḥ śūraiḥ śakuniḥ saubalastadā ,prayayau tatra yatrāsau putrastava narādhipa ,sarvataḥ saṁvṛto vīraiḥ samareṣvanivartibhiḥ.
english: O king of men, after Śakuni, the son of Subala, was thus addressed by those heroes, he then proceeded to where your son (Duryodhana) was, surrounded on all sides by valiant warriors who never retreat in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 6. O king of men, after Śakuni, the son of Subala, was thus addressed by those heroes, he then proceeded to where your son (Duryodhana) was, surrounded on all sides by valiant warriors who never retreat in battles.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनं दृष्ट्वा रथानीके व्यवस्थितम् ।सरथांस्तावकान्सर्वान्हर्षयञ्शकुनिस्ततः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaṁ dṛṣṭvā rathānīke vyavasthitam ,sarathāṁstāvakānsarvānharṣayañśakunistataḥ.
english: Then, seeing Duryodhana arrayed in the chariot formation, Shakuni, in turn, began gladdening all your chariot-mounted warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 7. Then, seeing Duryodhana arrayed in the chariot formation, Shakuni, in turn, began gladdening all your chariot-mounted warriors.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनमिदं वाक्यं हृष्टरूपो विशां पते ।कृतकार्यमिवात्मानं मन्यमानोऽब्रवीन्नृपम् ॥८॥
transliteration: duryodhanamidaṁ vākyaṁ hṛṣṭarūpo viśāṁ pate ,kṛtakāryamivātmānaṁ manyamāno'bravīnnṛpam.
english: O lord of the people (viśām pate), Shakuni, joyful in appearance and considering himself (ātman) to have accomplished his task, spoke this statement to King Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 8. O lord of the people (viśām pate), Shakuni, joyful in appearance and considering himself (ātman) to have accomplished his task, spoke this statement to King Duryodhana.

sanskrit: जहि राजन्रथानीकमश्वाः सर्वे जिता मया ।नात्यक्त्वा जीवितं संख्ये शक्यो जेतुं युधिष्ठिरः ॥९॥
transliteration: jahi rājanrathānīkamaśvāḥ sarve jitā mayā ,nātyaktvā jīvitaṁ saṁkhye śakyo jetuṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: "O King, destroy the chariot array! All their horses have been conquered by me. Yudhishthira cannot be defeated in battle without abandoning one's own life."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 9. "O King, destroy the chariot array! All their horses have been conquered by me. Yudhishthira cannot be defeated in battle without abandoning one's own life."

sanskrit: हते तस्मिन्रथानीके पाण्डवेनाभिपालिते ।गजानेतान्हनिष्यामः पदातींश्चेतरांस्तथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: hate tasminrathānīke pāṇḍavenābhipālite ,gajānetānhaniṣyāmaḥ padātīṁścetarāṁstathā.
english: Once that chariot formation, protected by the Pandava (pāṇḍava), is destroyed, we will then kill these elephants as well as the other foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 10. Once that chariot formation, protected by the Pandava (pāṇḍava), is destroyed, we will then kill these elephants as well as the other foot soldiers.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु वचनं तस्य तावका जयगृद्धिनः ।जवेनाभ्यपतन्हृष्टाः पाण्डवानामनीकिनीम् ॥११॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu vacanaṁ tasya tāvakā jayagṛddhinaḥ ,javenābhyapatanhṛṣṭāḥ pāṇḍavānāmanīkinīm.
english: Having heard his words, your men, greedy for victory and delighted, swiftly rushed upon the army of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 11. Having heard his words, your men, greedy for victory and delighted, swiftly rushed upon the army of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: सर्वे विवृततूणीराः प्रगृहीतशरासनाः ।शरासनानि धुन्वानाः सिंहनादं प्रचक्रिरे ॥१२॥
transliteration: sarve vivṛtatūṇīrāḥ pragṛhītaśarāsanāḥ ,śarāsanāni dhunvānāḥ siṁhanādaṁ pracakrire.
english: All of them, with their quivers open and bows grasped, twanging their bows, let out a lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 12. All of them, with their quivers open and bows grasped, twanging their bows, let out a lion's roar.

sanskrit: ततो ज्यातलनिर्घोषः पुनरासीद्विशां पते ।प्रादुरासीच्छराणां च सुमुक्तानां सुदारुणः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato jyātalanirghoṣaḥ punarāsīdviśāṁ pate ,prādurāsīccharāṇāṁ ca sumuktānāṁ sudāruṇaḥ.
english: Then again, O lord of men, there was the loud sound of bowstrings, and also the exceedingly dreadful sound of arrows well-released.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 13. Then again, O lord of men, there was the loud sound of bowstrings, and also the exceedingly dreadful sound of arrows well-released.

sanskrit: तान्समीपगतान्दृष्ट्वा जवेनोद्यतकार्मुकान् ।उवाच देवकीपुत्रं कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tānsamīpagatāndṛṣṭvā javenodyatakārmukān ,uvāca devakīputraṁ kuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Having seen those (warriors) who had swiftly approached with their bows raised, Dhanañjaya, the son of Kuntī, spoke to Devakī's son (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 14. Having seen those (warriors) who had swiftly approached with their bows raised, Dhanañjaya, the son of Kuntī, spoke to Devakī's son (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: चोदयाश्वानसंभ्रान्तः प्रविशैतद्बलार्णवम् ।अन्तमद्य गमिष्यामि शत्रूणां निशितैः शरैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: codayāśvānasaṁbhrāntaḥ praviśaitadbalārṇavam ,antamadya gamiṣyāmi śatrūṇāṁ niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Drive the horses without confusion; enter this ocean of forces. Today, I will reach the end of the enemies with sharpened arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 15. Drive the horses without confusion; enter this ocean of forces. Today, I will reach the end of the enemies with sharpened arrows.

sanskrit: अष्टादश दिनान्यद्य युद्धस्यास्य जनार्दन ।वर्तमानस्य महतः समासाद्य परस्परम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: aṣṭādaśa dinānyadya yuddhasyāsya janārdana ,vartamānasya mahataḥ samāsādya parasparam.
english: Eighteen days have passed today, Janārdana, of this great war which has been occurring mutually.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 16. Eighteen days have passed today, Janārdana, of this great war which has been occurring mutually.

sanskrit: अनन्तकल्पा ध्वजिनी भूत्वा ह्येषां महात्मनाम् ।क्षयमद्य गता युद्धे पश्य दैवं यथाविधम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: anantakalpā dhvajinī bhūtvā hyeṣāṁ mahātmanām ,kṣayamadya gatā yuddhe paśya daivaṁ yathāvidham.
english: Having been an immeasurable (anantakalpa) army for these great souls (mahātman), today it has met its destruction in battle. Behold fate (daiva) as it is!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 17. Having been an immeasurable (anantakalpa) army for these great souls (mahātman), today it has met its destruction in battle. Behold fate (daiva) as it is!

sanskrit: समुद्रकल्पं तु बलं धार्तराष्ट्रस्य माधव ।अस्मानासाद्य संजातं गोष्पदोपममच्युत ॥१८॥
transliteration: samudrakalpaṁ tu balaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya mādhava ,asmānāsādya saṁjātaṁ goṣpadopamamacyuta.
english: But the army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, which was like an ocean (samudrakalpa), Madhava, has become like a cow's hoof-print (goṣpadopama) after encountering us, Acyuta.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 18. But the army of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, which was like an ocean (samudrakalpa), Madhava, has become like a cow's hoof-print (goṣpadopama) after encountering us, Acyuta.

sanskrit: हते भीष्मे च संदध्याच्छिवं स्यादिह माधव ।न च तत्कृतवान्मूढो धार्तराष्ट्रः सुबालिशः ॥१९॥
transliteration: hate bhīṣme ca saṁdadhyācchivaṁ syādiha mādhava ,na ca tatkṛtavānmūḍho dhārtarāṣṭraḥ subāliśaḥ.
english: O Madhava, even after Bhishma was slain, peace could have been established, and auspiciousness would have ensued here. But that foolish and utterly childish son of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭra) did not do that.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 19. O Madhava, even after Bhishma was slain, peace could have been established, and auspiciousness would have ensued here. But that foolish and utterly childish son of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭra) did not do that.

sanskrit: उक्तं भीष्मेण यद्वाक्यं हितं पथ्यं च माधव ।तच्चापि नासौ कृतवान्वीतबुद्धिः सुयोधनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: uktaṁ bhīṣmeṇa yadvākyaṁ hitaṁ pathyaṁ ca mādhava ,taccāpi nāsau kṛtavānvītabuddhiḥ suyodhanaḥ.
english: O Madhava, even that beneficial and wholesome advice which was spoken by Bhishma, that Suyodhana, being utterly devoid of intelligence, also did not follow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 20. O Madhava, even that beneficial and wholesome advice which was spoken by Bhishma, that Suyodhana, being utterly devoid of intelligence, also did not follow.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु पतिते भीष्मे प्रच्युते पृथिवीतले ।न जाने कारणं किं नु येन युद्धमवर्तत ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu patite bhīṣme pracyute pṛthivītale ,na jāne kāraṇaṁ kiṁ nu yena yuddhamavartata.
english: But when that Bhishma had fallen, cast down upon the surface of the earth, I truly do not know what cause there was by which the battle (yuddha) continued.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 21. But when that Bhishma had fallen, cast down upon the surface of the earth, I truly do not know what cause there was by which the battle (yuddha) continued.

sanskrit: मूढांस्तु सर्वथा मन्ये धार्तराष्ट्रान्सुबालिशान् ।पतिते शंतनोः पुत्रे येऽकार्षुः संयुगं पुनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: mūḍhāṁstu sarvathā manye dhārtarāṣṭrānsubāliśān ,patite śaṁtanoḥ putre ye'kārṣuḥ saṁyugaṁ punaḥ.
english: But I consider the sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭra) to be utterly foolish and exceedingly childish, for they waged battle (saṃyuga) again even after the son of Shantanu (Bhishma) had fallen.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 22. But I consider the sons of Dhritarashtra (Dhārtarāṣṭra) to be utterly foolish and exceedingly childish, for they waged battle (saṃyuga) again even after the son of Shantanu (Bhishma) had fallen.

sanskrit: अनन्तरं च निहते द्रोणे ब्रह्मविदां वरे ।राधेये च विकर्णे च नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: anantaraṁ ca nihate droṇe brahmavidāṁ vare ,rādheye ca vikarṇe ca naivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: And afterwards, even when Drona, the foremost among the knowers of (brahman), along with Radheya (Karna) and Vikarna, had been slain, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 23. And afterwards, even when Drona, the foremost among the knowers of (brahman), along with Radheya (Karna) and Vikarna, had been slain, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.

sanskrit: अल्पावशिष्टे सैन्येऽस्मिन्सूतपुत्रे च पातिते ।सपुत्रे वै नरव्याघ्रे नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: alpāvaśiṣṭe sainye'sminsūtaputre ca pātite ,saputre vai naravyāghre naivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: Even when this army had only a few remaining, and when the son of Suta (Karna), that tiger among men, had fallen along with his son, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 24. Even when this army had only a few remaining, and when the son of Suta (Karna), that tiger among men, had fallen along with his son, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.

sanskrit: श्रुतायुषि हते शूरे जलसंधे च पौरवे ।श्रुतायुधे च नृपतौ नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: śrutāyuṣi hate śūre jalasaṁdhe ca paurave ,śrutāyudhe ca nṛpatau naivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: Even when the hero Śrutāyu was slain, and when Jaḷasaṃdha, a descendant of Puru, and King Śrutāyudha were slain, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 25. Even when the hero Śrutāyu was slain, and when Jaḷasaṃdha, a descendant of Puru, and King Śrutāyudha were slain, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.

sanskrit: भूरिश्रवसि शल्ये च शाल्वे चैव जनार्दन ।आवन्त्येषु च वीरेषु नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhūriśravasi śalye ca śālve caiva janārdana ,āvantyeṣu ca vīreṣu naivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: O Janārdana, even when Bhūriśravas, Śalya, and indeed Śālva, as well as the Avantya heroes, were slain, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 26. O Janārdana, even when Bhūriśravas, Śalya, and indeed Śālva, as well as the Avantya heroes, were slain, the slaughter (vaiśasam) did not indeed subside.

sanskrit: जयद्रथे च निहते राक्षसे चाप्यलायुधे ।बाह्लिके सोमदत्ते च नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: jayadrathe ca nihate rākṣase cāpyalāyudhe ,bāhlike somadatte ca naivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: Even after Jayadratha was killed, and likewise the demon Alayudha, along with Bahlika and Somadatta, the massacre certainly did not subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 27. Even after Jayadratha was killed, and likewise the demon Alayudha, along with Bahlika and Somadatta, the massacre certainly did not subside.

sanskrit: भगदत्ते हते शूरे काम्बोजे च सुदक्षिणे ।दुःशासने च निहते नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: bhagadatte hate śūre kāmboje ca sudakṣiṇe ,duḥśāsane ca nihate naivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: Even after the heroic Bhagadatta was slain, and Sudakshina, the Kamboja king, and Dushasana were also killed, the massacre certainly did not subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 28. Even after the heroic Bhagadatta was slain, and Sudakshina, the Kamboja king, and Dushasana were also killed, the massacre certainly did not subside.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा च निहताञ्शूरान्पृथङ्माण्डलिकान्नृपान् ।बलिनश्च रणे कृष्ण नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā ca nihatāñśūrānpṛthaṅmāṇḍalikānnṛpān ,balinaśca raṇe kṛṣṇa naivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: And even after seeing brave kings, various provincial rulers, and mighty warriors killed in battle, O Krishna, the massacre certainly did not subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 29. And even after seeing brave kings, various provincial rulers, and mighty warriors killed in battle, O Krishna, the massacre certainly did not subside.

sanskrit: अक्षौहिणीपतीन्दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनेन पातितान् ।मोहाद्वा यदि वा लोभान्नैवाशाम्यत वैशसम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇīpatīndṛṣṭvā bhīmasenena pātitān ,mohādvā yadi vā lobhānnaivāśāmyata vaiśasam.
english: Even after seeing the commanders of akṣauhiṇīs (armies) brought down by Bhimasena, whether due to delusion or greed, the massacre certainly did not subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 30. Even after seeing the commanders of akṣauhiṇīs (armies) brought down by Bhimasena, whether due to delusion or greed, the massacre certainly did not subside.

sanskrit: को नु राजकुले जातः कौरवेयो विशेषतः ।निरर्थकं महद्वैरं कुर्यादन्यः सुयोधनात् ॥३१॥
transliteration: ko nu rājakule jātaḥ kauraveyo viśeṣataḥ ,nirarthakaṁ mahadvairaṁ kuryādanyaḥ suyodhanāt.
english: Who else, born into a royal family, especially one of the Kuru lineage, would create such great, meaningless hostility, other than Suyodhana?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 31. Who else, born into a royal family, especially one of the Kuru lineage, would create such great, meaningless hostility, other than Suyodhana?

sanskrit: गुणतोऽभ्यधिकं ज्ञात्वा बलतः शौर्यतोऽपि वा ।अमूढः को नु युध्येत जानन्प्राज्ञो हिताहितम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: guṇato'bhyadhikaṁ jñātvā balataḥ śauryato'pi vā ,amūḍhaḥ ko nu yudhyeta jānanprājño hitāhitam.
english: Who, being a wise and undeluded person, would indeed fight after realizing (another person to be) superior in qualities, strength, or even valor, especially when knowing what is beneficial and what is harmful?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 32. Who, being a wise and undeluded person, would indeed fight after realizing (another person to be) superior in qualities, strength, or even valor, especially when knowing what is beneficial and what is harmful?

sanskrit: यन्न तस्य मनो ह्यासीत्त्वयोक्तस्य हितं वचः ।प्रशमे पाण्डवैः सार्धं सोऽन्यस्य शृणुयात्कथम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: yanna tasya mano hyāsīttvayoktasya hitaṁ vacaḥ ,praśame pāṇḍavaiḥ sārdhaṁ so'nyasya śṛṇuyātkatham.
english: Since his mind was not indeed receptive to the beneficial words spoken by you concerning reconciliation with the Pāṇḍavas, how would he possibly listen to anyone else?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 33. Since his mind was not indeed receptive to the beneficial words spoken by you concerning reconciliation with the Pāṇḍavas, how would he possibly listen to anyone else?

sanskrit: येन शांतनवो भीष्मो द्रोणो विदुर एव च ।प्रत्याख्याताः शमस्यार्थे किं नु तस्याद्य भेषजम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: yena śāṁtanavo bhīṣmo droṇo vidura eva ca ,pratyākhyātāḥ śamasyārthe kiṁ nu tasyādya bheṣajam.
english: Since he (Duryodhana) rejected Bhīṣma (the son of Śantanu), Droṇa, and even Vidura, all for the sake of peace, what indeed is the remedy for him today?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 34. Since he (Duryodhana) rejected Bhīṣma (the son of Śantanu), Droṇa, and even Vidura, all for the sake of peace, what indeed is the remedy for him today?

sanskrit: मौर्ख्याद्येन पिता वृद्धः प्रत्याख्यातो जनार्दन ।तथा माता हितं वाक्यं भाषमाणा हितैषिणी ।प्रत्याख्याता ह्यसत्कृत्य स कस्मै रोचयेद्वचः ॥३५॥
transliteration: maurkhyādyena pitā vṛddhaḥ pratyākhyāto janārdana ,tathā mātā hitaṁ vākyaṁ bhāṣamāṇā hitaiṣiṇī ,pratyākhyātā hyasatkṛtya sa kasmai rocayedvacaḥ.
english: O Janardana, by whom an old father has been repudiated out of foolishness, and similarly, a well-wishing mother speaking beneficial words has been rejected after being disrespected - to whom will such a person's words be pleasing?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 35. O Janardana, by whom an old father has been repudiated out of foolishness, and similarly, a well-wishing mother speaking beneficial words has been rejected after being disrespected - to whom will such a person's words be pleasing?

sanskrit: कुलान्तकरणो व्यक्तं जात एष जनार्दन ।तथास्य दृश्यते चेष्टा नीतिश्चैव विशां पते ।नैष दास्यति नो राज्यमिति मे मतिरच्युत ॥३६॥
transliteration: kulāntakaraṇo vyaktaṁ jāta eṣa janārdana ,tathāsya dṛśyate ceṣṭā nītiścaiva viśāṁ pate ,naiṣa dāsyati no rājyamiti me matiracyuta.
english: O Janardana, this one is clearly born to destroy the family. O Lord of people, his conduct and policy are seen to be such. This is my opinion (mati), O Acyuta, that he will not give us the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 36. O Janardana, this one is clearly born to destroy the family. O Lord of people, his conduct and policy are seen to be such. This is my opinion (mati), O Acyuta, that he will not give us the kingdom.

sanskrit: उक्तोऽहं बहुशस्तात विदुरेण महात्मना ।न जीवन्दास्यते भागं धार्तराष्ट्रः कथंचन ॥३७॥
transliteration: ukto'haṁ bahuśastāta vidureṇa mahātmanā ,na jīvandāsyate bhāgaṁ dhārtarāṣṭraḥ kathaṁcana.
english: My dear (tāta), the great-souled (mahātman) Vidura has told me many times: 'Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) will not give (us) any share by any means as long as he lives.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 37. My dear (tāta), the great-souled (mahātman) Vidura has told me many times: 'Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) will not give (us) any share by any means as long as he lives.'

sanskrit: यावत्प्राणा धमिष्यन्ति धार्तराष्ट्रस्य मानद ।तावद्युष्मास्वपापेषु प्रचरिष्यति पातकम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: yāvatprāṇā dhamiṣyanti dhārtarāṣṭrasya mānada ,tāvadyuṣmāsvapāpeṣu pracariṣyati pātakam.
english: O giver of honor (mānada), as long as life (prāṇa) remains in Dhritarashtra's son, misfortune will continue to prevail among you, even though you are faultless.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 38. O giver of honor (mānada), as long as life (prāṇa) remains in Dhritarashtra's son, misfortune will continue to prevail among you, even though you are faultless.

sanskrit: न स युक्तोऽन्यथा जेतुमृते युद्धेन माधव ।इत्यब्रवीत्सदा मां हि विदुरः सत्यदर्शनः ॥३९॥
transliteration: na sa yukto'nyathā jetumṛte yuddhena mādhava ,ityabravītsadā māṁ hi viduraḥ satyadarśanaḥ.
english: O Madhava, he is not capable of conquering in any other way except through battle. Vidura, who possesses true vision, always told me this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 39. O Madhava, he is not capable of conquering in any other way except through battle. Vidura, who possesses true vision, always told me this.

sanskrit: तत्सर्वमद्य जानामि व्यवसायं दुरात्मनः ।यदुक्तं वचनं तेन विदुरेण महात्मना ॥४०॥
transliteration: tatsarvamadya jānāmi vyavasāyaṁ durātmanaḥ ,yaduktaṁ vacanaṁ tena vidureṇa mahātmanā.
english: Today I understand the entire scheme of that wicked person, a scheme about which the great-souled (mahātman) Vidura had spoken.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 40. Today I understand the entire scheme of that wicked person, a scheme about which the great-souled (mahātman) Vidura had spoken.

sanskrit: यो हि श्रुत्वा वचः पथ्यं जामदग्न्याद्यथातथम् ।अवामन्यत दुर्बुद्धिर्ध्रुवं नाशमुखे स्थितः ॥४१॥
transliteration: yo hi śrutvā vacaḥ pathyaṁ jāmadagnyādyathātatham ,avāmanyata durbuddhirdhruvaṁ nāśamukhe sthitaḥ.
english: He who, being foolish, indeed disregarded the beneficial advice he heard truthfully from Jamadagni's son (Parashurama), is surely poised on the brink of ruin.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 41. He who, being foolish, indeed disregarded the beneficial advice he heard truthfully from Jamadagni's son (Parashurama), is surely poised on the brink of ruin.

sanskrit: उक्तं हि बहुभिः सिद्धैर्जातमात्रे सुयोधने ।एनं प्राप्य दुरात्मानं क्षयं क्षत्रं गमिष्यति ॥४२॥
transliteration: uktaṁ hi bahubhiḥ siddhairjātamātre suyodhane ,enaṁ prāpya durātmānaṁ kṣayaṁ kṣatraṁ gamiṣyati.
english: Indeed, as soon as Suyodhana (Duryodhana) was born, it was declared by many perfected beings (siddhas) that, on account of this wicked person, the kṣatriya class would face destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 42. Indeed, as soon as Suyodhana (Duryodhana) was born, it was declared by many perfected beings (siddhas) that, on account of this wicked person, the kṣatriya class would face destruction.

sanskrit: तदिदं वचनं तेषां निरुक्तं वै जनार्दन ।क्षयं याता हि राजानो दुर्योधनकृते भृशम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ vacanaṁ teṣāṁ niruktaṁ vai janārdana ,kṣayaṁ yātā hi rājāno duryodhanakṛte bhṛśam.
english: O Janardana, this statement was indeed declared by them: 'The kings have certainly met great ruin because of Duryodhana.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 43. O Janardana, this statement was indeed declared by them: 'The kings have certainly met great ruin because of Duryodhana.'

sanskrit: सोऽद्य सर्वान्रणे योधान्निहनिष्यामि माधव ।क्षत्रियेषु हतेष्वाशु शून्ये च शिबिरे कृते ॥४४॥
transliteration: so'dya sarvānraṇe yodhānnihaniṣyāmi mādhava ,kṣatriyeṣu hateṣvāśu śūnye ca śibire kṛte.
english: O Madhava, today I will slay all the warriors in battle. When the kṣatriyas have been quickly killed and the camp is rendered desolate...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 44. O Madhava, today I will slay all the warriors in battle. When the kṣatriyas have been quickly killed and the camp is rendered desolate...

sanskrit: वधाय चात्मनोऽस्माभिः संयुगं रोचयिष्यति ।तदन्तं हि भवेद्वैरमनुमानेन माधव ॥४५॥
transliteration: vadhāya cātmano'smābhiḥ saṁyugaṁ rocayiṣyati ,tadantaṁ hi bhavedvairamanumānena mādhava.
english: And he will indeed prefer battle for his own destruction at our hands. O Madhava, by inference, that enmity (vaira) will truly come to an end.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 45. And he will indeed prefer battle for his own destruction at our hands. O Madhava, by inference, that enmity (vaira) will truly come to an end.

sanskrit: एवं पश्यामि वार्ष्णेय चिन्तयन्प्रज्ञया स्वया ।विदुरस्य च वाक्येन चेष्टया च दुरात्मनः ॥४६॥
transliteration: evaṁ paśyāmi vārṣṇeya cintayanprajñayā svayā ,vidurasya ca vākyena ceṣṭayā ca durātmanaḥ.
english: O Vārṣṇeya, I thus perceive, pondering with my own intellect (prajñā), and also through the words of Vidura and the actions of that wicked person (durātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 46. O Vārṣṇeya, I thus perceive, pondering with my own intellect (prajñā), and also through the words of Vidura and the actions of that wicked person (durātman).

sanskrit: संयाहि भारतीं वीर यावद्धन्मि शितैः शरैः ।दुर्योधनं दुरात्मानं वाहिनीं चास्य संयुगे ॥४७॥
transliteration: saṁyāhi bhāratīṁ vīra yāvaddhanmi śitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,duryodhanaṁ durātmānaṁ vāhinīṁ cāsya saṁyuge.
english: O hero, advance to the battle (bhāratīm) while I slay the evil-minded Duryodhana and his army in combat with my sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 47. O hero, advance to the battle (bhāratīm) while I slay the evil-minded Duryodhana and his army in combat with my sharp arrows.

sanskrit: क्षेममद्य करिष्यामि धर्मराजस्य माधव ।हत्वैतद्दुर्बलं सैन्यं धार्तराष्ट्रस्य पश्यतः ॥४८॥
transliteration: kṣemamadya kariṣyāmi dharmarājasya mādhava ,hatvaitaddurbalaṁ sainyaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya paśyataḥ.
english: O Madhava, today I will secure the well-being (kṣemam) of Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira), having destroyed this weak army while Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) watches.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 48. O Madhava, today I will secure the well-being (kṣemam) of Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira), having destroyed this weak army while Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) watches.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अभीशुहस्तो दाशार्हस्तथोक्तः सव्यसाचिना ।तद्बलौघममित्राणामभीतः प्राविशद्रणे ॥४९॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,abhīśuhasto dāśārhastathoktaḥ savyasācinā ,tadbalaughamamitrāṇāmabhītaḥ prāviśadraṇe.
english: Sanjaya said: Thus addressed by Savyasacin (Arjuna), Daśārha's descendant (Krishna), with the reins in his hand, fearlessly entered that mass of enemy forces in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 49. Sanjaya said: Thus addressed by Savyasacin (Arjuna), Daśārha's descendant (Krishna), with the reins in his hand, fearlessly entered that mass of enemy forces in the battle.

sanskrit: शरासनवरं घोरं शक्तिकण्टकसंवृतम् ।गदापरिघपन्थानं रथनागमहाद्रुमम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: śarāsanavaraṁ ghoraṁ śaktikaṇṭakasaṁvṛtam ,gadāparighapanthānaṁ rathanāgamahādrumam.
english: (He entered the enemy host, which was) dreadful, outstanding with bows, covered with thorny spears, with maces and iron clubs forming its pathways, and chariots and elephants standing like great trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 50. (He entered the enemy host, which was) dreadful, outstanding with bows, covered with thorny spears, with maces and iron clubs forming its pathways, and chariots and elephants standing like great trees.

sanskrit: हयपत्तिलताकीर्णं गाहमानो महायशाः ।व्यचरत्तत्र गोविन्दो रथेनातिपताकिना ॥५१॥
transliteration: hayapattilatākīrṇaṁ gāhamāno mahāyaśāḥ ,vyacarattatra govindo rathenātipatākinā.
english: The greatly renowned Govinda (Krishna), entering that area crowded with multitudes of horses and foot soldiers, moved about there with his chariot, which was adorned with many banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 51. The greatly renowned Govinda (Krishna), entering that area crowded with multitudes of horses and foot soldiers, moved about there with his chariot, which was adorned with many banners.

sanskrit: ते हयाः पाण्डुरा राजन्वहन्तोऽर्जुनमाहवे ।दिक्षु सर्वास्वदृश्यन्त दाशार्हेण प्रचोदिताः ॥५२॥
transliteration: te hayāḥ pāṇḍurā rājanvahanto'rjunamāhave ,dikṣu sarvāsvadṛśyanta dāśārheṇa pracoditāḥ.
english: O king, those pale horses, carrying Arjuna in the battle, were seen in all directions, impelled by the Dāśārha (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 52. O king, those pale horses, carrying Arjuna in the battle, were seen in all directions, impelled by the Dāśārha (Krishna).

sanskrit: ततः प्रायाद्रथेनाजौ सव्यसाची परंतपः ।किरञ्शरशतांस्तीक्ष्णान्वारिधारा इवाम्बुदः ॥५३॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyādrathenājau savyasācī paraṁtapaḥ ,kirañśaraśatāṁstīkṣṇānvāridhārā ivāmbudaḥ.
english: Then, the tormentor of foes, Savyasācin (Arjuna), advanced in battle with his chariot, showering hundreds of sharp arrows just as a cloud pours forth streams of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 53. Then, the tormentor of foes, Savyasācin (Arjuna), advanced in battle with his chariot, showering hundreds of sharp arrows just as a cloud pours forth streams of water.

sanskrit: प्रादुरासीन्महाञ्शब्दः शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ।इषुभिश्छाद्यमानानां समरे सव्यसाचिना ॥५४॥
transliteration: prādurāsīnmahāñśabdaḥ śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām ,iṣubhiśchādyamānānāṁ samare savyasācinā.
english: A tremendous sound arose from the arrows, which had bent joints, and from those (enemies) who were being covered by the arrows of Savyasācin (Arjuna) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 54. A tremendous sound arose from the arrows, which had bent joints, and from those (enemies) who were being covered by the arrows of Savyasācin (Arjuna) in battle.

sanskrit: असज्जन्तस्तनुत्रेषु शरौघाः प्रापतन्भुवि ।इन्द्राशनिसमस्पर्शा गाण्डीवप्रेषिताः शराः ॥५५॥
transliteration: asajjantastanutreṣu śaraughāḥ prāpatanbhuvi ,indrāśanisamasparśā gāṇḍīvapreṣitāḥ śarāḥ.
english: The arrows, launched from the Gaṇḍīva bow, whose impact was like Indra's thunderbolt, did not get stuck in armors but fell to the ground in torrents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 55. The arrows, launched from the Gaṇḍīva bow, whose impact was like Indra's thunderbolt, did not get stuck in armors but fell to the ground in torrents.

sanskrit: नरान्नागान्समाहत्य हयांश्चापि विशां पते ।अपतन्त रणे बाणाः पतंगा इव घोषिणः ॥५६॥
transliteration: narānnāgānsamāhatya hayāṁścāpi viśāṁ pate ,apatanta raṇe bāṇāḥ pataṁgā iva ghoṣiṇaḥ.
english: O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), the arrows, which hissed and resembled moths or grasshoppers, struck men, elephants, and also horses, before falling down on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 56. O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), the arrows, which hissed and resembled moths or grasshoppers, struck men, elephants, and also horses, before falling down on the battlefield.

sanskrit: आसीत्सर्वमवच्छन्नं गाण्डीवप्रेषितैः शरैः ।न प्राज्ञायन्त समरे दिशो वा प्रदिशोऽपि वा ॥५७॥
transliteration: āsītsarvamavacchannaṁ gāṇḍīvapreṣitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,na prājñāyanta samare diśo vā pradiśo'pi vā.
english: The entire battlefield was covered by the arrows launched from the Gaṇḍīva bow. In the battle, neither the main directions nor the intermediate directions could be distinguished.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 57. The entire battlefield was covered by the arrows launched from the Gaṇḍīva bow. In the battle, neither the main directions nor the intermediate directions could be distinguished.

sanskrit: सर्वमासीज्जगत्पूर्णं पार्थनामाङ्कितैः शरैः ।रुक्मपुङ्खैस्तैलधौतैः कर्मारपरिमार्जितैः ॥५८॥
transliteration: sarvamāsījjagatpūrṇaṁ pārthanāmāṅkitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,rukmapuṅkhaistailadhautaiḥ karmāraparimārjitaiḥ.
english: The entire world (jagat) was filled with arrows that bore the name of Pārtha (Arjuna), possessing golden shafts, cleaned with oil, and meticulously polished by skilled smiths.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 58. The entire world (jagat) was filled with arrows that bore the name of Pārtha (Arjuna), possessing golden shafts, cleaned with oil, and meticulously polished by skilled smiths.

sanskrit: ते दह्यमानाः पार्थेन पावकेनेव कुञ्जराः ।समासीदन्त कौरव्या वध्यमानाः शितैः शरैः ॥५९॥
transliteration: te dahyamānāḥ pārthena pāvakeneva kuñjarāḥ ,samāsīdanta kauravyā vadhyamānāḥ śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Being scorched by Partha (Arjuna) as if by fire, those Kauravas, like elephants, were assailed, being struck down by sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 59. Being scorched by Partha (Arjuna) as if by fire, those Kauravas, like elephants, were assailed, being struck down by sharp arrows.

sanskrit: शरचापधरः पार्थः प्रज्वलन्निव भारत ।ददाह समरे योधान्कक्षमग्निरिव ज्वलन् ॥६०॥
transliteration: śaracāpadharaḥ pārthaḥ prajvalanniva bhārata ,dadāha samare yodhānkakṣamagniriva jvalan.
english: O Bhārata, Partha (Arjuna), bearing his bow and arrows, blazing as if he were fire, consumed the warriors in battle, just as a blazing fire burns up dry grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 60. O Bhārata, Partha (Arjuna), bearing his bow and arrows, blazing as if he were fire, consumed the warriors in battle, just as a blazing fire burns up dry grass.

sanskrit: यथा वनान्ते वनपैर्विसृष्टः कक्षं दहेत्कृष्णगतिः सघोषः ।भूरिद्रुमं शुष्कलतावितानं भृशं समृद्धो ज्वलनः प्रतापी ॥६१॥
transliteration: yathā vanānte vanapairvisṛṣṭaḥ; kakṣaṁ dahetkṛṣṇagatiḥ saghoṣaḥ ,bhūridrumaṁ śuṣkalatāvitānaṁ; bhṛśaṁ samṛddho jvalanaḥ pratāpī.
english: Just as a greatly thriving and powerful fire, released by forest dwellers at the edge of a forest, roaring and leaving a dark trail of smoke, would consume a dry forest abundant with trees and an expanse of dry creepers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 61. Just as a greatly thriving and powerful fire, released by forest dwellers at the edge of a forest, roaring and leaving a dark trail of smoke, would consume a dry forest abundant with trees and an expanse of dry creepers.

sanskrit: एवं स नाराचगणप्रतापी शरार्चिरुच्चावचतिग्मतेजाः ।ददाह सर्वां तव पुत्रसेनाममृष्यमाणस्तरसा तरस्वी ॥६२॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa nārācagaṇapratāpī; śarārciruccāvacatigmatejāḥ ,dadāha sarvāṁ tava putrasenā;mamṛṣyamāṇastarasā tarasvī.
english: Thus he (Arjuna), mighty with his multitude of arrows, whose arrows were like flames, and who possessed diverse, fierce energy, consumed your entire son's army with great force, unable to tolerate them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 62. Thus he (Arjuna), mighty with his multitude of arrows, whose arrows were like flames, and who possessed diverse, fierce energy, consumed your entire son's army with great force, unable to tolerate them.

sanskrit: तस्येषवः प्राणहराः सुमुक्ता नासज्जन्वै वर्मसु रुक्मपुङ्खाः ।न च द्वितीयं प्रमुमोच बाणं नरे हये वा परमद्विपे वा ॥६३॥
transliteration: tasyeṣavaḥ prāṇaharāḥ sumuktā; nāsajjanvai varmasu rukmapuṅkhāḥ ,na ca dvitīyaṁ pramumoca bāṇaṁ; nare haye vā paramadvipe vā.
english: His life-taking, well-shot arrows, feathered with gold, did not stick in the armor. Indeed, he did not need to release a second arrow at a man, a horse, or a mighty elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 63. His life-taking, well-shot arrows, feathered with gold, did not stick in the armor. Indeed, he did not need to release a second arrow at a man, a horse, or a mighty elephant.

sanskrit: अनेकरूपाकृतिभिर्हि बाणैर्महारथानीकमनुप्रविश्य ।स एव एकस्तव पुत्रसेनां जघान दैत्यानिव वज्रपाणिः ॥६४॥
transliteration: anekarūpākṛtibhirhi bāṇai;rmahārathānīkamanupraviśya ,sa eva ekastava putrasenāṁ; jaghāna daityāniva vajrapāṇiḥ.
english: Indeed, penetrating the army of great charioteers with arrows of many forms and shapes, he alone destroyed your son's army, just as Vajrapāṇi (Indra) destroyed the demons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 23 Verse 64. Indeed, penetrating the army of great charioteers with arrows of many forms and shapes, he alone destroyed your son's army, just as Vajrapāṇi (Indra) destroyed the demons.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अस्यतां यतमानानां शूराणामनिवर्तिनाम् ।संकल्पमकरोन्मोघं गाण्डीवेन धनंजयः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,asyatāṁ yatamānānāṁ śūrāṇāmanivartinām ,saṁkalpamakaronmoghaṁ gāṇḍīvena dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), with his Gāṇḍīva bow, rendered futile the resolve of those valiant heroes who were fighting, striving, and not retreating.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), with his Gāṇḍīva bow, rendered futile the resolve of those valiant heroes who were fighting, striving, and not retreating.

sanskrit: इन्द्राशनिसमस्पर्शानविषह्यान्महौजसः ।विसृजन्दृश्यते बाणान्धारा मुञ्चन्निवाम्बुदः ॥२॥
transliteration: indrāśanisamasparśānaviṣahyānmahaujasaḥ ,visṛjandṛśyate bāṇāndhārā muñcannivāmbudaḥ.
english: He was seen releasing arrows that were like the touch of Indra's thunderbolt, intolerable and of great energy, just like a cloud shedding streams of rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 2. He was seen releasing arrows that were like the touch of Indra's thunderbolt, intolerable and of great energy, just like a cloud shedding streams of rain.

sanskrit: तत्सैन्यं भरतश्रेष्ठ वध्यमानं किरीटिना ।संप्रदुद्राव संग्रामात्तव पुत्रस्य पश्यतः ॥३॥
transliteration: tatsainyaṁ bharataśreṣṭha vadhyamānaṁ kirīṭinā ,saṁpradudrāva saṁgrāmāttava putrasya paśyataḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, that army, being slaughtered by Arjuna (kirīṭin), fled from the battle while your son watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 3. O best among the Bharatas, that army, being slaughtered by Arjuna (kirīṭin), fled from the battle while your son watched.

sanskrit: हतधुर्या रथाः केचिद्धतसूतास्तथापरे ।भग्नाक्षयुगचक्रेषाः केचिदासन्विशां पते ॥४॥
transliteration: hatadhuryā rathāḥ keciddhatasūtāstathāpare ,bhagnākṣayugacakreṣāḥ kecidāsanviśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of men, some chariots (rathā) had their draught animals destroyed, others had their charioteers killed, and still others had their axles, yokes, wheels, and poles broken.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 4. O lord of men, some chariots (rathā) had their draught animals destroyed, others had their charioteers killed, and still others had their axles, yokes, wheels, and poles broken.

sanskrit: अन्येषां सायकाः क्षीणास्तथान्ये शरपीडिताः ।अक्षता युगपत्केचित्प्राद्रवन्भयपीडिताः ॥५॥
transliteration: anyeṣāṁ sāyakāḥ kṣīṇāstathānye śarapīḍitāḥ ,akṣatā yugapatkecitprādravanbhayapīḍitāḥ.
english: The arrows of some were exhausted, and others were wounded by arrows. Some, even those unwounded, fled simultaneously, afflicted by fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 5. The arrows of some were exhausted, and others were wounded by arrows. Some, even those unwounded, fled simultaneously, afflicted by fear.

sanskrit: केचित्पुत्रानुपादाय हतभूयिष्ठवाहनाः ।विचुक्रुशुः पितॄनन्ये सहायानपरे पुनः ॥६॥
transliteration: kecitputrānupādāya hatabhūyiṣṭhavāhanāḥ ,vicukruśuḥ pitṝnanye sahāyānapare punaḥ.
english: Some, whose vehicles were mostly destroyed, took their sons and cried out to their fathers. Still others cried out to their companions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 6. Some, whose vehicles were mostly destroyed, took their sons and cried out to their fathers. Still others cried out to their companions.

sanskrit: बान्धवांश्च नरव्याघ्र भ्रातॄन्संबन्धिनस्तथा ।दुद्रुवुः केचिदुत्सृज्य तत्र तत्र विशां पते ॥७॥
transliteration: bāndhavāṁśca naravyāghra bhrātṝnsaṁbandhinastathā ,dudruvuḥ kecidutsṛjya tatra tatra viśāṁ pate.
english: O tiger among men, O lord of the people, some abandoned their kinsmen, brothers, and relatives, and fled here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 7. O tiger among men, O lord of the people, some abandoned their kinsmen, brothers, and relatives, and fled here and there.

sanskrit: बहवोऽत्र भृशं विद्धा मुह्यमाना महारथाः ।निष्टनन्तः स्म दृश्यन्ते पार्थबाणहता नराः ॥८॥
transliteration: bahavo'tra bhṛśaṁ viddhā muhyamānā mahārathāḥ ,niṣṭanantaḥ sma dṛśyante pārthabāṇahatā narāḥ.
english: Here, many warriors (mahārathāḥ) were seen, severely wounded and fainting, groaning, struck by Arjuna's (Pārtha) arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 8. Here, many warriors (mahārathāḥ) were seen, severely wounded and fainting, groaning, struck by Arjuna's (Pārtha) arrows.

sanskrit: तानन्ये रथमारोप्य समाश्वास्य मुहूर्तकम् ।विश्रान्ताश्च वितृष्णाश्च पुनर्युद्धाय जग्मिरे ॥९॥
transliteration: tānanye rathamāropya samāśvāsya muhūrtakam ,viśrāntāśca vitṛṣṇāśca punaryuddhāya jagmire.
english: Others, having placed those [wounded] on chariots and comforted them for a moment, refreshed and free from thirst, went back to battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 9. Others, having placed those [wounded] on chariots and comforted them for a moment, refreshed and free from thirst, went back to battle.

sanskrit: तानपास्य गताः केचित्पुनरेव युयुत्सवः ।कुर्वन्तस्तव पुत्रस्य शासनं युद्धदुर्मदाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tānapāsya gatāḥ kecitpunareva yuyutsavaḥ ,kurvantastava putrasya śāsanaṁ yuddhadurmadāḥ.
english: Some of those who were fiercely eager for battle, abandoned those [wounded] and went back, carrying out your son's command.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 10. Some of those who were fiercely eager for battle, abandoned those [wounded] and went back, carrying out your son's command.

sanskrit: पानीयमपरे पीत्वा पर्याश्वास्य च वाहनम् ।वर्माणि च समारोप्य केचिद्भरतसत्तम ॥११॥
transliteration: pānīyamapare pītvā paryāśvāsya ca vāhanam ,varmāṇi ca samāropya kecidbharatasattama.
english: O best among the Bharatas, some others, having drunk water, having rested their vehicles, and having donned their armors...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 11. O best among the Bharatas, some others, having drunk water, having rested their vehicles, and having donned their armors...

sanskrit: समाश्वास्यापरे भ्रातॄन्निक्षिप्य शिबिरेऽपि च ।पुत्रानन्ये पितॄनन्ये पुनर्युद्धमरोचयन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: samāśvāsyāpare bhrātṝnnikṣipya śibire'pi ca ,putrānanye pitṝnanye punaryuddhamarocayan.
english: Some others, having reassured their brothers and placed their sons in the camp, and still others (having placed) their fathers, again desired battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 12. Some others, having reassured their brothers and placed their sons in the camp, and still others (having placed) their fathers, again desired battle.

sanskrit: सज्जयित्वा रथान्केचिद्यथामुख्यं विशां पते ।आप्लुत्य पाण्डवानीकं पुनर्युद्धमरोचयन् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sajjayitvā rathānkecidyathāmukhyaṁ viśāṁ pate ,āplutya pāṇḍavānīkaṁ punaryuddhamarocayan.
english: O lord of the people, some, having prepared their chariots according to rank, plunged into the Pandava army and again desired battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 13. O lord of the people, some, having prepared their chariots according to rank, plunged into the Pandava army and again desired battle.

sanskrit: ते शूराः किङ्किणीजालैः समाच्छन्ना बभासिरे ।त्रैलोक्यविजये युक्ता यथा दैतेयदानवाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: te śūrāḥ kiṅkiṇījālaiḥ samācchannā babhāsire ,trailokyavijaye yuktā yathā daiteyadānavāḥ.
english: Those heroes, adorned with networks of small bells, shone brightly, appearing like the Daityas and Danavas intent on the conquest of the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 14. Those heroes, adorned with networks of small bells, shone brightly, appearing like the Daityas and Danavas intent on the conquest of the three worlds.

sanskrit: आगम्य सहसा केचिद्रथैः स्वर्णविभूषितैः ।पाण्डवानामनीकेषु धृष्टद्युम्नमयोधयन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: āgamya sahasā kecidrathaiḥ svarṇavibhūṣitaiḥ ,pāṇḍavānāmanīkeṣu dhṛṣṭadyumnamayodhayan.
english: Some warriors, having suddenly arrived with chariots adorned with gold, fought against Dhṛṣṭadyumna within the armies of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 15. Some warriors, having suddenly arrived with chariots adorned with gold, fought against Dhṛṣṭadyumna within the armies of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नोऽपि पाञ्चाल्यः शिखण्डी च महारथः ।नाकुलिश्च शतानीको रथानीकमयोधयन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumno'pi pāñcālyaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca mahārathaḥ ,nākuliśca śatānīko rathānīkamayodhayan.
english: Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the prince of Pañcāla, and Śikhaṇḍī, the great chariot-warrior, along with Nākuli (Sahadeva) and Śatānīka, also fought against the chariot division.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 16. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the prince of Pañcāla, and Śikhaṇḍī, the great chariot-warrior, along with Nākuli (Sahadeva) and Śatānīka, also fought against the chariot division.

sanskrit: पाञ्चाल्यस्तु ततः क्रुद्धः सैन्येन महता वृतः ।अभ्यद्रवत्सुसंरब्धस्तावकान्हन्तुमुद्यतः ॥१७॥
transliteration: pāñcālyastu tataḥ kruddhaḥ sainyena mahatā vṛtaḥ ,abhyadravatsusaṁrabdhastāvakānhantumudyataḥ.
english: But then, the Pāñcāla prince, enraged and surrounded by a vast army, became exceedingly agitated and, prepared to kill your warriors, he charged forward.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 17. But then, the Pāñcāla prince, enraged and surrounded by a vast army, became exceedingly agitated and, prepared to kill your warriors, he charged forward.

sanskrit: ततस्त्वापततस्तस्य तव पुत्रो जनाधिप ।बाणसंघाननेकान्वै प्रेषयामास भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatastvāpatatastasya tava putro janādhipa ,bāṇasaṁghānanekānvai preṣayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O lord of men (janādhipa), O Bhārata, then your son indeed sent forth many groups of arrows against him who was rushing forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 18. O lord of men (janādhipa), O Bhārata, then your son indeed sent forth many groups of arrows against him who was rushing forth.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्ततो राजंस्तव पुत्रेण धन्विना ।नाराचैर्बहुभिः क्षिप्रं बाह्वोरुरसि चार्पितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastato rājaṁstava putreṇa dhanvinā ,nārācairbahubhiḥ kṣipraṁ bāhvorurasi cārpitaḥ.
english: Then, O king, Dhṛṣṭadyumna was quickly pierced by your archer son with many iron arrows on his arms and chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 19. Then, O king, Dhṛṣṭadyumna was quickly pierced by your archer son with many iron arrows on his arms and chest.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो महेष्वासस्तोत्त्रार्दित इव द्विपः ।तस्याश्वांश्चतुरो बाणैः प्रेषयामास मृत्यवे ।सारथेश्चास्य भल्लेन शिरः कायादपाहरत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: so'tividdho maheṣvāsastottrārdita iva dvipaḥ ,tasyāśvāṁścaturo bāṇaiḥ preṣayāmāsa mṛtyave ,sāratheścāsya bhallena śiraḥ kāyādapāharat.
english: He, the great archer (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), severely wounded like an elephant tormented by a goad, sent his four horses to their death with arrows. And with a crescent-shaped arrow, he severed his charioteer's head from his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 20. He, the great archer (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), severely wounded like an elephant tormented by a goad, sent his four horses to their death with arrows. And with a crescent-shaped arrow, he severed his charioteer's head from his body.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा पृष्ठमारुह्य वाजिनः ।अपाक्रामद्धतरथो नातिदूरमरिंदमः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā pṛṣṭhamāruhya vājinaḥ ,apākrāmaddhataratho nātidūramariṁdamaḥ.
english: Then King Duryodhana, whose chariot had been destroyed, mounted the back of a horse and retreated, O subduer of enemies (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), but not very far.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 21. Then King Duryodhana, whose chariot had been destroyed, mounted the back of a horse and retreated, O subduer of enemies (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), but not very far.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु हतविक्रान्तं स्वमनीकं महाबलः ।तव पुत्रो महाराज प्रययौ यत्र सौबलः ॥२२॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu hatavikrāntaṁ svamanīkaṁ mahābalaḥ ,tava putro mahārāja prayayau yatra saubalaḥ.
english: But having seen his own army with its valor destroyed, your greatly powerful son (Duryodhana), O great king, went to where Subala's son (Śakuni) was.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 22. But having seen his own army with its valor destroyed, your greatly powerful son (Duryodhana), O great king, went to where Subala's son (Śakuni) was.

sanskrit: ततो रथेषु भग्नेषु त्रिसाहस्रा महाद्विपाः ।पाण्डवान्रथिनः पञ्च समन्तात्पर्यवारयन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato ratheṣu bhagneṣu trisāhasrā mahādvipāḥ ,pāṇḍavānrathinaḥ pañca samantātparyavārayan.
english: Then, when their chariots were broken, three thousand great elephants surrounded the five Pandava charioteers from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 23. Then, when their chariots were broken, three thousand great elephants surrounded the five Pandava charioteers from all sides.

sanskrit: ते वृताः समरे पञ्च गजानीकेन भारत ।अशोभन्त नरव्याघ्रा ग्रहा व्याप्ता घनैरिव ॥२४॥
transliteration: te vṛtāḥ samare pañca gajānīkena bhārata ,aśobhanta naravyāghrā grahā vyāptā ghanairiva.
english: O Bhārata, those five tiger-like men, surrounded in battle by the elephant army, appeared like planets obscured by clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 24. O Bhārata, those five tiger-like men, surrounded in battle by the elephant army, appeared like planets obscured by clouds.
