sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो महाराज लब्धलक्षो महाभुजः ।विनिर्ययौ रथेनैव श्वेताश्वः कृष्णसारथिः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno mahārāja labdhalakṣo mahābhujaḥ ,viniryayau rathenaiva śvetāśvaḥ kṛṣṇasārathiḥ.
english: O great king, then the mighty-armed Arjuna, having achieved his aim, indeed sallied forth with his chariot, whose horses were white and whose charioteer was Krishna.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 25. O great king, then the mighty-armed Arjuna, having achieved his aim, indeed sallied forth with his chariot, whose horses were white and whose charioteer was Krishna.

sanskrit: तैः समन्तात्परिवृतः कुञ्जरैः पर्वतोपमैः ।नाराचैर्विमलैस्तीक्ष्णैर्गजानीकमपोथयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: taiḥ samantātparivṛtaḥ kuñjaraiḥ parvatopamaiḥ ,nārācairvimalaistīkṣṇairgajānīkamapothayat.
english: Surrounded from all sides by those mountain-like elephants, he (Arjuna) crushed the elephant army with his sharp, shining iron arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 26. Surrounded from all sides by those mountain-like elephants, he (Arjuna) crushed the elephant army with his sharp, shining iron arrows.

sanskrit: तत्रैकबाणनिहतानपश्याम महागजान् ।पतितान्पात्यमानांश्च विभिन्नान्सव्यसाचिना ॥२७॥
transliteration: tatraikabāṇanihatānapaśyāma mahāgajān ,patitānpātyamānāṁśca vibhinnānsavyasācinā.
english: We saw there mighty elephants, fallen and falling, pierced and struck down by a single arrow from Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 27. We saw there mighty elephants, fallen and falling, pierced and struck down by a single arrow from Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु तान्दृष्ट्वा नागान्मत्तगजोपमः ।करेण गृह्य महतीं गदामभ्यपतद्बली ।अवप्लुत्य रथात्तूर्णं दण्डपाणिरिवान्तकः ॥२८॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu tāndṛṣṭvā nāgānmattagajopamaḥ ,kareṇa gṛhya mahatīṁ gadāmabhyapatadbalī ,avaplutya rathāttūrṇaṁ daṇḍapāṇirivāntakaḥ.
english: But the mighty Bhimasena, seeing those elephants and resembling a rutting elephant himself, grasped his enormous mace with his hand and rushed forward. He quickly leaped down from his chariot, like Yama, the wielder of the staff (Daṇḍapāṇi).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 28. But the mighty Bhimasena, seeing those elephants and resembling a rutting elephant himself, grasped his enormous mace with his hand and rushed forward. He quickly leaped down from his chariot, like Yama, the wielder of the staff (Daṇḍapāṇi).

sanskrit: तमुद्यतगदं दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवानां महारथम् ।वित्रेसुस्तावकाः सैन्याः शकृन्मूत्रं प्रसुस्रुवुः ।आविग्नं च बलं सर्वं गदाहस्ते वृकोदरे ॥२९॥
transliteration: tamudyatagadaṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavānāṁ mahāratham ,vitresustāvakāḥ sainyāḥ śakṛnmūtraṁ prasusruvuḥ ,āvignaṁ ca balaṁ sarvaṁ gadāhaste vṛkodare.
english: Seeing him, the great chariot warrior of the Pāṇḍavas with an upraised mace, your soldiers trembled and discharged feces and urine. Indeed, the entire army was agitated when Vṛkodara (Bhimasena) held the mace in his hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 29. Seeing him, the great chariot warrior of the Pāṇḍavas with an upraised mace, your soldiers trembled and discharged feces and urine. Indeed, the entire army was agitated when Vṛkodara (Bhimasena) held the mace in his hand.

sanskrit: गदया भीमसेनेन भिन्नकुम्भान्रजस्वलान् ।धावमानानपश्याम कुञ्जरान्पर्वतोपमान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: gadayā bhīmasenena bhinnakumbhānrajasvalān ,dhāvamānānapaśyāma kuñjarānparvatopamān.
english: We saw mountain-like elephants, their temples shattered and covered in dust/blood by Bhimasena's mace, as they ran.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 30. We saw mountain-like elephants, their temples shattered and covered in dust/blood by Bhimasena's mace, as they ran.

sanskrit: प्रधाव्य कुञ्जरास्ते तु भीमसेनगदाहताः ।पेतुरार्तस्वरं कृत्वा छिन्नपक्षा इवाद्रयः ॥३१॥
transliteration: pradhāvya kuñjarāste tu bhīmasenagadāhatāḥ ,peturārtasvaraṁ kṛtvā chinnapakṣā ivādrayaḥ.
english: But those elephants, having rushed forth and been struck by Bhimasena's mace, fell to the ground making cries of pain, like mountains whose wings had been clipped.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 31. But those elephants, having rushed forth and been struck by Bhimasena's mace, fell to the ground making cries of pain, like mountains whose wings had been clipped.

sanskrit: तान्भिन्नकुम्भान्सुबहून्द्रवमाणानितस्ततः ।पतमानांश्च संप्रेक्ष्य वित्रेसुस्तव सैनिकाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tānbhinnakumbhānsubahūndravamāṇānitastataḥ ,patamānāṁśca saṁprekṣya vitresustava sainikāḥ.
english: Beholding those many elephants, with their temples shattered, fleeing here and there, and falling, your soldiers were terrified.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 32. Beholding those many elephants, with their temples shattered, fleeing here and there, and falling, your soldiers were terrified.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरोऽपि संक्रुद्धो माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।गृध्रपक्षैः शितैर्बाणैर्जघ्नुर्वै गजयोधिनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiro'pi saṁkruddho mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,gṛdhrapakṣaiḥ śitairbāṇairjaghnurvai gajayodhinaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, too, greatly enraged, along with the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, indeed struck the elephant-warriors with sharp arrows feathered with vulture wings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 33. Yudhiṣṭhira, too, greatly enraged, along with the two Pāṇḍava sons of Mādrī, indeed struck the elephant-warriors with sharp arrows feathered with vulture wings.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु समरे पराजित्य नराधिपम् ।अपक्रान्ते तव सुते हयपृष्ठं समाश्रिते ॥३४॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastu samare parājitya narādhipam ,apakrānte tava sute hayapṛṣṭhaṁ samāśrite.
english: But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having defeated the king in battle, while your son had retreated, having mounted the back of a horse...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 34. But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having defeated the king in battle, while your son had retreated, having mounted the back of a horse...

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा च पाण्डवान्सर्वान्कुञ्जरैः परिवारितान् ।धृष्टद्युम्नो महाराज सह सर्वैः प्रभद्रकैः ।पुत्रः पाञ्चालराजस्य जिघांसुः कुञ्जरान्ययौ ॥३५॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā ca pāṇḍavānsarvānkuñjaraiḥ parivāritān ,dhṛṣṭadyumno mahārāja saha sarvaiḥ prabhadrakaiḥ ,putraḥ pāñcālarājasya jighāṁsuḥ kuñjarānyayau.
english: O great king, Dhrishtadyumna, the son of the Panchala king, seeing all the Pandavas surrounded by elephants, advanced with all the Prabhadrakas, intending to kill those elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 35. O great king, Dhrishtadyumna, the son of the Panchala king, seeing all the Pandavas surrounded by elephants, advanced with all the Prabhadrakas, intending to kill those elephants.

sanskrit: अदृष्ट्वा तु रथानीके दुर्योधनमरिंदमम् ।अश्वत्थामा कृपश्चैव कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ।अपृच्छन्क्षत्रियांस्तत्र क्व नु दुर्योधनो गतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: adṛṣṭvā tu rathānīke duryodhanamariṁdamam ,aśvatthāmā kṛpaścaiva kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ ,apṛcchankṣatriyāṁstatra kva nu duryodhano gataḥ.
english: Indeed, not seeing Duryodhana, the subduer of enemies, among the chariot divisions, Ashvatthama, Kripa, and Kritavarma, the Satvata warrior, asked the other warriors (kṣatriyas) there, 'Where has Duryodhana gone?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 36. Indeed, not seeing Duryodhana, the subduer of enemies, among the chariot divisions, Ashvatthama, Kripa, and Kritavarma, the Satvata warrior, asked the other warriors (kṣatriyas) there, 'Where has Duryodhana gone?'

sanskrit: अपश्यमाना राजानं वर्तमाने जनक्षये ।मन्वाना निहतं तत्र तव पुत्रं महारथाः ।विषण्णवदना भूत्वा पर्यपृच्छन्त ते सुतम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: apaśyamānā rājānaṁ vartamāne janakṣaye ,manvānā nihataṁ tatra tava putraṁ mahārathāḥ ,viṣaṇṇavadanā bhūtvā paryapṛcchanta te sutam.
english: The great charioteers (mahārathas), not seeing the king amidst the ongoing destruction of men, and presuming your son had been slain there, became dejected and inquired after your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 37. The great charioteers (mahārathas), not seeing the king amidst the ongoing destruction of men, and presuming your son had been slain there, became dejected and inquired after your son.

sanskrit: आहुः केचिद्धते सूते प्रयातो यत्र सौबलः ।अपरे त्वब्रुवंस्तत्र क्षत्रिया भृशविक्षताः ॥३८॥
transliteration: āhuḥ keciddhate sūte prayāto yatra saubalaḥ ,apare tvabruvaṁstatra kṣatriyā bhṛśavikṣatāḥ.
english: Some said that when his charioteer was killed, he had gone to where Saubala (Śakuni) was. Others, severely wounded warriors (kṣatriyas), indeed spoke up there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 38. Some said that when his charioteer was killed, he had gone to where Saubala (Śakuni) was. Others, severely wounded warriors (kṣatriyas), indeed spoke up there.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनेन किं कार्यं द्रक्ष्यध्वं यदि जीवति ।युध्यध्वं सहिताः सर्वे किं वो राजा करिष्यति ॥३९॥
transliteration: duryodhanena kiṁ kāryaṁ drakṣyadhvaṁ yadi jīvati ,yudhyadhvaṁ sahitāḥ sarve kiṁ vo rājā kariṣyati.
english: What use is Duryodhana? You will observe if he survives. All of you, fight united! What can your king do to you?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 39. What use is Duryodhana? You will observe if he survives. All of you, fight united! What can your king do to you?

sanskrit: ते क्षत्रियाः क्षतैर्गात्रैर्हतभूयिष्ठवाहनाः ।शरैः संपीड्यमानाश्च नातिव्यक्तमिवाब्रुवन् ॥४०॥
transliteration: te kṣatriyāḥ kṣatairgātrairhatabhūyiṣṭhavāhanāḥ ,śaraiḥ saṁpīḍyamānāśca nātivyaktamivābruvan.
english: Those warriors (kṣatriya), with their bodies mangled by wounds and most of their mounts destroyed, tormented by arrows, spoke as if indistinctly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 40. Those warriors (kṣatriya), with their bodies mangled by wounds and most of their mounts destroyed, tormented by arrows, spoke as if indistinctly.

sanskrit: इदं सर्वं बलं हन्मो येन स्म परिवारिताः ।एते सर्वे गजान्हत्वा उपयान्ति स्म पाण्डवाः ॥४१॥
transliteration: idaṁ sarvaṁ balaṁ hanmo yena sma parivāritāḥ ,ete sarve gajānhatvā upayānti sma pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: We destroyed this entire army that had surrounded us. Then, all these Pāṇḍavas, having slain the elephants, advanced.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 41. We destroyed this entire army that had surrounded us. Then, all these Pāṇḍavas, having slain the elephants, advanced.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु वचनं तेषामश्वत्थामा महाबलः ।हित्वा पाञ्चालराजस्य तदनीकं दुरुत्सहम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu vacanaṁ teṣāmaśvatthāmā mahābalaḥ ,hitvā pāñcālarājasya tadanīkaṁ durutsaham.
english: But having heard their words, the immensely powerful Aśvatthāmā, having forsaken that formidable army of the Pañcāla king, ...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 42. But having heard their words, the immensely powerful Aśvatthāmā, having forsaken that formidable army of the Pañcāla king, ...

sanskrit: कृपश्च कृतवर्मा च प्रययुर्यत्र सौबलः ।रथानीकं परित्यज्य शूराः सुदृढधन्विनः ॥४३॥
transliteration: kṛpaśca kṛtavarmā ca prayayuryatra saubalaḥ ,rathānīkaṁ parityajya śūrāḥ sudṛḍhadhanvinaḥ.
english: Kripa and Kritavarma, those brave warriors and strong archers, abandoning their chariot division, went to where Saubala (Shakuni) was.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 43. Kripa and Kritavarma, those brave warriors and strong archers, abandoning their chariot division, went to where Saubala (Shakuni) was.

sanskrit: ततस्तेषु प्रयातेषु धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमाः ।आययुः पाण्डवा राजन्विनिघ्नन्तः स्म तावकान् ॥४४॥
transliteration: tatasteṣu prayāteṣu dhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamāḥ ,āyayuḥ pāṇḍavā rājanvinighnantaḥ sma tāvakān.
english: O King, after their departure, the Pāṇḍavas, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, advanced, continuously striking down your warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 44. O King, after their departure, the Pāṇḍavas, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, advanced, continuously striking down your warriors.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु तानापततः संप्रहृष्टान्महारथान् ।पराक्रान्तांस्ततो वीरान्निराशाञ्जीविते तदा ।विवर्णमुखभूयिष्ठमभवत्तावकं बलम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu tānāpatataḥ saṁprahṛṣṭānmahārathān ,parākrāntāṁstato vīrānnirāśāñjīvite tadā ,vivarṇamukhabhūyiṣṭhamabhavattāvakaṁ balam.
english: But when your army saw those greatly delighted and mighty great charioteers (the Pāṇḍavas) attacking, at that time, your warriors became mostly pale-faced and utterly hopeless about their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 45. But when your army saw those greatly delighted and mighty great charioteers (the Pāṇḍavas) attacking, at that time, your warriors became mostly pale-faced and utterly hopeless about their lives.

sanskrit: परिक्षीणायुधान्दृष्ट्वा तानहं परिवारितान् ।राजन्बलेन द्व्यङ्गेन त्यक्त्वा जीवितमात्मनः ॥४६॥
transliteration: parikṣīṇāyudhāndṛṣṭvā tānahaṁ parivāritān ,rājanbalena dvyaṅgena tyaktvā jīvitamātmanaḥ.
english: O King, I, seeing them (my warriors) with exhausted weapons, surrounded by a two-fold army, and having given up their own lives (ātman)... (the statement is incomplete and continues in the next verse).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 46. O King, I, seeing them (my warriors) with exhausted weapons, surrounded by a two-fold army, and having given up their own lives (ātman)... (the statement is incomplete and continues in the next verse).

sanskrit: आत्मनापञ्चमोऽयुध्यं पाञ्चालस्य बलेन ह ।तस्मिन्देशे व्यवस्थाप्य यत्र शारद्वतः स्थितः ॥४७॥
transliteration: ātmanāpañcamo'yudhyaṁ pāñcālasya balena ha ,tasmindeśe vyavasthāpya yatra śāradvataḥ sthitaḥ.
english: I myself, being the fifth (among our group), indeed fought against the army of the Pañcāla. Having positioned myself in that place where Śāradvata (Kṛpa) was situated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 47. I myself, being the fifth (among our group), indeed fought against the army of the Pañcāla. Having positioned myself in that place where Śāradvata (Kṛpa) was situated.

sanskrit: संप्रयुद्धा वयं पञ्च किरीटिशरपीडिताः ।धृष्टद्युम्नं महानीकं तत्र नोऽभूद्रणो महान् ।जितास्तेन वयं सर्वे व्यपयाम रणात्ततः ॥४८॥
transliteration: saṁprayuddhā vayaṁ pañca kirīṭiśarapīḍitāḥ ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ mahānīkaṁ tatra no'bhūdraṇo mahān ,jitāstena vayaṁ sarve vyapayāma raṇāttataḥ.
english: We five, afflicted by the arrows of Kiriṭin (Arjuna), engaged in battle with Dhṛṣṭadyumna's great army. There, a mighty battle occurred for us. We were all defeated by him, and consequently, we retreated from that battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 48. We five, afflicted by the arrows of Kiriṭin (Arjuna), engaged in battle with Dhṛṣṭadyumna's great army. There, a mighty battle occurred for us. We were all defeated by him, and consequently, we retreated from that battle.

sanskrit: अथापश्यं सात्यकिं तमुपायान्तं महारथम् ।रथैश्चतुःशतैर्वीरो मां चाभ्यद्रवदाहवे ॥४९॥
transliteration: athāpaśyaṁ sātyakiṁ tamupāyāntaṁ mahāratham ,rathaiścatuḥśatairvīro māṁ cābhyadravadāhave.
english: Then I saw that Sātyaki, the great charioteer, approaching. And that hero, with four hundred chariots, rushed towards me in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 49. Then I saw that Sātyaki, the great charioteer, approaching. And that hero, with four hundred chariots, rushed towards me in battle.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नादहं मुक्तः कथंचिच्छ्रान्तवाहनः ।पतितो माधवानीकं दुष्कृती नरकं यथा ।तत्र युद्धमभूद्घोरं मुहूर्तमतिदारुणम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnādahaṁ muktaḥ kathaṁcicchrāntavāhanaḥ ,patito mādhavānīkaṁ duṣkṛtī narakaṁ yathā ,tatra yuddhamabhūdghoraṁ muhūrtamatidāruṇam.
english: I, somehow freed from Dhṛṣṭadyumna with my chariot-steeds exhausted, fell into Mādhava's army, just as a wicked person falls into hell. There, for a moment, an exceedingly fierce and terrible battle raged.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 50. I, somehow freed from Dhṛṣṭadyumna with my chariot-steeds exhausted, fell into Mādhava's army, just as a wicked person falls into hell. There, for a moment, an exceedingly fierce and terrible battle raged.

sanskrit: सात्यकिस्तु महाबाहुर्मम हत्वा परिच्छदम् ।जीवग्राहमगृह्णान्मां मूर्छितं पतितं भुवि ॥५१॥
transliteration: sātyakistu mahābāhurmama hatvā paricchadam ,jīvagrāhamagṛhṇānmāṁ mūrchitaṁ patitaṁ bhuvi.
english: But the mighty-armed Sātyaki, after killing my retinue, seized me alive while I lay unconscious, having fallen to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 51. But the mighty-armed Sātyaki, after killing my retinue, seized me alive while I lay unconscious, having fallen to the ground.

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्तादिव तद्गजानीकमवध्यत ।गदया भीमसेनेन नाराचैरर्जुनेन च ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtādiva tadgajānīkamavadhyata ,gadayā bhīmasenena nārācairarjunena ca.
english: Then, in about a moment, that elephant army was destroyed by Bhīmasena with his mace and by Arjuna with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 52. Then, in about a moment, that elephant army was destroyed by Bhīmasena with his mace and by Arjuna with his arrows.

sanskrit: प्रतिपिष्टैर्महानागैः समन्तात्पर्वतोपमैः ।नातिप्रसिद्धेव गतिः पाण्डवानामजायत ॥५३॥
transliteration: pratipiṣṭairmahānāgaiḥ samantātparvatopamaiḥ ,nātiprasiddheva gatiḥ pāṇḍavānāmajāyata.
english: As great elephants, crushed and lying all around like mountains, the Pāṇḍavas' progress was not easily discernible.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 53. As great elephants, crushed and lying all around like mountains, the Pāṇḍavas' progress was not easily discernible.

sanskrit: रथमार्गांस्ततश्चक्रे भीमसेनो महाबलः ।पाण्डवानां महाराज व्यपकर्षन्महागजान् ॥५४॥
transliteration: rathamārgāṁstataścakre bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,pāṇḍavānāṁ mahārāja vyapakarṣanmahāgajān.
english: Then, O great king (mahārāja), the mighty Bhīmasena created chariot paths by pulling aside the great elephants for the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 54. Then, O great king (mahārāja), the mighty Bhīmasena created chariot paths by pulling aside the great elephants for the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा कृपश्चैव कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ।अपश्यन्तो रथानीके दुर्योधनमरिंदमम् ।राजानं मृगयामासुस्तव पुत्रं महारथम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā kṛpaścaiva kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ ,apaśyanto rathānīke duryodhanamariṁdamam ,rājānaṁ mṛgayāmāsustava putraṁ mahāratham.
english: Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, and Kṛtavarmā, the Sātvata, not seeing King Duryodhana - your son, a great chariot-fighter, and a subduer of enemies - in the chariot division, searched for him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 55. Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, and Kṛtavarmā, the Sātvata, not seeing King Duryodhana - your son, a great chariot-fighter, and a subduer of enemies - in the chariot division, searched for him.

sanskrit: परित्यज्य च पाञ्चालं प्रयाता यत्र सौबलः ।राज्ञोऽदर्शनसंविग्ना वर्तमाने जनक्षये ॥५६॥
transliteration: parityajya ca pāñcālaṁ prayātā yatra saubalaḥ ,rājño'darśanasaṁvignā vartamāne janakṣaye.
english: Having abandoned Pāñcāla, they departed to where Saubala (Śakuni) was, distressed by the king's disappearance, while the destruction of people was ongoing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 24 Verse 56. Having abandoned Pāñcāla, they departed to where Saubala (Śakuni) was, distressed by the king's disappearance, while the destruction of people was ongoing.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।गजानीके हते तस्मिन्पाण्डुपुत्रेण भारत ।वध्यमाने बले चैव भीमसेनेन संयुगे ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,gajānīke hate tasminpāṇḍuputreṇa bhārata ,vadhyamāne bale caiva bhīmasenena saṁyuge.
english: Sañjaya said: "O Bhārata, when that elephant division had been killed by the son of Pāṇḍu, and indeed when the army was being slaughtered by Bhīmasena in battle..."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: "O Bhārata, when that elephant division had been killed by the son of Pāṇḍu, and indeed when the army was being slaughtered by Bhīmasena in battle..."

sanskrit: चरन्तं च तथा दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनमरिंदमम् ।दण्डहस्तं यथा क्रुद्धमन्तकं प्राणहारिणम् ॥२॥
transliteration: carantaṁ ca tathā dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenamariṁdamam ,daṇḍahastaṁ yathā kruddhamantakaṁ prāṇahāriṇam.
english: ...and having seen Bhīmasena, the subduer of enemies, moving about thus, mace in hand, like an enraged Antaka (Yama), the taker of lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 2. ...and having seen Bhīmasena, the subduer of enemies, moving about thus, mace in hand, like an enraged Antaka (Yama), the taker of lives.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो रणे भीमः शिरो दुर्मर्षणस्य ह ।क्षुरप्रेण प्रमथ्याशु पातयामास भूतले ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho raṇe bhīmaḥ śiro durmarṣaṇasya ha ,kṣurapreṇa pramathyāśu pātayāmāsa bhūtale.
english: Then, enraged in battle, Bhima quickly struck Durmarṣaṇa's head with a razor-sharp arrow, causing it to fall to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 7. Then, enraged in battle, Bhima quickly struck Durmarṣaṇa's head with a razor-sharp arrow, causing it to fall to the ground.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरेण भल्लेन सर्वावरणभेदिना ।श्रुतान्तमवधीद्भीमस्तव पुत्रं महारथः ॥८॥
transliteration: tato'pareṇa bhallena sarvāvaraṇabhedinā ,śrutāntamavadhīdbhīmastava putraṁ mahārathaḥ.
english: Then, with another arrow, one that could pierce all armor, Bhima, the great charioteer, struck down your son, Śrutānta.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 8. Then, with another arrow, one that could pierce all armor, Bhima, the great charioteer, struck down your son, Śrutānta.

sanskrit: जयत्सेनं ततो विद्ध्वा नाराचेन हसन्निव ।पातयामास कौरव्यं रथोपस्थादरिंदमः ।स पपात रथाद्राजन्भूमौ तूर्णं ममार च ॥९॥
transliteration: jayatsenaṁ tato viddhvā nārācena hasanniva ,pātayāmāsa kauravyaṁ rathopasthādariṁdamaḥ ,sa papāta rathādrājanbhūmau tūrṇaṁ mamāra ca.
english: Then, the subduer of enemies (Bhima), as if laughing, pierced Jayatsena, the Kuru prince, with an iron arrow and caused him to fall from his chariot seat. O King, he quickly fell from the chariot to the ground and died.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 9. Then, the subduer of enemies (Bhima), as if laughing, pierced Jayatsena, the Kuru prince, with an iron arrow and caused him to fall from his chariot seat. O King, he quickly fell from the chariot to the ground and died.

sanskrit: श्रुतर्वा तु ततो भीमं क्रुद्धो विव्याध मारिष ।शतेन गृध्रवाजानां शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: śrutarvā tu tato bhīmaṁ kruddho vivyādha māriṣa ,śatena gṛdhravājānāṁ śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām.
english: But then, enraged, Śrutarvā, O honorable one, pierced Bhima with a hundred arrows, feathered with vulture plumes and having straight shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 10. But then, enraged, Śrutarvā, O honorable one, pierced Bhima with a hundred arrows, feathered with vulture plumes and having straight shafts.

sanskrit: ततः क्रुद्धो रणे भीमो जैत्रं भूरिबलं रविम् ।त्रीनेतांस्त्रिभिरानर्छद्विषाग्निप्रतिमैः शरैः ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ kruddho raṇe bhīmo jaitraṁ bhūribalaṁ ravim ,trīnetāṁstribhirānarchadviṣāgnipratimaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, enraged, Bhīma in battle struck these three - Jaitra, Bhūribala, and Ravi - with three arrows resembling poison and fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 11. Then, enraged, Bhīma in battle struck these three - Jaitra, Bhūribala, and Ravi - with three arrows resembling poison and fire.

sanskrit: ते हता न्यपतन्भूमौ स्यन्दनेभ्यो महारथाः ।वसन्ते पुष्पशबला निकृत्ता इव किंशुकाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: te hatā nyapatanbhūmau syandanebhyo mahārathāḥ ,vasante puṣpaśabalā nikṛttā iva kiṁśukāḥ.
english: Struck down, those great chariot-warriors fell from their chariots to the ground, just like variegated Kiṃśuka trees, laden with flowers, are cut down in spring.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 12. Struck down, those great chariot-warriors fell from their chariots to the ground, just like variegated Kiṃśuka trees, laden with flowers, are cut down in spring.

sanskrit: ततोऽपरेण तीक्ष्णेन नाराचेन परंतपः ।दुर्विमोचनमाहत्य प्रेषयामास मृत्यवे ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato'pareṇa tīkṣṇena nārācena paraṁtapaḥ ,durvimocanamāhatya preṣayāmāsa mṛtyave.
english: Then, the scorcher of foes (paraṃtapa) struck Durvimocana with another sharp arrow and sent him to his death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 13. Then, the scorcher of foes (paraṃtapa) struck Durvimocana with another sharp arrow and sent him to his death.

sanskrit: स हतः प्रापतद्भूमौ स्वरथाद्रथिनां वरः ।गिरेस्तु कूटजो भग्नो मारुतेनेव पादपः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa hataḥ prāpatadbhūmau svarathādrathināṁ varaḥ ,girestu kūṭajo bhagno māruteneva pādapaḥ.
english: Struck down, he, the best of charioteers, fell from his own chariot to the ground, just as a tree growing on a mountain peak is broken by the wind (māruta).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 14. Struck down, he, the best of charioteers, fell from his own chariot to the ground, just as a tree growing on a mountain peak is broken by the wind (māruta).

sanskrit: ततः श्रुतर्वा संक्रुद्धो धनुरायम्य सायकैः ।भीमसेनं रणे राजन्बाह्वोरुरसि चार्पयत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śrutarvā saṁkruddho dhanurāyamya sāyakaiḥ ,bhīmasenaṁ raṇe rājanbāhvorurasi cārpayat.
english: Then, O King, Śrutarvan, greatly enraged, drew his bow and struck Bhīmasena in battle with arrows, piercing his arms and chest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 23. Then, O King, Śrutarvan, greatly enraged, drew his bow and struck Bhīmasena in battle with arrows, piercing his arms and chest.

sanskrit: सोऽतिविद्धो महाराज तव पुत्रेण धन्विना ।भीमः संचुक्षुभे क्रुद्धः पर्वणीव महोदधिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: so'tividdho mahārāja tava putreṇa dhanvinā ,bhīmaḥ saṁcukṣubhe kruddhaḥ parvaṇīva mahodadhiḥ.
english: O great King, Bhīma, deeply wounded by your son, the archer, became greatly agitated and enraged, just like the great ocean during a full or new moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 24. O great King, Bhīma, deeply wounded by your son, the archer, became greatly agitated and enraged, just like the great ocean during a full or new moon.

sanskrit: ततो भीमो रुषाविष्टः पुत्रस्य तव मारिष ।सारथिं चतुरश्चाश्वान्बाणैर्निन्ये यमक्षयम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato bhīmo ruṣāviṣṭaḥ putrasya tava māriṣa ,sārathiṁ caturaścāśvānbāṇairninye yamakṣayam.
english: Then, O venerable one, Bhīma, overcome by fury, sent your son's charioteer and four horses to the abode of Yama (death) with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 25. Then, O venerable one, Bhīma, overcome by fury, sent your son's charioteer and four horses to the abode of Yama (death) with his arrows.

sanskrit: विरथं तं समालक्ष्य विशिखैर्लोमवाहिभिः ।अवाकिरदमेयात्मा दर्शयन्पाणिलाघवम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: virathaṁ taṁ samālakṣya viśikhairlomavāhibhiḥ ,avākiradameyātmā darśayanpāṇilāghavam.
english: Having seen him (the son) dismounted, Bhīma, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable, showered him with sharp, hair-piercing arrows, demonstrating his remarkable hand dexterity.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 26. Having seen him (the son) dismounted, Bhīma, whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable, showered him with sharp, hair-piercing arrows, demonstrating his remarkable hand dexterity.

sanskrit: श्रुतर्वा विरथो राजन्नाददे खड्गचर्मणी ।अथास्याददतः खड्गं शतचन्द्रं च भानुमत् ।क्षुरप्रेण शिरः कायात्पातयामास पाण्डवः ॥२७॥
transliteration: śrutarvā viratho rājannādade khaḍgacarmaṇī ,athāsyādadataḥ khaḍgaṁ śatacandraṁ ca bhānumat ,kṣurapreṇa śiraḥ kāyātpātayāmāsa pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: O King, Śrutarvā, having been deprived of his chariot, took up a sword and shield. Then, as he was taking up his luminous sword, which was marked with a hundred circles, the Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena) severed his head from his body with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 27. O King, Śrutarvā, having been deprived of his chariot, took up a sword and shield. Then, as he was taking up his luminous sword, which was marked with a hundred circles, the Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena) severed his head from his body with a razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra).

sanskrit: छिन्नोत्तमाङ्गस्य ततः क्षुरप्रेण महात्मनः ।पपात कायः स रथाद्वसुधामनुनादयन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: chinnottamāṅgasya tataḥ kṣurapreṇa mahātmanaḥ ,papāta kāyaḥ sa rathādvasudhāmanunādayan.
english: Then, the body of that great-souled (mahātman) warrior, whose head had been severed by the razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra), fell from the chariot, causing the earth (vasudhā) to reverberate.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 28. Then, the body of that great-souled (mahātman) warrior, whose head had been severed by the razor-sharp arrow (kṣurapra), fell from the chariot, causing the earth (vasudhā) to reverberate.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते वीरे तावका भयमोहिताः ।अभ्यद्रवन्त संग्रामे भीमसेनं युयुत्सवः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite vīre tāvakā bhayamohitāḥ ,abhyadravanta saṁgrāme bhīmasenaṁ yuyutsavaḥ.
english: When that hero (Śrutarvā) had fallen, your warriors, bewildered by fear, then rushed towards Bhīmasena in battle, eager to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 29. When that hero (Śrutarvā) had fallen, your warriors, bewildered by fear, then rushed towards Bhīmasena in battle, eager to fight.

sanskrit: तानापतत एवाशु हतशेषाद्बलार्णवात् ।दंशितः प्रतिजग्राह भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ।ते तु तं वै समासाद्य परिवव्रुः समन्ततः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tānāpatata evāśu hataśeṣādbalārṇavāt ,daṁśitaḥ pratijagrāha bhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān ,te tu taṁ vai samāsādya parivavruḥ samantataḥ.
english: The mighty Bhīmasena, clad in armor, swiftly received them – the remaining warriors from the ocean-like army – as they advanced. Having reached him, those warriors then surrounded him from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 30. The mighty Bhīmasena, clad in armor, swiftly received them – the remaining warriors from the ocean-like army – as they advanced. Having reached him, those warriors then surrounded him from all sides.

sanskrit: ततस्तु संवृतो भीमस्तावकैर्निशितैः शरैः ।पीडयामास तान्सर्वान्सहस्राक्ष इवासुरान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tatastu saṁvṛto bhīmastāvakairniśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,pīḍayāmāsa tānsarvānsahasrākṣa ivāsurān.
english: Then, Bhima, even though surrounded by your men's sharp arrows, tormented all of them, just as Indra (sahasrākṣa) would oppress the demons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 31. Then, Bhima, even though surrounded by your men's sharp arrows, tormented all of them, just as Indra (sahasrākṣa) would oppress the demons.

sanskrit: ततः पञ्चशतान्हत्वा सवरूथान्महारथान् ।जघान कुञ्जरानीकं पुनः सप्तशतं युधि ॥३२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pañcaśatānhatvā savarūthānmahārathān ,jaghāna kuñjarānīkaṁ punaḥ saptaśataṁ yudhi.
english: Then, having slain five hundred great charioteers along with their chariots, he further killed seven hundred (members of) the elephant contingent in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 32. Then, having slain five hundred great charioteers along with their chariots, he further killed seven hundred (members of) the elephant contingent in battle.

sanskrit: हत्वा दश सहस्राणि पत्तीनां परमेषुभिः ।वाजिनां च शतान्यष्टौ पाण्डवः स्म विराजते ॥३३॥
transliteration: hatvā daśa sahasrāṇi pattīnāṁ parameṣubhiḥ ,vājināṁ ca śatānyaṣṭau pāṇḍavaḥ sma virājate.
english: Having slain ten thousand foot-soldiers with his excellent arrows, and also eight hundred horses, the Pandava (Bhima) truly shone with glory.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 33. Having slain ten thousand foot-soldiers with his excellent arrows, and also eight hundred horses, the Pandava (Bhima) truly shone with glory.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु कौन्तेयो हत्वा युद्धे सुतांस्तव ।मेने कृतार्थमात्मानं सफलं जन्म च प्रभो ॥३४॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu kaunteyo hatvā yuddhe sutāṁstava ,mene kṛtārthamātmānaṁ saphalaṁ janma ca prabho.
english: Indeed, Bhimasena, the son of Kunti, having slain your sons in battle, considered himself (ātman) to have achieved his purpose and his birth to be fruitful, O lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 34. Indeed, Bhimasena, the son of Kunti, having slain your sons in battle, considered himself (ātman) to have achieved his purpose and his birth to be fruitful, O lord.

sanskrit: तं तथा युध्यमानं च विनिघ्नन्तं च तावकान् ।ईक्षितुं नोत्सहन्ते स्म तव सैन्यानि भारत ॥३५॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā yudhyamānaṁ ca vinighnantaṁ ca tāvakān ,īkṣituṁ notsahante sma tava sainyāni bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, your armies were unable to look upon him who was thus fighting and slaying your warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 35. O descendant of Bharata, your armies were unable to look upon him who was thus fighting and slaying your warriors.

sanskrit: विद्राव्य तु कुरून्सर्वांस्तांश्च हत्वा पदानुगान् ।दोर्भ्यां शब्दं ततश्चक्रे त्रासयानो महाद्विपान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: vidrāvya tu kurūnsarvāṁstāṁśca hatvā padānugān ,dorbhyāṁ śabdaṁ tataścakre trāsayāno mahādvipān.
english: Indeed, having put all the Kurus to flight and slain their foot-soldiers, he then made a loud sound with his two arms, terrifying even the great elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 36. Indeed, having put all the Kurus to flight and slain their foot-soldiers, he then made a loud sound with his two arms, terrifying even the great elephants.

sanskrit: हतभूयिष्ठयोधा तु तव सेना विशां पते ।किंचिच्छेषा महाराज कृपणा समपद्यत ॥३७॥
transliteration: hatabhūyiṣṭhayodhā tu tava senā viśāṁ pate ,kiṁciccheṣā mahārāja kṛpaṇā samapadyata.
english: O lord of the people, O great king, your army, with most of its warriors slain and only a few remaining, truly became a pitiable sight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 25 Verse 37. O lord of the people, O great king, your army, with most of its warriors slain and only a few remaining, truly became a pitiable sight.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।दुर्योधनो महाराज सुदर्शश्चापि ते सुतः ।हतशेषौ तदा संख्ये वाजिमध्ये व्यवस्थितौ ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,duryodhano mahārāja sudarśaścāpi te sutaḥ ,hataśeṣau tadā saṁkhye vājimadhye vyavasthitau.
english: Sanjaya said: O great king, then in the battle, your son Duryodhana and Sudarśa, survivors of the slain, were situated amidst the horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: O great king, then in the battle, your son Duryodhana and Sudarśa, survivors of the slain, were situated amidst the horses.

sanskrit: असौ तिष्ठति पाञ्चाल्यः श्रिया परमया युतः ।दुर्योधनबलं हत्वा सह सर्वैः प्रभद्रकैः ॥६॥
transliteration: asau tiṣṭhati pāñcālyaḥ śriyā paramayā yutaḥ ,duryodhanabalaṁ hatvā saha sarvaiḥ prabhadrakaiḥ.
english: There stands Dhrṣṭadyumna, endowed with supreme splendor, having defeated Duryodhana's army along with all his excellent warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 6. There stands Dhrṣṭadyumna, endowed with supreme splendor, having defeated Duryodhana's army along with all his excellent warriors.

sanskrit: असौ दुर्योधनः पार्थ वाजिमध्ये व्यवस्थितः ।छत्रेण ध्रियमाणेन प्रेक्षमाणो मुहुर्मुहुः ॥७॥
transliteration: asau duryodhanaḥ pārtha vājimadhye vyavasthitaḥ ,chatreṇa dhriyamāṇena prekṣamāṇo muhurmuhuḥ.
english: O Pārtha, there stands Duryodhana amidst the horses, with an umbrella held aloft, observing the scene again and again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 7. O Pārtha, there stands Duryodhana amidst the horses, with an umbrella held aloft, observing the scene again and again.

sanskrit: प्रतिव्यूह्य बलं सर्वं रणमध्ये व्यवस्थितः ।एनं हत्वा शितैर्बाणैः कृतकृत्यो भविष्यसि ॥८॥
transliteration: prativyūhya balaṁ sarvaṁ raṇamadhye vyavasthitaḥ ,enaṁ hatvā śitairbāṇaiḥ kṛtakṛtyo bhaviṣyasi.
english: Having arrayed his entire army in the midst of battle, he stands there. By killing him with sharp arrows, you will have accomplished your task.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 8. Having arrayed his entire army in the midst of battle, he stands there. By killing him with sharp arrows, you will have accomplished your task.

sanskrit: गजानीकं हतं दृष्ट्वा त्वां च प्राप्तमरिंदम ।यावन्न विद्रवन्त्येते तावज्जहि सुयोधनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: gajānīkaṁ hataṁ dṛṣṭvā tvāṁ ca prāptamariṁdama ,yāvanna vidravantyete tāvajjahi suyodhanam.
english: O destroyer of enemies (ariṃdama), seeing the elephant army annihilated and you arrived, kill Suyodhana before these (soldiers) scatter and flee.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 9. O destroyer of enemies (ariṃdama), seeing the elephant army annihilated and you arrived, kill Suyodhana before these (soldiers) scatter and flee.

sanskrit: यातु कश्चित्तु पाञ्चाल्यं क्षिप्रमागम्यतामिति ।परिश्रान्तबलस्तात नैष मुच्येत किल्बिषी ॥१०॥
transliteration: yātu kaścittu pāñcālyaṁ kṣipramāgamyatāmiti ,pariśrāntabalastāta naiṣa mucyeta kilbiṣī.
english: Someone should go quickly to the son of Pañcāla (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) and inform him to come. Dear one, this sinful man, whose army is exhausted, will not escape.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 10. Someone should go quickly to the son of Pañcāla (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) and inform him to come. Dear one, this sinful man, whose army is exhausted, will not escape.

sanskrit: तव हत्वा बलं सर्वं संग्रामे धृतराष्ट्रजः ।जितान्पाण्डुसुतान्मत्वा रूपं धारयते महत् ॥११॥
transliteration: tava hatvā balaṁ sarvaṁ saṁgrāme dhṛtarāṣṭrajaḥ ,jitānpāṇḍusutānmatvā rūpaṁ dhārayate mahat.
english: The son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having destroyed your entire army in battle, and believing the sons of Pāṇḍu to be conquered, assumes a formidable appearance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 11. The son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having destroyed your entire army in battle, and believing the sons of Pāṇḍu to be conquered, assumes a formidable appearance.

sanskrit: निहतं स्वबलं दृष्ट्वा पीडितं चापि पाण्डवैः ।ध्रुवमेष्यति संग्रामे वधायैवात्मनो नृपः ॥१२॥
transliteration: nihataṁ svabalaṁ dṛṣṭvā pīḍitaṁ cāpi pāṇḍavaiḥ ,dhruvameṣyati saṁgrāme vadhāyaivātmano nṛpaḥ.
english: Upon seeing his own army destroyed and oppressed by the Pāṇḍavas, the king will certainly come to battle for his very own demise.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 12. Upon seeing his own army destroyed and oppressed by the Pāṇḍavas, the king will certainly come to battle for his very own demise.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः फल्गुनस्तु कृष्णं वचनमब्रवीत् ।धृतराष्ट्रसुताः सर्वे हता भीमेन मानद ।यावेतावास्थितौ कृष्ण तावद्य न भविष्यतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ phalgunastu kṛṣṇaṁ vacanamabravīt ,dhṛtarāṣṭrasutāḥ sarve hatā bhīmena mānada ,yāvetāvāsthitau kṛṣṇa tāvadya na bhaviṣyataḥ.
english: Thus addressed, Arjuna then spoke these words to Kṛṣṇa: 'O revered one, all the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra have been killed by Bhīma. O Kṛṣṇa, these two (enemies) who stand firm, they will not exist today.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 13. Thus addressed, Arjuna then spoke these words to Kṛṣṇa: 'O revered one, all the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra have been killed by Bhīma. O Kṛṣṇa, these two (enemies) who stand firm, they will not exist today.'

sanskrit: हतो भीष्मो हतो द्रोणः कर्णो वैकर्तनो हतः ।मद्रराजो हतः शल्यो हतः कृष्ण जयद्रथः ॥१४॥
transliteration: hato bhīṣmo hato droṇaḥ karṇo vaikartano hataḥ ,madrarājo hataḥ śalyo hataḥ kṛṣṇa jayadrathaḥ.
english: Bhīṣma is slain, Droṇa is slain, Karṇa, the son of Vikartana, is slain. The king of Madras, Śalya, is slain, and Jayadratha is slain, O Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 14. Bhīṣma is slain, Droṇa is slain, Karṇa, the son of Vikartana, is slain. The king of Madras, Śalya, is slain, and Jayadratha is slain, O Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: हयाः पञ्चशताः शिष्टाः शकुनेः सौबलस्य च ।रथानां तु शते शिष्टे द्वे एव तु जनार्दन ।दन्तिनां च शतं साग्रं त्रिसाहस्राः पदातयः ॥१५॥
transliteration: hayāḥ pañcaśatāḥ śiṣṭāḥ śakuneḥ saubalasya ca ,rathānāṁ tu śate śiṣṭe dve eva tu janārdana ,dantināṁ ca śataṁ sāgraṁ trisāhasrāḥ padātayaḥ.
english: O Janārdana, only five hundred horses of Śakuni, son of Subala, remain. Indeed, only two hundred chariots remain. And a hundred elephants, plus three thousand foot soldiers (remain).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 15. O Janārdana, only five hundred horses of Śakuni, son of Subala, remain. Indeed, only two hundred chariots remain. And a hundred elephants, plus three thousand foot soldiers (remain).

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा कृपश्चैव त्रिगर्ताधिपतिस्तथा ।उलूकः शकुनिश्चैव कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā kṛpaścaiva trigartādhipatistathā ,ulūkaḥ śakuniścaiva kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ.
english: Aśvatthāman and Kṛpa, and also the lord of the Trigartas (Susarman), Ulūka and Śakuni, and Kṛtavarman of the Sātvata clan (these remain).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 16. Aśvatthāman and Kṛpa, and also the lord of the Trigartas (Susarman), Ulūka and Śakuni, and Kṛtavarman of the Sātvata clan (these remain).

sanskrit: एतद्बलमभूच्छेषं धार्तराष्ट्रस्य माधव ।मोक्षो न नूनं कालाद्धि विद्यते भुवि कस्यचित् ॥१७॥
transliteration: etadbalamabhūccheṣaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya mādhava ,mokṣo na nūnaṁ kālāddhi vidyate bhuvi kasyacit.
english: O Mādhava, this indeed was the remaining force of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son. Verily, for no one on earth is there final liberation (mokṣa) from time (fate).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 17. O Mādhava, this indeed was the remaining force of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son. Verily, for no one on earth is there final liberation (mokṣa) from time (fate).

sanskrit: तथा विनिहते सैन्ये पश्य दुर्योधनं स्थितम् ।अद्याह्ना हि महाराजो हतामित्रो भविष्यति ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathā vinihate sainye paśya duryodhanaṁ sthitam ,adyāhnā hi mahārājo hatāmitro bhaviṣyati.
english: Thus, as the army is being destroyed, behold Duryodhana standing. Indeed, today the great king will vanquish his foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 18. Thus, as the army is being destroyed, behold Duryodhana standing. Indeed, today the great king will vanquish his foes.

sanskrit: न हि मे मोक्ष्यते कश्चित्परेषामिति चिन्तये ।ये त्वद्य समरं कृष्ण न हास्यन्ति रणोत्कटाः ।तान्वै सर्वान्हनिष्यामि यद्यपि स्युरमानुषाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: na hi me mokṣyate kaścitpareṣāmiti cintaye ,ye tvadya samaraṁ kṛṣṇa na hāsyanti raṇotkaṭāḥ ,tānvai sarvānhaniṣyāmi yadyapi syuramānuṣāḥ.
english: Indeed, I do not believe anyone from the enemy ranks will escape me. Those who are eager for battle, O Kṛṣṇa, and will not abandon the fight today - I will surely kill all of them, even if they are superhuman.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 19. Indeed, I do not believe anyone from the enemy ranks will escape me. Those who are eager for battle, O Kṛṣṇa, and will not abandon the fight today - I will surely kill all of them, even if they are superhuman.

sanskrit: अद्य युद्धे सुसंक्रुद्धो दीर्घं राज्ञः प्रजागरम् ।अपनेष्यामि गान्धारं पातयित्वा शितैः शरैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: adya yuddhe susaṁkruddho dīrghaṁ rājñaḥ prajāgaram ,apaneṣyāmi gāndhāraṁ pātayitvā śitaiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Today, extremely enraged in battle, I will put an end to the king's long sleeplessness by striking down the Gandhara king with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 20. Today, extremely enraged in battle, I will put an end to the king's long sleeplessness by striking down the Gandhara king with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: निकृत्या वै दुराचारो यानि रत्नानि सौबलः ।सभायामहरद्द्यूते पुनस्तान्याहराम्यहम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: nikṛtyā vai durācāro yāni ratnāni saubalaḥ ,sabhāyāmaharaddyūte punastānyāharāmyaham.
english: Indeed, by deceit, that evil-doer Saubala (Śakuni) had seized whatever jewels in the assembly during the dice game - those I will recover again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 21. Indeed, by deceit, that evil-doer Saubala (Śakuni) had seized whatever jewels in the assembly during the dice game - those I will recover again.

sanskrit: अद्य ता अपि वेत्स्यन्ति सर्वा नागपुरस्त्रियः ।श्रुत्वा पतींश्च पुत्रांश्च पाण्डवैर्निहतान्युधि ॥२२॥
transliteration: adya tā api vetsyanti sarvā nāgapurastriyaḥ ,śrutvā patīṁśca putrāṁśca pāṇḍavairnihatānyudhi.
english: Today, all those women of Hastinapura will also know, having heard that their husbands and sons have been killed in battle by the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 22. Today, all those women of Hastinapura will also know, having heard that their husbands and sons have been killed in battle by the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: समाप्तमद्य वै कर्म सर्वं कृष्ण भविष्यति ।अद्य दुर्योधनो दीप्तां श्रियं प्राणांश्च त्यक्ष्यति ॥२३॥
transliteration: samāptamadya vai karma sarvaṁ kṛṣṇa bhaviṣyati ,adya duryodhano dīptāṁ śriyaṁ prāṇāṁśca tyakṣyati.
english: Indeed, O Kṛṣṇa, all (his) actions (karma) will be concluded today. Today, Duryodhana will abandon his resplendent glory and his very life.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 23. Indeed, O Kṛṣṇa, all (his) actions (karma) will be concluded today. Today, Duryodhana will abandon his resplendent glory and his very life.

sanskrit: नापयाति भयात्कृष्ण संग्रामाद्यदि चेन्मम ।निहतं विद्धि वार्ष्णेय धार्तराष्ट्रं सुबालिशम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: nāpayāti bhayātkṛṣṇa saṁgrāmādyadi cenmama ,nihataṁ viddhi vārṣṇeya dhārtarāṣṭraṁ subāliśam.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, if he does not flee from my battle out of fear, then know, O descendent of Vṛṣṇi, that the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the exceedingly foolish one, is slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 24. O Kṛṣṇa, if he does not flee from my battle out of fear, then know, O descendent of Vṛṣṇi, that the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the exceedingly foolish one, is slain.

sanskrit: मम ह्येतदशक्तं वै वाजिवृन्दमरिंदम ।सोढुं ज्यातलनिर्घोषं याहि यावन्निहन्म्यहम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: mama hyetadaśaktaṁ vai vājivṛndamariṁdama ,soḍhuṁ jyātalanirghoṣaṁ yāhi yāvannihanmyaham.
english: Indeed, O subduer of enemies, this host of my horses is truly unable to endure the twang of my bowstring. Go forth while I slay (him/them).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 25. Indeed, O subduer of enemies, this host of my horses is truly unable to endure the twang of my bowstring. Go forth while I slay (him/them).

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु दाशार्हः पाण्डवेन यशस्विना ।अचोदयद्धयान्राजन्दुर्योधनबलं प्रति ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktastu dāśārhaḥ pāṇḍavena yaśasvinā ,acodayaddhayānrājanduryodhanabalaṁ prati.
english: O King, thus addressed by the glorious Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), Daśārha (Krishna) urged his horses towards Duryodhana's army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 26. O King, thus addressed by the glorious Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), Daśārha (Krishna) urged his horses towards Duryodhana's army.

sanskrit: तदनीकमभिप्रेक्ष्य त्रयः सज्जा महारथाः ।भीमसेनोऽर्जुनश्चैव सहदेवश्च मारिष ।प्रययुः सिंहनादेन दुर्योधनजिघांसया ॥२७॥
transliteration: tadanīkamabhiprekṣya trayaḥ sajjā mahārathāḥ ,bhīmaseno'rjunaścaiva sahadevaśca māriṣa ,prayayuḥ siṁhanādena duryodhanajighāṁsayā.
english: O respected one, seeing that army, three prepared great warriors - Bhimasena, Arjuna, and Sahadeva - advanced with a lion's roar, motivated by the desire to kill Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 27. O respected one, seeing that army, three prepared great warriors - Bhimasena, Arjuna, and Sahadeva - advanced with a lion's roar, motivated by the desire to kill Duryodhana.

sanskrit: तान्प्रेक्ष्य सहितान्सर्वाञ्जवेनोद्यतकार्मुकान् ।सौबलोऽभ्यद्रवद्युद्धे पाण्डवानाततायिनः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tānprekṣya sahitānsarvāñjavenodyatakārmukān ,saubalo'bhyadravadyuddhe pāṇḍavānātatāyinaḥ.
english: Having seen all of them assembled and with bows raised, Saubala (Śakuni) swiftly rushed in battle towards the Pāṇḍavas, who were aggressors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 28. Having seen all of them assembled and with bows raised, Saubala (Śakuni) swiftly rushed in battle towards the Pāṇḍavas, who were aggressors.

sanskrit: सुदर्शनस्तव सुतो भीमसेनं समभ्ययात् ।सुशर्मा शकुनिश्चैव युयुधाते किरीटिना ।सहदेवं तव सुतो हयपृष्ठगतोऽभ्ययात् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sudarśanastava suto bhīmasenaṁ samabhyayāt ,suśarmā śakuniścaiva yuyudhāte kirīṭinā ,sahadevaṁ tava suto hayapṛṣṭhagato'bhyayāt.
english: Your son Sudarśana approached Bhimasena. Suśarmā and Śakuni indeed fought with the crowned one (Arjuna). Your son, mounted on a horse, approached Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 26 Verse 29. Your son Sudarśana approached Bhimasena. Suśarmā and Śakuni indeed fought with the crowned one (Arjuna). Your son, mounted on a horse, approached Sahadeva.

sanskrit: इह कीर्तिं समाधाय प्रेत्य लोकान्समश्नुते ।प्राणाञ्जहाति यो वीरो युधि पृष्ठमदर्शयन् ॥२०॥
transliteration: iha kīrtiṁ samādhāya pretya lokānsamaśnute ,prāṇāñjahāti yo vīro yudhi pṛṣṭhamadarśayan.
english: A hero who gives up his life in battle, not showing his back, establishes fame in this world and attains (higher) worlds after death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 20. A hero who gives up his life in battle, not showing his back, establishes fame in this world and attains (higher) worlds after death.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तास्तु ते राज्ञा सौबलस्य पदानुगाः ।पाण्डवानभ्यवर्तन्त मृत्युं कृत्वा निवर्तनम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: evamuktāstu te rājñā saubalasya padānugāḥ ,pāṇḍavānabhyavartanta mṛtyuṁ kṛtvā nivartanam.
english: Thus addressed by the king, those followers of Saubala advanced against the Pāṇḍavas, having resolved upon death instead of retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 21. Thus addressed by the king, those followers of Saubala advanced against the Pāṇḍavas, having resolved upon death instead of retreat.

sanskrit: द्रवद्भिस्तत्र राजेन्द्र कृतः शब्दोऽतिदारुणः ।क्षुब्धसागरसंकाशः क्षुभितः सर्वतोऽभवत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: dravadbhistatra rājendra kṛtaḥ śabdo'tidāruṇaḥ ,kṣubdhasāgarasaṁkāśaḥ kṣubhitaḥ sarvato'bhavat.
english: There, O king of kings, the rushing (warriors) created an exceedingly terrible sound, resembling an agitated ocean, and everything became agitated on all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 22. There, O king of kings, the rushing (warriors) created an exceedingly terrible sound, resembling an agitated ocean, and everything became agitated on all sides.

sanskrit: तांस्तदापततो दृष्ट्वा सौबलस्य पदानुगान् ।प्रत्युद्ययुर्महाराज पाण्डवा विजये वृताः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tāṁstadāpatato dṛṣṭvā saubalasya padānugān ,pratyudyayurmahārāja pāṇḍavā vijaye vṛtāḥ.
english: Then, O great king, having seen those followers of Saubala attacking, the Pāṇḍavas, intent on victory, advanced against them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 23. Then, O great king, having seen those followers of Saubala attacking, the Pāṇḍavas, intent on victory, advanced against them.

sanskrit: प्रत्याश्वस्य च दुर्धर्षः सहदेवो विशां पते ।शकुनिं दशभिर्विद्ध्वा हयांश्चास्य त्रिभिः शरैः ।धनुश्चिच्छेद च शरैः सौबलस्य हसन्निव ॥२४॥
transliteration: pratyāśvasya ca durdharṣaḥ sahadevo viśāṁ pate ,śakuniṁ daśabhirviddhvā hayāṁścāsya tribhiḥ śaraiḥ ,dhanuściccheda ca śaraiḥ saubalasya hasanniva.
english: And recovering, the unassailable Sahadeva, O lord of men, pierced Shakuni with ten (arrows) and his horses with three arrows. He also cut down the bow of Saubala's son with arrows, as if laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 24. And recovering, the unassailable Sahadeva, O lord of men, pierced Shakuni with ten (arrows) and his horses with three arrows. He also cut down the bow of Saubala's son with arrows, as if laughing.

sanskrit: अथान्यद्धनुरादाय शकुनिर्युद्धदुर्मदः ।विव्याध नकुलं षष्ट्या भीमसेनं च सप्तभिः ॥२५॥
transliteration: athānyaddhanurādāya śakuniryuddhadurmadaḥ ,vivyādha nakulaṁ ṣaṣṭyā bhīmasenaṁ ca saptabhiḥ.
english: Then Shakuni, who was fierce in battle, took another bow and pierced Nakula with sixty (arrows) and Bhimasena with seven (arrows).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 25. Then Shakuni, who was fierce in battle, took another bow and pierced Nakula with sixty (arrows) and Bhimasena with seven (arrows).

sanskrit: उलूकोऽपि महाराज भीमं विव्याध सप्तभिः ।सहदेवं च सप्तत्या परीप्सन्पितरं रणे ॥२६॥
transliteration: ulūko'pi mahārāja bhīmaṁ vivyādha saptabhiḥ ,sahadevaṁ ca saptatyā parīpsanpitaraṁ raṇe.
english: O great king, Uluka also pierced Bhima with seven (arrows) and Sahadeva with seventy (arrows), desiring to protect his father in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 26. O great king, Uluka also pierced Bhima with seven (arrows) and Sahadeva with seventy (arrows), desiring to protect his father in battle.

sanskrit: तं भीमसेनः समरे विव्याध निशितैः शरैः ।शकुनिं च चतुःषष्ट्या पार्श्वस्थांश्च त्रिभिस्त्रिभिः ॥२७॥
transliteration: taṁ bhīmasenaḥ samare vivyādha niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,śakuniṁ ca catuḥṣaṣṭyā pārśvasthāṁśca tribhistribhiḥ.
english: In battle, Bhimasena pierced him (Uluka) with sharp arrows, Shakuni with sixty-four (arrows), and those standing by his side with three (arrows) each.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 27. In battle, Bhimasena pierced him (Uluka) with sharp arrows, Shakuni with sixty-four (arrows), and those standing by his side with three (arrows) each.

sanskrit: ते हन्यमाना भीमेन नाराचैस्तैलपायितैः ।सहदेवं रणे क्रुद्धाश्छादयञ्शरवृष्टिभिः ।पर्वतं वारिधाराभिः सविद्युत इवाम्बुदाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: te hanyamānā bhīmena nārācaistailapāyitaiḥ ,sahadevaṁ raṇe kruddhāśchādayañśaravṛṣṭibhiḥ ,parvataṁ vāridhārābhiḥ savidyuta ivāmbudāḥ.
english: Though being struck by Bhima with oil-soaked iron arrows, they became enraged in battle and covered Sahadeva with a hail of arrows, just as lightning-bearing clouds shroud a mountain with torrents of rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 28. Though being struck by Bhima with oil-soaked iron arrows, they became enraged in battle and covered Sahadeva with a hail of arrows, just as lightning-bearing clouds shroud a mountain with torrents of rain.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्यापततः शूरः सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।उलूकस्य महाराज भल्लेनापाहरच्छिरः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato'syāpatataḥ śūraḥ sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,ulūkasya mahārāja bhallenāpāharacchiraḥ.
english: Then, O great king (Maharaja), the brave and mighty Sahadeva, with a broad-headed arrow, severed the head of Uluka as he charged.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 29. Then, O great king (Maharaja), the brave and mighty Sahadeva, with a broad-headed arrow, severed the head of Uluka as he charged.

sanskrit: स जगाम रथाद्भूमिं सहदेवेन पातितः ।रुधिराप्लुतसर्वाङ्गो नन्दयन्पाण्डवान्युधि ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa jagāma rathādbhūmiṁ sahadevena pātitaḥ ,rudhirāplutasarvāṅgo nandayanpāṇḍavānyudhi.
english: Struck down by Sahadeva, his entire body drenched in blood, he went from his chariot to the ground, thus delighting the Pandavas in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 30. Struck down by Sahadeva, his entire body drenched in blood, he went from his chariot to the ground, thus delighting the Pandavas in battle.

sanskrit: पुत्रं तु निहतं दृष्ट्वा शकुनिस्तत्र भारत ।साश्रुकण्ठो विनिःश्वस्य क्षत्तुर्वाक्यमनुस्मरन् ॥३१॥
transliteration: putraṁ tu nihataṁ dṛṣṭvā śakunistatra bhārata ,sāśrukaṇṭho viniḥśvasya kṣatturvākyamanusmaran.
english: But, O Bharata, Shakuni, seeing his son slain there, and with his throat choked by tears, sighed deeply, remembering the words of Vidura (kṣattṛ).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 31. But, O Bharata, Shakuni, seeing his son slain there, and with his throat choked by tears, sighed deeply, remembering the words of Vidura (kṣattṛ).

sanskrit: असिं दृष्ट्वा द्विधा छिन्नं प्रगृह्य महतीं गदाम् ।प्राहिणोत्सहदेवाय सा मोघा न्यपतद्भुवि ॥३६॥
transliteration: asiṁ dṛṣṭvā dvidhā chinnaṁ pragṛhya mahatīṁ gadām ,prāhiṇotsahadevāya sā moghā nyapatadbhuvi.
english: Having seen his sword cut in two, he grasped a great mace and sent it towards Sahadeva; but that mace fell uselessly to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 36. Having seen his sword cut in two, he grasped a great mace and sent it towards Sahadeva; but that mace fell uselessly to the ground.

sanskrit: ततः शक्तिं महाघोरां कालरात्रिमिवोद्यताम् ।प्रेषयामास संक्रुद्धः पाण्डवं प्रति सौबलः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaktiṁ mahāghorāṁ kālarātrimivodyatām ,preṣayāmāsa saṁkruddhaḥ pāṇḍavaṁ prati saubalaḥ.
english: Then, greatly enraged, Śakuni, son of Subala, sent forth a very terrible spear (śakti) towards the Pāṇḍava, Sahadeva, like the upraised Night of Dissolution (Kālarātri).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 37. Then, greatly enraged, Śakuni, son of Subala, sent forth a very terrible spear (śakti) towards the Pāṇḍava, Sahadeva, like the upraised Night of Dissolution (Kālarātri).

sanskrit: तामापतन्तीं सहसा शरैः काञ्चनभूषणैः ।त्रिधा चिच्छेद समरे सहदेवो हसन्निव ॥३८॥
transliteration: tāmāpatantīṁ sahasā śaraiḥ kāñcanabhūṣaṇaiḥ ,tridhā ciccheda samare sahadevo hasanniva.
english: Sahadeva, as if laughing, suddenly cut that descending spear (śakti) into three pieces with his gold-adorned arrows in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 38. Sahadeva, as if laughing, suddenly cut that descending spear (śakti) into three pieces with his gold-adorned arrows in battle.

sanskrit: सा पपात त्रिधा छिन्ना भूमौ कनकभूषणा ।शीर्यमाणा यथा दीप्ता गगनाद्वै शतह्रदा ॥३९॥
transliteration: sā papāta tridhā chinnā bhūmau kanakabhūṣaṇā ,śīryamāṇā yathā dīptā gaganādvai śatahradā.
english: That gold-adorned spear (śakti), severed into three pieces, fell to the ground, shattering just as brilliant lightning indeed falls from the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 39. That gold-adorned spear (śakti), severed into three pieces, fell to the ground, shattering just as brilliant lightning indeed falls from the sky.

sanskrit: शक्तिं विनिहतां दृष्ट्वा सौबलं च भयार्दितम् ।दुद्रुवुस्तावकाः सर्वे भये जाते ससौबलाः ॥४०॥
transliteration: śaktiṁ vinihatāṁ dṛṣṭvā saubalaṁ ca bhayārditam ,dudruvustāvakāḥ sarve bhaye jāte sasaubalāḥ.
english: Seeing their power (śakti) shattered and Saubala (Śakuni) afflicted by fear, all your warriors, accompanied by Saubala (Śakuni), fled when fear arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 40. Seeing their power (śakti) shattered and Saubala (Śakuni) afflicted by fear, all your warriors, accompanied by Saubala (Śakuni), fled when fear arose.

sanskrit: अथोत्क्रुष्टं महद्ध्यासीत्पाण्डवैर्जितकाशिभिः ।धार्तराष्ट्रास्ततः सर्वे प्रायशो विमुखाभवन् ॥४१॥
transliteration: athotkruṣṭaṁ mahaddhyāsītpāṇḍavairjitakāśibhiḥ ,dhārtarāṣṭrāstataḥ sarve prāyaśo vimukhābhavan.
english: Then, a great shout was raised by the Pāṇḍavas, who were shining with victory. Consequently, almost all the Dhārtarāṣṭras turned away, becoming disheartened.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 41. Then, a great shout was raised by the Pāṇḍavas, who were shining with victory. Consequently, almost all the Dhārtarāṣṭras turned away, becoming disheartened.

sanskrit: तान्वै विमनसो दृष्ट्वा माद्रीपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।शरैरनेकसाहस्रैर्वारयामास संयुगे ॥४२॥
transliteration: tānvai vimanaso dṛṣṭvā mādrīputraḥ pratāpavān ,śarairanekasāhasrairvārayāmāsa saṁyuge.
english: Indeed, seeing them disheartened, the mighty son of Mādrī (Sahadeva) checked them in battle with thousands of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 42. Indeed, seeing them disheartened, the mighty son of Mādrī (Sahadeva) checked them in battle with thousands of arrows.

sanskrit: ततो गान्धारकैर्गुप्तं पृष्ठैरश्वैर्जये धृतम् ।आससाद रणे यान्तं सहदेवोऽथ सौबलम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tato gāndhārakairguptaṁ pṛṣṭhairaśvairjaye dhṛtam ,āsasāda raṇe yāntaṁ sahadevo'tha saubalam.
english: Then, Sahadeva, in battle, approached Saubala (Śakuni), who was advancing, protected by the Gāndhāra warriors and by his horses from behind, and who was intent on victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 43. Then, Sahadeva, in battle, approached Saubala (Śakuni), who was advancing, protected by the Gāndhāra warriors and by his horses from behind, and who was intent on victory.

sanskrit: स्वमंशमवशिष्टं स संस्मृत्य शकुनिं नृप ।रथेन काञ्चनाङ्गेन सहदेवः समभ्ययात् ।अधिज्यं बलवत्कृत्वा व्याक्षिपन्सुमहद्धनुः ॥४४॥
transliteration: svamaṁśamavaśiṣṭaṁ sa saṁsmṛtya śakuniṁ nṛpa ,rathena kāñcanāṅgena sahadevaḥ samabhyayāt ,adhijyaṁ balavatkṛtvā vyākṣipansumahaddhanuḥ.
english: O King, recalling his remaining grievance (karma) against Shakuni, Sahadeva advanced in his chariot, which was adorned with gold. He powerfully strung his very great bow and repeatedly drew it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 44. O King, recalling his remaining grievance (karma) against Shakuni, Sahadeva advanced in his chariot, which was adorned with gold. He powerfully strung his very great bow and repeatedly drew it.

sanskrit: स सौबलमभिद्रुत्य गृध्रपत्रैः शिलाशितैः ।भृशमभ्यहनत्क्रुद्धस्तोत्त्रैरिव महाद्विपम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa saubalamabhidrutya gṛdhrapatraiḥ śilāśitaiḥ ,bhṛśamabhyahanatkruddhastottrairiva mahādvipam.
english: Enraged, he (Sahadeva), having rushed towards Shakuni (Saubala), struck him intensely with his stone-sharpened, vulture-feathered arrows, just as one would strike a great elephant with goads.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 45. Enraged, he (Sahadeva), having rushed towards Shakuni (Saubala), struck him intensely with his stone-sharpened, vulture-feathered arrows, just as one would strike a great elephant with goads.

sanskrit: उवाच चैनं मेधावी निगृह्य स्मारयन्निव ।क्षत्रधर्मे स्थितो भूत्वा युध्यस्व पुरुषो भव ॥४६॥
transliteration: uvāca cainaṁ medhāvī nigṛhya smārayanniva ,kṣatradharme sthito bhūtvā yudhyasva puruṣo bhava.
english: And the intelligent Sahadeva said to him, having admonished him and as if reminding him: "Having become established in the "kṣatriya"'s natural law (dharma), fight! Be a man (puruṣa)!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 46. And the intelligent Sahadeva said to him, having admonished him and as if reminding him: "Having become established in the "kṣatriya"'s natural law (dharma), fight! Be a man (puruṣa)!"

sanskrit: यत्तदा हृष्यसे मूढ ग्लहन्नक्षैः सभातले ।फलमद्य प्रपद्यस्व कर्मणस्तस्य दुर्मते ॥४७॥
transliteration: yattadā hṛṣyase mūḍha glahannakṣaiḥ sabhātale ,phalamadya prapadyasva karmaṇastasya durmate.
english: O foolish, evil-minded one! That over which you rejoiced then, while gambling with dice on the assembly hall floor - today, receive the fruit of that action (karma)!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 47. O foolish, evil-minded one! That over which you rejoiced then, while gambling with dice on the assembly hall floor - today, receive the fruit of that action (karma)!

sanskrit: निहतास्ते दुरात्मानो येऽस्मानवहसन्पुरा ।दुर्योधनः कुलाङ्गारः शिष्टस्त्वं तस्य मातुलः ॥४८॥
transliteration: nihatāste durātmāno ye'smānavahasanpurā ,duryodhanaḥ kulāṅgāraḥ śiṣṭastvaṁ tasya mātulaḥ.
english: Those wicked souls (durātmā) who formerly mocked us have been slain. Duryodhana, the disgrace of his family, remains, and you are his maternal uncle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 48. Those wicked souls (durātmā) who formerly mocked us have been slain. Duryodhana, the disgrace of his family, remains, and you are his maternal uncle.

sanskrit: अद्य ते विहनिष्यामि क्षुरेणोन्मथितं शिरः ।वृक्षात्फलमिवोद्धृत्य लगुडेन प्रमाथिना ॥४९॥
transliteration: adya te vihaniṣyāmi kṣureṇonmathitaṁ śiraḥ ,vṛkṣātphalamivoddhṛtya laguḍena pramāthinā.
english: Today I will destroy your mangled head with a razor, just as one pulls off a fruit from a tree with a crushing club.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 49. Today I will destroy your mangled head with a razor, just as one pulls off a fruit from a tree with a crushing club.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज सहदेवो महाबलः ।संक्रुद्धो नरशार्दूलो वेगेनाभिजगाम ह ॥५०॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja sahadevo mahābalaḥ ,saṁkruddho naraśārdūlo vegenābhijagāma ha.
english: Having spoken thus, O great king, Sahadeva, the mighty one, that tiger among men, became greatly enraged and indeed advanced swiftly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 50. Having spoken thus, O great king, Sahadeva, the mighty one, that tiger among men, became greatly enraged and indeed advanced swiftly.

sanskrit: अभिगम्य तु दुर्धर्षः सहदेवो युधां पतिः ।विकृष्य बलवच्चापं क्रोधेन प्रहसन्निव ॥५१॥
transliteration: abhigamya tu durdharṣaḥ sahadevo yudhāṁ patiḥ ,vikṛṣya balavaccāpaṁ krodhena prahasanniva.
english: Having approached, the formidable Sahadeva, lord of battles, drew his powerful bow, as if laughing in anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 51. Having approached, the formidable Sahadeva, lord of battles, drew his powerful bow, as if laughing in anger.

sanskrit: शकुनिं दशभिर्विद्ध्वा चतुर्भिश्चास्य वाजिनः ।छत्रं ध्वजं धनुश्चास्य छित्त्वा सिंह इवानदत् ॥५२॥
transliteration: śakuniṁ daśabhirviddhvā caturbhiścāsya vājinaḥ ,chatraṁ dhvajaṁ dhanuścāsya chittvā siṁha ivānadat.
english: He pierced Śakuni with ten arrows and his horses with four. Then, cutting down his umbrella, banner, and bow, he roared like a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 52. He pierced Śakuni with ten arrows and his horses with four. Then, cutting down his umbrella, banner, and bow, he roared like a lion.

sanskrit: छिन्नध्वजधनुश्छत्रः सहदेवेन सौबलः ।ततो विद्धश्च बहुभिः सर्वमर्मसु सायकैः ॥५३॥
transliteration: chinnadhvajadhanuśchatraḥ sahadevena saubalaḥ ,tato viddhaśca bahubhiḥ sarvamarmasu sāyakaiḥ.
english: The son of Subala (Śakuni), whose banner, bow, and umbrella had been severed by Sahadeva, was then pierced by many arrows in all his vital points.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 53. The son of Subala (Śakuni), whose banner, bow, and umbrella had been severed by Sahadeva, was then pierced by many arrows in all his vital points.

sanskrit: ततो भूयो महाराज सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।शकुनेः प्रेषयामास शरवृष्टिं दुरासदाम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: tato bhūyo mahārāja sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,śakuneḥ preṣayāmāsa śaravṛṣṭiṁ durāsadām.
english: Then again, O great king, the mighty Sahadeva sent forth an irresistible shower of arrows upon Śakuni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 54. Then again, O great king, the mighty Sahadeva sent forth an irresistible shower of arrows upon Śakuni.

sanskrit: ततस्तु क्रुद्धः सुबलस्य पुत्रो माद्रीसुतं सहदेवं विमर्दे ।प्रासेन जाम्बूनदभूषणेन जिघांसुरेकोऽभिपपात शीघ्रम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: tatastu kruddhaḥ subalasya putro; mādrīsutaṁ sahadevaṁ vimarde ,prāsena jāmbūnadabhūṣaṇena; jighāṁsureko'bhipapāta śīghram.
english: Then, enraged, the son of Subala (Śakuni) swiftly rushed forward alone in the battle, desiring to kill Sahadeva, the son of Mādrī, with a javelin adorned with Jambūnada gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 55. Then, enraged, the son of Subala (Śakuni) swiftly rushed forward alone in the battle, desiring to kill Sahadeva, the son of Mādrī, with a javelin adorned with Jambūnada gold.

sanskrit: माद्रीसुतस्तस्य समुद्यतं तं प्रासं सुवृत्तौ च भुजौ रणाग्रे ।भल्लैस्त्रिभिर्युगपत्संचकर्त ननाद चोच्चैस्तरसाजिमध्ये ॥५६॥
transliteration: mādrīsutastasya samudyataṁ taṁ; prāsaṁ suvṛttau ca bhujau raṇāgre ,bhallaistribhiryugapatsaṁcakarta; nanāda coccaistarasājimadhye.
english: At the forefront of the battle, the son of Madri (Mādrīsuta) simultaneously severed that uplifted spear and the opponent's two well-rounded arms with three broad-headed arrows. He then roared loudly and powerfully amidst the combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 56. At the forefront of the battle, the son of Madri (Mādrīsuta) simultaneously severed that uplifted spear and the opponent's two well-rounded arms with three broad-headed arrows. He then roared loudly and powerfully amidst the combat.

sanskrit: तस्याशुकारी सुसमाहितेन सुवर्णपुङ्खेन दृढायसेन ।भल्लेन सर्वावरणातिगेन शिरः शरीरात्प्रममाथ भूयः ॥५७॥
transliteration: tasyāśukārī susamāhitena; suvarṇapuṅkhena dṛḍhāyasena ,bhallena sarvāvaraṇātigena; śiraḥ śarīrātpramamātha bhūyaḥ.
english: Then, the swift-acting (Mādrīsuta), with a well-aimed, strong iron broad-headed arrow, which was feathered with gold and penetrated all armor, moreover severed his (opponent's) head from the body.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 57. Then, the swift-acting (Mādrīsuta), with a well-aimed, strong iron broad-headed arrow, which was feathered with gold and penetrated all armor, moreover severed his (opponent's) head from the body.

sanskrit: शरेण कार्तस्वरभूषितेन दिवाकराभेन सुसंशितेन ।हृतोत्तमाङ्गो युधि पाण्डवेन पपात भूमौ सुबलस्य पुत्रः ॥५८॥
transliteration: śareṇa kārtasvarabhūṣitena; divākarābhena susaṁśitena ,hṛtottamāṅgo yudhi pāṇḍavena; papāta bhūmau subalasya putraḥ.
english: In battle, the son of Subala, whose head was severed by the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍavena) with a well-sharpened arrow adorned with gold and resembling the sun, fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 58. In battle, the son of Subala, whose head was severed by the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍavena) with a well-sharpened arrow adorned with gold and resembling the sun, fell to the ground.

sanskrit: स तच्छिरो वेगवता शरेण सुवर्णपुङ्खेन शिलाशितेन ।प्रावेरयत्कुपितः पाण्डुपुत्रो यत्तत्कुरूणामनयस्य मूलम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: sa tacchiro vegavatā śareṇa; suvarṇapuṅkhena śilāśitena ,prāverayatkupitaḥ pāṇḍuputro; yattatkurūṇāmanayasya mūlam.
english: Enraged, the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍuputra) hurled that head, propelled by a swift arrow that was feathered with gold and sharpened on a stone, for that (head) was the very root of the Kurus' misrule and injustice.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 59. Enraged, the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍuputra) hurled that head, propelled by a swift arrow that was feathered with gold and sharpened on a stone, for that (head) was the very root of the Kurus' misrule and injustice.

sanskrit: हृतोत्तमाङ्गं शकुनिं समीक्ष्य भूमौ शयानं रुधिरार्द्रगात्रम् ।योधास्त्वदीया भयनष्टसत्त्वा दिशः प्रजग्मुः प्रगृहीतशस्त्राः ॥६०॥
transliteration: hṛtottamāṅgaṁ śakuniṁ samīkṣya; bhūmau śayānaṁ rudhirārdragātram ,yodhāstvadīyā bhayanaṣṭasattvā; diśaḥ prajagmuḥ pragṛhītaśastrāḥ.
english: Seeing Shakuni with his head severed, lying on the ground with his body soaked in blood, your warriors, having lost their courage due to fear, fled in all directions, still clutching their weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 60. Seeing Shakuni with his head severed, lying on the ground with his body soaked in blood, your warriors, having lost their courage due to fear, fled in all directions, still clutching their weapons.

sanskrit: विप्रद्रुताः शुष्कमुखा विसंज्ञा गाण्डीवघोषेण समाहताश्च ।भयार्दिता भग्नरथाश्वनागाः पदातयश्चैव सधार्तराष्ट्राः ॥६१॥
transliteration: vipradrutāḥ śuṣkamukhā visaṁjñā; gāṇḍīvaghoṣeṇa samāhatāśca ,bhayārditā bhagnarathāśvanāgāḥ; padātayaścaiva sadhārtarāṣṭrāḥ.
english: Scattered, with dry faces and bewildered, struck down by the sound of the Gāṇḍīva, distressed by fear, with their chariots, horses, and elephants broken, even the infantry and the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra) [were in this state].
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 61. Scattered, with dry faces and bewildered, struck down by the sound of the Gāṇḍīva, distressed by fear, with their chariots, horses, and elephants broken, even the infantry and the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra) [were in this state].

sanskrit: ततो रथाच्छकुनिं पातयित्वा मुदान्विता भारत पाण्डवेयाः ।शङ्खान्प्रदध्मुः समरे प्रहृष्टाः सकेशवाः सैनिकान्हर्षयन्तः ॥६२॥
transliteration: tato rathācchakuniṁ pātayitvā; mudānvitā bhārata pāṇḍaveyāḥ ,śaṅkhānpradadhmuḥ samare prahṛṣṭāḥ; sakeśavāḥ sainikānharṣayantaḥ.
english: Then, O Bhārata, the Pāṇḍavas, filled with joy, having thrown Shakuni from his chariot, and accompanied by Keśava (Krishna), loudly blew their conchs in battle, thus delighting their own soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 62. Then, O Bhārata, the Pāṇḍavas, filled with joy, having thrown Shakuni from his chariot, and accompanied by Keśava (Krishna), loudly blew their conchs in battle, thus delighting their own soldiers.

sanskrit: तं चापि सर्वे प्रतिपूजयन्तो हृष्टा ब्रुवाणाः सहदेवमाजौ ।दिष्ट्या हतो नैकृतिको दुरात्मा सहात्मजो वीर रणे त्वयेति ॥६३॥
transliteration: taṁ cāpi sarve pratipūjayanto; hṛṣṭā bruvāṇāḥ sahadevamājau ,diṣṭyā hato naikṛtiko durātmā; sahātmajo vīra raṇe tvayeti.
english: And all of them, joyfully honoring Sahadeva in the battle, exclaimed, "O hero, it is by good fortune that this deceitful, evil-minded one, along with his son(s), has been slain by you in battle!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 27 Verse 63. And all of them, joyfully honoring Sahadeva in the battle, exclaimed, "O hero, it is by good fortune that this deceitful, evil-minded one, along with his son(s), has been slain by you in battle!"

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततः क्रुद्धा महाराज सौबलस्य पदानुगाः ।त्यक्त्वा जीवितमाक्रन्दे पाण्डवान्पर्यवारयन् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataḥ kruddhā mahārāja saubalasya padānugāḥ ,tyaktvā jīvitamākrande pāṇḍavānparyavārayan.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, then the enraged followers of Saubala (Śakuni), abandoning their lives, surrounded the Pāṇḍavas amidst the clamor of battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O great king, then the enraged followers of Saubala (Śakuni), abandoning their lives, surrounded the Pāṇḍavas amidst the clamor of battle.

sanskrit: तानर्जुनः प्रत्यगृह्णात्सहदेवजये धृतः ।भीमसेनश्च तेजस्वी क्रुद्धाशीविषदर्शनः ॥२॥
transliteration: tānarjunaḥ pratyagṛhṇātsahadevajaye dhṛtaḥ ,bhīmasenaśca tejasvī kruddhāśīviṣadarśanaḥ.
english: Arjuna, resolute for Sahadeva's victory, met them; and the spirited Bhīmasena, appearing like an enraged venomous snake, also confronted them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 2. Arjuna, resolute for Sahadeva's victory, met them; and the spirited Bhīmasena, appearing like an enraged venomous snake, also confronted them.

sanskrit: शक्त्यृष्टिप्रासहस्तानां सहदेवं जिघांसताम् ।संकल्पमकरोन्मोघं गाण्डीवेन धनंजयः ॥३॥
transliteration: śaktyṛṣṭiprāsahastānāṁ sahadevaṁ jighāṁsatām ,saṁkalpamakaronmoghaṁ gāṇḍīvena dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with his Gāṇḍīva (bow), rendered futile the resolve of those who, holding javelins, lances, and pikes, intended to kill Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 3. Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with his Gāṇḍīva (bow), rendered futile the resolve of those who, holding javelins, lances, and pikes, intended to kill Sahadeva.

sanskrit: प्रगृहीतायुधान्बाहून्योधानामभिधावताम् ।भल्लैश्चिच्छेद बीभत्सुः शिरांस्यपि हयानपि ॥४॥
transliteration: pragṛhītāyudhānbāhūnyodhānāmabhidhāvatām ,bhallaiściccheda bībhatsuḥ śirāṁsyapi hayānapi.
english: Bībhatsu (Arjuna), with his arrows, severed the arms of those warriors who were rushing forward with weapons in hand; he also cut off their heads and even their horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 4. Bībhatsu (Arjuna), with his arrows, severed the arms of those warriors who were rushing forward with weapons in hand; he also cut off their heads and even their horses.

sanskrit: ते हताः प्रत्यपद्यन्त वसुधां विगतासवः ।त्वरिता लोकवीरेण प्रहताः सव्यसाचिना ॥५॥
transliteration: te hatāḥ pratyapadyanta vasudhāṁ vigatāsavaḥ ,tvaritā lokavīreṇa prahatāḥ savyasācinā.
english: They, struck down by the hero of the world, Savyasācin (Arjuna), swiftly fell lifeless to the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 5. They, struck down by the hero of the world, Savyasācin (Arjuna), swiftly fell lifeless to the earth.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा दृष्ट्वा स्वबलसंक्षयम् ।हतशेषान्समानीय क्रुद्धो रथशतान्विभो ॥६॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā dṛṣṭvā svabalasaṁkṣayam ,hataśeṣānsamānīya kruddho rathaśatānvibho.
english: Then, O mighty one (vibho), King Duryodhana, having seen the destruction of his own army, became enraged. He gathered the surviving warriors and hundreds of chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 6. Then, O mighty one (vibho), King Duryodhana, having seen the destruction of his own army, became enraged. He gathered the surviving warriors and hundreds of chariots.

sanskrit: कुञ्जरांश्च हयांश्चैव पादातांश्च परंतप ।उवाच सहितान्सर्वान्धार्तराष्ट्र इदं वचः ॥७॥
transliteration: kuñjarāṁśca hayāṁścaiva pādātāṁśca paraṁtapa ,uvāca sahitānsarvāndhārtarāṣṭra idaṁ vacaḥ.
english: And O scorcher of foes (parantapa), Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) addressed all of them – including elephants, horses, and foot soldiers – with these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 7. And O scorcher of foes (parantapa), Dhārtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) addressed all of them – including elephants, horses, and foot soldiers – with these words.

sanskrit: समासाद्य रणे सर्वान्पाण्डवान्ससुहृद्गणान् ।पाञ्चाल्यं चापि सबलं हत्वा शीघ्रं निवर्तत ॥८॥
transliteration: samāsādya raṇe sarvānpāṇḍavānsasuhṛdgaṇān ,pāñcālyaṁ cāpi sabalaṁ hatvā śīghraṁ nivartata.
english: Confront all the Pāṇḍavas, along with their host of friends, in battle. Furthermore, slay Pañcālya (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with his entire army, and then return quickly!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 8. Confront all the Pāṇḍavas, along with their host of friends, in battle. Furthermore, slay Pañcālya (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) with his entire army, and then return quickly!

sanskrit: तस्य ते शिरसा गृह्य वचनं युद्धदुर्मदाः ।प्रत्युद्ययू रणे पार्थांस्तव पुत्रस्य शासनात् ॥९॥
transliteration: tasya te śirasā gṛhya vacanaṁ yuddhadurmadāḥ ,pratyudyayū raṇe pārthāṁstava putrasya śāsanāt.
english: The warriors, maddened by battle, respectfully accepted his command and, at your son's behest, advanced in battle to confront the Pārthas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 9. The warriors, maddened by battle, respectfully accepted his command and, at your son's behest, advanced in battle to confront the Pārthas.

sanskrit: तानभ्यापततः शीघ्रं हतशेषान्महारणे ।शरैराशीविषाकारैः पाण्डवाः समवाकिरन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tānabhyāpatataḥ śīghraṁ hataśeṣānmahāraṇe ,śarairāśīviṣākāraiḥ pāṇḍavāḥ samavākiran.
english: The Pāṇḍavas swiftly assailed those remaining warriors, who were advancing quickly in the great battle, showering them with arrows resembling venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 10. The Pāṇḍavas swiftly assailed those remaining warriors, who were advancing quickly in the great battle, showering them with arrows resembling venomous snakes.

sanskrit: तत्सैन्यं भरतश्रेष्ठ मुहूर्तेन महात्मभिः ।अवध्यत रणं प्राप्य त्रातारं नाभ्यविन्दत ।प्रतिष्ठमानं तु भयान्नावतिष्ठत दंशितम् ॥११॥
transliteration: tatsainyaṁ bharataśreṣṭha muhūrtena mahātmabhiḥ ,avadhyata raṇaṁ prāpya trātāraṁ nābhyavindata ,pratiṣṭhamānaṁ tu bhayānnāvatiṣṭhata daṁśitam.
english: O best of the Bharatas, that army, having entered the battle, was slaughtered in an instant by the great-souled ones. It found no protector. And though armored, it could not stand firm due to fear, but instead retreated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 11. O best of the Bharatas, that army, having entered the battle, was slaughtered in an instant by the great-souled ones. It found no protector. And though armored, it could not stand firm due to fear, but instead retreated.

sanskrit: अश्वैर्विपरिधावद्भिः सैन्येन रजसा वृते ।न प्राज्ञायन्त समरे दिशश्च प्रदिशस्तथा ॥१२॥
