transliteration: aśvairviparidhāvadbhiḥ sainyena rajasā vṛte ,na prājñāyanta samare diśaśca pradiśastathā.
english: In the battle, with horses scattering and the army enveloped in dust, neither the cardinal directions nor the intermediate directions could be discerned.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 12. In the battle, with horses scattering and the army enveloped in dust, neither the cardinal directions nor the intermediate directions could be discerned.

sanskrit: ततस्तु पाण्डवानीकान्निःसृत्य बहवो जनाः ।अभ्यघ्नंस्तावकान्युद्धे मुहूर्तादिव भारत ।ततो निःशेषमभवत्तत्सैन्यं तव भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: tatastu pāṇḍavānīkānniḥsṛtya bahavo janāḥ ,abhyaghnaṁstāvakānyuddhe muhūrtādiva bhārata ,tato niḥśeṣamabhavattatsainyaṁ tava bhārata.
english: Then, O Bharata, many warriors emerging from the Pandava army attacked your forces in battle, as if in an instant. Consequently, O Bharata, that army of yours was utterly annihilated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 13. Then, O Bharata, many warriors emerging from the Pandava army attacked your forces in battle, as if in an instant. Consequently, O Bharata, that army of yours was utterly annihilated.

sanskrit: अक्षौहिण्यः समेतास्तु तव पुत्रस्य भारत ।एकादश हता युद्धे ताः प्रभो पाण्डुसृञ्जयैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇyaḥ sametāstu tava putrasya bhārata ,ekādaśa hatā yuddhe tāḥ prabho pāṇḍusṛñjayaiḥ.
english: Indeed, O Bharata, O Lord, those eleven Akṣauhiṇīs belonging to your son, which had gathered, were destroyed in battle by the Pandavas and Sṛñjayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 14. Indeed, O Bharata, O Lord, those eleven Akṣauhiṇīs belonging to your son, which had gathered, were destroyed in battle by the Pandavas and Sṛñjayas.

sanskrit: तेषु राजसहस्रेषु तावकेषु महात्मसु ।एको दुर्योधनो राजन्नदृश्यत भृशं क्षतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: teṣu rājasahasreṣu tāvakeṣu mahātmasu ,eko duryodhano rājannadṛśyata bhṛśaṁ kṣataḥ.
english: O King, among your thousands of mighty kings, only Duryodhana was seen, exceedingly wounded.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 15. O King, among your thousands of mighty kings, only Duryodhana was seen, exceedingly wounded.

sanskrit: ततो वीक्ष्य दिशः सर्वा दृष्ट्वा शून्यां च मेदिनीम् ।विहीनः सर्वयोधैश्च पाण्डवान्वीक्ष्य संयुगे ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato vīkṣya diśaḥ sarvā dṛṣṭvā śūnyāṁ ca medinīm ,vihīnaḥ sarvayodhaiśca pāṇḍavānvīkṣya saṁyuge.
english: Then, Duryodhana, having surveyed all directions and seen the earth desolate, found himself bereft of all his warriors, and observed the Pandavas on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 16. Then, Duryodhana, having surveyed all directions and seen the earth desolate, found himself bereft of all his warriors, and observed the Pandavas on the battlefield.

sanskrit: मुदितान्सर्वसिद्धार्थान्नर्दमानान्समन्ततः ।बाणशब्दरवांश्चैव श्रुत्वा तेषां महात्मनाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: muditānsarvasiddhārthānnardamānānsamantataḥ ,bāṇaśabdaravāṁścaiva śrutvā teṣāṁ mahātmanām.
english: Having heard those great-souled ones (mahātmans), who were joyful with all their objectives accomplished, roaring from all sides, and also hearing the sounds and roars of their arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 17. Having heard those great-souled ones (mahātmans), who were joyful with all their objectives accomplished, roaring from all sides, and also hearing the sounds and roars of their arrows.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो महाराज कश्मलेनाभिसंवृतः ।अपयाने मनश्चक्रे विहीनबलवाहनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: duryodhano mahārāja kaśmalenābhisaṁvṛtaḥ ,apayāne manaścakre vihīnabalavāhanaḥ.
english: O great king, Duryodhana, enveloped by confusion, decided to retreat, as he was devoid of soldiers and mounts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 18. O great king, Duryodhana, enveloped by confusion, decided to retreat, as he was devoid of soldiers and mounts.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।निहते मामके सैन्ये निःशेषे शिबिरे कृते ।पाण्डवानां बलं सूत किं नु शेषमभूत्तदा ।एतन्मे पृच्छतो ब्रूहि कुशलो ह्यसि संजय ॥१९॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,nihate māmake sainye niḥśeṣe śibire kṛte ,pāṇḍavānāṁ balaṁ sūta kiṁ nu śeṣamabhūttadā ,etanme pṛcchato brūhi kuśalo hyasi saṁjaya.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O charioteer (Sūta), when my army in the camp was completely annihilated, what strength did the Pāṇḍavas still possess at that time? Tell me this, as I am asking; for you are indeed expert, O Saṁjaya."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 19. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O charioteer (Sūta), when my army in the camp was completely annihilated, what strength did the Pāṇḍavas still possess at that time? Tell me this, as I am asking; for you are indeed expert, O Saṁjaya."

sanskrit: यच्च दुर्योधनो मन्दः कृतवांस्तनयो मम ।बलक्षयं तथा दृष्ट्वा स एकः पृथिवीपतिः ॥२०॥
transliteration: yacca duryodhano mandaḥ kṛtavāṁstanayo mama ,balakṣayaṁ tathā dṛṣṭvā sa ekaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ.
english: And what my foolish son Duryodhana did - having thus seen the destruction of the army, he, the sole ruler of the earth (pṛthivīpati).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 20. And what my foolish son Duryodhana did - having thus seen the destruction of the army, he, the sole ruler of the earth (pṛthivīpati).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।रथानां द्वे सहस्रे तु सप्त नागशतानि च ।पञ्च चाश्वसहस्राणि पत्तीनां च शतं शताः ॥२१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,rathānāṁ dve sahasre tu sapta nāgaśatāni ca ,pañca cāśvasahasrāṇi pattīnāṁ ca śataṁ śatāḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: Two thousand chariots, seven hundred elephants, five thousand horses, and ten thousand infantry remained.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 21. Sanjaya said: Two thousand chariots, seven hundred elephants, five thousand horses, and ten thousand infantry remained.

sanskrit: एतच्छेषमभूद्राजन्पाण्डवानां महद्बलम् ।परिगृह्य हि यद्युद्धे धृष्टद्युम्नो व्यवस्थितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: etaccheṣamabhūdrājanpāṇḍavānāṁ mahadbalam ,parigṛhya hi yadyuddhe dhṛṣṭadyumno vyavasthitaḥ.
english: O King, this was the remaining great army of the Pāṇḍavas, which Dhṛṣṭadyumna had indeed taken charge of in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 22. O King, this was the remaining great army of the Pāṇḍavas, which Dhṛṣṭadyumna had indeed taken charge of in the battle.

sanskrit: एकाकी भरतश्रेष्ठ ततो दुर्योधनो नृपः ।नापश्यत्समरे कंचित्सहायं रथिनां वरः ॥२३॥
transliteration: ekākī bharataśreṣṭha tato duryodhano nṛpaḥ ,nāpaśyatsamare kaṁcitsahāyaṁ rathināṁ varaḥ.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, then King Duryodhana, the best among charioteers, being alone, saw no one as an ally in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 23. O best of the Bhāratas, then King Duryodhana, the best among charioteers, being alone, saw no one as an ally in battle.

sanskrit: नर्दमानान्परांश्चैव स्वबलस्य च संक्षयम् ।हतं स्वहयमुत्सृज्य प्राङ्मुखः प्राद्रवद्भयात् ॥२४॥
transliteration: nardamānānparāṁścaiva svabalasya ca saṁkṣayam ,hataṁ svahayamutsṛjya prāṅmukhaḥ prādravadbhayāt.
english: Seeing his enemies roaring and the destruction of his own army, he abandoned his slain horse and fled eastward out of fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 24. Seeing his enemies roaring and the destruction of his own army, he abandoned his slain horse and fled eastward out of fear.

sanskrit: एकादशचमूभर्ता पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तव ।गदामादाय तेजस्वी पदातिः प्रस्थितो ह्रदम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: ekādaśacamūbhartā putro duryodhanastava ,gadāmādāya tejasvī padātiḥ prasthito hradam.
english: Your glorious son Duryodhana, the commander of eleven armies, taking his mace, set out on foot towards the lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 25. Your glorious son Duryodhana, the commander of eleven armies, taking his mace, set out on foot towards the lake.

sanskrit: नातिदूरं ततो गत्वा पद्भ्यामेव नराधिपः ।सस्मार वचनं क्षत्तुर्धर्मशीलस्य धीमतः ॥२६॥
transliteration: nātidūraṁ tato gatvā padbhyāmeva narādhipaḥ ,sasmāra vacanaṁ kṣatturdharmaśīlasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Having walked not very far from there, the king (narādhipa) remembered the words of the Kṣattṛ (Vidura), who was of righteous character (dharmaśīla) and intelligent.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 26. Having walked not very far from there, the king (narādhipa) remembered the words of the Kṣattṛ (Vidura), who was of righteous character (dharmaśīla) and intelligent.

sanskrit: इदं नूनं महाप्राज्ञो विदुरो दृष्टवान्पुरा ।महद्वैशसमस्माकं क्षत्रियाणां च संयुगे ॥२७॥
transliteration: idaṁ nūnaṁ mahāprājño viduro dṛṣṭavānpurā ,mahadvaiśasamasmākaṁ kṣatriyāṇāṁ ca saṁyuge.
english: Certainly, the greatly wise Vidura had foreseen this great calamity for us Kṣatriyas in battle (saṃyuga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 27. Certainly, the greatly wise Vidura had foreseen this great calamity for us Kṣatriyas in battle (saṃyuga).

sanskrit: एवं विचिन्तयानस्तु प्रविविक्षुर्ह्रदं नृपः ।दुःखसंतप्तहृदयो दृष्ट्वा राजन्बलक्षयम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: evaṁ vicintayānastu pravivikṣurhradaṁ nṛpaḥ ,duḥkhasaṁtaptahṛdayo dṛṣṭvā rājanbalakṣayam.
english: O King (rājan), as the monarch (nṛpa) thus pondered, desiring to enter the lake, his heart burning with sorrow upon seeing the destruction of his forces (balakṣaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 28. O King (rājan), as the monarch (nṛpa) thus pondered, desiring to enter the lake, his heart burning with sorrow upon seeing the destruction of his forces (balakṣaya).

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च महाराज धृष्टद्युम्नपुरोगमाः ।अभ्यधावन्त संक्रुद्धास्तव राजन्बलं प्रति ॥२९॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca mahārāja dhṛṣṭadyumnapurogamāḥ ,abhyadhāvanta saṁkruddhāstava rājanbalaṁ prati.
english: O great king, the Pāṇḍavas, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, furiously rushed towards your army, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 29. O great king, the Pāṇḍavas, led by Dhṛṣṭadyumna, furiously rushed towards your army, O king.

sanskrit: शक्त्यृष्टिप्रासहस्तानां बलानामभिगर्जताम् ।संकल्पमकरोन्मोघं गाण्डीवेन धनंजयः ॥३०॥
transliteration: śaktyṛṣṭiprāsahastānāṁ balānāmabhigarjatām ,saṁkalpamakaronmoghaṁ gāṇḍīvena dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with his Gāṇḍīva bow, rendered futile the resolve of the roaring armies who held spears, javelins, and lances in their hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 30. Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), with his Gāṇḍīva bow, rendered futile the resolve of the roaring armies who held spears, javelins, and lances in their hands.

sanskrit: तान्हत्वा निशितैर्बाणैः सामात्यान्सह बन्धुभिः ।रथे श्वेतहये तिष्ठन्नर्जुनो बह्वशोभत ॥३१॥
transliteration: tānhatvā niśitairbāṇaiḥ sāmātyānsaha bandhubhiḥ ,rathe śvetahaye tiṣṭhannarjuno bahvaśobhata.
english: Having slain them, along with their ministers and kinsmen, with sharp arrows, Arjuna, standing in his white-horsed chariot, shone magnificently.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 31. Having slain them, along with their ministers and kinsmen, with sharp arrows, Arjuna, standing in his white-horsed chariot, shone magnificently.

sanskrit: सुबलस्य हते पुत्रे सवाजिरथकुञ्जरे ।महावनमिव छिन्नमभवत्तावकं बलम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: subalasya hate putre savājirathakuñjare ,mahāvanamiva chinnamabhavattāvakaṁ balam.
english: When Subala's son, along with his horses, chariots, and elephants, was slain, your army, O king, became like a great forest cut down.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 32. When Subala's son, along with his horses, chariots, and elephants, was slain, your army, O king, became like a great forest cut down.

sanskrit: अनेकशतसाहस्रे बले दुर्योधनस्य ह ।नान्यो महारथो राजञ्जीवमानो व्यदृश्यत ॥३३॥
transliteration: anekaśatasāhasre bale duryodhanasya ha ,nānyo mahāratho rājañjīvamāno vyadṛśyata.
english: Indeed, O king, in Duryodhana's army of many hundreds of thousands, no other great chariot-warrior was seen alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 33. Indeed, O king, in Duryodhana's army of many hundreds of thousands, no other great chariot-warrior was seen alive.

sanskrit: द्रोणपुत्रादृते वीरात्तथैव कृतवर्मणः ।कृपाच्च गौतमाद्राजन्पार्थिवाच्च तवात्मजात् ॥३४॥
transliteration: droṇaputrādṛte vīrāttathaiva kṛtavarmaṇaḥ ,kṛpācca gautamādrājanpārthivācca tavātmajāt.
english: O king, except for Droṇa's heroic son, and Kṛtavarman, and Kṛpa Gautama, and your own son, the prince.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 34. O king, except for Droṇa's heroic son, and Kṛtavarman, and Kṛpa Gautama, and your own son, the prince.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नस्तु मां दृष्ट्वा हसन्सात्यकिमब्रवीत् ।किमनेन गृहीतेन नानेनार्थोऽस्ति जीवता ॥३५॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnastu māṁ dṛṣṭvā hasansātyakimabravīt ,kimanena gṛhītena nānenārtho'sti jīvatā.
english: But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having seen me and laughing, said to Sātyaki: "What is the point of capturing this one? There is no purpose in him being alive."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 35. But Dhṛṣṭadyumna, having seen me and laughing, said to Sātyaki: "What is the point of capturing this one? There is no purpose in him being alive."

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नवचः श्रुत्वा शिनेर्नप्ता महारथः ।उद्यम्य निशितं खड्गं हन्तुं मामुद्यतस्तदा ॥३६॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnavacaḥ śrutvā śinernaptā mahārathaḥ ,udyamya niśitaṁ khaḍgaṁ hantuṁ māmudyatastadā.
english: Then, the great chariot-warrior, the grandson of Śini (Sātyaki), having heard Dhṛṣṭadyumna's words, raised his sharpened sword and was ready to kill me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 36. Then, the great chariot-warrior, the grandson of Śini (Sātyaki), having heard Dhṛṣṭadyumna's words, raised his sharpened sword and was ready to kill me.

sanskrit: तमागम्य महाप्राज्ञः कृष्णद्वैपायनोऽब्रवीत् ।मुच्यतां संजयो जीवन्न हन्तव्यः कथंचन ॥३७॥
transliteration: tamāgamya mahāprājñaḥ kṛṣṇadvaipāyano'bravīt ,mucyatāṁ saṁjayo jīvanna hantavyaḥ kathaṁcana.
english: Having approached him, the greatly wise Krishna Dvaipayana spoke: 'Let Sanjaya be released alive. He is not to be killed by any means.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 37. Having approached him, the greatly wise Krishna Dvaipayana spoke: 'Let Sanjaya be released alive. He is not to be killed by any means.'

sanskrit: द्वैपायनवचः श्रुत्वा शिनेर्नप्ता कृताञ्जलिः ।ततो मामब्रवीन्मुक्त्वा स्वस्ति संजय साधय ॥३८॥
transliteration: dvaipāyanavacaḥ śrutvā śinernaptā kṛtāñjaliḥ ,tato māmabravīnmuktvā svasti saṁjaya sādhaya.
english: Having heard Dvaipayana's words, the grandson of Shini, with folded hands, then said to me, having released me: 'Farewell, Sanjaya, proceed!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 38. Having heard Dvaipayana's words, the grandson of Shini, with folded hands, then said to me, having released me: 'Farewell, Sanjaya, proceed!'

sanskrit: अनुज्ञातस्त्वहं तेन न्यस्तवर्मा निरायुधः ।प्रातिष्ठं येन नगरं सायाह्ने रुधिरोक्षितः ॥३९॥
transliteration: anujñātastvahaṁ tena nyastavarmā nirāyudhaḥ ,prātiṣṭhaṁ yena nagaraṁ sāyāhne rudhirokṣitaḥ.
english: Indeed, having been permitted by him, I, having laid down my armor and being unarmed, departed towards the city in the evening, covered in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 39. Indeed, having been permitted by him, I, having laid down my armor and being unarmed, departed towards the city in the evening, covered in blood.

sanskrit: क्रोशमात्रमपक्रान्तं गदापाणिमवस्थितम् ।एकं दुर्योधनं राजन्नपश्यं भृशविक्षतम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: krośamātramapakrāntaṁ gadāpāṇimavasthitam ,ekaṁ duryodhanaṁ rājannapaśyaṁ bhṛśavikṣatam.
english: O King, I saw Duryodhana, greatly wounded, standing alone with a mace in his hand, having gone away only a krosa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 40. O King, I saw Duryodhana, greatly wounded, standing alone with a mace in his hand, having gone away only a krosa.

sanskrit: स तु मामश्रुपूर्णाक्षो नाशक्नोदभिवीक्षितुम् ।उपप्रैक्षत मां दृष्ट्वा तदा दीनमवस्थितम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: sa tu māmaśrupūrṇākṣo nāśaknodabhivīkṣitum ,upapraikṣata māṁ dṛṣṭvā tadā dīnamavasthitam.
english: But he, with eyes full of tears, was unable to look directly at me. Then, seeing me standing in a pitiable state, he gazed at me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 41. But he, with eyes full of tears, was unable to look directly at me. Then, seeing me standing in a pitiable state, he gazed at me.

sanskrit: तं चाहमपि शोचन्तं दृष्ट्वैकाकिनमाहवे ।मुहूर्तं नाशकं वक्तुं किंचिद्दुःखपरिप्लुतः ॥४२॥
transliteration: taṁ cāhamapi śocantaṁ dṛṣṭvaikākinamāhave ,muhūrtaṁ nāśakaṁ vaktuṁ kiṁcidduḥkhapariplutaḥ.
english: And I, too, seeing him grieving and alone in the battle, was unable to speak anything for a moment, completely overwhelmed by sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 42. And I, too, seeing him grieving and alone in the battle, was unable to speak anything for a moment, completely overwhelmed by sorrow.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्मै तदहं सर्वमुक्तवान्ग्रहणं तदा ।द्वैपायनप्रसादाच्च जीवतो मोक्षमाहवे ॥४३॥
transliteration: tato'smai tadahaṁ sarvamuktavāngrahaṇaṁ tadā ,dvaipāyanaprasādācca jīvato mokṣamāhave.
english: Then I recounted to him all about that capture, and also, through the grace of Dvaipāyana, about the liberation (mokṣa) of the living from the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 43. Then I recounted to him all about that capture, and also, through the grace of Dvaipāyana, about the liberation (mokṣa) of the living from the battle.

sanskrit: मुहूर्तमिव च ध्यात्वा प्रतिलभ्य च चेतनाम् ।भ्रातॄंश्च सर्वसैन्यानि पर्यपृच्छत मां ततः ॥४४॥
transliteration: muhūrtamiva ca dhyātvā pratilabhya ca cetanām ,bhrātṝṁśca sarvasainyāni paryapṛcchata māṁ tataḥ.
english: And having meditated for a moment and having regained his senses, he then inquired of me about his brothers and all the armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 44. And having meditated for a moment and having regained his senses, he then inquired of me about his brothers and all the armies.

sanskrit: तस्मै तदहमाचक्षं सर्वं प्रत्यक्षदर्शिवान् ।भ्रातॄंश्च निहतान्सर्वान्सैन्यं च विनिपातितम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tasmai tadahamācakṣaṁ sarvaṁ pratyakṣadarśivān ,bhrātṝṁśca nihatānsarvānsainyaṁ ca vinipātitam.
english: I, as an eyewitness, reported all of that to him: all the brothers who had been killed and the army that had been destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 45. I, as an eyewitness, reported all of that to him: all the brothers who had been killed and the army that had been destroyed.

sanskrit: त्रयः किल रथाः शिष्टास्तावकानां नराधिप ।इति प्रस्थानकाले मां कृष्णद्वैपायनोऽब्रवीत् ॥४६॥
transliteration: trayaḥ kila rathāḥ śiṣṭāstāvakānāṁ narādhipa ,iti prasthānakāle māṁ kṛṣṇadvaipāyano'bravīt.
english: O lord of men (narādhipa), Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana indeed (kila) told me at the time of departure that only three chariots belonging to your side remained.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 46. O lord of men (narādhipa), Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana indeed (kila) told me at the time of departure that only three chariots belonging to your side remained.

sanskrit: स दीर्घमिव निःश्वस्य विप्रेक्ष्य च पुनः पुनः ।अंसे मां पाणिना स्पृष्ट्वा पुत्रस्ते पर्यभाषत ॥४७॥
transliteration: sa dīrghamiva niḥśvasya viprekṣya ca punaḥ punaḥ ,aṁse māṁ pāṇinā spṛṣṭvā putraste paryabhāṣata.
english: Your son, sighing deeply, looking again and again, and touching me on the shoulder with his hand, then spoke to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 47. Your son, sighing deeply, looking again and again, and touching me on the shoulder with his hand, then spoke to me.

sanskrit: त्वदन्यो नेह संग्रामे कश्चिज्जीवति संजय ।द्वितीयं नेह पश्यामि ससहायाश्च पाण्डवाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: tvadanyo neha saṁgrāme kaścijjīvati saṁjaya ,dvitīyaṁ neha paśyāmi sasahāyāśca pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: O Sañjaya, no one other than you is alive here in this battle. I see no second [person like you] here, and the Pāṇḍavas are accompanied by their allies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 48. O Sañjaya, no one other than you is alive here in this battle. I see no second [person like you] here, and the Pāṇḍavas are accompanied by their allies.

sanskrit: ब्रूयाः संजय राजानं प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमीश्वरम् ।दुर्योधनस्तव सुतः प्रविष्टो ह्रदमित्युत ॥४९॥
transliteration: brūyāḥ saṁjaya rājānaṁ prajñācakṣuṣamīśvaram ,duryodhanastava sutaḥ praviṣṭo hradamityuta.
english: O Saṃjaya, you should indeed inform the king, your lord, who perceives through wisdom (prajñācakṣus), that your son Duryodhana has entered the lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 49. O Saṃjaya, you should indeed inform the king, your lord, who perceives through wisdom (prajñācakṣus), that your son Duryodhana has entered the lake.

sanskrit: सुहृद्भिस्तादृशैर्हीनः पुत्रैर्भ्रातृभिरेव च ।पाण्डवैश्च हृते राज्ये को नु जीवति मादृशः ॥५०॥
transliteration: suhṛdbhistādṛśairhīnaḥ putrairbhrātṛbhireva ca ,pāṇḍavaiśca hṛte rājye ko nu jīvati mādṛśaḥ.
english: Having been deprived of such friends, as well as sons and brothers, and with the kingdom taken by the Pāṇḍavas, who else like me could possibly continue to live?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 50. Having been deprived of such friends, as well as sons and brothers, and with the kingdom taken by the Pāṇḍavas, who else like me could possibly continue to live?

sanskrit: आचक्षेथाः सर्वमिदं मां च मुक्तं महाहवात् ।अस्मिंस्तोयह्रदे सुप्तं जीवन्तं भृशविक्षतम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: ācakṣethāḥ sarvamidaṁ māṁ ca muktaṁ mahāhavāt ,asmiṁstoyahrade suptaṁ jīvantaṁ bhṛśavikṣatam.
english: You should report all this: that I, freed from the great battle, am sleeping in this water-lake, still living, and severely wounded.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 51. You should report all this: that I, freed from the great battle, am sleeping in this water-lake, still living, and severely wounded.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज प्राविशत्तं ह्रदं नृपः ।अस्तम्भयत तोयं च मायया मनुजाधिपः ॥५२॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja prāviśattaṁ hradaṁ nṛpaḥ ,astambhayata toyaṁ ca māyayā manujādhipaḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, O great king, the ruler (Duryodhana) entered that lake. And that lord of men (Duryodhana) immobilized the water through his mystic power (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 52. Having spoken thus, O great king, the ruler (Duryodhana) entered that lake. And that lord of men (Duryodhana) immobilized the water through his mystic power (māyā).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्ह्रदं प्रविष्टे तु त्रीन्रथाञ्श्रान्तवाहनान् ।अपश्यं सहितानेकस्तं देशं समुपेयुषः ॥५३॥
transliteration: tasminhradaṁ praviṣṭe tu trīnrathāñśrāntavāhanān ,apaśyaṁ sahitānekastaṁ deśaṁ samupeyuṣaḥ.
english: When he had entered the lake, I, being alone, saw three chariots whose horses were exhausted; they had arrived at that place together.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 53. When he had entered the lake, I, being alone, saw three chariots whose horses were exhausted; they had arrived at that place together.

sanskrit: कृपं शारद्वतं वीरं द्रौणिं च रथिनां वरम् ।भोजं च कृतवर्माणं सहिताञ्शरविक्षतान् ॥५४॥
transliteration: kṛpaṁ śāradvataṁ vīraṁ drauṇiṁ ca rathināṁ varam ,bhojaṁ ca kṛtavarmāṇaṁ sahitāñśaravikṣatān.
english: [I saw] Kṛpa, the heroic son of Śaradvan; Droṇi, the best among charioteers; and Bhoja (Kṛtavarman), all of them together and wounded by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 54. [I saw] Kṛpa, the heroic son of Śaradvan; Droṇi, the best among charioteers; and Bhoja (Kṛtavarman), all of them together and wounded by arrows.

sanskrit: ते सर्वे मामभिप्रेक्ष्य तूर्णमश्वानचोदयन् ।उपयाय च मामूचुर्दिष्ट्या जीवसि संजय ॥५५॥
transliteration: te sarve māmabhiprekṣya tūrṇamaśvānacodayan ,upayāya ca māmūcurdiṣṭyā jīvasi saṁjaya.
english: Seeing me, all of them quickly spurred their horses. Approaching me, they then said, "Fortunately, Sañjaya, you are alive!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 55. Seeing me, all of them quickly spurred their horses. Approaching me, they then said, "Fortunately, Sañjaya, you are alive!"

sanskrit: अपृच्छंश्चैव मां सर्वे पुत्रं तव जनाधिपम् ।कच्चिद्दुर्योधनो राजा स नो जीवति संजय ॥५६॥
transliteration: apṛcchaṁścaiva māṁ sarve putraṁ tava janādhipam ,kaccidduryodhano rājā sa no jīvati saṁjaya.
english: And all of them also asked me about your son, the king, the lord of men: "Sañjaya, does King Duryodhana still live for us?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 56. And all of them also asked me about your son, the king, the lord of men: "Sañjaya, does King Duryodhana still live for us?"

sanskrit: आख्यातवानहं तेभ्यस्तदा कुशलिनं नृपम् ।तच्चैव सर्वमाचक्षं यन्मां दुर्योधनोऽब्रवीत् ।ह्रदं चैवाहमाचष्ट यं प्रविष्टो नराधिपः ॥५७॥
transliteration: ākhyātavānahaṁ tebhyastadā kuśalinaṁ nṛpam ,taccaiva sarvamācakṣaṁ yanmāṁ duryodhano'bravīt ,hradaṁ caivāhamācaṣṭa yaṁ praviṣṭo narādhipaḥ.
english: I then informed them that the king was safe and well. I also recounted everything that Duryodhana had told me, and I described the lake into which the king had entered.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 57. I then informed them that the king was safe and well. I also recounted everything that Duryodhana had told me, and I described the lake into which the king had entered.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा तु तद्राजन्निशम्य वचनं मम ।तं ह्रदं विपुलं प्रेक्ष्य करुणं पर्यदेवयत् ॥५८॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā tu tadrājanniśamya vacanaṁ mama ,taṁ hradaṁ vipulaṁ prekṣya karuṇaṁ paryadevayat.
english: O King, Aśvatthāmā, having heard my words and then seeing that vast lake, lamented sorrowfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 58. O King, Aśvatthāmā, having heard my words and then seeing that vast lake, lamented sorrowfully.

sanskrit: अहो धिङ्न स जानाति जीवतोऽस्मान्नराधिपः ।पर्याप्ता हि वयं तेन सह योधयितुं परान् ॥५९॥
transliteration: aho dhiṅna sa jānāti jīvato'smānnarādhipaḥ ,paryāptā hi vayaṁ tena saha yodhayituṁ parān.
english: Alas, what a shame! That king does not know that we are still alive. Indeed, we are quite capable of fighting the enemies alongside him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 59. Alas, what a shame! That king does not know that we are still alive. Indeed, we are quite capable of fighting the enemies alongside him.

sanskrit: ते तु तत्र चिरं कालं विलप्य च महारथाः ।प्राद्रवन्रथिनां श्रेष्ठा दृष्ट्वा पाण्डुसुतान्रणे ॥६०॥
transliteration: te tu tatra ciraṁ kālaṁ vilapya ca mahārathāḥ ,prādravanrathināṁ śreṣṭhā dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍusutānraṇe.
english: But those great warriors, those best of charioteers, after lamenting there for a long time, fled upon seeing the sons of Pāṇḍu in the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 60. But those great warriors, those best of charioteers, after lamenting there for a long time, fled upon seeing the sons of Pāṇḍu in the battle.

sanskrit: ते तु मां रथमारोप्य कृपस्य सुपरिष्कृतम् ।सेनानिवेशमाजग्मुर्हतशेषास्त्रयो रथाः ॥६१॥
transliteration: te tu māṁ rathamāropya kṛpasya supariṣkṛtam ,senāniveśamājagmurhataśeṣāstrayo rathāḥ.
english: However, the three chariots that had survived the battle, after placing me in Kripa's well-equipped chariot, arrived at the army camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 61. However, the three chariots that had survived the battle, after placing me in Kripa's well-equipped chariot, arrived at the army camp.

sanskrit: तत्र गुल्माः परित्रस्ताः सूर्ये चास्तमिते सति ।सर्वे विचुक्रुशुः श्रुत्वा पुत्राणां तव संक्षयम् ॥६२॥
transliteration: tatra gulmāḥ paritrastāḥ sūrye cāstamite sati ,sarve vicukruśuḥ śrutvā putrāṇāṁ tava saṁkṣayam.
english: There, with the sun having set, all the terrified battalions cried out when they heard of the destruction of your sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 62. There, with the sun having set, all the terrified battalions cried out when they heard of the destruction of your sons.

sanskrit: ततो वृद्धा महाराज योषितां रक्षणो नराः ।राजदारानुपादाय प्रययुर्नगरं प्रति ॥६३॥
transliteration: tato vṛddhā mahārāja yoṣitāṁ rakṣaṇo narāḥ ,rājadārānupādāya prayayurnagaraṁ prati.
english: Then, O great king, the elderly men who were the guardians of the women, taking the royal ladies, proceeded towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 63. Then, O great king, the elderly men who were the guardians of the women, taking the royal ladies, proceeded towards the city.

sanskrit: तत्र विक्रोशतीनां च रुदतीनां च सर्वशः ।प्रादुरासीन्महाञ्शब्दः श्रुत्वा तद्बलसंक्षयम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: tatra vikrośatīnāṁ ca rudatīnāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,prādurāsīnmahāñśabdaḥ śrutvā tadbalasaṁkṣayam.
english: There, a great lamentation arose from the women, crying out and weeping everywhere, upon hearing of the destruction of that army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 64. There, a great lamentation arose from the women, crying out and weeping everywhere, upon hearing of the destruction of that army.

sanskrit: ततस्ता योषितो राजन्क्रन्दन्त्यो वै मुहुर्मुहुः ।कुरर्य इव शब्देन नादयन्त्यो महीतलम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: tatastā yoṣito rājankrandantyo vai muhurmuhuḥ ,kurarya iva śabdena nādayantyo mahītalam.
english: Then, O King, those women, crying out repeatedly and making the surface of the earth resound with their sounds like curlews.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 65. Then, O King, those women, crying out repeatedly and making the surface of the earth resound with their sounds like curlews.

sanskrit: आजघ्नुः करजैश्चापि पाणिभिश्च शिरांस्युत ।लुलुवुश्च तदा केशान्क्रोशन्त्यस्तत्र तत्र ह ॥६६॥
transliteration: ājaghnuḥ karajaiścāpi pāṇibhiśca śirāṁsyuta ,luluvuśca tadā keśānkrośantyastatra tatra ha.
english: They struck their heads with their nails and hands. And then, crying out, they tore out their hair here and there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 66. They struck their heads with their nails and hands. And then, crying out, they tore out their hair here and there.

sanskrit: हाहाकारविनादिन्यो विनिघ्नन्त्य उरांसि च ।क्रोशन्त्यस्तत्र रुरुदुः क्रन्दमाना विशां पते ॥६७॥
transliteration: hāhākāravinādinyo vinighnantya urāṁsi ca ,krośantyastatra ruruduḥ krandamānā viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, they wept there, crying out, striking their chests, and making sounds of lamentation ("hāhākāra") as they wailed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 67. O lord of the people, they wept there, crying out, striking their chests, and making sounds of lamentation ("hāhākāra") as they wailed.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनामात्याः साश्रुकण्ठा भृशातुराः ।राजदारानुपादाय प्रययुर्नगरं प्रति ॥६८॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanāmātyāḥ sāśrukaṇṭhā bhṛśāturāḥ ,rājadārānupādāya prayayurnagaraṁ prati.
english: Then, Duryodhana's ministers, greatly distressed and with their throats choked with tears, took the royal wives and departed towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 68. Then, Duryodhana's ministers, greatly distressed and with their throats choked with tears, took the royal wives and departed towards the city.

sanskrit: वेत्रजर्झरहस्ताश्च द्वाराध्यक्षा विशां पते ।शयनीयानि शुभ्राणि स्पर्ध्यास्तरणवन्ति च ।समादाय ययुस्तूर्णं नगरं दाररक्षिणः ॥६९॥
transliteration: vetrajarjharahastāśca dvārādhyakṣā viśāṁ pate ,śayanīyāni śubhrāṇi spardhyāstaraṇavanti ca ,samādāya yayustūrṇaṁ nagaraṁ dārarakṣiṇaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, the gatekeepers (dvārādhyakṣāḥ) whose hands held cane staffs, along with the chamberlains (dārarakṣiṇaḥ), quickly went to the city, taking with them bright beds that had valuable coverings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 69. O lord of the people, the gatekeepers (dvārādhyakṣāḥ) whose hands held cane staffs, along with the chamberlains (dārarakṣiṇaḥ), quickly went to the city, taking with them bright beds that had valuable coverings.

sanskrit: आस्थायाश्वतरीयुक्तान्स्यन्दनानपरे जनाः ।स्वान्स्वान्दारानुपादाय प्रययुर्नगरं प्रति ॥७०॥
transliteration: āsthāyāśvatarīyuktānsyandanānapare janāḥ ,svānsvāndārānupādāya prayayurnagaraṁ prati.
english: Other people, mounting chariots (syandanān) drawn by mules, and taking their own wives, went towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 70. Other people, mounting chariots (syandanān) drawn by mules, and taking their own wives, went towards the city.

sanskrit: अदृष्टपूर्वा या नार्यो भास्करेणापि वेश्मसु ।ददृशुस्ता महाराज जना यान्तीः पुरं प्रति ॥७१॥
transliteration: adṛṣṭapūrvā yā nāryo bhāskareṇāpi veśmasu ,dadṛśustā mahārāja janā yāntīḥ puraṁ prati.
english: O great king, people saw those women who, having never been seen before even by the sun (bhāskareṇa) within their homes, were now going towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 71. O great king, people saw those women who, having never been seen before even by the sun (bhāskareṇa) within their homes, were now going towards the city.

sanskrit: ताः स्त्रियो भरतश्रेष्ठ सौकुमार्यसमन्विताः ।प्रययुर्नगरं तूर्णं हतस्वजनबान्धवाः ॥७२॥
transliteration: tāḥ striyo bharataśreṣṭha saukumāryasamanvitāḥ ,prayayurnagaraṁ tūrṇaṁ hatasvajanabāndhavāḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharataśreṣṭha), those women (striyaḥ), endowed with great tenderness, whose own kinsmen and relatives had been killed, quickly departed for the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 72. O best of Bharatas (bharataśreṣṭha), those women (striyaḥ), endowed with great tenderness, whose own kinsmen and relatives had been killed, quickly departed for the city.

sanskrit: आ गोपालाविपालेभ्यो द्रवन्तो नगरं प्रति ।ययुर्मनुष्याः संभ्रान्ता भीमसेनभयार्दिताः ॥७३॥
transliteration: ā gopālāvipālebhyo dravanto nagaraṁ prati ,yayurmanuṣyāḥ saṁbhrāntā bhīmasenabhayārditāḥ.
english: Men, bewildered and afflicted by fear of Bhīmasena, ran towards the city, from (even) cowherds and shepherds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 73. Men, bewildered and afflicted by fear of Bhīmasena, ran towards the city, from (even) cowherds and shepherds.

sanskrit: अपि चैषां भयं तीव्रं पार्थेभ्योऽभूत्सुदारुणम् ।प्रेक्षमाणास्तदान्योन्यमाधावन्नगरं प्रति ॥७४॥
transliteration: api caiṣāṁ bhayaṁ tīvraṁ pārthebhyo'bhūtsudāruṇam ,prekṣamāṇāstadānyonyamādhāvannagaraṁ prati.
english: Moreover, an intense and very terrible fear arose in them from the Pāṇḍavas. Looking at each other then, they ran towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 74. Moreover, an intense and very terrible fear arose in them from the Pāṇḍavas. Looking at each other then, they ran towards the city.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तदा वर्तमाने विद्रवे भृशदारुणे ।युयुत्सुः शोकसंमूढः प्राप्तकालमचिन्तयत् ॥७५॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstadā vartamāne vidrave bhṛśadāruṇe ,yuyutsuḥ śokasaṁmūḍhaḥ prāptakālamacintayat.
english: While that exceedingly terrible rout was then occurring, Yuyutsu, confounded by grief, considered what was appropriate for the moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 75. While that exceedingly terrible rout was then occurring, Yuyutsu, confounded by grief, considered what was appropriate for the moment.

sanskrit: जितो दुर्योधनः संख्ये पाण्डवैर्भीमविक्रमैः ।एकादशचमूभर्ता भ्रातरश्चास्य सूदिताः ।हताश्च कुरवः सर्वे भीष्मद्रोणपुरःसराः ॥७६॥
transliteration: jito duryodhanaḥ saṁkhye pāṇḍavairbhīmavikramaiḥ ,ekādaśacamūbhartā bhrātaraścāsya sūditāḥ ,hatāśca kuravaḥ sarve bhīṣmadroṇapuraḥsarāḥ.
english: Duryodhana, the leader of eleven armies, has been conquered in battle by the Pāṇḍavas of terrible valor. His brothers have been slain, and all the Kurus, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, have been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 76. Duryodhana, the leader of eleven armies, has been conquered in battle by the Pāṇḍavas of terrible valor. His brothers have been slain, and all the Kurus, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, have been killed.

sanskrit: अहमेको विमुक्तस्तु भाग्ययोगाद्यदृच्छया ।विद्रुतानि च सर्वाणि शिबिराणि समन्ततः ॥७७॥
transliteration: ahameko vimuktastu bhāgyayogādyadṛcchayā ,vidrutāni ca sarvāṇi śibirāṇi samantataḥ.
english: I alone was freed, by a stroke of fortune and by chance. And all the camps were scattered in every direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 77. I alone was freed, by a stroke of fortune and by chance. And all the camps were scattered in every direction.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य सचिवा ये केचिदवशेषिताः ।राजदारानुपादाय व्यधावन्नगरं प्रति ॥७८॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya sacivā ye kecidavaśeṣitāḥ ,rājadārānupādāya vyadhāvannagaraṁ prati.
english: Those ministers of Duryodhana who remained, taking the royal women with them, fled towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 78. Those ministers of Duryodhana who remained, taking the royal women with them, fled towards the city.

sanskrit: प्राप्तकालमहं मन्ये प्रवेशं तैः सहाभिभो ।युधिष्ठिरमनुज्ञाप्य भीमसेनं तथैव च ॥७९॥
transliteration: prāptakālamahaṁ manye praveśaṁ taiḥ sahābhibho ,yudhiṣṭhiramanujñāpya bhīmasenaṁ tathaiva ca.
english: O Lord, I consider my entry with them to be timely, after obtaining permission from Yudhiṣṭhira and likewise from Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 79. O Lord, I consider my entry with them to be timely, after obtaining permission from Yudhiṣṭhira and likewise from Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: एतमर्थं महाबाहुरुभयोः स न्यवेदयत् ।तस्य प्रीतोऽभवद्राजा नित्यं करुणवेदिता ।परिष्वज्य महाबाहुर्वैश्यापुत्रं व्यसर्जयत् ॥८०॥
transliteration: etamarthaṁ mahābāhurubhayoḥ sa nyavedayat ,tasya prīto'bhavadrājā nityaṁ karuṇaveditā ,pariṣvajya mahābāhurvaiśyāputraṁ vyasarjayat.
english: The mighty-armed one (the son of the Vaiśya woman) reported this matter to both [Yudhiṣṭhira and Bhīmasena]. The king was pleased with him, [being a ruler] always mindful of compassion. Embracing the son of the Vaiśya woman, the mighty-armed one (the king) then dismissed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 80. The mighty-armed one (the son of the Vaiśya woman) reported this matter to both [Yudhiṣṭhira and Bhīmasena]. The king was pleased with him, [being a ruler] always mindful of compassion. Embracing the son of the Vaiśya woman, the mighty-armed one (the king) then dismissed him.

sanskrit: ततः स रथमास्थाय द्रुतमश्वानचोदयत् ।असंभावितवांश्चापि राजदारान्पुरं प्रति ॥८१॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rathamāsthāya drutamaśvānacodayat ,asaṁbhāvitavāṁścāpi rājadārānpuraṁ prati.
english: Then, having mounted the chariot, he quickly urged the horses. He also passed by the royal ladies without acknowledging them, heading towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 81. Then, having mounted the chariot, he quickly urged the horses. He also passed by the royal ladies without acknowledging them, heading towards the city.

sanskrit: तैश्चैव सहितः क्षिप्रमस्तं गच्छति भास्करे ।प्रविष्टो हास्तिनपुरं बाष्पकण्ठोऽश्रुलोचनः ॥८२॥
transliteration: taiścaiva sahitaḥ kṣipramastaṁ gacchati bhāskare ,praviṣṭo hāstinapuraṁ bāṣpakaṇṭho'śrulocanaḥ.
english: And indeed, as the sun was swiftly setting, he entered Hastinapura, accompanied (by his horses), with his throat choked by tears and his eyes full of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 82. And indeed, as the sun was swiftly setting, he entered Hastinapura, accompanied (by his horses), with his throat choked by tears and his eyes full of them.

sanskrit: अपश्यत महाप्राज्ञं विदुरं साश्रुलोचनम् ।राज्ञः समीपान्निष्क्रान्तं शोकोपहतचेतसम् ॥८३॥
transliteration: apaśyata mahāprājñaṁ viduraṁ sāśrulocanam ,rājñaḥ samīpānniṣkrāntaṁ śokopahatacetasam.
english: He saw the greatly wise Vidura, whose eyes were filled with tears, who had just exited from the king's presence, and whose mind was overcome with sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 83. He saw the greatly wise Vidura, whose eyes were filled with tears, who had just exited from the king's presence, and whose mind was overcome with sorrow.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीत्सत्यधृतिः प्रणतं त्वग्रतः स्थितम् ।अस्मिन्कुरुक्षये वृत्ते दिष्ट्या त्वं पुत्र जीवसि ॥८४॥
transliteration: tamabravītsatyadhṛtiḥ praṇataṁ tvagrataḥ sthitam ,asminkurukṣaye vṛtte diṣṭyā tvaṁ putra jīvasi.
english: Satyadhriti (Uddhava) said to him (Vidura), who was bowed and standing before him: 'O son, it is indeed fortunate that you are alive after this destruction of the Kurus has occurred!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 84. Satyadhriti (Uddhava) said to him (Vidura), who was bowed and standing before him: 'O son, it is indeed fortunate that you are alive after this destruction of the Kurus has occurred!'

sanskrit: विना राज्ञः प्रवेशाद्वै किमसि त्वमिहागतः ।एतन्मे कारणं सर्वं विस्तरेण निवेदय ॥८५॥
transliteration: vinā rājñaḥ praveśādvai kimasi tvamihāgataḥ ,etanme kāraṇaṁ sarvaṁ vistareṇa nivedaya.
english: Without the king's permission, why have you come here? Please explain the entire reason for this to me in detail.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 85. Without the king's permission, why have you come here? Please explain the entire reason for this to me in detail.

sanskrit: युयुत्सुरुवाच ।निहते शकुनौ तात सज्ञातिसुतबान्धवे ।हतशेषपरीवारो राजा दुर्योधनस्ततः ।स्वकं स हयमुत्सृज्य प्राङ्मुखः प्राद्रवद्भयात् ॥८६॥
transliteration: yuyutsuruvāca ,nihate śakunau tāta sajñātisutabāndhave ,hataśeṣaparīvāro rājā duryodhanastataḥ ,svakaṁ sa hayamutsṛjya prāṅmukhaḥ prādravadbhayāt.
english: Yuyutsu said: 'O dear one, when Śakuni was killed along with his kinsmen, sons, and relatives, King Duryodhana, with the rest of his retinue having been killed, then abandoned his own horse and fled, facing east, out of fear.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 86. Yuyutsu said: 'O dear one, when Śakuni was killed along with his kinsmen, sons, and relatives, King Duryodhana, with the rest of his retinue having been killed, then abandoned his own horse and fled, facing east, out of fear.'

sanskrit: अपक्रान्ते तु नृपतौ स्कन्धावारनिवेशनात् ।भयव्याकुलितं सर्वं प्राद्रवन्नगरं प्रति ॥८७॥
transliteration: apakrānte tu nṛpatau skandhāvāraniveśanāt ,bhayavyākulitaṁ sarvaṁ prādravannagaraṁ prati.
english: However, when the king departed from the encampment, everyone, agitated by fear, fled towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 87. However, when the king departed from the encampment, everyone, agitated by fear, fled towards the city.

sanskrit: ततो राज्ञः कलत्राणि भ्रातॄणां चास्य सर्वशः ।वाहनेषु समारोप्य स्त्र्यध्यक्षाः प्राद्रवन्भयात् ॥८८॥
transliteration: tato rājñaḥ kalatrāṇi bhrātṝṇāṁ cāsya sarvaśaḥ ,vāhaneṣu samāropya stryadhyakṣāḥ prādravanbhayāt.
english: Then, the supervisors of women, having placed all the wives of the king and his brothers onto vehicles, fled out of fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 88. Then, the supervisors of women, having placed all the wives of the king and his brothers onto vehicles, fled out of fear.

sanskrit: ततोऽहं समनुज्ञाप्य राजानं सहकेशवम् ।प्रविष्टो हास्तिनपुरं रक्षँल्लोकाद्धि वाच्यताम् ॥८९॥
transliteration: tato'haṁ samanujñāpya rājānaṁ sahakeśavam ,praviṣṭo hāstinapuraṁ rakṣaँllokāddhi vācyatām.
english: Then, having obtained permission from the king along with Keśava (Krishna), I entered Hastinapura to protect myself from the censure of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 89. Then, having obtained permission from the king along with Keśava (Krishna), I entered Hastinapura to protect myself from the censure of the people.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचनं वैश्यापुत्रेण भाषितम् ।प्राप्तकालमिति ज्ञात्वा विदुरः सर्वधर्मवित् ।अपूजयदमेयात्मा युयुत्सुं वाक्यकोविदम् ॥९०॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu vacanaṁ vaiśyāputreṇa bhāṣitam ,prāptakālamiti jñātvā viduraḥ sarvadharmavit ,apūjayadameyātmā yuyutsuṁ vākyakovidam.
english: Having heard this speech, spoken by the son of a Vaiśya, Vidura - the knower of all (natural) law (dharma) and one with an immeasurable spirit (ātman) - understood it to be timely and praised Yuyutsu, who was expert in speech.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 90. Having heard this speech, spoken by the son of a Vaiśya, Vidura - the knower of all (natural) law (dharma) and one with an immeasurable spirit (ātman) - understood it to be timely and praised Yuyutsu, who was expert in speech.

sanskrit: प्राप्तकालमिदं सर्वं भवतो भरतक्षये ।अद्य त्वमिह विश्रान्तः श्वोऽभिगन्ता युधिष्ठिरम् ॥९१॥
transliteration: prāptakālamidaṁ sarvaṁ bhavato bharatakṣaye ,adya tvamiha viśrāntaḥ śvo'bhigantā yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: All of this is appropriate for you concerning the impending destruction of the Bharatas. Having rested here today, you shall go to Yudhishthira tomorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 91. All of this is appropriate for you concerning the impending destruction of the Bharatas. Having rested here today, you shall go to Yudhishthira tomorrow.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं विदुरः सर्वधर्मवित् ।युयुत्सुं समनुज्ञाप्य प्रविवेश नृपक्षयम् ।युयुत्सुरपि तां रात्रिं स्वगृहे न्यवसत्तदा ॥९२॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā vacanaṁ viduraḥ sarvadharmavit ,yuyutsuṁ samanujñāpya praviveśa nṛpakṣayam ,yuyutsurapi tāṁ rātriṁ svagṛhe nyavasattadā.
english: Having spoken these words, Vidura, the knower of all (natural) law (dharma), dismissed Yuyutsu and entered the royal chambers. Yuyutsu, for his part, then stayed that night in his own house.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 28 Verse 92. Having spoken these words, Vidura, the knower of all (natural) law (dharma), dismissed Yuyutsu and entered the royal chambers. Yuyutsu, for his part, then stayed that night in his own house.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।हतेषु सर्वसैन्येषु पाण्डुपुत्रै रणाजिरे ।मम सैन्यावशिष्टास्ते किमकुर्वत संजय ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,hateṣu sarvasainyeṣu pāṇḍuputrai raṇājire ,mama sainyāvaśiṣṭāste kimakurvata saṁjaya.
english: Dhritarashtra said, "Sanjaya, what did those remaining from my army do after all my troops had been slain on the battlefield by the sons of Pandu?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said, "Sanjaya, what did those remaining from my army do after all my troops had been slain on the battlefield by the sons of Pandu?"

sanskrit: कृतवर्मा कृपश्चैव द्रोणपुत्रश्च वीर्यवान् ।दुर्योधनश्च मन्दात्मा राजा किमकरोत्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: kṛtavarmā kṛpaścaiva droṇaputraśca vīryavān ,duryodhanaśca mandātmā rājā kimakarottadā.
english: What then did Kritavarma, Kripa, the mighty son of Drona (Aśvatthāmā), and King Duryodhana, who was dull-witted, do?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 2. What then did Kritavarma, Kripa, the mighty son of Drona (Aśvatthāmā), and King Duryodhana, who was dull-witted, do?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।संप्राद्रवत्सु दारेषु क्षत्रियाणां महात्मनाम् ।विद्रुते शिबिरे शून्ये भृशोद्विग्नास्त्रयो रथाः ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,saṁprādravatsu dāreṣu kṣatriyāṇāṁ mahātmanām ,vidrute śibire śūnye bhṛśodvignāstrayo rathāḥ.
english: Sanjaya said, "When the wives of the great kṣatriyas were fleeing and the camp was deserted and dispersed, the three warriors (implied by 'chariots') were greatly agitated."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 3. Sanjaya said, "When the wives of the great kṣatriyas were fleeing and the camp was deserted and dispersed, the three warriors (implied by 'chariots') were greatly agitated."

sanskrit: निशम्य पाण्डुपुत्राणां तदा विजयिनां स्वनम् ।विद्रुतं शिबिरं दृष्ट्वा सायाह्ने राजगृद्धिनः ।स्थानं नारोचयंस्तत्र ततस्ते ह्रदमभ्ययुः ॥४॥
transliteration: niśamya pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ tadā vijayināṁ svanam ,vidrutaṁ śibiraṁ dṛṣṭvā sāyāhne rājagṛddhinaḥ ,sthānaṁ nārocayaṁstatra tataste hradamabhyayuḥ.
english: Having then heard the roar of the victorious sons of Pandu, and having seen the deserted camp in the evening, those who were greedy for sovereignty (rāja-gṛddhinaḥ) did not approve of staying there. Thereupon, they approached a lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 4. Having then heard the roar of the victorious sons of Pandu, and having seen the deserted camp in the evening, those who were greedy for sovereignty (rāja-gṛddhinaḥ) did not approve of staying there. Thereupon, they approached a lake.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरोऽपि धर्मात्मा भ्रातृभिः सहितो रणे ।हृष्टः पर्यपतद्राजन्दुर्योधनवधेप्सया ॥५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiro'pi dharmātmā bhrātṛbhiḥ sahito raṇe ,hṛṣṭaḥ paryapatadrājanduryodhanavadhepsayā.
english: O King, the righteous (dharmātmā) Yudhiṣṭhira, accompanied by his brothers, also joyfully rushed into battle, eager for Duryodhana's death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 5. O King, the righteous (dharmātmā) Yudhiṣṭhira, accompanied by his brothers, also joyfully rushed into battle, eager for Duryodhana's death.

sanskrit: मार्गमाणास्तु संक्रुद्धास्तव पुत्रं जयैषिणः ।यत्नतोऽन्वेषमाणास्तु नैवापश्यञ्जनाधिपम् ॥६॥
transliteration: mārgamāṇāstu saṁkruddhāstava putraṁ jayaiṣiṇaḥ ,yatnato'nveṣamāṇāstu naivāpaśyañjanādhipam.
english: But those enraged, victory-seeking (jaya-eṣiṇaḥ) [Pāṇḍavas], diligently searching for your son, did not see the king (janādhipa) at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 6. But those enraged, victory-seeking (jaya-eṣiṇaḥ) [Pāṇḍavas], diligently searching for your son, did not see the king (janādhipa) at all.

sanskrit: स हि तीव्रेण वेगेन गदापाणिरपाक्रमत् ।तं ह्रदं प्राविशच्चापि विष्टभ्यापः स्वमायया ॥७॥
transliteration: sa hi tīvreṇa vegena gadāpāṇirapākramat ,taṁ hradaṁ prāviśaccāpi viṣṭabhyāpaḥ svamāyayā.
english: Indeed, he, mace in hand (gadāpāṇi), retreated with great speed. And he also entered that pond, having magically held back the waters by his own power of illusion (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 7. Indeed, he, mace in hand (gadāpāṇi), retreated with great speed. And he also entered that pond, having magically held back the waters by his own power of illusion (māyā).

sanskrit: यदा तु पाण्डवाः सर्वे सुपरिश्रान्तवाहनाः ।ततः स्वशिबिरं प्राप्य व्यतिष्ठन्सहसैनिकाः ॥८॥
transliteration: yadā tu pāṇḍavāḥ sarve supariśrāntavāhanāḥ ,tataḥ svaśibiraṁ prāpya vyatiṣṭhansahasainikāḥ.
english: But when all the Pāṇḍavas had their mounts thoroughly exhausted, they then reached their own camp and remained there with their soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 8. But when all the Pāṇḍavas had their mounts thoroughly exhausted, they then reached their own camp and remained there with their soldiers.

sanskrit: ततः कृपश्च द्रौणिश्च कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ।संनिविष्टेषु पार्थेषु प्रयातास्तं ह्रदं शनैः ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛpaśca drauṇiśca kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ ,saṁniviṣṭeṣu pārtheṣu prayātāstaṁ hradaṁ śanaiḥ.
english: After the Pāṇḍavas were stationed, Kṛpa, Drauṇi, Kṛtavarmā, and Sātvata slowly proceeded towards that lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 9. After the Pāṇḍavas were stationed, Kṛpa, Drauṇi, Kṛtavarmā, and Sātvata slowly proceeded towards that lake.

sanskrit: ते तं ह्रदं समासाद्य यत्र शेते जनाधिपः ।अभ्यभाषन्त दुर्धर्षं राजानं सुप्तमम्भसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: te taṁ hradaṁ samāsādya yatra śete janādhipaḥ ,abhyabhāṣanta durdharṣaṁ rājānaṁ suptamambhasi.
english: Upon reaching that lake where the lord of men lay, they addressed the formidable king, who was sleeping in the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 10. Upon reaching that lake where the lord of men lay, they addressed the formidable king, who was sleeping in the water.

sanskrit: राजन्नुत्तिष्ठ युध्यस्व सहास्माभिर्युधिष्ठिरम् ।जित्वा वा पृथिवीं भुङ्क्ष्व हतो वा स्वर्गमाप्नुहि ॥११॥
transliteration: rājannuttiṣṭha yudhyasva sahāsmābhiryudhiṣṭhiram ,jitvā vā pṛthivīṁ bhuṅkṣva hato vā svargamāpnuhi.
english: "O King, rise up and fight Yudhiṣṭhira alongside us! Either conquer the earth and rule it, or, if you are killed, attain heaven."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 11. "O King, rise up and fight Yudhiṣṭhira alongside us! Either conquer the earth and rule it, or, if you are killed, attain heaven."

sanskrit: तेषामपि बलं सर्वं हतं दुर्योधन त्वया ।प्रतिरब्धाश्च भूयिष्ठं ये शिष्टास्तत्र सैनिकाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: teṣāmapi balaṁ sarvaṁ hataṁ duryodhana tvayā ,pratirabdhāśca bhūyiṣṭhaṁ ye śiṣṭāstatra sainikāḥ.
english: "O Duryodhana, even their entire army was devastated by you! And the great majority of our soldiers who remained there have rallied."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 12. "O Duryodhana, even their entire army was devastated by you! And the great majority of our soldiers who remained there have rallied."

sanskrit: न ते वेगं विषहितुं शक्तास्तव विशां पते ।अस्माभिरभिगुप्तस्य तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: na te vegaṁ viṣahituṁ śaktāstava viśāṁ pate ,asmābhirabhiguptasya tasmāduttiṣṭha bhārata.
english: O lord of the people, they are not capable of withstanding your impetus, as you are protected by us. Therefore, O Bhārata, arise!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 13. O lord of the people, they are not capable of withstanding your impetus, as you are protected by us. Therefore, O Bhārata, arise!

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।दिष्ट्या पश्यामि वो मुक्तानीदृशात्पुरुषक्षयात् ।पाण्डुकौरवसंमर्दाज्जीवमानान्नरर्षभान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,diṣṭyā paśyāmi vo muktānīdṛśātpuruṣakṣayāt ,pāṇḍukauravasaṁmardājjīvamānānnararṣabhān.
english: Duryodhana said: Fortunately, I see you, the best of men, who are surviving and freed from such a great destruction of men caused by the conflict between the Pāṇḍavas and Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 14. Duryodhana said: Fortunately, I see you, the best of men, who are surviving and freed from such a great destruction of men caused by the conflict between the Pāṇḍavas and Kauravas.

sanskrit: विजेष्यामो वयं सर्वे विश्रान्ता विगतक्लमाः ।भवन्तश्च परिश्रान्ता वयं च भृशविक्षताः ।उदीर्णं च बलं तेषां तेन युद्धं न रोचये ॥१५॥
transliteration: vijeṣyāmo vayaṁ sarve viśrāntā vigataklamāḥ ,bhavantaśca pariśrāntā vayaṁ ca bhṛśavikṣatāḥ ,udīrṇaṁ ca balaṁ teṣāṁ tena yuddhaṁ na rocaye.
english: We, all of us, will be victorious once we are rested and free from weariness. However, you are thoroughly fatigued, and we ourselves are severely wounded. Furthermore, their (the enemies') strength has greatly increased. Therefore, I do not approve of engaging in battle (now).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 15. We, all of us, will be victorious once we are rested and free from weariness. However, you are thoroughly fatigued, and we ourselves are severely wounded. Furthermore, their (the enemies') strength has greatly increased. Therefore, I do not approve of engaging in battle (now).

sanskrit: न त्वेतदद्भुतं वीरा यद्वो महदिदं मनः ।अस्मासु च परा भक्तिर्न तु कालः पराक्रमे ॥१६॥
transliteration: na tvetadadbhutaṁ vīrā yadvo mahadidaṁ manaḥ ,asmāsu ca parā bhaktirna tu kālaḥ parākrame.
english: O heroes, it is not at all surprising that your spirit is so great and that you have such supreme devotion (bhakti) towards us. But this is not the right time for a display of valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 16. O heroes, it is not at all surprising that your spirit is so great and that you have such supreme devotion (bhakti) towards us. But this is not the right time for a display of valor.

sanskrit: विश्रम्यैकां निशामद्य भवद्भिः सहितो रणे ।प्रतियोत्स्याम्यहं शत्रूञ्श्वो न मेऽस्त्यत्र संशयः ॥१७॥
transliteration: viśramyaikāṁ niśāmadya bhavadbhiḥ sahito raṇe ,pratiyotsyāmyahaṁ śatrūñśvo na me'styatra saṁśayaḥ.
english: Having rested for just one night today, I will, accompanied by all of you, fight back against the enemies in battle tomorrow. For me, there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 17. Having rested for just one night today, I will, accompanied by all of you, fight back against the enemies in battle tomorrow. For me, there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवमुक्तोऽब्रवीद्द्रौणी राजानं युद्धदुर्मदम् ।उत्तिष्ठ राजन्भद्रं ते विजेष्यामो रणे परान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evamukto'bravīddrauṇī rājānaṁ yuddhadurmadam ,uttiṣṭha rājanbhadraṁ te vijeṣyāmo raṇe parān.
english: Sanjaya said: Thus addressed, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) spoke to the king, who was intoxicated by battle: 'Arise, O King! May good fortune be with you. We shall surely conquer our enemies in battle.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 18. Sanjaya said: Thus addressed, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) spoke to the king, who was intoxicated by battle: 'Arise, O King! May good fortune be with you. We shall surely conquer our enemies in battle.'

sanskrit: इष्टापूर्तेन दानेन सत्येन च जपेन च ।शपे राजन्यथा ह्यद्य निहनिष्यामि सोमकान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: iṣṭāpūrtena dānena satyena ca japena ca ,śape rājanyathā hyadya nihaniṣyāmi somakān.
english: I swear, O King, by my Vedic sacrifices and pious acts (iṣṭāpūrta), by acts of charity (dāna), by truth, and by chanting (japa), that indeed today I shall slay the Somakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 19. I swear, O King, by my Vedic sacrifices and pious acts (iṣṭāpūrta), by acts of charity (dāna), by truth, and by chanting (japa), that indeed today I shall slay the Somakas.

sanskrit: मा स्म यज्ञकृतां प्रीतिं प्राप्नुयां सज्जनोचिताम् ।यदीमां रजनीं व्युष्टां न निहन्मि परान्रणे ॥२०॥
transliteration: mā sma yajñakṛtāṁ prītiṁ prāpnuyāṁ sajjanocitām ,yadīmāṁ rajanīṁ vyuṣṭāṁ na nihanmi parānraṇe.
english: May I not attain the delight suitable for virtuous performers of Vedic rituals (yajñakṛt), if I do not slay my enemies in battle when this night dawns.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 20. May I not attain the delight suitable for virtuous performers of Vedic rituals (yajñakṛt), if I do not slay my enemies in battle when this night dawns.

sanskrit: नाहत्वा सर्वपाञ्चालान्विमोक्ष्ये कवचं विभो ।इति सत्यं ब्रवीम्येतत्तन्मे शृणु जनाधिप ॥२१॥
transliteration: nāhatvā sarvapāñcālānvimokṣye kavacaṁ vibho ,iti satyaṁ bravīmyetattanme śṛṇu janādhipa.
english: O lord, I will not remove my armor until I have killed all the Panchalas. This is the truth I speak; therefore, O king of people, listen to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 21. O lord, I will not remove my armor until I have killed all the Panchalas. This is the truth I speak; therefore, O king of people, listen to me.

sanskrit: तेषु संभाषमाणेषु व्याधास्तं देशमाययुः ।मांसभारपरिश्रान्ताः पानीयार्थं यदृच्छया ॥२२॥
transliteration: teṣu saṁbhāṣamāṇeṣu vyādhāstaṁ deśamāyayuḥ ,māṁsabhārapariśrāntāḥ pānīyārthaṁ yadṛcchayā.
english: While they were conversing, hunters arrived at that spot, exhausted from carrying a load of meat, having come by chance in search of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 22. While they were conversing, hunters arrived at that spot, exhausted from carrying a load of meat, having come by chance in search of water.

sanskrit: ते हि नित्यं महाराज भीमसेनस्य लुब्धकाः ।मांसभारानुपाजह्रुर्भक्त्या परमया विभो ॥२३॥
transliteration: te hi nityaṁ mahārāja bhīmasenasya lubdhakāḥ ,māṁsabhārānupājahrurbhaktyā paramayā vibho.
english: Indeed, O great king, O lord, those hunters regularly brought loads of meat to Bhimasena with supreme devotion (bhakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 23. Indeed, O great king, O lord, those hunters regularly brought loads of meat to Bhimasena with supreme devotion (bhakti).

sanskrit: ते तत्र विष्ठितास्तेषां सर्वं तद्वचनं रहः ।दुर्योधनवचश्चैव शुश्रुवुः संगता मिथः ॥२४॥
transliteration: te tatra viṣṭhitāsteṣāṁ sarvaṁ tadvacanaṁ rahaḥ ,duryodhanavacaścaiva śuśruvuḥ saṁgatā mithaḥ.
english: Stationed there, they (the hunters), having gathered together, secretly heard all their (the Pandavas') conversation, as well as Duryodhana's words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 24. Stationed there, they (the hunters), having gathered together, secretly heard all their (the Pandavas') conversation, as well as Duryodhana's words.

sanskrit: तेऽपि सर्वे महेष्वासा अयुद्धार्थिनि कौरवे ।निर्बन्धं परमं चक्रुस्तदा वै युद्धकाङ्क्षिणः ॥२५॥
transliteration: te'pi sarve maheṣvāsā ayuddhārthini kaurave ,nirbandhaṁ paramaṁ cakrustadā vai yuddhakāṅkṣiṇaḥ.
english: Although the Kuru prince (Duryodhana) was unwilling to fight, all those great archers, who were themselves eager for battle, then indeed exerted extreme pressure on him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 25. Although the Kuru prince (Duryodhana) was unwilling to fight, all those great archers, who were themselves eager for battle, then indeed exerted extreme pressure on him.

sanskrit: तांस्तथा समुदीक्ष्याथ कौरवाणां महारथान् ।अयुद्धमनसं चैव राजानं स्थितमम्भसि ॥२६॥
transliteration: tāṁstathā samudīkṣyātha kauravāṇāṁ mahārathān ,ayuddhamanasaṁ caiva rājānaṁ sthitamambhasi.
english: Then, having thus observed those great charioteers among the Kurus, and indeed the king (Duryodhana) himself, who was averse to battle and situated in the water...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 26. Then, having thus observed those great charioteers among the Kurus, and indeed the king (Duryodhana) himself, who was averse to battle and situated in the water...

sanskrit: तेषां श्रुत्वा च संवादं राज्ञश्च सलिले सतः ।व्याधाभ्यजानन्राजेन्द्र सलिलस्थं सुयोधनम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ śrutvā ca saṁvādaṁ rājñaśca salile sataḥ ,vyādhābhyajānanrājendra salilasthaṁ suyodhanam.
english: O king of kings (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the hunters, having heard the conversation of those (warriors) and also of the king (Duryodhana) who was present in the water, recognized Suyodhana, who was situated in the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 27. O king of kings (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the hunters, having heard the conversation of those (warriors) and also of the king (Duryodhana) who was present in the water, recognized Suyodhana, who was situated in the water.

sanskrit: ते पूर्वं पाण्डुपुत्रेण पृष्टा ह्यासन्सुतं तव ।यदृच्छोपगतास्तत्र राजानं परिमार्गिताः ॥२८॥
transliteration: te pūrvaṁ pāṇḍuputreṇa pṛṣṭā hyāsansutaṁ tava ,yadṛcchopagatāstatra rājānaṁ parimārgitāḥ.
english: Indeed, these hunters had previously been questioned by the son of Pāṇḍu (Yudhiṣṭhira) about your son (Duryodhana). Having arrived there by chance, they were (also) searching for the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 28. Indeed, these hunters had previously been questioned by the son of Pāṇḍu (Yudhiṣṭhira) about your son (Duryodhana). Having arrived there by chance, they were (also) searching for the king.

sanskrit: ततस्ते पाण्डुपुत्रस्य स्मृत्वा तद्भाषितं तदा ।अन्योन्यमब्रुवन्राजन्मृगव्याधाः शनैरिदम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tataste pāṇḍuputrasya smṛtvā tadbhāṣitaṁ tadā ,anyonyamabruvanrājanmṛgavyādhāḥ śanairidam.
english: Then, O King, those hunters (mṛgavyādha), remembering the words of the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍuputra) spoken at that time, slowly said this to each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 29. Then, O King, those hunters (mṛgavyādha), remembering the words of the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍuputra) spoken at that time, slowly said this to each other.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं ख्यापयामो धनं दास्यति पाण्डवः ।सुव्यक्तमिति नः ख्यातो ह्रदे दुर्योधनो नृपः ॥३०॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ khyāpayāmo dhanaṁ dāsyati pāṇḍavaḥ ,suvyaktamiti naḥ khyāto hrade duryodhano nṛpaḥ.
english: Let us make Duryodhana known, then the Pāṇḍava will give us wealth. It is quite clear to us that King Duryodhana is in the lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 30. Let us make Duryodhana known, then the Pāṇḍava will give us wealth. It is quite clear to us that King Duryodhana is in the lake.

sanskrit: तस्माद्गच्छामहे सर्वे यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।आख्यातुं सलिले सुप्तं दुर्योधनममर्षणम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tasmādgacchāmahe sarve yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,ākhyātuṁ salile suptaṁ duryodhanamamarṣaṇam.
english: Therefore, let us all go to where King Yudhiṣṭhira is, to report about Duryodhana, who is wrathful (amarṣaṇa) and lying asleep in the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 31. Therefore, let us all go to where King Yudhiṣṭhira is, to report about Duryodhana, who is wrathful (amarṣaṇa) and lying asleep in the water.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रात्मजं तस्मै भीमसेनाय धीमते ।शयानं सलिले सर्वे कथयामो धनुर्भृते ॥३२॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrātmajaṁ tasmai bhīmasenāya dhīmate ,śayānaṁ salile sarve kathayāmo dhanurbhṛte.
english: We all will report about the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Dhṛtarāṣṭrātmaja), who is lying in the water, to that intelligent (dhīmat) Bhīmasena, the wielder of the bow (dhanurbhṛt).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 32. We all will report about the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Dhṛtarāṣṭrātmaja), who is lying in the water, to that intelligent (dhīmat) Bhīmasena, the wielder of the bow (dhanurbhṛt).

sanskrit: स नो दास्यति सुप्रीतो धनानि बहुलान्युत ।किं नो मांसेन शुष्केण परिक्लिष्टेन शोषिणा ॥३३॥
transliteration: sa no dāsyati suprīto dhanāni bahulānyuta ,kiṁ no māṁsena śuṣkeṇa parikliṣṭena śoṣiṇā.
english: Being very pleased, he will give us abundant wealth. What use is dry, withered, and wasted meat to us?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 33. Being very pleased, he will give us abundant wealth. What use is dry, withered, and wasted meat to us?

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा ततो व्याधाः संप्रहृष्टा धनार्थिनः ।मांसभारानुपादाय प्रययुः शिबिरं प्रति ॥३४॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tato vyādhāḥ saṁprahṛṣṭā dhanārthinaḥ ,māṁsabhārānupādāya prayayuḥ śibiraṁ prati.
english: Having spoken thus, the hunters, overjoyed and desiring wealth, then took up the loads of meat and departed towards the camp.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 34. Having spoken thus, the hunters, overjoyed and desiring wealth, then took up the loads of meat and departed towards the camp.

sanskrit: पाण्डवाश्च महाराज लब्धलक्षाः प्रहारिणः ।अपश्यमानाः समरे दुर्योधनमवस्थितम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāśca mahārāja labdhalakṣāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,apaśyamānāḥ samare duryodhanamavasthitam.
english: And the Pāṇḍavas, O great king, those skilled warriors who had achieved their objective, were not finding Duryodhana present in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 35. And the Pāṇḍavas, O great king, those skilled warriors who had achieved their objective, were not finding Duryodhana present in battle.

sanskrit: निकृतेस्तस्य पापस्य ते पारं गमनेप्सवः ।चारान्संप्रेषयामासुः समन्तात्तद्रणाजिरम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: nikṛtestasya pāpasya te pāraṁ gamanepsavaḥ ,cārānsaṁpreṣayāmāsuḥ samantāttadraṇājiram.
english: Desiring to overcome the deceit of that wicked one, they dispatched spies all around that battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 36. Desiring to overcome the deceit of that wicked one, they dispatched spies all around that battlefield.

sanskrit: आगम्य तु ततः सर्वे नष्टं दुर्योधनं नृपम् ।न्यवेदयन्त सहिता धर्मराजस्य सैनिकाः ॥३७॥
transliteration: āgamya tu tataḥ sarve naṣṭaṁ duryodhanaṁ nṛpam ,nyavedayanta sahitā dharmarājasya sainikāḥ.
english: Having arrived from that place, all the assembled soldiers of Dharmaraja (Yudhiṣṭhira) reported that King Duryodhana was missing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 37. Having arrived from that place, all the assembled soldiers of Dharmaraja (Yudhiṣṭhira) reported that King Duryodhana was missing.

sanskrit: तेषां तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा चाराणां भरतर्षभ ।चिन्तामभ्यगमत्तीव्रां निशश्वास च पार्थिवः ॥३८॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tadvacanaṁ śrutvā cārāṇāṁ bharatarṣabha ,cintāmabhyagamattīvrāṁ niśaśvāsa ca pārthivaḥ.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, upon hearing those words from the spies, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was overcome by intense anxiety and sighed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 38. O best among the Bhāratas, upon hearing those words from the spies, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was overcome by intense anxiety and sighed.

sanskrit: अथ स्थितानां पाण्डूनां दीनानां भरतर्षभ ।तस्माद्देशादपक्रम्य त्वरिता लुब्धका विभो ॥३९॥
transliteration: atha sthitānāṁ pāṇḍūnāṁ dīnānāṁ bharatarṣabha ,tasmāddeśādapakramya tvaritā lubdhakā vibho.
english: Then, O best among the Bhāratas, O lord, the swift hunters, having moved away from that place where the distressed Pāṇḍavas (Pāṇḍu's sons) were staying...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 39. Then, O best among the Bhāratas, O lord, the swift hunters, having moved away from that place where the distressed Pāṇḍavas (Pāṇḍu's sons) were staying...

sanskrit: आजग्मुः शिबिरं हृष्टा दृष्ट्वा दुर्योधनं नृपम् ।वार्यमाणाः प्रविष्टाश्च भीमसेनस्य पश्यतः ॥४०॥
transliteration: ājagmuḥ śibiraṁ hṛṣṭā dṛṣṭvā duryodhanaṁ nṛpam ,vāryamāṇāḥ praviṣṭāśca bhīmasenasya paśyataḥ.
english: Having seen King Duryodhana, they arrived at the camp joyful. And though obstructed, they entered even as Bhīmasena watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 40. Having seen King Duryodhana, they arrived at the camp joyful. And though obstructed, they entered even as Bhīmasena watched.

sanskrit: ते तु पाण्डवमासाद्य भीमसेनं महाबलम् ।तस्मै तत्सर्वमाचख्युर्यद्वृत्तं यच्च वै श्रुतम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: te tu pāṇḍavamāsādya bhīmasenaṁ mahābalam ,tasmai tatsarvamācakhyuryadvṛttaṁ yacca vai śrutam.
english: They, having indeed approached the mighty Bhīmasena, son of Pāṇḍu, narrated to him everything that had transpired and all that they had heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 41. They, having indeed approached the mighty Bhīmasena, son of Pāṇḍu, narrated to him everything that had transpired and all that they had heard.

sanskrit: ततो वृकोदरो राजन्दत्त्वा तेषां धनं बहु ।धर्मराजाय तत्सर्वमाचचक्षे परंतपः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tato vṛkodaro rājandattvā teṣāṁ dhanaṁ bahu ,dharmarājāya tatsarvamācacakṣe paraṁtapaḥ.
english: Then, O King, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), the tormentor of foes, having given them much wealth, narrated everything to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), the king of (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 42. Then, O King, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), the tormentor of foes, having given them much wealth, narrated everything to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), the king of (dharma).

sanskrit: असौ दुर्योधनो राजन्विज्ञातो मम लुब्धकैः ।संस्तभ्य सलिलं शेते यस्यार्थे परितप्यसे ॥४३॥
transliteration: asau duryodhano rājanvijñāto mama lubdhakaiḥ ,saṁstabhya salilaṁ śete yasyārthe paritapyase.
english: O King, my hunters have discovered this Duryodhana. He is resting in the water, having held back its flow, for whose sake you are lamenting.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 43. O King, my hunters have discovered this Duryodhana. He is resting in the water, having held back its flow, for whose sake you are lamenting.

sanskrit: तद्वचो भीमसेनस्य प्रियं श्रुत्वा विशां पते ।अजातशत्रुः कौन्तेयो हृष्टोऽभूत्सह सोदरैः ॥४४॥
transliteration: tadvaco bhīmasenasya priyaṁ śrutvā viśāṁ pate ,ajātaśatruḥ kaunteyo hṛṣṭo'bhūtsaha sodaraiḥ.
english: O Lord of the people, having heard that welcome message from Bhīmasena, Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), son of Kuntī, became delighted along with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 44. O Lord of the people, having heard that welcome message from Bhīmasena, Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), son of Kuntī, became delighted along with his brothers.

sanskrit: तं च श्रुत्वा महेष्वासं प्रविष्टं सलिलह्रदम् ।क्षिप्रमेव ततोऽगच्छत्पुरस्कृत्य जनार्दनम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: taṁ ca śrutvā maheṣvāsaṁ praviṣṭaṁ salilahradam ,kṣiprameva tato'gacchatpuraskṛtya janārdanam.
english: And upon hearing that the great archer had entered the lake, they quickly proceeded there, with Janardana (Krishna) at the forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 45. And upon hearing that the great archer had entered the lake, they quickly proceeded there, with Janardana (Krishna) at the forefront.

sanskrit: ततः किलकिलाशब्दः प्रादुरासीद्विशां पते ।पाण्डवानां प्रहृष्टानां पाञ्चालानां च सर्वशः ॥४६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kilakilāśabdaḥ prādurāsīdviśāṁ pate ,pāṇḍavānāṁ prahṛṣṭānāṁ pāñcālānāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, O lord of the people, a loud joyful cry arose from the exultant Pandavas and also from all the Panchalas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 46. Then, O lord of the people, a loud joyful cry arose from the exultant Pandavas and also from all the Panchalas.

sanskrit: सिंहनादांस्ततश्चक्रुः क्ष्वेडांश्च भरतर्षभ ।त्वरिताः क्षत्रिया राजञ्जग्मुर्द्वैपायनं ह्रदम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: siṁhanādāṁstataścakruḥ kṣveḍāṁśca bharatarṣabha ,tvaritāḥ kṣatriyā rājañjagmurdvaipāyanaṁ hradam.
english: Then, O best of Bharatas, they emitted lion-roars and war-cries. The swift Kshatriyas, O king, proceeded to Dvaipayana lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 47. Then, O best of Bharatas, they emitted lion-roars and war-cries. The swift Kshatriyas, O king, proceeded to Dvaipayana lake.

sanskrit: ज्ञातः पापो धार्तराष्ट्रो दृष्टश्चेत्यसकृद्रणे ।प्राक्रोशन्सोमकास्तत्र हृष्टरूपाः समन्ततः ॥४८॥
transliteration: jñātaḥ pāpo dhārtarāṣṭro dṛṣṭaścetyasakṛdraṇe ,prākrośansomakāstatra hṛṣṭarūpāḥ samantataḥ.
english: Thinking, 'The wicked son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra has been identified and seen, even repeatedly in battle,' the Somakas, looking joyful, cried out from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 48. Thinking, 'The wicked son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra has been identified and seen, even repeatedly in battle,' the Somakas, looking joyful, cried out from all sides.

sanskrit: तेषामाशु प्रयातानां रथानां तत्र वेगिनाम् ।बभूव तुमुलः शब्दो दिवस्पृक्पृथिवीपते ॥४९॥
transliteration: teṣāmāśu prayātānāṁ rathānāṁ tatra veginām ,babhūva tumulaḥ śabdo divaspṛkpṛthivīpate.
english: O lord of the earth, there arose a tumultuous sound, touching the very sky, from those swift chariots that were moving swiftly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 49. O lord of the earth, there arose a tumultuous sound, touching the very sky, from those swift chariots that were moving swiftly.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं परीप्सन्तस्तत्र तत्र युधिष्ठिरम् ।अन्वयुस्त्वरितास्ते वै राजानं श्रान्तवाहनाः ॥५०॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ parīpsantastatra tatra yudhiṣṭhiram ,anvayustvaritāste vai rājānaṁ śrāntavāhanāḥ.
english: Indeed, those warriors with weary mounts, swiftly followed King Duryodhana, while here and there desiring to seize Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 50. Indeed, those warriors with weary mounts, swiftly followed King Duryodhana, while here and there desiring to seize Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: अर्जुनो भीमसेनश्च माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।धृष्टद्युम्नश्च पाञ्चाल्यः शिखण्डी चापराजितः ॥५१॥
transliteration: arjuno bhīmasenaśca mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca pāñcālyaḥ śikhaṇḍī cāparājitaḥ.
english: Arjuna, Bhīmasena, and the two Pandava sons of Madri; as well as Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the prince of Pañcāla, and the unconquered Shikhaṇḍī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 51. Arjuna, Bhīmasena, and the two Pandava sons of Madri; as well as Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the prince of Pañcāla, and the unconquered Shikhaṇḍī.

sanskrit: उत्तमौजा युधामन्युः सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ।पाञ्चालानां च ये शिष्टा द्रौपदेयाश्च भारत ।हयाश्च सर्वे नागाश्च शतशश्च पदातयः ॥५२॥
transliteration: uttamaujā yudhāmanyuḥ sātyakiścāparājitaḥ ,pāñcālānāṁ ca ye śiṣṭā draupadeyāśca bhārata ,hayāśca sarve nāgāśca śataśaśca padātayaḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata, also present were Uttamaujas, Yudhāmanyu, and the unconquered Satyaki; furthermore, those who remained of the Pañcālas, the sons of Draupadi, and all the horses, elephants, and hundreds of foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 52. O descendant of Bharata, also present were Uttamaujas, Yudhāmanyu, and the unconquered Satyaki; furthermore, those who remained of the Pañcālas, the sons of Draupadi, and all the horses, elephants, and hundreds of foot soldiers.

sanskrit: ततः प्राप्तो महाराज धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।द्वैपायनह्रदं ख्यातं यत्र दुर्योधनोऽभवत् ॥५३॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāpto mahārāja dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,dvaipāyanahradaṁ khyātaṁ yatra duryodhano'bhavat.
english: Then, O great king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), arrived at the famous Dvaipāyana lake, where Duryodhana was located.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 53. Then, O great king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), arrived at the famous Dvaipāyana lake, where Duryodhana was located.

sanskrit: शीतामलजलं हृद्यं द्वितीयमिव सागरम् ।मायया सलिलं स्तभ्य यत्राभूत्ते सुतः स्थितः ॥५४॥
transliteration: śītāmalajalaṁ hṛdyaṁ dvitīyamiva sāgaram ,māyayā salilaṁ stabhya yatrābhūtte sutaḥ sthitaḥ.
english: It possessed cool, pure, and delightful water, resembling a second ocean. There, your son was situated, having immobilized the water through his power of illusion (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 54. It possessed cool, pure, and delightful water, resembling a second ocean. There, your son was situated, having immobilized the water through his power of illusion (māyā).

sanskrit: अत्यद्भुतेन विधिना दैवयोगेन भारत ।सलिलान्तर्गतः शेते दुर्दर्शः कस्यचित्प्रभो ।मानुषस्य मनुष्येन्द्र गदाहस्तो जनाधिपः ॥५५॥
transliteration: atyadbhutena vidhinā daivayogena bhārata ,salilāntargataḥ śete durdarśaḥ kasyacitprabho ,mānuṣasya manuṣyendra gadāhasto janādhipaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, by an extremely astonishing method, through divine intervention, your son lies submerged in the water, difficult for any human to perceive, O lord of men. This king, O sovereign of people, rests holding a mace in his hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 55. O Bhārata, by an extremely astonishing method, through divine intervention, your son lies submerged in the water, difficult for any human to perceive, O lord of men. This king, O sovereign of people, rests holding a mace in his hand.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा सलितान्तर्गतो वसन् ।शुश्रुवे तुमुलं शब्दं जलदोपमनिःस्वनम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā salitāntargato vasan ,śuśruve tumulaṁ śabdaṁ jaladopamaniḥsvanam.
english: Then, King Duryodhana, while residing submerged in the water, heard a tumultuous sound, which resembled the roar of a thundercloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 56. Then, King Duryodhana, while residing submerged in the water, heard a tumultuous sound, which resembled the roar of a thundercloud.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु राजेन्द्र ह्रदं तं सह सोदरैः ।आजगाम महाराज तव पुत्रवधाय वै ॥५७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu rājendra hradaṁ taṁ saha sodaraiḥ ,ājagāma mahārāja tava putravadhāya vai.
english: O King of kings (rājendra), O great king (mahārāja), Yudhiṣṭhira, along with his brothers, indeed came to that lake for the slaying of your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 57. O King of kings (rājendra), O great king (mahārāja), Yudhiṣṭhira, along with his brothers, indeed came to that lake for the slaying of your son.

sanskrit: महता शङ्खनादेन रथनेमिस्वनेन च ।उद्धुन्वंश्च महारेणुं कम्पयंश्चापि मेदिनीम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: mahatā śaṅkhanādena rathanemisvanena ca ,uddhunvaṁśca mahāreṇuṁ kampayaṁścāpi medinīm.
english: With a mighty sound of conch-shells and the clangor of chariot wheels, kicking up a great cloud of dust and also making the earth tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 58. With a mighty sound of conch-shells and the clangor of chariot wheels, kicking up a great cloud of dust and also making the earth tremble.

sanskrit: यौधिष्ठिरस्य सैन्यस्य श्रुत्वा शब्दं महारथाः ।कृतवर्मा कृपो द्रौणी राजानमिदमब्रुवन् ॥५९॥
