transliteration: yaudhiṣṭhirasya sainyasya śrutvā śabdaṁ mahārathāḥ ,kṛtavarmā kṛpo drauṇī rājānamidamabruvan.
english: Having heard the sound of Yudhiṣṭhira's army, the great charioteers Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, and Drauṇi spoke this to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 59. Having heard the sound of Yudhiṣṭhira's army, the great charioteers Kṛtavarmā, Kṛpa, and Drauṇi spoke this to the king.

sanskrit: इमे ह्यायान्ति संहृष्टाः पाण्डवा जितकाशिनः ।अपयास्यामहे तावदनुजानातु नो भवान् ॥६०॥
transliteration: ime hyāyānti saṁhṛṣṭāḥ pāṇḍavā jitakāśinaḥ ,apayāsyāmahe tāvadanujānātu no bhavān.
english: These Pāṇḍavas, indeed, are approaching, exultant and shining with victory. Let us retreat now; may Your Majesty permit us.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 60. These Pāṇḍavas, indeed, are approaching, exultant and shining with victory. Let us retreat now; may Your Majesty permit us.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तु तच्छ्रुत्वा तेषां तत्र यशस्विनाम् ।तथेत्युक्त्वा ह्रदं तं वै माययास्तम्भयत्प्रभो ॥६१॥
transliteration: duryodhanastu tacchrutvā teṣāṁ tatra yaśasvinām ,tathetyuktvā hradaṁ taṁ vai māyayāstambhayatprabho.
english: O Lord, upon hearing that from those glorious individuals there, Duryodhana, saying "So be it," indeed immobilized that lake by means of his mystic power (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 61. O Lord, upon hearing that from those glorious individuals there, Duryodhana, saying "So be it," indeed immobilized that lake by means of his mystic power (māyā).

sanskrit: ते त्वनुज्ञाप्य राजानं भृशं शोकपरायणाः ।जग्मुर्दूरं महाराज कृपप्रभृतयो रथाः ॥६२॥
transliteration: te tvanujñāpya rājānaṁ bhṛśaṁ śokaparāyaṇāḥ ,jagmurdūraṁ mahārāja kṛpaprabhṛtayo rathāḥ.
english: O great king, Kripa and the other warriors, deeply overcome by sorrow, took leave of the king (Duryodhana) and departed to a distant place.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 62. O great king, Kripa and the other warriors, deeply overcome by sorrow, took leave of the king (Duryodhana) and departed to a distant place.

sanskrit: ते गत्वा दूरमध्वानं न्यग्रोधं प्रेक्ष्य मारिष ।न्यविशन्त भृशं श्रान्ताश्चिन्तयन्तो नृपं प्रति ॥६३॥
transliteration: te gatvā dūramadhvānaṁ nyagrodhaṁ prekṣya māriṣa ,nyaviśanta bhṛśaṁ śrāntāścintayanto nṛpaṁ prati.
english: O respectable one, after traveling a long distance, they saw a banyan tree and, being greatly exhausted, sat down, reflecting upon the king (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 63. O respectable one, after traveling a long distance, they saw a banyan tree and, being greatly exhausted, sat down, reflecting upon the king (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: विष्टभ्य सलिलं सुप्तो धार्तराष्ट्रो महाबलः ।पाण्डवाश्चापि संप्राप्तास्तं देशं युद्धमीप्सवः ॥६४॥
transliteration: viṣṭabhya salilaṁ supto dhārtarāṣṭro mahābalaḥ ,pāṇḍavāścāpi saṁprāptāstaṁ deśaṁ yuddhamīpsavaḥ.
english: The mighty son of Dhritarashtra (Duryodhana) was sleeping there, having immobilized the water. Meanwhile, the Pāṇḍavas also arrived at that very spot, eager for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 64. The mighty son of Dhritarashtra (Duryodhana) was sleeping there, having immobilized the water. Meanwhile, the Pāṇḍavas also arrived at that very spot, eager for battle.

sanskrit: कथं नु युद्धं भविता कथं राजा भविष्यति ।कथं नु पाण्डवा राजन्प्रतिपत्स्यन्ति कौरवम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu yuddhaṁ bhavitā kathaṁ rājā bhaviṣyati ,kathaṁ nu pāṇḍavā rājanpratipatsyanti kauravam.
english: How, indeed, will the battle take place? What will become of the king? And how, O King, will the Pāṇḍavas deal with the Kaurava?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 65. How, indeed, will the battle take place? What will become of the king? And how, O King, will the Pāṇḍavas deal with the Kaurava?

sanskrit: इत्येवं चिन्तयन्तस्ते रथेभ्योऽश्वान्विमुच्य ह ।तत्रासां चक्रिरे राजन्कृपप्रभृतयो रथाः ॥६६॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ cintayantaste rathebhyo'śvānvimucya ha ,tatrāsāṁ cakrire rājankṛpaprabhṛtayo rathāḥ.
english: Indeed, while contemplating thus, O King, Kṛpa and his companions, having released the horses from their chariots, settled down there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 29 Verse 66. Indeed, while contemplating thus, O King, Kṛpa and his companions, having released the horses from their chariots, settled down there.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततस्तेष्वपयातेषु रथेषु त्रिषु पाण्डवाः ।तं ह्रदं प्रत्यपद्यन्त यत्र दुर्योधनोऽभवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tatasteṣvapayāteṣu ratheṣu triṣu pāṇḍavāḥ ,taṁ hradaṁ pratyapadyanta yatra duryodhano'bhavat.
english: Saṃjaya said: 'Then, after those three chariots had departed, the Pāṇḍavas went towards that lake where Duryodhana was located.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 1. Saṃjaya said: 'Then, after those three chariots had departed, the Pāṇḍavas went towards that lake where Duryodhana was located.'

sanskrit: आसाद्य च कुरुश्रेष्ठ तदा द्वैपायनह्रदम् ।स्तम्भितं धार्तराष्ट्रेण दृष्ट्वा तं सलिलाशयम् ।वासुदेवमिदं वाक्यमब्रवीत्कुरुनन्दनः ॥२॥
transliteration: āsādya ca kuruśreṣṭha tadā dvaipāyanahradam ,stambhitaṁ dhārtarāṣṭreṇa dṛṣṭvā taṁ salilāśayam ,vāsudevamidaṁ vākyamabravītkurunandanaḥ.
english: And then, O best of the Kurus, having reached the Dvaipāyana lake and seeing that water reservoir rendered motionless by the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), the delight of the Kurus (Yudhiṣṭhira) spoke these words to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 2. And then, O best of the Kurus, having reached the Dvaipāyana lake and seeing that water reservoir rendered motionless by the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), the delight of the Kurus (Yudhiṣṭhira) spoke these words to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: पश्येमां धार्तराष्ट्रेण मायामप्सु प्रयोजिताम् ।विष्टभ्य सलिलं शेते नास्य मानुषतो भयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: paśyemāṁ dhārtarāṣṭreṇa māyāmapsu prayojitām ,viṣṭabhya salilaṁ śete nāsya mānuṣato bhayam.
english: Behold this magical illusion (māyā) employed by Duryodhana in the waters! Having blocked the flow of water, he lies there. He has no fear from any human.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 3. Behold this magical illusion (māyā) employed by Duryodhana in the waters! Having blocked the flow of water, he lies there. He has no fear from any human.

sanskrit: दैवीं मायामिमां कृत्वा सलिलान्तर्गतो ह्ययम् ।निकृत्या निकृतिप्रज्ञो न मे जीवन्विमोक्ष्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: daivīṁ māyāmimāṁ kṛtvā salilāntargato hyayam ,nikṛtyā nikṛtiprajño na me jīvanvimokṣyate.
english: Indeed, having created this divine illusion (māyā), he has entered the water. This deceitfully intelligent one will certainly not escape me alive through trickery.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 4. Indeed, having created this divine illusion (māyā), he has entered the water. This deceitfully intelligent one will certainly not escape me alive through trickery.

sanskrit: यद्यस्य समरे साह्यं कुरुते वज्रभृत्स्वयम् ।तथाप्येनं हतं युद्धे लोको द्रक्ष्यति माधव ॥५॥
transliteration: yadyasya samare sāhyaṁ kurute vajrabhṛtsvayam ,tathāpyenaṁ hataṁ yuddhe loko drakṣyati mādhava.
english: O Madhava, even if Indra himself provides assistance to him in battle, still, the world will see him slain in this war.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 5. O Madhava, even if Indra himself provides assistance to him in battle, still, the world will see him slain in this war.

sanskrit: श्रीवासुदेव उवाच ।मायाविन इमां मायां मायया जहि भारत ।मायावी मायया वध्यः सत्यमेतद्युधिष्ठिर ॥६॥
transliteration: śrīvāsudeva uvāca ,māyāvina imāṁ māyāṁ māyayā jahi bhārata ,māyāvī māyayā vadhyaḥ satyametadyudhiṣṭhira.
english: Lord Vasudeva said: "O Bharata, defeat this illusion (māyā) of the master of illusion by employing (counter-)illusion (māyā)! O Yudhishthira, it is true that a master of illusion should be slain by (counter-)illusion."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 6. Lord Vasudeva said: "O Bharata, defeat this illusion (māyā) of the master of illusion by employing (counter-)illusion (māyā)! O Yudhishthira, it is true that a master of illusion should be slain by (counter-)illusion."

sanskrit: क्रियाभ्युपायैर्बहुलैर्मायामप्सु प्रयोज्य ह ।जहि त्वं भरतश्रेष्ठ पापात्मानं सुयोधनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: kriyābhyupāyairbahulairmāyāmapsu prayojya ha ,jahi tvaṁ bharataśreṣṭha pāpātmānaṁ suyodhanam.
english: O best of the Bharatas, you must indeed kill the evil-minded Suyodhana, having employed numerous means and deceptions (māyā) in the waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 7. O best of the Bharatas, you must indeed kill the evil-minded Suyodhana, having employed numerous means and deceptions (māyā) in the waters.

sanskrit: क्रियाभ्युपायैरिन्द्रेण निहता दैत्यदानवाः ।क्रियाभ्युपायैर्बहुभिर्बलिर्बद्धो महात्मना ॥८॥
transliteration: kriyābhyupāyairindreṇa nihatā daityadānavāḥ ,kriyābhyupāyairbahubhirbalirbaddho mahātmanā.
english: Through various stratagems, Indra slew the Daityas and Danavas. Similarly, by many means, Bali was bound by the great-souled one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 8. Through various stratagems, Indra slew the Daityas and Danavas. Similarly, by many means, Bali was bound by the great-souled one.

sanskrit: क्रियाभ्युपायैः पूर्वं हि हिरण्याक्षो महासुरः ।हिरण्यकशिपुश्चैव क्रिययैव निषूदितौ ।वृत्रश्च निहतो राजन्क्रिययैव न संशयः ॥९॥
transliteration: kriyābhyupāyaiḥ pūrvaṁ hi hiraṇyākṣo mahāsuraḥ ,hiraṇyakaśipuścaiva kriyayaiva niṣūditau ,vṛtraśca nihato rājankriyayaiva na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Indeed, formerly, by means of stratagems, the great asura Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu were both slain by similar stratagems. And Vritra, O King, was also killed by stratagems; there is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 9. Indeed, formerly, by means of stratagems, the great asura Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu were both slain by similar stratagems. And Vritra, O King, was also killed by stratagems; there is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: तथा पौलस्त्यतनयो रावणो नाम राक्षसः ।रामेण निहतो राजन्सानुबन्धः सहानुगः ।क्रियया योगमास्थाय तथा त्वमपि विक्रम ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathā paulastyatanayo rāvaṇo nāma rākṣasaḥ ,rāmeṇa nihato rājansānubandhaḥ sahānugaḥ ,kriyayā yogamāsthāya tathā tvamapi vikrama.
english: Similarly, O King, the demon named Ravana, the son of Pulastya, was slain by Rama, along with his relatives and followers. You too, having resorted to this application (yoga) of stratagem, should act with valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 10. Similarly, O King, the demon named Ravana, the son of Pulastya, was slain by Rama, along with his relatives and followers. You too, having resorted to this application (yoga) of stratagem, should act with valor.

sanskrit: क्रियाभ्युपायैर्निहतो मया राजन्पुरातने ।तारकश्च महादैत्यो विप्रचित्तिश्च वीर्यवान् ॥११॥
transliteration: kriyābhyupāyairnihato mayā rājanpurātane ,tārakaśca mahādaityo vipracittiśca vīryavān.
english: O King, in ancient times, the great demon Tāraka and the powerful Vipracitti were slain by me through skillful actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 11. O King, in ancient times, the great demon Tāraka and the powerful Vipracitti were slain by me through skillful actions.

sanskrit: वातापिरिल्वलश्चैव त्रिशिराश्च तथा विभो ।सुन्दोपसुन्दावसुरौ क्रिययैव निषूदितौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: vātāpirilvalaścaiva triśirāśca tathā vibho ,sundopasundāvasurau kriyayaiva niṣūditau.
english: And O Lord, Vātāpi, Ilvala, and Triśirā, as well as the two asuras Sunda and Upasunda, were similarly destroyed by action (kriyā) alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 12. And O Lord, Vātāpi, Ilvala, and Triśirā, as well as the two asuras Sunda and Upasunda, were similarly destroyed by action (kriyā) alone.

sanskrit: क्रियाभ्युपायैरिन्द्रेण त्रिदिवं भुज्यते विभो ।क्रिया बलवती राजन्नान्यत्किंचिद्युधिष्ठिर ॥१३॥
transliteration: kriyābhyupāyairindreṇa tridivaṁ bhujyate vibho ,kriyā balavatī rājannānyatkiṁcidyudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Mighty one, by skillful actions, heaven is enjoyed by Indra. O King Yudhiṣṭhira, action (kriyā) is powerful; there is nothing else.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 13. O Mighty one, by skillful actions, heaven is enjoyed by Indra. O King Yudhiṣṭhira, action (kriyā) is powerful; there is nothing else.

sanskrit: दैत्याश्च दानवाश्चैव राक्षसाः पार्थिवास्तथा ।क्रियाभ्युपायैर्निहताः क्रियां तस्मात्समाचर ॥१४॥
transliteration: daityāśca dānavāścaiva rākṣasāḥ pārthivāstathā ,kriyābhyupāyairnihatāḥ kriyāṁ tasmātsamācara.
english: And Daityas, Dānavas, Rākṣasas, and earthly kings were similarly destroyed by skillful actions. Therefore, perform actions (kriyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 14. And Daityas, Dānavas, Rākṣasas, and earthly kings were similarly destroyed by skillful actions. Therefore, perform actions (kriyā).

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।इत्युक्तो वासुदेवेन पाण्डवः संशितव्रतः ।जलस्थं तं महाराज तव पुत्रं महाबलम् ।अभ्यभाषत कौन्तेयः प्रहसन्निव भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,ityukto vāsudevena pāṇḍavaḥ saṁśitavrataḥ ,jalasthaṁ taṁ mahārāja tava putraṁ mahābalam ,abhyabhāṣata kaunteyaḥ prahasanniva bhārata.
english: Sañjaya said: O great king, O Bhārata, after Vāsudeva had spoken thus, Kounteya, who was firm in his vow, addressed your immensely powerful son, who was staying in the water, as if laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 15. Sañjaya said: O great king, O Bhārata, after Vāsudeva had spoken thus, Kounteya, who was firm in his vow, addressed your immensely powerful son, who was staying in the water, as if laughing.

sanskrit: सुयोधन किमर्थोऽयमारम्भोऽप्सु कृतस्त्वया ।सर्वं क्षत्रं घातयित्वा स्वकुलं च विशां पते ॥१६॥
transliteration: suyodhana kimartho'yamārambho'psu kṛtastvayā ,sarvaṁ kṣatraṁ ghātayitvā svakulaṁ ca viśāṁ pate.
english: O Suyodhana, O lord of the people, after having caused the destruction of all kṣatriyas and your own family, for what reason have you undertaken this endeavor in the water?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 16. O Suyodhana, O lord of the people, after having caused the destruction of all kṣatriyas and your own family, for what reason have you undertaken this endeavor in the water?

sanskrit: जलाशयं प्रविष्टोऽद्य वाञ्छञ्जीवितमात्मनः ।उत्तिष्ठ राजन्युध्यस्व सहास्माभिः सुयोधन ॥१७॥
transliteration: jalāśayaṁ praviṣṭo'dya vāñchañjīvitamātmanaḥ ,uttiṣṭha rājanyudhyasva sahāsmābhiḥ suyodhana.
english: Having entered the lake today, desiring to save your own life (ātman) – Rise up, O king Suyodhana, and fight with us!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 17. Having entered the lake today, desiring to save your own life (ātman) – Rise up, O king Suyodhana, and fight with us!

sanskrit: स च दर्पो नरश्रेष्ठ स च मानः क्व ते गतः ।यस्त्वं संस्तभ्य सलिलं भीतो राजन्व्यवस्थितः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa ca darpo naraśreṣṭha sa ca mānaḥ kva te gataḥ ,yastvaṁ saṁstabhya salilaṁ bhīto rājanvyavasthitaḥ.
english: O best of men, O king, where have that pride and that self-respect of yours gone, that you are now fearfully concealing yourself by holding back the water?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 18. O best of men, O king, where have that pride and that self-respect of yours gone, that you are now fearfully concealing yourself by holding back the water?

sanskrit: सर्वे त्वां शूर इत्येव जना जल्पन्ति संसदि ।व्यर्थं तद्भवतो मन्ये शौर्यं सलिलशायिनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sarve tvāṁ śūra ityeva janā jalpanti saṁsadi ,vyarthaṁ tadbhavato manye śauryaṁ salilaśāyinaḥ.
english: All the people in the assembly indeed proclaim you to be a hero. Yet, I consider that valor of yours, as you lie in the water, to be futile.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 19. All the people in the assembly indeed proclaim you to be a hero. Yet, I consider that valor of yours, as you lie in the water, to be futile.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठ राजन्युध्यस्व क्षत्रियोऽसि कुलोद्भवः ।कौरवेयो विशेषेण कुले जन्म च संस्मर ॥२०॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭha rājanyudhyasva kṣatriyo'si kulodbhavaḥ ,kauraveyo viśeṣeṇa kule janma ca saṁsmara.
english: Arise, O king, and fight! You are a warrior (kṣatriya) born of a noble lineage. Especially remember your birth into the Kuru family.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 20. Arise, O king, and fight! You are a warrior (kṣatriya) born of a noble lineage. Especially remember your birth into the Kuru family.

sanskrit: स कथं कौरवे वंशे प्रशंसञ्जन्म चात्मनः ।युद्धाद्भीतस्ततस्तोयं प्रविश्य प्रतितिष्ठसि ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa kathaṁ kaurave vaṁśe praśaṁsañjanma cātmanaḥ ,yuddhādbhītastatastoyaṁ praviśya pratitiṣṭhasi.
english: How can you, having praised your own (ātman) birth in the Kuru lineage, then, being afraid of battle, enter the water and remain there?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 21. How can you, having praised your own (ātman) birth in the Kuru lineage, then, being afraid of battle, enter the water and remain there?

sanskrit: अयुद्धमव्यवस्थानं नैष धर्मः सनातनः ।अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यं रणे राजन्पलायनम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: ayuddhamavyavasthānaṁ naiṣa dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ ,anāryajuṣṭamasvargyaṁ raṇe rājanpalāyanam.
english: Not fighting, not standing firm - this is not the eternal (sanātana) constitution (dharma). Fleeing in battle, O king, is not practiced by noble individuals and does not lead to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 22. Not fighting, not standing firm - this is not the eternal (sanātana) constitution (dharma). Fleeing in battle, O king, is not practiced by noble individuals and does not lead to heaven.

sanskrit: कथं पारमगत्वा हि युद्धे त्वं वै जिजीविषुः ।इमान्निपतितान्दृष्ट्वा पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्पितॄंस्तथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ pāramagatvā hi yuddhe tvaṁ vai jijīviṣuḥ ,imānnipatitāndṛṣṭvā putrānbhrātṝnpitṝṁstathā.
english: Having seen these sons, brothers, and paternal figures fallen, how can you, indeed, wish to live without having brought the battle to its conclusion?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 23. Having seen these sons, brothers, and paternal figures fallen, how can you, indeed, wish to live without having brought the battle to its conclusion?

sanskrit: संबन्धिनो वयस्यांश्च मातुलान्बान्धवांस्तथा ।घातयित्वा कथं तात ह्रदे तिष्ठसि सांप्रतम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: saṁbandhino vayasyāṁśca mātulānbāndhavāṁstathā ,ghātayitvā kathaṁ tāta hrade tiṣṭhasi sāṁpratam.
english: Dear one, having caused these relatives, friends, maternal uncles, and kinsmen to be slain, how can you remain here, calm as if in a tranquil pool, now?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 24. Dear one, having caused these relatives, friends, maternal uncles, and kinsmen to be slain, how can you remain here, calm as if in a tranquil pool, now?

sanskrit: शूरमानी न शूरस्त्वं मिथ्या वदसि भारत ।शूरोऽहमिति दुर्बुद्धे सर्वलोकस्य शृण्वतः ॥२५॥
transliteration: śūramānī na śūrastvaṁ mithyā vadasi bhārata ,śūro'hamiti durbuddhe sarvalokasya śṛṇvataḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata), you are not brave, though you consider yourself to be a hero. O foolish-minded one, you falsely declare 'I am a hero' while everyone in the world listens.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 25. O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata), you are not brave, though you consider yourself to be a hero. O foolish-minded one, you falsely declare 'I am a hero' while everyone in the world listens.

sanskrit: न हि शूराः पलायन्ते शत्रून्दृष्ट्वा कथंचन ।ब्रूहि वा त्वं यया धृत्या शूर त्यजसि संगरम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: na hi śūrāḥ palāyante śatrūndṛṣṭvā kathaṁcana ,brūhi vā tvaṁ yayā dhṛtyā śūra tyajasi saṁgaram.
english: Indeed, true heroes never flee upon seeing their enemies. So, tell me, O 'brave one' (śūra), by what sort of resolve (dhṛti) do you abandon this battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 26. Indeed, true heroes never flee upon seeing their enemies. So, tell me, O 'brave one' (śūra), by what sort of resolve (dhṛti) do you abandon this battle?

sanskrit: स त्वमुत्तिष्ठ युध्यस्व विनीय भयमात्मनः ।घातयित्वा सर्वसैन्यं भ्रातॄंश्चैव सुयोधन ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa tvamuttiṣṭha yudhyasva vinīya bhayamātmanaḥ ,ghātayitvā sarvasainyaṁ bhrātṝṁścaiva suyodhana.
english: O Suyodhana, having caused the destruction of the entire army and indeed your brothers, you yourself now rise up and fight, casting away the fear from your own being (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 27. O Suyodhana, having caused the destruction of the entire army and indeed your brothers, you yourself now rise up and fight, casting away the fear from your own being (ātman).

sanskrit: नेदानीं जीविते बुद्धिः कार्या धर्मचिकीर्षया ।क्षत्रधर्ममपाश्रित्य त्वद्विधेन सुयोधन ॥२८॥
transliteration: nedānīṁ jīvite buddhiḥ kāryā dharmacikīrṣayā ,kṣatradharmamapāśritya tvadvidhena suyodhana.
english: O Suyodhana, now, for one like you who has embraced the intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma) of a warrior, there should be no thought of preserving your life (jīvite buddhiḥ) with the desire to uphold natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 28. O Suyodhana, now, for one like you who has embraced the intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma) of a warrior, there should be no thought of preserving your life (jīvite buddhiḥ) with the desire to uphold natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: यत्तत्कर्णमुपाश्रित्य शकुनिं चापि सौबलम् ।अमर्त्य इव संमोहात्त्वमात्मानं न बुद्धवान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: yattatkarṇamupāśritya śakuniṁ cāpi saubalam ,amartya iva saṁmohāttvamātmānaṁ na buddhavān.
english: Because, having relied on Karna and also Shakuni, the son of Subala, you, due to great delusion, did not understand your own true being (ātman) as if you were an immortal.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 29. Because, having relied on Karna and also Shakuni, the son of Subala, you, due to great delusion, did not understand your own true being (ātman) as if you were an immortal.

sanskrit: तत्पापं सुमहत्कृत्वा प्रतियुध्यस्व भारत ।कथं हि त्वद्विधो मोहाद्रोचयेत पलायनम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tatpāpaṁ sumahatkṛtvā pratiyudhyasva bhārata ,kathaṁ hi tvadvidho mohādrocayeta palāyanam.
english: O descendant of Bharata, having indeed committed that enormous sin, now fight back! How could one like you, due to delusion, possibly prefer to flee?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 30. O descendant of Bharata, having indeed committed that enormous sin, now fight back! How could one like you, due to delusion, possibly prefer to flee?

sanskrit: क्व ते तत्पौरुषं यातं क्व च मानः सुयोधन ।क्व च विक्रान्तता याता क्व च विस्फूर्जितं महत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: kva te tatpauruṣaṁ yātaṁ kva ca mānaḥ suyodhana ,kva ca vikrāntatā yātā kva ca visphūrjitaṁ mahat.
english: O Suyodhana, where has that manliness of yours gone? Where is your pride? Where has your valor vanished? And where is that great display of might?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 31. O Suyodhana, where has that manliness of yours gone? Where is your pride? Where has your valor vanished? And where is that great display of might?

sanskrit: क्व ते कृतास्त्रता याता किं च शेषे जलाशये ।स त्वमुत्तिष्ठ युध्यस्व क्षत्रधर्मेण भारत ॥३२॥
transliteration: kva te kṛtāstratā yātā kiṁ ca śeṣe jalāśaye ,sa tvamuttiṣṭha yudhyasva kṣatradharmeṇa bhārata.
english: Where has your mastery of weapons gone? And why do you lie in the lake? Therefore, rise up and fight according to the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior, O Bhārata!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 32. Where has your mastery of weapons gone? And why do you lie in the lake? Therefore, rise up and fight according to the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior, O Bhārata!

sanskrit: अस्मान्वा त्वं पराजित्य प्रशाधि पृथिवीमिमाम् ।अथ वा निहतोऽस्माभिर्भूमौ स्वप्स्यसि भारत ॥३३॥
transliteration: asmānvā tvaṁ parājitya praśādhi pṛthivīmimām ,atha vā nihato'smābhirbhūmau svapsyasi bhārata.
english: Either you, having defeated us, rule this earth; or else, struck down by us, you will sleep on the ground, O Bhārata!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 33. Either you, having defeated us, rule this earth; or else, struck down by us, you will sleep on the ground, O Bhārata!

sanskrit: एष ते प्रथमो धर्मः सृष्टो धात्रा महात्मना ।तं कुरुष्व यथातथ्यं राजा भव महारथ ॥३४॥
transliteration: eṣa te prathamo dharmaḥ sṛṣṭo dhātrā mahātmanā ,taṁ kuruṣva yathātathyaṁ rājā bhava mahāratha.
english: This is your foremost intrinsic nature (dharma), created by the great-souled Creator. Fulfill that truly; be a king, O great charioteer!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 34. This is your foremost intrinsic nature (dharma), created by the great-souled Creator. Fulfill that truly; be a king, O great charioteer!

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।नैतच्चित्रं महाराज यद्भीः प्राणिनमाविशेत् ।न च प्राणभयाद्भीतो व्यपयातोऽस्मि भारत ॥३५॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,naitaccitraṁ mahārāja yadbhīḥ prāṇinamāviśet ,na ca prāṇabhayādbhīto vyapayāto'smi bhārata.
english: Duryodhana said: "O great king, it is not surprising that fear should enter a living being. But I have not fled because of fear for my life, O Bhārata."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 35. Duryodhana said: "O great king, it is not surprising that fear should enter a living being. But I have not fled because of fear for my life, O Bhārata."

sanskrit: अरथश्चानिषङ्गी च निहतः पार्ष्णिसारथिः ।एकश्चाप्यगणः संख्ये प्रत्याश्वासमरोचयम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: arathaścāniṣaṅgī ca nihataḥ pārṣṇisārathiḥ ,ekaścāpyagaṇaḥ saṁkhye pratyāśvāsamarocayam.
english: Though without a chariot, without a quiver, and with my charioteer killed, I, who was alone and without an army in battle, did not choose retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 36. Though without a chariot, without a quiver, and with my charioteer killed, I, who was alone and without an army in battle, did not choose retreat.

sanskrit: न प्राणहेतोर्न भयान्न विषादाद्विशां पते ।इदमम्भः प्रविष्टोऽस्मि श्रमात्त्विदमनुष्ठितम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: na prāṇahetorna bhayānna viṣādādviśāṁ pate ,idamambhaḥ praviṣṭo'smi śramāttvidamanuṣṭhitam.
english: O lord of the people, I have entered this water not for the sake of my life, nor out of fear, nor out of despondency. Rather, this was done due to exhaustion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 37. O lord of the people, I have entered this water not for the sake of my life, nor out of fear, nor out of despondency. Rather, this was done due to exhaustion.

sanskrit: त्वं चाश्वसिहि कौन्तेय ये चाप्यनुगतास्तव ।अहमुत्थाय वः सर्वान्प्रतियोत्स्यामि संयुगे ॥३८॥
transliteration: tvaṁ cāśvasihi kaunteya ye cāpyanugatāstava ,ahamutthāya vaḥ sarvānpratiyotsyāmi saṁyuge.
english: O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), you and all those who have followed you, take heart! I will rise and fight against all of you in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 38. O son of Kuntī (Kaunteya), you and all those who have followed you, take heart! I will rise and fight against all of you in battle.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।आश्वस्ता एव सर्वे स्म चिरं त्वां मृगयामहे ।तदिदानीं समुत्तिष्ठ युध्यस्वेह सुयोधन ॥३९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,āśvastā eva sarve sma ciraṁ tvāṁ mṛgayāmahe ,tadidānīṁ samuttiṣṭha yudhyasveha suyodhana.
english: Yudhishthira said: "All of us are indeed reassured; we have been searching for you for a long time. Therefore, rise up now, Suyodhana, and fight here!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 39. Yudhishthira said: "All of us are indeed reassured; we have been searching for you for a long time. Therefore, rise up now, Suyodhana, and fight here!"

sanskrit: हत्वा वा समरे पार्थान्स्फीतं राज्यमवाप्नुहि ।निहतो वा रणेऽस्माभिर्वीरलोकमवाप्स्यसि ॥४०॥
transliteration: hatvā vā samare pārthānsphītaṁ rājyamavāpnuhi ,nihato vā raṇe'smābhirvīralokamavāpsyasi.
english: Either, by killing the sons of Pritha (Pārthas) in battle, obtain a prosperous kingdom, or, if killed by us in battle, you will attain the world of heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 40. Either, by killing the sons of Pritha (Pārthas) in battle, obtain a prosperous kingdom, or, if killed by us in battle, you will attain the world of heroes.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।यदर्थं राज्यमिच्छामि कुरूणां कुरुनन्दन ।त इमे निहताः सर्वे भ्रातरो मे जनेश्वर ॥४१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,yadarthaṁ rājyamicchāmi kurūṇāṁ kurunandana ,ta ime nihatāḥ sarve bhrātaro me janeśvara.
english: Duryodhana said: "O joy of the Kurus (kuru-nandana), the very purpose for which I desired the kingdom, my brothers, all of them, O lord of men (jana-īśvara), are now slain!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 41. Duryodhana said: "O joy of the Kurus (kuru-nandana), the very purpose for which I desired the kingdom, my brothers, all of them, O lord of men (jana-īśvara), are now slain!"

sanskrit: क्षीणरत्नां च पृथिवीं हतक्षत्रियपुंगवाम् ।नाभ्युत्सहाम्यहं भोक्तुं विधवामिव योषितम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: kṣīṇaratnāṁ ca pṛthivīṁ hatakṣatriyapuṁgavām ,nābhyutsahāmyahaṁ bhoktuṁ vidhavāmiva yoṣitam.
english: I am unable to enjoy (rule) the earth, which is depleted of its treasures and has its finest warriors (kṣatriya-puṅgavam) slain, just as one would not enjoy a widowed woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 42. I am unable to enjoy (rule) the earth, which is depleted of its treasures and has its finest warriors (kṣatriya-puṅgavam) slain, just as one would not enjoy a widowed woman.

sanskrit: अद्यापि त्वहमाशंसे त्वां विजेतुं युधिष्ठिर ।भङ्क्त्वा पाञ्चालपाण्डूनामुत्साहं भरतर्षभ ॥४३॥
transliteration: adyāpi tvahamāśaṁse tvāṁ vijetuṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,bhaṅktvā pāñcālapāṇḍūnāmutsāhaṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, O best of Bharatas, even now I still hope to defeat you, after having crushed the spirit of the Pāñcālas and Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 43. O Yudhiṣṭhira, O best of Bharatas, even now I still hope to defeat you, after having crushed the spirit of the Pāñcālas and Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: न त्विदानीमहं मन्ये कार्यं युद्धेन कर्हिचित् ।द्रोणे कर्णे च संशान्ते निहते च पितामहे ॥४४॥
transliteration: na tvidānīmahaṁ manye kāryaṁ yuddhena karhicit ,droṇe karṇe ca saṁśānte nihate ca pitāmahe.
english: But now, I certainly do not consider engaging in war to be appropriate at all, since Drona and Karna have ceased to exist, and the paternal grandfather (Bhishma) has been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 44. But now, I certainly do not consider engaging in war to be appropriate at all, since Drona and Karna have ceased to exist, and the paternal grandfather (Bhishma) has been killed.

sanskrit: अस्त्विदानीमियं राजन्केवला पृथिवी तव ।असहायो हि को राजा राज्यमिच्छेत्प्रशासितुम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: astvidānīmiyaṁ rājankevalā pṛthivī tava ,asahāyo hi ko rājā rājyamicchetpraśāsitum.
english: O King, let this entire earth now be exclusively yours. For what king, being without allies, would wish to rule a kingdom?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 45. O King, let this entire earth now be exclusively yours. For what king, being without allies, would wish to rule a kingdom?

sanskrit: सुहृदस्तादृशान्हित्वा पुत्रान्भ्रातॄन्पितॄनपि ।भवद्भिश्च हृते राज्ये को नु जीवेत मादृशः ॥४६॥
transliteration: suhṛdastādṛśānhitvā putrānbhrātṝnpitṝnapi ,bhavadbhiśca hṛte rājye ko nu jīveta mādṛśaḥ.
english: After abandoning such friends, sons, brothers, and even fathers, and with the kingdom seized by you all, who like me would indeed want to live?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 46. After abandoning such friends, sons, brothers, and even fathers, and with the kingdom seized by you all, who like me would indeed want to live?

sanskrit: अहं वनं गमिष्यामि ह्यजिनैः प्रतिवासितः ।रतिर्हि नास्ति मे राज्ये हतपक्षस्य भारत ॥४७॥
transliteration: ahaṁ vanaṁ gamiṣyāmi hyajinaiḥ prativāsitaḥ ,ratirhi nāsti me rājye hatapakṣasya bhārata.
english: I will go to the forest, clad in deerskins. Indeed, I have no delight in the kingdom, O Bhārata, since my allies have been destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 47. I will go to the forest, clad in deerskins. Indeed, I have no delight in the kingdom, O Bhārata, since my allies have been destroyed.

sanskrit: हतबान्धवभूयिष्ठा हताश्वा हतकुञ्जरा ।एषा ते पृथिवी राजन्भुङ्क्ष्वैनां विगतज्वरः ॥४८॥
transliteration: hatabāndhavabhūyiṣṭhā hatāśvā hatakuñjarā ,eṣā te pṛthivī rājanbhuṅkṣvaināṁ vigatajvaraḥ.
english: O King, this earth is mostly filled with slain relatives, with its horses and elephants killed. Enjoy her, free from distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 48. O King, this earth is mostly filled with slain relatives, with its horses and elephants killed. Enjoy her, free from distress.

sanskrit: वनमेव गमिष्यामि वसानो मृगचर्मणी ।न हि मे निर्जितस्यास्ति जीवितेऽद्य स्पृहा विभो ॥४९॥
transliteration: vanameva gamiṣyāmi vasāno mṛgacarmaṇī ,na hi me nirjitasyāsti jīvite'dya spṛhā vibho.
english: I will indeed go to the forest, wearing deerskin garments. Truly, for me, the defeated, there is no longer any desire for life today, O mighty one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 49. I will indeed go to the forest, wearing deerskin garments. Truly, for me, the defeated, there is no longer any desire for life today, O mighty one.

sanskrit: गच्छ त्वं भुङ्क्ष्व राजेन्द्र पृथिवीं निहतेश्वराम् ।हतयोधां नष्टरत्नां क्षीणवप्रां यथासुखम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: gaccha tvaṁ bhuṅkṣva rājendra pṛthivīṁ nihateśvarām ,hatayodhāṁ naṣṭaratnāṁ kṣīṇavaprāṁ yathāsukham.
english: You, O great king, go forth and enjoy the earth as you please, an earth whose rulers are slain, whose warriors are killed, whose treasures are lost, and whose fortifications are diminished.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 50. You, O great king, go forth and enjoy the earth as you please, an earth whose rulers are slain, whose warriors are killed, whose treasures are lost, and whose fortifications are diminished.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।आर्तप्रलापान्मा तात सलिलस्थः प्रभाषथाः ।नैतन्मनसि मे राजन्वाशितं शकुनेरिव ॥५१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ārtapralāpānmā tāta salilasthaḥ prabhāṣathāḥ ,naitanmanasi me rājanvāśitaṁ śakuneriva.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "My dear father (tāta), while you are situated amidst the waters, do not utter such piteous lamentations of distress. O king, these words do not settle in my mind, just like the mere cry of a bird."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 51. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "My dear father (tāta), while you are situated amidst the waters, do not utter such piteous lamentations of distress. O king, these words do not settle in my mind, just like the mere cry of a bird."

sanskrit: यदि चापि समर्थः स्यास्त्वं दानाय सुयोधन ।नाहमिच्छेयमवनिं त्वया दत्तां प्रशासितुम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: yadi cāpi samarthaḥ syāstvaṁ dānāya suyodhana ,nāhamiccheyamavaniṁ tvayā dattāṁ praśāsitum.
english: Even if you, Suyodhana, were capable of giving (the kingdom), I would not wish to rule the earth (kingdom) that was given by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 52. Even if you, Suyodhana, were capable of giving (the kingdom), I would not wish to rule the earth (kingdom) that was given by you.

sanskrit: अधर्मेण न गृह्णीयां त्वया दत्तां महीमिमाम् ।न हि धर्मः स्मृतो राजन्क्षत्रियस्य प्रतिग्रहः ॥५३॥
transliteration: adharmeṇa na gṛhṇīyāṁ tvayā dattāṁ mahīmimām ,na hi dharmaḥ smṛto rājankṣatriyasya pratigrahaḥ.
english: I would not accept this land, given by you, through an act of unrighteousness (adharma). For, O king, the acceptance of gifts is certainly not considered the proper natural law (dharma) for a warrior (kṣatriya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 53. I would not accept this land, given by you, through an act of unrighteousness (adharma). For, O king, the acceptance of gifts is certainly not considered the proper natural law (dharma) for a warrior (kṣatriya).

sanskrit: त्वया दत्तां न चेच्छेयं पृथिवीमखिलामहम् ।त्वां तु युद्धे विनिर्जित्य भोक्तास्मि वसुधामिमाम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: tvayā dattāṁ na ceccheyaṁ pṛthivīmakhilāmaham ,tvāṁ tu yuddhe vinirjitya bhoktāsmi vasudhāmimām.
english: And I would not wish for the entire earth given by you. But having thoroughly defeated you in battle, I shall indeed rule this land.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 54. And I would not wish for the entire earth given by you. But having thoroughly defeated you in battle, I shall indeed rule this land.

sanskrit: अनीश्वरश्च पृथिवीं कथं त्वं दातुमिच्छसि ।त्वयेयं पृथिवी राजन्किं न दत्ता तदैव हि ॥५५॥
transliteration: anīśvaraśca pṛthivīṁ kathaṁ tvaṁ dātumicchasi ,tvayeyaṁ pṛthivī rājankiṁ na dattā tadaiva hi.
english: Being powerless, how do you wish to give the earth? O King, wasn't this very earth given by you back then?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 55. Being powerless, how do you wish to give the earth? O King, wasn't this very earth given by you back then?

sanskrit: धर्मतो याचमानानां शमार्थं च कुलस्य नः ।वार्ष्णेयं प्रथमं राजन्प्रत्याख्याय महाबलम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: dharmato yācamānānāṁ śamārthaṁ ca kulasya naḥ ,vārṣṇeyaṁ prathamaṁ rājanpratyākhyāya mahābalam.
english: O King, having first rejected the immensely powerful Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa), who was seeking (justice) according to natural law (dharma) for the sake of peace for our family...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 56. O King, having first rejected the immensely powerful Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa), who was seeking (justice) according to natural law (dharma) for the sake of peace for our family...

sanskrit: किमिदानीं ददासि त्वं को हि ते चित्तविभ्रमः ।अभियुक्तस्तु को राजा दातुमिच्छेद्धि मेदिनीम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: kimidānīṁ dadāsi tvaṁ ko hi te cittavibhramaḥ ,abhiyuktastu ko rājā dātumiccheddhi medinīm.
english: What are you giving now? What indeed is this delusion of your mind? What king, being assailed, would indeed wish to give the earth?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 57. What are you giving now? What indeed is this delusion of your mind? What king, being assailed, would indeed wish to give the earth?

sanskrit: न त्वमद्य महीं दातुमीशः कौरवनन्दन ।आच्छेत्तुं वा बलाद्राजन्स कथं दातुमिच्छसि ।मां तु निर्जित्य संग्रामे पालयेमां वसुंधराम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: na tvamadya mahīṁ dātumīśaḥ kauravanandana ,ācchettuṁ vā balādrājansa kathaṁ dātumicchasi ,māṁ tu nirjitya saṁgrāme pālayemāṁ vasuṁdharām.
english: O scion of Kuru, you are not capable of giving the earth today. Or, O King, how do you, having forcibly seized it, now wish to give it? However, only after conquering me in battle, may you rule this earth (vasuṃdharā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 58. O scion of Kuru, you are not capable of giving the earth today. Or, O King, how do you, having forcibly seized it, now wish to give it? However, only after conquering me in battle, may you rule this earth (vasuṃdharā).

sanskrit: सूच्यग्रेणापि यद्भूमेरपि ध्रीयेत भारत ।तन्मात्रमपि नो मह्यं न ददाति पुरा भवान् ॥५९॥
transliteration: sūcyagreṇāpi yadbhūmerapi dhrīyeta bhārata ,tanmātramapi no mahyaṁ na dadāti purā bhavān.
english: O descendant of Bharata, previously you would not grant even to us, nor even to me personally, that minute amount of land which could be held on the tip of a needle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 59. O descendant of Bharata, previously you would not grant even to us, nor even to me personally, that minute amount of land which could be held on the tip of a needle.

sanskrit: स कथं पृथिवीमेतां प्रददासि विशां पते ।सूच्यग्रं नात्यजः पूर्वं स कथं त्यजसि क्षितिम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: sa kathaṁ pṛthivīmetāṁ pradadāsi viśāṁ pate ,sūcyagraṁ nātyajaḥ pūrvaṁ sa kathaṁ tyajasi kṣitim.
english: O lord of the people, how can you give away this entire earth (pṛthivī)? You who previously did not relinquish even a needle's point (sūcyagram), how can you now abandon the whole earth (kṣitim)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 60. O lord of the people, how can you give away this entire earth (pṛthivī)? You who previously did not relinquish even a needle's point (sūcyagram), how can you now abandon the whole earth (kṣitim)?

sanskrit: एवमैश्वर्यमासाद्य प्रशास्य पृथिवीमिमाम् ।को हि मूढो व्यवस्येत शत्रोर्दातुं वसुंधराम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: evamaiśvaryamāsādya praśāsya pṛthivīmimām ,ko hi mūḍho vyavasyeta śatrordātuṁ vasuṁdharām.
english: Indeed, having thus attained such sovereignty (aiśvarya) and governed this entire earth (pṛthivī), what fool would resolve to give the land (vasuṃdharā) to an enemy?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 61. Indeed, having thus attained such sovereignty (aiśvarya) and governed this entire earth (pṛthivī), what fool would resolve to give the land (vasuṃdharā) to an enemy?

sanskrit: त्वं तु केवलमौर्ख्येण विमूढो नावबुध्यसे ।पृथिवीं दातुकामोऽपि जीवितेनाद्य मोक्ष्यसे ॥६२॥
transliteration: tvaṁ tu kevalamaurkhyeṇa vimūḍho nāvabudhyase ,pṛthivīṁ dātukāmo'pi jīvitenādya mokṣyase.
english: But you, utterly deluded by sheer foolishness, do not comprehend. Even if you desire to give away the earth (pṛthivī), today you will be released from life (i.e., you will die).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 62. But you, utterly deluded by sheer foolishness, do not comprehend. Even if you desire to give away the earth (pṛthivī), today you will be released from life (i.e., you will die).

sanskrit: अस्मान्वा त्वं पराजित्य प्रशाधि पृथिवीमिमाम् ।अथ वा निहतोऽस्माभिर्व्रज लोकाननुत्तमान् ॥६३॥
transliteration: asmānvā tvaṁ parājitya praśādhi pṛthivīmimām ,atha vā nihato'smābhirvraja lokānanuttamān.
english: Either you defeat us and rule this earth, or, being slain by us, you go to the most excellent worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 63. Either you defeat us and rule this earth, or, being slain by us, you go to the most excellent worlds.

sanskrit: आवयोर्जीवतो राजन्मयि च त्वयि च ध्रुवम् ।संशयः सर्वभूतानां विजये नो भविष्यति ॥६४॥
transliteration: āvayorjīvato rājanmayi ca tvayi ca dhruvam ,saṁśayaḥ sarvabhūtānāṁ vijaye no bhaviṣyati.
english: O King, while we two are living, a doubt will certainly arise among all beings regarding our victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 64. O King, while we two are living, a doubt will certainly arise among all beings regarding our victory.

sanskrit: जीवितं तव दुष्प्रज्ञ मयि संप्रति वर्तते ।जीवयेयं त्वहं कामं न तु त्वं जीवितुं क्षमः ॥६५॥
transliteration: jīvitaṁ tava duṣprajña mayi saṁprati vartate ,jīvayeyaṁ tvahaṁ kāmaṁ na tu tvaṁ jīvituṁ kṣamaḥ.
english: O foolish one, your life currently rests with me. I can easily let you live, but you are not capable of living (on your own).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 65. O foolish one, your life currently rests with me. I can easily let you live, but you are not capable of living (on your own).

sanskrit: दहने हि कृतो यत्नस्त्वयास्मासु विशेषतः ।आशीविषैर्विषैश्चापि जले चापि प्रवेशनैः ।त्वया विनिकृता राजन्राज्यस्य हरणेन च ॥६६॥
transliteration: dahane hi kṛto yatnastvayāsmāsu viśeṣataḥ ,āśīviṣairviṣaiścāpi jale cāpi praveśanaiḥ ,tvayā vinikṛtā rājanrājyasya haraṇena ca.
english: Indeed, O King, great effort was made by you, specifically against us, through burning, through poisonous snakes and (other) poisons, and by plunging (us) into water. And you have harmed me by seizing the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 66. Indeed, O King, great effort was made by you, specifically against us, through burning, through poisonous snakes and (other) poisons, and by plunging (us) into water. And you have harmed me by seizing the kingdom.

sanskrit: एतस्मात्कारणात्पाप जीवितं ते न विद्यते ।उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ युध्यस्व तत्ते श्रेयो भविष्यति ॥६७॥
transliteration: etasmātkāraṇātpāpa jīvitaṁ te na vidyate ,uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha yudhyasva tatte śreyo bhaviṣyati.
english: O sinful one, for this reason, your living is not justified. Arise, arise! Fight! That will be truly beneficial for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 67. O sinful one, for this reason, your living is not justified. Arise, arise! Fight! That will be truly beneficial for you.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवं तु विविधा वाचो जययुक्ताः पुनः पुनः ।कीर्तयन्ति स्म ते वीरास्तत्र तत्र जनाधिप ॥६८॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evaṁ tu vividhā vāco jayayuktāḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,kīrtayanti sma te vīrāstatra tatra janādhipa.
english: Sanjaya said: O lord of men, those heroes were indeed repeatedly proclaiming various words imbued with victory in different places.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 30 Verse 68. Sanjaya said: O lord of men, those heroes were indeed repeatedly proclaiming various words imbued with victory in different places.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।एवं संतर्ज्यमानस्तु मम पुत्रो महीपतिः ।प्रकृत्या मन्युमान्वीरः कथमासीत्परंतपः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,evaṁ saṁtarjyamānastu mama putro mahīpatiḥ ,prakṛtyā manyumānvīraḥ kathamāsītparaṁtapaḥ.
english: Dhritarashtra said: My son, the king, who by his intrinsic nature (prakṛti) is a wrathful hero and a tormentor of enemies, how did he react when thus being severely threatened?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: My son, the king, who by his intrinsic nature (prakṛti) is a wrathful hero and a tormentor of enemies, how did he react when thus being severely threatened?

sanskrit: न हि संतर्जना तेन श्रुतपूर्वा कदाचन ।राजभावेन मान्यश्च सर्वलोकस्य सोऽभवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: na hi saṁtarjanā tena śrutapūrvā kadācana ,rājabhāvena mānyaśca sarvalokasya so'bhavat.
english: Indeed, such a severe rebuke had never been heard by him before. And because of his royal status, he was respected by all people.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 2. Indeed, such a severe rebuke had never been heard by him before. And because of his royal status, he was respected by all people.

sanskrit: इयं च पृथिवी सर्वा सम्लेच्छाटविका भृशम् ।प्रसादाद्ध्रियते यस्य प्रत्यक्षं तव संजय ॥३॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca pṛthivī sarvā samlecchāṭavikā bhṛśam ,prasādāddhriyate yasya pratyakṣaṁ tava saṁjaya.
english: Sanjaya, this entire earth, extensively populated by Mlecchas and forest dwellers, is sustained by whose grace? This is directly evident to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 3. Sanjaya, this entire earth, extensively populated by Mlecchas and forest dwellers, is sustained by whose grace? This is directly evident to you.

sanskrit: स तथा तर्ज्यमानस्तु पाण्डुपुत्रैर्विशेषतः ।विहीनश्च स्वकैर्भृत्यैर्निर्जने चावृतो भृशम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa tathā tarjyamānastu pāṇḍuputrairviśeṣataḥ ,vihīnaśca svakairbhṛtyairnirjane cāvṛto bhṛśam.
english: He, being thus threatened, especially by the sons of Pandu, and also being bereft of his own servants, and greatly surrounded in a desolate place...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 4. He, being thus threatened, especially by the sons of Pandu, and also being bereft of his own servants, and greatly surrounded in a desolate place...

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा स कटुका वाचो जययुक्ताः पुनः पुनः ।किमब्रवीत्पाण्डवेयांस्तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥५॥
transliteration: śrutvā sa kaṭukā vāco jayayuktāḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,kimabravītpāṇḍaveyāṁstanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: Having heard those bitter, triumphant words again and again, what did he say to the Pandavas? Sanjaya, please tell me that.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 5. Having heard those bitter, triumphant words again and again, what did he say to the Pandavas? Sanjaya, please tell me that.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तर्ज्यमानस्तदा राजन्नुदकस्थस्तवात्मजः ।युधिष्ठिरेण राजेन्द्र भ्रातृभिः सहितेन ह ॥६॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tarjyamānastadā rājannudakasthastavātmajaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhireṇa rājendra bhrātṛbhiḥ sahitena ha.
english: Sanjaya said: O King, your son, who was then situated in the water, being threatened by Yudhishthira, who was accompanied by his brothers, O King of kings, indeed...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 6. Sanjaya said: O King, your son, who was then situated in the water, being threatened by Yudhishthira, who was accompanied by his brothers, O King of kings, indeed...

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा स कटुका वाचो विषमस्थो जनाधिपः ।दीर्घमुष्णं च निःश्वस्य सलिलस्थः पुनः पुनः ॥७॥
transliteration: śrutvā sa kaṭukā vāco viṣamastho janādhipaḥ ,dīrghamuṣṇaṁ ca niḥśvasya salilasthaḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Having heard those bitter words, the king (janādhipaḥ), who was in a difficult situation and standing in the water, sighed long and hot breaths again and again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 7. Having heard those bitter words, the king (janādhipaḥ), who was in a difficult situation and standing in the water, sighed long and hot breaths again and again.

sanskrit: सलिलान्तर्गतो राजा धुन्वन्हस्तौ पुनः पुनः ।मनश्चकार युद्धाय राजानं चाभ्यभाषत ॥८॥
transliteration: salilāntargato rājā dhunvanhastau punaḥ punaḥ ,manaścakāra yuddhāya rājānaṁ cābhyabhāṣata.
english: The king, submerged in the water and repeatedly shaking his hands, resolved to fight and then addressed the other king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 8. The king, submerged in the water and repeatedly shaking his hands, resolved to fight and then addressed the other king.

sanskrit: यूयं ससुहृदः पार्थाः सर्वे सरथवाहनाः ।अहमेकः परिद्यूनो विरथो हतवाहनः ॥९॥
transliteration: yūyaṁ sasuhṛdaḥ pārthāḥ sarve sarathavāhanāḥ ,ahamekaḥ paridyūno viratho hatavāhanaḥ.
english: "O Pārthas, all of you are accompanied by your friends, chariots, and mounts. I, however, am alone, utterly exhausted, without a chariot, and my mounts have been killed."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 9. "O Pārthas, all of you are accompanied by your friends, chariots, and mounts. I, however, am alone, utterly exhausted, without a chariot, and my mounts have been killed."

sanskrit: आत्तशस्त्रै रथगतैर्बहुभिः परिवारितः ।कथमेकः पदातिः सन्नशस्त्रो योद्धुमुत्सहे ॥१०॥
transliteration: āttaśastrai rathagatairbahubhiḥ parivāritaḥ ,kathamekaḥ padātiḥ sannaśastro yoddhumutsahe.
english: "How can I, alone, being on foot and unarmed, possibly dare to fight when surrounded by many who are armed and mounted on chariots?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 10. "How can I, alone, being on foot and unarmed, possibly dare to fight when surrounded by many who are armed and mounted on chariots?"

sanskrit: एकैकेन तु मां यूयं योधयध्वं युधिष्ठिर ।न ह्येको बहुभिर्वीरैर्न्याय्यं योधयितुं युधि ॥११॥
transliteration: ekaikena tu māṁ yūyaṁ yodhayadhvaṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,na hyeko bahubhirvīrairnyāyyaṁ yodhayituṁ yudhi.
english: However, Yudhishthira, you all should fight me one by one. Indeed, it is not proper for a single warrior to be fought by many heroes in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 11. However, Yudhishthira, you all should fight me one by one. Indeed, it is not proper for a single warrior to be fought by many heroes in battle.

sanskrit: विशेषतो विकवचः श्रान्तश्चापः समाश्रितः ।भृशं विक्षतगात्रश्च श्रान्तवाहनसैनिकः ॥१२॥
transliteration: viśeṣato vikavacaḥ śrāntaścāpaḥ samāśritaḥ ,bhṛśaṁ vikṣatagātraśca śrāntavāhanasainikaḥ.
english: Especially, I am without armor, exhausted, and have resorted to water; with my limbs severely wounded, and my charioteer and soldiers weary.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 12. Especially, I am without armor, exhausted, and have resorted to water; with my limbs severely wounded, and my charioteer and soldiers weary.

sanskrit: न मे त्वत्तो भयं राजन्न च पार्थाद्वृकोदरात् ।फल्गुनाद्वासुदेवाद्वा पाञ्चालेभ्योऽथ वा पुनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: na me tvatto bhayaṁ rājanna ca pārthādvṛkodarāt ,phalgunādvāsudevādvā pāñcālebhyo'tha vā punaḥ.
english: O king, I have no fear from you, nor from Partha (Arjuna) or Vrikodara (Bhima), nor from Phalguna (Arjuna) or Vasudeva (Krishna), or indeed from the Pañcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 13. O king, I have no fear from you, nor from Partha (Arjuna) or Vrikodara (Bhima), nor from Phalguna (Arjuna) or Vasudeva (Krishna), or indeed from the Pañcālas.

sanskrit: यमाभ्यां युयुधानाद्वा ये चान्ये तव सैनिकाः ।एकः सर्वानहं क्रुद्धो न तान्योद्धुमिहोत्सहे ॥१४॥
transliteration: yamābhyāṁ yuyudhānādvā ye cānye tava sainikāḥ ,ekaḥ sarvānahaṁ kruddho na tānyoddhumihotsahe.
english: Nor (do I fear) from the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) or from Yuyudhana (Satyaki), or indeed any other soldiers of yours. I, alone and enraged, am not able to fight all of them here (simultaneously).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 14. Nor (do I fear) from the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) or from Yuyudhana (Satyaki), or indeed any other soldiers of yours. I, alone and enraged, am not able to fight all of them here (simultaneously).

sanskrit: धर्ममूला सतां कीर्तिर्मनुष्याणां जनाधिप ।धर्मं चैवेह कीर्तिं च पालयन्प्रब्रवीम्यहम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: dharmamūlā satāṁ kīrtirmanuṣyāṇāṁ janādhipa ,dharmaṁ caiveha kīrtiṁ ca pālayanprabravīmyaham.
english: O ruler of people, the reputation of virtuous individuals among humankind is founded upon righteousness (dharma). Protecting both righteousness (dharma) and reputation in this world, I declare this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 15. O ruler of people, the reputation of virtuous individuals among humankind is founded upon righteousness (dharma). Protecting both righteousness (dharma) and reputation in this world, I declare this.

sanskrit: अहमुत्थाय वः सर्वान्प्रतियोत्स्यामि संयुगे ।अन्वंशाभ्यागतान्सर्वानृतून्संवत्सरो यथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: ahamutthāya vaḥ sarvānpratiyotsyāmi saṁyuge ,anvaṁśābhyāgatānsarvānṛtūnsaṁvatsaro yathā.
english: Having risen, I will fight against all of you in battle, against all who have come to join your lineage, just as the year confronts all the succeeding seasons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 16. Having risen, I will fight against all of you in battle, against all who have come to join your lineage, just as the year confronts all the succeeding seasons.

sanskrit: अद्य वः सरथान्साश्वानशस्त्रो विरथोऽपि सन् ।नक्षत्राणीव सर्वाणि सविता रात्रिसंक्षये ।तेजसा नाशयिष्यामि स्थिरीभवत पाण्डवाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: adya vaḥ sarathānsāśvānaśastro viratho'pi san ,nakṣatrāṇīva sarvāṇi savitā rātrisaṁkṣaye ,tejasā nāśayiṣyāmi sthirībhavata pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Today, even though I am weaponless and without a chariot, I will destroy all of you who are equipped with chariots and horses, just as the sun at the end of night disperses all the stars with its brilliance. O Pāṇḍavas, stand firm!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 17. Today, even though I am weaponless and without a chariot, I will destroy all of you who are equipped with chariots and horses, just as the sun at the end of night disperses all the stars with its brilliance. O Pāṇḍavas, stand firm!

sanskrit: अद्यानृण्यं गमिष्यामि क्षत्रियाणां यशस्विनाम् ।बाह्लीकद्रोणभीष्माणां कर्णस्य च महात्मनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: adyānṛṇyaṁ gamiṣyāmi kṣatriyāṇāṁ yaśasvinām ,bāhlīkadroṇabhīṣmāṇāṁ karṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ.
english: Today I shall achieve freedom from my debt to the glorious Kṣatriyas: Bāhlīka, Droṇa, Bhīṣma, and the great-souled Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 18. Today I shall achieve freedom from my debt to the glorious Kṣatriyas: Bāhlīka, Droṇa, Bhīṣma, and the great-souled Karṇa.

sanskrit: जयद्रथस्य शूरस्य भगदत्तस्य चोभयोः ।मद्रराजस्य शल्यस्य भूरिश्रवस एव च ॥१९॥
transliteration: jayadrathasya śūrasya bhagadattasya cobhayoḥ ,madrarājasya śalyasya bhūriśravasa eva ca.
english: Of Jayadratha, the hero, and of both Bhagadatta; and of the king of Madras, Śalya, and Bhūriśravas, too.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 19. Of Jayadratha, the hero, and of both Bhagadatta; and of the king of Madras, Śalya, and Bhūriśravas, too.

sanskrit: पुत्राणां भरतश्रेष्ठ शकुनेः सौबलस्य च ।मित्राणां सुहृदां चैव बान्धवानां तथैव च ॥२०॥
transliteration: putrāṇāṁ bharataśreṣṭha śakuneḥ saubalasya ca ,mitrāṇāṁ suhṛdāṁ caiva bāndhavānāṁ tathaiva ca.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, (of) the sons of Śakuni and Saubala; and likewise, of friends, well-wishers, and relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 20. O best among the Bhāratas, (of) the sons of Śakuni and Saubala; and likewise, of friends, well-wishers, and relatives.

sanskrit: आनृण्यमद्य गच्छामि हत्वा त्वां भ्रातृभिः सह ।एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं विरराम जनाधिपः ॥२१॥
transliteration: ānṛṇyamadya gacchāmi hatvā tvāṁ bhrātṛbhiḥ saha ,etāvaduktvā vacanaṁ virarāma janādhipaḥ.
english: Today I shall discharge my debt by killing you along with your brothers. Having spoken these words, the king (janādhipa) fell silent.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 21. Today I shall discharge my debt by killing you along with your brothers. Having spoken these words, the king (janādhipa) fell silent.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।दिष्ट्या त्वमपि जानीषे क्षत्रधर्मं सुयोधन ।दिष्ट्या ते वर्तते बुद्धिर्युद्धायैव महाभुज ॥२२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,diṣṭyā tvamapi jānīṣe kṣatradharmaṁ suyodhana ,diṣṭyā te vartate buddhiryuddhāyaiva mahābhuja.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "Fortunately, O Suyodhana, you also know the warrior's code (kṣatradharma). Fortunately, O mighty-armed one, your intellect is directed only towards war."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 22. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "Fortunately, O Suyodhana, you also know the warrior's code (kṣatradharma). Fortunately, O mighty-armed one, your intellect is directed only towards war."

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या शूरोऽसि कौरव्य दिष्ट्या जानासि संगरम् ।यस्त्वमेको हि नः सर्वान्संयुगे योद्धुमिच्छसि ॥२३॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā śūro'si kauravya diṣṭyā jānāsi saṁgaram ,yastvameko hi naḥ sarvānsaṁyuge yoddhumicchasi.
english: Fortunately, O Kaurava, you are brave, and fortunately, you understand warfare, since you alone indeed wish to fight all of us in this battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 23. Fortunately, O Kaurava, you are brave, and fortunately, you understand warfare, since you alone indeed wish to fight all of us in this battle.

sanskrit: एक एकेन संगम्य यत्ते संमतमायुधम् ।तत्त्वमादाय युध्यस्व प्रेक्षकास्ते वयं स्थिताः ॥२४॥
transliteration: eka ekena saṁgamya yatte saṁmatamāyudham ,tattvamādāya yudhyasva prekṣakāste vayaṁ sthitāḥ.
english: Having approached one another, let one (of us) fight with one (of you). Take up whatever weapon you approve of, and fight. We stand here as your spectators.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 24. Having approached one another, let one (of us) fight with one (of you). Take up whatever weapon you approve of, and fight. We stand here as your spectators.

sanskrit: अयमिष्टं च ते कामं वीर भूयो ददाम्यहम् ।हत्वैकं भवतो राज्यं हतो वा स्वर्गमाप्नुहि ॥२५॥
transliteration: ayamiṣṭaṁ ca te kāmaṁ vīra bhūyo dadāmyaham ,hatvaikaṁ bhavato rājyaṁ hato vā svargamāpnuhi.
english: O hero, I grant you this desired wish further: having killed one of us, you will obtain your kingdom, or, if you are killed, you will attain heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 25. O hero, I grant you this desired wish further: having killed one of us, you will obtain your kingdom, or, if you are killed, you will attain heaven.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।एकश्चेद्योद्धुमाक्रन्दे वरोऽद्य मम दीयते ।आयुधानामियं चापि वृता त्वत्संमते गदा ॥२६॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,ekaścedyoddhumākrande varo'dya mama dīyate ,āyudhānāmiyaṁ cāpi vṛtā tvatsaṁmate gadā.
english: Duryodhana said: 'If a boon is granted to me today to fight alone in battle, then among weapons, this mace has been chosen by me, with your approval.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 26. Duryodhana said: 'If a boon is granted to me today to fight alone in battle, then among weapons, this mace has been chosen by me, with your approval.'

sanskrit: भ्रातॄणां भवतामेकः शक्यं मां योऽभिमन्यते ।पदातिर्गदया संख्ये स युध्यतु मया सह ॥२७॥
transliteration: bhrātṝṇāṁ bhavatāmekaḥ śakyaṁ māṁ yo'bhimanyate ,padātirgadayā saṁkhye sa yudhyatu mayā saha.
english: Among your brothers, whoever considers himself capable against me, let that foot-soldier fight me with a mace in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 27. Among your brothers, whoever considers himself capable against me, let that foot-soldier fight me with a mace in battle.

sanskrit: वृत्तानि रथयुद्धानि विचित्राणि पदे पदे ।इदमेकं गदायुद्धं भवत्वद्याद्भुतं महत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: vṛttāni rathayuddhāni vicitrāṇi pade pade ,idamekaṁ gadāyuddhaṁ bhavatvadyādbhutaṁ mahat.
english: Many varied chariot battles have taken place repeatedly. Let this single mace battle today be wondrous and great.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 28. Many varied chariot battles have taken place repeatedly. Let this single mace battle today be wondrous and great.

sanskrit: अन्नानामपि पर्यायं कर्तुमिच्छन्ति मानवाः ।युद्धानामपि पर्यायो भवत्वनुमते तव ॥२९॥
transliteration: annānāmapi paryāyaṁ kartumicchanti mānavāḥ ,yuddhānāmapi paryāyo bhavatvanumate tava.
english: Humans wish to have variety even in foods. Therefore, let there be an alternative in battles as well, with your permission.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 29. Humans wish to have variety even in foods. Therefore, let there be an alternative in battles as well, with your permission.

sanskrit: गदया त्वां महाबाहो विजेष्यामि सहानुजम् ।पाञ्चालान्सृञ्जयांश्चैव ये चान्ये तव सैनिकाः ॥३०॥
transliteration: gadayā tvāṁ mahābāho vijeṣyāmi sahānujam ,pāñcālānsṛñjayāṁścaiva ye cānye tava sainikāḥ.
english: O mighty-armed one, I will conquer you with a mace, along with your younger brother(s), and also the Pañcālas, the Sṛñjayas, and any other soldiers of yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 30. O mighty-armed one, I will conquer you with a mace, along with your younger brother(s), and also the Pañcālas, the Sṛñjayas, and any other soldiers of yours.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ गान्धारे मां योधय सुयोधन ।एक एकेन संगम्य संयुगे गदया बली ॥३१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha gāndhāre māṁ yodhaya suyodhana ,eka ekena saṁgamya saṁyuge gadayā balī.
english: Yudhishthira said: "Get up, get up, O son of Gandhari, O Suyodhana! Encounter me one-on-one in battle with a mace, O strong one!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 31. Yudhishthira said: "Get up, get up, O son of Gandhari, O Suyodhana! Encounter me one-on-one in battle with a mace, O strong one!"

sanskrit: पुरुषो भव गान्धारे युध्यस्व सुसमाहितः ।अद्य ते जीवितं नास्ति यद्यपि त्वं मनोजवः ॥३२॥
transliteration: puruṣo bhava gāndhāre yudhyasva susamāhitaḥ ,adya te jīvitaṁ nāsti yadyapi tvaṁ manojavaḥ.
english: O son of Gandhari, show your manhood and fight with great concentration! Today your life will end, even though you are swift as thought.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 32. O son of Gandhari, show your manhood and fight with great concentration! Today your life will end, even though you are swift as thought.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एतत्स नरशार्दूलो नामृष्यत तवात्मजः ।सलिलान्तर्गतः श्वभ्रे महानाग इव श्वसन् ॥३३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,etatsa naraśārdūlo nāmṛṣyata tavātmajaḥ ,salilāntargataḥ śvabhre mahānāga iva śvasan.
english: Sanjaya said: "Your son, that tiger among men, could not tolerate this, breathing heavily like a great serpent (mahānāga) hidden within a watery pit."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 33. Sanjaya said: "Your son, that tiger among men, could not tolerate this, breathing heavily like a great serpent (mahānāga) hidden within a watery pit."

sanskrit: तथासौ वाक्प्रतोदेन तुद्यमानः पुनः पुनः ।वाचं न ममृषे धीमानुत्तमाश्वः कशामिव ॥३४॥
transliteration: tathāsau vākpratodena tudyamānaḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,vācaṁ na mamṛṣe dhīmānuttamāśvaḥ kaśāmiva.
english: Thus, tormented again and again by the goad of words, that intelligent one could not bear the speech, just as an excellent horse does not tolerate the whip.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 34. Thus, tormented again and again by the goad of words, that intelligent one could not bear the speech, just as an excellent horse does not tolerate the whip.

sanskrit: संक्षोभ्य सलिलं वेगाद्गदामादाय वीर्यवान् ।अद्रिसारमयीं गुर्वीं काञ्चनाङ्गदभूषणाम् ।अन्तर्जलात्समुत्तस्थौ नागेन्द्र इव निःश्वसन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: saṁkṣobhya salilaṁ vegādgadāmādāya vīryavān ,adrisāramayīṁ gurvīṁ kāñcanāṅgadabhūṣaṇām ,antarjalātsamuttasthau nāgendra iva niḥśvasan.
english: The mighty one, having forcefully churned the water, took his heavy mace, which was made of mountain essence and adorned with golden armlets. He then rose from beneath the water, hissing like a great serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 35. The mighty one, having forcefully churned the water, took his heavy mace, which was made of mountain essence and adorned with golden armlets. He then rose from beneath the water, hissing like a great serpent.

sanskrit: स भित्त्वा स्तम्भितं तोयं स्कन्धे कृत्वायसीं गदाम् ।उदतिष्ठत पुत्रस्ते प्रतपन्रश्मिमानिव ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa bhittvā stambhitaṁ toyaṁ skandhe kṛtvāyasīṁ gadām ,udatiṣṭhata putraste pratapanraśmimāniva.
english: Your son (Duryodhana), having split the still water, rose up placing the iron mace on his shoulder, like the blazing, radiant sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 36. Your son (Duryodhana), having split the still water, rose up placing the iron mace on his shoulder, like the blazing, radiant sun.

sanskrit: ततः शैक्यायसीं गुर्वीं जातरूपपरिष्कृताम् ।गदां परामृशद्धीमान्धार्तराष्ट्रो महाबलः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaikyāyasīṁ gurvīṁ jātarūpapariṣkṛtām ,gadāṁ parāmṛśaddhīmāndhārtarāṣṭro mahābalaḥ.
english: Then, the intelligent and mighty son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) seized his heavy mace, which was made of mountain iron and adorned with gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 37. Then, the intelligent and mighty son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) seized his heavy mace, which was made of mountain iron and adorned with gold.

sanskrit: गदाहस्तं तु तं दृष्ट्वा सशृङ्गमिव पर्वतम् ।प्रजानामिव संक्रुद्धं शूलपाणिमवस्थितम् ।सगदो भारतो भाति प्रतपन्भास्करो यथा ॥३८॥
transliteration: gadāhastaṁ tu taṁ dṛṣṭvā saśṛṅgamiva parvatam ,prajānāmiva saṁkruddhaṁ śūlapāṇimavasthitam ,sagado bhārato bhāti pratapanbhāskaro yathā.
english: But seeing him with mace in hand, like a mountain with peaks, or like a furious Śūlapāṇi (Śiva) standing before his subjects, that Bhārata (Duryodhana) shone with his mace like the blazing sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 38. But seeing him with mace in hand, like a mountain with peaks, or like a furious Śūlapāṇi (Śiva) standing before his subjects, that Bhārata (Duryodhana) shone with his mace like the blazing sun.

sanskrit: तमुत्तीर्णं महाबाहुं गदाहस्तमरिंदमम् ।मेनिरे सर्वभूतानि दण्डहस्तमिवान्तकम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tamuttīrṇaṁ mahābāhuṁ gadāhastamariṁdamam ,menire sarvabhūtāni daṇḍahastamivāntakam.
english: All beings considered that mighty-armed subduer of enemies, who had emerged with his mace in hand, to be like Death (Antaka) holding his staff.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 39. All beings considered that mighty-armed subduer of enemies, who had emerged with his mace in hand, to be like Death (Antaka) holding his staff.

sanskrit: वज्रहस्तं यथा शक्रं शूलहस्तं यथा हरम् ।ददृशुः सर्वपाञ्चालाः पुत्रं तव जनाधिप ॥४०॥
transliteration: vajrahastaṁ yathā śakraṁ śūlahastaṁ yathā haram ,dadṛśuḥ sarvapāñcālāḥ putraṁ tava janādhipa.
english: O ruler of the people (janādhipa), all the Pañcālas beheld your son, just like Indra holding his thunderbolt, and like Hara holding his trident.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 40. O ruler of the people (janādhipa), all the Pañcālas beheld your son, just like Indra holding his thunderbolt, and like Hara holding his trident.

sanskrit: तमुत्तीर्णं तु संप्रेक्ष्य समहृष्यन्त सर्वशः ।पाञ्चालाः पाण्डवेयाश्च तेऽन्योन्यस्य तलान्ददुः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tamuttīrṇaṁ tu saṁprekṣya samahṛṣyanta sarvaśaḥ ,pāñcālāḥ pāṇḍaveyāśca te'nyonyasya talāndaduḥ.
english: Indeed, having observed him emerged, all the Pañcālas and the Pāṇḍavas' followers rejoiced greatly. They (in turn) gave each other pats on the back (lit. 'palms').
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 41. Indeed, having observed him emerged, all the Pañcālas and the Pāṇḍavas' followers rejoiced greatly. They (in turn) gave each other pats on the back (lit. 'palms').

sanskrit: अवहासं तु तं मत्वा पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तव ।उद्वृत्य नयने क्रुद्धो दिधक्षुरिव पाण्डवान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: avahāsaṁ tu taṁ matvā putro duryodhanastava ,udvṛtya nayane kruddho didhakṣuriva pāṇḍavān.
english: But your son Duryodhana, considering that (joy) to be an insult, became enraged. He rolled his eyes as if wishing to burn the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 42. But your son Duryodhana, considering that (joy) to be an insult, became enraged. He rolled his eyes as if wishing to burn the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: त्रिशिखां भ्रुकुटीं कृत्वा संदष्टदशनच्छदः ।प्रत्युवाच ततस्तान्वै पाण्डवान्सहकेशवान् ॥४३॥
transliteration: triśikhāṁ bhrukuṭīṁ kṛtvā saṁdaṣṭadaśanacchadaḥ ,pratyuvāca tatastānvai pāṇḍavānsahakeśavān.
english: Frowning intensely and biting his lip, he then replied to those Pāṇḍavas and Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 43. Frowning intensely and biting his lip, he then replied to those Pāṇḍavas and Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: अवहासस्य वोऽस्याद्य प्रतिवक्तास्मि पाण्डवाः ।गमिष्यथ हताः सद्यः सपाञ्चाला यमक्षयम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: avahāsasya vo'syādya prativaktāsmi pāṇḍavāḥ ,gamiṣyatha hatāḥ sadyaḥ sapāñcālā yamakṣayam.
english: "O Pāṇḍavas, today I will be the one to retaliate for this ridicule of yours. Slain, you along with the Pañcālas will immediately go to Yama's abode."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 44. "O Pāṇḍavas, today I will be the one to retaliate for this ridicule of yours. Slain, you along with the Pañcālas will immediately go to Yama's abode."

sanskrit: उत्थितस्तु जलात्तस्मात्पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तव ।अतिष्ठत गदापाणी रुधिरेण समुक्षितः ॥४५॥
transliteration: utthitastu jalāttasmātputro duryodhanastava ,atiṣṭhata gadāpāṇī rudhireṇa samukṣitaḥ.
english: But your son Duryodhana, having emerged from that water, stood there, mace in hand, drenched in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 45. But your son Duryodhana, having emerged from that water, stood there, mace in hand, drenched in blood.

sanskrit: तस्य शोणितदिग्धस्य सलिलेन समुक्षितम् ।शरीरं स्म तदा भाति स्रवन्निव महीधरः ॥४६॥
transliteration: tasya śoṇitadigdhasya salilena samukṣitam ,śarīraṁ sma tadā bhāti sravanniva mahīdharaḥ.
english: His body, smeared with blood and drenched with water, then indeed shone like a mountain from which streams flow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 46. His body, smeared with blood and drenched with water, then indeed shone like a mountain from which streams flow.

sanskrit: तमुद्यतगदं वीरं मेनिरे तत्र पाण्डवाः ।वैवस्वतमिव क्रुद्धं किंकरोद्यतपाणिनम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tamudyatagadaṁ vīraṁ menire tatra pāṇḍavāḥ ,vaivasvatamiva kruddhaṁ kiṁkarodyatapāṇinam.
english: There, the Pāṇḍavas considered that hero, with an uplifted mace, to be like an enraged Yama (Vaivasvata), whose attendants had their hands raised.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 47. There, the Pāṇḍavas considered that hero, with an uplifted mace, to be like an enraged Yama (Vaivasvata), whose attendants had their hands raised.

sanskrit: स मेघनिनदो हर्षान्नदन्निव च गोवृषः ।आजुहाव ततः पार्थान्गदया युधि वीर्यवान् ॥४८॥
transliteration: sa meghaninado harṣānnadanniva ca govṛṣaḥ ,ājuhāva tataḥ pārthāngadayā yudhi vīryavān.
english: Then, that powerful one, with a thunderous roar and bellowing like a bull out of joy, challenged the Pārthas in battle with his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 48. Then, that powerful one, with a thunderous roar and bellowing like a bull out of joy, challenged the Pārthas in battle with his mace.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।एकैकेन च मां यूयमासीदत युधिष्ठिर ।न ह्येको बहुभिर्न्याय्यो वीर योधयितुं युधि ॥४९॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,ekaikena ca māṁ yūyamāsīdata yudhiṣṭhira ,na hyeko bahubhirnyāyyo vīra yodhayituṁ yudhi.
english: Duryodhana said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, you should approach me one by one. For indeed, O hero, it is not proper for one person to fight against many in battle.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 49. Duryodhana said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, you should approach me one by one. For indeed, O hero, it is not proper for one person to fight against many in battle.'

sanskrit: न्यस्तवर्मा विशेषेण श्रान्तश्चाप्सु परिप्लुतः ।भृशं विक्षतगात्रश्च हतवाहनसैनिकः ॥५०॥
transliteration: nyastavarmā viśeṣeṇa śrāntaścāpsu pariplutaḥ ,bhṛśaṁ vikṣatagātraśca hatavāhanasainikaḥ.
english: He had cast off his armor, was especially exhausted and submerged in the waters, his body was greatly wounded, and his chariots and soldiers were killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 50. He had cast off his armor, was especially exhausted and submerged in the waters, his body was greatly wounded, and his chariots and soldiers were killed.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।नाभूदियं तव प्रज्ञा कथमेवं सुयोधन ।यदाभिमन्युं बहवो जघ्नुर्युधि महारथाः ॥५१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,nābhūdiyaṁ tava prajñā kathamevaṁ suyodhana ,yadābhimanyuṁ bahavo jaghnuryudhi mahārathāḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O Suyodhana, how is it that you did not possess this intelligence when many great warriors (mahārathas) killed Abhimanyu in battle?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 51. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O Suyodhana, how is it that you did not possess this intelligence when many great warriors (mahārathas) killed Abhimanyu in battle?"

sanskrit: आमुञ्च कवचं वीर मूर्धजान्यमयस्व च ।यच्चान्यदपि ते नास्ति तदप्यादत्स्व भारत ।इममेकं च ते कामं वीर भूयो ददाम्यहम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: āmuñca kavacaṁ vīra mūrdhajānyamayasva ca ,yaccānyadapi te nāsti tadapyādatsva bhārata ,imamekaṁ ca te kāmaṁ vīra bhūyo dadāmyaham.
english: “O hero (vīra), put on your armor and bind your hair! And whatever else you do not have, O Bhārata, take that also! Furthermore, O hero (vīra), I grant you this one wish.”
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 52. “O hero (vīra), put on your armor and bind your hair! And whatever else you do not have, O Bhārata, take that also! Furthermore, O hero (vīra), I grant you this one wish.”

sanskrit: पञ्चानां पाण्डवेयानां येन योद्धुमिहेच्छसि ।तं हत्वा वै भवान्राजा हतो वा स्वर्गमाप्नुहि ।ऋते च जीविताद्वीर युद्धे किं कुर्म ते प्रियम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: pañcānāṁ pāṇḍaveyānāṁ yena yoddhumihecchasi ,taṁ hatvā vai bhavānrājā hato vā svargamāpnuhi ,ṛte ca jīvitādvīra yuddhe kiṁ kurma te priyam.
english: “With whichever of the five Pāṇḍavas you wish to fight here, having killed him, you will become king, or, being killed, you will attain heaven. And besides life (jīvita), O hero (vīra), what pleasant thing can we do for you in battle?”
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 53. “With whichever of the five Pāṇḍavas you wish to fight here, having killed him, you will become king, or, being killed, you will attain heaven. And besides life (jīvita), O hero (vīra), what pleasant thing can we do for you in battle?”

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततस्तव सुतो राजन्वर्म जग्राह काञ्चनम् ।विचित्रं च शिरस्त्राणं जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृतम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tatastava suto rājanvarma jagrāha kāñcanam ,vicitraṁ ca śirastrāṇaṁ jāmbūnadapariṣkṛtam.
english: Saṃjaya said: "Then, O King, your son took golden armor and a splendid helmet adorned with Jambūnada gold."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 54. Saṃjaya said: "Then, O King, your son took golden armor and a splendid helmet adorned with Jambūnada gold."

sanskrit: सोऽवबद्धशिरस्त्राणः शुभकाञ्चनवर्मभृत् ।रराज राजन्पुत्रस्ते काञ्चनः शैलराडिव ॥५५॥
transliteration: so'vabaddhaśirastrāṇaḥ śubhakāñcanavarmabhṛt ,rarāja rājanputraste kāñcanaḥ śailarāḍiva.
english: O King, your son, adorned with a helmet and bearing splendid golden armor, gleamed like a golden mountain king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 55. O King, your son, adorned with a helmet and bearing splendid golden armor, gleamed like a golden mountain king.

sanskrit: संनद्धः स गदी राजन्सज्जः संग्राममूर्धनि ।अब्रवीत्पाण्डवान्सर्वान्पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तव ॥५६॥
transliteration: saṁnaddhaḥ sa gadī rājansajjaḥ saṁgrāmamūrdhani ,abravītpāṇḍavānsarvānputro duryodhanastava.
english: O King, your son Duryodhana, equipped and ready with his mace at the forefront of the battle, spoke to all the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 56. O King, your son Duryodhana, equipped and ready with his mace at the forefront of the battle, spoke to all the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: भ्रातॄणां भवतामेको युध्यतां गदया मया ।सहदेवेन वा योत्स्ये भीमेन नकुलेन वा ॥५७॥
transliteration: bhrātṝṇāṁ bhavatāmeko yudhyatāṁ gadayā mayā ,sahadevena vā yotsye bhīmena nakulena vā.
english: Let one of you brothers fight me with a mace. I will contend with Bhīma, or Sahadeva, or Nakula.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 57. Let one of you brothers fight me with a mace. I will contend with Bhīma, or Sahadeva, or Nakula.

sanskrit: अथ वा फल्गुनेनाद्य त्वया वा भरतर्षभ ।योत्स्येऽहं संगरं प्राप्य विजेष्ये च रणाजिरे ॥५८॥
transliteration: atha vā phalgunenādya tvayā vā bharatarṣabha ,yotsye'haṁ saṁgaraṁ prāpya vijeṣye ca raṇājire.
english: Or else, O best among the Bhāratas, I will contend today with Arjuna (Phalguna) or with you (Yudhiṣṭhira); having joined the battle (saṃgara), I will certainly conquer in the arena of combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 58. Or else, O best among the Bhāratas, I will contend today with Arjuna (Phalguna) or with you (Yudhiṣṭhira); having joined the battle (saṃgara), I will certainly conquer in the arena of combat.

sanskrit: अहमद्य गमिष्यामि वैरस्यान्तं सुदुर्गमम् ।गदया पुरुषव्याघ्र हेमपट्टविनद्धया ॥५९॥
transliteration: ahamadya gamiṣyāmi vairasyāntaṁ sudurgamam ,gadayā puruṣavyāghra hemapaṭṭavinaddhayā.
english: O tiger among men, today I will bring this very difficult-to-overcome enmity to an end with my mace, which is adorned with golden plates.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 59. O tiger among men, today I will bring this very difficult-to-overcome enmity to an end with my mace, which is adorned with golden plates.

sanskrit: गदायुद्धे न मे कश्चित्सदृशोऽस्तीति चिन्तय ।गदया वो हनिष्यामि सर्वानेव समागतान् ।गृह्णातु स गदां यो वै युध्यतेऽद्य मया सह ॥६०॥
transliteration: gadāyuddhe na me kaścitsadṛśo'stīti cintaya ,gadayā vo haniṣyāmi sarvāneva samāgatān ,gṛhṇātu sa gadāṁ yo vai yudhyate'dya mayā saha.
english: Consider this: no one is my equal in mace-fighting. With my mace, I will surely strike down all of you who have assembled here. Let that person grasp a mace who will indeed fight with me today.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 31 Verse 60. Consider this: no one is my equal in mace-fighting. With my mace, I will surely strike down all of you who have assembled here. Let that person grasp a mace who will indeed fight with me today.
