sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।एवं दुर्योधने राजन्गर्जमाने मुहुर्मुहुः ।युधिष्ठिरस्य संक्रुद्धो वासुदेवोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,evaṁ duryodhane rājangarjamāne muhurmuhuḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasya saṁkruddho vāsudevo'bravīdidam.
english: Sañjaya said: O King, while Duryodhana was roaring repeatedly in this manner, Vāsudeva (Krishna), enraged, spoke the following to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: O King, while Duryodhana was roaring repeatedly in this manner, Vāsudeva (Krishna), enraged, spoke the following to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: यदि नाम ह्ययं युद्धे वरयेत्त्वां युधिष्ठिर ।अर्जुनं नकुलं वापि सहदेवमथापि वा ॥२॥
transliteration: yadi nāma hyayaṁ yuddhe varayettvāṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,arjunaṁ nakulaṁ vāpi sahadevamathāpi vā.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, if indeed he (Duryodhana) should choose you in battle, or even Arjuna, or Nakula, or Sahadeva...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 2. O Yudhiṣṭhira, if indeed he (Duryodhana) should choose you in battle, or even Arjuna, or Nakula, or Sahadeva...

sanskrit: किमिदं साहसं राजंस्त्वया व्याहृतमीदृशम् ।एकमेव निहत्याजौ भव राजा कुरुष्विति ॥३॥
transliteration: kimidaṁ sāhasaṁ rājaṁstvayā vyāhṛtamīdṛśam ,ekameva nihatyājau bhava rājā kuruṣviti.
english: O King, what is this audacity, this kind of statement made by you? 'Kill just one (of them) in battle, and become king!'  -  so you proclaim.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 3. O King, what is this audacity, this kind of statement made by you? 'Kill just one (of them) in battle, and become king!'  -  so you proclaim.

sanskrit: एतेन हि कृता योग्या वर्षाणीह त्रयोदश ।आयसे पुरुषे राजन्भीमसेनजिघांसया ॥४॥
transliteration: etena hi kṛtā yogyā varṣāṇīha trayodaśa ,āyase puruṣe rājanbhīmasenajighāṁsayā.
english: Indeed, by him (Duryodhana) thirteen years of fitting practice have been accomplished here, O King, (using) an iron effigy, with the desire to kill Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 4. Indeed, by him (Duryodhana) thirteen years of fitting practice have been accomplished here, O King, (using) an iron effigy, with the desire to kill Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: कथं नाम भवेत्कार्यमस्माभिर्भरतर्षभ ।साहसं कृतवांस्त्वं तु ह्यनुक्रोशान्नृपोत्तम ॥५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nāma bhavetkāryamasmābhirbharatarṣabha ,sāhasaṁ kṛtavāṁstvaṁ tu hyanukrośānnṛpottama.
english: O best of Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), how indeed could the task (be accomplished) by us? For you, O best of kings (nṛpottama), have performed this audacious act out of compassion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 5. O best of Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), how indeed could the task (be accomplished) by us? For you, O best of kings (nṛpottama), have performed this audacious act out of compassion.

sanskrit: नान्यमस्यानुपश्यामि प्रतियोद्धारमाहवे ।ऋते वृकोदरात्पार्थात्स च नातिकृतश्रमः ॥६॥
transliteration: nānyamasyānupaśyāmi pratiyoddhāramāhave ,ṛte vṛkodarātpārthātsa ca nātikṛtaśramaḥ.
english: I do not see any other challenger (pratiyoddhāra) for him in battle, except for Vṛkodara, son of Pṛthā (Pārtha). And he (Vṛkodara) is not very well-practiced/trained.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 6. I do not see any other challenger (pratiyoddhāra) for him in battle, except for Vṛkodara, son of Pṛthā (Pārtha). And he (Vṛkodara) is not very well-practiced/trained.

sanskrit: तदिदं द्यूतमारब्धं पुनरेव यथा पुरा ।विषमं शकुनेश्चैव तव चैव विशां पते ॥७॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ dyūtamārabdhaṁ punareva yathā purā ,viṣamaṁ śakuneścaiva tava caiva viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, this gambling match has been started again, just as it was before. It is dangerous both for Śakuni and for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 7. O lord of the people, this gambling match has been started again, just as it was before. It is dangerous both for Śakuni and for you.

sanskrit: बली भीमः समर्थश्च कृती राजा सुयोधनः ।बलवान्वा कृती वेति कृती राजन्विशिष्यते ॥८॥
transliteration: balī bhīmaḥ samarthaśca kṛtī rājā suyodhanaḥ ,balavānvā kṛtī veti kṛtī rājanviśiṣyate.
english: Bhima is strong and capable. King Suyodhana (Duryodhana) is skilled. O king, whether one is strong or skilled, the skilled one always excels.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 8. Bhima is strong and capable. King Suyodhana (Duryodhana) is skilled. O king, whether one is strong or skilled, the skilled one always excels.

sanskrit: सोऽयं राजंस्त्वया शत्रुः समे पथि निवेशितः ।न्यस्तश्चात्मा सुविषमे कृच्छ्रमापादिता वयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ rājaṁstvayā śatruḥ same pathi niveśitaḥ ,nyastaścātmā suviṣame kṛcchramāpāditā vayam.
english: O king, by you, this enemy has been placed on a favorable path, while our self (ātman) has been placed in an extremely difficult situation. We have been brought into hardship.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 9. O king, by you, this enemy has been placed on a favorable path, while our self (ātman) has been placed in an extremely difficult situation. We have been brought into hardship.

sanskrit: को नु सर्वान्विनिर्जित्य शत्रूनेकेन वैरिणा ।पणित्वा चैकपाणेन रोचयेदेवमाहवम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: ko nu sarvānvinirjitya śatrūnekena vairiṇā ,paṇitvā caikapāṇena rocayedevamāhavam.
english: Who indeed, after defeating all enemies, would then, by gambling with a single foe (Śakuni) with a single throw of dice, approve of such a conflict?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 10. Who indeed, after defeating all enemies, would then, by gambling with a single foe (Śakuni) with a single throw of dice, approve of such a conflict?

sanskrit: न हि पश्यामि तं लोके गदाहस्तं नरोत्तमम् ।युध्येद्दुर्योधनं संख्ये कृतित्वाद्धि विशेषयेत् ॥११॥
transliteration: na hi paśyāmi taṁ loke gadāhastaṁ narottamam ,yudhyedduryodhanaṁ saṁkhye kṛtitvāddhi viśeṣayet.
english: Indeed, I do not see anyone in this world, among the best of men, who, wielding a mace, could fight Duryodhana in battle and truly surpass him due to his skill.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 11. Indeed, I do not see anyone in this world, among the best of men, who, wielding a mace, could fight Duryodhana in battle and truly surpass him due to his skill.

sanskrit: फल्गुनं वा भवन्तं वा माद्रीपुत्रावथापि वा ।न समर्थानहं मन्ये गदाहस्तस्य संयुगे ॥१२॥
transliteration: phalgunaṁ vā bhavantaṁ vā mādrīputrāvathāpi vā ,na samarthānahaṁ manye gadāhastasya saṁyuge.
english: I do not consider Arjuna, or yourself, or even the sons of Madri, capable against the mace-wielder in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 12. I do not consider Arjuna, or yourself, or even the sons of Madri, capable against the mace-wielder in combat.

sanskrit: स कथं वदसे शत्रुं युध्यस्व गदयेति ह ।एकं च नो निहत्याजौ भव राजेति भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa kathaṁ vadase śatruṁ yudhyasva gadayeti ha ,ekaṁ ca no nihatyājau bhava rājeti bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, why do you say to the enemy, 'Fight with a mace!' and 'Having slain one of us in battle, become king!'?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 13. O Bhārata, why do you say to the enemy, 'Fight with a mace!' and 'Having slain one of us in battle, become king!'?

sanskrit: वृकोदरं समासाद्य संशयो विजये हि नः ।न्यायतो युध्यमानानां कृती ह्येष महाबलः ॥१४॥
transliteration: vṛkodaraṁ samāsādya saṁśayo vijaye hi naḥ ,nyāyato yudhyamānānāṁ kṛtī hyeṣa mahābalaḥ.
english: Indeed, if we have to face Vṛkodara, there is doubt about our victory, even among those who fight fairly, for this immensely powerful one is truly skilled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 14. Indeed, if we have to face Vṛkodara, there is doubt about our victory, even among those who fight fairly, for this immensely powerful one is truly skilled.

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।मधुसूदन मा कार्षीर्विषादं यदुनन्दन ।अद्य पारं गमिष्यामि वैरस्य भृशदुर्गमम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,madhusūdana mā kārṣīrviṣādaṁ yadunandana ,adya pāraṁ gamiṣyāmi vairasya bhṛśadurgamam.
english: Bhima said, "O Madhusudana, delight of the Yadus, do not be disheartened! Today I shall reach the end of this exceedingly difficult hostility."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 15. Bhima said, "O Madhusudana, delight of the Yadus, do not be disheartened! Today I shall reach the end of this exceedingly difficult hostility."

sanskrit: अहं सुयोधनं संख्ये हनिष्यामि न संशयः ।विजयो वै ध्रुवं कृष्ण धर्मराजस्य दृश्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: ahaṁ suyodhanaṁ saṁkhye haniṣyāmi na saṁśayaḥ ,vijayo vai dhruvaṁ kṛṣṇa dharmarājasya dṛśyate.
english: I will surely kill Suyodhana in battle; there is no doubt about it. O Krishna, the victory of the king who upholds natural law (dharmarāja) is indeed certain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 16. I will surely kill Suyodhana in battle; there is no doubt about it. O Krishna, the victory of the king who upholds natural law (dharmarāja) is indeed certain.

sanskrit: अध्यर्धेन गुणेनेयं गदा गुरुतरी मम ।न तथा धार्तराष्ट्रस्य मा कार्षीर्माधव व्यथाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: adhyardhena guṇeneyaṁ gadā gurutarī mama ,na tathā dhārtarāṣṭrasya mā kārṣīrmādhava vyathām.
english: This mace of mine is one and a half times more potent; Dhritarashtra's son's (Duryodhana's) is not so. O Madhava, do not feel distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 17. This mace of mine is one and a half times more potent; Dhritarashtra's son's (Duryodhana's) is not so. O Madhava, do not feel distress.

sanskrit: सामरानपि लोकांस्त्रीन्नानाशस्त्रधरान्युधि ।योधयेयं रणे हृष्टः किमुताद्य सुयोधनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sāmarānapi lokāṁstrīnnānāśastradharānyudhi ,yodhayeyaṁ raṇe hṛṣṭaḥ kimutādya suyodhanam.
english: Joyful in combat, I would fight even the three worlds along with the Maruts (deities), all bearing various weapons. How much more easily then can I fight Suyodhana today!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 18. Joyful in combat, I would fight even the three worlds along with the Maruts (deities), all bearing various weapons. How much more easily then can I fight Suyodhana today!

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तथा संभाषमाणं तु वासुदेवो वृकोदरम् ।हृष्टः संपूजयामास वचनं चेदमब्रवीत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tathā saṁbhāṣamāṇaṁ tu vāsudevo vṛkodaram ,hṛṣṭaḥ saṁpūjayāmāsa vacanaṁ cedamabravīt.
english: Sañjaya said: As Vṛkodara (Bhīma) was speaking thus, Vāsudeva (Krishna), delighted, showed him respect and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 19. Sañjaya said: As Vṛkodara (Bhīma) was speaking thus, Vāsudeva (Krishna), delighted, showed him respect and spoke these words.

sanskrit: त्वामाश्रित्य महाबाहो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।निहतारिः स्वकां दीप्तां श्रियं प्राप्तो न संशयः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tvāmāśritya mahābāho dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,nihatāriḥ svakāṁ dīptāṁ śriyaṁ prāpto na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O mighty-armed one, there is no doubt that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), relying on you and having vanquished his enemies, has regained his brilliant prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 20. O mighty-armed one, there is no doubt that King Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), relying on you and having vanquished his enemies, has regained his brilliant prosperity.

sanskrit: त्वया विनिहताः सर्वे धृतराष्ट्रसुता रणे ।राजानो राजपुत्राश्च नागाश्च विनिपातिताः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tvayā vinihatāḥ sarve dhṛtarāṣṭrasutā raṇe ,rājāno rājaputrāśca nāgāśca vinipātitāḥ.
english: In battle, all the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra were slain by you. Kings, princes, and elephants were also felled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 21. In battle, all the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra were slain by you. Kings, princes, and elephants were also felled.

sanskrit: कलिङ्गा मागधाः प्राच्या गान्धाराः कुरवस्तथा ।त्वामासाद्य महायुद्धे निहताः पाण्डुनन्दन ॥२२॥
transliteration: kaliṅgā māgadhāḥ prācyā gāndhārāḥ kuravastathā ,tvāmāsādya mahāyuddhe nihatāḥ pāṇḍunandana.
english: O son of Pāṇḍu, the Kaliṅgas, Magadhas, Prācyas, Gandhāras, and also the Kurus, were all slain in the great war after encountering you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 22. O son of Pāṇḍu, the Kaliṅgas, Magadhas, Prācyas, Gandhāras, and also the Kurus, were all slain in the great war after encountering you.

sanskrit: हत्वा दुर्योधनं चापि प्रयच्छोर्वीं ससागराम् ।धर्मराजाय कौन्तेय यथा विष्णुः शचीपतेः ॥२३॥
transliteration: hatvā duryodhanaṁ cāpi prayacchorvīṁ sasāgarām ,dharmarājāya kaunteya yathā viṣṇuḥ śacīpateḥ.
english: O son of Kunti, after killing Duryodhana, you should give the earth with its oceans to Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), just as Vishnu gave [the worlds back] to Indra, the husband of Śacī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 23. O son of Kunti, after killing Duryodhana, you should give the earth with its oceans to Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), just as Vishnu gave [the worlds back] to Indra, the husband of Śacī.

sanskrit: त्वां च प्राप्य रणे पापो धार्तराष्ट्रो विनङ्क्ष्यति ।त्वमस्य सक्थिनी भङ्क्त्वा प्रतिज्ञां पारयिष्यसि ॥२४॥
transliteration: tvāṁ ca prāpya raṇe pāpo dhārtarāṣṭro vinaṅkṣyati ,tvamasya sakthinī bhaṅktvā pratijñāṁ pārayiṣyasi.
english: When the wicked son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) encounters you in battle, he will be destroyed. You will fulfill your solemn vow by breaking his two thighs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 24. When the wicked son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) encounters you in battle, he will be destroyed. You will fulfill your solemn vow by breaking his two thighs.

sanskrit: यत्नेन तु सदा पार्थ योद्धव्यो धृतराष्ट्रजः ।कृती च बलवांश्चैव युद्धशौण्डश्च नित्यदा ॥२५॥
transliteration: yatnena tu sadā pārtha yoddhavyo dhṛtarāṣṭrajaḥ ,kṛtī ca balavāṁścaiva yuddhaśauṇḍaśca nityadā.
english: But, O son of Pṛthā (Arjuna), the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) must always be fought with great effort, for he is skilled, mighty, and perpetually devoted to battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 25. But, O son of Pṛthā (Arjuna), the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) must always be fought with great effort, for he is skilled, mighty, and perpetually devoted to battle.

sanskrit: ततस्तु सात्यकी राजन्पूजयामास पाण्डवम् ।विविधाभिश्च तां वाग्भिः पूजयामास माधवः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tatastu sātyakī rājanpūjayāmāsa pāṇḍavam ,vividhābhiśca tāṁ vāgbhiḥ pūjayāmāsa mādhavaḥ.
english: Then, O King, Satyaki indeed honored the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna). And Madhava (Krishna) also honored him with various words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 26. Then, O King, Satyaki indeed honored the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna). And Madhava (Krishna) also honored him with various words.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालाः पाण्डवेयाश्च धर्मराजपुरोगमाः ।तद्वचो भीमसेनस्य सर्व एवाभ्यपूजयन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: pāñcālāḥ pāṇḍaveyāśca dharmarājapurogamāḥ ,tadvaco bhīmasenasya sarva evābhyapūjayan.
english: The Pāñcālas and the Pāṇḍavas, with King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) at their forefront, all heartily approved of Bhīmasena's speech.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 27. The Pāñcālas and the Pāṇḍavas, with King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja) at their forefront, all heartily approved of Bhīmasena's speech.

sanskrit: ततो भीमबलो भीमो युधिष्ठिरमथाब्रवीत् ।सृञ्जयैः सह तिष्ठन्तं तपन्तमिव भास्करम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato bhīmabalo bhīmo yudhiṣṭhiramathābravīt ,sṛñjayaiḥ saha tiṣṭhantaṁ tapantamiva bhāskaram.
english: Then, Bhīma, mighty in strength, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, who stood with the Sṛñjayas, radiant like the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 28. Then, Bhīma, mighty in strength, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, who stood with the Sṛñjayas, radiant like the sun.

sanskrit: अहमेतेन संगम्य संयुगे योद्धुमुत्सहे ।न हि शक्तो रणे जेतुं मामेष पुरुषाधमः ॥२९॥
transliteration: ahametena saṁgamya saṁyuge yoddhumutsahe ,na hi śakto raṇe jetuṁ māmeṣa puruṣādhamaḥ.
english: I am eager to engage him in battle. Indeed, this lowest of men (puruṣādhama) is certainly not capable of defeating me in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 29. I am eager to engage him in battle. Indeed, this lowest of men (puruṣādhama) is certainly not capable of defeating me in combat.

sanskrit: अद्य क्रोधं विमोक्ष्यामि निहितं हृदये भृशम् ।सुयोधने धार्तराष्ट्रे खाण्डवेऽग्निमिवार्जुनः ॥३०॥
transliteration: adya krodhaṁ vimokṣyāmi nihitaṁ hṛdaye bhṛśam ,suyodhane dhārtarāṣṭre khāṇḍave'gnimivārjunaḥ.
english: Today, I shall release the immense anger (krodha) that has been deeply harbored in my heart, directing it at Suyodhana, son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, just as Arjuna unleashed fire upon the Khāṇḍava forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 30. Today, I shall release the immense anger (krodha) that has been deeply harbored in my heart, directing it at Suyodhana, son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, just as Arjuna unleashed fire upon the Khāṇḍava forest.

sanskrit: शल्यमद्योद्धरिष्यामि तव पाण्डव हृच्छयम् ।निहत्य गदया पापमद्य राजन्सुखी भव ॥३१॥
transliteration: śalyamadyoddhariṣyāmi tava pāṇḍava hṛcchayam ,nihatya gadayā pāpamadya rājansukhī bhava.
english: O son of Pāṇḍu, today I will extract this dart that lies within your heart. Having struck down the wicked one with my mace today, O King, be happy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 31. O son of Pāṇḍu, today I will extract this dart that lies within your heart. Having struck down the wicked one with my mace today, O King, be happy.

sanskrit: अद्य कीर्तिमयीं मालां प्रतिमोक्ष्ये तवानघ ।प्राणाञ्श्रियं च राज्यं च मोक्ष्यतेऽद्य सुयोधनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: adya kīrtimayīṁ mālāṁ pratimokṣye tavānagha ,prāṇāñśriyaṁ ca rājyaṁ ca mokṣyate'dya suyodhanaḥ.
english: O sinless one, today I will present to you a glorious garland of fame. Today, Duryodhana will abandon his life, prosperity, and kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 32. O sinless one, today I will present to you a glorious garland of fame. Today, Duryodhana will abandon his life, prosperity, and kingdom.

sanskrit: राजा च धृतराष्ट्रोऽद्य श्रुत्वा पुत्रं मया हतम् ।स्मरिष्यत्यशुभं कर्म यत्तच्छकुनिबुद्धिजम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: rājā ca dhṛtarāṣṭro'dya śrutvā putraṁ mayā hatam ,smariṣyatyaśubhaṁ karma yattacchakunibuddhijam.
english: And today, King Dhritarashtra, upon hearing that his son has been killed by me, will recall that inauspicious action (karma) which arose from Shakuni's intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 33. And today, King Dhritarashtra, upon hearing that his son has been killed by me, will recall that inauspicious action (karma) which arose from Shakuni's intellect.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा भरतश्रेष्ठो गदामुद्यम्य वीर्यवान् ।उदतिष्ठत युद्धाय शक्रो वृत्रमिवाह्वयन् ॥३४॥
transliteration: ityuktvā bharataśreṣṭho gadāmudyamya vīryavān ,udatiṣṭhata yuddhāya śakro vṛtramivāhvayan.
english: Having spoken thus, the valiant best of the Bharatas (Bhima), raising his mace, rose up for battle, just as Indra challenges Vritra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 34. Having spoken thus, the valiant best of the Bharatas (Bhima), raising his mace, rose up for battle, just as Indra challenges Vritra.

sanskrit: तमेकाकिनमासाद्य धार्तराष्ट्रं महाबलम् ।निर्यूथमिव मातङ्गं समहृष्यन्त पाण्डवाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tamekākinamāsādya dhārtarāṣṭraṁ mahābalam ,niryūthamiva mātaṅgaṁ samahṛṣyanta pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: When they found that very powerful son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) alone, like an elephant separated from its herd, the Pāṇḍavas greatly rejoiced.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 35. When they found that very powerful son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) alone, like an elephant separated from its herd, the Pāṇḍavas greatly rejoiced.

sanskrit: तमुद्यतगदं दृष्ट्वा कैलासमिव शृङ्गिणम् ।भीमसेनस्तदा राजन्दुर्योधनमथाब्रवीत् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tamudyatagadaṁ dṛṣṭvā kailāsamiva śṛṅgiṇam ,bhīmasenastadā rājanduryodhanamathābravīt.
english: O King, when Bhīmasena saw Duryodhana with his mace raised, appearing like a peaked Mount Kailāsa, he then addressed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 36. O King, when Bhīmasena saw Duryodhana with his mace raised, appearing like a peaked Mount Kailāsa, he then addressed him.

sanskrit: राज्ञापि धृतराष्ट्रेण त्वया चास्मासु यत्कृतम् ।स्मर तद्दुष्कृतं कर्म यद्वृत्तं वारणावते ॥३७॥
transliteration: rājñāpi dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa tvayā cāsmāsu yatkṛtam ,smara tadduṣkṛtaṁ karma yadvṛttaṁ vāraṇāvate.
english: Remember that wicked deed (karma) which was perpetrated against us by King Dhṛtarāṣṭra and by you, specifically what happened in Vāraṇāvata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 37. Remember that wicked deed (karma) which was perpetrated against us by King Dhṛtarāṣṭra and by you, specifically what happened in Vāraṇāvata.

sanskrit: द्रौपदी च परिक्लिष्टा सभामध्ये रजस्वला ।द्यूते यद्विजितो राजा शकुनेर्बुद्धिनिश्चयात् ॥३८॥
transliteration: draupadī ca parikliṣṭā sabhāmadhye rajasvalā ,dyūte yadvijito rājā śakunerbuddhiniścayāt.
english: And (remember also) Draupadī, who was humiliated in the midst of the assembly while menstruating, and how the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was defeated in the dice game due to Śakuni's clever plotting.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 38. And (remember also) Draupadī, who was humiliated in the midst of the assembly while menstruating, and how the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was defeated in the dice game due to Śakuni's clever plotting.

sanskrit: यानि चान्यानि दुष्टात्मन्पापानि कृतवानसि ।अनागःसु च पार्थेषु तस्य पश्य महत्फलम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: yāni cānyāni duṣṭātmanpāpāni kṛtavānasi ,anāgaḥsu ca pārtheṣu tasya paśya mahatphalam.
english: O wicked-minded one, behold the great consequence of all those other sins that you have committed against the innocent sons of Pṛthā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 39. O wicked-minded one, behold the great consequence of all those other sins that you have committed against the innocent sons of Pṛthā.

sanskrit: त्वत्कृते निहतः शेते शरतल्पे महायशाः ।गाङ्गेयो भरतश्रेष्ठः सर्वेषां नः पितामहः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tvatkṛte nihataḥ śete śaratalpe mahāyaśāḥ ,gāṅgeyo bharataśreṣṭhaḥ sarveṣāṁ naḥ pitāmahaḥ.
english: For your sake, the highly renowned Gaṅgeya, the best among the Bharatas and the grandfather of all of us, lies slain on a bed of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 40. For your sake, the highly renowned Gaṅgeya, the best among the Bharatas and the grandfather of all of us, lies slain on a bed of arrows.

sanskrit: हतो द्रोणश्च कर्णश्च हतः शल्यः प्रतापवान् ।वैरस्य चादिकर्तासौ शकुनिर्निहतो युधि ॥४१॥
transliteration: hato droṇaśca karṇaśca hataḥ śalyaḥ pratāpavān ,vairasya cādikartāsau śakunirnihato yudhi.
english: Droṇa and Karṇa have been killed; the mighty Śalya has been killed. And that Śakuni, the instigator of the enmity, has been slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 41. Droṇa and Karṇa have been killed; the mighty Śalya has been killed. And that Śakuni, the instigator of the enmity, has been slain in battle.

sanskrit: भ्रातरस्ते हताः शूराः पुत्राश्च सहसैनिकाः ।राजानश्च हताः शूराः समरेष्वनिवर्तिनः ॥४२॥
transliteration: bhrātaraste hatāḥ śūrāḥ putrāśca sahasainikāḥ ,rājānaśca hatāḥ śūrāḥ samareṣvanivartinaḥ.
english: Your brave brothers have been killed, and your sons, along with their soldiers. Also slain are your brave kings, who never retreated in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 42. Your brave brothers have been killed, and your sons, along with their soldiers. Also slain are your brave kings, who never retreated in battles.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च निहता बहवः क्षत्रियर्षभाः ।प्रातिकामी तथा पापो द्रौपद्याः क्लेशकृद्धतः ॥४३॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca nihatā bahavaḥ kṣatriyarṣabhāḥ ,prātikāmī tathā pāpo draupadyāḥ kleśakṛddhataḥ.
english: These and many other foremost warriors (kṣatriya) have been slain. And the sinful Prātikāmī, who caused distress to Draupadī, has also been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 43. These and many other foremost warriors (kṣatriya) have been slain. And the sinful Prātikāmī, who caused distress to Draupadī, has also been killed.

sanskrit: अवशिष्टस्त्वमेवैकः कुलघ्नोऽधमपूरुषः ।त्वामप्यद्य हनिष्यामि गदया नात्र संशयः ॥४४॥
transliteration: avaśiṣṭastvamevaikaḥ kulaghno'dhamapūruṣaḥ ,tvāmapyadya haniṣyāmi gadayā nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: You alone remain, the destroyer of your family (kula), an ignoble person (puruṣa). Today I will also kill you with a mace; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 44. You alone remain, the destroyer of your family (kula), an ignoble person (puruṣa). Today I will also kill you with a mace; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: अद्य तेऽहं रणे दर्पं सर्वं नाशयिता नृप ।राज्याशां विपुलां राजन्पाण्डवेषु च दुष्कृतम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: adya te'haṁ raṇe darpaṁ sarvaṁ nāśayitā nṛpa ,rājyāśāṁ vipulāṁ rājanpāṇḍaveṣu ca duṣkṛtam.
english: O king (nṛpa), today in battle I will destroy all your pride (darpa). O king (rājan), (I will destroy) your immense hope for a kingdom, and the wrongdoing you inflicted upon the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 45. O king (nṛpa), today in battle I will destroy all your pride (darpa). O king (rājan), (I will destroy) your immense hope for a kingdom, and the wrongdoing you inflicted upon the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।किं कत्थितेन बहुधा युध्यस्वाद्य मया सह ।अद्य तेऽहं विनेष्यामि युद्धश्रद्धां वृकोदर ॥४६॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,kiṁ katthitena bahudhā yudhyasvādya mayā saha ,adya te'haṁ vineṣyāmi yuddhaśraddhāṁ vṛkodara.
english: Duryodhana said: What is the point of so much boasting? Fight with me today! Today, O Vṛkodara, I will remove your eagerness for battle (yuddha-śraddhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 46. Duryodhana said: What is the point of so much boasting? Fight with me today! Today, O Vṛkodara, I will remove your eagerness for battle (yuddha-śraddhā).

sanskrit: किं न पश्यसि मां पाप गदायुद्धे व्यवस्थितम् ।हिमवच्छिखराकारां प्रगृह्य महतीं गदाम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: kiṁ na paśyasi māṁ pāpa gadāyuddhe vyavasthitam ,himavacchikharākārāṁ pragṛhya mahatīṁ gadām.
english: O wicked one, why do you not see me, standing ready for a mace-fight, holding a mighty mace that resembles the peak of the Himalayas?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 47. O wicked one, why do you not see me, standing ready for a mace-fight, holding a mighty mace that resembles the peak of the Himalayas?

sanskrit: गदिनं कोऽद्य मां पाप जेतुमुत्सहते रिपुः ।न्यायतो युध्यमानस्य देवेष्वपि पुरंदरः ॥४८॥
transliteration: gadinaṁ ko'dya māṁ pāpa jetumutsahate ripuḥ ,nyāyato yudhyamānasya deveṣvapi puraṁdaraḥ.
english: O wicked one, what enemy today dares to conquer me, who wield a mace? Not even Purandara [Indra] among the gods dares to, if I fight justly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 48. O wicked one, what enemy today dares to conquer me, who wield a mace? Not even Purandara [Indra] among the gods dares to, if I fight justly.

sanskrit: मा वृथा गर्ज कौन्तेय शारदाभ्रमिवाजलम् ।दर्शयस्व बलं युद्धे यावत्तत्तेऽद्य विद्यते ॥४९॥
transliteration: mā vṛthā garja kaunteya śāradābhramivājalam ,darśayasva balaṁ yuddhe yāvattatte'dya vidyate.
english: O son of Kunti, do not roar in vain like a waterless autumn cloud. Show your strength in battle, whatever you possess today.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 49. O son of Kunti, do not roar in vain like a waterless autumn cloud. Show your strength in battle, whatever you possess today.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा पाञ्चालाः सहसृञ्जयाः ।सर्वे संपूजयामासुस्तद्वचो विजिगीषवः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā pāñcālāḥ sahasṛñjayāḥ ,sarve saṁpūjayāmāsustadvaco vijigīṣavaḥ.
english: Hearing those words of his, all the Pañcālas, along with the Sṛñjayas, desiring victory, thoroughly honored those words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 50. Hearing those words of his, all the Pañcālas, along with the Sṛñjayas, desiring victory, thoroughly honored those words.

sanskrit: तं मत्तमिव मातङ्गं तलशब्देन मानवाः ।भूयः संहर्षयामासू राजन्दुर्योधनं नृपम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: taṁ mattamiva mātaṅgaṁ talaśabdena mānavāḥ ,bhūyaḥ saṁharṣayāmāsū rājanduryodhanaṁ nṛpam.
english: O King, the men further cheered King Duryodhana, who was like an intoxicated elephant, with the sound of their palms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 51. O King, the men further cheered King Duryodhana, who was like an intoxicated elephant, with the sound of their palms.

sanskrit: बृंहन्ति कुञ्जरास्तत्र हया हेषन्ति चासकृत् ।शस्त्राणि संप्रदीप्यन्ते पाण्डवानां जयैषिणाम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: bṛṁhanti kuñjarāstatra hayā heṣanti cāsakṛt ,śastrāṇi saṁpradīpyante pāṇḍavānāṁ jayaiṣiṇām.
english: There, elephants trumpeted, and horses neighed repeatedly. The weapons of the Pandavas, who desired victory, shone brightly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 32 Verse 52. There, elephants trumpeted, and horses neighed repeatedly. The weapons of the Pandavas, who desired victory, shone brightly.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तस्मिन्युद्धे महाराज संप्रवृत्ते सुदारुणे ।उपविष्टेषु सर्वेषु पाण्डवेषु महात्मसु ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tasminyuddhe mahārāja saṁpravṛtte sudāruṇe ,upaviṣṭeṣu sarveṣu pāṇḍaveṣu mahātmasu.
english: Sanjaya said: 'O great King, when that exceedingly fierce battle had commenced, and all the great-souled (mahātman) Pandavas were seated...'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: 'O great King, when that exceedingly fierce battle had commenced, and all the great-souled (mahātman) Pandavas were seated...'

sanskrit: ततस्तालध्वजो रामस्तयोर्युद्ध उपस्थिते ।श्रुत्वा तच्छिष्ययो राजन्नाजगाम हलायुधः ॥२॥
transliteration: tatastāladhvajo rāmastayoryuddha upasthite ,śrutvā tacchiṣyayo rājannājagāma halāyudhaḥ.
english: O King, then Balarama (Rāma), who has a palm tree on his banner (tāladhvaja) and a plough as his weapon (halāyudha), arrived after hearing that the fight between his two disciples had begun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 2. O King, then Balarama (Rāma), who has a palm tree on his banner (tāladhvaja) and a plough as his weapon (halāyudha), arrived after hearing that the fight between his two disciples had begun.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा परमप्रीताः पूजयित्वा नराधिपाः ।शिष्ययोः कौशलं युद्धे पश्य रामेति चाब्रुवन् ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā paramaprītāḥ pūjayitvā narādhipāḥ ,śiṣyayoḥ kauśalaṁ yuddhe paśya rāmeti cābruvan.
english: The kings, greatly pleased after seeing and honoring him, said, "O Rāma, behold the skill of the two disciples in battle."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 3. The kings, greatly pleased after seeing and honoring him, said, "O Rāma, behold the skill of the two disciples in battle."

sanskrit: अब्रवीच्च तदा रामो दृष्ट्वा कृष्णं च पाण्डवम् ।दुर्योधनं च कौरव्यं गदापाणिमवस्थितम् ॥४॥
transliteration: abravīcca tadā rāmo dṛṣṭvā kṛṣṇaṁ ca pāṇḍavam ,duryodhanaṁ ca kauravyaṁ gadāpāṇimavasthitam.
english: And then Rāma (Balarāma), having seen Kṛṣṇa, and the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) and the Kauravya (Duryodhana) standing with maces in hand, spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 4. And then Rāma (Balarāma), having seen Kṛṣṇa, and the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) and the Kauravya (Duryodhana) standing with maces in hand, spoke.

sanskrit: चत्वारिंशदहान्यद्य द्वे च मे निःसृतस्य वै ।पुष्येण संप्रयातोऽस्मि श्रवणे पुनरागतः ।शिष्ययोर्वै गदायुद्धं द्रष्टुकामोऽस्मि माधव ॥५॥
transliteration: catvāriṁśadahānyadya dve ca me niḥsṛtasya vai ,puṣyeṇa saṁprayāto'smi śravaṇe punarāgataḥ ,śiṣyayorvai gadāyuddhaṁ draṣṭukāmo'smi mādhava.
english: Today, forty-two days have passed since my departure. I departed under the Puṣya (constellation) and have returned under Śravaṇa. O Mādhava, I am indeed desirous of seeing the mace (gadā) fight of the two disciples.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 5. Today, forty-two days have passed since my departure. I departed under the Puṣya (constellation) and have returned under Śravaṇa. O Mādhava, I am indeed desirous of seeing the mace (gadā) fight of the two disciples.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा परिष्वज्य हलायुधम् ।स्वागतं कुशलं चास्मै पर्यपृच्छद्यथातथम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā pariṣvajya halāyudham ,svāgataṁ kuśalaṁ cāsmai paryapṛcchadyathātatham.
english: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, having embraced Halāyudha (Balarāma), duly inquired about his welcome and well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 6. Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, having embraced Halāyudha (Balarāma), duly inquired about his welcome and well-being.

sanskrit: कृष्णौ चापि महेष्वासावभिवाद्य हलायुधम् ।सस्वजाते परिप्रीतौ प्रियमाणौ यशस्विनौ ॥७॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇau cāpi maheṣvāsāvabhivādya halāyudham ,sasvajāte pariprītau priyamāṇau yaśasvinau.
english: And the two glorious, great archers, the Krishnas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna), having saluted Balarama (halāyudha), embraced him, brimming with great delight and showing affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 7. And the two glorious, great archers, the Krishnas (Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna), having saluted Balarama (halāyudha), embraced him, brimming with great delight and showing affection.

sanskrit: माद्रीपुत्रौ तथा शूरौ द्रौपद्याः पञ्च चात्मजाः ।अभिवाद्य स्थिता राजन्रौहिणेयं महाबलम् ॥८॥
transliteration: mādrīputrau tathā śūrau draupadyāḥ pañca cātmajāḥ ,abhivādya sthitā rājanrauhiṇeyaṁ mahābalam.
english: O King, similarly, the two brave sons of Madri and the five sons of Draupadi, having saluted the mighty son of Rohiṇī (Balarama), stood [before him].
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 8. O King, similarly, the two brave sons of Madri and the five sons of Draupadi, having saluted the mighty son of Rohiṇī (Balarama), stood [before him].

sanskrit: भीमसेनोऽथ बलवान्पुत्रस्तव जनाधिप ।तथैव चोद्यतगदौ पूजयामासतुर्बलम् ॥९॥
transliteration: bhīmaseno'tha balavānputrastava janādhipa ,tathaiva codyatagadau pūjayāmāsaturbalam.
english: Then, O Lord of men, your mighty son (Duryodhana), and Bhimasena, similarly, both holding up their maces, honored Balarama.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 9. Then, O Lord of men, your mighty son (Duryodhana), and Bhimasena, similarly, both holding up their maces, honored Balarama.

sanskrit: स्वागतेन च ते तत्र प्रतिपूज्य पुनः पुनः ।पश्य युद्धं महाबाहो इति ते राममब्रुवन् ।एवमूचुर्महात्मानं रौहिणेयं नराधिपाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: svāgatena ca te tatra pratipūjya punaḥ punaḥ ,paśya yuddhaṁ mahābāho iti te rāmamabruvan ,evamūcurmahātmānaṁ rauhiṇeyaṁ narādhipāḥ.
english: And there, having repeatedly offered a welcome and honored Balarama (rāma), the kings (narādhipāḥ) spoke to the great-souled (mahā-ātman) son of Rohiṇī (Balarama), saying: 'O mighty-armed one, behold the battle!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 10. And there, having repeatedly offered a welcome and honored Balarama (rāma), the kings (narādhipāḥ) spoke to the great-souled (mahā-ātman) son of Rohiṇī (Balarama), saying: 'O mighty-armed one, behold the battle!'

sanskrit: परिष्वज्य तदा रामः पाण्डवान्सृञ्जयानपि ।अपृच्छत्कुशलं सर्वान्पाण्डवांश्चामितौजसः ।तथैव ते समासाद्य पप्रच्छुस्तमनामयम् ॥११॥
transliteration: pariṣvajya tadā rāmaḥ pāṇḍavānsṛñjayānapi ,apṛcchatkuśalaṁ sarvānpāṇḍavāṁścāmitaujasaḥ ,tathaiva te samāsādya papracchustamanāmayam.
english: Then Balarama, embracing the Pandavas and also the Srinjayas, inquired about the well-being of all the Pandavas, who possessed immeasurable might. Similarly, they, upon meeting him, asked him about his health.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 11. Then Balarama, embracing the Pandavas and also the Srinjayas, inquired about the well-being of all the Pandavas, who possessed immeasurable might. Similarly, they, upon meeting him, asked him about his health.

sanskrit: प्रत्यभ्यर्च्य हली सर्वान्क्षत्रियांश्च महामनाः ।कृत्वा कुशलसंयुक्तां संविदं च यथावयः ॥१२॥
transliteration: pratyabhyarcya halī sarvānkṣatriyāṁśca mahāmanāḥ ,kṛtvā kuśalasaṁyuktāṁ saṁvidaṁ ca yathāvayaḥ.
english: The great-minded Balarama, having respectfully greeted all the kshatriyas, also engaged in conversation, inquiring about their well-being and showing due regard for their age and status.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 12. The great-minded Balarama, having respectfully greeted all the kshatriyas, also engaged in conversation, inquiring about their well-being and showing due regard for their age and status.

sanskrit: जनार्दनं सात्यकिं च प्रेम्णा स परिषस्वजे ।मूर्ध्नि चैतावुपाघ्राय कुशलं पर्यपृच्छत ॥१३॥
transliteration: janārdanaṁ sātyakiṁ ca premṇā sa pariṣasvaje ,mūrdhni caitāvupāghrāya kuśalaṁ paryapṛcchata.
english: Balarama lovingly embraced Janardana (Krishna) and Satyaki. He then kissed both of them on the head and inquired about their well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 13. Balarama lovingly embraced Janardana (Krishna) and Satyaki. He then kissed both of them on the head and inquired about their well-being.

sanskrit: तौ चैनं विधिवद्राजन्पूजयामासतुर्गुरुम् ।ब्रह्माणमिव देवेशमिन्द्रोपेन्द्रौ मुदा युतौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: tau cainaṁ vidhivadrājanpūjayāmāsaturgurum ,brahmāṇamiva deveśamindropendrau mudā yutau.
english: O King, those two (Krishna and Satyaki), filled with joy, respectfully honored him (Balarama), their elder and spiritual teacher (guru), according to the proper rites, just as Indra and Upendra (Vishnu) would worship Brahma, the lord of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 14. O King, those two (Krishna and Satyaki), filled with joy, respectfully honored him (Balarama), their elder and spiritual teacher (guru), according to the proper rites, just as Indra and Upendra (Vishnu) would worship Brahma, the lord of the gods.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीद्धर्मसुतो रौहिणेयमरिंदमम् ।इदं भ्रात्रोर्महायुद्धं पश्य रामेति भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: tato'bravīddharmasuto rauhiṇeyamariṁdamam ,idaṁ bhrātrormahāyuddhaṁ paśya rāmeti bhārata.
english: Then, the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) spoke to the enemy-subduer, the son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma), saying, "Rāma, behold this great battle of the two brothers, O descendant of Bharata!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 15. Then, the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) spoke to the enemy-subduer, the son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma), saying, "Rāma, behold this great battle of the two brothers, O descendant of Bharata!"

sanskrit: तेषां मध्ये महाबाहुः श्रीमान्केशवपूर्वजः ।न्यविशत्परमप्रीतः पूज्यमानो महारथैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ madhye mahābāhuḥ śrīmānkeśavapūrvajaḥ ,nyaviśatparamaprītaḥ pūjyamāno mahārathaiḥ.
english: Among them, the mighty-armed, glorious elder brother of Keśava (Balarāma) sat down, extremely pleased, being honored by the great charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 16. Among them, the mighty-armed, glorious elder brother of Keśava (Balarāma) sat down, extremely pleased, being honored by the great charioteers.

sanskrit: स बभौ राजमध्यस्थो नीलवासाः सितप्रभः ।दिवीव नक्षत्रगणैः परिकीर्णो निशाकरः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa babhau rājamadhyastho nīlavāsāḥ sitaprabhaḥ ,divīva nakṣatragaṇaiḥ parikīrṇo niśākaraḥ.
english: He shone, standing amidst the kings, clad in blue garments, and radiating a white splendor, like the moon (niśākaraḥ) surrounded by constellations of stars in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 17. He shone, standing amidst the kings, clad in blue garments, and radiating a white splendor, like the moon (niśākaraḥ) surrounded by constellations of stars in the sky.

sanskrit: ततस्तयोः संनिपातस्तुमुलो रोमहर्षणः ।आसीदन्तकरो राजन्वैरस्य तव पुत्रयोः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatastayoḥ saṁnipātastumulo romaharṣaṇaḥ ,āsīdantakaro rājanvairasya tava putrayoḥ.
english: Then, O King, the tumultuous and hair-raising clash of those two (your two sons) was the end of their enmity.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 33 Verse 18. Then, O King, the tumultuous and hair-raising clash of those two (your two sons) was the end of their enmity.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।पूर्वमेव यदा रामस्तस्मिन्युद्ध उपस्थिते ।आमन्त्र्य केशवं यातो वृष्णिभिः सहितः प्रभुः ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,pūrvameva yadā rāmastasminyuddha upasthite ,āmantrya keśavaṁ yāto vṛṣṇibhiḥ sahitaḥ prabhuḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: "When that war was imminent, Lord Rāma, having previously addressed Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), departed accompanied by the Vṛṣṇis."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "When that war was imminent, Lord Rāma, having previously addressed Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), departed accompanied by the Vṛṣṇis."

sanskrit: साहाय्यं धार्तराष्ट्रस्य न च कर्तास्मि केशव ।न चैव पाण्डुपुत्राणां गमिष्यामि यथागतम् ॥२॥
transliteration: sāhāyyaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya na ca kartāsmi keśava ,na caiva pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ gamiṣyāmi yathāgatam.
english: O Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), I will not provide aid to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, nor indeed to Pāṇḍu's sons; I will remain as I came (i.e., neutral).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 2. O Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), I will not provide aid to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's sons, nor indeed to Pāṇḍu's sons; I will remain as I came (i.e., neutral).

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तदा रामो यातः शत्रुनिबर्हणः ।तस्य चागमनं भूयो ब्रह्मञ्शंसितुमर्हसि ॥३॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tadā rāmo yātaḥ śatrunibarhaṇaḥ ,tasya cāgamanaṁ bhūyo brahmañśaṁsitumarhasi.
english: Having thus spoken, Rāma, the slayer of enemies, then departed. O Brāhmaṇa, you should describe his return again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 3. Having thus spoken, Rāma, the slayer of enemies, then departed. O Brāhmaṇa, you should describe his return again.

sanskrit: आख्याहि मे विस्तरतः कथं राम उपस्थितः ।कथं च दृष्टवान्युद्धं कुशलो ह्यसि सत्तम ॥४॥
transliteration: ākhyāhi me vistarataḥ kathaṁ rāma upasthitaḥ ,kathaṁ ca dṛṣṭavānyuddhaṁ kuśalo hyasi sattama.
english: Describe to me in detail how Rāma arrived and how he witnessed the war. Indeed, you are most skillful, O best among men!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 4. Describe to me in detail how Rāma arrived and how he witnessed the war. Indeed, you are most skillful, O best among men!

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उपप्लव्ये निविष्टेषु पाण्डवेषु महात्मसु ।प्रेषितो धृतराष्ट्रस्य समीपं मधुसूदनः ।शमं प्रति महाबाहो हितार्थं सर्वदेहिनाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,upaplavye niviṣṭeṣu pāṇḍaveṣu mahātmasu ,preṣito dhṛtarāṣṭrasya samīpaṁ madhusūdanaḥ ,śamaṁ prati mahābāho hitārthaṁ sarvadehinām.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When the great-souled Pāṇḍavas had settled in Upaplavya, Kṛṣṇa (Madhusūdana) was sent, O mighty-armed king, to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's presence for the sake of peace and the welfare of all embodied beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 5. Vaiśampāyana said: When the great-souled Pāṇḍavas had settled in Upaplavya, Kṛṣṇa (Madhusūdana) was sent, O mighty-armed king, to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's presence for the sake of peace and the welfare of all embodied beings.

sanskrit: स गत्वा हास्तिनपुरं धृतराष्ट्रं समेत्य च ।उक्तवान्वचनं तथ्यं हितं चैव विशेषतः ।न च तत्कृतवान्राजा यथाख्यातं हि ते पुरा ॥६॥
transliteration: sa gatvā hāstinapuraṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ sametya ca ,uktavānvacanaṁ tathyaṁ hitaṁ caiva viśeṣataḥ ,na ca tatkṛtavānrājā yathākhyātaṁ hi te purā.
english: Having gone to Hastināpura and met Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he (Kṛṣṇa) spoke words that were true and particularly beneficial. However, the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) did not act as it was narrated to you previously.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 6. Having gone to Hastināpura and met Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he (Kṛṣṇa) spoke words that were true and particularly beneficial. However, the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) did not act as it was narrated to you previously.

sanskrit: अनवाप्य शमं तत्र कृष्णः पुरुषसत्तमः ।आगच्छत महाबाहुरुपप्लव्यं जनाधिप ॥७॥
transliteration: anavāpya śamaṁ tatra kṛṣṇaḥ puruṣasattamaḥ ,āgacchata mahābāhurupaplavyaṁ janādhipa.
english: O mighty-armed king, Kṛṣṇa, the best among men, not having achieved peace there (in Hastināpura), returned to Upaplavya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 7. O mighty-armed king, Kṛṣṇa, the best among men, not having achieved peace there (in Hastināpura), returned to Upaplavya.

sanskrit: ततः प्रत्यागतः कृष्णो धार्तराष्ट्रविसर्जितः ।अक्रियायां नरव्याघ्र पाण्डवानिदमब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ pratyāgataḥ kṛṣṇo dhārtarāṣṭravisarjitaḥ ,akriyāyāṁ naravyāghra pāṇḍavānidamabravīt.
english: Then Kṛṣṇa, having returned after being dismissed by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, O tiger among men (Janamejaya), said this to the Pāṇḍavas due to (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) inaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 8. Then Kṛṣṇa, having returned after being dismissed by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, O tiger among men (Janamejaya), said this to the Pāṇḍavas due to (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) inaction.

sanskrit: न कुर्वन्ति वचो मह्यं कुरवः कालचोदिताः ।निर्गच्छध्वं पाण्डवेयाः पुष्येण सहिता मया ॥९॥
transliteration: na kurvanti vaco mahyaṁ kuravaḥ kālacoditāḥ ,nirgacchadhvaṁ pāṇḍaveyāḥ puṣyeṇa sahitā mayā.
english: The Kurus (Kauravas), impelled by destiny, do not heed my words. Therefore, O Pāṇḍavas, you should depart accompanied by me and the constellation Puṣya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 9. The Kurus (Kauravas), impelled by destiny, do not heed my words. Therefore, O Pāṇḍavas, you should depart accompanied by me and the constellation Puṣya.

sanskrit: ततो विभज्यमानेषु बलेषु बलिनां वरः ।प्रोवाच भ्रातरं कृष्णं रौहिणेयो महामनाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato vibhajyamāneṣu baleṣu balināṁ varaḥ ,provāca bhrātaraṁ kṛṣṇaṁ rauhiṇeyo mahāmanāḥ.
english: Then, while the armies were being divided, the great-minded son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma), the foremost among the strong, spoke to his brother Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 10. Then, while the armies were being divided, the great-minded son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma), the foremost among the strong, spoke to his brother Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: तेषामपि महाबाहो साहाय्यं मधुसूदन ।क्रियतामिति तत्कृष्णो नास्य चक्रे वचस्तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: teṣāmapi mahābāho sāhāyyaṁ madhusūdana ,kriyatāmiti tatkṛṣṇo nāsya cakre vacastadā.
english: "O mighty-armed (Kṛṣṇa), O slayer of Madhu (Madhusūdana), let help be rendered to them (the Pāṇḍavas) as well," (he said). But Kṛṣṇa then did not heed his (Balarāma's) words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 11. "O mighty-armed (Kṛṣṇa), O slayer of Madhu (Madhusūdana), let help be rendered to them (the Pāṇḍavas) as well," (he said). But Kṛṣṇa then did not heed his (Balarāma's) words.

sanskrit: ततो मन्युपरीतात्मा जगाम यदुनन्दनः ।तीर्थयात्रां हलधरः सरस्वत्यां महायशाः ।मैत्रे नक्षत्रयोगे स्म सहितः सर्वयादवैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tato manyuparītātmā jagāma yadunandanaḥ ,tīrthayātrāṁ haladharaḥ sarasvatyāṁ mahāyaśāḥ ,maitre nakṣatrayoge sma sahitaḥ sarvayādavaiḥ.
english: Then, his mind (ātman) overcome with anger, the greatly renowned son of Yadu (Yadunandana), the wielder of the plough (Haladhara - Balarāma), went on a pilgrimage to the Sarasvatī River, at the auspicious conjunction of the Maitra constellation, accompanied by all the Yadus.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 12. Then, his mind (ātman) overcome with anger, the greatly renowned son of Yadu (Yadunandana), the wielder of the plough (Haladhara - Balarāma), went on a pilgrimage to the Sarasvatī River, at the auspicious conjunction of the Maitra constellation, accompanied by all the Yadus.

sanskrit: आश्रयामास भोजस्तु दुर्योधनमरिंदमः ।युयुधानेन सहितो वासुदेवस्तु पाण्डवान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: āśrayāmāsa bhojastu duryodhanamariṁdamaḥ ,yuyudhānena sahito vāsudevastu pāṇḍavān.
english: King Bhoja, the vanquisher of enemies, took the side of Duryodhana. But Vāsudeva (Krishna), accompanied by Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), sided with the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 13. King Bhoja, the vanquisher of enemies, took the side of Duryodhana. But Vāsudeva (Krishna), accompanied by Yuyudhāna (Sātyaki), sided with the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: रौहिणेये गते शूरे पुष्येण मधुसूदनः ।पाण्डवेयान्पुरस्कृत्य ययावभिमुखः कुरून् ॥१४॥
transliteration: rauhiṇeye gate śūre puṣyeṇa madhusūdanaḥ ,pāṇḍaveyānpuraskṛtya yayāvabhimukhaḥ kurūn.
english: After the brave son of Rohiṇī (Balarama) had departed, Madhusūdana (Krishna), making the Pāṇḍavas his vanguard and under the auspicious constellation (puṣyeṇa), went forth to face the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 14. After the brave son of Rohiṇī (Balarama) had departed, Madhusūdana (Krishna), making the Pāṇḍavas his vanguard and under the auspicious constellation (puṣyeṇa), went forth to face the Kurus.

sanskrit: गच्छन्नेव पथिस्थस्तु रामः प्रेष्यानुवाच ह ।संभारांस्तीर्थयात्रायां सर्वोपकरणानि च ।आनयध्वं द्वारकाया अग्नीन्वै याजकांस्तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: gacchanneva pathisthastu rāmaḥ preṣyānuvāca ha ,saṁbhārāṁstīrthayātrāyāṁ sarvopakaraṇāni ca ,ānayadhvaṁ dvārakāyā agnīnvai yājakāṁstathā.
english: As he was proceeding on the road, Rāma (Balarama) addressed his servants, saying: "Bring immediately from Dvārakā the provisions for the pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā), all necessary equipment, as well as the sacred fires and the officiating priests."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 15. As he was proceeding on the road, Rāma (Balarama) addressed his servants, saying: "Bring immediately from Dvārakā the provisions for the pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā), all necessary equipment, as well as the sacred fires and the officiating priests."

sanskrit: सुवर्णं रजतं चैव धेनूर्वासांसि वाजिनः ।कुञ्जरांश्च रथांश्चैव खरोष्ट्रं वाहनानि च ।क्षिप्रमानीयतां सर्वं तीर्थहेतोः परिच्छदम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: suvarṇaṁ rajataṁ caiva dhenūrvāsāṁsi vājinaḥ ,kuñjarāṁśca rathāṁścaiva kharoṣṭraṁ vāhanāni ca ,kṣipramānīyatāṁ sarvaṁ tīrthahetoḥ paricchadam.
english: "Let all the necessary equipment for the pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā) - including gold, silver, cows, garments, horses, elephants, chariots, donkeys, camels, and other conveyances - be quickly brought forth."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 16. "Let all the necessary equipment for the pilgrimage (tīrthayātrā) - including gold, silver, cows, garments, horses, elephants, chariots, donkeys, camels, and other conveyances - be quickly brought forth."

sanskrit: प्रतिस्रोतः सरस्वत्या गच्छध्वं शीघ्रगामिनः ।ऋत्विजश्चानयध्वं वै शतशश्च द्विजर्षभान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: pratisrotaḥ sarasvatyā gacchadhvaṁ śīghragāminaḥ ,ṛtvijaścānayadhvaṁ vai śataśaśca dvijarṣabhān.
english: O swift-moving ones, go upstream along the Sarasvati (sarasvatī) river. And indeed, bring hundreds of priests and foremost Brahmins (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 17. O swift-moving ones, go upstream along the Sarasvati (sarasvatī) river. And indeed, bring hundreds of priests and foremost Brahmins (dvija).

sanskrit: एवं संदिश्य तु प्रेष्यान्बलदेवो महाबलः ।तीर्थयात्रां ययौ राजन्कुरूणां वैशसे तदा ।सरस्वतीं प्रतिस्रोतः समुद्रादभिजग्मिवान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁdiśya tu preṣyānbaladevo mahābalaḥ ,tīrthayātrāṁ yayau rājankurūṇāṁ vaiśase tadā ,sarasvatīṁ pratisrotaḥ samudrādabhijagmivān.
english: O king (rājan), after thus instructing his messengers, the mighty Baladeva (baladeva) then set out on a pilgrimage to sacred places (tīrthayātrā) at the time of the great slaughter of the Kurus. He travelled upstream along the Sarasvati (sarasvatī) starting from the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 18. O king (rājan), after thus instructing his messengers, the mighty Baladeva (baladeva) then set out on a pilgrimage to sacred places (tīrthayātrā) at the time of the great slaughter of the Kurus. He travelled upstream along the Sarasvati (sarasvatī) starting from the ocean.

sanskrit: ऋत्विग्भिश्च सुहृद्भिश्च तथान्यैर्द्विजसत्तमैः ।रथैर्गजैस्तथाश्वैश्च प्रेष्यैश्च भरतर्षभ ।गोखरोष्ट्रप्रयुक्तैश्च यानैश्च बहुभिर्वृतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ṛtvigbhiśca suhṛdbhiśca tathānyairdvijasattamaiḥ ,rathairgajaistathāśvaiśca preṣyaiśca bharatarṣabha ,gokharoṣṭraprayuktaiśca yānaiśca bahubhirvṛtaḥ.
english: O best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), he was accompanied by priests, by friends, and by other excellent Brahmins (dvija); by chariots, by elephants, by horses, and by messengers; and by many vehicles (yāna) drawn by cows, donkeys, and camels.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 19. O best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), he was accompanied by priests, by friends, and by other excellent Brahmins (dvija); by chariots, by elephants, by horses, and by messengers; and by many vehicles (yāna) drawn by cows, donkeys, and camels.

sanskrit: श्रान्तानां क्लान्तवपुषां शिशूनां विपुलायुषाम् ।तानि यानानि देशेषु प्रतीक्ष्यन्ते स्म भारत ।बुभुक्षितानामर्थाय कॢप्तमन्नं समन्ततः ॥२०॥
transliteration: śrāntānāṁ klāntavapuṣāṁ śiśūnāṁ vipulāyuṣām ,tāni yānāni deśeṣu pratīkṣyante sma bhārata ,bubhukṣitānāmarthāya kḷptamannaṁ samantataḥ.
english: O Bhārata (bhārata), those vehicles (yāna) were kept ready in various places for those who were tired, those with weary bodies, the children, and the aged. Food was also prepared everywhere for the hungry.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 20. O Bhārata (bhārata), those vehicles (yāna) were kept ready in various places for those who were tired, those with weary bodies, the children, and the aged. Food was also prepared everywhere for the hungry.

sanskrit: यो यो यत्र द्विजो भोक्तुं कामं कामयते तदा ।तस्य तस्य तु तत्रैवमुपजह्रुस्तदा नृप ॥२१॥
transliteration: yo yo yatra dvijo bhoktuṁ kāmaṁ kāmayate tadā ,tasya tasya tu tatraivamupajahrustadā nṛpa.
english: O King, at that time, whichever twice-born (dvija) person desired to eat whatever they wished, to each of them it was brought right there, in that very manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 21. O King, at that time, whichever twice-born (dvija) person desired to eat whatever they wished, to each of them it was brought right there, in that very manner.

sanskrit: तत्र स्थिता नरा राजन्रौहिणेयस्य शासनात् ।भक्ष्यपेयस्य कुर्वन्ति राशींस्तत्र समन्ततः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatra sthitā narā rājanrauhiṇeyasya śāsanāt ,bhakṣyapeyasya kurvanti rāśīṁstatra samantataḥ.
english: O King, the men standing there, by the command of Rohiṇeya, were making piles of food and drink all around in that place.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 22. O King, the men standing there, by the command of Rohiṇeya, were making piles of food and drink all around in that place.

sanskrit: वासांसि च महार्हाणि पर्यङ्कास्तरणानि च ।पूजार्थं तत्र कॢप्तानि विप्राणां सुखमिच्छताम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: vāsāṁsi ca mahārhāṇi paryaṅkāstaraṇāni ca ,pūjārthaṁ tatra kḷptāni viprāṇāṁ sukhamicchatām.
english: And there, valuable garments and couch coverings were arranged for the honor of the brahmins (vipra) who desired comfort.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 23. And there, valuable garments and couch coverings were arranged for the honor of the brahmins (vipra) who desired comfort.

sanskrit: यत्र यः स्वपते विप्रः क्षत्रियो वापि भारत ।तत्र तत्र तु तस्यैव सर्वं कॢप्तमदृश्यत ॥२४॥
transliteration: yatra yaḥ svapate vipraḥ kṣatriyo vāpi bhārata ,tatra tatra tu tasyaiva sarvaṁ kḷptamadṛśyata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, wherever any brahmin (vipra) or kṣatriya slept, everything arranged for him alone was seen there, in that very spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 24. O descendant of Bharata, wherever any brahmin (vipra) or kṣatriya slept, everything arranged for him alone was seen there, in that very spot.

sanskrit: यथासुखं जनः सर्वस्तिष्ठते याति वा तदा ।यातुकामस्य यानानि पानानि तृषितस्य च ॥२५॥
transliteration: yathāsukhaṁ janaḥ sarvastiṣṭhate yāti vā tadā ,yātukāmasya yānāni pānāni tṛṣitasya ca.
english: Then, all the people stayed or departed as they pleased. For those wishing to travel, there were vehicles, and for the thirsty, refreshing drinks.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 25. Then, all the people stayed or departed as they pleased. For those wishing to travel, there were vehicles, and for the thirsty, refreshing drinks.

sanskrit: बुभुक्षितस्य चान्नानि स्वादूनि भरतर्षभ ।उपजह्रुर्नरास्तत्र वस्त्राण्याभरणानि च ॥२६॥
transliteration: bubhukṣitasya cānnāni svādūni bharatarṣabha ,upajahrurnarāstatra vastrāṇyābharaṇāni ca.
english: And for the hungry, delicious foods were brought by people there, along with clothes and ornaments, O best of the Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 26. And for the hungry, delicious foods were brought by people there, along with clothes and ornaments, O best of the Bharatas.

sanskrit: स पन्थाः प्रबभौ राजन्सर्वस्यैव सुखावहः ।स्वर्गोपमस्तदा वीर नराणां तत्र गच्छताम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa panthāḥ prababhau rājansarvasyaiva sukhāvahaḥ ,svargopamastadā vīra narāṇāṁ tatra gacchatām.
english: That path, O King, shone brilliantly, bringing happiness to everyone. It was like heaven, O hero, for the people traveling there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 27. That path, O King, shone brilliantly, bringing happiness to everyone. It was like heaven, O hero, for the people traveling there.

sanskrit: नित्यप्रमुदितोपेतः स्वादुभक्षः शुभान्वितः ।विपण्यापणपण्यानां नानाजनशतैर्वृतः ।नानाद्रुमलतोपेतो नानारत्नविभूषितः ॥२८॥
transliteration: nityapramuditopetaḥ svādubhakṣaḥ śubhānvitaḥ ,vipaṇyāpaṇapaṇyānāṁ nānājanaśatairvṛtaḥ ,nānādrumalatopeto nānāratnavibhūṣitaḥ.
english: It (the path) was always filled with joyful people, offering delicious food, and endowed with auspiciousness. It was surrounded by hundreds of various people selling market goods, replete with diverse trees and vines, and adorned with many kinds of jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 28. It (the path) was always filled with joyful people, offering delicious food, and endowed with auspiciousness. It was surrounded by hundreds of various people selling market goods, replete with diverse trees and vines, and adorned with many kinds of jewels.

sanskrit: ततो महात्मा नियमे स्थितात्मा पुण्येषु तीर्थेषु वसूनि राजन् ।ददौ द्विजेभ्यः क्रतुदक्षिणाश्च यदुप्रवीरो हलभृत्प्रतीतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tato mahātmā niyame sthitātmā; puṇyeṣu tīrtheṣu vasūni rājan ,dadau dvijebhyaḥ kratudakṣiṇāśca; yadupravīro halabhṛtpratītaḥ.
english: Then, O King, the great-souled one, Balarama (halabhṛt), the chief among the Yadus, renowned and steadfast in self-restraint (niyama), gave abundant wealth and sacrificial fees to the brahmins at various sacred pilgrimage sites.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 29. Then, O King, the great-souled one, Balarama (halabhṛt), the chief among the Yadus, renowned and steadfast in self-restraint (niyama), gave abundant wealth and sacrificial fees to the brahmins at various sacred pilgrimage sites.

sanskrit: दोग्ध्रीश्च धेनूश्च सहस्रशो वै सुवाससः काञ्चनबद्धशृङ्गीः ।हयांश्च नानाविधदेशजातान्यानानि दासीश्च तथा द्विजेभ्यः ॥३०॥
transliteration: dogdhrīśca dhenūśca sahasraśo vai; suvāsasaḥ kāñcanabaddhaśṛṅgīḥ ,hayāṁśca nānāvidhadeśajātā;nyānāni dāsīśca tathā dvijebhyaḥ.
english: And indeed, to the brahmins he gave thousands of milk-giving cows, beautifully adorned and with horns bound in gold. Similarly, he gave horses born in various countries, as well as conveyances and maidservants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 30. And indeed, to the brahmins he gave thousands of milk-giving cows, beautifully adorned and with horns bound in gold. Similarly, he gave horses born in various countries, as well as conveyances and maidservants.

sanskrit: रत्नानि मुक्तामणिविद्रुमं च शृङ्गीसुवर्णं रजतं च शुभ्रम् ।अयस्मयं ताम्रमयं च भाण्डं ददौ द्विजातिप्रवरेषु रामः ॥३१॥
transliteration: ratnāni muktāmaṇividrumaṁ ca; śṛṅgīsuvarṇaṁ rajataṁ ca śubhram ,ayasmayaṁ tāmramayaṁ ca bhāṇḍaṁ; dadau dvijātipravareṣu rāmaḥ.
english: Rama (Balarama) gave jewels, pearls and coral, pure gold (śṛṅgīsuvarṇam), shining silver, and vessels made of iron and copper, to the foremost among the twice-born (dvijāti).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 31. Rama (Balarama) gave jewels, pearls and coral, pure gold (śṛṅgīsuvarṇam), shining silver, and vessels made of iron and copper, to the foremost among the twice-born (dvijāti).

sanskrit: एवं स वित्तं प्रददौ महात्मा सरस्वतीतीर्थवरेषु भूरि ।ययौ क्रमेणाप्रतिमप्रभावस्ततः कुरुक्षेत्रमुदारवृत्तः ॥३२॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa vittaṁ pradadau mahātmā; sarasvatītīrthavareṣu bhūri ,yayau krameṇāpratimaprabhāva;stataḥ kurukṣetramudāravṛttaḥ.
english: Thus, that great-souled one (mahātmā), of incomparable power and noble conduct, abundantly bestowed wealth at the foremost pilgrimage sites of the Sarasvati. Then, in due course, he proceeded from there to Kurukshetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 32. Thus, that great-souled one (mahātmā), of incomparable power and noble conduct, abundantly bestowed wealth at the foremost pilgrimage sites of the Sarasvati. Then, in due course, he proceeded from there to Kurukshetra.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।सारस्वतानां तीर्थानां गुणोत्पत्तिं वदस्व मे ।फलं च द्विपदां श्रेष्ठ कर्मनिर्वृत्तिमेव च ॥३३॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,sārasvatānāṁ tīrthānāṁ guṇotpattiṁ vadasva me ,phalaṁ ca dvipadāṁ śreṣṭha karmanirvṛttimeva ca.
english: Janamejaya said: "O best among bipeds, please describe to me the origin of the merits associated with the sacred places (tīrtha) of the Sarasvatī river, as well as the benefits derived from them, and the successful completion of rituals (karma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 33. Janamejaya said: "O best among bipeds, please describe to me the origin of the merits associated with the sacred places (tīrtha) of the Sarasvatī river, as well as the benefits derived from them, and the successful completion of rituals (karma)."

sanskrit: यथाक्रमं च भगवंस्तीर्थानामनुपूर्वशः ।ब्रह्मन्ब्रह्मविदां श्रेष्ठ परं कौतूहलं हि मे ॥३४॥
transliteration: yathākramaṁ ca bhagavaṁstīrthānāmanupūrvaśaḥ ,brahmanbrahmavidāṁ śreṣṭha paraṁ kautūhalaṁ hi me.
english: Janamejaya continued: "O revered one (bhagavan), and O Brāhmaṇa, O best among the knowers of the Absolute (brahman), please tell me about these sacred places (tīrtha) in their proper sequence and order. Indeed, my curiosity is immense."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 34. Janamejaya continued: "O revered one (bhagavan), and O Brāhmaṇa, O best among the knowers of the Absolute (brahman), please tell me about these sacred places (tīrtha) in their proper sequence and order. Indeed, my curiosity is immense."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तीर्थानां विस्तरं राजन्गुणोत्पत्तिं च सर्वशः ।मयोच्यमानां शृणु वै पुण्यां राजेन्द्र कृत्स्नशः ॥३५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tīrthānāṁ vistaraṁ rājanguṇotpattiṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,mayocyamānāṁ śṛṇu vai puṇyāṁ rājendra kṛtsnaśaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O King (rājan), O lord of kings (rājendra), listen carefully and completely to the extensive account of these sacred places (tīrtha) and the origin of their merits, which I am about to narrate."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 35. Vaiśampāyana said: "O King (rājan), O lord of kings (rājendra), listen carefully and completely to the extensive account of these sacred places (tīrtha) and the origin of their merits, which I am about to narrate."

sanskrit: पूर्वं महाराज यदुप्रवीर ऋत्विक्सुहृद्विप्रगणैश्च सार्धम् ।पुण्यं प्रभासं समुपाजगाम यत्रोडुराड्यक्ष्मणा क्लिश्यमानः ॥३६॥
transliteration: pūrvaṁ mahārāja yadupravīra; ṛtviksuhṛdvipragaṇaiśca sārdham ,puṇyaṁ prabhāsaṁ samupājagāma; yatroḍurāḍyakṣmaṇā kliśyamānaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana continued: "O great King (mahārāja), O chief of the Yadus (yadupravīra), in ancient times, accompanied by priests (ṛtvik), friends, and groups of Brāhmaṇas, he approached the sacred Prabhasa (tīrtha), where the Moon-god (uḍurāṭ) was afflicted by consumption."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 36. Vaiśampāyana continued: "O great King (mahārāja), O chief of the Yadus (yadupravīra), in ancient times, accompanied by priests (ṛtvik), friends, and groups of Brāhmaṇas, he approached the sacred Prabhasa (tīrtha), where the Moon-god (uḍurāṭ) was afflicted by consumption."

sanskrit: विमुक्तशापः पुनराप्य तेजः सर्वं जगद्भासयते नरेन्द्र ।एवं तु तीर्थप्रवरं पृथिव्यां प्रभासनात्तस्य ततः प्रभासः ॥३७॥
transliteration: vimuktaśāpaḥ punarāpya tejaḥ; sarvaṁ jagadbhāsayate narendra ,evaṁ tu tīrthapravaraṁ pṛthivyāṁ; prabhāsanāttasya tataḥ prabhāsaḥ.
english: O king, having been freed from the curse, it regained its splendor and illuminates the entire world. Thus, because of its shining forth, that foremost holy place on earth is called Prabhasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 37. O king, having been freed from the curse, it regained its splendor and illuminates the entire world. Thus, because of its shining forth, that foremost holy place on earth is called Prabhasa.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।किमर्थं भगवान्सोमो यक्ष्मणा समगृह्यत ।कथं च तीर्थप्रवरे तस्मिंश्चन्द्रो न्यमज्जत ॥३८॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kimarthaṁ bhagavānsomo yakṣmaṇā samagṛhyata ,kathaṁ ca tīrthapravare tasmiṁścandro nyamajjata.
english: Janamejaya asked, 'For what reason was the revered Soma afflicted by consumption (yakṣman)? And how did the Moon immerse itself in that foremost holy place?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 38. Janamejaya asked, 'For what reason was the revered Soma afflicted by consumption (yakṣman)? And how did the Moon immerse itself in that foremost holy place?'

sanskrit: कथमाप्लुत्य तस्मिंस्तु पुनराप्यायितः शशी ।एतन्मे सर्वमाचक्ष्व विस्तरेण महामुने ॥३९॥
transliteration: kathamāplutya tasmiṁstu punarāpyāyitaḥ śaśī ,etanme sarvamācakṣva vistareṇa mahāmune.
english: How was the Moon, having bathed in that very place, restored again? Please recount all this to me in detail, O great sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 39. How was the Moon, having bathed in that very place, restored again? Please recount all this to me in detail, O great sage.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।दक्षस्य तनया यास्ताः प्रादुरासन्विशां पते ।स सप्तविंशतिं कन्या दक्षः सोमाय वै ददौ ॥४०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dakṣasya tanayā yāstāḥ prādurāsanviśāṁ pate ,sa saptaviṁśatiṁ kanyā dakṣaḥ somāya vai dadau.
english: Vaiśampāyana said, 'O lord of the people, Dakṣa had daughters who were born; he indeed gave twenty-seven of these daughters to Soma.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 40. Vaiśampāyana said, 'O lord of the people, Dakṣa had daughters who were born; he indeed gave twenty-seven of these daughters to Soma.'

sanskrit: नक्षत्रयोगनिरताः संख्यानार्थं च भारत ।पत्न्यो वै तस्य राजेन्द्र सोमस्य शुभलक्षणाः ॥४१॥
transliteration: nakṣatrayoganiratāḥ saṁkhyānārthaṁ ca bhārata ,patnyo vai tasya rājendra somasya śubhalakṣaṇāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, O King of kings, the wives of Soma (the Moon god), who were endowed with auspicious signs, were dedicated to the conjunctions of the lunar mansions (nakṣatra) for the purpose of calculation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 41. O Bhārata, O King of kings, the wives of Soma (the Moon god), who were endowed with auspicious signs, were dedicated to the conjunctions of the lunar mansions (nakṣatra) for the purpose of calculation.

sanskrit: तास्तु सर्वा विशालाक्ष्यो रूपेणाप्रतिमा भुवि ।अत्यरिच्यत तासां तु रोहिणी रूपसंपदा ॥४२॥
transliteration: tāstu sarvā viśālākṣyo rūpeṇāpratimā bhuvi ,atyaricyata tāsāṁ tu rohiṇī rūpasaṁpadā.
english: All of those broad-eyed women were indeed incomparable in beauty on earth. However, among them, Rohiṇī particularly excelled in her abundance of beauty.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 42. All of those broad-eyed women were indeed incomparable in beauty on earth. However, among them, Rohiṇī particularly excelled in her abundance of beauty.

sanskrit: ततस्तस्यां स भगवान्प्रीतिं चक्रे निशाकरः ।सास्य हृद्या बभूवाथ तस्मात्तां बुभुजे सदा ॥४३॥
transliteration: tatastasyāṁ sa bhagavānprītiṁ cakre niśākaraḥ ,sāsya hṛdyā babhūvātha tasmāttāṁ bubhuje sadā.
english: Then, that venerable lord, the Moon god (niśākara), conceived great affection for her. She became dear to his heart, and therefore, he always enjoyed her company.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 43. Then, that venerable lord, the Moon god (niśākara), conceived great affection for her. She became dear to his heart, and therefore, he always enjoyed her company.

sanskrit: पुरा हि सोमो राजेन्द्र रोहिण्यामवसच्चिरम् ।ततोऽस्य कुपितान्यासन्नक्षत्राणि महात्मनः ॥४४॥
transliteration: purā hi somo rājendra rohiṇyāmavasacciram ,tato'sya kupitānyāsannakṣatrāṇi mahātmanaḥ.
english: Indeed, in the past, O King of kings, Soma resided for a long time exclusively with Rohiṇī. Consequently, the other lunar mansions (nakṣatra) belonging to that great being (ātman) became angry with him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 44. Indeed, in the past, O King of kings, Soma resided for a long time exclusively with Rohiṇī. Consequently, the other lunar mansions (nakṣatra) belonging to that great being (ātman) became angry with him.

sanskrit: ता गत्वा पितरं प्राहुः प्रजापतिमतन्द्रिताः ।सोमो वसति नास्मासु रोहिणीं भजते सदा ॥४५॥
transliteration: tā gatvā pitaraṁ prāhuḥ prajāpatimatandritāḥ ,somo vasati nāsmāsu rohiṇīṁ bhajate sadā.
english: Unwearied, they went to their father, Prajāpati, and said: 'Soma does not reside with us; he always favors Rohiṇī.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 45. Unwearied, they went to their father, Prajāpati, and said: 'Soma does not reside with us; he always favors Rohiṇī.'

sanskrit: ता वयं सहिताः सर्वास्त्वत्सकाशे प्रजेश्वर ।वत्स्यामो नियताहारास्तपश्चरणतत्पराः ॥४६॥
transliteration: tā vayaṁ sahitāḥ sarvāstvatsakāśe prajeśvara ,vatsyāmo niyatāhārāstapaścaraṇatatparāḥ.
english: O Lord of creatures (prajā-īśvara), all of us together, with regulated diets and intent on performing austerities (tapas), will reside in your presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 46. O Lord of creatures (prajā-īśvara), all of us together, with regulated diets and intent on performing austerities (tapas), will reside in your presence.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तासां तु वचनं दक्षः सोममथाब्रवीत् ।समं वर्तस्व भार्यासु मा त्वाधर्मो महान्स्पृशेत् ॥४७॥
transliteration: śrutvā tāsāṁ tu vacanaṁ dakṣaḥ somamathābravīt ,samaṁ vartasva bhāryāsu mā tvādharmo mahānspṛśet.
english: Indeed, having heard their words, Dakṣa then said to Soma: 'Behave impartially towards your wives, lest great unrighteousness (adharma) befall you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 47. Indeed, having heard their words, Dakṣa then said to Soma: 'Behave impartially towards your wives, lest great unrighteousness (adharma) befall you.'

sanskrit: ताश्च सर्वाब्रवीद्दक्षो गच्छध्वं सोममन्तिकात् ।समं वत्स्यति सर्वासु चन्द्रमा मम शासनात् ॥४८॥
transliteration: tāśca sarvābravīddakṣo gacchadhvaṁ somamantikāt ,samaṁ vatsyati sarvāsu candramā mama śāsanāt.
english: And Dakṣa said to all of them: 'Go to Soma from here! The Moon (candramā) will reside equally among all of you by my command.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 48. And Dakṣa said to all of them: 'Go to Soma from here! The Moon (candramā) will reside equally among all of you by my command.'

sanskrit: विसृष्टास्तास्तदा जग्मुः शीतांशुभवनं तदा ।तथापि सोमो भगवान्पुनरेव महीपते ।रोहिणीं निवसत्येव प्रीयमाणो मुहुर्मुहुः ॥४९॥
transliteration: visṛṣṭāstāstadā jagmuḥ śītāṁśubhavanaṁ tadā ,tathāpi somo bhagavānpunareva mahīpate ,rohiṇīṁ nivasatyeva prīyamāṇo muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Having been dismissed, they then went to the abode of the Moon. Nevertheless, O King, the venerable Soma repeatedly delighted in dwelling with Rohini alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 49. Having been dismissed, they then went to the abode of the Moon. Nevertheless, O King, the venerable Soma repeatedly delighted in dwelling with Rohini alone.

sanskrit: ततस्ताः सहिताः सर्वा भूयः पितरमब्रुवन् ।तव शुश्रूषणे युक्ता वत्स्यामो हि तवाश्रमे ।सोमो वसति नास्मासु नाकरोद्वचनं तव ॥५०॥
transliteration: tatastāḥ sahitāḥ sarvā bhūyaḥ pitaramabruvan ,tava śuśrūṣaṇe yuktā vatsyāmo hi tavāśrame ,somo vasati nāsmāsu nākarodvacanaṁ tava.
english: Then all of them, united, spoke again to their father: 'We shall indeed dwell in your hermitage (āśrama), engaged in serving you. Soma does not reside with us, nor did he follow your word.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 50. Then all of them, united, spoke again to their father: 'We shall indeed dwell in your hermitage (āśrama), engaged in serving you. Soma does not reside with us, nor did he follow your word.'

sanskrit: तासां तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा दक्षः सोममथाब्रवीत् ।समं वर्तस्व भार्यासु मा त्वां शप्स्ये विरोचन ॥५१॥
transliteration: tāsāṁ tadvacanaṁ śrutvā dakṣaḥ somamathābravīt ,samaṁ vartasva bhāryāsu mā tvāṁ śapsye virocana.
english: Having heard their words, Daksha then spoke to Soma: 'Behave equally towards your wives, lest I curse you, O Virocana!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 51. Having heard their words, Daksha then spoke to Soma: 'Behave equally towards your wives, lest I curse you, O Virocana!'

sanskrit: अनादृत्य तु तद्वाक्यं दक्षस्य भगवाञ्शशी ।रोहिण्या सार्धमवसत्ततस्ताः कुपिताः पुनः ॥५२॥
transliteration: anādṛtya tu tadvākyaṁ dakṣasya bhagavāñśaśī ,rohiṇyā sārdhamavasattatastāḥ kupitāḥ punaḥ.
english: But the venerable Moon, disregarding that command of Daksha, continued to dwell with Rohini. Thereupon, they (the other wives) became angry again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 52. But the venerable Moon, disregarding that command of Daksha, continued to dwell with Rohini. Thereupon, they (the other wives) became angry again.

sanskrit: गत्वा च पितरं प्राहुः प्रणम्य शिरसा तदा ।सोमो वसति नास्मासु तस्मान्नः शरणं भव ॥५३॥
transliteration: gatvā ca pitaraṁ prāhuḥ praṇamya śirasā tadā ,somo vasati nāsmāsu tasmānnaḥ śaraṇaṁ bhava.
english: And having gone to their father, they bowed with their heads and then said: "The Moon (Soma) does not reside with us. Therefore, be our refuge."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 53. And having gone to their father, they bowed with their heads and then said: "The Moon (Soma) does not reside with us. Therefore, be our refuge."

sanskrit: रोहिण्यामेव भगवन्सदा वसति चन्द्रमाः ।तस्मान्नस्त्राहि सर्वा वै यथा नः सोम आविशेत् ॥५४॥
transliteration: rohiṇyāmeva bhagavansadā vasati candramāḥ ,tasmānnastrāhi sarvā vai yathā naḥ soma āviśet.
english: "O Lord, the Moon (candra) always resides only in Rohiṇī. Therefore, protect all of us, so that the Moon (Soma) may visit us."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 54. "O Lord, the Moon (candra) always resides only in Rohiṇī. Therefore, protect all of us, so that the Moon (Soma) may visit us."

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा भगवान्क्रुद्धो यक्ष्माणं पृथिवीपते ।ससर्ज रोषात्सोमाय स चोडुपतिमाविशत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā bhagavānkruddho yakṣmāṇaṁ pṛthivīpate ,sasarja roṣātsomāya sa coḍupatimāviśat.
english: Having heard that, the Lord Dakṣa became enraged, O king. Out of anger, he released the disease of consumption (yakṣman) upon the Moon (Soma), and it entered the lord of stars (uḍupati), the Moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 55. Having heard that, the Lord Dakṣa became enraged, O king. Out of anger, he released the disease of consumption (yakṣman) upon the Moon (Soma), and it entered the lord of stars (uḍupati), the Moon.

sanskrit: स यक्ष्मणाभिभूतात्माक्षीयताहरहः शशी ।यत्नं चाप्यकरोद्राजन्मोक्षार्थं तस्य यक्ष्मणः ॥५६॥
transliteration: sa yakṣmaṇābhibhūtātmākṣīyatāharahaḥ śaśī ,yatnaṁ cāpyakarodrājanmokṣārthaṁ tasya yakṣmaṇaḥ.
english: That Moon (śaśin), whose very being (ātman) was overwhelmed by consumption (yakṣman), wasted away day by day. And he, O King, made efforts for the liberation (mokṣa) from that disease.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 56. That Moon (śaśin), whose very being (ātman) was overwhelmed by consumption (yakṣman), wasted away day by day. And he, O King, made efforts for the liberation (mokṣa) from that disease.

sanskrit: इष्ट्वेष्टिभिर्महाराज विविधाभिर्निशाकरः ।न चामुच्यत शापाद्वै क्षयं चैवाभ्यगच्छत ॥५७॥
transliteration: iṣṭveṣṭibhirmahārāja vividhābhirniśākaraḥ ,na cāmucyata śāpādvai kṣayaṁ caivābhyagacchata.
english: O great king, even though the Moon god (niśākara) performed various sacrifices, he was not released from the curse and indeed met with decline.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 57. O great king, even though the Moon god (niśākara) performed various sacrifices, he was not released from the curse and indeed met with decline.

sanskrit: क्षीयमाणे ततः सोमे ओषध्यो न प्रजज्ञिरे ।निरास्वादरसाः सर्वा हतवीर्याश्च सर्वशः ॥५८॥
transliteration: kṣīyamāṇe tataḥ some oṣadhyo na prajajñire ,nirāsvādarasāḥ sarvā hatavīryāśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, as the Moon god (soma) began to wane, medicinal plants (oṣadhi) ceased to grow. All of them became completely devoid of taste and essence, and entirely lost their potency.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 58. Then, as the Moon god (soma) began to wane, medicinal plants (oṣadhi) ceased to grow. All of them became completely devoid of taste and essence, and entirely lost their potency.

sanskrit: ओषधीनां क्षये जाते प्राणिनामपि संक्षयः ।कृशाश्चासन्प्रजाः सर्वाः क्षीयमाणे निशाकरे ॥५९॥
transliteration: oṣadhīnāṁ kṣaye jāte prāṇināmapi saṁkṣayaḥ ,kṛśāścāsanprajāḥ sarvāḥ kṣīyamāṇe niśākare.
english: When the plants (oṣadhi) began to diminish, there was also a complete decline of all living beings. All creatures became weak as the Moon god (niśākara) continued to wane.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 59. When the plants (oṣadhi) began to diminish, there was also a complete decline of all living beings. All creatures became weak as the Moon god (niśākara) continued to wane.

sanskrit: ततो देवाः समागम्य सोममूचुर्महीपते ।किमिदं भवतो रूपमीदृशं न प्रकाशते ॥६०॥
transliteration: tato devāḥ samāgamya somamūcurmahīpate ,kimidaṁ bhavato rūpamīdṛśaṁ na prakāśate.
english: Then, having gathered, the gods said to Soma (soma), 'O king, what is this form of yours? It does not shine like this (as it used to).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 60. Then, having gathered, the gods said to Soma (soma), 'O king, what is this form of yours? It does not shine like this (as it used to).'

sanskrit: कारणं ब्रूहि नः सर्वं येनेदं ते महद्भयम् ।श्रुत्वा तु वचनं त्वत्तो विधास्यामस्ततो वयम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: kāraṇaṁ brūhi naḥ sarvaṁ yenedaṁ te mahadbhayam ,śrutvā tu vacanaṁ tvatto vidhāsyāmastato vayam.
english: Tell us the complete reason for this great fear you are experiencing. After hearing your words, we will then determine what to do.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 61. Tell us the complete reason for this great fear you are experiencing. After hearing your words, we will then determine what to do.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः प्रत्युवाच सर्वांस्ताञ्शशलक्षणः ।शापं च कारणं चैव यक्ष्माणं च तथात्मनः ॥६२॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ pratyuvāca sarvāṁstāñśaśalakṣaṇaḥ ,śāpaṁ ca kāraṇaṁ caiva yakṣmāṇaṁ ca tathātmanaḥ.
english: Thus addressed, Soma, the one marked by a hare, replied to all of them, explaining the curse, its cause, and also the wasting disease (yakṣman) affecting his own being (ātman).
