search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 62. Thus addressed, Soma, the one marked by a hare, replied to all of them, explaining the curse, its cause, and also the wasting disease (yakṣman) affecting his own being (ātman).

sanskrit: देवास्तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा गत्वा दक्षमथाब्रुवन् ।प्रसीद भगवन्सोमे शापश्चैष निवर्त्यताम् ॥६३॥
transliteration: devāstasya vacaḥ śrutvā gatvā dakṣamathābruvan ,prasīda bhagavansome śāpaścaiṣa nivartyatām.
english: Having heard Soma's words, the gods then went to Dakṣa and pleaded, 'O venerable one (Dakṣa), be gracious concerning Soma! And let this curse be revoked.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 63. Having heard Soma's words, the gods then went to Dakṣa and pleaded, 'O venerable one (Dakṣa), be gracious concerning Soma! And let this curse be revoked.'

sanskrit: असौ हि चन्द्रमाः क्षीणः किंचिच्छेषो हि लक्ष्यते ।क्षयाच्चैवास्य देवेश प्रजाश्चापि गताः क्षयम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: asau hi candramāḥ kṣīṇaḥ kiṁciccheṣo hi lakṣyate ,kṣayāccaivāsya deveśa prajāścāpi gatāḥ kṣayam.
english: Indeed, this moon (Soma) has wasted away; only a small part of him is now visible. And because of his decline, O Lord of gods (Dakṣa), creatures are also facing destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 64. Indeed, this moon (Soma) has wasted away; only a small part of him is now visible. And because of his decline, O Lord of gods (Dakṣa), creatures are also facing destruction.

sanskrit: वीरुदोषधयश्चैव बीजानि विविधानि च ।तथा वयं लोकगुरो प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥६५॥
transliteration: vīrudoṣadhayaścaiva bījāni vividhāni ca ,tathā vayaṁ lokaguro prasādaṁ kartumarhasi.
english: O teacher of the world (lokaguru), just as there are creepers, medicinal plants, and various seeds, so too are we. Therefore, you should grant us your favor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 65. O teacher of the world (lokaguru), just as there are creepers, medicinal plants, and various seeds, so too are we. Therefore, you should grant us your favor.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तदा चिन्त्य प्राह वाक्यं प्रजापतिः ।नैतच्छक्यं मम वचो व्यावर्तयितुमन्यथा ।हेतुना तु महाभागा निवर्तिष्यति केनचित् ॥६६॥
transliteration: evamuktastadā cintya prāha vākyaṁ prajāpatiḥ ,naitacchakyaṁ mama vaco vyāvartayitumanyathā ,hetunā tu mahābhāgā nivartiṣyati kenacit.
english: Having been addressed in this way, Prajapati then pondered and spoke these words: 'This decree (vacas) of mine cannot be reversed in any other way. However, O highly fortunate ones, it will certainly cease due to some reason.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 66. Having been addressed in this way, Prajapati then pondered and spoke these words: 'This decree (vacas) of mine cannot be reversed in any other way. However, O highly fortunate ones, it will certainly cease due to some reason.'

sanskrit: समं वर्ततु सर्वासु शशी भार्यासु नित्यशः ।सरस्वत्या वरे तीर्थे उन्मज्जञ्शशलक्षणः ।पुनर्वर्धिष्यते देवास्तद्वै सत्यं वचो मम ॥६७॥
transliteration: samaṁ vartatu sarvāsu śaśī bhāryāsu nityaśaḥ ,sarasvatyā vare tīrthe unmajjañśaśalakṣaṇaḥ ,punarvardhiṣyate devāstadvai satyaṁ vaco mama.
english: Let the Moon (Śaśī) always treat all his wives equally. O gods, the one marked by a hare (śaśalakṣaṇaḥ), by immersing himself in the excellent holy place (tīrtha) associated with Sarasvati, will again flourish. This indeed is my truthful word.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 67. Let the Moon (Śaśī) always treat all his wives equally. O gods, the one marked by a hare (śaśalakṣaṇaḥ), by immersing himself in the excellent holy place (tīrtha) associated with Sarasvati, will again flourish. This indeed is my truthful word.

sanskrit: मासार्धं च क्षयं सोमो नित्यमेव गमिष्यति ।मासार्धं च सदा वृद्धिं सत्यमेतद्वचो मम ॥६८॥
transliteration: māsārdhaṁ ca kṣayaṁ somo nityameva gamiṣyati ,māsārdhaṁ ca sadā vṛddhiṁ satyametadvaco mama.
english: For half a month, the Moon (Soma) will always go into decline, and for half a month, he will always grow. This indeed is my truthful word.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 68. For half a month, the Moon (Soma) will always go into decline, and for half a month, he will always grow. This indeed is my truthful word.

sanskrit: सरस्वतीं ततः सोमो जगाम ऋषिशासनात् ।प्रभासं परमं तीर्थं सरस्वत्या जगाम ह ॥६९॥
transliteration: sarasvatīṁ tataḥ somo jagāma ṛṣiśāsanāt ,prabhāsaṁ paramaṁ tīrthaṁ sarasvatyā jagāma ha.
english: Then Soma, by the command of the sages, went to Sarasvati. Indeed, he went to Prabhasa, a supreme holy place situated along the Sarasvati river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 69. Then Soma, by the command of the sages, went to Sarasvati. Indeed, he went to Prabhasa, a supreme holy place situated along the Sarasvati river.

sanskrit: अमावास्यां महातेजास्तत्रोन्मज्जन्महाद्युतिः ।लोकान्प्रभासयामास शीतांशुत्वमवाप च ॥७०॥
transliteration: amāvāsyāṁ mahātejāstatronmajjanmahādyutiḥ ,lokānprabhāsayāmāsa śītāṁśutvamavāpa ca.
english: On the new moon day, the greatly effulgent and highly radiant Soma, by immersing himself there, illuminated the worlds and attained his cool-rayed nature (śītāṃśutva).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 70. On the new moon day, the greatly effulgent and highly radiant Soma, by immersing himself there, illuminated the worlds and attained his cool-rayed nature (śītāṃśutva).

sanskrit: देवाश्च सर्वे राजेन्द्र प्रभासं प्राप्य पुष्कलम् ।सोमेन सहिता भूत्वा दक्षस्य प्रमुखेऽभवन् ॥७१॥
transliteration: devāśca sarve rājendra prabhāsaṁ prāpya puṣkalam ,somena sahitā bhūtvā dakṣasya pramukhe'bhavan.
english: O King of kings, all the gods, having arrived at the excellent Prabhasa and being united with Soma, then appeared before Daksha.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 71. O King of kings, all the gods, having arrived at the excellent Prabhasa and being united with Soma, then appeared before Daksha.

sanskrit: ततः प्रजापतिः सर्वा विससर्जाथ देवताः ।सोमं च भगवान्प्रीतो भूयो वचनमब्रवीत् ॥७२॥
transliteration: tataḥ prajāpatiḥ sarvā visasarjātha devatāḥ ,somaṁ ca bhagavānprīto bhūyo vacanamabravīt.
english: Then Prajapati dismissed all the deities. And the divine (Bhagavān) Prajapati, being pleased, spoke further words to Soma.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 72. Then Prajapati dismissed all the deities. And the divine (Bhagavān) Prajapati, being pleased, spoke further words to Soma.

sanskrit: मावमंस्थाः स्त्रियः पुत्र मा च विप्रान्कदाचन ।गच्छ युक्तः सदा भूत्वा कुरु वै शासनं मम ॥७३॥
transliteration: māvamaṁsthāḥ striyaḥ putra mā ca viprānkadācana ,gaccha yuktaḥ sadā bhūtvā kuru vai śāsanaṁ mama.
english: Son, never disrespect women or Brahmins. Go forth, always be diligent, and indeed, carry out my command.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 73. Son, never disrespect women or Brahmins. Go forth, always be diligent, and indeed, carry out my command.

sanskrit: स विसृष्टो महाराज जगामाथ स्वमालयम् ।प्रजाश्च मुदिता भूत्वा भोजने च यथा पुरा ॥७४॥
transliteration: sa visṛṣṭo mahārāja jagāmātha svamālayam ,prajāśca muditā bhūtvā bhojane ca yathā purā.
english: O great king, being dismissed, he then went to his own abode. And the people, having become delighted, resumed their meals as before.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 74. O great king, being dismissed, he then went to his own abode. And the people, having become delighted, resumed their meals as before.

sanskrit: एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं यथा शप्तो निशाकरः ।प्रभासं च यथा तीर्थं तीर्थानां प्रवरं ह्यभूत् ॥७५॥
transliteration: etatte sarvamākhyātaṁ yathā śapto niśākaraḥ ,prabhāsaṁ ca yathā tīrthaṁ tīrthānāṁ pravaraṁ hyabhūt.
english: All this has been narrated to you: how the moon (niśākara) was cursed, and how Prabhāsa became a sacred place (tīrtha), indeed, the foremost among sacred places (tīrthas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 75. All this has been narrated to you: how the moon (niśākara) was cursed, and how Prabhāsa became a sacred place (tīrtha), indeed, the foremost among sacred places (tīrthas).

sanskrit: अमावास्यां महाराज नित्यशः शशलक्षणः ।स्नात्वा ह्याप्यायते श्रीमान्प्रभासे तीर्थ उत्तमे ॥७६॥
transliteration: amāvāsyāṁ mahārāja nityaśaḥ śaśalakṣaṇaḥ ,snātvā hyāpyāyate śrīmānprabhāse tīrtha uttame.
english: O great king, on the new moon day (amāvāsyā), the glorious moon, marked with a hare (śaśalakṣaṇa), regularly bathes in the excellent sacred place (tīrtha) of Prabhāsa and is indeed nourished (or grows full).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 76. O great king, on the new moon day (amāvāsyā), the glorious moon, marked with a hare (śaśalakṣaṇa), regularly bathes in the excellent sacred place (tīrtha) of Prabhāsa and is indeed nourished (or grows full).

sanskrit: अतश्चैनं प्रजानन्ति प्रभासमिति भूमिप ।प्रभां हि परमां लेभे तस्मिन्नुन्मज्ज्य चन्द्रमाः ॥७७॥
transliteration: ataścainaṁ prajānanti prabhāsamiti bhūmipa ,prabhāṁ hi paramāṁ lebhe tasminnunmajjya candramāḥ.
english: O king, people indeed call this place Prabhāsa. For it was there that the moon, emerging (from the water), attained supreme radiance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 77. O king, people indeed call this place Prabhāsa. For it was there that the moon, emerging (from the water), attained supreme radiance.

sanskrit: ततस्तु चमसोद्भेदमच्युतस्त्वगमद्बली ।चमसोद्भेद इत्येवं यं जनाः कथयन्त्युत ॥७८॥
transliteration: tatastu camasodbhedamacyutastvagamadbalī ,camasodbheda ityevaṁ yaṁ janāḥ kathayantyuta.
english: Then, the mighty Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) indeed went to Camasodbheda, which people indeed refer to by that very name.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 78. Then, the mighty Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) indeed went to Camasodbheda, which people indeed refer to by that very name.

sanskrit: तत्र दत्त्वा च दानानि विशिष्टानि हलायुधः ।उषित्वा रजनीमेकां स्नात्वा च विधिवत्तदा ॥७९॥
transliteration: tatra dattvā ca dānāni viśiṣṭāni halāyudhaḥ ,uṣitvā rajanīmekāṁ snātvā ca vidhivattadā.
english: Then, Halāyudha (Balarāma), having given special gifts (dāna) there, and having stayed for one night and bathed according to the prescribed rituals...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 79. Then, Halāyudha (Balarāma), having given special gifts (dāna) there, and having stayed for one night and bathed according to the prescribed rituals...

sanskrit: उदपानमथागच्छत्त्वरावान्केशवाग्रजः ।आद्यं स्वस्त्ययनं चैव तत्रावाप्य महत्फलम् ॥८०॥
transliteration: udapānamathāgacchattvarāvānkeśavāgrajaḥ ,ādyaṁ svastyayanaṁ caiva tatrāvāpya mahatphalam.
english: Then, the elder brother of Keśava (Balarāma), being swift, went to the well. There, having obtained a great fruit (phalam) and the foremost blessing (svastyayana)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 80. Then, the elder brother of Keśava (Balarāma), being swift, went to the well. There, having obtained a great fruit (phalam) and the foremost blessing (svastyayana)...

sanskrit: स्निग्धत्वादोषधीनां च भूमेश्च जनमेजय ।जानन्ति सिद्धा राजेन्द्र नष्टामपि सरस्वतीम् ॥८१॥
transliteration: snigdhatvādoṣadhīnāṁ ca bhūmeśca janamejaya ,jānanti siddhā rājendra naṣṭāmapi sarasvatīm.
english: O Janamejaya, O king of kings, the perfected ones (siddhāḥ) know the Sarasvati river, even its lost course, owing to the fertility of the herbs and the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 34 Verse 81. O Janamejaya, O king of kings, the perfected ones (siddhāḥ) know the Sarasvati river, even its lost course, owing to the fertility of the herbs and the earth.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्मान्नदीगतं चापि उदपानं यशस्विनः ।त्रितस्य च महाराज जगामाथ हलायुधः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasmānnadīgataṁ cāpi udapānaṁ yaśasvinaḥ ,tritasya ca mahārāja jagāmātha halāyudhaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O great king, Halāyudha (Balarāma) then went to that well (udapāna) which was by the river and belonged to the glorious Trita.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O great king, Halāyudha (Balarāma) then went to that well (udapāna) which was by the river and belonged to the glorious Trita.'

sanskrit: तत्र दत्त्वा बहु द्रव्यं पूजयित्वा तथा द्विजान् ।उपस्पृश्य च तत्रैव प्रहृष्टो मुसलायुधः ॥२॥
transliteration: tatra dattvā bahu dravyaṁ pūjayitvā tathā dvijān ,upaspṛśya ca tatraiva prahṛṣṭo musalāyudhaḥ.
english: There, having given much wealth and also honored the twice-born (dvija), and having performed ablutions there, Musalāyudha (Balarāma) became greatly delighted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 2. There, having given much wealth and also honored the twice-born (dvija), and having performed ablutions there, Musalāyudha (Balarāma) became greatly delighted.

sanskrit: तत्र धर्मपरो ह्यासीत्त्रितः स सुमहातपाः ।कूपे च वसता तेन सोमः पीतो महात्मना ॥३॥
transliteration: tatra dharmaparo hyāsīttritaḥ sa sumahātapāḥ ,kūpe ca vasatā tena somaḥ pīto mahātmanā.
english: Indeed, Trita, that greatly austere (tapas) sage, devoted to natural law (dharma), was there. And by him, the great-souled (mahātman) one, while residing in the well, Soma was drunk.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 3. Indeed, Trita, that greatly austere (tapas) sage, devoted to natural law (dharma), was there. And by him, the great-souled (mahātman) one, while residing in the well, Soma was drunk.

sanskrit: तत्र चैनं समुत्सृज्य भ्रातरौ जग्मतुर्गृहान् ।ततस्तौ वै शशापाथ त्रितो ब्राह्मणसत्तमः ॥४॥
transliteration: tatra cainaṁ samutsṛjya bhrātarau jagmaturgṛhān ,tatastau vai śaśāpātha trito brāhmaṇasattamaḥ.
english: And having abandoned him there, the two brothers went to their homes. Thereupon, Trita, the foremost among brahmins, indeed cursed those two.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 4. And having abandoned him there, the two brothers went to their homes. Thereupon, Trita, the foremost among brahmins, indeed cursed those two.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।उदपानं कथं ब्रह्मन्कथं च सुमहातपाः ।पतितः किं च संत्यक्तो भ्रातृभ्यां द्विजसत्तमः ॥५॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,udapānaṁ kathaṁ brahmankathaṁ ca sumahātapāḥ ,patitaḥ kiṁ ca saṁtyakto bhrātṛbhyāṁ dvijasattamaḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: "O brahmin, how did that one of great asceticism (tapas) fall into a well? And why was that foremost of the twice-born (dvija) abandoned by his two brothers?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 5. Janamejaya said: "O brahmin, how did that one of great asceticism (tapas) fall into a well? And why was that foremost of the twice-born (dvija) abandoned by his two brothers?"

sanskrit: कूपे कथं च हित्वैनं भ्रातरौ जग्मतुर्गृहान् ।एतदाचक्ष्व मे ब्रह्मन्यदि श्राव्यं हि मन्यसे ॥६॥
transliteration: kūpe kathaṁ ca hitvainaṁ bhrātarau jagmaturgṛhān ,etadācakṣva me brahmanyadi śrāvyaṁ hi manyase.
english: And how did the two brothers, having abandoned him in the well, go to their homes? O brahmin, tell this to me, if you indeed consider it worthy of being heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 6. And how did the two brothers, having abandoned him in the well, go to their homes? O brahmin, tell this to me, if you indeed consider it worthy of being heard.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।आसन्पूर्वयुगे राजन्मुनयो भ्रातरस्त्रयः ।एकतश्च द्वितश्चैव त्रितश्चादित्यसंनिभाः ॥७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,āsanpūrvayuge rājanmunayo bhrātarastrayaḥ ,ekataśca dvitaścaiva tritaścādityasaṁnibhāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, in a former age (yuga), there were three ascetic sages (muni), brothers: Ekata, Dvita, and Trita, who were all as splendid as the sun."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 7. Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, in a former age (yuga), there were three ascetic sages (muni), brothers: Ekata, Dvita, and Trita, who were all as splendid as the sun."

sanskrit: सर्वे प्रजापतिसमाः प्रजावन्तस्तथैव च ।ब्रह्मलोकजितः सर्वे तपसा ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥८॥
transliteration: sarve prajāpatisamāḥ prajāvantastathaiva ca ,brahmalokajitaḥ sarve tapasā brahmavādinaḥ.
english: All of them were like the progenitors (Prajāpatis), and similarly, they had numerous descendants. All of them had conquered the realm of Brahmā through their spiritual austerities (tapas) and were expounders of the sacred texts (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 8. All of them were like the progenitors (Prajāpatis), and similarly, they had numerous descendants. All of them had conquered the realm of Brahmā through their spiritual austerities (tapas) and were expounders of the sacred texts (brahman).

sanskrit: तेषां तु तपसा प्रीतो नियमेन दमेन च ।अभवद्गौतमो नित्यं पिता धर्मरतः सदा ॥९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu tapasā prīto niyamena damena ca ,abhavadgautamo nityaṁ pitā dharmarataḥ sadā.
english: Indeed, their father, Gautama, who was always dedicated to righteousness (dharma), became constantly pleased by their spiritual austerities (tapas), self-discipline, and self-control.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 9. Indeed, their father, Gautama, who was always dedicated to righteousness (dharma), became constantly pleased by their spiritual austerities (tapas), self-discipline, and self-control.

sanskrit: स तु दीर्घेण कालेन तेषां प्रीतिमवाप्य च ।जगाम भगवान्स्थानमनुरूपमिवात्मनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa tu dīrgheṇa kālena teṣāṁ prītimavāpya ca ,jagāma bhagavānsthānamanurūpamivātmanaḥ.
english: And that venerable Gautama, having obtained their affection after a long period of time, went to an abode that was appropriate for his own exalted nature (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 10. And that venerable Gautama, having obtained their affection after a long period of time, went to an abode that was appropriate for his own exalted nature (ātman).

sanskrit: राजानस्तस्य ये पूर्वे याज्या ह्यासन्महात्मनः ।ते सर्वे स्वर्गते तस्मिंस्तस्य पुत्रानपूजयन् ॥११॥
transliteration: rājānastasya ye pūrve yājyā hyāsanmahātmanaḥ ,te sarve svargate tasmiṁstasya putrānapūjayan.
english: All those kings, who were formerly served as priests by that great-souled (mahātman) Gautama, honored his sons after he had ascended to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 11. All those kings, who were formerly served as priests by that great-souled (mahātman) Gautama, honored his sons after he had ascended to heaven.

sanskrit: तेषां तु कर्मणा राजंस्तथैवाध्ययनेन च ।त्रितः स श्रेष्ठतां प्राप यथैवास्य पिता तथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu karmaṇā rājaṁstathaivādhyayanena ca ,tritaḥ sa śreṣṭhatāṁ prāpa yathaivāsya pitā tathā.
english: O king, among them, Trita attained superiority by his actions (karma) and likewise by his study, just as his father had.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 12. O king, among them, Trita attained superiority by his actions (karma) and likewise by his study, just as his father had.

sanskrit: तं स्म सर्वे महाभागा मुनयः पुण्यलक्षणाः ।अपूजयन्महाभागं तथा विद्वत्तयैव तु ॥१३॥
transliteration: taṁ sma sarve mahābhāgā munayaḥ puṇyalakṣaṇāḥ ,apūjayanmahābhāgaṁ tathā vidvattayaiva tu.
english: All those great and virtuous sages indeed honored him, that highly esteemed one, and also because of his extensive learning.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 13. All those great and virtuous sages indeed honored him, that highly esteemed one, and also because of his extensive learning.

sanskrit: कदाचिद्धि ततो राजन्भ्रातरावेकतद्वितौ ।यज्ञार्थं चक्रतुश्चित्तं धनार्थं च विशेषतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: kadāciddhi tato rājanbhrātarāvekatadvitau ,yajñārthaṁ cakratuścittaṁ dhanārthaṁ ca viśeṣataḥ.
english: O king, one time, the two brothers, united in their purpose, made up their minds to perform a ritual (yajña), especially for the sake of acquiring wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 14. O king, one time, the two brothers, united in their purpose, made up their minds to perform a ritual (yajña), especially for the sake of acquiring wealth.

sanskrit: तयोश्चिन्ता समभवत्त्रितं गृह्य परंतप ।याज्यान्सर्वानुपादाय प्रतिगृह्य पशूंस्ततः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tayościntā samabhavattritaṁ gṛhya paraṁtapa ,yājyānsarvānupādāya pratigṛhya paśūṁstataḥ.
english: O tormentor of foes, a concern arose in those two (brothers): "Having seized Trita, and then having obtained all the patrons (yājyān) for the ritual and received the sacrificial animals..."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 15. O tormentor of foes, a concern arose in those two (brothers): "Having seized Trita, and then having obtained all the patrons (yājyān) for the ritual and received the sacrificial animals..."

sanskrit: सोमं पास्यामहे हृष्टाः प्राप्य यज्ञं महाफलम् ।चक्रुश्चैव महाराज भ्रातरस्त्रय एव ह ॥१६॥
transliteration: somaṁ pāsyāmahe hṛṣṭāḥ prāpya yajñaṁ mahāphalam ,cakruścaiva mahārāja bhrātarastraya eva ha.
english: O great king, joyful, we shall drink the soma (soma) after attaining the Vedic ritual (yajña) that yields great results. Indeed, the three brothers performed this very act.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 16. O great king, joyful, we shall drink the soma (soma) after attaining the Vedic ritual (yajña) that yields great results. Indeed, the three brothers performed this very act.

sanskrit: तथा तु ते परिक्रम्य याज्यान्सर्वान्पशून्प्रति ।याजयित्वा ततो याज्याँल्लब्ध्वा च सुबहून्पशून् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tathā tu te parikramya yājyānsarvānpaśūnprati ,yājayitvā tato yājyāँllabdhvā ca subahūnpaśūn.
english: Thus, indeed, having circumambulated all the animals meant for sacrifice (yajya), and having caused them to be sacrificed, they then obtained many sacrificial animals (yajya) as well as numerous other animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 17. Thus, indeed, having circumambulated all the animals meant for sacrifice (yajya), and having caused them to be sacrificed, they then obtained many sacrificial animals (yajya) as well as numerous other animals.

sanskrit: याज्येन कर्मणा तेन प्रतिगृह्य विधानतः ।प्राचीं दिशं महात्मान आजग्मुस्ते महर्षयः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yājyena karmaṇā tena pratigṛhya vidhānataḥ ,prācīṁ diśaṁ mahātmāna ājagmuste maharṣayaḥ.
english: Having accepted (the offerings) in accordance with the rules through that sacrificial (yajya) ritual (karma), those great-souled (mahātman) great sages (maharṣi) then journeyed to the eastern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 18. Having accepted (the offerings) in accordance with the rules through that sacrificial (yajya) ritual (karma), those great-souled (mahātman) great sages (maharṣi) then journeyed to the eastern direction.

sanskrit: त्रितस्तेषां महाराज पुरस्ताद्याति हृष्टवत् ।एकतश्च द्वितश्चैव पृष्ठतः कालयन्पशून् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tritasteṣāṁ mahārāja purastādyāti hṛṣṭavat ,ekataśca dvitaścaiva pṛṣṭhataḥ kālayanpaśūn.
english: O great king, Trita joyfully walked ahead of them, while Eka and Dvita, from behind, drove the animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 19. O great king, Trita joyfully walked ahead of them, while Eka and Dvita, from behind, drove the animals.

sanskrit: तयोश्चिन्ता समभवद्दृष्ट्वा पशुगणं महत् ।कथं न स्युरिमा गाव आवाभ्यां वै विना त्रितम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tayościntā samabhavaddṛṣṭvā paśugaṇaṁ mahat ,kathaṁ na syurimā gāva āvābhyāṁ vai vinā tritam.
english: Upon seeing the large herd of cattle, a concern arose in the minds of the two of them: "How can these cows become exclusively ours, without Trit?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 20. Upon seeing the large herd of cattle, a concern arose in the minds of the two of them: "How can these cows become exclusively ours, without Trit?"

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यं समाभाष्य एकतश्च द्वितश्च ह ।यदूचतुर्मिथः पापौ तन्निबोध जनेश्वर ॥२१॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyaṁ samābhāṣya ekataśca dvitaśca ha ,yadūcaturmithaḥ pāpau tannibodha janeśvara.
english: Those two - Ekata and Dvita - having discussed with each other, O ruler of people, listen to what those two wicked ones then said to one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 21. Those two - Ekata and Dvita - having discussed with each other, O ruler of people, listen to what those two wicked ones then said to one another.

sanskrit: त्रितो यज्ञेषु कुशलस्त्रितो वेदेषु निष्ठितः ।अन्यास्त्रितो बहुतरा गावः समुपलप्स्यते ॥२२॥
transliteration: trito yajñeṣu kuśalastrito vedeṣu niṣṭhitaḥ ,anyāstrito bahutarā gāvaḥ samupalapsyate.
english: Trit is skilled in performing Vedic rituals (yajña), and Trit is proficient in the Vedas. Trit will obtain many other, more abundant cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 22. Trit is skilled in performing Vedic rituals (yajña), and Trit is proficient in the Vedas. Trit will obtain many other, more abundant cows.

sanskrit: तदावां सहितौ भूत्वा गाः प्रकाल्य व्रजावहे ।त्रितोऽपि गच्छतां काममावाभ्यां वै विनाकृतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tadāvāṁ sahitau bhūtvā gāḥ prakālya vrajāvahe ,trito'pi gacchatāṁ kāmamāvābhyāṁ vai vinākṛtaḥ.
english: Then, let us two unite, drive these cows away, and depart. Let Trit also go as he pleases, truly excluded from us two.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 23. Then, let us two unite, drive these cows away, and depart. Let Trit also go as he pleases, truly excluded from us two.

sanskrit: तेषामागच्छतां रात्रौ पथिस्थाने वृकोऽभवत् ।तथा कूपोऽविदूरेऽभूत्सरस्वत्यास्तटे महान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: teṣāmāgacchatāṁ rātrau pathisthāne vṛko'bhavat ,tathā kūpo'vidūre'bhūtsarasvatyāstaṭe mahān.
english: As they were traveling at night, a wolf appeared on the path. Also, a large well was located not far away, on the bank of the Sarasvatī River.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 24. As they were traveling at night, a wolf appeared on the path. Also, a large well was located not far away, on the bank of the Sarasvatī River.

sanskrit: अथ त्रितो वृकं दृष्ट्वा पथि तिष्ठन्तमग्रतः ।तद्भयादपसर्पन्वै तस्मिन्कूपे पपात ह ।अगाधे सुमहाघोरे सर्वभूतभयंकरे ॥२५॥
transliteration: atha trito vṛkaṁ dṛṣṭvā pathi tiṣṭhantamagrataḥ ,tadbhayādapasarpanvai tasminkūpe papāta ha ,agādhe sumahāghore sarvabhūtabhayaṁkare.
english: Then Trita, upon seeing the wolf standing in front of him on the path, retreated in fear of it and indeed fell into that well. It was unfathomably deep, exceedingly dreadful, and capable of striking fear into all creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 25. Then Trita, upon seeing the wolf standing in front of him on the path, retreated in fear of it and indeed fell into that well. It was unfathomably deep, exceedingly dreadful, and capable of striking fear into all creatures.

sanskrit: त्रितस्ततो महाभागः कूपस्थो मुनिसत्तमः ।आर्तनादं ततश्चक्रे तौ तु शुश्रुवतुर्मुनी ॥२६॥
transliteration: tritastato mahābhāgaḥ kūpastho munisattamaḥ ,ārtanādaṁ tataścakre tau tu śuśruvaturmunī.
english: Then Trita, the illustrious best of sages, trapped in the well, thereupon let out a cry of distress. Indeed, those two other sages heard him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 26. Then Trita, the illustrious best of sages, trapped in the well, thereupon let out a cry of distress. Indeed, those two other sages heard him.

sanskrit: तं ज्ञात्वा पतितं कूपे भ्रातरावेकतद्वितौ ।वृकत्रासाच्च लोभाच्च समुत्सृज्य प्रजग्मतुः ॥२७॥
transliteration: taṁ jñātvā patitaṁ kūpe bhrātarāvekatadvitau ,vṛkatrāsācca lobhācca samutsṛjya prajagmatuḥ.
english: Recognizing that he had fallen into the well, the two brothers, Ekat and Dvita, abandoned him due to both their fear of the wolf and their greed, and then departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 27. Recognizing that he had fallen into the well, the two brothers, Ekat and Dvita, abandoned him due to both their fear of the wolf and their greed, and then departed.

sanskrit: भ्रातृभ्यां पशुलुब्धाभ्यामुत्सृष्टः स महातपाः ।उदपाने महाराज निर्जले पांसुसंवृते ॥२८॥
transliteration: bhrātṛbhyāṁ paśulubdhābhyāmutsṛṣṭaḥ sa mahātapāḥ ,udapāne mahārāja nirjale pāṁsusaṁvṛte.
english: O great king, he, the great ascetic (tapasvin), was abandoned by his two brothers, who were greedy for cattle, in a dry well covered with dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 28. O great king, he, the great ascetic (tapasvin), was abandoned by his two brothers, who were greedy for cattle, in a dry well covered with dust.

sanskrit: त्रित आत्मानमालक्ष्य कूपे वीरुत्तृणावृते ।निमग्नं भरतश्रेष्ठ पापकृन्नरके यथा ॥२९॥
transliteration: trita ātmānamālakṣya kūpe vīruttṛṇāvṛte ,nimagnaṁ bharataśreṣṭha pāpakṛnnarake yathā.
english: O best of Bharatas, Trita, perceiving himself (ātman) sunken in the well, which was covered with creepers and grass, was like a sinner fallen into hell.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 29. O best of Bharatas, Trita, perceiving himself (ātman) sunken in the well, which was covered with creepers and grass, was like a sinner fallen into hell.

sanskrit: बुद्ध्या ह्यगणयत्प्राज्ञो मृत्योर्भीतो ह्यसोमपः ।सोमः कथं नु पातव्य इहस्थेन मया भवेत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: buddhyā hyagaṇayatprājño mṛtyorbhīto hyasomapaḥ ,somaḥ kathaṁ nu pātavya ihasthena mayā bhavet.
english: Indeed, the wise one, fearing death and being someone who had not consumed Soma (asomapa), considered with his intellect: "How indeed can the Soma be drunk by me, who is situated here?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 30. Indeed, the wise one, fearing death and being someone who had not consumed Soma (asomapa), considered with his intellect: "How indeed can the Soma be drunk by me, who is situated here?"

sanskrit: स एवमनुसंचिन्त्य तस्मिन्कूपे महातपाः ।ददर्श वीरुधं तत्र लम्बमानां यदृच्छया ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa evamanusaṁcintya tasminkūpe mahātapāḥ ,dadarśa vīrudhaṁ tatra lambamānāṁ yadṛcchayā.
english: Thus, having reflected in that well, the great ascetic (tapasvin) saw a creeper hanging there by chance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 31. Thus, having reflected in that well, the great ascetic (tapasvin) saw a creeper hanging there by chance.

sanskrit: पांसुग्रस्ते ततः कूपे विचिन्त्य सलिलं मुनिः ।अग्नीन्संकल्पयामास होत्रे चात्मानमेव च ॥३२॥
transliteration: pāṁsugraste tataḥ kūpe vicintya salilaṁ muniḥ ,agnīnsaṁkalpayāmāsa hotre cātmānameva ca.
english: Then, the sage, contemplating the water in the well that was covered with dust, resolved to consecrate the fires and himself (ātman) as the priest (hotṛ) for the ritual.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 32. Then, the sage, contemplating the water in the well that was covered with dust, resolved to consecrate the fires and himself (ātman) as the priest (hotṛ) for the ritual.

sanskrit: ततस्तां वीरुधं सोमं संकल्प्य सुमहातपाः ।ऋचो यजूंषि सामानि मनसा चिन्तयन्मुनिः ।ग्रावाणः शर्कराः कृत्वा प्रचक्रेऽभिषवं नृप ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatastāṁ vīrudhaṁ somaṁ saṁkalpya sumahātapāḥ ,ṛco yajūṁṣi sāmāni manasā cintayanmuniḥ ,grāvāṇaḥ śarkarāḥ kṛtvā pracakre'bhiṣavaṁ nṛpa.
english: O King, then that sage of great asceticism (tapas), having mentally conceived that creeper as the Soma (yajña plant), and meditating upon the Ṛg verses, Yajus formulas, and Sāman chants with his mind, made the large stones into small pressing-stones (grāvāṇaḥ) and performed the Soma pressing (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 33. O King, then that sage of great asceticism (tapas), having mentally conceived that creeper as the Soma (yajña plant), and meditating upon the Ṛg verses, Yajus formulas, and Sāman chants with his mind, made the large stones into small pressing-stones (grāvāṇaḥ) and performed the Soma pressing (yajña).

sanskrit: आज्यं च सलिलं चक्रे भागांश्च त्रिदिवौकसाम् ।सोमस्याभिषवं कृत्वा चकार तुमुलं ध्वनिम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: ājyaṁ ca salilaṁ cakre bhāgāṁśca tridivaukasām ,somasyābhiṣavaṁ kṛtvā cakāra tumulaṁ dhvanim.
english: And he made the water into clarified butter, and (mentally prepared) the shares for the dwellers of heaven (the gods). Having performed the pressing of the Soma, he produced a tumultuous sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 34. And he made the water into clarified butter, and (mentally prepared) the shares for the dwellers of heaven (the gods). Having performed the pressing of the Soma, he produced a tumultuous sound.

sanskrit: स चाविशद्दिवं राजन्स्वरः शैक्षस्त्रितस्य वै ।समवाप च तं यज्ञं यथोक्तं ब्रह्मवादिभिः ॥३५॥
transliteration: sa cāviśaddivaṁ rājansvaraḥ śaikṣastritasya vai ,samavāpa ca taṁ yajñaṁ yathoktaṁ brahmavādibhiḥ.
english: And that skilled voice (svara) of Trita, O king, indeed reached heaven. And he attained that Vedic ritual (yajña) as prescribed by those who expound the Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 35. And that skilled voice (svara) of Trita, O king, indeed reached heaven. And he attained that Vedic ritual (yajña) as prescribed by those who expound the Vedas.

sanskrit: वर्तमाने तथा यज्ञे त्रितस्य सुमहात्मनः ।आविग्नं त्रिदिवं सर्वं कारणं च न बुध्यते ॥३६॥
transliteration: vartamāne tathā yajñe tritasya sumahātmanaḥ ,āvignaṁ tridivaṁ sarvaṁ kāraṇaṁ ca na budhyate.
english: While Trita, the greatly elevated soul, was performing that Vedic ritual (yajña), all of heaven became agitated, and the cause of this agitation was not understood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 36. While Trita, the greatly elevated soul, was performing that Vedic ritual (yajña), all of heaven became agitated, and the cause of this agitation was not understood.

sanskrit: ततः सुतुमुलं शब्दं शुश्रावाथ बृहस्पतिः ।श्रुत्वा चैवाब्रवीद्देवान्सर्वान्देवपुरोहितः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sutumulaṁ śabdaṁ śuśrāvātha bṛhaspatiḥ ,śrutvā caivābravīddevānsarvāndevapurohitaḥ.
english: Then Bṛhaspati, the priest of the gods, heard a very tumultuous sound. And having heard it, he spoke to all the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 37. Then Bṛhaspati, the priest of the gods, heard a very tumultuous sound. And having heard it, he spoke to all the gods.

sanskrit: त्रितस्य वर्तते यज्ञस्तत्र गच्छामहे सुराः ।स हि क्रुद्धः सृजेदन्यान्देवानपि महातपाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tritasya vartate yajñastatra gacchāmahe surāḥ ,sa hi kruddhaḥ sṛjedanyāndevānapi mahātapāḥ.
english: Trita's Vedic ritual (yajña) is taking place there; let us go, O gods! For that greatly ascetic one (mahātapaḥ), if he becomes angry, could indeed create other gods too.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 38. Trita's Vedic ritual (yajña) is taking place there; let us go, O gods! For that greatly ascetic one (mahātapaḥ), if he becomes angry, could indeed create other gods too.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य सहिताः सर्वदेवताः ।प्रययुस्तत्र यत्रासौ त्रितयज्ञः प्रवर्तते ॥३९॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā vacanaṁ tasya sahitāḥ sarvadevatāḥ ,prayayustatra yatrāsau tritayajñaḥ pravartate.
english: Having heard his words, all the assembled deities went there, to the place where Trita's Vedic ritual (yajña) was being performed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 39. Having heard his words, all the assembled deities went there, to the place where Trita's Vedic ritual (yajña) was being performed.

sanskrit: ते तत्र गत्वा विबुधास्तं कूपं यत्र स त्रितः ।ददृशुस्तं महात्मानं दीक्षितं यज्ञकर्मसु ॥४०॥
transliteration: te tatra gatvā vibudhāstaṁ kūpaṁ yatra sa tritaḥ ,dadṛśustaṁ mahātmānaṁ dīkṣitaṁ yajñakarmasu.
english: Having gone there, those wise gods saw that great soul (mahātman) Trita in the well, consecrated for the performance of Vedic rituals (yajña-karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 40. Having gone there, those wise gods saw that great soul (mahātman) Trita in the well, consecrated for the performance of Vedic rituals (yajña-karma).

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा चैनं महात्मानं श्रिया परमया युतम् ।ऊचुश्चाथ महाभागं प्राप्ता भागार्थिनो वयम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā cainaṁ mahātmānaṁ śriyā paramayā yutam ,ūcuścātha mahābhāgaṁ prāptā bhāgārthino vayam.
english: And having seen that great soul (mahātman) endowed with supreme splendor, they then spoke to the highly fortunate one (Tritā): "We have arrived, seeking our share."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 41. And having seen that great soul (mahātman) endowed with supreme splendor, they then spoke to the highly fortunate one (Tritā): "We have arrived, seeking our share."

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीदृषिर्देवान्पश्यध्वं मां दिवौकसः ।अस्मिन्प्रतिभये कूपे निमग्नं नष्टचेतसम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: athābravīdṛṣirdevānpaśyadhvaṁ māṁ divaukasaḥ ,asminpratibhaye kūpe nimagnaṁ naṣṭacetasam.
english: Then the sage spoke to the gods (divaukasaḥ): "O dwellers of heaven! Behold me, submerged in this terrifying well, with my consciousness lost."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 42. Then the sage spoke to the gods (divaukasaḥ): "O dwellers of heaven! Behold me, submerged in this terrifying well, with my consciousness lost."

sanskrit: ततस्त्रितो महाराज भागांस्तेषां यथाविधि ।मन्त्रयुक्तान्समददात्ते च प्रीतास्तदाभवन् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tatastrito mahārāja bhāgāṁsteṣāṁ yathāvidhi ,mantrayuktānsamadadātte ca prītāstadābhavan.
english: Then, O great king, Tritā, in accordance with tradition, gave them their shares, endowed with mantras (mantra). And they became pleased at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 43. Then, O great king, Tritā, in accordance with tradition, gave them their shares, endowed with mantras (mantra). And they became pleased at that time.

sanskrit: ततो यथाविधि प्राप्तान्भागान्प्राप्य दिवौकसः ।प्रीतात्मानो ददुस्तस्मै वरान्यान्मनसेच्छति ॥४४॥
transliteration: tato yathāvidhi prāptānbhāgānprāpya divaukasaḥ ,prītātmāno dadustasmai varānyānmanasecchati.
english: Then, having properly received their portions, the gods (divaukasaḥ), their inner selves (ātman) pleased, granted him the boons that he desired mentally.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 44. Then, having properly received their portions, the gods (divaukasaḥ), their inner selves (ātman) pleased, granted him the boons that he desired mentally.

sanskrit: स तु वव्रे वरं देवांस्त्रातुमर्हथ मामितः ।यश्चेहोपस्पृशेत्कूपे स सोमपगतिं लभेत् ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa tu vavre varaṁ devāṁstrātumarhatha māmitaḥ ,yaścehopaspṛśetkūpe sa somapagatiṁ labhet.
english: But he requested a boon: "O gods, you ought to protect me from this (predicament). And whoever bathes in this well (kūpa) here, he shall attain the destination of those who drink Soma."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 45. But he requested a boon: "O gods, you ought to protect me from this (predicament). And whoever bathes in this well (kūpa) here, he shall attain the destination of those who drink Soma."

sanskrit: तत्र चोर्मिमती राजन्नुत्पपात सरस्वती ।तयोत्क्षिप्तस्त्रितस्तस्थौ पूजयंस्त्रिदिवौकसः ॥४६॥
transliteration: tatra cormimatī rājannutpapāta sarasvatī ,tayotkṣiptastritastasthau pūjayaṁstridivaukasaḥ.
english: And there, O king, the wavy Sarasvatī sprang forth. Trita, having been thrown up by her, stood worshipping the gods (tridivaukasaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 46. And there, O king, the wavy Sarasvatī sprang forth. Trita, having been thrown up by her, stood worshipping the gods (tridivaukasaḥ).

sanskrit: तथेति चोक्त्वा विबुधा जग्मू राजन्यथागतम् ।त्रितश्चाप्यगमत्प्रीतः स्वमेव निलयं तदा ॥४७॥
transliteration: tatheti coktvā vibudhā jagmū rājanyathāgatam ,tritaścāpyagamatprītaḥ svameva nilayaṁ tadā.
english: And saying "So be it," O king, the gods (vibudhāḥ) departed the way they had come. And Trita also, pleased, then went to his own abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 47. And saying "So be it," O king, the gods (vibudhāḥ) departed the way they had come. And Trita also, pleased, then went to his own abode.

sanskrit: क्रुद्धः स तु समासाद्य तावृषी भ्रातरौ तदा ।उवाच परुषं वाक्यं शशाप च महातपाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: kruddhaḥ sa tu samāsādya tāvṛṣī bhrātarau tadā ,uvāca paruṣaṁ vākyaṁ śaśāpa ca mahātapāḥ.
english: Then, that great ascetic, enraged, having approached those two sage brothers, spoke harsh words and cursed them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 48. Then, that great ascetic, enraged, having approached those two sage brothers, spoke harsh words and cursed them.

sanskrit: पशुलुब्धौ युवां यस्मान्मामुत्सृज्य प्रधावितौ ।तस्माद्रूपेण तेषां वै दंष्ट्रिणामभितश्चरौ ॥४९॥
transliteration: paśulubdhau yuvāṁ yasmānmāmutsṛjya pradhāvitau ,tasmādrūpeṇa teṣāṁ vai daṁṣṭriṇāmabhitaścarau.
english: Since you two, eager for animals, abandoned me and ran away, therefore, you two shall roam around in the form of those tusked creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 49. Since you two, eager for animals, abandoned me and ran away, therefore, you two shall roam around in the form of those tusked creatures.

sanskrit: भवितारौ मया शप्तौ पापेनानेन कर्मणा ।प्रसवश्चैव युवयोर्गोलाङ्गूलर्क्षवानराः ॥५०॥
transliteration: bhavitārau mayā śaptau pāpenānena karmaṇā ,prasavaścaiva yuvayorgolāṅgūlarkṣavānarāḥ.
english: Cursed by me for this sinful action (karma), you two will become [such creatures]. And your offspring will indeed be gorillas, bears, and monkeys.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 50. Cursed by me for this sinful action (karma), you two will become [such creatures]. And your offspring will indeed be gorillas, bears, and monkeys.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते तु तदा तेन क्षणादेव विशां पते ।तथाभूतावदृश्येतां वचनात्सत्यवादिनः ॥५१॥
transliteration: ityukte tu tadā tena kṣaṇādeva viśāṁ pate ,tathābhūtāvadṛśyetāṁ vacanātsatyavādinaḥ.
english: O king, when he had spoken thus, instantly, by the word of that truth-speaker, the two appeared in that very form.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 51. O king, when he had spoken thus, instantly, by the word of that truth-speaker, the two appeared in that very form.

sanskrit: तत्राप्यमितविक्रान्तः स्पृष्ट्वा तोयं हलायुधः ।दत्त्वा च विविधान्दायान्पूजयित्वा च वै द्विजान् ॥५२॥
transliteration: tatrāpyamitavikrāntaḥ spṛṣṭvā toyaṁ halāyudhaḥ ,dattvā ca vividhāndāyānpūjayitvā ca vai dvijān.
english: There, the immensely powerful Balarama, bearing the plough weapon (hala-āyudha), after touching the water and giving various gifts, also honored the twice-born (dvijān).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 52. There, the immensely powerful Balarama, bearing the plough weapon (hala-āyudha), after touching the water and giving various gifts, also honored the twice-born (dvijān).

sanskrit: उदपानं च तं दृष्ट्वा प्रशस्य च पुनः पुनः ।नदीगतमदीनात्मा प्राप्तो विनशनं तदा ॥५३॥
transliteration: udapānaṁ ca taṁ dṛṣṭvā praśasya ca punaḥ punaḥ ,nadīgatamadīnātmā prāpto vinaśanaṁ tadā.
english: Then, that noble-minded one (adīna-ātmā), having seen that well situated by the river, and repeatedly praised it, reached Vinaśana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 35 Verse 53. Then, that noble-minded one (adīna-ātmā), having seen that well situated by the river, and repeatedly praised it, reached Vinaśana.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो विनशनं राजन्नाजगाम हलायुधः ।शूद्राभीरान्प्रति द्वेषाद्यत्र नष्टा सरस्वती ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato vinaśanaṁ rājannājagāma halāyudhaḥ ,śūdrābhīrānprati dveṣādyatra naṣṭā sarasvatī.
english: Vaiśampayana said: 'O King, then Balarama (hala-āyudha) came to Vinaśana, where the Sarasvatī (Sarasvatī) river had disappeared due to her aversion towards the Śūdras and Ābhīras.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 1. Vaiśampayana said: 'O King, then Balarama (hala-āyudha) came to Vinaśana, where the Sarasvatī (Sarasvatī) river had disappeared due to her aversion towards the Śūdras and Ābhīras.'

sanskrit: यस्मात्सा भरतश्रेष्ठ द्वेषान्नष्टा सरस्वती ।तस्मात्तदृषयो नित्यं प्राहुर्विनशनेति ह ॥२॥
transliteration: yasmātsā bharataśreṣṭha dveṣānnaṣṭā sarasvatī ,tasmāttadṛṣayo nityaṁ prāhurvinaśaneti ha.
english: O best among the Bharatas, because that Sarasvatī (Sarasvatī) river disappeared due to aversion, therefore the sages always call that place Vinaśana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 2. O best among the Bharatas, because that Sarasvatī (Sarasvatī) river disappeared due to aversion, therefore the sages always call that place Vinaśana.

sanskrit: तच्चाप्युपस्पृश्य बलः सरस्वत्यां महाबलः ।सुभूमिकं ततोऽगच्छत्सरस्वत्यास्तटे वरे ॥३॥
transliteration: taccāpyupaspṛśya balaḥ sarasvatyāṁ mahābalaḥ ,subhūmikaṁ tato'gacchatsarasvatyāstaṭe vare.
english: After bathing in that (water) of the Sarasvatī, the mighty Bala (Bala) then went from there to Subhūmika, situated on the excellent bank of the Sarasvatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 3. After bathing in that (water) of the Sarasvatī, the mighty Bala (Bala) then went from there to Subhūmika, situated on the excellent bank of the Sarasvatī.

sanskrit: तत्र चाप्सरसः शुभ्रा नित्यकालमतन्द्रिताः ।क्रीडाभिर्विमलाभिश्च क्रीडन्ति विमलाननाः ॥४॥
transliteration: tatra cāpsarasaḥ śubhrā nityakālamatandritāḥ ,krīḍābhirvimalābhiśca krīḍanti vimalānanāḥ.
english: And there, bright and ever-vigilant Apsarases, with their spotless faces, constantly engage in pure and delightful games.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 4. And there, bright and ever-vigilant Apsarases, with their spotless faces, constantly engage in pure and delightful games.

sanskrit: तत्र देवाः सगन्धर्वा मासि मासि जनेश्वर ।अभिगच्छन्ति तत्तीर्थं पुण्यं ब्राह्मणसेवितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tatra devāḥ sagandharvā māsi māsi janeśvara ,abhigacchanti tattīrthaṁ puṇyaṁ brāhmaṇasevitam.
english: O lord of men, there, the gods, accompanied by Gandharvas, visit that sacred bathing place (tīrtha) month after month, which is holy and revered by Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 5. O lord of men, there, the gods, accompanied by Gandharvas, visit that sacred bathing place (tīrtha) month after month, which is holy and revered by Brahmins.

sanskrit: तत्रादृश्यन्त गन्धर्वास्तथैवाप्सरसां गणाः ।समेत्य सहिता राजन्यथाप्राप्तं यथासुखम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tatrādṛśyanta gandharvāstathaivāpsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ ,sametya sahitā rājanyathāprāptaṁ yathāsukham.
english: O King, there, Gandharvas were seen, and likewise, groups of Apsarases, having gathered together, united, arriving as they pleased and enjoying themselves comfortably.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 6. O King, there, Gandharvas were seen, and likewise, groups of Apsarases, having gathered together, united, arriving as they pleased and enjoying themselves comfortably.

sanskrit: तत्र मोदन्ति देवाश्च पितरश्च सवीरुधः ।पुण्यैः पुष्पैः सदा दिव्यैः कीर्यमाणाः पुनः पुनः ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra modanti devāśca pitaraśca savīrudhaḥ ,puṇyaiḥ puṣpaiḥ sadā divyaiḥ kīryamāṇāḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: There, the gods and ancestors, accompanied by plants and creepers, rejoice. They are constantly showered again and again with sacred, divine flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 7. There, the gods and ancestors, accompanied by plants and creepers, rejoice. They are constantly showered again and again with sacred, divine flowers.

sanskrit: आक्रीडभूमिः सा राजंस्तासामप्सरसां शुभा ।सुभूमिकेति विख्याता सरस्वत्यास्तटे वरे ॥८॥
transliteration: ākrīḍabhūmiḥ sā rājaṁstāsāmapsarasāṁ śubhā ,subhūmiketi vikhyātā sarasvatyāstaṭe vare.
english: O king, that beautiful playground belonging to those Apsaras (apsaras) is well-known as Subhūmikā, located on the excellent bank of the Sarasvatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 8. O king, that beautiful playground belonging to those Apsaras (apsaras) is well-known as Subhūmikā, located on the excellent bank of the Sarasvatī.

sanskrit: तत्र स्नात्वा च दत्त्वा च वसु विप्रेषु माधवः ।श्रुत्वा गीतं च तद्दिव्यं वादित्राणां च निःस्वनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tatra snātvā ca dattvā ca vasu vipreṣu mādhavaḥ ,śrutvā gītaṁ ca taddivyaṁ vāditrāṇāṁ ca niḥsvanam.
english: There, Mādhava, after bathing and giving wealth to the Brahmins, and after hearing that divine song and the sound of musical instruments...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 9. There, Mādhava, after bathing and giving wealth to the Brahmins, and after hearing that divine song and the sound of musical instruments...

sanskrit: छायाश्च विपुला दृष्ट्वा देवगन्धर्वरक्षसाम् ।गन्धर्वाणां ततस्तीर्थमागच्छद्रोहिणीसुतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: chāyāśca vipulā dṛṣṭvā devagandharvarakṣasām ,gandharvāṇāṁ tatastīrthamāgacchadrohiṇīsutaḥ.
english: Having seen the vast apparitions of gods, Gandharvas, and Rākṣasas, the son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma) then proceeded to the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of the Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 10. Having seen the vast apparitions of gods, Gandharvas, and Rākṣasas, the son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma) then proceeded to the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of the Gandharvas.

sanskrit: विश्वावसुमुखास्तत्र गन्धर्वास्तपसान्विताः ।नृत्तवादित्रगीतं च कुर्वन्ति सुमनोरमम् ॥११॥
transliteration: viśvāvasumukhāstatra gandharvāstapasānvitāḥ ,nṛttavāditragītaṁ ca kurvanti sumanoramam.
english: There, Gandharvas, led by Viśvāvasu and endowed with spiritual austerity (tapas), perform delightful dance, instrumental music, and song.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 11. There, Gandharvas, led by Viśvāvasu and endowed with spiritual austerity (tapas), perform delightful dance, instrumental music, and song.

sanskrit: तत्र दत्त्वा हलधरो विप्रेभ्यो विविधं वसु ।अजाविकं गोखरोष्ट्रं सुवर्णं रजतं तथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatra dattvā haladharo viprebhyo vividhaṁ vasu ,ajāvikaṁ gokharoṣṭraṁ suvarṇaṁ rajataṁ tathā.
english: There, the plough-bearer (Haladhara), after giving various kinds of wealth to the Brahmins - including goats, sheep, cows, donkeys, camels, gold, and silver -
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 12. There, the plough-bearer (Haladhara), after giving various kinds of wealth to the Brahmins - including goats, sheep, cows, donkeys, camels, gold, and silver -

sanskrit: भोजयित्वा द्विजान्कामैः संतर्प्य च महाधनैः ।प्रययौ सहितो विप्रैः स्तूयमानश्च माधवः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhojayitvā dvijānkāmaiḥ saṁtarpya ca mahādhanaiḥ ,prayayau sahito vipraiḥ stūyamānaśca mādhavaḥ.
english: After feeding the Brahmins and fulfilling their desires, and satisfying them with great wealth, Mādhava, accompanied by the Brahmins and being praised by them, proceeded onwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 13. After feeding the Brahmins and fulfilling their desires, and satisfying them with great wealth, Mādhava, accompanied by the Brahmins and being praised by them, proceeded onwards.

sanskrit: तस्माद्गन्धर्वतीर्थाच्च महाबाहुररिंदमः ।गर्गस्रोतो महातीर्थमाजगामैककुण्डली ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasmādgandharvatīrthācca mahābāhurariṁdamaḥ ,gargasroto mahātīrthamājagāmaikakuṇḍalī.
english: From that Gandharva-tīrtha (pilgrimage site), the mighty-armed vanquisher of foes, the one wearing a single earring, proceeded to the great pilgrimage site of Gargasrota (Garga's stream).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 14. From that Gandharva-tīrtha (pilgrimage site), the mighty-armed vanquisher of foes, the one wearing a single earring, proceeded to the great pilgrimage site of Gargasrota (Garga's stream).

sanskrit: यत्र गर्गेण वृद्धेन तपसा भावितात्मना ।कालज्ञानगतिश्चैव ज्योतिषां च व्यतिक्रमः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yatra gargeṇa vṛddhena tapasā bhāvitātmanā ,kālajñānagatiścaiva jyotiṣāṁ ca vyatikramaḥ.
english: Where, by the venerable Garga, whose inner self (ātman) was refined through spiritual discipline (tapas), there was knowledge of the course of time and the movements of celestial bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 15. Where, by the venerable Garga, whose inner self (ātman) was refined through spiritual discipline (tapas), there was knowledge of the course of time and the movements of celestial bodies.

sanskrit: उत्पाता दारुणाश्चैव शुभाश्च जनमेजय ।सरस्वत्याः शुभे तीर्थे विहिता वै महात्मना ।तस्य नाम्ना च तत्तीर्थं गर्गस्रोत इति स्मृतम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: utpātā dāruṇāścaiva śubhāśca janamejaya ,sarasvatyāḥ śubhe tīrthe vihitā vai mahātmanā ,tasya nāmnā ca tattīrthaṁ gargasrota iti smṛtam.
english: O Janamejaya, both dreadful and auspicious portents were indeed established by the great soul (Mahātmā) in the auspicious sacred place on the Sarasvati River. And that sacred place is known by his name as Gargastrota.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 16. O Janamejaya, both dreadful and auspicious portents were indeed established by the great soul (Mahātmā) in the auspicious sacred place on the Sarasvati River. And that sacred place is known by his name as Gargastrota.

sanskrit: तत्र गर्गं महाभागमृषयः सुव्रता नृप ।उपासां चक्रिरे नित्यं कालज्ञानं प्रति प्रभो ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatra gargaṁ mahābhāgamṛṣayaḥ suvratā nṛpa ,upāsāṁ cakrire nityaṁ kālajñānaṁ prati prabho.
english: O King, O Lord, there the sages (ṛṣi) of good vows constantly attended upon the greatly fortunate Garga, concerning the knowledge of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 17. O King, O Lord, there the sages (ṛṣi) of good vows constantly attended upon the greatly fortunate Garga, concerning the knowledge of time.

sanskrit: तत्र गत्वा महाराज बलः श्वेतानुलेपनः ।विधिवद्धि धनं दत्त्वा मुनीनां भावितात्मनाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatra gatvā mahārāja balaḥ śvetānulepanaḥ ,vidhivaddhi dhanaṁ dattvā munīnāṁ bhāvitātmanām.
english: O Great King, having gone there, Bala (Balarāma), who was smeared with white paste, indeed gave wealth according to the sacred rites to the sages (muni) whose inner selves (ātman) were purified.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 18. O Great King, having gone there, Bala (Balarāma), who was smeared with white paste, indeed gave wealth according to the sacred rites to the sages (muni) whose inner selves (ātman) were purified.

sanskrit: उच्चावचांस्तथा भक्ष्यान्द्विजेभ्यो विप्रदाय सः ।नीलवासास्ततोऽगच्छच्छङ्खतीर्थं महायशाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: uccāvacāṁstathā bhakṣyāndvijebhyo vipradāya saḥ ,nīlavāsāstato'gacchacchaṅkhatīrthaṁ mahāyaśāḥ.
english: Having distributed various kinds of food to the brahmins (dvija), he, clad in blue garments, the greatly renowned one, then went to Śaṅkhatīrtha.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 19. Having distributed various kinds of food to the brahmins (dvija), he, clad in blue garments, the greatly renowned one, then went to Śaṅkhatīrtha.

sanskrit: तत्रापश्यन्महाशङ्खं महामेरुमिवोच्छ्रितम् ।श्वेतपर्वतसंकाशमृषिसंघैर्निषेवितम् ।सरस्वत्यास्तटे जातं नगं तालध्वजो बली ॥२०॥
transliteration: tatrāpaśyanmahāśaṅkhaṁ mahāmerumivocchritam ,śvetaparvatasaṁkāśamṛṣisaṁghairniṣevitam ,sarasvatyāstaṭe jātaṁ nagaṁ tāladhvajo balī.
english: There, the powerful Tāladhvaja saw a great conch, which was elevated like Mount Meru, resembled a white mountain, was frequented by multitudes of sages, and was situated like a mountain on the bank of the Sarasvatī river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 20. There, the powerful Tāladhvaja saw a great conch, which was elevated like Mount Meru, resembled a white mountain, was frequented by multitudes of sages, and was situated like a mountain on the bank of the Sarasvatī river.

sanskrit: यक्षा विद्याधराश्चैव राक्षसाश्चामितौजसः ।पिशाचाश्चामितबला यत्र सिद्धाः सहस्रशः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yakṣā vidyādharāścaiva rākṣasāścāmitaujasaḥ ,piśācāścāmitabalā yatra siddhāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Where Yakshas, Vidyadharas, and Rakshasas of immeasurable prowess, along with Pishachas of immense strength, and thousands of perfected beings (Siddhas) reside.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 21. Where Yakshas, Vidyadharas, and Rakshasas of immeasurable prowess, along with Pishachas of immense strength, and thousands of perfected beings (Siddhas) reside.

sanskrit: ते सर्वे ह्यशनं त्यक्त्वा फलं तस्य वनस्पतेः ।व्रतैश्च नियमैश्चैव काले काले स्म भुञ्जते ॥२२॥
transliteration: te sarve hyaśanaṁ tyaktvā phalaṁ tasya vanaspateḥ ,vrataiśca niyamaiścaiva kāle kāle sma bhuñjate.
english: Indeed, all of them would abandon other food and, through vows and disciplines, would regularly partake of the fruit of that particular tree from time to time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 22. Indeed, all of them would abandon other food and, through vows and disciplines, would regularly partake of the fruit of that particular tree from time to time.

sanskrit: प्राप्तैश्च नियमैस्तैस्तैर्विचरन्तः पृथक्पृथक् ।अदृश्यमाना मनुजैर्व्यचरन्पुरुषर्षभ ॥२३॥
transliteration: prāptaiśca niyamaistaistairvicarantaḥ pṛthakpṛthak ,adṛśyamānā manujairvyacaranpuruṣarṣabha.
english: O best among men, adhering to those various observances they had undertaken, they wandered about individually, moving unseen by humans.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 23. O best among men, adhering to those various observances they had undertaken, they wandered about individually, moving unseen by humans.

sanskrit: एवं ख्यातो नरपते लोकेऽस्मिन्स वनस्पतिः ।तत्र तीर्थं सरस्वत्याः पावनं लोकविश्रुतम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: evaṁ khyāto narapate loke'sminsa vanaspatiḥ ,tatra tīrthaṁ sarasvatyāḥ pāvanaṁ lokaviśrutam.
english: O king, that sacred tree (vanaspati) is renowned in this world in this manner. And there, a purifying sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Sarasvati is famous throughout the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 24. O king, that sacred tree (vanaspati) is renowned in this world in this manner. And there, a purifying sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Sarasvati is famous throughout the world.

sanskrit: तस्मिंश्च यदुशार्दूलो दत्त्वा तीर्थे यशस्विनाम् ।ताम्रायसानि भाण्डानि वस्त्राणि विविधानि च ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasmiṁśca yaduśārdūlo dattvā tīrthe yaśasvinām ,tāmrāyasāni bhāṇḍāni vastrāṇi vividhāni ca.
english: And in that sacred bathing place (tīrtha), the best among the Yadus, having donated various copper and iron vessels and garments to the glorious ones (e.g., Brahmins)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 25. And in that sacred bathing place (tīrtha), the best among the Yadus, having donated various copper and iron vessels and garments to the glorious ones (e.g., Brahmins)...

sanskrit: पूजयित्वा द्विजांश्चैव पूजितश्च तपोधनैः ।पुण्यं द्वैतवनं राजन्नाजगाम हलायुधः ॥२६॥
transliteration: pūjayitvā dvijāṁścaiva pūjitaśca tapodhanaiḥ ,puṇyaṁ dvaitavanaṁ rājannājagāma halāyudhaḥ.
english: O king, after honoring the Brahmins (dvijān) and himself being honored by the ascetics (tapodhanaiḥ), Balarama (halāyudha) then came to the sacred Dwaita forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 26. O king, after honoring the Brahmins (dvijān) and himself being honored by the ascetics (tapodhanaiḥ), Balarama (halāyudha) then came to the sacred Dwaita forest.

sanskrit: तत्र गत्वा मुनीन्दृष्ट्वा नानावेषधरान्बलः ।आप्लुत्य सलिले चापि पूजयामास वै द्विजान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tatra gatvā munīndṛṣṭvā nānāveṣadharānbalaḥ ,āplutya salile cāpi pūjayāmāsa vai dvijān.
english: Having gone there, Balarama saw the sages, who were wearing various attires. After bathing in the water, he then worshipped the brahmins (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 27. Having gone there, Balarama saw the sages, who were wearing various attires. After bathing in the water, he then worshipped the brahmins (dvija).

sanskrit: तथैव दत्त्वा विप्रेभ्यः परिभोगान्सुपुष्कलान् ।ततः प्रायाद्बलो राजन्दक्षिणेन सरस्वतीम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tathaiva dattvā viprebhyaḥ paribhogānsupuṣkalān ,tataḥ prāyādbalo rājandakṣiṇena sarasvatīm.
english: O king, in the same way, after giving ample provisions to the brahmins (vipra), Balarama then proceeded south along the Sarasvati River.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 28. O king, in the same way, after giving ample provisions to the brahmins (vipra), Balarama then proceeded south along the Sarasvati River.

sanskrit: गत्वा चैव महाबाहुर्नातिदूरं महायशाः ।धर्मात्मा नागधन्वानं तीर्थमागमदच्युतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: gatvā caiva mahābāhurnātidūraṁ mahāyaśāḥ ,dharmātmā nāgadhanvānaṁ tīrthamāgamadacyutaḥ.
english: And having traveled not very far, the mighty-armed, greatly renowned, righteous (dharmātman) and infallible (acyuta) Balarama arrived at the sacred place (tīrtha) called Nāgadhanvan.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 29. And having traveled not very far, the mighty-armed, greatly renowned, righteous (dharmātman) and infallible (acyuta) Balarama arrived at the sacred place (tīrtha) called Nāgadhanvan.

sanskrit: यत्र पन्नगराजस्य वासुकेः संनिवेशनम् ।महाद्युतेर्महाराज बहुभिः पन्नगैर्वृतम् ।यत्रासन्नृषयः सिद्धाः सहस्राणि चतुर्दश ॥३०॥
transliteration: yatra pannagarājasya vāsukeḥ saṁniveśanam ,mahādyutermahārāja bahubhiḥ pannagairvṛtam ,yatrāsannṛṣayaḥ siddhāḥ sahasrāṇi caturdaśa.
english: O great king, this was the dwelling of the greatly radiant Vasuki, the king of serpents, surrounded by many other serpents. It was also the place where fourteen thousand sages and perfected beings (siddha) resided.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 30. O great king, this was the dwelling of the greatly radiant Vasuki, the king of serpents, surrounded by many other serpents. It was also the place where fourteen thousand sages and perfected beings (siddha) resided.

sanskrit: यत्र देवाः समागम्य वासुकिं पन्नगोत्तमम् ।सर्वपन्नगराजानमभ्यषिञ्चन्यथाविधि ।पन्नगेभ्यो भयं तत्र विद्यते न स्म कौरव ॥३१॥
transliteration: yatra devāḥ samāgamya vāsukiṁ pannagottamam ,sarvapannagarājānamabhyaṣiñcanyathāvidhi ,pannagebhyo bhayaṁ tatra vidyate na sma kaurava.
english: O Kaurava, where the gods, having assembled, duly consecrated Vasuki, who is the foremost of serpents and the king of all serpents, there was no fear for the serpents (pannaga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 31. O Kaurava, where the gods, having assembled, duly consecrated Vasuki, who is the foremost of serpents and the king of all serpents, there was no fear for the serpents (pannaga).

sanskrit: तत्रापि विधिवद्दत्त्वा विप्रेभ्यो रत्नसंचयान् ।प्रायात्प्राचीं दिशं राजन्दीप्यमानः स्वतेजसा ॥३२॥
transliteration: tatrāpi vidhivaddattvā viprebhyo ratnasaṁcayān ,prāyātprācīṁ diśaṁ rājandīpyamānaḥ svatejasā.
english: O King, having also duly (vidhivat) given heaps of jewels to the brahmins (vipra) there, he proceeded towards the eastern direction, shining with his own splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 32. O King, having also duly (vidhivat) given heaps of jewels to the brahmins (vipra) there, he proceeded towards the eastern direction, shining with his own splendor.

sanskrit: आप्लुत्य बहुशो हृष्टस्तेषु तीर्थेषु लाङ्गली ।दत्त्वा वसु द्विजातिभ्यो जगामाति तपस्विनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: āplutya bahuśo hṛṣṭasteṣu tīrtheṣu lāṅgalī ,dattvā vasu dvijātibhyo jagāmāti tapasvinaḥ.
english: The wielder of the plough (Lāṅgalī), delighted, having bathed many times in those sacred bathing places (tīrtha) and having given wealth to the twice-born (dvijāti), then proceeded to (the abode of) a great ascetic (tapasvin).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 33. The wielder of the plough (Lāṅgalī), delighted, having bathed many times in those sacred bathing places (tīrtha) and having given wealth to the twice-born (dvijāti), then proceeded to (the abode of) a great ascetic (tapasvin).

sanskrit: तत्रस्थानृषिसंघांस्तानभिवाद्य हलायुधः ।ततो रामोऽगमत्तीर्थमृषिभिः सेवितं महत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tatrasthānṛṣisaṁghāṁstānabhivādya halāyudhaḥ ,tato rāmo'gamattīrthamṛṣibhiḥ sevitaṁ mahat.
english: Halāyudha, having greeted those groups of sages (ṛṣisaṃgha) who resided there, then Rama went to the great sacred bathing place (tīrtha) that was revered by sages (ṛṣi).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 34. Halāyudha, having greeted those groups of sages (ṛṣisaṃgha) who resided there, then Rama went to the great sacred bathing place (tīrtha) that was revered by sages (ṛṣi).

sanskrit: यत्र भूयो निववृते प्राङ्मुखा वै सरस्वती ।ऋषीणां नैमिषेयाणामवेक्षार्थं महात्मनाम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: yatra bhūyo nivavṛte prāṅmukhā vai sarasvatī ,ṛṣīṇāṁ naimiṣeyāṇāmavekṣārthaṁ mahātmanām.
english: At that spot, the Sarasvati river, facing east, indeed turned back again, in order to attend to the great-souled sages of Naimiṣa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 35. At that spot, the Sarasvati river, facing east, indeed turned back again, in order to attend to the great-souled sages of Naimiṣa.

sanskrit: निवृत्तां तां सरिच्छ्रेष्ठां तत्र दृष्ट्वा तु लाङ्गली ।बभूव विस्मितो राजन्बलः श्वेतानुलेपनः ॥३६॥
transliteration: nivṛttāṁ tāṁ saricchreṣṭhāṁ tatra dṛṣṭvā tu lāṅgalī ,babhūva vismito rājanbalaḥ śvetānulepanaḥ.
english: Having seen that preeminent river (Sarasvati) turned back there, Balarama, O King, who was anointed with white substances, became astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 36. Having seen that preeminent river (Sarasvati) turned back there, Balarama, O King, who was anointed with white substances, became astonished.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कस्मात्सरस्वती ब्रह्मन्निवृत्ता प्राङ्मुखी ततः ।व्याख्यातुमेतदिच्छामि सर्वमध्वर्युसत्तम ॥३७॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kasmātsarasvatī brahmannivṛttā prāṅmukhī tataḥ ,vyākhyātumetadicchāmi sarvamadhvaryusattama.
english: Janamejaya said: 'O Brahmin, why did the Sarasvati river turn back from that point, facing east? O best among the adhvaryu priests, I wish for you to explain all this to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 37. Janamejaya said: 'O Brahmin, why did the Sarasvati river turn back from that point, facing east? O best among the adhvaryu priests, I wish for you to explain all this to me.'

sanskrit: कस्मिंश्च कारणे तत्र विस्मितो यदुनन्दनः ।विनिवृत्ता सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा कथमेतद्द्विजोत्तम ॥३८॥
transliteration: kasmiṁśca kāraṇe tatra vismito yadunandanaḥ ,vinivṛttā saricchreṣṭhā kathametaddvijottama.
english: And for what reason was the descendant of Yadu (Balarama) astonished there? How did this preeminent river (Sarasvati) turn back, O best of the twice-born (dvijottama)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 38. And for what reason was the descendant of Yadu (Balarama) astonished there? How did this preeminent river (Sarasvati) turn back, O best of the twice-born (dvijottama)?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।पूर्वं कृतयुगे राजन्नैमिषेयास्तपस्विनः ।वर्तमाने सुबहुले सत्रे द्वादशवार्षिके ।ऋषयो बहवो राजंस्तत्र संप्रतिपेदिरे ॥३९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pūrvaṁ kṛtayuge rājannaimiṣeyāstapasvinaḥ ,vartamāne subahule satre dvādaśavārṣike ,ṛṣayo bahavo rājaṁstatra saṁpratipedire.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O King (Janamejaya), previously, in the Kṛta age, while a very extensive twelve-year (Vedic ritual) (sattra) was ongoing by the ascetics of Naimiṣa forest, many sages, O King (Janamejaya), gathered there."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 39. Vaiśampāyana said: "O King (Janamejaya), previously, in the Kṛta age, while a very extensive twelve-year (Vedic ritual) (sattra) was ongoing by the ascetics of Naimiṣa forest, many sages, O King (Janamejaya), gathered there."

sanskrit: उषित्वा च महाभागास्तस्मिन्सत्रे यथाविधि ।निवृत्ते नैमिषेये वै सत्रे द्वादशवार्षिके ।आजग्मुरृषयस्तत्र बहवस्तीर्थकारणात् ॥४०॥
transliteration: uṣitvā ca mahābhāgāstasminsatre yathāvidhi ,nivṛtte naimiṣeye vai satre dvādaśavārṣike ,ājagmurṛṣayastatra bahavastīrthakāraṇāt.
english: And after those highly esteemed ones had resided in that (Vedic ritual) (sattra) according to the prescribed rites, when the twelve-year (Vedic ritual) (sattra) in Naimiṣa had concluded, many sages came there for the purpose of visiting holy sites.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 40. And after those highly esteemed ones had resided in that (Vedic ritual) (sattra) according to the prescribed rites, when the twelve-year (Vedic ritual) (sattra) in Naimiṣa had concluded, many sages came there for the purpose of visiting holy sites.

sanskrit: ऋषीणां बहुलत्वात्तु सरस्वत्या विशां पते ।तीर्थानि नगरायन्ते कूले वै दक्षिणे तदा ॥४१॥
transliteration: ṛṣīṇāṁ bahulatvāttu sarasvatyā viśāṁ pate ,tīrthāni nagarāyante kūle vai dakṣiṇe tadā.
english: But O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), due to the great multitude of sages (ṛṣi) along the Sarasvatī river, the holy places (tīrtha) on its southern bank then truly appeared like cities.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 41. But O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), due to the great multitude of sages (ṛṣi) along the Sarasvatī river, the holy places (tīrtha) on its southern bank then truly appeared like cities.

sanskrit: समन्तपञ्चकं यावत्तावत्ते द्विजसत्तमाः ।तीर्थलोभान्नरव्याघ्र नद्यास्तीरं समाश्रिताः ॥४२॥
transliteration: samantapañcakaṁ yāvattāvatte dvijasattamāḥ ,tīrthalobhānnaravyāghra nadyāstīraṁ samāśritāḥ.
english: O tiger among men (naravyāghra), those foremost among the twice-born (dvija), driven by their desire for holy places (tīrtha), took residence along the river's bank, extending as far as Samantapañcaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 42. O tiger among men (naravyāghra), those foremost among the twice-born (dvija), driven by their desire for holy places (tīrtha), took residence along the river's bank, extending as far as Samantapañcaka.

sanskrit: जुह्वतां तत्र तेषां तु मुनीनां भावितात्मनाम् ।स्वाध्यायेनापि महता बभूवुः पूरिता दिशः ॥४३॥
transliteration: juhvatāṁ tatra teṣāṁ tu munīnāṁ bhāvitātmanām ,svādhyāyenāpi mahatā babhūvuḥ pūritā diśaḥ.
english: There, the directions became filled, indeed, by the great self-recitation (svādhyāya) of those sages (muni) who were offering oblations and whose inner selves (ātman) were pure.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 43. There, the directions became filled, indeed, by the great self-recitation (svādhyāya) of those sages (muni) who were offering oblations and whose inner selves (ātman) were pure.

sanskrit: अग्निहोत्रैस्ततस्तेषां हूयमानैर्महात्मनाम् ।अशोभत सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा दीप्यमानैः समन्ततः ॥४४॥
transliteration: agnihotraistatasteṣāṁ hūyamānairmahātmanām ,aśobhata saricchreṣṭhā dīpyamānaiḥ samantataḥ.
english: Then, with the (Vedic fire rituals) (agnihotra) being offered by those great souls, the best of rivers shone, illuminated from all directions by the glowing flames.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 44. Then, with the (Vedic fire rituals) (agnihotra) being offered by those great souls, the best of rivers shone, illuminated from all directions by the glowing flames.

sanskrit: वालखिल्या महाराज अश्मकुट्टाश्च तापसाः ।दन्तोलूखलिनश्चान्ये संप्रक्षालास्तथापरे ॥४५॥
transliteration: vālakhilyā mahārāja aśmakuṭṭāśca tāpasāḥ ,dantolūkhalinaścānye saṁprakṣālāstathāpare.
english: O great king, there were ascetics (tapasvin) known as Vālakhilyas, others who pounded their food with stones (aśmakuṭṭa), and still others who ground food with their teeth (dantolūkhala), and similarly, there were those who practiced ritual cleansing (saṃprakṣāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 45. O great king, there were ascetics (tapasvin) known as Vālakhilyas, others who pounded their food with stones (aśmakuṭṭa), and still others who ground food with their teeth (dantolūkhala), and similarly, there were those who practiced ritual cleansing (saṃprakṣāla).

sanskrit: वायुभक्षा जलाहाराः पर्णभक्षाश्च तापसाः ।नानानियमयुक्ताश्च तथा स्थण्डिलशायिनः ॥४६॥
transliteration: vāyubhakṣā jalāhārāḥ parṇabhakṣāśca tāpasāḥ ,nānāniyamayuktāśca tathā sthaṇḍilaśāyinaḥ.
english: And there were ascetics (tapasvin) who subsisted on air (vāyubhakṣa), on water (jalāhāra), or on leaves (parṇabhakṣa); and others who were committed to various vows (nānāniyamayukta), and similarly, those who slept on the bare ground (sthaṇḍilaśāyin).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 46. And there were ascetics (tapasvin) who subsisted on air (vāyubhakṣa), on water (jalāhāra), or on leaves (parṇabhakṣa); and others who were committed to various vows (nānāniyamayukta), and similarly, those who slept on the bare ground (sthaṇḍilaśāyin).

sanskrit: आसन्वै मुनयस्तत्र सरस्वत्याः समीपतः ।शोभयन्तः सरिच्छ्रेष्ठां गङ्गामिव दिवौकसः ॥४७॥
transliteration: āsanvai munayastatra sarasvatyāḥ samīpataḥ ,śobhayantaḥ saricchreṣṭhāṁ gaṅgāmiva divaukasaḥ.
english: Indeed, sages were present there near the Sarasvati, adorning the best of rivers, just as the gods (divaukasaḥ) adorn the Gaṅgā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 47. Indeed, sages were present there near the Sarasvati, adorning the best of rivers, just as the gods (divaukasaḥ) adorn the Gaṅgā.

sanskrit: ततः पश्चात्समापेतुरृषयः सत्रयाजिनः ।तेऽवकाशं न ददृशुः कुरुक्षेत्रे महाव्रताः ॥४८॥
transliteration: tataḥ paścātsamāpeturṛṣayaḥ satrayājinaḥ ,te'vakāśaṁ na dadṛśuḥ kurukṣetre mahāvratāḥ.
english: Then, afterwards, the sages (ṛṣi), who were performing sacrificial sessions (sattra-yājin), gathered. Those who observed great vows (mahāvratāḥ) found no space in Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 48. Then, afterwards, the sages (ṛṣi), who were performing sacrificial sessions (sattra-yājin), gathered. Those who observed great vows (mahāvratāḥ) found no space in Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: ततो यज्ञोपवीतैस्ते तत्तीर्थं निर्मिमाय वै ।जुहुवुश्चाग्निहोत्राणि चक्रुश्च विविधाः क्रियाः ॥४९॥
transliteration: tato yajñopavītaiste tattīrthaṁ nirmimāya vai ,juhuvuścāgnihotrāṇi cakruśca vividhāḥ kriyāḥ.
english: Then, indeed, they created that sacred ford (tīrtha) by means of their sacred threads (yajñopavīta). And they offered Agnihotra sacrifices and performed various rituals.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 49. Then, indeed, they created that sacred ford (tīrtha) by means of their sacred threads (yajñopavīta). And they offered Agnihotra sacrifices and performed various rituals.

sanskrit: ततस्तमृषिसंघातं निराशं चिन्तयान्वितम् ।दर्शयामास राजेन्द्र तेषामर्थे सरस्वती ॥५०॥
transliteration: tatastamṛṣisaṁghātaṁ nirāśaṁ cintayānvitam ,darśayāmāsa rājendra teṣāmarthe sarasvatī.
english: Then, O King (rājendra), Sarasvati (the river/goddess) revealed [a solution or a path] to that multitude of sages (ṛṣisaṃghāta), who were despondent (nirāśa) and filled with anxiety (cintayānvita), for their sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 50. Then, O King (rājendra), Sarasvati (the river/goddess) revealed [a solution or a path] to that multitude of sages (ṛṣisaṃghāta), who were despondent (nirāśa) and filled with anxiety (cintayānvita), for their sake.

sanskrit: ततः कुञ्जान्बहून्कृत्वा संनिवृत्ता सरिद्वरा ।ऋषीणां पुण्यतपसां कारुण्याज्जनमेजय ॥५१॥
transliteration: tataḥ kuñjānbahūnkṛtvā saṁnivṛttā saridvarā ,ṛṣīṇāṁ puṇyatapasāṁ kāruṇyājjanamejaya.
english: Then, O Janamejaya, the best of rivers, having created many groves, turned back out of compassion for the sages whose austerities (tapas) were meritorious.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 51. Then, O Janamejaya, the best of rivers, having created many groves, turned back out of compassion for the sages whose austerities (tapas) were meritorious.

sanskrit: ततो निवृत्य राजेन्द्र तेषामर्थे सरस्वती ।भूयः प्रतीच्यभिमुखी सुस्राव सरितां वरा ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato nivṛtya rājendra teṣāmarthe sarasvatī ,bhūyaḥ pratīcyabhimukhī susrāva saritāṁ varā.
english: Then, O best of kings, the Sarasvati, the best of rivers, having turned back for their sake, flowed again, facing westward.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 52. Then, O best of kings, the Sarasvati, the best of rivers, having turned back for their sake, flowed again, facing westward.

sanskrit: अमोघा गमनं कृत्वा तेषां भूयो व्रजाम्यहम् ।इत्यद्भुतं महच्चक्रे ततो राजन्महानदी ॥५३॥
transliteration: amoghā gamanaṁ kṛtvā teṣāṁ bhūyo vrajāmyaham ,ityadbhutaṁ mahaccakre tato rājanmahānadī.
english: Thinking, 'I shall go to them again, making my journey fruitful,' the great river then performed a great wonder, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 53. Thinking, 'I shall go to them again, making my journey fruitful,' the great river then performed a great wonder, O king.

sanskrit: एवं स कुञ्जो राजेन्द्र नैमिषेय इति स्मृतः ।कुरुक्षेत्रे कुरुश्रेष्ठ कुरुष्व महतीः क्रियाः ॥५४॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa kuñjo rājendra naimiṣeya iti smṛtaḥ ,kurukṣetre kuruśreṣṭha kuruṣva mahatīḥ kriyāḥ.
english: Thus, O best of kings, that grove is remembered as Naimiṣeya. O best of Kurus, perform great rituals in Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 54. Thus, O best of kings, that grove is remembered as Naimiṣeya. O best of Kurus, perform great rituals in Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: तत्र कुञ्जान्बहून्दृष्ट्वा संनिवृत्तां च तां नदीम् ।बभूव विस्मयस्तत्र रामस्याथ महात्मनः ॥५५॥
transliteration: tatra kuñjānbahūndṛṣṭvā saṁnivṛttāṁ ca tāṁ nadīm ,babhūva vismayastatra rāmasyātha mahātmanaḥ.
english: Then, upon seeing many groves there and that river which had turned back, great wonder arose in the great-souled Rama.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 55. Then, upon seeing many groves there and that river which had turned back, great wonder arose in the great-souled Rama.

sanskrit: उपस्पृश्य तु तत्रापि विधिवद्यदुनन्दनः ।दत्त्वा दायान्द्विजातिभ्यो भाण्डानि विविधानि च ।भक्ष्यं पेयं च विविधं ब्राह्मणान्प्रत्यपादयत् ॥५६॥
transliteration: upaspṛśya tu tatrāpi vidhivadyadunandanaḥ ,dattvā dāyāndvijātibhyo bhāṇḍāni vividhāni ca ,bhakṣyaṁ peyaṁ ca vividhaṁ brāhmaṇānpratyapādayat.
english: Having duly performed purificatory rites there, the delight of the Yadus (Yadunandana) then bestowed gifts upon the twice-born (dvija), including various vessels, and presented them with diverse food and drink.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 56. Having duly performed purificatory rites there, the delight of the Yadus (Yadunandana) then bestowed gifts upon the twice-born (dvija), including various vessels, and presented them with diverse food and drink.

sanskrit: ततः प्रायाद्बलो राजन्पूज्यमानो द्विजातिभिः ।सरस्वतीतीर्थवरं नानाद्विजगणायुतम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyādbalo rājanpūjyamāno dvijātibhiḥ ,sarasvatītīrthavaraṁ nānādvijagaṇāyutam.
english: O King, then Balarama, being honored by the twice-born (dvija), proceeded to the excellent sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Sarasvati, which was teeming with various groups of twice-born (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 57. O King, then Balarama, being honored by the twice-born (dvija), proceeded to the excellent sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Sarasvati, which was teeming with various groups of twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: बदरेङ्गुदकाश्मर्यप्लक्षाश्वत्थविभीतकैः ।पनसैश्च पलाशैश्च करीरैः पीलुभिस्तथा ॥५८॥
transliteration: badareṅgudakāśmaryaplakṣāśvatthavibhītakaiḥ ,panasaiśca palāśaiśca karīraiḥ pīlubhistathā.
english: (The tīrtha was adorned) with jujube, ingudi, kāśmarya, plakṣa, aśvattha, and vibhītaka trees; and similarly with jackfruit, palāśa, karīra, and pīlu trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 58. (The tīrtha was adorned) with jujube, ingudi, kāśmarya, plakṣa, aśvattha, and vibhītaka trees; and similarly with jackfruit, palāśa, karīra, and pīlu trees.

sanskrit: सरस्वतीतीररुहैर्बन्धनैः स्यन्दनैस्तथा ।परूषकवनैश्चैव बिल्वैराम्रातकैस्तथा ॥५९॥
transliteration: sarasvatītīraruhairbandhanaiḥ syandanaistathā ,parūṣakavanaiścaiva bilvairāmrātakaistathā.
english: It was adorned with trees growing on the banks of the Sarasvati, along with bandhana and syandana trees, and moreover, with groves of parūṣaka trees, and also with bilva and āmrātaka trees.
