search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 59. It was adorned with trees growing on the banks of the Sarasvati, along with bandhana and syandana trees, and moreover, with groves of parūṣaka trees, and also with bilva and āmrātaka trees.

sanskrit: अतिमुक्तकषण्डैश्च पारिजातैश्च शोभितम् ।कदलीवनभूयिष्ठमिष्टं कान्तं मनोरमम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: atimuktakaṣaṇḍaiśca pārijātaiśca śobhitam ,kadalīvanabhūyiṣṭhamiṣṭaṁ kāntaṁ manoramam.
english: Adorned with clumps of atimuktaka and pārijāta trees, it was abundant with banana groves, desired, lovely, and delightful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 60. Adorned with clumps of atimuktaka and pārijāta trees, it was abundant with banana groves, desired, lovely, and delightful.

sanskrit: वाय्वम्बुफलपर्णादैर्दन्तोलूखलिकैरपि ।तथाश्मकुट्टैर्वानेयैर्मुनिभिर्बहुभिर्वृतम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: vāyvambuphalaparṇādairdantolūkhalikairapi ,tathāśmakuṭṭairvāneyairmunibhirbahubhirvṛtam.
english: It was surrounded by many sages, including forest hermits, those who subsisted on air, water, fruits, and leaves, and also those who used their teeth as mortars or crushed their food with stones.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 61. It was surrounded by many sages, including forest hermits, those who subsisted on air, water, fruits, and leaves, and also those who used their teeth as mortars or crushed their food with stones.

sanskrit: स्वाध्यायघोषसंघुष्टं मृगयूथशताकुलम् ।अहिंस्रैर्धर्मपरमैर्नृभिरत्यन्तसेवितम् ॥६२॥
transliteration: svādhyāyaghoṣasaṁghuṣṭaṁ mṛgayūthaśatākulam ,ahiṁsrairdharmaparamairnṛbhiratyantasevitam.
english: It was resounding with the sounds of Vedic recitation (svādhyāya), filled with hundreds of herds of deer, and intensely frequented by non-violent people devoted to natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 62. It was resounding with the sounds of Vedic recitation (svādhyāya), filled with hundreds of herds of deer, and intensely frequented by non-violent people devoted to natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: सप्तसारस्वतं तीर्थमाजगाम हलायुधः ।यत्र मङ्कणकः सिद्धस्तपस्तेपे महामुनिः ॥६३॥
transliteration: saptasārasvataṁ tīrthamājagāma halāyudhaḥ ,yatra maṅkaṇakaḥ siddhastapastepe mahāmuniḥ.
english: Hālāyudha arrived at the Saptasārasvata sacred place (tīrtha), where the great sage (mahāmuni) Maṅkaṇaka, who had achieved spiritual perfection (siddha), performed severe austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 36 Verse 63. Hālāyudha arrived at the Saptasārasvata sacred place (tīrtha), where the great sage (mahāmuni) Maṅkaṇaka, who had achieved spiritual perfection (siddha), performed severe austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।सप्तसारस्वतं कस्मात्कश्च मङ्कणको मुनिः ।कथं सिद्धश्च भगवान्कश्चास्य नियमोऽभवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,saptasārasvataṁ kasmātkaśca maṅkaṇako muniḥ ,kathaṁ siddhaśca bhagavānkaścāsya niyamo'bhavat.
english: Janamejaya said: "Why is it called Saptasārasvata? And who was the sage (muni) Maṅkaṇaka? How did he achieve spiritual perfection (siddha), and what was his practice (niyama)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "Why is it called Saptasārasvata? And who was the sage (muni) Maṅkaṇaka? How did he achieve spiritual perfection (siddha), and what was his practice (niyama)?"

sanskrit: कस्य वंशे समुत्पन्नः किं चाधीतं द्विजोत्तम ।एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं विधिवद्द्विजसत्तम ॥२॥
transliteration: kasya vaṁśe samutpannaḥ kiṁ cādhītaṁ dvijottama ,etadicchāmyahaṁ śrotuṁ vidhivaddvijasattama.
english: "In whose lineage was he born, and what did he study, O best of the twice-born (dvijottama)? I desire to hear this according to the rules (vidhivat), O best of Brahmins (dvijasattama)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 2. "In whose lineage was he born, and what did he study, O best of the twice-born (dvijottama)? I desire to hear this according to the rules (vidhivat), O best of Brahmins (dvijasattama)."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।राजन्सप्त सरस्वत्यो याभिर्व्याप्तमिदं जगत् ।आहूता बलवद्भिर्हि तत्र तत्र सरस्वती ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,rājansapta sarasvatyo yābhirvyāptamidaṁ jagat ,āhūtā balavadbhirhi tatra tatra sarasvatī.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O King, there are seven Sarasvatīs, by whom this world (jagat) is pervaded. Indeed, Sarasvatī was invoked in various places by powerful beings."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 3. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O King, there are seven Sarasvatīs, by whom this world (jagat) is pervaded. Indeed, Sarasvatī was invoked in various places by powerful beings."

sanskrit: सुप्रभा काञ्चनाक्षी च विशाला मानसह्रदा ।सरस्वती ओघवती सुवेणुर्विमलोदका ॥४॥
transliteration: suprabhā kāñcanākṣī ca viśālā mānasahradā ,sarasvatī oghavatī suveṇurvimalodakā.
english: She is radiant, golden-eyed, vast, and possesses lakes like Manasa; Saraswati flows with strong currents, adorned with beautiful reeds, and has pure waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 4. She is radiant, golden-eyed, vast, and possesses lakes like Manasa; Saraswati flows with strong currents, adorned with beautiful reeds, and has pure waters.

sanskrit: पितामहस्य महतो वर्तमाने महीतले ।वितते यज्ञवाटे वै समेतेषु द्विजातिषु ॥५॥
transliteration: pitāmahasya mahato vartamāne mahītale ,vitate yajñavāṭe vai sameteṣu dvijātiṣu.
english: When the great Lord Brahmā's (Pitāmaha's) (Vedic ritual) was being performed on earth, in the expansive ritual ground (yajñavāṭa), and indeed, when the twice-born (dvijāti) were assembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 5. When the great Lord Brahmā's (Pitāmaha's) (Vedic ritual) was being performed on earth, in the expansive ritual ground (yajñavāṭa), and indeed, when the twice-born (dvijāti) were assembled.

sanskrit: पुण्याहघोषैर्विमलैर्वेदानां निनदैस्तथा ।देवेषु चैव व्यग्रेषु तस्मिन्यज्ञविधौ तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: puṇyāhaghoṣairvimalairvedānāṁ ninadaistathā ,deveṣu caiva vyagreṣu tasminyajñavidhau tadā.
english: At that time, amidst pure declarations of auspiciousness and the chants of the Vedas, and with the gods themselves engaged in that Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 6. At that time, amidst pure declarations of auspiciousness and the chants of the Vedas, and with the gods themselves engaged in that Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: तत्र चैव महाराज दीक्षिते प्रपितामहे ।यजतस्तत्र सत्रेण सर्वकामसमृद्धिना ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra caiva mahārāja dīkṣite prapitāmahe ,yajatastatra satreṇa sarvakāmasamṛddhinā.
english: O great king, when the great-grandfather (Brahma) was initiated there, he performed (the Vedic ritual) there with a "sattra" (Vedic ritual) that fulfilled all desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 7. O great king, when the great-grandfather (Brahma) was initiated there, he performed (the Vedic ritual) there with a "sattra" (Vedic ritual) that fulfilled all desires.

sanskrit: मनसा चिन्तिता ह्यर्था धर्मार्थकुशलैस्तदा ।उपतिष्ठन्ति राजेन्द्र द्विजातींस्तत्र तत्र ह ॥८॥
transliteration: manasā cintitā hyarthā dharmārthakuśalaistadā ,upatiṣṭhanti rājendra dvijātīṁstatra tatra ha.
english: O King of kings, the desires conceived by the mind, specifically by those skilled in the constitution (dharma) and material prosperity (artha), indeed manifested themselves here and there to the twice-born (dvijātī) at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 8. O King of kings, the desires conceived by the mind, specifically by those skilled in the constitution (dharma) and material prosperity (artha), indeed manifested themselves here and there to the twice-born (dvijātī) at that time.

sanskrit: जगुश्च तत्र गन्धर्वा ननृतुश्चाप्सरोगणाः ।वादित्राणि च दिव्यानि वादयामासुरञ्जसा ॥९॥
transliteration: jaguśca tatra gandharvā nanṛtuścāpsarogaṇāḥ ,vāditrāṇi ca divyāni vādayāmāsurañjasā.
english: There, the Gandharvas sang, and groups of Apsaras danced. And they also made divine musical instruments sound forth beautifully.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 9. There, the Gandharvas sang, and groups of Apsaras danced. And they also made divine musical instruments sound forth beautifully.

sanskrit: तस्य यज्ञस्य संपत्त्या तुतुषुर्देवता अपि ।विस्मयं परमं जग्मुः किमु मानुषयोनयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasya yajñasya saṁpattyā tutuṣurdevatā api ,vismayaṁ paramaṁ jagmuḥ kimu mānuṣayonayaḥ.
english: Even the deities were pleased by the successful completion of that Vedic ritual (yajña) and experienced supreme astonishment; how much more so then those of human birth (mānuṣayonayaḥ)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 10. Even the deities were pleased by the successful completion of that Vedic ritual (yajña) and experienced supreme astonishment; how much more so then those of human birth (mānuṣayonayaḥ)?

sanskrit: वर्तमाने तथा यज्ञे पुष्करस्थे पितामहे ।अब्रुवन्नृषयो राजन्नायं यज्ञो महाफलः ।न दृश्यते सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा यस्मादिह सरस्वती ॥११॥
transliteration: vartamāne tathā yajñe puṣkarasthe pitāmahe ,abruvannṛṣayo rājannāyaṁ yajño mahāphalaḥ ,na dṛśyate saricchreṣṭhā yasmādiha sarasvatī.
english: O King, while that Vedic ritual (yajña) was in progress, and the Grandfather (Brahma) was situated in Puṣkara, the sages said, 'This Vedic ritual (yajña) will not bear great fruit, because the best of rivers, Sarasvatī, is not seen here.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 11. O King, while that Vedic ritual (yajña) was in progress, and the Grandfather (Brahma) was situated in Puṣkara, the sages said, 'This Vedic ritual (yajña) will not bear great fruit, because the best of rivers, Sarasvatī, is not seen here.'

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा भगवान्प्रीतः सस्माराथ सरस्वतीम् ।पितामहेन यजता आहूता पुष्करेषु वै ।सुप्रभा नाम राजेन्द्र नाम्ना तत्र सरस्वती ॥१२॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā bhagavānprītaḥ sasmārātha sarasvatīm ,pitāmahena yajatā āhūtā puṣkareṣu vai ,suprabhā nāma rājendra nāmnā tatra sarasvatī.
english: Having heard that, the revered lord (Bhagavān) was pleased and then remembered Sarasvatī. She had been invoked in Puṣkara by the grandfather (Brahmā) during his performance of a Vedic ritual (yajña). O best of kings, that Sarasvatī there was indeed known by the name Suprabhā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 12. Having heard that, the revered lord (Bhagavān) was pleased and then remembered Sarasvatī. She had been invoked in Puṣkara by the grandfather (Brahmā) during his performance of a Vedic ritual (yajña). O best of kings, that Sarasvatī there was indeed known by the name Suprabhā.

sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा मुनयस्तुष्टा वेगयुक्तां सरस्वतीम् ।पितामहं मानयन्तीं क्रतुं ते बहु मेनिरे ॥१३॥
transliteration: tāṁ dṛṣṭvā munayastuṣṭā vegayuktāṁ sarasvatīm ,pitāmahaṁ mānayantīṁ kratuṁ te bahu menire.
english: Having seen that Sarasvatī, who was swift-flowing and honoring the grandfather (Brahmā), the sages (munis) were greatly pleased. They highly regarded that sacrifice (kratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 13. Having seen that Sarasvatī, who was swift-flowing and honoring the grandfather (Brahmā), the sages (munis) were greatly pleased. They highly regarded that sacrifice (kratu).

sanskrit: एवमेषा सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा पुष्करेषु सरस्वती ।पितामहार्थं संभूता तुष्ट्यर्थं च मनीषिणाम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: evameṣā saricchreṣṭhā puṣkareṣu sarasvatī ,pitāmahārthaṁ saṁbhūtā tuṣṭyarthaṁ ca manīṣiṇām.
english: Thus, this Sarasvatī, the foremost of rivers in Puṣkara, originated for the purpose of the grandfather (Brahmā) and for the satisfaction of the wise sages (manīṣis).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 14. Thus, this Sarasvatī, the foremost of rivers in Puṣkara, originated for the purpose of the grandfather (Brahmā) and for the satisfaction of the wise sages (manīṣis).

sanskrit: नैमिषे मुनयो राजन्समागम्य समासते ।तत्र चित्राः कथा ह्यासन्वेदं प्रति जनेश्वर ॥१५॥
transliteration: naimiṣe munayo rājansamāgamya samāsate ,tatra citrāḥ kathā hyāsanvedaṁ prati janeśvara.
english: O King, the sages (munis), having assembled in Naimiṣa, reside there. O lord of men, there were indeed many varied and interesting stories concerning the Veda.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 15. O King, the sages (munis), having assembled in Naimiṣa, reside there. O lord of men, there were indeed many varied and interesting stories concerning the Veda.

sanskrit: तत्र ते मुनयो ह्यासन्नानास्वाध्यायवेदिनः ।ते समागम्य मुनयः सस्मरुर्वै सरस्वतीम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tatra te munayo hyāsannānāsvādhyāyavedinaḥ ,te samāgamya munayaḥ sasmarurvai sarasvatīm.
english: Indeed, those sages who were present there were not well-versed in scriptural knowledge. Having gathered together, those sages indeed remembered Sarasvatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 16. Indeed, those sages who were present there were not well-versed in scriptural knowledge. Having gathered together, those sages indeed remembered Sarasvatī.

sanskrit: सा तु ध्याता महाराज ऋषिभिः सत्रयाजिभिः ।समागतानां राजेन्द्र सहायार्थं महात्मनाम् ।आजगाम महाभागा तत्र पुण्या सरस्वती ॥१७॥
transliteration: sā tu dhyātā mahārāja ṛṣibhiḥ satrayājibhiḥ ,samāgatānāṁ rājendra sahāyārthaṁ mahātmanām ,ājagāma mahābhāgā tatra puṇyā sarasvatī.
english: O great king, that greatly fortunate and sacred Sarasvatī, having indeed been invoked by those sages performing the sacrifice for the sake of helping the assembled great-souled ones, came there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 17. O great king, that greatly fortunate and sacred Sarasvatī, having indeed been invoked by those sages performing the sacrifice for the sake of helping the assembled great-souled ones, came there.

sanskrit: नैमिषे काञ्चनाक्षी तु मुनीनां सत्रयाजिनाम् ।आगता सरितां श्रेष्ठा तत्र भारत पूजिता ॥१८॥
transliteration: naimiṣe kāñcanākṣī tu munīnāṁ satrayājinām ,āgatā saritāṁ śreṣṭhā tatra bhārata pūjitā.
english: O Bhārata, the golden-eyed one (kāñcanākṣī), indeed the best of rivers, came there to Naimiṣa, honored by those sages performing the sacrifice.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 18. O Bhārata, the golden-eyed one (kāñcanākṣī), indeed the best of rivers, came there to Naimiṣa, honored by those sages performing the sacrifice.

sanskrit: गयस्य यजमानस्य गयेष्वेव महाक्रतुम् ।आहूता सरितां श्रेष्ठा गययज्ञे सरस्वती ॥१९॥
transliteration: gayasya yajamānasya gayeṣveva mahākratum ,āhūtā saritāṁ śreṣṭhā gayayajñe sarasvatī.
english: The foremost of rivers, Sarasvatī, was indeed invited to the great Vedic ritual (mahākratu) of Gaya, the performer of Vedic rituals (yajamāna), specifically in Gaya itself, at Gaya's Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 19. The foremost of rivers, Sarasvatī, was indeed invited to the great Vedic ritual (mahākratu) of Gaya, the performer of Vedic rituals (yajamāna), specifically in Gaya itself, at Gaya's Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: विशालां तु गयेष्वाहुरृषयः संशितव्रताः ।सरित्सा हिमवत्पार्श्वात्प्रसूता शीघ्रगामिनी ॥२०॥
transliteration: viśālāṁ tu gayeṣvāhurṛṣayaḥ saṁśitavratāḥ ,saritsā himavatpārśvātprasūtā śīghragāminī.
english: The sages (ṛṣayaḥ) of firm vows (saṃśitavratāḥ) indeed say that Viśālā, the swiftly flowing river, originated from the side of the Himalayas in Gaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 20. The sages (ṛṣayaḥ) of firm vows (saṃśitavratāḥ) indeed say that Viśālā, the swiftly flowing river, originated from the side of the Himalayas in Gaya.

sanskrit: औद्दालकेस्तथा यज्ञे यजतस्तत्र भारत ।समेते सर्वतः स्फीते मुनीनां मण्डले तदा ॥२१॥
transliteration: auddālakestathā yajñe yajatastatra bhārata ,samete sarvataḥ sphīte munīnāṁ maṇḍale tadā.
english: O Bhārata, then, there, in the prosperous and extensive assembly (maṇḍala) of sages (muni), gathered from all sides, while Uddālaka was performing a Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 21. O Bhārata, then, there, in the prosperous and extensive assembly (maṇḍala) of sages (muni), gathered from all sides, while Uddālaka was performing a Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: उत्तरे कोसलाभागे पुण्ये राजन्महात्मनः ।औद्दालकेन यजता पूर्वं ध्याता सरस्वती ॥२२॥
transliteration: uttare kosalābhāge puṇye rājanmahātmanaḥ ,auddālakena yajatā pūrvaṁ dhyātā sarasvatī.
english: O King, in the sacred northern region of Kosala, the Sarasvatī [river], who was *of* the great-souled (mahātman) one, was formerly invoked (dhyātā) by Uddālaka as he was performing a Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 22. O King, in the sacred northern region of Kosala, the Sarasvatī [river], who was *of* the great-souled (mahātman) one, was formerly invoked (dhyātā) by Uddālaka as he was performing a Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: आजगाम सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा तं देशमृषिकारणात् ।पूज्यमाना मुनिगणैर्वल्कलाजिनसंवृतैः ।मनोह्रदेति विख्याता सा हि तैर्मनसा हृता ॥२३॥
transliteration: ājagāma saricchreṣṭhā taṁ deśamṛṣikāraṇāt ,pūjyamānā munigaṇairvalkalājinasaṁvṛtaiḥ ,manohradeti vikhyātā sā hi tairmanasā hṛtā.
english: The best of rivers (saricchreṣṭhā) came to that region (deśa) for the sake of the sage (ṛṣikāraṇāt), being honored (pūjyamānā) by groups of sages (munigaṇaiḥ) who were clad in bark and deer skins (valkalājinasaṃvṛtaiḥ). Indeed, she was renowned as Manohradā, for she was brought (hṛtā) by them (taiḥ) mentally (manasā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 23. The best of rivers (saricchreṣṭhā) came to that region (deśa) for the sake of the sage (ṛṣikāraṇāt), being honored (pūjyamānā) by groups of sages (munigaṇaiḥ) who were clad in bark and deer skins (valkalājinasaṃvṛtaiḥ). Indeed, she was renowned as Manohradā, for she was brought (hṛtā) by them (taiḥ) mentally (manasā).

sanskrit: सुवेणुरृषभद्वीपे पुण्ये राजर्षिसेविते ।कुरोश्च यजमानस्य कुरुक्षेत्रे महात्मनः ।आजगाम महाभागा सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा सरस्वती ॥२४॥
transliteration: suveṇurṛṣabhadvīpe puṇye rājarṣisevite ,kurośca yajamānasya kurukṣetre mahātmanaḥ ,ājagāma mahābhāgā saricchreṣṭhā sarasvatī.
english: The highly blessed, foremost river Sarasvatī came to Suveṇu, (a place) on the sacred Ṛṣabhadvīpa, which is frequented by royal sages, and also to Kurukṣetra, (the region) of the great-souled Kuru, the performer of Vedic rituals (yajamāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 24. The highly blessed, foremost river Sarasvatī came to Suveṇu, (a place) on the sacred Ṛṣabhadvīpa, which is frequented by royal sages, and also to Kurukṣetra, (the region) of the great-souled Kuru, the performer of Vedic rituals (yajamāna).

sanskrit: ओघवत्यपि राजेन्द्र वसिष्ठेन महात्मना ।समाहूता कुरुक्षेत्रे दिव्यतोया सरस्वती ॥२५॥
transliteration: oghavatyapi rājendra vasiṣṭhena mahātmanā ,samāhūtā kurukṣetre divyatoyā sarasvatī.
english: O king of kings, the Sarasvatī, though swift-flowing and possessing divine waters, was indeed invoked by the great-souled Vasiṣṭha in Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 25. O king of kings, the Sarasvatī, though swift-flowing and possessing divine waters, was indeed invoked by the great-souled Vasiṣṭha in Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: दक्षेण यजता चापि गङ्गाद्वारे सरस्वती ।विमलोदा भगवती ब्रह्मणा यजता पुनः ।समाहूता ययौ तत्र पुण्ये हैमवते गिरौ ॥२६॥
transliteration: dakṣeṇa yajatā cāpi gaṅgādvāre sarasvatī ,vimalodā bhagavatī brahmaṇā yajatā punaḥ ,samāhūtā yayau tatra puṇye haimavate girau.
english: The divine (bhagavatī) and pure-watered Sarasvatī, having been invited by Dakṣa who was performing Vedic rituals (yajña) at Gaṅgādvāra, and again by Brahmā who was also performing Vedic rituals, went there, to the sacred Himālaya mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 26. The divine (bhagavatī) and pure-watered Sarasvatī, having been invited by Dakṣa who was performing Vedic rituals (yajña) at Gaṅgādvāra, and again by Brahmā who was also performing Vedic rituals, went there, to the sacred Himālaya mountain.

sanskrit: एकीभूतास्ततस्तास्तु तस्मिंस्तीर्थे समागताः ।सप्तसारस्वतं तीर्थं ततस्तत्प्रथितं भुवि ॥२७॥
transliteration: ekībhūtāstatastāstu tasmiṁstīrthe samāgatāḥ ,saptasārasvataṁ tīrthaṁ tatastatprathitaṁ bhuvi.
english: Then, having become one, all those (streams) gathered at that sacred spot (tirtha). From then on, that tīrtha became renowned on earth as Saptasārasvata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 27. Then, having become one, all those (streams) gathered at that sacred spot (tirtha). From then on, that tīrtha became renowned on earth as Saptasārasvata.

sanskrit: इति सप्त सरस्वत्यो नामतः परिकीर्तिताः ।सप्तसारस्वतं चैव तीर्थं पुण्यं तथा स्मृतम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: iti sapta sarasvatyo nāmataḥ parikīrtitāḥ ,saptasārasvataṁ caiva tīrthaṁ puṇyaṁ tathā smṛtam.
english: Thus, these seven Sarasvatis have been enumerated by name. Similarly, the sacred site (tīrtha) known as Saptasārasvata is considered holy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 28. Thus, these seven Sarasvatis have been enumerated by name. Similarly, the sacred site (tīrtha) known as Saptasārasvata is considered holy.

sanskrit: शृणु मङ्कणकस्यापि कौमारब्रह्मचारिणः ।आपगामवगाढस्य राजन्प्रक्रीडितं महत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: śṛṇu maṅkaṇakasyāpi kaumārabrahmacāriṇaḥ ,āpagāmavagāḍhasya rājanprakrīḍitaṁ mahat.
english: O King, listen also to the great sport (prākṛīḍitam) of Maṅkaṇaka, the lifelong celibate (brahmacārin), who had entered the river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 29. O King, listen also to the great sport (prākṛīḍitam) of Maṅkaṇaka, the lifelong celibate (brahmacārin), who had entered the river.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा यदृच्छया तत्र स्त्रियमम्भसि भारत ।स्नायन्तीं रुचिरापाङ्गीं दिग्वाससमनिन्दिताम् ।सरस्वत्यां महाराज चस्कन्दे वीर्यमम्भसि ॥३०॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā yadṛcchayā tatra striyamambhasi bhārata ,snāyantīṁ rucirāpāṅgīṁ digvāsasamaninditām ,sarasvatyāṁ mahārāja caskande vīryamambhasi.
english: O Bhārata, O great king, having by chance seen there in the Sarasvatī river a blameless woman, bathing naked with beautiful eyes, he discharged his semen (vīrya) into the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 30. O Bhārata, O great king, having by chance seen there in the Sarasvatī river a blameless woman, bathing naked with beautiful eyes, he discharged his semen (vīrya) into the water.

sanskrit: तद्रेतः स तु जग्राह कलशे वै महातपाः ।सप्तधा प्रविभागं तु कलशस्थं जगाम ह ।तत्रर्षयः सप्त जाता जज्ञिरे मरुतां गणाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tadretaḥ sa tu jagrāha kalaśe vai mahātapāḥ ,saptadhā pravibhāgaṁ tu kalaśasthaṁ jagāma ha ,tatrarṣayaḥ sapta jātā jajñire marutāṁ gaṇāḥ.
english: Indeed, that great ascetic (mahātapas) collected that semen (retas) in a pot. Verily, that substance situated in the pot underwent a sevenfold division. From it, seven sages (ṛṣi) were born there, who became the hosts of the Maruts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 31. Indeed, that great ascetic (mahātapas) collected that semen (retas) in a pot. Verily, that substance situated in the pot underwent a sevenfold division. From it, seven sages (ṛṣi) were born there, who became the hosts of the Maruts.

sanskrit: वायुवेगो वायुबलो वायुहा वायुमण्डलः ।वायुज्वालो वायुरेता वायुचक्रश्च वीर्यवान् ।एवमेते समुत्पन्ना मरुतां जनयिष्णवः ॥३२॥
transliteration: vāyuvego vāyubalo vāyuhā vāyumaṇḍalaḥ ,vāyujvālo vāyuretā vāyucakraśca vīryavān ,evamete samutpannā marutāṁ janayiṣṇavaḥ.
english: He is of wind-speed, wind-strength, wind-destroying, a circle of wind, wind-flaming, a wind-mover, and a powerful wind-wheel. In this manner, these producers of the Maruts (storm gods) came into being.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 32. He is of wind-speed, wind-strength, wind-destroying, a circle of wind, wind-flaming, a wind-mover, and a powerful wind-wheel. In this manner, these producers of the Maruts (storm gods) came into being.

sanskrit: इदमन्यच्च राजेन्द्र शृण्वाश्चर्यतरं भुवि ।महर्षेश्चरितं यादृक्त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: idamanyacca rājendra śṛṇvāścaryataraṁ bhuvi ,maharṣeścaritaṁ yādṛktriṣu lokeṣu viśrutam.
english: O king (rājendra), hear this other thing, even more wonderful on earth: the character and deeds (carita) of the great sage (maharṣi), which are renowned in the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 33. O king (rājendra), hear this other thing, even more wonderful on earth: the character and deeds (carita) of the great sage (maharṣi), which are renowned in the three worlds.

sanskrit: पुरा मङ्कणकः सिद्धः कुशाग्रेणेति नः श्रुतम् ।क्षतः किल करे राजंस्तस्य शाकरसोऽस्रवत् ।स वि शाकरसं दृष्ट्वा हर्षाविष्टः प्रनृत्तवान् ॥३४॥
transliteration: purā maṅkaṇakaḥ siddhaḥ kuśāgreṇeti naḥ śrutam ,kṣataḥ kila kare rājaṁstasya śākaraso'sravat ,sa vi śākarasaṁ dṛṣṭvā harṣāviṣṭaḥ pranṛttavān.
english: Formerly, we have heard (naḥ śrutam) that Maṅkaṇaka, a perfected being (siddha), was wounded (kṣataḥ) in the hand (kare) by the tip of a kuśa grass blade (kuśāgreṇa). O king (rājan), it is said (kila) that from him, vegetable juice (śākarasa) flowed instead of blood. Seeing that vegetable juice, he became filled with joy (harṣāviṣṭaḥ) and began to dance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 34. Formerly, we have heard (naḥ śrutam) that Maṅkaṇaka, a perfected being (siddha), was wounded (kṣataḥ) in the hand (kare) by the tip of a kuśa grass blade (kuśāgreṇa). O king (rājan), it is said (kila) that from him, vegetable juice (śākarasa) flowed instead of blood. Seeing that vegetable juice, he became filled with joy (harṣāviṣṭaḥ) and began to dance.

sanskrit: ततस्तस्मिन्प्रनृत्ते वै स्थावरं जङ्गमं च यत् ।प्रनृत्तमुभयं वीर तेजसा तस्य मोहितम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tatastasminpranṛtte vai sthāvaraṁ jaṅgamaṁ ca yat ,pranṛttamubhayaṁ vīra tejasā tasya mohitam.
english: Then, when he (Maṅkaṇaka) began to dance (pranṛtte), whatever was stationary (sthāvara) and moving (jaṅgama), both (ubhayam) of these, O hero (vīra), were enchanted and danced (pranṛttam) by his spiritual power (tejas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 35. Then, when he (Maṅkaṇaka) began to dance (pranṛtte), whatever was stationary (sthāvara) and moving (jaṅgama), both (ubhayam) of these, O hero (vīra), were enchanted and danced (pranṛttam) by his spiritual power (tejas).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मादिभिः सुरै राजन्नृषिभिश्च तपोधनैः ।विज्ञप्तो वै महादेव ऋषेरर्थे नराधिप ।नायं नृत्येद्यथा देव तथा त्वं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥३६॥
transliteration: brahmādibhiḥ surai rājannṛṣibhiśca tapodhanaiḥ ,vijñapto vai mahādeva ṛṣerarthe narādhipa ,nāyaṁ nṛtyedyathā deva tathā tvaṁ kartumarhasi.
english: O King, O ruler of men (narādhipa), Mahādeva was indeed requested by Brahmā and other gods, and by sages (ṛṣi) whose wealth is austerity (tapas). O Lord, for the sake of the sage, you should act so that this one (the sage) does not dance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 36. O King, O ruler of men (narādhipa), Mahādeva was indeed requested by Brahmā and other gods, and by sages (ṛṣi) whose wealth is austerity (tapas). O Lord, for the sake of the sage, you should act so that this one (the sage) does not dance.

sanskrit: ततो देवो मुनिं दृष्ट्वा हर्षाविष्टमतीव ह ।सुराणां हितकामार्थं महादेवोऽभ्यभाषत ॥३७॥
transliteration: tato devo muniṁ dṛṣṭvā harṣāviṣṭamatīva ha ,surāṇāṁ hitakāmārthaṁ mahādevo'bhyabhāṣata.
english: Then, Mahādeva, having indeed seen the sage (muni) who was exceedingly filled with joy, spoke for the sake of the welfare of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 37. Then, Mahādeva, having indeed seen the sage (muni) who was exceedingly filled with joy, spoke for the sake of the welfare of the gods.

sanskrit: भो भो ब्राह्मण धर्मज्ञ किमर्थं नरिनर्त्सि वै ।हर्षस्थानं किमर्थं वै तवेदं मुनिसत्तम ।तपस्विनो धर्मपथे स्थितस्य द्विजसत्तम ॥३८॥
transliteration: bho bho brāhmaṇa dharmajña kimarthaṁ narinartsi vai ,harṣasthānaṁ kimarthaṁ vai tavedaṁ munisattama ,tapasvino dharmapathe sthitasya dvijasattama.
english: O Brahmin, O knower of natural law (dharma), why indeed do you dance so intensely? O best of sages (muni), O best of the twice-born (dvija), what is the reason for this great joy of yours, for you who are an ascetic established on the path of natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 38. O Brahmin, O knower of natural law (dharma), why indeed do you dance so intensely? O best of sages (muni), O best of the twice-born (dvija), what is the reason for this great joy of yours, for you who are an ascetic established on the path of natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: ऋषिरुवाच ।किं न पश्यसि मे ब्रह्मन्कराच्छाकरसं स्रुतम् ।यं दृष्ट्वा वै प्रनृत्तोऽहं हर्षेण महता विभो ॥३९॥
transliteration: ṛṣiruvāca ,kiṁ na paśyasi me brahmankarācchākarasaṁ srutam ,yaṁ dṛṣṭvā vai pranṛtto'haṁ harṣeṇa mahatā vibho.
english: The sage (ṛṣi) said: "O Brahmin (brahman), O Lord (vibhu), why do you not see the vegetable sap (śākarasa) oozing from my hand? Having seen that, I am indeed dancing with great joy."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 39. The sage (ṛṣi) said: "O Brahmin (brahman), O Lord (vibhu), why do you not see the vegetable sap (śākarasa) oozing from my hand? Having seen that, I am indeed dancing with great joy."

sanskrit: तं प्रहस्याब्रवीद्देवो मुनिं रागेण मोहितम् ।अहं न विस्मयं विप्र गच्छामीति प्रपश्य माम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: taṁ prahasyābravīddevo muniṁ rāgeṇa mohitam ,ahaṁ na vismayaṁ vipra gacchāmīti prapaśya mām.
english: The god, laughing, spoke to that sage who was deluded by passion: "O brahmin, I am not surprised; behold me!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 40. The god, laughing, spoke to that sage who was deluded by passion: "O brahmin, I am not surprised; behold me!"

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा मुनिश्रेष्ठं महादेवेन धीमता ।अङ्गुल्यग्रेण राजेन्द्र स्वाङ्गुष्ठस्ताडितोऽभवत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: evamuktvā muniśreṣṭhaṁ mahādevena dhīmatā ,aṅgulyagreṇa rājendra svāṅguṣṭhastāḍito'bhavat.
english: O king (rājendra), having spoken thus to the foremost (śreṣṭha) of sages, his own thumb was struck by the intelligent Mahādeva with the tip of his finger.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 41. O king (rājendra), having spoken thus to the foremost (śreṣṭha) of sages, his own thumb was struck by the intelligent Mahādeva with the tip of his finger.

sanskrit: ततो भस्म क्षताद्राजन्निर्गतं हिमसंनिभम् ।तद्दृष्ट्वा व्रीडितो राजन्स मुनिः पादयोर्गतः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tato bhasma kṣatādrājannirgataṁ himasaṁnibham ,taddṛṣṭvā vrīḍito rājansa muniḥ pādayorgataḥ.
english: Then, O king (rājan), snow-like ash emerged from the wound. Seeing that, O king (rājan), the sage, feeling ashamed, fell at his (Mahādeva's) feet.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 42. Then, O king (rājan), snow-like ash emerged from the wound. Seeing that, O king (rājan), the sage, feeling ashamed, fell at his (Mahādeva's) feet.

sanskrit: ऋषिरुवाच ।नान्यं देवादहं मन्ये रुद्रात्परतरं महत् ।सुरासुरस्य जगतो गतिस्त्वमसि शूलधृक् ॥४३॥
transliteration: ṛṣiruvāca ,nānyaṁ devādahaṁ manye rudrātparataraṁ mahat ,surāsurasya jagato gatistvamasi śūladhṛk.
english: The sage said: "I do not consider any other god to be more supreme or greater than Rudra. O wielder of the trident (śūladhṛk), you are the refuge (gati) of the world (jagata) of gods and demons (sura-asura)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 43. The sage said: "I do not consider any other god to be more supreme or greater than Rudra. O wielder of the trident (śūladhṛk), you are the refuge (gati) of the world (jagata) of gods and demons (sura-asura)."

sanskrit: त्वया सृष्टमिदं विश्वं वदन्तीह मनीषिणः ।त्वामेव सर्वं विशति पुनरेव युगक्षये ॥४४॥
transliteration: tvayā sṛṣṭamidaṁ viśvaṁ vadantīha manīṣiṇaḥ ,tvāmeva sarvaṁ viśati punareva yugakṣaye.
english: Indeed, the wise (manīṣiṇaḥ) declare that this universe was created by you. And again, at the dissolution of the cosmic age (yuga), everything merges back into you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 44. Indeed, the wise (manīṣiṇaḥ) declare that this universe was created by you. And again, at the dissolution of the cosmic age (yuga), everything merges back into you.

sanskrit: देवैरपि न शक्यस्त्वं परिज्ञातुं कुतो मया ।त्वयि सर्वे स्म दृश्यन्ते सुरा ब्रह्मादयोऽनघ ॥४५॥
transliteration: devairapi na śakyastvaṁ parijñātuṁ kuto mayā ,tvayi sarve sma dṛśyante surā brahmādayo'nagha.
english: You cannot be fully known even by the gods, so how could I possibly know you? O sinless one (anagha), all the gods, beginning with Brahmā, are indeed seen within you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 45. You cannot be fully known even by the gods, so how could I possibly know you? O sinless one (anagha), all the gods, beginning with Brahmā, are indeed seen within you.

sanskrit: सर्वस्त्वमसि देवानां कर्ता कारयिता च ह ।त्वत्प्रसादात्सुराः सर्वे मोदन्तीहाकुतोभयाः ॥४६॥
transliteration: sarvastvamasi devānāṁ kartā kārayitā ca ha ,tvatprasādātsurāḥ sarve modantīhākutobhayāḥ.
english: You are indeed the creator and the inspirer of all the gods. By your grace, all the gods in this world rejoice, utterly fearless.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 46. You are indeed the creator and the inspirer of all the gods. By your grace, all the gods in this world rejoice, utterly fearless.

sanskrit: एवं स्तुत्वा महादेवं स ऋषिः प्रणतोऽब्रवीत् ।भगवंस्त्वत्प्रसादाद्वै तपो मे न क्षरेदिति ॥४७॥
transliteration: evaṁ stutvā mahādevaṁ sa ṛṣiḥ praṇato'bravīt ,bhagavaṁstvatprasādādvai tapo me na kṣarediti.
english: Having thus praised the great god (mahādeva), that sage, bowing down, spoke: 'O Lord (bhagavan), by your grace, may my spiritual discipline (tapas) indeed not diminish.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 47. Having thus praised the great god (mahādeva), that sage, bowing down, spoke: 'O Lord (bhagavan), by your grace, may my spiritual discipline (tapas) indeed not diminish.'

sanskrit: ततो देवः प्रीतमनास्तमृषिं पुनरब्रवीत् ।तपस्ते वर्धतां विप्र मत्प्रसादात्सहस्रधा ।आश्रमे चेह वत्स्यामि त्वया सार्धमहं सदा ॥४८॥
transliteration: tato devaḥ prītamanāstamṛṣiṁ punarabravīt ,tapaste vardhatāṁ vipra matprasādātsahasradhā ,āśrame ceha vatsyāmi tvayā sārdhamahaṁ sadā.
english: Then the god, with a pleased mind, spoke again to that sage: "May your spiritual austerities (tapas) increase a thousandfold through my grace, O Brahmin (vipra). And I will always reside here in this hermitage with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 48. Then the god, with a pleased mind, spoke again to that sage: "May your spiritual austerities (tapas) increase a thousandfold through my grace, O Brahmin (vipra). And I will always reside here in this hermitage with you."

sanskrit: सप्तसारस्वते चास्मिन्यो मामर्चिष्यते नरः ।न तस्य दुर्लभं किंचिद्भवितेह परत्र च ।सारस्वतं च लोकं ते गमिष्यन्ति न संशयः ॥४९॥
transliteration: saptasārasvate cāsminyo māmarciṣyate naraḥ ,na tasya durlabhaṁ kiṁcidbhaviteha paratra ca ,sārasvataṁ ca lokaṁ te gamiṣyanti na saṁśayaḥ.
english: And any person who worships me in this region of the seven Sarasvatīs will find nothing difficult for them, neither in this world nor in the next. And without a doubt, they will attain the world of Sarasvatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 49. And any person who worships me in this region of the seven Sarasvatīs will find nothing difficult for them, neither in this world nor in the next. And without a doubt, they will attain the world of Sarasvatī.

sanskrit: एतन्मङ्कणकस्यापि चरितं भूरितेजसः ।स हि पुत्रः सजन्यायामुत्पन्नो मातरिश्वना ॥५०॥
transliteration: etanmaṅkaṇakasyāpi caritaṁ bhūritejasaḥ ,sa hi putraḥ sajanyāyāmutpanno mātariśvanā.
english: This is also the account of Maṅkaṇaka, who possessed great splendor. For he was the son born of Sajanyā by Mātariśvan.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 37 Verse 50. This is also the account of Maṅkaṇaka, who possessed great splendor. For he was the son born of Sajanyā by Mātariśvan.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उषित्वा तत्र रामस्तु संपूज्याश्रमवासिनः ।तथा मङ्कणके प्रीतिं शुभां चक्रे हलायुधः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,uṣitvā tatra rāmastu saṁpūjyāśramavāsinaḥ ,tathā maṅkaṇake prītiṁ śubhāṁ cakre halāyudhaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having resided there and duly honored the dwellers of the hermitage, Rāma (Hālāyudha) thus developed an auspicious affection for Maṅkaṇaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having resided there and duly honored the dwellers of the hermitage, Rāma (Hālāyudha) thus developed an auspicious affection for Maṅkaṇaka.

sanskrit: दत्त्वा दानं द्विजातिभ्यो रजनीं तामुपोष्य च ।पूजितो मुनिसंघैश्च प्रातरुत्थाय लाङ्गली ॥२॥
transliteration: dattvā dānaṁ dvijātibhyo rajanīṁ tāmupoṣya ca ,pūjito munisaṁghaiśca prātarutthāya lāṅgalī.
english: Having given gifts (dāna) to the twice-born (dvijāti), and having fasted that night, Rama (Lāṅgalī), honored by groups of sages (muni-saṃgha), rose in the morning.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 2. Having given gifts (dāna) to the twice-born (dvijāti), and having fasted that night, Rama (Lāṅgalī), honored by groups of sages (muni-saṃgha), rose in the morning.

sanskrit: अनुज्ञाप्य मुनीन्सर्वान्स्पृष्ट्वा तोयं च भारत ।प्रययौ त्वरितो रामस्तीर्थहेतोर्महाबलः ॥३॥
transliteration: anujñāpya munīnsarvānspṛṣṭvā toyaṁ ca bhārata ,prayayau tvarito rāmastīrthahetormahābalaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, having taken leave of all the sages and having touched water, the mighty Rama (mahābalaḥ), quickly set forth for the sake of pilgrimage (tīrtha).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 3. O Bhārata, having taken leave of all the sages and having touched water, the mighty Rama (mahābalaḥ), quickly set forth for the sake of pilgrimage (tīrtha).

sanskrit: तत औशनसं तीर्थमाजगाम हलायुधः ।कपालमोचनं नाम यत्र मुक्तो महामुनिः ॥४॥
transliteration: tata auśanasaṁ tīrthamājagāma halāyudhaḥ ,kapālamocanaṁ nāma yatra mukto mahāmuniḥ.
english: Then Balarama (Halāyudhaḥ) came to the holy place (tīrtha) named Auśanasa, also called Kapālamocana, where a great sage (mahāmuniḥ) was liberated (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 4. Then Balarama (Halāyudhaḥ) came to the holy place (tīrtha) named Auśanasa, also called Kapālamocana, where a great sage (mahāmuniḥ) was liberated (mokṣa).

sanskrit: महता शिरसा राजन्ग्रस्तजङ्घो महोदरः ।राक्षसस्य महाराज रामक्षिप्तस्य वै पुरा ॥५॥
transliteration: mahatā śirasā rājangrastajaṅgho mahodaraḥ ,rākṣasasya mahārāja rāmakṣiptasya vai purā.
english: O king, O great king, concerning the Rākṣasa Mahodara  -  who was great-bellied, had devoured thighs, and possessed a great head  -  he was indeed thrown by Rama (Parashurama) long ago.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 5. O king, O great king, concerning the Rākṣasa Mahodara  -  who was great-bellied, had devoured thighs, and possessed a great head  -  he was indeed thrown by Rama (Parashurama) long ago.

sanskrit: तत्र पूर्वं तपस्तप्तं काव्येन सुमहात्मना ।यत्रास्य नीतिरखिला प्रादुर्भूता महात्मनः ।तत्रस्थश्चिन्तयामास दैत्यदानवविग्रहम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tatra pūrvaṁ tapastaptaṁ kāvyena sumahātmanā ,yatrāsya nītirakhilā prādurbhūtā mahātmanaḥ ,tatrasthaścintayāmāsa daityadānavavigraham.
english: There, in ancient times, the greatly revered Kāvya performed severe asceticism (tapas). It was there that his entire system of moral and political wisdom emerged. While residing in that place, he pondered the ongoing conflict between the Daityas and Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 6. There, in ancient times, the greatly revered Kāvya performed severe asceticism (tapas). It was there that his entire system of moral and political wisdom emerged. While residing in that place, he pondered the ongoing conflict between the Daityas and Dānavas.

sanskrit: तत्प्राप्य च बलो राजंस्तीर्थप्रवरमुत्तमम् ।विधिवद्धि ददौ वित्तं ब्राह्मणानां महात्मनाम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tatprāpya ca balo rājaṁstīrthapravaramuttamam ,vidhivaddhi dadau vittaṁ brāhmaṇānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: And having obtained that excellent and supreme sacred place (tīrtha), O King, Bala properly gave wealth to the great-souled Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 7. And having obtained that excellent and supreme sacred place (tīrtha), O King, Bala properly gave wealth to the great-souled Brahmins.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कपालमोचनं ब्रह्मन्कथं यत्र महामुनिः ।मुक्तः कथं चास्य शिरो लग्नं केन च हेतुना ॥८॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kapālamocanaṁ brahmankathaṁ yatra mahāmuniḥ ,muktaḥ kathaṁ cāsya śiro lagnaṁ kena ca hetunā.
english: Janamejaya said: O Brahmin, how did the liberation from the skull (kapālamocana) occur there, by which the great sage was freed? And how did the head become attached to him, and for what reason?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 8. Janamejaya said: O Brahmin, how did the liberation from the skull (kapālamocana) occur there, by which the great sage was freed? And how did the head become attached to him, and for what reason?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।पुरा वै दण्डकारण्ये राघवेण महात्मना ।वसता राजशार्दूल राक्षसास्तत्र हिंसिताः ॥९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,purā vai daṇḍakāraṇye rāghaveṇa mahātmanā ,vasatā rājaśārdūla rākṣasāstatra hiṁsitāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), formerly, while the great-souled Rāghava (Rāma) was residing in the Daṇḍaka forest, the Rākṣasas were killed there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 9. Vaiśampāyana said: O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), formerly, while the great-souled Rāghava (Rāma) was residing in the Daṇḍaka forest, the Rākṣasas were killed there.

sanskrit: जनस्थाने शिरश्छिन्नं राक्षसस्य दुरात्मनः ।क्षुरेण शितधारेण तत्पपात महावने ॥१०॥
transliteration: janasthāne śiraśchinnaṁ rākṣasasya durātmanaḥ ,kṣureṇa śitadhāreṇa tatpapāta mahāvane.
english: In Janasthana, the severed head of the wicked-minded demon fell into a great forest, cut off by a sharp-edged razor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 10. In Janasthana, the severed head of the wicked-minded demon fell into a great forest, cut off by a sharp-edged razor.

sanskrit: महोदरस्य तल्लग्नं जङ्घायां वै यदृच्छया ।वने विचरतो राजन्नस्थि भित्त्वास्फुरत्तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: mahodarasya tallagnaṁ jaṅghāyāṁ vai yadṛcchayā ,vane vicarato rājannasthi bhittvāsphurattadā.
english: O King, that [severed head] then accidentally became attached to Mahodara's leg. It split the bone of him who was wandering in the forest and began to throb.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 11. O King, that [severed head] then accidentally became attached to Mahodara's leg. It split the bone of him who was wandering in the forest and began to throb.

sanskrit: स तेन लग्नेन तदा द्विजातिर्न शशाक ह ।अभिगन्तुं महाप्राज्ञस्तीर्थान्यायतनानि च ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa tena lagnena tadā dvijātirna śaśāka ha ,abhigantuṁ mahāprājñastīrthānyāyatanāni ca.
english: Then, with that [severed head] attached, the very wise Brahmin (dvijāti) was indeed unable to approach the sacred places (tīrtha) and temples.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 12. Then, with that [severed head] attached, the very wise Brahmin (dvijāti) was indeed unable to approach the sacred places (tīrtha) and temples.

sanskrit: स पूतिना विस्रवता वेदनार्तो महामुनिः ।जगाम सर्वतीर्थानि पृथिव्यामिति नः श्रुतम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa pūtinā visravatā vedanārto mahāmuniḥ ,jagāma sarvatīrthāni pṛthivyāmiti naḥ śrutam.
english: Suffering from pain caused by its putrid and oozing nature, that great sage (mahāmuni) visited all the sacred places (tīrtha) on earth, as we have heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 13. Suffering from pain caused by its putrid and oozing nature, that great sage (mahāmuni) visited all the sacred places (tīrtha) on earth, as we have heard.

sanskrit: स गत्वा सरितः सर्वाः समुद्रांश्च महातपाः ।कथयामास तत्सर्वमृषीणां भावितात्मनाम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa gatvā saritaḥ sarvāḥ samudrāṁśca mahātapāḥ ,kathayāmāsa tatsarvamṛṣīṇāṁ bhāvitātmanām.
english: The great ascetic (mahātapāḥ), having visited all rivers and oceans, narrated all that to the sages (ṛṣis) whose intrinsic nature (ātman) had been purified.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 14. The great ascetic (mahātapāḥ), having visited all rivers and oceans, narrated all that to the sages (ṛṣis) whose intrinsic nature (ātman) had been purified.

sanskrit: आप्लुतः सर्वतीर्थेषु न च मोक्षमवाप्तवान् ।स तु शुश्राव विप्रेन्द्रो मुनीनां वचनं महत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: āplutaḥ sarvatīrtheṣu na ca mokṣamavāptavān ,sa tu śuśrāva viprendro munīnāṁ vacanaṁ mahat.
english: Even after bathing in all sacred places (tīrthas), he did not achieve final liberation (mokṣa). However, that chief among brahmins (viprendra) then heard a significant teaching from the sages (munis).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 15. Even after bathing in all sacred places (tīrthas), he did not achieve final liberation (mokṣa). However, that chief among brahmins (viprendra) then heard a significant teaching from the sages (munis).

sanskrit: सरस्वत्यास्तीर्थवरं ख्यातमौशनसं तदा ।सर्वपापप्रशमनं सिद्धक्षेत्रमनुत्तमम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: sarasvatyāstīrthavaraṁ khyātamauśanasaṁ tadā ,sarvapāpapraśamanaṁ siddhakṣetramanuttamam.
english: At that time, he heard about the best and most renowned sacred place (tīrtha) of Sarasvati, known as Auśanasa, which alleviates all sins and is an unsurpassed region for achieving spiritual perfection.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 16. At that time, he heard about the best and most renowned sacred place (tīrtha) of Sarasvati, known as Auśanasa, which alleviates all sins and is an unsurpassed region for achieving spiritual perfection.

sanskrit: स तु गत्वा ततस्तत्र तीर्थमौशनसं द्विजः ।तत औशनसे तीर्थे तस्योपस्पृशतस्तदा ।तच्छिरश्चरणं मुक्त्वा पपातान्तर्जले तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa tu gatvā tatastatra tīrthamauśanasaṁ dvijaḥ ,tata auśanase tīrthe tasyopaspṛśatastadā ,tacchiraścaraṇaṁ muktvā papātāntarjale tadā.
english: So that twice-born (dvija), having gone there to the Auśanasa sacred place (tīrtha), then, as he was bathing in that Auśanasa tīrtha, leaving his head and feet, he subsequently fell into the water.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 17. So that twice-born (dvija), having gone there to the Auśanasa sacred place (tīrtha), then, as he was bathing in that Auśanasa tīrtha, leaving his head and feet, he subsequently fell into the water.

sanskrit: ततः स विरुजो राजन्पूतात्मा वीतकल्मषः ।आजगामाश्रमं प्रीतः कृतकृत्यो महोदरः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa virujo rājanpūtātmā vītakalmaṣaḥ ,ājagāmāśramaṁ prītaḥ kṛtakṛtyo mahodaraḥ.
english: Then, O king, Mahodara, free from illness, with a purified soul (ātman) and cleansed of sin, content and having fulfilled his purpose, returned to the hermitage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 18. Then, O king, Mahodara, free from illness, with a purified soul (ātman) and cleansed of sin, content and having fulfilled his purpose, returned to the hermitage.

sanskrit: सोऽथ गत्वाश्रमं पुण्यं विप्रमुक्तो महातपाः ।कथयामास तत्सर्वमृषीणां भावितात्मनाम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: so'tha gatvāśramaṁ puṇyaṁ vipramukto mahātapāḥ ,kathayāmāsa tatsarvamṛṣīṇāṁ bhāvitātmanām.
english: Then, having gone to that sacred hermitage, he, the great ascetic who was completely liberated (mokṣa), narrated everything to the sages whose souls (ātman) were purified.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 19. Then, having gone to that sacred hermitage, he, the great ascetic who was completely liberated (mokṣa), narrated everything to the sages whose souls (ātman) were purified.

sanskrit: ते श्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य ततस्तीर्थस्य मानद ।कपालमोचनमिति नाम चक्रुः समागताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: te śrutvā vacanaṁ tasya tatastīrthasya mānada ,kapālamocanamiti nāma cakruḥ samāgatāḥ.
english: Then, O giver of honor, having heard his account, those assembled (sages) named that sacred place 'Kapalamocana' (the liberation of the skull).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 20. Then, O giver of honor, having heard his account, those assembled (sages) named that sacred place 'Kapalamocana' (the liberation of the skull).

sanskrit: तत्र दत्त्वा बहून्दायान्विप्रान्संपूज्य माधवः ।जगाम वृष्णिप्रवरो रुषङ्गोराश्रमं तदा ॥२१॥
transliteration: tatra dattvā bahūndāyānviprānsaṁpūjya mādhavaḥ ,jagāma vṛṣṇipravaro ruṣaṅgorāśramaṁ tadā.
english: Then, Madhava, the foremost among the Vṛṣṇis, having given many donations there and having honored the Brahmins, went to the hermitage of Ruṣaṅga.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 21. Then, Madhava, the foremost among the Vṛṣṇis, having given many donations there and having honored the Brahmins, went to the hermitage of Ruṣaṅga.

sanskrit: यत्र तप्तं तपो घोरमार्ष्टिषेणेन भारत ।ब्राह्मण्यं लब्धवांस्तत्र विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yatra taptaṁ tapo ghoramārṣṭiṣeṇena bhārata ,brāhmaṇyaṁ labdhavāṁstatra viśvāmitro mahāmuniḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata, it was there that the great sage Viśvāmitra attained brahminhood, where Arṣṭiṣeṇa had performed terrible asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 22. O descendant of Bharata, it was there that the great sage Viśvāmitra attained brahminhood, where Arṣṭiṣeṇa had performed terrible asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: ततो हलधरः श्रीमान्ब्राह्मणैः परिवारितः ।जगाम यत्र राजेन्द्र रुषङ्गुस्तनुमत्यजत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato haladharaḥ śrīmānbrāhmaṇaiḥ parivāritaḥ ,jagāma yatra rājendra ruṣaṅgustanumatyajat.
english: Then, the glorious plough-bearer (Balarāma), accompanied by Brahmins, went to the place where, O best of kings, Ruṣaṅgu had abandoned his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 23. Then, the glorious plough-bearer (Balarāma), accompanied by Brahmins, went to the place where, O best of kings, Ruṣaṅgu had abandoned his body.

sanskrit: रुषङ्गुर्ब्राह्मणो वृद्धस्तपोनित्यश्च भारत ।देहन्यासे कृतमना विचिन्त्य बहुधा बहु ॥२४॥
transliteration: ruṣaṅgurbrāhmaṇo vṛddhastaponityaśca bhārata ,dehanyāse kṛtamanā vicintya bahudhā bahu.
english: O descendant of Bharata, Ruṣaṅgu, an aged Brahmin constantly devoted to asceticism (tapas), had resolved to abandon his body after having greatly pondered it in many ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 24. O descendant of Bharata, Ruṣaṅgu, an aged Brahmin constantly devoted to asceticism (tapas), had resolved to abandon his body after having greatly pondered it in many ways.

sanskrit: ततः सर्वानुपादाय तनयान्वै महातपाः ।रुषङ्गुरब्रवीत्तत्र नयध्वं मा पृथूदकम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarvānupādāya tanayānvai mahātapāḥ ,ruṣaṅgurabravīttatra nayadhvaṁ mā pṛthūdakam.
english: Then, the great ascetic (tapasvin) Ruṣaṅgu, having gathered all his sons, indeed said there, 'Take me to Pṛthūdaka.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 25. Then, the great ascetic (tapasvin) Ruṣaṅgu, having gathered all his sons, indeed said there, 'Take me to Pṛthūdaka.'

sanskrit: विज्ञायातीतवयसं रुषङ्गुं ते तपोधनाः ।तं वै तीर्थमुपानिन्युः सरस्वत्यास्तपोधनम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: vijñāyātītavayasaṁ ruṣaṅguṁ te tapodhanāḥ ,taṁ vai tīrthamupāninyuḥ sarasvatyāstapodhanam.
english: Those ascetics (tapas), realizing that Rushangu was advanced in age, indeed led that ascetic to a sacred bathing place (tīrtha) on the Sarasvatī river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 26. Those ascetics (tapas), realizing that Rushangu was advanced in age, indeed led that ascetic to a sacred bathing place (tīrtha) on the Sarasvatī river.

sanskrit: स तैः पुत्रैस्तदा धीमानानीतो वै सरस्वतीम् ।पुण्यां तीर्थशतोपेतां विप्रसंघैर्निषेविताम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ putraistadā dhīmānānīto vai sarasvatīm ,puṇyāṁ tīrthaśatopetāṁ viprasaṁghairniṣevitām.
english: That wise Rushangu (dhīmān) was then indeed brought by those sons to the sacred Sarasvatī, which was endowed with hundreds of sacred bathing places (tīrtha) and frequented by assemblies of brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 27. That wise Rushangu (dhīmān) was then indeed brought by those sons to the sacred Sarasvatī, which was endowed with hundreds of sacred bathing places (tīrtha) and frequented by assemblies of brahmins.

sanskrit: स तत्र विधिना राजन्नाप्लुतः सुमहातपाः ।ज्ञात्वा तीर्थगुणांश्चैव प्राहेदमृषिसत्तमः ।सुप्रीतः पुरुषव्याघ्र सर्वान्पुत्रानुपासतः ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa tatra vidhinā rājannāplutaḥ sumahātapāḥ ,jñātvā tīrthaguṇāṁścaiva prāhedamṛṣisattamaḥ ,suprītaḥ puruṣavyāghra sarvānputrānupāsataḥ.
english: O King (rājan), O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra)! The best of sages (ṛṣisattama), who possessed great austerity (tapas), having bathed there according to the ritual, and having understood the merits of that sacred bathing place (tīrtha), then spoke this, greatly pleased, while all his sons were attending upon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 28. O King (rājan), O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra)! The best of sages (ṛṣisattama), who possessed great austerity (tapas), having bathed there according to the ritual, and having understood the merits of that sacred bathing place (tīrtha), then spoke this, greatly pleased, while all his sons were attending upon him.

sanskrit: सरस्वत्युत्तरे तीरे यस्त्यजेदात्मनस्तनुम् ।पृथूदके जप्यपरो नैनं श्वोमरणं तपेत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sarasvatyuttare tīre yastyajedātmanastanum ,pṛthūdake japyaparo nainaṁ śvomaraṇaṁ tapet.
english: Whoever, intent on sacred recitation (japa), abandons their own body (ātman) on the northern bank of the Sarasvatī at Pṛthūdaka, that person will not be tormented by the death of tomorrow (śvomaraṇaṃ).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 29. Whoever, intent on sacred recitation (japa), abandons their own body (ātman) on the northern bank of the Sarasvatī at Pṛthūdaka, that person will not be tormented by the death of tomorrow (śvomaraṇaṃ).

sanskrit: तत्राप्लुत्य स धर्मात्मा उपस्पृश्य हलायुधः ।दत्त्वा चैव बहून्दायान्विप्राणां विप्रवत्सलः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tatrāplutya sa dharmātmā upaspṛśya halāyudhaḥ ,dattvā caiva bahūndāyānviprāṇāṁ vipravatsalaḥ.
english: Having bathed and performed ablutions there, he, Halāyudha, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness and who was very fond of brahmins, gave many gifts to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 30. Having bathed and performed ablutions there, he, Halāyudha, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) was righteousness and who was very fond of brahmins, gave many gifts to them.

sanskrit: ससर्ज यत्र भगवाँल्लोकाँल्लोकपितामहः ।यत्रार्ष्टिषेणः कौरव्य ब्राह्मण्यं संशितव्रतः ।तपसा महता राजन्प्राप्तवानृषिसत्तमः ॥३१॥
transliteration: sasarja yatra bhagavāँllokāँllokapitāmahaḥ ,yatrārṣṭiṣeṇaḥ kauravya brāhmaṇyaṁ saṁśitavrataḥ ,tapasā mahatā rājanprāptavānṛṣisattamaḥ.
english: O King (rājan), it was there that the venerable (bhagavān) grandfather of the worlds (lokapitāmaha), Brahmā, created the worlds. And it was there that Arṣṭiṣeṇa, the scion of Kuru, the best of sages (ṛṣisattama) and firm in his vows, achieved brahminhood through great ascetic practice (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 31. O King (rājan), it was there that the venerable (bhagavān) grandfather of the worlds (lokapitāmaha), Brahmā, created the worlds. And it was there that Arṣṭiṣeṇa, the scion of Kuru, the best of sages (ṛṣisattama) and firm in his vows, achieved brahminhood through great ascetic practice (tapas).

sanskrit: सिन्धुद्वीपश्च राजर्षिर्देवापिश्च महातपाः ।ब्राह्मण्यं लब्धवान्यत्र विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः ।महातपस्वी भगवानुग्रतेजा महातपाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sindhudvīpaśca rājarṣirdevāpiśca mahātapāḥ ,brāhmaṇyaṁ labdhavānyatra viśvāmitro mahāmuniḥ ,mahātapasvī bhagavānugratejā mahātapāḥ.
english: Where the royal sage (rājarṣi) Sindhudvīpa, and Devāpi, who performed great asceticism (mahātapāḥ), obtained brahminhood. Where the venerable (bhagavān) great sage (mahāmuni) Viśvāmitra, a great ascetic (mahātapasvī) of formidable energy (ugratejā) and immense ascetic practice (tapas), also attained brahminhood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 32. Where the royal sage (rājarṣi) Sindhudvīpa, and Devāpi, who performed great asceticism (mahātapāḥ), obtained brahminhood. Where the venerable (bhagavān) great sage (mahāmuni) Viśvāmitra, a great ascetic (mahātapasvī) of formidable energy (ugratejā) and immense ascetic practice (tapas), also attained brahminhood.

sanskrit: तत्राजगाम बलवान्बलभद्रःप्रतापवान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatrājagāmabalavānbalabhadraḥ pratāpavān.
english: There, the powerful (balavān) and glorious (pratāpavān) Balarāma arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 38 Verse 33. There, the powerful (balavān) and glorious (pratāpavān) Balarāma arrived.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कथमार्ष्टिषेणो भगवान्विपुलं तप्तवांस्तपः ।सिन्धुद्वीपः कथं चापि ब्राह्मण्यं लब्धवांस्तदा ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kathamārṣṭiṣeṇo bhagavānvipulaṁ taptavāṁstapaḥ ,sindhudvīpaḥ kathaṁ cāpi brāhmaṇyaṁ labdhavāṁstadā.
english: Janamejaya said: How did the venerable Arṣṭiṣeṇa perform great austerities (tapas)? And how did Sindhudvīpa then attain brahminhood?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: How did the venerable Arṣṭiṣeṇa perform great austerities (tapas)? And how did Sindhudvīpa then attain brahminhood?

sanskrit: देवापिश्च कथं ब्रह्मन्विश्वामित्रश्च सत्तम ।तन्ममाचक्ष्व भगवन्परं कौतूहलं हि मे ॥२॥
transliteration: devāpiśca kathaṁ brahmanviśvāmitraśca sattama ,tanmamācakṣva bhagavanparaṁ kautūhalaṁ hi me.
english: O venerable Brahmin, best among excellent ones, explain to me how Devāpi and Viśvāmitra (achieved such things) as well. My curiosity is indeed great.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 2. O venerable Brahmin, best among excellent ones, explain to me how Devāpi and Viśvāmitra (achieved such things) as well. My curiosity is indeed great.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।पुरा कृतयुगे राजन्नार्ष्टिषेणो द्विजोत्तमः ।वसन्गुरुकुले नित्यं नित्यमध्ययने रतः ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,purā kṛtayuge rājannārṣṭiṣeṇo dvijottamaḥ ,vasangurukule nityaṁ nityamadhyayane rataḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O King, formerly, in the Kṛta Yuga, Arṣṭiṣeṇa, the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), constantly resided in the guru's āśrama and was always devoted to study.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 3. Vaiśampāyana said: O King, formerly, in the Kṛta Yuga, Arṣṭiṣeṇa, the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), constantly resided in the guru's āśrama and was always devoted to study.

sanskrit: तस्य राजन्गुरुकुले वसतो नित्यमेव ह ।समाप्तिं नागमद्विद्या नापि वेदा विशां पते ॥४॥
transliteration: tasya rājangurukule vasato nityameva ha ,samāptiṁ nāgamadvidyā nāpi vedā viśāṁ pate.
english: O King, O lord of the people, even though he always resided in the guru's āśrama, his learning, nor even the Vedas, ever reached completion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 4. O King, O lord of the people, even though he always resided in the guru's āśrama, his learning, nor even the Vedas, ever reached completion.

sanskrit: स निर्विण्णस्ततो राजंस्तपस्तेपे महातपाः ।ततो वै तपसा तेन प्राप्य वेदाननुत्तमान् ॥५॥
transliteration: sa nirviṇṇastato rājaṁstapastepe mahātapāḥ ,tato vai tapasā tena prāpya vedānanuttamān.
english: O King, he, having become disillusioned from that state, performed intense spiritual austerities (tapas), being a great ascetic. Then, through those very austerities (tapas), he indeed obtained the unsurpassed Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 5. O King, he, having become disillusioned from that state, performed intense spiritual austerities (tapas), being a great ascetic. Then, through those very austerities (tapas), he indeed obtained the unsurpassed Vedas.

sanskrit: स विद्वान्वेदयुक्तश्च सिद्धश्चाप्यृषिसत्तमः ।तत्र तीर्थे वरान्प्रादात्त्रीनेव सुमहातपाः ॥६॥
transliteration: sa vidvānvedayuktaśca siddhaścāpyṛṣisattamaḥ ,tatra tīrthe varānprādāttrīneva sumahātapāḥ.
english: He, being learned, proficient in the Vedas, accomplished, and indeed the best among sages, that great ascetic (tapasvin) then bestowed exactly three boons at that sacred place (tīrtha).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 6. He, being learned, proficient in the Vedas, accomplished, and indeed the best among sages, that great ascetic (tapasvin) then bestowed exactly three boons at that sacred place (tīrtha).

sanskrit: अस्मिंस्तीर्थे महानद्या अद्यप्रभृति मानवः ।आप्लुतो वाजिमेधस्य फलं प्राप्नोति पुष्कलम् ॥७॥
transliteration: asmiṁstīrthe mahānadyā adyaprabhṛti mānavaḥ ,āpluto vājimedhasya phalaṁ prāpnoti puṣkalam.
english: From today onwards, a human being who bathes in this sacred ford (tīrtha) of the great river obtains the abundant fruit of the Vājimedha Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 7. From today onwards, a human being who bathes in this sacred ford (tīrtha) of the great river obtains the abundant fruit of the Vājimedha Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: अद्यप्रभृति नैवात्र भयं व्यालाद्भविष्यति ।अपि चाल्पेन यत्नेन फलं प्राप्स्यति पुष्कलम् ॥८॥
transliteration: adyaprabhṛti naivātra bhayaṁ vyālādbhaviṣyati ,api cālpena yatnena phalaṁ prāpsyati puṣkalam.
english: From today onwards, there will be no fear of venomous creatures here. Moreover, with even slight effort, one will achieve abundant results.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 8. From today onwards, there will be no fear of venomous creatures here. Moreover, with even slight effort, one will achieve abundant results.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजा जगाम त्रिदिवं मुनिः ।एवं सिद्धः स भगवानार्ष्टिषेणः प्रतापवान् ॥९॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahātejā jagāma tridivaṁ muniḥ ,evaṁ siddhaḥ sa bhagavānārṣṭiṣeṇaḥ pratāpavān.
english: Having thus spoken, the highly illustrious sage went to heaven. In this way, that powerful and venerable Arṣṭiṣeṇa became perfected.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 9. Having thus spoken, the highly illustrious sage went to heaven. In this way, that powerful and venerable Arṣṭiṣeṇa became perfected.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्नेव तदा तीर्थे सिन्धुद्वीपः प्रतापवान् ।देवापिश्च महाराज ब्राह्मण्यं प्रापतुर्महत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasminneva tadā tīrthe sindhudvīpaḥ pratāpavān ,devāpiśca mahārāja brāhmaṇyaṁ prāpaturmahat.
english: O great king, at that very holy place (tīrtha), the mighty Sindhudvīpa and Devāpi both then attained great brahminhood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 10. O great king, at that very holy place (tīrtha), the mighty Sindhudvīpa and Devāpi both then attained great brahminhood.

sanskrit: तथा च कौशिकस्तात तपोनित्यो जितेन्द्रियः ।तपसा वै सुतप्तेन ब्राह्मणत्वमवाप्तवान् ॥११॥
transliteration: tathā ca kauśikastāta taponityo jitendriyaḥ ,tapasā vai sutaptena brāhmaṇatvamavāptavān.
english: And similarly, O dear one, Kauśika, who was ever devoted to asceticism (tapas) and had controlled his senses, indeed attained brahminhood by intensely performed asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 11. And similarly, O dear one, Kauśika, who was ever devoted to asceticism (tapas) and had controlled his senses, indeed attained brahminhood by intensely performed asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: गाधिर्नाम महानासीत्क्षत्रियः प्रथितो भुवि ।तस्य पुत्रोऽभवद्राजन्विश्वामित्रः प्रतापवान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: gādhirnāma mahānāsītkṣatriyaḥ prathito bhuvi ,tasya putro'bhavadrājanviśvāmitraḥ pratāpavān.
english: There was a great kṣatriya named Gādhi, renowned on earth. O king, his son was the mighty Viśvāmitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 12. There was a great kṣatriya named Gādhi, renowned on earth. O king, his son was the mighty Viśvāmitra.

sanskrit: स राजा कौशिकस्तात महायोग्यभवत्किल ।स पुत्रमभिषिच्याथ विश्वामित्रं महातपाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa rājā kauśikastāta mahāyogyabhavatkila ,sa putramabhiṣicyātha viśvāmitraṁ mahātapāḥ.
english: O father, that King Kauśika truly became a great ascetic (tapas). Then, that great ascetic, having consecrated his son Viśvāmitra...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 13. O father, that King Kauśika truly became a great ascetic (tapas). Then, that great ascetic, having consecrated his son Viśvāmitra...

sanskrit: देहन्यासे मनश्चक्रे तमूचुः प्रणताः प्रजाः ।न गन्तव्यं महाप्राज्ञ त्राहि चास्मान्महाभयात् ॥१४॥
transliteration: dehanyāse manaścakre tamūcuḥ praṇatāḥ prajāḥ ,na gantavyaṁ mahāprājña trāhi cāsmānmahābhayāt.
english: He fixed his mind on abandoning his body. The prostrate people then said to him: "O greatly wise one, you should not go! And protect us from great fear!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 14. He fixed his mind on abandoning his body. The prostrate people then said to him: "O greatly wise one, you should not go! And protect us from great fear!"

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः प्रत्युवाच ततो गाधिः प्रजास्तदा ।विश्वस्य जगतो गोप्ता भविष्यति सुतो मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ pratyuvāca tato gādhiḥ prajāstadā ,viśvasya jagato goptā bhaviṣyati suto mama.
english: Thus addressed, Gādhi then replied to the people: "My son will be the protector of the entire world (jagat)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 15. Thus addressed, Gādhi then replied to the people: "My son will be the protector of the entire world (jagat)."

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा तु ततो गाधिर्विश्वामित्रं निवेश्य च ।जगाम त्रिदिवं राजन्विश्वामित्रोऽभवन्नृपः ।न च शक्नोति पृथिवीं यत्नवानपि रक्षितुम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ityuktvā tu tato gādhirviśvāmitraṁ niveśya ca ,jagāma tridivaṁ rājanviśvāmitro'bhavannṛpaḥ ,na ca śaknoti pṛthivīṁ yatnavānapi rakṣitum.
english: Having thus spoken, Gādhi then installed Viśvāmitra and went to heaven (tridiva), O King. Viśvāmitra became king; yet, even though he tried, he was unable to protect the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 16. Having thus spoken, Gādhi then installed Viśvāmitra and went to heaven (tridiva), O King. Viśvāmitra became king; yet, even though he tried, he was unable to protect the earth.

sanskrit: ततः शुश्राव राजा स राक्षसेभ्यो महाभयम् ।निर्ययौ नगराच्चापि चतुरङ्गबलान्वितः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ śuśrāva rājā sa rākṣasebhyo mahābhayam ,niryayau nagarāccāpi caturaṅgabalānvitaḥ.
english: The king then heard of a great danger from the Rākṣasas. And so, he set out from the city, accompanied by his four-fold army.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 17. The king then heard of a great danger from the Rākṣasas. And so, he set out from the city, accompanied by his four-fold army.

sanskrit: स गत्वा दूरमध्वानं वसिष्ठाश्रममभ्ययात् ।तस्य ते सैनिका राजंश्चक्रुस्तत्रानयान्बहून् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa gatvā dūramadhvānaṁ vasiṣṭhāśramamabhyayāt ,tasya te sainikā rājaṁścakrustatrānayānbahūn.
english: Having traveled a long distance, he reached Vasiṣṭha's hermitage (āśrama). O King (rājan), his soldiers created many disturbances there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 18. Having traveled a long distance, he reached Vasiṣṭha's hermitage (āśrama). O King (rājan), his soldiers created many disturbances there.

sanskrit: ततस्तु भगवान्विप्रो वसिष्ठोऽऽश्रममभ्ययात् ।ददृशे च ततः सर्वं भज्यमानं महावनम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tatastu bhagavānvipro vasiṣṭho''śramamabhyayāt ,dadṛśe ca tataḥ sarvaṁ bhajyamānaṁ mahāvanam.
english: But then, the venerable Brahmin Vasiṣṭha returned to his hermitage (āśrama). And he then saw the entire great forest being destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 19. But then, the venerable Brahmin Vasiṣṭha returned to his hermitage (āśrama). And he then saw the entire great forest being destroyed.

sanskrit: तस्य क्रुद्धो महाराज वसिष्ठो मुनिसत्तमः ।सृजस्व शबरान्घोरानिति स्वां गामुवाच ह ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasya kruddho mahārāja vasiṣṭho munisattamaḥ ,sṛjasva śabarānghorāniti svāṁ gāmuvāca ha.
english: Angered at him (the king), Vasiṣṭha, the foremost (sattama) among sages (muni), indeed spoke to his own cow, saying, "Create dreadful Śabaras!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 20. Angered at him (the king), Vasiṣṭha, the foremost (sattama) among sages (muni), indeed spoke to his own cow, saying, "Create dreadful Śabaras!"

sanskrit: तथोक्ता सासृजद्धेनुः पुरुषान्घोरदर्शनान् ।ते च तद्बलमासाद्य बभञ्जुः सर्वतोदिशम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathoktā sāsṛjaddhenuḥ puruṣānghoradarśanān ,te ca tadbalamāsādya babhañjuḥ sarvatodiśam.
english: Thus addressed, the cow created men of formidable appearance. And having acquired that power, they routed (the enemies) in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 21. Thus addressed, the cow created men of formidable appearance. And having acquired that power, they routed (the enemies) in all directions.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा विद्रुतं सैन्यं विश्वामित्रस्तु गाधिजः ।तपः परं मन्यमानस्तपस्येव मनो दधे ॥२२॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā vidrutaṁ sainyaṁ viśvāmitrastu gādhijaḥ ,tapaḥ paraṁ manyamānastapasyeva mano dadhe.
english: But upon seeing his army routed, Viśvāmitra, son of Gādhi, considering spiritual discipline (tapas) to be supreme, focused his mind solely on asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 22. But upon seeing his army routed, Viśvāmitra, son of Gādhi, considering spiritual discipline (tapas) to be supreme, focused his mind solely on asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: सोऽस्मिंस्तीर्थवरे राजन्सरस्वत्याः समाहितः ।नियमैश्चोपवासैश्च कर्शयन्देहमात्मनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: so'smiṁstīrthavare rājansarasvatyāḥ samāhitaḥ ,niyamaiścopavāsaiśca karśayandehamātmanaḥ.
english: O King, he, absorbed in this supreme sacred site (tīrtha) of the Sarasvatī river, emaciated his own body through observances (niyama) and fasts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 23. O King, he, absorbed in this supreme sacred site (tīrtha) of the Sarasvatī river, emaciated his own body through observances (niyama) and fasts.

sanskrit: जलाहारो वायुभक्षः पर्णाहारश्च सोऽभवत् ।तथा स्थण्डिलशायी च ये चान्ये नियमाः पृथक् ॥२४॥
transliteration: jalāhāro vāyubhakṣaḥ parṇāhāraśca so'bhavat ,tathā sthaṇḍilaśāyī ca ye cānye niyamāḥ pṛthak.
english: He became one who subsisted on water, then on air, and then on leaves. Likewise, he slept on the bare ground, and observed other various observances (niyama).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 24. He became one who subsisted on water, then on air, and then on leaves. Likewise, he slept on the bare ground, and observed other various observances (niyama).

sanskrit: असकृत्तस्य देवास्तु व्रतविघ्नं प्रचक्रिरे ।न चास्य नियमाद्बुद्धिरपयाति महात्मनः ॥२५॥
transliteration: asakṛttasya devāstu vratavighnaṁ pracakrire ,na cāsya niyamādbuddhirapayāti mahātmanaḥ.
english: The gods repeatedly created obstacles to his vow, but the great-souled one's resolve did not waver from his discipline.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 25. The gods repeatedly created obstacles to his vow, but the great-souled one's resolve did not waver from his discipline.

sanskrit: ततः परेण यत्नेन तप्त्वा बहुविधं तपः ।तेजसा भास्कराकारो गाधिजः समपद्यत ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ pareṇa yatnena taptvā bahuvidhaṁ tapaḥ ,tejasā bhāskarākāro gādhijaḥ samapadyata.
english: Then, having performed many kinds of austerity (tapas) with supreme effort, the son of Gādhi became radiant like the sun in his splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 26. Then, having performed many kinds of austerity (tapas) with supreme effort, the son of Gādhi became radiant like the sun in his splendor.

sanskrit: तपसा तु तथा युक्तं विश्वामित्रं पितामहः ।अमन्यत महातेजा वरदो वरमस्य तत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tapasā tu tathā yuktaṁ viśvāmitraṁ pitāmahaḥ ,amanyata mahātejā varado varamasya tat.
english: Indeed, the greatly radiant grandfather (Brahmā), the bestower of boons, thus considered Viśvāmitra, who was engaged in austerity (tapas), worthy of that boon of his.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 27. Indeed, the greatly radiant grandfather (Brahmā), the bestower of boons, thus considered Viśvāmitra, who was engaged in austerity (tapas), worthy of that boon of his.

sanskrit: स तु वव्रे वरं राजन्स्यामहं ब्राह्मणस्त्विति ।तथेति चाब्रवीद्ब्रह्मा सर्वलोकपितामहः ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa tu vavre varaṁ rājansyāmahaṁ brāhmaṇastviti ,tatheti cābravīdbrahmā sarvalokapitāmahaḥ.
english: O King, he (Viśvāmitra) indeed requested a boon, saying, 'May I be a Brahmin!' And Brahmā, the grandfather of all worlds, said, 'So be it!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 28. O King, he (Viśvāmitra) indeed requested a boon, saying, 'May I be a Brahmin!' And Brahmā, the grandfather of all worlds, said, 'So be it!'

sanskrit: स लब्ध्वा तपसोग्रेण ब्राह्मणत्वं महायशाः ।विचचार महीं कृत्स्नां कृतकामः सुरोपमः ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa labdhvā tapasogreṇa brāhmaṇatvaṁ mahāyaśāḥ ,vicacāra mahīṁ kṛtsnāṁ kṛtakāmaḥ suropamaḥ.
english: The greatly renowned one, having attained the status of a Brahmin through severe asceticism (tapas), and with his desires fulfilled, wandered the entire earth like a god.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 29. The greatly renowned one, having attained the status of a Brahmin through severe asceticism (tapas), and with his desires fulfilled, wandered the entire earth like a god.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तीर्थवरे रामः प्रदाय विविधं वसु ।पयस्विनीस्तथा धेनूर्यानानि शयनानि च ॥३०॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstīrthavare rāmaḥ pradāya vividhaṁ vasu ,payasvinīstathā dhenūryānāni śayanāni ca.
english: In that excellent sacred bathing place (tīrtha), Rāma generously bestowed various riches, as well as milk-yielding cows, conveyances, and beds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 30. In that excellent sacred bathing place (tīrtha), Rāma generously bestowed various riches, as well as milk-yielding cows, conveyances, and beds.

sanskrit: तथा वस्त्राण्यलंकारं भक्ष्यं पेयं च शोभनम् ।अददान्मुदितो राजन्पूजयित्वा द्विजोत्तमान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tathā vastrāṇyalaṁkāraṁ bhakṣyaṁ peyaṁ ca śobhanam ,adadānmudito rājanpūjayitvā dvijottamān.
english: O King, he joyfully bestowed garments, ornaments, and excellent food and drink, after having honored the best of Brahmins (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 31. O King, he joyfully bestowed garments, ornaments, and excellent food and drink, after having honored the best of Brahmins (dvija).

sanskrit: ययौ राजंस्ततो रामो बकस्याश्रममन्तिकात् ।यत्र तेपे तपस्तीव्रं दाल्भ्यो बक इति श्रुतिः ॥३२॥
transliteration: yayau rājaṁstato rāmo bakasyāśramamantikāt ,yatra tepe tapastīvraṁ dālbhyo baka iti śrutiḥ.
english: Then, O King, Rāma departed from that place and went to the hermitage (āśrama) of Baka, a place where, it is said, Dalbhya, also known as Baka, performed severe asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 39 Verse 32. Then, O King, Rāma departed from that place and went to the hermitage (āśrama) of Baka, a place where, it is said, Dalbhya, also known as Baka, performed severe asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ब्रह्मयोनिभिराकीर्णं जगाम यदुनन्दनः ।यत्र दाल्भ्यो बको राजन्पश्वर्थं सुमहातपाः ।जुहाव धृतराष्ट्रस्य राष्ट्रं वैचित्रवीर्यिणः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,brahmayonibhirākīrṇaṁ jagāma yadunandanaḥ ,yatra dālbhyo bako rājanpaśvarthaṁ sumahātapāḥ ,juhāva dhṛtarāṣṭrasya rāṣṭraṁ vaicitravīryiṇaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O King, the delight of the Yadus (Krishna) went to a place crowded with sages (brahmayonibhiḥ). There, Dalbhya Baka, a greatly ascetic sage, offered the kingdom of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Citravīrya, as a Vedic ritual (yajña) for the sake of cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O King, the delight of the Yadus (Krishna) went to a place crowded with sages (brahmayonibhiḥ). There, Dalbhya Baka, a greatly ascetic sage, offered the kingdom of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Citravīrya, as a Vedic ritual (yajña) for the sake of cattle.

sanskrit: तपसा घोररूपेण कर्शयन्देहमात्मनः ।क्रोधेन महताविष्टो धर्मात्मा वै प्रतापवान् ॥२॥
transliteration: tapasā ghorarūpeṇa karśayandehamātmanaḥ ,krodhena mahatāviṣṭo dharmātmā vai pratāpavān.
english: Weakening his own body through terrible austerity (tapas), and overwhelmed by great anger, he was indeed a righteous soul (dharmātmā) and powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 2. Weakening his own body through terrible austerity (tapas), and overwhelmed by great anger, he was indeed a righteous soul (dharmātmā) and powerful.

sanskrit: पुरा हि नैमिषेयाणां सत्रे द्वादशवार्षिके ।वृत्ते विश्वजितोऽन्ते वै पाञ्चालानृषयोऽगमन् ॥३॥
transliteration: purā hi naimiṣeyāṇāṁ satre dvādaśavārṣike ,vṛtte viśvajito'nte vai pāñcālānṛṣayo'gaman.
english: Formerly, after the twelve-year long Vedic ritual (satra) of the sages in Naimiṣa had concluded, and at the end of the Viśvajit ritual, the sages then went to the Pañcāla country.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 3. Formerly, after the twelve-year long Vedic ritual (satra) of the sages in Naimiṣa had concluded, and at the end of the Viśvajit ritual, the sages then went to the Pañcāla country.

sanskrit: तत्रेश्वरमयाचन्त दक्षिणार्थं मनीषिणः ।बलान्वितान्वत्सतरान्निर्व्याधीनेकविंशतिम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tatreśvaramayācanta dakṣiṇārthaṁ manīṣiṇaḥ ,balānvitānvatsatarānnirvyādhīnekaviṁśatim.
english: There, the wise sages asked the lord (Īśvara) for twenty-one strong, disease-free young calves as the sacrificial fee (dakṣiṇā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 4. There, the wise sages asked the lord (Īśvara) for twenty-one strong, disease-free young calves as the sacrificial fee (dakṣiṇā).

sanskrit: तानब्रवीद्बको वृद्धो विभजध्वं पशूनिति ।पशूनेतानहं त्यक्त्वा भिक्षिष्ये राजसत्तमम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tānabravīdbako vṛddho vibhajadhvaṁ paśūniti ,paśūnetānahaṁ tyaktvā bhikṣiṣye rājasattamam.
english: The old Baka told them, "Distribute these animals. As for me, having given up these animals, I will go begging from the best of kings."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 5. The old Baka told them, "Distribute these animals. As for me, having given up these animals, I will go begging from the best of kings."

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्नृषीन्सर्वान्प्रतापवान् ।जगाम धृतराष्ट्रस्य भवनं ब्राह्मणोत्तमः ॥६॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tato rājannṛṣīnsarvānpratāpavān ,jagāma dhṛtarāṣṭrasya bhavanaṁ brāhmaṇottamaḥ.
english: O king, having spoken thus to all the sages, that powerful, excellent Brahmin then went to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 6. O king, having spoken thus to all the sages, that powerful, excellent Brahmin then went to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's palace.

sanskrit: स समीपगतो भूत्वा धृतराष्ट्रं जनेश्वरम् ।अयाचत पशून्दाल्भ्यः स चैनं रुषितोऽब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: sa samīpagato bhūtvā dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janeśvaram ,ayācata paśūndālbhyaḥ sa cainaṁ ruṣito'bravīt.
english: Having approached Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of men, Dālbhya asked him for animals. And that king, becoming angry, spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 7. Having approached Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of men, Dālbhya asked him for animals. And that king, becoming angry, spoke to him.

sanskrit: यदृच्छया मृता दृष्ट्वा गास्तदा नृपसत्तम ।एतान्पशून्नय क्षिप्रं ब्रह्मबन्धो यदीच्छसि ॥८॥
transliteration: yadṛcchayā mṛtā dṛṣṭvā gāstadā nṛpasattama ,etānpaśūnnaya kṣipraṁ brahmabandho yadīcchasi.
