english: O best of kings, having seen these cows that died by chance, if you wish, O mere kinsman of Brahmins, take these animals quickly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 8. O best of kings, having seen these cows that died by chance, if you wish, O mere kinsman of Brahmins, take these animals quickly.

sanskrit: ऋषिस्त्वथ वचः श्रुत्वा चिन्तयामास धर्मवित् ।अहो बत नृशंसं वै वाक्यमुक्तोऽस्मि संसदि ॥९॥
transliteration: ṛṣistvatha vacaḥ śrutvā cintayāmāsa dharmavit ,aho bata nṛśaṁsaṁ vai vākyamukto'smi saṁsadi.
english: The sage, who understood natural law (dharma), then, having heard those words, pondered: 'Alas! I have indeed been spoken to with cruel words in this assembly.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 9. The sage, who understood natural law (dharma), then, having heard those words, pondered: 'Alas! I have indeed been spoken to with cruel words in this assembly.'

sanskrit: चिन्तयित्वा मुहूर्तं च रोषाविष्टो द्विजोत्तमः ।मतिं चक्रे विनाशाय धृतराष्ट्रस्य भूपतेः ॥१०॥
transliteration: cintayitvā muhūrtaṁ ca roṣāviṣṭo dvijottamaḥ ,matiṁ cakre vināśāya dhṛtarāṣṭrasya bhūpateḥ.
english: Having pondered for a moment, that best of the twice-born (dvija), overcome by anger, resolved upon the destruction of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 10. Having pondered for a moment, that best of the twice-born (dvija), overcome by anger, resolved upon the destruction of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: स उत्कृत्य मृतानां वै मांसानि द्विजसत्तमः ।जुहाव धृतराष्ट्रस्य राष्ट्रं नरपतेः पुरा ॥११॥
transliteration: sa utkṛtya mṛtānāṁ vai māṁsāni dvijasattamaḥ ,juhāva dhṛtarāṣṭrasya rāṣṭraṁ narapateḥ purā.
english: That best of the twice-born (dvija), indeed, having cut out the flesh of the dead, then, as a primary act, offered (as an oblation) the kingdom of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 11. That best of the twice-born (dvija), indeed, having cut out the flesh of the dead, then, as a primary act, offered (as an oblation) the kingdom of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: अवकीर्णे सरस्वत्यास्तीर्थे प्रज्वाल्य पावकम् ।बको दाल्भ्यो महाराज नियमं परमास्थितः ।स तैरेव जुहावास्य राष्ट्रं मांसैर्महातपाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: avakīrṇe sarasvatyāstīrthe prajvālya pāvakam ,bako dālbhyo mahārāja niyamaṁ paramāsthitaḥ ,sa taireva juhāvāsya rāṣṭraṁ māṁsairmahātapāḥ.
english: O Great King, Baka Dālbhya, who was firmly established in his vow (niyama) and possessed of great austerity (tapas), having kindled the fire in a secluded sacred bathing place (tīrtha) on the Sarasvati river, offered a kingdom with those very meats (as an oblation for its destruction).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 12. O Great King, Baka Dālbhya, who was firmly established in his vow (niyama) and possessed of great austerity (tapas), having kindled the fire in a secluded sacred bathing place (tīrtha) on the Sarasvati river, offered a kingdom with those very meats (as an oblation for its destruction).

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु विधिवत्सत्रे संप्रवृत्ते सुदारुणे ।अक्षीयत ततो राष्ट्रं धृतराष्ट्रस्य पार्थिव ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu vidhivatsatre saṁpravṛtte sudāruṇe ,akṣīyata tato rāṣṭraṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya pārthiva.
english: Indeed, when that extremely dreadful Vedic ritual (yajña) commenced, performed according to the prescribed rituals, then the kingdom of Dhṛtarāṣṭra began to dwindle, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 13. Indeed, when that extremely dreadful Vedic ritual (yajña) commenced, performed according to the prescribed rituals, then the kingdom of Dhṛtarāṣṭra began to dwindle, O king.

sanskrit: छिद्यमानं यथानन्तं वनं परशुना विभो ।बभूवापहतं तच्चाप्यवकीर्णमचेतनम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: chidyamānaṁ yathānantaṁ vanaṁ paraśunā vibho ,babhūvāpahataṁ taccāpyavakīrṇamacetanam.
english: Just as an immense forest (vana) is cut down by an axe, O mighty lord, so too that kingdom became devastated, scattered, and lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 14. Just as an immense forest (vana) is cut down by an axe, O mighty lord, so too that kingdom became devastated, scattered, and lifeless.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तदवकीर्णं तु राष्ट्रं स मनुजाधिपः ।बभूव दुर्मना राजंश्चिन्तयामास च प्रभुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tadavakīrṇaṁ tu rāṣṭraṁ sa manujādhipaḥ ,babhūva durmanā rājaṁścintayāmāsa ca prabhuḥ.
english: Having seen that devastated kingdom, that lord of men (manujādhipaḥ) became dejected, O king (rājan), and indeed, that powerful ruler (prabhuḥ) began to ponder.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 15. Having seen that devastated kingdom, that lord of men (manujādhipaḥ) became dejected, O king (rājan), and indeed, that powerful ruler (prabhuḥ) began to ponder.

sanskrit: मोक्षार्थमकरोद्यत्नं ब्राह्मणैः सहितः पुरा ।अथासौ पार्थिवः खिन्नस्ते च विप्रास्तदा नृप ॥१६॥
transliteration: mokṣārthamakarodyatnaṁ brāhmaṇaiḥ sahitaḥ purā ,athāsau pārthivaḥ khinnaste ca viprāstadā nṛpa.
english: Formerly, he, accompanied by Brahmins, made an effort towards final liberation (mokṣa). But then, O king (nṛpa), that ruler (pārthiva) became dejected, and so did those Brahmins (vipra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 16. Formerly, he, accompanied by Brahmins, made an effort towards final liberation (mokṣa). But then, O king (nṛpa), that ruler (pārthiva) became dejected, and so did those Brahmins (vipra).

sanskrit: यदा चापि न शक्नोति राष्ट्रं मोचयितुं नृप ।अथ वैप्राश्निकांस्तत्र पप्रच्छ जनमेजय ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadā cāpi na śaknoti rāṣṭraṁ mocayituṁ nṛpa ,atha vaiprāśnikāṁstatra papraccha janamejaya.
english: And when the king (Janamejaya) was unable to liberate his kingdom, then Janamejaya consulted the inquirers (vaiprāśnikas) there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 17. And when the king (Janamejaya) was unable to liberate his kingdom, then Janamejaya consulted the inquirers (vaiprāśnikas) there.

sanskrit: ततो वैप्राश्निकाः प्राहुः पशुविप्रकृतस्त्वया ।मांसैरभिजुहोतीति तव राष्ट्रं मुनिर्बकः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato vaiprāśnikāḥ prāhuḥ paśuviprakṛtastvayā ,māṁsairabhijuhotīti tava rāṣṭraṁ munirbakaḥ.
english: Then the inquirers said, 'The sage Baka in your kingdom is offering oblations with flesh (māṃsa), harming animals (paśu) with your (implicit) consent.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 18. Then the inquirers said, 'The sage Baka in your kingdom is offering oblations with flesh (māṃsa), harming animals (paśu) with your (implicit) consent.'

sanskrit: तेन ते हूयमानस्य राष्ट्रस्यास्य क्षयो महान् ।तस्यैतत्तपसः कर्म येन ते ह्यनयो महान् ।अपां कुञ्जे सरस्वत्यास्तं प्रसादय पार्थिव ॥१९॥
transliteration: tena te hūyamānasya rāṣṭrasyāsya kṣayo mahān ,tasyaitattapasaḥ karma yena te hyanayo mahān ,apāṁ kuñje sarasvatyāstaṁ prasādaya pārthiva.
english: By him (Baka), through the sacrificial offerings being made in this kingdom of yours, there is great destruction. This is the act (karma) of his ascetic practice (tapas), by which indeed a great misfortune has come upon you. O ruler (pārthiva), propitiate him in the water-grove (kuñja) by the Sarasvati river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 19. By him (Baka), through the sacrificial offerings being made in this kingdom of yours, there is great destruction. This is the act (karma) of his ascetic practice (tapas), by which indeed a great misfortune has come upon you. O ruler (pārthiva), propitiate him in the water-grove (kuñja) by the Sarasvati river.

sanskrit: सरस्वतीं ततो गत्वा स राजा बकमब्रवीत् ।निपत्य शिरसा भूमौ प्राञ्जलिर्भरतर्षभ ॥२०॥
transliteration: sarasvatīṁ tato gatvā sa rājā bakamabravīt ,nipatya śirasā bhūmau prāñjalirbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha)! Then, having gone to the Sarasvati (river), that king spoke to Baka, prostrating himself with his head on the ground and with folded hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 20. O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha)! Then, having gone to the Sarasvati (river), that king spoke to Baka, prostrating himself with his head on the ground and with folded hands.

sanskrit: प्रसादये त्वा भगवन्नपराधं क्षमस्व मे ।मम दीनस्य लुब्धस्य मौर्ख्येण हतचेतसः ।त्वं गतिस्त्वं च मे नाथः प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥२१॥
transliteration: prasādaye tvā bhagavannaparādhaṁ kṣamasva me ,mama dīnasya lubdhasya maurkhyeṇa hatacetasaḥ ,tvaṁ gatistvaṁ ca me nāthaḥ prasādaṁ kartumarhasi.
english: O Lord, I seek your favor; please forgive my offense. I am wretched, deluded, and my mind is bewildered by foolishness. You are my refuge and my protector. You should grant me your grace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 21. O Lord, I seek your favor; please forgive my offense. I am wretched, deluded, and my mind is bewildered by foolishness. You are my refuge and my protector. You should grant me your grace.

sanskrit: तं तथा विलपन्तं तु शोकोपहतचेतसम् ।दृष्ट्वा तस्य कृपा जज्ञे राष्ट्रं तच्च व्यमोचयत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā vilapantaṁ tu śokopahatacetasam ,dṛṣṭvā tasya kṛpā jajñe rāṣṭraṁ tacca vyamocayat.
english: Seeing him lamenting in such a state, with his mind overwhelmed by sorrow, compassion arose in the Rishi. And he liberated that kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 22. Seeing him lamenting in such a state, with his mind overwhelmed by sorrow, compassion arose in the Rishi. And he liberated that kingdom.

sanskrit: ऋषिः प्रसन्नस्तस्याभूत्संरम्भं च विहाय सः ।मोक्षार्थं तस्य राष्ट्रस्य जुहाव पुनराहुतिम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: ṛṣiḥ prasannastasyābhūtsaṁrambhaṁ ca vihāya saḥ ,mokṣārthaṁ tasya rāṣṭrasya juhāva punarāhutim.
english: The sage became gracious towards him, and abandoning his anger, he again offered an oblation (āhuti) for the liberation (mokṣa) of that kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 23. The sage became gracious towards him, and abandoning his anger, he again offered an oblation (āhuti) for the liberation (mokṣa) of that kingdom.

sanskrit: मोक्षयित्वा ततो राष्ट्रं प्रतिगृह्य पशून्बहून् ।हृष्टात्मा नैमिषारण्यं जगाम पुनरेव हि ॥२४॥
transliteration: mokṣayitvā tato rāṣṭraṁ pratigṛhya paśūnbahūn ,hṛṣṭātmā naimiṣāraṇyaṁ jagāma punareva hi.
english: Then, having liberated the kingdom and received many cattle, he, with a joyful heart (ātman), went back to Naimiṣāraṇya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 24. Then, having liberated the kingdom and received many cattle, he, with a joyful heart (ātman), went back to Naimiṣāraṇya.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रोऽपि धर्मात्मा स्वस्थचेता महामनाः ।स्वमेव नगरं राजा प्रतिपेदे महर्द्धिमत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭro'pi dharmātmā svasthacetā mahāmanāḥ ,svameva nagaraṁ rājā pratipede maharddhimat.
english: King Dhritarashtra, a man of righteous (dharma) character, with a tranquil and noble mind, also returned to his own very prosperous city.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 25. King Dhritarashtra, a man of righteous (dharma) character, with a tranquil and noble mind, also returned to his own very prosperous city.

sanskrit: तत्र तीर्थे महाराज बृहस्पतिरुदारधीः ।असुराणामभावाय भावाय च दिवौकसाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tatra tīrthe mahārāja bṛhaspatirudāradhīḥ ,asurāṇāmabhāvāya bhāvāya ca divaukasām.
english: O great king, there, at that sacred site, the magnanimous Brihaspati (performed a ritual) for the destruction of the Asuras and the prosperity of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 26. O great king, there, at that sacred site, the magnanimous Brihaspati (performed a ritual) for the destruction of the Asuras and the prosperity of the gods.

sanskrit: मांसैरपि जुहावेष्टिमक्षीयन्त ततोऽसुराः ।दैवतैरपि संभग्ना जितकाशिभिराहवे ॥२७॥
transliteration: māṁsairapi juhāveṣṭimakṣīyanta tato'surāḥ ,daivatairapi saṁbhagnā jitakāśibhirāhave.
english: Brihaspati offered a sacrifice (iṣṭi) even with meat. Consequently, the Asuras were diminished; they were utterly broken in battle by the victorious gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 27. Brihaspati offered a sacrifice (iṣṭi) even with meat. Consequently, the Asuras were diminished; they were utterly broken in battle by the victorious gods.

sanskrit: तत्रापि विधिवद्दत्त्वा ब्राह्मणेभ्यो महायशाः ।वाजिनः कुञ्जरांश्चैव रथांश्चाश्वतरीयुतान् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tatrāpi vidhivaddattvā brāhmaṇebhyo mahāyaśāḥ ,vājinaḥ kuñjarāṁścaiva rathāṁścāśvatarīyutān.
english: There, the very famous Brihaspati, having properly made donations (dāna) to the Brahmins of horses, elephants, and also chariots yoked with mules...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 28. There, the very famous Brihaspati, having properly made donations (dāna) to the Brahmins of horses, elephants, and also chariots yoked with mules...

sanskrit: रत्नानि च महार्हाणि धनं धान्यं च पुष्कलम् ।ययौ तीर्थं महाबाहुर्यायातं पृथिवीपते ॥२९॥
transliteration: ratnāni ca mahārhāṇi dhanaṁ dhānyaṁ ca puṣkalam ,yayau tīrthaṁ mahābāhuryāyātaṁ pṛthivīpate.
english: O lord of the earth, the mighty-armed one went to a sacred place (tīrtha) and to King Yayāti, (where there were) precious jewels, and abundant wealth and grain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 29. O lord of the earth, the mighty-armed one went to a sacred place (tīrtha) and to King Yayāti, (where there were) precious jewels, and abundant wealth and grain.

sanskrit: यत्र यज्ञे ययातेस्तु महाराज सरस्वती ।सर्पिः पयश्च सुस्राव नाहुषस्य महात्मनः ॥३०॥
transliteration: yatra yajñe yayātestu mahārāja sarasvatī ,sarpiḥ payaśca susrāva nāhuṣasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: O great king, where, in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of Yayāti, the son of the great-souled Nahuṣa, the Sarasvatī river indeed flowed abundantly with ghee and milk.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 30. O great king, where, in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of Yayāti, the son of the great-souled Nahuṣa, the Sarasvatī river indeed flowed abundantly with ghee and milk.

sanskrit: तत्रेष्ट्वा पुरुषव्याघ्रो ययातिः पृथिवीपतिः ।आक्रामदूर्ध्वं मुदितो लेभे लोकांश्च पुष्कलान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tatreṣṭvā puruṣavyāghro yayātiḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ ,ākrāmadūrdhvaṁ mudito lebhe lokāṁśca puṣkalān.
english: There, having performed the ritual (iṣṭi), the tiger among men, King Yayāti, delighted, ascended upwards and obtained abundant worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 31. There, having performed the ritual (iṣṭi), the tiger among men, King Yayāti, delighted, ascended upwards and obtained abundant worlds.

sanskrit: ययातेर्यजमानस्य यत्र राजन्सरस्वती ।प्रसृता प्रददौ कामान्ब्राह्मणानां महात्मनाम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: yayāteryajamānasya yatra rājansarasvatī ,prasṛtā pradadau kāmānbrāhmaṇānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: O King, where, for Yayāti, the sacrificer (yajamāna), the (river) Sarasvatī flowed forth and granted the desires of the great-souled brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 32. O King, where, for Yayāti, the sacrificer (yajamāna), the (river) Sarasvatī flowed forth and granted the desires of the great-souled brahmins.

sanskrit: यत्र यत्र हि यो विप्रो यान्यान्कामानभीप्सति ।तत्र तत्र सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा ससर्ज सुबहून्रसान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: yatra yatra hi yo vipro yānyānkāmānabhīpsati ,tatra tatra saricchreṣṭhā sasarja subahūnrasān.
english: Wherever any brahmin (vipra) desired various things, there the best of rivers produced an abundance of essences.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 33. Wherever any brahmin (vipra) desired various things, there the best of rivers produced an abundance of essences.

sanskrit: तत्र देवाः सगन्धर्वाः प्रीता यज्ञस्य संपदा ।विस्मिता मानुषाश्चासन्दृष्ट्वा तां यज्ञसंपदम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tatra devāḥ sagandharvāḥ prītā yajñasya saṁpadā ,vismitā mānuṣāścāsandṛṣṭvā tāṁ yajñasaṁpadam.
english: There, the gods along with the gandharvas were pleased by the opulence of the Vedic ritual (yajña). And humans were astonished, having seen that same opulence of the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 34. There, the gods along with the gandharvas were pleased by the opulence of the Vedic ritual (yajña). And humans were astonished, having seen that same opulence of the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: ततस्तालकेतुर्महाधर्मसेतुर्महात्मा कृतात्मा महादाननित्यः ।वसिष्ठापवाहं महाभीमवेगं धृतात्मा जितात्मा समभ्याजगाम ॥३५॥
transliteration: tatastālaketurmahādharmasetu;rmahātmā kṛtātmā mahādānanityaḥ ,vasiṣṭhāpavāhaṁ mahābhīmavegaṁ; dhṛtātmā jitātmā samabhyājagāma.
english: Then Tālaketu, who was a great upholder of natural law (dharma), noble-souled (mahātman), one who had controlled his inner self (ātman), constantly engaged in great acts of giving (dāna), possessed a resolute mind (ātman), and had conquered his lower self (ātman), approached the carrier of Vasiṣṭha, which moved with tremendous, dreadful speed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 40 Verse 35. Then Tālaketu, who was a great upholder of natural law (dharma), noble-souled (mahātman), one who had controlled his inner self (ātman), constantly engaged in great acts of giving (dāna), possessed a resolute mind (ātman), and had conquered his lower self (ātman), approached the carrier of Vasiṣṭha, which moved with tremendous, dreadful speed.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।वसिष्ठस्यापवाहो वै भीमवेगः कथं नु सः ।किमर्थं च सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा तमृषिं प्रत्यवाहयत् ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,vasiṣṭhasyāpavāho vai bhīmavegaḥ kathaṁ nu saḥ ,kimarthaṁ ca saricchreṣṭhā tamṛṣiṁ pratyavāhayat.
english: Janamejaya said: "How, indeed, was Vasiṣṭha's carrier of such dreadful speed? And for what purpose did the best of rivers carry away that sage?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "How, indeed, was Vasiṣṭha's carrier of such dreadful speed? And for what purpose did the best of rivers carry away that sage?"

sanskrit: केन चास्याभवद्वैरं कारणं किं च तत्प्रभो ।शंस पृष्टो महाप्राज्ञ न हि तृप्यामि कथ्यताम् ॥२॥
transliteration: kena cāsyābhavadvairaṁ kāraṇaṁ kiṁ ca tatprabho ,śaṁsa pṛṣṭo mahāprājña na hi tṛpyāmi kathyatām.
english: O Lord, by whom did enmity arise for him, and what was its cause? Please tell me, O greatly wise one, for I am not satisfied; let it be recounted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 2. O Lord, by whom did enmity arise for him, and what was its cause? Please tell me, O greatly wise one, for I am not satisfied; let it be recounted.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।विश्वामित्रस्य चैवर्षेर्वसिष्ठस्य च भारत ।भृशं वैरमभूद्राजंस्तपःस्पर्धाकृतं महत् ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,viśvāmitrasya caivarṣervasiṣṭhasya ca bhārata ,bhṛśaṁ vairamabhūdrājaṁstapaḥspardhākṛtaṁ mahat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), O King, indeed, a great enmity arose between the sage Viśvāmitra and Vasiṣṭha, caused by rivalry in their ascetic practices (tapas).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 3. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), O King, indeed, a great enmity arose between the sage Viśvāmitra and Vasiṣṭha, caused by rivalry in their ascetic practices (tapas).'

sanskrit: आश्रमो वै वसिष्ठस्य स्थाणुतीर्थेऽभवन्महान् ।पूर्वतः पश्चिमश्चासीद्विश्वामित्रस्य धीमतः ॥४॥
transliteration: āśramo vai vasiṣṭhasya sthāṇutīrthe'bhavanmahān ,pūrvataḥ paścimaścāsīdviśvāmitrasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Indeed, Vasiṣṭha's great hermitage (āśrama) was situated at Sthāṇutīrtha. To its east and west was the hermitage (āśrama) of the wise Viśvāmitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 4. Indeed, Vasiṣṭha's great hermitage (āśrama) was situated at Sthāṇutīrtha. To its east and west was the hermitage (āśrama) of the wise Viśvāmitra.

sanskrit: यत्र स्थाणुर्महाराज तप्तवान्सुमहत्तपः ।यत्रास्य कर्म तद्घोरं प्रवदन्ति मनीषिणः ॥५॥
transliteration: yatra sthāṇurmahārāja taptavānsumahattapaḥ ,yatrāsya karma tadghoraṁ pravadanti manīṣiṇaḥ.
english: O great king, it was at that very spot that Sthāṇu (Śiva) performed exceedingly great asceticism (tapas). There, the wise ones (manīṣiṇaḥ) declare, his terrible act (karma) occurred.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 5. O great king, it was at that very spot that Sthāṇu (Śiva) performed exceedingly great asceticism (tapas). There, the wise ones (manīṣiṇaḥ) declare, his terrible act (karma) occurred.

sanskrit: यत्रेष्ट्वा भगवान्स्थाणुः पूजयित्वा सरस्वतीम् ।स्थापयामास तत्तीर्थं स्थाणुतीर्थमिति प्रभो ॥६॥
transliteration: yatreṣṭvā bhagavānsthāṇuḥ pūjayitvā sarasvatīm ,sthāpayāmāsa tattīrthaṁ sthāṇutīrthamiti prabho.
english: O Lord, it was there that the divine Sthāṇu (Śiva), having performed a sacrifice and worshipped Sarasvatī, established that holy place (tīrtha) known as Sthāṇutīrtha.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 6. O Lord, it was there that the divine Sthāṇu (Śiva), having performed a sacrifice and worshipped Sarasvatī, established that holy place (tīrtha) known as Sthāṇutīrtha.

sanskrit: तत्र सर्वे सुराः स्कन्दमभ्यषिञ्चन्नराधिप ।सेनापत्येन महता सुरारिविनिबर्हणम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra sarve surāḥ skandamabhyaṣiñcannarādhipa ,senāpatyena mahatā surārivinibarhaṇam.
english: There, O King, all the gods consecrated Skanda to the great generalship (senāpatya), as he was the vanquisher of the enemies of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 7. There, O King, all the gods consecrated Skanda to the great generalship (senāpatya), as he was the vanquisher of the enemies of the gods.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्सरस्वतीतीर्थे विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः ।वसिष्ठं चालयामास तपसोग्रेण तच्छृणु ॥८॥
transliteration: tasminsarasvatītīrthe viśvāmitro mahāmuniḥ ,vasiṣṭhaṁ cālayāmāsa tapasogreṇa tacchṛṇu.
english: In that holy place (tīrtha) of Sarasvatī, the great sage (mahāmuni) Viśvāmitra caused Vasiṣṭha to waver from his position through his fierce asceticism (tapas). Listen to that account!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 8. In that holy place (tīrtha) of Sarasvatī, the great sage (mahāmuni) Viśvāmitra caused Vasiṣṭha to waver from his position through his fierce asceticism (tapas). Listen to that account!

sanskrit: विश्वामित्रवसिष्ठौ तावहन्यहनि भारत ।स्पर्धां तपःकृतां तीव्रां चक्रतुस्तौ तपोधनौ ॥९॥
transliteration: viśvāmitravasiṣṭhau tāvahanyahani bhārata ,spardhāṁ tapaḥkṛtāṁ tīvrāṁ cakratustau tapodhanau.
english: O Bhārata, those two, Viśvāmitra and Vasiṣṭha, who considered asceticism (tapas) their sole wealth (tapodhana), engaged daily in an intense rivalry (spardhā) stemming from their ascetic practices.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 9. O Bhārata, those two, Viśvāmitra and Vasiṣṭha, who considered asceticism (tapas) their sole wealth (tapodhana), engaged daily in an intense rivalry (spardhā) stemming from their ascetic practices.

sanskrit: तत एनं वेपमाना विवर्णा प्राञ्जलिस्तदा ।उपतस्थे मुनिवरं विश्वामित्रं सरस्वती ॥१४॥
transliteration: tata enaṁ vepamānā vivarṇā prāñjalistadā ,upatasthe munivaraṁ viśvāmitraṁ sarasvatī.
english: Then, trembling and pale, with folded hands, Sarasvatī approached the most excellent sage, Viśvāmitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 14. Then, trembling and pale, with folded hands, Sarasvatī approached the most excellent sage, Viśvāmitra.

sanskrit: हतवीरा यथा नारी साभवद्दुःखिता भृशम् ।ब्रूहि किं करवाणीति प्रोवाच मुनिसत्तमम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: hatavīrā yathā nārī sābhavadduḥkhitā bhṛśam ,brūhi kiṁ karavāṇīti provāca munisattamam.
english: Like a woman whose valiant ones have been slain, she became exceedingly distressed. She then said to the most excellent sage, "Tell me, what should I do?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 15. Like a woman whose valiant ones have been slain, she became exceedingly distressed. She then said to the most excellent sage, "Tell me, what should I do?"

sanskrit: तामुवाच मुनिः क्रुद्धो वसिष्ठं शीघ्रमानय ।यावदेनं निहन्म्यद्य तच्छ्रुत्वा व्यथिता नदी ॥१६॥
transliteration: tāmuvāca muniḥ kruddho vasiṣṭhaṁ śīghramānaya ,yāvadenaṁ nihanmyadya tacchrutvā vyathitā nadī.
english: The angry sage said to her, "Bring Vasiṣṭha quickly, so that I may kill him now!" Hearing that, the river became distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 16. The angry sage said to her, "Bring Vasiṣṭha quickly, so that I may kill him now!" Hearing that, the river became distressed.

sanskrit: साञ्जलिं तु ततः कृत्वा पुण्डरीकनिभेक्षणा ।विव्यथे सुविरूढेव लता वायुसमीरिता ॥१७॥
transliteration: sāñjaliṁ tu tataḥ kṛtvā puṇḍarīkanibhekṣaṇā ,vivyathe suvirūḍheva latā vāyusamīritā.
english: Then, having made her hands into a respectful gesture (añjali) and with lotus-like eyes, she became distressed, like a well-rooted creeper shaken by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 17. Then, having made her hands into a respectful gesture (añjali) and with lotus-like eyes, she became distressed, like a well-rooted creeper shaken by the wind.

sanskrit: तथागतां तु तां दृष्ट्वा वेपमानां कृताञ्जलिम् ।विश्वामित्रोऽब्रवीत्क्रुद्धो वसिष्ठं शीघ्रमानय ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathāgatāṁ tu tāṁ dṛṣṭvā vepamānāṁ kṛtāñjalim ,viśvāmitro'bravītkruddho vasiṣṭhaṁ śīghramānaya.
english: Seeing her, who had arrived in that manner, trembling and with folded hands, Viśvāmitra, enraged, said, "Bring Vasiṣṭha quickly!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 18. Seeing her, who had arrived in that manner, trembling and with folded hands, Viśvāmitra, enraged, said, "Bring Vasiṣṭha quickly!"

sanskrit: ततो भीता सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा चिन्तयामास भारत ।उभयोः शापयोर्भीता कथमेतद्भविष्यति ॥१९॥
transliteration: tato bhītā saricchreṣṭhā cintayāmāsa bhārata ,ubhayoḥ śāpayorbhītā kathametadbhaviṣyati.
english: O Bhārata, then the best of rivers, terrified, pondered, "How will this come to pass, fearful as I am of both curses?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 19. O Bhārata, then the best of rivers, terrified, pondered, "How will this come to pass, fearful as I am of both curses?"

sanskrit: साभिगम्य वसिष्ठं तु इममर्थमचोदयत् ।यदुक्ता सरितां श्रेष्ठा विश्वामित्रेण धीमता ॥२०॥
transliteration: sābhigamya vasiṣṭhaṁ tu imamarthamacodayat ,yaduktā saritāṁ śreṣṭhā viśvāmitreṇa dhīmatā.
english: And she, the best of rivers, having approached Vasiṣṭha, implored him regarding this matter, that which had been spoken by the intelligent Viśvāmitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 20. And she, the best of rivers, having approached Vasiṣṭha, implored him regarding this matter, that which had been spoken by the intelligent Viśvāmitra.

sanskrit: उभयोः शापयोर्भीता वेपमाना पुनः पुनः ।चिन्तयित्वा महाशापमृषिवित्रासिता भृशम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: ubhayoḥ śāpayorbhītā vepamānā punaḥ punaḥ ,cintayitvā mahāśāpamṛṣivitrāsitā bhṛśam.
english: Terrified by both curses and repeatedly trembling, she, having contemplated the severe curse, was extremely frightened by the sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 21. Terrified by both curses and repeatedly trembling, she, having contemplated the severe curse, was extremely frightened by the sage.

sanskrit: तां कृशां च विवर्णां च दृष्ट्वा चिन्तासमन्विताम् ।उवाच राजन्धर्मात्मा वसिष्ठो द्विपदां वरः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tāṁ kṛśāṁ ca vivarṇāṁ ca dṛṣṭvā cintāsamanvitām ,uvāca rājandharmātmā vasiṣṭho dvipadāṁ varaḥ.
english: O king, Vasiṣṭha, who possessed a righteous character (dharma) and was the best among humans, seeing her emaciated, pale, and filled with worry, spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 22. O king, Vasiṣṭha, who possessed a righteous character (dharma) and was the best among humans, seeing her emaciated, pale, and filled with worry, spoke.

sanskrit: त्राह्यात्मानं सरिच्छ्रेष्ठे वह मां शीघ्रगामिनी ।विश्वामित्रः शपेद्धि त्वां मा कृथास्त्वं विचारणाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: trāhyātmānaṁ saricchreṣṭhe vaha māṁ śīghragāminī ,viśvāmitraḥ śapeddhi tvāṁ mā kṛthāstvaṁ vicāraṇām.
english: O best of rivers, O swiftly flowing one, protect yourself (ātman) and carry me! For Viśvāmitra will certainly curse you. Do not hesitate or deliberate.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 23. O best of rivers, O swiftly flowing one, protect yourself (ātman) and carry me! For Viśvāmitra will certainly curse you. Do not hesitate or deliberate.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा कृपाशीलस्य सा सरित् ।चिन्तयामास कौरव्य किं कृतं सुकृतं भवेत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā kṛpāśīlasya sā sarit ,cintayāmāsa kauravya kiṁ kṛtaṁ sukṛtaṁ bhavet.
english: O Kauravya, hearing those words of the compassionate Vasiṣṭha, that river pondered, "What merit (sukṛta) might be achieved?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 24. O Kauravya, hearing those words of the compassionate Vasiṣṭha, that river pondered, "What merit (sukṛta) might be achieved?"

sanskrit: तस्याश्चिन्ता समुत्पन्ना वसिष्ठो मय्यतीव हि ।कृतवान्हि दयां नित्यं तस्य कार्यं हितं मया ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasyāścintā samutpannā vasiṣṭho mayyatīva hi ,kṛtavānhi dayāṁ nityaṁ tasya kāryaṁ hitaṁ mayā.
english: A thought arose in her: "Vasiṣṭha has indeed always shown me great kindness. Therefore, that which is beneficial for him must be accomplished by me."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 25. A thought arose in her: "Vasiṣṭha has indeed always shown me great kindness. Therefore, that which is beneficial for him must be accomplished by me."

sanskrit: अथ कूले स्वके राजञ्जपन्तमृषिसत्तमम् ।जुह्वानं कौशिकं प्रेक्ष्य सरस्वत्यभ्यचिन्तयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: atha kūle svake rājañjapantamṛṣisattamam ,juhvānaṁ kauśikaṁ prekṣya sarasvatyabhyacintayat.
english: Now, O king, Sarasvatī saw Viśvāmitra, the foremost among sages, on her own bank, performing japa and offering oblations. Having seen him, she began to ponder.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 26. Now, O king, Sarasvatī saw Viśvāmitra, the foremost among sages, on her own bank, performing japa and offering oblations. Having seen him, she began to ponder.

sanskrit: इदमन्तरमित्येव ततः सा सरितां वरा ।कूलापहारमकरोत्स्वेन वेगेन सा सरित् ॥२७॥
transliteration: idamantaramityeva tataḥ sā saritāṁ varā ,kūlāpahāramakarotsvena vegena sā sarit.
english: "This is the opportune moment," thinking this, that best of rivers (Sarasvatī) then, with her own powerful current, caused the bank to be swept away.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 27. "This is the opportune moment," thinking this, that best of rivers (Sarasvatī) then, with her own powerful current, caused the bank to be swept away.

sanskrit: तेन कूलापहारेण मैत्रावरुणिरौह्यत ।उह्यमानश्च तुष्टाव तदा राजन्सरस्वतीम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tena kūlāpahāreṇa maitrāvaruṇirauhyata ,uhyamānaśca tuṣṭāva tadā rājansarasvatīm.
english: By that sweeping away of the bank, Maitrāvaruṇi (Vasiṣṭha) was carried off. And as he was being carried away, O king, he then praised Sarasvatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 28. By that sweeping away of the bank, Maitrāvaruṇi (Vasiṣṭha) was carried off. And as he was being carried away, O king, he then praised Sarasvatī.

sanskrit: पितामहस्य सरसः प्रवृत्तासि सरस्वति ।व्याप्तं चेदं जगत्सर्वं तवैवाम्भोभिरुत्तमैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: pitāmahasya sarasaḥ pravṛttāsi sarasvati ,vyāptaṁ cedaṁ jagatsarvaṁ tavaivāmbhobhiruttamaiḥ.
english: "O Sarasvatī, you have emerged from the lake of the Grandfather (Brahmā). Indeed, this entire universe is pervaded solely by your excellent waters."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 29. "O Sarasvatī, you have emerged from the lake of the Grandfather (Brahmā). Indeed, this entire universe is pervaded solely by your excellent waters."

sanskrit: त्वमेवाकाशगा देवि मेघेषूत्सृजसे पयः ।सर्वाश्चापस्त्वमेवेति त्वत्तो वयमधीमहे ॥३०॥
transliteration: tvamevākāśagā devi megheṣūtsṛjase payaḥ ,sarvāścāpastvameveti tvatto vayamadhīmahe.
english: O Goddess, you alone move through the sky and release water into the clouds. Indeed, all waters are you; thus, we comprehend this from you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 30. O Goddess, you alone move through the sky and release water into the clouds. Indeed, all waters are you; thus, we comprehend this from you.

sanskrit: पुष्टिर्द्युतिस्तथा कीर्तिः सिद्धिर्वृद्धिरुमा तथा ।त्वमेव वाणी स्वाहा त्वं त्वय्यायत्तमिदं जगत् ।त्वमेव सर्वभूतेषु वससीह चतुर्विधा ॥३१॥
transliteration: puṣṭirdyutistathā kīrtiḥ siddhirvṛddhirumā tathā ,tvameva vāṇī svāhā tvaṁ tvayyāyattamidaṁ jagat ,tvameva sarvabhūteṣu vasasīha caturvidhā.
english: You are nourishment, radiance, and fame; success, prosperity, and Umā (the Divine Mother). You are indeed the goddess of speech (Vāṇī) and you are Svāhā. This entire world depends upon you. You alone dwell here in all beings (bhūteṣu) in four forms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 31. You are nourishment, radiance, and fame; success, prosperity, and Umā (the Divine Mother). You are indeed the goddess of speech (Vāṇī) and you are Svāhā. This entire world depends upon you. You alone dwell here in all beings (bhūteṣu) in four forms.

sanskrit: एवं सरस्वती राजन्स्तूयमाना महर्षिणा ।वेगेनोवाह तं विप्रं विश्वामित्राश्रमं प्रति ।न्यवेदयत चाभीक्ष्णं विश्वामित्राय तं मुनिम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: evaṁ sarasvatī rājanstūyamānā maharṣiṇā ,vegenovāha taṁ vipraṁ viśvāmitrāśramaṁ prati ,nyavedayata cābhīkṣṇaṁ viśvāmitrāya taṁ munim.
english: O King, thus, Sarasvati, being praised by the great sage, swiftly carried that Brahmin towards Viśvāmitra's hermitage (āśrama). And she repeatedly presented that sage to Viśvāmitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 32. O King, thus, Sarasvati, being praised by the great sage, swiftly carried that Brahmin towards Viśvāmitra's hermitage (āśrama). And she repeatedly presented that sage to Viśvāmitra.

sanskrit: तमानीतं सरस्वत्या दृष्ट्वा कोपसमन्वितः ।अथान्वेषत्प्रहरणं वसिष्ठान्तकरं तदा ॥३३॥
transliteration: tamānītaṁ sarasvatyā dṛṣṭvā kopasamanvitaḥ ,athānveṣatpraharaṇaṁ vasiṣṭhāntakaraṁ tadā.
english: Upon seeing that sage, who had been brought by Sarasvati, and filled with anger, he then searched for a weapon that would bring about the end of Vasiṣṭha at that moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 33. Upon seeing that sage, who had been brought by Sarasvati, and filled with anger, he then searched for a weapon that would bring about the end of Vasiṣṭha at that moment.

sanskrit: तं तु क्रुद्धमभिप्रेक्ष्य ब्रह्महत्याभयान्नदी ।अपोवाह वसिष्ठं तु प्राचीं दिशमतन्द्रिता ।उभयोः कुर्वती वाक्यं वञ्चयित्वा तु गाधिजम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: taṁ tu kruddhamabhiprekṣya brahmahatyābhayānnadī ,apovāha vasiṣṭhaṁ tu prācīṁ diśamatandritā ,ubhayoḥ kurvatī vākyaṁ vañcayitvā tu gādhijam.
english: Perceiving him (Viśvāmitra) to be enraged, and fearing the sin of brahminicide (brahmahatyā), the river, being diligent, carried Vasiṣṭha away towards the eastern direction. Thus, by deceiving Gādhi's son (Viśvāmitra), it was acting in accordance with the wishes of both (Vasiṣṭha and Viśvāmitra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 34. Perceiving him (Viśvāmitra) to be enraged, and fearing the sin of brahminicide (brahmahatyā), the river, being diligent, carried Vasiṣṭha away towards the eastern direction. Thus, by deceiving Gādhi's son (Viśvāmitra), it was acting in accordance with the wishes of both (Vasiṣṭha and Viśvāmitra).

sanskrit: ततोऽपवाहितं दृष्ट्वा वसिष्ठमृषिसत्तमम् ।अब्रवीदथ संक्रुद्धो विश्वामित्रो ह्यमर्षणः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tato'pavāhitaṁ dṛṣṭvā vasiṣṭhamṛṣisattamam ,abravīdatha saṁkruddho viśvāmitro hyamarṣaṇaḥ.
english: Then, having seen Vasiṣṭha, the best of sages, carried away, Viśvāmitra, greatly enraged and intolerant, spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 35. Then, having seen Vasiṣṭha, the best of sages, carried away, Viśvāmitra, greatly enraged and intolerant, spoke.

sanskrit: यस्मान्मा त्वं सरिच्छ्रेष्ठे वञ्चयित्वा पुनर्गता ।शोणितं वह कल्याणि रक्षोग्रामणिसंमतम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: yasmānmā tvaṁ saricchreṣṭhe vañcayitvā punargatā ,śoṇitaṁ vaha kalyāṇi rakṣogrāmaṇisaṁmatam.
english: "Because you, O best of rivers, have deceived me and gone away, O auspicious one, carry blood, (blood that is) acceptable to the chiefs of the Rākṣasas!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 36. "Because you, O best of rivers, have deceived me and gone away, O auspicious one, carry blood, (blood that is) acceptable to the chiefs of the Rākṣasas!"

sanskrit: ततः सरस्वती शप्ता विश्वामित्रेण धीमता ।अवहच्छोणितोन्मिश्रं तोयं संवत्सरं तदा ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarasvatī śaptā viśvāmitreṇa dhīmatā ,avahacchoṇitonmiśraṁ toyaṁ saṁvatsaraṁ tadā.
english: Then Sarasvatī, cursed by the intelligent Viśvāmitra, carried water mixed with blood for a year at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 37. Then Sarasvatī, cursed by the intelligent Viśvāmitra, carried water mixed with blood for a year at that time.

sanskrit: अथर्षयश्च देवाश्च गन्धर्वाप्सरसस्तथा ।सरस्वतीं तथा दृष्ट्वा बभूवुर्भृशदुःखिताः ॥३८॥
transliteration: atharṣayaśca devāśca gandharvāpsarasastathā ,sarasvatīṁ tathā dṛṣṭvā babhūvurbhṛśaduḥkhitāḥ.
english: Then, seeing Sarasvati in that condition, the sages, gods, Gandharvas, and Apsaras became greatly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 38. Then, seeing Sarasvati in that condition, the sages, gods, Gandharvas, and Apsaras became greatly distressed.

sanskrit: एवं वसिष्ठापवाहो लोके ख्यातो जनाधिप ।आगच्छच्च पुनर्मार्गं स्वमेव सरितां वरा ॥३९॥
transliteration: evaṁ vasiṣṭhāpavāho loke khyāto janādhipa ,āgacchacca punarmārgaṁ svameva saritāṁ varā.
english: O king, this diversion (apavāha) caused by Vasiṣṭha became well-known in the world. And then, the best of rivers, Sarasvati, returned to her own course.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 41 Verse 39. O king, this diversion (apavāha) caused by Vasiṣṭha became well-known in the world. And then, the best of rivers, Sarasvati, returned to her own course.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सा शप्ता तेन क्रुद्धेन विश्वामित्रेण धीमता ।तस्मिंस्तीर्थवरे शुभ्रे शोणितं समुपावहत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sā śaptā tena kruddhena viśvāmitreṇa dhīmatā ,tasmiṁstīrthavare śubhre śoṇitaṁ samupāvahat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Cursed by that enraged and wise Viśvāmitra, she (Sarasvati) began to carry forth blood in that excellent and pure sacred bathing place.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Cursed by that enraged and wise Viśvāmitra, she (Sarasvati) began to carry forth blood in that excellent and pure sacred bathing place.

sanskrit: अथाजग्मुस्ततो राजन्राक्षसास्तत्र भारत ।तत्र ते शोणितं सर्वे पिबन्तः सुखमासते ॥२॥
transliteration: athājagmustato rājanrākṣasāstatra bhārata ,tatra te śoṇitaṁ sarve pibantaḥ sukhamāsate.
english: Then, O King, O Bhārata, Rākṣasas arrived there. All of them dwelled there happily, drinking the blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 2. Then, O King, O Bhārata, Rākṣasas arrived there. All of them dwelled there happily, drinking the blood.

sanskrit: तृप्ताश्च सुभृशं तेन सुखिता विगतज्वराः ।नृत्यन्तश्च हसन्तश्च यथा स्वर्गजितस्तथा ॥३॥
transliteration: tṛptāśca subhṛśaṁ tena sukhitā vigatajvarāḥ ,nṛtyantaśca hasantaśca yathā svargajitastathā.
english: And by that, they were greatly satisfied, happy, and free from distress. They were dancing and laughing, just like those who have conquered heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 3. And by that, they were greatly satisfied, happy, and free from distress. They were dancing and laughing, just like those who have conquered heaven.

sanskrit: कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य ऋषयः सतपोधनाः ।तीर्थयात्रां समाजग्मुः सरस्वत्यां महीपते ॥४॥
transliteration: kasyacittvatha kālasya ṛṣayaḥ satapodhanāḥ ,tīrthayātrāṁ samājagmuḥ sarasvatyāṁ mahīpate.
english: Now, after some time, O king, the sages who possessed great ascetic wealth (tapas) undertook a pilgrimage to the Sarasvatī river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 4. Now, after some time, O king, the sages who possessed great ascetic wealth (tapas) undertook a pilgrimage to the Sarasvatī river.

sanskrit: तेषु सर्वेषु तीर्थेषु आप्लुत्य मुनिपुंगवाः ।प्राप्य प्रीतिं परां चापि तपोलुब्धा विशारदाः ।प्रययुर्हि ततो राजन्येन तीर्थं हि तत्तथा ॥५॥
transliteration: teṣu sarveṣu tīrtheṣu āplutya munipuṁgavāḥ ,prāpya prītiṁ parāṁ cāpi tapolubdhā viśāradāḥ ,prayayurhi tato rājanyena tīrthaṁ hi tattathā.
english: Having bathed in all those holy places, the excellent sages (munipuṅgavāḥ), who were expert (viśāradāḥ) and eager for greater ascetic merit (tapas), attained supreme satisfaction (prīti). Indeed, O king, they then departed from there to that particular holy place that was in such a condition.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 5. Having bathed in all those holy places, the excellent sages (munipuṅgavāḥ), who were expert (viśāradāḥ) and eager for greater ascetic merit (tapas), attained supreme satisfaction (prīti). Indeed, O king, they then departed from there to that particular holy place that was in such a condition.

sanskrit: अथागम्य महाभागास्तत्तीर्थं दारुणं तदा ।दृष्ट्वा तोयं सरस्वत्याः शोणितेन परिप्लुतम् ।पीयमानं च रक्षोभिर्बहुभिर्नृपसत्तम ॥६॥
transliteration: athāgamya mahābhāgāstattīrthaṁ dāruṇaṁ tadā ,dṛṣṭvā toyaṁ sarasvatyāḥ śoṇitena pariplutam ,pīyamānaṁ ca rakṣobhirbahubhirnṛpasattama.
english: Then, O best of kings (nṛpasattama), having arrived at that dreadful holy place, the great-souled (mahābhāgāḥ) sages saw the water of the Sarasvatī river drenched with blood and being drunk by many Rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 6. Then, O best of kings (nṛpasattama), having arrived at that dreadful holy place, the great-souled (mahābhāgāḥ) sages saw the water of the Sarasvatī river drenched with blood and being drunk by many Rākṣasas.

sanskrit: तान्दृष्ट्वा राक्षसान्राजन्मुनयः संशितव्रताः ।परित्राणे सरस्वत्याः परं यत्नं प्रचक्रिरे ॥७॥
transliteration: tāndṛṣṭvā rākṣasānrājanmunayaḥ saṁśitavratāḥ ,paritrāṇe sarasvatyāḥ paraṁ yatnaṁ pracakrire.
english: O King, seeing those demons, the sages, who were steadfast in their vows, made a great effort to protect Sarasvati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 7. O King, seeing those demons, the sages, who were steadfast in their vows, made a great effort to protect Sarasvati.

sanskrit: ते तु सर्वे महाभागाः समागम्य महाव्रताः ।आहूय सरितां श्रेष्ठामिदं वचनमब्रुवन् ॥८॥
transliteration: te tu sarve mahābhāgāḥ samāgamya mahāvratāḥ ,āhūya saritāṁ śreṣṭhāmidaṁ vacanamabruvan.
english: All those glorious sages, who were bound by great vows, gathered together, and having summoned the best of rivers, they spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 8. All those glorious sages, who were bound by great vows, gathered together, and having summoned the best of rivers, they spoke these words.

sanskrit: कारणं ब्रूहि कल्याणि किमर्थं ते ह्रदो ह्ययम् ।एवमाकुलतां यातः श्रुत्वा पास्यामहे वयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: kāraṇaṁ brūhi kalyāṇi kimarthaṁ te hrado hyayam ,evamākulatāṁ yātaḥ śrutvā pāsyāmahe vayam.
english: O auspicious one, tell us the reason: why has this lake of yours become so agitated? Having heard [your explanation], we shall drink.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 9. O auspicious one, tell us the reason: why has this lake of yours become so agitated? Having heard [your explanation], we shall drink.

sanskrit: ततः सा सर्वमाचष्ट यथावृत्तं प्रवेपती ।दुःखितामथ तां दृष्ट्वा त ऊचुर्वै तपोधनाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sā sarvamācaṣṭa yathāvṛttaṁ pravepatī ,duḥkhitāmatha tāṁ dṛṣṭvā ta ūcurvai tapodhanāḥ.
english: Then, trembling, she recounted everything exactly as it had happened. Seeing her distressed, those ascetics (tapodhanāḥ) then spoke indeed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 10. Then, trembling, she recounted everything exactly as it had happened. Seeing her distressed, those ascetics (tapodhanāḥ) then spoke indeed.

sanskrit: कारणं श्रुतमस्माभिः शापश्चैव श्रुतोऽनघे ।करिष्यन्ति तु यत्प्राप्तं सर्व एव तपोधनाः ॥११॥
transliteration: kāraṇaṁ śrutamasmābhiḥ śāpaścaiva śruto'naghe ,kariṣyanti tu yatprāptaṁ sarva eva tapodhanāḥ.
english: O sinless one, we have heard both the reason and the curse (śāpa). But all the ascetics (tapodhana) will certainly do what is proper.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 11. O sinless one, we have heard both the reason and the curse (śāpa). But all the ascetics (tapodhana) will certainly do what is proper.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा सरिच्छ्रेष्ठामूचुस्तेऽथ परस्परम् ।विमोचयामहे सर्वे शापादेतां सरस्वतीम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: evamuktvā saricchreṣṭhāmūcuste'tha parasparam ,vimocayāmahe sarve śāpādetāṁ sarasvatīm.
english: Having thus spoken to the foremost river, they then said to each other, 'All of us shall release this Sarasvati from the curse (śāpa).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 12. Having thus spoken to the foremost river, they then said to each other, 'All of us shall release this Sarasvati from the curse (śāpa).'

sanskrit: तेषां तु वचनादेव प्रकृतिस्था सरस्वती ।प्रसन्नसलिला जज्ञे यथा पूर्वं तथैव हि ।विमुक्ता च सरिच्छ्रेष्ठा विबभौ सा यथा पुरा ॥१३॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu vacanādeva prakṛtisthā sarasvatī ,prasannasalilā jajñe yathā pūrvaṁ tathaiva hi ,vimuktā ca saricchreṣṭhā vibabhau sā yathā purā.
english: Indeed, by their very words, Sarasvati became restored to her natural state (prakṛti), with clear water, just as she was before. And that foremost river (sarit śreṣṭhā), having been freed, shone brightly just as she did in the past.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 13. Indeed, by their very words, Sarasvati became restored to her natural state (prakṛti), with clear water, just as she was before. And that foremost river (sarit śreṣṭhā), having been freed, shone brightly just as she did in the past.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तोयं सरस्वत्या मुनिभिस्तैस्तथा कृतम् ।कृताञ्जलीस्ततो राजन्राक्षसाः क्षुधयार्दिताः ।ऊचुस्तान्वै मुनीन्सर्वान्कृपायुक्तान्पुनः पुनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā toyaṁ sarasvatyā munibhistaistathā kṛtam ,kṛtāñjalīstato rājanrākṣasāḥ kṣudhayārditāḥ ,ūcustānvai munīnsarvānkṛpāyuktānpunaḥ punaḥ.
english: O king, when the Rākṣasas, afflicted by hunger, saw that the water of Sarasvati had been purified by those sages, they then, with folded hands, repeatedly addressed all those compassionate sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 14. O king, when the Rākṣasas, afflicted by hunger, saw that the water of Sarasvati had been purified by those sages, they then, with folded hands, repeatedly addressed all those compassionate sages.

sanskrit: वयं हि क्षुधिताश्चैव धर्माद्धीनाश्च शाश्वतात् ।न च नः कामकारोऽयं यद्वयं पापकारिणः ॥१५॥
transliteration: vayaṁ hi kṣudhitāścaiva dharmāddhīnāśca śāśvatāt ,na ca naḥ kāmakāro'yaṁ yadvayaṁ pāpakāriṇaḥ.
english: Indeed, we are hungry and deprived of the eternal natural law (dharma). And it is not by our own desire that we are committing sinful acts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 15. Indeed, we are hungry and deprived of the eternal natural law (dharma). And it is not by our own desire that we are committing sinful acts.

sanskrit: युष्माकं चाप्रसादेन दुष्कृतेन च कर्मणा ।पक्षोऽयं वर्धतेऽस्माकं यतः स्म ब्रह्मराक्षसाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: yuṣmākaṁ cāprasādena duṣkṛtena ca karmaṇā ,pakṣo'yaṁ vardhate'smākaṁ yataḥ sma brahmarākṣasāḥ.
english: And it is due to your displeasure and our own misdeeds (karma) that our faction grows, for we have become brahmarākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 16. And it is due to your displeasure and our own misdeeds (karma) that our faction grows, for we have become brahmarākṣasas.

sanskrit: एवं हि वैश्यशूद्राणां क्षत्रियाणां तथैव च ।ये ब्राह्मणान्प्रद्विषन्ति ते भवन्तीह राक्षसाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: evaṁ hi vaiśyaśūdrāṇāṁ kṣatriyāṇāṁ tathaiva ca ,ye brāhmaṇānpradviṣanti te bhavantīha rākṣasāḥ.
english: Indeed, in the same way, those among the Vaiśyas, Śūdras, and Kṣatriyas who harbor hatred for Brahmins become rākṣasas in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 17. Indeed, in the same way, those among the Vaiśyas, Śūdras, and Kṣatriyas who harbor hatred for Brahmins become rākṣasas in this world.

sanskrit: आचार्यमृत्विजं चैव गुरुं वृद्धजनं तथा ।प्राणिनो येऽवमन्यन्ते ते भवन्तीह राक्षसाः ।योषितां चैव पापानां योनिदोषेण वर्धते ॥१८॥
transliteration: ācāryamṛtvijaṁ caiva guruṁ vṛddhajanaṁ tathā ,prāṇino ye'vamanyante te bhavantīha rākṣasāḥ ,yoṣitāṁ caiva pāpānāṁ yonidoṣeṇa vardhate.
english: Those living beings who disrespect their preceptor, the officiating priest, their spiritual teacher (guru), and elders – they certainly become rākṣasas in this world. And among sinful women, (this nature) thrives due to a defect in their lineage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 18. Those living beings who disrespect their preceptor, the officiating priest, their spiritual teacher (guru), and elders – they certainly become rākṣasas in this world. And among sinful women, (this nature) thrives due to a defect in their lineage.

sanskrit: तत्कुरुध्वमिहास्माकं कारुण्यं द्विजसत्तमाः ।शक्ता भवन्तः सर्वेषां लोकानामपि तारणे ॥१९॥
transliteration: tatkurudhvamihāsmākaṁ kāruṇyaṁ dvijasattamāḥ ,śaktā bhavantaḥ sarveṣāṁ lokānāmapi tāraṇe.
english: Therefore, O best among the twice-born, please show compassion to us here. You are capable of saving even all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 19. Therefore, O best among the twice-born, please show compassion to us here. You are capable of saving even all beings.

sanskrit: तेषां ते मुनयः श्रुत्वा तुष्टुवुस्तां महानदीम् ।मोक्षार्थं रक्षसां तेषामूचुः प्रयतमानसाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ te munayaḥ śrutvā tuṣṭuvustāṁ mahānadīm ,mokṣārthaṁ rakṣasāṁ teṣāmūcuḥ prayatamānasāḥ.
english: Hearing them, those sages praised that great river. With purified minds, they spoke for the liberation (mokṣa) of those rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 20. Hearing them, those sages praised that great river. With purified minds, they spoke for the liberation (mokṣa) of those rākṣasas.

sanskrit: क्षुतकीटावपन्नं च यच्चोच्छिष्टाशितं भवेत् ।केशावपन्नमाधूतमारुग्णमपि यद्भवेत् ।श्वभिः संस्पृष्टमन्नं च भागोऽसौ रक्षसामिह ॥२१॥
transliteration: kṣutakīṭāvapannaṁ ca yaccocchiṣṭāśitaṁ bhavet ,keśāvapannamādhūtamārugṇamapi yadbhavet ,śvabhiḥ saṁspṛṣṭamannaṁ ca bhāgo'sau rakṣasāmiha.
english: Whatever (food) is fallen into saliva or insects, and whatever has been eaten as leavings (uchchhiṣṭa); whatever has hair fallen into it, or has been shaken, or broken or crushed; and food touched by dogs - that (food), here, is the share of the rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 21. Whatever (food) is fallen into saliva or insects, and whatever has been eaten as leavings (uchchhiṣṭa); whatever has hair fallen into it, or has been shaken, or broken or crushed; and food touched by dogs - that (food), here, is the share of the rākṣasas.

sanskrit: तस्माज्ज्ञात्वा सदा विद्वानेतान्यन्नानि वर्जयेत् ।राक्षसान्नमसौ भुङ्क्ते यो भुङ्क्ते ह्यन्नमीदृशम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tasmājjñātvā sadā vidvānetānyannāni varjayet ,rākṣasānnamasau bhuṅkte yo bhuṅkte hyannamīdṛśam.
english: Therefore, a wise person, always understanding this, should avoid these foods. For whoever eats such food indeed eats the food of rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 22. Therefore, a wise person, always understanding this, should avoid these foods. For whoever eats such food indeed eats the food of rākṣasas.

sanskrit: शोधयित्वा ततस्तीर्थमृषयस्ते तपोधनाः ।मोक्षार्थं राक्षसानां च नदीं तां प्रत्यचोदयन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: śodhayitvā tatastīrthamṛṣayaste tapodhanāḥ ,mokṣārthaṁ rākṣasānāṁ ca nadīṁ tāṁ pratyacodayan.
english: Then, those sages, whose wealth was asceticism (tapas), after purifying that sacred bathing place, directed that river [to flow] for the liberation (mokṣa) of the Rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 23. Then, those sages, whose wealth was asceticism (tapas), after purifying that sacred bathing place, directed that river [to flow] for the liberation (mokṣa) of the Rākṣasas.

sanskrit: महर्षीणां मतं ज्ञात्वा ततः सा सरितां वरा ।अरुणामानयामास स्वां तनुं पुरुषर्षभ ॥२४॥
transliteration: maharṣīṇāṁ mataṁ jñātvā tataḥ sā saritāṁ varā ,aruṇāmānayāmāsa svāṁ tanuṁ puruṣarṣabha.
english: O best among men (puruṣarṣabha)! Then, understanding the intention of the great sages, that supreme river manifested her own form as Arunā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 24. O best among men (puruṣarṣabha)! Then, understanding the intention of the great sages, that supreme river manifested her own form as Arunā.

sanskrit: तस्यां ते राक्षसाः स्नात्वा तनूस्त्यक्त्वा दिवं गताः ।अरुणायां महाराज ब्रह्महत्यापहा हि सा ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ te rākṣasāḥ snātvā tanūstyaktvā divaṁ gatāḥ ,aruṇāyāṁ mahārāja brahmahatyāpahā hi sā.
english: O great king (mahārāja)! Having bathed in her (the river), those Rākṣasas abandoned their bodies and ascended to heaven; for she, Arunā, indeed removes the sin of brahminicide.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 25. O great king (mahārāja)! Having bathed in her (the river), those Rākṣasas abandoned their bodies and ascended to heaven; for she, Arunā, indeed removes the sin of brahminicide.

sanskrit: एतमर्थमभिज्ञाय देवराजः शतक्रतुः ।तस्मिंस्तीर्थवरे स्नात्वा विमुक्तः पाप्मना किल ॥२६॥
transliteration: etamarthamabhijñāya devarājaḥ śatakratuḥ ,tasmiṁstīrthavare snātvā vimuktaḥ pāpmanā kila.
english: Having understood this matter, Indra, the king of the gods and performer of a hundred Vedic rituals (śatakratu), indeed became free from sin (pāpman) after bathing in that excellent sacred bathing place.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 26. Having understood this matter, Indra, the king of the gods and performer of a hundred Vedic rituals (śatakratu), indeed became free from sin (pāpman) after bathing in that excellent sacred bathing place.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।किमर्थं भगवाञ्शक्रो ब्रह्महत्यामवाप्तवान् ।कथमस्मिंश्च तीर्थे वै आप्लुत्याकल्मषोऽभवत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kimarthaṁ bhagavāñśakro brahmahatyāmavāptavān ,kathamasmiṁśca tīrthe vai āplutyākalmaṣo'bhavat.
english: Janamejaya asked: "For what reason did the venerable Indra (Śakra) incur the sin of brahmin-killing (brahmahatyā)? And how, indeed, did he become sinless by bathing in this holy place (tīrtha)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 27. Janamejaya asked: "For what reason did the venerable Indra (Śakra) incur the sin of brahmin-killing (brahmahatyā)? And how, indeed, did he become sinless by bathing in this holy place (tīrtha)?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।शृणुष्वैतदुपाख्यानं यथावृत्तं जनेश्वर ।यथा बिभेद समयं नमुचेर्वासवः पुरा ॥२८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śṛṇuṣvaitadupākhyānaṁ yathāvṛttaṁ janeśvara ,yathā bibheda samayaṁ namucervāsavaḥ purā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O king (janeśvara), listen to this story (upākhyāna) exactly as it happened: how Indra (Vāsava) formerly broke his agreement (samaya) with Namuci."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 28. Vaiśampāyana said: "O king (janeśvara), listen to this story (upākhyāna) exactly as it happened: how Indra (Vāsava) formerly broke his agreement (samaya) with Namuci."

sanskrit: नमुचिर्वासवाद्भीतः सूर्यरश्मिं समाविशत् ।तेनेन्द्रः सख्यमकरोत्समयं चेदमब्रवीत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: namucirvāsavādbhītaḥ sūryaraśmiṁ samāviśat ,tenendraḥ sakhyamakarotsamayaṁ cedamabravīt.
english: Namuci, frightened by Indra (Vāsava), entered into a sunbeam for refuge. Thereupon, Indra made a pact of friendship with him and uttered this agreement (samaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 29. Namuci, frightened by Indra (Vāsava), entered into a sunbeam for refuge. Thereupon, Indra made a pact of friendship with him and uttered this agreement (samaya).

sanskrit: नार्द्रेण त्वा न शुष्केण न रात्रौ नापि वाहनि ।वधिष्याम्यसुरश्रेष्ठ सखे सत्येन ते शपे ॥३०॥
transliteration: nārdreṇa tvā na śuṣkeṇa na rātrau nāpi vāhani ,vadhiṣyāmyasuraśreṣṭha sakhe satyena te śape.
english: “O best of asuras (asuraśreṣṭha), O friend, I will not kill you with anything wet, nor with anything dry, neither at night nor during the day. I swear to you by my truth (satya)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 30. “O best of asuras (asuraśreṣṭha), O friend, I will not kill you with anything wet, nor with anything dry, neither at night nor during the day. I swear to you by my truth (satya)."

sanskrit: एवं स कृत्वा समयं सृष्ट्वा नीहारमीश्वरः ।चिच्छेदास्य शिरो राजन्नपां फेनेन वासवः ॥३१॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa kṛtvā samayaṁ sṛṣṭvā nīhāramīśvaraḥ ,cicchedāsya śiro rājannapāṁ phenena vāsavaḥ.
english: O king, having thus made a pact (samaya) and created a mist, Indra, the lord, severed his head with the foam of the waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 31. O king, having thus made a pact (samaya) and created a mist, Indra, the lord, severed his head with the foam of the waters.

sanskrit: तच्छिरो नमुचेश्छिन्नं पृष्ठतः शक्रमन्वयात् ।हे मित्रहन्पाप इति ब्रुवाणं शक्रमन्तिकात् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tacchiro namuceśchinnaṁ pṛṣṭhataḥ śakramanvayāt ,he mitrahanpāpa iti bruvāṇaṁ śakramantikāt.
english: That severed head of Namuci followed Indra from behind, addressing him from nearby with the words, 'O slayer of a friend! O sinful one!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 32. That severed head of Namuci followed Indra from behind, addressing him from nearby with the words, 'O slayer of a friend! O sinful one!'

sanskrit: एवं स शिरसा तेन चोद्यमानः पुनः पुनः ।पितामहाय संतप्त एवमर्थं न्यवेदयत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa śirasā tena codyamānaḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,pitāmahāya saṁtapta evamarthaṁ nyavedayat.
english: Thus, being repeatedly prompted by that head, he (Indra), distressed, reported the matter to his grandfather (pitāmaha, Brahmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 33. Thus, being repeatedly prompted by that head, he (Indra), distressed, reported the matter to his grandfather (pitāmaha, Brahmā).

sanskrit: तमब्रवील्लोकगुरुररुणायां यथाविधि ।इष्ट्वोपस्पृश देवेन्द्र ब्रह्महत्यापहा हि सा ॥३४॥
transliteration: tamabravīllokagururaruṇāyāṁ yathāvidhi ,iṣṭvopaspṛśa devendra brahmahatyāpahā hi sā.
english: The preceptor of the world (lokaguru), Brahmā, said to him, 'O lord of the gods (devendra), having sacrificed according to the prescribed rites in Aruṇā, perform ablutions, for that indeed removes the sin of killing a brāhmaṇa (brahmahatyā).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 34. The preceptor of the world (lokaguru), Brahmā, said to him, 'O lord of the gods (devendra), having sacrificed according to the prescribed rites in Aruṇā, perform ablutions, for that indeed removes the sin of killing a brāhmaṇa (brahmahatyā).'

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः स सरस्वत्याः कुञ्जे वै जनमेजय ।इष्ट्वा यथावद्बलभिदरुणायामुपास्पृशत् ॥३५॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ sa sarasvatyāḥ kuñje vai janamejaya ,iṣṭvā yathāvadbalabhidaruṇāyāmupāspṛśat.
english: O Janamejaya, thus addressed, he, Indra (balabhid), indeed bathed in the Aruna [river/place] in the grove of Sarasvati, having performed worship according to the prescribed rites.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 35. O Janamejaya, thus addressed, he, Indra (balabhid), indeed bathed in the Aruna [river/place] in the grove of Sarasvati, having performed worship according to the prescribed rites.

sanskrit: स मुक्तः पाप्मना तेन ब्रह्महत्याकृतेन ह ।जगाम संहृष्टमनास्त्रिदिवं त्रिदशेश्वरः ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa muktaḥ pāpmanā tena brahmahatyākṛtena ha ,jagāma saṁhṛṣṭamanāstridivaṁ tridaśeśvaraḥ.
english: Freed from that sin (pāpman) born of the killing of a brahmin (brahmahatyā), the lord of the gods (tridaśeśvara), with a gladdened mind, ascended to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 36. Freed from that sin (pāpman) born of the killing of a brahmin (brahmahatyā), the lord of the gods (tridaśeśvara), with a gladdened mind, ascended to heaven.

sanskrit: शिरस्तच्चापि नमुचेस्तत्रैवाप्लुत्य भारत ।लोकान्कामदुघान्प्राप्तमक्षयान्राजसत्तम ॥३७॥
transliteration: śirastaccāpi namucestatraivāplutya bhārata ,lokānkāmadughānprāptamakṣayānrājasattama.
english: O Bharata, O best of kings (rājasattama), by bathing in that very place, even that head of Namuci attained inexhaustible, wish-fulfilling worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 37. O Bharata, O best of kings (rājasattama), by bathing in that very place, even that head of Namuci attained inexhaustible, wish-fulfilling worlds.

sanskrit: तत्राप्युपस्पृश्य बलो महात्मा दत्त्वा च दानानि पृथग्विधानि ।अवाप्य धर्मं परमार्यकर्मा जगाम सोमस्य महत्स तीर्थम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatrāpyupaspṛśya balo mahātmā; dattvā ca dānāni pṛthagvidhāni ,avāpya dharmaṁ paramāryakarmā; jagāma somasya mahatsa tīrtham.
english: And there also, the great-souled Bala, having bathed and given various kinds of donations (dāna), attained supreme merit (dharma) through noble actions, and then went to that great pilgrimage site (tīrtha) of Soma.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 38. And there also, the great-souled Bala, having bathed and given various kinds of donations (dāna), attained supreme merit (dharma) through noble actions, and then went to that great pilgrimage site (tīrtha) of Soma.

sanskrit: यत्रायजद्राजसूयेन सोमः साक्षात्पुरा विधिवत्पार्थिवेन्द्र ।अत्रिर्धीमान्विप्रमुख्यो बभूव होता यस्मिन्क्रतुमुख्ये महात्मा ॥३९॥
transliteration: yatrāyajadrājasūyena somaḥ; sākṣātpurā vidhivatpārthivendra ,atrirdhīmānvipramukhyo babhūva; hotā yasminkratumukhye mahātmā.
english: Oh king (pārthivendra)! In that place, Soma himself formerly performed the rājasūya (yajña) according to the prescribed rites. In that principal ritual (kratumukhya), the wise, great-souled (mahātman) Atri became the chief priest (hotā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 39. Oh king (pārthivendra)! In that place, Soma himself formerly performed the rājasūya (yajña) according to the prescribed rites. In that principal ritual (kratumukhya), the wise, great-souled (mahātman) Atri became the chief priest (hotā).

sanskrit: यस्यान्तेऽभूत्सुमहान्दानवानां दैतेयानां राक्षसानां च देवैः ।स संग्रामस्तारकाख्यः सुतीव्रो यत्र स्कन्दस्तारकाख्यं जघान ॥४०॥
transliteration: yasyānte'bhūtsumahāndānavānāṁ; daiteyānāṁ rākṣasānāṁ ca devaiḥ ,sa saṁgrāmastārakākhyaḥ sutīvro; yatra skandastārakākhyaṁ jaghāna.
english: At the conclusion of which, a very great battle ensued between the Dānavas, Daityas, and Rākṣasas, along with the gods. That exceedingly fierce battle, known as Tāraka, was where Skanda slew (the demon) Tāraka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 40. At the conclusion of which, a very great battle ensued between the Dānavas, Daityas, and Rākṣasas, along with the gods. That exceedingly fierce battle, known as Tāraka, was where Skanda slew (the demon) Tāraka.

sanskrit: सेनापत्यं लब्धवान्देवतानां महासेनो यत्र दैत्यान्तकर्ता ।साक्षाच्चात्र न्यवसत्कार्त्तिकेयः सदा कुमारो यत्र स प्लक्षराजः ॥४१॥
transliteration: senāpatyaṁ labdhavāndevatānāṁ; mahāseno yatra daityāntakartā ,sākṣāccātra nyavasatkārttikeyaḥ; sadā kumāro yatra sa plakṣarājaḥ.
english: There, Mahāsena, the destroyer of Daityas, obtained the position of general of the gods. And in that very place, Kumāra Kārttikeya himself always resided. That is the lord of the Plakṣa trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 42 Verse 41. There, Mahāsena, the destroyer of Daityas, obtained the position of general of the gods. And in that very place, Kumāra Kārttikeya himself always resided. That is the lord of the Plakṣa trees.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।सरस्वत्याः प्रभावोऽयमुक्तस्ते द्विजसत्तम ।कुमारस्याभिषेकं तु ब्रह्मन्व्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,sarasvatyāḥ prabhāvo'yamuktaste dvijasattama ,kumārasyābhiṣekaṁ tu brahmanvyākhyātumarhasi.
english: Janamejaya said: "O best among the twice-born (dvijasattama)! This influence (prabhāva) of Sarasvatī has been recounted to you. But O knower of Brahman (brahman)! You ought to explain the consecration of Kumāra."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "O best among the twice-born (dvijasattama)! This influence (prabhāva) of Sarasvatī has been recounted to you. But O knower of Brahman (brahman)! You ought to explain the consecration of Kumāra."

sanskrit: यस्मिन्काले च देशे च यथा च वदतां वर ।यैश्चाभिषिक्तो भगवान्विधिना येन च प्रभुः ॥२॥
transliteration: yasminkāle ca deśe ca yathā ca vadatāṁ vara ,yaiścābhiṣikto bhagavānvidhinā yena ca prabhuḥ.
english: O best among speakers, please tell me at what time, in what place, and in what manner, by whom, and with what ritual the glorious lord (Bhagavān) was consecrated, he who is the master.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 2. O best among speakers, please tell me at what time, in what place, and in what manner, by whom, and with what ritual the glorious lord (Bhagavān) was consecrated, he who is the master.

sanskrit: स्कन्दो यथा च दैत्यानामकरोत्कदनं महत् ।तथा मे सर्वमाचक्ष्व परं कौतूहलं हि मे ॥३॥
transliteration: skando yathā ca daityānāmakarotkadanaṁ mahat ,tathā me sarvamācakṣva paraṁ kautūhalaṁ hi me.
english: And how Skanda brought about the great destruction of the Daityas - please recount all of that to me, for my curiosity is immense.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 3. And how Skanda brought about the great destruction of the Daityas - please recount all of that to me, for my curiosity is immense.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।कुरुवंशस्य सदृशमिदं कौतूहलं तव ।हर्षमुत्पादयत्येतद्वचो मे जनमेजय ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kuruvaṁśasya sadṛśamidaṁ kautūhalaṁ tava ,harṣamutpādayatyetadvaco me janamejaya.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O Janamejaya, this curiosity of yours, which is fitting for the Kuru dynasty, generates joy in me."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: "O Janamejaya, this curiosity of yours, which is fitting for the Kuru dynasty, generates joy in me."

sanskrit: हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि शृण्वानस्य जनाधिप ।अभिषेकं कुमारस्य प्रभावं च महात्मनः ॥५॥
transliteration: hanta te kathayiṣyāmi śṛṇvānasya janādhipa ,abhiṣekaṁ kumārasya prabhāvaṁ ca mahātmanaḥ.
english: Indeed, O king, I shall narrate to you, as you listen, the consecration of Kumāra and the immense power of that great-souled one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 5. Indeed, O king, I shall narrate to you, as you listen, the consecration of Kumāra and the immense power of that great-souled one.

sanskrit: तेजो माहेश्वरं स्कन्नमग्नौ प्रपतितं पुरा ।तत्सर्वभक्षो भगवान्नाशकद्दग्धुमक्षयम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tejo māheśvaraṁ skannamagnau prapatitaṁ purā ,tatsarvabhakṣo bhagavānnāśakaddagdhumakṣayam.
english: Formerly, the spilled brilliance of Maheśvara (Śiva), which had fallen into the fire, was imperishable; the revered all-devouring god [Agni] was unable to burn it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 6. Formerly, the spilled brilliance of Maheśvara (Śiva), which had fallen into the fire, was imperishable; the revered all-devouring god [Agni] was unable to burn it.

sanskrit: तेनासीदति तेजस्वी दीप्तिमान्हव्यवाहनः ।न चैव धारयामास गर्भं तेजोमयं तदा ॥७॥
transliteration: tenāsīdati tejasvī dīptimānhavyavāhanaḥ ,na caiva dhārayāmāsa garbhaṁ tejomayaṁ tadā.
english: By that [brilliance], the carrier of oblations (Agni) became exceedingly brilliant and radiant. Yet, he was certainly unable to bear that fiery seed (garbha) at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 7. By that [brilliance], the carrier of oblations (Agni) became exceedingly brilliant and radiant. Yet, he was certainly unable to bear that fiery seed (garbha) at that time.

sanskrit: स गङ्गामभिसंगम्य नियोगाद्ब्रह्मणः प्रभुः ।गर्भमाहितवान्दिव्यं भास्करोपमतेजसम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sa gaṅgāmabhisaṁgamya niyogādbrahmaṇaḥ prabhuḥ ,garbhamāhitavāndivyaṁ bhāskaropamatejasam.
english: Approaching Gaṅgā by the command of Brahmā, that lord [Agni] deposited the divine seed (garbha) whose brilliance was like that of the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 8. Approaching Gaṅgā by the command of Brahmā, that lord [Agni] deposited the divine seed (garbha) whose brilliance was like that of the sun.

sanskrit: अथ गङ्गापि तं गर्भमसहन्ती विधारणे ।उत्ससर्ज गिरौ रम्ये हिमवत्यमरार्चिते ॥९॥
transliteration: atha gaṅgāpi taṁ garbhamasahantī vidhāraṇe ,utsasarja girau ramye himavatyamarārcite.
english: Then, Gaṅgā, also unable to endure holding that seed (garbha), discharged it on the beautiful mountain Himavat, which is worshipped by the immortals.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 9. Then, Gaṅgā, also unable to endure holding that seed (garbha), discharged it on the beautiful mountain Himavat, which is worshipped by the immortals.

sanskrit: स तत्र ववृधे लोकानावृत्य ज्वलनात्मजः ।ददृशुर्ज्वलनाकारं तं गर्भमथ कृत्तिकाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa tatra vavṛdhe lokānāvṛtya jvalanātmajaḥ ,dadṛśurjvalanākāraṁ taṁ garbhamatha kṛttikāḥ.
english: The son of Agni (Jvalanātmaja) grew there, enveloping the worlds. Then the Krittikas saw that child, who possessed a form like blazing fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 10. The son of Agni (Jvalanātmaja) grew there, enveloping the worlds. Then the Krittikas saw that child, who possessed a form like blazing fire.

sanskrit: शरस्तम्बे महात्मानमनलात्मजमीश्वरम् ।ममायमिति ताः सर्वाः पुत्रार्थिन्योऽभिचक्रमुः ॥११॥
transliteration: śarastambe mahātmānamanalātmajamīśvaram ,mamāyamiti tāḥ sarvāḥ putrārthinyo'bhicakramuḥ.
english: In a clump of reeds, all of them, desiring a son, approached the great-souled (mahātman), the son of fire (analātmaja), the lord (īśvara), thinking, "This one is mine."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 11. In a clump of reeds, all of them, desiring a son, approached the great-souled (mahātman), the son of fire (analātmaja), the lord (īśvara), thinking, "This one is mine."

sanskrit: तासां विदित्वा भावं तं मातॄणां भगवान्प्रभुः ।प्रस्नुतानां पयः षड्भिर्वदनैरपिबत्तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tāsāṁ viditvā bhāvaṁ taṁ mātṝṇāṁ bhagavānprabhuḥ ,prasnutānāṁ payaḥ ṣaḍbhirvadanairapibattadā.
english: Having understood that intention of those mothers, the blessed (bhagavān) lord (prabhu) then drank their flowing milk with his six faces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 12. Having understood that intention of those mothers, the blessed (bhagavān) lord (prabhu) then drank their flowing milk with his six faces.

sanskrit: तं प्रभावं समालक्ष्य तस्य बालस्य कृत्तिकाः ।परं विस्मयमापन्ना देव्यो दिव्यवपुर्धराः ॥१३॥
transliteration: taṁ prabhāvaṁ samālakṣya tasya bālasya kṛttikāḥ ,paraṁ vismayamāpannā devyo divyavapurdharāḥ.
english: Having carefully observed that power (prabhāva) of that child, the Krittikas, who were goddesses (devyo) possessing divine bodies, became filled with extreme astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 13. Having carefully observed that power (prabhāva) of that child, the Krittikas, who were goddesses (devyo) possessing divine bodies, became filled with extreme astonishment.

sanskrit: यत्रोत्सृष्टः स भगवान्गङ्गया गिरिमूर्धनि ।स शैलः काञ्चनः सर्वः संबभौ कुरुसत्तम ॥१४॥
transliteration: yatrotsṛṣṭaḥ sa bhagavāngaṅgayā girimūrdhani ,sa śailaḥ kāñcanaḥ sarvaḥ saṁbabhau kurusattama.
english: Where that divine being was deposited by Gaṅgā on the mountain peak, that entire mountain became golden and shone brightly, O best of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 14. Where that divine being was deposited by Gaṅgā on the mountain peak, that entire mountain became golden and shone brightly, O best of the Kurus.

sanskrit: वर्धता चैव गर्भेण पृथिवी तेन रञ्जिता ।अतश्च सर्वे संवृत्ता गिरयः काञ्चनाकराः ॥१५॥
transliteration: vardhatā caiva garbheṇa pṛthivī tena rañjitā ,ataśca sarve saṁvṛttā girayaḥ kāñcanākarāḥ.
english: And indeed, by that growing embryo, the earth itself was imbued with color. Thus, all mountains became mines of gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 15. And indeed, by that growing embryo, the earth itself was imbued with color. Thus, all mountains became mines of gold.

sanskrit: कुमारश्च महावीर्यः कार्त्तिकेय इति स्मृतः ।गाङ्गेयः पूर्वमभवन्महायोगबलान्वितः ॥१६॥
transliteration: kumāraśca mahāvīryaḥ kārttikeya iti smṛtaḥ ,gāṅgeyaḥ pūrvamabhavanmahāyogabalānvitaḥ.
english: And Kumāra, the greatly valorous one, is known as Kārttikeya. Formerly, he, the son of Gaṅgā, came into being, endowed with great power from his spiritual discipline (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 16. And Kumāra, the greatly valorous one, is known as Kārttikeya. Formerly, he, the son of Gaṅgā, came into being, endowed with great power from his spiritual discipline (yoga).

sanskrit: स देवस्तपसा चैव वीर्येण च समन्वितः ।ववृधेऽतीव राजेन्द्र चन्द्रवत्प्रियदर्शनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa devastapasā caiva vīryeṇa ca samanvitaḥ ,vavṛdhe'tīva rājendra candravatpriyadarśanaḥ.
english: That god, endowed with both asceticism (tapas) and valor, grew exceedingly, O king of kings, with an appearance as pleasing as the moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 17. That god, endowed with both asceticism (tapas) and valor, grew exceedingly, O king of kings, with an appearance as pleasing as the moon.

sanskrit: धनुर्वेदश्चतुष्पादः शस्त्रग्रामः ससंग्रहः ।तत्रैनं समुपातिष्ठत्साक्षाद्वाणी च केवला ॥२२॥
transliteration: dhanurvedaścatuṣpādaḥ śastragrāmaḥ sasaṁgrahaḥ ,tatrainaṁ samupātiṣṭhatsākṣādvāṇī ca kevalā.
english: The complete knowledge of archery (dhanurveda), with its four divisions, and the entire collection of weapons along with their methods, directly approached him. Speech (Vāṇī) herself, in her pure form, also appeared there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 22. The complete knowledge of archery (dhanurveda), with its four divisions, and the entire collection of weapons along with their methods, directly approached him. Speech (Vāṇī) herself, in her pure form, also appeared there.

sanskrit: स ददर्श महावीर्यं देवदेवमुमापतिम् ।शैलपुत्र्या सहासीनं भूतसंघशतैर्वृतम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: sa dadarśa mahāvīryaṁ devadevamumāpatim ,śailaputryā sahāsīnaṁ bhūtasaṁghaśatairvṛtam.
english: He saw the extremely powerful Lord of Umā (Umāpati), the God of gods, seated with the daughter of the mountain (Śailaputrī) and surrounded by hundreds of hosts of spirits (bhūtas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 23. He saw the extremely powerful Lord of Umā (Umāpati), the God of gods, seated with the daughter of the mountain (Śailaputrī) and surrounded by hundreds of hosts of spirits (bhūtas).

sanskrit: निकाया भूतसंघानां परमाद्भुतदर्शनाः ।विकृता विकृताकारा विकृताभरणध्वजाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: nikāyā bhūtasaṁghānāṁ paramādbhutadarśanāḥ ,vikṛtā vikṛtākārā vikṛtābharaṇadhvajāḥ.
english: The hosts of spirits (bhūtas) were of extremely wonderful appearance; they were distorted, had grotesque forms, and bore distorted ornaments and banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 24. The hosts of spirits (bhūtas) were of extremely wonderful appearance; they were distorted, had grotesque forms, and bore distorted ornaments and banners.

sanskrit: व्याघ्रसिंहर्क्षवदना बिडालमकराननाः ।वृषदंशमुखाश्चान्ये गजोष्ट्रवदनास्तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: vyāghrasiṁharkṣavadanā biḍālamakarānanāḥ ,vṛṣadaṁśamukhāścānye gajoṣṭravadanāstathā.
english: They had faces of tigers, lions, and bears; with faces of cats and crocodiles; and some others had faces of dogs (vṛṣadaṃśa); and still others, likewise, had faces of elephants and camels.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 25. They had faces of tigers, lions, and bears; with faces of cats and crocodiles; and some others had faces of dogs (vṛṣadaṃśa); and still others, likewise, had faces of elephants and camels.

sanskrit: उलूकवदनाः केचिद्गृध्रगोमायुदर्शनाः ।क्रौञ्चपारावतनिभैर्वदनै राङ्कवैरपि ॥२६॥
transliteration: ulūkavadanāḥ kecidgṛdhragomāyudarśanāḥ ,krauñcapārāvatanibhairvadanai rāṅkavairapi.
english: Some had owl-like faces; others appeared like vultures and jackals. Still others had faces resembling cranes, pigeons, and also antelopes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 26. Some had owl-like faces; others appeared like vultures and jackals. Still others had faces resembling cranes, pigeons, and also antelopes.

sanskrit: श्वाविच्छल्यकगोधानां खरैडकगवां तथा ।सदृशानि वपूंष्यन्ये तत्र तत्र व्यधारयन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: śvāvicchalyakagodhānāṁ kharaiḍakagavāṁ tathā ,sadṛśāni vapūṁṣyanye tatra tatra vyadhārayan.
english: Others, here and there, assumed bodies resembling those of porcupines, hedgehogs, monitor lizards, donkeys, sheep, and cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 27. Others, here and there, assumed bodies resembling those of porcupines, hedgehogs, monitor lizards, donkeys, sheep, and cows.

sanskrit: केचिच्छैलाम्बुदप्रख्याश्चक्रालातगदायुधाः ।केचिदञ्जनपुञ्जाभाः केचिच्छ्वेताचलप्रभाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: kecicchailāmbudaprakhyāścakrālātagadāyudhāḥ ,kecidañjanapuñjābhāḥ kecicchvetācalaprabhāḥ.
english: Some appeared like mountains and clouds, armed with discuses, firebrands, and maces. Others had the dark luster of heaps of collyrium, and some shone with the brilliance of white mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 28. Some appeared like mountains and clouds, armed with discuses, firebrands, and maces. Others had the dark luster of heaps of collyrium, and some shone with the brilliance of white mountains.

sanskrit: सप्तमातृगणाश्चैव समाजग्मुर्विशां पते ।साध्या विश्वेऽथ मरुतो वसवः पितरस्तथा ॥२९॥
transliteration: saptamātṛgaṇāścaiva samājagmurviśāṁ pate ,sādhyā viśve'tha maruto vasavaḥ pitarastathā.
english: O lord of the people, the groups of the seven Mothers (Saptamātṛgaṇas) also assembled. Then came the Sadhyas, the Viśve Devas, the Maruts, the Vasus, and the ancestors (pitaraḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 29. O lord of the people, the groups of the seven Mothers (Saptamātṛgaṇas) also assembled. Then came the Sadhyas, the Viśve Devas, the Maruts, the Vasus, and the ancestors (pitaraḥ).

sanskrit: ततः कुमारमादाय देवा ब्रह्मपुरोगमाः ।अभिषेकार्थमाजग्मुः शैलेन्द्रं सहितास्ततः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tataḥ kumāramādāya devā brahmapurogamāḥ ,abhiṣekārthamājagmuḥ śailendraṁ sahitāstataḥ.
english: Then, the gods, led by Brahma, took the boy. Having assembled, they came to the king of mountains for the consecration.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 50. Then, the gods, led by Brahma, took the boy. Having assembled, they came to the king of mountains for the consecration.

sanskrit: पुण्यां हैमवतीं देवीं सरिच्छ्रेष्ठां सरस्वतीम् ।समन्तपञ्चके या वै त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुता ॥५१॥
transliteration: puṇyāṁ haimavatīṁ devīṁ saricchreṣṭhāṁ sarasvatīm ,samantapañcake yā vai triṣu lokeṣu viśrutā.
english: The sacred goddess Sarasvati, daughter of Himavat, the best of rivers, who is indeed renowned throughout the three worlds, in the Samantapañcaka region.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 51. The sacred goddess Sarasvati, daughter of Himavat, the best of rivers, who is indeed renowned throughout the three worlds, in the Samantapañcaka region.

sanskrit: तत्र तीरे सरस्वत्याः पुण्ये सर्वगुणान्विते ।निषेदुर्देवगन्धर्वाः सर्वे संपूर्णमानसाः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tatra tīre sarasvatyāḥ puṇye sarvaguṇānvite ,niṣedurdevagandharvāḥ sarve saṁpūrṇamānasāḥ.
