english: There, on the sacred bank of Sarasvati, which possessed all good qualities, all the gods and Gandharvas sat down, their minds completely satisfied.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 43 Verse 52. There, on the sacred bank of Sarasvati, which possessed all good qualities, all the gods and Gandharvas sat down, their minds completely satisfied.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततोऽभिषेकसंभारान्सर्वान्संभृत्य शास्त्रतः ।बृहस्पतिः समिद्धेऽग्नौ जुहावाज्यं यथाविधि ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato'bhiṣekasaṁbhārānsarvānsaṁbhṛtya śāstrataḥ ,bṛhaspatiḥ samiddhe'gnau juhāvājyaṁ yathāvidhi.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Bṛhaspati, having gathered all the necessary materials for the consecration ritual according to the scriptures, offered clarified butter into the blazing fire in the prescribed manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Bṛhaspati, having gathered all the necessary materials for the consecration ritual according to the scriptures, offered clarified butter into the blazing fire in the prescribed manner.

sanskrit: उच्चैःश्रवा हयश्रेष्ठो नागराजश्च वामनः ।अरुणो गरुडश्चैव वृक्षाश्चौषधिभिः सह ॥१४॥
transliteration: uccaiḥśravā hayaśreṣṭho nāgarājaśca vāmanaḥ ,aruṇo garuḍaścaiva vṛkṣāścauṣadhibhiḥ saha.
english: Uccaiḥśravas, the foremost of horses, and Nāgarāja (the king of serpents), and Vāmana, as well as Aruṇa and Garuḍa, and trees together with medicinal plants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 14. Uccaiḥśravas, the foremost of horses, and Nāgarāja (the king of serpents), and Vāmana, as well as Aruṇa and Garuḍa, and trees together with medicinal plants.

sanskrit: धर्मश्च भगवान्देवः समाजग्मुर्हि संगताः ।कालो यमश्च मृत्युश्च यमस्यानुचराश्च ये ॥१५॥
transliteration: dharmaśca bhagavāndevaḥ samājagmurhi saṁgatāḥ ,kālo yamaśca mṛtyuśca yamasyānucarāśca ye.
english: And the divine lord (dharma), Kāla (Time), Yama, and Mṛtyu (Death), as well as all those who are Yama's attendants, indeed gathered together.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 15. And the divine lord (dharma), Kāla (Time), Yama, and Mṛtyu (Death), as well as all those who are Yama's attendants, indeed gathered together.

sanskrit: बहुलत्वाच्च नोक्ता ये विविधा देवतागणाः ।ते कुमाराभिषेकार्थं समाजग्मुस्ततस्ततः ॥१६॥
transliteration: bahulatvācca noktā ye vividhā devatāgaṇāḥ ,te kumārābhiṣekārthaṁ samājagmustatastataḥ.
english: And those various groups of deities who were not mentioned due to their great number, they also gathered from all directions for the consecration of Kumāra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 16. And those various groups of deities who were not mentioned due to their great number, they also gathered from all directions for the consecration of Kumāra.

sanskrit: जगृहुस्ते तदा राजन्सर्व एव दिवौकसः ।आभिषेचनिकं भाण्डं मङ्गलानि च सर्वशः ॥१७॥
transliteration: jagṛhuste tadā rājansarva eva divaukasaḥ ,ābhiṣecanikaṁ bhāṇḍaṁ maṅgalāni ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, O King, all those celestial beings indeed gathered all the items for the anointing ceremony and all kinds of auspicious objects.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 17. Then, O King, all those celestial beings indeed gathered all the items for the anointing ceremony and all kinds of auspicious objects.

sanskrit: जयशब्दं ततश्चक्रुर्देवाः सर्वे सवासवाः ।गन्धर्वयक्षरक्षांसि मुनयः पितरस्तथा ॥२६॥
transliteration: jayaśabdaṁ tataścakrurdevāḥ sarve savāsavāḥ ,gandharvayakṣarakṣāṁsi munayaḥ pitarastathā.
english: Then, all the gods, along with Indra (Vasava), the Gandharvas, Yakṣas, Rakṣasas, sages, and ancestors (pitaraḥ), raised a cry of victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 26. Then, all the gods, along with Indra (Vasava), the Gandharvas, Yakṣas, Rakṣasas, sages, and ancestors (pitaraḥ), raised a cry of victory.

sanskrit: यमः प्रादादनुचरौ यमकालोपमावुभौ ।उन्माथं च प्रमाथं च महावीर्यौ महाद्युती ॥२७॥
transliteration: yamaḥ prādādanucarau yamakālopamāvubhau ,unmāthaṁ ca pramāthaṁ ca mahāvīryau mahādyutī.
english: Yama gave two attendants, both resembling Yama and Kāla, named Unmatha and Pramatha, who possessed great power and radiance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 27. Yama gave two attendants, both resembling Yama and Kāla, named Unmatha and Pramatha, who possessed great power and radiance.

sanskrit: सुभ्राजो भास्करश्चैव यौ तौ सूर्यानुयायिनौ ।तौ सूर्यः कार्त्तिकेयाय ददौ प्रीतः प्रतापवान् ॥२८॥
transliteration: subhrājo bhāskaraścaiva yau tau sūryānuyāyinau ,tau sūryaḥ kārttikeyāya dadau prītaḥ pratāpavān.
english: The glorious (pratāpavān) Sun, being pleased, bestowed upon Kārttikeya those two, Subhrāja and Bhāskara, who were his followers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 28. The glorious (pratāpavān) Sun, being pleased, bestowed upon Kārttikeya those two, Subhrāja and Bhāskara, who were his followers.

sanskrit: कैलासशृङ्गसंकाशौ श्वेतमाल्यानुलेपनौ ।सोमोऽप्यनुचरौ प्रादान्मणिं सुमणिमेव च ॥२९॥
transliteration: kailāsaśṛṅgasaṁkāśau śvetamālyānulepanau ,somo'pyanucarau prādānmaṇiṁ sumaṇimeva ca.
english: Soma (the moon god) also gave two attendants, Mani and Sumani, who resembled the peaks of Kailasa and were adorned with white garlands and ointments.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 29. Soma (the moon god) also gave two attendants, Mani and Sumani, who resembled the peaks of Kailasa and were adorned with white garlands and ointments.

sanskrit: ज्वालाजिह्वं तथा ज्योतिरात्मजाय हुताशनः ।ददावनुचरौ शूरौ परसैन्यप्रमाथिनौ ॥३०॥
transliteration: jvālājihvaṁ tathā jyotirātmajāya hutāśanaḥ ,dadāvanucarau śūrau parasainyapramāthinau.
english: The fire god (hutāśana) gave his son (skanda) two heroic attendants (anucara), Jvalajihva and Jyoti, who were destroyers of enemy armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 30. The fire god (hutāśana) gave his son (skanda) two heroic attendants (anucara), Jvalajihva and Jyoti, who were destroyers of enemy armies.

sanskrit: परिघं च वटं चैव भीमं च सुमहाबलम् ।दहतिं दहनं चैव प्रचण्डौ वीर्यसंमतौ ।अंशोऽप्यनुचरान्पञ्च ददौ स्कन्दाय धीमते ॥३१॥
transliteration: parighaṁ ca vaṭaṁ caiva bhīmaṁ ca sumahābalam ,dahatiṁ dahanaṁ caiva pracaṇḍau vīryasaṁmatau ,aṁśo'pyanucarānpañca dadau skandāya dhīmate.
english: Aṃśa also gave five attendants (anucara) to the wise Skanda (skanda): Parigha, Vata, Bhima (who was extremely powerful), and Dahati and Dahana (who were both fierce and esteemed for their valor).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 31. Aṃśa also gave five attendants (anucara) to the wise Skanda (skanda): Parigha, Vata, Bhima (who was extremely powerful), and Dahati and Dahana (who were both fierce and esteemed for their valor).

sanskrit: उत्क्रोशं पङ्कजं चैव वज्रदण्डधरावुभौ ।ददावनलपुत्राय वासवः परवीरहा ।तौ हि शत्रून्महेन्द्रस्य जघ्नतुः समरे बहून् ॥३२॥
transliteration: utkrośaṁ paṅkajaṁ caiva vajradaṇḍadharāvubhau ,dadāvanalaputrāya vāsavaḥ paravīrahā ,tau hi śatrūnmahendrasya jaghnatuḥ samare bahūn.
english: Vasava (Indra), the destroyer of enemy heroes (paravīrahā), indeed gave both Utkrosha and Pankaja, who carried the thunderbolt-staff, to the son of Agni (analaputra) (skanda). For those two attendants (anucara) had indeed killed many of Mahendra's (indra's) enemies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 32. Vasava (Indra), the destroyer of enemy heroes (paravīrahā), indeed gave both Utkrosha and Pankaja, who carried the thunderbolt-staff, to the son of Agni (analaputra) (skanda). For those two attendants (anucara) had indeed killed many of Mahendra's (indra's) enemies in battle.

sanskrit: चक्रं विक्रमकं चैव संक्रमं च महाबलम् ।स्कन्दाय त्रीननुचरान्ददौ विष्णुर्महायशाः ॥३३॥
transliteration: cakraṁ vikramakaṁ caiva saṁkramaṁ ca mahābalam ,skandāya trīnanucarāndadau viṣṇurmahāyaśāḥ.
english: The highly renowned Viṣṇu (viṣṇu) indeed gave three attendants (anucara) to Skanda (skanda): Chakra, Vikramaka, and Saṃkrama, who was very powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 33. The highly renowned Viṣṇu (viṣṇu) indeed gave three attendants (anucara) to Skanda (skanda): Chakra, Vikramaka, and Saṃkrama, who was very powerful.

sanskrit: वर्धनं नन्दनं चैव सर्वविद्याविशारदौ ।स्कन्दाय ददतुः प्रीतावश्विनौ भरतर्षभ ॥३४॥
transliteration: vardhanaṁ nandanaṁ caiva sarvavidyāviśāradau ,skandāya dadatuḥ prītāvaśvinau bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among the Bharatas, the two Aśvins, who are skilled in all sciences and pleased, gave Vardhana and Nandana to Skanda.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 34. O best among the Bharatas, the two Aśvins, who are skilled in all sciences and pleased, gave Vardhana and Nandana to Skanda.

sanskrit: कुन्दनं कुसुमं चैव कुमुदं च महायशाः ।डम्बराडम्बरौ चैव ददौ धाता महात्मने ॥३५॥
transliteration: kundanaṁ kusumaṁ caiva kumudaṁ ca mahāyaśāḥ ,ḍambarāḍambarau caiva dadau dhātā mahātmane.
english: The highly renowned (Dhātā) gave Kundana, Kusuma, and Kumuda, and also Ḍambara and Āḍambara, to the great-souled (Skanda).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 35. The highly renowned (Dhātā) gave Kundana, Kusuma, and Kumuda, and also Ḍambara and Āḍambara, to the great-souled (Skanda).

sanskrit: वक्रानुवक्रौ बलिनौ मेषवक्त्रौ बलोत्कटौ ।ददौ त्वष्टा महामायौ स्कन्दायानुचरौ वरौ ॥३६॥
transliteration: vakrānuvakrau balinau meṣavaktrau balotkaṭau ,dadau tvaṣṭā mahāmāyau skandāyānucarau varau.
english: Tvaṣṭā gave the excellent attendants, Vakra and Anuvakra, to Skanda. These two were strong, ram-faced, exceedingly powerful, and possessed of great creative power (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 36. Tvaṣṭā gave the excellent attendants, Vakra and Anuvakra, to Skanda. These two were strong, ram-faced, exceedingly powerful, and possessed of great creative power (māyā).

sanskrit: सुव्रतं सत्यसंधं च ददौ मित्रो महात्मने ।कुमाराय महात्मानौ तपोविद्याधरौ प्रभुः ॥३७॥
transliteration: suvrataṁ satyasaṁdhaṁ ca dadau mitro mahātmane ,kumārāya mahātmānau tapovidyādharau prabhuḥ.
english: Lord Mitra gave the great-souled (Suvrata) and the truthful (Satyasandha) to the great-souled Kumāra (Skanda). These two were endowed with ascetic power (tapas) and knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 37. Lord Mitra gave the great-souled (Suvrata) and the truthful (Satyasandha) to the great-souled Kumāra (Skanda). These two were endowed with ascetic power (tapas) and knowledge.

sanskrit: सुदर्शनीयौ वरदौ त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतौ ।सुप्रभं च महात्मानं शुभकर्माणमेव च ।कार्त्तिकेयाय संप्रादाद्विधाता लोकविश्रुतौ ॥३८॥
transliteration: sudarśanīyau varadau triṣu lokeṣu viśrutau ,suprabhaṁ ca mahātmānaṁ śubhakarmāṇameva ca ,kārttikeyāya saṁprādādvidhātā lokaviśrutau.
english: The creator (Vidhātā) bestowed upon Kārttikeya Sudarśanīya and Varada, who are both beautiful, bestowers of boons, and widely renowned in the three worlds. He also gave (him) Suprabha, Mahātman, and Śubhakarman.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 38. The creator (Vidhātā) bestowed upon Kārttikeya Sudarśanīya and Varada, who are both beautiful, bestowers of boons, and widely renowned in the three worlds. He also gave (him) Suprabha, Mahātman, and Śubhakarman.

sanskrit: पालितकं कालिकं च महामायाविनावुभौ ।पूषा च पार्षदौ प्रादात्कार्त्तिकेयाय भारत ॥३९॥
transliteration: pālitakaṁ kālikaṁ ca mahāmāyāvināvubhau ,pūṣā ca pārṣadau prādātkārttikeyāya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Pūṣan also bestowed upon Kārttikeya (Karttikeya) two attendants (pārṣadau), Pālitaka and Kālika, both of whom were great illusionists.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 39. O Bhārata, Pūṣan also bestowed upon Kārttikeya (Karttikeya) two attendants (pārṣadau), Pālitaka and Kālika, both of whom were great illusionists.

sanskrit: बलं चातिबलं चैव महावक्त्रौ महाबलौ ।प्रददौ कार्त्तिकेयाय वायुर्भरतसत्तम ॥४०॥
transliteration: balaṁ cātibalaṁ caiva mahāvaktrau mahābalau ,pradadau kārttikeyāya vāyurbharatasattama.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, Vāyu (the wind god) bestowed upon Kārttikeya (Karttikeya) Bala and Atibala, who both possessed great mouths and immense strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 40. O best of the Bhāratas, Vāyu (the wind god) bestowed upon Kārttikeya (Karttikeya) Bala and Atibala, who both possessed great mouths and immense strength.

sanskrit: घसं चातिघसं चैव तिमिवक्त्रौ महाबलौ ।प्रददौ कार्त्तिकेयाय वरुणः सत्यसंगरः ॥४१॥
transliteration: ghasaṁ cātighasaṁ caiva timivaktrau mahābalau ,pradadau kārttikeyāya varuṇaḥ satyasaṁgaraḥ.
english: And Varuṇa (the god Varuṇa), whose resolve is true, bestowed upon Kārttikeya (Karttikeya) Ghasa and Atighasa, who both had mouths like whales and were immensely powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 41. And Varuṇa (the god Varuṇa), whose resolve is true, bestowed upon Kārttikeya (Karttikeya) Ghasa and Atighasa, who both had mouths like whales and were immensely powerful.

sanskrit: सुवर्चसं महात्मानं तथैवाप्यतिवर्चसम् ।हिमवान्प्रददौ राजन्हुताशनसुताय वै ॥४२॥
transliteration: suvarcasaṁ mahātmānaṁ tathaivāpyativarcasam ,himavānpradadau rājanhutāśanasutāya vai.
english: O King, Himavat bestowed Suvarcasa, the great-souled (mahātman), and similarly Ativarca, who was exceedingly brilliant, upon the son of Hutāśana (Agni).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 42. O King, Himavat bestowed Suvarcasa, the great-souled (mahātman), and similarly Ativarca, who was exceedingly brilliant, upon the son of Hutāśana (Agni).

sanskrit: काञ्चनं च महात्मानं मेघमालिनमेव च ।ददावनुचरौ मेरुरग्निपुत्राय भारत ॥४३॥
transliteration: kāñcanaṁ ca mahātmānaṁ meghamālinameva ca ,dadāvanucarau meruragniputrāya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Mount Meru gave the great-souled (mahātman) Kāñcana and Meghamālin as two attendants to the son of Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 43. O Bhārata, Mount Meru gave the great-souled (mahātman) Kāñcana and Meghamālin as two attendants to the son of Agni.

sanskrit: स्थिरं चातिस्थिरं चैव मेरुरेवापरौ ददौ ।महात्मनेऽग्निपुत्राय महाबलपराक्रमौ ॥४४॥
transliteration: sthiraṁ cātisthiraṁ caiva merurevāparau dadau ,mahātmane'gniputrāya mahābalaparākramau.
english: And Mount Meru indeed gave two other attendants, Sthira and Atisthira, who possessed great strength and valor, to the great-souled (mahātman) son of Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 44. And Mount Meru indeed gave two other attendants, Sthira and Atisthira, who possessed great strength and valor, to the great-souled (mahātman) son of Agni.

sanskrit: उच्छ्रितं चातिशृङ्गं च महापाषाणयोधिनौ ।प्रददावग्निपुत्राय विन्ध्यः पारिषदावुभौ ॥४५॥
transliteration: ucchritaṁ cātiśṛṅgaṁ ca mahāpāṣāṇayodhinau ,pradadāvagniputrāya vindhyaḥ pāriṣadāvubhau.
english: Mount Vindhya bestowed both Ucchrita and Atiśṛṅga, mighty stone-fighters, as two attendants upon the son of Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 45. Mount Vindhya bestowed both Ucchrita and Atiśṛṅga, mighty stone-fighters, as two attendants upon the son of Agni.

sanskrit: संग्रहं विग्रहं चैव समुद्रोऽपि गदाधरौ ।प्रददावग्निपुत्राय महापारिषदावुभौ ॥४६॥
transliteration: saṁgrahaṁ vigrahaṁ caiva samudro'pi gadādharau ,pradadāvagniputrāya mahāpāriṣadāvubhau.
english: The Ocean (samudraḥ) also gave Saṅgraha and Vigraha, both of whom were mace-wielders and great attendants, to the son of Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 46. The Ocean (samudraḥ) also gave Saṅgraha and Vigraha, both of whom were mace-wielders and great attendants, to the son of Agni.

sanskrit: उन्मादं पुष्पदन्तं च शङ्कुकर्णं तथैव च ।प्रददावग्निपुत्राय पार्वती शुभदर्शना ॥४७॥
transliteration: unmādaṁ puṣpadantaṁ ca śaṅkukarṇaṁ tathaiva ca ,pradadāvagniputrāya pārvatī śubhadarśanā.
english: And also, Pārvatī, who possesses an auspicious appearance, gave Unmāda, Puṣpadanta, and Śaṅkukarṇa to the son of Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 47. And also, Pārvatī, who possesses an auspicious appearance, gave Unmāda, Puṣpadanta, and Śaṅkukarṇa to the son of Agni.

sanskrit: जयं महाजयं चैव नागौ ज्वलनसूनवे ।प्रददौ पुरुषव्याघ्र वासुकिः पन्नगेश्वरः ॥४८॥
transliteration: jayaṁ mahājayaṁ caiva nāgau jvalanasūnave ,pradadau puruṣavyāghra vāsukiḥ pannageśvaraḥ.
english: O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), Vāsuki, the lord of serpents, gave Jaya and Mahājaya, who were two Nāgas, to the son of Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 48. O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), Vāsuki, the lord of serpents, gave Jaya and Mahājaya, who were two Nāgas, to the son of Agni.

sanskrit: एवं साध्याश्च रुद्राश्च वसवः पितरस्तथा ।सागराः सरितश्चैव गिरयश्च महाबलाः ॥४९॥
transliteration: evaṁ sādhyāśca rudrāśca vasavaḥ pitarastathā ,sāgarāḥ saritaścaiva girayaśca mahābalāḥ.
english: In this way, the Sādhyas, the Rudras, the Vasus, and the ancestors (pitaraḥ), and similarly the oceans, the rivers, and also the greatly powerful (mahābalāḥ) mountains (all offered their gifts).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 49. In this way, the Sādhyas, the Rudras, the Vasus, and the ancestors (pitaraḥ), and similarly the oceans, the rivers, and also the greatly powerful (mahābalāḥ) mountains (all offered their gifts).

sanskrit: ददुः सेनागणाध्यक्षाञ्शूलपट्टिशधारिणः ।दिव्यप्रहरणोपेतान्नानावेषविभूषितान् ॥५०॥
transliteration: daduḥ senāgaṇādhyakṣāñśūlapaṭṭiśadhāriṇaḥ ,divyapraharaṇopetānnānāveṣavibhūṣitān.
english: They appointed commanders of army groups who carried spears and "paṭṭiśa" weapons, were equipped with divine armaments, and adorned in various kinds of attire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 50. They appointed commanders of army groups who carried spears and "paṭṭiśa" weapons, were equipped with divine armaments, and adorned in various kinds of attire.

sanskrit: शृणु नामानि चान्येषां येऽन्ये स्कन्दस्य सैनिकाः ।विविधायुधसंपन्नाश्चित्राभरणवर्मिणः ॥५१॥
transliteration: śṛṇu nāmāni cānyeṣāṁ ye'nye skandasya sainikāḥ ,vividhāyudhasaṁpannāścitrābharaṇavarmiṇaḥ.
english: Listen also to the names of others, who are other soldiers of Skanda, equipped with various weapons and wearing colorful ornaments and armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 51. Listen also to the names of others, who are other soldiers of Skanda, equipped with various weapons and wearing colorful ornaments and armor.

sanskrit: शङ्कुकर्णो निकुम्भश्च पद्मः कुमुद एव च ।अनन्तो द्वादशभुजस्तथा कृष्णोपकृष्णकौ ॥५२॥
transliteration: śaṅkukarṇo nikumbhaśca padmaḥ kumuda eva ca ,ananto dvādaśabhujastathā kṛṣṇopakṛṣṇakau.
english: Śaṅkukarṇa, Nikumbha, Padma, and Kumuda; also Ananta and Dvādaśabhuja; and Kṛṣṇa and Upakṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 52. Śaṅkukarṇa, Nikumbha, Padma, and Kumuda; also Ananta and Dvādaśabhuja; and Kṛṣṇa and Upakṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: द्रोणश्रवाः कपिस्कन्धः काञ्चनाक्षो जलंधमः ।अक्षसंतर्जनो राजन्कुनदीकस्तमोभ्रकृत् ॥५३॥
transliteration: droṇaśravāḥ kapiskandhaḥ kāñcanākṣo jalaṁdhamaḥ ,akṣasaṁtarjano rājankunadīkastamobhrakṛt.
english: Droṇaśravā, Kapiskandha, Kāñcanākṣa, Jalandhama, Akṣasaṃtarjana, O King, Kunadīka, and Tamobhrakṛt.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 53. Droṇaśravā, Kapiskandha, Kāñcanākṣa, Jalandhama, Akṣasaṃtarjana, O King, Kunadīka, and Tamobhrakṛt.

sanskrit: एकाक्षो द्वादशाक्षश्च तथैवैकजटः प्रभुः ।सहस्रबाहुर्विकटो व्याघ्राक्षः क्षितिकम्पनः ॥५४॥
transliteration: ekākṣo dvādaśākṣaśca tathaivaikajaṭaḥ prabhuḥ ,sahasrabāhurvikaṭo vyāghrākṣaḥ kṣitikampanaḥ.
english: He is one-eyed, twelve-eyed, and also has a single matted lock; the Lord. He has a thousand arms, is formidable, tiger-eyed, and shakes the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 54. He is one-eyed, twelve-eyed, and also has a single matted lock; the Lord. He has a thousand arms, is formidable, tiger-eyed, and shakes the earth.

sanskrit: पुण्यनामा सुनामा च सुवक्त्रः प्रियदर्शनः ।परिश्रुतः कोकनदः प्रियमाल्यानुलेपनः ॥५५॥
transliteration: puṇyanāmā sunāmā ca suvaktraḥ priyadarśanaḥ ,pariśrutaḥ kokanadaḥ priyamālyānulepanaḥ.
english: He has a sacred name and a good name; he has a beautiful face and a pleasing appearance. He is renowned, like a red lotus (kokanada), and is fond of garlands and unguents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 55. He has a sacred name and a good name; he has a beautiful face and a pleasing appearance. He is renowned, like a red lotus (kokanada), and is fond of garlands and unguents.

sanskrit: अजोदरो गजशिराः स्कन्धाक्षः शतलोचनः ।ज्वालाजिह्वः करालश्च सितकेशो जटी हरिः ॥५६॥
transliteration: ajodaro gajaśirāḥ skandhākṣaḥ śatalocanaḥ ,jvālājihvaḥ karālaśca sitakeśo jaṭī hariḥ.
english: He has a goat's belly, an elephant's head, eyes on his shoulders, and a hundred eyes. He is flame-tongued and fearsome; white-haired, matted-haired, and the divine Hari.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 56. He has a goat's belly, an elephant's head, eyes on his shoulders, and a hundred eyes. He is flame-tongued and fearsome; white-haired, matted-haired, and the divine Hari.

sanskrit: चतुर्दंष्ट्रोऽष्टजिह्वश्च मेघनादः पृथुश्रवाः ।विद्युदक्षो धनुर्वक्त्रो जठरो मारुताशनः ॥५७॥
transliteration: caturdaṁṣṭro'ṣṭajihvaśca meghanādaḥ pṛthuśravāḥ ,vidyudakṣo dhanurvaktro jaṭharo mārutāśanaḥ.
english: He is four-tusked and eight-tongued; he has a thunderous voice and is widely renowned. He has lightning-like eyes, a bow-shaped mouth, a large belly, and is a wind-eater (mārutāśana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 57. He is four-tusked and eight-tongued; he has a thunderous voice and is widely renowned. He has lightning-like eyes, a bow-shaped mouth, a large belly, and is a wind-eater (mārutāśana).

sanskrit: उदराक्षो झषाक्षश्च वज्रनाभो वसुप्रभः ।समुद्रवेगो राजेन्द्र शैलकम्पी तथैव च ॥५८॥
transliteration: udarākṣo jhaṣākṣaśca vajranābho vasuprabhaḥ ,samudravego rājendra śailakampī tathaiva ca.
english: O King of Kings (rājendra), Udaraksha, Jhashaksha, Vajranabha, Vasuprabha, Samudravega, and Shailakampi are also there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 58. O King of Kings (rājendra), Udaraksha, Jhashaksha, Vajranabha, Vasuprabha, Samudravega, and Shailakampi are also there.

sanskrit: पुत्रमेषः प्रवाहश्च तथा नन्दोपनन्दकौ ।धूम्रः श्वेतः कलिङ्गश्च सिद्धार्थो वरदस्तथा ॥५९॥
transliteration: putrameṣaḥ pravāhaśca tathā nandopanandakau ,dhūmraḥ śvetaḥ kaliṅgaśca siddhārtho varadastathā.
english: Putramesha, Pravaha, and Nanda and Upananda, Dhūmra, Shveta, Kalinga, Siddhartha, and Varada are similarly present.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 59. Putramesha, Pravaha, and Nanda and Upananda, Dhūmra, Shveta, Kalinga, Siddhartha, and Varada are similarly present.

sanskrit: प्रियकश्चैव नन्दश्च गोनन्दश्च प्रतापवान् ।आनन्दश्च प्रमोदश्च स्वस्तिको ध्रुवकस्तथा ॥६०॥
transliteration: priyakaścaiva nandaśca gonandaśca pratāpavān ,ānandaśca pramodaśca svastiko dhruvakastathā.
english: Priyaka, and Nanda, and the powerful Gonanda, and similarly Ānanda, Pramoda, Svastika, and Dhruvaka are present.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 60. Priyaka, and Nanda, and the powerful Gonanda, and similarly Ānanda, Pramoda, Svastika, and Dhruvaka are present.

sanskrit: क्षेमवापः सुजातश्च सिद्धयात्रश्च भारत ।गोव्रजः कनकापीडो महापारिषदेश्वरः ॥६१॥
transliteration: kṣemavāpaḥ sujātaśca siddhayātraśca bhārata ,govrajaḥ kanakāpīḍo mahāpāriṣadeśvaraḥ.
english: O Bhārata, Kshemavapa, Sujata, and Siddhayatra, as well as Govraja, Kanakapida, and Mahaparishadeshvara are present.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 61. O Bhārata, Kshemavapa, Sujata, and Siddhayatra, as well as Govraja, Kanakapida, and Mahaparishadeshvara are present.

sanskrit: गायनो हसनश्चैव बाणः खड्गश्च वीर्यवान् ।वैताली चातिताली च तथा कतिकवातिकौ ॥६२॥
transliteration: gāyano hasanaścaiva bāṇaḥ khaḍgaśca vīryavān ,vaitālī cātitālī ca tathā katikavātikau.
english: There are Gāyana, Hasana, Bāṇa, and Khaḍga the valiant; Vaitālī and Atitālī; and also Katika and Vātika.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 62. There are Gāyana, Hasana, Bāṇa, and Khaḍga the valiant; Vaitālī and Atitālī; and also Katika and Vātika.

sanskrit: हंसजः पङ्कदिग्धाङ्गः समुद्रोन्मादनश्च ह ।रणोत्कटः प्रहासश्च श्वेतशीर्षश्च नन्दकः ॥६३॥
transliteration: haṁsajaḥ paṅkadigdhāṅgaḥ samudronmādanaśca ha ,raṇotkaṭaḥ prahāsaśca śvetaśīrṣaśca nandakaḥ.
english: Haṃsaja, Paṅkadigdhāṅga (whose limbs are smeared with mud), and indeed Samudronmādana (the agitator of the ocean); Raṇotkaṭa (fierce in battle), Prahāsa, Śvetaśīrṣa (the white-headed), and Nandaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 63. Haṃsaja, Paṅkadigdhāṅga (whose limbs are smeared with mud), and indeed Samudronmādana (the agitator of the ocean); Raṇotkaṭa (fierce in battle), Prahāsa, Śvetaśīrṣa (the white-headed), and Nandaka.

sanskrit: कालकण्ठः प्रभासश्च तथा कुम्भाण्डकोऽपरः ।कालकाक्षः सितश्चैव भूतलोन्मथनस्तथा ॥६४॥
transliteration: kālakaṇṭhaḥ prabhāsaśca tathā kumbhāṇḍako'paraḥ ,kālakākṣaḥ sitaścaiva bhūtalonmathanastathā.
english: Kālakaṇṭha (the dark-throated), and Prabhāsa; and also another, Kumbhāṇḍaka; Kālakākṣa (the dark-eyed), and also Sita; and furthermore Bhūtalonmathana (the agitator of the earth).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 64. Kālakaṇṭha (the dark-throated), and Prabhāsa; and also another, Kumbhāṇḍaka; Kālakākṣa (the dark-eyed), and also Sita; and furthermore Bhūtalonmathana (the agitator of the earth).

sanskrit: यज्ञवाहः प्रवाहश्च देवयाजी च सोमपः ।सजालश्च महातेजाः क्रथक्राथौ च भारत ॥६५॥
transliteration: yajñavāhaḥ pravāhaśca devayājī ca somapaḥ ,sajālaśca mahātejāḥ krathakrāthau ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Yajñavāha and Pravāha, Devayājī and Somapa, and the highly glorious Sajāla, as well as Kratha and Krātha.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 65. O Bhārata, Yajñavāha and Pravāha, Devayājī and Somapa, and the highly glorious Sajāla, as well as Kratha and Krātha.

sanskrit: तुहनश्च तुहानश्च चित्रदेवश्च वीर्यवान् ।मधुरः सुप्रसादश्च किरीटी च महाबलः ॥६६॥
transliteration: tuhanaśca tuhānaśca citradevaśca vīryavān ,madhuraḥ suprasādaśca kirīṭī ca mahābalaḥ.
english: Tuhana, Tuhāna, and Citradeva the valiant; Madhura, Suprasāda, Kirīṭī, and Mahābala.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 66. Tuhana, Tuhāna, and Citradeva the valiant; Madhura, Suprasāda, Kirīṭī, and Mahābala.

sanskrit: वसनो मधुवर्णश्च कलशोदर एव च ।धमन्तो मन्मथकरः सूचीवक्त्रश्च वीर्यवान् ॥६७॥
transliteration: vasano madhuvarṇaśca kalaśodara eva ca ,dhamanto manmathakaraḥ sūcīvaktraśca vīryavān.
english: Vasana, Madhuvarṇa, and Kalaśodara; and also Dhamanta, Manmathakara, and Sūcīvaktra the valiant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 67. Vasana, Madhuvarṇa, and Kalaśodara; and also Dhamanta, Manmathakara, and Sūcīvaktra the valiant.

sanskrit: श्वेतवक्त्रः सुवक्त्रश्च चारुवक्त्रश्च पाण्डुरः ।दण्डबाहुः सुबाहुश्च रजः कोकिलकस्तथा ॥६८॥
transliteration: śvetavaktraḥ suvaktraśca cāruvaktraśca pāṇḍuraḥ ,daṇḍabāhuḥ subāhuśca rajaḥ kokilakastathā.
english: Śvetavaktra, Suvaktra, Cāruvaktra, and Pāṇḍura; Daṇḍabāhu, Subāhu, Rajaḥ, and Kokilaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 68. Śvetavaktra, Suvaktra, Cāruvaktra, and Pāṇḍura; Daṇḍabāhu, Subāhu, Rajaḥ, and Kokilaka.

sanskrit: अचलः कनकाक्षश्च बालानामयिकः प्रभुः ।संचारकः कोकनदो गृध्रवक्त्रश्च जम्बुकः ॥६९॥
transliteration: acalaḥ kanakākṣaśca bālānāmayikaḥ prabhuḥ ,saṁcārakaḥ kokanado gṛdhravaktraśca jambukaḥ.
english: Acala, Kanakākṣa, and the lord Bālānāmayika; Saṃcāraka, Kokanada, Gṛdhravaktra, and Jambuka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 69. Acala, Kanakākṣa, and the lord Bālānāmayika; Saṃcāraka, Kokanada, Gṛdhravaktra, and Jambuka.

sanskrit: लोहाशवक्त्रो जठरः कुम्भवक्त्रश्च कुण्डकः ।मद्गुग्रीवश्च कृष्णौजा हंसवक्त्रश्च चन्द्रभाः ॥७०॥
transliteration: lohāśavaktro jaṭharaḥ kumbhavaktraśca kuṇḍakaḥ ,madgugrīvaśca kṛṣṇaujā haṁsavaktraśca candrabhāḥ.
english: Jaṭhara, with an iron-like mouth; and Kuṇḍaka, with a pot-like mouth; and Kṛṣṇaujas, with a neck like a cormorant; and Candrabhā, with a swan-like mouth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 70. Jaṭhara, with an iron-like mouth; and Kuṇḍaka, with a pot-like mouth; and Kṛṣṇaujas, with a neck like a cormorant; and Candrabhā, with a swan-like mouth.

sanskrit: पाणिकूर्मा च शम्बूकः पञ्चवक्त्रश्च शिक्षकः ।चाषवक्त्रश्च जम्बूकः शाकवक्त्रश्च कुण्डकः ॥७१॥
transliteration: pāṇikūrmā ca śambūkaḥ pañcavaktraśca śikṣakaḥ ,cāṣavaktraśca jambūkaḥ śākavaktraśca kuṇḍakaḥ.
english: And Śambūka, who had tortoise-like hands; and Śikṣaka, with five mouths; and Jambūka, with a mouth like a cāṣa bird; and Kuṇḍaka, with a mouth like a śāka bird.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 71. And Śambūka, who had tortoise-like hands; and Śikṣaka, with five mouths; and Jambūka, with a mouth like a cāṣa bird; and Kuṇḍaka, with a mouth like a śāka bird.

sanskrit: योगयुक्ता महात्मानः सततं ब्राह्मणप्रियाः ।पैतामहा महात्मानो महापारिषदाश्च ह ।यौवनस्थाश्च बालाश्च वृद्धाश्च जनमेजय ॥७२॥
transliteration: yogayuktā mahātmānaḥ satataṁ brāhmaṇapriyāḥ ,paitāmahā mahātmāno mahāpāriṣadāśca ha ,yauvanasthāśca bālāśca vṛddhāśca janamejaya.
english: O Janamejaya, these great-souled (mahātman) beings, constantly engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga) and dear to brahmins, were indeed great-souled (mahātman) descendants of Brahmā and great courtiers. They were present as youths, children, and elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 72. O Janamejaya, these great-souled (mahātman) beings, constantly engaged in spiritual discipline (yoga) and dear to brahmins, were indeed great-souled (mahātman) descendants of Brahmā and great courtiers. They were present as youths, children, and elders.

sanskrit: सहस्रशः पारिषदाः कुमारमुपतस्थिरे ।वक्त्रैर्नानाविधैर्ये तु शृणु ताञ्जनमेजय ॥७३॥
transliteration: sahasraśaḥ pāriṣadāḥ kumāramupatasthire ,vaktrairnānāvidhairye tu śṛṇu tāñjanamejaya.
english: O Janamejaya, thousands of attendants, who indeed had various kinds of mouths and faces, approached Kumāra. Listen about them!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 73. O Janamejaya, thousands of attendants, who indeed had various kinds of mouths and faces, approached Kumāra. Listen about them!

sanskrit: कूर्मकुक्कुटवक्त्राश्च शशोलूकमुखास्तथा ।खरोष्ट्रवदनाश्चैव वराहवदनास्तथा ॥७४॥
transliteration: kūrmakukkuṭavaktrāśca śaśolūkamukhāstathā ,kharoṣṭravadanāścaiva varāhavadanāstathā.
english: Some had faces like tortoises and roosters, and others had faces like hares and owls. Still others had faces like donkeys and camels, and also like boars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 74. Some had faces like tortoises and roosters, and others had faces like hares and owls. Still others had faces like donkeys and camels, and also like boars.

sanskrit: मनुष्यमेषवक्त्राश्च सृगालवदनास्तथा ।भीमा मकरवक्त्राश्च शिंशुमारमुखास्तथा ॥७५॥
transliteration: manuṣyameṣavaktrāśca sṛgālavadanāstathā ,bhīmā makaravaktrāśca śiṁśumāramukhāstathā.
english: Some had faces like humans and rams, and others like jackals. There were also terrifying ones with faces like crocodiles and dolphins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 75. Some had faces like humans and rams, and others like jackals. There were also terrifying ones with faces like crocodiles and dolphins.

sanskrit: मार्जारशशवक्त्राश्च दीर्घवक्त्राश्च भारत ।नकुलोलूकवक्त्राश्च श्ववक्त्राश्च तथापरे ॥७६॥
transliteration: mārjāraśaśavaktrāśca dīrghavaktrāśca bhārata ,nakulolūkavaktrāśca śvavaktrāśca tathāpare.
english: O Bhārata, others had faces like cats and hares, and some had long faces. Still others had faces like mongooses and owls, and also like dogs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 76. O Bhārata, others had faces like cats and hares, and some had long faces. Still others had faces like mongooses and owls, and also like dogs.

sanskrit: आखुबभ्रुकवक्त्राश्च मयूरवदनास्तथा ।मत्स्यमेषाननाश्चान्ये अजाविमहिषाननाः ॥७७॥
transliteration: ākhubabhrukavaktrāśca mayūravadanāstathā ,matsyameṣānanāścānye ajāvimahiṣānanāḥ.
english: Others had faces like mice and mongooses, and also like peacocks. Still others had faces like fish and rams, and some had faces like goats, sheep, and buffaloes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 77. Others had faces like mice and mongooses, and also like peacocks. Still others had faces like fish and rams, and some had faces like goats, sheep, and buffaloes.

sanskrit: ऋक्षशार्दूलवक्त्राश्च द्वीपिसिंहाननास्तथा ।भीमा गजाननाश्चैव तथा नक्रमुखाः परे ॥७८॥
transliteration: ṛkṣaśārdūlavaktrāśca dvīpisiṁhānanāstathā ,bhīmā gajānanāścaiva tathā nakramukhāḥ pare.
english: And others, formidable in appearance, had faces like bears and tigers, or like leopards and lions; and also like elephants, and still others had crocodile faces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 78. And others, formidable in appearance, had faces like bears and tigers, or like leopards and lions; and also like elephants, and still others had crocodile faces.

sanskrit: गरुडाननाः खड्गमुखा वृककाकमुखास्तथा ।गोखरोष्ट्रमुखाश्चान्ये वृषदंशमुखास्तथा ॥७९॥
transliteration: garuḍānanāḥ khaḍgamukhā vṛkakākamukhāstathā ,gokharoṣṭramukhāścānye vṛṣadaṁśamukhāstathā.
english: Others had faces like Garudas, or like rhinoceroses, wolves, and crows. And still others had faces like cows, donkeys, camels, and cats.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 79. Others had faces like Garudas, or like rhinoceroses, wolves, and crows. And still others had faces like cows, donkeys, camels, and cats.

sanskrit: महाजठरपादाङ्गास्तारकाक्षाश्च भारत ।पारावतमुखाश्चान्ये तथा वृषमुखाः परे ॥८०॥
transliteration: mahājaṭharapādāṅgāstārakākṣāśca bhārata ,pārāvatamukhāścānye tathā vṛṣamukhāḥ pare.
english: O Bhārata, some had huge bellies and feet as limbs, and eyes like stars. And still others had faces like pigeons, and others like bulls.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 80. O Bhārata, some had huge bellies and feet as limbs, and eyes like stars. And still others had faces like pigeons, and others like bulls.

sanskrit: कोकिलावदनाश्चान्ये श्येनतित्तिरिकाननाः ।कृकलासमुखाश्चैव विरजोम्बरधारिणः ॥८१॥
transliteration: kokilāvadanāścānye śyenatittirikānanāḥ ,kṛkalāsamukhāścaiva virajombaradhāriṇaḥ.
english: And others had faces like cuckoos, or like hawks and partridges; and still others had chameleon faces and wore spotless garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 81. And others had faces like cuckoos, or like hawks and partridges; and still others had chameleon faces and wore spotless garments.

sanskrit: व्यालवक्त्राः शूलमुखाश्चण्डवक्त्राः शताननाः ।आशीविषाश्चीरधरा गोनासावरणास्तथा ॥८२॥
transliteration: vyālavaktrāḥ śūlamukhāścaṇḍavaktrāḥ śatānanāḥ ,āśīviṣāścīradharā gonāsāvaraṇāstathā.
english: They had faces like snakes, mouths like spears, fierce countenances, and a hundred faces. They were like venomous snakes, wore garments made of bark, and had coverings resembling cow-noses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 82. They had faces like snakes, mouths like spears, fierce countenances, and a hundred faces. They were like venomous snakes, wore garments made of bark, and had coverings resembling cow-noses.

sanskrit: स्थूलोदराः कृशाङ्गाश्च स्थूलाङ्गाश्च कृशोदराः ।ह्रस्वग्रीवा महाकर्णा नानाव्यालविभूषिताः ॥८३॥
transliteration: sthūlodarāḥ kṛśāṅgāśca sthūlāṅgāśca kṛśodarāḥ ,hrasvagrīvā mahākarṇā nānāvyālavibhūṣitāḥ.
english: Some had large bellies and slender limbs, while others had stout limbs and slender bellies. They had short necks, large ears, and were adorned with various snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 83. Some had large bellies and slender limbs, while others had stout limbs and slender bellies. They had short necks, large ears, and were adorned with various snakes.

sanskrit: गजेन्द्रचर्मवसनास्तथा कृष्णाजिनाम्बराः ।स्कन्धेमुखा महाराज तथा ह्युदरतोमुखाः ॥८४॥
transliteration: gajendracarmavasanāstathā kṛṣṇājināmbarāḥ ,skandhemukhā mahārāja tathā hyudaratomukhāḥ.
english: They wore garments made of elephant hide, and likewise clothes made of black antelope skin. O great king, some had faces on their shoulders, and others had faces on their bellies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 84. They wore garments made of elephant hide, and likewise clothes made of black antelope skin. O great king, some had faces on their shoulders, and others had faces on their bellies.

sanskrit: पृष्ठेमुखा हनुमुखास्तथा जङ्घामुखा अपि ।पार्श्वाननाश्च बहवो नानादेशमुखास्तथा ॥८५॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭhemukhā hanumukhāstathā jaṅghāmukhā api ,pārśvānanāśca bahavo nānādeśamukhāstathā.
english: Some had faces on their backs, faces on their chins, and also faces on their thighs. And many had faces on their sides, as well as faces from various directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 85. Some had faces on their backs, faces on their chins, and also faces on their thighs. And many had faces on their sides, as well as faces from various directions.

sanskrit: तथा कीटपतंगानां सदृशास्या गणेश्वराः ।नानाव्यालमुखाश्चान्ये बहुबाहुशिरोधराः ॥८६॥
transliteration: tathā kīṭapataṁgānāṁ sadṛśāsyā gaṇeśvarāḥ ,nānāvyālamukhāścānye bahubāhuśirodharāḥ.
english: Similarly, there were lords of the Gaṇas whose faces resembled those of insects and birds. And others had faces of various serpents, and possessed many arms and heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 86. Similarly, there were lords of the Gaṇas whose faces resembled those of insects and birds. And others had faces of various serpents, and possessed many arms and heads.

sanskrit: नानावृक्षभुजाः केचित्कटिशीर्षास्तथापरे ।भुजंगभोगवदना नानागुल्मनिवासिनः ॥८७॥
transliteration: nānāvṛkṣabhujāḥ kecitkaṭiśīrṣāstathāpare ,bhujaṁgabhogavadanā nānāgulmanivāsinaḥ.
english: Some had various trees for arms, while others, similarly, had heads at their waists. There were also those with faces like serpent-hoods, dwelling in various thickets.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 87. Some had various trees for arms, while others, similarly, had heads at their waists. There were also those with faces like serpent-hoods, dwelling in various thickets.

sanskrit: चीरसंवृतगात्राश्च तथा फलकवाससः ।नानावेषधराश्चैव चर्मवासस एव च ॥८८॥
transliteration: cīrasaṁvṛtagātrāśca tathā phalakavāsasaḥ ,nānāveṣadharāścaiva carmavāsasa eva ca.
english: And some had their bodies covered with bark-garments, and similarly, others wore planks as clothing. Still others were adorned in various costumes, and indeed, clad in animal skins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 88. And some had their bodies covered with bark-garments, and similarly, others wore planks as clothing. Still others were adorned in various costumes, and indeed, clad in animal skins.

sanskrit: उष्णीषिणो मुकुटिनः कम्बुग्रीवाः सुवर्चसः ।किरीटिनः पञ्चशिखास्तथा कठिनमूर्धजाः ॥८९॥
transliteration: uṣṇīṣiṇo mukuṭinaḥ kambugrīvāḥ suvarcasaḥ ,kirīṭinaḥ pañcaśikhāstathā kaṭhinamūrdhajāḥ.
english: Some wore turbans, others crowns; some had conch-like necks and were radiant. Others wore diadems, had five top-knots, and similarly, stiff hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 89. Some wore turbans, others crowns; some had conch-like necks and were radiant. Others wore diadems, had five top-knots, and similarly, stiff hair.

sanskrit: त्रिशिखा द्विशिखाश्चैव तथा सप्तशिखाः परे ।शिखण्डिनो मुकुटिनो मुण्डाश्च जटिलास्तथा ॥९०॥
transliteration: triśikhā dviśikhāścaiva tathā saptaśikhāḥ pare ,śikhaṇḍino mukuṭino muṇḍāśca jaṭilāstathā.
english: Some have three tufts of hair, others two, and still others seven; some have top-knots, some wear crowns, and others are bald or have matted hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 90. Some have three tufts of hair, others two, and still others seven; some have top-knots, some wear crowns, and others are bald or have matted hair.

sanskrit: चित्रमाल्यधराः केचित्केचिद्रोमाननास्तथा ।दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधराः सततं प्रियविग्रहाः ॥९१॥
transliteration: citramālyadharāḥ kecitkecidromānanāstathā ,divyamālyāmbaradharāḥ satataṁ priyavigrahāḥ.
english: Some wear colorful garlands, others have hairy faces; and some wear divine garlands and garments, and they always possess pleasing forms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 91. Some wear colorful garlands, others have hairy faces; and some wear divine garlands and garments, and they always possess pleasing forms.

sanskrit: कृष्णा निर्मांसवक्त्राश्च दीर्घपृष्ठा निरूदराः ।स्थूलपृष्ठा ह्रस्वपृष्ठाः प्रलम्बोदरमेहनाः ॥९२॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇā nirmāṁsavaktrāśca dīrghapṛṣṭhā nirūdarāḥ ,sthūlapṛṣṭhā hrasvapṛṣṭhāḥ pralambodaramehanāḥ.
english: They are black-colored, with fleshless faces; some have long backs and no bellies, while others have thick backs, short backs, or pendulous bellies and genitals.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 92. They are black-colored, with fleshless faces; some have long backs and no bellies, while others have thick backs, short backs, or pendulous bellies and genitals.

sanskrit: महाभुजा ह्रस्वभुजा ह्रस्वगात्राश्च वामनाः ।कुब्जाश्च दीर्घजङ्घाश्च हस्तिकर्णशिरोधराः ॥९३॥
transliteration: mahābhujā hrasvabhujā hrasvagātrāśca vāmanāḥ ,kubjāśca dīrghajaṅghāśca hastikarṇaśirodharāḥ.
english: (Some have) long arms, (others) short arms, and (still others) short bodies; (some are) dwarfs, and hunchbacks; and (some have) long legs, and heads with elephant ears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 93. (Some have) long arms, (others) short arms, and (still others) short bodies; (some are) dwarfs, and hunchbacks; and (some have) long legs, and heads with elephant ears.

sanskrit: हस्तिनासाः कूर्मनासा वृकनासास्तथापरे ।दीर्घोष्ठा दीर्घजिह्वाश्च विकराला ह्यधोमुखाः ॥९४॥
transliteration: hastināsāḥ kūrmanāsā vṛkanāsāstathāpare ,dīrghoṣṭhā dīrghajihvāśca vikarālā hyadhomukhāḥ.
english: Others, indeed, had noses like elephants, noses like tortoises, or noses like wolves. They had long lips, long tongues, were hideous, and had faces turned downwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 94. Others, indeed, had noses like elephants, noses like tortoises, or noses like wolves. They had long lips, long tongues, were hideous, and had faces turned downwards.

sanskrit: महादंष्ट्रा ह्रस्वदंष्ट्राश्चतुर्दंष्ट्रास्तथापरे ।वारणेन्द्रनिभाश्चान्ये भीमा राजन्सहस्रशः ॥९५॥
transliteration: mahādaṁṣṭrā hrasvadaṁṣṭrāścaturdaṁṣṭrāstathāpare ,vāraṇendranibhāścānye bhīmā rājansahasraśaḥ.
english: O king, there were thousands of others, some with great tusks or fangs, others with short tusks or fangs, and some with four tusks or fangs. Still others resembled mighty elephants and were formidable.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 95. O king, there were thousands of others, some with great tusks or fangs, others with short tusks or fangs, and some with four tusks or fangs. Still others resembled mighty elephants and were formidable.

sanskrit: सुविभक्तशरीराश्च दीप्तिमन्तः स्वलंकृताः ।पिङ्गाक्षाः शङ्कुकर्णाश्च वक्रनासाश्च भारत ॥९६॥
transliteration: suvibhaktaśarīrāśca dīptimantaḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,piṅgākṣāḥ śaṅkukarṇāśca vakranāsāśca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, they had well-proportioned bodies, were radiant and well-adorned. They also had tawny eyes, pointed ears, and crooked noses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 96. O descendant of Bharata, they had well-proportioned bodies, were radiant and well-adorned. They also had tawny eyes, pointed ears, and crooked noses.

sanskrit: पृथुदंष्ट्रा महादंष्ट्राः स्थूलौष्ठा हरिमूर्धजाः ।नानापादौष्ठदंष्ट्राश्च नानाहस्तशिरोधराः ।नानावर्मभिराच्छन्ना नानाभाषाश्च भारत ॥९७॥
transliteration: pṛthudaṁṣṭrā mahādaṁṣṭrāḥ sthūlauṣṭhā harimūrdhajāḥ ,nānāpādauṣṭhadaṁṣṭrāśca nānāhastaśirodharāḥ ,nānāvarmabhirācchannā nānābhāṣāśca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, they had broad or great tusks/fangs, thick lips, and hair like a lion's mane. They possessed various types of feet, lips, and tusks/fangs, and different kinds of hands and heads. Covered in diverse armors, they also spoke various languages.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 97. O descendant of Bharata, they had broad or great tusks/fangs, thick lips, and hair like a lion's mane. They possessed various types of feet, lips, and tusks/fangs, and different kinds of hands and heads. Covered in diverse armors, they also spoke various languages.

sanskrit: कुशला देशभाषासु जल्पन्तोऽन्योन्यमीश्वराः ।हृष्टाः परिपतन्ति स्म महापारिषदास्तथा ॥९८॥
transliteration: kuśalā deśabhāṣāsu jalpanto'nyonyamīśvarāḥ ,hṛṣṭāḥ paripatanti sma mahāpāriṣadāstathā.
english: The powerful lords, adept in the regional languages, conversed with each other and moved about joyfully. Similarly, the chief courtiers also hurried about.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 98. The powerful lords, adept in the regional languages, conversed with each other and moved about joyfully. Similarly, the chief courtiers also hurried about.

sanskrit: दीर्घग्रीवा दीर्घनखा दीर्घपादशिरोभुजाः ।पिङ्गाक्षा नीलकण्ठाश्च लम्बकर्णाश्च भारत ॥९९॥
transliteration: dīrghagrīvā dīrghanakhā dīrghapādaśirobhujāḥ ,piṅgākṣā nīlakaṇṭhāśca lambakarṇāśca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, they had long necks, long nails, long feet, heads, and arms; with yellowish eyes, blue throats, and long ears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 99. O Bhārata, they had long necks, long nails, long feet, heads, and arms; with yellowish eyes, blue throats, and long ears.

sanskrit: वृकोदरनिभाश्चैव केचिदञ्जनसंनिभाः ।श्वेताङ्गा लोहितग्रीवाः पिङ्गाक्षाश्च तथापरे ।कल्माषा बहवो राजंश्चित्रवर्णाश्च भारत ॥१००॥
transliteration: vṛkodaranibhāścaiva kecidañjanasaṁnibhāḥ ,śvetāṅgā lohitagrīvāḥ piṅgākṣāśca tathāpare ,kalmāṣā bahavo rājaṁścitravarṇāśca bhārata.
english: O King, O Bhārata, some were indeed like Vṛkodara (Bhīma), while others were dark, like collyrium. Still others had white bodies, red necks, and yellowish eyes. Many were variegated and multi-colored.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 100. O King, O Bhārata, some were indeed like Vṛkodara (Bhīma), while others were dark, like collyrium. Still others had white bodies, red necks, and yellowish eyes. Many were variegated and multi-colored.

sanskrit: चामरापीडकनिभाः श्वेतलोहितराजयः ।नानावर्णाः सवर्णाश्च मयूरसदृशप्रभाः ॥१०१॥
transliteration: cāmarāpīḍakanibhāḥ śvetalohitarājayaḥ ,nānāvarṇāḥ savarṇāśca mayūrasadṛśaprabhāḥ.
english: They resembled decorative chowries or diadems, featuring white and red stripes. They were of various colors, and some were of uniform hue, possessing a splendor like that of peacocks.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 101. They resembled decorative chowries or diadems, featuring white and red stripes. They were of various colors, and some were of uniform hue, possessing a splendor like that of peacocks.

sanskrit: पुनः प्रहरणान्येषां कीर्त्यमानानि मे शृणु ।शेषैः कृतं पारिषदैरायुधानां परिग्रहम् ॥१०२॥
transliteration: punaḥ praharaṇānyeṣāṁ kīrtyamānāni me śṛṇu ,śeṣaiḥ kṛtaṁ pāriṣadairāyudhānāṁ parigraham.
english: Listen again to me as I describe their weapons. The remaining attendants have also taken up their arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 102. Listen again to me as I describe their weapons. The remaining attendants have also taken up their arms.

sanskrit: पाशोद्यतकराः केचिद्व्यादितास्याः खराननाः ।पृथ्वक्षा नीलकण्ठाश्च तथा परिघबाहवः ॥१०३॥
transliteration: pāśodyatakarāḥ kecidvyāditāsyāḥ kharānanāḥ ,pṛthvakṣā nīlakaṇṭhāśca tathā parighabāhavaḥ.
english: Some of them have nooses raised in their hands, wide-open mouths, and faces like donkeys. Others have broad eyes, blue throats, and arms like iron clubs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 103. Some of them have nooses raised in their hands, wide-open mouths, and faces like donkeys. Others have broad eyes, blue throats, and arms like iron clubs.

sanskrit: शतघ्नीचक्रहस्ताश्च तथा मुसलपाणयः ।शूलासिहस्ताश्च तथा महाकाया महाबलाः ॥१०४॥
transliteration: śataghnīcakrahastāśca tathā musalapāṇayaḥ ,śūlāsihastāśca tathā mahākāyā mahābalāḥ.
english: Similarly, some hold śataghnīs and discs in their hands, and others hold maces. And likewise, some carry spears and swords in their hands, possessing colossal bodies and immense strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 104. Similarly, some hold śataghnīs and discs in their hands, and others hold maces. And likewise, some carry spears and swords in their hands, possessing colossal bodies and immense strength.

sanskrit: गदाभुशुण्डिहस्ताश्च तथा तोमरपाणयः ।असिमुद्गरहस्ताश्च दण्डहस्ताश्च भारत ॥१०५॥
transliteration: gadābhuśuṇḍihastāśca tathā tomarapāṇayaḥ ,asimudgarahastāśca daṇḍahastāśca bhārata.
english: O Bharata, similarly, some carry maces and bhuśuṇḍis in their hands, while others hold javelins. Still others have swords, hammers, and staffs in their hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 105. O Bharata, similarly, some carry maces and bhuśuṇḍis in their hands, while others hold javelins. Still others have swords, hammers, and staffs in their hands.

sanskrit: आयुधैर्विविधैर्घोरैर्महात्मानो महाजवाः ।महाबला महावेगा महापारिषदास्तथा ॥१०६॥
transliteration: āyudhairvividhairghorairmahātmāno mahājavāḥ ,mahābalā mahāvegā mahāpāriṣadāstathā.
english: Great-souled, exceedingly swift, immensely strong, and mighty-speeded great attendants (mahāpāriṣadāḥ) were also present, equipped with various dreadful weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 106. Great-souled, exceedingly swift, immensely strong, and mighty-speeded great attendants (mahāpāriṣadāḥ) were also present, equipped with various dreadful weapons.

sanskrit: अभिषेकं कुमारस्य दृष्ट्वा हृष्टा रणप्रियाः ।घण्टाजालपिनद्धाङ्गा ननृतुस्ते महौजसः ॥१०७॥
transliteration: abhiṣekaṁ kumārasya dṛṣṭvā hṛṣṭā raṇapriyāḥ ,ghaṇṭājālapinaddhāṅgā nanṛtuste mahaujasaḥ.
english: Having witnessed the consecration of the prince (Kumāra), those mighty (mahaujasaḥ) and battle-loving attendants, whose bodies were adorned with networks of bells, danced with joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 107. Having witnessed the consecration of the prince (Kumāra), those mighty (mahaujasaḥ) and battle-loving attendants, whose bodies were adorned with networks of bells, danced with joy.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो महापारिषदा नृप ।उपतस्थुर्महात्मानं कार्त्तिकेयं यशस्विनम् ॥१०८॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo mahāpāriṣadā nṛpa ,upatasthurmahātmānaṁ kārttikeyaṁ yaśasvinam.
english: O king, these and many other great attendants (mahāpāriṣadāḥ) stood by the glorious and great-souled (mahātman) Kartikeya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 108. O king, these and many other great attendants (mahāpāriṣadāḥ) stood by the glorious and great-souled (mahātman) Kartikeya.

sanskrit: दिव्याश्चाप्यान्तरिक्षाश्च पार्थिवाश्चानिलोपमाः ।व्यादिष्टा दैवतैः शूराः स्कन्दस्यानुचराभवन् ॥१०९॥
transliteration: divyāścāpyāntarikṣāśca pārthivāścānilopamāḥ ,vyādiṣṭā daivataiḥ śūrāḥ skandasyānucarābhavan.
english: Moreover, celestial, aerial, and terrestrial beings, swift as the wind, who were brave and commanded by the gods, became the followers of Skanda.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 109. Moreover, celestial, aerial, and terrestrial beings, swift as the wind, who were brave and commanded by the gods, became the followers of Skanda.

sanskrit: तादृशानां सहस्राणि प्रयुतान्यर्बुदानि च ।अभिषिक्तं महात्मानं परिवार्योपतस्थिरे ॥११०॥
transliteration: tādṛśānāṁ sahasrāṇi prayutānyarbudāni ca ,abhiṣiktaṁ mahātmānaṁ parivāryopatasthire.
english: Thousands, millions, and even hundreds of millions of such beings surrounded the consecrated great soul (ātman) and stood in attendance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 44 Verse 110. Thousands, millions, and even hundreds of millions of such beings surrounded the consecrated great soul (ātman) and stood in attendance.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।शृणु मातृगणान्राजन्कुमारानुचरानिमान् ।कीर्त्यमानान्मया वीर सपत्नगणसूदनान् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śṛṇu mātṛgaṇānrājankumārānucarānimān ,kīrtyamānānmayā vīra sapatnagaṇasūdanān.
english: Vaiśampaayana said: "O King, O hero, listen as I recount these groups of mothers, followers of Kumāra, who destroy hordes of enemies."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 1. Vaiśampaayana said: "O King, O hero, listen as I recount these groups of mothers, followers of Kumāra, who destroy hordes of enemies."

sanskrit: यशस्विनीनां मातॄणां शृणु नामानि भारत ।याभिर्व्याप्तास्त्रयो लोकाः कल्याणीभिश्चराचराः ॥२॥
transliteration: yaśasvinīnāṁ mātṝṇāṁ śṛṇu nāmāni bhārata ,yābhirvyāptāstrayo lokāḥ kalyāṇībhiścarācarāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, listen to the names of these glorious mothers, by whom, these auspicious ones, the three worlds, both moving and unmoving, are pervaded.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 2. O Bhārata, listen to the names of these glorious mothers, by whom, these auspicious ones, the three worlds, both moving and unmoving, are pervaded.

sanskrit: प्रभावती विशालाक्षी पलिता गोनसी तथा ।श्रीमती बहुला चैव तथैव बहुपुत्रिका ॥३॥
transliteration: prabhāvatī viśālākṣī palitā gonasī tathā ,śrīmatī bahulā caiva tathaiva bahuputrikā.
english: These mothers include Prabhāvatī, Viśālākṣī, Palitā, and Gonasī; similarly, Śrīmatī, Bahulā, and also Bahuputrikā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 3. These mothers include Prabhāvatī, Viśālākṣī, Palitā, and Gonasī; similarly, Śrīmatī, Bahulā, and also Bahuputrikā.

sanskrit: अप्सुजाता च गोपाली बृहदम्बालिका तथा ।जयावती मालतिका ध्रुवरत्ना भयंकरी ॥४॥
transliteration: apsujātā ca gopālī bṛhadambālikā tathā ,jayāvatī mālatikā dhruvaratnā bhayaṁkarī.
english: Apsujātā (born in water), and Gopālī, as well as Bṛhadambālikā; also Jayāvatī, Mālatikā, Dhruvaratnā, and Bhayaṅkarī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 4. Apsujātā (born in water), and Gopālī, as well as Bṛhadambālikā; also Jayāvatī, Mālatikā, Dhruvaratnā, and Bhayaṅkarī.

sanskrit: वसुदामा सुदामा च विशोका नन्दिनी तथा ।एकचूडा महाचूडा चक्रनेमिश्च भारत ॥५॥
transliteration: vasudāmā sudāmā ca viśokā nandinī tathā ,ekacūḍā mahācūḍā cakranemiśca bhārata.
english: Vasudāmā, and Sudāmā, and Viśokā, and Nandinī also; Ekacūḍā, Mahācūḍā, and Cakranemi, O descendant of Bharata!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 5. Vasudāmā, and Sudāmā, and Viśokā, and Nandinī also; Ekacūḍā, Mahācūḍā, and Cakranemi, O descendant of Bharata!

sanskrit: उत्तेजनी जयत्सेना कमलाक्ष्यथ शोभना ।शत्रुंजया तथा चैव क्रोधना शलभी खरी ॥६॥
transliteration: uttejanī jayatsenā kamalākṣyatha śobhanā ,śatruṁjayā tathā caiva krodhanā śalabhī kharī.
english: Uttejanī, Jayatsenā, Kamalākṣī, and also Śobhanā; Śatruñjayā, and indeed Krodhanā, Śalabhī, and Kharī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 6. Uttejanī, Jayatsenā, Kamalākṣī, and also Śobhanā; Śatruñjayā, and indeed Krodhanā, Śalabhī, and Kharī.

sanskrit: माधवी शुभवक्त्रा च तीर्थनेमिश्च भारत ।गीतप्रिया च कल्याणी कद्रुला चामिताशना ॥७॥
transliteration: mādhavī śubhavaktrā ca tīrthanemiśca bhārata ,gītapriyā ca kalyāṇī kadrulā cāmitāśanā.
english: Mādhavī, Śubhavaktrā, and Tīrthanemi, O descendant of Bharata; and Gītapriyā, Kalyāṇī, Kadrulā, and Amitāśanā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 7. Mādhavī, Śubhavaktrā, and Tīrthanemi, O descendant of Bharata; and Gītapriyā, Kalyāṇī, Kadrulā, and Amitāśanā.

sanskrit: मेघस्वना भोगवती सुभ्रूश्च कनकावती ।अलाताक्षी वीर्यवती विद्युज्जिह्वा च भारत ॥८॥
transliteration: meghasvanā bhogavatī subhrūśca kanakāvatī ,alātākṣī vīryavatī vidyujjihvā ca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, (there were also) Meghasvanā, Bhogavatī, Subhrū, Kanakāvatī, Alātākṣī, Vīryavatī, and Vidyujjihvā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 8. O descendant of Bharata, (there were also) Meghasvanā, Bhogavatī, Subhrū, Kanakāvatī, Alātākṣī, Vīryavatī, and Vidyujjihvā.

sanskrit: पद्मावती सुनक्षत्रा कन्दरा बहुयोजना ।संतानिका च कौरव्य कमला च महाबला ॥९॥
transliteration: padmāvatī sunakṣatrā kandarā bahuyojanā ,saṁtānikā ca kauravya kamalā ca mahābalā.
english: O descendant of Kuru, (they were also) Padmāvatī, Sunakṣatrā, Kandarā, Bahuyojanā, Saṃtānikā, Kamalā, and Mahābalā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 9. O descendant of Kuru, (they were also) Padmāvatī, Sunakṣatrā, Kandarā, Bahuyojanā, Saṃtānikā, Kamalā, and Mahābalā.

sanskrit: सुदामा बहुदामा च सुप्रभा च यशस्विनी ।नृत्यप्रिया च राजेन्द्र शतोलूखलमेखला ॥१०॥
transliteration: sudāmā bahudāmā ca suprabhā ca yaśasvinī ,nṛtyapriyā ca rājendra śatolūkhalamekhalā.
english: O chief of kings, (they were also) Sudāmā, Bahudāmā, Suprabhā, Yaśasvinī, Nṛtyapriyā, and Śatoulūkhalamekhalā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 10. O chief of kings, (they were also) Sudāmā, Bahudāmā, Suprabhā, Yaśasvinī, Nṛtyapriyā, and Śatoulūkhalamekhalā.

sanskrit: शतघण्टा शतानन्दा भगनन्दा च भामिनी ।वपुष्मती चन्द्रशीता भद्रकाली च भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: śataghaṇṭā śatānandā bhaganandā ca bhāminī ,vapuṣmatī candraśītā bhadrakālī ca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, (they were also) Śataghaṇṭā, Śatānandā, Bhaganandā, Bhāminī, Vapuṣmatī, Candraśītā, and Bhadrakālī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 11. O descendant of Bharata, (they were also) Śataghaṇṭā, Śatānandā, Bhaganandā, Bhāminī, Vapuṣmatī, Candraśītā, and Bhadrakālī.

sanskrit: संकारिका निष्कुटिका भ्रमा चत्वरवासिनी ।सुमङ्गला स्वस्तिमती वृद्धिकामा जयप्रिया ॥१२॥
transliteration: saṁkārikā niṣkuṭikā bhramā catvaravāsinī ,sumaṅgalā svastimatī vṛddhikāmā jayapriyā.
english: Saṃkārikā, Niṣkuṭikā, Bhramā, and Catvaravāsinī (Dweller of Crossroads); Sumaṅgalā (Very Auspicious), Svastimatī (Possessing Welfare), Vṛddhikāmā (Desiring Growth), and Jayapriyā (Fond of Victory).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 12. Saṃkārikā, Niṣkuṭikā, Bhramā, and Catvaravāsinī (Dweller of Crossroads); Sumaṅgalā (Very Auspicious), Svastimatī (Possessing Welfare), Vṛddhikāmā (Desiring Growth), and Jayapriyā (Fond of Victory).

sanskrit: धनदा सुप्रसादा च भवदा च जलेश्वरी ।एडी भेडी समेडी च वेतालजननी तथा ।कण्डूतिः कालिका चैव देवमित्रा च भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: dhanadā suprasādā ca bhavadā ca jaleśvarī ,eḍī bheḍī sameḍī ca vetālajananī tathā ,kaṇḍūtiḥ kālikā caiva devamitrā ca bhārata.
english: Also, Dhanadā (Bestower of Wealth), Suprasādā (Very Gracious), and Bhavadā (Bestower of Existence), and Jaleśvarī (Mistress of Water); Eḍī, Bheḍī, and Sameḍī, and also Vetālajanani (Mother of Vetālas); and indeed Kaṇḍūti, Kālikā, and Devamitrā (Friend of the Gods), O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 13. Also, Dhanadā (Bestower of Wealth), Suprasādā (Very Gracious), and Bhavadā (Bestower of Existence), and Jaleśvarī (Mistress of Water); Eḍī, Bheḍī, and Sameḍī, and also Vetālajanani (Mother of Vetālas); and indeed Kaṇḍūti, Kālikā, and Devamitrā (Friend of the Gods), O Bhārata.

sanskrit: लम्बसी केतकी चैव चित्रसेना तथा बला ।कुक्कुटिका शङ्खनिका तथा जर्जरिका नृप ॥१४॥
transliteration: lambasī ketakī caiva citrasenā tathā balā ,kukkuṭikā śaṅkhanikā tathā jarjarikā nṛpa.
english: And indeed Lambasī, Ketakī, Citrasenā (Having a Variegated Army), and also Balā (Strength); Kukkuṭikā, Śaṅkhanikā, and also Jarjarikā (Decrepit One), O King.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 14. And indeed Lambasī, Ketakī, Citrasenā (Having a Variegated Army), and also Balā (Strength); Kukkuṭikā, Śaṅkhanikā, and also Jarjarikā (Decrepit One), O King.

sanskrit: कुण्डारिका कोकलिका कण्डरा च शतोदरी ।उत्क्राथिनी जरेणा च महावेगा च कङ्कणा ॥१५॥
transliteration: kuṇḍārikā kokalikā kaṇḍarā ca śatodarī ,utkrāthinī jareṇā ca mahāvegā ca kaṅkaṇā.
english: Kuṇḍārikā, Kokalikā, Kaṇḍarā, and Śatodarī (Having a Hundred Bellies); Utkrāthinī, Jareṇā, and also Mahāvegā (Of Great Speed), and Kaṅkaṇā (Bangle).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 15. Kuṇḍārikā, Kokalikā, Kaṇḍarā, and Śatodarī (Having a Hundred Bellies); Utkrāthinī, Jareṇā, and also Mahāvegā (Of Great Speed), and Kaṅkaṇā (Bangle).

sanskrit: मनोजवा कण्टकिनी प्रघसा पूतना तथा ।खशया चुर्व्युटिर्वामा क्रोशनाथ तडित्प्रभा ॥१६॥
transliteration: manojavā kaṇṭakinī praghasā pūtanā tathā ,khaśayā curvyuṭirvāmā krośanātha taḍitprabhā.
english: Manojava, Kaṇṭakinī, Praghasā, Pūtanā, and similarly, Khaśayā, Curvyuṭi, Vāmā, Krośanā, and then Taḍitprabhā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 16. Manojava, Kaṇṭakinī, Praghasā, Pūtanā, and similarly, Khaśayā, Curvyuṭi, Vāmā, Krośanā, and then Taḍitprabhā.

sanskrit: मण्डोदरी च तुण्डा च कोटरा मेघवासिनी ।सुभगा लम्बिनी लम्बा वसुचूडा विकत्थनी ॥१७॥
transliteration: maṇḍodarī ca tuṇḍā ca koṭarā meghavāsinī ,subhagā lambinī lambā vasucūḍā vikatthanī.
english: Maṇḍodarī, Tuṇḍā, Koṭarā, Meghavāsinī; Subhagā, Lambinī, Lambā, Vasucūḍā, and Vikatthanī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 17. Maṇḍodarī, Tuṇḍā, Koṭarā, Meghavāsinī; Subhagā, Lambinī, Lambā, Vasucūḍā, and Vikatthanī.

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्ववेणीधरा चैव पिङ्गाक्षी लोहमेखला ।पृथुवक्त्रा मधुरिका मधुकुम्भा तथैव च ॥१८॥
transliteration: ūrdhvaveṇīdharā caiva piṅgākṣī lohamekhalā ,pṛthuvaktrā madhurikā madhukumbhā tathaiva ca.
english: Ūrdhvaverīdharā, and also Piṅgākṣī and Lohamekhālā; Pṛthuvaktrā, Madhurikā, and Madhukumbhā, likewise.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 18. Ūrdhvaverīdharā, and also Piṅgākṣī and Lohamekhālā; Pṛthuvaktrā, Madhurikā, and Madhukumbhā, likewise.

sanskrit: पक्षालिका मन्थनिका जरायुर्जर्जरानना ।ख्याता दहदहा चैव तथा धमधमा नृप ॥१९॥
transliteration: pakṣālikā manthanikā jarāyurjarjarānanā ,khyātā dahadahā caiva tathā dhamadhamā nṛpa.
english: Pakṣālikā, Manthanikā, Jarāyu, and Jarjarānanā; and also the renowned Dahadahā and Dhamadhamā, O King.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 19. Pakṣālikā, Manthanikā, Jarāyu, and Jarjarānanā; and also the renowned Dahadahā and Dhamadhamā, O King.

sanskrit: खण्डखण्डा च राजेन्द्र पूषणा मणिकुण्डला ।अमोचा चैव कौरव्य तथा लम्बपयोधरा ॥२०॥
transliteration: khaṇḍakhaṇḍā ca rājendra pūṣaṇā maṇikuṇḍalā ,amocā caiva kauravya tathā lambapayodharā.
english: O King (rājendra), there is one who is fragmented, and (another named) Pūṣaṇā, adorned with jeweled earrings. O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), likewise, there is (one named) Amocā and (one with) pendulous breasts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 20. O King (rājendra), there is one who is fragmented, and (another named) Pūṣaṇā, adorned with jeweled earrings. O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), likewise, there is (one named) Amocā and (one with) pendulous breasts.

sanskrit: वेणुवीणाधरा चैव पिङ्गाक्षी लोहमेखला ।शशोलूकमुखी कृष्णा खरजङ्घा महाजवा ॥२१॥
transliteration: veṇuvīṇādharā caiva piṅgākṣī lohamekhalā ,śaśolūkamukhī kṛṣṇā kharajaṅghā mahājavā.
english: And indeed, (there is one) holding a flute and a lyre, with tawny eyes, and wearing an iron girdle. (There is also one) with the face of a rabbit and an owl, dark-complexioned, with donkey-like shanks, and of great speed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 21. And indeed, (there is one) holding a flute and a lyre, with tawny eyes, and wearing an iron girdle. (There is also one) with the face of a rabbit and an owl, dark-complexioned, with donkey-like shanks, and of great speed.

sanskrit: शिशुमारमुखी श्वेता लोहिताक्षी विभीषणा ।जटालिका कामचरी दीर्घजिह्वा बलोत्कटा ॥२२॥
transliteration: śiśumāramukhī śvetā lohitākṣī vibhīṣaṇā ,jaṭālikā kāmacarī dīrghajihvā balotkaṭā.
english: (There is one) with the face of a porpoise, white-complexioned, with red eyes, and terrifying. (There is also one) with matted locks, moving at will, with a long tongue, and exceedingly powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 22. (There is one) with the face of a porpoise, white-complexioned, with red eyes, and terrifying. (There is also one) with matted locks, moving at will, with a long tongue, and exceedingly powerful.

sanskrit: कालेडिका वामनिका मुकुटा चैव भारत ।लोहिताक्षी महाकाया हरिपिण्डी च भूमिप ॥२३॥
transliteration: kāleḍikā vāmanikā mukuṭā caiva bhārata ,lohitākṣī mahākāyā haripiṇḍī ca bhūmipa.
english: O descendant of Bharata, indeed, (there is) Kāleḍikā, dwarfish, and Mukuṭā (crowned). O protector of the earth, (there is one) with red eyes, of huge body, and with a lion's body/form.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 23. O descendant of Bharata, indeed, (there is) Kāleḍikā, dwarfish, and Mukuṭā (crowned). O protector of the earth, (there is one) with red eyes, of huge body, and with a lion's body/form.

sanskrit: एकाक्षरा सुकुसुमा कृष्णकर्णी च भारत ।क्षुरकर्णी चतुष्कर्णी कर्णप्रावरणा तथा ॥२४॥
transliteration: ekākṣarā sukusumā kṛṣṇakarṇī ca bhārata ,kṣurakarṇī catuṣkarṇī karṇaprāvaraṇā tathā.
english: O Bhārata, she is one-syllable, adorned with beautiful flowers, black-eared, and similarly, razor-eared, four-eared, and having covered ears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 24. O Bhārata, she is one-syllable, adorned with beautiful flowers, black-eared, and similarly, razor-eared, four-eared, and having covered ears.

sanskrit: चतुष्पथनिकेता च गोकर्णी महिषानना ।खरकर्णी महाकर्णी भेरीस्वनमहास्वना ॥२५॥
transliteration: catuṣpathaniketā ca gokarṇī mahiṣānanā ,kharakarṇī mahākarṇī bherīsvanamahāsvanā.
english: And residing at crossroads, cow-eared, buffalo-faced, donkey-eared, large-eared, and having a great sound like a war-drum.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 25. And residing at crossroads, cow-eared, buffalo-faced, donkey-eared, large-eared, and having a great sound like a war-drum.

sanskrit: शङ्खकुम्भस्वना चैव भङ्गदा च महाबला ।गणा च सुगणा चैव तथाभीत्यथ कामदा ॥२६॥
transliteration: śaṅkhakumbhasvanā caiva bhaṅgadā ca mahābalā ,gaṇā ca sugaṇā caiva tathābhītyatha kāmadā.
english: She has the sound of a conch and a pot, and indeed causes destruction, and is greatly powerful; she belongs to a group, and has a good group, and similarly fearless, and then grants desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 26. She has the sound of a conch and a pot, and indeed causes destruction, and is greatly powerful; she belongs to a group, and has a good group, and similarly fearless, and then grants desires.

sanskrit: चतुष्पथरता चैव भूतितीर्थान्यगोचरा ।पशुदा वित्तदा चैव सुखदा च महायशाः ।पयोदा गोमहिषदा सुविषाणा च भारत ॥२७॥
transliteration: catuṣpatharatā caiva bhūtitīrthānyagocarā ,paśudā vittadā caiva sukhadā ca mahāyaśāḥ ,payodā gomahiṣadā suviṣāṇā ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, and indeed delighting in crossroads, whose domain extends to places of well-being (bhūti), holy sites (tīrtha), and other regions, granter of cattle, granter of wealth, and indeed granter of happiness, and greatly famous; she is also granter of milk, granter of cows and buffaloes, and well-horned.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 27. O Bhārata, and indeed delighting in crossroads, whose domain extends to places of well-being (bhūti), holy sites (tīrtha), and other regions, granter of cattle, granter of wealth, and indeed granter of happiness, and greatly famous; she is also granter of milk, granter of cows and buffaloes, and well-horned.

sanskrit: प्रतिष्ठा सुप्रतिष्ठा च रोचमाना सुरोचना ।गोकर्णी च सुकर्णी च ससिरा स्थेरिका तथा ।एकचक्रा मेघरवा मेघमाला विरोचना ॥२८॥
transliteration: pratiṣṭhā supratiṣṭhā ca rocamānā surocanā ,gokarṇī ca sukarṇī ca sasirā stherikā tathā ,ekacakrā megharavā meghamālā virocanā.
english: Pratiṣṭhā, Supratiṣṭhā, Rocamānā, Surocanā, Gokārṇī, Sukarṇī, Sasirā, Stherikā, Ekacakrā, Megharavā, Meghamālā, and Virocanā (are among them).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 28. Pratiṣṭhā, Supratiṣṭhā, Rocamānā, Surocanā, Gokārṇī, Sukarṇī, Sasirā, Stherikā, Ekacakrā, Megharavā, Meghamālā, and Virocanā (are among them).

sanskrit: एताश्चान्याश्च बहवो मातरो भरतर्षभ ।कार्त्तिकेयानुयायिन्यो नानारूपाः सहस्रशः ॥२९॥
transliteration: etāścānyāśca bahavo mātaro bharatarṣabha ,kārttikeyānuyāyinyo nānārūpāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas, these and many other mothers (mātaraḥ), followers of Kārttikeya, exist in thousands and possess various forms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 29. O best among the Bharatas, these and many other mothers (mātaraḥ), followers of Kārttikeya, exist in thousands and possess various forms.

sanskrit: दीर्घनख्यो दीर्घदन्त्यो दीर्घतुण्ड्यश्च भारत ।सरला मधुराश्चैव यौवनस्थाः स्वलंकृताः ॥३०॥
transliteration: dīrghanakhyo dīrghadantyo dīrghatuṇḍyaśca bhārata ,saralā madhurāścaiva yauvanasthāḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, they are long-clawed, long-toothed, long-snouted, and also simple, charming, youthful, and well-adorned.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 30. O Bhārata, they are long-clawed, long-toothed, long-snouted, and also simple, charming, youthful, and well-adorned.

sanskrit: माहात्म्येन च संयुक्ताः कामरूपधरास्तथा ।निर्मांसगात्र्यः श्वेताश्च तथा काञ्चनसंनिभाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: māhātmyena ca saṁyuktāḥ kāmarūpadharāstathā ,nirmāṁsagātryaḥ śvetāśca tathā kāñcanasaṁnibhāḥ.
english: They are endowed with great majesty, capable of assuming any form at will, and appear with fleshless white bodies, or similarly, gleaming like gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 31. They are endowed with great majesty, capable of assuming any form at will, and appear with fleshless white bodies, or similarly, gleaming like gold.

sanskrit: कृष्णमेघनिभाश्चान्या धूम्राश्च भरतर्षभ ।अरुणाभा महाभागा दीर्घकेश्यः सिताम्बराः ॥३२॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇameghanibhāścānyā dhūmrāśca bharatarṣabha ,aruṇābhā mahābhāgā dīrghakeśyaḥ sitāmbarāḥ.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, there were others resembling dark clouds, and some were smoky-grey. Others had a reddish glow, were greatly fortunate, long-haired, and dressed in white garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 32. O best among the Bhāratas, there were others resembling dark clouds, and some were smoky-grey. Others had a reddish glow, were greatly fortunate, long-haired, and dressed in white garments.

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्ववेणीधराश्चैव पिङ्गाक्ष्यो लम्बमेखलाः ।लम्बोदर्यो लम्बकर्णास्तथा लम्बपयोधराः ॥३३॥
transliteration: ūrdhvaveṇīdharāścaiva piṅgākṣyo lambamekhalāḥ ,lambodaryo lambakarṇāstathā lambapayodharāḥ.
english: And indeed, there were others with braided hair piled upwards, tawny eyes, long or hanging girdles, pendulous bellies, long ears, and also pendulous breasts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 33. And indeed, there were others with braided hair piled upwards, tawny eyes, long or hanging girdles, pendulous bellies, long ears, and also pendulous breasts.

sanskrit: ताम्राक्ष्यस्ताम्रवर्णाश्च हर्यक्ष्यश्च तथापराः ।वरदाः कामचारिण्यो नित्यप्रमुदितास्तथा ॥३४॥
transliteration: tāmrākṣyastāmravarṇāśca haryakṣyaśca tathāparāḥ ,varadāḥ kāmacāriṇyo nityapramuditāstathā.
english: Some had coppery eyes and coppery complexions, and others had greenish-yellow eyes. They granted boons, moved about freely, and were always joyous.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 34. Some had coppery eyes and coppery complexions, and others had greenish-yellow eyes. They granted boons, moved about freely, and were always joyous.

sanskrit: याम्यो रौद्र्यस्तथा सौम्याः कौबेर्योऽथ महाबलाः ।वारुण्योऽथ च माहेन्द्र्यस्तथाग्नेय्यः परंतप ॥३५॥
transliteration: yāmyo raudryastathā saumyāḥ kauberyo'tha mahābalāḥ ,vāruṇyo'tha ca māhendryastathāgneyyaḥ paraṁtapa.
english: O tormentor of foes, there were also those belonging to Yama, those belonging to Rudra, and those belonging to Soma. Then there were the immensely powerful ones associated with Kubera, and also those of Varuṇa, and Mahendra, and Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 35. O tormentor of foes, there were also those belonging to Yama, those belonging to Rudra, and those belonging to Soma. Then there were the immensely powerful ones associated with Kubera, and also those of Varuṇa, and Mahendra, and Agni.

sanskrit: वायव्यश्चाथ कौमार्यो ब्राह्म्यश्च भरतर्षभ ।रूपेणाप्सरसां तुल्या जवे वायुसमास्तथा ॥३६॥
transliteration: vāyavyaścātha kaumāryo brāhmyaśca bharatarṣabha ,rūpeṇāpsarasāṁ tulyā jave vāyusamāstathā.
english: O best among Bharatas, these (beings) are associated with Vayu, Kumara, and Brahma. In beauty, they are like Apsaras, and similarly, in speed, they are like the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 36. O best among Bharatas, these (beings) are associated with Vayu, Kumara, and Brahma. In beauty, they are like Apsaras, and similarly, in speed, they are like the wind.

sanskrit: परपुष्टोपमा वाक्ये तथर्द्ध्या धनदोपमाः ।शक्रवीर्योपमाश्चैव दीप्त्या वह्निसमास्तथा ॥३७॥
transliteration: parapuṣṭopamā vākye tatharddhyā dhanadopamāḥ ,śakravīryopamāścaiva dīptyā vahnisamāstathā.
english: Moreover, they are sweet-voiced like cuckoos, and in prosperity, they are like Kubera. Indeed, in valor they resemble Indra's might, and in brilliance, they are like fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 37. Moreover, they are sweet-voiced like cuckoos, and in prosperity, they are like Kubera. Indeed, in valor they resemble Indra's might, and in brilliance, they are like fire.

sanskrit: वृक्षचत्वरवासिन्यश्चतुष्पथनिकेतनाः ।गुहाश्मशानवासिन्यः शैलप्रस्रवणालयाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: vṛkṣacatvaravāsinyaścatuṣpathaniketanāḥ ,guhāśmaśānavāsinyaḥ śailaprasravaṇālayāḥ.
english: They are residents of trees and courtyards, and their abodes are crossroads. They dwell in caves and cremation grounds, and mountain springs serve as their homes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 38. They are residents of trees and courtyards, and their abodes are crossroads. They dwell in caves and cremation grounds, and mountain springs serve as their homes.

sanskrit: नानाभरणधारिण्यो नानामाल्याम्बरास्तथा ।नानाविचित्रवेषाश्च नानाभाषास्तथैव च ॥३९॥
transliteration: nānābharaṇadhāriṇyo nānāmālyāmbarāstathā ,nānāvicitraveṣāśca nānābhāṣāstathaiva ca.
english: They wear diverse ornaments and are adorned with various garlands and garments. Moreover, they possess various wonderful attires and also speak many different languages.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 39. They wear diverse ornaments and are adorned with various garlands and garments. Moreover, they possess various wonderful attires and also speak many different languages.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो गणाः शत्रुभयंकराः ।अनुजग्मुर्महात्मानं त्रिदशेन्द्रस्य संमते ॥४०॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo gaṇāḥ śatrubhayaṁkarāḥ ,anujagmurmahātmānaṁ tridaśendrasya saṁmate.
english: These and many other hosts, terrifying to their enemies, followed the great-souled one with the consent of the lord of the gods (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 40. These and many other hosts, terrifying to their enemies, followed the great-souled one with the consent of the lord of the gods (Indra).

sanskrit: ततः शक्त्यस्त्रमददद्भगवान्पाकशासनः ।गुहाय राजशार्दूल विनाशाय सुरद्विषाम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaktyastramadadadbhagavānpākaśāsanaḥ ,guhāya rājaśārdūla vināśāya suradviṣām.
english: Then, O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), the divine Indra, the slayer of Pāka, gave the "śakti" weapon to Guha for the destruction of the enemies of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 41. Then, O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), the divine Indra, the slayer of Pāka, gave the "śakti" weapon to Guha for the destruction of the enemies of the gods.

sanskrit: महास्वनां महाघण्टां द्योतमानां सितप्रभाम् ।तरुणादित्यवर्णां च पताकां भरतर्षभ ॥४२॥
transliteration: mahāsvanāṁ mahāghaṇṭāṁ dyotamānāṁ sitaprabhām ,taruṇādityavarṇāṁ ca patākāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), [he gave him] a flag that possessed a mighty sound, a great bell, was radiant with white brilliance, and had the color of the rising sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 42. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), [he gave him] a flag that possessed a mighty sound, a great bell, was radiant with white brilliance, and had the color of the rising sun.

sanskrit: ददौ पशुपतिस्तस्मै सर्वभूतमहाचमूम् ।उग्रां नानाप्रहरणां तपोवीर्यबलान्विताम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: dadau paśupatistasmai sarvabhūtamahācamūm ,ugrāṁ nānāpraharaṇāṁ tapovīryabalānvitām.
english: Pashupati (Śiva) gave to him (Guha) a great army composed of all beings, which was fierce, equipped with various weapons, and endowed with ascetic power (tapas) and strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 43. Pashupati (Śiva) gave to him (Guha) a great army composed of all beings, which was fierce, equipped with various weapons, and endowed with ascetic power (tapas) and strength.

sanskrit: विष्णुर्ददौ वैजयन्तीं मालां बलविवर्धिनीम् ।उमा ददौ चारजसी वाससी सूर्यसप्रभे ॥४४॥
transliteration: viṣṇurdadau vaijayantīṁ mālāṁ balavivardhinīm ,umā dadau cārajasī vāsasī sūryasaprabhe.
english: Viṣṇu gave him the Vaijayantī garland, which increases strength. Umā also gave two spotless garments, radiant like the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 44. Viṣṇu gave him the Vaijayantī garland, which increases strength. Umā also gave two spotless garments, radiant like the sun.

sanskrit: गङ्गा कमण्डलुं दिव्यममृतोद्भवमुत्तमम् ।ददौ प्रीत्या कुमाराय दण्डं चैव बृहस्पतिः ॥४५॥
transliteration: gaṅgā kamaṇḍaluṁ divyamamṛtodbhavamuttamam ,dadau prītyā kumārāya daṇḍaṁ caiva bṛhaspatiḥ.
english: Gaṅgā gave a divine, excellent water-pot (kamaṇḍalu), which arose from nectar. And Bṛhaspati, with affection, gave a staff to the Kumāra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 45. Gaṅgā gave a divine, excellent water-pot (kamaṇḍalu), which arose from nectar. And Bṛhaspati, with affection, gave a staff to the Kumāra.

sanskrit: गरुडो दयितं पुत्रं मयूरं चित्रबर्हिणम् ।अरुणस्ताम्रचूडं च प्रददौ चरणायुधम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: garuḍo dayitaṁ putraṁ mayūraṁ citrabarhiṇam ,aruṇastāmracūḍaṁ ca pradadau caraṇāyudham.
english: Garuḍa gave his beloved son, the peacock Citrabarhiṇa (meaning 'having variegated tail feathers'). And Aruṇa gave Tāmracūḍa (the cock, whose 'weapons' are his feet).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 46. Garuḍa gave his beloved son, the peacock Citrabarhiṇa (meaning 'having variegated tail feathers'). And Aruṇa gave Tāmracūḍa (the cock, whose 'weapons' are his feet).

sanskrit: पाशं तु वरुणो राजा बलवीर्यसमन्वितम् ।कृष्णाजिनं तथा ब्रह्मा ब्रह्मण्याय ददौ प्रभुः ।समरेषु जयं चैव प्रददौ लोकभावनः ॥४७॥
transliteration: pāśaṁ tu varuṇo rājā balavīryasamanvitam ,kṛṣṇājinaṁ tathā brahmā brahmaṇyāya dadau prabhuḥ ,samareṣu jayaṁ caiva pradadau lokabhāvanaḥ.
english: But King Varuṇa gave a noose (pāśa) endowed with strength and valor. Likewise, the lord Brahmā gave a black antelope skin to Brahmanya. And the creator of worlds (lokabhāvanaḥ) also gave victory in battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 47. But King Varuṇa gave a noose (pāśa) endowed with strength and valor. Likewise, the lord Brahmā gave a black antelope skin to Brahmanya. And the creator of worlds (lokabhāvanaḥ) also gave victory in battles.

sanskrit: सेनापत्यमनुप्राप्य स्कन्दो देवगणस्य ह ।शुशुभे ज्वलितोऽर्चिष्मान्द्वितीय इव पावकः ।ततः पारिषदैश्चैव मातृभिश्च समन्वितः ॥४८॥
transliteration: senāpatyamanuprāpya skando devagaṇasya ha ,śuśubhe jvalito'rciṣmāndvitīya iva pāvakaḥ ,tataḥ pāriṣadaiścaiva mātṛbhiśca samanvitaḥ.
