english: Having obtained the leadership of the army of the host of gods, Skanda shone, blazing and radiant, like a second fire. Thereafter, he was accompanied by his attendants and also by the mothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 48. Having obtained the leadership of the army of the host of gods, Skanda shone, blazing and radiant, like a second fire. Thereafter, he was accompanied by his attendants and also by the mothers.

sanskrit: सा सेना नैरृती भीमा सघण्टोच्छ्रितकेतना ।सभेरीशङ्खमुरजा सायुधा सपताकिनी ।शारदी द्यौरिवाभाति ज्योतिर्भिरुपशोभिता ॥४९॥
transliteration: sā senā nairṛtī bhīmā saghaṇṭocchritaketanā ,sabherīśaṅkhamurajā sāyudhā sapatākinī ,śāradī dyaurivābhāti jyotirbhirupaśobhitā.
english: That terrible, demonic army, with its upraised banners and bells, with its drums, conches, and tabors, armed with weapons, and with its standards, shone like an autumnal sky adorned with stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 49. That terrible, demonic army, with its upraised banners and bells, with its drums, conches, and tabors, armed with weapons, and with its standards, shone like an autumnal sky adorned with stars.

sanskrit: ततो देवनिकायास्ते भूतसेनागणास्तथा ।वादयामासुरव्यग्रा भेरीशङ्खांश्च पुष्कलान् ॥५०॥
transliteration: tato devanikāyāste bhūtasenāgaṇāstathā ,vādayāmāsuravyagrā bherīśaṅkhāṁśca puṣkalān.
english: Then, those hosts of gods and likewise the groups of spirit armies, without agitation, played their loud drums and conches.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 50. Then, those hosts of gods and likewise the groups of spirit armies, without agitation, played their loud drums and conches.

sanskrit: पटहाञ्झर्झरांश्चैव कृकचान्गोविषाणिकान् ।आडम्बरान्गोमुखांश्च डिण्डिमांश्च महास्वनान् ॥५१॥
transliteration: paṭahāñjharjharāṁścaiva kṛkacāngoviṣāṇikān ,āḍambarāngomukhāṁśca ḍiṇḍimāṁśca mahāsvanān.
english: They also played loud-sounding kettledrums, jharjharas, kṛkacas, cow-horns, āḍambaras, gomukhas, and ḍiṇḍimas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 51. They also played loud-sounding kettledrums, jharjharas, kṛkacas, cow-horns, āḍambaras, gomukhas, and ḍiṇḍimas.

sanskrit: तुष्टुवुस्ते कुमारं च सर्वे देवाः सवासवाः ।जगुश्च देवगन्धर्वा ननृतुश्चाप्सरोगणाः ॥५२॥
transliteration: tuṣṭuvuste kumāraṁ ca sarve devāḥ savāsavāḥ ,jaguśca devagandharvā nanṛtuścāpsarogaṇāḥ.
english: All the gods, along with Indra, praised Kumara (Skanda). The celestial gandharvas sang, and the groups of apsaras danced.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 52. All the gods, along with Indra, praised Kumara (Skanda). The celestial gandharvas sang, and the groups of apsaras danced.

sanskrit: ततः प्रीतो महासेनस्त्रिदशेभ्यो वरं ददौ ।रिपून्हन्तास्मि समरे ये वो वधचिकीर्षवः ॥५३॥
transliteration: tataḥ prīto mahāsenastridaśebhyo varaṁ dadau ,ripūnhantāsmi samare ye vo vadhacikīrṣavaḥ.
english: Then, the pleased Mahasena (Skanda) gave a boon to the gods: "I will be the killer of those enemies in battle who desire to kill you."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 53. Then, the pleased Mahasena (Skanda) gave a boon to the gods: "I will be the killer of those enemies in battle who desire to kill you."

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य वरं देवास्तस्माद्विबुधसत्तमात् ।प्रीतात्मानो महात्मानो मेनिरे निहतान्रिपून् ॥५४॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya varaṁ devāstasmādvibudhasattamāt ,prītātmāno mahātmāno menire nihatānripūn.
english: Having received the boon from that best of gods, the great-souled gods, with their minds pleased, considered their enemies already slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 54. Having received the boon from that best of gods, the great-souled gods, with their minds pleased, considered their enemies already slain.

sanskrit: सर्वेषां भूतसंघानां हर्षान्नादः समुत्थितः ।अपूरयत लोकांस्त्रीन्वरे दत्ते महात्मना ॥५५॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ bhūtasaṁghānāṁ harṣānnādaḥ samutthitaḥ ,apūrayata lokāṁstrīnvare datte mahātmanā.
english: A cry of joy arose from all groups of living beings. It filled the three worlds when the boon was granted by the great-souled one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 55. A cry of joy arose from all groups of living beings. It filled the three worlds when the boon was granted by the great-souled one.

sanskrit: स निर्ययौ महासेनो महत्या सेनया वृतः ।वधाय युधि दैत्यानां रक्षार्थं च दिवौकसाम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: sa niryayau mahāseno mahatyā senayā vṛtaḥ ,vadhāya yudhi daityānāṁ rakṣārthaṁ ca divaukasām.
english: Accompanied by a great army, Mahasena departed to slay the asuras in battle and to protect the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 56. Accompanied by a great army, Mahasena departed to slay the asuras in battle and to protect the gods.

sanskrit: व्यवसायो जयो धर्मः सिद्धिर्लक्ष्मीर्धृतिः स्मृतिः ।महासेनस्य सैन्यानामग्रे जग्मुर्नराधिप ॥५७॥
transliteration: vyavasāyo jayo dharmaḥ siddhirlakṣmīrdhṛtiḥ smṛtiḥ ,mahāsenasya sainyānāmagre jagmurnarādhipa.
english: O king of men, determination, victory, natural law (dharma), success, fortune, steadfastness, and memory marched at the forefront of Mahasena's armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 57. O king of men, determination, victory, natural law (dharma), success, fortune, steadfastness, and memory marched at the forefront of Mahasena's armies.

sanskrit: स तया भीमया देवः शूलमुद्गरहस्तया ।गदामुसलनाराचशक्तितोमरहस्तया ।दृप्तसिंहनिनादिन्या विनद्य प्रययौ गुहः ॥५८॥
transliteration: sa tayā bhīmayā devaḥ śūlamudgarahastayā ,gadāmusalanārācaśaktitomarahastayā ,dṛptasiṁhaninādinyā vinadya prayayau guhaḥ.
english: He, the divine Guha, set out, roaring loudly, accompanied by his terrifying power (śakti), which held a spear and hammer, and also a mace, pestle, arrow, spear, and javelin, and resonated with the roar of a proud lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 58. He, the divine Guha, set out, roaring loudly, accompanied by his terrifying power (śakti), which held a spear and hammer, and also a mace, pestle, arrow, spear, and javelin, and resonated with the roar of a proud lion.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा सर्वदैतेया राक्षसा दानवास्तथा ।व्यद्रवन्त दिशः सर्वा भयोद्विग्नाः समन्ततः ।अभ्यद्रवन्त देवास्तान्विविधायुधपाणयः ॥५९॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā sarvadaiteyā rākṣasā dānavāstathā ,vyadravanta diśaḥ sarvā bhayodvignāḥ samantataḥ ,abhyadravanta devāstānvividhāyudhapāṇayaḥ.
english: Upon seeing him, all the daityas, rakshasas, and danavas, agitated by fear, scattered in all directions. Meanwhile, the gods, with various weapons in their hands, rushed forward to confront them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 59. Upon seeing him, all the daityas, rakshasas, and danavas, agitated by fear, scattered in all directions. Meanwhile, the gods, with various weapons in their hands, rushed forward to confront them.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा च स ततः क्रुद्धः स्कन्दस्तेजोबलान्वितः ।शक्त्यस्त्रं भगवान्भीमं पुनः पुनरवासृजत् ।आदधच्चात्मनस्तेजो हविषेद्ध इवानलः ॥६०॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā ca sa tataḥ kruddhaḥ skandastejobalānvitaḥ ,śaktyastraṁ bhagavānbhīmaṁ punaḥ punaravāsṛjat ,ādadhaccātmanastejo haviṣeddha ivānalaḥ.
english: Upon seeing that, Skanda, who was endowed with immense energy and strength, became enraged. The divine lord repeatedly discharged his formidable Śakti weapon, drawing upon his own brilliant energy like a fire fueled by clarified butter (havis).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 60. Upon seeing that, Skanda, who was endowed with immense energy and strength, became enraged. The divine lord repeatedly discharged his formidable Śakti weapon, drawing upon his own brilliant energy like a fire fueled by clarified butter (havis).

sanskrit: अभ्यस्यमाने शक्त्यस्त्रे स्कन्देनामिततेजसा ।उल्काज्वाला महाराज पपात वसुधातले ॥६१॥
transliteration: abhyasyamāne śaktyastre skandenāmitatejasā ,ulkājvālā mahārāja papāta vasudhātale.
english: O great king, as the immensely energetic Skanda wielded the Śakti weapon, a fiery meteor fell upon the surface of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 61. O great king, as the immensely energetic Skanda wielded the Śakti weapon, a fiery meteor fell upon the surface of the earth.

sanskrit: संह्रादयन्तश्च तथा निर्घाताश्चापतन्क्षितौ ।यथान्तकालसमये सुघोराः स्युस्तथा नृप ॥६२॥
transliteration: saṁhrādayantaśca tathā nirghātāścāpatankṣitau ,yathāntakālasamaye sughorāḥ syustathā nṛpa.
english: And similarly, O king, terrifying thunderbolts, crashing loudly, fell upon the earth, just as dreadful ones would at the time of the dissolution of the universe (antakālasamaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 62. And similarly, O king, terrifying thunderbolts, crashing loudly, fell upon the earth, just as dreadful ones would at the time of the dissolution of the universe (antakālasamaya).

sanskrit: क्षिप्ता ह्येका तथा शक्तिः सुघोरानलसूनुना ।ततः कोट्यो विनिष्पेतुः शक्तीनां भरतर्षभ ॥६३॥
transliteration: kṣiptā hyekā tathā śaktiḥ sughorānalasūnunā ,tataḥ koṭyo viniṣpetuḥ śaktīnāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Indeed, when one Śakti weapon was thus hurled by the son of the very dreadful fire, O best among the Bhāratas, then tens of millions of similar Śakti weapons shot forth from it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 63. Indeed, when one Śakti weapon was thus hurled by the son of the very dreadful fire, O best among the Bhāratas, then tens of millions of similar Śakti weapons shot forth from it.

sanskrit: स शक्त्यस्त्रेण संग्रामे जघान भगवान्प्रभुः ।दैत्येन्द्रं तारकं नाम महाबलपराक्रमम् ।वृतं दैत्यायुतैर्वीरैर्बलिभिर्दशभिर्नृप ॥६४॥
transliteration: sa śaktyastreṇa saṁgrāme jaghāna bhagavānprabhuḥ ,daityendraṁ tārakaṁ nāma mahābalaparākramam ,vṛtaṁ daityāyutairvīrairbalibhirdaśabhirnṛpa.
english: O king, the divine lord (bhagavān), in battle, struck down the demon king named Tāraka, who possessed immense strength and valor, and was surrounded by ten thousand valiant and powerful demons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 64. O king, the divine lord (bhagavān), in battle, struck down the demon king named Tāraka, who possessed immense strength and valor, and was surrounded by ten thousand valiant and powerful demons.

sanskrit: महिषं चाष्टभिः पद्मैर्वृतं संख्ये निजघ्निवान् ।त्रिपादं चायुतशतैर्जघान दशभिर्वृतम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: mahiṣaṁ cāṣṭabhiḥ padmairvṛtaṁ saṁkhye nijaghnivān ,tripādaṁ cāyutaśatairjaghāna daśabhirvṛtam.
english: And in battle, he struck down Mahiṣa, who was surrounded by eight *padmas* (a huge numerical unit). He also struck down Tripāda, who was encompassed by ten *ayutaśatas* (hundreds of ten thousands).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 65. And in battle, he struck down Mahiṣa, who was surrounded by eight *padmas* (a huge numerical unit). He also struck down Tripāda, who was encompassed by ten *ayutaśatas* (hundreds of ten thousands).

sanskrit: ह्रदोदरं निखर्वैश्च वृतं दशभिरीश्वरः ।जघानानुचरैः सार्धं विविधायुधपाणिभिः ॥६६॥
transliteration: hradodaraṁ nikharvaiśca vṛtaṁ daśabhirīśvaraḥ ,jaghānānucaraiḥ sārdhaṁ vividhāyudhapāṇibhiḥ.
english: The Lord (īśvara), along with his attendants who held various weapons in their hands, struck down Hradodara, who was surrounded by ten *nikharvas* (a very large numerical unit).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 66. The Lord (īśvara), along with his attendants who held various weapons in their hands, struck down Hradodara, who was surrounded by ten *nikharvas* (a very large numerical unit).

sanskrit: तत्राकुर्वन्त विपुलं नादं वध्यत्सु शत्रुषु ।कुमारानुचरा राजन्पूरयन्तो दिशो दश ॥६७॥
transliteration: tatrākurvanta vipulaṁ nādaṁ vadhyatsu śatruṣu ,kumārānucarā rājanpūrayanto diśo daśa.
english: There, O king, as the enemies were being slain, Kumāra's attendants made a mighty roar, filling all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 67. There, O king, as the enemies were being slain, Kumāra's attendants made a mighty roar, filling all ten directions.

sanskrit: शक्त्यस्त्रस्य तु राजेन्द्र ततोऽर्चिर्भिः समन्ततः ।दग्धाः सहस्रशो दैत्या नादैः स्कन्दस्य चापरे ॥६८॥
transliteration: śaktyastrasya tu rājendra tato'rcirbhiḥ samantataḥ ,dagdhāḥ sahasraśo daityā nādaiḥ skandasya cāpare.
english: O king of kings, then, all around, thousands of demons were incinerated by the flames of the power weapon (śakti). And others [were destroyed] by Skanda's roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 68. O king of kings, then, all around, thousands of demons were incinerated by the flames of the power weapon (śakti). And others [were destroyed] by Skanda's roars.

sanskrit: पताकयावधूताश्च हताः केचित्सुरद्विषः ।केचिद्घण्टारवत्रस्ता निपेतुर्वसुधातले ।केचित्प्रहरणैश्छिन्ना विनिपेतुर्गतासवः ॥६९॥
transliteration: patākayāvadhūtāśca hatāḥ kecitsuradviṣaḥ ,kecidghaṇṭāravatrastā nipeturvasudhātale ,kecitpraharaṇaiśchinnā vinipeturgatāsavaḥ.
english: And some of the enemies of the gods were agitated and slain by the banner. Some, terrified by the sound of the bell, fell to the ground. Others, severed by weapons, collapsed lifeless.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 69. And some of the enemies of the gods were agitated and slain by the banner. Some, terrified by the sound of the bell, fell to the ground. Others, severed by weapons, collapsed lifeless.

sanskrit: एवं सुरद्विषोऽनेकान्बलवानाततायिनः ।जघान समरे वीरः कार्त्तिकेयो महाबलः ॥७०॥
transliteration: evaṁ suradviṣo'nekānbalavānātatāyinaḥ ,jaghāna samare vīraḥ kārttikeyo mahābalaḥ.
english: Thus, the mighty and greatly powerful hero, Kārttikeya, slew many aggressive enemies of the gods in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 70. Thus, the mighty and greatly powerful hero, Kārttikeya, slew many aggressive enemies of the gods in battle.

sanskrit: बाणो नामाथ दैतेयो बलेः पुत्रो महाबलः ।क्रौञ्चं पर्वतमासाद्य देवसंघानबाधत ॥७१॥
transliteration: bāṇo nāmātha daiteyo baleḥ putro mahābalaḥ ,krauñcaṁ parvatamāsādya devasaṁghānabādhata.
english: Then, a mighty demon named Bāṇa, the son of Bali, having approached the Krauñca mountain, began to harass the hosts of gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 71. Then, a mighty demon named Bāṇa, the son of Bali, having approached the Krauñca mountain, began to harass the hosts of gods.

sanskrit: तमभ्ययान्महासेनः सुरशत्रुमुदारधीः ।स कार्त्तिकेयस्य भयात्क्रौञ्चं शरणमेयिवान् ॥७२॥
transliteration: tamabhyayānmahāsenaḥ suraśatrumudāradhīḥ ,sa kārttikeyasya bhayātkrauñcaṁ śaraṇameyivān.
english: The noble-minded Mahasena (Karttikeya) approached that enemy of the gods. Fearing Karttikeya, the enemy had sought refuge in the Krauñca mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 72. The noble-minded Mahasena (Karttikeya) approached that enemy of the gods. Fearing Karttikeya, the enemy had sought refuge in the Krauñca mountain.

sanskrit: ततः क्रौञ्चं महामन्युः क्रौञ्चनादनिनादितम् ।शक्त्या बिभेद भगवान्कार्त्तिकेयोऽग्निदत्तया ॥७३॥
transliteration: tataḥ krauñcaṁ mahāmanyuḥ krauñcanādanināditam ,śaktyā bibheda bhagavānkārttikeyo'gnidattayā.
english: Then, the divine, mighty-wrathful Karttikeya pierced the Krauñca mountain, which resounded with echoing cries, using the spear (śakti) gifted by Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 73. Then, the divine, mighty-wrathful Karttikeya pierced the Krauñca mountain, which resounded with echoing cries, using the spear (śakti) gifted by Agni.

sanskrit: सशालस्कन्धसरलं त्रस्तवानरवारणम् ।पुलिनत्रस्तविहगं विनिष्पतितपन्नगम् ॥७४॥
transliteration: saśālaskandhasaralaṁ trastavānaravāraṇam ,pulinatrastavihagaṁ viniṣpatitapannagam.
english: It (the mountain) possessed its śāla trees and sarala trees with their trunks; its monkeys and elephants were terrified; its birds on the sandbanks were frightened; and its serpents tumbled out.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 74. It (the mountain) possessed its śāla trees and sarala trees with their trunks; its monkeys and elephants were terrified; its birds on the sandbanks were frightened; and its serpents tumbled out.

sanskrit: गोलाङ्गूलर्क्षसंघैश्च द्रवद्भिरनुनादितम् ।कुरङ्गगतिनिर्घोषमुद्भ्रान्तसृमराचितम् ॥७५॥
transliteration: golāṅgūlarkṣasaṁghaiśca dravadbhiranunāditam ,kuraṅgagatinirghoṣamudbhrāntasṛmarācitam.
english: And it (the mountain) was reverberating with the sounds of fleeing troops of langurs and bears; it resonated with the loud noise of scattering deer; and it was filled with bewildered sṛmara antelopes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 75. And it (the mountain) was reverberating with the sounds of fleeing troops of langurs and bears; it resonated with the loud noise of scattering deer; and it was filled with bewildered sṛmara antelopes.

sanskrit: विनिष्पतद्भिः शरभैः सिंहैश्च सहसा द्रुतैः ।शोच्यामपि दशां प्राप्तो रराजैव स पर्वतः ॥७६॥
transliteration: viniṣpatadbhiḥ śarabhaiḥ siṁhaiśca sahasā drutaiḥ ,śocyāmapi daśāṁ prāpto rarājaiva sa parvataḥ.
english: That mountain, even though it had reached a pitiable state due to lions and sharabhas (mythical beasts) swiftly rushing and flying out from it, still shone brightly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 76. That mountain, even though it had reached a pitiable state due to lions and sharabhas (mythical beasts) swiftly rushing and flying out from it, still shone brightly.

sanskrit: विद्याधराः समुत्पेतुस्तस्य शृङ्गनिवासिनः ।किंनराश्च समुद्विग्नाः शक्तिपातरवोद्धताः ॥७७॥
transliteration: vidyādharāḥ samutpetustasya śṛṅganivāsinaḥ ,kiṁnarāśca samudvignāḥ śaktipātaravoddhatāḥ.
english: The Vidyadharas (celestial beings), who dwelt on its peaks, flew up. And the Kinnaras (mythical beings), greatly agitated and roused by the sound of the śakti-weapon's impact, also flew up.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 77. The Vidyadharas (celestial beings), who dwelt on its peaks, flew up. And the Kinnaras (mythical beings), greatly agitated and roused by the sound of the śakti-weapon's impact, also flew up.

sanskrit: ततो दैत्या विनिष्पेतुः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।प्रदीप्तात्पर्वतश्रेष्ठाद्विचित्राभरणस्रजः ॥७८॥
transliteration: tato daityā viniṣpetuḥ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ ,pradīptātparvataśreṣṭhādvicitrābharaṇasrajaḥ.
english: Then, by hundreds and thousands, the Daityas (demons), adorned with various ornaments and garlands, rushed out from that blazing, foremost of mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 78. Then, by hundreds and thousands, the Daityas (demons), adorned with various ornaments and garlands, rushed out from that blazing, foremost of mountains.

sanskrit: तान्निजघ्नुरतिक्रम्य कुमारानुचरा मृधे ।बिभेद शक्त्या क्रौञ्चं च पावकिः परवीरहा ॥७९॥
transliteration: tānnijaghnuratikramya kumārānucarā mṛdhe ,bibheda śaktyā krauñcaṁ ca pāvakiḥ paravīrahā.
english: In battle, Kumara's (Kartikeya's) followers, having overwhelmed them, struck them down. And Pāvaki (Kartikeya), the slayer of enemy heroes, pierced Mount Krauñca with his śakti-weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 79. In battle, Kumara's (Kartikeya's) followers, having overwhelmed them, struck them down. And Pāvaki (Kartikeya), the slayer of enemy heroes, pierced Mount Krauñca with his śakti-weapon.

sanskrit: बहुधा चैकधा चैव कृत्वात्मानं महात्मना ।शक्तिः क्षिप्ता रणे तस्य पाणिमेति पुनः पुनः ॥८०॥
transliteration: bahudhā caikadhā caiva kṛtvātmānaṁ mahātmanā ,śaktiḥ kṣiptā raṇe tasya pāṇimeti punaḥ punaḥ.
english: The great-souled one (mahātman) transformed himself, manifesting in many forms and also as a single entity. His divine power (śakti), when hurled in battle, would return to his hand again and again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 80. The great-souled one (mahātman) transformed himself, manifesting in many forms and also as a single entity. His divine power (śakti), when hurled in battle, would return to his hand again and again.

sanskrit: एवंप्रभावो भगवानतो भूयश्च पावकिः ।क्रौञ्चस्तेन विनिर्भिन्नो दैत्याश्च शतशो हताः ॥८१॥
transliteration: evaṁprabhāvo bhagavānato bhūyaśca pāvakiḥ ,krauñcastena vinirbhinno daityāśca śataśo hatāḥ.
english: Such was the power of the glorious (bhagavān) son of fire (Pāvaki). By him, the Krauñca mountain-demon was pierced, and hundreds of demons (daitya) were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 81. Such was the power of the glorious (bhagavān) son of fire (Pāvaki). By him, the Krauñca mountain-demon was pierced, and hundreds of demons (daitya) were slain.

sanskrit: ततः स भगवान्देवो निहत्य विबुधद्विषः ।सभाज्यमानो विबुधैः परं हर्षमवाप ह ॥८२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa bhagavāndevo nihatya vibudhadviṣaḥ ,sabhājyamāno vibudhaiḥ paraṁ harṣamavāpa ha.
english: Thereupon, that glorious (bhagavān) god, having slain the enemies of the gods, and being greatly honored by the divine beings (vibudha), attained supreme joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 82. Thereupon, that glorious (bhagavān) god, having slain the enemies of the gods, and being greatly honored by the divine beings (vibudha), attained supreme joy.

sanskrit: ततो दुन्दुभयो राजन्नेदुः शङ्खाश्च भारत ।मुमुचुर्देवयोषाश्च पुष्पवर्षमनुत्तमम् ॥८३॥
transliteration: tato dundubhayo rājanneduḥ śaṅkhāśca bhārata ,mumucurdevayoṣāśca puṣpavarṣamanuttamam.
english: Then, O King (rājan), O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), the war-drums sounded and conch-shells blared. And the celestial women (devayoṣā) showered an unsurpassed rain of flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 83. Then, O King (rājan), O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), the war-drums sounded and conch-shells blared. And the celestial women (devayoṣā) showered an unsurpassed rain of flowers.

sanskrit: दिव्यगन्धमुपादाय ववौ पुण्यश्च मारुतः ।गन्धर्वास्तुष्टुवुश्चैनं यज्वानश्च महर्षयः ॥८४॥
transliteration: divyagandhamupādāya vavau puṇyaśca mārutaḥ ,gandharvāstuṣṭuvuścainaṁ yajvānaśca maharṣayaḥ.
english: The holy wind blew, carrying a divine fragrance. The Gandharvas, along with the performers of Vedic rituals (yajvan) and the great sages, praised him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 84. The holy wind blew, carrying a divine fragrance. The Gandharvas, along with the performers of Vedic rituals (yajvan) and the great sages, praised him.

sanskrit: केचिदेनं व्यवस्यन्ति पितामहसुतं प्रभुम् ।सनत्कुमारं सर्वेषां ब्रह्मयोनिं तमग्रजम् ॥८५॥
transliteration: kecidenaṁ vyavasyanti pitāmahasutaṁ prabhum ,sanatkumāraṁ sarveṣāṁ brahmayoniṁ tamagrajam.
english: Some consider him to be Sanatkumāra, the Lord, the grandson of Brahmā, the primeval one (agrajam) and the source of all (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 85. Some consider him to be Sanatkumāra, the Lord, the grandson of Brahmā, the primeval one (agrajam) and the source of all (brahman).

sanskrit: केचिन्महेश्वरसुतं केचित्पुत्रं विभावसोः ।उमायाः कृत्तिकानां च गङ्गायाश्च वदन्त्युत ॥८६॥
transliteration: kecinmaheśvarasutaṁ kecitputraṁ vibhāvasoḥ ,umāyāḥ kṛttikānāṁ ca gaṅgāyāśca vadantyuta.
english: Some declare him to be the son of Maheśvara (Shiva); others, moreover, declare him to be the son of Vibhāvasu (the Sun god), of Umā, of the Kṛttikās, and of Gaṅgā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 86. Some declare him to be the son of Maheśvara (Shiva); others, moreover, declare him to be the son of Vibhāvasu (the Sun god), of Umā, of the Kṛttikās, and of Gaṅgā.

sanskrit: एकधा च द्विधा चैव चतुर्धा च महाबलम् ।योगिनामीश्वरं देवं शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥८७॥
transliteration: ekadhā ca dvidhā caiva caturdhā ca mahābalam ,yogināmīśvaraṁ devaṁ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: And they describe the mighty god, the lord of (yogins), in one way, in two ways, and indeed in four ways, by hundreds and by thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 87. And they describe the mighty god, the lord of (yogins), in one way, in two ways, and indeed in four ways, by hundreds and by thousands.

sanskrit: एतत्ते कथितं राजन्कार्त्तिकेयाभिषेचनम् ।शृणु चैव सरस्वत्यास्तीर्थवंशस्य पुण्यताम् ॥८८॥
transliteration: etatte kathitaṁ rājankārttikeyābhiṣecanam ,śṛṇu caiva sarasvatyāstīrthavaṁśasya puṇyatām.
english: O King, I have recounted to you the anointing of Kārttikeya. Now, listen also to the holiness of the lineage of sacred sites (tīrtha) associated with Sarasvatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 88. O King, I have recounted to you the anointing of Kārttikeya. Now, listen also to the holiness of the lineage of sacred sites (tīrtha) associated with Sarasvatī.

sanskrit: बभूव तीर्थप्रवरं हतेषु सुरशत्रुषु ।कुमारेण महाराज त्रिविष्टपमिवापरम् ॥८९॥
transliteration: babhūva tīrthapravaraṁ hateṣu suraśatruṣu ,kumāreṇa mahārāja triviṣṭapamivāparam.
english: O Great King, after Kumāra (Kārttikeya) had slain the enemies of the gods, that sacred site (tīrtha) became exceptionally prominent, like a second heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 89. O Great King, after Kumāra (Kārttikeya) had slain the enemies of the gods, that sacred site (tīrtha) became exceptionally prominent, like a second heaven.

sanskrit: ऐश्वर्याणि च तत्रस्थो ददावीशः पृथक्पृथक् ।तदा नैरृतमुख्येभ्यस्त्रैलोक्ये पावकात्मजः ॥९०॥
transliteration: aiśvaryāṇi ca tatrastho dadāvīśaḥ pṛthakpṛthak ,tadā nairṛtamukhyebhyastrailokye pāvakātmajaḥ.
english: Being present at that sacred site (tīrtha), the lord, Kārttikeya (pāvakātmaja), then bestowed various sovereignties (aiśvarya) individually upon the chiefs of the Nairṛtas (a class of Rākṣasas) throughout the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 90. Being present at that sacred site (tīrtha), the lord, Kārttikeya (pāvakātmaja), then bestowed various sovereignties (aiśvarya) individually upon the chiefs of the Nairṛtas (a class of Rākṣasas) throughout the three worlds.

sanskrit: एवं स भगवांस्तस्मिंस्तीर्थे दैत्यकुलान्तकः ।अभिषिक्तो महाराज देवसेनापतिः सुरैः ॥९१॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa bhagavāṁstasmiṁstīrthe daityakulāntakaḥ ,abhiṣikto mahārāja devasenāpatiḥ suraiḥ.
english: O Great King, thus that divine (bhagavān) Kārttikeya, the destroyer of the demon lineage (daityakulāntaka) and commander of the gods' army (devasenāpati), was consecrated by the gods at that sacred site (tīrtha).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 91. O Great King, thus that divine (bhagavān) Kārttikeya, the destroyer of the demon lineage (daityakulāntaka) and commander of the gods' army (devasenāpati), was consecrated by the gods at that sacred site (tīrtha).

sanskrit: औजसं नाम तत्तीर्थं यत्र पूर्वमपां पतिः ।अभिषिक्तः सुरगणैर्वरुणो भरतर्षभ ॥९२॥
transliteration: aujasaṁ nāma tattīrthaṁ yatra pūrvamapāṁ patiḥ ,abhiṣiktaḥ suragaṇairvaruṇo bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among Bharatas, that holy place is named Aujasa, where Varuṇa, the lord of waters, was formerly consecrated by the hosts of gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 92. O best among Bharatas, that holy place is named Aujasa, where Varuṇa, the lord of waters, was formerly consecrated by the hosts of gods.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तीर्थवरे स्नात्वा स्कन्दं चाभ्यर्च्य लाङ्गली ।ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददौ रुक्मं वासांस्याभरणानि च ॥९३॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstīrthavare snātvā skandaṁ cābhyarcya lāṅgalī ,brāhmaṇebhyo dadau rukmaṁ vāsāṁsyābharaṇāni ca.
english: Having bathed in that excellent holy place and worshipped Skanda, Balarāma (lāṅgalī) gave gold, garments, and ornaments to the Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 93. Having bathed in that excellent holy place and worshipped Skanda, Balarāma (lāṅgalī) gave gold, garments, and ornaments to the Brahmins.

sanskrit: उषित्वा रजनीं तत्र माधवः परवीरहा ।पूज्य तीर्थवरं तच्च स्पृष्ट्वा तोयं च लाङ्गली ।हृष्टः प्रीतमनाश्चैव ह्यभवन्माधवोत्तमः ॥९४॥
transliteration: uṣitvā rajanīṁ tatra mādhavaḥ paravīrahā ,pūjya tīrthavaraṁ tacca spṛṣṭvā toyaṁ ca lāṅgalī ,hṛṣṭaḥ prītamanāścaiva hyabhavanmādhavottamaḥ.
english: Having stayed there for the night, Balarāma (mādhava, paravīrahā), after venerating that excellent holy place and touching its waters, became joyful and pleased in mind; indeed, he was truly an excellent Mādhava (mādhavottama).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 94. Having stayed there for the night, Balarāma (mādhava, paravīrahā), after venerating that excellent holy place and touching its waters, became joyful and pleased in mind; indeed, he was truly an excellent Mādhava (mādhavottama).

sanskrit: एतत्ते सर्वमाख्यातं यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ।यथाभिषिक्तो भगवान्स्कन्दो देवैः समागतैः ॥९५॥
transliteration: etatte sarvamākhyātaṁ yanmāṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi ,yathābhiṣikto bhagavānskando devaiḥ samāgataiḥ.
english: All this, about which you asked me, has now been recounted to you, specifically how the revered Skanda (skanda) was consecrated by the assembled gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 45 Verse 95. All this, about which you asked me, has now been recounted to you, specifically how the revered Skanda (skanda) was consecrated by the assembled gods.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।अत्यद्भुतमिदं ब्रह्मञ्श्रुतवानस्मि तत्त्वतः ।अभिषेकं कुमारस्य विस्तरेण यथाविधि ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,atyadbhutamidaṁ brahmañśrutavānasmi tattvataḥ ,abhiṣekaṁ kumārasya vistareṇa yathāvidhi.
english: Janamejaya said: "O Brahmin (brahman), I have truly heard this exceedingly amazing account of Kumāra's consecration, in detail and according to the proper rites."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "O Brahmin (brahman), I have truly heard this exceedingly amazing account of Kumāra's consecration, in detail and according to the proper rites."

sanskrit: यच्छ्रुत्वा पूतमात्मानं विजानामि तपोधन ।प्रहृष्टानि च रोमाणि प्रसन्नं च मनो मम ॥२॥
transliteration: yacchrutvā pūtamātmānaṁ vijānāmi tapodhana ,prahṛṣṭāni ca romāṇi prasannaṁ ca mano mama.
english: O ascetic (tapodhana), having heard which, I recognize my own self (ātman) as purified; my body-hairs are bristling with joy, and my mind is serene.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 2. O ascetic (tapodhana), having heard which, I recognize my own self (ātman) as purified; my body-hairs are bristling with joy, and my mind is serene.

sanskrit: अभिषेकं कुमारस्य दैत्यानां च वधं तथा ।श्रुत्वा मे परमा प्रीतिर्भूयः कौतूहलं हि मे ॥३॥
transliteration: abhiṣekaṁ kumārasya daityānāṁ ca vadhaṁ tathā ,śrutvā me paramā prītirbhūyaḥ kautūhalaṁ hi me.
english: Having heard of Kumāra's consecration and also the destruction of the Daityas, a supreme joy has arisen in me, and indeed, further curiosity.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 3. Having heard of Kumāra's consecration and also the destruction of the Daityas, a supreme joy has arisen in me, and indeed, further curiosity.

sanskrit: अपां पतिः कथं ह्यस्मिन्नभिषिक्तः सुरासुरैः ।तन्मे ब्रूहि महाप्राज्ञ कुशलो ह्यसि सत्तम ॥४॥
transliteration: apāṁ patiḥ kathaṁ hyasminnabhiṣiktaḥ surāsuraiḥ ,tanme brūhi mahāprājña kuśalo hyasi sattama.
english: How indeed was the Lord of Waters consecrated in this manner by the gods and demons? O greatly wise one, tell me that, for you are indeed skilled and the best of good people.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 4. How indeed was the Lord of Waters consecrated in this manner by the gods and demons? O greatly wise one, tell me that, for you are indeed skilled and the best of good people.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।शृणु राजन्निदं चित्रं पूर्वकल्पे यथातथम् ।आदौ कृतयुगे तस्मिन्वर्तमाने यथाविधि ।वरुणं देवताः सर्वाः समेत्येदमथाब्रुवन् ॥५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śṛṇu rājannidaṁ citraṁ pūrvakalpe yathātatham ,ādau kṛtayuge tasminvartamāne yathāvidhi ,varuṇaṁ devatāḥ sarvāḥ sametyedamathābruvan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Listen, O King, to this wondrous event, exactly as it happened in a previous age (kalpa). In the beginning, during that Kṛta-yuga, as it was proceeding according to proper custom, all the deities, having assembled, then spoke this to Varuṇa."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 5. Vaiśampāyana said: "Listen, O King, to this wondrous event, exactly as it happened in a previous age (kalpa). In the beginning, during that Kṛta-yuga, as it was proceeding according to proper custom, all the deities, having assembled, then spoke this to Varuṇa."

sanskrit: यथास्मान्सुरराट्शक्रो भयेभ्यः पाति सर्वदा ।तथा त्वमपि सर्वासां सरितां वै पतिर्भव ॥६॥
transliteration: yathāsmānsurarāṭśakro bhayebhyaḥ pāti sarvadā ,tathā tvamapi sarvāsāṁ saritāṁ vai patirbhava.
english: "Just as Śakra, the king of the gods, always protects us from fears, so too you become the lord of all rivers."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 6. "Just as Śakra, the king of the gods, always protects us from fears, so too you become the lord of all rivers."

sanskrit: वासश्च ते सदा देव सागरे मकरालये ।समुद्रोऽयं तव वशे भविष्यति नदीपतिः ॥७॥
transliteration: vāsaśca te sadā deva sāgare makarālaye ,samudro'yaṁ tava vaśe bhaviṣyati nadīpatiḥ.
english: "And your dwelling, O God, will always be in the ocean, the abode of aquatic creatures. This ocean will be under your control; you will be the lord of rivers."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 7. "And your dwelling, O God, will always be in the ocean, the abode of aquatic creatures. This ocean will be under your control; you will be the lord of rivers."

sanskrit: सोमेन सार्धं च तव हानिवृद्धी भविष्यतः ।एवमस्त्विति तान्देवान्वरुणो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: somena sārdhaṁ ca tava hānivṛddhī bhaviṣyataḥ ,evamastviti tāndevānvaruṇo vākyamabravīt.
english: "And your waning and waxing will occur in conjunction with Soma. 'So be it!' - Varuṇa spoke this word to those deities."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 8. "And your waning and waxing will occur in conjunction with Soma. 'So be it!' - Varuṇa spoke this word to those deities."

sanskrit: समागम्य ततः सर्वे वरुणं सागरालयम् ।अपां पतिं प्रचक्रुर्हि विधिदृष्टेन कर्मणा ॥९॥
transliteration: samāgamya tataḥ sarve varuṇaṁ sāgarālayam ,apāṁ patiṁ pracakrurhi vidhidṛṣṭena karmaṇā.
english: Then, having gathered together, all of them indeed appointed Varuna, who resides in the ocean and is the lord of waters, through a ritual (karma) prescribed by sacred law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 9. Then, having gathered together, all of them indeed appointed Varuna, who resides in the ocean and is the lord of waters, through a ritual (karma) prescribed by sacred law (dharma).

sanskrit: अभिषिच्य ततो देवा वरुणं यादसां पतिम् ।जग्मुः स्वान्येव स्थानानि पूजयित्वा जलेश्वरम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: abhiṣicya tato devā varuṇaṁ yādasāṁ patim ,jagmuḥ svānyeva sthānāni pūjayitvā jaleśvaram.
english: Then, after anointing Varuna, the lord of aquatic creatures, and having honored the lord of waters, the gods went to their own places.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 10. Then, after anointing Varuna, the lord of aquatic creatures, and having honored the lord of waters, the gods went to their own places.

sanskrit: अभिषिक्तस्ततो देवैर्वरुणोऽपि महायशाः ।सरितः सागरांश्चैव नदांश्चैव सरांसि च ।पालयामास विधिना यथा देवाञ्शतक्रतुः ॥११॥
transliteration: abhiṣiktastato devairvaruṇo'pi mahāyaśāḥ ,saritaḥ sāgarāṁścaiva nadāṁścaiva sarāṁsi ca ,pālayāmāsa vidhinā yathā devāñśatakratuḥ.
english: Then, Varuna, also greatly renowned, having been consecrated by the gods, protected rivers, oceans, large rivers, and lakes according to sacred law (dharma), just as Śatakratu (Indra) protects the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 11. Then, Varuna, also greatly renowned, having been consecrated by the gods, protected rivers, oceans, large rivers, and lakes according to sacred law (dharma), just as Śatakratu (Indra) protects the gods.

sanskrit: ततस्तत्राप्युपस्पृश्य दत्त्वा च विविधं वसु ।अग्नितीर्थं महाप्राज्ञः स जगाम प्रलम्बहा ।नष्टो न दृश्यते यत्र शमीगर्भे हुताशनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatastatrāpyupaspṛśya dattvā ca vividhaṁ vasu ,agnitīrthaṁ mahāprājñaḥ sa jagāma pralambahā ,naṣṭo na dṛśyate yatra śamīgarbhe hutāśanaḥ.
english: Then, having bathed there and having given various gifts, the greatly wise one, the slayer of Pralamba (Balarama), went to Agnitīrtha, where the fire (Agni) is not seen, having disappeared into the interior of a śamī tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 12. Then, having bathed there and having given various gifts, the greatly wise one, the slayer of Pralamba (Balarama), went to Agnitīrtha, where the fire (Agni) is not seen, having disappeared into the interior of a śamī tree.

sanskrit: लोकालोकविनाशे च प्रादुर्भूते तदानघ ।उपतस्थुर्महात्मानं सर्वलोकपितामहम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: lokālokavināśe ca prādurbhūte tadānagha ,upatasthurmahātmānaṁ sarvalokapitāmaham.
english: Then, O sinless one, when the destruction of the Lokāloka mountain appeared, they approached the great-souled (mahātman) grandfather of all worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 13. Then, O sinless one, when the destruction of the Lokāloka mountain appeared, they approached the great-souled (mahātman) grandfather of all worlds.

sanskrit: अग्निः प्रनष्टो भगवान्कारणं च न विद्महे ।सर्वलोकक्षयो मा भूत्संपादयतु नोऽनलम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: agniḥ pranaṣṭo bhagavānkāraṇaṁ ca na vidmahe ,sarvalokakṣayo mā bhūtsaṁpādayatu no'nalam.
english: The divine Agni has disappeared, and we do not know the reason. May there not be the destruction of all worlds; may he bring forth fire for us.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 14. The divine Agni has disappeared, and we do not know the reason. May there not be the destruction of all worlds; may he bring forth fire for us.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।किमर्थं भगवानग्निः प्रनष्टो लोकभावनः ।विज्ञातश्च कथं देवैस्तन्ममाचक्ष्व तत्त्वतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kimarthaṁ bhagavānagniḥ pranaṣṭo lokabhāvanaḥ ,vijñātaśca kathaṁ devaistanmamācakṣva tattvataḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: 'Why has the divine Agni, the sustainer of worlds, disappeared? And how was he discovered by the gods? Please tell me that in detail.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 15. Janamejaya said: 'Why has the divine Agni, the sustainer of worlds, disappeared? And how was he discovered by the gods? Please tell me that in detail.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।भृगोः शापाद्भृशं भीतो जातवेदाः प्रतापवान् ।शमीगर्भमथासाद्य ननाश भगवांस्ततः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,bhṛgoḥ śāpādbhṛśaṁ bhīto jātavedāḥ pratāpavān ,śamīgarbhamathāsādya nanāśa bhagavāṁstataḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'The powerful, divine Jātavedas (Agni), greatly frightened by Bhṛgu's curse, then, having entered the interior of a śamī tree, disappeared.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 16. Vaiśampāyana said: 'The powerful, divine Jātavedas (Agni), greatly frightened by Bhṛgu's curse, then, having entered the interior of a śamī tree, disappeared.'

sanskrit: प्रनष्टे तु तदा वह्नौ देवाः सर्वे सवासवाः ।अन्वेषन्त तदा नष्टं ज्वलनं भृशदुःखिताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: pranaṣṭe tu tadā vahnau devāḥ sarve savāsavāḥ ,anveṣanta tadā naṣṭaṁ jvalanaṁ bhṛśaduḥkhitāḥ.
english: When the fire (vahnau) disappeared, all the gods, including Indra, became greatly distressed and then searched for the lost flame.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 17. When the fire (vahnau) disappeared, all the gods, including Indra, became greatly distressed and then searched for the lost flame.

sanskrit: ततोऽग्नितीर्थमासाद्य शमीगर्भस्थमेव हि ।ददृशुर्ज्वलनं तत्र वसमानं यथाविधि ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato'gnitīrthamāsādya śamīgarbhasthameva hi ,dadṛśurjvalanaṁ tatra vasamānaṁ yathāvidhi.
english: Then, having reached the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Agni, they indeed saw the fire residing there within a śamī tree, living in accordance with sacred custom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 18. Then, having reached the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Agni, they indeed saw the fire residing there within a śamī tree, living in accordance with sacred custom.

sanskrit: देवाः सर्वे नरव्याघ्र बृहस्पतिपुरोगमाः ।ज्वलनं तं समासाद्य प्रीताभूवन्सवासवाः ।पुनर्यथागतं जग्मुः सर्वभक्षश्च सोऽभवत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: devāḥ sarve naravyāghra bṛhaspatipurogamāḥ ,jvalanaṁ taṁ samāsādya prītābhūvansavāsavāḥ ,punaryathāgataṁ jagmuḥ sarvabhakṣaśca so'bhavat.
english: O tiger among men, all the gods, led by Bṛhaspati, became pleased along with Indra after finding that fire. They returned again as they had come, and he (the fire) became the devourer of all.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 19. O tiger among men, all the gods, led by Bṛhaspati, became pleased along with Indra after finding that fire. They returned again as they had come, and he (the fire) became the devourer of all.

sanskrit: भृगोः शापान्महीपाल यदुक्तं ब्रह्मवादिना ।तत्राप्याप्लुत्य मतिमान्ब्रह्मयोनिं जगाम ह ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhṛgoḥ śāpānmahīpāla yaduktaṁ brahmavādinā ,tatrāpyāplutya matimānbrahmayoniṁ jagāma ha.
english: O protector of the earth, by the curse of Bhṛgu, which was declared by the knower of sacred texts (brahmavādin), the intelligent one, after bathing there, indeed attained the source of (brahman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 20. O protector of the earth, by the curse of Bhṛgu, which was declared by the knower of sacred texts (brahmavādin), the intelligent one, after bathing there, indeed attained the source of (brahman).

sanskrit: ससर्ज भगवान्यत्र सर्वलोकपितामहः ।तत्राप्लुत्य ततो ब्रह्मा सह देवैः प्रभुः पुरा ।ससर्ज चान्नानि तथा देवतानां यथाविधि ॥२१॥
transliteration: sasarja bhagavānyatra sarvalokapitāmahaḥ ,tatrāplutya tato brahmā saha devaiḥ prabhuḥ purā ,sasarja cānnāni tathā devatānāṁ yathāvidhi.
english: Where the divine Lord, Brahma, the grandfather of all worlds, formerly performed creation, having bathed there, that Lord, along with the gods, then created foods for the gods according to the proper rituals.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 21. Where the divine Lord, Brahma, the grandfather of all worlds, formerly performed creation, having bathed there, that Lord, along with the gods, then created foods for the gods according to the proper rituals.

sanskrit: तत्र स्नात्वा च दत्त्वा च वसूनि विविधानि च ।कौबेरं प्रययौ तीर्थं तत्र तप्त्वा महत्तपः ।धनाधिपत्यं संप्राप्तो राजन्नैलबिलः प्रभुः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tatra snātvā ca dattvā ca vasūni vividhāni ca ,kauberaṁ prayayau tīrthaṁ tatra taptvā mahattapaḥ ,dhanādhipatyaṁ saṁprāpto rājannailabilaḥ prabhuḥ.
english: O King, having bathed there (at Brahma's creation site) and given various forms of wealth, the lord Ailabila (Kubera) then went to Kubera's sacred bathing place (tīrtham). There, having performed great austerity (tapas), he obtained sovereignty over wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 22. O King, having bathed there (at Brahma's creation site) and given various forms of wealth, the lord Ailabila (Kubera) then went to Kubera's sacred bathing place (tīrtham). There, having performed great austerity (tapas), he obtained sovereignty over wealth.

sanskrit: तत्रस्थमेव तं राजन्धनानि निधयस्तथा ।उपतस्थुर्नरश्रेष्ठ तत्तीर्थं लाङ्गली ततः ।गत्वा स्नात्वा च विधिवद्ब्राह्मणेभ्यो धनं ददौ ॥२३॥
transliteration: tatrasthameva taṁ rājandhanāni nidhayastathā ,upatasthurnaraśreṣṭha tattīrthaṁ lāṅgalī tataḥ ,gatvā snātvā ca vidhivadbrāhmaṇebhyo dhanaṁ dadau.
english: O King, O best among men, while he (Kubera) remained there, riches and treasures themselves came to him. Then, Balarama (lāṅgalī), having gone to that sacred bathing place (tīrtham) and having bathed according to the proper rituals, gave wealth to the brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 23. O King, O best among men, while he (Kubera) remained there, riches and treasures themselves came to him. Then, Balarama (lāṅgalī), having gone to that sacred bathing place (tīrtham) and having bathed according to the proper rituals, gave wealth to the brahmins.

sanskrit: ददृशे तत्र तत्स्थानं कौबेरे काननोत्तमे ।पुरा यत्र तपस्तप्तं विपुलं सुमहात्मना ॥२४॥
transliteration: dadṛśe tatra tatsthānaṁ kaubere kānanottame ,purā yatra tapastaptaṁ vipulaṁ sumahātmanā.
english: There, in Kubera's excellent forest, that place was seen where, formerly, great and extensive austerity (tapas) had been performed by a very noble soul.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 24. There, in Kubera's excellent forest, that place was seen where, formerly, great and extensive austerity (tapas) had been performed by a very noble soul.

sanskrit: यत्र राज्ञा कुबेरेण वरा लब्धाश्च पुष्कलाः ।धनाधिपत्यं सख्यं च रुद्रेणामिततेजसा ॥२५॥
transliteration: yatra rājñā kubereṇa varā labdhāśca puṣkalāḥ ,dhanādhipatyaṁ sakhyaṁ ca rudreṇāmitatejasā.
english: Where King Kubera obtained many great boons: the lordship over wealth and friendship with Rudra, who possesses immeasurable splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 25. Where King Kubera obtained many great boons: the lordship over wealth and friendship with Rudra, who possesses immeasurable splendor.

sanskrit: सुरत्वं लोकपालत्वं पुत्रं च नलकूबरम् ।यत्र लेभे महाबाहो धनाधिपतिरञ्जसा ॥२६॥
transliteration: suratvaṁ lokapālatvaṁ putraṁ ca nalakūbaram ,yatra lebhe mahābāho dhanādhipatirañjasā.
english: Where the lord of wealth (Kubera), O mighty-armed one, truly obtained divinity, the status of a world-protector, and a son, Nalakūbara.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 26. Where the lord of wealth (Kubera), O mighty-armed one, truly obtained divinity, the status of a world-protector, and a son, Nalakūbara.

sanskrit: अभिषिक्तश्च तत्रैव समागम्य मरुद्गणैः ।वाहनं चास्य तद्दत्तं हंसयुक्तं मनोरमम् ।विमानं पुष्पकं दिव्यं नैरृतैश्वर्यमेव च ॥२७॥
transliteration: abhiṣiktaśca tatraiva samāgamya marudgaṇaiḥ ,vāhanaṁ cāsya taddattaṁ haṁsayuktaṁ manoramam ,vimānaṁ puṣpakaṁ divyaṁ nairṛtaiśvaryameva ca.
english: And right there, after assembling with the groups of Maruts, he was consecrated. Also, that charming vehicle, the divine Puṣpaka aerial chariot drawn by swans, was given to him, along with the sovereignty over the Nairṛtas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 27. And right there, after assembling with the groups of Maruts, he was consecrated. Also, that charming vehicle, the divine Puṣpaka aerial chariot drawn by swans, was given to him, along with the sovereignty over the Nairṛtas.

sanskrit: तत्राप्लुत्य बलो राजन्दत्त्वा दायांश्च पुष्कलान् ।जगाम त्वरितो रामस्तीर्थं श्वेतानुलेपनः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tatrāplutya balo rājandattvā dāyāṁśca puṣkalān ,jagāma tvarito rāmastīrthaṁ śvetānulepanaḥ.
english: There, O king, the mighty Rama, having bathed and given many lavish gifts (dāya), quickly proceeded to the sacred bathing place, adorned with white unguents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 28. There, O king, the mighty Rama, having bathed and given many lavish gifts (dāya), quickly proceeded to the sacred bathing place, adorned with white unguents.

sanskrit: निषेवितं सर्वसत्त्वैर्नाम्ना बदरपाचनम् ।नानर्तुकवनोपेतं सदापुष्पफलं शुभम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: niṣevitaṁ sarvasattvairnāmnā badarapācanam ,nānartukavanopetaṁ sadāpuṣpaphalaṁ śubham.
english: It was frequented by all beings and known by the name Badarapācana. It was endowed with forests that produced flowers and fruits out of season, and it was auspicious, always bearing flowers and fruits.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 46 Verse 29. It was frequented by all beings and known by the name Badarapācana. It was endowed with forests that produced flowers and fruits out of season, and it was auspicious, always bearing flowers and fruits.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्तीर्थवरं रामो ययौ बदरपाचनम् ।तपस्विसिद्धचरितं यत्र कन्या धृतव्रता ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tatastīrthavaraṁ rāmo yayau badarapācanam ,tapasvisiddhacaritaṁ yatra kanyā dhṛtavratā.
english: Vaiśampayana said: Then Rama went to Badarapācana, that excellent holy place (tīrtha), which was frequented by ascetics and perfected beings (siddhas), and where a maiden who had undertaken a vow (vrata) resided.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 1. Vaiśampayana said: Then Rama went to Badarapācana, that excellent holy place (tīrtha), which was frequented by ascetics and perfected beings (siddhas), and where a maiden who had undertaken a vow (vrata) resided.

sanskrit: भरद्वाजस्य दुहिता रूपेणाप्रतिमा भुवि ।स्रुचावती नाम विभो कुमारी ब्रह्मचारिणी ॥२॥
transliteration: bharadvājasya duhitā rūpeṇāpratimā bhuvi ,srucāvatī nāma vibho kumārī brahmacāriṇī.
english: O lord (vibho), she was Srucāvatī by name, the daughter of Bharadvaja, a maiden observing celibacy (brahmacāriṇī), whose beauty was unequalled on earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 2. O lord (vibho), she was Srucāvatī by name, the daughter of Bharadvaja, a maiden observing celibacy (brahmacāriṇī), whose beauty was unequalled on earth.

sanskrit: तपश्चचार सात्युग्रं नियमैर्बहुभिर्नृप ।भर्ता मे देवराजः स्यादिति निश्चित्य भामिनी ॥३॥
transliteration: tapaścacāra sātyugraṁ niyamairbahubhirnṛpa ,bhartā me devarājaḥ syāditi niścitya bhāminī.
english: O king (nṛpa), that beautiful woman (bhāminī) resolved, 'My husband should be the king of the gods,' and having thus determined, she performed extremely severe asceticism (tapas) with many strict observances.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 3. O king (nṛpa), that beautiful woman (bhāminī) resolved, 'My husband should be the king of the gods,' and having thus determined, she performed extremely severe asceticism (tapas) with many strict observances.

sanskrit: समास्तस्या व्यतिक्रान्ता बह्व्यः कुरुकुलोद्वह ।चरन्त्या नियमांस्तांस्तान्स्त्रीभिस्तीव्रान्सुदुश्चरान् ॥४॥
transliteration: samāstasyā vyatikrāntā bahvyaḥ kurukulodvaha ,carantyā niyamāṁstāṁstānstrībhistīvrānsuduścarān.
english: O upholder of the Kuru lineage, many years passed for her, as she, accompanied by other women, was observing those various severe and extremely difficult vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 4. O upholder of the Kuru lineage, many years passed for her, as she, accompanied by other women, was observing those various severe and extremely difficult vows.

sanskrit: तस्यास्तु तेन वृत्तेन तपसा च विशां पते ।भक्त्या च भगवान्प्रीतः परया पाकशासनः ॥५॥
transliteration: tasyāstu tena vṛttena tapasā ca viśāṁ pate ,bhaktyā ca bhagavānprītaḥ parayā pākaśāsanaḥ.
english: But for her, O lord of the people, Lord Pākaśāsana (Indra) was pleased by that conduct, and by her asceticism (tapas), and by her supreme devotion (bhakti).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 5. But for her, O lord of the people, Lord Pākaśāsana (Indra) was pleased by that conduct, and by her asceticism (tapas), and by her supreme devotion (bhakti).

sanskrit: आजगामाश्रमं तस्यास्त्रिदशाधिपतिः प्रभुः ।आस्थाय रूपं विप्रर्षेर्वसिष्ठस्य महात्मनः ॥६॥
transliteration: ājagāmāśramaṁ tasyāstridaśādhipatiḥ prabhuḥ ,āsthāya rūpaṁ viprarṣervasiṣṭhasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: The mighty lord, the ruler of the gods, came to her hermitage, having assumed the form of the great-souled (mahātman) Brahmin sage Vasiṣṭha.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 6. The mighty lord, the ruler of the gods, came to her hermitage, having assumed the form of the great-souled (mahātman) Brahmin sage Vasiṣṭha.

sanskrit: सा तं दृष्ट्वोग्रतपसं वसिष्ठं तपतां वरम् ।आचारैर्मुनिभिर्दृष्टैः पूजयामास भारत ॥७॥
transliteration: sā taṁ dṛṣṭvogratapasaṁ vasiṣṭhaṁ tapatāṁ varam ,ācārairmunibhirdṛṣṭaiḥ pūjayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, when she saw him – Vasiṣṭha, who was of fierce asceticism (tapas) and the best among ascetics – she worshipped him with customary rites (ācāra) as observed by sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 7. O descendant of Bharata, when she saw him – Vasiṣṭha, who was of fierce asceticism (tapas) and the best among ascetics – she worshipped him with customary rites (ācāra) as observed by sages.

sanskrit: उवाच नियमज्ञा च कल्याणी सा प्रियंवदा ।भगवन्मुनिशार्दूल किमाज्ञापयसि प्रभो ॥८॥
transliteration: uvāca niyamajñā ca kalyāṇī sā priyaṁvadā ,bhagavanmuniśārdūla kimājñāpayasi prabho.
english: And she, auspicious and sweet-speaking, who understood the sacred observances, spoke: "O revered one, O tiger among sages, O lord, what do you command?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 8. And she, auspicious and sweet-speaking, who understood the sacred observances, spoke: "O revered one, O tiger among sages, O lord, what do you command?"

sanskrit: सर्वमद्य यथाशक्ति तव दास्यामि सुव्रत ।शक्रभक्त्या तु ते पाणिं न दास्यामि कथंचन ॥९॥
transliteration: sarvamadya yathāśakti tava dāsyāmi suvrata ,śakrabhaktyā tu te pāṇiṁ na dāsyāmi kathaṁcana.
english: O virtuous one, I will give you everything today according to my capacity. However, due to my devotion (bhakti) to Indra, I will never give you my hand by any means.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 9. O virtuous one, I will give you everything today according to my capacity. However, due to my devotion (bhakti) to Indra, I will never give you my hand by any means.

sanskrit: व्रतैश्च नियमैश्चैव तपसा च तपोधन ।शक्रस्तोषयितव्यो वै मया त्रिभुवनेश्वरः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vrataiśca niyamaiścaiva tapasā ca tapodhana ,śakrastoṣayitavyo vai mayā tribhuvaneśvaraḥ.
english: O ascetic (tapodhana), Indra, the lord of the three worlds, must surely be propitiated by me through vows, observances, and spiritual austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 10. O ascetic (tapodhana), Indra, the lord of the three worlds, must surely be propitiated by me through vows, observances, and spiritual austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो भगवान्देवः स्मयन्निव निरीक्ष्य ताम् ।उवाच नियमज्ञां तां सान्त्वयन्निव भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: ityukto bhagavāndevaḥ smayanniva nirīkṣya tām ,uvāca niyamajñāṁ tāṁ sāntvayanniva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, having been addressed thus, the revered god, as if smiling, looked at her and spoke to that woman, who understood the sacred observances, as if comforting her.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 11. O Bhārata, having been addressed thus, the revered god, as if smiling, looked at her and spoke to that woman, who understood the sacred observances, as if comforting her.

sanskrit: उग्रं तपश्चरसि वै विदिता मेऽसि सुव्रते ।यदर्थमयमारम्भस्तव कल्याणि हृद्गतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: ugraṁ tapaścarasi vai viditā me'si suvrate ,yadarthamayamārambhastava kalyāṇi hṛdgataḥ.
english: O virtuous one, you are indeed performing severe austerities (tapas), and I know the purpose for which this endeavor, cherished in your heart, has been undertaken by you, O auspicious one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 12. O virtuous one, you are indeed performing severe austerities (tapas), and I know the purpose for which this endeavor, cherished in your heart, has been undertaken by you, O auspicious one.

sanskrit: तच्च सर्वं यथाभूतं भविष्यति वरानने ।तपसा लभ्यते सर्वं सर्वं तपसि तिष्ठति ॥१३॥
transliteration: tacca sarvaṁ yathābhūtaṁ bhaviṣyati varānane ,tapasā labhyate sarvaṁ sarvaṁ tapasi tiṣṭhati.
english: And all that will come to pass as it truly is, O beautiful-faced one. Everything is attained through austerity (tapas); everything rests in austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 13. And all that will come to pass as it truly is, O beautiful-faced one. Everything is attained through austerity (tapas); everything rests in austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: यानि स्थानानि दिव्यानि विबुधानां शुभानने ।तपसा तानि प्राप्यानि तपोमूलं महत्सुखम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: yāni sthānāni divyāni vibudhānāṁ śubhānane ,tapasā tāni prāpyāni tapomūlaṁ mahatsukham.
english: O auspicious-faced one, whatever divine realms belong to the gods, those are to be attained through austerity (tapas). Great happiness has its root in austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 14. O auspicious-faced one, whatever divine realms belong to the gods, those are to be attained through austerity (tapas). Great happiness has its root in austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: इह कृत्वा तपो घोरं देहं संन्यस्य मानवाः ।देवत्वं यान्ति कल्याणि शृणु चेदं वचो मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: iha kṛtvā tapo ghoraṁ dehaṁ saṁnyasya mānavāḥ ,devatvaṁ yānti kalyāṇi śṛṇu cedaṁ vaco mama.
english: O auspicious one, having performed fierce austerity (tapas) in this world and having renounced their bodies, human beings attain divinity. Now, listen to this word of mine.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 15. O auspicious one, having performed fierce austerity (tapas) in this world and having renounced their bodies, human beings attain divinity. Now, listen to this word of mine.

sanskrit: पचस्वैतानि सुभगे बदराणि शुभव्रते ।पचेत्युक्त्वा स भगवाञ्जगाम बलसूदनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: pacasvaitāni subhage badarāṇi śubhavrate ,pacetyuktvā sa bhagavāñjagāma balasūdanaḥ.
english: O beautiful one, O lady of auspicious vows, cook these berries! Having said 'cook!', that divine lord (bhagavān), the slayer of Bala, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 16. O beautiful one, O lady of auspicious vows, cook these berries! Having said 'cook!', that divine lord (bhagavān), the slayer of Bala, departed.

sanskrit: आमन्त्र्य तां तु कल्याणीं ततो जप्यं जजाप सः ।अविदूरे ततस्तस्मादाश्रमात्तीर्थ उत्तमे ।इन्द्रतीर्थे महाराज त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुते ॥१७॥
transliteration: āmantrya tāṁ tu kalyāṇīṁ tato japyaṁ jajāpa saḥ ,avidūre tatastasmādāśramāttīrtha uttame ,indratīrthe mahārāja triṣu lokeṣu viśrute.
english: Having bid farewell to that auspicious lady, he then recited a mantra (japyām). Not far from that hermitage, O great king, at an excellent sacred bathing place (tīrtha) named Indratīrtha, which is renowned throughout the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 17. Having bid farewell to that auspicious lady, he then recited a mantra (japyām). Not far from that hermitage, O great king, at an excellent sacred bathing place (tīrtha) named Indratīrtha, which is renowned throughout the three worlds.

sanskrit: तस्या जिज्ञासनार्थं स भगवान्पाकशासनः ।बदराणामपचनं चकार विबुधाधिपः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasyā jijñāsanārthaṁ sa bhagavānpākaśāsanaḥ ,badarāṇāmapacanaṁ cakāra vibudhādhipaḥ.
english: For the purpose of testing her, that divine lord (bhagavān), the subduer of Pāka and chief of the gods, made the berries impossible to cook.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 18. For the purpose of testing her, that divine lord (bhagavān), the subduer of Pāka and chief of the gods, made the berries impossible to cook.

sanskrit: ततः स प्रयता राजन्वाग्यता विगतक्लमा ।तत्परा शुचिसंवीता पावके समधिश्रयत् ।अपचद्राजशार्दूल बदराणि महाव्रता ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa prayatā rājanvāgyatā vigataklamā ,tatparā śucisaṁvītā pāvake samadhiśrayat ,apacadrājaśārdūla badarāṇi mahāvratā.
english: Then, O King, that self-controlled lady, pure, disciplined in speech, free from weariness, intently devoted to that task, and clad in purity, applied herself to the fire. She, the lady of great vows, cooked the berries, O tiger among kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 19. Then, O King, that self-controlled lady, pure, disciplined in speech, free from weariness, intently devoted to that task, and clad in purity, applied herself to the fire. She, the lady of great vows, cooked the berries, O tiger among kings.

sanskrit: तस्याः पचन्त्याः सुमहान्कालोऽगात्पुरुषर्षभ ।न च स्म तान्यपच्यन्त दिनं च क्षयमभ्यगात् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasyāḥ pacantyāḥ sumahānkālo'gātpuruṣarṣabha ,na ca sma tānyapacyanta dinaṁ ca kṣayamabhyagāt.
english: O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), a very long time passed as she cooked. However, the items remained uncooked, and the day came to an end.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 20. O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), a very long time passed as she cooked. However, the items remained uncooked, and the day came to an end.

sanskrit: हुताशनेन दग्धश्च यस्तस्याः काष्ठसंचयः ।अकाष्ठमग्निं सा दृष्ट्वा स्वशरीरमथादहत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: hutāśanena dagdhaśca yastasyāḥ kāṣṭhasaṁcayaḥ ,akāṣṭhamagniṁ sā dṛṣṭvā svaśarīramathādahat.
english: The pile of wood she had gathered was also consumed by the fire. When she saw the fire burning without any fuel (wood), she then offered her own body to it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 21. The pile of wood she had gathered was also consumed by the fire. When she saw the fire burning without any fuel (wood), she then offered her own body to it.

sanskrit: पादौ प्रक्षिप्य सा पूर्वं पावके चारुदर्शना ।दग्धौ दग्धौ पुनः पादावुपावर्तयतानघा ॥२२॥
transliteration: pādau prakṣipya sā pūrvaṁ pāvake cārudarśanā ,dagdhau dagdhau punaḥ pādāvupāvartayatānaghā.
english: O sinless one (anaghā), she, beautiful to behold (cārudarśanā), first placed her feet into the fire. As they were burnt, she repeatedly withdrew them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 22. O sinless one (anaghā), she, beautiful to behold (cārudarśanā), first placed her feet into the fire. As they were burnt, she repeatedly withdrew them.

sanskrit: चरणौ दह्यमानौ च नाचिन्तयदनिन्दिता ।दुःखं कमलपत्राक्षी महर्षेः प्रियकाम्यया ॥२३॥
transliteration: caraṇau dahyamānau ca nācintayadaninditā ,duḥkhaṁ kamalapatrākṣī maharṣeḥ priyakāmyayā.
english: And the blameless (aninditā), lotus-petal-eyed (kamalapatrākṣī) woman did not consider the suffering (duḥkha) even as her feet were being burnt, all for the sake of pleasing the great sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 23. And the blameless (aninditā), lotus-petal-eyed (kamalapatrākṣī) woman did not consider the suffering (duḥkha) even as her feet were being burnt, all for the sake of pleasing the great sage.

sanskrit: अथ तत्कर्म दृष्ट्वास्याः प्रीतस्त्रिभुवनेश्वरः ।ततः संदर्शयामास कन्यायै रूपमात्मनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: atha tatkarma dṛṣṭvāsyāḥ prītastribhuvaneśvaraḥ ,tataḥ saṁdarśayāmāsa kanyāyai rūpamātmanaḥ.
english: Then, the Lord of the three worlds, pleased upon seeing her action (karma), thereupon revealed his own divine form (ātman) to the maiden.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 24. Then, the Lord of the three worlds, pleased upon seeing her action (karma), thereupon revealed his own divine form (ātman) to the maiden.

sanskrit: उवाच च सुरश्रेष्ठस्तां कन्यां सुदृढव्रताम् ।प्रीतोऽस्मि ते शुभे भक्त्या तपसा नियमेन च ॥२५॥
transliteration: uvāca ca suraśreṣṭhastāṁ kanyāṁ sudṛḍhavratām ,prīto'smi te śubhe bhaktyā tapasā niyamena ca.
english: And the chief of the gods said to that maiden, who was firm in her vow: 'O auspicious one, I am pleased with you because of your devotion (bhakti), austerity (tapas), and discipline.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 25. And the chief of the gods said to that maiden, who was firm in her vow: 'O auspicious one, I am pleased with you because of your devotion (bhakti), austerity (tapas), and discipline.'

sanskrit: तस्माद्योऽभिमतः कामः स ते संपत्स्यते शुभे ।देहं त्यक्त्वा महाभागे त्रिदिवे मयि वत्स्यसि ॥२६॥
transliteration: tasmādyo'bhimataḥ kāmaḥ sa te saṁpatsyate śubhe ,dehaṁ tyaktvā mahābhāge tridive mayi vatsyasi.
english: Therefore, O auspicious one, your desired wish will be fulfilled for you. O greatly fortunate one, having abandoned your body, you will reside with me in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 26. Therefore, O auspicious one, your desired wish will be fulfilled for you. O greatly fortunate one, having abandoned your body, you will reside with me in heaven.

sanskrit: इदं च ते तीर्थवरं स्थिरं लोके भविष्यति ।सर्वपापापहं सुभ्रु नाम्ना बदरपाचनम् ।विख्यातं त्रिषु लोकेषु ब्रह्मर्षिभिरभिप्लुतम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: idaṁ ca te tīrthavaraṁ sthiraṁ loke bhaviṣyati ,sarvapāpāpahaṁ subhru nāmnā badarapācanam ,vikhyātaṁ triṣu lokeṣu brahmarṣibhirabhiplutam.
english: And, O beautiful-browed one, this supreme holy place of yours will remain eternal in the world. Named 'Badarapācana,' it will remove all sins, be renowned in the three worlds, and be frequented by Brahma-sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 27. And, O beautiful-browed one, this supreme holy place of yours will remain eternal in the world. Named 'Badarapācana,' it will remove all sins, be renowned in the three worlds, and be frequented by Brahma-sages.

sanskrit: अस्मिन्खलु महाभागे शुभे तीर्थवरे पुरा ।त्यक्त्वा सप्तर्षयो जग्मुर्हिमवन्तमरुन्धतीम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: asminkhalu mahābhāge śubhe tīrthavare purā ,tyaktvā saptarṣayo jagmurhimavantamarundhatīm.
english: Indeed, in the past, the Seven Sages, accompanied by Arundhatī, abandoned this highly auspicious and excellent sacred place and went to the Himalayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 28. Indeed, in the past, the Seven Sages, accompanied by Arundhatī, abandoned this highly auspicious and excellent sacred place and went to the Himalayas.

sanskrit: ततस्ते वै महाभागा गत्वा तत्र सुसंशिताः ।वृत्त्यर्थं फलमूलानि समाहर्तुं ययुः किल ॥२९॥
transliteration: tataste vai mahābhāgā gatvā tatra susaṁśitāḥ ,vṛttyarthaṁ phalamūlāni samāhartuṁ yayuḥ kila.
english: Then, those illustrious and well-disciplined sages, having arrived there, indeed went to gather fruits and roots for their sustenance, so it is said.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 29. Then, those illustrious and well-disciplined sages, having arrived there, indeed went to gather fruits and roots for their sustenance, so it is said.

sanskrit: तेषां वृत्त्यर्थिनां तत्र वसतां हिमवद्वने ।अनावृष्टिरनुप्राप्ता तदा द्वादशवार्षिकी ॥३०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ vṛttyarthināṁ tatra vasatāṁ himavadvane ,anāvṛṣṭiranuprāptā tadā dvādaśavārṣikī.
english: As they (the Sages) resided there in the Himalayan forest, striving for their livelihood, a twelve-year-long drought then afflicted them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 30. As they (the Sages) resided there in the Himalayan forest, striving for their livelihood, a twelve-year-long drought then afflicted them.

sanskrit: ते कृत्वा चाश्रमं तत्र न्यवसन्त तपस्विनः ।अरुन्धत्यपि कल्याणी तपोनित्याभवत्तदा ॥३१॥
transliteration: te kṛtvā cāśramaṁ tatra nyavasanta tapasvinaḥ ,arundhatyapi kalyāṇī taponityābhavattadā.
english: And there, those ascetics, having built a hermitage (āśrama), resided. At that time, the blessed Arundhatī also became constantly devoted to austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 31. And there, those ascetics, having built a hermitage (āśrama), resided. At that time, the blessed Arundhatī also became constantly devoted to austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: अरुन्धतीं ततो दृष्ट्वा तीव्रं नियममास्थिताम् ।अथागमत्त्रिनयनः सुप्रीतो वरदस्तदा ॥३२॥
transliteration: arundhatīṁ tato dṛṣṭvā tīvraṁ niyamamāsthitām ,athāgamattrinayanaḥ suprīto varadastadā.
english: Then, having seen Arundhati observing a severe vow, the three-eyed god (Shiva), who was very pleased and a giver of boons, arrived at that moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 32. Then, having seen Arundhati observing a severe vow, the three-eyed god (Shiva), who was very pleased and a giver of boons, arrived at that moment.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मं रूपं ततः कृत्वा महादेवो महायशाः ।तामभ्येत्याब्रवीद्देवो भिक्षामिच्छाम्यहं शुभे ॥३३॥
transliteration: brāhmaṁ rūpaṁ tataḥ kṛtvā mahādevo mahāyaśāḥ ,tāmabhyetyābravīddevo bhikṣāmicchāmyahaṁ śubhe.
english: Then, the greatly glorious Mahadeva, assuming a divine form and approaching her, said, "O auspicious one, I desire alms."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 33. Then, the greatly glorious Mahadeva, assuming a divine form and approaching her, said, "O auspicious one, I desire alms."

sanskrit: प्रत्युवाच ततः सा तं ब्राह्मणं चारुदर्शना ।क्षीणोऽन्नसंचयो विप्र बदराणीह भक्षय ।ततोऽब्रवीन्महादेवः पचस्वैतानि सुव्रते ॥३४॥
transliteration: pratyuvāca tataḥ sā taṁ brāhmaṇaṁ cārudarśanā ,kṣīṇo'nnasaṁcayo vipra badarāṇīha bhakṣaya ,tato'bravīnmahādevaḥ pacasvaitāni suvrate.
english: Then, Arundhati, who was beautiful to behold, replied to that Brahmin: "O Brahmin, my store of food is depleted. Eat these berries here instead." Then Mahadeva said, "O observer of good vows, please cook these (berries)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 34. Then, Arundhati, who was beautiful to behold, replied to that Brahmin: "O Brahmin, my store of food is depleted. Eat these berries here instead." Then Mahadeva said, "O observer of good vows, please cook these (berries)."

sanskrit: इत्युक्ता सापचत्तानि ब्राह्मणप्रियकाम्यया ।अधिश्रित्य समिद्धेऽग्नौ बदराणि यशस्विनी ॥३५॥
transliteration: ityuktā sāpacattāni brāhmaṇapriyakāmyayā ,adhiśritya samiddhe'gnau badarāṇi yaśasvinī.
english: Thus addressed, the glorious Arundhati, desiring to please the Brahmin, placed those berries on the blazing fire and cooked them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 35. Thus addressed, the glorious Arundhati, desiring to please the Brahmin, placed those berries on the blazing fire and cooked them.

sanskrit: दिव्या मनोरमाः पुण्याः कथाः शुश्राव सा तदा ।अतीता सा त्वनावृष्टिर्घोरा द्वादशवार्षिकी ॥३६॥
transliteration: divyā manoramāḥ puṇyāḥ kathāḥ śuśrāva sā tadā ,atītā sā tvanāvṛṣṭirghorā dvādaśavārṣikī.
english: She then listened to divine, delightful, and sacred stories. That terrible twelve-year drought had (already) passed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 36. She then listened to divine, delightful, and sacred stories. That terrible twelve-year drought had (already) passed.

sanskrit: अनश्नन्त्याः पचन्त्याश्च शृण्वन्त्याश्च कथाः शुभाः ।अहःसमः स तस्यास्तु कालोऽतीतः सुदारुणः ॥३७॥
transliteration: anaśnantyāḥ pacantyāśca śṛṇvantyāśca kathāḥ śubhāḥ ,ahaḥsamaḥ sa tasyāstu kālo'tītaḥ sudāruṇaḥ.
english: While she was neither eating nor cooking, but was listening to auspicious stories, that extremely terrible time, which seemed to pass as quickly as a single day, passed for her.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 37. While she was neither eating nor cooking, but was listening to auspicious stories, that extremely terrible time, which seemed to pass as quickly as a single day, passed for her.

sanskrit: ततस्ते मुनयः प्राप्ताः फलान्यादाय पर्वतात् ।ततः स भगवान्प्रीतः प्रोवाचारुन्धतीं तदा ॥३८॥
transliteration: tataste munayaḥ prāptāḥ phalānyādāya parvatāt ,tataḥ sa bhagavānprītaḥ provācārundhatīṁ tadā.
english: Then, those sages (muni) arrived, bringing fruits from the mountain. Subsequently, that divine (bhagavān) being, pleased, then spoke to Arundhatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 38. Then, those sages (muni) arrived, bringing fruits from the mountain. Subsequently, that divine (bhagavān) being, pleased, then spoke to Arundhatī.

sanskrit: उपसर्पस्व धर्मज्ञे यथापूर्वमिमानृषीन् ।प्रीतोऽस्मि तव धर्मज्ञे तपसा नियमेन च ॥३९॥
transliteration: upasarpasva dharmajñe yathāpūrvamimānṛṣīn ,prīto'smi tava dharmajñe tapasā niyamena ca.
english: O knower of natural law (dharma), approach these sages (ṛṣi) as before. O knower of natural law (dharma), I am pleased with your austerity (tapas) and discipline.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 39. O knower of natural law (dharma), approach these sages (ṛṣi) as before. O knower of natural law (dharma), I am pleased with your austerity (tapas) and discipline.

sanskrit: ततः संदर्शयामास स्वरूपं भगवान्हरः ।ततोऽब्रवीत्तदा तेभ्यस्तस्यास्तच्चरितं महत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁdarśayāmāsa svarūpaṁ bhagavānharaḥ ,tato'bravīttadā tebhyastasyāstaccaritaṁ mahat.
english: Then the divine Hara (Śiva) revealed his own true nature. Thereafter, at that time, he spoke to them about her great conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 40. Then the divine Hara (Śiva) revealed his own true nature. Thereafter, at that time, he spoke to them about her great conduct.

sanskrit: भवद्भिर्हिमवत्पृष्ठे यत्तपः समुपार्जितम् ।अस्याश्च यत्तपो विप्रा न समं तन्मतं मम ॥४१॥
transliteration: bhavadbhirhimavatpṛṣṭhe yattapaḥ samupārjitam ,asyāśca yattapo viprā na samaṁ tanmataṁ mama.
english: O sages (viprāḥ), the spiritual merit (tapas) you have accumulated on the slopes of the Himalayas is not, in my opinion, equal to the spiritual merit (tapas) of this lady.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 41. O sages (viprāḥ), the spiritual merit (tapas) you have accumulated on the slopes of the Himalayas is not, in my opinion, equal to the spiritual merit (tapas) of this lady.

sanskrit: अनया हि तपस्विन्या तपस्तप्तं सुदुश्चरम् ।अनश्नन्त्या पचन्त्या च समा द्वादश पारिताः ॥४२॥
transliteration: anayā hi tapasvinyā tapastaptaṁ suduścaram ,anaśnantyā pacantyā ca samā dvādaśa pāritāḥ.
english: Indeed, this female ascetic performed an extremely arduous austerity (tapas). By not eating and not cooking (for herself), she completed twelve years.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 42. Indeed, this female ascetic performed an extremely arduous austerity (tapas). By not eating and not cooking (for herself), she completed twelve years.

sanskrit: ततः प्रोवाच भगवांस्तामेवारुन्धतीं पुनः ।वरं वृणीष्व कल्याणि यत्तेऽभिलषितं हृदि ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ provāca bhagavāṁstāmevārundhatīṁ punaḥ ,varaṁ vṛṇīṣva kalyāṇi yatte'bhilaṣitaṁ hṛdi.
english: Then the divine lord (Bhagavān) spoke again, specifically to Arundhatī: "O auspicious one, choose whatever boon is desired in your heart."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 43. Then the divine lord (Bhagavān) spoke again, specifically to Arundhatī: "O auspicious one, choose whatever boon is desired in your heart."

sanskrit: साब्रवीत्पृथुताम्राक्षी देवं सप्तर्षिसंसदि ।भगवान्यदि मे प्रीतस्तीर्थं स्यादिदमुत्तमम् ।सिद्धदेवर्षिदयितं नाम्ना बदरपाचनम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: sābravītpṛthutāmrākṣī devaṁ saptarṣisaṁsadi ,bhagavānyadi me prītastīrthaṁ syādidamuttamam ,siddhadevarṣidayitaṁ nāmnā badarapācanam.
english: The one with broad, coppery eyes spoke to the god in the assembly of the seven sages: "O Lord, if you are pleased with me, may this excellent sacred place (tīrtha), beloved by perfected beings (siddha), gods, and sages, be known by the name Badarapācana."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 44. The one with broad, coppery eyes spoke to the god in the assembly of the seven sages: "O Lord, if you are pleased with me, may this excellent sacred place (tīrtha), beloved by perfected beings (siddha), gods, and sages, be known by the name Badarapācana."

sanskrit: तथास्मिन्देवदेवेश त्रिरात्रमुषितः शुचिः ।प्राप्नुयादुपवासेन फलं द्वादशवार्षिकम् ।एवमस्त्विति तां चोक्त्वा हरो यातस्तदा दिवम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tathāsmindevadeveśa trirātramuṣitaḥ śuciḥ ,prāpnuyādupavāsena phalaṁ dvādaśavārṣikam ,evamastviti tāṁ coktvā haro yātastadā divam.
english: "O Lord of gods, similarly, whoever stays here for three nights, pure, and fasts, may that person obtain the fruit of twelve years (of devotion)." And having said to her, "So be it!" Hara (Śiva) then went to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 45. "O Lord of gods, similarly, whoever stays here for three nights, pure, and fasts, may that person obtain the fruit of twelve years (of devotion)." And having said to her, "So be it!" Hara (Śiva) then went to heaven.

sanskrit: ऋषयो विस्मयं जग्मुस्तां दृष्ट्वा चाप्यरुन्धतीम् ।अश्रान्तां चाविवर्णां च क्षुत्पिपासासहां सतीम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: ṛṣayo vismayaṁ jagmustāṁ dṛṣṭvā cāpyarundhatīm ,aśrāntāṁ cāvivarṇāṁ ca kṣutpipāsāsahāṁ satīm.
english: The sages were amazed, having seen Arundhatī, who was unwearied, unchanged in complexion, and enduring hunger and thirst.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 46. The sages were amazed, having seen Arundhatī, who was unwearied, unchanged in complexion, and enduring hunger and thirst.

sanskrit: एवं सिद्धिः परा प्राप्ता अरुन्धत्या विशुद्धया ।यथा त्वया महाभागे मदर्थं संशितव्रते ॥४७॥
transliteration: evaṁ siddhiḥ parā prāptā arundhatyā viśuddhayā ,yathā tvayā mahābhāge madarthaṁ saṁśitavrate.
english: Thus, supreme perfection (siddhi) was attained by the pure Arundhatī, just as it was attained by you, O fortunate one, O one with firm vows, for my sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 47. Thus, supreme perfection (siddhi) was attained by the pure Arundhatī, just as it was attained by you, O fortunate one, O one with firm vows, for my sake.

sanskrit: विशेषो हि त्वया भद्रे व्रते ह्यस्मिन्समर्पितः ।तथा चेदं ददाम्यद्य नियमेन सुतोषितः ॥४८॥
transliteration: viśeṣo hi tvayā bhadre vrate hyasminsamarpitaḥ ,tathā cedaṁ dadāmyadya niyamena sutoṣitaḥ.
english: Indeed, O auspicious one, an extraordinary offering has been made by you in this observance. And so, being greatly pleased by your discipline (niyama), I give this today.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 48. Indeed, O auspicious one, an extraordinary offering has been made by you in this observance. And so, being greatly pleased by your discipline (niyama), I give this today.

sanskrit: विशेषं तव कल्याणि प्रयच्छामि वरं वरे ।अरुन्धत्या वरस्तस्या यो दत्तो वै महात्मना ॥४९॥
transliteration: viśeṣaṁ tava kalyāṇi prayacchāmi varaṁ vare ,arundhatyā varastasyā yo datto vai mahātmanā.
english: O auspicious and excellent one, I grant you a special boon - that very boon which was indeed given by the great soul (mahātman) to Arundhati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 49. O auspicious and excellent one, I grant you a special boon - that very boon which was indeed given by the great soul (mahātman) to Arundhati.

sanskrit: तस्य चाहं प्रसादेन तव कल्याणि तेजसा ।प्रवक्ष्याम्यपरं भूयो वरमत्र यथाविधि ॥५०॥
transliteration: tasya cāhaṁ prasādena tava kalyāṇi tejasā ,pravakṣyāmyaparaṁ bhūyo varamatra yathāvidhi.
english: And by his grace and your splendor, O auspicious one, I shall again, in due form, declare another boon here.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 50. And by his grace and your splendor, O auspicious one, I shall again, in due form, declare another boon here.

sanskrit: यस्त्वेकां रजनीं तीर्थे वत्स्यते सुसमाहितः ।स स्नात्वा प्राप्स्यते लोकान्देहन्यासाच्च दुर्लभान् ॥५१॥
transliteration: yastvekāṁ rajanīṁ tīrthe vatsyate susamāhitaḥ ,sa snātvā prāpsyate lokāndehanyāsācca durlabhān.
english: Whoever, being perfectly composed (dhyāna), resides for one night in a sacred bathing place (tīrtha), he, having bathed, will attain worlds that are difficult to obtain even by abandoning the body.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 51. Whoever, being perfectly composed (dhyāna), resides for one night in a sacred bathing place (tīrtha), he, having bathed, will attain worlds that are difficult to obtain even by abandoning the body.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा भगवान्देवः सहस्राक्षः प्रतापवान् ।स्रुचावतीं ततः पुण्यां जगाम त्रिदिवं पुनः ॥५२॥
transliteration: ityuktvā bhagavāndevaḥ sahasrākṣaḥ pratāpavān ,srucāvatīṁ tataḥ puṇyāṁ jagāma tridivaṁ punaḥ.
english: Having thus spoken, the powerful, thousand-eyed Lord (deva) then proceeded to the sacred Srucāvatī and returned again to heaven (tridivam).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 52. Having thus spoken, the powerful, thousand-eyed Lord (deva) then proceeded to the sacred Srucāvatī and returned again to heaven (tridivam).

sanskrit: गते वज्रधरे राजंस्तत्र वर्षं पपात ह ।पुष्पाणां भरतश्रेष्ठ दिव्यानां दिव्यगन्धिनाम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: gate vajradhare rājaṁstatra varṣaṁ papāta ha ,puṣpāṇāṁ bharataśreṣṭha divyānāṁ divyagandhinām.
