english: O King (rājaṃs), O best of Bharatas (bharataśreṣṭha), when the thunderbolt-bearer (vajradhara) had departed, a shower of divine, divinely fragrant flowers indeed fell there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 53. O King (rājaṃs), O best of Bharatas (bharataśreṣṭha), when the thunderbolt-bearer (vajradhara) had departed, a shower of divine, divinely fragrant flowers indeed fell there.

sanskrit: नेदुर्दुन्दुभयश्चापि समन्तात्सुमहास्वनाः ।मारुतश्च ववौ युक्त्या पुण्यगन्धो विशां पते ॥५४॥
transliteration: nedurdundubhayaścāpi samantātsumahāsvanāḥ ,mārutaśca vavau yuktyā puṇyagandho viśāṁ pate.
english: And drums with very loud sounds resounded from all sides. Also, O lord of the people (viśām pate), the wind blew appropriately, bearing a sacred fragrance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 54. And drums with very loud sounds resounded from all sides. Also, O lord of the people (viśām pate), the wind blew appropriately, bearing a sacred fragrance.

sanskrit: उत्सृज्य तु शुभं देहं जगामेन्द्रस्य भार्यताम् ।तपसोग्रेण सा लब्ध्वा तेन रेमे सहाच्युत ॥५५॥
transliteration: utsṛjya tu śubhaṁ dehaṁ jagāmendrasya bhāryatām ,tapasogreṇa sā labdhvā tena reme sahācyuta.
english: But having abandoned her beautiful body, she became Indra's wife. Having attained that through severe asceticism (tapas), she rejoiced with him, O Acyuta (acyuta).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 55. But having abandoned her beautiful body, she became Indra's wife. Having attained that through severe asceticism (tapas), she rejoiced with him, O Acyuta (acyuta).

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।का तस्या भगवन्माता क्व संवृद्धा च शोभना ।श्रोतुमिच्छाम्यहं ब्रह्मन्परं कौतूहलं हि मे ॥५६॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kā tasyā bhagavanmātā kva saṁvṛddhā ca śobhanā ,śrotumicchāmyahaṁ brahmanparaṁ kautūhalaṁ hi me.
english: King Janamejaya said: "O revered one, who was her mother, and where was that beautiful lady raised? O Brahmin, I wish to hear, for I have great curiosity."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 56. King Janamejaya said: "O revered one, who was her mother, and where was that beautiful lady raised? O Brahmin, I wish to hear, for I have great curiosity."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।भारद्वाजस्य विप्रर्षेः स्कन्नं रेतो महात्मनः ।दृष्ट्वाप्सरसमायान्तीं घृताचीं पृथुलोचनाम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,bhāradvājasya viprarṣeḥ skannaṁ reto mahātmanaḥ ,dṛṣṭvāpsarasamāyāntīṁ ghṛtācīṁ pṛthulocanām.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "The semen of the great-souled sage among Brahmins, Bhāradvāja, fell when he saw the wide-eyed Apsaras Ghṛtācī approaching."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 57. Vaiśampāyana said: "The semen of the great-souled sage among Brahmins, Bhāradvāja, fell when he saw the wide-eyed Apsaras Ghṛtācī approaching."

sanskrit: स तु जग्राह तद्रेतः करेण जपतां वरः ।तदावपत्पर्णपुटे तत्र सा संभवच्छुभा ॥५८॥
transliteration: sa tu jagrāha tadretaḥ kareṇa japatāṁ varaḥ ,tadāvapatparṇapuṭe tatra sā saṁbhavacchubhā.
english: But he, the foremost among those who recite sacred texts (japatām varaḥ), took that semen with his hand. He then placed it in a leaf-cup, and there, that beautiful girl was born.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 58. But he, the foremost among those who recite sacred texts (japatām varaḥ), took that semen with his hand. He then placed it in a leaf-cup, and there, that beautiful girl was born.

sanskrit: तस्यास्तु जातकर्मादि कृत्वा सर्वं तपोधनः ।नाम चास्याः स कृतवान्भारद्वाजो महामुनिः ॥५९॥
transliteration: tasyāstu jātakarmādi kṛtvā sarvaṁ tapodhanaḥ ,nāma cāsyāḥ sa kṛtavānbhāradvājo mahāmuniḥ.
english: The great sage Bhāradvāja, whose wealth was asceticism (tapodhana), performed all her birth rites (saṃskāra) and then gave her a name.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 59. The great sage Bhāradvāja, whose wealth was asceticism (tapodhana), performed all her birth rites (saṃskāra) and then gave her a name.

sanskrit: स्रुचावतीति धर्मात्मा तदर्षिगणसंसदि ।स च तामाश्रमे न्यस्य जगाम हिमवद्वनम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: srucāvatīti dharmātmā tadarṣigaṇasaṁsadi ,sa ca tāmāśrame nyasya jagāma himavadvanam.
english: And with reference to Srucāvatī, the man of righteousness (dharmātmā), then in that assembly of groups of sages, having placed her in the hermitage, went to the Himalayan forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 60. And with reference to Srucāvatī, the man of righteousness (dharmātmā), then in that assembly of groups of sages, having placed her in the hermitage, went to the Himalayan forest.

sanskrit: तत्राप्युपस्पृश्य महानुभावो वसूनि दत्त्वा च महाद्विजेभ्यः ।जगाम तीर्थं सुसमाहितात्मा शक्रस्य वृष्णिप्रवरस्तदानीम् ॥६१॥
transliteration: tatrāpyupaspṛśya mahānubhāvo; vasūni dattvā ca mahādvijebhyaḥ ,jagāma tīrthaṁ susamāhitātmā; śakrasya vṛṣṇipravarastadānīm.
english: There too, having performed ablutions, the magnanimous one, with a well-composed mind (ātman), and having given riches to great Brahmins, the foremost among the Vṛṣṇis then went to the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 47 Verse 61. There too, having performed ablutions, the magnanimous one, with a well-composed mind (ātman), and having given riches to great Brahmins, the foremost among the Vṛṣṇis then went to the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Indra.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इन्द्रतीर्थं ततो गत्वा यदूनां प्रवरो बली ।विप्रेभ्यो धनरत्नानि ददौ स्नात्वा यथाविधि ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,indratīrthaṁ tato gatvā yadūnāṁ pravaro balī ,viprebhyo dhanaratnāni dadau snātvā yathāvidhi.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the mighty one, the foremost among the Yadus, having gone to Indra's sacred bathing place (tīrtha) and bathed according to proper ritual, gave wealth and jewels to the Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the mighty one, the foremost among the Yadus, having gone to Indra's sacred bathing place (tīrtha) and bathed according to proper ritual, gave wealth and jewels to the Brahmins.

sanskrit: तत्र ह्यमरराजोऽसावीजे क्रतुशतेन ह ।बृहस्पतेश्च देवेशः प्रददौ विपुलं धनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tatra hyamararājo'sāvīje kratuśatena ha ,bṛhaspateśca deveśaḥ pradadau vipulaṁ dhanam.
english: Indeed, there the king of immortals (Indra) performed a hundred Vedic rituals (kratu). And this lord of the gods gave abundant wealth to Bṛhaspati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 2. Indeed, there the king of immortals (Indra) performed a hundred Vedic rituals (kratu). And this lord of the gods gave abundant wealth to Bṛhaspati.

sanskrit: निरर्गलान्सजारूथ्यान्सर्वान्विविधदक्षिणान् ।आजहार क्रतूंस्तत्र यथोक्तान्वेदपारगैः ॥३॥
transliteration: nirargalānsajārūthyānsarvānvividhadakṣiṇān ,ājahāra kratūṁstatra yathoktānvedapāragaiḥ.
english: He performed all those great sacrifices (kratu), unimpeded and well-attended, offering various gifts (dakṣiṇā), exactly as prescribed there by those who had mastered the Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 3. He performed all those great sacrifices (kratu), unimpeded and well-attended, offering various gifts (dakṣiṇā), exactly as prescribed there by those who had mastered the Vedas.

sanskrit: तान्क्रतून्भरतश्रेष्ठ शतकृत्वो महाद्युतिः ।पूरयामास विधिवत्ततः ख्यातः शतक्रतुः ॥४॥
transliteration: tānkratūnbharataśreṣṭha śatakṛtvo mahādyutiḥ ,pūrayāmāsa vidhivattataḥ khyātaḥ śatakratuḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, that greatly effulgent one, Śatakratu, completed those sacrifices (kratu) a hundred times, exactly as prescribed. Therefore, he became known as Śatakratu.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 4. O best of Bharatas, that greatly effulgent one, Śatakratu, completed those sacrifices (kratu) a hundred times, exactly as prescribed. Therefore, he became known as Śatakratu.

sanskrit: तस्य नाम्ना च तत्तीर्थं शिवं पुण्यं सनातनम् ।इन्द्रतीर्थमिति ख्यातं सर्वपापप्रमोचनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tasya nāmnā ca tattīrthaṁ śivaṁ puṇyaṁ sanātanam ,indratīrthamiti khyātaṁ sarvapāpapramocanam.
english: And by his name, that auspicious, sacred, and eternal holy place (tīrtha) became renowned as "Indratīrtha," which liberates from all sins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 5. And by his name, that auspicious, sacred, and eternal holy place (tīrtha) became renowned as "Indratīrtha," which liberates from all sins.

sanskrit: उपस्पृश्य च तत्रापि विधिवन्मुसलायुधः ।ब्राह्मणान्पूजयित्वा च पानाच्छादनभोजनैः ।शुभं तीर्थवरं तस्माद्रामतीर्थं जगाम ह ॥६॥
transliteration: upaspṛśya ca tatrāpi vidhivanmusalāyudhaḥ ,brāhmaṇānpūjayitvā ca pānācchādanabhojanaiḥ ,śubhaṁ tīrthavaraṁ tasmādrāmatīrthaṁ jagāma ha.
english: And having ritually bathed there, Musalāyudha (Balarama) also, after honoring the Brahmins with drinks, clothing, and food, then went from that place to the auspicious and excellent holy place (tīrtha) known as Rāmatīrtha.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 6. And having ritually bathed there, Musalāyudha (Balarama) also, after honoring the Brahmins with drinks, clothing, and food, then went from that place to the auspicious and excellent holy place (tīrtha) known as Rāmatīrtha.

sanskrit: यत्र रामो महाभागो भार्गवः सुमहातपाः ।असकृत्पृथिवीं सर्वां हतक्षत्रियपुंगवाम् ॥७॥
transliteration: yatra rāmo mahābhāgo bhārgavaḥ sumahātapāḥ ,asakṛtpṛthivīṁ sarvāṁ hatakṣatriyapuṁgavām.
english: Where the greatly esteemed Rama (Bhārgava), the descendant of Bhrigu, renowned for his immense austerity (tapas), repeatedly rid the entire earth of its chief Kṣatriyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 7. Where the greatly esteemed Rama (Bhārgava), the descendant of Bhrigu, renowned for his immense austerity (tapas), repeatedly rid the entire earth of its chief Kṣatriyas.

sanskrit: उपाध्यायं पुरस्कृत्य कश्यपं मुनिसत्तमम् ।अयजद्वाजपेयेन सोऽश्वमेधशतेन च ।प्रददौ दक्षिणार्थं च पृथिवीं वै ससागराम् ॥८॥
transliteration: upādhyāyaṁ puraskṛtya kaśyapaṁ munisattamam ,ayajadvājapeyena so'śvamedhaśatena ca ,pradadau dakṣiṇārthaṁ ca pṛthivīṁ vai sasāgarām.
english: Having honored Kaśyapa, the foremost among sages, as his preceptor, he performed the Vājapeya (Vedic ritual) (yajña) and a hundred Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) (yajña). And for the ritual fees, he indeed gave the earth along with its oceans.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 8. Having honored Kaśyapa, the foremost among sages, as his preceptor, he performed the Vājapeya (Vedic ritual) (yajña) and a hundred Aśvamedha (Vedic ritual) (yajña). And for the ritual fees, he indeed gave the earth along with its oceans.

sanskrit: रामो दत्त्वा धनं तत्र द्विजेभ्यो जनमेजय ।उपस्पृश्य यथान्यायं पूजयित्वा तथा द्विजान् ॥९॥
transliteration: rāmo dattvā dhanaṁ tatra dvijebhyo janamejaya ,upaspṛśya yathānyāyaṁ pūjayitvā tathā dvijān.
english: O Janamejaya, there, Rama, having given wealth to the twice-born (dvijas), and having bathed according to the proper custom, then, having likewise honored the twice-born (dvijas)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 9. O Janamejaya, there, Rama, having given wealth to the twice-born (dvijas), and having bathed according to the proper custom, then, having likewise honored the twice-born (dvijas)...

sanskrit: पुण्ये तीर्थे शुभे देशे वसु दत्त्वा शुभाननः ।मुनींश्चैवाभिवाद्याथ यमुनातीर्थमागमत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: puṇye tīrthe śubhe deśe vasu dattvā śubhānanaḥ ,munīṁścaivābhivādyātha yamunātīrthamāgamat.
english: Having given wealth in a sacred bathing place (tīrtha) and an auspicious region, he, whose face was radiant (śubhānana), then, having indeed respectfully saluted the sages, went to the sacred bathing place on the Yamuna river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 10. Having given wealth in a sacred bathing place (tīrtha) and an auspicious region, he, whose face was radiant (śubhānana), then, having indeed respectfully saluted the sages, went to the sacred bathing place on the Yamuna river.

sanskrit: यत्रानयामास तदा राजसूयं महीपते ।पुत्रोऽदितेर्महाभागो वरुणो वै सितप्रभः ॥११॥
transliteration: yatrānayāmāsa tadā rājasūyaṁ mahīpate ,putro'ditermahābhāgo varuṇo vai sitaprabhaḥ.
english: There, at that time, Varuṇa, the greatly fortunate son of Aditi, gleaming with white splendor, caused the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) (yajña) to be performed, O king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 11. There, at that time, Varuṇa, the greatly fortunate son of Aditi, gleaming with white splendor, caused the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) (yajña) to be performed, O king.

sanskrit: तत्र निर्जित्य संग्रामे मानुषान्दैवतांस्तथा ।वरं क्रतुं समाजह्रे वरुणः परवीरहा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatra nirjitya saṁgrāme mānuṣāndaivatāṁstathā ,varaṁ kratuṁ samājahre varuṇaḥ paravīrahā.
english: Then, having conquered both humans and gods in battle, Varuna, the vanquisher of enemy heroes, performed an excellent Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 12. Then, having conquered both humans and gods in battle, Varuna, the vanquisher of enemy heroes, performed an excellent Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्क्रतुवरे वृत्ते संग्रामः समजायत ।देवानां दानवानां च त्रैलोक्यस्य क्षयावहः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasminkratuvare vṛtte saṁgrāmaḥ samajāyata ,devānāṁ dānavānāṁ ca trailokyasya kṣayāvahaḥ.
english: When that excellent Vedic ritual (yajña) was completed, a battle arose between the gods and the Dānavas (demons), bringing about the destruction of the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 13. When that excellent Vedic ritual (yajña) was completed, a battle arose between the gods and the Dānavas (demons), bringing about the destruction of the three worlds.

sanskrit: राजसूये क्रतुश्रेष्ठे निवृत्ते जनमेजय ।जायते सुमहाघोरः संग्रामः क्षत्रियान्प्रति ॥१४॥
transliteration: rājasūye kratuśreṣṭhe nivṛtte janamejaya ,jāyate sumahāghoraḥ saṁgrāmaḥ kṣatriyānprati.
english: O Janamejaya, when the Rājasūya, the greatest of Vedic rituals (yajña), is completed, a very great and terrible battle arises against the kṣatriyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 14. O Janamejaya, when the Rājasūya, the greatest of Vedic rituals (yajña), is completed, a very great and terrible battle arises against the kṣatriyas.

sanskrit: सीरायुधस्तदा रामस्तस्मिंस्तीर्थवरे तदा ।तत्र स्नात्वा च दत्त्वा च द्विजेभ्यो वसु माधवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: sīrāyudhastadā rāmastasmiṁstīrthavare tadā ,tatra snātvā ca dattvā ca dvijebhyo vasu mādhavaḥ.
english: Then, Balarama, the wielder of the plow-weapon (Sīrāyudha), also known as Madhava, having bathed in that excellent sacred place and given wealth to the twice-born (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 15. Then, Balarama, the wielder of the plow-weapon (Sīrāyudha), also known as Madhava, having bathed in that excellent sacred place and given wealth to the twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: वनमाली ततो हृष्टः स्तूयमानो द्विजातिभिः ।तस्मादादित्यतीर्थं च जगाम कमलेक्षणः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vanamālī tato hṛṣṭaḥ stūyamāno dvijātibhiḥ ,tasmādādityatīrthaṁ ca jagāma kamalekṣaṇaḥ.
english: Then, the lotus-eyed (kamalekṣaṇa) Balarama, wearing a forest garland (vanamālī), delighted and being praised by the twice-born (dvijāti), proceeded from there to the sacred place of Aditya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 16. Then, the lotus-eyed (kamalekṣaṇa) Balarama, wearing a forest garland (vanamālī), delighted and being praised by the twice-born (dvijāti), proceeded from there to the sacred place of Aditya.

sanskrit: यत्रेष्ट्वा भगवाञ्ज्योतिर्भास्करो राजसत्तम ।ज्योतिषामाधिपत्यं च प्रभावं चाभ्यपद्यत ॥१७॥
transliteration: yatreṣṭvā bhagavāñjyotirbhāskaro rājasattama ,jyotiṣāmādhipatyaṁ ca prabhāvaṁ cābhyapadyata.
english: O best of kings (rājasattama), it was there that the venerable sun god (Bhāskara), a great luminary, by performing a sacrifice (iṣṭvā), attained dominion (ādhipatyam) over the other luminaries and also great power.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 17. O best of kings (rājasattama), it was there that the venerable sun god (Bhāskara), a great luminary, by performing a sacrifice (iṣṭvā), attained dominion (ādhipatyam) over the other luminaries and also great power.

sanskrit: तस्या नद्यास्तु तीरे वै सर्वे देवाः सवासवाः ।विश्वेदेवाः समरुतो गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्च ह ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasyā nadyāstu tīre vai sarve devāḥ savāsavāḥ ,viśvedevāḥ samaruto gandharvāpsarasaśca ha.
english: Indeed, on the bank of that river were all the gods, including Indra (Vasava), the Viśvedevas, along with the Maruts, and also the Gandharvas and Apsaras.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 18. Indeed, on the bank of that river were all the gods, including Indra (Vasava), the Viśvedevas, along with the Maruts, and also the Gandharvas and Apsaras.

sanskrit: द्वैपायनः शुकश्चैव कृष्णश्च मधुसूदनः ।यक्षाश्च राक्षसाश्चैव पिशाचाश्च विशां पते ॥१९॥
transliteration: dvaipāyanaḥ śukaścaiva kṛṣṇaśca madhusūdanaḥ ,yakṣāśca rākṣasāścaiva piśācāśca viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), Dvaipāyana, Śuka, Kṛṣṇa (Madhusūdana), Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, and Piśācas were all present.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 19. O lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), Dvaipāyana, Śuka, Kṛṣṇa (Madhusūdana), Yakṣas, Rākṣasas, and Piśācas were all present.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो योगसिद्धाः सहस्रशः ।तस्मिंस्तीर्थे सरस्वत्याः शिवे पुण्ये परंतप ॥२०॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo yogasiddhāḥ sahasraśaḥ ,tasmiṁstīrthe sarasvatyāḥ śive puṇye paraṁtapa.
english: O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), these and many other thousands of perfected adepts (yoga-siddha) are at that auspicious and sacred ford (tīrtha) of the Sarasvatī river.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 20. O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), these and many other thousands of perfected adepts (yoga-siddha) are at that auspicious and sacred ford (tīrtha) of the Sarasvatī river.

sanskrit: तत्र हत्वा पुरा विष्णुरसुरौ मधुकैटभौ ।आप्लुतो भरतश्रेष्ठ तीर्थप्रवर उत्तमे ॥२१॥
transliteration: tatra hatvā purā viṣṇurasurau madhukaiṭabhau ,āpluto bharataśreṣṭha tīrthapravara uttame.
english: O best among the Bhāratas (bharataśreṣṭha), Viṣṇu, having formerly slain the two demons Madhu and Kaiṭabha there, bathed in that preeminent and supreme ford (tīrtha).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 21. O best among the Bhāratas (bharataśreṣṭha), Viṣṇu, having formerly slain the two demons Madhu and Kaiṭabha there, bathed in that preeminent and supreme ford (tīrtha).

sanskrit: द्वैपायनश्च धर्मात्मा तत्रैवाप्लुत्य भारत ।संप्राप्तः परमं योगं सिद्धिं च परमां गतः ॥२२॥
transliteration: dvaipāyanaśca dharmātmā tatraivāplutya bhārata ,saṁprāptaḥ paramaṁ yogaṁ siddhiṁ ca paramāṁ gataḥ.
english: O Bhārata (bhārata), Dvaipāyana, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness, also bathed there and attained the highest communion (yoga), having reached supreme perfection (siddhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 22. O Bhārata (bhārata), Dvaipāyana, whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness, also bathed there and attained the highest communion (yoga), having reached supreme perfection (siddhi).

sanskrit: असितो देवलश्चैव तस्मिन्नेव महातपाः ।परमं योगमास्थाय ऋषिर्योगमवाप्तवान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: asito devalaścaiva tasminneva mahātapāḥ ,paramaṁ yogamāsthāya ṛṣiryogamavāptavān.
english: In that very context, the great ascetic (mahatapas) sage Asita, also known as Devala, having established himself in the supreme spiritual discipline (yoga), attained ultimate spiritual union (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 48 Verse 23. In that very context, the great ascetic (mahatapas) sage Asita, also known as Devala, having established himself in the supreme spiritual discipline (yoga), attained ultimate spiritual union (yoga).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्मिन्नेव तु धर्मात्मा वसति स्म तपोधनः ।गार्हस्थ्यं धर्ममास्थाय असितो देवलः पुरा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasminneva tu dharmātmā vasati sma tapodhanaḥ ,gārhasthyaṁ dharmamāsthāya asito devalaḥ purā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: However, in that very context, the righteous-souled (dharmātman) Asita Devala, whose wealth was austerity (tapas), formerly resided, having adopted the householder's natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: However, in that very context, the righteous-souled (dharmātman) Asita Devala, whose wealth was austerity (tapas), formerly resided, having adopted the householder's natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: धर्मनित्यः शुचिर्दान्तो न्यस्तदण्डो महातपाः ।कर्मणा मनसा वाचा समः सर्वेषु जन्तुषु ॥२॥
transliteration: dharmanityaḥ śucirdānto nyastadaṇḍo mahātapāḥ ,karmaṇā manasā vācā samaḥ sarveṣu jantuṣu.
english: Always devoted to natural law (dharma), pure, self-controlled, and one who has renounced violence, the great ascetic (mahatapas) was impartial towards all living beings in his actions, thoughts, and words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 2. Always devoted to natural law (dharma), pure, self-controlled, and one who has renounced violence, the great ascetic (mahatapas) was impartial towards all living beings in his actions, thoughts, and words.

sanskrit: अक्रोधनो महाराज तुल्यनिन्दाप्रियाप्रियः ।काञ्चने लोष्टके चैव समदर्शी महातपाः ॥३॥
transliteration: akrodhano mahārāja tulyanindāpriyāpriyaḥ ,kāñcane loṣṭake caiva samadarśī mahātapāḥ.
english: O great king, he was free from anger, impartial towards criticism and things pleasant or unpleasant. Indeed, the great ascetic (mahatapas) saw gold and a clod of earth with equal vision.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 3. O great king, he was free from anger, impartial towards criticism and things pleasant or unpleasant. Indeed, the great ascetic (mahatapas) saw gold and a clod of earth with equal vision.

sanskrit: देवताः पूजयन्नित्यमतिथींश्च द्विजैः सह ।ब्रह्मचर्यरतो नित्यं सदा धर्मपरायणः ॥४॥
transliteration: devatāḥ pūjayannityamatithīṁśca dvijaiḥ saha ,brahmacaryarato nityaṁ sadā dharmaparāyaṇaḥ.
english: Constantly worshipping the deities and guests, along with brahmins (dvija), always devoted to celibacy (brahmacarya), he was ever dedicated to his natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 4. Constantly worshipping the deities and guests, along with brahmins (dvija), always devoted to celibacy (brahmacarya), he was ever dedicated to his natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: ततोऽभ्येत्य महाराज योगमास्थाय भिक्षुकः ।जैगीषव्यो मुनिर्धीमांस्तस्मिंस्तीर्थे समाहितः ॥५॥
transliteration: tato'bhyetya mahārāja yogamāsthāya bhikṣukaḥ ,jaigīṣavyo munirdhīmāṁstasmiṁstīrthe samāhitaḥ.
english: Then, O great king, the wise sage (muni) Jaigīṣavya, a mendicant, having approached that holy place (tīrtha) and resorted to yoga (yoga), became deeply concentrated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 5. Then, O great king, the wise sage (muni) Jaigīṣavya, a mendicant, having approached that holy place (tīrtha) and resorted to yoga (yoga), became deeply concentrated.

sanskrit: देवलस्याश्रमे राजन्न्यवसत्स महाद्युतिः ।योगनित्यो महाराज सिद्धिं प्राप्तो महातपाः ॥६॥
transliteration: devalasyāśrame rājannyavasatsa mahādyutiḥ ,yoganityo mahārāja siddhiṁ prāpto mahātapāḥ.
english: O king, that greatly radiant one, who was constantly devoted to yoga (yoga) and performed great austerity (tapas), dwelt in Devala's hermitage (āśrama) and attained spiritual accomplishment (siddhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 6. O king, that greatly radiant one, who was constantly devoted to yoga (yoga) and performed great austerity (tapas), dwelt in Devala's hermitage (āśrama) and attained spiritual accomplishment (siddhi).

sanskrit: तं तत्र वसमानं तु जैगीषव्यं महामुनिम् ।देवलो दर्शयन्नेव नैवायुञ्जत धर्मतः ॥७॥
transliteration: taṁ tatra vasamānaṁ tu jaigīṣavyaṁ mahāmunim ,devalo darśayanneva naivāyuñjata dharmataḥ.
english: But Devala, merely pointing out that great sage (muni) Jaigīṣavya who was dwelling there, certainly did not engage (with him) in accordance with natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 7. But Devala, merely pointing out that great sage (muni) Jaigīṣavya who was dwelling there, certainly did not engage (with him) in accordance with natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: एवं तयोर्महाराज दीर्घकालो व्यतिक्रमत् ।जैगीषव्यं मुनिं चैव न ददर्शाथ देवलः ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁ tayormahārāja dīrghakālo vyatikramat ,jaigīṣavyaṁ muniṁ caiva na dadarśātha devalaḥ.
english: O great king, a long time thus passed for those two. Then, Devala did not see the sage Jaigīṣavya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 8. O great king, a long time thus passed for those two. Then, Devala did not see the sage Jaigīṣavya.

sanskrit: आहारकाले मतिमान्परिव्राड्जनमेजय ।उपातिष्ठत धर्मज्ञो भैक्षकाले स देवलम् ॥९॥
transliteration: āhārakāle matimānparivrāḍjanamejaya ,upātiṣṭhata dharmajño bhaikṣakāle sa devalam.
english: O Janamejaya, at mealtime, that wise wandering ascetic, the knower of natural law (dharma), approached Devala at the time for alms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 9. O Janamejaya, at mealtime, that wise wandering ascetic, the knower of natural law (dharma), approached Devala at the time for alms.

sanskrit: स दृष्ट्वा भिक्षुरूपेण प्राप्तं तत्र महामुनिम् ।गौरवं परमं चक्रे प्रीतिं च विपुलां तथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa dṛṣṭvā bhikṣurūpeṇa prāptaṁ tatra mahāmunim ,gauravaṁ paramaṁ cakre prītiṁ ca vipulāṁ tathā.
english: Having seen the great sage arrived there in the form of a mendicant, he (Devala) then showed supreme reverence and also great affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 10. Having seen the great sage arrived there in the form of a mendicant, he (Devala) then showed supreme reverence and also great affection.

sanskrit: देवलस्तु यथाशक्ति पूजयामास भारत ।ऋषिदृष्टेन विधिना समा बह्व्यः समाहितः ॥११॥
transliteration: devalastu yathāśakti pūjayāmāsa bhārata ,ṛṣidṛṣṭena vidhinā samā bahvyaḥ samāhitaḥ.
english: But Devala, O Bhārata, being concentrated (in his devotion), worshipped (him) for many years with the method prescribed by sages (ṛṣi-dṛṣṭa), according to his ability.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 11. But Devala, O Bhārata, being concentrated (in his devotion), worshipped (him) for many years with the method prescribed by sages (ṛṣi-dṛṣṭa), according to his ability.

sanskrit: कदाचित्तस्य नृपते देवलस्य महात्मनः ।चिन्ता सुमहती जाता मुनिं दृष्ट्वा महाद्युतिम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: kadācittasya nṛpate devalasya mahātmanaḥ ,cintā sumahatī jātā muniṁ dṛṣṭvā mahādyutim.
english: One day, great anxiety overcame that noble-souled (ātman) king Devala, when he saw the highly radiant sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 12. One day, great anxiety overcame that noble-souled (ātman) king Devala, when he saw the highly radiant sage.

sanskrit: समास्तु समतिक्रान्ता बह्व्यः पूजयतो मम ।न चायमलसो भिक्षुरभ्यभाषत किंचन ॥१३॥
transliteration: samāstu samatikrāntā bahvyaḥ pūjayato mama ,na cāyamalaso bhikṣurabhyabhāṣata kiṁcana.
english: Many years (samāḥ) have certainly passed during which I have been honoring him, yet this lazy mendicant (bhikṣu) has not spoken a single word.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 13. Many years (samāḥ) have certainly passed during which I have been honoring him, yet this lazy mendicant (bhikṣu) has not spoken a single word.

sanskrit: एवं विगणयन्नेव स जगाम महोदधिम् ।अन्तरिक्षचरः श्रीमान्कलशं गृह्य देवलः ॥१४॥
transliteration: evaṁ vigaṇayanneva sa jagāma mahodadhim ,antarikṣacaraḥ śrīmānkalaśaṁ gṛhya devalaḥ.
english: Reflecting in this way, the glorious Devala, who moved through the sky, took a pot and went to the great ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 14. Reflecting in this way, the glorious Devala, who moved through the sky, took a pot and went to the great ocean.

sanskrit: गच्छन्नेव स धर्मात्मा समुद्रं सरितां पतिम् ।जैगीषव्यं ततोऽपश्यद्गतं प्रागेव भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: gacchanneva sa dharmātmā samudraṁ saritāṁ patim ,jaigīṣavyaṁ tato'paśyadgataṁ prāgeva bhārata.
english: As he was going, that virtuous (dharma) soul, O Bhārata, then saw Jaigīṣavya, who had already arrived at the ocean, the lord of rivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 15. As he was going, that virtuous (dharma) soul, O Bhārata, then saw Jaigīṣavya, who had already arrived at the ocean, the lord of rivers.

sanskrit: ततः सविस्मयश्चिन्तां जगामाथासितः प्रभुः ।कथं भिक्षुरयं प्राप्तः समुद्रे स्नात एव च ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ savismayaścintāṁ jagāmāthāsitaḥ prabhuḥ ,kathaṁ bhikṣurayaṁ prāptaḥ samudre snāta eva ca.
english: Then, Lord Asita, filled with wonder, began to ponder: "How has this mendicant arrived, seemingly having just bathed in the ocean?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 16. Then, Lord Asita, filled with wonder, began to ponder: "How has this mendicant arrived, seemingly having just bathed in the ocean?"

sanskrit: इत्येवं चिन्तयामास महर्षिरसितस्तदा ।स्नात्वा समुद्रे विधिवच्छुचिर्जप्यं जजाप ह ॥१७॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ cintayāmāsa maharṣirasitastadā ,snātvā samudre vidhivacchucirjapyaṁ jajāpa ha.
english: Thinking thus, the great sage (maharṣi) Asita then pondered. Having bathed ritually in the ocean, he, now pure, indeed chanted his daily prayers (japa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 17. Thinking thus, the great sage (maharṣi) Asita then pondered. Having bathed ritually in the ocean, he, now pure, indeed chanted his daily prayers (japa).

sanskrit: कृतजप्याह्निकः श्रीमानाश्रमं च जगाम ह ।कलशं जलपूर्णं वै गृहीत्वा जनमेजय ॥१८॥
transliteration: kṛtajapyāhnikaḥ śrīmānāśramaṁ ca jagāma ha ,kalaśaṁ jalapūrṇaṁ vai gṛhītvā janamejaya.
english: O Janamejaya, having completed his daily prayers and become glorious, he took a water-filled pot and indeed went to his hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 18. O Janamejaya, having completed his daily prayers and become glorious, he took a water-filled pot and indeed went to his hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: ततः स प्रविशन्नेव स्वमाश्रमपदं मुनिः ।आसीनमाश्रमे तत्र जैगीषव्यमपश्यत ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa praviśanneva svamāśramapadaṁ muniḥ ,āsīnamāśrame tatra jaigīṣavyamapaśyata.
english: Then, as that sage (muni) was just entering his own hermitage (āśrama), he saw Jaigishavya seated there within the hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 19. Then, as that sage (muni) was just entering his own hermitage (āśrama), he saw Jaigishavya seated there within the hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: न व्याहरति चैवैनं जैगीषव्यः कथंचन ।काष्ठभूतोऽऽश्रमपदे वसति स्म महातपाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: na vyāharati caivainaṁ jaigīṣavyaḥ kathaṁcana ,kāṣṭhabhūto''śramapade vasati sma mahātapāḥ.
english: Jaigīṣavya did not speak to him (Asita Devala) at all. The great ascetic (mahātapāḥ) dwelled in the hermitage motionless like a log.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 20. Jaigīṣavya did not speak to him (Asita Devala) at all. The great ascetic (mahātapāḥ) dwelled in the hermitage motionless like a log.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा चाप्लुतं तोये सागरे सागरोपमम् ।प्रविष्टमाश्रमं चापि पूर्वमेव ददर्श सः ॥२१॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā cāplutaṁ toye sāgare sāgaropamam ,praviṣṭamāśramaṁ cāpi pūrvameva dadarśa saḥ.
english: Having seen him (Jaigīṣavya), who was vast like the ocean, plunged in the ocean's waters, he (Asita Devala) then also saw him already entered into the hermitage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 21. Having seen him (Jaigīṣavya), who was vast like the ocean, plunged in the ocean's waters, he (Asita Devala) then also saw him already entered into the hermitage.

sanskrit: असितो देवलो राजंश्चिन्तयामास बुद्धिमान् ।दृष्टः प्रभावं तपसो जैगीषव्यस्य योगजम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: asito devalo rājaṁścintayāmāsa buddhimān ,dṛṣṭaḥ prabhāvaṁ tapaso jaigīṣavyasya yogajam.
english: O king, the wise Asita Devala pondered, for he had seen the power, born of spiritual discipline (yoga), of Jaigīṣavya's asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 22. O king, the wise Asita Devala pondered, for he had seen the power, born of spiritual discipline (yoga), of Jaigīṣavya's asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: चिन्तयामास राजेन्द्र तदा स मुनिसत्तमः ।मया दृष्टः समुद्रे च आश्रमे च कथं त्वयम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: cintayāmāsa rājendra tadā sa munisattamaḥ ,mayā dṛṣṭaḥ samudre ca āśrame ca kathaṁ tvayam.
english: O best of kings, the best of sages (Asita Devala) then pondered: "How was this one (Jaigīṣavya) seen by me both in the ocean and in the hermitage (āśrama)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 23. O best of kings, the best of sages (Asita Devala) then pondered: "How was this one (Jaigīṣavya) seen by me both in the ocean and in the hermitage (āśrama)?"

sanskrit: एवं विगणयन्नेव स मुनिर्मन्त्रपारगः ।उत्पपाताश्रमात्तस्मादन्तरिक्षं विशां पते ।जिज्ञासार्थं तदा भिक्षोर्जैगीषव्यस्य देवलः ॥२४॥
transliteration: evaṁ vigaṇayanneva sa munirmantrapāragaḥ ,utpapātāśramāttasmādantarikṣaṁ viśāṁ pate ,jijñāsārthaṁ tadā bhikṣorjaigīṣavyasya devalaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, as he was thus contemplating, that sage Devala, who was adept in mantras, then flew up from that hermitage into the sky, intending to inquire about the mendicant Jaigīṣavya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 24. O lord of the people, as he was thus contemplating, that sage Devala, who was adept in mantras, then flew up from that hermitage into the sky, intending to inquire about the mendicant Jaigīṣavya.

sanskrit: सोऽन्तरिक्षचरान्सिद्धान्समपश्यत्समाहितान् ।जैगीषव्यं च तैः सिद्धैः पूज्यमानमपश्यत ॥२५॥
transliteration: so'ntarikṣacarānsiddhānsamapaśyatsamāhitān ,jaigīṣavyaṁ ca taiḥ siddhaiḥ pūjyamānamapaśyata.
english: He saw the Siddhas (accomplished beings) moving in the sky, absorbed in meditation. And he also saw Jaigīṣavya being revered by those very Siddhas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 25. He saw the Siddhas (accomplished beings) moving in the sky, absorbed in meditation. And he also saw Jaigīṣavya being revered by those very Siddhas.

sanskrit: ततोऽसितः सुसंरब्धो व्यवसायी दृढव्रतः ।अपश्यद्वै दिवं यान्तं जैगीषव्यं स देवलः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tato'sitaḥ susaṁrabdho vyavasāyī dṛḍhavrataḥ ,apaśyadvai divaṁ yāntaṁ jaigīṣavyaṁ sa devalaḥ.
english: Then that sage Devala, described as Asita, greatly agitated, resolute, and of firm resolve, certainly saw Jaigīṣavya ascending to the celestial realm.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 26. Then that sage Devala, described as Asita, greatly agitated, resolute, and of firm resolve, certainly saw Jaigīṣavya ascending to the celestial realm.

sanskrit: तस्माच्च पितृलोकं तं व्रजन्तं सोऽन्वपश्यत ।पितृलोकाच्च तं यान्तं याम्यं लोकमपश्यत ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasmācca pitṛlokaṁ taṁ vrajantaṁ so'nvapaśyata ,pitṛlokācca taṁ yāntaṁ yāmyaṁ lokamapaśyata.
english: And from that celestial realm, Devala observed Jaigīṣavya going to the world of ancestors (Pitṛloka). Then, from the world of ancestors, he saw him proceeding to Yama's realm (Yamya Loka).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 27. And from that celestial realm, Devala observed Jaigīṣavya going to the world of ancestors (Pitṛloka). Then, from the world of ancestors, he saw him proceeding to Yama's realm (Yamya Loka).

sanskrit: तस्मादपि समुत्पत्य सोमलोकमभिष्टुतम् ।व्रजन्तमन्वपश्यत्स जैगीषव्यं महामुनिम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasmādapi samutpatya somalokamabhiṣṭutam ,vrajantamanvapaśyatsa jaigīṣavyaṁ mahāmunim.
english: Having ascended from that (world), he also saw the great sage Jaigīṣavya going towards the revered world of Soma.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 28. Having ascended from that (world), he also saw the great sage Jaigīṣavya going towards the revered world of Soma.

sanskrit: लोकान्समुत्पतन्तं च शुभानेकान्तयाजिनाम् ।ततोऽग्निहोत्रिणां लोकांस्तेभ्यश्चाप्युत्पपात ह ॥२९॥
transliteration: lokānsamutpatantaṁ ca śubhānekāntayājinām ,tato'gnihotriṇāṁ lokāṁstebhyaścāpyutpapāta ha.
english: And the soul ascended to the auspicious realms of those who perform exclusive Vedic rituals (yajña); then, it rose to the realms of those who perform the Agnihotra (Vedic fire ritual), and indeed, from those, it flew up even further.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 29. And the soul ascended to the auspicious realms of those who perform exclusive Vedic rituals (yajña); then, it rose to the realms of those who perform the Agnihotra (Vedic fire ritual), and indeed, from those, it flew up even further.

sanskrit: दर्शं च पौर्णमासं च ये यजन्ति तपोधनाः ।तेभ्यः स ददृशे धीमाँल्लोकेभ्यः पशुयाजिनाम् ।व्रजन्तं लोकममलमपश्यद्देवपूजितम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: darśaṁ ca paurṇamāsaṁ ca ye yajanti tapodhanāḥ ,tebhyaḥ sa dadṛśe dhīmāँllokebhyaḥ paśuyājinām ,vrajantaṁ lokamamalamapaśyaddevapūjitam.
english: The wise Sañjaya saw the worlds destined for those ascetics (tapasvin) who perform the Darśa and Paurṇamāsa Vedic rituals (yajña). He also beheld the worlds of those who perform animal Vedic rituals (yajña), and he perceived the pure world, revered by the gods, as he went towards it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 30. The wise Sañjaya saw the worlds destined for those ascetics (tapasvin) who perform the Darśa and Paurṇamāsa Vedic rituals (yajña). He also beheld the worlds of those who perform animal Vedic rituals (yajña), and he perceived the pure world, revered by the gods, as he went towards it.

sanskrit: चातुर्मास्यैर्बहुविधैर्यजन्ते ये तपोधनाः ।तेषां स्थानं तथा यान्तं तथाग्निष्टोमयाजिनाम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: cāturmāsyairbahuvidhairyajante ye tapodhanāḥ ,teṣāṁ sthānaṁ tathā yāntaṁ tathāgniṣṭomayājinām.
english: He (the wise Sañjaya) saw those ascetics (tapasvin) who perform various kinds of Cāturmāsya Vedic rituals (yajña), and their abode. He likewise saw those who perform Agniṣṭoma Vedic rituals (yajña) moving towards their abodes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 31. He (the wise Sañjaya) saw those ascetics (tapasvin) who perform various kinds of Cāturmāsya Vedic rituals (yajña), and their abode. He likewise saw those who perform Agniṣṭoma Vedic rituals (yajña) moving towards their abodes.

sanskrit: अग्निष्टुतेन च तथा ये यजन्ति तपोधनाः ।तत्स्थानमनुसंप्राप्तमन्वपश्यत देवलः ॥३२॥
transliteration: agniṣṭutena ca tathā ye yajanti tapodhanāḥ ,tatsthānamanusaṁprāptamanvapaśyata devalaḥ.
english: And similarly, Devala observed the realm attained by those ascetics (tapodhanāḥ) whose wealth is spiritual austerity, and who perform the Agniṣṭut sacrifice.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 32. And similarly, Devala observed the realm attained by those ascetics (tapodhanāḥ) whose wealth is spiritual austerity, and who perform the Agniṣṭut sacrifice.

sanskrit: वाजपेयं क्रतुवरं तथा बहुसुवर्णकम् ।आहरन्ति महाप्राज्ञास्तेषां लोकेष्वपश्यत ॥३३॥
transliteration: vājapeyaṁ kratuvaraṁ tathā bahusuvarṇakam ,āharanti mahāprājñāsteṣāṁ lokeṣvapaśyata.
english: Devala observed the realms of those exceptionally wise individuals who perform the excellent Vājapeya sacrifice and the Bahusuvarṇaka sacrifice, which involves abundant gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 33. Devala observed the realms of those exceptionally wise individuals who perform the excellent Vājapeya sacrifice and the Bahusuvarṇaka sacrifice, which involves abundant gold.

sanskrit: यजन्ते पुण्डरीकेण राजसूयेन चैव ये ।तेषां लोकेष्वपश्यच्च जैगीषव्यं स देवलः ॥३४॥
transliteration: yajante puṇḍarīkeṇa rājasūyena caiva ye ,teṣāṁ lokeṣvapaśyacca jaigīṣavyaṁ sa devalaḥ.
english: And Devala himself saw Jaigīṣavya in the realms of those who perform the Puṇḍarīka and Rājasūya sacrifices.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 34. And Devala himself saw Jaigīṣavya in the realms of those who perform the Puṇḍarīka and Rājasūya sacrifices.

sanskrit: अश्वमेधं क्रतुवरं नरमेधं तथैव च ।आहरन्ति नरश्रेष्ठास्तेषां लोकेष्वपश्यत ॥३५॥
transliteration: aśvamedhaṁ kratuvaraṁ naramedhaṁ tathaiva ca ,āharanti naraśreṣṭhāsteṣāṁ lokeṣvapaśyata.
english: Devala observed the realms of those who are the best among men (naraśreṣṭhāḥ) and who perform the excellent Aśvamedha sacrifice, as well as the Naramegha sacrifice.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 35. Devala observed the realms of those who are the best among men (naraśreṣṭhāḥ) and who perform the excellent Aśvamedha sacrifice, as well as the Naramegha sacrifice.

sanskrit: सर्वमेधं च दुष्प्रापं तथा सौत्रामणिं च ये ।तेषां लोकेष्वपश्यच्च जैगीषव्यं स देवलः ॥३६॥
transliteration: sarvamedhaṁ ca duṣprāpaṁ tathā sautrāmaṇiṁ ca ye ,teṣāṁ lokeṣvapaśyacca jaigīṣavyaṁ sa devalaḥ.
english: And Devala saw Jaigīṣavya in the worlds of those who perform the difficult-to-obtain Sarvamedha (sacrifice) and the Sautrāmaṇi (sacrifice).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 36. And Devala saw Jaigīṣavya in the worlds of those who perform the difficult-to-obtain Sarvamedha (sacrifice) and the Sautrāmaṇi (sacrifice).

sanskrit: द्वादशाहैश्च सत्रैर्ये यजन्ते विविधैर्नृप ।तेषां लोकेष्वपश्यच्च जैगीषव्यं स देवलः ॥३७॥
transliteration: dvādaśāhaiśca satrairye yajante vividhairnṛpa ,teṣāṁ lokeṣvapaśyacca jaigīṣavyaṁ sa devalaḥ.
english: O king, that Devala saw Jaigīṣavya in the worlds of those who perform various twelve-day "sattras" (sacrifices).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 37. O king, that Devala saw Jaigīṣavya in the worlds of those who perform various twelve-day "sattras" (sacrifices).

sanskrit: मित्रावरुणयोर्लोकानादित्यानां तथैव च ।सलोकतामनुप्राप्तमपश्यत ततोऽसितः ॥३८॥
transliteration: mitrāvaruṇayorlokānādityānāṁ tathaiva ca ,salokatāmanuprāptamapaśyata tato'sitaḥ.
english: Then Asita saw (Jaigīṣavya) who had attained residence in the same worlds as Mitra and Varuṇa, and likewise the Ādityas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 38. Then Asita saw (Jaigīṣavya) who had attained residence in the same worlds as Mitra and Varuṇa, and likewise the Ādityas.

sanskrit: रुद्राणां च वसूनां च स्थानं यच्च बृहस्पतेः ।तानि सर्वाण्यतीतं च समपश्यत्ततोऽसितः ॥३९॥
transliteration: rudrāṇāṁ ca vasūnāṁ ca sthānaṁ yacca bṛhaspateḥ ,tāni sarvāṇyatītaṁ ca samapaśyattato'sitaḥ.
english: And Asita then saw that (Jaigīṣavya) had surpassed all those abodes: those of the Rudras and the Vasus, and that of Bṛhaspati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 39. And Asita then saw that (Jaigīṣavya) had surpassed all those abodes: those of the Rudras and the Vasus, and that of Bṛhaspati.

sanskrit: आरुह्य च गवां लोकं प्रयान्तं ब्रह्मसत्रिणाम् ।लोकानपश्यद्गच्छन्तं जैगीषव्यं ततोऽसितः ॥४०॥
transliteration: āruhya ca gavāṁ lokaṁ prayāntaṁ brahmasatriṇām ,lokānapaśyadgacchantaṁ jaigīṣavyaṁ tato'sitaḥ.
english: Then Asita saw Jaigīṣavya going forth, having ascended to the world of cows, and also traversing the worlds of those who perform great sacrificial sessions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 40. Then Asita saw Jaigīṣavya going forth, having ascended to the world of cows, and also traversing the worlds of those who perform great sacrificial sessions.

sanskrit: त्रीँल्लोकानपरान्विप्रमुत्पतन्तं स्वतेजसा ।पतिव्रतानां लोकांश्च व्रजन्तं सोऽन्वपश्यत ॥४१॥
transliteration: trīँllokānaparānvipramutpatantaṁ svatejasā ,pativratānāṁ lokāṁśca vrajantaṁ so'nvapaśyata.
english: He (Asita) observed the brahmin (Jaigīṣavya) ascending, by his own effulgence, to three other worlds, and also traversing the realms of chaste wives.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 41. He (Asita) observed the brahmin (Jaigīṣavya) ascending, by his own effulgence, to three other worlds, and also traversing the realms of chaste wives.

sanskrit: ततो मुनिवरं भूयो जैगीषव्यमथासितः ।नान्वपश्यत योगस्थमन्तर्हितमरिंदम ॥४२॥
transliteration: tato munivaraṁ bhūyo jaigīṣavyamathāsitaḥ ,nānvapaśyata yogasthamantarhitamariṁdama.
english: Then, O subduer of foes, Asita no longer saw the best of sages, Jaigīṣavya, who had disappeared while absorbed in (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 42. Then, O subduer of foes, Asita no longer saw the best of sages, Jaigīṣavya, who had disappeared while absorbed in (yoga).

sanskrit: सोऽचिन्तयन्महाभागो जैगीषव्यस्य देवलः ।प्रभावं सुव्रतत्वं च सिद्धिं योगस्य चातुलाम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: so'cintayanmahābhāgo jaigīṣavyasya devalaḥ ,prabhāvaṁ suvratatvaṁ ca siddhiṁ yogasya cātulām.
english: That greatly fortunate sage Devala (Asita) pondered Jaigīṣavya's power, his strict adherence to vows, and the incomparable spiritual perfection (siddhi) of his (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 43. That greatly fortunate sage Devala (Asita) pondered Jaigīṣavya's power, his strict adherence to vows, and the incomparable spiritual perfection (siddhi) of his (yoga).

sanskrit: असितोऽपृच्छत तदा सिद्धाँल्लोकेषु सत्तमान् ।प्रयतः प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा धीरस्तान्ब्रह्मसत्रिणः ॥४४॥
transliteration: asito'pṛcchata tadā siddhāँllokeṣu sattamān ,prayataḥ prāñjalirbhūtvā dhīrastānbrahmasatriṇaḥ.
english: Then Asita, self-controlled and with folded hands, steadfastly asked those excellent perfected beings (siddhas) who were performing a great ritual session (brahma-sattra) in these worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 44. Then Asita, self-controlled and with folded hands, steadfastly asked those excellent perfected beings (siddhas) who were performing a great ritual session (brahma-sattra) in these worlds.

sanskrit: जैगीषव्यं न पश्यामि तं शंसत महौजसम् ।एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं परं कौतूहलं हि मे ॥४५॥
transliteration: jaigīṣavyaṁ na paśyāmi taṁ śaṁsata mahaujasam ,etadicchāmyahaṁ śrotuṁ paraṁ kautūhalaṁ hi me.
english: I do not see Jaigiṣavya; please speak of that greatly powerful one. I truly wish to hear this, for my curiosity is profound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 45. I do not see Jaigiṣavya; please speak of that greatly powerful one. I truly wish to hear this, for my curiosity is profound.

sanskrit: सिद्धा ऊचुः ।शृणु देवल भूतार्थं शंसतां नो दृढव्रत ।जैगीषव्यो गतो लोकं शाश्वतं ब्रह्मणोऽव्ययम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: siddhā ūcuḥ ,śṛṇu devala bhūtārthaṁ śaṁsatāṁ no dṛḍhavrata ,jaigīṣavyo gato lokaṁ śāśvataṁ brahmaṇo'vyayam.
english: The perfected beings (siddhas) said: 'Listen, O Devala, O man of firm vows, to the true state of affairs from us who speak. Jaigiṣavya has gone to the eternal, imperishable realm of Brahman (brahman).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 46. The perfected beings (siddhas) said: 'Listen, O Devala, O man of firm vows, to the true state of affairs from us who speak. Jaigiṣavya has gone to the eternal, imperishable realm of Brahman (brahman).'

sanskrit: स श्रुत्वा वचनं तेषां सिद्धानां ब्रह्मसत्रिणाम् ।असितो देवलस्तूर्णमुत्पपात पपात च ॥४७॥
transliteration: sa śrutvā vacanaṁ teṣāṁ siddhānāṁ brahmasatriṇām ,asito devalastūrṇamutpapāta papāta ca.
english: Having heard those words of those perfected beings (siddhas) who were performing the great ritual session (brahma-sattra), Asita Devala swiftly rose up and (then) fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 47. Having heard those words of those perfected beings (siddhas) who were performing the great ritual session (brahma-sattra), Asita Devala swiftly rose up and (then) fell.

sanskrit: ततः सिद्धास्त ऊचुर्हि देवलं पुनरेव ह ।न देवल गतिस्तत्र तव गन्तुं तपोधन ।ब्रह्मणः सदनं विप्र जैगीषव्यो यदाप्तवान् ॥४८॥
transliteration: tataḥ siddhāsta ūcurhi devalaṁ punareva ha ,na devala gatistatra tava gantuṁ tapodhana ,brahmaṇaḥ sadanaṁ vipra jaigīṣavyo yadāptavān.
english: Then those perfected beings indeed said to Devala again: "O Devala, O ascetic rich in asceticism (tapas), there is no access for you to go to that abode of Brahman (brahman) which the Brahmin Jaigīṣavya attained."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 48. Then those perfected beings indeed said to Devala again: "O Devala, O ascetic rich in asceticism (tapas), there is no access for you to go to that abode of Brahman (brahman) which the Brahmin Jaigīṣavya attained."

sanskrit: तेषां तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा सिद्धानां देवलः पुनः ।आनुपूर्व्येण लोकांस्तान्सर्वानवततार ह ॥४९॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tadvacanaṁ śrutvā siddhānāṁ devalaḥ punaḥ ,ānupūrvyeṇa lokāṁstānsarvānavatatāra ha.
english: Having heard that statement of those perfected beings, Devala then successively descended through all those worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 49. Having heard that statement of those perfected beings, Devala then successively descended through all those worlds.

sanskrit: स्वमाश्रमपदं पुण्यमाजगाम पतंगवत् ।प्रविशन्नेव चापश्यज्जैगीषव्यं स देवलः ॥५०॥
transliteration: svamāśramapadaṁ puṇyamājagāma pataṁgavat ,praviśanneva cāpaśyajjaigīṣavyaṁ sa devalaḥ.
english: He reached his own sacred hermitage (āśrama) like a bird. And just as that Devala was entering, he saw Jaigīṣavya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 50. He reached his own sacred hermitage (āśrama) like a bird. And just as that Devala was entering, he saw Jaigīṣavya.

sanskrit: ततो बुद्ध्या व्यगणयद्देवलो धर्मयुक्तया ।दृष्ट्वा प्रभावं तपसो जैगीषव्यस्य योगजम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: tato buddhyā vyagaṇayaddevalo dharmayuktayā ,dṛṣṭvā prabhāvaṁ tapaso jaigīṣavyasya yogajam.
english: Then Devala, with an intellect endowed with natural law (dharma), comprehended, having seen the power born of the yoga (yoga) and asceticism (tapas) of Jaigīṣavya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 51. Then Devala, with an intellect endowed with natural law (dharma), comprehended, having seen the power born of the yoga (yoga) and asceticism (tapas) of Jaigīṣavya.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्महात्मानं जैगीषव्यं स देवलः ।विनयावनतो राजन्नुपसर्प्य महामुनिम् ।मोक्षधर्मं समास्थातुमिच्छेयं भगवन्नहम् ॥५२॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmahātmānaṁ jaigīṣavyaṁ sa devalaḥ ,vinayāvanato rājannupasarpya mahāmunim ,mokṣadharmaṁ samāsthātumiccheyaṁ bhagavannaham.
english: Then, O King, Devala, humbly approaching the great sage, the great-souled Jaigīṣavya, spoke: 'O venerable one, I desire to undertake the practice (dharma) of liberation (mokṣa).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 52. Then, O King, Devala, humbly approaching the great sage, the great-souled Jaigīṣavya, spoke: 'O venerable one, I desire to undertake the practice (dharma) of liberation (mokṣa).'

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा उपदेशं चकार सः ।विधिं च योगस्य परं कार्याकार्यं च शास्त्रतः ॥५३॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā upadeśaṁ cakāra saḥ ,vidhiṁ ca yogasya paraṁ kāryākāryaṁ ca śāstrataḥ.
english: Having heard those words from him (Devala), he (Jaigīṣavya) gave instruction. He also imparted the supreme method of yoga (yoga), as well as what should and should not be done according to scriptural injunctions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 53. Having heard those words from him (Devala), he (Jaigīṣavya) gave instruction. He also imparted the supreme method of yoga (yoga), as well as what should and should not be done according to scriptural injunctions.

sanskrit: संन्यासकृतबुद्धिं तं ततो दृष्ट्वा महातपाः ।सर्वाश्चास्य क्रियाश्चक्रे विधिदृष्टेन कर्मणा ॥५४॥
transliteration: saṁnyāsakṛtabuddhiṁ taṁ tato dṛṣṭvā mahātapāḥ ,sarvāścāsya kriyāścakre vidhidṛṣṭena karmaṇā.
english: Then, having seen him (Devala) whose mind was resolved on renunciation (saṃnyāsa), the great ascetic (Jaigīṣavya) performed all his (Devala's) rites and actions with prescribed ceremonies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 54. Then, having seen him (Devala) whose mind was resolved on renunciation (saṃnyāsa), the great ascetic (Jaigīṣavya) performed all his (Devala's) rites and actions with prescribed ceremonies.

sanskrit: संन्यासकृतबुद्धिं तं भूतानि पितृभिः सह ।ततो दृष्ट्वा प्ररुरुदुः कोऽस्मान्संविभजिष्यति ॥५५॥
transliteration: saṁnyāsakṛtabuddhiṁ taṁ bhūtāni pitṛbhiḥ saha ,tato dṛṣṭvā praruruduḥ ko'smānsaṁvibhajiṣyati.
english: Then, having seen him (Devala) whose mind was resolved on renunciation (saṃnyāsa), the creatures and the ancestors (pitṛs) together wailed, (thinking), 'Who will offer us our share (of oblations)?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 55. Then, having seen him (Devala) whose mind was resolved on renunciation (saṃnyāsa), the creatures and the ancestors (pitṛs) together wailed, (thinking), 'Who will offer us our share (of oblations)?'

sanskrit: देवलस्तु वचः श्रुत्वा भूतानां करुणं तथा ।दिशो दश व्याहरतां मोक्षं त्यक्तुं मनो दधे ॥५६॥
transliteration: devalastu vacaḥ śrutvā bhūtānāṁ karuṇaṁ tathā ,diśo daśa vyāharatāṁ mokṣaṁ tyaktuṁ mano dadhe.
english: But Devala, having heard the pitiable words of the beings crying out in the ten directions, made up his mind to give up final liberation (mokṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 56. But Devala, having heard the pitiable words of the beings crying out in the ten directions, made up his mind to give up final liberation (mokṣa).

sanskrit: ततस्तु फलमूलानि पवित्राणि च भारत ।पुष्पाण्योषधयश्चैव रोरूयन्ते सहस्रशः ॥५७॥
transliteration: tatastu phalamūlāni pavitrāṇi ca bhārata ,puṣpāṇyoṣadhayaścaiva rorūyante sahasraśaḥ.
english: Then, O Bhārata, the pure fruits, roots, flowers, and medicinal plants, indeed cried out by the thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 57. Then, O Bhārata, the pure fruits, roots, flowers, and medicinal plants, indeed cried out by the thousands.

sanskrit: पुनर्नो देवलः क्षुद्रो नूनं छेत्स्यति दुर्मतिः ।अभयं सर्वभूतेभ्यो यो दत्त्वा नावबुध्यते ॥५८॥
transliteration: punarno devalaḥ kṣudro nūnaṁ chetsyati durmatiḥ ,abhayaṁ sarvabhūtebhyo yo dattvā nāvabudhyate.
english: Surely that mean and evil-minded Devala will again cut us down, he who, after granting safety to all beings, fails to comprehend (his actions).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 58. Surely that mean and evil-minded Devala will again cut us down, he who, after granting safety to all beings, fails to comprehend (his actions).

sanskrit: ततो भूयो व्यगणयत्स्वबुद्ध्या मुनिसत्तमः ।मोक्षे गार्हस्थ्यधर्मे वा किं नु श्रेयस्करं भवेत् ॥५९॥
transliteration: tato bhūyo vyagaṇayatsvabuddhyā munisattamaḥ ,mokṣe gārhasthyadharme vā kiṁ nu śreyaskaraṁ bhavet.
english: Then, the best of sages further considered with his own intellect: 'What indeed would be more beneficial, final liberation (mokṣa) or the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a householder's life?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 59. Then, the best of sages further considered with his own intellect: 'What indeed would be more beneficial, final liberation (mokṣa) or the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a householder's life?'

sanskrit: इति निश्चित्य मनसा देवलो राजसत्तम ।त्यक्त्वा गार्हस्थ्यधर्मं स मोक्षधर्ममरोचयत् ॥६०॥
transliteration: iti niścitya manasā devalo rājasattama ,tyaktvā gārhasthyadharmaṁ sa mokṣadharmamarocayat.
english: Having thus resolved in his mind, Devala, O best of kings, abandoned the way of life (dharma) of a householder and chose the path leading to final liberation (mokṣa-dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 60. Having thus resolved in his mind, Devala, O best of kings, abandoned the way of life (dharma) of a householder and chose the path leading to final liberation (mokṣa-dharma).

sanskrit: एवमादीनि संचिन्त्य देवलो निश्चयात्ततः ।प्राप्तवान्परमां सिद्धिं परं योगं च भारत ॥६१॥
transliteration: evamādīni saṁcintya devalo niścayāttataḥ ,prāptavānparamāṁ siddhiṁ paraṁ yogaṁ ca bhārata.
english: Having reflected on these matters in this way, Devala, due to his firm resolve, attained supreme accomplishment and the highest spiritual discipline (yoga), O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 61. Having reflected on these matters in this way, Devala, due to his firm resolve, attained supreme accomplishment and the highest spiritual discipline (yoga), O Bhārata.

sanskrit: ततो देवाः समागम्य बृहस्पतिपुरोगमाः ।जैगीषव्यं तपश्चास्य प्रशंसन्ति तपस्विनः ॥६२॥
transliteration: tato devāḥ samāgamya bṛhaspatipurogamāḥ ,jaigīṣavyaṁ tapaścāsya praśaṁsanti tapasvinaḥ.
english: Then the gods, led by Bṛhaspati, having assembled, themselves being ascetics, praised Jaigīṣavya and his spiritual discipline (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 62. Then the gods, led by Bṛhaspati, having assembled, themselves being ascetics, praised Jaigīṣavya and his spiritual discipline (tapas).

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीदृषिवरो देवान्वै नारदस्तदा ।जैगीषव्ये तपो नास्ति विस्मापयति योऽसितम् ॥६३॥
transliteration: athābravīdṛṣivaro devānvai nāradastadā ,jaigīṣavye tapo nāsti vismāpayati yo'sitam.
english: Then, the foremost sage Nārada indeed spoke to the gods at that time: 'There is no spiritual discipline (tapas) in Jaigīṣavya that could astonish Asita.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 63. Then, the foremost sage Nārada indeed spoke to the gods at that time: 'There is no spiritual discipline (tapas) in Jaigīṣavya that could astonish Asita.'

sanskrit: तमेवंवादिनं धीरं प्रत्यूचुस्ते दिवौकसः ।मैवमित्येव शंसन्तो जैगीषव्यं महामुनिम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: tamevaṁvādinaṁ dhīraṁ pratyūcuste divaukasaḥ ,maivamityeva śaṁsanto jaigīṣavyaṁ mahāmunim.
english: The celestials replied to that steadfast sage who spoke thus, praising the great sage Jaigīṣavya and saying, "It is not so!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 64. The celestials replied to that steadfast sage who spoke thus, praising the great sage Jaigīṣavya and saying, "It is not so!"

sanskrit: तत्राप्युपस्पृश्य ततो महात्मा दत्त्वा च वित्तं हलभृद्द्विजेभ्यः ।अवाप्य धर्मं परमार्यकर्मा जगाम सोमस्य महत्स तीर्थम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: tatrāpyupaspṛśya tato mahātmā; dattvā ca vittaṁ halabhṛddvijebhyaḥ ,avāpya dharmaṁ paramāryakarmā; jagāma somasya mahatsa tīrtham.
english: The great-souled one, of supremely noble actions, having bathed there and then having given wealth to the Brahmins of Balarāma, obtained merit (dharma) and went to that great sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Soma.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 49 Verse 65. The great-souled one, of supremely noble actions, having bathed there and then having given wealth to the Brahmins of Balarāma, obtained merit (dharma) and went to that great sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of Soma.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।यत्रेजिवानुडुपती राजसूयेन भारत ।तस्मिन्वृत्ते महानासीत्संग्रामस्तारकामयः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,yatrejivānuḍupatī rājasūyena bhārata ,tasminvṛtte mahānāsītsaṁgrāmastārakāmayaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O Bhārata, where the moon (Uḍupati), the lord of the stars, had performed the Rājasūya Vedic ritual (yajña) – when that (ritual) was completed, a great war known as Tārakāmaya occurred.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O Bhārata, where the moon (Uḍupati), the lord of the stars, had performed the Rājasūya Vedic ritual (yajña) – when that (ritual) was completed, a great war known as Tārakāmaya occurred.

sanskrit: तत्राप्युपस्पृश्य बलो दत्त्वा दानानि चात्मवान् ।सारस्वतस्य धर्मात्मा मुनेस्तीर्थं जगाम ह ॥२॥
transliteration: tatrāpyupaspṛśya balo dattvā dānāni cātmavān ,sārasvatasya dharmātmā munestīrthaṁ jagāma ha.
english: The righteous-souled (dharma) and self-controlled Balarāma, having bathed there and given gifts (dāna), indeed went to the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of the sage Sarasvata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 2. The righteous-souled (dharma) and self-controlled Balarāma, having bathed there and given gifts (dāna), indeed went to the sacred bathing place (tīrtha) of the sage Sarasvata.

sanskrit: यत्र द्वादशवार्षिक्यामनावृष्ट्यां द्विजोत्तमान् ।वेदानध्यापयामास पुरा सारस्वतो मुनिः ॥३॥
transliteration: yatra dvādaśavārṣikyāmanāvṛṣṭyāṁ dvijottamān ,vedānadhyāpayāmāsa purā sārasvato muniḥ.
english: In ancient times, where the sage Sārasvata taught the Vedas to the best of the twice-born (dvija) during a twelve-year drought.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 3. In ancient times, where the sage Sārasvata taught the Vedas to the best of the twice-born (dvija) during a twelve-year drought.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कथं द्वादशवार्षिक्यामनावृष्ट्यां तपोधनः ।वेदानध्यापयामास पुरा सारस्वतो मुनिः ॥४॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kathaṁ dvādaśavārṣikyāmanāvṛṣṭyāṁ tapodhanaḥ ,vedānadhyāpayāmāsa purā sārasvato muniḥ.
english: Janamejaya asked: "How, during a twelve-year drought, did the sage Sārasvata - whose wealth was austerity (tapas) - manage to teach the Vedas in ancient times?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 4. Janamejaya asked: "How, during a twelve-year drought, did the sage Sārasvata - whose wealth was austerity (tapas) - manage to teach the Vedas in ancient times?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।आसीत्पूर्वं महाराज मुनिर्धीमान्महातपाः ।दधीच इति विख्यातो ब्रह्मचारी जितेन्द्रियः ॥५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,āsītpūrvaṁ mahārāja munirdhīmānmahātapāḥ ,dadhīca iti vikhyāto brahmacārī jitendriyaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O great king, formerly there was a wise sage of great austerity (tapas), known as Dadīca, a celibate student (brahmacārī) who had mastered his senses."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 5. Vaiśampāyana said: "O great king, formerly there was a wise sage of great austerity (tapas), known as Dadīca, a celibate student (brahmacārī) who had mastered his senses."

sanskrit: तस्यातितपसः शक्रो बिभेति सततं विभो ।न स लोभयितुं शक्यः फलैर्बहुविधैरपि ॥६॥
transliteration: tasyātitapasaḥ śakro bibheti satataṁ vibho ,na sa lobhayituṁ śakyaḥ phalairbahuvidhairapi.
english: O lord, Indra constantly fears him because of his immense austerity (tapas). He could not be enticed even by numerous kinds of rewards.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 6. O lord, Indra constantly fears him because of his immense austerity (tapas). He could not be enticed even by numerous kinds of rewards.

sanskrit: प्रलोभनार्थं तस्याथ प्राहिणोत्पाकशासनः ।दिव्यामप्सरसं पुण्यां दर्शनीयामलम्बुसाम् ॥७॥
transliteration: pralobhanārthaṁ tasyātha prāhiṇotpākaśāsanaḥ ,divyāmapsarasaṁ puṇyāṁ darśanīyāmalambusām.
english: Then Indra (Pākaśāsana) sent the divine, pure, and beautiful Apsaras Alambusā to tempt him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 7. Then Indra (Pākaśāsana) sent the divine, pure, and beautiful Apsaras Alambusā to tempt him.

sanskrit: तस्य तर्पयतो देवान्सरस्वत्यां महात्मनः ।समीपतो महाराज सोपातिष्ठत भामिनी ॥८॥
transliteration: tasya tarpayato devānsarasvatyāṁ mahātmanaḥ ,samīpato mahārāja sopātiṣṭhata bhāminī.
english: O great king, the beautiful woman (Alambusā) approached that great-souled sage as he was propitiating the gods on the banks of the Sarasvati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 8. O great king, the beautiful woman (Alambusā) approached that great-souled sage as he was propitiating the gods on the banks of the Sarasvati.

sanskrit: तां दिव्यवपुषं दृष्ट्वा तस्यर्षेर्भावितात्मनः ।रेतः स्कन्नं सरस्वत्यां तत्सा जग्राह निम्नगा ॥९॥
transliteration: tāṁ divyavapuṣaṁ dṛṣṭvā tasyarṣerbhāvitātmanaḥ ,retaḥ skannaṁ sarasvatyāṁ tatsā jagrāha nimnagā.
english: Having seen that divine-bodied Apsaras, the semen (retas) of that sage, whose self (ātman) was cultivated, fell into the Sarasvati. The river (nimnagā) itself then took that.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 9. Having seen that divine-bodied Apsaras, the semen (retas) of that sage, whose self (ātman) was cultivated, fell into the Sarasvati. The river (nimnagā) itself then took that.

sanskrit: कुक्षौ चाप्यदधद्दृष्ट्वा तद्रेतः पुरुषर्षभ ।सा दधार च तं गर्भं पुत्रहेतोर्महानदी ॥१०॥
transliteration: kukṣau cāpyadadhaddṛṣṭvā tadretaḥ puruṣarṣabha ,sā dadhāra ca taṁ garbhaṁ putrahetormahānadī.
english: O best of men (puruṣarṣabha), having seen that semen (retas), the great river (mahānadī) then placed it in her womb and carried that embryo for the sake of a son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 10. O best of men (puruṣarṣabha), having seen that semen (retas), the great river (mahānadī) then placed it in her womb and carried that embryo for the sake of a son.

sanskrit: सुषुवे चापि समये पुत्रं सा सरितां वरा ।जगाम पुत्रमादाय तमृषिं प्रति च प्रभो ॥११॥
transliteration: suṣuve cāpi samaye putraṁ sā saritāṁ varā ,jagāma putramādāya tamṛṣiṁ prati ca prabho.
english: And at the opportune moment, that foremost among rivers gave birth to a son. Then, taking that son, O Lord, she approached that sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 11. And at the opportune moment, that foremost among rivers gave birth to a son. Then, taking that son, O Lord, she approached that sage.

sanskrit: ऋषिसंसदि तं दृष्ट्वा सा नदी मुनिसत्तमम् ।ततः प्रोवाच राजेन्द्र ददती पुत्रमस्य तम् ।ब्रह्मर्षे तव पुत्रोऽयं त्वद्भक्त्या धारितो मया ॥१२॥
transliteration: ṛṣisaṁsadi taṁ dṛṣṭvā sā nadī munisattamam ,tataḥ provāca rājendra dadatī putramasya tam ,brahmarṣe tava putro'yaṁ tvadbhaktyā dhārito mayā.
english: Having seen that best of sages ("munisattama") in the assembly of sages, that river then spoke, O king, presenting him with his son: 'O brahmarṣi, this son of yours was carried by me due to my devotion (bhakti) to you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 12. Having seen that best of sages ("munisattama") in the assembly of sages, that river then spoke, O king, presenting him with his son: 'O brahmarṣi, this son of yours was carried by me due to my devotion (bhakti) to you.'

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तेऽप्सरसं रेतो यत्स्कन्नं प्रागलम्बुसाम् ।तत्कुक्षिणा वै ब्रह्मर्षे त्वद्भक्त्या धृतवत्यहम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā te'psarasaṁ reto yatskannaṁ prāgalambusām ,tatkukṣiṇā vai brahmarṣe tvadbhaktyā dhṛtavatyaham.
english: O brahmarṣi, that semen ("retas") of yours, which fell when you saw the apsara and were in a dangling posture ("prāgalambusām"), that very semen I indeed held in my womb by means of my devotion (bhakti) to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 13. O brahmarṣi, that semen ("retas") of yours, which fell when you saw the apsara and were in a dangling posture ("prāgalambusām"), that very semen I indeed held in my womb by means of my devotion (bhakti) to you.

sanskrit: न विनाशमिदं गच्छेत्त्वत्तेज इति निश्चयात् ।प्रतिगृह्णीष्व पुत्रं स्वं मया दत्तमनिन्दितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: na vināśamidaṁ gacchettvatteja iti niścayāt ,pratigṛhṇīṣva putraṁ svaṁ mayā dattamaninditam.
english: With the conviction (niścaya) that 'this "tejas" (energy or splendor) of yours should not be destroyed,' please accept your own blameless son, given by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 14. With the conviction (niścaya) that 'this "tejas" (energy or splendor) of yours should not be destroyed,' please accept your own blameless son, given by me.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रतिजग्राह प्रीतिं चावाप उत्तमाम् ।मन्त्रवच्चोपजिघ्रत्तं मूर्ध्नि प्रेम्णा द्विजोत्तमः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ pratijagrāha prītiṁ cāvāpa uttamām ,mantravaccopajighrattaṁ mūrdhni premṇā dvijottamaḥ.
english: After being spoken to in this manner, the excellent Brahmin (dvijottama) accepted him and experienced supreme joy. With great affection, he ritually smelled his head, accompanied by sacred recitations (mantra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 15. After being spoken to in this manner, the excellent Brahmin (dvijottama) accepted him and experienced supreme joy. With great affection, he ritually smelled his head, accompanied by sacred recitations (mantra).

sanskrit: परिष्वज्य चिरं कालं तदा भरतसत्तम ।सरस्वत्यै वरं प्रादात्प्रीयमाणो महामुनिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: pariṣvajya ciraṁ kālaṁ tadā bharatasattama ,sarasvatyai varaṁ prādātprīyamāṇo mahāmuniḥ.
english: Then, O best of the Bharatas, after embracing for a long time, the great sage, being pleased, granted a boon to Sarasvati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 16. Then, O best of the Bharatas, after embracing for a long time, the great sage, being pleased, granted a boon to Sarasvati.

sanskrit: विश्वे देवाः सपितरो गन्धर्वाप्सरसां गणाः ।तृप्तिं यास्यन्ति सुभगे तर्प्यमाणास्तवाम्भसा ॥१७॥
transliteration: viśve devāḥ sapitaro gandharvāpsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ ,tṛptiṁ yāsyanti subhage tarpyamāṇāstavāmbhasā.
english: O fortunate one, all the gods, along with the ancestors (pitṛs), and the hosts of Gandharvas and Apsaras, will achieve satisfaction when propitiated by your waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 17. O fortunate one, all the gods, along with the ancestors (pitṛs), and the hosts of Gandharvas and Apsaras, will achieve satisfaction when propitiated by your waters.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा स तु तुष्टाव वचोभिर्वै महानदीम् ।प्रीतः परमहृष्टात्मा यथावच्छृणु पार्थिव ॥१८॥
transliteration: ityuktvā sa tu tuṣṭāva vacobhirvai mahānadīm ,prītaḥ paramahṛṣṭātmā yathāvacchṛṇu pārthiva.
english: Having spoken thus, he, delighted and with a supremely joyful heart (ātman), praised the great river (Sarasvati) with these words. O ruler (pārthiva), listen to this properly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 18. Having spoken thus, he, delighted and with a supremely joyful heart (ātman), praised the great river (Sarasvati) with these words. O ruler (pārthiva), listen to this properly.

sanskrit: प्रसृतासि महाभागे सरसो ब्रह्मणः पुरा ।जानन्ति त्वां सरिच्छ्रेष्ठे मुनयः संशितव्रताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: prasṛtāsi mahābhāge saraso brahmaṇaḥ purā ,jānanti tvāṁ saricchreṣṭhe munayaḥ saṁśitavratāḥ.
english: O highly revered one (mahābhāge), you formerly emerged from the lake of Brahmā. O best of rivers (saricchreṣṭhe), the sages who are firm in their vows (saṃśitavratāḥ) know you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 19. O highly revered one (mahābhāge), you formerly emerged from the lake of Brahmā. O best of rivers (saricchreṣṭhe), the sages who are firm in their vows (saṃśitavratāḥ) know you.

sanskrit: मम प्रियकरी चापि सततं प्रियदर्शने ।तस्मात्सारस्वतः पुत्रो महांस्ते वरवर्णिनि ॥२०॥
transliteration: mama priyakarī cāpi satataṁ priyadarśane ,tasmātsārasvataḥ putro mahāṁste varavarṇini.
english: O lady delightful to behold (priyadarśane), you are always dear and beneficial to me. Therefore, O beautiful lady (varavarṇini), a great son, Sarasvata, will be born to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 20. O lady delightful to behold (priyadarśane), you are always dear and beneficial to me. Therefore, O beautiful lady (varavarṇini), a great son, Sarasvata, will be born to you.

sanskrit: तवैव नाम्ना प्रथितः पुत्रस्ते लोकभावनः ।सारस्वत इति ख्यातो भविष्यति महातपाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tavaiva nāmnā prathitaḥ putraste lokabhāvanaḥ ,sārasvata iti khyāto bhaviṣyati mahātapāḥ.
english: Your son will indeed be famous by your very name, renowned as Sarasvata, the sustainer of the world, and possessing great spiritual discipline (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 21. Your son will indeed be famous by your very name, renowned as Sarasvata, the sustainer of the world, and possessing great spiritual discipline (tapas).

sanskrit: एष द्वादशवार्षिक्यामनावृष्ट्यां द्विजर्षभान् ।सारस्वतो महाभागे वेदानध्यापयिष्यति ॥२२॥
transliteration: eṣa dvādaśavārṣikyāmanāvṛṣṭyāṁ dvijarṣabhān ,sārasvato mahābhāge vedānadhyāpayiṣyati.
english: O highly revered one (mahābhāge), this Sarasvata will teach the Vedas to the foremost Brahmins (dvija) during a twelve-year drought.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 22. O highly revered one (mahābhāge), this Sarasvata will teach the Vedas to the foremost Brahmins (dvija) during a twelve-year drought.

sanskrit: पुण्याभ्यश्च सरिद्भ्यस्त्वं सदा पुण्यतमा शुभे ।भविष्यसि महाभागे मत्प्रसादात्सरस्वति ॥२३॥
transliteration: puṇyābhyaśca saridbhyastvaṁ sadā puṇyatamā śubhe ,bhaviṣyasi mahābhāge matprasādātsarasvati.
english: O auspicious and greatly fortunate Sarasvati, by my grace, you will always be the holiest among all holy rivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 23. O auspicious and greatly fortunate Sarasvati, by my grace, you will always be the holiest among all holy rivers.

sanskrit: एवं सा संस्तुता तेन वरं लब्ध्वा महानदी ।पुत्रमादाय मुदिता जगाम भरतर्षभ ॥२४॥
transliteration: evaṁ sā saṁstutā tena varaṁ labdhvā mahānadī ,putramādāya muditā jagāma bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bharatas, thus praised by him, that great river (Sarasvati), delighted, obtained a boon and departed, taking her son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 24. O best of the Bharatas, thus praised by him, that great river (Sarasvati), delighted, obtained a boon and departed, taking her son.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु विरोधे देवदानवैः ।शक्रः प्रहरणान्वेषी लोकांस्त्रीन्विचचार ह ॥२५॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu virodhe devadānavaiḥ ,śakraḥ praharaṇānveṣī lokāṁstrīnvicacāra ha.
english: Indeed, at this very time, due to the conflict between the gods and the Dānavas, Indra (Śakra), seeking a weapon, wandered through the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 25. Indeed, at this very time, due to the conflict between the gods and the Dānavas, Indra (Śakra), seeking a weapon, wandered through the three worlds.

sanskrit: न चोपलेभे भगवाञ्शक्रः प्रहरणं तदा ।यद्वै तेषां भवेद्योग्यं वधाय विबुधद्विषाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: na copalebhe bhagavāñśakraḥ praharaṇaṁ tadā ,yadvai teṣāṁ bhavedyogyaṁ vadhāya vibudhadviṣām.
english: And then, the venerable Indra (Śakra) did not find a weapon that would indeed be suitable for the slaying of the enemies of the gods (Dānavas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 26. And then, the venerable Indra (Śakra) did not find a weapon that would indeed be suitable for the slaying of the enemies of the gods (Dānavas).

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीत्सुराञ्शक्रो न मे शक्या महासुराः ।ऋतेऽस्थिभिर्दधीचस्य निहन्तुं त्रिदशद्विषः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tato'bravītsurāñśakro na me śakyā mahāsurāḥ ,ṛte'sthibhirdadhīcasya nihantuṁ tridaśadviṣaḥ.
english: Then Indra addressed the gods, saying, "The great asuras, these enemies of the gods, cannot be slain by me without the bones of Dadhīci."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 27. Then Indra addressed the gods, saying, "The great asuras, these enemies of the gods, cannot be slain by me without the bones of Dadhīci."

sanskrit: तस्माद्गत्वा ऋषिश्रेष्ठो याच्यतां सुरसत्तमाः ।दधीचास्थीनि देहीति तैर्वधिष्यामहे रिपून् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasmādgatvā ṛṣiśreṣṭho yācyatāṁ surasattamāḥ ,dadhīcāsthīni dehīti tairvadhiṣyāmahe ripūn.
english: Therefore, O foremost among gods, go and implore that great sage (Dadhīci), saying, 'Give us Dadhīci's bones!' With them, we shall conquer our foes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 28. Therefore, O foremost among gods, go and implore that great sage (Dadhīci), saying, 'Give us Dadhīci's bones!' With them, we shall conquer our foes.

sanskrit: स देवैर्याचितोऽस्थीनि यत्नादृषिवरस्तदा ।प्राणत्यागं कुरुष्वेति चकारैवाविचारयन् ।स लोकानक्षयान्प्राप्तो देवप्रियकरस्तदा ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa devairyācito'sthīni yatnādṛṣivarastadā ,prāṇatyāgaṁ kuruṣveti cakāraivāvicārayan ,sa lokānakṣayānprāpto devapriyakarastadā.
english: Then, that foremost sage (Dadhīci), being earnestly implored by the gods for his bones, resolved upon the sacrifice of his life (prāṇatyāga) without any hesitation. Thus, that benefactor of the gods attained imperishable worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 29. Then, that foremost sage (Dadhīci), being earnestly implored by the gods for his bones, resolved upon the sacrifice of his life (prāṇatyāga) without any hesitation. Thus, that benefactor of the gods attained imperishable worlds.

sanskrit: तस्यास्थिभिरथो शक्रः संप्रहृष्टमनास्तदा ।कारयामास दिव्यानि नानाप्रहरणान्युत ।वज्राणि चक्राणि गदा गुरुदण्डांश्च पुष्कलान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tasyāsthibhiratho śakraḥ saṁprahṛṣṭamanāstadā ,kārayāmāsa divyāni nānāpraharaṇānyuta ,vajrāṇi cakrāṇi gadā gurudaṇḍāṁśca puṣkalān.
english: Then, with those bones of his, Indra, whose mind was greatly delighted, caused various divine weapons to be made. These included thunderbolts (vajra), discus-weapons (cakra), maces, and many powerful heavy staffs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 30. Then, with those bones of his, Indra, whose mind was greatly delighted, caused various divine weapons to be made. These included thunderbolts (vajra), discus-weapons (cakra), maces, and many powerful heavy staffs.

sanskrit: स हि तीव्रेण तपसा संभृतः परमर्षिणा ।प्रजापतिसुतेनाथ भृगुणा लोकभावनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: sa hi tīvreṇa tapasā saṁbhṛtaḥ paramarṣiṇā ,prajāpatisutenātha bhṛguṇā lokabhāvanaḥ.
english: Indeed, he, the sustainer of the worlds, was created by the great sage Bhṛgu, the son of Prajāpati, through intense spiritual discipline (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 31. Indeed, he, the sustainer of the worlds, was created by the great sage Bhṛgu, the son of Prajāpati, through intense spiritual discipline (tapas).

sanskrit: अतिकायः स तेजस्वी लोकसारविनिर्मितः ।जज्ञे शैलगुरुः प्रांशुर्महिम्ना प्रथितः प्रभुः ।नित्यमुद्विजते चास्य तेजसा पाकशासनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: atikāyaḥ sa tejasvī lokasāravinirmitaḥ ,jajñe śailaguruḥ prāṁśurmahimnā prathitaḥ prabhuḥ ,nityamudvijate cāsya tejasā pākaśāsanaḥ.
english: That enormously bodied, powerful being, fashioned from the essence of the worlds, was born; he was tall and vast as a mountain, a lord renowned for his majesty. And Pākaśāsana (Indra) constantly trembles due to his radiance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 32. That enormously bodied, powerful being, fashioned from the essence of the worlds, was born; he was tall and vast as a mountain, a lord renowned for his majesty. And Pākaśāsana (Indra) constantly trembles due to his radiance.

sanskrit: तेन वज्रेण भगवान्मन्त्रयुक्तेन भारत ।भृशं क्रोधविसृष्टेन ब्रह्मतेजोभवेन च ।दैत्यदानववीराणां जघान नवतीर्नव ॥३३॥
transliteration: tena vajreṇa bhagavānmantrayuktena bhārata ,bhṛśaṁ krodhavisṛṣṭena brahmatejobhavena ca ,daityadānavavīrāṇāṁ jaghāna navatīrnava.
english: O Bhārata, with that thunderbolt, which was imbued with sacred incantations (mantras), released with great fury, and born from spiritual power (brahma-tejas), the divine one slew nine times ninety heroes of the Daityas and Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 33. O Bhārata, with that thunderbolt, which was imbued with sacred incantations (mantras), released with great fury, and born from spiritual power (brahma-tejas), the divine one slew nine times ninety heroes of the Daityas and Dānavas.

sanskrit: अथ काले व्यतिक्रान्ते महत्यतिभयंकरे ।अनावृष्टिरनुप्राप्ता राजन्द्वादशवार्षिकी ॥३४॥
transliteration: atha kāle vyatikrānte mahatyatibhayaṁkare ,anāvṛṣṭiranuprāptā rājandvādaśavārṣikī.
english: Then, O king, after a considerable and extremely dreadful period of time had elapsed, a drought lasting twelve years occurred.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 34. Then, O king, after a considerable and extremely dreadful period of time had elapsed, a drought lasting twelve years occurred.

sanskrit: तस्यां द्वादशवार्षिक्यामनावृष्ट्यां महर्षयः ।वृत्त्यर्थं प्राद्रवन्राजन्क्षुधार्ताः सर्वतोदिशम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ dvādaśavārṣikyāmanāvṛṣṭyāṁ maharṣayaḥ ,vṛttyarthaṁ prādravanrājankṣudhārtāḥ sarvatodiśam.
english: O King, during that twelve-year-long drought, the great sages, afflicted by hunger, fled in all directions for their livelihood.
