search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 35. O King, during that twelve-year-long drought, the great sages, afflicted by hunger, fled in all directions for their livelihood.

sanskrit: दिग्भ्यस्तान्प्रद्रुतान्दृष्ट्वा मुनिः सारस्वतस्तदा ।गमनाय मतिं चक्रे तं प्रोवाच सरस्वती ॥३६॥
transliteration: digbhyastānpradrutāndṛṣṭvā muniḥ sārasvatastadā ,gamanāya matiṁ cakre taṁ provāca sarasvatī.
english: Seeing those [sages] who had fled from various directions, the sage Sarasvata then resolved to leave. Sarasvati then spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 36. Seeing those [sages] who had fled from various directions, the sage Sarasvata then resolved to leave. Sarasvati then spoke to him.

sanskrit: न गन्तव्यमितः पुत्र तवाहारमहं सदा ।दास्यामि मत्स्यप्रवरानुष्यतामिह भारत ॥३७॥
transliteration: na gantavyamitaḥ putra tavāhāramahaṁ sadā ,dāsyāmi matsyapravarānuṣyatāmiha bhārata.
english: Son, you must not go from here. I will always provide you with your food, excellent fish. Remain here, O descendant of Bharata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 37. Son, you must not go from here. I will always provide you with your food, excellent fish. Remain here, O descendant of Bharata.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तस्तर्पयामास स पितॄन्देवतास्तथा ।आहारमकरोन्नित्यं प्राणान्वेदांश्च धारयन् ॥३८॥
transliteration: ityuktastarpayāmāsa sa pitṝndevatāstathā ,āhāramakaronnityaṁ prāṇānvedāṁśca dhārayan.
english: Thus addressed, he (Sarasvata) then propitiated the ancestors and the deities. Maintaining his vital breaths (prāṇas) and the Vedas, he partook of food daily.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 38. Thus addressed, he (Sarasvata) then propitiated the ancestors and the deities. Maintaining his vital breaths (prāṇas) and the Vedas, he partook of food daily.

sanskrit: अथ तस्यामतीतायामनावृष्ट्यां महर्षयः ।अन्योन्यं परिपप्रच्छुः पुनः स्वाध्यायकारणात् ॥३९॥
transliteration: atha tasyāmatītāyāmanāvṛṣṭyāṁ maharṣayaḥ ,anyonyaṁ paripapracchuḥ punaḥ svādhyāyakāraṇāt.
english: Then, when that drought had passed, the great sages questioned each other again concerning the study of sacred texts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 39. Then, when that drought had passed, the great sages questioned each other again concerning the study of sacred texts.

sanskrit: तेषां क्षुधापरीतानां नष्टा वेदा विधावताम् ।सर्वेषामेव राजेन्द्र न कश्चित्प्रतिभानवान् ॥४०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ kṣudhāparītānāṁ naṣṭā vedā vidhāvatām ,sarveṣāmeva rājendra na kaścitpratibhānavān.
english: O king of kings, for all of them, who were overcome by hunger and constantly moving, the Vedas were lost, and not one among them was truly sagacious.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 40. O king of kings, for all of them, who were overcome by hunger and constantly moving, the Vedas were lost, and not one among them was truly sagacious.

sanskrit: अथ कश्चिदृषिस्तेषां सारस्वतमुपेयिवान् ।कुर्वाणं संशितात्मानं स्वाध्यायमृषिसत्तमम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: atha kaścidṛṣisteṣāṁ sārasvatamupeyivān ,kurvāṇaṁ saṁśitātmānaṁ svādhyāyamṛṣisattamam.
english: Then, one sage among them approached Sarasvata, who was the best of sages and was performing his sacred study with a disciplined mind (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 41. Then, one sage among them approached Sarasvata, who was the best of sages and was performing his sacred study with a disciplined mind (ātman).

sanskrit: स गत्वाचष्ट तेभ्यश्च सारस्वतमतिप्रभम् ।स्वाध्यायममरप्रख्यं कुर्वाणं विजने जने ॥४२॥
transliteration: sa gatvācaṣṭa tebhyaśca sārasvatamatiprabham ,svādhyāyamamaraprakhyaṁ kurvāṇaṁ vijane jane.
english: And he, having gone, reported to them about Sarasvata, who was exceedingly brilliant and god-like, performing sacred study in a solitary place.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 42. And he, having gone, reported to them about Sarasvata, who was exceedingly brilliant and god-like, performing sacred study in a solitary place.

sanskrit: ततः सर्वे समाजग्मुस्तत्र राजन्महर्षयः ।सारस्वतं मुनिश्रेष्ठमिदमूचुः समागताः ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarve samājagmustatra rājanmaharṣayaḥ ,sārasvataṁ muniśreṣṭhamidamūcuḥ samāgatāḥ.
english: Then, O King, all the great sages (maharṣayaḥ) gathered there. Having assembled, they said this to Sarasvata, the foremost among sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 43. Then, O King, all the great sages (maharṣayaḥ) gathered there. Having assembled, they said this to Sarasvata, the foremost among sages.

sanskrit: अस्मानध्यापयस्वेति तानुवाच ततो मुनिः ।शिष्यत्वमुपगच्छध्वं विधिवद्भो ममेत्युत ॥४४॥
transliteration: asmānadhyāpayasveti tānuvāca tato muniḥ ,śiṣyatvamupagacchadhvaṁ vidhivadbho mametyuta.
english: 'Teach us!'  -  thus the sage then said to them, 'O sirs, become my disciples (śiṣyatvam) according to the prescribed rites.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 44. 'Teach us!'  -  thus the sage then said to them, 'O sirs, become my disciples (śiṣyatvam) according to the prescribed rites.'

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीदृषिगणो बालस्त्वमसि पुत्रक ।स तानाह न मे धर्मो नश्येदिति पुनर्मुनीन् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tato'bravīdṛṣigaṇo bālastvamasi putraka ,sa tānāha na me dharmo naśyediti punarmunīn.
english: Then the group of sages (ṛṣigaṇaḥ) said, 'My dear son (putrakā), you are just a child!' But he replied to those sages again, saying, 'My proper conduct (dharma) must not perish.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 45. Then the group of sages (ṛṣigaṇaḥ) said, 'My dear son (putrakā), you are just a child!' But he replied to those sages again, saying, 'My proper conduct (dharma) must not perish.'

sanskrit: यो ह्यधर्मेण विब्रूयाद्गृह्णीयाद्वाप्यधर्मतः ।म्रियतां तावुभौ क्षिप्रं स्यातां वा वैरिणावुभौ ॥४६॥
transliteration: yo hyadharmeṇa vibrūyādgṛhṇīyādvāpyadharmataḥ ,mriyatāṁ tāvubhau kṣipraṁ syātāṁ vā vairiṇāvubhau.
english: Indeed, whoever teaches by unrighteous means (adharmeṇa) or accepts (knowledge) unrighteously (adharmataḥ)  -  may both of them quickly die, or may both become enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 46. Indeed, whoever teaches by unrighteous means (adharmeṇa) or accepts (knowledge) unrighteously (adharmataḥ)  -  may both of them quickly die, or may both become enemies.

sanskrit: न हायनैर्न पलितैर्न वित्तेन न बन्धुभिः ।ऋषयश्चक्रिरे धर्मं योऽनूचानः स नो महान् ॥४७॥
transliteration: na hāyanairna palitairna vittena na bandhubhiḥ ,ṛṣayaścakrire dharmaṁ yo'nūcānaḥ sa no mahān.
english: The sages determined that what constitutes intrinsic nature (dharma) is not based on age, grey hairs, wealth, or family connections; rather, the one who is learned (anūcāna) is considered great by us.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 47. The sages determined that what constitutes intrinsic nature (dharma) is not based on age, grey hairs, wealth, or family connections; rather, the one who is learned (anūcāna) is considered great by us.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचस्तस्य मुनयस्ते विधानतः ।तस्माद्वेदाननुप्राप्य पुनर्धर्मं प्रचक्रिरे ॥४८॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā vacastasya munayaste vidhānataḥ ,tasmādvedānanuprāpya punardharmaṁ pracakrire.
english: Having heard his words, those sages then properly received the Vedas from him and subsequently re-established the natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 48. Having heard his words, those sages then properly received the Vedas from him and subsequently re-established the natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: षष्टिर्मुनिसहस्राणि शिष्यत्वं प्रतिपेदिरे ।सारस्वतस्य विप्रर्षेर्वेदस्वाध्यायकारणात् ॥४९॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭirmunisahasrāṇi śiṣyatvaṁ pratipedire ,sārasvatasya viprarṣervedasvādhyāyakāraṇāt.
english: For the sake of Vedic self-study, sixty thousand sages accepted discipleship under the Brahmin-sage Sarasvata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 49. For the sake of Vedic self-study, sixty thousand sages accepted discipleship under the Brahmin-sage Sarasvata.

sanskrit: मुष्टिं मुष्टिं ततः सर्वे दर्भाणां तेऽभ्युपाहरन् ।तस्यासनार्थं विप्रर्षेर्बालस्यापि वशे स्थिताः ॥५०॥
transliteration: muṣṭiṁ muṣṭiṁ tataḥ sarve darbhāṇāṁ te'bhyupāharan ,tasyāsanārthaṁ viprarṣerbālasyāpi vaśe sthitāḥ.
english: Then, all of them, handful by handful, brought darbha grass for the seat of that Brahmin-sage, remaining obedient to him even though he was a child.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 50. Then, all of them, handful by handful, brought darbha grass for the seat of that Brahmin-sage, remaining obedient to him even though he was a child.

sanskrit: तत्रापि दत्त्वा वसु रौहिणेयो महाबलः केशवपूर्वजोऽथ ।जगाम तीर्थं मुदितः क्रमेण ख्यातं महद्वृद्धकन्या स्म यत्र ॥५१॥
transliteration: tatrāpi dattvā vasu rauhiṇeyo; mahābalaḥ keśavapūrvajo'tha ,jagāma tīrthaṁ muditaḥ krameṇa; khyātaṁ mahadvṛddhakanyā sma yatra.
english: Then, Balarāma, the mighty son of Rohiṇī and elder brother of Keśava, after distributing wealth there, proceeded joyfully, step by step, to a great and renowned pilgrimage site where an old maiden (vṛddhakanyā) once lived.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 50 Verse 51. Then, Balarāma, the mighty son of Rohiṇī and elder brother of Keśava, after distributing wealth there, proceeded joyfully, step by step, to a great and renowned pilgrimage site where an old maiden (vṛddhakanyā) once lived.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कथं कुमारी भगवंस्तपोयुक्ता ह्यभूत्पुरा ।किमर्थं च तपस्तेपे को वास्या नियमोऽभवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kathaṁ kumārī bhagavaṁstapoyuktā hyabhūtpurā ,kimarthaṁ ca tapastepe ko vāsyā niyamo'bhavat.
english: Janamejaya said: 'O revered one, how did that maiden become devoted to severe penance (tapas) in ancient times? For what reason did she perform penance (tapas)? And what was her specific vow (niyama)?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: 'O revered one, how did that maiden become devoted to severe penance (tapas) in ancient times? For what reason did she perform penance (tapas)? And what was her specific vow (niyama)?'

sanskrit: सुदुष्करमिदं ब्रह्मंस्त्वत्तः श्रुतमनुत्तमम् ।आख्याहि तत्त्वमखिलं यथा तपसि सा स्थिता ॥२॥
transliteration: suduṣkaramidaṁ brahmaṁstvattaḥ śrutamanuttamam ,ākhyāhi tattvamakhilaṁ yathā tapasi sā sthitā.
english: O Brāhmaṇa, I have heard from you this most excellent account of great difficulty. Please narrate the entire truth of how she persevered in her penance (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 2. O Brāhmaṇa, I have heard from you this most excellent account of great difficulty. Please narrate the entire truth of how she persevered in her penance (tapas).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ऋषिरासीन्महावीर्यः कुणिर्गार्ग्यो महायशाः ।स तप्त्वा विपुलं राजंस्तपो वै तपतां वरः ।मानसीं स सुतां सुभ्रूं समुत्पादितवान्विभुः ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ṛṣirāsīnmahāvīryaḥ kuṇirgārgyo mahāyaśāḥ ,sa taptvā vipulaṁ rājaṁstapo vai tapatāṁ varaḥ ,mānasīṁ sa sutāṁ subhrūṁ samutpāditavānvibhuḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'O King, there was once a greatly powerful and highly renowned sage named Kuṇi, belonging to the Garga lineage. That mighty sage, indeed the foremost among those who perform penance (tapas), after practicing extensive penance (tapas), mentally created a beautiful-browed daughter.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 3. Vaiśampāyana said: 'O King, there was once a greatly powerful and highly renowned sage named Kuṇi, belonging to the Garga lineage. That mighty sage, indeed the foremost among those who perform penance (tapas), after practicing extensive penance (tapas), mentally created a beautiful-browed daughter.'

sanskrit: तां च दृष्ट्वा भृशं प्रीतः कुणिर्गार्ग्यो महायशाः ।जगाम त्रिदिवं राजन्संत्यज्येह कलेवरम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tāṁ ca dṛṣṭvā bhṛśaṁ prītaḥ kuṇirgārgyo mahāyaśāḥ ,jagāma tridivaṁ rājansaṁtyajyeha kalevaram.
english: And seeing her, the greatly renowned Kuṇi Gārgya became exceedingly pleased, O King, and went to heaven, abandoning his body here.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 4. And seeing her, the greatly renowned Kuṇi Gārgya became exceedingly pleased, O King, and went to heaven, abandoning his body here.

sanskrit: सुभ्रूः सा ह्यथ कल्याणी पुण्डरीकनिभेक्षणा ।महता तपसोग्रेण कृत्वाश्रममनिन्दिता ॥५॥
transliteration: subhrūḥ sā hyatha kalyāṇī puṇḍarīkanibhekṣaṇā ,mahatā tapasogreṇa kṛtvāśramamaninditā.
english: That blameless, auspicious woman, with beautiful brows and eyes like lotus petals, then performed severe asceticism (tapas), having established an hermitage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 5. That blameless, auspicious woman, with beautiful brows and eyes like lotus petals, then performed severe asceticism (tapas), having established an hermitage.

sanskrit: उपवासैः पूजयन्ती पितॄन्देवांश्च सा पुरा ।तस्यास्तु तपसोग्रेण महान्कालोऽत्यगान्नृप ॥६॥
transliteration: upavāsaiḥ pūjayantī pitṝndevāṁśca sā purā ,tasyāstu tapasogreṇa mahānkālo'tyagānnṛpa.
english: That woman, in ancient times, honored the ancestors and the gods with fasts. But, O King, a long time passed due to her severe asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 6. That woman, in ancient times, honored the ancestors and the gods with fasts. But, O King, a long time passed due to her severe asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: सा पित्रा दीयमानापि भर्त्रे नैच्छदनिन्दिता ।आत्मनः सदृशं सा तु भर्तारं नान्वपश्यत ॥७॥
transliteration: sā pitrā dīyamānāpi bhartre naicchadaninditā ,ātmanaḥ sadṛśaṁ sā tu bhartāraṁ nānvapaśyata.
english: Even when offered by her father to a husband, that blameless woman did not desire that particular spouse. Indeed, she did not find a husband similar to her own self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 7. Even when offered by her father to a husband, that blameless woman did not desire that particular spouse. Indeed, she did not find a husband similar to her own self (ātman).

sanskrit: ततः सा तपसोग्रेण पीडयित्वात्मनस्तनुम् ।पितृदेवार्चनरता बभूव विजने वने ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sā tapasogreṇa pīḍayitvātmanastanum ,pitṛdevārcanaratā babhūva vijane vane.
english: Then she, having afflicted her own body with severe asceticism (tapas), became dedicated to the worship of ancestors and gods in a solitary forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 8. Then she, having afflicted her own body with severe asceticism (tapas), became dedicated to the worship of ancestors and gods in a solitary forest.

sanskrit: सात्मानं मन्यमानापि कृतकृत्यं श्रमान्विता ।वार्द्धकेन च राजेन्द्र तपसा चैव कर्शिता ॥९॥
transliteration: sātmānaṁ manyamānāpi kṛtakṛtyaṁ śramānvitā ,vārddhakena ca rājendra tapasā caiva karśitā.
english: O King (rājendra), although she considered herself to have accomplished her purpose, she was nevertheless exhausted and emaciated by old age and by her asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 9. O King (rājendra), although she considered herself to have accomplished her purpose, she was nevertheless exhausted and emaciated by old age and by her asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: सा नाशकद्यदा गन्तुं पदात्पदमपि स्वयम् ।चकार गमने बुद्धिं परलोकाय वै तदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: sā nāśakadyadā gantuṁ padātpadamapi svayam ,cakāra gamane buddhiṁ paralokāya vai tadā.
english: When she herself was no longer able to move even a single step, then she firmly resolved to journey to the next world.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 10. When she herself was no longer able to move even a single step, then she firmly resolved to journey to the next world.

sanskrit: मोक्तुकामां तु तां दृष्ट्वा शरीरं नारदोऽब्रवीत् ।असंस्कृतायाः कन्यायाः कुतो लोकास्तवानघे ॥११॥
transliteration: moktukāmāṁ tu tāṁ dṛṣṭvā śarīraṁ nārado'bravīt ,asaṁskṛtāyāḥ kanyāyāḥ kuto lokāstavānaghe.
english: But seeing her desirous of abandoning her body, Nārada spoke: "O sinless one (anaghe), how can there be worlds [of attainment] for an unsacramented (asaṃskṛtāyāḥ) maiden?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 11. But seeing her desirous of abandoning her body, Nārada spoke: "O sinless one (anaghe), how can there be worlds [of attainment] for an unsacramented (asaṃskṛtāyāḥ) maiden?"

sanskrit: एवं हि श्रुतमस्माभिर्देवलोके महाव्रते ।तपः परमकं प्राप्तं न तु लोकास्त्वया जिताः ॥१२॥
transliteration: evaṁ hi śrutamasmābhirdevaloke mahāvrate ,tapaḥ paramakaṁ prāptaṁ na tu lokāstvayā jitāḥ.
english: Indeed, we have heard that through your great vow (mahāvrata) in the divine realm (devaloka), supreme asceticism (tapas) has been attained; however, the worlds have not been conquered by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 12. Indeed, we have heard that through your great vow (mahāvrata) in the divine realm (devaloka), supreme asceticism (tapas) has been attained; however, the worlds have not been conquered by you.

sanskrit: तन्नारदवचः श्रुत्वा साब्रवीदृषिसंसदि ।तपसोऽर्धं प्रयच्छामि पाणिग्राहस्य सत्तमाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tannāradavacaḥ śrutvā sābravīdṛṣisaṁsadi ,tapaso'rdhaṁ prayacchāmi pāṇigrāhasya sattamāḥ.
english: Having heard Nārada's words, she declared in the assembly of sages, 'O best of sages, I will give half of my asceticism (tapas) to the one who takes my hand.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 13. Having heard Nārada's words, she declared in the assembly of sages, 'O best of sages, I will give half of my asceticism (tapas) to the one who takes my hand.'

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते चास्या जग्राह पाणिं गालवसंभवः ।ऋषिः प्राक्शृङ्गवान्नाम समयं चेदमब्रवीत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: ityukte cāsyā jagrāha pāṇiṁ gālavasaṁbhavaḥ ,ṛṣiḥ prākśṛṅgavānnāma samayaṁ cedamabravīt.
english: And when she had spoken thus, a sage (ṛṣi) named Prākśṛṅgavān, born from Gālava, took her hand and spoke this condition (samaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 14. And when she had spoken thus, a sage (ṛṣi) named Prākśṛṅgavān, born from Gālava, took her hand and spoke this condition (samaya).

sanskrit: समयेन तवाद्याहं पाणिं स्प्रक्ष्यामि शोभने ।यद्येकरात्रं वस्तव्यं त्वया सह मयेति ह ॥१५॥
transliteration: samayena tavādyāhaṁ pāṇiṁ sprakṣyāmi śobhane ,yadyekarātraṁ vastavyaṁ tvayā saha mayeti ha.
english: O beautiful one, I will take your hand today on this condition (samaya): if I am to dwell with you for a single night.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 15. O beautiful one, I will take your hand today on this condition (samaya): if I am to dwell with you for a single night.

sanskrit: तथेति सा प्रतिश्रुत्य तस्मै पाणिं ददौ तदा ।चक्रे च पाणिग्रहणं तस्योद्वाहं च गालविः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tatheti sā pratiśrutya tasmai pāṇiṁ dadau tadā ,cakre ca pāṇigrahaṇaṁ tasyodvāhaṁ ca gālaviḥ.
english: "So be it," she promised, and then she gave her hand to him. Gālavi then performed the ritual of taking her hand (pāṇigrahaṇa), her marriage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 16. "So be it," she promised, and then she gave her hand to him. Gālavi then performed the ritual of taking her hand (pāṇigrahaṇa), her marriage.

sanskrit: सा रात्रावभवद्राजंस्तरुणी देववर्णिनी ।दिव्याभरणवस्त्रा च दिव्यस्रगनुलेपना ॥१७॥
transliteration: sā rātrāvabhavadrājaṁstaruṇī devavarṇinī ,divyābharaṇavastrā ca divyasraganulepanā.
english: O King, that night she became a young woman with a divine complexion, adorned with divine ornaments and garments, and divine garlands and unguents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 17. O King, that night she became a young woman with a divine complexion, adorned with divine ornaments and garments, and divine garlands and unguents.

sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा गालविः प्रीतो दीपयन्तीमिवात्मना ।उवास च क्षपामेकां प्रभाते साब्रवीच्च तम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tāṁ dṛṣṭvā gālaviḥ prīto dīpayantīmivātmanā ,uvāsa ca kṣapāmekāṁ prabhāte sābravīcca tam.
english: Seeing her, Gālavi was pleased, as she seemed to be illuminating the place with her own radiance (ātman). He spent one night [with her], and in the morning, she spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 18. Seeing her, Gālavi was pleased, as she seemed to be illuminating the place with her own radiance (ātman). He spent one night [with her], and in the morning, she spoke to him.

sanskrit: यस्त्वया समयो विप्र कृतो मे तपतां वर ।तेनोषितास्मि भद्रं ते स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु व्रजाम्यहम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: yastvayā samayo vipra kṛto me tapatāṁ vara ,tenoṣitāsmi bhadraṁ te svasti te'stu vrajāmyaham.
english: O Brahmin, best among ascetics (tapas), I have stayed here according to the agreement (samaya) you made with me. May there be welfare for you; may you be blessed. I now depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 19. O Brahmin, best among ascetics (tapas), I have stayed here according to the agreement (samaya) you made with me. May there be welfare for you; may you be blessed. I now depart.

sanskrit: सानुज्ञाताब्रवीद्भूयो योऽस्मिंस्तीर्थे समाहितः ।वत्स्यते रजनीमेकां तर्पयित्वा दिवौकसः ॥२०॥
transliteration: sānujñātābravīdbhūyo yo'smiṁstīrthe samāhitaḥ ,vatsyate rajanīmekāṁ tarpayitvā divaukasaḥ.
english: Having been granted permission, she spoke again: "Whoever remains devoted in this sacred bathing place (tīrtha) for one night, after satisfying the gods (divaukasaḥ)..."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 20. Having been granted permission, she spoke again: "Whoever remains devoted in this sacred bathing place (tīrtha) for one night, after satisfying the gods (divaukasaḥ)..."

sanskrit: चत्वारिंशतमष्टौ च द्वे चाष्टौ सम्यगाचरेत् ।यो ब्रह्मचर्यं वर्षाणि फलं तस्य लभेत सः ।एवमुक्त्वा ततः साध्वी देहं त्यक्त्वा दिवं गता ॥२१॥
transliteration: catvāriṁśatamaṣṭau ca dve cāṣṭau samyagācaret ,yo brahmacaryaṁ varṣāṇi phalaṁ tasya labheta saḥ ,evamuktvā tataḥ sādhvī dehaṁ tyaktvā divaṁ gatā.
english: Whoever properly observes celibacy (brahmacarya) for forty-eight years and for twenty-eight years, he obtains its proper fruit. Having spoken thus, the virtuous woman then abandoned her body and went to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 21. Whoever properly observes celibacy (brahmacarya) for forty-eight years and for twenty-eight years, he obtains its proper fruit. Having spoken thus, the virtuous woman then abandoned her body and went to heaven.

sanskrit: ऋषिरप्यभवद्दीनस्तस्या रूपं विचिन्तयन् ।समयेन तपोऽर्धं च कृच्छ्रात्प्रतिगृहीतवान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: ṛṣirapyabhavaddīnastasyā rūpaṁ vicintayan ,samayena tapo'rdhaṁ ca kṛcchrātpratigṛhītavān.
english: The sage (ṛṣi) too became dejected, constantly contemplating her form. Gradually, with great difficulty, he reclaimed half of his austerity (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 22. The sage (ṛṣi) too became dejected, constantly contemplating her form. Gradually, with great difficulty, he reclaimed half of his austerity (tapas).

sanskrit: साधयित्वा तदात्मानं तस्याः स गतिमन्वयात् ।दुःखितो भरतश्रेष्ठ तस्या रूपबलात्कृतः ।एतत्ते वृद्धकन्याया व्याख्यातं चरितं महत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: sādhayitvā tadātmānaṁ tasyāḥ sa gatimanvayāt ,duḥkhito bharataśreṣṭha tasyā rūpabalātkṛtaḥ ,etatte vṛddhakanyāyā vyākhyātaṁ caritaṁ mahat.
english: Having thus disciplined himself, he then followed her path. Distressed and captivated by her beauty (rūpa), O best of Bharatas, this great tale of the old maiden has been narrated to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 23. Having thus disciplined himself, he then followed her path. Distressed and captivated by her beauty (rūpa), O best of Bharatas, this great tale of the old maiden has been narrated to you.

sanskrit: तत्रस्थश्चापि शुश्राव हतं शल्यं हलायुधः ।तत्रापि दत्त्वा दानानि द्विजातिभ्यः परंतप ।शुशोच शल्यं संग्रामे निहतं पाण्डवैस्तदा ॥२४॥
transliteration: tatrasthaścāpi śuśrāva hataṁ śalyaṁ halāyudhaḥ ,tatrāpi dattvā dānāni dvijātibhyaḥ paraṁtapa ,śuśoca śalyaṁ saṁgrāme nihataṁ pāṇḍavaistadā.
english: Standing there, Balarāma, who bore the plough as his weapon, also heard that Śalya had been killed. Even there, O tormentor of foes, after giving gifts (dāna) to the twice-born (dvijāti), he then grieved for Śalya, who had been slain by the Pāṇḍavas in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 24. Standing there, Balarāma, who bore the plough as his weapon, also heard that Śalya had been killed. Even there, O tormentor of foes, after giving gifts (dāna) to the twice-born (dvijāti), he then grieved for Śalya, who had been slain by the Pāṇḍavas in battle.

sanskrit: समन्तपञ्चकद्वारात्ततो निष्क्रम्य माधवः ।पप्रच्छर्षिगणान्रामः कुरुक्षेत्रस्य यत्फलम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: samantapañcakadvārāttato niṣkramya mādhavaḥ ,papraccharṣigaṇānrāmaḥ kurukṣetrasya yatphalam.
english: Then, having exited through the gate of Samantapañcaka, Balarāma (Mādhava) asked the groups of sages about the merit (phala) of Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 25. Then, having exited through the gate of Samantapañcaka, Balarāma (Mādhava) asked the groups of sages about the merit (phala) of Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: ते पृष्टा यदुसिंहेन कुरुक्षेत्रफलं विभो ।समाचख्युर्महात्मानस्तस्मै सर्वं यथातथम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: te pṛṣṭā yadusiṁhena kurukṣetraphalaṁ vibho ,samācakhyurmahātmānastasmai sarvaṁ yathātatham.
english: O illustrious one (vibho), those great souls (mahātman), being questioned by Balarāma, the lion among the Yadus, about the merit (phala) of Kurukṣetra, recounted everything to him exactly as it truly was.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 51 Verse 26. O illustrious one (vibho), those great souls (mahātman), being questioned by Balarāma, the lion among the Yadus, about the merit (phala) of Kurukṣetra, recounted everything to him exactly as it truly was.

sanskrit: ऋषय ऊचुः ।प्रजापतेरुत्तरवेदिरुच्यते सनातना राम समन्तपञ्चकम् ।समीजिरे यत्र पुरा दिवौकसो वरेण सत्रेण महावरप्रदाः ॥१॥
transliteration: ṛṣaya ūcuḥ ,prajāpateruttaravedirucyate; sanātanā rāma samantapañcakam ,samījire yatra purā divaukaso; vareṇa satreṇa mahāvarapradāḥ.
english: The sages said: "O eternal Rāma, Samantapañcaka is known as the northern altar of Prajāpati, where, in ancient times, the gods (divaukasaḥ), who are givers of great boons, performed worship with an excellent sacrifice."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 1. The sages said: "O eternal Rāma, Samantapañcaka is known as the northern altar of Prajāpati, where, in ancient times, the gods (divaukasaḥ), who are givers of great boons, performed worship with an excellent sacrifice."

sanskrit: पुरा च राजर्षिवरेण धीमता बहूनि वर्षाण्यमितेन तेजसा ।प्रकृष्टमेतत्कुरुणा महात्मना ततः कुरुक्षेत्रमितीह पप्रथे ॥२॥
transliteration: purā ca rājarṣivareṇa dhīmatā; bahūni varṣāṇyamitena tejasā ,prakṛṣṭametatkuruṇā mahātmanā; tataḥ kurukṣetramitīha paprathe.
english: And formerly, for many years, this land was ploughed by the wise, most excellent royal sage, the great-souled Kuru, with immense splendor. Therefore, it became known here as Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 2. And formerly, for many years, this land was ploughed by the wise, most excellent royal sage, the great-souled Kuru, with immense splendor. Therefore, it became known here as Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: राम उवाच ।किमर्थं कुरुणा कृष्टं क्षेत्रमेतन्महात्मना ।एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं कथ्यमानं तपोधनाः ॥३॥
transliteration: rāma uvāca ,kimarthaṁ kuruṇā kṛṣṭaṁ kṣetrametanmahātmanā ,etadicchāmyahaṁ śrotuṁ kathyamānaṁ tapodhanāḥ.
english: Rama said: "For what purpose was this field ploughed by the great-souled Kuru? O ascetics, I wish to hear this story being narrated."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 3. Rama said: "For what purpose was this field ploughed by the great-souled Kuru? O ascetics, I wish to hear this story being narrated."

sanskrit: ऋषय ऊचुः ।पुरा किल कुरुं राम कृषन्तं सततोत्थितम् ।अभ्येत्य शक्रस्त्रिदिवात्पर्यपृच्छत कारणम् ॥४॥
transliteration: ṛṣaya ūcuḥ ,purā kila kuruṁ rāma kṛṣantaṁ satatotthitam ,abhyetya śakrastridivātparyapṛcchata kāraṇam.
english: The sages said: "O Rama, indeed, formerly, Indra (Śakra), having descended from heaven, approached Kuru, who was constantly striving and ploughing, and asked him the reason (for his actions)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 4. The sages said: "O Rama, indeed, formerly, Indra (Śakra), having descended from heaven, approached Kuru, who was constantly striving and ploughing, and asked him the reason (for his actions)."

sanskrit: किमिदं वर्तते राजन्प्रयत्नेन परेण च ।राजर्षे किमभिप्रेतं येनेयं कृष्यते क्षितिः ॥५॥
transliteration: kimidaṁ vartate rājanprayatnena pareṇa ca ,rājarṣe kimabhipretaṁ yeneyaṁ kṛṣyate kṣitiḥ.
english: "O King, O royal sage, what is this that you are doing with such extreme effort? What is your intention, by which this land is being ploughed?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 5. "O King, O royal sage, what is this that you are doing with such extreme effort? What is your intention, by which this land is being ploughed?"

sanskrit: कुरुरुवाच ।इह ये पुरुषाः क्षेत्रे मरिष्यन्ति शतक्रतो ।ते गमिष्यन्ति सुकृताँल्लोकान्पापविवर्जितान् ॥६॥
transliteration: kururuvāca ,iha ye puruṣāḥ kṣetre mariṣyanti śatakrato ,te gamiṣyanti sukṛtāँllokānpāpavivarjitān.
english: Kuru said: "O Indra (Śatakratu), those men (puruṣa) who will die here in this field will go to the meritorious realms, free from sin."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 6. Kuru said: "O Indra (Śatakratu), those men (puruṣa) who will die here in this field will go to the meritorious realms, free from sin."

sanskrit: अवहस्य ततः शक्रो जगाम त्रिदिवं प्रभुः ।राजर्षिरप्यनिर्विण्णः कर्षत्येव वसुंधराम् ॥७॥
transliteration: avahasya tataḥ śakro jagāma tridivaṁ prabhuḥ ,rājarṣirapyanirviṇṇaḥ karṣatyeva vasuṁdharām.
english: After laughing, Indra (Śakra), the lord, then returned to heaven. Yet, the royal sage (rājarṣi), undeterred, continued to cultivate the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 7. After laughing, Indra (Śakra), the lord, then returned to heaven. Yet, the royal sage (rājarṣi), undeterred, continued to cultivate the earth.

sanskrit: आगम्यागम्य चैवैनं भूयो भूयोऽवहस्य च ।शतक्रतुरनिर्विण्णं पृष्ट्वा पृष्ट्वा जगाम ह ॥८॥
transliteration: āgamyāgamya caivainaṁ bhūyo bhūyo'vahasya ca ,śatakraturanirviṇṇaṁ pṛṣṭvā pṛṣṭvā jagāma ha.
english: Indra (Śatakratu), repeatedly approaching him, repeatedly mocking him, and repeatedly questioning the undeterred (anirviṇṇa) king, finally departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 8. Indra (Śatakratu), repeatedly approaching him, repeatedly mocking him, and repeatedly questioning the undeterred (anirviṇṇa) king, finally departed.

sanskrit: यदा तु तपसोग्रेण चकर्ष वसुधां नृपः ।ततः शक्रोऽब्रवीद्देवान्राजर्षेर्यच्चिकीर्षितम् ॥९॥
transliteration: yadā tu tapasogreṇa cakarṣa vasudhāṁ nṛpaḥ ,tataḥ śakro'bravīddevānrājarṣeryaccikīrṣitam.
english: But when the king cultivated the earth through severe ascetic practice (tapas), then Indra (Śakra) informed the gods of the royal sage's (rājarṣi) intention.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 9. But when the king cultivated the earth through severe ascetic practice (tapas), then Indra (Śakra) informed the gods of the royal sage's (rājarṣi) intention.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा चाब्रुवन्देवाः सहस्राक्षमिदं वचः ।वरेण च्छन्द्यतां शक्र राजर्षिर्यदि शक्यते ॥१०॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā cābruvandevāḥ sahasrākṣamidaṁ vacaḥ ,vareṇa cchandyatāṁ śakra rājarṣiryadi śakyate.
english: Hearing that, the gods then said this to the thousand-eyed one (Indra): "O Indra, if it is possible, let the royal sage (rājarṣi) be gratified with a boon."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 10. Hearing that, the gods then said this to the thousand-eyed one (Indra): "O Indra, if it is possible, let the royal sage (rājarṣi) be gratified with a boon."

sanskrit: यदि ह्यत्र प्रमीता वै स्वर्गं गच्छन्ति मानवाः ।अस्माननिष्ट्वा क्रतुभिर्भागो नो न भविष्यति ॥११॥
transliteration: yadi hyatra pramītā vai svargaṁ gacchanti mānavāḥ ,asmānaniṣṭvā kratubhirbhāgo no na bhaviṣyati.
english: Indeed, if people who die in this world (atra) attain heaven, then our share (the portion due to us) will not exist if they do not perform Vedic rituals (kratu) for us.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 11. Indeed, if people who die in this world (atra) attain heaven, then our share (the portion due to us) will not exist if they do not perform Vedic rituals (kratu) for us.

sanskrit: आगम्य च ततः शक्रस्तदा राजर्षिमब्रवीत् ।अलं खेदेन भवतः क्रियतां वचनं मम ॥१२॥
transliteration: āgamya ca tataḥ śakrastadā rājarṣimabravīt ,alaṁ khedena bhavataḥ kriyatāṁ vacanaṁ mama.
english: And then Indra, having arrived, said to the royal sage (rājarṣi): "Enough with your sorrow. Let my words be done."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 12. And then Indra, having arrived, said to the royal sage (rājarṣi): "Enough with your sorrow. Let my words be done."

sanskrit: मानवा ये निराहारा देहं त्यक्ष्यन्त्यतन्द्रिताः ।युधि वा निहताः सम्यगपि तिर्यग्गता नृप ॥१३॥
transliteration: mānavā ye nirāhārā dehaṁ tyakṣyantyatandritāḥ ,yudhi vā nihatāḥ samyagapi tiryaggatā nṛpa.
english: O king, those mortals who, fasting and diligently, abandon their bodies, or those who are properly slain in battle, and even those who have attained animal births (tiryaggatāḥ) [will go to heaven].
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 13. O king, those mortals who, fasting and diligently, abandon their bodies, or those who are properly slain in battle, and even those who have attained animal births (tiryaggatāḥ) [will go to heaven].

sanskrit: ते स्वर्गभाजो राजेन्द्र भवन्त्विति महामते ।तथास्त्विति ततो राजा कुरुः शक्रमुवाच ह ॥१४॥
transliteration: te svargabhājo rājendra bhavantviti mahāmate ,tathāstviti tato rājā kuruḥ śakramuvāca ha.
english: King Kuru spoke to Indra: "O great-minded one, O best of kings, let those people become partakers of heaven!" Then (Indra replied), "So be it."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 14. King Kuru spoke to Indra: "O great-minded one, O best of kings, let those people become partakers of heaven!" Then (Indra replied), "So be it."

sanskrit: ततस्तमभ्यनुज्ञाप्य प्रहृष्टेनान्तरात्मना ।जगाम त्रिदिवं भूयः क्षिप्रं बलनिषूदनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tatastamabhyanujñāpya prahṛṣṭenāntarātmanā ,jagāma tridivaṁ bhūyaḥ kṣipraṁ balaniṣūdanaḥ.
english: Then, the destroyer of Bala (Indra), having taken leave of him (Kuru) with a delighted inner self (ātman), quickly went back to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 15. Then, the destroyer of Bala (Indra), having taken leave of him (Kuru) with a delighted inner self (ātman), quickly went back to heaven.

sanskrit: एवमेतद्यदुश्रेष्ठ कृष्टं राजर्षिणा पुरा ।शक्रेण चाप्यनुज्ञातं पुण्यं प्राणान्विमुञ्चताम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: evametadyaduśreṣṭha kṛṣṭaṁ rājarṣiṇā purā ,śakreṇa cāpyanujñātaṁ puṇyaṁ prāṇānvimuñcatām.
english: O best of Yadus, this sacred field was thus cultivated by the royal sage long ago, and it was also approved by Indra as a meritorious deed for those who relinquish their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 16. O best of Yadus, this sacred field was thus cultivated by the royal sage long ago, and it was also approved by Indra as a meritorious deed for those who relinquish their lives.

sanskrit: अपि चात्र स्वयं शक्रो जगौ गाथां सुराधिपः ।कुरुक्षेत्रे निबद्धां वै तां शृणुष्व हलायुध ॥१७॥
transliteration: api cātra svayaṁ śakro jagau gāthāṁ surādhipaḥ ,kurukṣetre nibaddhāṁ vai tāṁ śṛṇuṣva halāyudha.
english: And moreover, here (regarding this matter), Indra himself, the lord of gods, sang a verse that was composed about Kurukṣetra. O wielder of the plough (Halayudha), indeed listen to that (verse).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 17. And moreover, here (regarding this matter), Indra himself, the lord of gods, sang a verse that was composed about Kurukṣetra. O wielder of the plough (Halayudha), indeed listen to that (verse).

sanskrit: पांसवोऽपि कुरुक्षेत्राद्वायुना समुदीरिताः ।अपि दुष्कृतकर्माणं नयन्ति परमां गतिम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: pāṁsavo'pi kurukṣetrādvāyunā samudīritāḥ ,api duṣkṛtakarmāṇaṁ nayanti paramāṁ gatim.
english: Even dust particles, when stirred up by the wind from Kurukshetra, lead those who have performed evil deeds to the supreme state.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 18. Even dust particles, when stirred up by the wind from Kurukshetra, lead those who have performed evil deeds to the supreme state.

sanskrit: सुरर्षभा ब्राह्मणसत्तमाश्च तथा नृगाद्या नरदेवमुख्याः ।इष्ट्वा महार्हैः क्रतुभिर्नृसिंह संन्यस्य देहान्सुगतिं प्रपन्नाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: surarṣabhā brāhmaṇasattamāśca; tathā nṛgādyā naradevamukhyāḥ ,iṣṭvā mahārhaiḥ kratubhirnṛsiṁha; saṁnyasya dehānsugatiṁ prapannāḥ.
english: O Nṛsiṃha, the foremost among the gods, the best among Brahmins, and similarly prominent human kings like Nṛga and others, having performed great sacrifices (yajñas), attained a good destination after renouncing their bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 19. O Nṛsiṃha, the foremost among the gods, the best among Brahmins, and similarly prominent human kings like Nṛga and others, having performed great sacrifices (yajñas), attained a good destination after renouncing their bodies.

sanskrit: तरन्तुकारन्तुकयोर्यदन्तरं रामह्रदानां च मचक्रुकस्य ।एतत्कुरुक्षेत्रसमन्तपञ्चकं प्रजापतेरुत्तरवेदिरुच्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: tarantukārantukayoryadantaraṁ; rāmahradānāṁ ca macakrukasya ,etatkurukṣetrasamantapañcakaṁ; prajāpateruttaravedirucyate.
english: The region which lies between Taraṇtuka and Araṇtuka, including Rāma's lakes and the area of Macakruka, this circuit of five yojanas around Kurukshetra (Kurukṣetra-Samantapañcaka) is called the northern altar of Prajāpati.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 20. The region which lies between Taraṇtuka and Araṇtuka, including Rāma's lakes and the area of Macakruka, this circuit of five yojanas around Kurukshetra (Kurukṣetra-Samantapañcaka) is called the northern altar of Prajāpati.

sanskrit: शिवं महत्पुण्यमिदं दिवौकसां सुसंमतं स्वर्गगुणैः समन्वितम् ।अतश्च सर्वेऽपि वसुंधराधिपा हता गमिष्यन्ति महात्मनां गतिम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: śivaṁ mahatpuṇyamidaṁ divaukasāṁ; susaṁmataṁ svargaguṇaiḥ samanvitam ,ataśca sarve'pi vasuṁdharādhipā; hatā gamiṣyanti mahātmanāṁ gatim.
english: This place (Kurukshetra) is auspicious, greatly meritorious, and highly esteemed by the gods, being endowed with heavenly qualities. Therefore, all kings of the earth who are slain here will attain the destination (gati) of great souls.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 52 Verse 21. This place (Kurukshetra) is auspicious, greatly meritorious, and highly esteemed by the gods, being endowed with heavenly qualities. Therefore, all kings of the earth who are slain here will attain the destination (gati) of great souls.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।कुरुक्षेत्रं ततो दृष्ट्वा दत्त्वा दायांश्च सात्वतः ।आश्रमं सुमहद्दिव्यमगमज्जनमेजय ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kurukṣetraṁ tato dṛṣṭvā dattvā dāyāṁśca sātvataḥ ,āśramaṁ sumahaddivyamagamajjanamejaya.
english: Vaiśampayana said: O Janamejaya, after seeing Kurukshetra and distributing gifts, the descendant of Sātvata (Balarama) then went to a very great and divine hermitage (āśrama).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 1. Vaiśampayana said: O Janamejaya, after seeing Kurukshetra and distributing gifts, the descendant of Sātvata (Balarama) then went to a very great and divine hermitage (āśrama).

sanskrit: मधूकाम्रवनोपेतं प्लक्षन्यग्रोधसंकुलम् ।चिरिबिल्वयुतं पुण्यं पनसार्जुनसंकुलम् ॥२॥
transliteration: madhūkāmravanopetaṁ plakṣanyagrodhasaṁkulam ,ciribilvayutaṁ puṇyaṁ panasārjunasaṁkulam.
english: It was endowed with groves of Madhuka and mango trees, densely filled with Plakṣa and Nyagrodha trees, abundant with Chiribilva trees, sacred, and crowded with Panasa and Arjuna trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 2. It was endowed with groves of Madhuka and mango trees, densely filled with Plakṣa and Nyagrodha trees, abundant with Chiribilva trees, sacred, and crowded with Panasa and Arjuna trees.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा यादवश्रेष्ठः प्रवरं पुण्यलक्षणम् ।पप्रच्छ तानृषीन्सर्वान्कस्याश्रमवरस्त्वयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā yādavaśreṣṭhaḥ pravaraṁ puṇyalakṣaṇam ,papraccha tānṛṣīnsarvānkasyāśramavarastvayam.
english: Having seen that most excellent hermitage (āśrama), which was marked by sacred features, the chief of the Yadus (Balarama) asked all those sages, 'Whose distinguished hermitage (āśrama) is this?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 3. Having seen that most excellent hermitage (āśrama), which was marked by sacred features, the chief of the Yadus (Balarama) asked all those sages, 'Whose distinguished hermitage (āśrama) is this?'

sanskrit: ते तु सर्वे महात्मानमूचू राजन्हलायुधम् ।शृणु विस्तरतो राम यस्यायं पूर्वमाश्रमः ॥४॥
transliteration: te tu sarve mahātmānamūcū rājanhalāyudham ,śṛṇu vistarato rāma yasyāyaṁ pūrvamāśramaḥ.
english: But all those sages said to the great-souled Balarama, the King: 'O Rama, listen in detail about whose hermitage (āśrama) this formerly was.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 4. But all those sages said to the great-souled Balarama, the King: 'O Rama, listen in detail about whose hermitage (āśrama) this formerly was.'

sanskrit: अत्र विष्णुः पुरा देवस्तप्तवांस्तप उत्तमम् ।अत्रास्य विधिवद्यज्ञाः सर्वे वृत्ताः सनातनाः ॥५॥
transliteration: atra viṣṇuḥ purā devastaptavāṁstapa uttamam ,atrāsya vidhivadyajñāḥ sarve vṛttāḥ sanātanāḥ.
english: Here, the god Vishnu formerly performed excellent asceticism (tapas). In this very place, all his traditional Vedic rituals (yajña) were duly conducted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 5. Here, the god Vishnu formerly performed excellent asceticism (tapas). In this very place, all his traditional Vedic rituals (yajña) were duly conducted.

sanskrit: अत्रैव ब्राह्मणी सिद्धा कौमारब्रह्मचारिणी ।योगयुक्ता दिवं याता तपःसिद्धा तपस्विनी ॥६॥
transliteration: atraiva brāhmaṇī siddhā kaumārabrahmacāriṇī ,yogayuktā divaṁ yātā tapaḥsiddhā tapasvinī.
english: In this very place, a brahmin woman, who was accomplished (siddhā) and a lifelong celibate (brahmacarya), having attained perfection through asceticism (tapas) and being absorbed in spiritual discipline (yoga), departed to heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 6. In this very place, a brahmin woman, who was accomplished (siddhā) and a lifelong celibate (brahmacarya), having attained perfection through asceticism (tapas) and being absorbed in spiritual discipline (yoga), departed to heaven.

sanskrit: बभूव श्रीमती राजञ्शाण्डिल्यस्य महात्मनः ।सुता धृतव्रता साध्वी नियता ब्रह्मचारिणी ॥७॥
transliteration: babhūva śrīmatī rājañśāṇḍilyasya mahātmanaḥ ,sutā dhṛtavratā sādhvī niyatā brahmacāriṇī.
english: O king (rājan), she was the revered daughter of the great-souled (mahātman) Śāṇḍilya, steadfast in her vows, virtuous, self-controlled, and a celibate (brahmacāriṇī).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 7. O king (rājan), she was the revered daughter of the great-souled (mahātman) Śāṇḍilya, steadfast in her vows, virtuous, self-controlled, and a celibate (brahmacāriṇī).

sanskrit: सा तु प्राप्य परं योगं गता स्वर्गमनुत्तमम् ।भुक्त्वाश्रमेऽश्वमेधस्य फलं फलवतां शुभा ।गता स्वर्गं महाभागा पूजिता नियतात्मभिः ॥८॥
transliteration: sā tu prāpya paraṁ yogaṁ gatā svargamanuttamam ,bhuktvāśrame'śvamedhasya phalaṁ phalavatāṁ śubhā ,gatā svargaṁ mahābhāgā pūjitā niyatātmabhiḥ.
english: Having attained the supreme spiritual discipline (yoga), she indeed went to the unsurpassed heaven. Having experienced the reward (phala) of an aśvamedha (horse sacrifice) in that hermitage (āśrama) – a reward enjoyed by the meritorious – that auspicious and greatly blessed woman departed to heaven, revered by those whose selves (ātman) were disciplined.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 8. Having attained the supreme spiritual discipline (yoga), she indeed went to the unsurpassed heaven. Having experienced the reward (phala) of an aśvamedha (horse sacrifice) in that hermitage (āśrama) – a reward enjoyed by the meritorious – that auspicious and greatly blessed woman departed to heaven, revered by those whose selves (ātman) were disciplined.

sanskrit: अभिगम्याश्रमं पुण्यं दृष्ट्वा च यदुपुंगवः ।ऋषींस्तानभिवाद्याथ पार्श्वे हिमवतोऽच्युतः ।स्कन्धावाराणि सर्वाणि निवर्त्यारुरुहेऽचलम् ॥९॥
transliteration: abhigamyāśramaṁ puṇyaṁ dṛṣṭvā ca yadupuṁgavaḥ ,ṛṣīṁstānabhivādyātha pārśve himavato'cyutaḥ ,skandhāvārāṇi sarvāṇi nivartyāruruhe'calam.
english: The best of the Yadus (yadupuṅgavaḥ), the infallible Acyuta, approached the sacred hermitage (āśramam), saw those sages, and then greeted them. Having dismissed all his camps (skandhāvārāṇi) on the side of the Himalaya, he ascended the mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 9. The best of the Yadus (yadupuṅgavaḥ), the infallible Acyuta, approached the sacred hermitage (āśramam), saw those sages, and then greeted them. Having dismissed all his camps (skandhāvārāṇi) on the side of the Himalaya, he ascended the mountain.

sanskrit: नातिदूरं ततो गत्वा नगं तालध्वजो बली ।पुण्यं तीर्थवरं दृष्ट्वा विस्मयं परमं गतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: nātidūraṁ tato gatvā nagaṁ tāladhvajo balī ,puṇyaṁ tīrthavaraṁ dṛṣṭvā vismayaṁ paramaṁ gataḥ.
english: Having journeyed not very far from there, the mighty Balarama (tāladhvajaḥ), who bears a palm-tree banner, beheld a sacred and excellent holy place (tīrthavaram) on a mountain, and was thereupon filled with supreme wonder.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 10. Having journeyed not very far from there, the mighty Balarama (tāladhvajaḥ), who bears a palm-tree banner, beheld a sacred and excellent holy place (tīrthavaram) on a mountain, and was thereupon filled with supreme wonder.

sanskrit: प्रभवं च सरस्वत्याः प्लक्षप्रस्रवणं बलः ।संप्राप्तः कारपचनं तीर्थप्रवरमुत्तमम् ॥११॥
transliteration: prabhavaṁ ca sarasvatyāḥ plakṣaprasravaṇaṁ balaḥ ,saṁprāptaḥ kārapacanaṁ tīrthapravaramuttamam.
english: And Balarama (balaḥ) reached Plakṣaprasravaṇa, the source of the Sarasvatī River, which is identified as the supreme and excellent holy place, Kārapacana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 11. And Balarama (balaḥ) reached Plakṣaprasravaṇa, the source of the Sarasvatī River, which is identified as the supreme and excellent holy place, Kārapacana.

sanskrit: हलायुधस्तत्र चापि दत्त्वा दानं महाबलः ।आप्लुतः सलिले शीते तस्माच्चापि जगाम ह ।आश्रमं परमप्रीतो मित्रस्य वरुणस्य च ॥१२॥
transliteration: halāyudhastatra cāpi dattvā dānaṁ mahābalaḥ ,āplutaḥ salile śīte tasmāccāpi jagāma ha ,āśramaṁ paramaprīto mitrasya varuṇasya ca.
english: And there, the immensely powerful Halāyudha (Balarama), having offered gifts (dāna), bathed in the cold water. From that place, greatly pleased, he then went to the hermitage (āśramam) of Mitra and Varuṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 12. And there, the immensely powerful Halāyudha (Balarama), having offered gifts (dāna), bathed in the cold water. From that place, greatly pleased, he then went to the hermitage (āśramam) of Mitra and Varuṇa.

sanskrit: इन्द्रोऽग्निरर्यमा चैव यत्र प्राक्प्रीतिमाप्नुवन् ।तं देशं कारपचनाद्यमुनायां जगाम ह ॥१३॥
transliteration: indro'gniraryamā caiva yatra prākprītimāpnuvan ,taṁ deśaṁ kārapacanādyamunāyāṁ jagāma ha.
english: He then went to that very spot on the Yamunā river where Indra, Agni, and Aryaman had previously found great pleasure, having come from Kārapacana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 13. He then went to that very spot on the Yamunā river where Indra, Agni, and Aryaman had previously found great pleasure, having come from Kārapacana.

sanskrit: स्नात्वा तत्रापि धर्मात्मा परां तुष्टिमवाप्य च ।ऋषिभिश्चैव सिद्धैश्च सहितो वै महाबलः ।उपविष्टः कथाः शुभ्राः शुश्राव यदुपुंगवः ॥१४॥
transliteration: snātvā tatrāpi dharmātmā parāṁ tuṣṭimavāpya ca ,ṛṣibhiścaiva siddhaiśca sahito vai mahābalaḥ ,upaviṣṭaḥ kathāḥ śubhrāḥ śuśrāva yadupuṁgavaḥ.
english: Having bathed there, that righteous soul (dharma-ātman) attained supreme satisfaction. Then, accompanied by sages and perfected beings, the mighty chief of the Yadus sat down and listened to pure and auspicious stories.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 14. Having bathed there, that righteous soul (dharma-ātman) attained supreme satisfaction. Then, accompanied by sages and perfected beings, the mighty chief of the Yadus sat down and listened to pure and auspicious stories.

sanskrit: तथा तु तिष्ठतां तेषां नारदो भगवानृषिः ।आजगामाथ तं देशं यत्र रामो व्यवस्थितः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tathā tu tiṣṭhatāṁ teṣāṁ nārado bhagavānṛṣiḥ ,ājagāmātha taṁ deśaṁ yatra rāmo vyavasthitaḥ.
english: While they were thus resting, the revered sage Nārada arrived at the place where Rāma was present.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 15. While they were thus resting, the revered sage Nārada arrived at the place where Rāma was present.

sanskrit: जटामण्डलसंवीतः स्वर्णचीरी महातपाः ।हेमदण्डधरो राजन्कमण्डलुधरस्तथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: jaṭāmaṇḍalasaṁvītaḥ svarṇacīrī mahātapāḥ ,hemadaṇḍadharo rājankamaṇḍaludharastathā.
english: O King, he was adorned with a mass of matted hair, wore golden bark garments, possessed great ascetic fervor (tapas), carried a golden staff, and also held a water pot (kamaṇḍalu).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 16. O King, he was adorned with a mass of matted hair, wore golden bark garments, possessed great ascetic fervor (tapas), carried a golden staff, and also held a water pot (kamaṇḍalu).

sanskrit: कच्छपीं सुखशब्दां तां गृह्य वीणां मनोरमाम् ।नृत्ये गीते च कुशलो देवब्राह्मणपूजितः ॥१७॥
transliteration: kacchapīṁ sukhaśabdāṁ tāṁ gṛhya vīṇāṁ manoramām ,nṛtye gīte ca kuśalo devabrāhmaṇapūjitaḥ.
english: Having taken that delightful vīṇā of the kacchapī type, which produced pleasant sounds, he was skilled in dance and song, and honored by gods and Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 17. Having taken that delightful vīṇā of the kacchapī type, which produced pleasant sounds, he was skilled in dance and song, and honored by gods and Brahmins.

sanskrit: प्रकर्ता कलहानां च नित्यं च कलहप्रियः ।तं देशमगमद्यत्र श्रीमान्रामो व्यवस्थितः ॥१८॥
transliteration: prakartā kalahānāṁ ca nityaṁ ca kalahapriyaḥ ,taṁ deśamagamadyatra śrīmānrāmo vyavasthitaḥ.
english: He was an instigator of quarrels and constantly fond of them. He then went to that place where the glorious Rāma was residing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 18. He was an instigator of quarrels and constantly fond of them. He then went to that place where the glorious Rāma was residing.

sanskrit: प्रत्युत्थाय तु ते सर्वे पूजयित्वा यतव्रतम् ।देवर्षिं पर्यपृच्छन्त यथावृत्तं कुरून्प्रति ॥१९॥
transliteration: pratyutthāya tu te sarve pūjayitvā yatavratam ,devarṣiṁ paryapṛcchanta yathāvṛttaṁ kurūnprati.
english: All of them, having risen to meet him and having honored the divine sage (devarṣi) who was firm in his vows, then inquired about what had transpired regarding the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 19. All of them, having risen to meet him and having honored the divine sage (devarṣi) who was firm in his vows, then inquired about what had transpired regarding the Kurus.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्याकथयद्राजन्नारदः सर्वधर्मवित् ।सर्वमेव यथावृत्तमतीतं कुरुसंक्षयम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato'syākathayadrājannāradaḥ sarvadharmavit ,sarvameva yathāvṛttamatītaṁ kurusaṁkṣayam.
english: Then, O king, Nārada, who was conversant with all principles of natural law (dharma), recounted to him everything that had transpired – the past destruction of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 20. Then, O king, Nārada, who was conversant with all principles of natural law (dharma), recounted to him everything that had transpired – the past destruction of the Kurus.

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीद्रौहिणेयो नारदं दीनया गिरा ।किमवस्थं तु तत्क्षत्रं ये च तत्राभवन्नृपाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato'bravīdrauhiṇeyo nāradaṁ dīnayā girā ,kimavasthaṁ tu tatkṣatraṁ ye ca tatrābhavannṛpāḥ.
english: Then Balarāma, with a humble voice, asked Nārada, 'What was the condition of that warrior (kṣatra) class, and what became of the kings who were present there?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 21. Then Balarāma, with a humble voice, asked Nārada, 'What was the condition of that warrior (kṣatra) class, and what became of the kings who were present there?'

sanskrit: श्रुतमेतन्मया पूर्वं सर्वमेव तपोधन ।विस्तरश्रवणे जातं कौतूहलमतीव मे ॥२२॥
transliteration: śrutametanmayā pūrvaṁ sarvameva tapodhana ,vistaraśravaṇe jātaṁ kautūhalamatīva me.
english: O treasure of asceticism (tapodhana)! I have indeed heard all of this previously, but a great curiosity has arisen in me to hear the details.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 22. O treasure of asceticism (tapodhana)! I have indeed heard all of this previously, but a great curiosity has arisen in me to hear the details.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।पूर्वमेव हतो भीष्मो द्रोणः सिन्धुपतिस्तथा ।हतो वैकर्तनः कर्णः पुत्राश्चास्य महारथाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,pūrvameva hato bhīṣmo droṇaḥ sindhupatistathā ,hato vaikartanaḥ karṇaḥ putrāścāsya mahārathāḥ.
english: Nārada said: 'Indeed, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and the lord of Sindhu were killed previously. Karṇa, the son of Vikartana, and his sons, who were great warriors, were also slain.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 23. Nārada said: 'Indeed, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and the lord of Sindhu were killed previously. Karṇa, the son of Vikartana, and his sons, who were great warriors, were also slain.'

sanskrit: भूरिश्रवा रौहिणेय मद्रराजश्च वीर्यवान् ।एते चान्ये च बहवस्तत्र तत्र महाबलाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: bhūriśravā rauhiṇeya madrarājaśca vīryavān ,ete cānye ca bahavastatra tatra mahābalāḥ.
english: O son of Rohiṇī (rauhiṇeya), Bhūriśravas, and the valorous King of Madra (Śalya), and many other greatly powerful (mahābala) warriors were killed there, in various places.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 24. O son of Rohiṇī (rauhiṇeya), Bhūriśravas, and the valorous King of Madra (Śalya), and many other greatly powerful (mahābala) warriors were killed there, in various places.

sanskrit: प्रियान्प्राणान्परित्यज्य प्रियार्थं कौरवस्य वै ।राजानो राजपुत्राश्च समरेष्वनिवर्तिनः ॥२५॥
transliteration: priyānprāṇānparityajya priyārthaṁ kauravasya vai ,rājāno rājaputrāśca samareṣvanivartinaḥ.
english: Indeed, the kings and princes, unwavering in battles, sacrificed their dear lives for the sake of the beloved Kaurava (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 25. Indeed, the kings and princes, unwavering in battles, sacrificed their dear lives for the sake of the beloved Kaurava (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: अहतांस्तु महाबाहो शृणु मे तत्र माधव ।धार्तराष्ट्रबले शेषाः कृपो भोजश्च वीर्यवान् ।अश्वत्थामा च विक्रान्तो भग्नसैन्या दिशो गताः ॥२६॥
transliteration: ahatāṁstu mahābāho śṛṇu me tatra mādhava ,dhārtarāṣṭrabale śeṣāḥ kṛpo bhojaśca vīryavān ,aśvatthāmā ca vikrānto bhagnasainyā diśo gatāḥ.
english: O mighty-armed Mādhava (Krishna), hear from me concerning those who survived in Duryodhana's army: Kripa, the valiant Bhoja, and the mighty Ashwatthama. Their forces shattered, they fled in various directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 26. O mighty-armed Mādhava (Krishna), hear from me concerning those who survived in Duryodhana's army: Kripa, the valiant Bhoja, and the mighty Ashwatthama. Their forces shattered, they fled in various directions.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो हते सैन्ये प्रद्रुतेषु कृपादिषु ।ह्रदं द्वैपायनं नाम विवेश भृशदुःखितः ॥२७॥
transliteration: duryodhano hate sainye pradruteṣu kṛpādiṣu ,hradaṁ dvaipāyanaṁ nāma viveśa bhṛśaduḥkhitaḥ.
english: With his army annihilated and Kripa and others having fled, Duryodhana, deeply distressed, entered the lake named Dvaipayana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 27. With his army annihilated and Kripa and others having fled, Duryodhana, deeply distressed, entered the lake named Dvaipayana.

sanskrit: शयानं धार्तराष्ट्रं तु स्तम्भिते सलिले तदा ।पाण्डवाः सह कृष्णेन वाग्भिरुग्राभिरार्दयन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: śayānaṁ dhārtarāṣṭraṁ tu stambhite salile tadā ,pāṇḍavāḥ saha kṛṣṇena vāgbhirugrābhirārdayan.
english: Then, as Duryodhana (the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra) lay in the water that had been made motionless (by his mystic power), the Pandavas, along with Krishna, tormented him with harsh words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 28. Then, as Duryodhana (the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra) lay in the water that had been made motionless (by his mystic power), the Pandavas, along with Krishna, tormented him with harsh words.

sanskrit: स तुद्यमानो बलवान्वाग्भी राम समन्ततः ।उत्थितः प्राग्घ्रदाद्वीरः प्रगृह्य महतीं गदाम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa tudyamāno balavānvāgbhī rāma samantataḥ ,utthitaḥ prāgghradādvīraḥ pragṛhya mahatīṁ gadām.
english: O Rāma, that powerful hero, being provoked by words from all sides, rose up from the lake, having seized his mighty mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 29. O Rāma, that powerful hero, being provoked by words from all sides, rose up from the lake, having seized his mighty mace.

sanskrit: स चाप्युपगतो युद्धं भीमेन सह सांप्रतम् ।भविष्यति च तत्सद्यस्तयो राम सुदारुणम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa cāpyupagato yuddhaṁ bhīmena saha sāṁpratam ,bhaviṣyati ca tatsadyastayo rāma sudāruṇam.
english: O Rāma, he has now also engaged in battle with Bhīma, and that combat between the two of them will immediately become very terrible.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 30. O Rāma, he has now also engaged in battle with Bhīma, and that combat between the two of them will immediately become very terrible.

sanskrit: यदि कौतूहलं तेऽस्ति व्रज माधव मा चिरम् ।पश्य युद्धं महाघोरं शिष्ययोर्यदि मन्यसे ॥३१॥
transliteration: yadi kautūhalaṁ te'sti vraja mādhava mā ciram ,paśya yuddhaṁ mahāghoraṁ śiṣyayoryadi manyase.
english: O Mādhava, if you have curiosity, go without delay! See the very dreadful battle of the two disciples, if you approve.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 31. O Mādhava, if you have curiosity, go without delay! See the very dreadful battle of the two disciples, if you approve.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।नारदस्य वचः श्रुत्वा तानभ्यर्च्य द्विजर्षभान् ।सर्वान्विसर्जयामास ये तेनाभ्यागताः सह ।गम्यतां द्वारकां चेति सोऽन्वशादनुयायिनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,nāradasya vacaḥ śrutvā tānabhyarcya dvijarṣabhān ,sarvānvisarjayāmāsa ye tenābhyāgatāḥ saha ,gamyatāṁ dvārakāṁ ceti so'nvaśādanuyāyinaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard the words of Nārada, he honored all those foremost among the twice-born (dvija) who had arrived with him, and then dismissed them all. He then instructed his followers, saying, 'Proceed to Dwārakā!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 32. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard the words of Nārada, he honored all those foremost among the twice-born (dvija) who had arrived with him, and then dismissed them all. He then instructed his followers, saying, 'Proceed to Dwārakā!'

sanskrit: सोऽवतीर्याचलश्रेष्ठात्प्लक्षप्रस्रवणाच्छुभात् ।ततः प्रीतमना रामः श्रुत्वा तीर्थफलं महत् ।विप्राणां संनिधौ श्लोकमगायदिदमच्युतः ॥३३॥
transliteration: so'vatīryācalaśreṣṭhātplakṣaprasravaṇācchubhāt ,tataḥ prītamanā rāmaḥ śrutvā tīrthaphalaṁ mahat ,viprāṇāṁ saṁnidhau ślokamagāyadidamacyutaḥ.
english: Having descended from the auspicious best of mountains, from the source of the Plakṣa tree, Lord Rama (acyuta), whose mind was filled with joy, then, having heard of the great benefit of visiting this sacred place (tīrtha), sang this verse in the presence of the brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 33. Having descended from the auspicious best of mountains, from the source of the Plakṣa tree, Lord Rama (acyuta), whose mind was filled with joy, then, having heard of the great benefit of visiting this sacred place (tīrtha), sang this verse in the presence of the brahmins.

sanskrit: सरस्वतीवाससमा कुतो रतिः सरस्वतीवाससमाः कुतो गुणाः ।सरस्वतीं प्राप्य दिवं गता जनाः सदा स्मरिष्यन्ति नदीं सरस्वतीम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: sarasvatīvāsasamā kuto ratiḥ; sarasvatīvāsasamāḥ kuto guṇāḥ ,sarasvatīṁ prāpya divaṁ gatā janāḥ; sadā smariṣyanti nadīṁ sarasvatīm.
english: How can there be any pleasure or merit (guṇa) comparable to dwelling by the Sarasvatī? People who have attained the Sarasvatī (river) and thereby gone to heaven will always remember the river Sarasvatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 34. How can there be any pleasure or merit (guṇa) comparable to dwelling by the Sarasvatī? People who have attained the Sarasvatī (river) and thereby gone to heaven will always remember the river Sarasvatī.

sanskrit: सरस्वती सर्वनदीषु पुण्या सरस्वती लोकसुखावहा सदा ।सरस्वतीं प्राप्य जनाः सुदुष्कृताः सदा न शोचन्ति परत्र चेह च ॥३५॥
transliteration: sarasvatī sarvanadīṣu puṇyā; sarasvatī lokasukhāvahā sadā ,sarasvatīṁ prāpya janāḥ suduṣkṛtāḥ; sadā na śocanti paratra ceha ca.
english: The Sarasvatī (river) is the most sacred among all rivers, and it always brings well-being to the world. People, even those who have committed great misdeeds, having reached the Sarasvatī, never grieve, neither in the other world nor in this one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 35. The Sarasvatī (river) is the most sacred among all rivers, and it always brings well-being to the world. People, even those who have committed great misdeeds, having reached the Sarasvatī, never grieve, neither in the other world nor in this one.

sanskrit: ततो मुहुर्मुहुः प्रीत्या प्रेक्षमाणः सरस्वतीम् ।हयैर्युक्तं रथं शुभ्रमातिष्ठत परंतपः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tato muhurmuhuḥ prītyā prekṣamāṇaḥ sarasvatīm ,hayairyuktaṁ rathaṁ śubhramātiṣṭhata paraṁtapaḥ.
english: Then, the vanquisher of foes (paraṃtapa), lovingly gazing at the Sarasvatī (river) again and again, ascended his splendid chariot yoked with horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 36. Then, the vanquisher of foes (paraṃtapa), lovingly gazing at the Sarasvatī (river) again and again, ascended his splendid chariot yoked with horses.

sanskrit: स शीघ्रगामिना तेन रथेन यदुपुंगवः ।दिदृक्षुरभिसंप्राप्तः शिष्ययुद्धमुपस्थितम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sa śīghragāminā tena rathena yadupuṁgavaḥ ,didṛkṣurabhisaṁprāptaḥ śiṣyayuddhamupasthitam.
english: The best among the Yadus (Krishna) arrived in that swiftly moving chariot, eager to witness the battle between his disciples which had commenced.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 53 Verse 37. The best among the Yadus (Krishna) arrived in that swiftly moving chariot, eager to witness the battle between his disciples which had commenced.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं तदभवद्युद्धं तुमुलं जनमेजय ।यत्र दुःखान्वितो राजा धृतराष्ट्रोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ tadabhavadyuddhaṁ tumulaṁ janamejaya ,yatra duḥkhānvito rājā dhṛtarāṣṭro'bravīdidam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O Janamejaya, such was that tumultuous battle, during which King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, filled with sorrow, spoke these words."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "O Janamejaya, such was that tumultuous battle, during which King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, filled with sorrow, spoke these words."

sanskrit: रामं संनिहितं दृष्ट्वा गदायुद्ध उपस्थिते ।मम पुत्रः कथं भीमं प्रत्ययुध्यत संजय ॥२॥
transliteration: rāmaṁ saṁnihitaṁ dṛṣṭvā gadāyuddha upasthite ,mama putraḥ kathaṁ bhīmaṁ pratyayudhyata saṁjaya.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra asked: "O Sañjaya, with Rāma (Balarāma) present and the mace-fight having begun, how did my son confront Bhīma in battle?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 2. Dhṛtarāṣṭra asked: "O Sañjaya, with Rāma (Balarāma) present and the mace-fight having begun, how did my son confront Bhīma in battle?"

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।रामसांनिध्यमासाद्य पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तव ।युद्धकामो महाबाहुः समहृष्यत वीर्यवान् ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,rāmasāṁnidhyamāsādya putro duryodhanastava ,yuddhakāmo mahābāhuḥ samahṛṣyata vīryavān.
english: Sañjaya said: "Having attained the presence of Rāma (Balarāma), your mighty-armed and valiant son Duryodhana, eager for battle, greatly rejoiced."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 3. Sañjaya said: "Having attained the presence of Rāma (Balarāma), your mighty-armed and valiant son Duryodhana, eager for battle, greatly rejoiced."

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा लाङ्गलिनं राजा प्रत्युत्थाय च भारत ।प्रीत्या परमया युक्तो युधिष्ठिरमथाब्रवीत् ॥४॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā lāṅgalinaṁ rājā pratyutthāya ca bhārata ,prītyā paramayā yukto yudhiṣṭhiramathābravīt.
english: O Bhārata, having seen Balarāma (the wielder of the plough), the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) rose to meet him and, filled with supreme affection, then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 4. O Bhārata, having seen Balarāma (the wielder of the plough), the king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) rose to meet him and, filled with supreme affection, then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: समन्तपञ्चकं क्षिप्रमितो याम विशां पते ।प्रथितोत्तरवेदी सा देवलोके प्रजापतेः ॥५॥
transliteration: samantapañcakaṁ kṣipramito yāma viśāṁ pate ,prathitottaravedī sā devaloke prajāpateḥ.
english: O ruler of men, let us quickly go from here to Samantapañcaka. That place is renowned as Prajāpati's northern altar in the world of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 5. O ruler of men, let us quickly go from here to Samantapañcaka. That place is renowned as Prajāpati's northern altar in the world of the gods.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्महापुण्यतमे त्रैलोक्यस्य सनातने ।संग्रामे निधनं प्राप्य ध्रुवं स्वर्गो भविष्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: tasminmahāpuṇyatame trailokyasya sanātane ,saṁgrāme nidhanaṁ prāpya dhruvaṁ svargo bhaviṣyati.
english: In that supremely sacred, everlasting battle (saṅgrāma) of the three worlds, certainly, one who attains death will achieve heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 6. In that supremely sacred, everlasting battle (saṅgrāma) of the three worlds, certainly, one who attains death will achieve heaven.

sanskrit: तथेत्युक्त्वा महाराज कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।समन्तपञ्चकं वीरः प्रायादभिमुखः प्रभुः ॥७॥
transliteration: tathetyuktvā mahārāja kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,samantapañcakaṁ vīraḥ prāyādabhimukhaḥ prabhuḥ.
english: O great king, having said "So be it," Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, the valiant lord, then proceeded towards Samantapañcaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 7. O great king, having said "So be it," Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, the valiant lord, then proceeded towards Samantapañcaka.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा प्रगृह्य महतीं गदाम् ।पद्भ्याममर्षाद्द्युतिमानगच्छत्पाण्डवैः सह ॥८॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā pragṛhya mahatīṁ gadām ,padbhyāmamarṣāddyutimānagacchatpāṇḍavaiḥ saha.
english: Then, the radiant King Duryodhana, full of indignation, took up his mighty mace and went on foot accompanied by the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 8. Then, the radiant King Duryodhana, full of indignation, took up his mighty mace and went on foot accompanied by the Pandavas.

sanskrit: तथा यान्तं गदाहस्तं वर्मणा चापि दंशितम् ।अन्तरिक्षगता देवाः साधु साध्वित्यपूजयन् ।वातिकाश्च नरा येऽत्र दृष्ट्वा ते हर्षमागताः ॥९॥
transliteration: tathā yāntaṁ gadāhastaṁ varmaṇā cāpi daṁśitam ,antarikṣagatā devāḥ sādhu sādhvityapūjayan ,vātikāśca narā ye'tra dṛṣṭvā te harṣamāgatāḥ.
english: As he thus proceeded, mace in hand and clad in armor, the gods dwelling in the sky praised him, exclaiming 'Excellent! Excellent!' Moreover, the men present there, upon seeing him, were filled with joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 9. As he thus proceeded, mace in hand and clad in armor, the gods dwelling in the sky praised him, exclaiming 'Excellent! Excellent!' Moreover, the men present there, upon seeing him, were filled with joy.

sanskrit: स पाण्डवैः परिवृतः कुरुराजस्तवात्मजः ।मत्तस्येव गजेन्द्रस्य गतिमास्थाय सोऽव्रजत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa pāṇḍavaiḥ parivṛtaḥ kururājastavātmajaḥ ,mattasyeva gajendrasya gatimāsthāya so'vrajat.
english: Your son, the king of the Kurus, surrounded by the Pandavas, walked with the gait of an intoxicated lordly elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 10. Your son, the king of the Kurus, surrounded by the Pandavas, walked with the gait of an intoxicated lordly elephant.

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खनिनादेन भेरीणां च महास्वनैः ।सिंहनादैश्च शूराणां दिशः सर्वाः प्रपूरिताः ॥११॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhaninādena bherīṇāṁ ca mahāsvanaiḥ ,siṁhanādaiśca śūrāṇāṁ diśaḥ sarvāḥ prapūritāḥ.
english: Then, all directions were completely filled by the sounds of conch shells, the mighty roars of drums, and the lion-like roars of heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 11. Then, all directions were completely filled by the sounds of conch shells, the mighty roars of drums, and the lion-like roars of heroes.

sanskrit: प्रतीच्यभिमुखं देशं यथोद्दिष्टं सुतेन ते ।गत्वा च तैः परिक्षिप्तं समन्तात्सर्वतोदिशम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: pratīcyabhimukhaṁ deśaṁ yathoddiṣṭaṁ sutena te ,gatvā ca taiḥ parikṣiptaṁ samantātsarvatodiśam.
english: Having reached the western region, which your son had indicated, and which was completely surrounded by them on all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 12. Having reached the western region, which your son had indicated, and which was completely surrounded by them on all sides.

sanskrit: दक्षिणेन सरस्वत्याः स्वयनं तीर्थमुत्तमम् ।तस्मिन्देशे त्वनिरिणे तत्र युद्धमरोचयन् ॥१३॥
transliteration: dakṣiṇena sarasvatyāḥ svayanaṁ tīrthamuttamam ,tasmindeśe tvaniriṇe tatra yuddhamarocayan.
english: To the south of the Sarasvatī River, there was an excellent, naturally flowing holy site. In that fertile region, they indeed decided to wage battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 13. To the south of the Sarasvatī River, there was an excellent, naturally flowing holy site. In that fertile region, they indeed decided to wage battle.

sanskrit: ततो भीमो महाकोटिं गदां गृह्याथ वर्मभृत् ।बिभ्रद्रूपं महाराज सदृशं हि गरुत्मतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato bhīmo mahākoṭiṁ gadāṁ gṛhyātha varmabhṛt ,bibhradrūpaṁ mahārāja sadṛśaṁ hi garutmataḥ.
english: Then Bhīma, having taken up a mace with a great point and wearing his armor, assumed a form, O great king, that was indeed similar to that of Garuḍa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 14. Then Bhīma, having taken up a mace with a great point and wearing his armor, assumed a form, O great king, that was indeed similar to that of Garuḍa.

sanskrit: अवबद्धशिरस्त्राणः संख्ये काञ्चनवर्मभृत् ।रराज राजन्पुत्रस्ते काञ्चनः शैलराडिव ॥१५॥
transliteration: avabaddhaśirastrāṇaḥ saṁkhye kāñcanavarmabhṛt ,rarāja rājanputraste kāñcanaḥ śailarāḍiva.
english: Your son, O king, having his helmet fastened and clad in golden armor in battle, shone like a golden king of mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 15. Your son, O king, having his helmet fastened and clad in golden armor in battle, shone like a golden king of mountains.

sanskrit: वर्मभ्यां संवृतौ वीरौ भीमदुर्योधनावुभौ ।संयुगे च प्रकाशेते संरब्धाविव कुञ्जरौ ॥१६॥
transliteration: varmabhyāṁ saṁvṛtau vīrau bhīmaduryodhanāvubhau ,saṁyuge ca prakāśete saṁrabdhāviva kuñjarau.
english: Both heroes, Bhima and Duryodhana, covered in armor, appeared in battle like two enraged elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 16. Both heroes, Bhima and Duryodhana, covered in armor, appeared in battle like two enraged elephants.

sanskrit: रणमण्डलमध्यस्थौ भ्रातरौ तौ नरर्षभौ ।अशोभेतां महाराज चन्द्रसूर्याविवोदितौ ॥१७॥
transliteration: raṇamaṇḍalamadhyasthau bhrātarau tau nararṣabhau ,aśobhetāṁ mahārāja candrasūryāvivoditau.
english: O great king, those two brothers, those best of men, standing in the midst of the battle arena, shone like the risen moon and sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 17. O great king, those two brothers, those best of men, standing in the midst of the battle arena, shone like the risen moon and sun.

sanskrit: तावन्योन्यं निरीक्षेतां क्रुद्धाविव महाद्विपौ ।दहन्तौ लोचनै राजन्परस्परवधैषिणौ ॥१८॥
transliteration: tāvanyonyaṁ nirīkṣetāṁ kruddhāviva mahādvipau ,dahantau locanai rājanparasparavadhaiṣiṇau.
english: O king, those two looked at each other, like two enraged great elephants, burning (with anger) with their eyes, desiring each other's death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 18. O king, those two looked at each other, like two enraged great elephants, burning (with anger) with their eyes, desiring each other's death.

sanskrit: संप्रहृष्टमना राजन्गदामादाय कौरवः ।सृक्किणी संलिहन्राजन्क्रोधरक्तेक्षणः श्वसन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁprahṛṣṭamanā rājangadāmādāya kauravaḥ ,sṛkkiṇī saṁlihanrājankrodharaktekṣaṇaḥ śvasan.
english: O king, the Kuru (Duryodhana), with a very delighted mind, having taken up his mace, licking the corners of his mouth, with eyes red from anger, and breathing heavily.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 19. O king, the Kuru (Duryodhana), with a very delighted mind, having taken up his mace, licking the corners of his mouth, with eyes red from anger, and breathing heavily.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा गदामादाय वीर्यवान् ।भीमसेनमभिप्रेक्ष्य गजो गजमिवाह्वयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā gadāmādāya vīryavān ,bhīmasenamabhiprekṣya gajo gajamivāhvayat.
english: Then, the powerful King Duryodhana, taking his mace, challenged Bhimasena, just as one elephant challenges another.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 20. Then, the powerful King Duryodhana, taking his mace, challenged Bhimasena, just as one elephant challenges another.

sanskrit: अद्रिसारमयीं भीमस्तथैवादाय वीर्यवान् ।आह्वयामास नृपतिं सिंहः सिंहं यथा वने ॥२१॥
transliteration: adrisāramayīṁ bhīmastathaivādāya vīryavān ,āhvayāmāsa nṛpatiṁ siṁhaḥ siṁhaṁ yathā vane.
english: Similarly, the powerful Bhima, taking his mace which was as strong as a mountain, challenged the king (Duryodhana), just as a lion challenges another lion in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 21. Similarly, the powerful Bhima, taking his mace which was as strong as a mountain, challenged the king (Duryodhana), just as a lion challenges another lion in the forest.

sanskrit: तावुद्यतगदापाणी दुर्योधनवृकोदरौ ।संयुगे स्म प्रकाशेते गिरी सशिखराविव ॥२२॥
transliteration: tāvudyatagadāpāṇī duryodhanavṛkodarau ,saṁyuge sma prakāśete girī saśikharāviva.
english: With maces raised in their hands, Duryodhana and Vṛkodara (Bhima) shone in battle like two mountains with peaks.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 22. With maces raised in their hands, Duryodhana and Vṛkodara (Bhima) shone in battle like two mountains with peaks.

sanskrit: तावुभावभिसंक्रुद्धावुभौ भीमपराक्रमौ ।उभौ शिष्यौ गदायुद्धे रौहिणेयस्य धीमतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tāvubhāvabhisaṁkruddhāvubhau bhīmaparākramau ,ubhau śiṣyau gadāyuddhe rauhiṇeyasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Both of them, greatly enraged and possessing formidable valor, were also both disciples of the wise son of Rohiṇī (Balarama) in the art of mace-fighting.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 23. Both of them, greatly enraged and possessing formidable valor, were also both disciples of the wise son of Rohiṇī (Balarama) in the art of mace-fighting.

sanskrit: उभौ सदृशकर्माणौ यमवासवयोरिव ।तथा सदृशकर्माणौ वरुणस्य महाबलौ ॥२४॥
transliteration: ubhau sadṛśakarmāṇau yamavāsavayoriva ,tathā sadṛśakarmāṇau varuṇasya mahābalau.
english: Both were similar in their actions (karma), like Yama and Vasava (Indra). Similarly, these two exceedingly powerful individuals were comparable to Varuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 24. Both were similar in their actions (karma), like Yama and Vasava (Indra). Similarly, these two exceedingly powerful individuals were comparable to Varuna.

sanskrit: वासुदेवस्य रामस्य तथा वैश्रवणस्य च ।सदृशौ तौ महाराज मधुकैटभयोर्युधि ॥२५॥
transliteration: vāsudevasya rāmasya tathā vaiśravaṇasya ca ,sadṛśau tau mahārāja madhukaiṭabhayoryudhi.
english: O great king, they were comparable to Vasudeva (Krishna), Rama, and Vaishravana (Kubera); and in battle, they were like Madhu and Kaiṭabha (in their formidable might).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 25. O great king, they were comparable to Vasudeva (Krishna), Rama, and Vaishravana (Kubera); and in battle, they were like Madhu and Kaiṭabha (in their formidable might).

sanskrit: उभौ सदृशकर्माणौ रणे सुन्दोपसुन्दयोः ।तथैव कालस्य समौ मृत्योश्चैव परंतपौ ॥२६॥
transliteration: ubhau sadṛśakarmāṇau raṇe sundopasundayoḥ ,tathaiva kālasya samau mṛtyoścaiva paraṁtapau.
english: Both were similar in their deeds in battle, like Sunda and Upasunda. Just so, these two vanquishers of foes were equal to Time and Death (in their destructive power).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 26. Both were similar in their deeds in battle, like Sunda and Upasunda. Just so, these two vanquishers of foes were equal to Time and Death (in their destructive power).

sanskrit: अन्योन्यमभिधावन्तौ मत्ताविव महाद्विपौ ।वाशितासंगमे दृप्तौ शरदीव मदोत्कटौ ॥२७॥
transliteration: anyonyamabhidhāvantau mattāviva mahādvipau ,vāśitāsaṁgame dṛptau śaradīva madotkaṭau.
english: They charged at each other like two intoxicated great elephants. Proud and fiercely in rut, like elephants in autumn at the gathering of female elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 27. They charged at each other like two intoxicated great elephants. Proud and fiercely in rut, like elephants in autumn at the gathering of female elephants.

sanskrit: मत्ताविव जिगीषन्तौ मातङ्गौ भरतर्षभौ ।उभौ क्रोधविषं दीप्तं वमन्तावुरगाविव ॥२८॥
transliteration: mattāviva jigīṣantau mātaṅgau bharatarṣabhau ,ubhau krodhaviṣaṁ dīptaṁ vamantāvuragāviva.
english: Like two intoxicated elephants eager for victory, both the best among the Bharatas were spewing blazing poison of wrath, just like two serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 28. Like two intoxicated elephants eager for victory, both the best among the Bharatas were spewing blazing poison of wrath, just like two serpents.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यमभिसंरब्धौ प्रेक्षमाणावरिंदमौ ।उभौ भरतशार्दूलौ विक्रमेण समन्वितौ ॥२९॥
transliteration: anyonyamabhisaṁrabdhau prekṣamāṇāvariṁdamau ,ubhau bharataśārdūlau vikrameṇa samanvitau.
english: Both the subduers of foes, these two tigers among the Bharatas, were mutually enraged and looking at one another, endowed with great valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 29. Both the subduers of foes, these two tigers among the Bharatas, were mutually enraged and looking at one another, endowed with great valor.

sanskrit: सिंहाविव दुराधर्षौ गदायुद्धे परंतपौ ।नखदंष्ट्रायुधौ वीरौ व्याघ्राविव दुरुत्सहौ ॥३०॥
transliteration: siṁhāviva durādharṣau gadāyuddhe paraṁtapau ,nakhadaṁṣṭrāyudhau vīrau vyāghrāviva durutsahau.
english: Like two irresistible lions in a mace-fight, these two heroes, the tormentors of foes, were like two unconquerable tigers whose weapons are their claws and fangs.
