search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 30. Like two irresistible lions in a mace-fight, these two heroes, the tormentors of foes, were like two unconquerable tigers whose weapons are their claws and fangs.

sanskrit: प्रजासंहरणे क्षुब्धौ समुद्राविव दुस्तरौ ।लोहिताङ्गाविव क्रुद्धौ प्रतपन्तौ महारथौ ॥३१॥
transliteration: prajāsaṁharaṇe kṣubdhau samudrāviva dustarau ,lohitāṅgāviva kruddhau pratapantau mahārathau.
english: These two great warriors, agitated like two oceans difficult to cross in the annihilation of creatures, and enraged like two planets Mars (Lohitāṅga), were scorching everything.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 31. These two great warriors, agitated like two oceans difficult to cross in the annihilation of creatures, and enraged like two planets Mars (Lohitāṅga), were scorching everything.

sanskrit: रश्मिमन्तौ महात्मानौ दीप्तिमन्तौ महाबलौ ।ददृशाते कुरुश्रेष्ठौ कालसूर्याविवोदितौ ॥३२॥
transliteration: raśmimantau mahātmānau dīptimantau mahābalau ,dadṛśāte kuruśreṣṭhau kālasūryāvivoditau.
english: The two noble (mahātman), greatly powerful, and radiant best among the Kurus appeared like two suns risen at the time of cosmic dissolution.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 32. The two noble (mahātman), greatly powerful, and radiant best among the Kurus appeared like two suns risen at the time of cosmic dissolution.

sanskrit: व्याघ्राविव सुसंरब्धौ गर्जन्ताविव तोयदौ ।जहृषाते महाबाहू सिंहौ केसरिणाविव ॥३३॥
transliteration: vyāghrāviva susaṁrabdhau garjantāviva toyadau ,jahṛṣāte mahābāhū siṁhau kesariṇāviva.
english: The two mighty-armed ones (mahābāhu) greatly rejoiced; they were like fiercely enraged tigers, like thundering clouds, and like maned lions (keśarin).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 33. The two mighty-armed ones (mahābāhu) greatly rejoiced; they were like fiercely enraged tigers, like thundering clouds, and like maned lions (keśarin).

sanskrit: गजाविव सुसंरब्धौ ज्वलिताविव पावकौ ।ददृशुस्तौ महात्मानौ सशृङ्गाविव पर्वतौ ॥३४॥
transliteration: gajāviva susaṁrabdhau jvalitāviva pāvakau ,dadṛśustau mahātmānau saśṛṅgāviva parvatau.
english: Onlookers saw those two noble (mahātman) figures, who were like greatly enraged elephants, like blazing fires, and like mountains with their peaks.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 34. Onlookers saw those two noble (mahātman) figures, who were like greatly enraged elephants, like blazing fires, and like mountains with their peaks.

sanskrit: रोषात्प्रस्फुरमाणोष्ठौ निरीक्षन्तौ परस्परम् ।तौ समेतौ महात्मानौ गदाहस्तौ नरोत्तमौ ॥३५॥
transliteration: roṣātprasphuramāṇoṣṭhau nirīkṣantau parasparam ,tau sametau mahātmānau gadāhastau narottamau.
english: Those two best of men (narottama) and noble (mahātman) individuals, who had now met, glared at each other, their lips trembling with rage, each holding a mace in hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 35. Those two best of men (narottama) and noble (mahātman) individuals, who had now met, glared at each other, their lips trembling with rage, each holding a mace in hand.

sanskrit: उभौ परमसंहृष्टावुभौ परमसंमतौ ।सदश्वाविव हेषन्तौ बृंहन्ताविव कुञ्जरौ ॥३६॥
transliteration: ubhau paramasaṁhṛṣṭāvubhau paramasaṁmatau ,sadaśvāviva heṣantau bṛṁhantāviva kuñjarau.
english: Both of them, exceedingly delighted and highly esteemed, were neighing like fine horses and trumpeting like elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 36. Both of them, exceedingly delighted and highly esteemed, were neighing like fine horses and trumpeting like elephants.

sanskrit: वृषभाविव गर्जन्तौ दुर्योधनवृकोदरौ ।दैत्याविव बलोन्मत्तौ रेजतुस्तौ नरोत्तमौ ॥३७॥
transliteration: vṛṣabhāviva garjantau duryodhanavṛkodarau ,daityāviva balonmattau rejatustau narottamau.
english: Duryodhana and Vṛkodara, roaring like bulls, and intoxicated with strength like demons, those two finest among men shone brightly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 37. Duryodhana and Vṛkodara, roaring like bulls, and intoxicated with strength like demons, those two finest among men shone brightly.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजन्निदमाह युधिष्ठिरम् ।सृञ्जयैः सह तिष्ठन्तं तपन्तमिव भास्करम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājannidamāha yudhiṣṭhiram ,sṛñjayaiḥ saha tiṣṭhantaṁ tapantamiva bhāskaram.
english: Then, O King, Duryodhana spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, who was standing with the Sṛñjayas, radiating brilliance like the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 38. Then, O King, Duryodhana spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, who was standing with the Sṛñjayas, radiating brilliance like the sun.

sanskrit: इदं व्यवसितं युद्धं मम भीमस्य चोभयोः ।उपोपविष्टाः पश्यध्वं विमर्दं नृपसत्तमाः ॥३९॥
transliteration: idaṁ vyavasitaṁ yuddhaṁ mama bhīmasya cobhayoḥ ,upopaviṣṭāḥ paśyadhvaṁ vimardaṁ nṛpasattamāḥ.
english: This battle has been determined for both myself and Bhīma. You, the best of kings, seated nearby, observe this fierce conflict!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 39. This battle has been determined for both myself and Bhīma. You, the best of kings, seated nearby, observe this fierce conflict!

sanskrit: ततः समुपविष्टं तत्सुमहद्राजमण्डलम् ।विराजमानं ददृशे दिवीवादित्यमण्डलम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: tataḥ samupaviṣṭaṁ tatsumahadrājamaṇḍalam ,virājamānaṁ dadṛśe divīvādityamaṇḍalam.
english: Then, that vast assembly of kings, seated and resplendent, was seen, appearing like the solar disk in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 40. Then, that vast assembly of kings, seated and resplendent, was seen, appearing like the solar disk in the sky.

sanskrit: तेषां मध्ये महाबाहुः श्रीमान्केशवपूर्वजः ।उपविष्टो महाराज पूज्यमानः समन्ततः ॥४१॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ madhye mahābāhuḥ śrīmānkeśavapūrvajaḥ ,upaviṣṭo mahārāja pūjyamānaḥ samantataḥ.
english: Among them, O great king, the glorious and mighty-armed elder brother of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) was seated, being honored from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 41. Among them, O great king, the glorious and mighty-armed elder brother of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) was seated, being honored from all directions.

sanskrit: शुशुभे राजमध्यस्थो नीलवासाः सितप्रभः ।नक्षत्रैरिव संपूर्णो वृतो निशि निशाकरः ॥४२॥
transliteration: śuśubhe rājamadhyastho nīlavāsāḥ sitaprabhaḥ ,nakṣatrairiva saṁpūrṇo vṛto niśi niśākaraḥ.
english: He shone, standing amidst the kings, clad in blue garments and radiating a white splendor, like the full moon surrounded by stars in the night.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 42. He shone, standing amidst the kings, clad in blue garments and radiating a white splendor, like the full moon surrounded by stars in the night.

sanskrit: तौ तथा तु महाराज गदाहस्तौ दुरासदौ ।अन्योन्यं वाग्भिरुग्राभिस्तक्षमाणौ व्यवस्थितौ ॥४३॥
transliteration: tau tathā tu mahārāja gadāhastau durāsadau ,anyonyaṁ vāgbhirugrābhistakṣamāṇau vyavasthitau.
english: But those two, O great king, holding maces in their hands and formidable, stood confronting each other, assailing with fierce words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 43. But those two, O great king, holding maces in their hands and formidable, stood confronting each other, assailing with fierce words.

sanskrit: अप्रियाणि ततोऽन्योन्यमुक्त्वा तौ कुरुपुंगवौ ।उदीक्षन्तौ स्थितौ वीरौ वृत्रशक्राविवाहवे ॥४४॥
transliteration: apriyāṇi tato'nyonyamuktvā tau kurupuṁgavau ,udīkṣantau sthitau vīrau vṛtraśakrāvivāhave.
english: Thereafter, those two heroes, the foremost among the Kurus, having exchanged unpleasant words with each other, stood gazing, just like Vṛtra and Indra in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 54 Verse 44. Thereafter, those two heroes, the foremost among the Kurus, having exchanged unpleasant words with each other, stood gazing, just like Vṛtra and Indra in battle.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो वाग्युद्धमभवत्तुमुलं जनमेजय ।यत्र दुःखान्वितो राजा धृतराष्ट्रोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato vāgyuddhamabhavattumulaṁ janamejaya ,yatra duḥkhānvito rājā dhṛtarāṣṭro'bravīdidam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Thereafter, O Janamejaya, a tumultuous word-battle ensued, during which King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, afflicted by sorrow, spoke these words."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "Thereafter, O Janamejaya, a tumultuous word-battle ensued, during which King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, afflicted by sorrow, spoke these words."

sanskrit: धिगस्तु खलु मानुष्यं यस्य निष्ठेयमीदृशी ।एकादशचमूभर्ता यत्र पुत्रो ममाभिभूः ॥२॥
transliteration: dhigastu khalu mānuṣyaṁ yasya niṣṭheyamīdṛśī ,ekādaśacamūbhartā yatra putro mamābhibhūḥ.
english: Indeed, woe to the human condition (mānuṣyam), whose ultimate fate is such! Where my son, the commander of eleven armies, is now utterly defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 2. Indeed, woe to the human condition (mānuṣyam), whose ultimate fate is such! Where my son, the commander of eleven armies, is now utterly defeated.

sanskrit: आज्ञाप्य सर्वान्नृपतीन्भुक्त्वा चेमां वसुंधराम् ।गदामादाय वेगेन पदातिः प्रस्थितो रणम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ājñāpya sarvānnṛpatīnbhuktvā cemāṁ vasuṁdharām ,gadāmādāya vegena padātiḥ prasthito raṇam.
english: Having issued commands to all kings and ruled over this entire earth (vasundharā), he has now, swiftly taking up his mace, set out for battle on foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 3. Having issued commands to all kings and ruled over this entire earth (vasundharā), he has now, swiftly taking up his mace, set out for battle on foot.

sanskrit: भूत्वा हि जगतो नाथो ह्यनाथ इव मे सुतः ।गदामुद्यम्य यो याति किमन्यद्भागधेयतः ॥४॥
transliteration: bhūtvā hi jagato nātho hyanātha iva me sutaḥ ,gadāmudyamya yo yāti kimanyadbhāgadheyataḥ.
english: Though he has become the protector of the world, my son, like an orphan, goes forth wielding his mace. What else can this be but his destiny?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 4. Though he has become the protector of the world, my son, like an orphan, goes forth wielding his mace. What else can this be but his destiny?

sanskrit: अहो दुःखं महत्प्राप्तं पुत्रेण मम संजय ।एवमुक्त्वा स दुःखार्तो विरराम जनाधिपः ॥५॥
transliteration: aho duḥkhaṁ mahatprāptaṁ putreṇa mama saṁjaya ,evamuktvā sa duḥkhārto virarāma janādhipaḥ.
english: Oh, great suffering has come upon my son, Sañjaya. Having spoken thus, that king, overcome with grief, became silent.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 5. Oh, great suffering has come upon my son, Sañjaya. Having spoken thus, that king, overcome with grief, became silent.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।स मेघनिनदो हर्षाद्विनदन्निव गोवृषः ।आजुहाव ततः पार्थं युद्धाय युधि वीर्यवान् ॥६॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,sa meghaninado harṣādvinadanniva govṛṣaḥ ,ājuhāva tataḥ pārthaṁ yuddhāya yudhi vīryavān.
english: Sañjaya said: Then, that powerful one, with a roar like thunder, like a bellowing bull, joyfully summoned Pārtha for battle in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 6. Sañjaya said: Then, that powerful one, with a roar like thunder, like a bellowing bull, joyfully summoned Pārtha for battle in the fight.

sanskrit: भीममाह्वयमाने तु कुरुराजे महात्मनि ।प्रादुरासन्सुघोराणि रूपाणि विविधान्युत ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīmamāhvayamāne tu kururāje mahātmani ,prādurāsansughorāṇi rūpāṇi vividhānyuta.
english: Indeed, while the great-souled Kuru king was summoning Bhīma, exceedingly terrifying and diverse forms appeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 7. Indeed, while the great-souled Kuru king was summoning Bhīma, exceedingly terrifying and diverse forms appeared.

sanskrit: ववुर्वाताः सनिर्घाताः पांसुवर्षं पपात च ।बभूवुश्च दिशः सर्वास्तिमिरेण समावृताः ॥८॥
transliteration: vavurvātāḥ sanirghātāḥ pāṁsuvarṣaṁ papāta ca ,babhūvuśca diśaḥ sarvāstimireṇa samāvṛtāḥ.
english: Winds blew with thunderous crashes, and a rain of dust fell. All directions became enveloped in darkness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 8. Winds blew with thunderous crashes, and a rain of dust fell. All directions became enveloped in darkness.

sanskrit: महास्वनाः सनिर्घातास्तुमुला रोमहर्षणाः ।पेतुस्तथोल्काः शतशः स्फोटयन्त्यो नभस्तलम् ॥९॥
transliteration: mahāsvanāḥ sanirghātāstumulā romaharṣaṇāḥ ,petustatholkāḥ śataśaḥ sphoṭayantyo nabhastalam.
english: Hundreds of meteors fell, emitting great, thunderous, tumultuous, hair-raising sounds, as they burst open the firmament.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 9. Hundreds of meteors fell, emitting great, thunderous, tumultuous, hair-raising sounds, as they burst open the firmament.

sanskrit: राहुश्चाग्रसदादित्यमपर्वणि विशां पते ।चकम्पे च महाकम्पं पृथिवी सवनद्रुमा ॥१०॥
transliteration: rāhuścāgrasadādityamaparvaṇi viśāṁ pate ,cakampe ca mahākampaṁ pṛthivī savanadrumā.
english: O lord of the people, Rahu swallowed the sun on an unprescribed day (not an eclipse day). And the earth, along with its forests and trees, trembled intensely.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 10. O lord of the people, Rahu swallowed the sun on an unprescribed day (not an eclipse day). And the earth, along with its forests and trees, trembled intensely.

sanskrit: रूक्षाश्च वाताः प्रववुर्नीचैः शर्करवर्षिणः ।गिरीणां शिखराण्येव न्यपतन्त महीतले ॥११॥
transliteration: rūkṣāśca vātāḥ pravavurnīcaiḥ śarkaravarṣiṇaḥ ,girīṇāṁ śikharāṇyeva nyapatanta mahītale.
english: And harsh winds blew low, showering gravel. Even the peaks of the mountains fell down onto the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 11. And harsh winds blew low, showering gravel. Even the peaks of the mountains fell down onto the ground.

sanskrit: मृगा बहुविधाकाराः संपतन्ति दिशो दश ।दीप्ताः शिवाश्चाप्यनदन्घोररूपाः सुदारुणाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: mṛgā bahuvidhākārāḥ saṁpatanti diśo daśa ,dīptāḥ śivāścāpyanadanghorarūpāḥ sudāruṇāḥ.
english: Animals of many kinds rushed in all ten directions. Fierce and terribly formed jackals, exceedingly dreadful, also howled.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 12. Animals of many kinds rushed in all ten directions. Fierce and terribly formed jackals, exceedingly dreadful, also howled.

sanskrit: निर्घाताश्च महाघोरा बभूवू रोमहर्षणाः ।दीप्तायां दिशि राजेन्द्र मृगाश्चाशुभवादिनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: nirghātāśca mahāghorā babhūvū romaharṣaṇāḥ ,dīptāyāṁ diśi rājendra mṛgāścāśubhavādinaḥ.
english: And very terrible, hair-raising thunderclaps occurred. O king of kings, in the blazing direction, animals also made inauspicious sounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 13. And very terrible, hair-raising thunderclaps occurred. O king of kings, in the blazing direction, animals also made inauspicious sounds.

sanskrit: उदपानगताश्चापो व्यवर्धन्त समन्ततः ।अशरीरा महानादाः श्रूयन्ते स्म तदा नृप ॥१४॥
transliteration: udapānagatāścāpo vyavardhanta samantataḥ ,aśarīrā mahānādāḥ śrūyante sma tadā nṛpa.
english: And waters from wells swelled everywhere. Then, O king, great disembodied sounds were heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 14. And waters from wells swelled everywhere. Then, O king, great disembodied sounds were heard.

sanskrit: एवमादीनि दृष्ट्वाथ निमित्तानि वृकोदरः ।उवाच भ्रातरं ज्येष्ठं धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: evamādīni dṛṣṭvātha nimittāni vṛkodaraḥ ,uvāca bhrātaraṁ jyeṣṭhaṁ dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Then, having seen omens like these, Vrikodara (Bhīma) spoke to his elder brother, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 15. Then, having seen omens like these, Vrikodara (Bhīma) spoke to his elder brother, Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: नैष शक्तो रणे जेतुं मन्दात्मा मां सुयोधनः ।अद्य क्रोधं विमोक्ष्यामि निगूढं हृदये चिरम् ।सुयोधने कौरवेन्द्रे खाण्डवे पावको यथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: naiṣa śakto raṇe jetuṁ mandātmā māṁ suyodhanaḥ ,adya krodhaṁ vimokṣyāmi nigūḍhaṁ hṛdaye ciram ,suyodhane kauravendre khāṇḍave pāvako yathā.
english: This slow-witted Suyodhana is not capable of conquering me in battle. Today, I will unleash the anger (krodha) that has been long hidden in my heart, directing it at Suyodhana, the chief of the Kurus, just as fire (pāvaka) consumed the Khāṇḍava forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 16. This slow-witted Suyodhana is not capable of conquering me in battle. Today, I will unleash the anger (krodha) that has been long hidden in my heart, directing it at Suyodhana, the chief of the Kurus, just as fire (pāvaka) consumed the Khāṇḍava forest.

sanskrit: शल्यमद्योद्धरिष्यामि तव पाण्डव हृच्छयम् ।निहत्य गदया पापमिमं कुरुकुलाधमम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: śalyamadyoddhariṣyāmi tava pāṇḍava hṛcchayam ,nihatya gadayā pāpamimaṁ kurukulādhamam.
english: Today, O Pāṇḍava, I will remove this thorn (śalya) that lies in your heart by killing this sinful and most wretched person of the Kuru lineage (kurukulādhamam) with my mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 17. Today, O Pāṇḍava, I will remove this thorn (śalya) that lies in your heart by killing this sinful and most wretched person of the Kuru lineage (kurukulādhamam) with my mace.

sanskrit: अद्य कीर्तिमयीं मालां प्रतिमोक्ष्याम्यहं त्वयि ।हत्वेमं पापकर्माणं गदया रणमूर्धनि ॥१८॥
transliteration: adya kīrtimayīṁ mālāṁ pratimokṣyāmyahaṁ tvayi ,hatvemaṁ pāpakarmāṇaṁ gadayā raṇamūrdhani.
english: Today, I will bestow a garland of glory upon you by killing this sinful-doer (pāpakarmāṇam) with my mace on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 18. Today, I will bestow a garland of glory upon you by killing this sinful-doer (pāpakarmāṇam) with my mace on the battlefield.

sanskrit: अद्यास्य शतधा देहं भिनद्मि गदयानया ।नायं प्रवेष्टा नगरं पुनर्वारणसाह्वयम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: adyāsya śatadhā dehaṁ bhinadmi gadayānayā ,nāyaṁ praveṣṭā nagaraṁ punarvāraṇasāhvayam.
english: Today, with this mace, I will smash his body into a hundred pieces. He will not enter the city named Vāraṇasāhva (Hastinapura) again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 19. Today, with this mace, I will smash his body into a hundred pieces. He will not enter the city named Vāraṇasāhva (Hastinapura) again.

sanskrit: सर्पोत्सर्गस्य शयने विषदानस्य भोजने ।प्रमाणकोट्यां पातस्य दाहस्य जतुवेश्मनि ॥२०॥
transliteration: sarpotsargasya śayane viṣadānasya bhojane ,pramāṇakoṭyāṁ pātasya dāhasya jatuveśmani.
english: (These are the accounts) of releasing snakes into my bed, of giving me poison in my food, of my being pushed from a great height, and of burning me in the lac-house.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 20. (These are the accounts) of releasing snakes into my bed, of giving me poison in my food, of my being pushed from a great height, and of burning me in the lac-house.

sanskrit: सभायामवहासस्य सर्वस्वहरणस्य च ।वर्षमज्ञातवासस्य वनवासस्य चानघ ॥२१॥
transliteration: sabhāyāmavahāsasya sarvasvaharaṇasya ca ,varṣamajñātavāsasya vanavāsasya cānagha.
english: (Also, the humiliation) of ridicule in the assembly, and of the seizure of all my possessions. (There was also) a year of living in disguise, and the long period of dwelling in the forest, O sinless one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 21. (Also, the humiliation) of ridicule in the assembly, and of the seizure of all my possessions. (There was also) a year of living in disguise, and the long period of dwelling in the forest, O sinless one.

sanskrit: अद्यान्तमेषां दुःखानां गन्ता भरतसत्तम ।एकाह्ना विनिहत्येमं भविष्याम्यात्मनोऽनृणः ॥२२॥
transliteration: adyāntameṣāṁ duḥkhānāṁ gantā bharatasattama ,ekāhnā vinihatyemaṁ bhaviṣyāmyātmano'nṛṇaḥ.
english: Today will be the end of these sorrows (duḥkha), O best among the Bhāratas. Within a single day, by destroying this one, I shall become free from my obligation to my own self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 22. Today will be the end of these sorrows (duḥkha), O best among the Bhāratas. Within a single day, by destroying this one, I shall become free from my obligation to my own self (ātman).

sanskrit: अद्यायुर्धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्मतेरकृतात्मनः ।समाप्तं भरतश्रेष्ठ मातापित्रोश्च दर्शनम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: adyāyurdhārtarāṣṭrasya durmaterakṛtātmanaḥ ,samāptaṁ bharataśreṣṭha mātāpitrośca darśanam.
english: O best among the Bhāratas, today the life of the evil-minded, uncultivated son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra is finished, as is his parents' ability to see him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 23. O best among the Bhāratas, today the life of the evil-minded, uncultivated son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra is finished, as is his parents' ability to see him.

sanskrit: अद्यायं कुरुराजस्य शंतनोः कुलपांसनः ।प्राणाञ्श्रियं च राज्यं च त्यक्त्वा शेष्यति भूतले ॥२४॥
transliteration: adyāyaṁ kururājasya śaṁtanoḥ kulapāṁsanaḥ ,prāṇāñśriyaṁ ca rājyaṁ ca tyaktvā śeṣyati bhūtale.
english: Today, this disgrace of Śantanu's lineage, the king of the Kurus, will abandon his life, glory, and kingdom and lie dead on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 24. Today, this disgrace of Śantanu's lineage, the king of the Kurus, will abandon his life, glory, and kingdom and lie dead on the ground.

sanskrit: राजा च धृतराष्ट्रोऽद्य श्रुत्वा पुत्रं मया हतम् ।स्मरिष्यत्यशुभं कर्म यत्तच्छकुनिबुद्धिजम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: rājā ca dhṛtarāṣṭro'dya śrutvā putraṁ mayā hatam ,smariṣyatyaśubhaṁ karma yattacchakunibuddhijam.
english: And King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, today, having heard that his son was killed by me, will remember that inauspicious action (karma) which originated from Śakuni's advice.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 25. And King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, today, having heard that his son was killed by me, will remember that inauspicious action (karma) which originated from Śakuni's advice.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा राजशार्दूल गदामादाय वीर्यवान् ।अवातिष्ठत युद्धाय शक्रो वृत्रमिवाह्वयन् ॥२६॥
transliteration: ityuktvā rājaśārdūla gadāmādāya vīryavān ,avātiṣṭhata yuddhāya śakro vṛtramivāhvayan.
english: Having spoken thus, O tiger among kings, the mighty Bhīma took up his mace and stood ready for battle, just as Indra challenged Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 26. Having spoken thus, O tiger among kings, the mighty Bhīma took up his mace and stood ready for battle, just as Indra challenged Vṛtra.

sanskrit: तमुद्यतगदं दृष्ट्वा कैलासमिव शृङ्गिणम् ।भीमसेनः पुनः क्रुद्धो दुर्योधनमुवाच ह ॥२७॥
transliteration: tamudyatagadaṁ dṛṣṭvā kailāsamiva śṛṅgiṇam ,bhīmasenaḥ punaḥ kruddho duryodhanamuvāca ha.
english: Seeing him (Duryodhana) with his mace raised, like a peaked Mount Kailāsa, Bhīmasena, once again enraged, spoke to Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 27. Seeing him (Duryodhana) with his mace raised, like a peaked Mount Kailāsa, Bhīmasena, once again enraged, spoke to Duryodhana.

sanskrit: राज्ञश्च धृतराष्ट्रस्य तथा त्वमपि चात्मनः ।स्मर तद्दुष्कृतं कर्म यद्वृत्तं वारणावते ॥२८॥
transliteration: rājñaśca dhṛtarāṣṭrasya tathā tvamapi cātmanaḥ ,smara tadduṣkṛtaṁ karma yadvṛttaṁ vāraṇāvate.
english: Remember that evil deed (karma), both of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra and of yourself, which occurred in Vāraṇāvata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 28. Remember that evil deed (karma), both of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra and of yourself, which occurred in Vāraṇāvata.

sanskrit: द्रौपदी च परिक्लिष्टा सभायां यद्रजस्वला ।द्यूते च वञ्चितो राजा यत्त्वया सौबलेन च ॥२९॥
transliteration: draupadī ca parikliṣṭā sabhāyāṁ yadrajasvalā ,dyūte ca vañcito rājā yattvayā saubalena ca.
english: And remember that Draupadī was tormented in the assembly while she was menstruating, and that the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was cheated in the dice game by you and Saubala (Śakuni).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 29. And remember that Draupadī was tormented in the assembly while she was menstruating, and that the king (Yudhiṣṭhira) was cheated in the dice game by you and Saubala (Śakuni).

sanskrit: वने दुःखं च यत्प्राप्तमस्माभिस्त्वत्कृतं महत् ।विराटनगरे चैव योन्यन्तरगतैरिव ।तत्सर्वं यातयाम्यद्य दिष्ट्या दृष्टोऽसि दुर्मते ॥३०॥
transliteration: vane duḥkhaṁ ca yatprāptamasmābhistvatkṛtaṁ mahat ,virāṭanagare caiva yonyantaragatairiva ,tatsarvaṁ yātayāmyadya diṣṭyā dṛṣṭo'si durmate.
english: And the great suffering we endured in the forest, caused by you, and also in the city of Virāṭa, where we lived as if reborn into other forms - all that I now bring to an end. Fortunately, you are now seen, O evil-minded one!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 30. And the great suffering we endured in the forest, caused by you, and also in the city of Virāṭa, where we lived as if reborn into other forms - all that I now bring to an end. Fortunately, you are now seen, O evil-minded one!

sanskrit: त्वत्कृतेऽसौ हतः शेते शरतल्पे प्रतापवान् ।गाङ्गेयो रथिनां श्रेष्ठो निहतो याज्ञसेनिना ॥३१॥
transliteration: tvatkṛte'sau hataḥ śete śaratalpe pratāpavān ,gāṅgeyo rathināṁ śreṣṭho nihato yājñaseninā.
english: Because of you, that mighty Gaṅgeya (Bhīṣma), the foremost of charioteers, lies dead on a bed of arrows, having been killed by Yājñasenī (Dhṛṣṭadyumna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 31. Because of you, that mighty Gaṅgeya (Bhīṣma), the foremost of charioteers, lies dead on a bed of arrows, having been killed by Yājñasenī (Dhṛṣṭadyumna).

sanskrit: हतो द्रोणश्च कर्णश्च तथा शल्यः प्रतापवान् ।वैराग्नेरादिकर्ता च शकुनिः सौबलो हतः ॥३२॥
transliteration: hato droṇaśca karṇaśca tathā śalyaḥ pratāpavān ,vairāgnerādikartā ca śakuniḥ saubalo hataḥ.
english: Droṇa has been killed, and Karṇa, and also the mighty Śalya. And Śakuni, son of Subala, the instigator of the fire of enmity, has also been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 32. Droṇa has been killed, and Karṇa, and also the mighty Śalya. And Śakuni, son of Subala, the instigator of the fire of enmity, has also been killed.

sanskrit: प्रातिकामी तथा पापो द्रौपद्याः क्लेशकृद्धतः ।भ्रातरस्ते हताः सर्वे शूरा विक्रान्तयोधिनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: prātikāmī tathā pāpo draupadyāḥ kleśakṛddhataḥ ,bhrātaraste hatāḥ sarve śūrā vikrāntayodhinaḥ.
english: And also Prātikāmī, the sinful tormentor of Draupadī, has been killed. All your brothers, the brave and valiant fighters, have been killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 33. And also Prātikāmī, the sinful tormentor of Draupadī, has been killed. All your brothers, the brave and valiant fighters, have been killed.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो निहतास्त्वत्कृते नृपाः ।त्वामद्य निहनिष्यामि गदया नात्र संशयः ॥३४॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo nihatāstvatkṛte nṛpāḥ ,tvāmadya nihaniṣyāmi gadayā nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: These, and many other kings, have been killed because of you. Today, I will kill you with my mace; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 34. These, and many other kings, have been killed because of you. Today, I will kill you with my mace; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: इत्येवमुच्चै राजेन्द्र भाषमाणं वृकोदरम् ।उवाच वीतभी राजन्पुत्रस्ते सत्यविक्रमः ॥३५॥
transliteration: ityevamuccai rājendra bhāṣamāṇaṁ vṛkodaram ,uvāca vītabhī rājanputraste satyavikramaḥ.
english: Thus, while Vṛkodara (Bhīma) was speaking loudly, O king of kings, your fearless son, whose valor is true, spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 35. Thus, while Vṛkodara (Bhīma) was speaking loudly, O king of kings, your fearless son, whose valor is true, spoke.

sanskrit: किं कत्थितेन बहुधा युध्यस्व त्वं वृकोदर ।अद्य तेऽहं विनेष्यामि युद्धश्रद्धां कुलाधम ॥३६॥
transliteration: kiṁ katthitena bahudhā yudhyasva tvaṁ vṛkodara ,adya te'haṁ vineṣyāmi yuddhaśraddhāṁ kulādhama.
english: What is the use of much boasting? Fight, you son of Vṛka! Today, O disgrace of your family, I will surely remove your eagerness for battle (śraddhā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 36. What is the use of much boasting? Fight, you son of Vṛka! Today, O disgrace of your family, I will surely remove your eagerness for battle (śraddhā).

sanskrit: नैव दुर्योधनः क्षुद्र केनचित्त्वद्विधेन वै ।शक्यस्त्रासयितुं वाचा यथान्यः प्राकृतो नरः ॥३७॥
transliteration: naiva duryodhanaḥ kṣudra kenacittvadvidhena vai ,śakyastrāsayituṁ vācā yathānyaḥ prākṛto naraḥ.
english: O wretched one, Duryodhana is certainly not one who can be frightened by words by anyone like you, like any other common man.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 37. O wretched one, Duryodhana is certainly not one who can be frightened by words by anyone like you, like any other common man.

sanskrit: चिरकालेप्सितं दिष्ट्या हृदयस्थमिदं मम ।त्वया सह गदायुद्धं त्रिदशैरुपपादितम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: cirakālepsitaṁ diṣṭyā hṛdayasthamidaṁ mama ,tvayā saha gadāyuddhaṁ tridaśairupapāditam.
english: Fortunately, this mace-fight with you, which has been long-desired and held within my heart, has been brought about by the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 38. Fortunately, this mace-fight with you, which has been long-desired and held within my heart, has been brought about by the gods.

sanskrit: किं वाचा बहुनोक्तेन कत्थितेन च दुर्मते ।वाणी संपद्यतामेषा कर्मणा मा चिरं कृथाः ॥३९॥
transliteration: kiṁ vācā bahunoktena katthitena ca durmate ,vāṇī saṁpadyatāmeṣā karmaṇā mā ciraṁ kṛthāḥ.
english: O evil-minded one, what is the use of much spoken by words and boasting? Let this speech be fulfilled by action (karma); do not delay!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 39. O evil-minded one, what is the use of much spoken by words and boasting? Let this speech be fulfilled by action (karma); do not delay!

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा सर्व एवाभ्यपूजयन् ।राजानः सोमकाश्चैव ये तत्रासन्समागताः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā sarva evābhyapūjayan ,rājānaḥ somakāścaiva ye tatrāsansamāgatāḥ.
english: Hearing those words of his, all the kings and Somakas who had assembled there indeed honored him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 40. Hearing those words of his, all the kings and Somakas who had assembled there indeed honored him.

sanskrit: ततः संपूजितः सर्वैः संप्रहृष्टतनूरुहः ।भूयो धीरं मनश्चक्रे युद्धाय कुरुनन्दनः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁpūjitaḥ sarvaiḥ saṁprahṛṣṭatanūruhaḥ ,bhūyo dhīraṁ manaścakre yuddhāya kurunandanaḥ.
english: Then, having been greatly honored by all, and with his body hairs bristling with exhilaration, the delight of the Kurus (Duryodhana) again made his resolve firm for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 41. Then, having been greatly honored by all, and with his body hairs bristling with exhilaration, the delight of the Kurus (Duryodhana) again made his resolve firm for battle.

sanskrit: तं मत्तमिव मातङ्गं तलतालैर्नराधिपाः ।भूयः संहर्षयां चक्रुर्दुर्योधनममर्षणम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: taṁ mattamiva mātaṅgaṁ talatālairnarādhipāḥ ,bhūyaḥ saṁharṣayāṁ cakrurduryodhanamamarṣaṇam.
english: The kings, with the clapping of their hands, again greatly encouraged that indignant Duryodhana, who was like an intoxicated elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 42. The kings, with the clapping of their hands, again greatly encouraged that indignant Duryodhana, who was like an intoxicated elephant.

sanskrit: तं महात्मा महात्मानं गदामुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ।अभिदुद्राव वेगेन धार्तराष्ट्रं वृकोदरः ॥४३॥
transliteration: taṁ mahātmā mahātmānaṁ gadāmudyamya pāṇḍavaḥ ,abhidudrāva vegena dhārtarāṣṭraṁ vṛkodaraḥ.
english: Raising his mace, the great-souled Pāṇḍava, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), swiftly rushed towards that great-souled son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 43. Raising his mace, the great-souled Pāṇḍava, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), swiftly rushed towards that great-souled son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: बृंहन्ति कुञ्जरास्तत्र हया हेषन्ति चासकृत् ।शस्त्राणि चाप्यदीप्यन्त पाण्डवानां जयैषिणाम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: bṛṁhanti kuñjarāstatra hayā heṣanti cāsakṛt ,śastrāṇi cāpyadīpyanta pāṇḍavānāṁ jayaiṣiṇām.
english: There, elephants trumpeted and horses neighed repeatedly. The weapons of the Pandavas, who sought victory, also shone brilliantly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 55 Verse 44. There, elephants trumpeted and horses neighed repeatedly. The weapons of the Pandavas, who sought victory, also shone brilliantly.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततो दुर्योधनो दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनं तथागतम् ।प्रत्युद्ययावदीनात्मा वेगेन महता नदन् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tato duryodhano dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenaṁ tathāgatam ,pratyudyayāvadīnātmā vegena mahatā nadan.
english: Sañjaya said: Then Duryodhana, whose spirit was undaunted (adīnātmā), seeing Bhīmasena arrive, rushed towards him with great speed, roaring.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: Then Duryodhana, whose spirit was undaunted (adīnātmā), seeing Bhīmasena arrive, rushed towards him with great speed, roaring.

sanskrit: समापेततुरानद्य शृङ्गिणौ वृषभाविव ।महानिर्घातघोषश्च संप्रहारस्तयोरभूत् ॥२॥
transliteration: samāpetaturānadya śṛṅgiṇau vṛṣabhāviva ,mahānirghātaghoṣaśca saṁprahārastayorabhūt.
english: They rushed together, having roared loudly, like two horned bulls. A great, thunderous sound (mahānirghātaghoṣa) and a fierce clash (samprāhāra) occurred between the two of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 2. They rushed together, having roared loudly, like two horned bulls. A great, thunderous sound (mahānirghātaghoṣa) and a fierce clash (samprāhāra) occurred between the two of them.

sanskrit: अभवच्च तयोर्युद्धं तुमुलं रोमहर्षणम् ।जिगीषतोर्युधान्योन्यमिन्द्रप्रह्रादयोरिव ॥३॥
transliteration: abhavacca tayoryuddhaṁ tumulaṁ romaharṣaṇam ,jigīṣatoryudhānyonyamindraprahrādayoriva.
english: And a tumultuous, hair-raising battle occurred between those two, who were eager to conquer each other through combat, like that between Indra and Prahrāda.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 3. And a tumultuous, hair-raising battle occurred between those two, who were eager to conquer each other through combat, like that between Indra and Prahrāda.

sanskrit: रुधिरोक्षितसर्वाङ्गौ गदाहस्तौ मनस्विनौ ।ददृशाते महात्मानौ पुष्पिताविव किंशुकौ ॥४॥
transliteration: rudhirokṣitasarvāṅgau gadāhastau manasvinau ,dadṛśāte mahātmānau puṣpitāviva kiṁśukau.
english: The two resolute (manasvin), great souls (mahātman), with all their limbs drenched in blood and holding maces, appeared like two blossoming kimśuka trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 4. The two resolute (manasvin), great souls (mahātman), with all their limbs drenched in blood and holding maces, appeared like two blossoming kimśuka trees.

sanskrit: तथा तस्मिन्महायुद्धे वर्तमाने सुदारुणे ।खद्योतसंघैरिव खं दर्शनीयं व्यरोचत ॥५॥
transliteration: tathā tasminmahāyuddhe vartamāne sudāruṇe ,khadyotasaṁghairiva khaṁ darśanīyaṁ vyarocata.
english: And thus, as that great, exceedingly dreadful battle raged, the sky shone beautifully, as if illuminated by swarms of fireflies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 5. And thus, as that great, exceedingly dreadful battle raged, the sky shone beautifully, as if illuminated by swarms of fireflies.

sanskrit: तथा तस्मिन्वर्तमाने संकुले तुमुले भृशम् ।उभावपि परिश्रान्तौ युध्यमानावरिंदमौ ॥६॥
transliteration: tathā tasminvartamāne saṁkule tumule bhṛśam ,ubhāvapi pariśrāntau yudhyamānāvariṁdamau.
english: And so, as that crowded and tumultuous (battle) raged, both of them, the subjugators of enemies, fighting, became exceedingly exhausted.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 6. And so, as that crowded and tumultuous (battle) raged, both of them, the subjugators of enemies, fighting, became exceedingly exhausted.

sanskrit: तौ मुहूर्तं समाश्वस्य पुनरेव परंतपौ ।अभ्यहारयतां तत्र संप्रगृह्य गदे शुभे ॥७॥
transliteration: tau muhūrtaṁ samāśvasya punareva paraṁtapau ,abhyahārayatāṁ tatra saṁpragṛhya gade śubhe.
english: Those two, the tormentors of their enemies, having revived for a moment, then again initiated an attack there, firmly grasping their two auspicious maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 7. Those two, the tormentors of their enemies, having revived for a moment, then again initiated an attack there, firmly grasping their two auspicious maces.

sanskrit: तौ तु दृष्ट्वा महावीर्यौ समाश्वस्तौ नरर्षभौ ।बलिनौ वारणौ यद्वद्वाशितार्थे मदोत्कटौ ॥८॥
transliteration: tau tu dṛṣṭvā mahāvīryau samāśvastau nararṣabhau ,balinau vāraṇau yadvadvāśitārthe madotkaṭau.
english: But having observed those two supremely powerful and confident best among men, who were strong like two infuriated elephants in rut, fighting for a female.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 8. But having observed those two supremely powerful and confident best among men, who were strong like two infuriated elephants in rut, fighting for a female.

sanskrit: अपारवीर्यौ संप्रेक्ष्य प्रगृहीतगदावुभौ ।विस्मयं परमं जग्मुर्देवगन्धर्वदानवाः ॥९॥
transliteration: apāravīryau saṁprekṣya pragṛhītagadāvubhau ,vismayaṁ paramaṁ jagmurdevagandharvadānavāḥ.
english: Having clearly seen both of them, wielding their maces and possessing immeasurable might, the gods, Gandharvas, and Danavas were struck with supreme astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 9. Having clearly seen both of them, wielding their maces and possessing immeasurable might, the gods, Gandharvas, and Danavas were struck with supreme astonishment.

sanskrit: प्रगृहीतगदौ दृष्ट्वा दुर्योधनवृकोदरौ ।संशयः सर्वभूतानां विजये समपद्यत ॥१०॥
transliteration: pragṛhītagadau dṛṣṭvā duryodhanavṛkodarau ,saṁśayaḥ sarvabhūtānāṁ vijaye samapadyata.
english: Upon seeing Duryodhana and Vṛkodara, both wielding their maces, doubt arose among all beings concerning the outcome of the victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 10. Upon seeing Duryodhana and Vṛkodara, both wielding their maces, doubt arose among all beings concerning the outcome of the victory.

sanskrit: समागम्य ततो भूयो भ्रातरौ बलिनां वरौ ।अन्योन्यस्यान्तरप्रेप्सू प्रचक्रातेऽन्तरं प्रति ॥११॥
transliteration: samāgamya tato bhūyo bhrātarau balināṁ varau ,anyonyasyāntaraprepsū pracakrāte'ntaraṁ prati.
english: Then, the two brothers, best among the strong, having approached each other again, began to seek out each other's weak points.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 11. Then, the two brothers, best among the strong, having approached each other again, began to seek out each other's weak points.

sanskrit: यमदण्डोपमां गुर्वीमिन्द्राशनिमिवोद्यताम् ।ददृशुः प्रेक्षका राजन्रौद्रीं विशसनीं गदाम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: yamadaṇḍopamāṁ gurvīmindrāśanimivodyatām ,dadṛśuḥ prekṣakā rājanraudrīṁ viśasanīṁ gadām.
english: O King, the spectators saw the terrifying and destructive mace, which was heavy, resembled Yama's mace, and was raised like Indra's thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 12. O King, the spectators saw the terrifying and destructive mace, which was heavy, resembled Yama's mace, and was raised like Indra's thunderbolt.

sanskrit: आविध्यतो गदां तस्य भीमसेनस्य संयुगे ।शब्दः सुतुमुलो घोरो मुहूर्तं समपद्यत ॥१३॥
transliteration: āvidhyato gadāṁ tasya bhīmasenasya saṁyuge ,śabdaḥ sutumulo ghoro muhūrtaṁ samapadyata.
english: In that battle, a very loud and dreadful sound, produced by Bhīmasena whirling his mace, arose for a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 13. In that battle, a very loud and dreadful sound, produced by Bhīmasena whirling his mace, arose for a moment.

sanskrit: आविध्यन्तमभिप्रेक्ष्य धार्तराष्ट्रोऽथ पाण्डवम् ।गदामलघुवेगां तां विस्मितः संबभूव ह ॥१४॥
transliteration: āvidhyantamabhiprekṣya dhārtarāṣṭro'tha pāṇḍavam ,gadāmalaghuvegāṁ tāṁ vismitaḥ saṁbabhūva ha.
english: Then, seeing that Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena) whirling the swiftly-moving mace, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) was indeed astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 14. Then, seeing that Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena) whirling the swiftly-moving mace, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) was indeed astonished.

sanskrit: चरंश्च विविधान्मार्गान्मण्डलानि च भारत ।अशोभत तदा वीरो भूय एव वृकोदरः ॥१५॥
transliteration: caraṁśca vividhānmārgānmaṇḍalāni ca bhārata ,aśobhata tadā vīro bhūya eva vṛkodaraḥ.
english: O Bhārata, then the hero Vṛkodara (Bhīmasena), executing various maneuvers and circular movements, appeared even more glorious.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 15. O Bhārata, then the hero Vṛkodara (Bhīmasena), executing various maneuvers and circular movements, appeared even more glorious.

sanskrit: तौ परस्परमासाद्य यत्तावन्योन्यरक्षणे ।मार्जाराविव भक्षार्थे ततक्षाते मुहुर्मुहुः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tau parasparamāsādya yattāvanyonyarakṣaṇe ,mārjārāviva bhakṣārthe tatakṣāte muhurmuhuḥ.
english: Having come upon each other, and being intent on their mutual self-defense, they struck each other again and again, like two cats fighting for prey.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 16. Having come upon each other, and being intent on their mutual self-defense, they struck each other again and again, like two cats fighting for prey.

sanskrit: अचरद्भीमसेनस्तु मार्गान्बहुविधांस्तथा ।मण्डलानि विचित्राणि स्थानानि विविधानि च ॥१७॥
transliteration: acaradbhīmasenastu mārgānbahuvidhāṁstathā ,maṇḍalāni vicitrāṇi sthānāni vividhāni ca.
english: Bhimasena, however, performed many kinds of movements, as well as various and elaborate circular maneuvers and diverse stances.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 17. Bhimasena, however, performed many kinds of movements, as well as various and elaborate circular maneuvers and diverse stances.

sanskrit: गोमूत्रिकाणि चित्राणि गतप्रत्यागतानि च ।परिमोक्षं प्रहाराणां वर्जनं परिधावनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: gomūtrikāṇi citrāṇi gatapratyāgatāni ca ,parimokṣaṁ prahārāṇāṁ varjanaṁ paridhāvanam.
english: (He displayed) varied cow-urine-like movements, advances and retreats, and (he excelled in) warding off blows, avoiding them, and circumambulation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 18. (He displayed) varied cow-urine-like movements, advances and retreats, and (he excelled in) warding off blows, avoiding them, and circumambulation.

sanskrit: अभिद्रवणमाक्षेपमवस्थानं सविग्रहम् ।परावर्तनसंवर्तमवप्लुतमथाप्लुतम् ।उपन्यस्तमपन्यस्तं गदायुद्धविशारदौ ॥१९॥
transliteration: abhidravaṇamākṣepamavasthānaṁ savigraham ,parāvartanasaṁvartamavaplutamathāplutam ,upanyastamapanyastaṁ gadāyuddhaviśāradau.
english: These two, skilled in mace-fighting (gadā-yuddha), displayed rushing attacks, parrying, firm stances with resistance, turning and whirling, plunging and leaping, as well as close-quarter movements and feints.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 19. These two, skilled in mace-fighting (gadā-yuddha), displayed rushing attacks, parrying, firm stances with resistance, turning and whirling, plunging and leaping, as well as close-quarter movements and feints.

sanskrit: एवं तौ विचरन्तौ तु न्यघ्नतां वै परस्परम् ।वञ्चयन्तौ पुनश्चैव चेरतुः कुरुसत्तमौ ॥२०॥
transliteration: evaṁ tau vicarantau tu nyaghnatāṁ vai parasparam ,vañcayantau punaścaiva ceratuḥ kurusattamau.
english: Thus, moving about, those two (champions) indeed struck each other. Deceiving one another again and again, those two best among the Kurus continued to maneuver.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 20. Thus, moving about, those two (champions) indeed struck each other. Deceiving one another again and again, those two best among the Kurus continued to maneuver.

sanskrit: विक्रीडन्तौ सुबलिनौ मण्डलानि प्रचेरतुः ।गदाहस्तौ ततस्तौ तु मण्डलावस्थितौ बली ॥२१॥
transliteration: vikrīḍantau subalinau maṇḍalāni praceratuḥ ,gadāhastau tatastau tu maṇḍalāvasthitau balī.
english: Those two very strong warriors, skillfully engaging, performed circular maneuvers. Then, maces in hand, those two powerful men took up circular stances.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 21. Those two very strong warriors, skillfully engaging, performed circular maneuvers. Then, maces in hand, those two powerful men took up circular stances.

sanskrit: दक्षिणं मण्डलं राजन्धार्तराष्ट्रोऽभ्यवर्तत ।सव्यं तु मण्डलं तत्र भीमसेनोऽभ्यवर्तत ॥२२॥
transliteration: dakṣiṇaṁ maṇḍalaṁ rājandhārtarāṣṭro'bhyavartata ,savyaṁ tu maṇḍalaṁ tatra bhīmaseno'bhyavartata.
english: O King, Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) moved towards the right flank. But Bhimasena, there, moved towards the left flank.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 22. O King, Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana) moved towards the right flank. But Bhimasena, there, moved towards the left flank.

sanskrit: तथा तु चरतस्तस्य भीमस्य रणमूर्धनि ।दुर्योधनो महाराज पार्श्वदेशेऽभ्यताडयत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tathā tu caratastasya bhīmasya raṇamūrdhani ,duryodhano mahārāja pārśvadeśe'bhyatāḍayat.
english: Thus, while Bhima was maneuvering in the thick of battle, O great king, Duryodhana struck him on his flank.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 23. Thus, while Bhima was maneuvering in the thick of battle, O great king, Duryodhana struck him on his flank.

sanskrit: आहतस्तु तदा भीमस्तव पुत्रेण भारत ।आविध्यत गदां गुर्वीं प्रहारं तमचिन्तयन् ॥२४॥
transliteration: āhatastu tadā bhīmastava putreṇa bhārata ,āvidhyata gadāṁ gurvīṁ prahāraṁ tamacintayan.
english: O Bhārata, at that moment, Bhīma, having been struck by your son, disregarded that blow and whirled his mighty mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 24. O Bhārata, at that moment, Bhīma, having been struck by your son, disregarded that blow and whirled his mighty mace.

sanskrit: इन्द्राशनिसमां घोरां यमदण्डमिवोद्यताम् ।ददृशुस्ते महाराज भीमसेनस्य तां गदाम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: indrāśanisamāṁ ghorāṁ yamadaṇḍamivodyatām ,dadṛśuste mahārāja bhīmasenasya tāṁ gadām.
english: O great king, they saw that mace of Bhīmasena's, dreadful, like Indra's thunderbolt, and as if it were the uplifted rod of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 25. O great king, they saw that mace of Bhīmasena's, dreadful, like Indra's thunderbolt, and as if it were the uplifted rod of Yama.

sanskrit: आविध्यन्तं गदां दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनं तवात्मजः ।समुद्यम्य गदां घोरां प्रत्यविध्यदरिंदमः ॥२६॥
transliteration: āvidhyantaṁ gadāṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenaṁ tavātmajaḥ ,samudyamya gadāṁ ghorāṁ pratyavidhyadariṁdamaḥ.
english: Having seen Bhīmasena whirling his mace, your son, O subduer of enemies, also raised his dreadful mace and struck back.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 26. Having seen Bhīmasena whirling his mace, your son, O subduer of enemies, also raised his dreadful mace and struck back.

sanskrit: गदामारुतवेगेन तव पुत्रस्य भारत ।शब्द आसीत्सुतुमुलस्तेजश्च समजायत ॥२७॥
transliteration: gadāmārutavegena tava putrasya bhārata ,śabda āsītsutumulastejaśca samajāyata.
english: O Bhārata, by the force of your son's mace-wind, a very tumultuous sound arose, and great brilliance was generated.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 27. O Bhārata, by the force of your son's mace-wind, a very tumultuous sound arose, and great brilliance was generated.

sanskrit: स चरन्विविधान्मार्गान्मण्डलानि च भागशः ।समशोभत तेजस्वी भूयो भीमात्सुयोधनः ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa caranvividhānmārgānmaṇḍalāni ca bhāgaśaḥ ,samaśobhata tejasvī bhūyo bhīmātsuyodhanaḥ.
english: As he moved through various paths and formations, division by division, the brilliant Duryodhana appeared even more glorious than Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 28. As he moved through various paths and formations, division by division, the brilliant Duryodhana appeared even more glorious than Bhīma.

sanskrit: आविद्धा सर्ववेगेन भीमेन महती गदा ।सधूमं सार्चिषं चाग्निं मुमोचोग्रा महास्वना ॥२९॥
transliteration: āviddhā sarvavegena bhīmena mahatī gadā ,sadhūmaṁ sārciṣaṁ cāgniṁ mumocogrā mahāsvanā.
english: The great mace, swung with all its force by Bhīma, emitted a terrible, loud roar and discharged fire along with smoke and flames.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 29. The great mace, swung with all its force by Bhīma, emitted a terrible, loud roar and discharged fire along with smoke and flames.

sanskrit: आधूतां भीमसेनेन गदां दृष्ट्वा सुयोधनः ।अद्रिसारमयीं गुर्वीमाविध्यन्बह्वशोभत ॥३०॥
transliteration: ādhūtāṁ bhīmasenena gadāṁ dṛṣṭvā suyodhanaḥ ,adrisāramayīṁ gurvīmāvidhyanbahvaśobhata.
english: Having seen the mace wielded by Bhīmasena, Duryodhana, swinging his own heavy mace, which was as hard as rock, appeared exceedingly glorious.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 30. Having seen the mace wielded by Bhīmasena, Duryodhana, swinging his own heavy mace, which was as hard as rock, appeared exceedingly glorious.

sanskrit: गदामारुतवेगं हि दृष्ट्वा तस्य महात्मनः ।भयं विवेश पाण्डून्वै सर्वानेव ससोमकान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: gadāmārutavegaṁ hi dṛṣṭvā tasya mahātmanaḥ ,bhayaṁ viveśa pāṇḍūnvai sarvāneva sasomakān.
english: Indeed, having perceived the tremendous force of that great-souled (mahātman) Duryodhana's mace, fear certainly entered all the Pāṇḍavas, along with their companions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 31. Indeed, having perceived the tremendous force of that great-souled (mahātman) Duryodhana's mace, fear certainly entered all the Pāṇḍavas, along with their companions.

sanskrit: तौ दर्शयन्तौ समरे युद्धक्रीडां समन्ततः ।गदाभ्यां सहसान्योन्यमाजघ्नतुररिंदमौ ॥३२॥
transliteration: tau darśayantau samare yuddhakrīḍāṁ samantataḥ ,gadābhyāṁ sahasānyonyamājaghnaturariṁdamau.
english: Displaying their martial prowess throughout the battlefield, those two subdue-ers of enemies suddenly struck each other with their maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 32. Displaying their martial prowess throughout the battlefield, those two subdue-ers of enemies suddenly struck each other with their maces.

sanskrit: तौ परस्परमासाद्य दंष्ट्राभ्यां द्विरदौ यथा ।अशोभेतां महाराज शोणितेन परिप्लुतौ ॥३३॥
transliteration: tau parasparamāsādya daṁṣṭrābhyāṁ dviradau yathā ,aśobhetāṁ mahārāja śoṇitena pariplutau.
english: O great king, just like two elephants assailing each other with their tusks, those two combatants, drenched in blood, appeared splendid.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 33. O great king, just like two elephants assailing each other with their tusks, those two combatants, drenched in blood, appeared splendid.

sanskrit: एवं तदभवद्युद्धं घोररूपमसंवृतम् ।परिवृत्तेऽहनि क्रूरं वृत्रवासवयोरिव ॥३४॥
transliteration: evaṁ tadabhavadyuddhaṁ ghorarūpamasaṁvṛtam ,parivṛtte'hani krūraṁ vṛtravāsavayoriva.
english: Thus, that terrifying and unrestrained battle took place, fierce like the one between Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra), as the day progressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 34. Thus, that terrifying and unrestrained battle took place, fierce like the one between Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra), as the day progressed.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा व्यवस्थितं भीमं तव पुत्रो महाबलः ।चरंश्चित्रतरान्मार्गान्कौन्तेयमभिदुद्रुवे ॥३५॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā vyavasthitaṁ bhīmaṁ tava putro mahābalaḥ ,caraṁścitratarānmārgānkaunteyamabhidudruve.
english: Having seen Bhīma standing firm, your mighty son, traversing many intricate maneuvers, rushed towards the son of Kuntī (Kaunteya).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 35. Having seen Bhīma standing firm, your mighty son, traversing many intricate maneuvers, rushed towards the son of Kuntī (Kaunteya).

sanskrit: तस्य भीमो महावेगां जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृताम् ।अभिक्रुद्धस्य क्रुद्धस्तु ताडयामास तां गदाम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tasya bhīmo mahāvegāṁ jāmbūnadapariṣkṛtām ,abhikruddhasya kruddhastu tāḍayāmāsa tāṁ gadām.
english: But Bhima, enraged, struck that very powerful mace, adorned with Jambunada gold, belonging to the exceedingly wrathful (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 36. But Bhima, enraged, struck that very powerful mace, adorned with Jambunada gold, belonging to the exceedingly wrathful (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: सविस्फुलिङ्गो निर्ह्रादस्तयोस्तत्राभिघातजः ।प्रादुरासीन्महाराज सृष्टयोर्वज्रयोरिव ॥३७॥
transliteration: savisphuliṅgo nirhrādastayostatrābhighātajaḥ ,prādurāsīnmahārāja sṛṣṭayorvajrayoriva.
english: O great king, a roar accompanied by sparks, born from the impact of those two, appeared there, just like the clash of two thunderbolts (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 37. O great king, a roar accompanied by sparks, born from the impact of those two, appeared there, just like the clash of two thunderbolts (vajra).

sanskrit: वेगवत्या तया तत्र भीमसेनप्रमुक्तया ।निपतन्त्या महाराज पृथिवी समकम्पत ॥३८॥
transliteration: vegavatyā tayā tatra bhīmasenapramuktayā ,nipatantyā mahārāja pṛthivī samakampata.
english: O great king, there, the earth trembled due to that swift and falling (mace) released by Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 38. O great king, there, the earth trembled due to that swift and falling (mace) released by Bhimasena.

sanskrit: तां नामृष्यत कौरव्यो गदां प्रतिहतां रणे ।मत्तो द्विप इव क्रुद्धः प्रतिकुञ्जरदर्शनात् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tāṁ nāmṛṣyata kauravyo gadāṁ pratihatāṁ raṇe ,matto dvipa iva kruddhaḥ pratikuñjaradarśanāt.
english: The Kuru prince (Duryodhana), enraged, did not endure that mace, which had been repelled in battle, just like an intoxicated elephant (dvipa) at the sight of a rival elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 39. The Kuru prince (Duryodhana), enraged, did not endure that mace, which had been repelled in battle, just like an intoxicated elephant (dvipa) at the sight of a rival elephant.

sanskrit: स सव्यं मण्डलं राजन्नुद्भ्राम्य कृतनिश्चयः ।आजघ्ने मूर्ध्नि कौन्तेयं गदया भीमवेगया ॥४०॥
transliteration: sa savyaṁ maṇḍalaṁ rājannudbhrāmya kṛtaniścayaḥ ,ājaghne mūrdhni kaunteyaṁ gadayā bhīmavegayā.
english: O King, having made a firm resolve, he whirled [his mace] in a leftward circle and struck Kunti's son (Bhima) on the head with a mace of terrible force.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 40. O King, having made a firm resolve, he whirled [his mace] in a leftward circle and struck Kunti's son (Bhima) on the head with a mace of terrible force.

sanskrit: तया त्वभिहतो भीमः पुत्रेण तव पाण्डवः ।नाकम्पत महाराज तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: tayā tvabhihato bhīmaḥ putreṇa tava pāṇḍavaḥ ,nākampata mahārāja tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: However, O great king, Bhima, the Pāṇḍava, though struck by your son with that mace, did not tremble. That was truly a wonder.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 41. However, O great king, Bhima, the Pāṇḍava, though struck by your son with that mace, did not tremble. That was truly a wonder.

sanskrit: आश्चर्यं चापि तद्राजन्सर्वसैन्यान्यपूजयन् ।यद्गदाभिहतो भीमो नाकम्पत पदात्पदम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: āścaryaṁ cāpi tadrājansarvasainyānyapūjayan ,yadgadābhihato bhīmo nākampata padātpadam.
english: And indeed, O King, all the armies praised that astonishing feat, that Bhima, though struck by the mace, did not budge an inch.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 42. And indeed, O King, all the armies praised that astonishing feat, that Bhima, though struck by the mace, did not budge an inch.

sanskrit: ततो गुरुतरां दीप्तां गदां हेमपरिष्कृताम् ।दुर्योधनाय व्यसृजद्भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ॥४३॥
transliteration: tato gurutarāṁ dīptāṁ gadāṁ hemapariṣkṛtām ,duryodhanāya vyasṛjadbhīmo bhīmaparākramaḥ.
english: Then Bhima, whose might was terrible, hurled a heavier, radiant mace, adorned with gold, at Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 43. Then Bhima, whose might was terrible, hurled a heavier, radiant mace, adorned with gold, at Duryodhana.

sanskrit: तं प्रहारमसंभ्रान्तो लाघवेन महाबलः ।मोघं दुर्योधनश्चक्रे तत्राभूद्विस्मयो महान् ॥४४॥
transliteration: taṁ prahāramasaṁbhrānto lāghavena mahābalaḥ ,moghaṁ duryodhanaścakre tatrābhūdvismayo mahān.
english: The mighty Duryodhana, undisturbed and with agility, rendered that blow ineffective. A great astonishment arose at this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 44. The mighty Duryodhana, undisturbed and with agility, rendered that blow ineffective. A great astonishment arose at this.

sanskrit: सा तु मोघा गदा राजन्पतन्ती भीमचोदिता ।चालयामास पृथिवीं महानिर्घातनिस्वना ॥४५॥
transliteration: sā tu moghā gadā rājanpatantī bhīmacoditā ,cālayāmāsa pṛthivīṁ mahānirghātanisvanā.
english: But that mace, O King, though rendered ineffective and falling, having been hurled by Bhima, shook the earth with a great thunderous roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 45. But that mace, O King, though rendered ineffective and falling, having been hurled by Bhima, shook the earth with a great thunderous roar.

sanskrit: आस्थाय कौशिकान्मार्गानुत्पतन्स पुनः पुनः ।गदानिपातं प्रज्ञाय भीमसेनमवञ्चयत् ॥४६॥
transliteration: āsthāya kauśikānmārgānutpatansa punaḥ punaḥ ,gadānipātaṁ prajñāya bhīmasenamavañcayat.
english: Resorting to subtle maneuvers and springing up again and again, he (Duryodhana), perceiving the mace's impact, evaded Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 46. Resorting to subtle maneuvers and springing up again and again, he (Duryodhana), perceiving the mace's impact, evaded Bhimasena.

sanskrit: वञ्चयित्वा तथा भीमं गदया कुरुसत्तमः ।ताडयामास संक्रुद्धो वक्षोदेशे महाबलः ॥४७॥
transliteration: vañcayitvā tathā bhīmaṁ gadayā kurusattamaḥ ,tāḍayāmāsa saṁkruddho vakṣodeśe mahābalaḥ.
english: Having thus evaded Bhima, the best of the Kurus, the mighty Duryodhana, greatly enraged, struck him in the chest with his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 47. Having thus evaded Bhima, the best of the Kurus, the mighty Duryodhana, greatly enraged, struck him in the chest with his mace.

sanskrit: गदयाभिहतो भीमो मुह्यमानो महारणे ।नाभ्यमन्यत कर्तव्यं पुत्रेणाभ्याहतस्तव ॥४८॥
transliteration: gadayābhihato bhīmo muhyamāno mahāraṇe ,nābhyamanyata kartavyaṁ putreṇābhyāhatastava.
english: Struck by the mace in the great battle, Bhima became bewildered. He did not consider what duty (dharma) was to be performed, having been attacked by your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 48. Struck by the mace in the great battle, Bhima became bewildered. He did not consider what duty (dharma) was to be performed, having been attacked by your son.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तथा वर्तमाने राजन्सोमकपाण्डवाः ।भृशोपहतसंकल्पा नहृष्टमनसोऽभवन् ॥४९॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstathā vartamāne rājansomakapāṇḍavāḥ ,bhṛśopahatasaṁkalpā nahṛṣṭamanaso'bhavan.
english: O King, while he (Bhima) was in such a state, the Somakas and Pandavas, their resolves (saṅkalpa) greatly shattered, became dispirited.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 49. O King, while he (Bhima) was in such a state, the Somakas and Pandavas, their resolves (saṅkalpa) greatly shattered, became dispirited.

sanskrit: स तु तेन प्रहारेण मातङ्ग इव रोषितः ।हस्तिवद्धस्तिसंकाशमभिदुद्राव ते सुतम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: sa tu tena prahāreṇa mātaṅga iva roṣitaḥ ,hastivaddhastisaṁkāśamabhidudrāva te sutam.
english: But he (Bhima), enraged by that blow like an elephant (mātaṅga), charged at your son, who resembled an elephant (hastisaṃkāśa), just as an elephant (hastin) charges.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 50. But he (Bhima), enraged by that blow like an elephant (mātaṅga), charged at your son, who resembled an elephant (hastisaṃkāśa), just as an elephant (hastin) charges.

sanskrit: ततस्तु रभसो भीमो गदया तनयं तव ।अभिदुद्राव वेगेन सिंहो वनगजं यथा ॥५१॥
transliteration: tatastu rabhaso bhīmo gadayā tanayaṁ tava ,abhidudrāva vegena siṁho vanagajaṁ yathā.
english: Then, the impetuous Bhima, with his mace, charged at your son with great speed, just as a lion (siṃha) charges at a wild elephant (vanagaja).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 51. Then, the impetuous Bhima, with his mace, charged at your son with great speed, just as a lion (siṃha) charges at a wild elephant (vanagaja).

sanskrit: उपसृत्य तु राजानं गदामोक्षविशारदः ।आविध्यत गदां राजन्समुद्दिश्य सुतं तव ॥५२॥
transliteration: upasṛtya tu rājānaṁ gadāmokṣaviśāradaḥ ,āvidhyata gadāṁ rājansamuddiśya sutaṁ tava.
english: But, O King, the one expert in wielding the mace, having approached your son, hurled his mace, aiming at him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 52. But, O King, the one expert in wielding the mace, having approached your son, hurled his mace, aiming at him.

sanskrit: अताडयद्भीमसेनः पार्श्वे दुर्योधनं तदा ।स विह्वलः प्रहारेण जानुभ्यामगमन्महीम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: atāḍayadbhīmasenaḥ pārśve duryodhanaṁ tadā ,sa vihvalaḥ prahāreṇa jānubhyāmagamanmahīm.
english: Then Bhīmasena struck Duryodhana on the side. He (Duryodhana), overcome by that blow, fell to the ground on his knees.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 53. Then Bhīmasena struck Duryodhana on the side. He (Duryodhana), overcome by that blow, fell to the ground on his knees.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु भरतश्रेष्ठे जानुभ्यामवनीं गते ।उदतिष्ठत्ततो नादः सृञ्जयानां जगत्पते ॥५४॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu bharataśreṣṭhe jānubhyāmavanīṁ gate ,udatiṣṭhattato nādaḥ sṛñjayānāṁ jagatpate.
english: But, O Lord of the world, when that best of the Bhāratas (Duryodhana) had fallen to the earth on his knees, then a great roar arose from the Sṛñjayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 54. But, O Lord of the world, when that best of the Bhāratas (Duryodhana) had fallen to the earth on his knees, then a great roar arose from the Sṛñjayas.

sanskrit: तेषां तु निनदं श्रुत्वा सृञ्जयानां नरर्षभः ।अमर्षाद्भरतश्रेष्ठ पुत्रस्ते समकुप्यत ॥५५॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tu ninadaṁ śrutvā sṛñjayānāṁ nararṣabhaḥ ,amarṣādbharataśreṣṭha putraste samakupyata.
english: But, having heard the roar of those Sṛñjayas, your son, the best among men, the best of the Bhāratas, became enraged out of indignation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 55. But, having heard the roar of those Sṛñjayas, your son, the best among men, the best of the Bhāratas, became enraged out of indignation.

sanskrit: उत्थाय तु महाबाहुः क्रुद्धो नाग इव श्वसन् ।दिधक्षन्निव नेत्राभ्यां भीमसेनमवैक्षत ॥५६॥
transliteration: utthāya tu mahābāhuḥ kruddho nāga iva śvasan ,didhakṣanniva netrābhyāṁ bhīmasenamavaikṣata.
english: The mighty-armed one (Duryodhana), having risen, breathing heavily like an enraged serpent, glared at Bhīmasena as if to burn him with his eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 56. The mighty-armed one (Duryodhana), having risen, breathing heavily like an enraged serpent, glared at Bhīmasena as if to burn him with his eyes.

sanskrit: ततः स भरतश्रेष्ठो गदापाणिरभिद्रवत् ।प्रमथिष्यन्निव शिरो भीमसेनस्य संयुगे ॥५७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa bharataśreṣṭho gadāpāṇirabhidravat ,pramathiṣyanniva śiro bhīmasenasya saṁyuge.
english: Then, O best of the Bhāratas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he (Duryodhana), mace in hand, rushed forward in battle as if he would crush Bhīmasena's head.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 57. Then, O best of the Bhāratas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), he (Duryodhana), mace in hand, rushed forward in battle as if he would crush Bhīmasena's head.

sanskrit: स महात्मा महात्मानं भीमं भीमपराक्रमः ।अताडयच्छङ्खदेशे स चचालाचलोपमः ॥५८॥
transliteration: sa mahātmā mahātmānaṁ bhīmaṁ bhīmaparākramaḥ ,atāḍayacchaṅkhadeśe sa cacālācalopamaḥ.
english: That great-souled one (Duryodhana), who possessed terrible prowess, struck the great-souled Bhīma on the temple region. And Bhīma, though resembling a mountain, staggered.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 58. That great-souled one (Duryodhana), who possessed terrible prowess, struck the great-souled Bhīma on the temple region. And Bhīma, though resembling a mountain, staggered.

sanskrit: स भूयः शुशुभे पार्थस्ताडितो गदया रणे ।उद्भिन्नरुधिरो राजन्प्रभिन्न इव कुञ्जरः ॥५९॥
transliteration: sa bhūyaḥ śuśubhe pārthastāḍito gadayā raṇe ,udbhinnarudhiro rājanprabhinna iva kuñjaraḥ.
english: O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Pārtha (Bhīma), struck by the mace in battle, shone splendidly even more, with blood gushing forth, like an elephant in rut.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 59. O King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), Pārtha (Bhīma), struck by the mace in battle, shone splendidly even more, with blood gushing forth, like an elephant in rut.

sanskrit: ततो गदां वीरहणीमयस्मयीं प्रगृह्य वज्राशनितुल्यनिस्वनाम् ।अताडयच्छत्रुममित्रकर्शनो बलेन विक्रम्य धनंजयाग्रजः ॥६०॥
transliteration: tato gadāṁ vīrahaṇīmayasmayīṁ; pragṛhya vajrāśanitulyanisvanām ,atāḍayacchatrumamitrakarśano; balena vikramya dhanaṁjayāgrajaḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna's elder brother (Bhīmasena), the tormentor of enemies, having powerfully attacked and grasped a hero-slaying, iron-made mace that sounded like a thunderbolt, struck the enemy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 60. Then, Arjuna's elder brother (Bhīmasena), the tormentor of enemies, having powerfully attacked and grasped a hero-slaying, iron-made mace that sounded like a thunderbolt, struck the enemy.

sanskrit: स भीमसेनाभिहतस्तवात्मजः पपात संकम्पितदेहबन्धनः ।सुपुष्पितो मारुतवेगताडितो महावने साल इवावघूर्णितः ॥६१॥
transliteration: sa bhīmasenābhihatastavātmajaḥ; papāta saṁkampitadehabandhanaḥ ,supuṣpito mārutavegatāḍito; mahāvane sāla ivāvaghūrṇitaḥ.
english: That son of yours (Duryodhana), struck by Bhīmasena, fell, his bodily joints trembling violently, like a śāla tree in full bloom, struck and swayed by the force of the wind in a great forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 61. That son of yours (Duryodhana), struck by Bhīmasena, fell, his bodily joints trembling violently, like a śāla tree in full bloom, struck and swayed by the force of the wind in a great forest.

sanskrit: ततः प्रणेदुर्जहृषुश्च पाण्डवाः समीक्ष्य पुत्रं पतितं क्षितौ तव ।ततः सुतस्ते प्रतिलभ्य चेतनां समुत्पपात द्विरदो यथा ह्रदात् ॥६२॥
transliteration: tataḥ praṇedurjahṛṣuśca pāṇḍavāḥ; samīkṣya putraṁ patitaṁ kṣitau tava ,tataḥ sutaste pratilabhya cetanāṁ; samutpapāta dvirado yathā hradāt.
english: Then, seeing your son (Duryodhana) fallen on the ground, the Pāṇḍavas roared and rejoiced. Then your son, having regained consciousness, sprang up just like an elephant from a lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 62. Then, seeing your son (Duryodhana) fallen on the ground, the Pāṇḍavas roared and rejoiced. Then your son, having regained consciousness, sprang up just like an elephant from a lake.

sanskrit: स पार्थिवो नित्यममर्षितस्तदा महारथः शिक्षितवत्परिभ्रमन् ।अताडयत्पाण्डवमग्रतः स्थितं स विह्वलाङ्गो जगतीमुपास्पृशत् ॥६३॥
transliteration: sa pārthivo nityamamarṣitastadā; mahārathaḥ śikṣitavatparibhraman ,atāḍayatpāṇḍavamagrataḥ sthitaṁ; sa vihvalāṅgo jagatīmupāspṛśat.
english: Then that king (Duryodhana), the great chariot-warrior, perpetually enraged, moving expertly, struck the Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena) who stood before him. Thereupon, he (Bhīmasena), with his body bewildered, touched the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 63. Then that king (Duryodhana), the great chariot-warrior, perpetually enraged, moving expertly, struck the Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena) who stood before him. Thereupon, he (Bhīmasena), with his body bewildered, touched the ground.

sanskrit: स सिंहनादान्विननाद कौरवो निपात्य भूमौ युधि भीममोजसा ।बिभेद चैवाशनितुल्यतेजसा गदानिपातेन शरीररक्षणम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: sa siṁhanādānvinanāda kauravo; nipātya bhūmau yudhi bhīmamojasā ,bibheda caivāśanitulyatejasā; gadānipātena śarīrarakṣaṇam.
english: Having thrown Bhima to the ground in battle with his immense might, that Kuru (Duryodhana) roared with lion-like cries. And indeed, with a mace-blow of thunderbolt-like force, he shattered Bhima's armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 64. Having thrown Bhima to the ground in battle with his immense might, that Kuru (Duryodhana) roared with lion-like cries. And indeed, with a mace-blow of thunderbolt-like force, he shattered Bhima's armor.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्तरिक्षे निनदो महानभूद्दिवौकसामप्सरसां च नेदुषाम् ।पपात चोच्चैरमरप्रवेरितं विचित्रपुष्पोत्करवर्षमुत्तमम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: tato'ntarikṣe ninado mahānabhū;ddivaukasāmapsarasāṁ ca neduṣām ,papāta coccairamarapraveritaṁ; vicitrapuṣpotkaravarṣamuttamam.
english: Then, a great roar (or shout) arose in the sky from the gods and celestial nymphs (apsarases) who were exulting. And an excellent shower of diverse, abundant flowers, impelled by the gods, fell from above.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 65. Then, a great roar (or shout) arose in the sky from the gods and celestial nymphs (apsarases) who were exulting. And an excellent shower of diverse, abundant flowers, impelled by the gods, fell from above.

sanskrit: ततः परानाविशदुत्तमं भयं समीक्ष्य भूमौ पतितं नरोत्तमम् ।अहीयमानं च बलेन कौरवं निशम्य भेदं च दृढस्य वर्मणः ॥६६॥
transliteration: tataḥ parānāviśaduttamaṁ bhayaṁ; samīkṣya bhūmau patitaṁ narottamam ,ahīyamānaṁ ca balena kauravaṁ; niśamya bhedaṁ ca dṛḍhasya varmaṇaḥ.
english: Then, a great fear seized their adversaries, as they saw the best of men (Bhima) fallen to the ground, and observed the Kuru's (Duryodhana's) undiminished strength, while also hearing about the piercing of Bhima's strong armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 66. Then, a great fear seized their adversaries, as they saw the best of men (Bhima) fallen to the ground, and observed the Kuru's (Duryodhana's) undiminished strength, while also hearing about the piercing of Bhima's strong armor.

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्तादुपलभ्य चेतनां प्रमृज्य वक्त्रं रुधिरार्द्रमात्मनः ।धृतिं समालम्ब्य विवृत्तलोचनो बलेन संस्तभ्य वृकोदरः स्थितः ॥६७॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtādupalabhya cetanāṁ; pramṛjya vaktraṁ rudhirārdramātmanaḥ ,dhṛtiṁ samālambya vivṛttalocano; balena saṁstabhya vṛkodaraḥ sthitaḥ.
english: Then, after a moment, Vrikodara (Bhima) regained consciousness. Wiping his own blood-soaked face and steadying himself with resolute strength, he stood with wide-open eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 56 Verse 67. Then, after a moment, Vrikodara (Bhima) regained consciousness. Wiping his own blood-soaked face and steadying himself with resolute strength, he stood with wide-open eyes.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।समुदीर्णं ततो दृष्ट्वा संग्रामं कुरुमुख्ययोः ।अथाब्रवीदर्जुनस्तु वासुदेवं यशस्विनम् ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,samudīrṇaṁ tato dṛṣṭvā saṁgrāmaṁ kurumukhyayoḥ ,athābravīdarjunastu vāsudevaṁ yaśasvinam.
english: Sanjaya said: Having then seen the fierce battle between the two foremost Kurus, Arjuna then spoke to the glorious Vāsudeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Having then seen the fierce battle between the two foremost Kurus, Arjuna then spoke to the glorious Vāsudeva.

sanskrit: अनयोर्वीरयोर्युद्धे को ज्यायान्भवतो मतः ।कस्य वा को गुणो भूयानेतद्वद जनार्दन ॥२॥
transliteration: anayorvīrayoryuddhe ko jyāyānbhavato mataḥ ,kasya vā ko guṇo bhūyānetadvada janārdana.
english: Janārdana, who among these two warriors do you consider superior in battle? And whose quality is greater? Please tell me this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 2. Janārdana, who among these two warriors do you consider superior in battle? And whose quality is greater? Please tell me this.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।उपदेशोऽनयोस्तुल्यो भीमस्तु बलवत्तरः ।कृतयत्नतरस्त्वेष धार्तराष्ट्रो वृकोदरात् ॥३॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,upadeśo'nayostulyo bhīmastu balavattaraḥ ,kṛtayatnatarastveṣa dhārtarāṣṭro vṛkodarāt.
english: Vāsudeva said: "The instruction given to these two is equal, but Bhīma is stronger. However, this son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) has made a greater effort than Vṛkodara (Bhīma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 3. Vāsudeva said: "The instruction given to these two is equal, but Bhīma is stronger. However, this son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) has made a greater effort than Vṛkodara (Bhīma)."

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु धर्मेण युध्यमानो न जेष्यति ।अन्यायेन तु युध्यन्वै हन्यादेष सुयोधनम् ॥४॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu dharmeṇa yudhyamāno na jeṣyati ,anyāyena tu yudhyanvai hanyādeṣa suyodhanam.
english: But Bhīmasena, fighting righteously (by natural law [dharma]), will not win. However, if he fights unjustly, this one (Bhīma) will certainly kill Suyodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 4. But Bhīmasena, fighting righteously (by natural law [dharma]), will not win. However, if he fights unjustly, this one (Bhīma) will certainly kill Suyodhana.

sanskrit: मायया निर्जिता देवैरसुरा इति नः श्रुतम् ।विरोचनश्च शक्रेण मायया निर्जितः सखे ।मायया चाक्षिपत्तेजो वृत्रस्य बलसूदनः ॥५॥
transliteration: māyayā nirjitā devairasurā iti naḥ śrutam ,virocanaśca śakreṇa māyayā nirjitaḥ sakhe ,māyayā cākṣipattejo vṛtrasya balasūdanaḥ.
english: It has been heard by us that the asuras were defeated by the gods through deceptive means (māyā). And, O friend, Virocana was conquered by Śakra also through illusion (māyā). Moreover, the slayer of Bala (Indra) seized Vṛtra's power through trickery (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 5. It has been heard by us that the asuras were defeated by the gods through deceptive means (māyā). And, O friend, Virocana was conquered by Śakra also through illusion (māyā). Moreover, the slayer of Bala (Indra) seized Vṛtra's power through trickery (māyā).

sanskrit: प्रतिज्ञातं तु भीमेन द्यूतकाले धनंजय ।ऊरू भेत्स्यामि ते संख्ये गदयेति सुयोधनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: pratijñātaṁ tu bhīmena dyūtakāle dhanaṁjaya ,ūrū bhetsyāmi te saṁkhye gadayeti suyodhanam.
english: O Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), Bhīma indeed vowed to Suyodhana at the time of the dice game, saying, "I will break your thighs with my mace in battle."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 6. O Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), Bhīma indeed vowed to Suyodhana at the time of the dice game, saying, "I will break your thighs with my mace in battle."

sanskrit: सोऽयं प्रतिज्ञां तां चापि पारयित्वारिकर्शनः ।मायाविनं च राजानं माययैव निकृन्ततु ॥७॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ pratijñāṁ tāṁ cāpi pārayitvārikarśanaḥ ,māyāvinaṁ ca rājānaṁ māyayaiva nikṛntatu.
english: May this tormentor of foes, having fulfilled that vow, indeed cut down that deceitful king by means of trickery (māyā) itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 7. May this tormentor of foes, having fulfilled that vow, indeed cut down that deceitful king by means of trickery (māyā) itself.

sanskrit: यद्येष बलमास्थाय न्यायेन प्रहरिष्यति ।विषमस्थस्ततो राजा भविष्यति युधिष्ठिरः ॥८॥
transliteration: yadyeṣa balamāsthāya nyāyena prahariṣyati ,viṣamasthastato rājā bhaviṣyati yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: If this one (Duryodhana), relying on his strength, were to strike justly, then King Yudhiṣṭhira would be in a difficult position.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 8. If this one (Duryodhana), relying on his strength, were to strike justly, then King Yudhiṣṭhira would be in a difficult position.

sanskrit: पुनरेव च वक्ष्यामि पाण्डवेदं निबोध मे ।धर्मराजापराधेन भयं नः पुनरागतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: punareva ca vakṣyāmi pāṇḍavedaṁ nibodha me ,dharmarājāparādhena bhayaṁ naḥ punarāgatam.
english: O Pāṇḍava, I will speak again; please understand this from me. Due to the transgression committed by the king who upholds natural law (dharma-rāja), fear has again come upon us.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 9. O Pāṇḍava, I will speak again; please understand this from me. Due to the transgression committed by the king who upholds natural law (dharma-rāja), fear has again come upon us.

sanskrit: कृत्वा हि सुमहत्कर्म हत्वा भीष्ममुखान्कुरून् ।जयः प्राप्तो यशश्चाग्र्यं वैरं च प्रतियातितम् ।तदेवं विजयः प्राप्तः पुनः संशयितः कृतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛtvā hi sumahatkarma hatvā bhīṣmamukhānkurūn ,jayaḥ prāpto yaśaścāgryaṁ vairaṁ ca pratiyātitam ,tadevaṁ vijayaḥ prāptaḥ punaḥ saṁśayitaḥ kṛtaḥ.
english: Indeed, after accomplishing a truly great feat and slaying the prominent Kurus led by Bhīṣma, victory was attained, along with supreme glory, and the enmity was avenged. However, this very victory, thus achieved, has once again been made doubtful.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 10. Indeed, after accomplishing a truly great feat and slaying the prominent Kurus led by Bhīṣma, victory was attained, along with supreme glory, and the enmity was avenged. However, this very victory, thus achieved, has once again been made doubtful.

sanskrit: अबुद्धिरेषा महती धर्मराजस्य पाण्डव ।यदेकविजये युद्धं पणितं कृतमीदृशम् ।सुयोधनः कृती वीर एकायनगतस्तथा ॥११॥
transliteration: abuddhireṣā mahatī dharmarājasya pāṇḍava ,yadekavijaye yuddhaṁ paṇitaṁ kṛtamīdṛśam ,suyodhanaḥ kṛtī vīra ekāyanagatastathā.
english: O Pāṇḍava, this is a grave lack of judgment on the part of the king who upholds natural law (dharma-rāja), that the war was wagered in such a way for just one victory. Furthermore, Suyodhana, that accomplished and brave warrior, has become utterly resolute (ekāyanagata).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 11. O Pāṇḍava, this is a grave lack of judgment on the part of the king who upholds natural law (dharma-rāja), that the war was wagered in such a way for just one victory. Furthermore, Suyodhana, that accomplished and brave warrior, has become utterly resolute (ekāyanagata).

sanskrit: अपि चोशनसा गीतः श्रूयतेऽयं पुरातनः ।श्लोकस्तत्त्वार्थसहितस्तन्मे निगदतः शृणु ॥१२॥
transliteration: api cośanasā gītaḥ śrūyate'yaṁ purātanaḥ ,ślokastattvārthasahitastanme nigadataḥ śṛṇu.
english: Moreover, this ancient verse, imbued with profound meaning, sung by Uśanas (Śukrācārya), is heard. Therefore, listen as I recite it to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 12. Moreover, this ancient verse, imbued with profound meaning, sung by Uśanas (Śukrācārya), is heard. Therefore, listen as I recite it to you.

sanskrit: पुनरावर्तमानानां भग्नानां जीवितैषिणाम् ।भेतव्यमरिशेषाणामेकायनगता हि ते ॥१३॥
transliteration: punarāvartamānānāṁ bhagnānāṁ jīvitaiṣiṇām ,bhetavyamariśeṣāṇāmekāyanagatā hi te.
english: Indeed, those who are returning, defeated, desirous of life, and who are the remaining enemies, should be feared, for they have now committed to a single, resolute path.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 13. Indeed, those who are returning, defeated, desirous of life, and who are the remaining enemies, should be feared, for they have now committed to a single, resolute path.

sanskrit: सुयोधनमिमं भग्नं हतसैन्यं ह्रदं गतम् ।पराजितं वनप्रेप्सुं निराशं राज्यलम्भने ॥१४॥
transliteration: suyodhanamimaṁ bhagnaṁ hatasainyaṁ hradaṁ gatam ,parājitaṁ vanaprepsuṁ nirāśaṁ rājyalambhane.
english: This Suyodhana - defeated, with his army destroyed, having retreated to the lake, utterly vanquished, now desiring to escape to the forest, and without any hope of regaining the kingdom -
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 14. This Suyodhana - defeated, with his army destroyed, having retreated to the lake, utterly vanquished, now desiring to escape to the forest, and without any hope of regaining the kingdom -

sanskrit: को न्वेष संयुगे प्राज्ञः पुनर्द्वंद्वे समाह्वयेत् ।अपि वो निर्जितं राज्यं न हरेत सुयोधनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ko nveṣa saṁyuge prājñaḥ punardvaṁdve samāhvayet ,api vo nirjitaṁ rājyaṁ na hareta suyodhanaḥ.
english: Which wise person would indeed challenge this man to another duel in battle? Or would Suyodhana not seize the kingdom already won by you all?
