search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 15. Which wise person would indeed challenge this man to another duel in battle? Or would Suyodhana not seize the kingdom already won by you all?

sanskrit: यस्त्रयोदशवर्षाणि गदया कृतनिश्रमः ।चरत्यूर्ध्वं च तिर्यक्च भीमसेनजिघांसया ॥१६॥
transliteration: yastrayodaśavarṣāṇi gadayā kṛtaniśramaḥ ,caratyūrdhvaṁ ca tiryakca bhīmasenajighāṁsayā.
english: He (Duryodhana) who, for thirteen years, has assiduously trained (kṛtaniśramaḥ) with his mace, moving it both upwards and sideways, all driven by the desire to kill Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 16. He (Duryodhana) who, for thirteen years, has assiduously trained (kṛtaniśramaḥ) with his mace, moving it both upwards and sideways, all driven by the desire to kill Bhimasena.

sanskrit: एवं चेन्न महाबाहुरन्यायेन हनिष्यति ।एष वः कौरवो राजा धार्तराष्ट्रो भविष्यति ॥१७॥
transliteration: evaṁ cenna mahābāhuranyāyena haniṣyati ,eṣa vaḥ kauravo rājā dhārtarāṣṭro bhaviṣyati.
english: If the mighty-armed one (Bhīma) does not kill (Duryodhana), then, by injustice, this son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra will become your Kaurava king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 17. If the mighty-armed one (Bhīma) does not kill (Duryodhana), then, by injustice, this son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra will become your Kaurava king.

sanskrit: धनंजयस्तु श्रुत्वैतत्केशवस्य महात्मनः ।प्रेक्षतो भीमसेनस्य हस्तेनोरुमताडयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayastu śrutvaitatkeśavasya mahātmanaḥ ,prekṣato bhīmasenasya hastenorumatāḍayat.
english: But Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), having heard this from the great-souled (mahātman) Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), and with Bhīmasena watching, struck his own thigh with his hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 18. But Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), having heard this from the great-souled (mahātman) Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), and with Bhīmasena watching, struck his own thigh with his hand.

sanskrit: गृह्य संज्ञां ततो भीमो गदया व्यचरद्रणे ।मण्डलानि विचित्राणि यमकानीतराणि च ॥१९॥
transliteration: gṛhya saṁjñāṁ tato bhīmo gadayā vyacaradraṇe ,maṇḍalāni vicitrāṇi yamakānītarāṇi ca.
english: Having received the signal, Bhīma then moved about in battle with his mace, performing various intricate circular maneuvers, both paired (yamaka) and other kinds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 19. Having received the signal, Bhīma then moved about in battle with his mace, performing various intricate circular maneuvers, both paired (yamaka) and other kinds.

sanskrit: दक्षिणं मण्डलं सव्यं गोमूत्रकमथापि च ।व्यचरत्पाण्डवो राजन्नरिं संमोहयन्निव ॥२०॥
transliteration: dakṣiṇaṁ maṇḍalaṁ savyaṁ gomūtrakamathāpi ca ,vyacaratpāṇḍavo rājannariṁ saṁmohayanniva.
english: O king, the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) performed right and left circular movements, and also the 'cow's urine' (gomūtraka) maneuver, as if bewildering the enemy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 20. O king, the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) performed right and left circular movements, and also the 'cow's urine' (gomūtraka) maneuver, as if bewildering the enemy.

sanskrit: तथैव तव पुत्रोऽपि गदामार्गविशारदः ।व्यचरल्लघु चित्रं च भीमसेनजिघांसया ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathaiva tava putro'pi gadāmārgaviśāradaḥ ,vyacarallaghu citraṁ ca bhīmasenajighāṁsayā.
english: Similarly, your son, an expert in mace-fighting, also moved swiftly and wonderfully with the intent to kill Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 21. Similarly, your son, an expert in mace-fighting, also moved swiftly and wonderfully with the intent to kill Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: आधुन्वन्तौ गदे घोरे चन्दनागरुरूषिते ।वैरस्यान्तं परीप्सन्तौ रणे क्रुद्धाविवान्तकौ ॥२२॥
transliteration: ādhunvantau gade ghore candanāgarurūṣite ,vairasyāntaṁ parīpsantau raṇe kruddhāvivāntakau.
english: Brandishing two terrible maces, which were smeared with sandalwood and aloe wood, the two (warriors) sought to bring an end to the hostility in battle, like two enraged Yamas (gods of death).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 22. Brandishing two terrible maces, which were smeared with sandalwood and aloe wood, the two (warriors) sought to bring an end to the hostility in battle, like two enraged Yamas (gods of death).

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं तौ जिघांसन्तौ प्रवीरौ पुरुषर्षभौ ।युयुधाते गरुत्मन्तौ यथा नागामिषैषिणौ ॥२३॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ tau jighāṁsantau pravīrau puruṣarṣabhau ,yuyudhāte garutmantau yathā nāgāmiṣaiṣiṇau.
english: Wishing to kill each other, those two great heroes, who were like bulls among men (puruṣa), fought just like two winged Garuḍas desiring the flesh of serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 23. Wishing to kill each other, those two great heroes, who were like bulls among men (puruṣa), fought just like two winged Garuḍas desiring the flesh of serpents.

sanskrit: मण्डलानि विचित्राणि चरतोर्नृपभीमयोः ।गदासंपातजास्तत्र प्रजज्ञुः पावकार्चिषः ॥२४॥
transliteration: maṇḍalāni vicitrāṇi caratornṛpabhīmayoḥ ,gadāsaṁpātajāstatra prajajñuḥ pāvakārciṣaḥ.
english: While the king (Duryodhana) and Bhima were moving in varied, intricate circles, sparks of fire were then produced there from the impact of their maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 24. While the king (Duryodhana) and Bhima were moving in varied, intricate circles, sparks of fire were then produced there from the impact of their maces.

sanskrit: समं प्रहरतोस्तत्र शूरयोर्बलिनोर्मृधे ।क्षुब्धयोर्वायुना राजन्द्वयोरिव समुद्रयोः ॥२५॥
transliteration: samaṁ praharatostatra śūrayorbalinormṛdhe ,kṣubdhayorvāyunā rājandvayoriva samudrayoḥ.
english: O King, there, in that battle, the two mighty heroes struck equally, just like two oceans agitated by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 25. O King, there, in that battle, the two mighty heroes struck equally, just like two oceans agitated by the wind.

sanskrit: तयोः प्रहरतोस्तुल्यं मत्तकुञ्जरयोरिव ।गदानिर्घातसंह्रादः प्रहाराणामजायत ॥२६॥
transliteration: tayoḥ praharatostulyaṁ mattakuñjarayoriva ,gadānirghātasaṁhrādaḥ prahārāṇāmajāyata.
english: As those two struck, just like two rutting elephants, a tremendous clamor arose from the clash of their maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 26. As those two struck, just like two rutting elephants, a tremendous clamor arose from the clash of their maces.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तदा संप्रहारे दारुणे संकुले भृशम् ।उभावपि परिश्रान्तौ युध्यमानावरिंदमौ ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstadā saṁprahāre dāruṇe saṁkule bhṛśam ,ubhāvapi pariśrāntau yudhyamānāvariṁdamau.
english: Then, in that fierce and greatly tumultuous combat, both subduers of enemies became utterly exhausted while fighting.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 27. Then, in that fierce and greatly tumultuous combat, both subduers of enemies became utterly exhausted while fighting.

sanskrit: तौ मुहूर्तं समाश्वस्य पुनरेव परंतपौ ।अभ्यहारयतां क्रुद्धौ प्रगृह्य महती गदे ॥२८॥
transliteration: tau muhūrtaṁ samāśvasya punareva paraṁtapau ,abhyahārayatāṁ kruddhau pragṛhya mahatī gade.
english: Those two tormentors of enemies, after resting for a moment, then again, enraged, seized their mighty maces and resumed striking.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 28. Those two tormentors of enemies, after resting for a moment, then again, enraged, seized their mighty maces and resumed striking.

sanskrit: तयोः समभवद्युद्धं घोररूपमसंवृतम् ।गदानिपातै राजेन्द्र तक्षतोर्वै परस्परम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tayoḥ samabhavadyuddhaṁ ghorarūpamasaṁvṛtam ,gadānipātai rājendra takṣatorvai parasparam.
english: O best of kings, an intensely fierce and unrestrained battle (yuddha) occurred between the two of them, as they struck each other with their mace-blows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 29. O best of kings, an intensely fierce and unrestrained battle (yuddha) occurred between the two of them, as they struck each other with their mace-blows.

sanskrit: व्यायामप्रद्रुतौ तौ तु वृषभाक्षौ तरस्विनौ ।अन्योन्यं जघ्नतुर्वीरौ पङ्कस्थौ महिषाविव ॥३०॥
transliteration: vyāyāmapradrutau tau tu vṛṣabhākṣau tarasvinau ,anyonyaṁ jaghnaturvīrau paṅkasthau mahiṣāviva.
english: Those two brave heroes, mighty and bull-eyed, drenched with sweat from their strenuous exertion, indeed struck each other like two buffaloes (mahiṣa) standing in mud.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 30. Those two brave heroes, mighty and bull-eyed, drenched with sweat from their strenuous exertion, indeed struck each other like two buffaloes (mahiṣa) standing in mud.

sanskrit: जर्जरीकृतसर्वाङ्गौ रुधिरेणाभिसंप्लुतौ ।ददृशाते हिमवति पुष्पिताविव किंशुकौ ॥३१॥
transliteration: jarjarīkṛtasarvāṅgau rudhireṇābhisaṁplutau ,dadṛśāte himavati puṣpitāviva kiṁśukau.
english: With all their limbs mangled and drenched with blood, they appeared like two palash trees (kiṃśuka) blooming on Mount Himalaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 31. With all their limbs mangled and drenched with blood, they appeared like two palash trees (kiṃśuka) blooming on Mount Himalaya.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनेन पार्थस्तु विवरे संप्रदर्शिते ।ईषदुत्स्मयमानस्तु सहसा प्रससार ह ॥३२॥
transliteration: duryodhanena pārthastu vivare saṁpradarśite ,īṣadutsmayamānastu sahasā prasasāra ha.
english: But when a vulnerability (vivara) was revealed by Duryodhana, Arjuna (Pārtha) smiled slightly and suddenly rushed forward.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 32. But when a vulnerability (vivara) was revealed by Duryodhana, Arjuna (Pārtha) smiled slightly and suddenly rushed forward.

sanskrit: तमभ्याशगतं प्राज्ञो रणे प्रेक्ष्य वृकोदरः ।अवाक्षिपद्गदां तस्मै वेगेन महता बली ॥३३॥
transliteration: tamabhyāśagataṁ prājño raṇe prekṣya vṛkodaraḥ ,avākṣipadgadāṁ tasmai vegena mahatā balī.
english: Observing him (Duryodhana) approaching in battle, the sagacious Vrikodara (Bhīma), who was mighty, hurled his mace at him with great force.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 33. Observing him (Duryodhana) approaching in battle, the sagacious Vrikodara (Bhīma), who was mighty, hurled his mace at him with great force.

sanskrit: अवक्षेपं तु तं दृष्ट्वा पुत्रस्तव विशां पते ।अपासर्पत्ततः स्थानात्सा मोघा न्यपतद्भुवि ॥३४॥
transliteration: avakṣepaṁ tu taṁ dṛṣṭvā putrastava viśāṁ pate ,apāsarpattataḥ sthānātsā moghā nyapatadbhuvi.
english: But, O lord of people, upon seeing that hurled mace, your son (Duryodhana) moved away from that spot. Thus, it (the mace) fell uselessly to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 34. But, O lord of people, upon seeing that hurled mace, your son (Duryodhana) moved away from that spot. Thus, it (the mace) fell uselessly to the ground.

sanskrit: मोक्षयित्वा प्रहारं तं सुतस्तव स संभ्रमात् ।भीमसेनं च गदया प्राहरत्कुरुसत्तमः ॥३५॥
transliteration: mokṣayitvā prahāraṁ taṁ sutastava sa saṁbhramāt ,bhīmasenaṁ ca gadayā prāharatkurusattamaḥ.
english: Your son (Duryodhana), having avoided that blow, and agitated, that best of Kurus (Duryodhana) then struck Bhīmasena with his mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 35. Your son (Duryodhana), having avoided that blow, and agitated, that best of Kurus (Duryodhana) then struck Bhīmasena with his mace.

sanskrit: तस्य विष्यन्दमानेन रुधिरेणामितौजसः ।प्रहारगुरुपाताच्च मूर्छेव समजायत ॥३६॥
transliteration: tasya viṣyandamānena rudhireṇāmitaujasaḥ ,prahāragurupātācca mūrcheva samajāyata.
english: Due to the gushing blood from that one of immense energy (Bhīma), and from the heavy impact of the blow, it was as if a swoon overcame him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 36. Due to the gushing blood from that one of immense energy (Bhīma), and from the heavy impact of the blow, it was as if a swoon overcame him.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तं च वेद पीडितं पाण्डवं रणे ।धारयामास भीमोऽपि शरीरमतिपीडितम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: duryodhanastaṁ ca veda pīḍitaṁ pāṇḍavaṁ raṇe ,dhārayāmāsa bhīmo'pi śarīramatipīḍitam.
english: Duryodhana also knew that the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) was suffering greatly in battle. Bhīma, though exceedingly pained himself, maintained control of his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 37. Duryodhana also knew that the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) was suffering greatly in battle. Bhīma, though exceedingly pained himself, maintained control of his body.

sanskrit: अमन्यत स्थितं ह्येनं प्रहरिष्यन्तमाहवे ।अतो न प्राहरत्तस्मै पुनरेव तवात्मजः ॥३८॥
transliteration: amanyata sthitaṁ hyenaṁ prahariṣyantamāhave ,ato na prāharattasmai punareva tavātmajaḥ.
english: Indeed, your son (Duryodhana) considered him (Bhīma) to be standing firm and about to strike in battle. Therefore, your son did not strike him again.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 38. Indeed, your son (Duryodhana) considered him (Bhīma) to be standing firm and about to strike in battle. Therefore, your son did not strike him again.

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्तमाश्वस्य दुर्योधनमवस्थितम् ।वेगेनाभ्यद्रवद्राजन्भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtamāśvasya duryodhanamavasthitam ,vegenābhyadravadrājanbhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān.
english: Then, O King, the powerful Bhīmasena, having recovered for a moment, swiftly attacked Duryodhana, who was standing firm.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 39. Then, O King, the powerful Bhīmasena, having recovered for a moment, swiftly attacked Duryodhana, who was standing firm.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं संप्रेक्ष्य संरब्धममितौजसम् ।मोघमस्य प्रहारं तं चिकीर्षुर्भरतर्षभ ॥४०॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ saṁprekṣya saṁrabdhamamitaujasam ,moghamasya prahāraṁ taṁ cikīrṣurbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, seeing him (Bhīmasena) approaching, enraged and of immeasurable might, Duryodhana desired to render his (Bhīma's) strike futile.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 40. O best of the Bhāratas, seeing him (Bhīmasena) approaching, enraged and of immeasurable might, Duryodhana desired to render his (Bhīma's) strike futile.

sanskrit: अवस्थाने मतिं कृत्वा पुत्रस्तव महामनाः ।इयेषोत्पतितुं राजंश्छलयिष्यन्वृकोदरम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: avasthāne matiṁ kṛtvā putrastava mahāmanāḥ ,iyeṣotpatituṁ rājaṁśchalayiṣyanvṛkodaram.
english: O king, your determined son, having resolved to hold his ground, wished to leap up, intending to deceive Bhima (vṛkodara).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 41. O king, your determined son, having resolved to hold his ground, wished to leap up, intending to deceive Bhima (vṛkodara).

sanskrit: अबुध्यद्भीमसेनस्तद्राज्ञस्तस्य चिकीर्षितम् ।अथास्य समभिद्रुत्य समुत्क्रम्य च सिंहवत् ॥४२॥
transliteration: abudhyadbhīmasenastadrājñastasya cikīrṣitam ,athāsya samabhidrutya samutkramya ca siṁhavat.
english: Bhimasena (bhīmasena) understood that king's (Duryodhana's) intention. Thereupon, rushing towards him and leaping up like a lion, (he acted).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 42. Bhimasena (bhīmasena) understood that king's (Duryodhana's) intention. Thereupon, rushing towards him and leaping up like a lion, (he acted).

sanskrit: सृत्या वञ्चयतो राजन्पुनरेवोत्पतिष्यतः ।ऊरुभ्यां प्राहिणोद्राजन्गदां वेगेन पाण्डवः ॥४३॥
transliteration: sṛtyā vañcayato rājanpunarevotpatiṣyataḥ ,ūrubhyāṁ prāhiṇodrājangadāṁ vegena pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: O king, at him (Duryodhana) who was deceiving with a feigned movement and was about to leap up again, the Pāṇḍava (Bhima) hurled his mace (gadā) forcefully towards his thighs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 43. O king, at him (Duryodhana) who was deceiving with a feigned movement and was about to leap up again, the Pāṇḍava (Bhima) hurled his mace (gadā) forcefully towards his thighs.

sanskrit: सा वज्रनिष्पेषसमा प्रहिता भीमकर्मणा ।ऊरू दुर्योधनस्याथ बभञ्ज प्रियदर्शनौ ॥४४॥
transliteration: sā vajraniṣpeṣasamā prahitā bhīmakarmaṇā ,ūrū duryodhanasyātha babhañja priyadarśanau.
english: That mace (gadā), hurled by Bhima (bhīmakarman) whose deeds were terrible, and which was like the impact of a thunderbolt, then shattered Duryodhana's two beautiful thighs.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 44. That mace (gadā), hurled by Bhima (bhīmakarman) whose deeds were terrible, and which was like the impact of a thunderbolt, then shattered Duryodhana's two beautiful thighs.

sanskrit: स पपात नरव्याघ्रो वसुधामनुनादयन् ।भग्नोरुर्भीमसेनेन पुत्रस्तव महीपते ॥४५॥
transliteration: sa papāta naravyāghro vasudhāmanunādayan ,bhagnorurbhīmasenena putrastava mahīpate.
english: That tiger among men (naravyāghra), your son, O King, fell, making the earth resound, his thighs broken by Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 45. That tiger among men (naravyāghra), your son, O King, fell, making the earth resound, his thighs broken by Bhimasena.

sanskrit: ववुर्वाताः सनिर्घाताः पांसुवर्षं पपात च ।चचाल पृथिवी चापि सवृक्षक्षुपपर्वता ॥४६॥
transliteration: vavurvātāḥ sanirghātāḥ pāṁsuvarṣaṁ papāta ca ,cacāla pṛthivī cāpi savṛkṣakṣupaparvatā.
english: Winds blew accompanied by thunder, and a shower of dust fell. Also, the earth trembled, along with its trees, shrubs, and mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 46. Winds blew accompanied by thunder, and a shower of dust fell. Also, the earth trembled, along with its trees, shrubs, and mountains.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्निपतिते वीरे पत्यौ सर्वमहीक्षिताम् ।महास्वना पुनर्दीप्ता सनिर्घाता भयंकरी ।पपात चोल्का महती पतिते पृथिवीपतौ ॥४७॥
transliteration: tasminnipatite vīre patyau sarvamahīkṣitām ,mahāsvanā punardīptā sanirghātā bhayaṁkarī ,papāta colkā mahatī patite pṛthivīpatau.
english: When that hero, the lord of all sovereigns, fell, a meteor (ulkā)  -  great-sounding, re-blazing, thunderous, and terrifying  -  fell. Indeed, a great meteor (ulkā) also fell at the demise of the ruler of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 47. When that hero, the lord of all sovereigns, fell, a meteor (ulkā)  -  great-sounding, re-blazing, thunderous, and terrifying  -  fell. Indeed, a great meteor (ulkā) also fell at the demise of the ruler of the earth.

sanskrit: तथा शोणितवर्षं च पांसुवर्षं च भारत ।ववर्ष मघवांस्तत्र तव पुत्रे निपातिते ॥४८॥
transliteration: tathā śoṇitavarṣaṁ ca pāṁsuvarṣaṁ ca bhārata ,vavarṣa maghavāṁstatra tava putre nipātite.
english: Thus, O Bhārata, when your son was struck down there, Maghavan (Indra) rained down both showers of blood and showers of dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 48. Thus, O Bhārata, when your son was struck down there, Maghavan (Indra) rained down both showers of blood and showers of dust.

sanskrit: यक्षाणां राक्षसानां च पिशाचानां तथैव च ।अन्तरिक्षे महानादः श्रूयते भरतर्षभ ॥४९॥
transliteration: yakṣāṇāṁ rākṣasānāṁ ca piśācānāṁ tathaiva ca ,antarikṣe mahānādaḥ śrūyate bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among the Bharatas, a mighty roar is heard in the sky, from the Yakshas, Rakshasas, and Pishachas alike.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 49. O best among the Bharatas, a mighty roar is heard in the sky, from the Yakshas, Rakshasas, and Pishachas alike.

sanskrit: तेन शब्देन घोरेण मृगाणामथ पक्षिणाम् ।जज्ञे घोरतमः शब्दो बहूनां सर्वतोदिशम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: tena śabdena ghoreṇa mṛgāṇāmatha pakṣiṇām ,jajñe ghoratamaḥ śabdo bahūnāṁ sarvatodiśam.
english: From that dreadful noise, an even more terrifying sound arose from numerous deer and birds in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 50. From that dreadful noise, an even more terrifying sound arose from numerous deer and birds in all directions.

sanskrit: ये तत्र वाजिनः शेषा गजाश्च मनुजैः सह ।मुमुचुस्ते महानादं तव पुत्रे निपातिते ॥५१॥
transliteration: ye tatra vājinaḥ śeṣā gajāśca manujaiḥ saha ,mumucuste mahānādaṁ tava putre nipātite.
english: When your son was struck down, the remaining horses and elephants, along with the men present there, all let out a mighty roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 51. When your son was struck down, the remaining horses and elephants, along with the men present there, all let out a mighty roar.

sanskrit: भेरीशङ्खमृदङ्गानामभवच्च स्वनो महान् ।अन्तर्भूमिगतश्चैव तव पुत्रे निपातिते ॥५२॥
transliteration: bherīśaṅkhamṛdaṅgānāmabhavacca svano mahān ,antarbhūmigataścaiva tava putre nipātite.
english: And a great sound from the kettledrums, conches, and mridangas arose, which truly penetrated deep into the earth, when your son was struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 52. And a great sound from the kettledrums, conches, and mridangas arose, which truly penetrated deep into the earth, when your son was struck down.

sanskrit: बहुपादैर्बहुभुजैः कबन्धैर्घोरदर्शनैः ।नृत्यद्भिर्भयदैर्व्याप्ता दिशस्तत्राभवन्नृप ॥५३॥
transliteration: bahupādairbahubhujaiḥ kabandhairghoradarśanaiḥ ,nṛtyadbhirbhayadairvyāptā diśastatrābhavannṛpa.
english: O King, there the directions became filled with terrifying, dancing headless trunks (kabandha) that had many feet, many arms, and a dreadful appearance.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 53. O King, there the directions became filled with terrifying, dancing headless trunks (kabandha) that had many feet, many arms, and a dreadful appearance.

sanskrit: ध्वजवन्तोऽस्त्रवन्तश्च शस्त्रवन्तस्तथैव च ।प्राकम्पन्त ततो राजंस्तव पुत्रे निपातिते ॥५४॥
transliteration: dhvajavanto'stravantaśca śastravantastathaiva ca ,prākampanta tato rājaṁstava putre nipātite.
english: O King, when your son was struck down, those bearing banners, those with missiles (astra), and those with weapons (śastra) all trembled greatly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 54. O King, when your son was struck down, those bearing banners, those with missiles (astra), and those with weapons (śastra) all trembled greatly.

sanskrit: ह्रदाः कूपाश्च रुधिरमुद्वेमुर्नृपसत्तम ।नद्यश्च सुमहावेगाः प्रतिस्रोतोवहाभवन् ॥५५॥
transliteration: hradāḥ kūpāśca rudhiramudvemurnṛpasattama ,nadyaśca sumahāvegāḥ pratisrotovahābhavan.
english: O best among kings, lakes and wells gushed forth blood, and rivers, with very great currents, began to flow against their usual course.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 55. O best among kings, lakes and wells gushed forth blood, and rivers, with very great currents, began to flow against their usual course.

sanskrit: पुल्लिङ्गा इव नार्यस्तु स्त्रीलिङ्गाः पुरुषाभवन् ।दुर्योधने तदा राजन्पतिते तनये तव ॥५६॥
transliteration: pulliṅgā iva nāryastu strīliṅgāḥ puruṣābhavan ,duryodhane tadā rājanpatite tanaye tava.
english: O King, when your son Duryodhana fell, women appeared like men (pulliṅgāḥ) and men (puruṣa) appeared like women (strīliṅgāḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 56. O King, when your son Duryodhana fell, women appeared like men (pulliṅgāḥ) and men (puruṣa) appeared like women (strīliṅgāḥ).

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तानद्भुतोत्पातान्पाञ्चालाः पाण्डवैः सह ।आविग्नमनसः सर्वे बभूवुर्भरतर्षभ ॥५७॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tānadbhutotpātānpāñcālāḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha ,āvignamanasaḥ sarve babhūvurbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, having witnessed those astonishing phenomena, all the Pāñcālas, together with the Pāṇḍavas, became agitated in mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 57. O best of the Bhāratas, having witnessed those astonishing phenomena, all the Pāñcālas, together with the Pāṇḍavas, became agitated in mind.

sanskrit: ययुर्देवा यथाकामं गन्धर्वाप्सरसस्तथा ।कथयन्तोऽद्भुतं युद्धं सुतयोस्तव भारत ॥५८॥
transliteration: yayurdevā yathākāmaṁ gandharvāpsarasastathā ,kathayanto'dbhutaṁ yuddhaṁ sutayostava bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, the gods, along with the Gandharvas and Apsaras, departed as they wished, recounting the astonishing battle of your two sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 58. O Bhārata, the gods, along with the Gandharvas and Apsaras, departed as they wished, recounting the astonishing battle of your two sons.

sanskrit: तथैव सिद्धा राजेन्द्र तथा वातिकचारणाः ।नरसिंहौ प्रशंसन्तौ विप्रजग्मुर्यथागतम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: tathaiva siddhā rājendra tathā vātikacāraṇāḥ ,narasiṁhau praśaṁsantau viprajagmuryathāgatam.
english: In the same manner, O king of kings, the Siddhas, and also the Vātikas and Cāraṇas, praising the two heroes, departed as they had come.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 57 Verse 59. In the same manner, O king of kings, the Siddhas, and also the Vātikas and Cāraṇas, praising the two heroes, departed as they had come.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तं पातितं ततो दृष्ट्वा महाशालमिवोद्गतम् ।प्रहृष्टमनसः सर्वे बभूवुस्तत्र पाण्डवाः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,taṁ pātitaṁ tato dṛṣṭvā mahāśālamivodgatam ,prahṛṣṭamanasaḥ sarve babhūvustatra pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Sañjaya said: 'Then, having seen him, who was like a towering great śāla tree, fallen, all the Pāṇḍavas there became exceedingly joyful in their minds.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 1. Sañjaya said: 'Then, having seen him, who was like a towering great śāla tree, fallen, all the Pāṇḍavas there became exceedingly joyful in their minds.'

sanskrit: उन्मत्तमिव मातङ्गं सिंहेन विनिपातितम् ।ददृशुर्हृष्टरोमाणः सर्वे ते चापि सोमकाः ॥२॥
transliteration: unmattamiva mātaṅgaṁ siṁhena vinipātitam ,dadṛśurhṛṣṭaromāṇaḥ sarve te cāpi somakāḥ.
english: All those Somakas, with their hair standing on end, saw him struck down by a lion, just like a mad elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 2. All those Somakas, with their hair standing on end, saw him struck down by a lion, just like a mad elephant.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनं हत्वा भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ।पतितं कौरवेन्द्रं तमुपगम्येदमब्रवीत् ॥३॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaṁ hatvā bhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān ,patitaṁ kauravendraṁ tamupagamyedamabravīt.
english: Then, the mighty Bhimasena, having killed Duryodhana, approached that fallen chief of the Kurus and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 3. Then, the mighty Bhimasena, having killed Duryodhana, approached that fallen chief of the Kurus and spoke these words.

sanskrit: गौर्गौरिति पुरा मन्द द्रौपदीमेकवाससम् ।यत्सभायां हसन्नस्मांस्तदा वदसि दुर्मते ।तस्यावहासस्य फलमद्य त्वं समवाप्नुहि ॥४॥
transliteration: gaurgauriti purā manda draupadīmekavāsasam ,yatsabhāyāṁ hasannasmāṁstadā vadasi durmate ,tasyāvahāsasya phalamadya tvaṁ samavāpnuhi.
english: O dull-witted and evil-minded one, because you formerly, in the assembly, laughing, addressed us and Draupadi, who was clad in a single garment, with "Cow, cow!" - therefore, today, you shall reap the consequence of that ridicule.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 4. O dull-witted and evil-minded one, because you formerly, in the assembly, laughing, addressed us and Draupadi, who was clad in a single garment, with "Cow, cow!" - therefore, today, you shall reap the consequence of that ridicule.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा स वामेन पदा मौलिमुपास्पृशत् ।शिरश्च राजसिंहस्य पादेन समलोडयत् ॥५॥
transliteration: evamuktvā sa vāmena padā maulimupāspṛśat ,śiraśca rājasiṁhasya pādena samaloḍayat.
english: Having spoken thus, he touched the crown (of Duryodhana's head) with his left foot, and he trampled the head of that lion-like king with his (other) foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 5. Having spoken thus, he touched the crown (of Duryodhana's head) with his left foot, and he trampled the head of that lion-like king with his (other) foot.

sanskrit: तथैव क्रोधसंरक्तो भीमः परबलार्दनः ।पुनरेवाब्रवीद्वाक्यं यत्तच्छृणु नराधिप ॥६॥
transliteration: tathaiva krodhasaṁrakto bhīmaḥ parabalārdanaḥ ,punarevābravīdvākyaṁ yattacchṛṇu narādhipa.
english: Just so, Bhima, red with rage and the tormentor of enemy forces, again spoke these words. O king, hear what he said.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 6. Just so, Bhima, red with rage and the tormentor of enemy forces, again spoke these words. O king, hear what he said.

sanskrit: येऽस्मान्पुरोऽपनृत्यन्त पुनर्गौरिति गौरिति ।तान्वयं प्रतिनृत्यामः पुनर्गौरिति गौरिति ॥७॥
transliteration: ye'smānpuro'panṛtyanta punargauriti gauriti ,tānvayaṁ pratinṛtyāmaḥ punargauriti gauriti.
english: We will mock them back by repeatedly shouting "cow! cow!", just as they previously mocked us by dancing and repeatedly shouting "cow! cow!".
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 7. We will mock them back by repeatedly shouting "cow! cow!", just as they previously mocked us by dancing and repeatedly shouting "cow! cow!".

sanskrit: नास्माकं निकृतिर्वह्निर्नाक्षद्यूतं न वञ्चना ।स्वबाहुबलमाश्रित्य प्रबाधामो वयं रिपून् ॥८॥
transliteration: nāsmākaṁ nikṛtirvahnirnākṣadyūtaṁ na vañcanā ,svabāhubalamāśritya prabādhāmo vayaṁ ripūn.
english: We do not employ deceit, arson, dice-play, or any other form of trickery. Instead, relying on the strength of our own arms, we overpower our enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 8. We do not employ deceit, arson, dice-play, or any other form of trickery. Instead, relying on the strength of our own arms, we overpower our enemies.

sanskrit: सोऽवाप्य वैरस्य परस्य पारं वृकोदरः प्राह शनैः प्रहस्य ।युधिष्ठिरं केशवसृञ्जयांश्च धनंजयं माद्रवतीसुतौ च ॥९॥
transliteration: so'vāpya vairasya parasya pāraṁ; vṛkodaraḥ prāha śanaiḥ prahasya ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ keśavasṛñjayāṁśca; dhanaṁjayaṁ mādravatīsutau ca.
english: Having finally brought the feud with the other party to an end, Bhima (Vṛkodara) laughed softly and spoke to Yudhishthira, Krishna and the Srinjayas, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), and the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 9. Having finally brought the feud with the other party to an end, Bhima (Vṛkodara) laughed softly and spoke to Yudhishthira, Krishna and the Srinjayas, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), and the two sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva).

sanskrit: रजस्वलां द्रौपदीमानयन्ये ये चाप्यकुर्वन्त सदस्यवस्त्राम् ।तान्पश्यध्वं पाण्डवैर्धार्तराष्ट्रान्रणे हतांस्तपसा याज्ञसेन्याः ॥१०॥
transliteration: rajasvalāṁ draupadīmānayanye; ye cāpyakurvanta sadasyavastrām ,tānpaśyadhvaṁ pāṇḍavairdhārtarāṣṭrā;nraṇe hatāṁstapasā yājñasenyāḥ.
english: Behold those sons of Dhritarashtra who dragged the menstruating Draupadi and disrobed her in the assembly; see them now killed in battle by the Pandavas, by virtue of Yājñasenī's ascetic power (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 10. Behold those sons of Dhritarashtra who dragged the menstruating Draupadi and disrobed her in the assembly; see them now killed in battle by the Pandavas, by virtue of Yājñasenī's ascetic power (tapas).

sanskrit: ये नः पुरा षण्ढतिलानवोचन्क्रूरा राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः ।ते नो हताः सगणाः सानुबन्धाः कामं स्वर्गं नरकं वा व्रजामः ॥११॥
transliteration: ye naḥ purā ṣaṇḍhatilānavoca;nkrūrā rājño dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putrāḥ ,te no hatāḥ sagaṇāḥ sānubandhāḥ; kāmaṁ svargaṁ narakaṁ vā vrajāmaḥ.
english: Those cruel sons of King Dhritarashtra who formerly called us 'eunuch-sesames' (implying barrenness or impotence) – they have now been killed by us, along with their retinues and dependents. Now, let us go to heaven or to hell, as we wish.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 11. Those cruel sons of King Dhritarashtra who formerly called us 'eunuch-sesames' (implying barrenness or impotence) – they have now been killed by us, along with their retinues and dependents. Now, let us go to heaven or to hell, as we wish.

sanskrit: पुनश्च राज्ञः पतितस्य भूमौ स तां गदां स्कन्धगतां निरीक्ष्य ।वामेन पादेन शिरः प्रमृद्य दुर्योधनं नैकृतिकेत्यवोचत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: punaśca rājñaḥ patitasya bhūmau; sa tāṁ gadāṁ skandhagatāṁ nirīkṣya ,vāmena pādena śiraḥ pramṛdya; duryodhanaṁ naikṛtiketyavocat.
english: And again, when that king had fallen to the ground, he, observing the mace lying on Duryodhana's shoulder, pressed his head with his left foot and addressed Duryodhana, saying, 'O deceitful one!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 12. And again, when that king had fallen to the ground, he, observing the mace lying on Duryodhana's shoulder, pressed his head with his left foot and addressed Duryodhana, saying, 'O deceitful one!'

sanskrit: हृष्टेन राजन्कुरुपार्थिवस्य क्षुद्रात्मना भीमसेनेन पादम् ।दृष्ट्वा कृतं मूर्धनि नाभ्यनन्दन्धर्मात्मानः सोमकानां प्रबर्हाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: hṛṣṭena rājankurupārthivasya; kṣudrātmanā bhīmasenena pādam ,dṛṣṭvā kṛtaṁ mūrdhani nābhyananda;ndharmātmānaḥ somakānāṁ prabarhāḥ.
english: O King, seeing the act of Bhimasena, who was overjoyed and ignoble, placing his foot on the head of the Kuru ruler, the virtuous leaders (dharma-ātman) among the Somakas did not approve.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 13. O King, seeing the act of Bhimasena, who was overjoyed and ignoble, placing his foot on the head of the Kuru ruler, the virtuous leaders (dharma-ātman) among the Somakas did not approve.

sanskrit: तव पुत्रं तथा हत्वा कत्थमानं वृकोदरम् ।नृत्यमानं च बहुशो धर्मराजोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tava putraṁ tathā hatvā katthamānaṁ vṛkodaram ,nṛtyamānaṁ ca bahuśo dharmarājo'bravīdidam.
english: Having thus killed your son, and repeatedly boasting and dancing, Bhimasena was addressed by Yudhishthira (dharmarāja) with these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 14. Having thus killed your son, and repeatedly boasting and dancing, Bhimasena was addressed by Yudhishthira (dharmarāja) with these words.

sanskrit: मा शिरोऽस्य पदा मर्दीर्मा धर्मस्तेऽत्यगान्महान् ।राजा ज्ञातिर्हतश्चायं नैतन्न्याय्यं तवानघ ॥१५॥
transliteration: mā śiro'sya padā mardīrmā dharmaste'tyagānmahān ,rājā jñātirhataścāyaṁ naitannyāyyaṁ tavānagha.
english: Do not crush his head with your foot. Let not your great virtue (dharma) depart from you. This king, your kinsman, is slain. This is not proper for you, O sinless one.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 15. Do not crush his head with your foot. Let not your great virtue (dharma) depart from you. This king, your kinsman, is slain. This is not proper for you, O sinless one.

sanskrit: विध्वस्तोऽयं हतामात्यो हतभ्राता हतप्रजः ।उत्सन्नपिण्डो भ्राता च नैतन्न्याय्यं कृतं त्वया ॥१६॥
transliteration: vidhvasto'yaṁ hatāmātyo hatabhrātā hataprajaḥ ,utsannapiṇḍo bhrātā ca naitannyāyyaṁ kṛtaṁ tvayā.
english: This brother, whose ministers are slain, whose brothers are slain, whose subjects are slain, and for whom funeral cakes (piṇḍa) can no longer be offered, is utterly destroyed. This act done by you is not proper.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 16. This brother, whose ministers are slain, whose brothers are slain, whose subjects are slain, and for whom funeral cakes (piṇḍa) can no longer be offered, is utterly destroyed. This act done by you is not proper.

sanskrit: धार्मिको भीमसेनोऽसावित्याहुस्त्वां पुरा जनाः ।स कस्माद्भीमसेन त्वं राजानमधितिष्ठसि ॥१७॥
transliteration: dhārmiko bhīmaseno'sāvityāhustvāṁ purā janāḥ ,sa kasmādbhīmasena tvaṁ rājānamadhitiṣṭhasi.
english: In the past, people used to say of you, 'This Bhimasena is righteous (dhārmika).' Why then, O Bhimasena, do you stand over the king?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 17. In the past, people used to say of you, 'This Bhimasena is righteous (dhārmika).' Why then, O Bhimasena, do you stand over the king?

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा दुर्योधनं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रस्तथागतम् ।नेत्राभ्यामश्रुपूर्णाभ्यामिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā duryodhanaṁ rājā kuntīputrastathāgatam ,netrābhyāmaśrupūrṇābhyāmidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kunti, seeing Duryodhana in that state, spoke these words with his eyes filled with tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 18. King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kunti, seeing Duryodhana in that state, spoke these words with his eyes filled with tears.

sanskrit: नूनमेतद्बलवता धात्रादिष्टं महात्मना ।यद्वयं त्वां जिघांसामस्त्वं चास्मान्कुरुसत्तम ॥१९॥
transliteration: nūnametadbalavatā dhātrādiṣṭaṁ mahātmanā ,yadvayaṁ tvāṁ jighāṁsāmastvaṁ cāsmānkurusattama.
english: O best of Kurus, it is certainly ordained by the powerful and great-souled creator (dhātṛ) that we desire to kill you, and you desire to kill us.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 19. O best of Kurus, it is certainly ordained by the powerful and great-souled creator (dhātṛ) that we desire to kill you, and you desire to kill us.

sanskrit: आत्मनो ह्यपराधेन महद्व्यसनमीदृशम् ।प्राप्तवानसि यल्लोभान्मदाद्बाल्याच्च भारत ॥२०॥
transliteration: ātmano hyaparādhena mahadvyasanamīdṛśam ,prāptavānasi yallobhānmadādbālyācca bhārata.
english: Indeed, O Bhārata, you have incurred such a great misfortune because of your own offense (aparādha), stemming from greed, arrogance, and foolishness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 20. Indeed, O Bhārata, you have incurred such a great misfortune because of your own offense (aparādha), stemming from greed, arrogance, and foolishness.

sanskrit: घातयित्वा वयस्यांश्च भ्रातॄनथ पितॄंस्तथा ।पुत्रान्पौत्रांस्तथाचार्यांस्ततोऽसि निधनं गतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: ghātayitvā vayasyāṁśca bhrātṝnatha pitṝṁstathā ,putrānpautrāṁstathācāryāṁstato'si nidhanaṁ gataḥ.
english: Having caused to be killed your friends, brothers, elders, sons, grandsons, and teachers, you have therefore met your death.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 21. Having caused to be killed your friends, brothers, elders, sons, grandsons, and teachers, you have therefore met your death.

sanskrit: तवापराधादस्माभिर्भ्रातरस्ते महारथाः ।निहता ज्ञातयश्चान्ये दिष्टं मन्ये दुरत्ययम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tavāparādhādasmābhirbhrātaraste mahārathāḥ ,nihatā jñātayaścānye diṣṭaṁ manye duratyayam.
english: Due to your offense, your great charioteer brothers and other relatives have been killed by us. I believe this fate to be insurmountable.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 22. Due to your offense, your great charioteer brothers and other relatives have been killed by us. I believe this fate to be insurmountable.

sanskrit: स्नुषाश्च प्रस्नुषाश्चैव धृतराष्ट्रस्य विह्वलाः ।गर्हयिष्यन्ति नो नूनं विधवाः शोककर्शिताः ॥२३॥
transliteration: snuṣāśca prasnuṣāścaiva dhṛtarāṣṭrasya vihvalāḥ ,garhayiṣyanti no nūnaṁ vidhavāḥ śokakarśitāḥ.
english: The distressed daughters-in-law and granddaughters-in-law of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, now widows and emaciated by grief, will certainly censure us.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 23. The distressed daughters-in-law and granddaughters-in-law of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, now widows and emaciated by grief, will certainly censure us.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा सुदुःखार्तो निशश्वास स पार्थिवः ।विललाप चिरं चापि धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ॥२४॥
transliteration: evamuktvā suduḥkhārto niśaśvāsa sa pārthivaḥ ,vilalāpa ciraṁ cāpi dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, that king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (constitutional law), greatly afflicted by sorrow, sighed and also lamented for a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 58 Verse 24. Having spoken thus, that king, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (constitutional law), greatly afflicted by sorrow, sighed and also lamented for a long time.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अधर्मेण हतं दृष्ट्वा राजानं माधवोत्तमः ।किमब्रवीत्तदा सूत बलदेवो महाबलः ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,adharmeṇa hataṁ dṛṣṭvā rājānaṁ mādhavottamaḥ ,kimabravīttadā sūta baladevo mahābalaḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sūta, what did the immensely powerful Baladeva, the best of the Madhavas, say then, having seen the king (Duryodhana) killed by unrighteous means?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "O Sūta, what did the immensely powerful Baladeva, the best of the Madhavas, say then, having seen the king (Duryodhana) killed by unrighteous means?"

sanskrit: गदायुद्धविशेषज्ञो गदायुद्धविशारदः ।कृतवान्रौहिणेयो यत्तन्ममाचक्ष्व संजय ॥२॥
transliteration: gadāyuddhaviśeṣajño gadāyuddhaviśāradaḥ ,kṛtavānrauhiṇeyo yattanmamācakṣva saṁjaya.
english: O Sañjaya, please tell me what the son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma), who is highly skilled and an expert in mace-fighting, did.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 2. O Sañjaya, please tell me what the son of Rohiṇī (Balarāma), who is highly skilled and an expert in mace-fighting, did.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।शिरस्यभिहतं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनेन ते सुतम् ।रामः प्रहरतां श्रेष्ठश्चुक्रोध बलवद्बली ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śirasyabhihataṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenena te sutam ,rāmaḥ praharatāṁ śreṣṭhaścukrodha balavadbalī.
english: Sañjaya said: Seeing your son struck on the head by Bhīmasena, Rāma (Balarāma), who was mighty and strong and the foremost among warriors, became intensely angry.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 3. Sañjaya said: Seeing your son struck on the head by Bhīmasena, Rāma (Balarāma), who was mighty and strong and the foremost among warriors, became intensely angry.

sanskrit: ततो मध्ये नरेन्द्राणामूर्ध्वबाहुर्हलायुधः ।कुर्वन्नार्तस्वरं घोरं धिग्धिग्भीमेत्युवाच ह ॥४॥
transliteration: tato madhye narendrāṇāmūrdhvabāhurhalāyudhaḥ ,kurvannārtasvaraṁ ghoraṁ dhigdhigbhīmetyuvāca ha.
english: Then, amidst the kings, Hālāyudha (Balarāma) raised his arms and, uttering a dreadful cry of anguish, exclaimed, "Shame! Shame upon Bhīma!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 4. Then, amidst the kings, Hālāyudha (Balarāma) raised his arms and, uttering a dreadful cry of anguish, exclaimed, "Shame! Shame upon Bhīma!"

sanskrit: अहो धिग्यदधो नाभेः प्रहृतं शुद्धविक्रमे ।नैतद्दृष्टं गदायुद्धे कृतवान्यद्वृकोदरः ॥५॥
transliteration: aho dhigyadadho nābheḥ prahṛtaṁ śuddhavikrame ,naitaddṛṣṭaṁ gadāyuddhe kṛtavānyadvṛkodaraḥ.
english: Oh, shame! That a blow was struck below the navel against one of pure valor (śuddhavikrame)! Such a thing as Vṛkodara (Bhīma) did has never been witnessed in mace-fighting.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 5. Oh, shame! That a blow was struck below the navel against one of pure valor (śuddhavikrame)! Such a thing as Vṛkodara (Bhīma) did has never been witnessed in mace-fighting.

sanskrit: अधो नाभ्या न हन्तव्यमिति शास्त्रस्य निश्चयः ।अयं त्वशास्त्रविन्मूढः स्वच्छन्दात्संप्रवर्तते ॥६॥
transliteration: adho nābhyā na hantavyamiti śāstrasya niścayaḥ ,ayaṁ tvaśāstravinmūḍhaḥ svacchandātsaṁpravartate.
english: It is the established rule (śāstra) that one should not strike below the navel. However, this foolish person, ignorant of the scripture (śāstra), acts purely out of his own accord.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 6. It is the established rule (śāstra) that one should not strike below the navel. However, this foolish person, ignorant of the scripture (śāstra), acts purely out of his own accord.

sanskrit: तस्य तत्तद्ब्रुवाणस्य रोषः समभवन्महान् ।ततो लाङ्गलमुद्यम्य भीममभ्यद्रवद्बली ॥७॥
transliteration: tasya tattadbruvāṇasya roṣaḥ samabhavanmahān ,tato lāṅgalamudyamya bhīmamabhyadravadbalī.
english: As he (Baladeva) was uttering those words, great anger arose in him. Then, lifting his plough, the mighty one (Baladeva) charged towards Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 7. As he (Baladeva) was uttering those words, great anger arose in him. Then, lifting his plough, the mighty one (Baladeva) charged towards Bhīma.

sanskrit: तस्योर्ध्वबाहोः सदृशं रूपमासीन्महात्मनः ।बहुधातुविचित्रस्य श्वेतस्येव महागिरेः ॥८॥
transliteration: tasyordhvabāhoḥ sadṛśaṁ rūpamāsīnmahātmanaḥ ,bahudhātuvicitrasya śvetasyeva mahāgireḥ.
english: The form of that great-souled one (mahātman), with his arms raised, resembled a great white mountain variegated with numerous minerals.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 8. The form of that great-souled one (mahātman), with his arms raised, resembled a great white mountain variegated with numerous minerals.

sanskrit: तमुत्पतन्तं जग्राह केशवो विनयानतः ।बाहुभ्यां पीनवृत्ताभ्यां प्रयत्नाद्बलवद्बली ॥९॥
transliteration: tamutpatantaṁ jagrāha keśavo vinayānataḥ ,bāhubhyāṁ pīnavṛttābhyāṁ prayatnādbalavadbalī.
english: Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), bowing humbly, seized him as he was leaping upwards. The mighty one (Keśava) grasped him with great effort and strength, using his thick, rounded arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 9. Keśava (Kṛṣṇa), bowing humbly, seized him as he was leaping upwards. The mighty one (Keśava) grasped him with great effort and strength, using his thick, rounded arms.

sanskrit: सितासितौ यदुवरौ शुशुभातेऽधिकं ततः ।नभोगतौ यथा राजंश्चन्द्रसूर्यौ दिनक्षये ॥१०॥
transliteration: sitāsitau yaduvarau śuśubhāte'dhikaṁ tataḥ ,nabhogatau yathā rājaṁścandrasūryau dinakṣaye.
english: Then, O King, the two foremost among the Yadus, the white and the dark ones (Balarama and Krishna), shone exceedingly, just as the moon and the sun appear in the sky at twilight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 10. Then, O King, the two foremost among the Yadus, the white and the dark ones (Balarama and Krishna), shone exceedingly, just as the moon and the sun appear in the sky at twilight.

sanskrit: उवाच चैनं संरब्धं शमयन्निव केशवः ।आत्मवृद्धिर्मित्रवृद्धिर्मित्रमित्रोदयस्तथा ।विपरीतं द्विषत्स्वेतत्षड्विधा वृद्धिरात्मनः ॥११॥
transliteration: uvāca cainaṁ saṁrabdhaṁ śamayanniva keśavaḥ ,ātmavṛddhirmitravṛddhirmitramitrodayastathā ,viparītaṁ dviṣatsvetatṣaḍvidhā vṛddhirātmanaḥ.
english: And Keshava (Krishna), as if pacifying his agitation, spoke to him: 'The advancement of oneself, the prosperity of friends, and similarly, the rise of one's friends' friends – these three, along with their opposites concerning enemies, constitute the six-fold path to one's own prosperity (ātman).'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 11. And Keshava (Krishna), as if pacifying his agitation, spoke to him: 'The advancement of oneself, the prosperity of friends, and similarly, the rise of one's friends' friends – these three, along with their opposites concerning enemies, constitute the six-fold path to one's own prosperity (ātman).'

sanskrit: आत्मन्यपि च मित्रेषु विपरीतं यदा भवेत् ।तदा विद्यान्मनोज्यानिमाशु शान्तिकरो भवेत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: ātmanyapi ca mitreṣu viparītaṁ yadā bhavet ,tadā vidyānmanojyānimāśu śāntikaro bhavet.
english: And when the opposite (adversity) occurs concerning oneself and one's friends, then one should understand that this brings mental distress, and one should quickly become a peacemaker.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 12. And when the opposite (adversity) occurs concerning oneself and one's friends, then one should understand that this brings mental distress, and one should quickly become a peacemaker.

sanskrit: अस्माकं सहजं मित्रं पाण्डवाः शुद्धपौरुषाः ।स्वकाः पितृष्वसुः पुत्रास्ते परैर्निकृता भृशम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: asmākaṁ sahajaṁ mitraṁ pāṇḍavāḥ śuddhapauruṣāḥ ,svakāḥ pitṛṣvasuḥ putrāste parairnikṛtā bhṛśam.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, men of pure valor, are our natural friends; they are the sons of our own paternal aunt. Nevertheless, they have been greatly humiliated by others.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 13. The Pāṇḍavas, men of pure valor, are our natural friends; they are the sons of our own paternal aunt. Nevertheless, they have been greatly humiliated by others.

sanskrit: प्रतिज्ञापारणं धर्मः क्षत्रियस्येति वेत्थ ह ।सुयोधनस्य गदया भङ्क्तास्म्यूरू महाहवे ।इति पूर्वं प्रतिज्ञातं भीमेन हि सभातले ॥१४॥
transliteration: pratijñāpāraṇaṁ dharmaḥ kṣatriyasyeti vettha ha ,suyodhanasya gadayā bhaṅktāsmyūrū mahāhave ,iti pūrvaṁ pratijñātaṁ bhīmena hi sabhātale.
english: You certainly know that fulfilling a vow is the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatriya). I am one who will break Suyodhana's thighs with my mace in the great battle. Indeed, this was previously vowed by Bhīma on the assembly floor.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 14. You certainly know that fulfilling a vow is the intrinsic nature (dharma) of a warrior (kṣatriya). I am one who will break Suyodhana's thighs with my mace in the great battle. Indeed, this was previously vowed by Bhīma on the assembly floor.

sanskrit: मैत्रेयेणाभिशप्तश्च पूर्वमेव महर्षिणा ।ऊरू भेत्स्यति ते भीमो गदयेति परंतप ।अतो दोषं न पश्यामि मा क्रुधस्त्वं प्रलम्बहन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: maitreyeṇābhiśaptaśca pūrvameva maharṣiṇā ,ūrū bhetsyati te bhīmo gadayeti paraṁtapa ,ato doṣaṁ na paśyāmi mā krudhastvaṁ pralambahan.
english: And, O tormentor of foes (parantapa), you were cursed by the great sage Maitreya himself long ago, who said, 'Bhīma will break your thighs with his mace.' Therefore, I see no fault. O slayer of Pralamba (pralambahan), do not be angry.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 15. And, O tormentor of foes (parantapa), you were cursed by the great sage Maitreya himself long ago, who said, 'Bhīma will break your thighs with his mace.' Therefore, I see no fault. O slayer of Pralamba (pralambahan), do not be angry.

sanskrit: यौनैर्हार्दैश्च संबन्धैः संबद्धाः स्मेह पाण्डवैः ।तेषां वृद्ध्याभिवृद्धिर्नो मा क्रुधः पुरुषर्षभ ॥१६॥
transliteration: yaunairhārdaiśca saṁbandhaiḥ saṁbaddhāḥ smeha pāṇḍavaiḥ ,teṣāṁ vṛddhyābhivṛddhirno mā krudhaḥ puruṣarṣabha.
english: We are connected to the Pāṇḍavas through familial relations by marriage (yauna) and by heartfelt bonds. Our prosperity increases with their prosperity. O best of men (puruṣarṣabha), do not be angry.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 16. We are connected to the Pāṇḍavas through familial relations by marriage (yauna) and by heartfelt bonds. Our prosperity increases with their prosperity. O best of men (puruṣarṣabha), do not be angry.

sanskrit: राम उवाच ।धर्मः सुचरितः सद्भिः सह द्वाभ्यां नियच्छति ।अर्थश्चात्यर्थलुब्धस्य कामश्चातिप्रसङ्गिनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: rāma uvāca ,dharmaḥ sucaritaḥ sadbhiḥ saha dvābhyāṁ niyacchati ,arthaścātyarthalubdhasya kāmaścātiprasaṅginaḥ.
english: Rāma said: The natural law (dharma), when diligently practiced by the virtuous, brings both material gain (artha) and desire (kāma) under control. Otherwise, wealth (artha) tends towards the excessively greedy, and desire (kāma) towards the overly indulgent.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 17. Rāma said: The natural law (dharma), when diligently practiced by the virtuous, brings both material gain (artha) and desire (kāma) under control. Otherwise, wealth (artha) tends towards the excessively greedy, and desire (kāma) towards the overly indulgent.

sanskrit: धर्मार्थौ धर्मकामौ च कामार्थौ चाप्यपीडयन् ।धर्मार्थकामान्योऽभ्येति सोऽत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते ॥१८॥
transliteration: dharmārthau dharmakāmau ca kāmārthau cāpyapīḍayan ,dharmārthakāmānyo'bhyeti so'tyantaṁ sukhamaśnute.
english: One who pursues natural law (dharma), wealth (artha), and desire (kāma) without letting natural law conflict with wealth, or natural law conflict with desire, or desire conflict with wealth, such a person attains ultimate happiness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 18. One who pursues natural law (dharma), wealth (artha), and desire (kāma) without letting natural law conflict with wealth, or natural law conflict with desire, or desire conflict with wealth, such a person attains ultimate happiness.

sanskrit: तदिदं व्याकुलं सर्वं कृतं धर्मस्य पीडनात् ।भीमसेनेन गोविन्द कामं त्वं तु यथात्थ माम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ vyākulaṁ sarvaṁ kṛtaṁ dharmasya pīḍanāt ,bhīmasenena govinda kāmaṁ tvaṁ tu yathāttha mām.
english: O Govinda, this entire situation has been rendered chaotic due to the violation (pīḍanāt) of natural law (dharma). This was brought about by Bhīmasena. However, you certainly instruct me (mām) as you deem fit (yathāttha).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 19. O Govinda, this entire situation has been rendered chaotic due to the violation (pīḍanāt) of natural law (dharma). This was brought about by Bhīmasena. However, you certainly instruct me (mām) as you deem fit (yathāttha).

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।अरोषणो हि धर्मात्मा सततं धर्मवत्सलः ।भवान्प्रख्यायते लोके तस्मात्संशाम्य मा क्रुधः ॥२०॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,aroṣaṇo hi dharmātmā satataṁ dharmavatsalaḥ ,bhavānprakhyāyate loke tasmātsaṁśāmya mā krudhaḥ.
english: Vāsudeva said: 'Indeed, you are known in the world as one who is not easily angered (aroṣaṇaḥ), whose essence is righteousness (dharmātmā), and who is constantly affectionate towards natural law (dharma-vatsalaḥ). Therefore, calm yourself; do not be angry.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 20. Vāsudeva said: 'Indeed, you are known in the world as one who is not easily angered (aroṣaṇaḥ), whose essence is righteousness (dharmātmā), and who is constantly affectionate towards natural law (dharma-vatsalaḥ). Therefore, calm yourself; do not be angry.'

sanskrit: प्राप्तं कलियुगं विद्धि प्रतिज्ञां पाण्डवस्य च ।आनृण्यं यातु वैरस्य प्रतिज्ञायाश्च पाण्डवः ॥२१॥
transliteration: prāptaṁ kaliyugaṁ viddhi pratijñāṁ pāṇḍavasya ca ,ānṛṇyaṁ yātu vairasya pratijñāyāśca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Know (viddhi) that the Kali Yuga has arrived, and that the Pāṇḍava's (Bhīmasena's) vow (pratijñām) has been fulfilled. Let the Pāṇḍava (Yudhishthira) be freed from the debt (ānṛṇyam yātu) related to this enmity and the (previous) vow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 21. Know (viddhi) that the Kali Yuga has arrived, and that the Pāṇḍava's (Bhīmasena's) vow (pratijñām) has been fulfilled. Let the Pāṇḍava (Yudhishthira) be freed from the debt (ānṛṇyam yātu) related to this enmity and the (previous) vow.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।धर्मच्छलमपि श्रुत्वा केशवात्स विशां पते ।नैव प्रीतमना रामो वचनं प्राह संसदि ॥२२॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,dharmacchalamapi śrutvā keśavātsa viśāṁ pate ,naiva prītamanā rāmo vacanaṁ prāha saṁsadi.
english: Sañjaya said: O lord of men, even after hearing from Keśava about the deception concerning the natural law (dharma), Balarāma was by no means pleased in his mind and spoke these words in the assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 22. Sañjaya said: O lord of men, even after hearing from Keśava about the deception concerning the natural law (dharma), Balarāma was by no means pleased in his mind and spoke these words in the assembly.

sanskrit: हत्वाधर्मेण राजानं धर्मात्मानं सुयोधनम् ।जिह्मयोधीति लोकेऽस्मिन्ख्यातिं यास्यति पाण्डवः ॥२३॥
transliteration: hatvādharmeṇa rājānaṁ dharmātmānaṁ suyodhanam ,jihmayodhīti loke'sminkhyātiṁ yāsyati pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Having killed King Suyodhana, who was righteous (dharmātman) in his intrinsic nature (dharma), by unrighteous means (adharma), the Pāṇḍava will gain the reputation in this world of being a deceitful fighter.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 23. Having killed King Suyodhana, who was righteous (dharmātman) in his intrinsic nature (dharma), by unrighteous means (adharma), the Pāṇḍava will gain the reputation in this world of being a deceitful fighter.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनोऽपि धर्मात्मा गतिं यास्यति शाश्वतीम् ।ऋजुयोधी हतो राजा धार्तराष्ट्रो नराधिपः ॥२४॥
transliteration: duryodhano'pi dharmātmā gatiṁ yāsyati śāśvatīm ,ṛjuyodhī hato rājā dhārtarāṣṭro narādhipaḥ.
english: Duryodhana, who also possessed a righteous intrinsic nature (dharma), will attain an eternal state. The king, the lord of men, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, was killed as a fair fighter.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 24. Duryodhana, who also possessed a righteous intrinsic nature (dharma), will attain an eternal state. The king, the lord of men, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, was killed as a fair fighter.

sanskrit: युद्धदीक्षां प्रविश्याजौ रणयज्ञं वितत्य च ।हुत्वात्मानममित्राग्नौ प्राप चावभृथं यशः ॥२५॥
transliteration: yuddhadīkṣāṁ praviśyājau raṇayajñaṁ vitatya ca ,hutvātmānamamitrāgnau prāpa cāvabhṛthaṁ yaśaḥ.
english: Having undertaken the solemn vow of battle (yuddhādīkṣā) in the conflict, and having performed the battle-sacrifice (raṇayajña) by offering his very self (ātman) into the fire of his enemies, he attained the concluding ritual bath (avabhṛtha) of fame.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 25. Having undertaken the solemn vow of battle (yuddhādīkṣā) in the conflict, and having performed the battle-sacrifice (raṇayajña) by offering his very self (ātman) into the fire of his enemies, he attained the concluding ritual bath (avabhṛtha) of fame.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा रथमास्थाय रौहिणेयः प्रतापवान् ।श्वेताभ्रशिखराकारः प्रययौ द्वारकां प्रति ॥२६॥
transliteration: ityuktvā rathamāsthāya rauhiṇeyaḥ pratāpavān ,śvetābhraśikharākāraḥ prayayau dvārakāṁ prati.
english: Having said this, the mighty son of Rohiṇī (Rohiṇeya), whose form resembled the peak of a white cloud, mounted his chariot and set off towards Dvārakā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 26. Having said this, the mighty son of Rohiṇī (Rohiṇeya), whose form resembled the peak of a white cloud, mounted his chariot and set off towards Dvārakā.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालाश्च सवार्ष्णेयाः पाण्डवाश्च विशां पते ।रामे द्वारवतीं याते नातिप्रमनसोऽभवन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: pāñcālāśca savārṣṇeyāḥ pāṇḍavāśca viśāṁ pate ,rāme dvāravatīṁ yāte nātipramanaso'bhavan.
english: O lord of the people, when Balarāma (Rāma) had departed for Dvārakā, the Pāñcālas, along with the Vārṣṇeyas and the Pāṇḍavas, were not very pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 27. O lord of the people, when Balarāma (Rāma) had departed for Dvārakā, the Pāñcālas, along with the Vārṣṇeyas and the Pāṇḍavas, were not very pleased.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरं दीनं चिन्तापरमधोमुखम् ।शोकोपहतसंकल्पं वासुदेवोऽब्रवीदिदम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiraṁ dīnaṁ cintāparamadhomukham ,śokopahatasaṁkalpaṁ vāsudevo'bravīdidam.
english: Then Vāsudeva addressed these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, who was distressed, deeply absorbed in worry, with his face downcast, and whose resolve was shattered by grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 28. Then Vāsudeva addressed these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, who was distressed, deeply absorbed in worry, with his face downcast, and whose resolve was shattered by grief.

sanskrit: धर्मराज किमर्थं त्वमधर्ममनुमन्यसे ।हतबन्धोर्यदेतस्य पतितस्य विचेतसः ॥२९॥
transliteration: dharmarāja kimarthaṁ tvamadharmamanumanyase ,hatabandhoryadetasya patitasya vicetasaḥ.
english: O King of Righteousness (dharma-rāja), why do you approve of this unrighteousness (adharma) committed by this fallen, senseless person whose relatives have been killed?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 29. O King of Righteousness (dharma-rāja), why do you approve of this unrighteousness (adharma) committed by this fallen, senseless person whose relatives have been killed?

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य भीमेन मृद्यमानं शिरः पदा ।उपप्रेक्षसि कस्मात्त्वं धर्मज्ञः सन्नराधिप ॥३०॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya bhīmena mṛdyamānaṁ śiraḥ padā ,upaprekṣasi kasmāttvaṁ dharmajñaḥ sannarādhipa.
english: O king of men, why do you, being a knower of (natural law) dharma, merely watch as Duryodhana's head is being crushed by Bhima's foot?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 30. O king of men, why do you, being a knower of (natural law) dharma, merely watch as Duryodhana's head is being crushed by Bhima's foot?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।न ममैतत्प्रियं कृष्ण यद्राजानं वृकोदरः ।पदा मूर्ध्न्यस्पृशत्क्रोधान्न च हृष्ये कुलक्षये ॥३१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,na mamaitatpriyaṁ kṛṣṇa yadrājānaṁ vṛkodaraḥ ,padā mūrdhnyaspṛśatkrodhānna ca hṛṣye kulakṣaye.
english: Yudhishthira said: "O Krishna, this is not pleasing to me, that Vrikodara (Bhima), out of anger, touched the king's head with his foot. Nor do I rejoice in the destruction of the family."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 31. Yudhishthira said: "O Krishna, this is not pleasing to me, that Vrikodara (Bhima), out of anger, touched the king's head with his foot. Nor do I rejoice in the destruction of the family."

sanskrit: निकृत्या निकृता नित्यं धृतराष्ट्रसुतैर्वयम् ।बहूनि परुषाण्युक्त्वा वनं प्रस्थापिताः स्म ह ॥३२॥
transliteration: nikṛtyā nikṛtā nityaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasutairvayam ,bahūni paruṣāṇyuktvā vanaṁ prasthāpitāḥ sma ha.
english: We were constantly wronged by the sons of Dhritarashtra through deceit. After they spoke many harsh words, we were indeed sent into the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 32. We were constantly wronged by the sons of Dhritarashtra through deceit. After they spoke many harsh words, we were indeed sent into the forest.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्य तद्दुःखमतीव हृदि वर्तते ।इति संचिन्त्य वार्ष्णेय मयैतत्समुपेक्षितम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: bhīmasenasya tadduḥkhamatīva hṛdi vartate ,iti saṁcintya vārṣṇeya mayaitatsamupekṣitam.
english: That immense suffering of Bhimasena (Bhima) remains deeply in his heart. Having reflected on this, O Vrishni's descendant (Krishna), I tolerated this act.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 33. That immense suffering of Bhimasena (Bhima) remains deeply in his heart. Having reflected on this, O Vrishni's descendant (Krishna), I tolerated this act.

sanskrit: तस्माद्धत्वाकृतप्रज्ञं लुब्धं कामवशानुगम् ।लभतां पाण्डवः कामं धर्मेऽधर्मेऽपि वा कृते ॥३४॥
transliteration: tasmāddhatvākṛtaprajñaṁ lubdhaṁ kāmavaśānugam ,labhatāṁ pāṇḍavaḥ kāmaṁ dharme'dharme'pi vā kṛte.
english: Therefore, having slain this ignorant, greedy man who was controlled by desire, let the Pāṇḍava achieve his purpose, whether it was done according to natural law (dharma) or even outside it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 34. Therefore, having slain this ignorant, greedy man who was controlled by desire, let the Pāṇḍava achieve his purpose, whether it was done according to natural law (dharma) or even outside it.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।इत्युक्ते धर्मराजेन वासुदेवोऽब्रवीदिदम् ।काममस्त्वेवमिति वै कृच्छ्राद्यदुकुलोद्वहः ॥३५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,ityukte dharmarājena vāsudevo'bravīdidam ,kāmamastvevamiti vai kṛcchrādyadukulodvahaḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said: When the King of Righteousness (dharma-rāja) had spoken thus, Vāsudeva - the scion of the Yadu clan - replied with difficulty, "Indeed, let it be so!"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 35. Saṃjaya said: When the King of Righteousness (dharma-rāja) had spoken thus, Vāsudeva - the scion of the Yadu clan - replied with difficulty, "Indeed, let it be so!"

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो वासुदेवेन भीमप्रियहितैषिणा ।अन्वमोदत तत्सर्वं यद्भीमेन कृतं युधि ॥३६॥
transliteration: ityukto vāsudevena bhīmapriyahitaiṣiṇā ,anvamodata tatsarvaṁ yadbhīmena kṛtaṁ yudhi.
english: Thus addressed by Vāsudeva, who was a well-wisher and dear to Bhīma, (he, i.e., Yudhishthira) approved of everything that Bhīma had done in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 36. Thus addressed by Vāsudeva, who was a well-wisher and dear to Bhīma, (he, i.e., Yudhishthira) approved of everything that Bhīma had done in battle.

sanskrit: भीमसेनोऽपि हत्वाजौ तव पुत्रममर्षणः ।अभिवाद्याग्रतः स्थित्वा संप्रहृष्टः कृताञ्जलिः ॥३७॥
transliteration: bhīmaseno'pi hatvājau tava putramamarṣaṇaḥ ,abhivādyāgrataḥ sthitvā saṁprahṛṣṭaḥ kṛtāñjaliḥ.
english: Enraged, Bhīmasena also, having killed your son in battle, stood before them with folded hands, greatly delighted, and paid his respects.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 37. Enraged, Bhīmasena also, having killed your son in battle, stood before them with folded hands, greatly delighted, and paid his respects.

sanskrit: प्रोवाच सुमहातेजा धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।हर्षादुत्फुल्लनयनो जितकाशी विशां पते ॥३८॥
transliteration: provāca sumahātejā dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,harṣādutphullanayano jitakāśī viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of the people, the exceedingly brilliant one, with eyes wide open from joy, a conqueror, spoke to Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 38. O lord of the people, the exceedingly brilliant one, with eyes wide open from joy, a conqueror, spoke to Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: तवाद्य पृथिवी राजन्क्षेमा निहतकण्टका ।तां प्रशाधि महाराज स्वधर्ममनुपालयन् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tavādya pṛthivī rājankṣemā nihatakaṇṭakā ,tāṁ praśādhi mahārāja svadharmamanupālayan.
english: O King, your earth is now safe and freed from its enemies. O great king, rule her, diligently upholding your own specific duty (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 39. O King, your earth is now safe and freed from its enemies. O great king, rule her, diligently upholding your own specific duty (dharma).

sanskrit: यस्तु कर्तास्य वैरस्य निकृत्या निकृतिप्रियः ।सोऽयं विनिहतः शेते पृथिव्यां पृथिवीपते ॥४०॥
transliteration: yastu kartāsya vairasya nikṛtyā nikṛtipriyaḥ ,so'yaṁ vinihataḥ śete pṛthivyāṁ pṛthivīpate.
english: But he who was the perpetrator of this enmity, who was fond of treachery, and (acted) with treachery - this very one now lies completely slain on the earth, O lord of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 40. But he who was the perpetrator of this enmity, who was fond of treachery, and (acted) with treachery - this very one now lies completely slain on the earth, O lord of the earth.

sanskrit: दुःशासनप्रभृतयः सर्वे ते चोग्रवादिनः ।राधेयः शकुनिश्चापि निहतास्तव शत्रवः ॥४१॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanaprabhṛtayaḥ sarve te cogravādinaḥ ,rādheyaḥ śakuniścāpi nihatāstava śatravaḥ.
english: Dushasana and all those cruel-tongued ones, as well as Radheya (Karna) and Shakuni, all your enemies, have been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 41. Dushasana and all those cruel-tongued ones, as well as Radheya (Karna) and Shakuni, all your enemies, have been slain.

sanskrit: सेयं रत्नसमाकीर्णा मही सवनपर्वता ।उपावृत्ता महाराज त्वामद्य निहतद्विषम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: seyaṁ ratnasamākīrṇā mahī savanaparvatā ,upāvṛttā mahārāja tvāmadya nihatadviṣam.
english: O great king, this earth, adorned with jewels and including its forests and mountains, has now returned to you, whose enemies have been vanquished today.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 42. O great king, this earth, adorned with jewels and including its forests and mountains, has now returned to you, whose enemies have been vanquished today.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।गतं वैरस्य निधनं हतो राजा सुयोधनः ।कृष्णस्य मतमास्थाय विजितेयं वसुंधरा ॥४३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,gataṁ vairasya nidhanaṁ hato rājā suyodhanaḥ ,kṛṣṇasya matamāsthāya vijiteyaṁ vasuṁdharā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'The enmity has reached its end; King Suyodhana has been killed. This earth has been conquered by following Kṛṣṇa's counsel.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 43. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'The enmity has reached its end; King Suyodhana has been killed. This earth has been conquered by following Kṛṣṇa's counsel.'

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या गतस्त्वमानृण्यं मातुः कोपस्य चोभयोः ।दिष्ट्या जयसि दुर्धर्ष दिष्ट्या शत्रुर्निपातितः ॥४४॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā gatastvamānṛṇyaṁ mātuḥ kopasya cobhayoḥ ,diṣṭyā jayasi durdharṣa diṣṭyā śatrurnipātitaḥ.
english: Fortunately, you have been freed from your debt to your mother and from both (her and Draupadi's) angers. Fortunately, O unconquerable one, you are victorious! Fortunately, the enemy has been struck down.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 59 Verse 44. Fortunately, you have been freed from your debt to your mother and from both (her and Draupadi's) angers. Fortunately, O unconquerable one, you are victorious! Fortunately, the enemy has been struck down.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।हतं दुर्योधनं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनेन संयुगे ।पाण्डवाः सृञ्जयाश्चैव किमकुर्वत संजय ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,hataṁ duryodhanaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenena saṁyuge ,pāṇḍavāḥ sṛñjayāścaiva kimakurvata saṁjaya.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'O Saṃjaya, after seeing Duryodhana killed by Bhīmasena in battle, what then did the Pāṇḍavas and the Sṛñjayas do?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'O Saṃjaya, after seeing Duryodhana killed by Bhīmasena in battle, what then did the Pāṇḍavas and the Sṛñjayas do?'

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।हतं दुर्योधनं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनेन संयुगे ।सिंहेनेव महाराज मत्तं वनगजं वने ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,hataṁ duryodhanaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenena saṁyuge ,siṁheneva mahārāja mattaṁ vanagajaṁ vane.
english: Sanjaya said: "O great king, having seen Duryodhana slain by Bhimasena in battle, just as a lion [slays] a rutting wild elephant in the forest."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 2. Sanjaya said: "O great king, having seen Duryodhana slain by Bhimasena in battle, just as a lion [slays] a rutting wild elephant in the forest."

sanskrit: प्रहृष्टमनसस्तत्र कृष्णेन सह पाण्डवाः ।पाञ्चालाः सृञ्जयाश्चैव निहते कुरुनन्दने ॥३॥
transliteration: prahṛṣṭamanasastatra kṛṣṇena saha pāṇḍavāḥ ,pāñcālāḥ sṛñjayāścaiva nihate kurunandane.
english: There, the Pandavas, along with Krishna, and the Panchalas and Srinjayas, became exceedingly joyful in mind when the Kuru prince was slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 3. There, the Pandavas, along with Krishna, and the Panchalas and Srinjayas, became exceedingly joyful in mind when the Kuru prince was slain.

sanskrit: आविध्यन्नुत्तरीयाणि सिंहनादांश्च नेदिरे ।नैतान्हर्षसमाविष्टानियं सेहे वसुंधरा ॥४॥
transliteration: āvidhyannuttarīyāṇi siṁhanādāṁśca nedire ,naitānharṣasamāviṣṭāniyaṁ sehe vasuṁdharā.
english: They waved their upper garments and roared lion-like roars. This earth (vasuṃdharā) could not bear those people who were filled with such joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 4. They waved their upper garments and roared lion-like roars. This earth (vasuṃdharā) could not bear those people who were filled with such joy.

sanskrit: धनूंष्यन्ये व्याक्षिपन्त ज्याश्चाप्यन्ये तथाक्षिपन् ।दध्मुरन्ये महाशङ्खानन्ये जघ्नुश्च दुन्दुभीः ॥५॥
transliteration: dhanūṁṣyanye vyākṣipanta jyāścāpyanye tathākṣipan ,dadhmuranye mahāśaṅkhānanye jaghnuśca dundubhīḥ.
english: Some tossed their bows, and others likewise tossed their bowstrings. Still others blew great conches, and others beat drums.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 5. Some tossed their bows, and others likewise tossed their bowstrings. Still others blew great conches, and others beat drums.

sanskrit: चिक्रीडुश्च तथैवान्ये जहसुश्च तवाहिताः ।अब्रुवंश्चासकृद्वीरा भीमसेनमिदं वचः ॥६॥
transliteration: cikrīḍuśca tathaivānye jahasuśca tavāhitāḥ ,abruvaṁścāsakṛdvīrā bhīmasenamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Your enemies and others likewise engaged in sport and laughter. And repeatedly, these heroes spoke these words to Bhīmasena:
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 6. Your enemies and others likewise engaged in sport and laughter. And repeatedly, these heroes spoke these words to Bhīmasena:

sanskrit: दुष्करं भवता कर्म रणेऽद्य सुमहत्कृतम् ।कौरवेन्द्रं रणे हत्वा गदयातिकृतश्रमम् ॥७॥
transliteration: duṣkaraṁ bhavatā karma raṇe'dya sumahatkṛtam ,kauravendraṁ raṇe hatvā gadayātikṛtaśramam.
english: Today, in battle, a very great and difficult deed has been accomplished by you. You have slain the king of the Kauravas, who was excessively exhausted, in battle with your mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 7. Today, in battle, a very great and difficult deed has been accomplished by you. You have slain the king of the Kauravas, who was excessively exhausted, in battle with your mace.

sanskrit: इन्द्रेणेव हि वृत्रस्य वधं परमसंयुगे ।त्वया कृतममन्यन्त शत्रोर्वधमिमं जनाः ॥८॥
transliteration: indreṇeva hi vṛtrasya vadhaṁ paramasaṁyuge ,tvayā kṛtamamanyanta śatrorvadhamimaṁ janāḥ.
english: Indeed, people considered this slaying of the enemy, accomplished by you in the supreme battle, to be like the slaying of Vṛtra by Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 8. Indeed, people considered this slaying of the enemy, accomplished by you in the supreme battle, to be like the slaying of Vṛtra by Indra.

sanskrit: चरन्तं विविधान्मार्गान्मण्डलानि च सर्वशः ।दुर्योधनमिमं शूरं कोऽन्यो हन्याद्वृकोदरात् ॥९॥
transliteration: carantaṁ vividhānmārgānmaṇḍalāni ca sarvaśaḥ ,duryodhanamimaṁ śūraṁ ko'nyo hanyādvṛkodarāt.
english: Who else but Vṛkodara (Bhīma) could have slain this brave Duryodhana, who was skillfully moving in various paths and performing circular maneuvers everywhere?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 9. Who else but Vṛkodara (Bhīma) could have slain this brave Duryodhana, who was skillfully moving in various paths and performing circular maneuvers everywhere?

sanskrit: वैरस्य च गतः पारं त्वमिहान्यैः सुदुर्गमम् ।अशक्यमेतदन्येन संपादयितुमीदृशम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vairasya ca gataḥ pāraṁ tvamihānyaiḥ sudurgamam ,aśakyametadanyena saṁpādayitumīdṛśam.
english: You have reached the end of enmity, a feat extremely difficult for others to overcome. Indeed, such an accomplishment is impossible for anyone else to achieve.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 10. You have reached the end of enmity, a feat extremely difficult for others to overcome. Indeed, such an accomplishment is impossible for anyone else to achieve.

sanskrit: कुञ्जरेणेव मत्तेन वीर संग्राममूर्धनि ।दुर्योधनशिरो दिष्ट्या पादेन मृदितं त्वया ॥११॥
transliteration: kuñjareṇeva mattena vīra saṁgrāmamūrdhani ,duryodhanaśiro diṣṭyā pādena mṛditaṁ tvayā.
english: O hero, on the battlefield, Duryodhana's head was fortunately crushed by your foot, just like (a foe is crushed) by a frenzied elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 11. O hero, on the battlefield, Duryodhana's head was fortunately crushed by your foot, just like (a foe is crushed) by a frenzied elephant.

sanskrit: सिंहेन महिषस्येव कृत्वा संगरमद्भुतम् ।दुःशासनस्य रुधिरं दिष्ट्या पीतं त्वयानघ ॥१२॥
transliteration: siṁhena mahiṣasyeva kṛtvā saṁgaramadbhutam ,duḥśāsanasya rudhiraṁ diṣṭyā pītaṁ tvayānagha.
english: O sinless one (anagha), fortunately, you have drunk Duḥśāsana's blood after performing an astonishing battle, just as a lion (would drink the blood) of a buffalo.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 12. O sinless one (anagha), fortunately, you have drunk Duḥśāsana's blood after performing an astonishing battle, just as a lion (would drink the blood) of a buffalo.

sanskrit: ये विप्रकुर्वन्राजानं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।मूर्ध्नि तेषां कृतः पादो दिष्ट्या ते स्वेन कर्मणा ॥१३॥
transliteration: ye viprakurvanrājānaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,mūrdhni teṣāṁ kṛtaḥ pādo diṣṭyā te svena karmaṇā.
english: Fortunately, by your own actions (karma), your foot has been placed upon the heads of those who insulted King Yudhishthira, the one whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 13. Fortunately, by your own actions (karma), your foot has been placed upon the heads of those who insulted King Yudhishthira, the one whose intrinsic nature (dharma) is righteousness.

sanskrit: अमित्राणामधिष्ठानाद्वधाद्दुर्योधनस्य च ।भीम दिष्ट्या पृथिव्यां ते प्रथितं सुमहद्यशः ॥१४॥
transliteration: amitrāṇāmadhiṣṭhānādvadhādduryodhanasya ca ,bhīma diṣṭyā pṛthivyāṁ te prathitaṁ sumahadyaśaḥ.
english: O Bhīma, fortunately, your exceedingly great glory is renowned on earth, due to the subjugation of your enemies and the slaying of Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 14. O Bhīma, fortunately, your exceedingly great glory is renowned on earth, due to the subjugation of your enemies and the slaying of Duryodhana.

sanskrit: एवं नूनं हते वृत्रे शक्रं नन्दन्ति बन्दिनः ।तथा त्वां निहतामित्रं वयं नन्दाम भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: evaṁ nūnaṁ hate vṛtre śakraṁ nandanti bandinaḥ ,tathā tvāṁ nihatāmitraṁ vayaṁ nandāma bhārata.
english: Just as, surely, after Vṛtra was slain, the bards praised Indra, similarly, O Bhārata, we rejoice for you, whose enemies have been destroyed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 15. Just as, surely, after Vṛtra was slain, the bards praised Indra, similarly, O Bhārata, we rejoice for you, whose enemies have been destroyed.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनवधे यानि रोमाणि हृषितानि नः ।अद्यापि न विहृष्यन्ति तानि तद्विद्धि भारत ।इत्यब्रुवन्भीमसेनं वातिकास्तत्र संगताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: duryodhanavadhe yāni romāṇi hṛṣitāni naḥ ,adyāpi na vihṛṣyanti tāni tadviddhi bhārata ,ityabruvanbhīmasenaṁ vātikāstatra saṁgatāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, know that the hairs on our bodies, which bristled with joy at the slaying of Duryodhana, have not yet ceased to thrill even today. Thus spoke the Vātikas, who were gathered there, to Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 16. O Bhārata, know that the hairs on our bodies, which bristled with joy at the slaying of Duryodhana, have not yet ceased to thrill even today. Thus spoke the Vātikas, who were gathered there, to Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: तान्हृष्टान्पुरुषव्याघ्रान्पाञ्चालान्पाण्डवैः सह ।ब्रुवतः सदृशं तत्र प्रोवाच मधुसूदनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tānhṛṣṭānpuruṣavyāghrānpāñcālānpāṇḍavaiḥ saha ,bruvataḥ sadṛśaṁ tatra provāca madhusūdanaḥ.
english: Then, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) spoke befittingly to those delighted, foremost among men, the Pāñcālas, who were gathered there with the Pāṇḍavas and had just spoken.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 17. Then, Madhusūdana (Kṛṣṇa) spoke befittingly to those delighted, foremost among men, the Pāñcālas, who were gathered there with the Pāṇḍavas and had just spoken.

sanskrit: न न्याय्यं निहतः शत्रुर्भूयो हन्तुं जनाधिपाः ।असकृद्वाग्भिरुग्राभिर्निहतो ह्येष मन्दधीः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na nyāyyaṁ nihataḥ śatrurbhūyo hantuṁ janādhipāḥ ,asakṛdvāgbhirugrābhirnihato hyeṣa mandadhīḥ.
english: O kings, it is not proper to strike down an enemy who has already been killed. Indeed, this dull-witted person has already been repeatedly struck down by harsh words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 18. O kings, it is not proper to strike down an enemy who has already been killed. Indeed, this dull-witted person has already been repeatedly struck down by harsh words.

sanskrit: तदैवैष हतः पापो यदैव निरपत्रपः ।लुब्धः पापसहायश्च सुहृदां शासनातिगः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tadaivaiṣa hataḥ pāpo yadaiva nirapatrapaḥ ,lubdhaḥ pāpasahāyaśca suhṛdāṁ śāsanātigaḥ.
english: This wicked person was indeed destroyed at the very moment he became shameless, greedy, an accomplice to sin, and disregardful of his friends' counsel.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 19. This wicked person was indeed destroyed at the very moment he became shameless, greedy, an accomplice to sin, and disregardful of his friends' counsel.

sanskrit: बहुशो विदुरद्रोणकृपगाङ्गेयसृञ्जयैः ।पाण्डुभ्यः प्रोच्यमानोऽपि पित्र्यमंशं न दत्तवान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: bahuśo viduradroṇakṛpagāṅgeyasṛñjayaiḥ ,pāṇḍubhyaḥ procyamāno'pi pitryamaṁśaṁ na dattavān.
english: Even though he was repeatedly advised by Vidura, Droṇa, Kṛpa, Gāṅgeya, and Sañjaya, he did not grant the Pāṇḍavas their ancestral share.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 20. Even though he was repeatedly advised by Vidura, Droṇa, Kṛpa, Gāṅgeya, and Sañjaya, he did not grant the Pāṇḍavas their ancestral share.

sanskrit: नैष योग्योऽद्य मित्रं वा शत्रुर्वा पुरुषाधमः ।किमनेनातिनुन्नेन वाग्भिः काष्ठसधर्मणा ॥२१॥
transliteration: naiṣa yogyo'dya mitraṁ vā śatrurvā puruṣādhamaḥ ,kimanenātinunnena vāgbhiḥ kāṣṭhasadharmaṇā.
english: This vilest of men is now no longer fit to be either a friend or an enemy. What is the use of repeatedly urging this one with words, as he is like a block of wood?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 21. This vilest of men is now no longer fit to be either a friend or an enemy. What is the use of repeatedly urging this one with words, as he is like a block of wood?

sanskrit: रथेष्वारोहत क्षिप्रं गच्छामो वसुधाधिपाः ।दिष्ट्या हतोऽयं पापात्मा सामात्यज्ञातिबान्धवः ॥२२॥
transliteration: ratheṣvārohata kṣipraṁ gacchāmo vasudhādhipāḥ ,diṣṭyā hato'yaṁ pāpātmā sāmātyajñātibāndhavaḥ.
