english: O kings, quickly mount your chariots; let us go! Fortunately, this wicked soul, along with his ministers, relatives, and kinsmen, has been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 22. O kings, quickly mount your chariots; let us go! Fortunately, this wicked soul, along with his ministers, relatives, and kinsmen, has been slain.

sanskrit: इति श्रुत्वा त्वधिक्षेपं कृष्णाद्दुर्योधनो नृपः ।अमर्षवशमापन्न उदतिष्ठद्विशां पते ॥२३॥
transliteration: iti śrutvā tvadhikṣepaṁ kṛṣṇādduryodhano nṛpaḥ ,amarṣavaśamāpanna udatiṣṭhadviśāṁ pate.
english: Having heard such an insult from Krishna, King Duryodhana, falling under the sway of indignation, stood up, O lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 23. Having heard such an insult from Krishna, King Duryodhana, falling under the sway of indignation, stood up, O lord of men.

sanskrit: स्फिग्देशेनोपविष्टः स दोर्भ्यां विष्टभ्य मेदिनीम् ।दृष्टिं भ्रूसंकटां कृत्वा वासुदेवे न्यपातयत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: sphigdeśenopaviṣṭaḥ sa dorbhyāṁ viṣṭabhya medinīm ,dṛṣṭiṁ bhrūsaṁkaṭāṁ kṛtvā vāsudeve nyapātayat.
english: He sat down, resting on his hip, and propping himself up with his two arms on the ground. Then, with a furrowed brow, he fixed his gaze upon Vasudeva (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 24. He sat down, resting on his hip, and propping himself up with his two arms on the ground. Then, with a furrowed brow, he fixed his gaze upon Vasudeva (Krishna).

sanskrit: अर्धोन्नतशरीरस्य रूपमासीन्नृपस्य तत् ।क्रुद्धस्याशीविषस्येव च्छिन्नपुच्छस्य भारत ॥२५॥
transliteration: ardhonnataśarīrasya rūpamāsīnnṛpasya tat ,kruddhasyāśīviṣasyeva cchinnapucchasya bhārata.
english: That form of the king, with his body half-raised, was like an enraged venomous snake whose tail has been cut off, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 25. That form of the king, with his body half-raised, was like an enraged venomous snake whose tail has been cut off, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: प्राणान्तकरणीं घोरां वेदनामविचिन्तयन् ।दुर्योधनो वासुदेवं वाग्भिरुग्राभिरार्दयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: prāṇāntakaraṇīṁ ghorāṁ vedanāmavicintayan ,duryodhano vāsudevaṁ vāgbhirugrābhirārdayat.
english: Duryodhana, disregarding the terrible, life-ending pain, tormented Vāsudeva with harsh words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 26. Duryodhana, disregarding the terrible, life-ending pain, tormented Vāsudeva with harsh words.

sanskrit: कंसदासस्य दायाद न ते लज्जास्त्यनेन वै ।अधर्मेण गदायुद्धे यदहं विनिपातितः ॥२७॥
transliteration: kaṁsadāsasya dāyāda na te lajjāstyanena vai ,adharmeṇa gadāyuddhe yadahaṁ vinipātitaḥ.
english: "Oh heir of Kamsa's slave, do you not feel shame (lajjā) at this, that I was struck down in mace combat by a violation of natural law (adharma)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 27. "Oh heir of Kamsa's slave, do you not feel shame (lajjā) at this, that I was struck down in mace combat by a violation of natural law (adharma)?"

sanskrit: ऊरू भिन्धीति भीमस्य स्मृतिं मिथ्या प्रयच्छता ।किं न विज्ञातमेतन्मे यदर्जुनमवोचथाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: ūrū bhindhīti bhīmasya smṛtiṁ mithyā prayacchatā ,kiṁ na vijñātametanme yadarjunamavocathāḥ.
english: "By you, who falsely gave Bhima the instruction, 'Break (his) thighs!', was it not known to me, what you told Arjuna?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 28. "By you, who falsely gave Bhima the instruction, 'Break (his) thighs!', was it not known to me, what you told Arjuna?"

sanskrit: घातयित्वा महीपालानृजुयुद्धान्सहस्रशः ।जिह्मैरुपायैर्बहुभिर्न ते लज्जा न ते घृणा ॥२९॥
transliteration: ghātayitvā mahīpālānṛjuyuddhānsahasraśaḥ ,jihmairupāyairbahubhirna te lajjā na te ghṛṇā.
english: "Having caused thousands of kings, who fought fairly, to be killed by many crooked means, you feel no shame (lajjā) and no disgust."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 29. "Having caused thousands of kings, who fought fairly, to be killed by many crooked means, you feel no shame (lajjā) and no disgust."

sanskrit: अहन्यहनि शूराणां कुर्वाणः कदनं महत् ।शिखण्डिनं पुरस्कृत्य घातितस्ते पितामहः ॥३०॥
transliteration: ahanyahani śūrāṇāṁ kurvāṇaḥ kadanaṁ mahat ,śikhaṇḍinaṁ puraskṛtya ghātitaste pitāmahaḥ.
english: Your grandfather, who was daily causing great slaughter among valiant warriors, was caused to be killed by you, with Śikhaṇḍī placed in front.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 30. Your grandfather, who was daily causing great slaughter among valiant warriors, was caused to be killed by you, with Śikhaṇḍī placed in front.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थाम्नः सनामानं हत्वा नागं सुदुर्मते ।आचार्यो न्यासितः शस्त्रं किं तन्न विदितं मम ॥३१॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmnaḥ sanāmānaṁ hatvā nāgaṁ sudurmate ,ācāryo nyāsitaḥ śastraṁ kiṁ tanna viditaṁ mama.
english: O extremely evil-minded one! Is it not known to me how, by killing the elephant named Aśvatthāman, the preceptor (guru) was made to lay down his weapon?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 31. O extremely evil-minded one! Is it not known to me how, by killing the elephant named Aśvatthāman, the preceptor (guru) was made to lay down his weapon?

sanskrit: स चानेन नृशंसेन धृष्टद्युम्नेन वीर्यवान् ।पात्यमानस्त्वया दृष्टो न चैनं त्वमवारयः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa cānena nṛśaṁsena dhṛṣṭadyumnena vīryavān ,pātyamānastvayā dṛṣṭo na cainaṁ tvamavārayaḥ.
english: And that mighty warrior, as he was being felled by this cruel Dhṛṣṭadyumna, was seen by you, yet you did not stop him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 32. And that mighty warrior, as he was being felled by this cruel Dhṛṣṭadyumna, was seen by you, yet you did not stop him.

sanskrit: वधार्थं पाण्डुपुत्रस्य याचितां शक्तिमेव च ।घटोत्कचे व्यंसयथाः कस्त्वत्तः पापकृत्तमः ॥३३॥
transliteration: vadhārthaṁ pāṇḍuputrasya yācitāṁ śaktimeva ca ,ghaṭotkace vyaṁsayathāḥ kastvattaḥ pāpakṛttamaḥ.
english: And you, indeed, caused the divine power (śakti) weapon, which was sought for the destruction of the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), to be wasted on Ghaṭotkaca. Who is more sinful than you?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 33. And you, indeed, caused the divine power (śakti) weapon, which was sought for the destruction of the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), to be wasted on Ghaṭotkaca. Who is more sinful than you?

sanskrit: छिन्नबाहुः प्रायगतस्तथा भूरिश्रवा बली ।त्वया निसृष्टेन हतः शैनेयेन दुरात्मना ॥३४॥
transliteration: chinnabāhuḥ prāyagatastathā bhūriśravā balī ,tvayā nisṛṣṭena hataḥ śaineyena durātmanā.
english: And the powerful Bhuriśravas, his arms severed and having undertaken a fast unto death, was killed by the wicked-souled Sātyaki, whom you dispatched.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 34. And the powerful Bhuriśravas, his arms severed and having undertaken a fast unto death, was killed by the wicked-souled Sātyaki, whom you dispatched.

sanskrit: कुर्वाणश्चोत्तमं कर्म कर्णः पार्थजिगीषया ।व्यंसनेनाश्वसेनस्य पन्नगेन्द्रसुतस्य वै ॥३५॥
transliteration: kurvāṇaścottamaṁ karma karṇaḥ pārthajigīṣayā ,vyaṁsanenāśvasenasya pannagendrasutasya vai.
english: And Karna, while performing his supreme deed (karma) with the desire to conquer Arjuna, was indeed afflicted by the misfortune involving Ashvasena, the son of the king of serpents.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 35. And Karna, while performing his supreme deed (karma) with the desire to conquer Arjuna, was indeed afflicted by the misfortune involving Ashvasena, the son of the king of serpents.

sanskrit: पुनश्च पतिते चक्रे व्यसनार्तः पराजितः ।पातितः समरे कर्णश्चक्रव्यग्रोऽग्रणीर्नृणाम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: punaśca patite cakre vyasanārtaḥ parājitaḥ ,pātitaḥ samare karṇaścakravyagro'graṇīrnṛṇām.
english: And again, when his chariot wheel had sunk, Karna, afflicted by misfortune and defeated, was struck down in battle while preoccupied with his wheel – he, the foremost leader of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 36. And again, when his chariot wheel had sunk, Karna, afflicted by misfortune and defeated, was struck down in battle while preoccupied with his wheel – he, the foremost leader of men.

sanskrit: यदि मां चापि कर्णं च भीष्मद्रोणौ च संयुगे ।ऋजुना प्रतियुध्येथा न ते स्याद्विजयो ध्रुवम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: yadi māṁ cāpi karṇaṁ ca bhīṣmadroṇau ca saṁyuge ,ṛjunā pratiyudhyethā na te syādvijayo dhruvam.
english: If you were to fight against me, Karna, Bhishma, and Drona fairly in battle, victory would certainly not be yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 37. If you were to fight against me, Karna, Bhishma, and Drona fairly in battle, victory would certainly not be yours.

sanskrit: त्वया पुनरनार्येण जिह्ममार्गेण पार्थिवाः ।स्वधर्ममनुतिष्ठन्तो वयं चान्ये च घातिताः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tvayā punaranāryeṇa jihmamārgeṇa pārthivāḥ ,svadharmamanutiṣṭhanto vayaṁ cānye ca ghātitāḥ.
english: But you, ignoble one, by following a crooked path, have slain kings, and also us and others who were upholding our own intrinsic nature (sva-dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 38. But you, ignoble one, by following a crooked path, have slain kings, and also us and others who were upholding our own intrinsic nature (sva-dharma).

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।हतस्त्वमसि गान्धारे सभ्रातृसुतबान्धवः ।सगणः ससुहृच्चैव पापमार्गमनुष्ठितः ॥३९॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,hatastvamasi gāndhāre sabhrātṛsutabāndhavaḥ ,sagaṇaḥ sasuhṛccaiva pāpamārgamanuṣṭhitaḥ.
english: Vāsudeva said: O son of Gāndhārī, you who have followed the path of evil, are slain along with your brothers, sons, and relatives, indeed with your retinue and friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 39. Vāsudeva said: O son of Gāndhārī, you who have followed the path of evil, are slain along with your brothers, sons, and relatives, indeed with your retinue and friends.

sanskrit: तवैव दुष्कृतैर्वीरौ भीष्मद्रोणौ निपातितौ ।कर्णश्च निहतः संख्ये तव शीलानुवर्तकः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tavaiva duṣkṛtairvīrau bhīṣmadroṇau nipātitau ,karṇaśca nihataḥ saṁkhye tava śīlānuvartakaḥ.
english: Indeed, by your own misdeeds, the two heroes Bhīṣma and Droṇa were brought down. And Karṇa, who was a follower of your character, was also killed in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 40. Indeed, by your own misdeeds, the two heroes Bhīṣma and Droṇa were brought down. And Karṇa, who was a follower of your character, was also killed in battle.

sanskrit: याच्यमानो मया मूढ पित्र्यमंशं न दित्ससि ।पाण्डवेभ्यः स्वराज्यार्धं लोभाच्छकुनिनिश्चयात् ॥४१॥
transliteration: yācyamāno mayā mūḍha pitryamaṁśaṁ na ditsasi ,pāṇḍavebhyaḥ svarājyārdhaṁ lobhācchakuniniścayāt.
english: O foolish one, you do not wish to give the paternal share, which is half of their kingdom, to the Pāṇḍavas, even though I have requested it. This is out of greed and because of Śakuni's resolve.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 41. O foolish one, you do not wish to give the paternal share, which is half of their kingdom, to the Pāṇḍavas, even though I have requested it. This is out of greed and because of Śakuni's resolve.

sanskrit: विषं ते भीमसेनाय दत्तं सर्वे च पाण्डवाः ।प्रदीपिता जतुगृहे मात्रा सह सुदुर्मते ॥४२॥
transliteration: viṣaṁ te bhīmasenāya dattaṁ sarve ca pāṇḍavāḥ ,pradīpitā jatugṛhe mātrā saha sudurmate.
english: You gave poison to Bhimasena. And all the Pāṇḍavas, along with their mother, were set ablaze in the house of lac, O extremely wicked-minded one!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 42. You gave poison to Bhimasena. And all the Pāṇḍavas, along with their mother, were set ablaze in the house of lac, O extremely wicked-minded one!

sanskrit: सभायां याज्ञसेनी च कृष्टा द्यूते रजस्वला ।तदैव तावद्दुष्टात्मन्वध्यस्त्वं निरपत्रपः ॥४३॥
transliteration: sabhāyāṁ yājñasenī ca kṛṣṭā dyūte rajasvalā ,tadaiva tāvadduṣṭātmanvadhyastvaṁ nirapatrapaḥ.
english: And in the assembly, Yājñasenī (Draupadī), in a state of menstruation, was dragged by force during the dice game. Therefore, O wicked-minded one, you, O shameless one, should have been killed right at that very moment!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 43. And in the assembly, Yājñasenī (Draupadī), in a state of menstruation, was dragged by force during the dice game. Therefore, O wicked-minded one, you, O shameless one, should have been killed right at that very moment!

sanskrit: अनक्षज्ञं च धर्मज्ञं सौबलेनाक्षवेदिना ।निकृत्या यत्पराजैषीस्तस्मादसि हतो रणे ॥४४॥
transliteration: anakṣajñaṁ ca dharmajñaṁ saubalenākṣavedinā ,nikṛtyā yatparājaiṣīstasmādasi hato raṇe.
english: Because you deceitfully defeated the one (Yudhiṣṭhira) who, though unfamiliar with dice, was knowledgeable in (natural) law (dharma), by means of Saubala (Śakuni), who was an expert in dice, therefore you are now slain in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 44. Because you deceitfully defeated the one (Yudhiṣṭhira) who, though unfamiliar with dice, was knowledgeable in (natural) law (dharma), by means of Saubala (Śakuni), who was an expert in dice, therefore you are now slain in battle.

sanskrit: जयद्रथेन पापेन यत्कृष्णा क्लेशिता वने ।यातेषु मृगयां तेषु तृणबिन्दोरथाश्रमे ॥४५॥
transliteration: jayadrathena pāpena yatkṛṣṇā kleśitā vane ,yāteṣu mṛgayāṁ teṣu tṛṇabindorathāśrame.
english: Because Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was tormented in the forest by the wicked Jayadratha, when they (the Pāṇḍavas) had gone hunting, and this occurred in the hermitage (āśrama) of Tṛṇabindu.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 45. Because Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was tormented in the forest by the wicked Jayadratha, when they (the Pāṇḍavas) had gone hunting, and this occurred in the hermitage (āśrama) of Tṛṇabindu.

sanskrit: अभिमन्युश्च यद्बाल एको बहुभिराहवे ।त्वद्दोषैर्निहतः पाप तस्मादसि हतो रणे ॥४६॥
transliteration: abhimanyuśca yadbāla eko bahubhirāhave ,tvaddoṣairnihataḥ pāpa tasmādasi hato raṇe.
english: Because Abhimanyu, though a mere boy, was killed by many in battle due to your faults, O sinner, therefore you yourself are now killed in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 46. Because Abhimanyu, though a mere boy, was killed by many in battle due to your faults, O sinner, therefore you yourself are now killed in battle.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।अधीतं विधिवद्दत्तं भूः प्रशास्ता ससागरा ।मूर्ध्नि स्थितममित्राणां को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥४७॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,adhītaṁ vidhivaddattaṁ bhūḥ praśāstā sasāgarā ,mūrdhni sthitamamitrāṇāṁ ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: Duryodhana said: "I have studied properly, given generously, and ruled the entire earth, including its oceans. I have stood victorious over my enemies. Who, then, is happier than I?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 47. Duryodhana said: "I have studied properly, given generously, and ruled the entire earth, including its oceans. I have stood victorious over my enemies. Who, then, is happier than I?"

sanskrit: यदिष्टं क्षत्रबन्धूनां स्वधर्ममनुपश्यताम् ।तदिदं निधनं प्राप्तं को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥४८॥
transliteration: yadiṣṭaṁ kṣatrabandhūnāṁ svadharmamanupaśyatām ,tadidaṁ nidhanaṁ prāptaṁ ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: This desired death, which is sought by those of warrior descent (kṣatrabandhū) who follow their own natural law (svadharma), I have now obtained. Who, then, is happier than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 48. This desired death, which is sought by those of warrior descent (kṣatrabandhū) who follow their own natural law (svadharma), I have now obtained. Who, then, is happier than I?

sanskrit: देवार्हा मानुषा भोगाः प्राप्ता असुलभा नृपैः ।ऐश्वर्यं चोत्तमं प्राप्तं को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥४९॥
transliteration: devārhā mānuṣā bhogāḥ prāptā asulabhā nṛpaiḥ ,aiśvaryaṁ cottamaṁ prāptaṁ ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: I obtained human enjoyments (bhogāḥ) that are worthy of gods and difficult for other kings to acquire. I also attained supreme sovereignty. Who, then, is happier than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 49. I obtained human enjoyments (bhogāḥ) that are worthy of gods and difficult for other kings to acquire. I also attained supreme sovereignty. Who, then, is happier than I?

sanskrit: ससुहृत्सानुबन्धश्च स्वर्गं गन्ताहमच्युत ।यूयं विहतसंकल्पाः शोचन्तो वर्तयिष्यथ ॥५०॥
transliteration: sasuhṛtsānubandhaśca svargaṁ gantāhamacyuta ,yūyaṁ vihatasaṁkalpāḥ śocanto vartayiṣyatha.
english: O Acyuta, I shall go to heaven with my friends and relatives. You all, however, with your resolves shattered, will live on lamenting.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 50. O Acyuta, I shall go to heaven with my friends and relatives. You all, however, with your resolves shattered, will live on lamenting.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।अस्य वाक्यस्य निधने कुरुराजस्य भारत ।अपतत्सुमहद्वर्षं पुष्पाणां पुण्यगन्धिनाम् ॥५१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,asya vākyasya nidhane kururājasya bhārata ,apatatsumahadvarṣaṁ puṣpāṇāṁ puṇyagandhinām.
english: Sañjaya said: O Bhārata, at the conclusion of this speech by the king of the Kurus, a great shower of sweetly fragrant flowers fell.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 51. Sañjaya said: O Bhārata, at the conclusion of this speech by the king of the Kurus, a great shower of sweetly fragrant flowers fell.

sanskrit: अवादयन्त गन्धर्वा जगुश्चाप्सरसां गणाः ।सिद्धाश्च मुमुचुर्वाचः साधु साध्विति भारत ॥५२॥
transliteration: avādayanta gandharvā jaguścāpsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ ,siddhāśca mumucurvācaḥ sādhu sādhviti bhārata.
english: And the Gandharvas played music, and hosts of Apsarases sang. And the Siddhas, O Bhārata, uttered words of praise, saying, 'Excellent! Excellent!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 52. And the Gandharvas played music, and hosts of Apsarases sang. And the Siddhas, O Bhārata, uttered words of praise, saying, 'Excellent! Excellent!'

sanskrit: ववौ च सुरभिर्वायुः पुण्यगन्धो मृदुः सुखः ।व्यराजतामलं चैव नभो वैडूर्यसंनिभम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: vavau ca surabhirvāyuḥ puṇyagandho mṛduḥ sukhaḥ ,vyarājatāmalaṁ caiva nabho vaiḍūryasaṁnibham.
english: And a fragrant, sweetly-scented, gentle, and pleasant wind blew. Indeed, the clear sky also shone like a lapis lazuli gem.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 53. And a fragrant, sweetly-scented, gentle, and pleasant wind blew. Indeed, the clear sky also shone like a lapis lazuli gem.

sanskrit: अत्यद्भुतानि ते दृष्ट्वा वासुदेवपुरोगमाः ।दुर्योधनस्य पूजां च दृष्ट्वा व्रीडामुपागमन् ॥५४॥
transliteration: atyadbhutāni te dṛṣṭvā vāsudevapurogamāḥ ,duryodhanasya pūjāṁ ca dṛṣṭvā vrīḍāmupāgaman.
english: Those headed by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), having witnessed those exceedingly marvelous things (of the Pāṇḍavas), and also having observed the honor shown to Duryodhana, subsequently felt embarrassment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 54. Those headed by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), having witnessed those exceedingly marvelous things (of the Pāṇḍavas), and also having observed the honor shown to Duryodhana, subsequently felt embarrassment.

sanskrit: हतांश्चाधर्मतः श्रुत्वा शोकार्ताः शुशुचुर्हि ते ।भीष्मं द्रोणं तथा कर्णं भूरिश्रवसमेव च ॥५५॥
transliteration: hatāṁścādharmataḥ śrutvā śokārtāḥ śuśucurhi te ,bhīṣmaṁ droṇaṁ tathā karṇaṁ bhūriśravasameva ca.
english: And having heard that Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Karṇa, and also Bhūriśravas were slain unrighteously (adharma), they (the Pāṇḍavas and their allies), afflicted by grief, indeed lamented.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 55. And having heard that Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Karṇa, and also Bhūriśravas were slain unrighteously (adharma), they (the Pāṇḍavas and their allies), afflicted by grief, indeed lamented.

sanskrit: तांस्तु चिन्तापरान्दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवान्दीनचेतसः ।प्रोवाचेदं वचः कृष्णो मेघदुन्दुभिनिस्वनः ॥५६॥
transliteration: tāṁstu cintāparāndṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavāndīnacetasaḥ ,provācedaṁ vacaḥ kṛṣṇo meghadundubhinisvanaḥ.
english: But seeing those Pāṇḍavas absorbed in worry and dejected in spirit, Kṛṣṇa, whose voice resonated like a deep war-drum, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 56. But seeing those Pāṇḍavas absorbed in worry and dejected in spirit, Kṛṣṇa, whose voice resonated like a deep war-drum, spoke these words.

sanskrit: नैष शक्योऽतिशीघ्रास्त्रस्ते च सर्वे महारथाः ।ऋजुयुद्धेन विक्रान्ता हन्तुं युष्माभिराहवे ॥५७॥
transliteration: naiṣa śakyo'tiśīghrāstraste ca sarve mahārathāḥ ,ṛjuyuddhena vikrāntā hantuṁ yuṣmābhirāhave.
english: For this one (referring to a formidable warrior like Droṇa or Bhīṣma), whose weapons are extremely swift, and all those great charioteers (mahārathas) who are valorous in straightforward (ṛjuyuddha) combat, could not possibly be killed by you in battle (āhave) through fair means.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 57. For this one (referring to a formidable warrior like Droṇa or Bhīṣma), whose weapons are extremely swift, and all those great charioteers (mahārathas) who are valorous in straightforward (ṛjuyuddha) combat, could not possibly be killed by you in battle (āhave) through fair means.

sanskrit: उपाया विहिता ह्येते मया तस्मान्नराधिपाः ।अन्यथा पाण्डवेयानां नाभविष्यज्जयः क्वचित् ॥५८॥
transliteration: upāyā vihitā hyete mayā tasmānnarādhipāḥ ,anyathā pāṇḍaveyānāṁ nābhaviṣyajjayaḥ kvacit.
english: Indeed, these strategies were devised by me, O kings. Otherwise, the Pāṇḍavas would never have achieved victory anywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 58. Indeed, these strategies were devised by me, O kings. Otherwise, the Pāṇḍavas would never have achieved victory anywhere.

sanskrit: ते हि सर्वे महात्मानश्चत्वारोऽतिरथा भुवि ।न शक्या धर्मतो हन्तुं लोकपालैरपि स्वयम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: te hi sarve mahātmānaścatvāro'tirathā bhuvi ,na śakyā dharmato hantuṁ lokapālairapi svayam.
english: For all four of them, those great-souled ones and mighty warriors on earth, could not be killed in accordance with natural law (dharma), even by the world-guardians themselves.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 59. For all four of them, those great-souled ones and mighty warriors on earth, could not be killed in accordance with natural law (dharma), even by the world-guardians themselves.

sanskrit: तथैवायं गदापाणिर्धार्तराष्ट्रो गतक्लमः ।न शक्यो धर्मतो हन्तुं कालेनापीह दण्डिना ॥६०॥
transliteration: tathaivāyaṁ gadāpāṇirdhārtarāṣṭro gataklamaḥ ,na śakyo dharmato hantuṁ kālenāpīha daṇḍinā.
english: Similarly, this mace-wielding and untiring son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra could not be killed in accordance with natural law (dharma) here, even by Yama, the wielder of the rod.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 60. Similarly, this mace-wielding and untiring son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra could not be killed in accordance with natural law (dharma) here, even by Yama, the wielder of the rod.

sanskrit: न च वो हृदि कर्तव्यं यदयं घातितो नृपः ।मिथ्यावध्यास्तथोपायैर्बहवः शत्रवोऽधिकाः ॥६१॥
transliteration: na ca vo hṛdi kartavyaṁ yadayaṁ ghātito nṛpaḥ ,mithyāvadhyāstathopāyairbahavaḥ śatravo'dhikāḥ.
english: And it should not be held in your hearts that this king was killed improperly. Similarly, many formidable enemies, who were otherwise inviolable, were slain through these strategies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 61. And it should not be held in your hearts that this king was killed improperly. Similarly, many formidable enemies, who were otherwise inviolable, were slain through these strategies.

sanskrit: पूर्वैरनुगतो मार्गो देवैरसुरघातिभिः ।सद्भिश्चानुगतः पन्थाः स सर्वैरनुगम्यते ॥६२॥
transliteration: pūrvairanugato mārgo devairasuraghātibhiḥ ,sadbhiścānugataḥ panthāḥ sa sarvairanugamyate.
english: The path trodden by the ancients, by the gods who vanquish the asuras, and the way followed by the virtuous - that (path) should be followed by everyone.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 62. The path trodden by the ancients, by the gods who vanquish the asuras, and the way followed by the virtuous - that (path) should be followed by everyone.

sanskrit: कृतकृत्याः स्म सायाह्ने निवासं रोचयामहे ।साश्वनागरथाः सर्वे विश्रमामो नराधिपाः ॥६३॥
transliteration: kṛtakṛtyāḥ sma sāyāhne nivāsaṁ rocayāmahe ,sāśvanāgarathāḥ sarve viśramāmo narādhipāḥ.
english: We have accomplished our duties, and it is evening; we wish to take up residence. All of us, O kings, with our horses, elephants, and chariots, let us rest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 63. We have accomplished our duties, and it is evening; we wish to take up residence. All of us, O kings, with our horses, elephants, and chariots, let us rest.

sanskrit: वासुदेववचः श्रुत्वा तदानीं पाण्डवैः सह ।पाञ्चाला भृशसंहृष्टा विनेदुः सिंहसंघवत् ॥६४॥
transliteration: vāsudevavacaḥ śrutvā tadānīṁ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha ,pāñcālā bhṛśasaṁhṛṣṭā vineduḥ siṁhasaṁghavat.
english: Having heard the words of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the Pāñcālas, along with the Pāṇḍavas, became exceedingly joyful and roared like a multitude of lions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 64. Having heard the words of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the Pāñcālas, along with the Pāṇḍavas, became exceedingly joyful and roared like a multitude of lions.

sanskrit: ततः प्राध्मापयञ्शङ्खान्पाञ्चजन्यं च माधवः ।हृष्टा दुर्योधनं दृष्ट्वा निहतं पुरुषर्षभाः ॥६५॥
transliteration: tataḥ prādhmāpayañśaṅkhānpāñcajanyaṁ ca mādhavaḥ ,hṛṣṭā duryodhanaṁ dṛṣṭvā nihataṁ puruṣarṣabhāḥ.
english: Then, (they) blew their conch shells, and Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa) blew Pāñcajanya. O best of men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), (they were) delighted, having seen Duryodhana slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 60 Verse 65. Then, (they) blew their conch shells, and Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa) blew Pāñcajanya. O best of men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), (they were) delighted, having seen Duryodhana slain.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ततस्ते प्रययुः सर्वे निवासाय महीक्षितः ।शङ्खान्प्रध्मापयन्तो वै हृष्टाः परिघबाहवः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tataste prayayuḥ sarve nivāsāya mahīkṣitaḥ ,śaṅkhānpradhmāpayanto vai hṛṣṭāḥ parighabāhavaḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: Then all of them, joyful and strong-armed, went forth to the king's encampment, loudly blowing their conch shells.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: Then all of them, joyful and strong-armed, went forth to the king's encampment, loudly blowing their conch shells.

sanskrit: पाण्डवान्गच्छतश्चापि शिबिरं नो विशां पते ।महेष्वासोऽन्वगात्पश्चाद्युयुत्सुः सात्यकिस्तथा ॥२॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāngacchataścāpi śibiraṁ no viśāṁ pate ,maheṣvāso'nvagātpaścādyuyutsuḥ sātyakistathā.
english: O lord of men, Yuyutsu and Sātyaki, the great archers, also followed behind to our camp as the Pāṇḍavas were going.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 2. O lord of men, Yuyutsu and Sātyaki, the great archers, also followed behind to our camp as the Pāṇḍavas were going.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नः शिखण्डी च द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः ।सर्वे चान्ये महेष्वासा ययुः स्वशिबिराण्युत ॥३॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca draupadeyāśca sarvaśaḥ ,sarve cānye maheṣvāsā yayuḥ svaśibirāṇyuta.
english: And Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and all the sons of Draupadī, and indeed all other great archers, went to their own camps.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 3. And Dhṛṣṭadyumna, Śikhaṇḍī, and all the sons of Draupadī, and indeed all other great archers, went to their own camps.

sanskrit: ततस्ते प्राविशन्पार्था हतत्विट्कं हतेश्वरम् ।दुर्योधनस्य शिबिरं रङ्गवद्विसृते जने ॥४॥
transliteration: tataste prāviśanpārthā hatatviṭkaṁ hateśvaram ,duryodhanasya śibiraṁ raṅgavadvisṛte jane.
english: Then the sons of Pṛthā (Pāṇḍavas) entered Duryodhana's camp, which had lost its splendor and whose lord was defeated, while the people were scattered, like a stage (after a performance).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 4. Then the sons of Pṛthā (Pāṇḍavas) entered Duryodhana's camp, which had lost its splendor and whose lord was defeated, while the people were scattered, like a stage (after a performance).

sanskrit: गतोत्सवं पुरमिव हृतनागमिव ह्रदम् ।स्त्रीवर्षवरभूयिष्ठं वृद्धामात्यैरधिष्ठितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: gatotsavaṁ puramiva hṛtanāgamiva hradam ,strīvarṣavarabhūyiṣṭhaṁ vṛddhāmātyairadhiṣṭhitam.
english: A city deprived of festivities, like a lake from which its elephant has been removed; it was filled mostly with women, eunuchs, and old men, and governed by aged ministers.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 5. A city deprived of festivities, like a lake from which its elephant has been removed; it was filled mostly with women, eunuchs, and old men, and governed by aged ministers.

sanskrit: तत्रैतान्पर्युपातिष्ठन्दुर्योधनपुरःसराः ।कृताञ्जलिपुटा राजन्काषायमलिनाम्बराः ॥६॥
transliteration: tatraitānparyupātiṣṭhanduryodhanapuraḥsarāḥ ,kṛtāñjalipuṭā rājankāṣāyamalināmbarāḥ.
english: O King, there, with Duryodhana at their head, and with their palms joined in reverence, they stood surrounding those (Pandavas), wearing soiled saffron robes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 6. O King, there, with Duryodhana at their head, and with their palms joined in reverence, they stood surrounding those (Pandavas), wearing soiled saffron robes.

sanskrit: शिबिरं समनुप्राप्य कुरुराजस्य पाण्डवाः ।अवतेरुर्महाराज रथेभ्यो रथसत्तमाः ॥७॥
transliteration: śibiraṁ samanuprāpya kururājasya pāṇḍavāḥ ,avaterurmahārāja rathebhyo rathasattamāḥ.
english: O Great King, having arrived at the Kuru king's camp, the Pandavas, who were excellent charioteers, descended from their chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 7. O Great King, having arrived at the Kuru king's camp, the Pandavas, who were excellent charioteers, descended from their chariots.

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवधन्वानमभ्यभाषत केशवः ।स्थितः प्रियहिते नित्यमतीव भरतर्षभ ॥८॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvadhanvānamabhyabhāṣata keśavaḥ ,sthitaḥ priyahite nityamatīva bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among the Bharatas, then Keśava (Krishna), who was always exceedingly committed to his dear one's (Arjuna's) welfare, addressed Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 8. O best among the Bharatas, then Keśava (Krishna), who was always exceedingly committed to his dear one's (Arjuna's) welfare, addressed Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: अवरोपय गाण्डीवमक्षय्यौ च महेषुधी ।अथाहमवरोक्ष्यामि पश्चाद्भरतसत्तम ॥९॥
transliteration: avaropaya gāṇḍīvamakṣayyau ca maheṣudhī ,athāhamavarokṣyāmi paścādbharatasattama.
english: Lower the Gāṇḍīva bow and the two inexhaustible quivers. Then, O best of the Bhāratas, I will descend afterwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 9. Lower the Gāṇḍīva bow and the two inexhaustible quivers. Then, O best of the Bhāratas, I will descend afterwards.

sanskrit: स्वयं चैवावरोह त्वमेतच्छ्रेयस्तवानघ ।तच्चाकरोत्तथा वीरः पाण्डुपुत्रो धनंजयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: svayaṁ caivāvaroha tvametacchreyastavānagha ,taccākarottathā vīraḥ pāṇḍuputro dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: And you yourself should descend, O sinless one; this is indeed beneficial for you. The hero Arjuna (Pāṇḍu's son) then did just that.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 10. And you yourself should descend, O sinless one; this is indeed beneficial for you. The hero Arjuna (Pāṇḍu's son) then did just that.

sanskrit: अथ पश्चात्ततः कृष्णो रश्मीनुत्सृज्य वाजिनाम् ।अवारोहत मेधावी रथाद्गाण्डीवधन्वनः ॥११॥
transliteration: atha paścāttataḥ kṛṣṇo raśmīnutsṛjya vājinām ,avārohata medhāvī rathādgāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ.
english: Then, after that, the intelligent Kṛṣṇa, releasing the reins of the horses, descended from the chariot of the wielder of Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 11. Then, after that, the intelligent Kṛṣṇa, releasing the reins of the horses, descended from the chariot of the wielder of Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अथावतीर्णे भूतानामीश्वरे सुमहात्मनि ।कपिरन्तर्दधे दिव्यो ध्वजो गाण्डीवधन्वनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: athāvatīrṇe bhūtānāmīśvare sumahātmani ,kapirantardadhe divyo dhvajo gāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ.
english: Then, as the greatly magnanimous Lord (īśvara) of all beings, very great-souled (sumahātman), descended, the divine monkey banner on the flag of the wielder of Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna) disappeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 12. Then, as the greatly magnanimous Lord (īśvara) of all beings, very great-souled (sumahātman), descended, the divine monkey banner on the flag of the wielder of Gāṇḍīva (Arjuna) disappeared.

sanskrit: स दग्धो द्रोणकर्णाभ्यां दिव्यैरस्त्रैर्महारथः ।अथ दीप्तोऽग्निना ह्याशु प्रजज्वाल महीपते ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa dagdho droṇakarṇābhyāṁ divyairastrairmahārathaḥ ,atha dīpto'gninā hyāśu prajajvāla mahīpate.
english: O King, that magnificent chariot, already damaged by Droṇa and Karṇa's divine weapons, then quickly burst into flames, ignited by fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 13. O King, that magnificent chariot, already damaged by Droṇa and Karṇa's divine weapons, then quickly burst into flames, ignited by fire.

sanskrit: सोपासङ्गः सरश्मिश्च साश्वः सयुगबन्धुरः ।भस्मीभूतोऽपतद्भूमौ रथो गाण्डीवधन्वनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sopāsaṅgaḥ saraśmiśca sāśvaḥ sayugabandhuraḥ ,bhasmībhūto'patadbhūmau ratho gāṇḍīvadhanvanaḥ.
english: The chariot of Arjuna (gāṇḍīvadhanvan), complete with its quivers, reins, horses, yoke, and frame, was reduced to ashes and fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 14. The chariot of Arjuna (gāṇḍīvadhanvan), complete with its quivers, reins, horses, yoke, and frame, was reduced to ashes and fell to the ground.

sanskrit: तं तथा भस्मभूतं तु दृष्ट्वा पाण्डुसुताः प्रभो ।अभवन्विस्मिता राजन्नर्जुनश्चेदमब्रवीत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā bhasmabhūtaṁ tu dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍusutāḥ prabho ,abhavanvismitā rājannarjunaścedamabravīt.
english: O Lord, O King, when the sons of Pāṇḍu saw that chariot thus reduced to ashes, they were astonished, and Arjuna spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 15. O Lord, O King, when the sons of Pāṇḍu saw that chariot thus reduced to ashes, they were astonished, and Arjuna spoke these words.

sanskrit: कृताञ्जलिः सप्रणयं प्रणिपत्याभिवाद्य च ।गोविन्द कस्माद्भगवन्रथो दग्धोऽयमग्निना ॥१६॥
transliteration: kṛtāñjaliḥ sapraṇayaṁ praṇipatyābhivādya ca ,govinda kasmādbhagavanratho dagdho'yamagninā.
english: With folded hands, reverently bowing down and saluting, Arjuna asked: 'O Govinda, O Lord, for what reason was this chariot consumed by fire?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 16. With folded hands, reverently bowing down and saluting, Arjuna asked: 'O Govinda, O Lord, for what reason was this chariot consumed by fire?'

sanskrit: किमेतन्महदाश्चर्यमभवद्यदुनन्दन ।तन्मे ब्रूहि महाबाहो श्रोतव्यं यदि मन्यसे ॥१७॥
transliteration: kimetanmahadāścaryamabhavadyadunandana ,tanme brūhi mahābāho śrotavyaṁ yadi manyase.
english: O delight of the Yadus, O mighty-armed one, what is this great wonder that has occurred? Please tell me about it if you consider it worth hearing.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 17. O delight of the Yadus, O mighty-armed one, what is this great wonder that has occurred? Please tell me about it if you consider it worth hearing.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।अस्त्रैर्बहुविधैर्दग्धः पूर्वमेवायमर्जुन ।मदधिष्ठितत्वात्समरे न विशीर्णः परंतप ॥१८॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,astrairbahuvidhairdagdhaḥ pūrvamevāyamarjuna ,madadhiṣṭhitatvātsamare na viśīrṇaḥ paraṁtapa.
english: Vāsudeva said: "O Arjuna, O scorcher of foes, this (standard) was previously burnt by many kinds of weapons. Yet, because of my presence (mad-adhiṣṭhitatvāt) in the battle, it was not shattered."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 18. Vāsudeva said: "O Arjuna, O scorcher of foes, this (standard) was previously burnt by many kinds of weapons. Yet, because of my presence (mad-adhiṣṭhitatvāt) in the battle, it was not shattered."

sanskrit: इदानीं तु विशीर्णोऽयं दग्धो ब्रह्मास्त्रतेजसा ।मया विमुक्तः कौन्तेय त्वय्यद्य कृतकर्मणि ॥१९॥
transliteration: idānīṁ tu viśīrṇo'yaṁ dagdho brahmāstratejasā ,mayā vimuktaḥ kaunteya tvayyadya kṛtakarmaṇi.
english: But now, O son of Kunti, this (standard) has been shattered and burnt by the power of the Brahmāstra, because I released my protection from it, as your task is now accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 19. But now, O son of Kunti, this (standard) has been shattered and burnt by the power of the Brahmāstra, because I released my protection from it, as your task is now accomplished.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।ईषदुत्स्मयमानश्च भगवान्केशवोऽरिहा ।परिष्वज्य च राजानं युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२०॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,īṣadutsmayamānaśca bhagavānkeśavo'rihā ,pariṣvajya ca rājānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Sanjaya said: And the divine (bhagavān) Keśava, the destroyer of enemies, smiling slightly, embraced King Yudhishthira and then spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 20. Sanjaya said: And the divine (bhagavān) Keśava, the destroyer of enemies, smiling slightly, embraced King Yudhishthira and then spoke.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या जयसि कौन्तेय दिष्ट्या ते शत्रवो जिताः ।दिष्ट्या गाण्डीवधन्वा च भीमसेनश्च पाण्डवः ॥२१॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā jayasi kaunteya diṣṭyā te śatravo jitāḥ ,diṣṭyā gāṇḍīvadhanvā ca bhīmasenaśca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Fortunately, O son of Kunti, you are victorious! Fortunately, your enemies have been conquered. And fortunately, O Pāṇḍava, both Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, and Bhīmasena are safe and sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 21. Fortunately, O son of Kunti, you are victorious! Fortunately, your enemies have been conquered. And fortunately, O Pāṇḍava, both Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, and Bhīmasena are safe and sound.

sanskrit: त्वं चापि कुशली राजन्माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ।मुक्ता वीरक्षयादस्मात्संग्रामान्निहतद्विषः ।क्षिप्रमुत्तरकालानि कुरु कार्याणि भारत ॥२२॥
transliteration: tvaṁ cāpi kuśalī rājanmādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau ,muktā vīrakṣayādasmātsaṁgrāmānnihatadviṣaḥ ,kṣipramuttarakālāni kuru kāryāṇi bhārata.
english: O King, you are also well, as are the two sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), the Pāṇḍavas. Having slain your foes, you are all released from this battle, which caused the destruction of heroes. O Bhārata, quickly undertake the duties of the post-war period.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 22. O King, you are also well, as are the two sons of Mādrī (Nakula and Sahadeva), the Pāṇḍavas. Having slain your foes, you are all released from this battle, which caused the destruction of heroes. O Bhārata, quickly undertake the duties of the post-war period.

sanskrit: उपयातमुपप्लव्यं सह गाण्डीवधन्वना ।आनीय मधुपर्कं मां यत्पुरा त्वमवोचथाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: upayātamupaplavyaṁ saha gāṇḍīvadhanvanā ,ānīya madhuparkaṁ māṁ yatpurā tvamavocathāḥ.
english: What you, after arriving at Upaplavya with Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, and after presenting me with the madhuparka offering, previously said to me -
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 23. What you, after arriving at Upaplavya with Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, and after presenting me with the madhuparka offering, previously said to me -

sanskrit: एष भ्राता सखा चैव तव कृष्ण धनंजयः ।रक्षितव्यो महाबाहो सर्वास्वापत्स्विति प्रभो ।तव चैवं ब्रुवाणस्य तथेत्येवाहमब्रुवम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: eṣa bhrātā sakhā caiva tava kṛṣṇa dhanaṁjayaḥ ,rakṣitavyo mahābāho sarvāsvāpatsviti prabho ,tava caivaṁ bruvāṇasya tathetyevāhamabruvam.
english: 'This Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) is your brother and indeed your friend, O Kṛṣṇa! O mighty-armed one, he must be protected in all dangers,' this is what you said, O Lord. And when you spoke thus, I indeed replied, 'So be it!'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 24. 'This Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) is your brother and indeed your friend, O Kṛṣṇa! O mighty-armed one, he must be protected in all dangers,' this is what you said, O Lord. And when you spoke thus, I indeed replied, 'So be it!'

sanskrit: स सव्यसाची गुप्तस्ते विजयी च नरेश्वर ।भ्रातृभिः सह राजेन्द्र शूरः सत्यपराक्रमः ।मुक्तो वीरक्षयादस्मात्संग्रामाद्रोमहर्षणात् ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa savyasācī guptaste vijayī ca nareśvara ,bhrātṛbhiḥ saha rājendra śūraḥ satyaparākramaḥ ,mukto vīrakṣayādasmātsaṁgrāmādromaharṣaṇāt.
english: O ruler of men (nareśvara), O king of kings (rājendra), that ambidextrous Arjuna (savyasācī), who is under your protection, is victorious, brave, and of true valor, along with his brothers. He has been freed from this terrifying battle, which brought about the destruction of warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 25. O ruler of men (nareśvara), O king of kings (rājendra), that ambidextrous Arjuna (savyasācī), who is under your protection, is victorious, brave, and of true valor, along with his brothers. He has been freed from this terrifying battle, which brought about the destruction of warriors.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु कृष्णेन धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।हृष्टरोमा महाराज प्रत्युवाच जनार्दनम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktastu kṛṣṇena dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,hṛṣṭaromā mahārāja pratyuvāca janārdanam.
english: O great king (mahārāja), Yudhishthira (Yudhiṣṭhira), the King of dharma, thus addressed by Krishna (Kṛṣṇa), his hair standing on end (hṛṣṭaromā), replied to Janardana (Janārdana).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 26. O great king (mahārāja), Yudhishthira (Yudhiṣṭhira), the King of dharma, thus addressed by Krishna (Kṛṣṇa), his hair standing on end (hṛṣṭaromā), replied to Janardana (Janārdana).

sanskrit: प्रमुक्तं द्रोणकर्णाभ्यां ब्रह्मास्त्रमरिमर्दन ।कस्त्वदन्यः सहेत्साक्षादपि वज्री पुरंदरः ॥२७॥
transliteration: pramuktaṁ droṇakarṇābhyāṁ brahmāstramarimardana ,kastvadanyaḥ sahetsākṣādapi vajrī puraṁdaraḥ.
english: O destroyer of foes (arimardana), who else but you could directly endure the (brahmāstra), which was released by Drona and Karna? Not even Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrī) and destroyer of cities (purandara), could withstand it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 27. O destroyer of foes (arimardana), who else but you could directly endure the (brahmāstra), which was released by Drona and Karna? Not even Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrī) and destroyer of cities (purandara), could withstand it.

sanskrit: भवतस्तु प्रसादेन संग्रामे बहवो जिताः ।महारणगतः पार्थो यच्च नासीत्पराङ्मुखः ॥२८॥
transliteration: bhavatastu prasādena saṁgrāme bahavo jitāḥ ,mahāraṇagataḥ pārtho yacca nāsītparāṅmukhaḥ.
english: Indeed, by your grace, many were conquered in battle. And because Partha (Pārtha) entered that great war, he did not turn away from it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 28. Indeed, by your grace, many were conquered in battle. And because Partha (Pārtha) entered that great war, he did not turn away from it.

sanskrit: तथैव च महाबाहो पर्यायैर्बहुभिर्मया ।कर्मणामनुसंतानं तेजसश्च गतिः शुभा ॥२९॥
transliteration: tathaiva ca mahābāho paryāyairbahubhirmayā ,karmaṇāmanusaṁtānaṁ tejasaśca gatiḥ śubhā.
english: And similarly, O mighty-armed one, by me, through many successive explanations, the continuous flow of actions (karma) and the auspicious trajectory of energy have been described.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 29. And similarly, O mighty-armed one, by me, through many successive explanations, the continuous flow of actions (karma) and the auspicious trajectory of energy have been described.

sanskrit: उपप्लव्ये महर्षिर्मे कृष्णद्वैपायनोऽब्रवीत् ।यतो धर्मस्ततः कृष्णो यतः कृष्णस्ततो जयः ॥३०॥
transliteration: upaplavye maharṣirme kṛṣṇadvaipāyano'bravīt ,yato dharmastataḥ kṛṣṇo yataḥ kṛṣṇastato jayaḥ.
english: At Upaplavya, the great sage (maharṣi) Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana told me: 'Where there is natural law (dharma), there is Kṛṣṇa; where there is Kṛṣṇa, there is victory.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 30. At Upaplavya, the great sage (maharṣi) Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana told me: 'Where there is natural law (dharma), there is Kṛṣṇa; where there is Kṛṣṇa, there is victory.'

sanskrit: इत्येवमुक्ते ते वीराः शिबिरं तव भारत ।प्रविश्य प्रत्यपद्यन्त कोशरत्नर्द्धिसंचयान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: ityevamukte te vīrāḥ śibiraṁ tava bhārata ,praviśya pratyapadyanta kośaratnarddhisaṁcayān.
english: O Bhārata, once this was said, those heroes, having entered your camp, recovered the collected wealth of treasures and jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 31. O Bhārata, once this was said, those heroes, having entered your camp, recovered the collected wealth of treasures and jewels.

sanskrit: रजतं जातरूपं च मणीनथ च मौक्तिकान् ।भूषणान्यथ मुख्यानि कम्बलान्यजिनानि च ।दासीदासमसंख्येयं राज्योपकरणानि च ॥३२॥
transliteration: rajataṁ jātarūpaṁ ca maṇīnatha ca mauktikān ,bhūṣaṇānyatha mukhyāni kambalānyajināni ca ,dāsīdāsamasaṁkhyeyaṁ rājyopakaraṇāni ca.
english: Silver and gold, then jewels and pearls, and then principal ornaments; blankets and animal skins; and innumerable male and female servants along with equipment for the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 32. Silver and gold, then jewels and pearls, and then principal ornaments; blankets and animal skins; and innumerable male and female servants along with equipment for the kingdom.

sanskrit: ते प्राप्य धनमक्षय्यं त्वदीयं भरतर्षभ ।उदक्रोशन्महेष्वासा नरेन्द्र विजितारयः ॥३३॥
transliteration: te prāpya dhanamakṣayyaṁ tvadīyaṁ bharatarṣabha ,udakrośanmaheṣvāsā narendra vijitārayaḥ.
english: O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), O king (narendra), those great archers (maheṣvāsāḥ), who had conquered their enemies (vijitārayaḥ), having obtained your imperishable wealth, roared (in triumph).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 33. O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), O king (narendra), those great archers (maheṣvāsāḥ), who had conquered their enemies (vijitārayaḥ), having obtained your imperishable wealth, roared (in triumph).

sanskrit: ते तु वीराः समाश्वस्य वाहनान्यवमुच्य च ।अतिष्ठन्त मुहुः सर्वे पाण्डवाः सात्यकिस्तथा ॥३४॥
transliteration: te tu vīrāḥ samāśvasya vāhanānyavamucya ca ,atiṣṭhanta muhuḥ sarve pāṇḍavāḥ sātyakistathā.
english: But all those heroes, the Pāṇḍavas and Sātyaki, having refreshed themselves and unharnessed their vehicles, remained there for some time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 34. But all those heroes, the Pāṇḍavas and Sātyaki, having refreshed themselves and unharnessed their vehicles, remained there for some time.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीन्महाराज वासुदेवो महायशाः ।अस्माभिर्मङ्गलार्थाय वस्तव्यं शिबिराद्बहिः ॥३५॥
transliteration: athābravīnmahārāja vāsudevo mahāyaśāḥ ,asmābhirmaṅgalārthāya vastavyaṁ śibirādbahiḥ.
english: Then, O great king (mahārāja), the greatly renowned (mahāyaśāḥ) Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) said: "For good fortune, we should reside outside the camp."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 35. Then, O great king (mahārāja), the greatly renowned (mahāyaśāḥ) Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) said: "For good fortune, we should reside outside the camp."

sanskrit: तथेत्युक्त्वा च ते सर्वे पाण्डवाः सात्यकिस्तथा ।वासुदेवेन सहिता मङ्गलार्थं ययुर्बहिः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tathetyuktvā ca te sarve pāṇḍavāḥ sātyakistathā ,vāsudevena sahitā maṅgalārthaṁ yayurbahiḥ.
english: And saying 'So be it,' all those Pāṇḍavas and Sātyaki, accompanied by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), went outside for good fortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 36. And saying 'So be it,' all those Pāṇḍavas and Sātyaki, accompanied by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), went outside for good fortune.

sanskrit: ते समासाद्य सरितं पुण्यामोघवतीं नृप ।न्यवसन्नथ तां रात्रिं पाण्डवा हतशत्रवः ॥३७॥
transliteration: te samāsādya saritaṁ puṇyāmoghavatīṁ nṛpa ,nyavasannatha tāṁ rātriṁ pāṇḍavā hataśatravaḥ.
english: O King, the Pāṇḍavas, having slain their enemies, reached the sacred river Amoghavatī and then spent that night there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 37. O King, the Pāṇḍavas, having slain their enemies, reached the sacred river Amoghavatī and then spent that night there.

sanskrit: ततः संप्रेषयामासुर्यादवं नागसाह्वयम् ।स च प्रायाज्जवेनाशु वासुदेवः प्रतापवान् ।दारुकं रथमारोप्य येन राजाम्बिकासुतः ॥३८॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁpreṣayāmāsuryādavaṁ nāgasāhvayam ,sa ca prāyājjavenāśu vāsudevaḥ pratāpavān ,dārukaṁ rathamāropya yena rājāmbikāsutaḥ.
english: Then, the Pāṇḍavas sent the mighty Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), a Yādava identified with Nāgasāhva. And he swiftly proceeded, making Dāruka mount the chariot, towards King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 38. Then, the Pāṇḍavas sent the mighty Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), a Yādava identified with Nāgasāhva. And he swiftly proceeded, making Dāruka mount the chariot, towards King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā.

sanskrit: तमूचुः संप्रयास्यन्तं सैन्यसुग्रीववाहनम् ।प्रत्याश्वासय गान्धारीं हतपुत्रां यशस्विनीम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tamūcuḥ saṁprayāsyantaṁ sainyasugrīvavāhanam ,pratyāśvāsaya gāndhārīṁ hataputrāṁ yaśasvinīm.
english: They said to him (Kṛṣṇa), who was about to depart, whose chariot was drawn by the excellent horses Sainya and Sugrīva: "Console the glorious Gāndhārī, whose sons have been slain."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 39. They said to him (Kṛṣṇa), who was about to depart, whose chariot was drawn by the excellent horses Sainya and Sugrīva: "Console the glorious Gāndhārī, whose sons have been slain."

sanskrit: स प्रायात्पाण्डवैरुक्तस्तत्पुरं सात्वतां वरः ।आससादयिषुः क्षिप्रं गान्धारीं निहतात्मजाम् ॥४०॥
transliteration: sa prāyātpāṇḍavairuktastatpuraṁ sātvatāṁ varaḥ ,āsasādayiṣuḥ kṣipraṁ gāndhārīṁ nihatātmajām.
english: He (Kṛṣṇa), the best among the Sātvatas, thus instructed by the Pāṇḍavas, quickly proceeded to that city, desiring to meet Gāndhārī, whose children (ātmajā) had been slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 61 Verse 40. He (Kṛṣṇa), the best among the Sātvatas, thus instructed by the Pāṇḍavas, quickly proceeded to that city, desiring to meet Gāndhārī, whose children (ātmajā) had been slain.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।किमर्थं राजशार्दूलो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।गान्धार्याः प्रेषयामास वासुदेवं परंतपम् ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kimarthaṁ rājaśārdūlo dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,gāndhāryāḥ preṣayāmāsa vāsudevaṁ paraṁtapam.
english: Janamejaya said: "For what reason did Yudhishthira, that tiger among kings and king of (dharma) righteousness, send Vasudeva (Krishna), the tormentor of enemies, to Gandhari?"
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: "For what reason did Yudhishthira, that tiger among kings and king of (dharma) righteousness, send Vasudeva (Krishna), the tormentor of enemies, to Gandhari?"

sanskrit: यदा पूर्वं गतः कृष्णः शमार्थं कौरवान्प्रति ।न च तं लब्धवान्कामं ततो युद्धमभूदिदम् ॥२॥
transliteration: yadā pūrvaṁ gataḥ kṛṣṇaḥ śamārthaṁ kauravānprati ,na ca taṁ labdhavānkāmaṁ tato yuddhamabhūdidam.
english: Previously, when Krishna went to the Kauravas for the sake of peace, and he did not achieve that desired outcome, then this war occurred.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 2. Previously, when Krishna went to the Kauravas for the sake of peace, and he did not achieve that desired outcome, then this war occurred.

sanskrit: निहतेषु तु योधेषु हते दुर्योधने तथा ।पृथिव्यां पाण्डवेयस्य निःसपत्ने कृते युधि ॥३॥
transliteration: nihateṣu tu yodheṣu hate duryodhane tathā ,pṛthivyāṁ pāṇḍaveyasya niḥsapatne kṛte yudhi.
english: Indeed, when the warriors were slain, and Duryodhana was similarly killed, and the earth, through the war, had been made free of rivals for the Pandava (Yudhishthira)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 3. Indeed, when the warriors were slain, and Duryodhana was similarly killed, and the earth, through the war, had been made free of rivals for the Pandava (Yudhishthira)...

sanskrit: विद्रुते शिबिरे शून्ये प्राप्ते यशसि चोत्तमे ।किं नु तत्कारणं ब्रह्मन्येन कृष्णो गतः पुनः ॥४॥
transliteration: vidrute śibire śūnye prāpte yaśasi cottame ,kiṁ nu tatkāraṇaṁ brahmanyena kṛṣṇo gataḥ punaḥ.
english: When the camp was deserted and empty, and supreme glory had been attained, what indeed was that reason, O brahmin, for which Krishna went again?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 4. When the camp was deserted and empty, and supreme glory had been attained, what indeed was that reason, O brahmin, for which Krishna went again?

sanskrit: न चैतत्कारणं ब्रह्मन्नल्पं वै प्रतिभाति मे ।यत्रागमदमेयात्मा स्वयमेव जनार्दनः ॥५॥
transliteration: na caitatkāraṇaṁ brahmannalpaṁ vai pratibhāti me ,yatrāgamadameyātmā svayameva janārdanaḥ.
english: And this cause, O Brahmin, indeed seems insignificant to me, given that Janārdana himself, whose nature (ātman) is immeasurable, arrived there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 5. And this cause, O Brahmin, indeed seems insignificant to me, given that Janārdana himself, whose nature (ātman) is immeasurable, arrived there.

sanskrit: तत्त्वतो वै समाचक्ष्व सर्वमध्वर्युसत्तम ।यच्चात्र कारणं ब्रह्मन्कार्यस्यास्य विनिश्चये ॥६॥
transliteration: tattvato vai samācakṣva sarvamadhvaryusattama ,yaccātra kāraṇaṁ brahmankāryasyāsya viniścaye.
english: O best of the priests (adhvaryu), indeed explain everything to me truthfully and completely, and disclose, O Brahmin, whatever the cause (kāraṇa) is here regarding the ascertainment of this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 6. O best of the priests (adhvaryu), indeed explain everything to me truthfully and completely, and disclose, O Brahmin, whatever the cause (kāraṇa) is here regarding the ascertainment of this matter.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।त्वद्युक्तोऽयमनुप्रश्नो यन्मां पृच्छसि पार्थिव ।तत्तेऽहं संप्रवक्ष्यामि यथावद्भरतर्षभ ॥७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tvadyukto'yamanupraśno yanmāṁ pṛcchasi pārthiva ,tatte'haṁ saṁpravakṣyāmi yathāvadbharatarṣabha.
english: Vaiśampaayana said: "This inquiry is fitting for you, O king (pārthiva), because you ask me. Therefore, I will explain that to you completely and truthfully (yathāvat), O best of the Bharatas."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 7. Vaiśampaayana said: "This inquiry is fitting for you, O king (pārthiva), because you ask me. Therefore, I will explain that to you completely and truthfully (yathāvat), O best of the Bharatas."

sanskrit: हतं दुर्योधनं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनेन संयुगे ।व्युत्क्रम्य समयं राजन्धार्तराष्ट्रं महाबलम् ॥८॥
transliteration: hataṁ duryodhanaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmasenena saṁyuge ,vyutkramya samayaṁ rājandhārtarāṣṭraṁ mahābalam.
english: O King, having seen the mighty son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Duryodhana, killed by Bhīmasena in battle, after he had violated the agreement (samaya)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 8. O King, having seen the mighty son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Duryodhana, killed by Bhīmasena in battle, after he had violated the agreement (samaya)...

sanskrit: अन्यायेन हतं दृष्ट्वा गदायुद्धेन भारत ।युधिष्ठिरं महाराज महद्भयमथाविशत् ॥९॥
transliteration: anyāyena hataṁ dṛṣṭvā gadāyuddhena bhārata ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ mahārāja mahadbhayamathāviśat.
english: O Bhārata, O great king, then great fear entered Yudhiṣṭhira upon seeing (Duryodhana) struck unlawfully in the mace-fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 9. O Bhārata, O great king, then great fear entered Yudhiṣṭhira upon seeing (Duryodhana) struck unlawfully in the mace-fight.

sanskrit: चिन्तयानो महाभागां गान्धारीं तपसान्विताम् ।घोरेण तपसा युक्तां त्रैलोक्यमपि सा दहेत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: cintayāno mahābhāgāṁ gāndhārīṁ tapasānvitām ,ghoreṇa tapasā yuktāṁ trailokyamapi sā dahet.
english: Reflecting on the highly revered Gāndhārī, who is endowed with intense asceticism (tapas), he (Yudhiṣṭhira wondered if) she, possessed of such fierce asceticism (tapas), might even incinerate the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 10. Reflecting on the highly revered Gāndhārī, who is endowed with intense asceticism (tapas), he (Yudhiṣṭhira wondered if) she, possessed of such fierce asceticism (tapas), might even incinerate the three worlds.

sanskrit: तस्य चिन्तयमानस्य बुद्धिः समभवत्तदा ।गान्धार्याः क्रोधदीप्तायाः पूर्वं प्रशमनं भवेत् ॥११॥
transliteration: tasya cintayamānasya buddhiḥ samabhavattadā ,gāndhāryāḥ krodhadīptāyāḥ pūrvaṁ praśamanaṁ bhavet.
english: Then, as he (Yudhiṣṭhira) was reflecting, this idea arose in his mind: Gāndhārī, who is blazing with anger, must first be appeased.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 11. Then, as he (Yudhiṣṭhira) was reflecting, this idea arose in his mind: Gāndhārī, who is blazing with anger, must first be appeased.

sanskrit: सा हि पुत्रवधं श्रुत्वा कृतमस्माभिरीदृशम् ।मानसेनाग्निना क्रुद्धा भस्मसान्नः करिष्यति ॥१२॥
transliteration: sā hi putravadhaṁ śrutvā kṛtamasmābhirīdṛśam ,mānasenāgninā kruddhā bhasmasānnaḥ kariṣyati.
english: Indeed, upon hearing of the killing of her sons, and of such a deed (as this) committed by us, she, enraged, will incinerate us with her mental fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 12. Indeed, upon hearing of the killing of her sons, and of such a deed (as this) committed by us, she, enraged, will incinerate us with her mental fire.

sanskrit: कथं दुःखमिदं तीव्रं गान्धारी प्रसहिष्यति ।श्रुत्वा विनिहतं पुत्रं छलेनाजिह्मयोधिनम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ duḥkhamidaṁ tīvraṁ gāndhārī prasahiṣyati ,śrutvā vinihataṁ putraṁ chalenājihmayodhinam.
english: How will Gandhari endure this intense sorrow, having heard that her son, who fought fairly, was slain by deceit?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 13. How will Gandhari endure this intense sorrow, having heard that her son, who fought fairly, was slain by deceit?

sanskrit: एवं विचिन्त्य बहुधा भयशोकसमन्वितः ।वासुदेवमिदं वाक्यं धर्मराजोऽभ्यभाषत ॥१४॥
transliteration: evaṁ vicintya bahudhā bhayaśokasamanvitaḥ ,vāsudevamidaṁ vākyaṁ dharmarājo'bhyabhāṣata.
english: Having pondered thus in many ways, and filled with fear and sorrow, King Yudhishthira (Dharmarāja) addressed these words to Vasudeva (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 14. Having pondered thus in many ways, and filled with fear and sorrow, King Yudhishthira (Dharmarāja) addressed these words to Vasudeva (Krishna).

sanskrit: तव प्रसादाद्गोविन्द राज्यं निहतकण्टकम् ।अप्राप्यं मनसापीह प्राप्तमस्माभिरच्युत ॥१५॥
transliteration: tava prasādādgovinda rājyaṁ nihatakaṇṭakam ,aprāpyaṁ manasāpīha prāptamasmābhiracyuta.
english: O Govinda, O Acyuta, by your grace, this kingdom, free from enemies and unattainable even by thought (manas), has been obtained by us here.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 15. O Govinda, O Acyuta, by your grace, this kingdom, free from enemies and unattainable even by thought (manas), has been obtained by us here.

sanskrit: प्रत्यक्षं मे महाबाहो संग्रामे रोमहर्षणे ।विमर्दः सुमहान्प्राप्तस्त्वया यादवनन्दन ॥१६॥
transliteration: pratyakṣaṁ me mahābāho saṁgrāme romaharṣaṇe ,vimardaḥ sumahānprāptastvayā yādavanandana.
english: O mighty-armed one, O delight of the Yadus, the immense destruction in that hair-raising battle was directly witnessed by me and brought about by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 16. O mighty-armed one, O delight of the Yadus, the immense destruction in that hair-raising battle was directly witnessed by me and brought about by you.

sanskrit: त्वया देवासुरे युद्धे वधार्थममरद्विषाम् ।यथा साह्यं पुरा दत्तं हताश्च विबुधद्विषः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tvayā devāsure yuddhe vadhārthamamaradviṣām ,yathā sāhyaṁ purā dattaṁ hatāśca vibudhadviṣaḥ.
english: Just as formerly, in the war between the gods and asuras, help was given by you for the destruction of the enemies of the immortals, and those enemies of the wise were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 17. Just as formerly, in the war between the gods and asuras, help was given by you for the destruction of the enemies of the immortals, and those enemies of the wise were slain.

sanskrit: साह्यं तथा महाबाहो दत्तमस्माकमच्युत ।सारथ्येन च वार्ष्णेय भवता यद्धृता वयम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sāhyaṁ tathā mahābāho dattamasmākamacyuta ,sārathyena ca vārṣṇeya bhavatā yaddhṛtā vayam.
english: Similarly, O mighty-armed (mahābāho) and infallible one (acyuta), such help has been given to us, O descendant of Vṛṣṇi (vārṣṇeya), because we are sustained by your charioteering.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 18. Similarly, O mighty-armed (mahābāho) and infallible one (acyuta), such help has been given to us, O descendant of Vṛṣṇi (vārṣṇeya), because we are sustained by your charioteering.

sanskrit: यदि न त्वं भवेन्नाथः फल्गुनस्य महारणे ।कथं शक्यो रणे जेतुं भवेदेष बलार्णवः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yadi na tvaṁ bhavennāthaḥ phalgunasya mahāraṇe ,kathaṁ śakyo raṇe jetuṁ bhavedeṣa balārṇavaḥ.
english: If you were not the protector of Arjuna (phālguna) in this great war, how could this ocean of strength (balārṇava) possibly be conquered in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 19. If you were not the protector of Arjuna (phālguna) in this great war, how could this ocean of strength (balārṇava) possibly be conquered in battle?

sanskrit: गदाप्रहारा विपुलाः परिघैश्चापि ताडनम् ।शक्तिभिर्भिण्डिपालैश्च तोमरैः सपरश्वधैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: gadāprahārā vipulāḥ parighaiścāpi tāḍanam ,śaktibhirbhiṇḍipālaiśca tomaraiḥ saparaśvadhaiḥ.
english: There were numerous blows from maces, and also strikes from iron clubs; with spears (śakti), "bhiṇḍipālas" (a type of javelin), and lances, along with axes.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 20. There were numerous blows from maces, and also strikes from iron clubs; with spears (śakti), "bhiṇḍipālas" (a type of javelin), and lances, along with axes.

sanskrit: वाचश्च परुषाः प्राप्तास्त्वया ह्यस्मद्धितैषिणा ।ताश्च ते सफलाः सर्वा हते दुर्योधनेऽच्युत ॥२१॥
transliteration: vācaśca paruṣāḥ prāptāstvayā hyasmaddhitaiṣiṇā ,tāśca te saphalāḥ sarvā hate duryodhane'cyuta.
english: O Acyuta, harsh words were indeed received from you, who wished for our welfare. And all those words of yours will now be successful, with Duryodhana killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 21. O Acyuta, harsh words were indeed received from you, who wished for our welfare. And all those words of yours will now be successful, with Duryodhana killed.

sanskrit: गान्धार्या हि महाबाहो क्रोधं बुध्यस्व माधव ।सा हि नित्यं महाभागा तपसोग्रेण कर्शिता ॥२२॥
transliteration: gāndhāryā hi mahābāho krodhaṁ budhyasva mādhava ,sā hi nityaṁ mahābhāgā tapasogreṇa karśitā.
english: O mighty-armed (Mahabaho) Madhava, indeed, understand Gandhari's anger. For that illustrious lady (Mahabhaga) is constantly emaciated by severe asceticism (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 22. O mighty-armed (Mahabaho) Madhava, indeed, understand Gandhari's anger. For that illustrious lady (Mahabhaga) is constantly emaciated by severe asceticism (tapas).

sanskrit: पुत्रपौत्रवधं श्रुत्वा ध्रुवं नः संप्रधक्ष्यति ।तस्याः प्रसादनं वीर प्राप्तकालं मतं मम ॥२३॥
transliteration: putrapautravadhaṁ śrutvā dhruvaṁ naḥ saṁpradhakṣyati ,tasyāḥ prasādanaṁ vīra prāptakālaṁ mataṁ mama.
english: Having heard of the killing of her sons and grandsons, she will surely completely consume us. O hero, I consider her appeasement to be timely.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 23. Having heard of the killing of her sons and grandsons, she will surely completely consume us. O hero, I consider her appeasement to be timely.

sanskrit: कश्च तां क्रोधदीप्ताक्षीं पुत्रव्यसनकर्शिताम् ।वीक्षितुं पुरुषः शक्तस्त्वामृते पुरुषोत्तम ॥२४॥
transliteration: kaśca tāṁ krodhadīptākṣīṁ putravyasanakarśitām ,vīkṣituṁ puruṣaḥ śaktastvāmṛte puruṣottama.
english: And who among men (puruṣa) would be able to look at her - whose eyes are ablaze with rage and who is emaciated by the sorrow for her sons - except for you, O best among men (puruṣottama)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 24. And who among men (puruṣa) would be able to look at her - whose eyes are ablaze with rage and who is emaciated by the sorrow for her sons - except for you, O best among men (puruṣottama)?

sanskrit: तत्र मे गमनं प्राप्तं रोचते तव माधव ।गान्धार्याः क्रोधदीप्तायाः प्रशमार्थमरिंदम ॥२५॥
transliteration: tatra me gamanaṁ prāptaṁ rocate tava mādhava ,gāndhāryāḥ krodhadīptāyāḥ praśamārthamariṁdama.
english: O Madhava, O subduer of enemies (arimdama), my going there seems appropriate to you, and it pleases me, for the sake of appeasing Gandhari, who is blazing with anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 25. O Madhava, O subduer of enemies (arimdama), my going there seems appropriate to you, and it pleases me, for the sake of appeasing Gandhari, who is blazing with anger.

sanskrit: त्वं हि कर्ता विकर्ता च लोकानां प्रभवाप्ययः ।हेतुकारणसंयुक्तैर्वाक्यैः कालसमीरितैः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi kartā vikartā ca lokānāṁ prabhavāpyayaḥ ,hetukāraṇasaṁyuktairvākyaiḥ kālasamīritaiḥ.
english: Indeed, you are the creator and the destroyer, the origin and dissolution of the worlds, (and you will achieve this purpose) through words filled with sound reasoning and appropriate causes, spoken at the right time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 26. Indeed, you are the creator and the destroyer, the origin and dissolution of the worlds, (and you will achieve this purpose) through words filled with sound reasoning and appropriate causes, spoken at the right time.

sanskrit: क्षिप्रमेव महाप्राज्ञ गान्धारीं शमयिष्यसि ।पितामहश्च भगवान्कृष्णस्तत्र भविष्यति ॥२७॥
transliteration: kṣiprameva mahāprājña gāndhārīṁ śamayiṣyasi ,pitāmahaśca bhagavānkṛṣṇastatra bhaviṣyati.
english: O greatly wise one, you will quickly appease Gandhari. And the venerable Pitamaha (Bhishma) and Lord Krishna will be present there.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 27. O greatly wise one, you will quickly appease Gandhari. And the venerable Pitamaha (Bhishma) and Lord Krishna will be present there.

sanskrit: सर्वथा ते महाबाहो गान्धार्याः क्रोधनाशनम् ।कर्तव्यं सात्वतश्रेष्ठ पाण्डवानां हितैषिणा ॥२८॥
transliteration: sarvathā te mahābāho gāndhāryāḥ krodhanāśanam ,kartavyaṁ sātvataśreṣṭha pāṇḍavānāṁ hitaiṣiṇā.
english: O mighty-armed one, O best among the Satvatas, the appeasement of Gandhari's anger must certainly be accomplished by you, who are a well-wisher of the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 28. O mighty-armed one, O best among the Satvatas, the appeasement of Gandhari's anger must certainly be accomplished by you, who are a well-wisher of the Pandavas.

sanskrit: धर्मराजस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा यदुकुलोद्वहः ।आमन्त्र्य दारुकं प्राह रथः सज्जो विधीयताम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: dharmarājasya vacanaṁ śrutvā yadukulodvahaḥ ,āmantrya dārukaṁ prāha rathaḥ sajjo vidhīyatām.
english: Having heard the words of the king of righteousness (dharma), Krishna, the uplifter of the Yadu dynasty, addressed Dāruka and instructed, "Let the chariot be made ready."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 29. Having heard the words of the king of righteousness (dharma), Krishna, the uplifter of the Yadu dynasty, addressed Dāruka and instructed, "Let the chariot be made ready."

sanskrit: केशवस्य वचः श्रुत्वा त्वरमाणोऽथ दारुकः ।न्यवेदयद्रथं सज्जं केशवाय महात्मने ॥३०॥
transliteration: keśavasya vacaḥ śrutvā tvaramāṇo'tha dārukaḥ ,nyavedayadrathaṁ sajjaṁ keśavāya mahātmane.
english: Having heard Keśava's words, Dāruka, promptly and eagerly, reported the prepared chariot to the great-souled Keśava.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 30. Having heard Keśava's words, Dāruka, promptly and eagerly, reported the prepared chariot to the great-souled Keśava.

sanskrit: तं रथं यादवश्रेष्ठः समारुह्य परंतपः ।जगाम हास्तिनपुरं त्वरितः केशवो विभुः ॥३१॥
transliteration: taṁ rathaṁ yādavaśreṣṭhaḥ samāruhya paraṁtapaḥ ,jagāma hāstinapuraṁ tvaritaḥ keśavo vibhuḥ.
english: Having mounted that chariot, Keśava, the best among the Yadavas, the tormentor of enemies, the mighty lord, swiftly proceeded to Hastinapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 31. Having mounted that chariot, Keśava, the best among the Yadavas, the tormentor of enemies, the mighty lord, swiftly proceeded to Hastinapura.

sanskrit: ततः प्रायान्महाराज माधवो भगवान्रथी ।नागसाह्वयमासाद्य प्रविवेश च वीर्यवान् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tataḥ prāyānmahārāja mādhavo bhagavānrathī ,nāgasāhvayamāsādya praviveśa ca vīryavān.
english: O great king, then the powerful Mādhava, the divine charioteer, departed, and having arrived at the city named Nāga (Hastinapura), he entered.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 32. O great king, then the powerful Mādhava, the divine charioteer, departed, and having arrived at the city named Nāga (Hastinapura), he entered.

sanskrit: प्रविश्य नगरं वीरो रथघोषेण नादयन् ।विदितो धृतराष्ट्रस्य सोऽवतीर्य रथोत्तमात् ॥३३॥
transliteration: praviśya nagaraṁ vīro rathaghoṣeṇa nādayan ,vidito dhṛtarāṣṭrasya so'vatīrya rathottamāt.
english: The hero entered the city, making it resound with the sound of his chariot. Known to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he then descended from his excellent chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 33. The hero entered the city, making it resound with the sound of his chariot. Known to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he then descended from his excellent chariot.

sanskrit: अभ्यगच्छददीनात्मा धृतराष्ट्रनिवेशनम् ।पूर्वं चाभिगतं तत्र सोऽपश्यदृषिसत्तमम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: abhyagacchadadīnātmā dhṛtarāṣṭraniveśanam ,pūrvaṁ cābhigataṁ tatra so'paśyadṛṣisattamam.
english: The noble-hearted (ātman) one then approached Dhṛtarāṣṭra's residence. There he saw the most excellent sage who had arrived before him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 34. The noble-hearted (ātman) one then approached Dhṛtarāṣṭra's residence. There he saw the most excellent sage who had arrived before him.

sanskrit: पादौ प्रपीड्य कृष्णस्य राज्ञश्चापि जनार्दनः ।अभ्यवादयदव्यग्रो गान्धारीं चापि केशवः ॥३५॥
transliteration: pādau prapīḍya kṛṣṇasya rājñaścāpi janārdanaḥ ,abhyavādayadavyagro gāndhārīṁ cāpi keśavaḥ.
english: The composed Janārdana, after pressing the feet of Kṛṣṇa (Vyāsa) and also those of the King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), then Keśava also greeted Gāndhārī.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 35. The composed Janārdana, after pressing the feet of Kṛṣṇa (Vyāsa) and also those of the King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), then Keśava also greeted Gāndhārī.

sanskrit: ततस्तु यादवश्रेष्ठो धृतराष्ट्रमधोक्षजः ।पाणिमालम्ब्य राज्ञः स सस्वरं प्ररुरोद ह ॥३६॥
transliteration: tatastu yādavaśreṣṭho dhṛtarāṣṭramadhokṣajaḥ ,pāṇimālambya rājñaḥ sa sasvaraṁ praruroda ha.
english: Then, that best among the Yadus, Adhokṣaja (Kṛṣṇa), holding the king's (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) hand, indeed wept aloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 36. Then, that best among the Yadus, Adhokṣaja (Kṛṣṇa), holding the king's (Dhṛtarāṣṭra's) hand, indeed wept aloud.

sanskrit: स मुहूर्तमिवोत्सृज्य बाष्पं शोकसमुद्भवम् ।प्रक्षाल्य वारिणा नेत्रे आचम्य च यथाविधि ।उवाच प्रश्रितं वाक्यं धृतराष्ट्रमरिंदमः ॥३७॥
transliteration: sa muhūrtamivotsṛjya bāṣpaṁ śokasamudbhavam ,prakṣālya vāriṇā netre ācamya ca yathāvidhi ,uvāca praśritaṁ vākyaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭramariṁdamaḥ.
english: For a moment, having released the tears born of sorrow, and then having washed his eyes with water and performed the ritual sipping of water (ācamana) as prescribed, the subduer of enemies spoke humble words to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 37. For a moment, having released the tears born of sorrow, and then having washed his eyes with water and performed the ritual sipping of water (ācamana) as prescribed, the subduer of enemies spoke humble words to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: न तेऽस्त्यविदितं किंचिद्भूतभव्यस्य भारत ।कालस्य च यथा वृत्तं तत्ते सुविदितं प्रभो ॥३८॥
transliteration: na te'styaviditaṁ kiṁcidbhūtabhavyasya bhārata ,kālasya ca yathā vṛttaṁ tatte suviditaṁ prabho.
english: O Bhārata, nothing of the past or future is unknown to you. And how the course of time has transpired, that is also very well known to you, O lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 38. O Bhārata, nothing of the past or future is unknown to you. And how the course of time has transpired, that is also very well known to you, O lord.

sanskrit: यदिदं पाण्डवैः सर्वैस्तव चित्तानुरोधिभिः ।कथं कुलक्षयो न स्यात्तथा क्षत्रस्य भारत ॥३९॥
transliteration: yadidaṁ pāṇḍavaiḥ sarvaistava cittānurodhibhiḥ ,kathaṁ kulakṣayo na syāttathā kṣatrasya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, by these Pāṇḍavas, all of whom act in accordance with your wishes, how could there not be the destruction of the family (kula) and thus of the kṣatriya class?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 39. O Bhārata, by these Pāṇḍavas, all of whom act in accordance with your wishes, how could there not be the destruction of the family (kula) and thus of the kṣatriya class?

sanskrit: भ्रातृभिः समयं कृत्वा क्षान्तवान्धर्मवत्सलः ।द्यूतच्छलजितैः शक्तैर्वनवासोऽभ्युपागतः ॥४०॥
transliteration: bhrātṛbhiḥ samayaṁ kṛtvā kṣāntavāndharmavatsalaḥ ,dyūtacchalajitaiḥ śaktairvanavāso'bhyupāgataḥ.
english: The one devoted to natural law (dharma), having made an agreement with his brothers, endured [the injustice]. Though powerful, they (the Pāṇḍavas), having been defeated by the trickery of dice, accepted forest exile.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 40. The one devoted to natural law (dharma), having made an agreement with his brothers, endured [the injustice]. Though powerful, they (the Pāṇḍavas), having been defeated by the trickery of dice, accepted forest exile.

sanskrit: अज्ञातवासचर्या च नानावेशसमावृतैः ।अन्ये च बहवः क्लेशास्त्वशक्तैरिव नित्यदा ॥४१॥
transliteration: ajñātavāsacaryā ca nānāveśasamāvṛtaiḥ ,anye ca bahavaḥ kleśāstvaśaktairiva nityadā.
english: And the practice of living incognito, cloaked in various disguises, along with many other troubles (kleśa), constantly afflicted us as if we were powerless.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 41. And the practice of living incognito, cloaked in various disguises, along with many other troubles (kleśa), constantly afflicted us as if we were powerless.

sanskrit: मया च स्वयमागम्य युद्धकाल उपस्थिते ।सर्वलोकस्य सांनिध्ये ग्रामांस्त्वं पञ्च याचितः ॥४२॥
transliteration: mayā ca svayamāgamya yuddhakāla upasthite ,sarvalokasya sāṁnidhye grāmāṁstvaṁ pañca yācitaḥ.
english: And I, having personally come when the time of war was at hand, requested five villages from you in the presence of all people.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 42. And I, having personally come when the time of war was at hand, requested five villages from you in the presence of all people.

sanskrit: त्वया कालोपसृष्टेन लोभतो नापवर्जिताः ।तवापराधान्नृपते सर्वं क्षत्रं क्षयं गतम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tvayā kālopasṛṣṭena lobhato nāpavarjitāḥ ,tavāparādhānnṛpate sarvaṁ kṣatraṁ kṣayaṁ gatam.
english: Influenced by fate and driven by greed, you did not release (the villages). O king, because of your offense, the entire warrior class (kṣatram) has met its destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 43. Influenced by fate and driven by greed, you did not release (the villages). O king, because of your offense, the entire warrior class (kṣatram) has met its destruction.

sanskrit: भीष्मेण सोमदत्तेन बाह्लिकेन कृपेण च ।द्रोणेन च सपुत्रेण विदुरेण च धीमता ।याचितस्त्वं शमं नित्यं न च तत्कृतवानसि ॥४४॥
transliteration: bhīṣmeṇa somadattena bāhlikena kṛpeṇa ca ,droṇena ca saputreṇa vidureṇa ca dhīmatā ,yācitastvaṁ śamaṁ nityaṁ na ca tatkṛtavānasi.
english: You were constantly entreated for peace by Bhishma, Somadatta, Balhika, Kripa, and Drona with his son, as well as by the intelligent Vidura, yet you did not act upon it.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 44. You were constantly entreated for peace by Bhishma, Somadatta, Balhika, Kripa, and Drona with his son, as well as by the intelligent Vidura, yet you did not act upon it.

sanskrit: कालोपहतचित्तो हि सर्वो मुह्यति भारत ।यथा मूढो भवान्पूर्वमस्मिन्नर्थे समुद्यते ॥४५॥
transliteration: kālopahatacitto hi sarvo muhyati bhārata ,yathā mūḍho bhavānpūrvamasminnarthe samudyate.
english: Indeed, everyone whose mind is afflicted by time becomes deluded, O Bhārata, just as you were bewildered before when this matter arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 45. Indeed, everyone whose mind is afflicted by time becomes deluded, O Bhārata, just as you were bewildered before when this matter arose.

sanskrit: किमन्यत्कालयोगाद्धि दिष्टमेव परायणम् ।मा च दोषं महाराज पाण्डवेषु निवेशय ॥४६॥
transliteration: kimanyatkālayogāddhi diṣṭameva parāyaṇam ,mā ca doṣaṁ mahārāja pāṇḍaveṣu niveśaya.
english: What else can there be but the conjunction of time, for destiny is indeed the supreme resort. And, O great king, do not attribute blame to the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 46. What else can there be but the conjunction of time, for destiny is indeed the supreme resort. And, O great king, do not attribute blame to the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: अल्पोऽप्यतिक्रमो नास्ति पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ।धर्मतो न्यायतश्चैव स्नेहतश्च परंतप ॥४७॥
transliteration: alpo'pyatikramo nāsti pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām ,dharmato nyāyataścaiva snehataśca paraṁtapa.
english: There is not even a slight transgression on the part of the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, O scorcher of foes, whether by virtue of natural law (dharma), justice, or affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 47. There is not even a slight transgression on the part of the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, O scorcher of foes, whether by virtue of natural law (dharma), justice, or affection.

sanskrit: एतत्सर्वं तु विज्ञाय आत्मदोषकृतं फलम् ।असूयां पाण्डुपुत्रेषु न भवान्कर्तुमर्हति ॥४८॥
transliteration: etatsarvaṁ tu vijñāya ātmadoṣakṛtaṁ phalam ,asūyāṁ pāṇḍuputreṣu na bhavānkartumarhati.
english: Therefore, realizing all this to be the consequence of your own faults, you should not harbor malice towards the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 48. Therefore, realizing all this to be the consequence of your own faults, you should not harbor malice towards the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: कुलं वंशश्च पिण्डश्च यच्च पुत्रकृतं फलम् ।गान्धार्यास्तव चैवाद्य पाण्डवेषु प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: kulaṁ vaṁśaśca piṇḍaśca yacca putrakṛtaṁ phalam ,gāndhāryāstava caivādya pāṇḍaveṣu pratiṣṭhitam.
english: Today, your family (kula), your lineage (vaṃśa), and the ancestral offerings (piṇḍa), as well as any benefits gained through sons, are all dependent on the Pandavas; the same applies to Gandhari.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 49. Today, your family (kula), your lineage (vaṃśa), and the ancestral offerings (piṇḍa), as well as any benefits gained through sons, are all dependent on the Pandavas; the same applies to Gandhari.

sanskrit: एतत्सर्वमनुध्यात्वा आत्मनश्च व्यतिक्रमम् ।शिवेन पाण्डवान्ध्याहि नमस्ते भरतर्षभ ॥५०॥
transliteration: etatsarvamanudhyātvā ātmanaśca vyatikramam ,śivena pāṇḍavāndhyāhi namaste bharatarṣabha.
english: Having reflected upon all this, and upon your own transgressions (vyatikrama) against your true self (ātman), regard the Pandavas with benevolence. Salutations to you, O bull among the Bharatas!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 50. Having reflected upon all this, and upon your own transgressions (vyatikrama) against your true self (ātman), regard the Pandavas with benevolence. Salutations to you, O bull among the Bharatas!
