sanskrit: जानासि च महाबाहो धर्मराजस्य या त्वयि ।भक्तिर्भरतशार्दूल स्नेहश्चापि स्वभावतः ॥५१॥
transliteration: jānāsi ca mahābāho dharmarājasya yā tvayi ,bhaktirbharataśārdūla snehaścāpi svabhāvataḥ.
english: And you know, O mighty-armed one, the devotion (bhakti) that Dharmaraja (dharma) holds for you, and also his natural affection, O tiger among the Bharatas!
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 51. And you know, O mighty-armed one, the devotion (bhakti) that Dharmaraja (dharma) holds for you, and also his natural affection, O tiger among the Bharatas!

sanskrit: एतच्च कदनं कृत्वा शत्रूणामपकारिणाम् ।दह्यते स्म दिवारात्रं न च शर्माधिगच्छति ॥५२॥
transliteration: etacca kadanaṁ kṛtvā śatrūṇāmapakāriṇām ,dahyate sma divārātraṁ na ca śarmādhigacchati.
english: And having caused this destruction of the harmful enemies, he was tormented day and night and did not attain peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 52. And having caused this destruction of the harmful enemies, he was tormented day and night and did not attain peace.

sanskrit: त्वां चैव नरशार्दूल गान्धारीं च यशस्विनीम् ।स शोचन्भरतश्रेष्ठ न शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥५३॥
transliteration: tvāṁ caiva naraśārdūla gāndhārīṁ ca yaśasvinīm ,sa śocanbharataśreṣṭha na śāntimadhigacchati.
english: O best among the Bhāratas (bharata), O tiger among men (naraśārdūla), Dhṛtarāṣṭra, grieving for you and also for the glorious Gāndhārī, does not attain peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 53. O best among the Bhāratas (bharata), O tiger among men (naraśārdūla), Dhṛtarāṣṭra, grieving for you and also for the glorious Gāndhārī, does not attain peace.

sanskrit: ह्रिया च परयाविष्टो भवन्तं नाधिगच्छति ।पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्तं बुद्धिव्याकुलितेन्द्रियम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: hriyā ca parayāviṣṭo bhavantaṁ nādhigacchati ,putraśokābhisaṁtaptaṁ buddhivyākulitendriyam.
english: And, overcome by immense shame, with his senses agitated by a bewildered intellect, and intensely afflicted by sorrow for his sons, he (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) cannot approach you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 54. And, overcome by immense shame, with his senses agitated by a bewildered intellect, and intensely afflicted by sorrow for his sons, he (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) cannot approach you.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा महाराज धृतराष्ट्रं यदूत्तमः ।उवाच परमं वाक्यं गान्धारीं शोककर्शिताम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: evamuktvā mahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ yadūttamaḥ ,uvāca paramaṁ vākyaṁ gāndhārīṁ śokakarśitām.
english: O great king (mahārāja), having thus spoken to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Kṛṣṇa, the best of the Yadus (yadūttama), then addressed the sorrow-emaciated Gāndhārī with supreme words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 55. O great king (mahārāja), having thus spoken to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Kṛṣṇa, the best of the Yadus (yadūttama), then addressed the sorrow-emaciated Gāndhārī with supreme words.

sanskrit: सौबलेयि निबोध त्वं यत्त्वां वक्ष्यामि सुव्रते ।त्वत्समा नास्ति लोकेऽस्मिन्नद्य सीमन्तिनी शुभे ॥५६॥
transliteration: saubaleyi nibodha tvaṁ yattvāṁ vakṣyāmi suvrate ,tvatsamā nāsti loke'sminnadya sīmantinī śubhe.
english: O daughter of Subala (saubaleyī), O virtuous one (suvrate), O auspicious lady (śubhe), understand what I will tell you. In this world, there is no woman equal to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 56. O daughter of Subala (saubaleyī), O virtuous one (suvrate), O auspicious lady (śubhe), understand what I will tell you. In this world, there is no woman equal to you.

sanskrit: जानामि च यथा राज्ञि सभायां मम संनिधौ ।धर्मार्थसहितं वाक्यमुभयोः पक्षयोर्हितम् ।उक्तवत्यसि कल्याणि न च ते तनयैः श्रुतम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: jānāmi ca yathā rājñi sabhāyāṁ mama saṁnidhau ,dharmārthasahitaṁ vākyamubhayoḥ pakṣayorhitam ,uktavatyasi kalyāṇi na ca te tanayaiḥ śrutam.
english: O queen, I know how, in the assembly and in my presence, you, O blessed one, spoke words that were beneficial to both sides and aligned with natural law (dharma) and purpose. However, those words were not heeded by your sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 57. O queen, I know how, in the assembly and in my presence, you, O blessed one, spoke words that were beneficial to both sides and aligned with natural law (dharma) and purpose. However, those words were not heeded by your sons.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्त्वया चोक्तो जयार्थी परुषं वचः ।शृणु मूढ वचो मह्यं यतो धर्मस्ततो जयः ॥५८॥
transliteration: duryodhanastvayā cokto jayārthī paruṣaṁ vacaḥ ,śṛṇu mūḍha vaco mahyaṁ yato dharmastato jayaḥ.
english: You also spoke harsh words to Duryodhana, O seeker of victory: "Listen, O fool, to my words: where there is natural law (dharma), there is victory."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 58. You also spoke harsh words to Duryodhana, O seeker of victory: "Listen, O fool, to my words: where there is natural law (dharma), there is victory."

sanskrit: तदिदं समनुप्राप्तं तव वाक्यं नृपात्मजे ।एवं विदित्वा कल्याणि मा स्म शोके मनः कृथाः ।पाण्डवानां विनाशाय मा ते बुद्धिः कदाचन ॥५९॥
transliteration: tadidaṁ samanuprāptaṁ tava vākyaṁ nṛpātmaje ,evaṁ viditvā kalyāṇi mā sma śoke manaḥ kṛthāḥ ,pāṇḍavānāṁ vināśāya mā te buddhiḥ kadācana.
english: O daughter of a king, that very statement of yours has now come to pass. Therefore, knowing this, O blessed one, do not let your mind be steeped in sorrow. And may your intellect (buddhi) never, ever be inclined towards the destruction of the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 59. O daughter of a king, that very statement of yours has now come to pass. Therefore, knowing this, O blessed one, do not let your mind be steeped in sorrow. And may your intellect (buddhi) never, ever be inclined towards the destruction of the Pandavas.

sanskrit: शक्ता चासि महाभागे पृथिवीं सचराचराम् ।चक्षुषा क्रोधदीप्तेन निर्दग्धुं तपसो बलात् ॥६०॥
transliteration: śaktā cāsi mahābhāge pṛthivīṁ sacarācarām ,cakṣuṣā krodhadīptena nirdagdhuṁ tapaso balāt.
english: O greatly fortunate one, you are capable of burning up this entire earth, with all its moving and unmoving beings, through the power of your asceticism (tapas) and with an eye blazing with wrath.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 60. O greatly fortunate one, you are capable of burning up this entire earth, with all its moving and unmoving beings, through the power of your asceticism (tapas) and with an eye blazing with wrath.

sanskrit: वासुदेववचः श्रुत्वा गान्धारी वाक्यमब्रवीत् ।एवमेतन्महाबाहो यथा वदसि केशव ॥६१॥
transliteration: vāsudevavacaḥ śrutvā gāndhārī vākyamabravīt ,evametanmahābāho yathā vadasi keśava.
english: Having heard the words of Vasudeva, Gandhari spoke a statement: "It is indeed so, O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O Keshava, as you say."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 61. Having heard the words of Vasudeva, Gandhari spoke a statement: "It is indeed so, O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O Keshava, as you say."

sanskrit: आधिभिर्दह्यमानाया मतिः संचलिता मम ।सा मे व्यवस्थिता श्रुत्वा तव वाक्यं जनार्दन ॥६२॥
transliteration: ādhibhirdahyamānāyā matiḥ saṁcalitā mama ,sā me vyavasthitā śrutvā tava vākyaṁ janārdana.
english: My mind (mati) was agitated, being tormented by mental anguish (ādhibhiḥ). But having heard your words, O Janardana, that mind of mine has now become steady.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 62. My mind (mati) was agitated, being tormented by mental anguish (ādhibhiḥ). But having heard your words, O Janardana, that mind of mine has now become steady.

sanskrit: राज्ञस्त्वन्धस्य वृद्धस्य हतपुत्रस्य केशव ।त्वं गतिः सह तैर्वीरैः पाण्डवैर्द्विपदां वर ॥६३॥
transliteration: rājñastvandhasya vṛddhasya hataputrasya keśava ,tvaṁ gatiḥ saha tairvīraiḥ pāṇḍavairdvipadāṁ vara.
english: But for the blind, aged king (rājñaḥ) whose sons have been slain, O Keshava, you are the refuge (gati), along with those brave Pāṇḍavas, O best among men (dvipadām vara).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 63. But for the blind, aged king (rājñaḥ) whose sons have been slain, O Keshava, you are the refuge (gati), along with those brave Pāṇḍavas, O best among men (dvipadām vara).

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं मुखं प्रच्छाद्य वाससा ।पुत्रशोकाभिसंतप्ता गान्धारी प्ररुरोद ह ॥६४॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā vacanaṁ mukhaṁ pracchādya vāsasā ,putraśokābhisaṁtaptā gāndhārī praruroda ha.
english: Having spoken these words, Gandhari, tormented by the grief for her sons, covered her face with a cloth and indeed wept.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 64. Having spoken these words, Gandhari, tormented by the grief for her sons, covered her face with a cloth and indeed wept.

sanskrit: तत एनां महाबाहुः केशवः शोककर्शिताम् ।हेतुकारणसंयुक्तैर्वाक्यैराश्वासयत्प्रभुः ॥६५॥
transliteration: tata enāṁ mahābāhuḥ keśavaḥ śokakarśitām ,hetukāraṇasaṁyuktairvākyairāśvāsayatprabhuḥ.
english: Then, the mighty-armed Lord Keśava consoled her, who was emaciated by grief, with logical and reasoned words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 65. Then, the mighty-armed Lord Keśava consoled her, who was emaciated by grief, with logical and reasoned words.

sanskrit: समाश्वास्य च गान्धारीं धृतराष्ट्रं च माधवः ।द्रौणेः संकल्पितं भावमन्वबुध्यत केशवः ॥६६॥
transliteration: samāśvāsya ca gāndhārīṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca mādhavaḥ ,drauṇeḥ saṁkalpitaṁ bhāvamanvabudhyata keśavaḥ.
english: And having consoled Gāndhārī and Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Mādhava (Keśava) then perceived the intention conceived by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 66. And having consoled Gāndhārī and Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Mādhava (Keśava) then perceived the intention conceived by Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā).

sanskrit: ततस्त्वरित उत्थाय पादौ मूर्ध्ना प्रणम्य च ।द्वैपायनस्य राजेन्द्र ततः कौरवमब्रवीत् ॥६७॥
transliteration: tatastvarita utthāya pādau mūrdhnā praṇamya ca ,dvaipāyanasya rājendra tataḥ kauravamabravīt.
english: Then, O king, having quickly risen and bowed his head at Dvīpāyana's feet, he then spoke to the Kaurava (Dhṛtarāṣṭra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 67. Then, O king, having quickly risen and bowed his head at Dvīpāyana's feet, he then spoke to the Kaurava (Dhṛtarāṣṭra).

sanskrit: आपृच्छे त्वां कुरुश्रेष्ठ मा च शोके मनः कृथाः ।द्रौणेः पापोऽस्त्यभिप्रायस्तेनास्मि सहसोत्थितः ।पाण्डवानां वधे रात्रौ बुद्धिस्तेन प्रदर्शिता ॥६८॥
transliteration: āpṛcche tvāṁ kuruśreṣṭha mā ca śoke manaḥ kṛthāḥ ,drauṇeḥ pāpo'styabhiprāyastenāsmi sahasotthitaḥ ,pāṇḍavānāṁ vadhe rātrau buddhistena pradarśitā.
english: O best of Kurus, I bid you farewell. Do not let your mind succumb to grief. Droṇa's son has a wicked intention, and that is why I have risen so abruptly. A plan for the nocturnal slaughter of the Pāṇḍavas has been revealed by him.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 68. O best of Kurus, I bid you farewell. Do not let your mind succumb to grief. Droṇa's son has a wicked intention, and that is why I have risen so abruptly. A plan for the nocturnal slaughter of the Pāṇḍavas has been revealed by him.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु वचनं गान्धार्या सहितोऽब्रवीत् ।धृतराष्ट्रो महाबाहुः केशवं केशिसूदनम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu vacanaṁ gāndhāryā sahito'bravīt ,dhṛtarāṣṭro mahābāhuḥ keśavaṁ keśisūdanam.
english: Having heard this speech, the mighty-armed Dhritarashtra, accompanied by Gandhari, spoke to Keshava, the slayer of Keshi.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 69. Having heard this speech, the mighty-armed Dhritarashtra, accompanied by Gandhari, spoke to Keshava, the slayer of Keshi.

sanskrit: शीघ्रं गच्छ महाबाहो पाण्डवान्परिपालय ।भूयस्त्वया समेष्यामि क्षिप्रमेव जनार्दन ।प्रायात्ततस्तु त्वरितो दारुकेण सहाच्युतः ॥७०॥
transliteration: śīghraṁ gaccha mahābāho pāṇḍavānparipālaya ,bhūyastvayā sameṣyāmi kṣiprameva janārdana ,prāyāttatastu tvarito dārukeṇa sahācyutaḥ.
english: (Dhritarashtra said:) 'Go quickly, O mighty-armed (mahābāho) Janardana, and protect the Pandavas. I shall meet with you again very soon.' Then Acyuta (Krishna), swiftly, with Daruka, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 70. (Dhritarashtra said:) 'Go quickly, O mighty-armed (mahābāho) Janardana, and protect the Pandavas. I shall meet with you again very soon.' Then Acyuta (Krishna), swiftly, with Daruka, departed.

sanskrit: वासुदेवे गते राजन्धृतराष्ट्रं जनेश्वरम् ।आश्वासयदमेयात्मा व्यासो लोकनमस्कृतः ॥७१॥
transliteration: vāsudeve gate rājandhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janeśvaram ,āśvāsayadameyātmā vyāso lokanamaskṛtaḥ.
english: O King, when Vasudeva (Krishna) had departed, Vyasa, whose soul was immeasurable (ameyātmā) and who was revered by the world, comforted Dhritarashtra, the lord of the people (janeśvaram).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 71. O King, when Vasudeva (Krishna) had departed, Vyasa, whose soul was immeasurable (ameyātmā) and who was revered by the world, comforted Dhritarashtra, the lord of the people (janeśvaram).

sanskrit: वासुदेवोऽपि धर्मात्मा कृतकृत्यो जगाम ह ।शिबिरं हास्तिनपुराद्दिदृक्षुः पाण्डवान्नृप ॥७२॥
transliteration: vāsudevo'pi dharmātmā kṛtakṛtyo jagāma ha ,śibiraṁ hāstinapurāddidṛkṣuḥ pāṇḍavānnṛpa.
english: O King, the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) Vasudeva (Krishna), having accomplished his task, indeed departed from Hastinapura to the camp, desiring to see the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 72. O King, the righteous-souled (dharmātmā) Vasudeva (Krishna), having accomplished his task, indeed departed from Hastinapura to the camp, desiring to see the Pandavas.

sanskrit: आगम्य शिबिरं रात्रौ सोऽभ्यगच्छत पाण्डवान् ।तच्च तेभ्यः समाख्याय सहितस्तैः समाविशत् ॥७३॥
transliteration: āgamya śibiraṁ rātrau so'bhyagacchata pāṇḍavān ,tacca tebhyaḥ samākhyāya sahitastaiḥ samāviśat.
english: He arrived at the camp during the night, approached the Pāṇḍavas, and after narrating that incident to them, he joined them in sitting down.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 62 Verse 73. He arrived at the camp during the night, approached the Pāṇḍavas, and after narrating that incident to them, he joined them in sitting down.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अधिष्ठितः पदा मूर्ध्नि भग्नसक्थो महीं गतः ।शौटीरमानी पुत्रो मे कान्यभाषत संजय ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,adhiṣṭhitaḥ padā mūrdhni bhagnasaktho mahīṁ gataḥ ,śauṭīramānī putro me kānyabhāṣata saṁjaya.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Saṃjaya, to whom did my arrogant son, whose head had been trampled by a foot and whose thighs were broken, fallen to the ground, speak?'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 1. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Saṃjaya, to whom did my arrogant son, whose head had been trampled by a foot and whose thighs were broken, fallen to the ground, speak?'

sanskrit: अत्यर्थं कोपनो राजा जातवैरश्च पाण्डुषु ।व्यसनं परमं प्राप्तः किमाह परमाहवे ॥२॥
transliteration: atyarthaṁ kopano rājā jātavairaśca pāṇḍuṣu ,vyasanaṁ paramaṁ prāptaḥ kimāha paramāhave.
english: What did the king, who was exceedingly wrathful and held deep animosity towards the Pāṇḍavas, and who had suffered the ultimate calamity, say during the great war?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 2. What did the king, who was exceedingly wrathful and held deep animosity towards the Pāṇḍavas, and who had suffered the ultimate calamity, say during the great war?

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।शृणु राजन्प्रवक्ष्यामि यथावृत्तं नराधिप ।राज्ञा यदुक्तं भग्नेन तस्मिन्व्यसन आगते ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,śṛṇu rājanpravakṣyāmi yathāvṛttaṁ narādhipa ,rājñā yaduktaṁ bhagnena tasminvyasana āgate.
english: Saṃjaya said: 'O King, O Lord of Men, listen! I will recount exactly what was said by the defeated king when that great calamity had occurred.'
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 3. Saṃjaya said: 'O King, O Lord of Men, listen! I will recount exactly what was said by the defeated king when that great calamity had occurred.'

sanskrit: भग्नसक्थो नृपो राजन्पांसुना सोऽवगुण्ठितः ।यमयन्मूर्धजांस्तत्र वीक्ष्य चैव दिशो दश ॥४॥
transliteration: bhagnasaktho nṛpo rājanpāṁsunā so'vaguṇṭhitaḥ ,yamayanmūrdhajāṁstatra vīkṣya caiva diśo daśa.
english: O King, the king (Duryodhana), with his thigh broken and covered in dust, was there, arranging his hair and looking intently in all ten directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 4. O King, the king (Duryodhana), with his thigh broken and covered in dust, was there, arranging his hair and looking intently in all ten directions.

sanskrit: केशान्नियम्य यत्नेन निःश्वसन्नुरगो यथा ।संरम्भाश्रुपरीताभ्यां नेत्राभ्यामभिवीक्ष्य माम् ॥५॥
transliteration: keśānniyamya yatnena niḥśvasannurago yathā ,saṁrambhāśruparītābhyāṁ netrābhyāmabhivīkṣya mām.
english: Arranging his hair with effort, sighing like a snake, he looked at me with his eyes filled with tears of rage.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 5. Arranging his hair with effort, sighing like a snake, he looked at me with his eyes filled with tears of rage.

sanskrit: बाहू धरण्यां निष्पिष्य मुहुर्मत्त इव द्विपः ।प्रकीर्णान्मूर्धजान्धुन्वन्दन्तैर्दन्तानुपस्पृशन् ।गर्हयन्पाण्डवं ज्येष्ठं निःश्वस्येदमथाब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: bāhū dharaṇyāṁ niṣpiṣya muhurmatta iva dvipaḥ ,prakīrṇānmūrdhajāndhunvandantairdantānupaspṛśan ,garhayanpāṇḍavaṁ jyeṣṭhaṁ niḥśvasyedamathābravīt.
english: Pressing his two arms repeatedly on the earth like an enraged elephant, shaking his disheveled hair, and grinding his teeth, he censured the eldest Pāṇḍava; then, sighing, he spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 6. Pressing his two arms repeatedly on the earth like an enraged elephant, shaking his disheveled hair, and grinding his teeth, he censured the eldest Pāṇḍava; then, sighing, he spoke these words.

sanskrit: भीष्मे शांतनवे नाथे कर्णे चास्त्रभृतां वरे ।गौतमे शकुनौ चापि द्रोणे चास्त्रभृतां वरे ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīṣme śāṁtanave nāthe karṇe cāstrabhṛtāṁ vare ,gautame śakunau cāpi droṇe cāstrabhṛtāṁ vare.
english: When Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, was our protector; when Karṇa, the foremost among weapon-bearers; when Gautama (Kṛpa), Śakuni, and Droṇa, also the foremost among weapon-bearers, (were on our side)...
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 7. When Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, was our protector; when Karṇa, the foremost among weapon-bearers; when Gautama (Kṛpa), Śakuni, and Droṇa, also the foremost among weapon-bearers, (were on our side)...

sanskrit: अश्वत्थाम्नि तथा शल्ये शूरे च कृतवर्मणि ।इमामवस्थां प्राप्तोऽस्मि कालो हि दुरतिक्रमः ॥८॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmni tathā śalye śūre ca kṛtavarmaṇi ,imāmavasthāṁ prāpto'smi kālo hi duratikramaḥ.
english: Even with Ashwatthama, Shalya, and the brave Kritavarma, I have reached this state. Indeed, time (kāla) is insurmountable.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 8. Even with Ashwatthama, Shalya, and the brave Kritavarma, I have reached this state. Indeed, time (kāla) is insurmountable.

sanskrit: एकादशचमूभर्ता सोऽहमेतां दशां गतः ।कालं प्राप्य महाबाहो न कश्चिदतिवर्तते ॥९॥
transliteration: ekādaśacamūbhartā so'hametāṁ daśāṁ gataḥ ,kālaṁ prāpya mahābāho na kaścidativartate.
english: I, who was the lord of eleven armies, have come to this condition. O mighty-armed one, no one can surpass time (kāla) once it has arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 9. I, who was the lord of eleven armies, have come to this condition. O mighty-armed one, no one can surpass time (kāla) once it has arrived.

sanskrit: आख्यातव्यं मदीयानां येऽस्मिञ्जीवन्ति संगरे ।यथाहं भीमसेनेन व्युत्क्रम्य समयं हतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ākhyātavyaṁ madīyānāṁ ye'smiñjīvanti saṁgare ,yathāhaṁ bhīmasenena vyutkramya samayaṁ hataḥ.
english: It must be narrated to my men who survive this battle how I was killed by Bhimasena, who violated the established rule.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 10. It must be narrated to my men who survive this battle how I was killed by Bhimasena, who violated the established rule.

sanskrit: बहूनि सुनृशंसानि कृतानि खलु पाण्डवैः ।भूरिश्रवसि कर्णे च भीष्मे द्रोणे च श्रीमति ॥११॥
transliteration: bahūni sunṛśaṁsāni kṛtāni khalu pāṇḍavaiḥ ,bhūriśravasi karṇe ca bhīṣme droṇe ca śrīmati.
english: Indeed, many extremely cruel deeds were committed by the Pandavas, especially against Bhurishravas, Karna, Bhishma, and the glorious Drona.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 11. Indeed, many extremely cruel deeds were committed by the Pandavas, especially against Bhurishravas, Karna, Bhishma, and the glorious Drona.

sanskrit: इदं चाकीर्तिजं कर्म नृशंसैः पाण्डवैः कृतम् ।येन ते सत्सु निर्वेदं गमिष्यन्तीति मे मतिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: idaṁ cākīrtijaṁ karma nṛśaṁsaiḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ kṛtam ,yena te satsu nirvedaṁ gamiṣyantīti me matiḥ.
english: And this deed, born of infamy, was done by the cruel Pāṇḍavas, by which they will certainly experience disgrace (nirveda) among good people; this is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 12. And this deed, born of infamy, was done by the cruel Pāṇḍavas, by which they will certainly experience disgrace (nirveda) among good people; this is my opinion.

sanskrit: का प्रीतिः सत्त्वयुक्तस्य कृत्वोपधिकृतं जयम् ।को वा समयभेत्तारं बुधः संमन्तुमर्हति ॥१३॥
transliteration: kā prītiḥ sattvayuktasya kṛtvopadhikṛtaṁ jayam ,ko vā samayabhettāraṁ budhaḥ saṁmantumarhati.
english: What satisfaction can there be for one endowed with integrity (sattva) who achieves a victory gained by deceit? Or what wise person would approve of a breaker of pledges?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 13. What satisfaction can there be for one endowed with integrity (sattva) who achieves a victory gained by deceit? Or what wise person would approve of a breaker of pledges?

sanskrit: अधर्मेण जयं लब्ध्वा को नु हृष्येत पण्डितः ।यथा संहृष्यते पापः पाण्डुपुत्रो वृकोदरः ॥१४॥
transliteration: adharmeṇa jayaṁ labdhvā ko nu hṛṣyeta paṇḍitaḥ ,yathā saṁhṛṣyate pāpaḥ pāṇḍuputro vṛkodaraḥ.
english: Having gained victory through unrighteousness (adharma), what wise person would truly rejoice? Only a wicked one like the son of Pāṇḍu, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), would greatly rejoice.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 14. Having gained victory through unrighteousness (adharma), what wise person would truly rejoice? Only a wicked one like the son of Pāṇḍu, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), would greatly rejoice.

sanskrit: किं नु चित्रमतस्त्वद्य भग्नसक्थस्य यन्मम ।क्रुद्धेन भीमसेनेन पादेन मृदितं शिरः ॥१५॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu citramatastvadya bhagnasakthasya yanmama ,kruddhena bhīmasenena pādena mṛditaṁ śiraḥ.
english: What, then, is surprising about this, that today my head, belonging to me with broken thighs, was crushed by the foot of the enraged Bhīmasena?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 15. What, then, is surprising about this, that today my head, belonging to me with broken thighs, was crushed by the foot of the enraged Bhīmasena?

sanskrit: प्रतपन्तं श्रिया जुष्टं वर्तमानं च बन्धुषु ।एवं कुर्यान्नरो यो हि स वै संजय पूजितः ॥१६॥
transliteration: pratapantaṁ śriyā juṣṭaṁ vartamānaṁ ca bandhuṣu ,evaṁ kuryānnaro yo hi sa vai saṁjaya pūjitaḥ.
english: Indeed, the man who is powerful, endowed with glory, and behaves appropriately among his relatives - he, O Sanjaya, is truly honored.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 16. Indeed, the man who is powerful, endowed with glory, and behaves appropriately among his relatives - he, O Sanjaya, is truly honored.

sanskrit: अभिज्ञौ क्षत्रधर्मस्य मम माता पिता च मे ।तौ हि संजय दुःखार्तौ विज्ञाप्यौ वचनान्मम ॥१७॥
transliteration: abhijñau kṣatradharmasya mama mātā pitā ca me ,tau hi saṁjaya duḥkhārtau vijñāpyau vacanānmama.
english: My mother and my father are indeed knowledgeable about the constitutional principles (dharma) of the warrior class. Therefore, O Sanjaya, those two, distressed by sorrow, must be informed of my words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 17. My mother and my father are indeed knowledgeable about the constitutional principles (dharma) of the warrior class. Therefore, O Sanjaya, those two, distressed by sorrow, must be informed of my words.

sanskrit: इष्टं भृत्या भृताः सम्यग्भूः प्रशास्ता ससागरा ।मूर्ध्नि स्थितममित्राणां जीवतामेव संजय ॥१८॥
transliteration: iṣṭaṁ bhṛtyā bhṛtāḥ samyagbhūḥ praśāstā sasāgarā ,mūrdhni sthitamamitrāṇāṁ jīvatāmeva saṁjaya.
english: My desires have been fulfilled, my servants properly maintained, and the entire earth, including its oceans, has been well-governed. Indeed, O Sanjaya, [my authority is] established upon the heads of my living enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 18. My desires have been fulfilled, my servants properly maintained, and the entire earth, including its oceans, has been well-governed. Indeed, O Sanjaya, [my authority is] established upon the heads of my living enemies.

sanskrit: दत्ता दाया यथाशक्ति मित्राणां च प्रियं कृतम् ।अमित्रा बाधिताः सर्वे को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥१९॥
transliteration: dattā dāyā yathāśakti mitrāṇāṁ ca priyaṁ kṛtam ,amitrā bādhitāḥ sarve ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: Inheritances have been distributed according to ability, and kindness has been shown to friends. All enemies have been thwarted. Who, indeed, is more fortunate than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 19. Inheritances have been distributed according to ability, and kindness has been shown to friends. All enemies have been thwarted. Who, indeed, is more fortunate than I?

sanskrit: यातानि परराष्ट्राणि नृपा भुक्ताश्च दासवत् ।प्रियेभ्यः प्रकृतं साधु को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥२०॥
transliteration: yātāni pararāṣṭrāṇi nṛpā bhuktāśca dāsavat ,priyebhyaḥ prakṛtaṁ sādhu ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: Foreign countries have been traversed; kings have been brought under dominion as if they were servants; good things have been done for dear ones. Who, indeed, is happier than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 20. Foreign countries have been traversed; kings have been brought under dominion as if they were servants; good things have been done for dear ones. Who, indeed, is happier than I?

sanskrit: मानिता बान्धवाः सर्वे मान्यः संपूजितो जनः ।त्रितयं सेवितं सर्वं को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥२१॥
transliteration: mānitā bāndhavāḥ sarve mānyaḥ saṁpūjito janaḥ ,tritayaṁ sevitaṁ sarvaṁ ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: All relatives have been honored; venerable people have been revered; and all three (moral duty (dharma), prosperity, and pleasure) have been served. Who, indeed, is happier than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 21. All relatives have been honored; venerable people have been revered; and all three (moral duty (dharma), prosperity, and pleasure) have been served. Who, indeed, is happier than I?

sanskrit: आज्ञप्तं नृपमुख्येषु मानः प्राप्तः सुदुर्लभः ।आजानेयैस्तथा यातं को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥२२॥
transliteration: ājñaptaṁ nṛpamukhyeṣu mānaḥ prāptaḥ sudurlabhaḥ ,ājāneyaistathā yātaṁ ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: Commands have been issued to principal kings; honor (māna), which is exceedingly difficult to obtain, has been acquired; and noble horses have been ridden. Who, indeed, is happier than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 22. Commands have been issued to principal kings; honor (māna), which is exceedingly difficult to obtain, has been acquired; and noble horses have been ridden. Who, indeed, is happier than I?

sanskrit: अधीतं विधिवद्दत्तं प्राप्तमायुर्निरामयम् ।स्वधर्मेण जिता लोकाः को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥२३॥
transliteration: adhītaṁ vidhivaddattaṁ prāptamāyurnirāmayam ,svadharmeṇa jitā lokāḥ ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: Knowledge has been acquired methodically, and gifts have been given; a life free from illness has been attained; and worlds have been conquered through adherence to one's own intrinsic nature (dharma). Who, indeed, is happier than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 23. Knowledge has been acquired methodically, and gifts have been given; a life free from illness has been attained; and worlds have been conquered through adherence to one's own intrinsic nature (dharma). Who, indeed, is happier than I?

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या नाहं जितः संख्ये परान्प्रेष्यवदाश्रितः ।दिष्ट्या मे विपुला लक्ष्मीर्मृते त्वन्यं गता विभो ॥२४॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā nāhaṁ jitaḥ saṁkhye parānpreṣyavadāśritaḥ ,diṣṭyā me vipulā lakṣmīrmṛte tvanyaṁ gatā vibho.
english: Fortunately, I was not defeated in battle and forced to live as a servant dependent on others. Fortunately, upon my death, my vast fortune did not pass to another, O lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 24. Fortunately, I was not defeated in battle and forced to live as a servant dependent on others. Fortunately, upon my death, my vast fortune did not pass to another, O lord.

sanskrit: यदिष्टं क्षत्रबन्धूनां स्वधर्ममनुतिष्ठताम् ।निधनं तन्मया प्राप्तं को नु स्वन्ततरो मया ॥२५॥
transliteration: yadiṣṭaṁ kṣatrabandhūnāṁ svadharmamanutiṣṭhatām ,nidhanaṁ tanmayā prāptaṁ ko nu svantataro mayā.
english: That death which is desired by kṣatriya kinsmen upholding their own constitution (dharma) - that death has been attained by me. Who, indeed, is more fortunate than I?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 25. That death which is desired by kṣatriya kinsmen upholding their own constitution (dharma) - that death has been attained by me. Who, indeed, is more fortunate than I?

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या नाहं परावृत्तो वैरात्प्राकृतवज्जितः ।दिष्ट्या न विमतिं कांचिद्भजित्वा तु पराजितः ॥२६॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā nāhaṁ parāvṛtto vairātprākṛtavajjitaḥ ,diṣṭyā na vimatiṁ kāṁcidbhajitvā tu parājitaḥ.
english: Fortunately, I was not defeated and forced to retreat from the conflict like an ordinary person. Fortunately, I was not utterly vanquished by embracing any wrong notion or internal disagreement.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 26. Fortunately, I was not defeated and forced to retreat from the conflict like an ordinary person. Fortunately, I was not utterly vanquished by embracing any wrong notion or internal disagreement.

sanskrit: सुप्तं वाथ प्रमत्तं वा यथा हन्याद्विषेण वा ।एवं व्युत्क्रान्तधर्मेण व्युत्क्रम्य समयं हतः ॥२७॥
transliteration: suptaṁ vātha pramattaṁ vā yathā hanyādviṣeṇa vā ,evaṁ vyutkrāntadharmeṇa vyutkramya samayaṁ hataḥ.
english: Just as one might kill someone who is sleeping, or careless, or with poison, so too have I been killed by one who transgressed the natural law (dharma), having violated the agreement.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 27. Just as one might kill someone who is sleeping, or careless, or with poison, so too have I been killed by one who transgressed the natural law (dharma), having violated the agreement.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा महाभागः कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ।कृपः शारद्वतश्चैव वक्तव्या वचनान्मम ॥२८॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā mahābhāgaḥ kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ ,kṛpaḥ śāradvataścaiva vaktavyā vacanānmama.
english: Aśvatthāmā, the greatly fortunate one, and Kṛtavarmā, the Sātvata, as well as Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat – these should be addressed with my words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 28. Aśvatthāmā, the greatly fortunate one, and Kṛtavarmā, the Sātvata, as well as Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvat – these should be addressed with my words.

sanskrit: अधर्मेण प्रवृत्तानां पाण्डवानामनेकशः ।विश्वासं समयघ्नानां न यूयं गन्तुमर्हथ ॥२९॥
transliteration: adharmeṇa pravṛttānāṁ pāṇḍavānāmanekaśaḥ ,viśvāsaṁ samayaghnānāṁ na yūyaṁ gantumarhatha.
english: You all should not place your trust (viśvāsa) in the Pāṇḍavas, who are repeatedly engaged in unrighteousness (adharma) and are breakers of their vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 29. You all should not place your trust (viśvāsa) in the Pāṇḍavas, who are repeatedly engaged in unrighteousness (adharma) and are breakers of their vows.

sanskrit: वातिकांश्चाब्रवीद्राजा पुत्रस्ते सत्यविक्रमः ।अधर्माद्भीमसेनेन निहतोऽहं यथा रणे ॥३०॥
transliteration: vātikāṁścābravīdrājā putraste satyavikramaḥ ,adharmādbhīmasenena nihato'haṁ yathā raṇe.
english: Your son, the king of true valor, spoke to the Vātikas and others, saying, "I was slain in battle by Bhīmasena through unrighteousness (adharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 30. Your son, the king of true valor, spoke to the Vātikas and others, saying, "I was slain in battle by Bhīmasena through unrighteousness (adharma)."

sanskrit: सोऽहं द्रोणं स्वर्गगतं शल्यकर्णावुभौ तथा ।वृषसेनं महावीर्यं शकुनिं चापि सौबलम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: so'haṁ droṇaṁ svargagataṁ śalyakarṇāvubhau tathā ,vṛṣasenaṁ mahāvīryaṁ śakuniṁ cāpi saubalam.
english: I, that very one, (am now with) Droṇa, who has gone to heaven; and also both Śalya and Karṇa; and the greatly valorous Vṛṣasena; and also Śakuni, the son of Subala.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 31. I, that very one, (am now with) Droṇa, who has gone to heaven; and also both Śalya and Karṇa; and the greatly valorous Vṛṣasena; and also Śakuni, the son of Subala.

sanskrit: जलसंधं महावीर्यं भगदत्तं च पार्थिवम् ।सौमदत्तिं महेष्वासं सैन्धवं च जयद्रथम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: jalasaṁdhaṁ mahāvīryaṁ bhagadattaṁ ca pārthivam ,saumadattiṁ maheṣvāsaṁ saindhavaṁ ca jayadratham.
english: Jalasandha, the highly powerful, and King Bhagadatta; Saumadatti, the great archer, and Jayadratha, the king of Sindhu.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 32. Jalasandha, the highly powerful, and King Bhagadatta; Saumadatti, the great archer, and Jayadratha, the king of Sindhu.

sanskrit: दुःशासनपुरोगांश्च भ्रातॄनात्मसमांस्तथा ।दौःशासनिं च विक्रान्तं लक्ष्मणं चात्मजावुभौ ॥३३॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanapurogāṁśca bhrātṝnātmasamāṁstathā ,dauḥśāsaniṁ ca vikrāntaṁ lakṣmaṇaṁ cātmajāvubhau.
english: And likewise, my brothers led by Duḥśāsana, who are equal to myself; and both my sons, the valiant Dauḥśāsani and Lakṣmaṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 33. And likewise, my brothers led by Duḥśāsana, who are equal to myself; and both my sons, the valiant Dauḥśāsani and Lakṣmaṇa.

sanskrit: एतांश्चान्यांश्च सुबहून्मदीयांश्च सहस्रशः ।पृष्ठतोऽनुगमिष्यामि सार्थहीन इवाध्वगः ॥३४॥
transliteration: etāṁścānyāṁśca subahūnmadīyāṁśca sahasraśaḥ ,pṛṣṭhato'nugamiṣyāmi sārthahīna ivādhvagaḥ.
english: And these, and many thousands of my other men, I shall follow from behind, like a traveler who is without companions.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 34. And these, and many thousands of my other men, I shall follow from behind, like a traveler who is without companions.

sanskrit: कथं भ्रातॄन्हताञ्श्रुत्वा भर्तारं च स्वसा मम ।रोरूयमाणा दुःखार्ता दुःशला सा भविष्यति ॥३५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ bhrātṝnhatāñśrutvā bhartāraṁ ca svasā mama ,rorūyamāṇā duḥkhārtā duḥśalā sā bhaviṣyati.
english: How will my sister Duḥśalā be, grief-stricken and crying loudly, after hearing that her brothers and her husband have been slain?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 35. How will my sister Duḥśalā be, grief-stricken and crying loudly, after hearing that her brothers and her husband have been slain?

sanskrit: स्नुषाभिः प्रस्नुषाभिश्च वृद्धो राजा पिता मम ।गान्धारीसहितः क्रोशन्कां गतिं प्रतिपत्स्यते ॥३६॥
transliteration: snuṣābhiḥ prasnuṣābhiśca vṛddho rājā pitā mama ,gāndhārīsahitaḥ krośankāṁ gatiṁ pratipatsyate.
english: What state will my aged father, the king, attain, accompanied by Gāndhārī and lamenting with his daughters-in-law and granddaughters-in-law?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 36. What state will my aged father, the king, attain, accompanied by Gāndhārī and lamenting with his daughters-in-law and granddaughters-in-law?

sanskrit: नूनं लक्ष्मणमातापि हतपुत्रा हतेश्वरा ।विनाशं यास्यति क्षिप्रं कल्याणी पृथुलोचना ॥३७॥
transliteration: nūnaṁ lakṣmaṇamātāpi hataputrā hateśvarā ,vināśaṁ yāsyati kṣipraṁ kalyāṇī pṛthulocanā.
english: Surely, Lakṣmaṇa's mother, the blessed, broad-eyed woman, having lost her son and husband, will swiftly meet her destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 37. Surely, Lakṣmaṇa's mother, the blessed, broad-eyed woman, having lost her son and husband, will swiftly meet her destruction.

sanskrit: यदि जानाति चार्वाकः परिव्राड्वाग्विशारदः ।करिष्यति महाभागो ध्रुवं सोऽपचितिं मम ॥३८॥
transliteration: yadi jānāti cārvākaḥ parivrāḍvāgviśāradaḥ ,kariṣyati mahābhāgo dhruvaṁ so'pacitiṁ mama.
english: If Cārvāka, the eloquent wandering ascetic, knows this, that highly esteemed person will certainly carry out retribution for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 38. If Cārvāka, the eloquent wandering ascetic, knows this, that highly esteemed person will certainly carry out retribution for me.

sanskrit: समन्तपञ्चके पुण्ये त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुते ।अहं निधनमासाद्य लोकान्प्राप्स्यामि शाश्वतान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: samantapañcake puṇye triṣu lokeṣu viśrute ,ahaṁ nidhanamāsādya lokānprāpsyāmi śāśvatān.
english: Having met my death in the sacred Samantapañcaka, which is renowned in the three worlds, I will attain eternal realms.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 39. Having met my death in the sacred Samantapañcaka, which is renowned in the three worlds, I will attain eternal realms.

sanskrit: ततो जनसहस्राणि बाष्पपूर्णानि मारिष ।प्रलापं नृपतेः श्रुत्वा विद्रवन्ति दिशो दश ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato janasahasrāṇi bāṣpapūrṇāni māriṣa ,pralāpaṁ nṛpateḥ śrutvā vidravanti diśo daśa.
english: O respected one, then thousands of people, their eyes filled with tears, fled in all ten directions after hearing the king's lamentation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 40. O respected one, then thousands of people, their eyes filled with tears, fled in all ten directions after hearing the king's lamentation.

sanskrit: ससागरवना घोरा पृथिवी सचराचरा ।चचालाथ सनिर्ह्रादा दिशश्चैवाविलाभवन् ॥४१॥
transliteration: sasāgaravanā ghorā pṛthivī sacarācarā ,cacālātha sanirhrādā diśaścaivāvilābhavan.
english: The terrible earth, along with its oceans, forests, and all animate and inanimate beings, then trembled with a great roar, and the directions indeed became turbulent.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 41. The terrible earth, along with its oceans, forests, and all animate and inanimate beings, then trembled with a great roar, and the directions indeed became turbulent.

sanskrit: ते द्रोणपुत्रमासाद्य यथावृत्तं न्यवेदयन् ।व्यवहारं गदायुद्धे पार्थिवस्य च घातनम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: te droṇaputramāsādya yathāvṛttaṁ nyavedayan ,vyavahāraṁ gadāyuddhe pārthivasya ca ghātanam.
english: Having approached the son of Droṇa, they reported the entire sequence of events: the conduct of the mace-fight and the killing of the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 42. Having approached the son of Droṇa, they reported the entire sequence of events: the conduct of the mace-fight and the killing of the king.

sanskrit: तदाख्याय ततः सर्वे द्रोणपुत्रस्य भारत ।ध्यात्वा च सुचिरं कालं जग्मुरार्ता यथागतम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: tadākhyāya tataḥ sarve droṇaputrasya bhārata ,dhyātvā ca suciraṁ kālaṁ jagmurārtā yathāgatam.
english: O descendant of Bharata, having reported that to the son of Droṇa, all of them, distressed, then reflected (dhyāna) for a very long time, and departed the way they had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 63 Verse 43. O descendant of Bharata, having reported that to the son of Droṇa, all of them, distressed, then reflected (dhyāna) for a very long time, and departed the way they had arrived.

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।वातिकानां सकाशात्तु श्रुत्वा दुर्योधनं हतम् ।हतशिष्टास्ततो राजन्कौरवाणां महारथाः ॥१॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,vātikānāṁ sakāśāttu śrutvā duryodhanaṁ hatam ,hataśiṣṭāstato rājankauravāṇāṁ mahārathāḥ.
english: Sanjaya said: "O King, having heard from the messengers that Duryodhana was killed, then the remaining great charioteers of the Kauravas - "
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 1. Sanjaya said: "O King, having heard from the messengers that Duryodhana was killed, then the remaining great charioteers of the Kauravas - "

sanskrit: विनिर्भिन्नाः शितैर्बाणैर्गदातोमरशक्तिभिः ।अश्वत्थामा कृपश्चैव कृतवर्मा च सात्वतः ।त्वरिता जवनैरश्वैरायोधनमुपागमन् ॥२॥
transliteration: vinirbhinnāḥ śitairbāṇairgadātomaraśaktibhiḥ ,aśvatthāmā kṛpaścaiva kṛtavarmā ca sātvataḥ ,tvaritā javanairaśvairāyodhanamupāgaman.
english: - pierced by sharp arrows, maces, javelins, and spears - Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, and Kṛtavarmā the Sātvata, hastened on swift horses and approached the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 2. - pierced by sharp arrows, maces, javelins, and spears - Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, and Kṛtavarmā the Sātvata, hastened on swift horses and approached the battlefield.

sanskrit: तत्रापश्यन्महात्मानं धार्तराष्ट्रं निपातितम् ।प्रभग्नं वायुवेगेन महाशालं यथा वने ॥३॥
transliteration: tatrāpaśyanmahātmānaṁ dhārtarāṣṭraṁ nipātitam ,prabhagnaṁ vāyuvegena mahāśālaṁ yathā vane.
english: There they saw the great-souled son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) lying fallen, broken by the force of the wind, just like a great śāla tree in a forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 3. There they saw the great-souled son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) lying fallen, broken by the force of the wind, just like a great śāla tree in a forest.

sanskrit: भूमौ विवेष्टमानं तं रुधिरेण समुक्षितम् ।महागजमिवारण्ये व्याधेन विनिपातितम् ॥४॥
transliteration: bhūmau viveṣṭamānaṁ taṁ rudhireṇa samukṣitam ,mahāgajamivāraṇye vyādhena vinipātitam.
english: They saw him writhing on the ground, soaked in blood, like a great elephant struck down by a hunter in a forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 4. They saw him writhing on the ground, soaked in blood, like a great elephant struck down by a hunter in a forest.

sanskrit: विवर्तमानं बहुशो रुधिरौघपरिप्लुतम् ।यदृच्छया निपतितं चक्रमादित्यगोचरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: vivartamānaṁ bahuśo rudhiraughapariplutam ,yadṛcchayā nipatitaṁ cakramādityagocaram.
english: Repeatedly struggling and turning, completely drenched in torrents of blood, fallen by chance, like a sun-bright disc.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 5. Repeatedly struggling and turning, completely drenched in torrents of blood, fallen by chance, like a sun-bright disc.

sanskrit: महावातसमुत्थेन संशुष्कमिव सागरम् ।पूर्णचन्द्रमिव व्योम्नि तुषारावृतमण्डलम् ॥६॥
transliteration: mahāvātasamutthena saṁśuṣkamiva sāgaram ,pūrṇacandramiva vyomni tuṣārāvṛtamaṇḍalam.
english: Like an ocean dried up by a great wind, or like a full moon in the sky whose orb is covered with frost.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 6. Like an ocean dried up by a great wind, or like a full moon in the sky whose orb is covered with frost.

sanskrit: रेणुध्वस्तं दीर्घभुजं मातङ्गसमविक्रमम् ।वृतं भूतगणैर्घोरैः क्रव्यादैश्च समन्ततः ।यथा धनं लिप्समानैर्भृत्यैर्नृपतिसत्तमम् ॥७॥
transliteration: reṇudhvastaṁ dīrghabhujaṁ mātaṅgasamavikramam ,vṛtaṁ bhūtagaṇairghoraiḥ kravyādaiśca samantataḥ ,yathā dhanaṁ lipsamānairbhṛtyairnṛpatisattamam.
english: Covered in dust, long-armed, having prowess equal to an elephant; surrounded on all sides by terrifying hordes of spirits and flesh-eaters, just as an excellent king is [surrounded] by servants desiring wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 7. Covered in dust, long-armed, having prowess equal to an elephant; surrounded on all sides by terrifying hordes of spirits and flesh-eaters, just as an excellent king is [surrounded] by servants desiring wealth.

sanskrit: भ्रुकुटीकृतवक्त्रान्तं क्रोधादुद्वृत्तचक्षुषम् ।सामर्षं तं नरव्याघ्रं व्याघ्रं निपतितं यथा ॥८॥
transliteration: bhrukuṭīkṛtavaktrāntaṁ krodhādudvṛttacakṣuṣam ,sāmarṣaṁ taṁ naravyāghraṁ vyāghraṁ nipatitaṁ yathā.
english: With the corners of his mouth furrowed in a frown, and eyes rolled up from anger, that tiger among men was filled with indignation, just like a fallen tiger.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 8. With the corners of his mouth furrowed in a frown, and eyes rolled up from anger, that tiger among men was filled with indignation, just like a fallen tiger.

sanskrit: ते तु दृष्ट्वा महेष्वासा भूतले पतितं नृपम् ।मोहमभ्यागमन्सर्वे कृपप्रभृतयो रथाः ॥९॥
transliteration: te tu dṛṣṭvā maheṣvāsā bhūtale patitaṁ nṛpam ,mohamabhyāgamansarve kṛpaprabhṛtayo rathāḥ.
english: But they, the great archers and charioteers led by Kripa, having seen the king fallen on the ground, all became overcome with unconsciousness (moha).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 9. But they, the great archers and charioteers led by Kripa, having seen the king fallen on the ground, all became overcome with unconsciousness (moha).

sanskrit: अवतीर्य रथेभ्यस्तु प्राद्रवन्राजसंनिधौ ।दुर्योधनं च संप्रेक्ष्य सर्वे भूमावुपाविशन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: avatīrya rathebhyastu prādravanrājasaṁnidhau ,duryodhanaṁ ca saṁprekṣya sarve bhūmāvupāviśan.
english: Descending from their chariots, they ran forth into the king's presence. And having seen Duryodhana, all of them sat down on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 10. Descending from their chariots, they ran forth into the king's presence. And having seen Duryodhana, all of them sat down on the ground.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणिर्महाराज बाष्पपूर्णेक्षणः श्वसन् ।उवाच भरतश्रेष्ठं सर्वलोकेश्वरेश्वरम् ॥११॥
transliteration: tato drauṇirmahārāja bāṣpapūrṇekṣaṇaḥ śvasan ,uvāca bharataśreṣṭhaṁ sarvalokeśvareśvaram.
english: Then, O great king, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), with eyes full of tears and breathing heavily, spoke to the best of the Bharatas, who was the lord of the lords of all worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 11. Then, O great king, Droṇa's son (Drauṇi), with eyes full of tears and breathing heavily, spoke to the best of the Bharatas, who was the lord of the lords of all worlds.

sanskrit: न नूनं विद्यतेऽसह्यं मानुष्ये किंचिदेव हि ।यत्र त्वं पुरुषव्याघ्र शेषे पांसुषु रूषितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: na nūnaṁ vidyate'sahyaṁ mānuṣye kiṁcideva hi ,yatra tvaṁ puruṣavyāghra śeṣe pāṁsuṣu rūṣitaḥ.
english: Indeed, nothing is truly unbearable in human existence, since you, O tiger among men, are lying covered in dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 12. Indeed, nothing is truly unbearable in human existence, since you, O tiger among men, are lying covered in dust.

sanskrit: भूत्वा हि नृपतिः पूर्वं समाज्ञाप्य च मेदिनीम् ।कथमेकोऽद्य राजेन्द्र तिष्ठसे निर्जने वने ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhūtvā hi nṛpatiḥ pūrvaṁ samājñāpya ca medinīm ,kathameko'dya rājendra tiṣṭhase nirjane vane.
english: Having indeed been a king previously and having commanded the entire earth, how is it, O King (rājendra), that today you remain alone in a desolate forest?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 13. Having indeed been a king previously and having commanded the entire earth, how is it, O King (rājendra), that today you remain alone in a desolate forest?

sanskrit: दुःशासनं न पश्यामि नापि कर्णं महारथम् ।नापि तान्सुहृदः सर्वान्किमिदं भरतर्षभ ॥१४॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanaṁ na paśyāmi nāpi karṇaṁ mahāratham ,nāpi tānsuhṛdaḥ sarvānkimidaṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: I do not see Duḥśāsana, nor the great warrior (mahāratha) Karṇa, nor all those friends. What is this, O Best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha)?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 14. I do not see Duḥśāsana, nor the great warrior (mahāratha) Karṇa, nor all those friends. What is this, O Best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha)?

sanskrit: दुःखं नूनं कृतान्तस्य गतिं ज्ञातुं कथंचन ।लोकानां च भवान्यत्र शेते पांसुषु रूषितः ॥१५॥
transliteration: duḥkhaṁ nūnaṁ kṛtāntasya gatiṁ jñātuṁ kathaṁcana ,lokānāṁ ca bhavānyatra śete pāṁsuṣu rūṣitaḥ.
english: It is indeed difficult to truly know the course of destiny (kṛtānta). And where you, revered sir, lie covered in dust among the common people (loka).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 15. It is indeed difficult to truly know the course of destiny (kṛtānta). And where you, revered sir, lie covered in dust among the common people (loka).

sanskrit: एष मूर्धावसिक्तानामग्रे गत्वा परंतपः ।सतृणं ग्रसते पांसुं पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: eṣa mūrdhāvasiktānāmagre gatvā paraṁtapaḥ ,satṛṇaṁ grasate pāṁsuṁ paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: This tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), after having gone before those whose heads were anointed (mūrdhāvasikta), now eats dust with grass. Behold the change (paryaya) of time (kāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 16. This tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), after having gone before those whose heads were anointed (mūrdhāvasikta), now eats dust with grass. Behold the change (paryaya) of time (kāla).

sanskrit: क्व ते तदमलं छत्रं व्यजनं क्व च पार्थिव ।सा च ते महती सेना क्व गता पार्थिवोत्तम ॥१७॥
transliteration: kva te tadamalaṁ chatraṁ vyajanaṁ kva ca pārthiva ,sā ca te mahatī senā kva gatā pārthivottama.
english: O King, where are your spotless royal umbrella and fan? And O best among kings, where has that mighty army of yours gone?
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 17. O King, where are your spotless royal umbrella and fan? And O best among kings, where has that mighty army of yours gone?

sanskrit: दुर्विज्ञेया गतिर्नूनं कार्याणां कारणान्तरे ।यद्वै लोकगुरुर्भूत्वा भवानेतां दशां गतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: durvijñeyā gatirnūnaṁ kāryāṇāṁ kāraṇāntare ,yadvai lokagururbhūtvā bhavānetāṁ daśāṁ gataḥ.
english: Indeed, the course of affairs is difficult to comprehend, influenced by various underlying causes; for you, who became a preceptor (guru) to the world, have now reached this condition.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 18. Indeed, the course of affairs is difficult to comprehend, influenced by various underlying causes; for you, who became a preceptor (guru) to the world, have now reached this condition.

sanskrit: अध्रुवा सर्वमर्त्येषु ध्रुवं श्रीरुपलक्ष्यते ।भवतो व्यसनं दृष्ट्वा शक्रविस्पर्धिनो भृशम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: adhruvā sarvamartyeṣu dhruvaṁ śrīrupalakṣyate ,bhavato vyasanaṁ dṛṣṭvā śakravispardhino bhṛśam.
english: Prosperity is certainly observed to be transient among all mortals, especially after seeing your immense misfortune, you who were a rival of Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 19. Prosperity is certainly observed to be transient among all mortals, especially after seeing your immense misfortune, you who were a rival of Indra.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा दुःखितस्य विशेषतः ।उवाच राजन्पुत्रस्ते प्राप्तकालमिदं वचः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā duḥkhitasya viśeṣataḥ ,uvāca rājanputraste prāptakālamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Having heard those words of his, especially since he was distressed, your son, O King, then spoke these opportune words.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 20. Having heard those words of his, especially since he was distressed, your son, O King, then spoke these opportune words.

sanskrit: विमृज्य नेत्रे पाणिभ्यां शोकजं बाष्पमुत्सृजन् ।कृपादीन्स तदा वीरान्सर्वानेव नराधिपः ॥२१॥
transliteration: vimṛjya netre pāṇibhyāṁ śokajaṁ bāṣpamutsṛjan ,kṛpādīnsa tadā vīrānsarvāneva narādhipaḥ.
english: Then, the king, wiping his eyes with his hands and shedding tears born of sorrow, [addressed] all those heroes, including Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 21. Then, the king, wiping his eyes with his hands and shedding tears born of sorrow, [addressed] all those heroes, including Kṛpa.

sanskrit: ईदृशो मर्त्यधर्मोऽयं धात्रा निर्दिष्ट उच्यते ।विनाशः सर्वभूतानां कालपर्यायकारितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: īdṛśo martyadharmo'yaṁ dhātrā nirdiṣṭa ucyate ,vināśaḥ sarvabhūtānāṁ kālaparyāyakāritaḥ.
english: This mortal intrinsic nature (dharma) is declared to be ordained by the Creator. The destruction of all beings is caused by the succession of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 22. This mortal intrinsic nature (dharma) is declared to be ordained by the Creator. The destruction of all beings is caused by the succession of time.

sanskrit: सोऽयं मां समनुप्राप्तः प्रत्यक्षं भवतां हि यः ।पृथिवीं पालयित्वाहमेतां निष्ठामुपागतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ māṁ samanuprāptaḥ pratyakṣaṁ bhavatāṁ hi yaḥ ,pṛthivīṁ pālayitvāhametāṁ niṣṭhāmupāgataḥ.
english: Indeed, this very [destruction] which is manifest before your eyes, has now reached me. Having protected this earth, I have arrived at this end.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 23. Indeed, this very [destruction] which is manifest before your eyes, has now reached me. Having protected this earth, I have arrived at this end.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या नाहं परावृत्तो युद्धे कस्यांचिदापदि ।दिष्ट्याहं निहतः पापैश्छलेनैव विशेषतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā nāhaṁ parāvṛtto yuddhe kasyāṁcidāpadi ,diṣṭyāhaṁ nihataḥ pāpaiśchalenaiva viśeṣataḥ.
english: Luckily, I never retreated in any calamity during battle. Fortunately, I was killed by the wicked, especially through deceit alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 24. Luckily, I never retreated in any calamity during battle. Fortunately, I was killed by the wicked, especially through deceit alone.

sanskrit: उत्साहश्च कृतो नित्यं मया दिष्ट्या युयुत्सता ।दिष्ट्या चास्मि हतो युद्धे निहतज्ञातिबान्धवः ॥२५॥
transliteration: utsāhaśca kṛto nityaṁ mayā diṣṭyā yuyutsatā ,diṣṭyā cāsmi hato yuddhe nihatajñātibāndhavaḥ.
english: Fortunately, I always maintained my enthusiasm (utsāha) for battle, desiring to fight. And fortunately, I have been killed in this war, having seen my kinsmen and relatives (jñāti-bāndhava) also slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 25. Fortunately, I always maintained my enthusiasm (utsāha) for battle, desiring to fight. And fortunately, I have been killed in this war, having seen my kinsmen and relatives (jñāti-bāndhava) also slain.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या च वोऽहं पश्यामि मुक्तानस्माज्जनक्षयात् ।स्वस्तियुक्तांश्च कल्यांश्च तन्मे प्रियमनुत्तमम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā ca vo'haṁ paśyāmi muktānasmājjanakṣayāt ,svastiyuktāṁśca kalyāṁśca tanme priyamanuttamam.
english: And fortunately, I see all of you freed from this devastation of people, safe and healthy. That is my supreme delight (priyam) and unsurpassed.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 26. And fortunately, I see all of you freed from this devastation of people, safe and healthy. That is my supreme delight (priyam) and unsurpassed.

sanskrit: मा भवन्तोऽनुतप्यन्तां सौहृदान्निधनेन मे ।यदि वेदाः प्रमाणं वो जिता लोका मयाक्षयाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: mā bhavanto'nutapyantāṁ sauhṛdānnidhanena me ,yadi vedāḥ pramāṇaṁ vo jitā lokā mayākṣayāḥ.
english: May you not grieve for my death out of friendship (sauhṛda). If the Vedas are your authority (pramāṇa), then imperishable (akṣaya) worlds have been conquered by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 27. May you not grieve for my death out of friendship (sauhṛda). If the Vedas are your authority (pramāṇa), then imperishable (akṣaya) worlds have been conquered by me.

sanskrit: मन्यमानः प्रभावं च कृष्णस्यामिततेजसः ।तेन न च्यावितश्चाहं क्षत्रधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: manyamānaḥ prabhāvaṁ ca kṛṣṇasyāmitatejasaḥ ,tena na cyāvitaścāhaṁ kṣatradharmātsvanuṣṭhitāt.
english: And considering the power (prabhāva) of Krishna, whose splendor (tejas) is immeasurable, I was not dislodged from my well-observed natural law (dharma) as a warrior (kṣatra).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 28. And considering the power (prabhāva) of Krishna, whose splendor (tejas) is immeasurable, I was not dislodged from my well-observed natural law (dharma) as a warrior (kṣatra).

sanskrit: स मया समनुप्राप्तो नास्मि शोच्यः कथंचन ।कृतं भवद्भिः सदृशमनुरूपमिवात्मनः ।यतितं विजये नित्यं दैवं तु दुरतिक्रमम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sa mayā samanuprāpto nāsmi śocyaḥ kathaṁcana ,kṛtaṁ bhavadbhiḥ sadṛśamanurūpamivātmanaḥ ,yatitaṁ vijaye nityaṁ daivaṁ tu duratikramam.
english: I have reached him (the desired state), and thus I am not to be grieved for in any way. What you all have done is fitting and appropriate to your own intrinsic nature (ātman). You always strived for victory, but destiny is indeed insurmountable.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 29. I have reached him (the desired state), and thus I am not to be grieved for in any way. What you all have done is fitting and appropriate to your own intrinsic nature (ātman). You always strived for victory, but destiny is indeed insurmountable.

sanskrit: एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं बाष्पव्याकुललोचनः ।तूष्णीं बभूव राजेन्द्र रुजासौ विह्वलो भृशम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: etāvaduktvā vacanaṁ bāṣpavyākulalocanaḥ ,tūṣṇīṁ babhūva rājendra rujāsau vihvalo bhṛśam.
english: Having spoken these words, with his eyes agitated by tears, he became silent, O king of kings (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), for he was extremely overcome by pain.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 30. Having spoken these words, with his eyes agitated by tears, he became silent, O king of kings (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), for he was extremely overcome by pain.

sanskrit: तथा तु दृष्ट्वा राजानं बाष्पशोकसमन्वितम् ।द्रौणिः क्रोधेन जज्वाल यथा वह्निर्जगत्क्षये ॥३१॥
transliteration: tathā tu dṛṣṭvā rājānaṁ bāṣpaśokasamanvitam ,drauṇiḥ krodhena jajvāla yathā vahnirjagatkṣaye.
english: But having seen the king thus afflicted with tears and sorrow, Drauṇi blazed with anger, just like the fire at the dissolution of the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 31. But having seen the king thus afflicted with tears and sorrow, Drauṇi blazed with anger, just like the fire at the dissolution of the world.

sanskrit: स तु क्रोधसमाविष्टः पाणौ पाणिं निपीड्य च ।बाष्पविह्वलया वाचा राजानमिदमब्रवीत् ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa tu krodhasamāviṣṭaḥ pāṇau pāṇiṁ nipīḍya ca ,bāṣpavihvalayā vācā rājānamidamabravīt.
english: But he, overcome by anger and pressing his hand with his (other) hand, spoke this to the king with a voice choked with tears.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 32. But he, overcome by anger and pressing his hand with his (other) hand, spoke this to the king with a voice choked with tears.

sanskrit: पिता मे निहतः क्षुद्रैः सुनृशंसेन कर्मणा ।न तथा तेन तप्यामि यथा राजंस्त्वयाद्य वै ॥३३॥
transliteration: pitā me nihataḥ kṣudraiḥ sunṛśaṁsena karmaṇā ,na tathā tena tapyāmi yathā rājaṁstvayādya vai.
english: My father was killed by ignoble people through an extremely cruel deed (karma). O King, I do not grieve so much by that (death) as I do by you today.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 33. My father was killed by ignoble people through an extremely cruel deed (karma). O King, I do not grieve so much by that (death) as I do by you today.

sanskrit: शृणु चेदं वचो मह्यं सत्येन वदतः प्रभो ।इष्टापूर्तेन दानेन धर्मेण सुकृतेन च ॥३४॥
transliteration: śṛṇu cedaṁ vaco mahyaṁ satyena vadataḥ prabho ,iṣṭāpūrtena dānena dharmeṇa sukṛtena ca.
english: O Lord, listen to these words of mine, spoken truthfully. By (the merit of) sacrifices and charitable deeds (iṣṭāpūrta), by giving (dāna), by natural law (dharma), and by virtuous acts.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 34. O Lord, listen to these words of mine, spoken truthfully. By (the merit of) sacrifices and charitable deeds (iṣṭāpūrta), by giving (dāna), by natural law (dharma), and by virtuous acts.

sanskrit: अद्याहं सर्वपाञ्चालान्वासुदेवस्य पश्यतः ।सर्वोपायैर्हि नेष्यामि प्रेतराजनिवेशनम् ।अनुज्ञां तु महाराज भवान्मे दातुमर्हति ॥३५॥
transliteration: adyāhaṁ sarvapāñcālānvāsudevasya paśyataḥ ,sarvopāyairhi neṣyāmi pretarājaniveśanam ,anujñāṁ tu mahārāja bhavānme dātumarhati.
english: Today, by all means, I will surely lead all the Pāñcālas to the abode of the king of the departed (Yama), in the very sight of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa). But, O Great King, you should grant me permission for this.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 35. Today, by all means, I will surely lead all the Pāñcālas to the abode of the king of the departed (Yama), in the very sight of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa). But, O Great King, you should grant me permission for this.

sanskrit: इति श्रुत्वा तु वचनं द्रोणपुत्रस्य कौरवः ।मनसः प्रीतिजननं कृपं वचनमब्रवीत् ।आचार्य शीघ्रं कलशं जलपूर्णं समानय ॥३६॥
transliteration: iti śrutvā tu vacanaṁ droṇaputrasya kauravaḥ ,manasaḥ prītijananaṁ kṛpaṁ vacanamabravīt ,ācārya śīghraṁ kalaśaṁ jalapūrṇaṁ samānaya.
english: Having heard these words of Drona's son, the Kaurava (Duryodhana) spoke these joy-producing words to Kṛpa: "O Teacher, quickly bring a jar filled with water."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 36. Having heard these words of Drona's son, the Kaurava (Duryodhana) spoke these joy-producing words to Kṛpa: "O Teacher, quickly bring a jar filled with water."

sanskrit: स तद्वचनमाज्ञाय राज्ञो ब्राह्मणसत्तमः ।कलशं पूर्णमादाय राज्ञोऽन्तिकमुपागमत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sa tadvacanamājñāya rājño brāhmaṇasattamaḥ ,kalaśaṁ pūrṇamādāya rājño'ntikamupāgamat.
english: That excellent brahmin, having understood the king's command, took a full pitcher and approached the king's presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 37. That excellent brahmin, having understood the king's command, took a full pitcher and approached the king's presence.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीन्महाराज पुत्रस्तव विशां पते ।ममाज्ञया द्विजश्रेष्ठ द्रोणपुत्रोऽभिषिच्यताम् ।सेनापत्येन भद्रं ते मम चेदिच्छसि प्रियम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tamabravīnmahārāja putrastava viśāṁ pate ,mamājñayā dvijaśreṣṭha droṇaputro'bhiṣicyatām ,senāpatyena bhadraṁ te mama cedicchasi priyam.
english: O great king, lord of the people, he said to him, "By my command, let your son, Droṇa's son, that best of brahmins, be consecrated as commander. If you desire my favor, may good fortune be yours."
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 38. O great king, lord of the people, he said to him, "By my command, let your son, Droṇa's son, that best of brahmins, be consecrated as commander. If you desire my favor, may good fortune be yours."

sanskrit: राज्ञो नियोगाद्योद्धव्यं ब्राह्मणेन विशेषतः ।वर्तता क्षत्रधर्मेण ह्येवं धर्मविदो विदुः ॥३९॥
transliteration: rājño niyogādyoddhavyaṁ brāhmaṇena viśeṣataḥ ,vartatā kṣatradharmeṇa hyevaṁ dharmavido viduḥ.
english: By the king's command, a brahmin should especially fight. Indeed, those who understand (natural) law (dharma) know that one should act according to the intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma) of a warrior in such a situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 39. By the king's command, a brahmin should especially fight. Indeed, those who understand (natural) law (dharma) know that one should act according to the intrinsic nature (kṣatradharma) of a warrior in such a situation.

sanskrit: राज्ञस्तु वचनं श्रुत्वा कृपः शारद्वतस्ततः ।द्रौणिं राज्ञो नियोगेन सेनापत्येऽभ्यषेचयत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: rājñastu vacanaṁ śrutvā kṛpaḥ śāradvatastataḥ ,drauṇiṁ rājño niyogena senāpatye'bhyaṣecayat.
english: But then, having heard the king's command, Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, consecrated Droṇa's son as commander, according to the king's appointment.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 40. But then, having heard the king's command, Kṛpa, the son of Śaradvan, consecrated Droṇa's son as commander, according to the king's appointment.

sanskrit: सोऽभिषिक्तो महाराज परिष्वज्य नृपोत्तमम् ।प्रययौ सिंहनादेन दिशः सर्वा विनादयन् ॥४१॥
transliteration: so'bhiṣikto mahārāja pariṣvajya nṛpottamam ,prayayau siṁhanādena diśaḥ sarvā vinādayan.
english: O great King, he (Aśvatthāman), having been consecrated, embraced the best of kings (Duryodhana) and then departed, making all directions resound with a lion's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 41. O great King, he (Aśvatthāman), having been consecrated, embraced the best of kings (Duryodhana) and then departed, making all directions resound with a lion's roar.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनोऽपि राजेन्द्र शोणितौघपरिप्लुतः ।तां निशां प्रतिपेदेऽथ सर्वभूतभयावहाम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: duryodhano'pi rājendra śoṇitaughapariplutaḥ ,tāṁ niśāṁ pratipede'tha sarvabhūtabhayāvahām.
english: O King of kings, Duryodhana also, bathed in streams of blood, then experienced that night which was terrifying to all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 42. O King of kings, Duryodhana also, bathed in streams of blood, then experienced that night which was terrifying to all beings.

sanskrit: अपक्रम्य तु ते तूर्णं तस्मादायोधनान्नृप ।शोकसंविग्नमनसश्चिन्ताध्यानपराभवन् ॥४३॥
transliteration: apakramya tu te tūrṇaṁ tasmādāyodhanānnṛpa ,śokasaṁvignamanasaścintādhyānaparābhavan.
english: But O King, they (the remaining warriors), having quickly retreated from that battlefield, their minds agitated by sorrow, were overcome by anxious contemplation (dhyāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Śalya Parva Chapter 64 Verse 43. But O King, they (the remaining warriors), having quickly retreated from that battlefield, their minds agitated by sorrow, were overcome by anxious contemplation (dhyāna).
